text:
My iPhone 6 was hacked and totally locked up. iHospital unlocked it, cleaned it and restored it from iCloud for me in less than half an hour.  They said they'd never seen anything like what was appearing on my locked screen - clearly a deliberate hack into my phone.  

Sean Odell, the store manager at the iHospital on Kennedy Blvd. in South Tampa was SO great to work with and saved my phone!  I highly recommend them!!

Recently hired Terminix to tent my home they left my attic exhaust fan on pulling out all the gas I come home to a house full of live termites. They refuse to admit they did any wrong. They sent somebody out that insulted my intelligence by telling me the exhaust fan would not draw the gas out of the attic. The exhaust fan pulls 415 cubic feet per minute of air so it would have pulled out all the gas from the attic where the termites are in a matter of a few minutes. Their employee talk to me very condescending. I told him about a piece of my rain gutter in my outside light but the guys that put the tent on broke he tried to tell me they did not work for Terminix even though they're contracted by Terminix and all wore shirts saying Terminix and drove a truck that said Terminix LOL I will be talking to a lawyer do yourself a favor don't put yourself what I have went through do not hire Terminix the only reason I gave them one star is because that was the lowest rating if possible I would have gave them negative 5 stars

The service, owners, staff, and juices are THE BEST! I'm hooked- and my fiancé is hooked as well. Our favorites include: Orange You Glad, We Got The Beet, and Sweet Greens. We love to order/pick up their essential cleanse (5 flavors), take it home and use it as meal replacements for a few days.
Tips:
- Order ahead and pick up if you are stopping in for more than one juice/smoothie; the line can get really long
- It's not cheap $5.50-$7.50 per juice; but worth every delicious penny. It's also better fuel for you/your body than a Happy Meal/Taco Bell

The glass is complimentary with your wine tasting! =D

Loved the indoor seating area!  The lights and being surrounded by wine barrels really set the mood =)

Great place to have a bite or breakfast.  Tons of space in back court yard to relax and maintain the 6 feet distance.  Great coffee. Free wifi and the staff was amazing.  Cannot go wrong coming to this local gem.

They have a F rating on BBB.  I should have known better.  Craigslist is more reliable.  I bought appliances from them years ago and they worked great.  But fridge died and I called them.  Guy that use to work there doesn't anymore, that's probably the problem.  Bought another fridge from them and immediately the thermostat wouldn't work, it's either on all the time or turns off.  Calls and calls and they won't come to the house to fix or replace.  Waste of money.

Used a travelzoo deal for a romantic dinner. Really enjoyed the following:

- city views
- intimate environment
- food
- friendly staff

I don't think I would go here without a deal since the total $ i spent using the deal was around $120-140.

We were here at the same time as the st. Patty pub crawl and the service was still excellent. We didn't have to wait long on drinks or food. Our server was personable even though she was extremely busy. Glad to stopped here for a beer!

Love this place. Probably have been here 10-14 times and I am not sure why I haven't done a review yet but I guess today is the day! The food is so delicious. The staff is all friendly. I have pretty much always had great service, but today we had Carol I believe her name was and I would say the service was outstanding! She was everything I would expect a server to be and it was nice to have that good of service! We will continue coming back.

My wife and I had a couples massage here yesterday and it was great! Both therapists listened to the kind of massage we were hoping for and delivered exactly that. Upon checkout, the front desk was not pushy, either. We would definitely go back to this location! Much better than some of the other membership-based spa businesses in the area.
love the homemade salsa and chili con queso. sad that they changed the chips they serve.
Happy that Riverview finally got a car wash that you can get your vehicle detailed or just exterior.. the employees are pretty much friendly also ..
Very authentic, tasty and fresh. Food just like their other location on Tarpon Ave. but larger and more tables for dining in.

Absolutely delicious and a wonderful dining experience. Everything we ordered was yummy: oysters, Parmesan-crusted sea bass, lobster risotto, cioppino and seared blackened tuna. Service was attentive and pleasant. We sat inside but our table of 4 was spaced far from the others diners. The next time that we are in Santa Barbara, we will be back!

It's insanely loud and I guess I forgot how overpriced an arcade was. But overall the layout was nice, they have games, bowling and pool. The later you come it becomes a kid free zone so definitely plan on coming back for a fun adult only game night. There is a bar as well for anyone who would like to enjoy a drink with a game.

My wife & I had dinner here at 6:30 pm on a Friday night. We were seated immediately and greeted by James, our waiter, who was extremely personable.
He was extremely knowledgeable and provided excellent recommendations in both wine and entrees. We were delighted with both.

The restaurant started to fill up about 7 pm but the service continued to be very good.
Our table was cleared and check brought in a timely manner.

The jazz groups play from 7-10 on Friday & Saturday nights but we had other plans and couldn't stay. We did like the way the jazz bar is separated by glass from the restaurant so the music is not overwhelming.

We recommend this venue and plan to return again soon.

7 days a week! On my day off it have to be twice a day! 
That how much i love dingtea 
My favorite you can try: 
1st: signature milktea 30% sweetness + crytal boba + honey boba!
2nd : strawberry yogurt 50% sweetness +aloe vera + honey boba!
3rd: olong milktea 50% sweetness more tea than milk + honey boba! 
4th : kumquat lemon ice tea reg sweet + aloe vera! 
Enjoyyy
True professionals. These people know what they are doing. Great customer service!
LOVE City Market. There is something for everyone here. My personal favorite is the Crepe restaurant. Awesome environment and so many wonderful restaurants to choose from.

I've been going to Bory for about 2 years now and every haircut he does not seem to disappoint me. He'll give great recommendations if you don't know what type of haircut that you want. Haircut always come out sharp/clean. His shop is a nice chilling and clean environment + great music choices. I highly recommend going to Blend Lab if you're looking for a great cut!

If you have mental issues, stay clear of COPE!   I would actually give this facility a minus zero. 
I was being seen at Green Valley Office and was very happy with my video therapist who was dealing with my issues. I was having a problem with finding the right medication as I was having allergic reactions to all that were prescribed (a total of 5).  My therapist suggested a DNA test to see what meds would be compatible.  Unfortunately, I had a negative reaction to the new meds also, back to square one again.   COPE  "suits" decided to send my therapist to the Tucson office and that therapist was sent to GV office. I was very comfortable with my therapist and trusted him.  I am bipolar 2, mostly dealing with depression but do have manic moments.  I had an appt. to see the "new" therapist last week and was unable to keep the appt. due to a bad case of food poisoning, so I needed to reschedule.  The "earliest"  appt. was not until October 14th.  Unacceptable!  Have been off any medication for over a month.  Was told I could come in as a "walk in" and if there was a cancellation, I would be seen.  So I could sit there for hours, hoping to be seen or not.  I also called my "Recovery Coach", left a message letting him know my situation and that I was going through a very rough time and needed help.  Never received a return phone call.  
To say I am disgusted is an understatement.  My problem is with the way things are scheduled.  The receptionist is the one who schedules these appts.  My past history is one of working in a doctor's office scheduling  patients to be seen.  I ALWAYS kept an open slot  for emergencies. This is not the case at COPE.   I find the receptionist at COPE to be apathetic and unhelpful.  Too bad if you are in crisis and need to be seen ASAP.  
I am terminating my ties with this facility.  My suggestion to anyone thinking of going to the Green Valley Office of COPE is to look elsewhere for help.

Buco is a lovely restaurant with charm and atmosphere you would expect in a downtown location in the suburbs (great for those of us who don't want to make the trek to places like Whyte or downtown for dinner!). I had a moderately sized dinner party here (eight people total) for my birthday and while I loved the food and atmosphere, our service was dismally non-existent. 

We got the meat and cheese board to start, and my husband and I shared the pappardelle pasta and the margharita pizza. The pizza was light, with a good chewy crust and good sauce, and the pasta was rich and tasty.  We brought home leftovers, which is always a great sign. The food was flavored well and on par with some of the other great Italian restaurants in Edmonton. 

But, the service. After we got our menus we were sitting and talking for 15 minutes until someone finally went and found our server so we could place our order.  Throughout the meal no one checked on us, and after we finished the carafes set out on the table when we arrived, we were all looking around with empty water glasses.  After about 2 hours when we wanted to leave, someone again had to go get our server so we could get bills and pay.  Three other people in my group commented on how poor the service was and it was a low point in what should have been a great dining experience. The restaurant was largely empty so I can't blame it on understaffing or a busy night. 

I'm looking forward to giving Buco another chance because I liked it so much, but they really need to do better.

There's 2 areas you can be seated. Expect to feel pretty cramped in both. The larger of the two was poorly lit and just felt off. The service was good, though. Everyone was friendly and our waiter was especially kind and helped us make decisions on what to choose since we aren't from New Orleans. The food portions looked small, but don't let that fool you. We left absolutely stuffed. The gator bites as an appetizer were well seasoned and perfect. I had the taste of New Orleans trio plate and I couldn't finish it. Delicious.

Came for a special anniversary dinner and it was a great experience. I appreciated how they had non spirited cocktails which was refreshing. We split the deviled eggs and crab dip appetizer to start. Both were amazing the dip was my favorite. The main course was rainbow trout which consisted of chewy ancient farro and barley with the toasted hazelnut, the perfectly cooked fish, and the green sauce. The flavors all complimented each other. We picked one dessert after havinf a hard time deciding and the waiter was so kind to bring us the other dessert we wanted on the house! Thank you for the hospitality and amazing food.

Stopped in here on a lunch break to pick up a wrap.  I went with the thai chicken on a spinach wrap.  
If you check in with yelp, you get a free Lager or Bud!  It was a pleasant surprise while waiting for my wrap and my to-go order was ready before I could get halfway through it.  Quick service!

The wrap was pretty good, but I found the thai peanut sauce to be very bland.  I liked that the chicken was shredded, so there were no chunks falling out of it.  It was an okay meal for $10.  I am definitely interested to try other wraps in hopes that their sauces aren't so bland.

Explored Dunedin this morning  and stumbled upon Crown & Bull. The outside patio and live music free is in immediately and we had a wonderful brunch. The vibe and outside seating is a great way to enjoy the Florida weather and this great spot. 

My mom LOVED the coffee, like wanted to buy it she liked it so much. I had the best cafe con leche and it a unique twist but tasted classic. 

Our server was great and the overall vibe of the staff and place was incredible. The people bringing our food out to all of the tables were so kind and truly added to the pleasant environment.

The food was great!! My mom has the smoked salmon Bennie and it was so Smokey and savory. I loved the fit bowl and it was a great way to eat out, enjoy good flavors while staying on target with my New Years resolution of eating healthy. 

I will definitely be back and can't wait to tell more people about this great spot!

Majestic. Longwood never fails to amaze us. Fountains. Beer Garden. Luminaries. The conservatory, walkways, tree houses and everything in between is breathe taking.  Must see- every season if possible.  As someone put it: if you don't have the luxury of visiting the world, you get the benefits of the sights of Longwood to give you a glimpse.

This restaurant was recommended to me by someone who had experienced Roy's establishment in Phoenix Arizona area. I knew that it was a fusion with seafood and steak restaurants. The ambiance was very inviting with perfect trendy lighting and well set tables. The wait staff was very courteous and paid attention to us throughout the dinning experience. 
The menu has everything on it to suffice the tastes of the seafood lover from Sushi to lobster and everything to take care of the steak lover from Filet Mignon to a bone-in steak. I did a little of both. I had the ahi tuna first. I had never seen ahi tuna in the size that it was presented. It was ample in quantity and terrific in taste. I finished with a filet mignon steak and it was cooked to perfection. We had a bottle of wine to go with it and that sealed everything just right.
I recommend this restaurant to anyone who lives or travels to the Tampa FL area. It is very close to the airport and the hotels that surround it.

Not awful, but with some of the other options nearby unlikely that we'll be coming back. 
Our server was nice, and the food was ok. The salsa bar is nice but the chips were stale.

Ahhhhh, a nostalgic, idyllic, relaxing trip on an old train. Conductors and Hostesses (I know - the latter term is not politically correct any longer; I'm just trying to stick to the era-theme) all dressed to the nines in their 50's-appropriate regalia. What could be better?

Well, for starters, maybe the smell. Musty, mildewy, moldy? Not sure what it is, but these cars all reek. And for a trip sipping and savoring wine, where the "nose" is an integral part, that's very unfortunate. These cars need to be thoroughly cleaned and disinfected, steamed and sanitized, then completely dried.

Smells aside, the trip was also a bit bumpy, simply not as smooth as some vintage train lines we've travelled. And we stopped no fewer than 6 or 8 times on our trip to the winery while crews cleared the track of debris from the previous night's storm. Something like that can't be avoided and it simply added an air of mystery - sort of - to the excursion. 

The tasting of 12 samples from DelMonaco Vineyards? Well it was ok. Our server was knowledgable and congenial. And all-in-all it was an enjoyable sippin' trip. The wines were not stellar vintages, there was a mash-mash of styles and varietals - acceptable but limited. We tried some light, some cidic, a dry or two, some sweet, and a dessert-style. The latter was surprisingly delightful - an old-fashioned, full-bodied sweet (but not too sweet) Blackberry!

Our excursion tickets also included a foil-wrapped sausage biscuit, acceptable for a quick nibble, but hardly a palate cleanser between samples. Then again, crackers and cheese  were available for purchase in the "dining car"...(a better label might be "snack car".)

Our return trip from the winery was raucous and musically entertaining as two separate groups - a "cowboy-themed, and a jazz-themed - made their way through the cars to entertain with live performances. Nice!

I will admit that my overall judgment in this review is clouded slightly by some of the more elegant and refined excursions I've travelled by train (especially the Napa Valley Wine Train). But for the price, this nostalgic ride was fun and worth a day trip by rail.

HALLELUJAH, HALLELUJAH!!
Rouses has fixed their security problem. Now, shoppers with large bags and backpacks check their parcels at the customer service desk. It was the most pleasant, least invasive shopping experience I've ever had that involved a bag check. 

And one of the stockers danced with me on Aisle 8. Shopping here is a dream. 

Accessibility notes: Ramps and automatic doors at all entrances. Store is single level. Aisles can be crowded for mobility devices. Shelves and coolers are quite tall; seated shoppers may require reaching assistance. All food service counters (deli, butcher, cheese, prepared food) are high. No braille signage. Carts available for shoppers needing assistance. Can get loud at peak hours.

Outstanding brunch/lunch spot, and clearly popular judging by the crowds! 1h45m projected wait turned out to be just under an hour; TXT-based waitlist system is really handy. Turkey Brie sandwich was delicious. Chicken & waffles equally good; the batter on the chicken was crispy and cooked perfectly.

I ordered carry out this evening - cod platter.  When I opened the box at home, it appeared nothing more than fish sticks.  I've been with family members when they ordered cod that was much better, just a few months ago, from this Sahm's (Allisonville & 116th street).  Overall - I am very disappointed in my meal and will think twice before dining with Sahm's in the future.

I try to come here every year since they opened up and for me it gets better every year.  They do only have beer but it's good beer and they do have finger foods.  They do have a few houses that you can wear a necklace and this means the monsters can touch you and scare you extra. I do not do that because I'm a chicken when it comes to that.  I would recommend everyone trying this out and to me it's better than howl o scream at bush gardens.  But I love Halloween and it's one of my favorite holidays.

Surprisingly really good for a restaurant inside a Best Western! I had a burger with the whisky sauce and the local cheddar cheese, and my mom had the chicken flat bread - both really tasty! Reasonably priced, great drinks, and excellent service.
This place is great. I discovered it while walking my dog and we love it now! Very dog friendly and the staff is always nice!

I was craving Indian. My boyfriend loves Indian food and took me to a joint in Trooper. I wasn't too fond of that place and we figured we would give this one a try. The poppadoms were delicious as well as all the chutneys. The lamb samosas were amazing. We had the chicken tikka masala.
Sauce was good. I didn't like the chicken breast... wish they would have used thigh meat. Very very laid back service. I will most likely come back to try their other dishes :)

I will admit in advance that I will always have a soft place in my heart for Bern's because we came here the night we got engaged, a little over a year ago.

This place is fantastic.  Between the Farm to Table practice and the extensive and very reasonably priced wine list, the experience is a dream.  Sure, the place is decorated like Disney's Haunted Castle and it's a nightmare actually getting a table, but the food is wonderful.  And the service!  We were served by Matt last night, and he was one of the best waiters I've ever had.  He was warm and friendly but also very knowledgeable about the food.  The sommolier was also really great.  There was nothing pretentious about this place.  

I am biased as a Californian, but it's amazing being able to have a 41 year old Rioja and a beautiful steak cooked for two and very tasty crab cakes for less than $250.  I pay a lot more for a 2005 bottle and mediocre food and crappy service in San Francisco.

The most unprofessional company I've seen in any setting.

I called first appliance, but the company that showed up is called triple a appliance now? Although first appliance is the only company with the correct address. 

No call no showed on the first appointment.

Declined any sort of diagnosis and said, all there is to do with an ice maker is to replace it, that's so that could be going wrong.

They forgot to activate the ice maker, and left several bolts in the bottom of the ice tray. Probably don't need those, right?

We had agreed over the phone that I could pay with debit card, but in person they demanded cash or check.

No reciept or invoice provided. After asking them on several occasions, the receptionist emailed me something that looked like it was created in Microsoft paint. The content of the invoice was even worse. Almost all of the basic information was incorrect and they neglected to even document what the service was or even a breakdown of parts and labor. It simply said "invoice... My name and address... Ice maker... Total"

The ice maker was still not working and we tried to reschedule them to come back out, but they were "busy" 

A problem occurred with the check and when calling me about it, they threatened to come rip the ice maker out. At that point, I didn't even realize the check had a problem and never declined to pay the agreed upon price.

I ended up paying them the couple hundred dollars just to not have to deal with them again. Called a brand name certified repair to fix whatever issues they left us, since the ice maker was still not working. This took them literally five minutes and less than half the price. 

Looking at all the other reviews along with my experience with this company, their sole purpose seems to be to collect easy warranty policy payoffs and simply bolt on some random part to justify the service being done. Reguardless of whether it will sort your issue.

We ordered the... (Not good enough for the dish to be remembered) Yorkshire pudding with sausage and mashed potatoes accompanied by green peas. The mashed were dry and somehow seemed like they came from the hospital cafeteria across the street. Everything was unmistakably old, frozen, bland and the peas looked like my wrinkled cat, puckered. Never again.
This place was amazing. The food, service, drinks all great. A little on the pricier side but still a great spot in Philadelphia!

I just dropped off a beautiful husky there I found and the man at the counter (Alexander Davis) barely told me what to do. He didn't even tell me the process. I asked him questions and he straight ignored me and moved on to the next person after taking my license. I have never found a dog before and being a dog lover, I wanted to make sure the dog was going to be taken care of. They just took the dog and ignored me. He did not even tell me when he was done after they took him. Worst service I have ever experienced at a supposed "friendly animal shelter/ clinic".

Definitely my favorite on Frenchman and, thus, my favorite place to see music in New Orleans.  Incredible acoustics, magical lighting, books the best acts, etc.  And the beer selection is stellar.  

Do they always serve drinks in plastic cups though?  This quality beer does not deserve such poor treatment.  Thought about downgrading one star for that but then I thought: 

1.  The Glen David Andrews birthday show last night was just way too special. 

AND

2.  No one cares how many stars I gave d.b.a., because it's d.b.a. and I'm just some guy writing a Yelp review of it.

Very nice people with a solid takeout system where they text you when your food is almost ready. This truck (it's a food truck/trailer permanently located next to an automotive repair shop) has some great options, all of them delicious and reasonably priced, and the folks there are kind and great with customized orders. 

Check their Facebook or website for their specials--they seem like they update both places daily with the soups and food specials they're running and you can't go wrong picking from those.

Beware !! Check your lug nuts before leaving they forgot my front passenger lug nuts they never tighten them which after 3 days of tire wobbling my hub bearings in the passenger front tire started making real bad sound which they said I need it replaced which they claim the loose nuts would not cause it to go , so it cost me $300 
  Plus alignments are not done there they drive your vehicle elsewhere to have it done and don't give any print outs on your alignment , just found out another customer tire fell off because they forgot to put lug nuts on his front tire

Tasty, quality food, & great deals!

I tried their borscht the other night and was pleased with it. The spices were interesting... different than my own recipe in that I tasted more cinnamon or nutmeg than the usual dill. Still very good though, and it comes with a nice little garlic roll. Only $6 a bowl!

Also tried the fries and there were so many, I shared with the rest of the table. Good crisp "real" fries, as my friend put it. Again, only $2.50!

My friends got sandwiches and pierogis, and I was heavily envying the latter. Please, Green Room... if you read this... add a vegan pierogi to the menu. Cheese messes me up, but damn, I love them.

I've always avoided Crabby bills but with this new one right down the street and hubby starving we decided to give it a shot.

Sat at the bar area with Heather as our server, who was a delight , never left our glass empty and had food out nice & hot.

We had the boom boom shrimp ($6.99) which were ok, they would have been better with more sauce and not such a heavy breading. Also had a bowl of gumbo ($4.99) which was delicious, full of shrimp and piping hot! Then tried the Shrimp feta toast ($7) which was shrimp sauteed with garlic, butter and feta then served over garlic toast, delicious! Definitely a rich dish but very good to split between two or three.

The margarita was good ($7.5) and they have lots of happy hour drink specials. The draught selection was poor though, consisting of Miller, Miller lite,and two kinds of Sam Adams.

The place is just like the old Bennigans that was here but the green has turned to wood, only difference. And yes, they do have outdoor seating here. 

I DID NOT like the "live" lobster tank and the $2.00 claw drop game. It's bad enough they have to suffer in a tank but the claw thing is a joke. The water was filthy and the lobsters were half dead as well.

We'll stop in again, as it's close, the food was not bad, they have a HUGe menu and we're looking forward to trying some other items, especially the sandwiches.

I stop in here as much as I possibly can!  It's very easy to miss; I've been stopping at the Crystal Flash gas station for months and never noticed Holy Cow, Cupcakes until very recently.  The cupcakes are absolutely amazing!  They vary from day to day but my absolute favorite is the Red Velvet with buttercream frosting.  I wish I had them available to me every day!  I stop in every Wednesday, and even picked up some for my coworkers the other day and they said they were to die for (which I totally agree).  Karen is very sweet and always cheery, and the cupcakes taste even better with every visit.  Highly recommended!

This review is for the Hemo's on 38th, near Walnut.

While many people seem to get cheesesteaks at Hemo's, I like their customized grilled chicken sandwiches (Hemo's Classic) much better. It's extremely cheap for a nice balanced meal (carbs, vegetables, meat, fruit/tomato, eggs, cheese, etc.). The first time I had Hemo's, the chicken's juicy flavor really stood out to me. Unfortunately, I haven't had the most consistent experiences at Hemo's, and none have been as awesome as my first time -- but they're all still great anyway.

I did notice that he uses the same hands to count money and serve food, which grossed me out a little at first, but I'm over it now. My biggest issue is he sometimes takes an extremely long time and may serve people out of order when everyone's sandwiches pretty much take about the same amount of time to make. Try to text your order 30 minutes ahead of time! For the price (always under $5 for me), I can't really complain though.

Really charming restaurant, especially with its large outdoor cobblestoned courtyard. We decided to pop in for a nibble on a sunny day closer to lunchtime. We ordered a few small plates to share, including the mozzarella fireballs, Amelie mixed salad, and gumbo. Everything tasted really good. The cheese was fresh with a slightly too-oily dressing, although the mixed salad was fantastic with the unique combination of blue cheese, grapes, pecans and apples. Gumbo was OK but not the best in NOLA by far. Service was pretty good, and overall it was a pleasant experience dining al fresco.

This is such a great brewery option for people in the Bay Area. The selection of beers is great, and their sours (if you like sours) were some of the best I've tried! I recommend trying their "Hurricane party." The Oktoberfest beer is also a must-try if it's available. The food menu was good too, nothing extraordinary but the fries were really good! Definitely try this place if you're looking for a new favorite brewery. They have a large outdoor space that is dog-friendly too!

The atmosphere here is amazing. Customer service is friendly and helpful. My friend and I sat at the bar and our food came in less than 10 minutes. This was our first time at the Grub Burger Bar and the bartender was very helpful in assisting us in looking over the menu. My friend got the Texas Luau burger and DEVOURED it! I had the mint chocolate chip milkshake and the Mac & Cheese Cheeseburger (I had to add the skinny fries, they didn't come with the burger but they were very good). I thought the milkshake was amazing and would definitely come back for another, but the burger was average and could have used a lot more bacon. Our bill came out to about $26 - a decent price. Overall our experience was pretty good and I would recommend to a friend!

IF YOU ARE ON VACATION and you need a place that has good coffee this is where to go. if you are looking for something that can serve a good cup like intelligentsia or handsome, come here. handlebar isnt as good but i think theyre on thier way. 

lattes are great.
they dont offer pour overs here.
but they have a decent drip machine that is above standard for a commercial drip machine. (we all know nothing beats a v60, chemex, etc.)

I was cued to revise my review of sbux due to reading a past review I had written of it. I posted that their pastries etc were superior to the competition down the street. Well, the competition down the street must have changed their vendor because now they are on an equal playing field when referring to food selection. However, I still find the customer service second rate to the "competition." the only reason I utilize this sbux is because I don't feel like walking my venti American ass a little bit further to the competition. I'm a lazy bitch some days.

I really like the vibe at Pure. It's a little funky, with art on the walls and a bright green paint job inside, and I dig that. 

I ate here for lunch a couple of days ago and had the Purrito. Let me preface this by saying I'm a big fan of burritos, I've had them all over Indy. And prior to this nothing topped my list of favorites higher than the steak and cheese burrito at El Rodeo. But the Purrito? Well it's my new favorite. The ingredients are fresh, and you can tell. The chicken is really tender, and the corn salsa and pico are fantastic. The southwest dressing is just the right amount. Enough that you get a little with every bite, but not so much that it's leaking out of the burrito halfway through eating it. And to top it off, they toast the outside of it. So the tortilla has just the tiniest bit of crunch to it. It's damn near perfect. And I can't wait to have it again.

The chips it came with were okay. But honestly, they may have been the best potato chips ever and I wouldn't have been able to tell. I don't know if they could have put anything on the plate that could compare to that burrito. It was just too good.

I love beer so naturally I love this place. There are a crazy amount of beers on tap from all over the place. I always like to try local beers and there are plenty to choose from.

We sat on the patio for dinner which has a decent amount of tables. Our waiter was super friendly and very attentive which made the experience that much better. 

I ordered the Summer thyme burger and my husband got the Cuban. I loved my burger with the fresh arugula and balsamic drizzle. The Cuban was not as good as I've had elsewhere. They didn't panini press it and there just wasn't a ton of flavor. The fries with both were delicious and perfectly greasy.

The check in process was pleasant, but some parts of the hotel are looking a little shabby like the hallway carpet. The grout in the shower is stained and poor caulking and maintenance make it look pretty bad. The bed squeaks but was still comfortable. The chair seat had a problem and was very uncomfortable to sit in.

What a neat place. Luv chef Staib.  Had the crab cakes, outstanding. Tried the barley soup, very good. Had one of the salad with Rockford? Cheese.  Dressing was mild.  Had the fried chicken cutlet and seafood linguini, both very good. For dessert the Martha Washington dessert.  Light and flavorful.  

Too bad we didn't see chef Staib or that would of topped the night.

I'm not sure why this is, but of the three or four El Rodeo's I've eaten at, this wasn't the same type of experience.  Food was just 'ok' and the service was fine, just something missing.  Not a very impressive review, just know that when I think of El Rodeo I think higher than this one, so maybe it was a one-time experience problem.

Nice selection of sushi, sashimi and Thai favorites. Large portions/servings. 

Large screen TV, quite dining atmosphere. Sushi bar, table and booths for seating.

I keep coming back for the French onion soup which is served very hot and delectable.Chili is good as well.The people here are friendly and the  wait staff is attentive.Special kudos to Angelica for making my visits here 
especially pleasant.I've been coming to Jon's off and on for 20 years,and 
the place is always welcoming.Bravo!

Do yourself a favor and try the Crawfish Corn Bisque. You will NOT be disappointed. The orange juice is freshly squeezed and it was amazing. DH recommend the Down the Bayou omelette. It was flavorful and absolutely delish!!! Service was a little slow, but we were in no rush... and everyone was very friendly!!!
We love Blue Sky. Every week we come to Blue sky for Martini Monday. The bartenders are amazing and so are the owners. Try the choking Italian and the chicken lollipops!
This was my first sushi experiance and im sold. They were helpful for a newbie and i was shocked at how good sushi is. Hope its all this good

My first time at Acme Ouster House even though I have walked past it a dozen times since being here in NOLA. I finally came early and decided to have "breakfast" here. Waiting on my char-broiled oysters as we speak.

They put cars through too close!!!! I've almost hit someone who hit their breaks in the car wash and the back end of my car never gets washed bc the car behind me is too close. Their mat cleaner chewed my floor mats to shreds.

I love the way that this Mexican taqueria treats me like family every single time I walk through the door. My favorite thing to eat is the steak fajitas with my daughter. My husband always gets the burrito and eats all of it. I love how all the proportions are perfect and they never mess up our orders.
Food was great! I had the steak street tacos with rice and refined beans. I also ordered flan. Omg! It was amazing. Sorry, I didn't take a picture of food. I would definitely recommend.

Flavors... yes please. I would honestly have give them a 5 Stars review if the portions were bigger, what can I say, I think of big portions when greasy Asian food is involved :). Let's give them COVID and portion control but I'm afraid that is the reality of the situation there my friends :)... Also, parking! Ugh... I seriously don't know how people don't think of location and parking for customers. Very limited on site. Goes well with the "Sex and the City +EastNash" blend decor of the place. Those boots are made for walking, right? :). 

The food comes out in random order. Loved how the waitress asked us if that was ok like we could have changed it. We got lucky and ours came perfectly from smaller plates to bigger plates. Our food came out decently fast after we ordered too. The kitchen seemed to be delivering the orders fast. 

I want more! That's all I can say! I love that we ordered chicken Steamed Bao's but got pork instead. Wow, that piece of pork that I sinked my teeth into was phenomenal! What a random surprise bomb of delicious confusing flavors. I really hope that whoever got our chicken Bao's had the same experience but with chicken. We need to try the chicken Bao's now, ha! Great excuse to have to come back. 

The atmosphere was very "Sex and the City + EastNash" blend. I personally love it but my husband has mix reviews :). The bathroom were fairly clean (they were very busy) and they didn't smell bad. That's a plus. They offer a diaper changing table at the last stall. The decor is clean.

The restaurant has a bar (draft beer, wine and cocktails) and fairly big. They offer separation flex glass between tables and the staff wears mask. Not much of social distancing offered before you get to a super secure cubed table. They have a seating area for you to wait. The space feels open and they kept a big door opened that connected to the outside dine-in seating area. Can't remember if they offer hand sanitizer on the table but they do by the entry door. 

Definitely a place to visit and explore the flavors.

Oh man this was so so good. The baleadas- kind of a burrito with a thick tortilla was so good. Got a traditional breakfast w/ fried plantains and the enchiladas which are more like tostadas were phenomenal. No English on the menu but I came in prepared to order and the owner (I think) was very helpful.
Happy hour is the shit here and bomb ass wings! What more would you want? Did I mention there's a shitton if TVs with all my teams I want to watch?

I have been to Bascom's several times throughout the last few years and they never disappoint. The food is always good. Steak made just the way you like it. Wait staff friendly and accommodating. They are following CDC guidelines so I felt very safe when visiting them in early October. I had the Atlantic Salmon and my date had the filet we both truly enjoyed our dinner. Great wine selection.  With Covid the other diners were across the room and no issues with 6 feet. Face masks worn by everyone.  Bascom is a little more pricey than some other steak houses but well worth it. 

Side dishes are ala carte but enough for two people. 

I would certainly make reservations since capacity constraints consistently change with this pandemic but usually reservations are very easy to get.

Margretta is the best host ever. Like below... Super friendly and not annoying. Dogs are way cute. Well decorated. Sometimes b&bs can be way too kitch but definitely way classy! Great bed, great stocked bath, room with scanner and printer, great breakfast.

Nice new Walmart Supercenter. Well stocked. The problem is not with store, the problem is with leaving Walmart. Exit on 73 South near impossible during rush hour unless you want to enter Fellowship Rd lane. If you are trying to get into the 73 South lane you have to move over two lanes. It's four lanes of traffic. The first lane is Fellowship Rd the second lane is for the NJ Turnpike. Basically the traffic in the Fellowship Rd lane is non-stop. You have to find someone who will let you onto 73 first. No one stops. Then you have very little time just to get into the NJ Turnpike lane just to have a shot to get into the 73 South. The Fellowship Rd exit from the Supercenter is no bargain either. There is no light so you have fight three lanes of traffic. One is for 73 North, the middle is for Fellowship Rd West and the third is for 73 South. Walmart knew there was going to be issues. There are plans for major redesign of 73 and Fellowship Rd. It is a massive project that will absolutely cause headaches for several years. The 295, NJ Turnpike and Fellowship intersection is one of the biggest bottlenecks in the entire state. Try making a left turn out of the Greyhound terminal during rush hour to get onto 73. I gave up after 5 minutes and went right and out of my way to get onto 73. The only vehicles that can force their way out and make a left are the buses. They block Fellowship West and cars will stop to let them get into Fellowship East.

The Nashville airport is a sad and miserable experience with few things open.  Travelers must go through security again to make a connection in another terminal.  Gibson Cafe is the only sit down restaurant open.  Instead of a peaceful place to get a meal, they have some guy singing at the top of his lungs over unmasked diners.  How a) is his spittle remotely safe and b) why ruin a quiet place to eat?  Yuck.

Halloween weekend 2020 was my first time visiting Philadelphia and the Cambria hotel did not disappoint me. Checked in on Friday afternoon and it was a very smooth process.  I stayed in the Corner Suite and it was very spacious and clean, with a perfect view of the city with wrap around windows. The bathroom mirror has bluetooth for you to connect your phone to play music. Our room was missing the bluetooth passcode, so I had to call the front desk and was provided the code right away. I also called the front desk for additional towels and a staff member was at my room within 5 minutues. The lobby area and convenient store area was clean and had modern decor. This hotel is not exceptional, but is a very nice place to stay.

Cute neighborhood pub!  We ordered their St. Louis style pizza which was VERY good!  Can't beat that it was 1/2 price either!  Would definitely go back as long as the weather was nice and we could sit outside again.  It was really smokey inside, but they warn you with signage before you walk in.  If I smoked, this would be my bar!  Dimly lit, quaint, and neighborhoody!
Average bar food, average service, average crowd, average prices (for the area.) The draw here is none of the above...it's the location for watching the sunset.

Kara with a K was an outstanding bartender. She was always there when we needed her and went above and beyond when it can to service. She took care of us not only when it came to our drinks but when it came to individuals trying to be rude. She took care of it quick and professionally. I would recommend this place to anyone Visiting nashville!!!
It was a lot larger than I expected.Friendly staff and very clean

Usually do not have a problem here but Father's Day 7/17 my wife went to pick up breakfast and when she got home my steak egg and cheese bagel was a steak and bacon bagel????? No egg , not worth driving back for but from now on checking food before I or she leaves. Steak and bacon are a strange combo and tasted pretty gross.
The delivery was quick and courteous. The good was very good. This was my first time ordering from here but won't be my last.

Smaller location than others. Men's area is barely stocked if at all. Cellphone department staff not familiar with devices or plans. Lights in the store not always on. Bathrooms always need attending to or out of order

We've used STL Pest Services a few times now and have really seen a difference. In our time using the company, we have LOVED when Larry is the service tech. Whether it be his incredibly insightful advice, his careful application of the chemicals (we have pets always tip-toeing around) or his willingness to go the extra mile to eliminate some pesky spiders / ants, Larry is hands down the real MVP. Thanks to STL Pest Services and to Larry for making us feel like the most important customer you have!

Best brunch in Philadelphia. This is a newer place, unfortunately not frequented as much as Honey's which is at the corner of the street. The owner and his wife (who does most of the cooking) are both extremely pleasant. It's a small, quaint, but well decorated place with a nice feel. They serve La Colombe coffee, and it's well brewed. I've had a latte and drip, both are quite good for a brunch place (as opposed to a dedicated coffee shop). 

The food is amazing. Whether sweet or savory, there's a good option and it's beautifully plated. I've tasted the enfrijolada, open mediterranean burrito, foul mamalade, and the french toast. All awesome. 

Even better, the wait isn't that bad!

Worst service I've ever had, both waitresses we had were very unfriendly and acted like their service was a favor to us, and to top it all off the food wasn't even great. There's far better options in town. I will not go back.
111 Hatery is bigoted and hypocritical. They won't bake cakes for a samesex couple, but they will profit from an arts festival on Talbott Street, which is very gay.
Great food- I've had the flat steak and pork chops.  Both are excellent.  I wouldn't recommend eating there during lunch IF you are expecting an hour.  Usually, it's a much longer wait.
The best coffee in town. French press or drip, it handily defeats the competition. Well balanced, never burned.

What happened to this restaurant? It used to be a perfect one, and we loved to eat there. Yesterday we wanted to eat something good - so we went to sampan. It was a big disappointment. The food lacked flavor - the Ceviche had so much onion - you couldn't test the fish. The Brussel Sprouts so bitter; the fish was so blah. Our waiter was good, and the two of the small plate were okay. We asked the host something when we got into the restaurant - she told us that she is going to check for us - and we are still waiting for the answer. 
I'm so sad and hope they will improve and be the great restaurant they use to be .

Same place you remember which can be a good thing or in some cases mean your stuck in a time warp. Prices are reasonable and staff is polite. Pot pie is the best in my opinion. You get what you pay for. If your wanting a Thanksgiving or Christmas dinner and it isn't Thanksgiving or Christmas, Boston Market is for you.

Read the good reviews and wanted to come in because I love Lebanese. I had the Lebanese special and my friend had the Venezuelan. The gyro was fresh and tasty and so was the pulled beef. 

The sandwiches were approx. 7 dollars each which is a steal for the quality they give you. 


Only reason I gave four and not five stars is because there was only one person making the sandwiches and so it took some 10 minutes to take our order even though there was no line

This is an odd experience. I went online and registered and made an appointment for 4:30pm. No problems doing so, and got there with 10 minutes to spare. I was informed that appointments made online are not recognized because there may be other things going on at the store. I was told numerous times I would have a long wait. I probably should have left but I can't keep driving around on a tire with a nail in it losing air every few days. Less than 15 minutes later my vehicle is in a bay being worked on. The staff working on the cars, great, the desk personnel, he could use some customer service training. Interesting day.......

Fairways are in awful shape. Made the tee time over the phone and was not told that they were in the process of replacing them. The website states "You will notice that some of the fairways appear to be discolored and others may still be wet. This is all part of the process of providing a better turf for your playing enjoyment." Discolored isn't even close. The condition is awful especially for the price of $60.00. Tried to discuss with the staff and GM. Asked the gentleman working the pro shop if he would pay this price for the shape the course was in and he didn't answer. Went to discuss with the GM who said if we didn't like the repairs they are making then we might not be the types of golfers they want. Over all horrible customer experience.

From the very first time i went to Big Ez Seafood,ive always gotten very friendly ,courteous and swift service. The first time i went i got the12in Shrimp poboy,which was loaded with very good sized shrimp and very tasty for $10. The next time i went in i got the Ya Kat Mein and it was excellent and loaded with shrimp,pork , chicken and other really delicious fillings. They let you sample the gumbo and other things on their menu. The prices for everything are very good and like i said before,you cant beat the service. I HIGHLY recommend this place to anyone who loves good seafood!

Just had a steak and cheese sub for the first time here. It was very good. Whatever cut of steak they use had a great flavor. We were trying to find a good place in Wesley Chapel for a great steak and cheese sub but in a pinch or until we find one this will do.

Amazing food!  We drove through this area on our way home from vacation.  We wanted good barbecue...we found it.  Great food, friendly staff, and a great atmosphere.  I highly recommend.  Next time we drive through this area we will make a stop to Hogwood BBQ.

The only food in the French quarter I actually crave.  I just wish they didn't close for summer.  I love The imperial rolls, and since I love pho it satisfies my need. I could eat here almost every day off the week
the taco tuesday special is pretty good ($5 for 2 tacos, chips & a drink), but their desserts are what keep me coming back! and all the hot sauce too :)

Stopped by with my little foodie group for a delightful brunch. The space is cute, in other words, I like the decor. 
And there is ample parking behind the building... in case you're not finding any out front. 
There are some favorite menu items but also those that you can not always find on the typical Mexican menu. So that's a big plus. And the brunch items are not that easy to find around town. 
I enjoyed having chicarrones for starters instead of the usual chips and salsa. Big plus for that. 
I had the breakfast torta with potatoes on the side. Btw, those breakfast potatoes are delicious. There are other veggies in there, like a hash of some sorts and then pan fried I believe. Either way I totally recommend those. My huge torta was also quite yummy, lots of meaty goodness. 
The portions were hearty and the prices reasonable. Could you eat cheaper? Yes, but would you get this quality of food? Probably not. 
Why not 5 stars you ask? The service was friendly but quite sketchy.

First the good - the food is great, the prices are reasonable, and the portions are generous.  Now the not so good - the restaurant is quite small, so it can quickly become very crowded.  We had what I would call a "good" table, however, there were a couple occasions where I felt that I had to duck as the servers went by with their trays.

My real problem had to do with the pasta, and the reason for only 3 stars (would be 4 otherwise).    

To explain, this past Saturday night my wife and I and another couple had dinner at the restaurant.  When each of us gave our entrée order, the server asked, "is penne ok"?  To which our responses were - "what are my choices", and, "do you have linguini"?  When 2 of us ordered different pastas, what the server didn't mention - linguini (and some others) is considered homemade, and thus comes with a $4 upcharge.  My wife didn't want pasta at all with her dinner, so she asked if she could substitute with vegetables.  "Sure, would you like asparagus", was the server's response.  There was no mention of a $5 (25%) upcharge. 

Of our 4 entrees, 3 had upcharges (I had the penne, which by some good fortune did not come with an upcharge).  Upon complaining later when the bill came, we were reminded that the menu (in a section separate from our entrees) did mention the upcharges.  We were wrong; the pasta upcharges were not removed.

We have dined at That's Amore several times, and will probably go back.  However, we will have learned a lesson about what pasta to order, and to ask about upcharges for ANY changes.

PS.  The calamari Caesar salad is outstanding !
The hype is real.  Cocktail shrimp is a must.  Steaks were all cooked with precision. Service was great. The T-bone was T-aste!

Waitress was very nice but inattentive. Long time between getting appetizers, which were pretty good. We always order stuffed mushrooms, will go with the plain calamari next time instead of Molly's version. Entree ordered and took long time to get food. Ordered baked potato and got luke warm mashed taters. Salmon on plank hot and tasty. Chicken (naked) wings medium were okay, nothing special though. I will go back again, it's a nice place dine.

Every time I stop in here the staff is welcoming and ready to go. I am in and out very quickly and ingredients are always fresh. They also helped me with a last minute catering order for 50 people! It was fresh and right on time. I will for sure be back! Make sure to sign up for jimmy rewards for free stuff! I will be back.  Also, the GM is extremely good looking and a breath of fresh air. Come for the speedy delivery / freshness , stay for the hotties that work there.

I took my parents here for dinner and was not disappointed. We are usually accustom to the Cantonese/Hong Kong style dim sum where you can select items right from the cart. This was a nice change of pace. 
The soup dumplings were amazing! I would highly recommend those, as they're something everyone would enjoy. Everything we ordered was wonderful. I am a second generation Chinese-American and I found the food to be very authentic. We ordered the savory rice cakes, which was something my grandfather always used to made for Chinese New Year. They're hard to find other places, so it was a very nostalgic experience.
The only downside is that they are cash only. Please make sure you have cash with you're planning on dining there.

Enjoying the view of downtown Indianapolis is exemplified when paired with a smooth fruity shot of espresso and pour over with an eye level view of the city! 
Indianapolis Commissary has the vintage walls of the catacombs blended with the decor lending chill vibes of the morning sunshine.  If serving Tinker Coffee isn't good enough, the barista was very knowledgeable about the coffee which added to the positive experience. 
The space itself is off of Mass ave a few steps below street level which is cool because when you are enjoying your spro you can people watch from eye level giving your morning a different point of view.  Too early to get a haircut but will stop by later in the day to complete the experience probably with an adult beverage .

This is a speak easy bar located next to Budweiser brewery in St.Louis, Missouri. The true location is in Soulard. It offers a luxury space for a wonderful date night that offers a live piano player or band. The upstairs also includes a venue that someone is looking for to rent out for either a business meeting, wedding shower, bachelorette/ bachelor celebration, or even special events. The chef that cooks here is ambitious and offers quite the menu to taste when enjoying a drink. The owner is also offering a service for yearly cost to become a vip wine holder in the actual restaurant. There is a outside patio for downstairs with a full bar and also an upstairs patio. The upstairs patio is for guests that would like to enjoy a cigar. The luxury of this speak easy is nothing that can be compared to in St.Louis. This is a must visit bar to enjoy an evening out!

Oh my, I do love this whimsical artist. There's whimsy and innocence with a touch of sass, but most of all, her love odd ask things animal, all things with paws, shines through. She also rehabs possums, and has several. In face, she's got a huge Instagram account for her possums. The only problem I saw with the store is there was so much I wanted to just take home with me.

Let me start by saying I love the Taco Bus downtown but this was terrible!  I ordered a chicken quesadilla.  I specifically said I only want rice, cheese and a very small amount of chicken.  What I was served was anything but!  Two of the slices were filled with onions!  YUCK!  Two more were filled with pea, carrots & corn, huh?!  And the other with what looks like leaves!!?  I don't know what's going on here but it's nothing like the DT location.

The indoor/outdoor seating is nice but it's kind of loud inside...

I don't know if this is the new theft prevention program but this store doesn't encourage people to shop.  My husband went in yesterday to shop.  He looks at things and buys them.  As he walked in, he was asked many questions and said it was 5 hours before someone could help him.  He explained he just wanted to look around.  After many more questions, they allowed him..  He did decide to buy something.  He asked someone for help and since there is no counter, was told to stand in a specific area.  He did, nothing happened. He left.  Too bad because he said I should have a new desktop..........

I brought my 10 year old car here with a groupon and it came out excellent. I was getting ready to sell it and it needed to be professionally cleaned.  The guy at foam factory (can't remember his name) rearranged their Friday schedule so that I could bring in my car. I dropped the car off and picked it up later that day.  The car was nicely cleaned and the carpets smelled fresh for for at least a month after. They did add a gratuity, but I was going to give them one anyways.  I see people complaining about the added gratuity,  but come'on it's manual labor and pretty much always recommended when someone cleans your car.  Also, I don't remember if the gratuity was based on groupon price or the full price, but if you think about it.. it's like using a coupon at a restaurant.If your meal is $50 and you have a $20 off coupon, you still are supposed to tip on the $50 not the remaining $30... they still brought you $50 worth of food.  In this case, they are still doing the full price worth of work and should be compensated. After I took my car there, two other family members bought the groupon.  No problems at all.
We ordered through door dash and were a little nervous that our specific needs would be met. They were fulfilled and everything was a delicious as if we dined it. Thank you!!

Came here for dinner on Saturday night. Based on the reviews i was surprised at how empty the place was. It was a nice night so im guessing the lack of patio is why. I had the spinach and lamb dish and it was amazing. The lamb was super tasty as well as the spinach. My wife had a chicken dish that she was happy with. The Naan was amazing as well. Great little BYOB to check out when its too hot to eat outside!
Best breakfast around! Food is fresh, delicious and the service is good. Great for breakfast, & Lunch!

"THE THANKSGIVING PLACE!"

I come to this part of Philly once a year for a weekend and this is my ABSOLUTE favorite place at the famous Reading Terminal Market! 

The Thanksgiving sandwich is a must-try! 

But that Southwest sandwich with all those peppers is FIRE too!

I would be embarrassed to bring anyone here.
Sat for ten minutes and only had a menu tossed in front of me. Later!

i have been to this restaurant a couple of times and i have mixed feelings about it. it is right around the corner from my house so i really want to like it, but i am not totally sure. the dinning room is cute and sort of romantic, but last night the stealers/ chargers game on an there were some rather excited people hooping and hollering, which was rather distracting. then they didn't have the beer on tap that i was looking for, but eh thats no big deal.  we ordered some nachos to start, which were yummy with a totally delicious sauce.  but when it came to dinner, their online menu and their actual menu were not the same, meaning the steak with truffles (the whole reason my roommate wanted to come) was not on the menu. so she went with the chill instead, yummy, but it was $15?!? i had the fish tacos which were not so bad, a little fish-y, but pretty good. for dessert we had the bread pudding and it was very very good. 

so all and all this place has its very good parts, and its bad parts. its just a matter of what you get.

Love this new neighborhood spot!  We came for the lunch buffet and were very impressed.  Excellent variety of dishes, all of them very tasty.  We loved the flavors and spice in each dish.  We'll definitely be back!

Simple menu, nice beer selection. So why do I feel like I've been ripped off? Because I felt like I had to show the bartender how to pour, the wait staff how to tend tables, and the cooks (read: non-chefs) how to finish and plate. Nicely selected menu items, but prepared with workaday ingredients, slapped together by disinterested staff who will soon be making appearances at a car wash, Old Navy, or Golden Moon.  I would rather drive past Mt. Holly and get a real meal for these prices. Won't bother with a return visit. Too bad; something always missing with Mt. Holly dining, and this one doesn't have it, either.  Don't quit your day jobs, "Locals".

Holy wow  

Home run 

Super great menu 

Crisp knowledgeable service 

Great presentation

Creative approach 

Great quality 

Wanted to order half the menu

My mum asked me if I wanted to have lunch here today. I was really surprised! Last time i went years ago I was not impressed nor thought it was fine dining. It reminded me so much as a sportsbar. Keep in mind this may have been decades ago! I haven't went since I was a teen.

Basically I wasn't expecting much.

The service was pretty good- the waitress was polite but didn't come across as friendly. 

The cheddar biscuits were okay. I like Red Lobster's more because they taste more savory than the sweet version here.

My mum had grilled salmon which was really delicious! And I had grilled shrimp (it was meh). 

We both had tomatoes in balsalmic vinegar as a side. Simply satisfying! 

And then we had the double choco cake. Tasty though I've had better. The icecream was really good. I wonder if it's homemade because of the colour and taste. :,) I was bad and ate more of it than I should have (I'm lactose intolerant). It was worth it though.

I look forward to going again. If me and my boyfriend have the munchies sometime I'll suggest a visit here!

A perfect place to bring a date!  Tucked away in the corner of one of their many seating areas or right up at the bar, I have enjoyed every visit to Loa.  Definitely one of the best cocktail bars in New Orleans!  
    My favorite is sitting at the bar and watching in awe as they build these creations from the ground up...all the while somehow managing to smile, greet customers and attend to special requests...I have no idea how they juggle it all!  
    Alan and his crew have created an out of this world cocktail menu using unique spirits, homemade syrups and fresh herbs.  My girlfriend loves the extensive whiskey selection but I am biased towards the well thought out selection of rare and local craft beers.  
     New Orleans is home to me and I love playing tourist in my own town...Loa is one of those places that allows me another reason to brag about my city! 

Cheers!

Don't waste your time. I was told when I booked this you get 3 wine tastings and an olive oil tasting. They picked us up 35 minutes late. When we got to the first winery we couldn't taste because we were late. We went to 2 other wineries and the pours were very small. We went to a bar after the 2 wineries where the pours were equally as small. Because of the late start we never go to do the Olive Oil tasting which was offered over the phone and supposed to be included in the price. They didn't even offer anything to make up for not being able to go other than we have no time. The ride back to drop off all the people in the van is way too long. They should have one drop off and pick up place. You have to sit there like a shuttle bus and wait for everyone to be picked up and dropped off and you can't drink. Not worth the money. Take a limo instead. It's a buzz kill.

Love the Pizza and house salad here, we always get the Cappy's deep dish and a regular cheese for the kids and their cheese bread. Never had any complaints, if it's not super humid out we love to sit outside and once in a while we bring our dog. The waitress even brought out a bowl of water for our dog! You won't be disappointed
It is not open 24/7 on Saturday that is a bold faced lie to anyone reading this!!!!!

I am so glad I found this place. I just abandoned Tres Amigos due to bad customer service. I was having a get together on Saturday and I give this place a try. First of all, let me tell you this is the real deal. Talk to the owner Maria and you will find out. These are real recipes made by real people and they are real good.

I know sometimes I can be hard on Mexican food and people that present it badly. But this is not one of those. First of all this is the kind of business we need to support here in Sparks.

Go in and try their salsas, those alone will sell you. I bought every version and they were all amazing. Then they will sell you the tastiest marinated meats.

I have a new home, go out and support these people Sparks, we need them and their old school flavors. 

Update---try the OMG torta's.  OMG.  The bread is toasted so yummy.  I got the green carnitas.  Wow wow wow wow wow.

Rating: 4.4

I have lived in Downtown St. Louis for over two years, and I have walked or run by Chili Mac's Diner hundreds of times. Since I work during the week, I have not gotten a chance to give it a try. This morning on a walk before work, I decided to make an impromptu stop since I saw the menu on the window features Hot Cakes. As y'all know, I am all about pancakes. The waitress was very friendly, and I was seated promptly. I immediately ordered the full stack (3) of Hot Cakes. The Hot Cakes came out quickly, which was fantastic. Why? Because these Hot Cakes were excellent. They were light and fluffy and had great flavor. For only $4.50, the portion size was quite generous, and this meal was a total bargain. The syrup was also good, and it complemented the Hot Cakes well. The diner vibe of Chili Mac's was obvious, and I hope to come back in the future to give these another try. Check it out!
Great place. Clean. Friendly staff awesome restaurants and bars. Fun games. Table games are a blast here due to amazing dealers.

Where to even begin about the service and absolute satisfaction I receive each time I walk in the door of Aucoin Hart.  My husband chose to have my engagement ring created there and we had both of our wedding bands done as well.  We also created my band and the price had gone up from when we did the original drawings until when we ordered and they had no problem keeping the price the same as the range they originally gave us.  I would go there again and again...and I have!

We made an appointment to see a specific car (we made the appointment earlier in the morning to see the car the same day). While making the appointment, we made sure to let the associate know that we were coming from Delaware to see the car (a 1.5 hour drive). When we got there, the car had just been sold. A phone call to save us the trip would have been nice. When we spoke to the manager, he basically blew us off- there was zero effort to try to improve our experience. The silver lining for us is that they won't be getting our money. If you're thinking about going out of your way to see a car here, save yourself the hassle.

Italian restaurants are usually not my first choice picks... but I came here with my extended family back in May. I was pleasantly surprised by this restaurant. Our big group got several different appetizers/entrees... from salads to chicken to oysters... and everyone seemed pleased with their choices. The menu is on the pricier side but worth a try if you want Italian food for a nice occasion, date night, or just because.

I give a 1 only because this site will not let me give a ZERO.  I have been to this place twice . Each visit worse the the previous . My first time in I was helped by a older lady Jackie . I was looking at a new Dyson . I also was having one in for a service with a coupon . Half off . While I was dealing with checking in the old one JACKIE was interrupted by the " manager " Jenny . Who made sure I knew she was the manager at least 3 times . She started telling me that the vacuum I was picking out would never work I need to upgrade to a simplicity . I say tell her I am happy with Dyson and I want the DC50 I was looking at . She rolls her eyes and starts bad mouthing my choice acting indignant and condescending to the point I just told her I would think about it and left without buying . Went to target and bought one 10 minuets later .  

After a month I got a call that my vacuum was ready . I went to pick it up and Sharon was this women's name . She rings me up and I tells me that will be $85. And change . I said I had a half off a coupon. She said yeah but you had a clog and its a 29.99 charge for a clog and it was 49.99 with your coupon .  I said NO . IT WAS 79.99 half off would be 39.99 . She tried o argue with me . Even after I pointed out what was on my service paper . Even if that where true it would have only been $70 something besides I never approved any extra work . They bring my vacuum out and it really looks no better then before . The tank looked like it had dried mud in it and the brush still had hair on it .  At this point I just wanted to be done . She would not come off the price.  I just paid and took my vacuum home .  I tried to take my copy of the service ticket with me but she refused to let me have it back . She said she has to have it for the office . I will Never give any business to this place again . 2 out of the 3 people I was "helped " by where the worse people I have very meet in retail .

This theater has had some changes lately. On top of the popcorn bucket you can purchase and then bring back each visit for cheap refills,  I love the butter your own popcorn station next to the Coke Freestyle machine.  This option of having all your choices of flavored drinks and unlimited refills is amazing.  I also love that they have an area that has games and things for kids to do while you are waiting for a movie to start seating.  This is a lifesaver when you have an excited toddler wanting to get into the movie.  My only complaint is about the reclining seats.  If you do not want it to recline you should be able to lock it into place.  This drives me crazy.  The fact that the armrests between the seats comes up is genius and helps when you have a little one who halfway through the movie wants you to hold them.
I agree with Becky B's comment. I'm a black male and would never go to a Chuck E Cheese because of all the black people there!

After searching for delivery for thai restaurants, we came across this one. Have to say, some of the best coconut rice and green curry I have tried! Ordered from them a few times after and ordered the same thing. Definitely recommend!

Decent place to eat. I ordered some seafood pasta with vodka sauce. Unfortunately I found out the hard way that the sauce turned out to be extremely fishy, which was not enjoyable. I should have put the two together, but my mind wasn't working quick enough. The place is byob. It appeared the people that eat here are frequent customers because everyone seemed to know eachother.

Aaron from the maintenance team was very helpful across multiple situations where we needed a prompt response to an on-going problem we had with the windows. He was kind and courteous and detail oriented! Thank you for everything Aaron.

Visited this place for the first time since ever in the past week. The food was really good and the drinks were great. 

My favorite part was the braised pork belly crostato. Very delicious, had so many complimenting flavors. 

For the entree I got the bbq burger which was also good. I was happy when I tasted it and the bbq sauce had a little kick to it. They definitely put their own creative flavors into their food and made the dinner very unique.

Great concept! 

Excellent food. Quick service and friendly service.

Cauliflower Rice with the Chinese Chicken Bowl. 
Very filling and flavorful.

Lots of seating. Very open and clean.
Cannot wait to go back.

This was a very nice find. I love sushi but I do not want to have the rice with the sushi anymore. This place has that option. All the items that are with sushi but put in a salad. Awesome place. Works great with my Keto.

Thieves, cheats & lies. When we booked the tour Danny took our party's deposit and quoted us a price but then when it was actually the time of the tour when we checked in, the lady was EXTREMELY rude and demanded MORE money than what Danny quoted to us. We reluctantly paid the extra thinking it will still be worth it... NOT! We didn't get to see half the things promised. DO NOT WASTE YOUR MONEY ON THIS YALL!!! You will be sooooo disappointed and BORED

This is one of the few surviving Friendly's in SE Pennsylvania.  A recent trip with my mother had us struggling to reach the $30 groupon balance.  Friendly's is barely more expensive than fast food.  Service is A-OK and efficient, and the wait wasn't too long, despite a line up out the door when we arrived.  Manager checked in with us to make sure our meal was going well.  Where else can you get a $5 sandwich with fries in a mall?  They also offer 10% off and a free sundae to seniors!

If there was a way to put 0 stars and still write a review that is what I would do. These people are totally unfriendly and not customer service oriented they actually get mad and scold you in the event that you ask them to correct a haircut that wasn't done to your liking because you're just a customer and not a barber so you don't really know what haircut should look like so you're wrong and they're right even though you can tell that they missed a spot.  Avoid going to this establishment if you would like to have a haircut that is actually a haircut in addition to that avoid going if you like I don't know getting scolded when you're spending money on a service

Simply stated: this is what all great supermarkets should aspire to be.

This new, huge Whole Foods is the bomb.  Yeah, I said it. 

I'll be here everyday during lunch.  Come find me.
Excellent sushi, friendly staff and cool atmosphere.  What else do you need?

Yes, the food was great. Yes, the atmosphere was cozy and certainly equipped with multiple flat screen TV's for watching the Big Game or all 6 games at once. Yes, the little boats in the cases are a sweet decor touch. But none of this would have felt nearly as special without one wonderful person: Melanie. Our first ambassador to Nashville after a red eye flight from LA. We needed food and a place to rest our swollen dogs. Melanie welcomed us into Sam's Place with all the hospitality that the South is known for. She turned a very good meal (thanks cooks!) into an amazing experience that prepared us to face Nashville with full bellies and even fuller hearts!  

The bloody Mary's and French Dip gave me goosebumps with each bite of amazing flavor but Melanie has warmed my heat and renewed my faith in great customer service. She has reminded me that extremely good and kind people can be found anywhere you go, you just have to take the time to look for them and be open to letting kindness beget kindness. Happiness and contentment can be highly contagious when you have the opportunity to meet gems like Melanie.

Not a good atmosphere. Went on a Saturday afternoon, and there was only what I assume to be "regulars" there. I was constantly looked at, and even approached about the way I was playing and my companion. I wanted to have a good time but this place seemed judgemental. I left after an hour...leaving the pitcher of beer and all. I guess you must fit a profile to play here.

The tl;dr version of this summary is MEH.

So, if you browse my profile, you'd quickly find out my thing is cheap, huge portions, with compassion for sacrificed quality and service to get those cheap, huge portions.  I should have been a pig in shit at Q.T., then.  A fancy sandwich?  For less than five dollars?  For 4.95$ to be exact?

Problem is, some things you want done right and can get done right for even cheaper (South Philly Viet food, be gentle with my heart).

I ordered a #7, which is a plain pork banh mi (and they come with pate, fyi).  The meat was actually pretty good: very fatty, smoky.  I personally liked the roll, which was a little crunchier than some others I've had.  

What SANK this sandwich for me was the vegetables.  There weren't really enough of them, for starters, and they were totally dry.  The carrots were the kind you can get in bagged salad mixes, you know what I'm talking about.  There was no hint of vinegar nor even a mild pickle anywhere.  Which is a little awkward for me to point out, since their menu promised pickled vegetables.

The banh mi also wasn't as filling as I'd have liked, but I've found most banh mi to run on the smaller end, so no penalties there.

I'd actually give this place a 2.5, but the older woman and her daughter (?) who are running the joint were really sweet and friendly.  Also, it is really small, not that you need a lot of room to sword-swallow a sub.

I am fan of this food truck for sure. I had the flying pig and lumpia and both we were delicious.  The flying pig had great flavor and wasn't spicy which is perfect.  The lumpia was good and seemed healthly. Adding to the repeat list.

I don't know if the food is good. The lack of hospitality was so huge I didn't get a chance to find out. ACME across the street has a line so we thought we would try Felix's instead. Apparently that was the wrong decision. When we walked the place was fairly empty (perhaps that alone should have been a clue.) The waitress wanted to seat us at a table but I asked for to sit at the bar to see them shucking the oysters. When I moved a chair one foot to the right the bartender yelled "No moving chairs!"  Look, I'm not thin-skinned and I don't get offended easily but let's be honest, I'm the customer and she was rude. We end up just going to ACME. 5 minutes later we were being welcomed in and treated like family. Bottom line, Felix's is empty for a reason.

A nice dog park with plenty of small dogs on Sunday late morning. There are 2 poop bag stations and 2 watering stations.

There is seating under cover.  And there are 3 nice picnic tables under the trees. The cool breezes are great here. 

I will definitely be back whenever I am in the area.

The parking lot is huge so very easily fit my 25.5' RV and I saw a Class A in the lot too.  There is a restroom conveniently close.
This is the best Asian restaurant in the Lansdale area!

We went in here to get some relief from the scorching temperatures outside and get some water. We sat near the back and ordered water.
