There was a man in the restroom handing out soap and paper towels for tips, which I find extremely annoying but paid him anyway because he's probably struggling. Our waitress came up and asked us if we wanted to order anything, but we told her we were fine. She then came up again and showed us the menu as if we had to purchase something even though the place wasn't nearly full at 11:00am on a Thursday. Finally, so she'd leave us alone, we ordered a Jack & Coke on her third attempt. It was in a 6 oz plastic cup and it cost us $10. This place is kind of a joke if you just want to get water and listen to music. Be ready to spend your money.

I feel like Roux spends most of the effort on fancy decor in a casual shopping plaza. The food was ok but nothing impressive. Monkey bread tasted stale and food was not very warm. Bathrooms spelled pretty bad and service struggled with our large party. I would not say they really understand southern soul breakfast food but they made an ok attempt. Best thing about this place was the strong coffee!

Do. Not. Go. Here. 

It has taken me two months to stifle my anger enough to write a coherent statement explaining why even one star is too much. 
1. Waited an hour and fifteen minutes past my appointment time. The owner acted as if it were a regular occurrence. 
2. Accused his employees of stealing his food in front of me. 
3. HORRIFICALLY BLAND HAIRCUT that I had to fix at home three separate times. 
4. The worst is: when I scheduled, I asked the person on the phone how much a haircut was and I was told "around $45." I allowed her to schedule me with whomever, as I made it clear that this was my first time, and she scheduled me with someone named Billy. Little did I know that it would be DOUBLE that price because Billy is the owner. This was never mentioned until I checked out.

I am disgusted that I paid for this experience. You should not do the same.

When you think of a real breakfast spot, not a "brunch spot" or a "diner," but like a place that specializes in feeding breakfast to the neighborhood --you think of a place like this. This is a real, authentic west philly Saturday morning neighborhood spot.  They have a pretty large menu and everything, I mean everything, is reasonably priced. 

The service is friendly and quick enough. The place gets pretty packed on weekend mornings so get there early or expect a wait. 

Anyone reading this review from center city, university city or the burbs go out of your way to visit this spot so you can intermix with the locals who are friendly, jovial, and lively. You'll often overhear old friends running into each other or families celebrating birthdays. It's a great place that you should all check out.

Some of the best BBQ I've ever had in my life. Scratch that, THE BEST BBQ I've ever had in my life. 

About 5 feet from pulling in the parking lot you can smell smoked goodness in the air and it only gets better as you get closer to the building. 

Pro tip: get here early, because once those meats are gone, sorry Charlie! One guy in line said he had to come back because the line was out the door and wrapped around the building the last time he went. 

After you place your order, it's not much of a wait before you get your food. I ordered the BBQ sampler which came with chicken, ribs, brisket, and pulled pork and a side of Mac and cheese and collard greens. The meats were so amazing, perfectly smoked and bursting with flavor. I liked the sweet bbq sauce as an addition to the meats, the spicy sauce was a little too hot for me. 

As for the sides, the Mac and cheese was great, flavorful but not too salty or rich. The collard greens were okay, they have a strong spicy vinegar kick to them. I wish they cooked them using a chicken or vegetable stock back to truly give the greens flavor. 

Other than that, I'm definitely coming back. No doubt!
La Parada is a hidden gem. They have great prices and amazing food. Generally, they are your typical Mexican restaurant but with lower prices. The service was also very good!

I think that places that don't have their staff wear something to identify themselves or a hostess to welcome you when you enter are just being exclusionary.  Regulars will walk right in and ask for a drink but someone new will just feel uncomfortable since they don't know where to go or who to talk to.  Although, after sitting down I realized that the staff did in fact have a uniform since they were all dressed like Hipsters.  The men were all wearing plaid or check buttoned-up shirts and the two bartenders were sporting old-timey suspenders and a Zach Galifianakis beard, respectively.  Meanwhile the women were all dressed in thrift store chic, most with funky hairstyles and/or glasses with thick black frames.  However, no one seemed snobby so I think the other reviewers are just noting the overall vibe of the place rather than any specific person's demeanor.  Everyone we spoke to was friendly, just not very competent or knowledgeable.  I did like the decor.  There were lots of little touches that blended the modern and antique which made the place look really cool.  However, I also agree with the other reviewers that the seating is not comfortable.  And I was really disappointed in the bathroom situation.

Overall, I would have only given this place two stars if it weren't for the food.  We were both starving and wanted something more substantial than sandwiches or a cheese plate.  But their oven was broken when we were there so they could not make a pizza.  This may have turned out to be a good thing if what the "worst pizza ever" reviewer says is true.  Since it was a chilly night I got the grilled cheese with a cup of tomato soup.  The soup was a $2 up-charge which seems dumb but it was pretty good soup.  The sandwich was just perfect.  Toasted crunchy on the outside with tomatoes, caramelized onions and creamy Gruyere on the inside.  I also swiped quite a few of the truffle fries my hubby ordered and those were truly amazing. The second blessing in disguise of the night was when they were out of the wine I wanted.  Our server brought me generous (maybe 2 oz) samples of a couple others and I found one I had never tried before but really enjoyed for only $6 a glass.  I agree that the prices at this place are high for the type of food they serve but I don't mind paying more for quality.  I finished off the night with a dark chocolate salted caramel tart that was absolutely fantastic and went really well with my second glass of wine.

This is not going on my list of favorite places in Tucson (sorry Corey) but I would return if I were in the UA area and wanted someplace quiet to hang out enjoy a glass of wine.  They had a nice list of herbal teas and coffee drinks as well, though their beer list was a disappointment.  Classic Hipster mistake, trying too hard to get stuff that no one has ever heard of instead of just serving some good quality (preferably local) craft brews with wide appeal.  Ah, well, that might be forgivable if you could train your staff to explain and describe the selections.  Keep trying, Wilko.

Happy with their service. I brought my two cars in to get detailed. They did an excellent job. 
They do take their time cleaning the cars so expect to wait a little bit specially it you are getting your car detailed. 
They have a waiting area with free wifi and vending machines. 
If you use yelp and check in they do offer you 20% off for first time costumers but only on basic cleaning services.

A beautiful studio that is inviting and not at all pretentious. 
Heather is a great instructor and is very helpful with beginners/part time students like myself but certainly has the knowledge and skill to work with all levels. An oasis on Frankford ave

Avoid.  Believe the bad reviews.  Overpriced and poor quality dentistry.
The dentists and office staff change frequently.  I had pain for months after an incomplete root canal by Dr Jean-Baptiste, had to pay an endodontist to repair.  After I complained they phoned to offer me a full refund, have yet to see a check.  Do not go here!  Avoid!

I stayed at this hotel this past November.
Hotel is valet only you are approached as soon as you pull into the drive and are informed this. Valet took my car and apparently parked it within their valet lot in the fire lane around a bend and not in a parking spot. My car suffered over 15 thousand dollars in damage. I never moved my car the entire time I was there. Hotel claimed no responsibility for the damage even though valet parking is your only option and they charge 25 dollars per day for the parking. While there I witnessed valet drivers moving guests cars with the driver door wide open. Its a pity that Hilton is so greedy that they charge you for parking while staying at their hotel then on top of it damage your car and then claim no responsibility. If you check the hotel policy online their policy on parking is valet only. I will never stay at a Hilton property ever again

I have dined here several times with the same experience.... Absolutely amazing.  From the ambiance to the food to the staff five stars!!!! Everyone should give Barclay a try at least once...to me service is just as important as your meal and sincerely speaking its a tie... the service is second to none and the food will melt in your mouth...not to even mention the terrific drink menu ... any weight or a waitress that I have had at this location has been so informative and knowledgeable about every slice of meat to every piece of fish down to the silverware which you get to choose by the way for cutting...cant say enough
Great food, great customer service. Will definitely be back to Amma's. Server & chef went out  of their way to accommodate dietary needs. Good portion sizes.

The rooms are big, but not worth it. The walls are super thin, so if you're okay with waking up cause of neighbors playing super loud music then having to fall asleep to the sound of the train and bars then you'll be fine.

I called BAKED ON 8th after the concierge at the JW Marriot said they were fantastic.  YEP!  Fantastic customer service, local folks that love what they do. 
I needed a cake for a 50th birthday, and spoke to Grace on the phone for a while,and she talked me through suggestions.  I told her a little bit about the woman, that she was Canadian, and recently moved to NYC for a great job, and the cake they did was marvelous! Chocolate and delicious.  The apple on the top is actually cake... it's not a real apple.  I was so impressed. Great value, great local business!!  A++++
Good food. Bad service. Called in a carry out at 5. Told me 35 minutes. Wasn't ready until 6:10. Staff seems lazy besides the tall light skinned guy. He seems to be doing the job for 3 people.

Really good spot for some delicious pizza and dough buds (garlic knots)! We went with a half and half (pepperoni and the trap). 

The pepperoni was delicious, and I loved the crust. The trap was pretty good, however realized that shrimp on pizza is not quite for me. I have some family that I know would love it, and can't wait to get that for them! They have a bunch of other pizzas I'd like to try, and am looking forward to eating here again!

Food isn't great. Waited 2 hours for a salad and sandwich. Called them after 1 hour and they tell me the food is already out for delivery (LIE). Another hour later they finally show up then has the nerve to raise his voice at me and give me attitude. Don't waste your time or money with these incompetent shnooks....
Worst bar in Newtown.  Too crowded and rude staff.
Started with hummus and finished with the beet salad. The beets were plentiful and seasoned to perfection. Served on house made lebnah. Yummy!

I am originally from Lafitte, but now live in NC. Everytime we come home to visit, we usually just settle for Mcdonalds for breakfast. I really wanted some beignets, but didn't wanna drag my boys out to the French Quarter. My mom told me about this place, and that they had beignets.

We ended up eating breakfast here twice on our trip. The first time, I just had beignets. They were really good! Maybe not quite as good as Cafe Du Monde, but they hit the spot. Paired with coffee, it was a very good breakfast. 

My husband also had beignets, along with a meat omelet. He loved his omelet. Said it was the best he'd ever had. My son had eggs and grits and he seemed pleased. The server we had was nice but not so friendly. She seemed to either be overwhelmed or just not happy to be there. At least she did her job well. 

The next morning, we met a friend there. It was a little chaotic because we needed a bigger place to sit and it was packed, so the servers were having trouble getting together enough tables and seemed to be getting frustrated. Once we got settled, things went pretty smoothly. 

I ordered eggs, bacon, grits, and a biscuit. Everything was decent. Wish my bacon would've been a little more cooked. My husband ordered another omelet, adding on some extra meat as a side. My son had an egg and cheese sandwich. I asked them to fry the egg instead or scramble, but it was scrambled anyways. He still ate it but had a little trouble as it kept falling off the bread. Service was meh this time around.

I'd still rather have breakfast here than McDonald's or Shoney's. It's good food for a good price. Just be prepared for a ton of people and a little lack on service.

Very good authentic food. The soup broth was amazing and full of flavor. I will definitely be back tito try the Tom yum soup and pad Thai. Check this place out if you want to be amazed by the flavors and authenticity.

A perfect experience. Endless amazing music from different genres at multiple stages, huge selection of any type of food you could possibly want (hellooooo crawfish Monica), and a great freakin atmosphere. 

Jazz fest 2016 was perfect. Everyone listening to good music, having a good time, indulging in some fabulous Cajun or creole cuisine. Nothing better. Let the good times roll indeed!!

Did notice that as the day progressed the festival became wayyyyyy too crowded. I think they need to get their ish together and figure out a way to handle this as it's honestly a safety hazard.

Didn't even finish my food! It wasn't all that great. I ordered the.... darn I forgot! It's the one under the portobello burger, starts with an "S". But anyway, I've had better. Way better. The fries were good, just overly salted. If you want an awesome burger joint to go to that's in the Nampa area, go to blazin burger. It's the best. Cheaper too.

Place is has one of the best  tacos I've ever had! They make such fresh soft tacos carnitas and the barbacoa tacos are bangin! Just so delicious and all from a food truck. It is truly one of the of the hidden secrets in Onley but it cash only but it's so worth it! Seasoning is so good and the meats are just mouth watering and they do not SKIMP On the meat! I'm a regular customer now! Highly recommend!

I have been coming here for a long time and each time I am treated with warm welcomes and friendly smiles. Rosa and Dr. Lin are very good at what they do and show great patience when explaining things or beginning their work. If you have concerns or just need a regular cleaning this is a great place to go.

This is my favorite Chinese restaurant in terms of flavors, presentation and cost.   The dishes are satisfying, beautifully presented and varied.  Take out in recyclable containers.  Please open a branch downtown!

Me and three friends when there for Korean BBQ. We all ordered the all you can eat.  I guess all you can eat has a different meaning to this place because they cut us off. The food was great but that's because you cook it your self and we had a professional chef with us.

Stay away from this place. 
Receptionist is rude and clearly hates her job. 
I came here because I had something in my eye, I asked the receptionist if they were able to help me with that, she said yes.
Waited one hour to be seen when the health care provider came in, she literally just looked at my eye and said we can't help you here. 
$120 down the drain.
They refused to give a refund and were also very unprofessional about it.
Yummy.  Try the velvet mocha in vanilla with caramel swirls on top.  This is a nice treat after running around outside in the humidity.

Great casual for take out or dine in. Best falafel in town. I am also pretty attached to their fries and hummus. And bonus that it is locally owned and not a chain. I have also catered from here with rave reviews. On my weekly dining circuit.
I had a basic Swedish massage a couple of days ago - Zarek was the masseuse. He did a nice job. The spa facilities were very nice and the staff was very good.

The options for Chinese in Pasco are pretty bad - 
I was pretty excited to see such a wonderful presentation of food packaging. We ordered sesame chicken (4). Chicken cashew(3) bourbon chicken(3) chicken & broccoli (3) 

Wasn't to fond of the bourbon chicken tastes to much like Chinese spare ribs

The chicken and broccoli had a different sauce then all normal places and full of carrots not enough broccoli 

Chicken cashew would be much better with the chicken from chicken and broccoli instead of the boiled shreds

We loved the plain chow mein and ragu - the egg rolls were pretty good as well . 
All said and done we would try more items next time

Menu was good, and the food was very tasty. The pulled pork was not dry at all, but I added some specialty sauces anyways. The sides were really good, and the pickles were very fresh and good unlike some places with old stale soggy pickle slices. Tables were clean and the service was really good. If I lived around here I'd probably come back again

We drove 30 minutes to pick up lunch for Father's Day. The package seemed small so I asked if this was the only bag. They insisted it was the complete order.
When we got to our lunch locale, there were things missing which we were charged for.
They said come back and get the missing items which would take an hour back and forth. They REFUSED to credit us. Said we could come back and get the rice and slaw whenever we wanted. We had to run to a supermarket close by.
I was stunned, I'd never experienced anything like this.

We decided to try The Wayward for my friend's birthday dinner. Wayward had just opened this week, we had reservations for a Sunday at 8:15 pm. The ambiance and weather were perfect that night. They have lights strung up and you don't seem to notice you are in the middle of Center City. The tables were spaced out for adequate social distancing. All the waiters had masks and gloves and were very attentive and knowledgeable. We decided to try ALL the starters on the menu. The corn beignets were my personal favorite. The meals have a French inspiration. The cocktails are reasonably priced and very tasty. I tried The End of the Road, which had tequila, sufmato, apple, and honey. I had the Wayward Burger, which was fresh and delicious. They fries were great too, but by that time I could not eat many. I would recommend this for an intimate dinner. At the moment, they aren't set up for large groups, I believe 5 is the limit. The bathrooms are nice, with individual stalls where the doors go top to bottom, no gaping holes. I plan to come back. Their menu is limited, so I'm hoping to see rotation or items or more items added. Please check it out and tell them a Yelp Elite sent you!

They actually gave me tzatziki this time! Of course I double-checked before leaving, but hey, can't complain. This earns them back a well deserved star. Food is delicious as always, great pricing for what you get (can't beat the falafel platter). Also feel like the hummus tasted even better this time around.

Shoe Warehouse is inexpensive and very nice. They have a decent selection of shoelaces and they are less than half the price of what other stores in the mall offer. In contrast to the majority of shoe stores who have no faintest idea as to which shoes they have in your size, Show Warehouse has all of their shoes on display with all the boxes with available sizes visible. Amazing.
Pickle's is my favorite place for lunch in downtown St. Louis.  The pastrami and Swiss with light mayo on pretzel bread is the best sandwich I've ever had.

Best Chinese Spot in town n country for traditional Chinese takeout ... also.m very reasonably priced.
The staff is always welcoming and they make sure to get my order right everytime. My favorite items are their crab Rangoon's, and the egg foo young. Also try their house fried rice spicy! Believe me you won't regret it

OH MY YUMMINESS!!! I read about this place on line- but it was the picture of the Yoda cocktail ( done as a Star Wars tribute not available the day I went :(  ) 
That made me want to try it out - 
Even without having my Yoda moment the food and drink - I can't remember the name of the cocktail but I got to keep the flamingo float  :) - it was a phenomenal experience - I had the cheesy Todd - a bacon burger that has deep fried mac and cheese hockey puck sized discs for the buns - sweet mother of pearl!! I was in my happy place- I'm still in my happy place!! Our waitress was well informed took great care of us she recommended a chocolate desert that I am driving  back to Tampa for . The decor of the restaurant is cool and a little edgy- comfortable for an older or younger crowd - I am visiting Tampa in a few weeks I will be back at Datz to try the rest of their amazing menu!!!

All the girls who helped with the party were so sweet and patient with the girls! I was really impressed with them :) my daughter and her friends had a great time!  I would def recommend it. Thank you!
Great food, service and atmosphere. Their happy hour appetizers are fantastic.

I discovered this wonderful, cozy, unique bookstore at the Manayunk Eco-Arts Festival this June.  They had a table set up and on it was a copy of Anna Karenina, a nice, old hardback edition.  I just couldn't snap it up quick enough!  Ever since, I've spent at least an hour in their shop twice a month, I just love it!  The owners are so friendly and knowledgeable and they keep some really fantastic books circulating their shelves.  This bookstore is a gem and a fine addition to the Manayunk/Roxborough area!
Food was ok. But not as good as before. They don't know the recipe for a bloody mary. so I switched to well drinks...which were weak and definitely bottom shelf.

Coming from an Italian family, I take italian food very seriously. And Paola has never let me down. From the delicious homemade pastas to the many tasty pizzas to my favorite, tomato basil mozzarella salad Paola's is always a good choice. Not to mention Paola is one of the kindest friendliest women you'll ever meet!! Such a great restaurant and staff and it's also BYOB
On point. Every. Single. Time. Chuys's ain't got  nothing on this. Service is always good. Handmade corn and flour tortillas on demand. Best Tex-Mex in Nashville.

The interior of Elixr is really beautiful, and is an excellent place to chat with friends. I enjoyed a Flora latte, and Cortado. If I'm being picky, both coffees had a ton of foam, but it still tasted great. 
A barista friend of mine says this is his favorite place to go in the area, so definitely worth the try!

Not sure why this place is rated so highly. Terrible sushi where the raw fish tastes spoiled. The salmon was nauseating. As a Japanese who has been eating sushi for all my life, this was one of the worst experiences I've had especially for the price. Service was good tho but does not compensate for terrible sushi that is way too expensive.

I've been to this location several times. I've had great service every time! The food on occasion has not met my expectations but the situation is always corrected. Last night's dinner was very good and as always excellent service!

Disgusting place do yourself a favor will definitely be beneficial to your overall health and safety, never eat at this place it is a disgusting place with even sicker(mental) employees. I purchased a large pizza from these people online that included a waterbug that was definitely an inch in length. I definitely believe thus was done purposely though it is also possible it was a mistake. I had a pic I wanted to share but unfortunately for you though fortunate for them I deleted it from my cellphone photos. I got tired of looking at it cause it disgusted me. Take my advice and never eat here.
Absolutely delicious food. Good service. A perfect restaurant. Food was delivered quickly. The waiters are very nice.

Not coming back! Expect a super long wait!

My Wife and I saw Savarino's on Diners, Drive-ins & Dives and got excited when we saw it was located here in Nashville. We live in Franklin, but made the small trip to downtown Nashville just to eat at Savarino's. Being a Saturday, free parking in the area was filled up, so we parked in the paid parking, which is like $3.00 an hour. We only paid for only an hour thinking there is no way we would be in Savarino's longer than that.

But boy were we wrong! We simply ordered two sandwiches and we literally had to wait over 50 minutes for them! Then when we got them, I expected them to taste like heaven for that long of a wait, but instead it was something barely better than you could get at Subway. Our meal was over $20 Dollars for two sandwiches and two waters.

While we were waiting for our sandwhiches, I had to go back and pay for another hour of parking, so I ended up spending $6.00 dollars in parking. I know the parking situation isn't necessarily Savarino's fault and it comes with the territory, but the main issue is having to wait over 50 minutes for just two semi-ok sandwhiches.

Everyone around us was getting mad about their orders taking forever as well. An old couple near us who also ordered two sandwiches asked the rather large lady who works at the register, why it takes almost an hour for two sandwiches, and she just replied in an rude manner "it's first come first serve!" and then walked away from them.

We were really disappointed with Savarino's and we don't plan on coming back. I really think they have big heads now that they have been on TV and they think they can treat customers like crap and still get our business!

PROS

-It was on TV?

CONS

-Wait time (Horrible!)
-Number of tables
-Rude employees
-Food is overhyped

Great gift shop!  It's that go-to place when you're looking for a fun gift. Especially great for people with a sense of humor. 
While it's not local for me, I'd definitely go again if I'm in the area!

Signed up with move for seniors to move my mother from Hot Springs, AR to Bend, OR.  It has now been 19 business days / 27 days since pick up and no delivery. I am now on the third projected delivery date.    Initial quote seemed reasonable, but at the end they claimed more weight and added $1,000 to the original quote.  They say they have there own moving team, but an independent mover in ARK had to pick up first, then to storage, then who knows how many other companies have touched the shipment.  I feel the firm low balled the quote, misrepresented that "their" people move the furniture, and their communication on delivery date is terrible.  I was also supposed to receive a 10% discount for referral from Holiday Retirement, but quotes and invoices never reflected the discount.    I would not recommend moveforseniors for across country moves.

FOOD:  GREAT!  had the tuna, the quality was superb.  No skimping and no weirdness.  The croquetas were perfect!  They are like elite hushpuppies (but made with potatoes).
SERVICE:  subpar.  Kind of act like take out is a hassle,  took fifty minutes for a sandwich (Saturday lunch rush), and guy at the bar put off that its a privledge that you are able to step foot in there...  oh well.  Croquetas. Update: went back recently and there were three very friendly men working on a Saturday night.  And...  food is still awesome!

I love the food. Deviled crab actually tastes fresh (not your average frozen mass produced deviled crabs). Pulled pork and yucca are my favorite thing to get here. Excellent service with friendly staff. Dining area could use an update.

In my neck of the woods a sudden yearning for  take out sushi used to result in a stop at the grocery store for an uninspired version of the delicacy.  Then I saw Yoshi Cafe, nestled next to a chinese place and a dry cleaner.  When my latest craving hit, I took a chance.  Walking in, I  discovered a bland and austere interior, but thank goodness I didn't let atmosphere be the final word.

 The sushi itself was very good and, at times, absolutely delicious.   The rice was perfectly cooked and seasoned. The fish fresh, the tempura crunchy but not greasy. I don't go for a lot of the heavily sauced sushi, so this review is limited to the basic masago/ unagi/ spicy tuna variety sushi which were all delicious and traditionally prepared.  I did step outside the box and order their Outback roll with grilled beef and scallions.  It  was delightfully tasty and I would order it again, especially for those in my world who don't do sashimi.

 They are a tad on the pricey side, but it's a price I gladly pay for fresh, tasty sushi.  I'll be back.

Conveniently located, well maintained, very friendly staff, but when I went to have a prescription filled for a glucose monitor, they didn't have the one my healthcare provider specified.  I'll have to return in a couple of days, wasting a total of over an hour.

The absolute best Philly cheesesteak I've had in my life!! My cousin told me this is his fav cheesesteak, and I trust his judgment so I tried this place out opposed to the other top contenders, even though it was out of the way. Came for lunch and the line wasn't too long, and got my food quickly, very lucky apparently. Ordered a cheesesteak whiz wit. Came out piping hot- the meat was cooked perfectly and flavorful, chopped up the right size. Onions were nice and caramelized, and they gave me plenty of whiz! Also had chopped peppers on the side which gave it a nice kick. Seating is pretty limited, and I chose to eat outside, but all the tables were taken, so I just walked down the street to the park and ate it there. If it wasn't so far from my hotel room I'd definitely have gone to back to get it again before leaving Philly.

Man With a Van provided excellent service. Their cost was reasonable and the movers were kind, trustworthy, and efficient. They handled my boxes and furniture with care and and followed my lead regarding how I wanted the move to go. I'm a satisfied customer.

To complete the first item on our Florida Foodie Bucket List, my girlfriend and I decided to eat at Bodega so that she could order the much famed Cuban. The Cuban lived up to its reputation and she said it was the best she'd ever eaten; crisp, flavorful, well balanced and filling.  

I ordered the Vegetarian Plato and Café con leche.  The mojo grilled tofu was excellent!  I have eaten tofu prepared countless ways but their mojo was a real treat and matched the morning weather - bright, moist and with a little bit of heat. The rice and beans were bland but the maduros rounded out a good meal.  The Café con leche was, in a word, disappointing.  The coffee tasted burnt and was very bitter/ashy.  The milk cut the worst of it but much of the beverage ended up in the trash. 

Overall we had a pleasant experience and I understand why a long line started forming several minutes before the lunch hour.

Hello. This shop saved me. I was on vaca from Cali and didn't have any gas with me. Tried their CBD flower and a few CBD concentrates, and it kept me right until I could head back. These folks have class and style. Gave great advice and got me set in the right direction, because I'm not knowledgeable on CBD products. Thanks to all the staff that helped me. I will definitely be back when I get back to FL!

We were going to try another place but our Uber driver said we HAD to go here. It was across the street from our hotel so it was convenient too. I had a loaded baked potato with bbq on top-amazing!! The line moved fast and there was plenty of room. Downstairs is more like a restaurant and upstairs is party central. Once we were upstairs we realized there was another entrance off a side street and a line to order that was much shorter!

Well, we had a muffaletta and quite honestly I was not impressed.

We were in  the French Market the day before and there was another place where we had the famed NOLA sandwich and it was crazy good.  Toasted and overflowing with olive spread, so much we needed a few forks as it was like a side salad!

The next day we found that this was the "Original" and were excited... until we ordered and the counter person reaches behind her and grabs a pre wrapped cold muffaletta...  I was taken aback for a second, as if it were like a "Grab and Go"...

We opened the $12 half and found it lacking compared to the place in the market place...  honestly, this was yesterday and 1/4 of the 1/2 we got is still sitting in the hotel mini fridge...  Just not feeling it...

Its like Pat and Genos in Philly, just because you "created" the first cheesesteak, does not mean its the best...  Just like San Diegos El Indio, just because they claim to be the best Mexican food and Oldest etc etc... its not...  someone has improved your product...

All that's left is a 30-acre estate, but the mansion tour is led by a charismatic tour guide, who ends by dropping you off to some amusing wine staff for a tasting. Included as an option with our Access pass, so it just required a drive and waiting for the tour to start as we wandered the grounds.

I ordered to go a miso soup, mushroom rolls and salmon+avocado rolls. 
OMG!!!! This place reaaaaly impressed me!!! Miso soup was excellent!!!  mushroom (shiitake)rolls were on point! And salmon+avocado rolls were absolutely amazing!!!! There were so big pieces of salmon!!!!

I Hope You Like Waiting!! We went on Yelp list from home at 5:00, which said it was a 2 hour wait. The Yelp wait list told us to arrive at the restaurant by 6:30. We got there at 6:25 and were told we'd be seated in about 20 minutes. Turns out we were seated at 8:20!!! Almost 2 hours after we arrived, after being told repeatedly it wouldn't be much longer. We would have never ever waited had we known. EVER! I really can't stand people who have no respect for your time. The staff we interacted with certainly had no regard, I'm sure could have cared less. Perhaps the incredible increase in prices they had in the last week will help reduce the crowds. Decent food but nothing is close to worth that wait, especially when you were deceived

The food is okay, by no means terrible, but for some reason I go here at least once every couple months

The blood orange margaritas are usually to DIE for, and although the food (esp. the guacamole) can get pricey, they are pretty large portions.

I'm not sure what they marinade their mushrooms in, but it ruins the texture of the portebellos that come with the vegetable fajitas

They do have sweet velvet booths, and a little picture booth that my boyfriend and i take pictures in every time we go

So great to find a late night spot that serves food (and drinks)! Nice little patio area with fans. The food was super delicious! My husband and I got the spinach dip (the best!) and a roast beef poboy with the freshest tomatoes and most savory gravy! Not to mention the Pear 75. Definitely pleasantly surprised and will be a new "regular."

Yes! So glad to have another location aside from the crowded Avon one. This one is far more quiet which is surprising given the number of people that are regularly dining here.  Been a few times since they've opened and service is great and the food is consistently good!
Joe uses the freshest ingredients and has built an incredible following.  Great neighborhood pizza place.  Thanks Joe

Vu is so amazing and friendly. He always works so hard and gives us amazing, beautiful nails. my acrylics stayed for 4 weeks with no fill! absolutely give this place a try- $15 gel, $25 gel mani, $25 full set acrylic, $35 full set acrylic with gel. Love this place!

Acrop til you drop! Such a fun restaurant and definitely the best bar tenders I've ever met that exceed all expectations (especially Hope)! 
The food is also amazing, my favorite is the spinach dip appetizer and the lemon soup

I'm gonna be honest with you here... I frequent Yats almost every Sunday for lunch. If you haven't learned yet, this place is a great cure for the uh... Sunday morning headache? 

I've heard a negative complaint about the appearance of the food. That's kinda silly. Maybe this isn't the place to hit up on a first date. If you're looking for good food under $9, you cant miss it.  

If you love yourself, order a half and half of Chili Cheese Etouffe and Chicken Maque Choux. Extra bread. You won't be disappointed. 

Also, the stockpile of extra cajun sauces and spices is wonderful. I don't ever really look at what i'm grabbing, I kinda just throw it all on. It's all good.

I've done take-out from this restaurant several times when I'm in this part of Philly. 
If you have read my other reviews on yelp, you know I have tried several different Indian restaurants, so I'm very familiar with the cuisine and what to expect.
What I like about Mumbai Bistro:
1) The prices -- most affordable Indian food. If you're on a budget like me, this is perfect.
2) Simple, easy-to-understand menu. It also tells you what's spicy, what's gluten free, what contains dairy.
3) Fast service. Even if you don't call in and order ahead for take-out, they get your to-go order ready in 5-10 mins.
4) The food taste and quality is good. Good selection of appetizers and entrees. It's not THE best Indian food ever (you get what you pay for), but it's always good, enjoyable.

There's nothing I really dislike about this place.

First try at dinner on a Monday night.  Decent service.  But music was too loud and/or our waiter was too aloof and couldn't hear what we ordered so he only brought out one dessert instead of two.  

Shredded pork was very dry.  Had to swipe the sauce off the beef dish to make it edible.  Shredded  beef was good. The fried plaintain things (forgot what they called) that came with both of our meals were dry and old/stale.  The combo ceviche was very ood.

This place is very expensive and the quality of the food is horrible even a simple fried vegetable spring rolls they don't get it right... crispy from outside side but gooey inside... very disappointing!!! Definitely NOT coming back.

This place is amazing. They give you a lot of personal attention. Camille is my stylist and does amazing color. Enjoy a glass of vino or hot cup of coffee while getting your head rubbed for an excessive amount of time: yes please! Go. Staff is great and the place is just fun!

We usually enjoy our meals here but recently on a Saturday morning we had an awful experience.  To begin with both the hostess and our server were miserable and it seemed as if we were inconveniencing them by asking questions!  My wife's pancake sampler was excellent as always but my grilled cheese was lackluster!  What really burned me was the extra $1.50 charge for bacon on my grilled cheese, it comes with Ham and when I asked to substitute bacon, our miserable server just smirked.  I simply swapped bacon for ham, why the charge?  When I asked the cashier at the end of the meal she just said "thats what they do"  and rung us up...I guess they have enough business to just not care...thats sad!

Been eating at Roberto's for 30 years or so, first, down in Vegas, and for a few years now in Reno & Sparks. Never disappoints, though I do miss the salsa bar having cucumber & tomato slices, green onion stalks, chopped cilantro, and (dried) oregano (also miss the fresh squeezed orange and carrot juice options from long ago). Enjoy the cheese enchilada & chili relleno plate, but can't beat the special that includes a wet burrito & drink (Jamaica, yes!) and a Veterans discount to boot...Oh yeah! Thank you Roberto's for all the great meals over the years, and to many more!

i've ordered delivery from these guys a couple of times, whilst working. not only did i enjoy it, i enjoyed to the extent of OMGTHATSAGREATSAMMICH!!!!

seriously....i dig the zucchini sticks, all fried up, too.

(btw--the sammich of awesome is the veggie hoagie--eggplant, ahoy!)

May 26, 2021 approx 2:30. This day had to have been a bad day for this crew. It was not a shop friendly place. I've shopped here before and never had much of a problem other than a shortage for product shopped for. Though there was at least 5 or 6 workers on the floor, not one took the time to ask if I needed anything or if I was finding everything needed. After I tried searching for a particular product for a few minutes but decided to leave and shop somewhere else that would offer better customer service. Before leaving I asked for the manager and told her my concerns and lack of service. I left feeling as though she didn't give a hoot either. I'm sure if the manager is like this with most of Petco's customers, District needs to be made aware.

Well damn. 

Since my one former location of 20th & Locust near Rittenhouse Square is now eliminated (and apparently has been since 2008, I just found this 5-star rated establishment and figured I'd add my 2 cents in.

You don't understand how much I miss this place. For those of you who still have access, I hear/read your complaints (on other locations, not this particular one), but yet, you still go. Not I, where I now live in a land where I have to settle for gas station shops and 7-11's. Nope, nothing holds a candle to you, WaWa.*

I hear you even went behind my back and got milkshake machines after I've been gone. I feel the appropriate way that I should feel: jealousy, hurt with a tinge of envy. Even when you got rid of the Dunkin Donuts years back (don't get me started on those either), I still maintained my loyalty with your sandwiches, your fresh hash browns in the am, most excellent of sides (where else can I get some good mashed spuds past 1am?) and your french vanilla 'coffee' dispensed out of your noisy contraption. Even your fuel was reasonably priced and easy on-the-go. 

I hope when I return for a visit, you will welcome me back with open arms (or at least open doors). 

*I DO have to up the ante by saying I hear Sheetz is onto you...but they aren't accessible within these parts, so no need to worry.
Don't come here it is closed despite speaking with a Peppermill manager who lied and said it was still open. Open 24 hours, a 24 hour lie!

Good food- busy atmosphere. Or waiter was absolutely stellar and on the ball. We went on a very busy evening, though the food did not reflect their hectic schedule. It was amazingly well flavored and a large portion- a bonus since my husband and I both have dietary restrictions. I would recommend it to anyone with Celiac Sprue or gluten sensitivity. It is a bit pricey, but you are getting your money's worth

Lesley was our tour guide for the Garden District walking tour and it was fantastic! She was so friendly and knowledgeable. The tour was very interesting and I highly recommend it if you have the opportunity. The two hours flew by and when we are back in New Orleans we will definitely be booking another tour!

I have to totally disagree with the guy who left the latest review. McCrossen's has become my favorite neighborhood spot since moving there a few months ago. The first night we checked it out, my roomate and I sat at the bar and struck up a conversation with the bartender. He was super friendly and even offered us 2 drinks on the house as a welcome to the neighboorhood  gesture. They have a great beer selection on tap and the food is great for a pub. It looked so good that night that we went back the following week to check it out for ourselves. Some favorites are the Sheeps milk ricotta with warm flatbread, the calamari and the wings. The wings are sooo good.  A good mix of typical bar food ( burgers, wings, fries) mixed with a few surprises ( ricotta, pork chop, pasta).  A great place to grab a drink with a friend, catch a game , or even bring the family for a nice dinner. The atmosphere is so warm and cozy.

This place is definitely the Cheers of Fairmount. Always a good mixed crowd in there and the staff is so friendly.

Advertised incorrectly. Says we will get complimentary breakfast, there will be a jacuzzi, and none of those things are true. My key to get in my room doesn't work and the woman said she would have to downgrade me (AT THE SAME PRICE OF $200 A NIGHT) to be able to have a functioning key???? Just shitty service and policies at an unfair price. Really sad I chose to book here. Trying to celebrate my friends getting engaged and we are being bombarded with bad news from this hotel.

This place was a great find. Who doesn't want unlimited sushi?? The first visit was wonderful, the staff was extremely attentive. The sushi is decent, I'm a big stickler on shrimp tempura being warm in sushi rolls and this place didn't fulfill this for me. The second time we went the food was great but the service was horrific. We got our first order in and had to wait 25 minutes for our next rolls. We thankfully weren't starving anymore because of the first role. Our waitress had horrible time management.

Craving a burger and nothing I wanted was close because of the pope. Stumble upon this place looking through yelp. Chill relaxing atmosphere. Friendly staff. The burgers here are amazing and beefy . Like a burger on steriods and full of flavor. Definitely going to come back agian
Ps. Also had the pork belly Bahn mi. My favorite off the menu

Pretty good wood fired pizza. I've never been to Italy so I'm not going to go on about any comparisons, but this tasted good, so I'll be back. I went with the Casanova's Delight, with prosciutto and taleggio, without even really noticing it was a white pizza (which I love) so that was a big plus. The crust is nice and chewy, plenty of cheese, and the prosciutto was a good salty accompaniment. No complaints at all. I was there with a large party, all of whom were pretty happy with what they got, so I'd like to go back and try a few other items. It looks like the menu rotates at least with the seasons, so there's a little bit of a "farm to table" aspect, which...whatever. But the rotating menu might be nice to keep things from getting stale if you visited frequently. Out waitress was fun too, which is always nice, and prices are reasonable for what you get. I'm looking forward to returning.

Fantastic place: great atmosphere, nice people, and deeeeelicious food! My girlfriend and I came here for the first time and very much enjoyed ourselves. Jessica, the woman who assisted us, was very pleasant and knowledgeable.

As for the food, we both built our own bowls; my girlfriend had shredded beef in hers, and I had bison in mine. All the ingredients are extremely fresh, and the food is prepared and assembled upon ordering.

I highly recommend this spot if you love eating healthily and/or are into fitness!

Love their food. Was first introduced to this place at the Jazz festival, and now we eat every time we come back home to Tampa. 

We're not Cuban so I can't say whether it's super authentic or not, but it's a great tasting meal that's reasonably priced. Lots of options. Please visit, you will not regret.

We have had a number of positive experiences here. Their covid protocols are very good and we have felt safe dining since. I would recommend lunch over dinner, just to steer clear of busier more crowded times. 

The servers are great, the food is good, and the experience is a ton of fun. 

We don't have much to choose from when it comes to Korean BBQ in Reno, so it's nice to have this solid option available.

Sometimes I think I'm being too generous with my ratings, but I hate giving a bad review. I honestly do really like this place. I only get my hair done professionally once a year around my birthday as a splurge. This review is for May 2010 when I was last here. They were able to fit me in on a one day notice. The salon is very clean and has a very nice welcoming feel to it. My hairdresser was very friendly and nice. I came in for a cut and for partial highlights. I have dark brown hair and had highlights from a year ago that had grown out but still visible. I asked her to cut my hair and match the color and style of my highlights. The cut was great. The layering and eveything was perfect. However, the highlight came out WAY too light. I looked almost blond. She could not match them at all. She offered to fix them at no charge when I came back (they were closing), but I didn't really feel comfortable. She sent me a nice card in the mail, and the cut was very nice.This is why I feel bad not giving a 5 star, but I feel like the highlights were done so bad, I couldn't go back there. I went back to LA to my regular stylist to get them fixed. From now on, I think I'm just going to have to deal with the 2 hr drive to La for my hair appointments.

STOP reading other reviews and looky here.  Brothers and Sisters, the Bacon is so GOOD and ADDICTIVE, you'd snort it if your nose could taste it.  

Oh Laaawd this food is maddeningly good!

If anyone is pitchin' a fit about this place, you need to ignore them.  They're just mad because this place was so busy they had to wait over 60 seconds the sweet sweet lovin' of that Brioche French Toast.  

Thank You Jessica!  May monuments be built in your honor.  I bring nothing but a few dollars and you give me BACON with a smile.  We're Not Worthy! We're Not Worthy! We're Not Worthy!

Stopped here to start off our 4th of July get away. So nice. Relaxed. Attentive. Good food. We were the first ones there so it was empty, but filled up quickly. I had the Hudson cakes so good and big. Frank had the crepelle very good as well. Unlimited coffee. Such a nice way to start our   
day.
Random visit and definitely will be coming back. They are not skimpy with their fillings for their jelly filled and Boston cremes. The donuts themselves are super soft and delicious. 10+/10!
Food would have been better if packaged correctly and presentable. U judge by sight first. And this is not what I wanted to see.
Fantastic food!! I love the clams, it was very fresh and tasty!!! I will definitely come back again!!

Our Costco is one of our favorite places to shop. Especially during this time of wearing masks and staying 6 feet away from each other. They have organized the store so well, that you have no fear of being too close to people and everyone wears masks, during this Covid-19 time. 
And of course the real reason for shopping there is the excellent selection of food items, and many other items that we need! From clothing, shoes, my husband buying a step ladder, books, and many other items. Another advantage to shopping at Costco is their employees, always courteous and helpful. Thank you Costco!

We reached out to Southern Funeral Care to hold the service of my mother. While there was a bit of a scheduling mix up in the beginning. The staff made sure to make it right. The set up is quite large with a lot of seating. They provide tables and podium for photos and flowers. They have a dvd video system to play a memorial video if you have one made. We did and it was lovely. There was plenty of parking as well. We are thankful for them and helping us say goodbye to such a beautiful soul.

This is one of the most gorgeous parks in the entire country.  It's really hard to believe that it's within Philadelphia City Limits! I love the ruins of old mills, houses, stone walls, wells, etc.! My favorite spot to sit and meditate is Devil's Pool, truly gorgeous!   I believe that Forbidden Drive was once the PA Turnpike back when people traveled by horse and 
carriage, long before 76 was built!

Slowly but surely I'm trying to make my way to every John Besh restaurant in New Orleans. After attempting to come numerous times during happy hour, each time being a bust due to an hour + wait, I was able to finally snag a table with a group of friends. I've never heard of a restaurant where you have to make a reservation for a table during happy hour, but due to the popularity of this place, I can see why it's necessary. Everyone at the table ordered different pizzas, half and half so fortunately I was able to sample more than one. 

-Clam. My absolute favorite. The combination of flavors on this thing was amazing. I regretted not ordering a whole one. 
-Wild Mushroom. I personally don't like mushrooms but for anyone that does I can see this being a favorite. Everything else on this pizza (tomato, fontina, caramelized onions, bacon and egg) made it delicious. Might I add this is probably the best bacon I've ever had. 
-Tartufo (burrata-yeah I had to google it too lol. It's some type of Italian cheese made from mozzarella. Mascarpone, truffle oil, and egg.) Probably my least favorite. It just wasn't hitting it like the others. 
-Prosciutto. Wasn't bad but wasn't outstanding either. I love prosciutto but this pizza just wasn't great. 
-Calabrese (tomato, spicy salami, mozzarella capers and olives). Really really good! 

Overall each pizza had its own unique taste and was pretty good. The crust is delicious too. Would I say this is the best place to get pizza in Nola? Not even. Definitely worth trying though at least once. And especially during happy hour when they are half off and the drinks are as well. I'm looking forward to coming back and trying some of their other dishes as well as the dessert which I've heard fantastic things about.

Richard White PA is easily one of the WORST "doctors" I have ever come into contact with. I need excused absences from work so I don't get fired and lose my job and he stated that's "against banners policies" and when I asked to see this "policy" in writing he told me he does not have to show me that policy "because I am not an employee" It is well within my patient rights to have access to that material. All I wanted was antibiotics for my leg and a note so I could rest my leg and not get fired from my job for having unexcused absences. Richard even told me I could lose my leg from this infection but refused to give me more than 1 1/2 days off of work to heal. I will be contacting Richards supervisor Anabel Bevan at 520-381-6777 and filing a formal complaint against this "doctor" and the banner urgent care as a whole. In my notes it says he advised me of things he absolutely never advised me of and that I was "satisfied with my visit" which is absolutely not the case. When I came back in to the office to ask why Richard lied in his notes I could hear him talking poorly of me with co workers and the reason for my visit using my name and speaking ill of me stating that I am "just being lazy and not wanting to work" I will be in touch with an attorney for medical malpractice and falsifying official notes.
Very good food and drinks. We enjoyed the music. Very fast and friendly service. We would definitely come back.

Carol's Sweets is a haven for anyone who has a sweet tooth. It feels like an old-fashioned candy shop with a modern twist. There are candies in glass jars, instead of plastic bulk bins like at the grocery store. They also carry a lot of imported sweets, for those who like something a little different.

They have anything and everything you're sugary taste buds could be craving, from truffles and nougats to jellies and lollipops. I grew up loving Pez candies, and Carol's has loads of them in all sorts of different characters. There are boxed chocolates for quick gifts, but you may want to take some time and try one of their truffles from their counter. It's like a little piece of chocolatey heaven melting in your mouth. Honestly, I think I got goosebumps because it was so sinfully good.

Carol's also has cookies and chocolates at their counter, and right now everything is Halloween-themed. There are treats shaped like cats, pumpkins, bats, and witches. A great idea for party treats, if you're thinking of throwing a Hallow's Eve bash this year.

I was surprised to see they also sell "passion paint", for those feeling a little amorous, I guess. Looks like there's something for both kids and adults in this candy shop!

I believe that this place possibly serves the best ramen in Philadelphia centre city. I had the miso ramen and garlic chicken wings. Both dishes were really good! This place is a little smaller so their capacity for seating are slightly more limited. It was very interesting to watch the chef prepare our food (it was a great free appetiser to make you hungrier if you ask me). I guess the only bad experience I had there was my male server with semi red-Mohawk hairstyle was a little unfriendly in my (and my friend's) opinion.

One of my favorite juiceries in town.  Always consistency with every juice.  Awesomeness in every bottle.  The staff is always friendly and helpful with suggestions.  The facility is always clean and inviting.  With  variety of juices, smoothies and healthy food options, you can't go wrong here.

4 and a half stars for sure... wide variety of calzones, tasty food, friendly service, and quiet. We're visited in the middle of the afternoon in a week day. Such a great stop for a meal. Wine was good too!

Foster's was amazing! I had to go in due to a radiator problem and they got right to it. I do not know much about cars but they found and fixed the problem right away. The price was way cheaper than expected and as far as I know, it was pretty fair. As well, I had work and could not make it in time to pick up my car before closing and the crew was nice enough to open the shop the next morning on their day off just for me to come pick up my car. Overall, a great experience and the next time I have car problems (which I'm sure will be soon) I will definitely be back.

Amazing meal! I've been looking forward to eating here as MoPho is one of my favorite restaurants in the city. The ingredients are of the highest quality and the flavors are nothing to be matched. I had the Red Bean Pacherri which our waitress explained as a dish for the adventurous and I was immediately all in. We dined with some colleagues from Dallas and they did not stop raving about every bite they took. Can't wait to go back!

This was the worst stay at a hotel in Reno. Non smoking floor yes, but we smelled smoke and cigars all night on our floor. Kids biting the walls, bottles and glassware up and down the halls and the elevators, we aired 25 min and no service. We had to take the service  elevator only after we called down for help. The room was ok but dirty lipstick glasses. Just bad all around

Been here the last 4 mornings. Food is delicious, especially the breakfast omelette. Bloody Mary's are unbelievably good also. Service has been great every time we've been. Check your bill & ask questions. 50% of the time they will try to slip in extra charges. We are staying at Holiday Inn & have the breakfast voucher but most of the waitresses still try to charge you something. Kids are suppose to eat free, but that's only happened 1 of the mornings. We have 2 more mornings here in Clearwater Beach & will spend them eating Breakfast at Jimmy's!!!
Food mediocre.  Nothing special.  We were placed outside with no heaters working and it was cold 56 degrees.   Disappointed as the restaurant was recommended to us.

Kingfish Grill is average, at best. Our 3-rating is actually generous. We stayed at a hotel nearby and figured this place served fresh, local seafood, which they had little of. While our food was ok, nothing we ordered would make anyone crave a return and while our server was very good, the food didn't match her effort, unfortunately. Again, if you come expecting an ok meal you'll be satisfied. Anything more, however, and you'll feel like you made a mistake by coming here. 

Even the drinks were very average. The martini my friend ordered had olives and blue cheese that tasted nasty and the appetizers weren't the flavorful coconut shrimp and calamari we expect. The shrimp was nearly burned (you can see in the photo we took) and the calamari was dry. 

Our dishes were fair, too. I ordered the frutti de mar, which came lukewarm and the spicy marinara had little to no flavor. There was definitely no spice.  None of the seafood (there were two mussels, two clams and two shrimp) tasted local. My friend ordered the fish tacos and the waitress warned us it was frozen fish, nothing local. Come on, guys, everyone has local grouper. Use that! It would make the difference in the world. 

Still trying to figure out the identity of this place. There's no great food (they said they used to have sushi, but it bombed and they took it off), the drinks are average and while the atmosphere is pretty good, what makes a person return here? That I can't answer. We wouldn't come back.
Very cleanly with all the new guidelines. A little pricey but that was expected. The food was wonderful as well as the drinks.

3rd visit in a year.
1st visit: met friends there for drinks. Arrived to get the last pint off a pitcher and they had several before me. Was told we all were cut off and no more beer for anyone, even after the waitress acknowledged I just arrived. Dumb move. Understand dram shop laws, etc. 

2nd visit:  Met friends with girlfriend for Sunday lunch. Waitress brought our beer to the table and spilled a full beer on my girlfriend. Their answer was to give a free beer! Didn't even offer to comp her meal or anything, instead she gets a beer while wearing one. 

3rd visit: went today and was told sit anywhere. 80 degrees and sunny so we sat at the patio bar beside 4 other people. After 13 mins no one acknowledged us till we picked up our keys and started leaving. Was told by the bartender they were trying to raise the garage door on the bar. If she can say that when we are leaving, could she not say that when we sat down?

Will never go back. 3rd strike means they are dead to me.

I was in the area on business and found this gem. Being a NYer , hard to find a NY style diner, but this place comes pretty close. Ordered the Gyro Omelette and it was very good , nice size omelette , home fries and toast for $8 , cant go wrong. From looking at their menu, most of the items were priced very well , unlike a diner in NY/NJ. Wish they had a place closer to Tampa where I reside,as I would frequent it often.

Super cute interior and friendly staff. Loved the mint green tea and all of our desserts. However, our tea cups were very dusty..

Prices are okay. Cute date, hang out spot or even brunch with some friends/family.

Great day today on a sweet little course.  We only played 9 but it was quiet and easy.  People were nice and I lived the birds, the mountains, the little patio homes and water feature.  I'm not a good golfer so par 3 and 4 was just right!

There is nothing like this place in St. Louis.  It's located in the basement of the Vin de Set building on Chouteau.  There is an L-shaped bar with stools and several bar tables with stools, along with several couches to lounge on.  They have several flat screen TV's.  There are over 50 beers on tap, along with probably over 100 more bottles, and the bartenders are very helpful and knowledgeable of all of the beers.  You can order a pint or a 6 oz small "taster" for half the price of the pint.  My wife isn't a huge beer drinker so she loved the smaller pour option.  The food comes from PW pizza which is a restaurant located above this place.  The food was delicious.  Portion sizes were huge.   The atmosphere is very intimate but fun and casual.  No complaints here.

The WORST salon, most poorly managed, and unorganized business I've ever worked at.  I worked there for a year and was belittled, degraded, worked six days a week.  Often nine  or ten days in a row working conditions, and the hourly rate of pay is low and measly.  The customers are extremely generous with their tips; however credit card charged tips are taxed so heavily that the meager hourly pay (9.50) isn't worth it.  All the money spent on fancy surveillance cameras could be funneled to perhaps purchase the employees lunch once in a while; and/or contribute to health care costs or 401k.  The year I was there the owner never bought us lunch, not once.  
It's disgraceful to hard working stylists, whom all have paid for our education out of pocket ourselves, not by our parents whom most never graduated high school; to work so hard everyday for a meager hourly rate, then have your company owner walk past you in Gucci designer shoes that none of us could ever afford not on a months wages.  The mgr is an egomaniac dictator that got really upset when her regulars started choosing me over her.  I was on duty when the shoplifter was shot.  I would have appreciated being sent home that day.  But to the mgmt that's just normal life and happens everyday.  No thank you !!  I have never been worked like a robot or treated like a robot at any salon or any job like I was there.   Out of the 80 great clips in St. Louis this franchise is the worst and I will NEVER, EVER, recommend or work at a Terry Crow owned Great Clips.  False advertising, when religious folks walk in a ask for the a male stylist and it's really a woman dressed in drag.  It's a weird setup and vibe in there.  Ever notice no blonde Caucasian pretty ladies working in there?  I was one and was the minority.  Super uncomfortable my nickname was Ellie Mae Clampitt, the dumb hick from the Beverly Hillbilly's.  Degrading and super toxic atmosphere!!! 
Name the song on the radio was the only thing for the stylist to grasp.  A hell hole.

I have to say, even though I did not find my dress at MBC, I truly do believe they are a top tier bridal shop in the Midwest. 

My check-in went smoothly - I was paired with Lauren who I highly recommend. She and the owner, Young helped pull many dressed and listened to what I liked and didn't like about each one.

I'm looking for something very specific and Young was kind enough to give me designer recommendations to fit my needs - that's going above and beyond what they needed to do, and I greatly appreciated it.

I'm super picky... I have something very specific in mind. Unfortunately what I'm looking for is not super common in the Midwest so I totally understand! 

Overall, very impressed with the service. 

If you are a bride in indy, scheduling an appointment here is a must! They have the largest selection of dresses out of anywhere. Happy hunting!

I decided to give this company a try as they got such good reviews on yelp and I was not disappointed. Roy was able to come and fix my dryer the same day I called! He was efficient, courteous, knowledgeable and very polite... now I can get back to the laundry; oh joy!

The BEST bagels in NL and Fishtown, hands down. Nicest owners, their whitefish salad is on point too. Actually, everything we have ever had from here has been excellent. This is the kind of neighborhood establishment we need to support!

Run away from these movers. We did a cross country move with them through Budget Van Lines and it did not go well. Since your move is based on weight, our van showed up unweighed!  So, we had no way to trust them on our household goods weight. The laborers were polite and efficient, but that is the only part of the move that went well. We waited several weeks to receive our goods, and that would have been okay, except that this company does not call their customers and let them know that the shipment will be late. When it was finally delivered, we learned that Gulf Coast had subbed out our move to another company. The delivery driver was high, the assistant mover was drinking beer out of a Slurpee cup and there were boxes missing. We also did not receive our king mattress and box springs (how do you lose that?) and some valuable furniture. The boxes marked Silver, China, and Pewter were missing along with boxes marked Tools. Through all this, I fault their communication and total lack of care. Movers are scammers.

Less than one.  Although appointments are made, they seem to always be overbooked.  On one occasion, the technician was more than 20 min late while I waited in their station.  The policy is they charge the customer late fees if we are late but the same is not true for their tardiness.

The five stars is for the amazing manager Jesus.  Our party of six was there on Saturday evening about 5:30.  There were several large parties already seated so the wait time was about forty minutes.  Shortly after being seated Jesus delivered three delicious appetizers, on the house, and thanked us for being patient.  During our meal we watched Jesus work along side the staff, moving tables and chairs and delivering meals.  I don't think I've ever witnessed such dedication to a job and his customers.  Jesus and his staff were able to get people in, seated and fed very quickly.  The entire staff was polite and helpful, I'm sure in part to Jesus' leadership.  Thank you for your very hard work.

The popcorn chicken is as close as to home as I can find here. Being from the bay area, this snack is held on a pedalstool for me. It's good!

My only complaint is that for $9 you would assume the portion size would equate to that price point as this is a snack that you'd typically find at Boba Tea spots for no more than $5 with a whopping shareable portion. Here, i ordered online picked it up and assumed my little box was packed full of chicken - it was packed full of cabbage and basil leaves, maybe 8-10 scraggly pieces of chicken. The chicken is good, but the value is not here for that price.
The pizza today was not up to Newks normal pizza. Crust was soggy. Apparently used too much oil. Do not know
I got the Hash pannekoeken and I left full and happy! Good coffee and good service. A nice addition to the downtown scene. Will definitely return when I'm in Edmonton.

First impression is that the diner is exactly what I hoped to find.  Genuine diner decor and atmosphere.   My husband ordered the Reuben and was happy with how tender the corned beef was, and the generosity of the portions.  Onion rings are homemade.  I had the Texas wrap, and it was exactly as it was described.  Will come back to try some of the other menu items, which are varied enough that it is hard to pick what to have!  Server was attentive but not overly so, and we were seated quickly and the food came in a short time.

We went here for my mom's birthday in November. Our server was very nice. It was also nice that they allowed us to make a reservation that afternoon for our dinner party that evening on a Saturday. Everyone enjoyed their food. I wasn't hungry so I just ordered the caprese salad and it was AMAZING, one of the best that I've had a restaurant.

I am assuming that they let us make a reseravation due to it not being very busy. The past few times we've been to this location there haven't been many other people dining during busy hours. I'm not sure how long this location will be open due to this being a consistent pattern. 

Of course the well known biscuits were AMAZING as well!!!

This place has gotten worst. Went in for lunch and was greeted by someone that doesn't speak English and couldn't answer why I was charged $56.39 for 3. Food is cold and I tried to tell the server and they just nodded and walked off. Fried items were multi-colored but still cold inside. Wish I would've spent my money elsewhere.

Lincoln Sq to me is one of the most consistent eating experiences you can have all across their 7-9? locations. Whatever it is they are doing they have it down pat and it shows. Brother was in town after working a 12hr ER stint he told me to meet him for some food, since I had the yute with me, was dressed like a bum, and wanted something surefire I told him to meet me here.

Place is always busy, got the corn beef hash and eggs, bro got a salad, the yute Mickey Mouse pancakes. Everything came out quick, corn beef hash was great, brother liked the salad and kid ate half the pancake. Server left us a full pot on the table, much appreciated. 

Menu is huge, something for everyone, price point is the truth. Service is quick, and always good, the food is legit. Two thumbs up for the Square, I will return again and again.

So today was my FIRST time in Philadelphia. I wanted to have a Philly Cheesesteak. I was told by a Philly resident to go to the Reading Terminal Market if I'd like a tasty cheesesteak. There I joined a long line at Carmen's Famous Italian Hoagies! The service was great! Michelle the cashier was pleasant and friendly! Dan the server you ROCK! and Jeremy on the Grill you made a MEAN Kamikaze Cheesesteak! MY CHEESESTEAK WAS MOUTHWATERING! Right amount of heat! The flavor was on point, it was worth the wait. I can't wait to return to Philly see you guys in the near future! 

Recommendation:
If you're planning on being in the Philadelphia area, please stop by the Reading Terminal Market for the MOST DELICIOUS CHEESESTEAK from CARMEN'S! 

Sign 4 of clubs!

Food was good, atmosphere was on point but the prices!!!! Getting "drive-in" food in "drive-in" serving and "drive-in" serving sizes, the prices are pretty crazy. Each add-on to a burger (jalapenos, cheese, etc) costs $0.75, and all sides are $3 for not too much (roughly 8 sweet potato tots, roughly 4 onion rings). The food was good so that almost made up for it, but if I walk up to the front to order and my sides come in a paper bag, I'm usually not expecting to spend $35 for two people.
Every time we're in St Pete and crave burgers this is the ONLY place to go! Their burgers are consistently juicy, tender and cooked to perfection!

I can't believe I haven't reviewed Ken Sushi because I come here often. Driving by you wouldn't think this would be such a good restaurant and it is can be easy to miss if you are not paying. I've have not experienced dining in because I always order to go. Every time I've gone in it is really busy but the servers are so nice. It is so cool to see the people making the sushi in the front. Their sushi is out of this world amazing! All of the sushi combinations are so good!! I also like their Gyoza and Fried Calamari. This place is a must try if you like sushi and are in the area!

Good Thai food in a convenient location. The early bird lunch deal is perfect- a small portion of appetizer and a cup of soup. The chicken pot stickers are wonderfully crispy. The entrees are generous portions- the pad Thai was up to par.

A bit disappointed when I ordered from here and when I got the food it was cold. I like to do pick up because I know that I'll have it more fresh than doing delivery. The food was delicious, and most definitely will be wanting to come back and eat it at the restaurant instead of ordering.

Ok this will be short and sweet because I love this place . First off comfort food and good portions I almost always get a open face turkey of it aint broke ..... also the burger and shake is a good deal oh and they have a full bar for my drinkers out there . Great spot is my go to over pegs or Lou lous or whatever that place is on kietzke.

Mike was our guide and he was awesome! My mother and I were joined by one other young couple for an all day adventure. We visited 4 wineries at a very leisurely pace- we could have stayed out all day if Mike didn't kindly reign us back in! The wineries were very well chosen and Mike's knowledge about everything in the area was so helpful and interesting. He had a great soundtrack too. The open top Hummer was great! We went on a weekday and lunch was included- we found it to be a great value for the money considering we were driven around all day. Yes the tastings don't cost all that much compared to the total price of the tour, but it's worth it to have someone drive you around with your hair blowin in the wind while picking up some knowledge about the area. We got along so well with the other couple on out trip we all went to dinner after. We also made the reservation very last minute, a day or two in advance, and they were happy to accommodate us. Highly recommend!!!!! Especially the Carhartt tasting room- so cute!!

First and last time here. Waited 1/2 hour to get a 10 min pedicure. Then asked for an ombré dip powder French manicure. Waited another 15 mins till the only person that could do it was available. She ended up making my nails all white, not ombré. Super expensive as well.

This restaurant is simple, charming and has a mom & pop's old world feel to it. I felt like I was in another country. Our waiter, Randy, was the only server on shift, but he was phenomenal and worked that place like a rockstar. Very personable, helpful in explaining the menu, and very attentive to our needs. The food was incredible, much better than I expected. I had the milkfish braised in coconut milk, ginger and garlic with shrimp, served over garlic rice and topped with spicy red cabbage. I felt like literally licking the plate clean, how I wish it was socially acceptable to do so. It's a must try for all you yelpers out there. The dessert Sampler was also good but didn't really blow my socks off. Would I order it again? Yeah, I believe I would. To go to the bathroom you have to walk through the kitchen. I thought it was cool, it adding to the feel of being in a restaurant located in a different country. My final thought, cool place and a fun experience, loved it!!

Heard that this was a fantastic restaurant in New Orleans. When we arrived, I soon realized this wasn't the restaurant I had seen pictures of or read about.  The newly renovated decor is terrible...a bad 80's rendition of pastels and grays. The menu is completely new so we couldn't order what we had been hoping to.  The food was just ok...nothing to write home about but we couldn't get over the ambiance of the restaurant. It felt like we were in a diner. Unfortunately, we won't be back.

We got the cheese steak to go so didn't have it right away but the the steak is not finely chopped and you can see that the meat is high quality without too much fat. We got it the cooper sharp cheese and it was really good. The sandwich was on a smaller side but it was still very satisfying. It's not greasy but provides a cheesy steak sandwich. 

The Yelp check-in gets you a free order of fries which we really enjoyed. 

This would be where we go if we ever want a (relatively) healthy high quality cheese steak.

Traditional japanese flavors!! Food is superb. Great service, staff very friendly.
Mochi ice cream is fantastic !!
Try the green tea and mango flavors :)

We came here after walking around the Garden District looking at hold houses and the cemetery. This was a great spot for lunch! We ended up ordering fries, fried artichoke heats and a small salad. Between the two of us, more than enough food! Everything good, service was friendly and it was fairly busy for a weekday at lunch. Would certainly come back to try a more regular meal versus the sampling of apps we opted for. Oh, fair prices as well.

Good value for the money. Has private beach, but small and gulf front with lots of fish, and hence, pelicans,which are fun to watch,but not swim with. 
Nice heated pool with hot tub. It was nice being able to hear the bands from neighboring properties late into the night, but pity the people trying to sleep early, because it was pretty loud.
