Rooms were dated but offered lots of amenities like stovetop, frig, and balcony for fresh air. Downers are housekeeping not very thorough and skimpy towels.  Pros are close location to everything.
Best coffee joint on the East Side. Great coffee, great people, not pretentious, & reasonably priced. Tons of places to sit and enjoy your meal..inside or out

Hit or miss with this place, tried the Deluxe and it was DELICIOUS, next time tried the Tuna Deluxe and again DELICIOUS. Went back today to get the tuna, had some Mexican lady help me (its ok im Mexican too). I asked her for a Tuna Deluxe, she gave me tuna with olives and tomatoes, wrapped it up so when i got to my office i was super disappointed and disgusted. Next time I'll have to ask for one of the guys that work there. This lady seemed to be pre occupied talking to I understand she didnt hear or care about what I ordered

I got some wings for super bowl Sunday for my Italian family (who had never had wings before). 
I figured they would be super busy so I called ahead and told them them I would be there to pick them up just before gametime. 
I was pleased that the wings were ready and waiting when I got there. They were delicious and a big hit with the family. I will definitely be going back for BBQ in the future.
Okay if you like very rich food.  The cooked food is very heavy and rich.  I feel like I swallowed motor oil.  Atmosphere is good.

Everytime my family is in the mood for take out we always go here. Never have we been disappointed. General Tso's and Sweet and Sour Chicken are a must and anything that you might try will be good. Definately a must if your in the area.

Oh my gosh, I had seen this place on Nashville Flipped, and it far succeeded my expectations. The people were helpful and super nice. I plan to go back now that I've figured out placement for some pieces.
Great food and the bartender is amazing. He offered to make me specialty cocktails and made a toots troll martini AMAZING!  sushi is fresh and delicious

Definitely the best General Tsau I have ever tasted.  I'm picky about it and I thought my him Motown of Nashville produced the best Chinese Food  (I know it sounds weird) This place impressed me.

My boyfriend lives in NOLA and I was visiting and we went here from  his request.  So good.  Nigori was amazing and fresh.  Definitely 5 stars!

One of the few non-smoking bars on Main Street. Was quiet for a Monday, which was perfect! Had a one-man-band, not too bad. As more people filtered in they went to the upstairs area, where smoking is allowed. We were told we could move upstairs, was a good point to call it a night. All in all, cool bar, they're trying with the drink menu, could expand a bit more.

Did not enjoy my lunch experience. Was directed to a seat and then promptly forgotten by the one waitress working. When the supervisor finally noticed I hadn't been served another waitress finally appeared and got me a menu. 
My order of vermicelli with grilled pork and spring roll arrive soon after I ordered it but was fairly flavourless. 

With poor service and meh food, nothing really inspires me to return to this restaurant.
Hands down the best bar in all of Nashville. Bands are always amazing and the bartenders even with the huge crowds always have your drinks full.

A great lunch spot or informal dinner spot.

Very tasty. I got the new "Pro Bowl" which is Paleo.

Just meat/fish and veggies.

It wasn't the heavy Indian that you think of when you hear "Indian."

One of the owners, Robby, came over to our table and asked us how we liked it. Excellent customers service.

I also got a fresh naan bread and it was very hot and freshly made out of the clay oven.

A very clean environment with a high-tech hand-washing machine.

Will definitely go back and will recommend it to my family members who usually don't like Indian food.

The sauces in the condiments bar were also very delicious!!!

Thank you, Robby, for a great experience.

My first visit here was wonderful aside from the disregard for our reservation (we were seated a little over half an hour after our reserved time). However, today I came back for a second time for the brunch and bottomless mimosas and received some of the worst service I've ever had anywhere! Our waitress brought us our checks quite a while before we were done, which would have been fine, but then once we paid we watched her leave and she said nothing about doing so! We hadn't had a single refill on water and had only had a couple of refills on the mimosas, and as it was pouring rain outside my friend and I had planned to stay and chat and wait out the weather a bit before leaving but no longer felt inclined to do so. Because of this, we decided to try to speak to a manger but it took us almost half an hour to grab anyone's attention to get a manager over and once he did come by our table he seemed incredibly unconcerned with our dissatisfaction. Overall this experience has made me not want to return come here again.

Extremely overpriced. Got the 1/4 bbq chicken with baked beans for $9. Chicken was actually very good, but the piece of leg/thigh was extremely small. Here on business for ur next 6 weeks, gonna be tough to come back.

I've heard nothing but bad things about this place, but I figured since it's across the street from where I work I'd let my taste buds make their own decision. Welp, the reviews were spot on. I got the cheeseburger and fries combo, which from initial appearance had me at 3.5 stars. After the first bite I thought, okay this is about a 3 star burger. It tasted very similar to a burger I could make on my own grill. As I continued to eat it, I hit patches of the burger that tasted like they dumped pepper or some kind of seasoning all over it. So now I'm down at 2 stars for the taste, because I can make a better burger at my house.The fries were pretty good, but nothing I haven't tasted before. 

What brought these guys down to 1 star is the price for the quality. Spending over $10 to eat something that mediocre is robbery. Don't be like me...DO NOT WASTE YOUR MONEY!
A local establishment; an institution here in St. Pete for years.  Stone Soup has great sandwiches with excellent sides.  Their salads are especially worth a try.

Very affordable.
We brought our cat here to get spayed and it was $10 .
I was very upset with the fact I didn't get to meet the vet before hand and that no one went over the procedure with me.
We went back a week later for her shots,she was given to shots to cover multiple things and medicine for hookworm prevention it was about $80 for all.

I live around the corner from this place. I love the beer. They always seem to have some great dark beers and a decent IPA on tap. Their bar service is quick. The different types of hummus are really good, but it isn't a place you'd go for dinner. I'd say it's a great place for a drinking snack. 

Definitely check this place out. Their beer is excellent and their atmosphere is great.
Love this place, very cute atmosphere, fast service, and the pho is soooooo Good! I highly recommend this place to everyone. Prices are very reasonable. I always have leftovers to take home.

Clogged sink on Good Friday afternoon. Ugh!!! Turned down by 2 other companies! Called McGill and they said they would be right out. Ryan got to our home within 15 minutes.  He was friendly and professional. He found the problem very quickly (it wasn't just in the trap). He went over and above to make sure he got the whole clog out from way down in the pipe system. He put everything back together and made sure everything was working properly. He even cleaned up! I am very grateful to Ryan and to McGill Plumbing. I will call them first from now on!!!!

Another place with bad service.  I am real big on customer service and I did not get it initially.  The food was not as hot as we thought it should have been.  I am sure they will do just fine here, but not with my money!

Amazing restaurant!!!! My favorite vegan place by far! I went before seeing Cats with my mom and grandparents. My mom and I are vegan and vegetarian. My grandparents are neither, but they will eat some vegan alternatives. I liked that the menu had both vegan and non-vegan sides. That meant that my grandparents could get real meat if they wanted it, and my mom and I could get vegan alternatives without having to pick out what on the menu was vegan. Our server was extremely accommodating to my mom's citrus allergy and to my grandfather's dementia. Overall 100/10!

OMG a good old fashioned bakery. Crumb cakes cinnamon rolls cakes and Latin pastries.  Along with all of that they have a great selection of savory eateries. Inside and outside seating.  Spanish and English speaking service staff. Friendly comfortable and all around good eating.

Absolutely the best Caribbean fine dining I've had in Tampa. Today's our second visit and we could not be more pleased! My boneless Jerk Chicken was a perfect blend of heat and flavor. The husband's Ox Tails were fantastic. And like it's mentioned on all the reviews, the service is exceptional, friendly and passionate about their food and making sure everyone has a great experience. A MUST try!

After using Yelp in the Philadelphia area, it's clear to me that people are only writing to complain but won't take the time to put useful information that is positive even though it may help other users visiting the area.

After reading horrible reviews of the Hilton on City Line Avenue, we decided to stay one night at it's daughter property Homewood Suites at the same location. We found none of the problems that the Hilton has. The staff was friendly and helpful on check-in, the room was clean and neat. The bed was heavenly. The only thing missing were the robes.

But this is intended for extended stays by business people. It costs the same or less than the rundown hotel next door. We saw several families staying and it certainly fit the bill for a romantic evening.

The breakfast was included, hot and decent enough, had way more than the standard hotel muffin and coffee fare.

I consider this a completely different property though it shares the same address with it's parent property the HIlton.

And Philadelphians, share some positive info for crying out loud.

I had lunch here today now that they're open for dine-in & food was as tasty & authentic as it was pre-COVID.  I had the pig ears & seaweed salad from the cold appetizers case & the lucious small steamed dumplings w/a nice dipping sauce. For my entree I chose the fried eggplant w/sliced green peppers & steamed rice. Plenty left for lunch tomorrow!

The food here was amazing! This restaurant is tiny and the decor is so unique, you'll feel like you've walked into Wonderland. Be prepared to wait though. We got here about 15 minutes after they opened and the wait was already 45 minutes for 2 people. You'll have to get there before they open if you want a table right away.

Highlights: FREE AMAZING CORNBREAD! The cornbread was moist and buttery and they give you as many as you want! Shrimp and alligator sausage cheesecake is a must try as well. This savory cheesecake was really filling and heavy but good, definitely a dish to share. 

Skip the fried green tomatoes though. It was kind of bland. We got the redfish as the entree which was good. Overall the food and service were really good.

I have visited this place three times, and my experience has been consistently positive. This place has enough space for big groups and more private space for couples. 

Not surprisingly, their food isn't quite authentic but that's not what they claim to be. They have an extensive menu with a lot of varieties for rolls. (Sadly they don't offer using noodle and bento boxes, which is a minus to me.) Quite honestly, they can use some of their creativity at naming the rolls for varying the flavors. Once my group ordered 5 rolls at a time. They all tasted good, but I had a hard time distinguishing one from another. There are a few options for tacos/burgers/sandwiches, but I haven't tried any. 
-appertizers: my favorite is the fried calamari. Crunchy but not dry. Very flavorful. I had to order that twice. The crab and asiago dip is also good, but better to be shared among a large group.
-specialty rolls: ehhh can't remember which ones I ordered (7 different rolls in total). They are good so my guess is that whichever you pick will be fine. 
-the bowl: I got somen noodle with friend tofu. This is my least favorite. Despite its great presentation, it is very tasteless. I had to add a lot of soy sauce to make it edible. 
- aburi sake: basically salmon seared rare on avocado sauce. This is the one! Very fresh and elegant. I highly recommend this dish. 
-desserts: nama truffle and nama nanner are both good, but I prefer the former.

Great prices and open 24/7. They also have a wine and beer section and a Starbucks. The Starbucks doesn't have a full menu and the staff is about as knowledgeable as my dog, but they have wine so all is forgiven. They have great mix sixes and other cool IPAs.

Looking all around the NE Philly area tint businesses there were some that offered great prices but seems like they've only worked on domestic cars for the most part which kinda worried me having a shop working on my BMW 540i. 

What caught my eye was just amount of exotic/high-end cars this place has done amazing jobs so my 540i shouldn't right? Exactly.

Without any hassle, I called these guys and scheduled an appointment and was qouted $180 which is fair for all windows except front windshield. My appointment was for today, but I got a call yesterday from these guys that they had an open slot and they could take me then if I could make it. I couldn't but that's great customer service right there alone and deserved every penny for the job.

Upon arriving to the shop everyone of the staff members were helpful going through all the details and procedures on taking care of the tint. Took about an hour and a half since it seemed pretty busy but one of the guys explained that he had to redo one of my windows because there was a problem but nonetheless did an incredible job.

I recommend if you come to these guys to bring cash for payment since they could knock a few bucks off since they don't have to pay the credit card fees.

I can't speak highly enough of these guys, and they will have my business for any of my future cars.

Food is amazing and we got seated right away. 
Cons: It took a long time for them to bring our drinks and ranch cost extra.

We still laugh about how bad our one experience here was months ago - worst dining experience of my life.  I've had lackluster, and even bad service before - but we have never been treated so patently rude as at Beachcomber.  The dining room is cramped, so adjacent tables might as well be attached... our server was falling over herself with the tables on either side of us, while mostly refusing to acknowledge our presence. (difference of couples in their 30's (us) and 70's (other tables)?  It's not like we came in off the beach in our bathing suits...  My wife's med-rare filet came out pittsburgh rare - and went back TWICE with the same tough charred cut and cold sides returning. Not only did the manager not visit our table, but the server just unapologetically dropped it never to return till the bill.  Never got beverage refills, and actually had the server walk away mid-request at one point.  The price of the meal took the experience from ridiculous to just laughable.  Stay away

Long wait to order, long wait to get order. When inquired why people ordering after received order before mine, response was made individually and differences in freezing. As observation of orders being made I am not convinced as true. Quality ok, but not outstanding. There is better ice cream. A novelty for sure, but wait and quality not worth it. I will not return or recommend it to anyone.

Stayed here for 6 nights for Mardi Gras.  We've stayed at a decent share of hotels in New Orleans from ones we care not to ever mention again to others we wish we could live in.  For being right in the heart of Bourbon Street during the most lively event of the year, we loved our stay!  If you're here for Mardi Gras then you're not looking for peace and quiet ;) 

The room - very spacious and extremely clean.  The bed was super comfy although the pillows could have used more girth.  The bathroom was sufficiently spacious even in my wheelchair.  No matter what crazy hours we left our room, the staff managed to get it made up for us before we returned!

The staff - everyone was extremely helpful and kind! Maybe it's because I was crazy enough to come to Mardi Gras in a wheelchair and crutches that made us notable to the staff, but even when my husband would step outside without me, they would ask him how I was doing! They always held the doors open and helped us get to where we needed to be... promoted help without being asked... A+ service and attentiveness!

The jazz - we only went to the jazz bar once but I wish we went more! It was excellent!  The drinks were delicious and the ambience was perfect.  Perfect lighting and perfect performers really made for a classy evening.

The food - I only wish they had more food selections for late night dining other than pizza, lol, but I can't be too picky... at least they have a 2am option!  Room service food was pretty darn good for in room dining!  We had the gumbo, a couple pizzas, crab grilled cheese, and burger... all very delicious. And R'evolutions.... fricken yum!  Cajun fried 
quail and waffle! 

The price - well, I did mention it was Mardi Gras ;) worth every penny... and I won't lie... there were a lot of them, lol!

I wanted to click a picture of the thali, but I just jumped on it as soon as it came. The dosa thali is just amazing for price. I would recommend veg thali over non veg thali. It has white chutney, red chutney, sambar, upma, sweet, pongal, paratha and dosa.
I love their $8 turkey legs! Can easily be 2-3 meals. Smokey, tender, meaty... what's more there to say?

First: Kelsey, our server, was exceptional. Thank you for thoughtful, prompt service. You were a delight. 

Appetizers tried: oysters (New Zealand, huge, sweet, meaty... ditch the barbecue sauce... it does nothing for these beauties), frog legs (favorite), bacon popcorn (very fun), mussels (the spice was a nice surprise), goat cheese relleno (creamy and a nice change from the usual)

Entrees: Tchoup (the chop was oh-my-god sized and incredible), the drunken angel (nicely cooked seafood in a delicious sauce), trout (great portion), shrimp and grits (the collards shine and were *almost as good my own... mmkay, as good). 

Desserts: bread pudding (I wish I had more stomach to eat more), mole s'mores (what!?!?!  Did you really do that!?!?  Outstanding ::clap emoji::)

This is a lively place. The food was dressed up familiars from my home of Virginia. We will certainly be back to try more. Keep up the attention to detail. Happy to find this place as a new transplant to the area. Bravo.
Great coffee and great owners. They use coffee ice cubes in their iced drinks, only top quality places do that. Glad to have them nearby, just wish they were open on Sundays too.

I love first watch. There are healthy options and not so healthy options and every restaurant has a unique look. They usually have a very long wait, but they've made it very easy with a waitlist right on Yelp or their app. You can join the waitlist while you're at home and drive over when your table is almost ready. I've never had a dish I didn't like and their seasonal menu is amazing. My current favorite is the chicken pesto quinoa bowl but I'll keep coming back to try new things.
Sadly, they're not mask compliant, and even more sadly, they responded quite aggressively and rudely to extremely polite feedback and a simply request for mask compliance of their staff.

Been going there [not so often] for 25 yrs. Everytime we go there seems a little more down hill. This time stuffed mushrooms were the size of a nickel. My burger was dry. Wife had whiskey sour $12.00 plus $0.75 for on the rocks [?]  When ask the manager about the drink charges [Julie],, she said she had never seen that charge before. She handed me my receipt back, walked away. Won't be back, JULIE

The first time I had chicken pad thai, I ordered a 3 star and it was super spicy.  The next time I went back I ordered a 2 star and it was extremely bland.  Earlier this week my bf and I ordered the chicken pad thai 1 star and a 3 star and they tasted exactly the same.  Of course, each star is going to taste differently to everyone but just bummed bc I want to love this place but in my opinion the flavoring is inconsistent.  I was bummed bc I spent $40 on take out (ordered appetizers as well) and when I called to confirm if I got two 1 starts or I actually received a chicken pad thai one star and three star and the employee was pretty much like yah well we gave you the right thing and that is how it taste.  I rather eat the Indian food next door at Monsoon!  I'll pass on this place in the future but I will say they have a nice sitting area inside.

Stayed in Tucson later than planned. Needed to eat before we headed home to Phoenix.........long story short - the place looked inviting from the street!  No pre-conceived expectations just glad they were open!

LINGUINI WITH TRUFFLE OIL & ROSEMARY - linguini tossed with Roma tomatoes, mushroom, olive oil, rosemary, garlic and a drizzle of truffle oil

These are just words. Words can't describe feelings. I felt GOOD and HAPPY eating this dish along with the plump perfectly cooked shrimp.  Holy cow just writing this review my mouth is starting to water!  Pasta was perfectly cooked. Mushrooms firm. Roma tomatoes were roasted and had greate al dente texture.  It was just a Delightful dining experience.

I regret that I was driving and could not pair it with wine. Next Time!

I have eaten here for years. It's always best to go to their Taco Tuesday bc the tacos are much cheaper and you can try a bunch of diff kind. All the tacos are delicious! I think they literally have everything even for a picky eater to like. The side dishes are good. The nachos though, those are to dieeeee for! I dream about them nachos lol. The alcoholic beverages are tasty which is why you should watch out bc they can sneak up on you lol! The only problem I have had with this place is the wait. Sometimes it's super long but really that just depends on the time you go! If you're in the area, definitely try this place. You won't be disappointed

I understand that is is Xmas Eve and that you don't want to be at work but it is my money and I want what I ordered. I ordered a number 5 with a soft shell taco with a Baja Blast Mountain Dew with no ice, I got to the window and the girl has the bitch face syndrome and just hands me my food. I'm driving and I go to drink my drink when I realized I never got one so I go right Bach and she hands me the Pepsi she runged in but I never asked for so I told her that's not my drink, so she slams the window and makes my drink and hand it to me. I asked for a straw and she said we don't have any with the nastiest attitude and slams the window. I go home, pulled my taco out my bag and it's a hard shell so I decided to call, they don't answered or they answered and then hangs up. The service and food was terrible and I would have went back but just like them I have to work today also. This place needs a make over and I'll thinking about contacting corporate on them. I repeated my order twice and she rung in what she wanted me to have and they wasn't even busy.

First time vist during Downtown Restaurant Week. Amazing!!! My starter was the lobster bites...small portion but incredibly tasty. Main course was the ribeye....ordered it rare and got a true rare! Yay! Very simple on the seasoning which I prefer as I love the taste of red meat. The side oredr of mushroom shallot risotto was TO DIE FOR! It was a huge portion and I ate it all. Best I've had. 3 course-meal was finished with some amazing cheesecake. Simple, with no topping but rich and smooth.
I probably could never afford to really check this place out except during DRW.
Our server in the bar area was very good and attentive. Great experience.

This is one of my favorite local record store. They have a vast selection of new music, and next door at their "too" store they have an abundance of "preloved" music. They supply their consumers with all formats: cd, vinyl, cassettes. But what really makes them stand out is they do a lot of activities to bring artists into the stores and make them accessible to their patrons. They have many release shows and after hours "The Basement" located obviously beneath the store is a cozy little live music bar to catch a local artist performance. This is a special kind of record store that I recommend to anyone that stops in Nashville...yes we do have other records stores than Third Man. This place is like going into the Renaissance for music. This place holds a special place in my heart just because it's so cool.

Great burgers and beers! Always great service and there are always deals through social media to try desserts or appetizers for free-items I otherwise wouldn't have tried. Happy Hour during the week is our favorite. Check them out, you won't be disappointed!

They charged me $25 to dry clean a vintage fur jacket I adored. The button was coming off but I was going to replace it anyway. My goal was to get the vintage smell out of it.

I also want to note I wore the jacket once. That being said, I just picked up my coat and the smell is still there. I also notice a huge hole in the armpit along the seam. I started to just go ahead and sew it but then I noticed a huge chunk of thread hanging out the bottom and to my horror I notice the whole seam came undone!!! This coat was in great shape when I dropped it off and now I have to stitch the entire coat because I wouldn't trust them to sew it properly. It was my first and last time going 

Update: they were willing to fix it! Now I was also told it couldn't be their fault and the coat was old (in other words not their fault) but hopefully they will remedy the stitching and I'll gently clean it at home.

Final say: my coat is fixed but the seam came undone under the other sleeve. Idk what their process is for dry cleaning but it's rough.

Took advantage of a $5 coupon. Had a slow start, but once we were finally greeted by our server, we were well taken care of. Philly cheese steak was good, not like real Philly but still tasty. My chicken sandwich was a full breast and large enough for two lunches. Ranch dressing was delicious on the sandwich.

I just visited this store, and walked out. My incredibly generous sister always brings me chocolate from here, and since I was in the area I decided to stop by. After waiting for 10 minutes to be served, these two ladies who walked in 5 minutes after me went up to the store employee and asked to order a gift certificate, the employee asked how much. I was so upset that the lady skipped me that I left without saying a word. Someone needs to take a course on how to properly serve customers!

The most important feature of an airport transport company is to show up ON TIME. I selected a service as opposed to Uber specifically because I needed a car ready to roll as soon as my meeting was over in order to make a flight. I called at 5 mins after scheduled time to check status. They said 5 more mins then modified to 7 to 10 when challenged. Overall they were 20 mins late and blamed traffic. In my experience with limo services they build potential traffic delays into their pick up times. Gave one star because the driver was polite and did make an effort to get to the airport quickly.

I can't believe a simple search on yelp doesn't bring this business up. I LOVE Wings to Go and their sides are pretty good too. As the title shows, they sell wings. GOOD wings. I will post pictures soon.

STAY FAR AWAY from Dream Dental in Goodlettsville. They do not know how to do billing. They bill wrong insurance providers then months later you get a bill from them on something you though was paid in full. Then they argue with you and accuse you of making the mistake. I allowed it once then we went over there and made sure they had the correct information then they did it again. Their customer service is lousy.

I read how another person felt like they were being had by a used car lot.  I agree.

Got there at 11 o'clock on my 21st birthday because all of my other friends were already of age and wanted to start drinking. We were told they were closing early, at 11:30, even though they aren't supposed to close until 1. There were a good amount of people already in there, and they turned down a good amount of us. They lost out on a lot of money by turning us away. Every other time I've been there I haven't had an issue. Great food and good happy hour prices.

Super fast and professional on every level. Dr. Sullivan exceeded all of my expectations. My father-in-law was a dentist so I know what makes a good dentist. Dr. Rick and his staff are top-notch. Thank you!

Purchased a used Mercury Mountaineer from them. They told me the truck had new brakes when I bought it so why was I replacing brakes and rotors to the tune of $700 just 5 months after buying it?? When I contacted them about this, they denied telling me the brakes were new... like the salesman would remember me or my purchase 5 months later.

It also had a squeel in the engine like a belt problem that was supposed to be fixed before I picked it up and surprisingly hadn't been!! When I called them to repair this they told me "You bought it. It's yours now!" 

Auffenberg will bend anyone over to make a buck!!

Such a cute setup at this location. Luckily the line wasn't out the door like the other locations. The food was very tasteful and the kids really enjoyed their famous bonuts. Will definitely return to this location again!

You both are right, the hotel is hard to find!  We just came back from a 4 night stay there.  The room was very  nice and clean.  The hotel restaurant sucked!  We went down to the restaurant around 7:30 for dinner and were told that they were out of food and that we could only order a few of the sandwich items.  Soooo, because we were tired and didn't feel like walking across the street, I ordered a chicken sandwich and my parents both ordered the Lobster Quesadillas.  Well, after about a half hour my sandwich finally came out and the waitress told my parents that the bar stole one of the Quesadillas and they had no more left!  So my mom and I shared my sandwich and they finally brough out the quesadillas to my dad and it was about the size of a hamburger bun!  So needless to say, we didn't have to pay.  We also got free breakfast each day since we were with a big group staying there and the food was not very good.

There was also a dog show going on and part of the hotel was full of dogs, so we had yapping dogs in the next room.  

Housekeeping didn't come around too much. Outside the room next to ours, 2 empty dirty plates, forks, dirty towels and a glass sat for about 2 days.  They also didn't have enough clean towels to give us on Sunday.

This review is only for my takeout experience.  I've eaten in the restaurant before and it was a decent experience.  I ordered 2 pizzas and waited about 20 minutes past the promised 25 minutes from my order.  The ingredients on the pizza was good but the crust tasted like cardboard and was undercooked as well.  I'll go there to order other items than pizza the next time I dine in there.  I don't like how the credit card charge ask you to put in a tip when paying.  At that point I didn't have any service to warrant a tip.

Took the Hot Yoga class at Jtown Hot Yoga. Proud to say, I survived! Lolz... I did not find Jtown Hot Yoga very welcoming upon entering the studio as a newbie. However, gorgeous studio, clean & had an amazing workout.

Just an overall wonderful service.

Returned my call with a text within an hour, and set up an appointment for two days later to address a non-emergent clogged pipe.

Arrived PROMPTLY at the time we agreed, was friendly with my dog and cats, and was careful to not track mud or dirt through the house. Took care of the problem quickly and efficiently, and even highlighted other areas of improvement that were more expensive but could be considered in the future given the age of my house (but in no way was pushing for them to be done).

In an out in under 45 minutes, reasonable price, and multiple payment options (including Venmo)! Would highly recommend!

Came for restaurant week. Just horribly disappointing. $35 for a soggy bruschetta, monotone veal on a bed of mushy pasta with flavorless tomato sauce, and a tiny cannoli with gritty cheese. Our whole party was extremely disappointed with everything we got. The servers were nice and attentive, so I can't give it 1 star, but I would honestly second guess eating here if it were free. Maybe it's better when it's not restaurant week.

I went here with a large group during a conference and had a wonderful experience. The restaurant arranged for us all to sit in a private room. It was bright and sunny and decorated with fun dog-themed accents. The waiters were friendly and accommodating to food allergies. We also were not rushed at all. I ate the mushroom bisque and it was really filling, as it was a surprisingly large portion.

None of their employees were wearing masks.  I don't think these guys have the safety of the public as a priority.  I think its some kind of political statement.    Neither I nor anyone I know will eat there or order take-out until after we are sure the staff is vaccinated or they are wearing masks.   How unprofessional.

Oh so slow. Came first thing in the am for a oil change. Said 1-1.5 hours. Over 2.5 hours later - ughhh. Friendly enough but this is just ridiculous. Had I known I wouldn't have left my car here. Unless you don't have anything else better to do but wait an undetermined amount of time bring your car elsewhere. Good luck getting an eta too, called and asked and was told they weren't sure how much longer it would be.

Everything was amazing from start to finish. We had great food, great drinks and great service. I had the grilled filet mignon and it was prepared perfectly. My friends and I shared the creme brûlée and bananas foster and both were delicious. I would 100% recommend this place to anyone looking for delicious food and great service!

Darn tootin' good breakfast spot.  Everyone at my table was overwhelmingly satisfied with the homestyle food and the colorful waitstaff.  I had the migas and homemade salsa - it was the best I've had in N.O. by far.  Also ordered the blueberry pancakes (great).  This place is worth visiting for a non-fancy wholesome breakfast with the locals.

Cash only

We love Roller Kingdom! I've been coming here since I was a kid and although the carpet is the same as it was back then, they recently fixed the roller skating floor. There were some uneven sections in the back right hand corner but they fixed it and resurfaced the entire skating floor. 

     Thursday night's is family night from 6:30-9pm. It's $6 for the head of the household and $3 for every family member after that. They rent skates but we were coming so much we all bought our own. All the items in the snack bar are reasonably priced and I like the salted pretzels with the spicy cheese.
  
    Sunday night's is adult skate from 7:30-10 for 18+. It's super fun and there is no better people watching I assure you! My only complaint is they rarely to never do reverse skate anymore. Come on Brad you are killing my trucks! In my perfect world 1/2 of the skate session would be opposite skate! Just sayn'....

Horrible place. Food is awful. Have eaten here before and enjoyed the food and the atmosphere. Something has changed drastically! Started with a very disinterested waitress, now I know why. We were dining with friends and three of the four of us could not eat our food. Meat was over cooked and some of the food had a nasty flavor. Everything was overpriced. Do yourself a favor go someplace else.

Too expensive. I live in New York and a manicure is $12 and here it was $20. Also, we were asked to pay AFTER our nails were painted Vs before so we had to fumble for our wallets to ensure our nails didn't get ruined. My manicure looks very nice though.

I got the cauliflower sandwich, the cheese did not go well with the vinegar in the cole slaw. It was also way too saucy. I would have liked to have seen more crunch with fresh cabbage and shaved carrots.
Erika is the best! Great service:) Food is great! Will come back again! Food is very fresh

We frequently order pizza here but I really love their eggplant parmigiana! They have a great game room to keep the kids busy while you wait for your food to come out as well. It has a nice NY pizza parlor vibe.

I wish I had seen these earlier. I purchased the laser genesis livingsocial 6 session package and at the consult was "upgraded" to the Mixto Fractional Resurfacing, 1 session that is supposed to last 5 or 6 years because he thought it would be better for me.  Somehow without really taking a good look at my skin at all.  I was not told what I would experience and now a bit worried that I'll be scarred for life.  He seemed rushed through the process and left the room several times which took over 2 hours to complete.  He said the waiting time was a few weeks to get in but I was in within the week.  Now taking time off work to recover from the burns.

Such a fun and memorable experience. Being from tampa we didn't want to drive far to see some gators so we went on yelp and found this place. Our group leader was Dylan and he was so nice and knew a lot of info and really cool facts. It's a smaller building but they have so much to see and do. We were able to hold a baby gator and feed the turtles. I recommend this place 100% but I suggest calling to make an appointment. We made one the morning of and we were able to bring 5 people with ease. Ask for Dylan!

I had a spare day in New Orleans so I made a point to visit one of it's more famous restaurants for lunch. I was running a bit late on account of the street cars moving at a snails pace, so I called up the restaurant to let them know I was running 10-15 minutes behind. They were more than happy to accommodate me. 

When I did arrive, I was greeted by the hostess and a platoon of waitstaff, one of which was summoned to show me to my table. He led me through the kitchen and to a room on the far end of the patio. Being a former chef, I was happy to see the inner workings of the restaurant. As it happened, my waiter attended my alma matter, which made for some fun conversation. I was seated, and offered a black napkin to compliment my suit. 

A parade of waiters followed, delivering water, plates, a sampling from the kitchen and perfectly arranging the silverware without being intrusive at all. They must train their waitstaff well, because the service is immaculate. My water glass never got below 3/4 full, and dishes were immediately cleared when empty, though they always asked if I was done with the dish (with a polite "excuse me sir, but have you finished with your dish?). It was the attention to the smallest details that were impressive. 

For lunch I ordered a trio of soups (on the suggestion of my waiter), the lacquered quail, and the bread pudding souffle, all as part of the tasting menu. Lunch also means 25 cent martinis at the Commanders Palace, and who can refuse that? They set the limit at 3 martinis because,  as they say, 'its plenty'. They're right. The martinis themselves are well mixed and I particularly like the gin martini.

The food itself was quite good for the most part. I enjoyed tasting all the soups (turtle, gumbo, and squash with oysters) though the squash soup was best in my opinion. The turtle soup is good, but be sure to mix in the sherry, it can be a bit overpowering. I really enjoyed the lacquered quail, although it was more stuffing than quail. The meat was tender and perfectly cooked, absolutely delicious. The bread pudding left me wanting. It looked great, but it's a bit too eggy, as others have noted. 

I give the Commander's Palace a lot of credit. They have a good thing going, but I feel tradition is holding them back somewhat. The food is for the most part excellent, and I think this is large part due to the organization of the kitchen. The menu is simple and consequently the kitchen can run more like a catering operation. They perfect a few dishes and churn out hundreds of it. There's nothing wrong with that necessarily as it makes for a very good, very consistent product. The drawback here is that it limits creativity in the menu. The restaurant is shackled to its history to a degree, but for what it is, Commanders Palace is quite good. 

As a side note, if you are looking for a more modern take by the Commanders Palace creative team, check out SoBou at the W in the French Quarter. Very unique and also good.

The singular reason to go to Koja: bulgogi steak sandwiches. Your choice of beef, chicken, or spicy pork with sauteed veggies and cheese. At $3 each, these are a steal. Sure, they're a bit on the smallish side to stand alone as a full lunch, but what do you expect for $3? At that price, you can afford to be a fatty and get two. 

I haven't tried the rest of the food, but it seems a little expensive for a food truck. Especially since Koreana is two blocks away, and a few extra dollars can get you a ton of food there. However, all meals are discounted (I think they're all $4.50 or so) after 3 pm on Fridays, so that may be a good time to check out the rest of the menu.

When in the French Quarter for a vacation you will start to feel that the food is a little too much. Either too much grease, too much decadence, or too much spice. After a few days in, your palette will be craving something relatively simple, fresh, but filling. Cafe Beignet is the perfect spot for just that. My husband and I would stop here for a quick bite, me going with a turkey club, and he a shrimp po-boy. Our choices were all that they should be. Fresh rolls, crispy bacon, lightly fried shrimp, etc. 

But it wasn't just the straightforward fare that made this a favorite. It's located in a little courtyard filled with fountains, friendly birds begging for bits, and live jazz music. Just a perfect little spot for a quiet French Quarter lunch.....And don't worry. You can get booze here too ;)

Wonderful atmosphere!  Wonderful food!  You have a "team" that waits on you. One person is responsible for your drinks, while the other takes care of any questions you may have about the menu.  Absolutely fabulous service!!

Best pizza in the valleys!!! I have not once had a bad pizza or experience by them! The pizzas are made fresh in front of you. They taste great and the prices are amazing. I love how friendly the staff is everytime I go in. I'm so glad we atlctually have a great pizza place in valley that's actually worth the money. Only wish they delivered!!

WOW! I haven't had Mexican food like this since...I was in Mexico and even then it wasn't THIS good! 

The tortilla's are homemade and delicious! The tacos we had were so good and the tequila selection was amazing. Karol the owner was so knowledgeable about the tequilas and gave us a tasting sampler and taught us the different  types of tequila and how good tequila can be beyond the typical Cuervo stuff everyone knows. I was sipping tequila which I NEVER would have done before and I LOVED it!

They served us a 6 pound lobster that had spices and sauces that honestly might have been the best thing I have ever eaten in my life! It was tender and delicious and not reliant on butter to taste good. 

This place is the real deal and the prices are excellent!

Make sure you try the guacamole. it is the best I have ever had in my entire life. Seriously. It is outrageously good! 
Everything was perfect and I am already planning to return on Friday night!

Delicious! My wife, son and I are in St Louis for vacation from Rochester, NY. When traveling I love looking for local breakfast spots. This place was perfect. Quaint, in a cool looking area, great coffee and delicious food. I got a slinger. It's a St. Louis specialty that many restaurants offer but this was great because it came with sausage patties instead of burgers. Easily reminds me of a garbage plate that I get home. Staff was so friendly to us (especially with a 17 month old fussy boy). Will definitely be back next time we're in town.

So far all I had was the New York style pizza very greasy but was really good compared to other pizza places especially the pizza chains like dominos or papa johns Angelo's pizza was by far better than those places and you get your money's worth

Little bit smaller than most Beefs.  Service was pretty good.

Wings were OK.  Not great, not bad, but OK.  It seems like across the board, most Beefs locations are kinda going down hill.

When seated, we had sun in our eyes the whole time.  Depending on the time of day, the sun comes right in through the outdoor seating area.  That wasn't fun.

It looked like they had a more extensive beer selection than other Beefs, but I couldn't tell what they had.  The big chalk board just listed a bunch of breweries and said they get them sometimes.  It'd be nice if it had listed EXACTLY what was on tap.

I am currently in NOLA on travel and stopped in here this morning on a whim. What a special place. The studio is in an old library with amazing high ceilings. The class was 90 minutes and included chanting. Loved the teacher and want to come here again. He was so welcoming. One of the best classes I have had in a long time. I went to the cafe in the space for a nourishing meal after my class. Great way to start my day
It's Dali what can you say. The building is beautiful and the location makes it even better. A great place to bring family.

You will LOVE the live music and the energy of this little hole in the wall. Great place to come when we have some of our local French Quarter parades. The parades will roll right in front of the Kerry. 

The only complaint I have with the Kerry is that it is a lil too smokey for me. The ventilation could use some upgrading.

The lobby is fantastic aesthetically. The late night snacks of PBJs and hot chocolate chocolate are a great and unique tradition. The breakfast buffet is worth five stars. The staff is highly efficient and friendly.  The rooms are no better than a Holiday inn. They are cramped  and smallish. Beds are okay. The rooftop pool is cool and fun. The Jacuzzi stunk. If they offer you Room 630, don't bother. My kids called it the Addams family suite. The sitting room, with the dead flowers and the UNCOMFORTABLE FURNITURE, make it useless for sitting. The TV was on the floor in a cabinet. Have not figured out that one yet. If we saw a ghost in this Addams family retread it would have been worth it. However, I don't think a ghost would have been COMFORTABLE in this room. Good hotel but not great.

So fresh and delicious. The Salmon Tropical is where it's at. The sauce is amazing!! The rice and beans are delicious. I got a crispy beef taco on the side and it was ok, but there specials are amazing. Get the horchata for sure. Flan is ok - I like ones with a creamier consistency.

I am from Cali and went to NO for a wedding. As we walked around French qtr we found this place and oh what a gem it is!!! The woman at the register had a huge knowledge base of the fragrances. There is an abundant choice of ready made perfume and if none suit you then you can make your own.! The only thing that is "meh" but doesn't take away from the 5 star review is that as the store is so old, the smells are a little old fashioned/mature. Even so, they are classic! Definitely worth a shot. Also, I rec buying the largest size avail, as it is the best deal!!!

Great food, excellent service. The availability wasn't as great, they took away the tacos, and they were out of the wings, whole fish, banana purée, and other items. It was a Saturday night right at prime time, a little disappointed but it was good. Bar tender was awesome, made great recommendations and made the drinks so smooth.

I visited Jack's with my best friend. The food is really good. I got chicken, green beans, and mac and cheese. The green beans were a little too spicy/seasoned for me. The staff members were very friendly and they had the line moving quickly (the place was packed on a Friday night). I would definitely return if I'm in the Nashville area again!

I grabbed two bags of potato chips because they were buy one get one free. When I went to pay, they did not come up with the correct price. I told the cashier that they were buy one get one free. We both walked to the aisle to verify the price. Unfortunately, the buy one get one free price on the shelf had expired 3 weeks ago. He stated that the pricing manager had not been in recently. He was willing to give me the price, but told me I would have to pay now and come back in the morning when there's someone at customer service to get my refund for the difference. I said 'yeah right' and left.

What a great night out! 
Reservations are a must, this place is hoppin'. 
Service is slow, but very attentive. I do not mind slower service, just know this going in. 
The bread is delicious and I loved the balsamic in olive oil, a nice touch.
I started with the meatballs and tried the arancini.
I was disappointed by the meatballs, they did not taste homemade to me and they were very dense. I would have liked more whipped ricotta. 
The arancini was delicious. Lots of flavor and the perfect size, inclusive of 2 dipping sauces.
I ordered the vodka penne which was perfect! The pasta was al dente and the sauce delicious. 
The chicken parm was to die for. It is pounded thin, and very flavorful and cheesy. 
The complimentary limoncello is such a nice and appreciated touch. I ordered a cappuccino which I highly recommend.
I can't wait to return!

I thought I really had a good spot for Pho and any noodle dish that fit my fancy but I was pleasantly surprised by this place.  Came here for lunch with a colleague and the food, service and atmosphere was great.  Number 49 beef noodle dish with lettuce and fish sauce.. YUM very tasty and SUPER QUICK service.  Have been back about 5 times since. Brilliant every time!
We're very disappointed.  We ordered at 8:20 and it's 9:40 and STILL don't have our food. We've checked on it twice. And keep being told it's being worked on... and not politely either :(

RUN don't walk away from this company.
My situation the AC was not keeping the house as cool as I like it ie set at 70 would get up to 75. So this wasn't an emergency call and I told them that from the get go. I talked with Gretchen (wonderful person) and set up a time and a day for them to come out, $49 inspection/service call. After greeting the owner Cero and a technician at the door I showed them my out side unit and with in 30 seconds the technician seen a little leak. And it would make sense that it was a little leak because the unit was only 6 months old. So it took it that long to start giving me an issue.
Cero took me in side tried selling me on pulling out my coil and cleaning it.(He never even seen my coil to say it was dirty) Told me after they were done with the leak and 3 pounds of 410-A and to put a UV dye (Yellow Jacket dye less than $20 each) in the system to check for other leaks today would be $750. I thought it was high but hey they are here and the technician already started the repairs so I thought.So I paid him. I won't even talk about the issues they had with the dye or the $100 he said he would give me back because of the issues with the dye that I ever seen. Then after they were done playing around out side and getting ready to leave he said we will fix that leak next time we come back. WAIT $750 and you didn't fit the problem. Oh i should of been more clear he said. fixing it wasn't part of today. We will have to come back another day to do that. So i have a leak you just added freon so you could charge me for more freon next time he came.
I could go on more about him releasing my units freon for 11 seconds out to the open air and killing my grass where he did so. At the end of the day I called a more trustworthy company that came out the same day and FIXED the leak and added 1 pound of 410-A for $170. If you care about money or even just want your problem solved STAY away from Cero.
Definitely a local spot! Make sure to make a reservation beforehand. Food  and Atmosphere is good but can get kind of loud.

Great food and friendly staff.  Laid back cafe with better than expected food! Sandwich came with four side item options, and deserts a real hit.  Glad to have this restaurant/cafe in the neighborhood.

The food here was ok, not the best I've had at a buffet. Plus the price was ridiculous for dinner. I didn't eat as much as I wanted to because i was stuffed by the first plate.  But on a good note, the variety was plentiful. You'll pretty much find whatever cuisine or food to feed whatever your craving!

But the only memorable thing about this buffet was that thier customer service was spot on . These people were not rude and actually seemed like they cared for Thier patrons. What caught my attention was the dessert man who was tending to little children and Thier orders of Ice cream. This man was so caring and was actually trying to entertain the kids .

Girls night out? Yes please! We had a great time at Time.

Our server was a total gem. We sent back two drinks because one was the wrong wine (not his fault) and I would NOT recommend the Dark and Stormy. But, the service was stellar! We paid our check and it started raining so we ran back in.... Then, we opened yet another tab and our bartender was nothing but smiles. He was so nice.

Food was ok, liked the fries a lot! Nice sauces. Risotto balls were kinda salty. 

But, all in all, super fun!

Pro's:
- Fast service
- Friendly
- Easy going
- Nice looking bar
- Cute dudes for my single girls :)

Cons:
- I am not sure where parking is - but it didn't matter to us because we walked
- Not so great happy hour prices but that's just how it is these days I guess!


:)
Thanks for fun!!

Thai Mama is serving up some delicious dishes that are perfectly balanced with fresh ingredients and accurate portion size.  I had the Pork Won Ton Soup. A lot of places ruin this soup with overly salting or adding too much oil or butter. That's not the case with Thai Mama's prep of this item.  Their preparation tasted light and fresh with broccoli, carrot, cabbage, zucchini, bean sprouts.    I also tired their Drunken Noodles - stir fried  pad Thai with broccoli, egg, carrot and Thai oyster sauce.  This dish by itself also tasted light and bursting with delis opus goodness. The veggies were cooked through yet crunchy, the noodles were soft but not mushy. The oyster sauce portion was perfectly balanced to draw out the overall flavor without any overpowering of any one ingredient.  I decided to combine these two items together that resulted in a hearty and mouthwatering noodle soup with meat, wonton, veggies, and  broth.   

SECRET TIP : Combined these two items complemented each other while enhancing  the taste, in my opinion 10 fold. :-)  I was thrilled at this discovery.  I did delivery this time, so this review is strictly on the food.  When it's safer I will surely return to try more of their items.

We tried to make reservation for the same day but was told dinner was full.  It was recommended that we come for an early dinner and we could possibly grab seats at the bar since its just the two of us.  So we followed the recommendation and found seats at the bar.  The staff are friendly and super nice.  Our bartender was a charming and friendly young gentleman.  He helped us with the menu and offered some other helpful recommendations.  The ambience is warm, cozy, casual and somewhat classy-it's a nice place for a dinner date.              

Beef brisket with garlic parmesan fries: The meat was tender.  The fries were absolutely delicious and addicting.  

Bread pudding: was very sweet and rich but still pretty good.

Leylin was absolutely amazing! She was able to find my dream wedding band within our budget and delivered the best customer service we've received, including from other jewelry companies. We will definitely be back to work with her for all our jewelry needs!
I love this place!! All of the girls are so Awesome!! They are so accommodating.  My tan turns out great Many beds to choose from! It's always CLEAN  I recommend this place 110%

We have stayed at Two Rivers Campground twice and definitely RECOMMEND it!! The location is excellent. Very close to Opry and Opry Mills Mall. This campground is sandwiched between 2 RV dealerships. Convenient if you need anything. I do want to mention the great addition of a small DOG PARK!! What a great idea for all the dogs the hang out in campers all day. Our dog loved the chance to run!! Will stay at Two Rivers every time we visit Nashville. Bravo! Full hook up, WIFI, and CABLE TV

The food here is fantastic.  I had the rissoto with squash, kale and snap peas.  I ate every last bite of it.  The service was good as well.  The food came out quickly and the folks working at the counter were very friendly.  The portion size was perfect.  I also like the flavored water (today's was mint and orange) available at the bar.  Since I was on my lunch hour, I didn't have wine, but none the less, was impressed with the selection.  Can't wait to try their "crush" wine next time.

I gave Bowl a 4 instead of 5 due to a couple kinks they need to work out.  

First, the menu.  Its on a chalk board and is handwritten which is fine, but the writing itself is impossible to read.  

The other thing that is a big problem for me (which will prevent me from doing anything other than takeout for several months) is that it is absolutely FREEZING in the dining area.  I get that it's part of West Street Market so it's going to have more of an outdoor market feel but seriously my toes were frozen and my nose was red by the time I left.  Please get some outdoor heaters!
Flies everywhere. Server unfriendly. Side waffle $11? And then my friend showed me the rat video gimme a break lol
They charge for water even when you order food. They don't have prices on the menu for their cocktails or beer because they are so overpriced no one would order them otherwise.
Ordered 2 Big Macs and only gave me 1 meat. At home so too late to turn around for a refund

My best friend and I were in NOLA for a bachelorette party 2 years ago and ended up missing our flight out of town at the end of the weekend and therefore had to stay an extra night. After half a week of partying and staying in a fancy hotel, we decided to take the adventurous route and "rough it" that last night to save some money, and this place was nothing short of "rough". I can't begin to explain how disgusting and unaccommodating this place was. I've read all the other negative reviews and they are all accurate, not remotely exaggerated. Not sure where these 4 and 5 star reviews are coming from, but I would hate to see what these people are ranking with 1 or 2 stars. This place was truly sick and all we could do was laugh to keep from crying. Maybe not every room was as bad as ours, but I don't see how any other rooms could have possibly been worse. I'll give 2 stars because the location is pretty great and the price was....okay. If you do choose to stay here, bring all of your own sheets and towels, flip flops for the shower, and a bottle of 409 to spray surfaces down before you touch them. You will leave here with your skin crawling.

Hospitals like TGH are why sites like WebMD were created, so no one has to deal with unprofessional and incompetent medical personnel. TGH checked me in as a visitor when my girlfriend was brought to the ER while unconscious. When she regained consciousness, they told her no one came for her and let her walk home without shoes. Meanwhile, I had been in the waiting room for over two hours while nurses gossiped. TGH called my girlfriend over 15 times this week to admit her following abnormal labs and EKGs. When we called back, everyone from the switchboard operator to the nurses was rude and condescending. When we got to the hospital, we waited three hours for her bed after they made us rush for a bed "within the hour." There were no orders in place, all they offered her was a flu vaccine. Orders should have been placed by Dr. Knox of USF health, but care through him has always been sub-par and inconsistent. TGH harassed both of us for over a week to admit her for flu vaccines, apparently. She could've gone to CVS for those. We needed to remind the nurse to do the standard check in procedures like take blood pressure, check heart rate, etc. The homophobic redneck roommate has been a really nice touch, too. Don't ask me why this place is award-winning. I will slam my own head in a door before I recommend this hospital to anyone.

5th street deli is now called Eli's.  Waited 15 minutes to be waited on !!!!!!!!!  Seriously.   1 customer in front us.  Another 5 minutes to make a corn beef sandwich and a pastrami sandwich.  $30.00 plus tax.   Pesky flies everywhere.  Bread was stale.  Been there, done that, not EVER going back.

Great restaurant, first time for lunch.  Happy, always good seafood, salad bar could use some fruit. MY only complaint is next time i'll bring my own silverware.  PLEASE buy some better utensils, feels like IKEA, or when I was in East Germany.

It was a cold Sunday and we wanted to try out something new. We came here wanting hot soup and hot drinks to beat the chill.  The dining space here is warm and inviting and they were busy this day.  We didn't wait long before someone showed up to take our drink orders.  My wife and kids got hot chocolate and I wanted a Latte that was written largely across the board on the side wall.  Unfortunately the machine was broken and I ended up with just water. . The hot chocolates were amazing, served in big cups with whip cream and chocolate syrup.
So the Sunday brunch here is all breakfast, not much lunch.  I found something unique in the breakfast pita nachos.  This had pita chips with sausage gravy, salsa, quacamol a, cheese and it tasted awesome.  My daughter got the full order of biscuit and gravy and it was too much for her.  The scotch eggs were good and everyone left happy.  I will definitely be back here to try out a lunch menu.
I Found them by mistake years ago after going to several local vets and having a poor experience.  No one comes close as far as care and professionalism.

This really is a charming store. Lots of really cute dresses/sandals/jewelry. Some home decor. 
Very unique, everything cute. Prices weren't too bad and I did find that some jewelry & sandals were 40% off so I grabbed up a delicate necklace.  Will definitely go back to see the new things.
Love this place! Have had numerous positive experiences here both alone and with a group.

We loved everything about El Camino Real except the cheesesteak.  El Camino Real, big fun place.  I liked the window art.  The cheesesteak came with french fries.  The french fries were heavily seasoned and the ketchup has either a Sriracha ketchup or BBQ ketchup or BBQ sauce, whatever it was, I like the Heavenly season french fries with a barbecuey sauce.  The Cheesesteak was just a miss.  The roll was big, bready and didn't work with his cheesesteak.  They use a cheese sauce which should be able to get through all the meat but it didn't.  The meat was a little dry.  We loved everything else.  Maybe go to El Camino Real and don't get the cheese steak
Solid Happy Hour deal (3$ draft on their pick of beer and tasty snacks for 4$). Great tunes and atmosphere. Gonna come back to eat a full meal!

The food is always great. I love the shrimp feast which is truly a feast made for a queen. The food comes out fresh, hot, and flavorful. This place is normally packed for lunch but the staff has the routine down to a "t" and get the tickets completed quickly and your meal to the table in under 7 minutes.

We picked this place because the Yelp reviews mentioned craft beer and it seemed like kids were allowed. Glad we did!
The service was super friendly and quick and our waiter/host was very understanding of the 2 toddlers we had in tow. 
We had the cheesesteak dip (or something - I forget what it was called) and the namesake hamdog (which is kinda like meatloaf on a hot dog bun). Both were good and reasonably priced.
They had about 15 beers on tap, which included some local selections and some really interesting ones I hadn't seen before. Again, reasonably priced.
They had football on and were totally open to us hanging out and watching as long as we wanted.
One knock: the ambiance is pretty plain. I know they just opened, so maybe they're working on it. Overall, if in Reno with the kids again, I would definitely go back to Hamdogs.

We are in from Chicago and wanted something that wasn't a chain and would accommodate vegan and gluten free.  This place was amazing! Andrew was a fun and informative server, who must love his job. His recommendations were spot on. Everything was fresh and tasty and we can't wait to. One back. 
So so so highly recommend!
Big portions... And that's all that is good. Screwed up our order, everything had eel sauce and tastes the same. Rude service. I will not return!

You won't find a better TEAM to clean your house. They have all the right tools, from backpack vacumes for curtains and baseboard to chemicals made for the job.
I had just had our fireplace resurfaced with real stone and the place was covered in stone dust. The girls stayed longer and took care of the mess. I am very particular about my house and its care and maintaince but the maids to the BEST job.

Will definitely come back to this restaurant in the future! While we were waiting for our table, we sat at the bar for a drink. The bartender was really helpful and offered some cocktail options that haven't made it on the menu yet. The drink didn't have a name but I'm sure they'll know once you say rum, oranges and jerk seasoning! It was sooooo good!
 
We had the short ribs for our entree which was amazing with the pommes frites. The customer service, the food and "no name" drink made it a great choice for our first night in New Orleans!

We had just arrived in Center City after a trip from Northern Virginia and wanted to grab a bite to eat before our Mystery and Mayhem Night Tour.  Wandering the streets, with two picky eater teenagers, we stumbled upon Pizzicato.  Glad we did!!

The service was fantastic.  Our server was new and when we started asking questions about the menu, another server immediately came over to help.  

We got the Truffled Fries  and Caprese Salad to share as apps.  The fries were amazingly good.  Left me wanting more!  They were crispy shoestring fries cooked in a truffle oil and mixed with parmesan cheese.  The Caprese Salad was, just that, as Caprese Salad.  Nothing spectacular but good all the same.

Our entrees included the Fettuccini Bolognese, the Cavatelli, Linguini with clams and the Mediterranean White Pizza.  All were really good!  The only suggestion would be to go a little easier on the red pepper flakes in the linguini, it was a bit hot!  

We would definitely return and suggest to friends.

Mixto is has good food, with a great atmosphere and very attentive service. 

I went here yesterday for a casual happy hour and was very satisfied. We ordered chicken empandads, all their skewers, and an order of calamari along with some drinks. 

Cuban food is typically pretty greasy, and this place is no exception (however, it is not overly greasy).

The calamari was VERY good. Very soft and tender. The skewers were the happy hour special for $2 a skew. If you are hungry, these are not for you. They come with two small pieces of meat/fish with peppers and onions. They were small but delicious. All  the meats were tender and juicy. The chicken empandas were great too. The salsa they came with had the perfect kick to it that made me order a second. 

The bread served is amazing. If they don't serve it to you, ASK FOR IT. It is pounded with butter, but so good. 

Lastly, order the guava mojito. Any cuban loves guava. I've never had a guava mojito, but did at mixto, and was surprised on how good it was. 

This is not a place I would go out of my way to go to, but definitely a place I wouldn't mind going to again.

I was in town for a business trip and yelped this place. I am glad I did because it is now my new favorite place! I wish they had this restaurant in southern California. The server was friendly and made delicious recommendations. I had the lobster, lasagna, and lemon grass dessert pre fixe menu for $45 and everything was superb! It's well worth the price! Loved it

Beer is fantastic. Can get their standard offerings in addition to some limited release brews. 

I'll keep this review simple: if you are in the area and appreciate good beer come here and try a few beers *hint hint* their stouts and IPAs are all awesome. 

Docking one star because there is no food in house. They have a food truck posted outside (lines have been long and service is slow). Also can bring your own food in but just having some big old pretzels in the brewery would go a long way.

My mom and I took a walking tour of the Lafayette Cemetery and the Garden District with Jesse yesterday. It was great. The tour was informative, fun, and engaging. We loved Jesse's sense of humor. I'd definitely do another tour with Jesse if we were in town longer.

Many times I have used this company to move persons in our family since 2010. In the past I highly recommended this company BUT after our recent move May 2021 - I can no longer recommend this company.
May 2021 - First phone call with Debbie in ABC office - she started to argue with me when I was listing items I needed help moving. I got past this bc maybe she was having a bad day. Second phone call - 24 hr b/4 move day - Debbie tells me that I am not on the schedule. I got past this bc she continued to check and found us on the schedule.  2 days after the move, we realize we are missing a metal step ladder - We call ABC to ask if the ladder was delivered to wrong place or they have it not know who it belongs to.  They said they would ask around and call me back. No return call. 2 weeks later we call back and speak with Megan, Office Manager, she said it was our responsibility to check to make sure we have everything and they cannot help find the ladder nor pay to replace it. "My hands are tied - I can't help you".  I asked for the name of the owner and she would not give me his name (I have spoke with owner in the past but wanted to see if there has been a change in ownership bc something has changed in the way they treat their customers.)  I told her I was going to make a complaint with BBB and she laughed at me and said "Ok - make a complaint over a step ladder...really?". I am disappointed in writing this review. I always felt better about moving knowing I could call ABC but those days are over. Something has changed at this office so please find ask around and find another moving company. That is what I will do.
Mary M.
Eureka, MO

The chicken was very delicious, and everything seems to be made fresh. But after waiting over an hour ( especially when there are only three people in the restaurant) it doesnt matter how good the chicken is. My wife and I ordered some chicken tenders and a waffle, and my brother ordered some chicken tenders and a waffle. Yet they couldn't quite seem to get it together in the kitchen. As other guest arrived and frustrations arose the kitchen still couldn't get it together. I knew something was wrong when I heard the girl half heartedly tell someone on the phone it would only be 20 to 30 minutes while myself and the other customers had been waiting well over an hour. The worst part is they lost my brothers ticket and in the fifteen minute process to get my brothers order fixed thet never once apologized. In short the chicken was good but the service was horrendous.
Wonderful food and our waiter Semaj was great!! And the manager Amanda was great also.

This is our go to take out at least once a week. Food is great. My kids love the burritos. I just wish they are being consistent of giving out the salsa. Most of the time we got two salsa (1 mild & 1 hot) for every take out. We usually order 2 burritos. However, some other time they only give out one or NONE at all, making up excuses for not giving them or try to charge us for it if we ask them. Well, that excuses just DOES NOT fly when you order the same thing 2 days ago and got two salsa with it.
Craving pizza? Don't go anywhere else. I highly recommend the San Gennaro! DeSano has a wonderful, relaxed environment and is BYOB. It doesn't get better than this.

I usually come to use the computer and print no more than 4 pages at a time. I've always always always been charged a different price. $3.56(2 pages). $1.90 (4 pages)  $2.82 (2 pages)... etc. I have receipts for these. I always ask to see a sign with what is costing what. A manager told me "we just bought a new printer/copier so all prices went up." 
Okay... Why is it my problem that you needed new equipment? Ups is a million dollar company.. if not billion. Why am I footing the bill? And obviously if you needed a new one it's because the old one got used and you were paid each time a customer used it. And she still didn't even show me a sign that breaks down my charges! 
I literally don't know what's going on in that store. They're making up their own prices or something.

Loved the brunch! We got the huevos tierra caliente and huevos a la mexicana. Served with warm corn tortillas, yum! The staff are nice here and it's a relaxing vibe. We've come here a few times now and the dishes have all tasted great without seeming ultra unhealthy (not overloaded with sauces and grease, just tasty ingredients).

I only get my nails done on special occasions and I decided to try Blooming Nails. They did a great job, but it was unenjoyable. I was very disappointed. I got a mani and a pedi and they did them at the same time. I was unable to sit back and relax and enjoy my pedi because someone was painting my nails at the same time. I wanted a design so I had to watch and explain during my amazing foot rub. If they were treated as 2 separate services they would have gotten way more stars.

The restaurant could use some updates especially the bathrooms. The Mongolian beef was a little to sweet for me & the crunchy noodles looked nice, but I could have done without eating them. The egg drop soup was very hot, but it had corn in it and I felt it influenced the taste too much. The service was very good. If I return it will be to try a different dish and a different soup.

I came in here on a Sunday night with 2 of my girlfriends. It took us about 5 minutes to be served (and there was not alot of customers there). 

When he did serve us, he was rude ... and lied and told us there were no drink specials. (even though we saw on the board later that there was!) Then he rung us up all together without asking if we wanted to be rung up together (or if we wanted to start a tab).

We left without finishing our drinks.

Last night my friends and I stopped here for dinner and I am so glad we did!! Now you MUST make reservations at least a week in advance but it is well worth the wait.  It's located inside The Harpeth Hotel in Downtown Franklin on 2nd Street which is great for walking around after dinner.  We each ordered cocktails (best Manhattan I've had in a long time) and the whipped feta - it was so rich and creamy... topped with pine nuts, pesto, and olive oil - spread on top of Franklin Bakehouse's Sourdough and wow - it was heaven!  Next I had the vegetarian bolognese sauce with mushrooms and truffle oil served over parppadelle (put fingers to lips and kiss) - delicious!  To top the night off we all shared a butterscotch pudding topped with vanilla cream and I highly recommend ordering this dessert with a glass of their sherry!  It complimented the entire night.  Our waiter Jimmy was awesome!! The restaurant is very elegantly decorated and I cannot wait to go back again and again!!

With the opening of The Golden Bowl a little bit of Washington Avenue has come to the Main Line. Golden Bowl's chef has expanded his ostensibly Chinese kitchen creations into the realm of Korean and Vietnamese soups and entrees. While Golden Bowl's menu gives a nod to traditional Chinese American dishes the offerings encompass a far greater taste experience. Take the time to savor the exceptional list of "Big Bowl Noodle Broths" (Vietnamese Pho) and the inventive list of "Golden Bowl Favorites." Then too, Golden Bowl's kitchen offers enticing lists of "Small Dishes" and "Starters" to say nothing of the extensive selection of noodle preparations.  If there is a problem with this menu it's that it would take many visits to try all the enticing offerings. Before I mention our selections for the evening I must comment on the décor. 
The Golden Bowl is dressed in simple quiet elegance. There are two dining areas. One is set with tables and chairs. The other, the larger of the two, has one wall whose continues bank curves gracefully on the perimeter. Simple rose wood chairs stud the other side of dark enameled tables. The rooms are calm and quite. Tables are set with cloth napkins and small vases of fresh flowers. The atmosphere is calm and conducive to dinner conversation. The service is most affable and the staff is well informed. They happily explain any of the menu details.
With our menus in hand, it didn't take us long to make our decision. We would share three ways. For our starter we selected the Xe-Lua combination noodle bowl. For the entrées we selected two, one from the "Favorites," the XO Shrimp, and one from the "Traditional," the Mango Chicken. When the noodle bowl arrived it was, as expected, easily enough for more than three. The bowl was heaped high with a rich white noodle that our waiter described as something between Thai and Korean. The meat selection offered sizeable slices of beef, several meat balls, slices of tripe and other tender bits whose identity I do not know. The broth, rich and flavorful, could be seasoned with sides of hot sauce, Hoisin sauce, fresh lime and mint. The noodle's silky ribbons played well against the meats' textural variety. The server then brought our entrées.
 (I would say that we asked for covers to keep them warm. Why have Chinese restaurants abandoned the silver covers they once used? Chinese food on open platters does get cold very quickly, especially in air-conditioning.)
 The entrées arrived in artistic arrangements. Here again, the portions were more than adequate.  I started with the shrimp in XO sauce. Now, I had read about XO sauce in David Chang's "Momofuko" and was anxious to give it a try. While the red pepper on the menu suggested this was to be a spicy dish, we did not find it so. The shrimp were full and sweet. The accompanying variety of mushrooms, asparagus and snow peas rounded out the dish in flavor and texture.
