Our traditional entrée, the Mango Chicken offered broad slices of chicken and full slices of mango.  While this choice was quite good in its own right, it was a bit on the sweet side. Of course, we knew that when we ordered it. But it was good to see how The Golden Bowl would treat old time standards.
I have dined at Golden Bowl some six times since it opened.  While the personal aspect of the service is impeccable, the practical side is problematic. The wait staff is affable and accommodating. What you order, what they put on the table and when they put it on the table is problematic.  Not once, in six sessions, has the order been correct. The worst was an order of Korean dumpling soup that had no dumplings. I hope the Main Line will find their way beyond the traditional dishes to those far better Asian recipes found on Washington Avenue and now at The Golden Bowl.

My dad and I ate here for dinner the night before the national championship game so the restaurant was decently busy due to the visiting crowd being in town. I made reservations early in the day so I'm not sure how long the wait would be when you're just walking in. I've eaten at many Japanese places where they cook in front of you but this one is nothing special compared to the others. Don't get me wrong, the food was tasty and the staff were all nice but it isn't any different than the many other Japanese restaurants. If you have younger kids in your party they will love the show while preparing your dinner but if it was up to me, we would've sat at a regular table because once you've seen the show, it isn't any different every other time. My teriyaki chicken, friend rice and appetizer shrimp were very good and flavorful. I'm giving this place 4 stars because it isn't anything special BUT it's definitely worth checking out if you want some good Japanese food. I will note that it is on the more pricey side but that's normal for any "cook in front of you" restaurants.

I was so excited to hear about a poke place finally coming to Indy! I like their concept of ordering food, it' similar to Qdoba/Subway in that you pick your seafood, veggies, rice, sauces, etc. They have a great selection, and I enjoyed the marinated tuna a bit more than the marinated salmon. I also loved their house sauce mixed with their spicy mayo and eel sauce. I felt that the "small" size was a perfect portion for lunch. I definitely want to come back and try everything, I'm sure it will all be just as good. 

I wish they a soda fountain (they have a fridge with bottled drinks and also kombucha), and that there isn't a whole lot of seating space available for the amount of people that come through. I also think it'd be helpful to have pictures of popular or recommended bowls for people who haven't tried poke before and might feel rushed or overwhelmed by the choices. 

But with that being said, the interior is very clean and modern. I will definitely make a trip back whenever I end up visiting the north side, and I think it'd be awesome if they open more locations (like on the west side) in the future.

It's worth the wait! While I did not have a bad beignet in all of NOLA, this place stuck out. Their beignets were by far the thickest and most dense, I really enjoyed them. The wait goes super fast, I made it a point to stop here twice. The staff is super friendly and quick moving. It is cash only!

I want to say Great Harvest has THE Best breads I have ever enjoyed. I am 66 and have had some great bread in my life, so I will say I have NEVER had better bread and think theirs just might be number one. But who cares!  They have wonderful flavorful FRESH, NATURAL, CLEAN BREAD. I visit at least once a week ... if I lived closer I would be there 3-4 times per week. The Asiago and Orange/Cranberry are 2 of my favorites but I love them all. Their sandwiches, as you might guess, are super. And their pastries are wonderful ... As you can tell I am a big fan. Top that off with a friendly, neighborhood, " you are a GUEST in our home" feel and it makes for my favorite Boise coffee/sandwich/bread bakery shop. Well done!!!

100x better than Canadian tire, and 85% cheaper! The sales are kept on the computer system so if you lose your receipt, it's not an issue at all. The staff is always friendly and ready to help you get what you need.
The only issue I have is I am a Class 1 operator (drive a big rig) and there not a whole lot for that department, besides bungies, flagging, warning markers, grease for my 5th wheel etc, I'd love to see more of a trucker section. It will bringing business!

This is a Boise staple. It's great little ice cream parlor and burger joint. It's clean, it's good, and best of all affordable. Their chocolate dipped cone are so much better than Fancy Freeze's. Delsa's is a thick and rich dip, unlike the liquidy, thin coating at Fancy Freeze. The workers are ready and efficient at their job. Not sure what other reviews are talking about when they weren't greeted- I was greeted just fine by the teenaged boy working tonight with a "Hey how's it goin'? What can I get for you?" Unless the other reviewers were looking for a madman smiling crazy and ecstatic tone of voice with fake enthusiasm...I thought the greeting was just fine. But then again, I'm typically pleased with a job well done and a clean place. Some people can not be pleased no matter what; that's why I like to look at that person's other reviews.**update 9/30/16: Delsa's has stopped serving soft serve ice cream...thus no perfectly chocolate dipped cones. Why? Miss Delsa did you stop serving my excuse for coming to see you and buy lots of yummies? Please bring back your rich dipped soft serve cones!!!**

This place is amazing! Delicious and fresh food. Great prices and service. My husband and I shared the Doner sandwich and red lentil soup and it was plenty of food. Staff is so friendly and attentive. Pita bread with hummus and tsaziki is brought to the table as a free appetizer.

This little cafe is supposedly affiliated with Bella's in S. Tampa, (one of my favs for years), but the food here is awful!! The Pasta Fagioli soup doesn't have one morsel of pasta in it, and it looks like paste....thick with no broth or veggies!!! The cup was filled only to the halfway point, and after looking at it and taking one bite, I opted out of asking them to fill the cup to the rim.

Taco waffle fries: absolutely impeccable 

Waffle fries: crunchy and thick 
White cheddar cheese sauce: yummy
Pico de gallo: daily serving of veggies 
Grilled chicken: very nicely flavored 


My first dining experience at cleavers was very enjoyable. I came during the heat of lunch rush but I received my food in less than 10 minutes. I will definitely be back to try their epic looking cheese steaks!

First, I am a pizza lover. If pizza is not my favorite food, that is only because lobster edged it out. I am very particular about my pizza and would choose something else if the pizza available is subpar. Hence, I avoid chain pizza and supermarket pizza,  both frozen and fresh. I am not interested in pizza that is "not bad" and won't give it a second chance. 

After a hair appointment that ran late and still needing to grocery shop at Trader Joe's, I decided to try Pizza Luna for a quick bite in the Joe's shopping center at Speedway and Wilmot. 

The restaurant had been a Cup It Up. The scuttlebutt was that they closed due to politics, but likely it was because the food wasn't great. I tried it once, deemed it okay, and that was that. 

Walking into Luna, the first Impression was olfactory: The garlicky tomatoey fragrance could bring any pizza lover to his knees. I was enthusiastically welcomed at the order counter and was asked if this was my first time. Since it was, I was handed a menu and offered a very helpful guide to ordering. Three different styles of pizza are offered and each can be tailored with the toppings of choice. My guide raved about the fig, but I opted to go the more traditional route of house-made fennel sausage and onion. Advised that my pizza would be ready in seven minutes., I was thrilled; it was now 2:00 and I was starving. 

Seven minutes later, my pizza was ceremoniously delivered to my table along with its intoxicating aroma. I picked up the first piece, dripping molten cheese, burned my tongue, and didn't care. The crust is perfect: thin - but not at all cardboardy - with a delightfully chewy edge. The sauce is faintly sweet and deliciously present but not dominating. The fennel sausage is, frankly, scrumptious. I'd take credit for amazing ordering, but the young man at the counter deserves a nod and Pizza Luna deserves the rest of the credit. 

If someone in your party is not a pizza lover (what?!), lovely salads and an antipasto board are available. A lunch special offers a slice with a salad. Beers and wines are on menu as well. 

Tucson had two pizzas I will eat; then there are three.

My wife and I went on the tour this weekend and I must say it was a lot of fun! If you are a movie fan than you will like this tour. We rode around the city in the movie van watching clips of films while stopping at the locations where the respective clip was filmed. Our guide also explained some of the lingo in the movie business, as well as showing us where some of the movie star's houses were in the area. Overall it was a blast! Would recommend to friends and family!

Nice good clean place. Took time to do nails. Vin did a great job cleaning my nails and giving a good experience. He got right to work and did not waste time.  Overall good experience and good pricing. Can't go wrong choosing here. Would come again!!

Adel's is fantastic! It doesn't look like much from the outside and the decor on the inside is outdated but let's not kid ourselves... these are the types of places where some of the best food is found! The gyro meat is flavorful and very tender and of abundance! Great value for the price and amazing service every time! Adel and his wife are very loving and kind and personable to every customer. Please remember that this is a small, family run restaurant and all food is made to order so during peak times you may have a bit of a wait but I assure you it is worth every minute! Please try Adel's! You won't regret it!
Went here for a holiday dinner with some friends recently.  Love the décor of the place.  it is a very cool setup.  The food was good but not great.

Empanadas for the people!  Meat pies of the heavenly skies!  Meridian is lucky to have a place like this and I hope they get the support from this burger, pizza, IPA town.  Location might be hard to get attention so I really hope anyone reading this will tell others to support this business!!  They have desert empanadas, and a mix of many beautiful flavored savories.  

I would suggest they use no green bell peppers in the food as many people need to avoid them specifically.  By being green you are most likely serving peppers that aren't even close to ripe, which has several downsides.  People deserve to eat ripe foods and green bell peppers do not have near the nutrition as other colors, and are hard on some digestion tracts.  Also, they are high in oxalates and red are not.  My favorite restaurants generally don't last in this sports bar/soccer mom town of Meridian, for what my guess is, lack of people that try new things.  Wake up and love these puffy baked pillows of perfection!!
Best rib Rub I have found so far in Idaho. Meaty Ribs very juicy . Service was fast and Friendly staff .

The service here is AMAZING. We had Carlos as our server and he was so helpful and entertaining. When I come back I'm going to ask for him specifically. Plus one of the best happy hours in town. Will def be back.
I love this place. They don't just make pizzas. They have a wide selection from wraps to salads to desserts.. If I want something late night I choose this place!!

I would give this place 10 stars if I could! After living in Philly for 2 years and trying almost every nail salon and spa in and around Philly, I am SO happy to say I finally found my nail soul mate. 

The ambiance is so peaceful, clean, and just simply relaxing.  Jada did my manicure and pedicure and I fell asleep! Her hands are so strong and she did such a thorough job.  I walked in at 8:30pm and even though they close at that time, she took her time and did an EXCELLENT job!

The massage, then the hot stone massage, then the parrafin hand treatment, then the neck and shoulder massage while the parrafin was drying up.  All this is included in the manicure and pedicure price of $30!!!! 

I'm going back today to get a full body massage.  She knows the pressure points and after my foot massage yesterday with the pedicure and manicure, I know I will be more relaxed than any spa I have ever been to.  And trust me, I am an avid spa goer...this woman knows how to give a proper massage and has exceptionally strong hands for the tiny woman she is!

Everyone working there are just lovely people who really work hard. I live on 21st and Market, but will go to this location only!! This is what getting pampered is really about and how every customer should be treated.

As a fan of mexican food i was skeptical of a mexican food place in the middle of a casino but i was way wrong! I ordered the nachos for like $8 and the biggest plate of nachos the size of my head came out and had all the fixins,meat,guacamole,cheese,sour cream and the most delicious beans ive had in awhile! The Atmosphere was nice and laid back but still had an elegant factor to it, My friend Ordered the wet burrito and it was way to big for him to finish even after i stole bites. The Marghearitass were affordable and wasn't made form some pre made mix out of a bottle,it was fresh and not over the top. I loved Panchos so much ive been back at least once a week!

The best burgers around!! Great employees as well. I noticed there was a customer snapping pics with a Yelp Expert Reviewer shirt on and a Yelp hat on it was Steve and he happened to leave a nasty review  he was just mad everyone laughed at him and some little boy squirted mustard on him and he was incoherently asking for his Yelp elite discount he was asked to leave!!
Thank you for quickly resolving my issue. Great customer service! We will definitely be back.

Just added a new favorite restaurant to the list! 
Came here for lunch and it was a lot less hectic than dinner time. The night before the line was out the door. 
Lunch time was perfect to try something new. Ordered the Cajun Crawfish Popcorn which was awesome. Loved the batter, it was light and flavorful. For the main course ordered the Creole Crawfish Crabcakes. Wow, probably one of the best crabcake I have ever had. The crawfish gives it a great flavor. It was topped with a Jalapeño Beurre-Blanc Sauce along with a side of veggies and rice. And of course the mini muffins -- the banana ones were warm and moist, very flavorful. My friend ordered the Big Easy BBQ Beef Po'boy sandwich -- it was ok. 
We both agreed that Crawfish is a must t to try here. It's definitely a place I would come back to -- maybe save room to try the Soufflé.

Worst experience ever. Went in to have lunch with friends & was not greeted by staff. They sat us at a dirty table & never offered to clean it. I had to personally removed the plates after waiting for 10 minutes. 
They refused to allow a highchair, claiming it was a fire hazard. When we asked to see the fire hazard, the manager said he did not know where it was. Completely against federal law. Horrible staff & service. Highly recommend avoiding this restaurant at all

25 minutes from ordering to getting food. Order was tossed into my hands and wrong. Ordered you meals and got three. Had to wait to get the cashier's attention to tell him and then way for the additional meal. By the time I got home everything was cold.

LOOK ELSEWHERE!!  I requested a quote to install a mini split air conditioner in my condo. The rep arrived (30 min late) and looked at my property to determine the best place to install. It took about 20 minutes. Later that evening he sent the quote and it was over $7000.00!!  I had already searched these units online and they run about $800.00. HOW DID THEY ARRIVE AT $7000!!??   I asked for a detailed breakdown of the quote, and he responded that they don't provide that information!!??  Are you kidding??  Really - if they sent 3 guys (which they wouldn't), and it took hours (which it wouldn't), and they paid them each $100/hour (which they wouldn't), and they doubled the cost of the unit (which they would) ~ with those inflated costs it would cost $4000.00.  What a ridiculous company. Got another quote and got the job done for $2300.  LOOK ELSEWHERE!!
Doc Hamant has retired and the practice sold to a conglomerate.  I've moved on to another doctor.

I live 5 minutes away from Topgolf but never went until an office Christmas party. We had the Fairway Feast: cheeseburger sliders, chicken strips, chips and dip, and buffalo meatballs. The food was surprisingly really good. Service was very friendly and helpful. Only had 2 complaints. The heater setting options sucks, you're either cold or on fire. Whoever fills the drink orders needs to differentiate the drinks, we were left guessing which was diet Coke, regular Coke, and Dr. Pepper.

I don't even like biscuits and oh my goodness, these biscuits were EVERYTHING. They were pure deliciousness. I tried a little bit of everything with my group but the East Nasty was absolutely my favorite. So savory and perfect. We also had bonuts. Oh my goodness (again), I did not know that such yumminess was could exist. The drinks were great too. My only complaint is the line you must wait in to get in... I understand it's because the food is delicious and popular, but I don't like waiting in the cold.

What an excellent sales experience. From the hassle free test drive to the car delivery, this was the smoothest car buy I've ever experienced. The financing options were straight forward and the buying process was organized and no BS. I can't say enough positive things compared to many shady and substandard dealerships out there.

I really really really want to love Kunitz. It's locally owned, has a decent selection of good quality shoes, and has a (supposedly - I've never actually purchased) really great service - including a basically never-ending warranty. If you find something you like and they don't have your size, they'll gladly call the other store and order it in for you (if you pre-pay), and you can return sale items.

But I just 'like' Kunitz. Their selection is just plain not really my style. Every once in a while I'll find something I like but the fit is always super strange (what is it with higher end shoes always been so freakin' narrow?). 

But the thing I REALLY dislike about Kunitz? Their sale section. I know, I know. I LOVE sale sections. But their's is outside, over top of a grated sidewalk with an almost constant breeze coming up it from the LRT or whatever passing below. So if you dare to look at their sale shoes, you will get a constant breeze of dust and debris in your face and up your pant legs. It's SO gross.

Kunitz: There is a reason you can't seem to get rid of those sale shoes. Just sayin'.

I took my wife here for our 3rd anniversary. We've walked past many times but never knew what the inside looked like. Very classy and elegant. The server was just awesome and it shows he really loves what he does. Very thorough in telling us what he recommends us to try. I like that. I had the rack of lamb and it was the best tasting dish I've ever had. Dessert was amazing. Overall great experience. Would recommend anyone trying it for sure!

A great pedi for a great price ($20 + tip)! They were very careful and took their time. Side note: I have leg hair and they never once judged or talked about it behind my back which is something that has happened at other places...I felt very comfortable and taken care of. Will definitely return in future!

Dr. Kessler has been my dentist for about 20 years, give or take. I am extremely satisfied with the practice. The quality of dentistry is extremely high--I've seen all the dentists there at one time or another, in addition to Dr. Kessler, and felt in excellent hands. The office staff is also very nice--they are accommodating when you have an emergency, need to reschedule, and they are always, always friendly. Not the norm for dentistry practices. Finally, the hygienists and other professional assistants are all very good at their jobs and make being there actually fun. I have never had "fun" at any other doctor's or dentist office. It's just a well run and easy going practice with highly credentialed, friendly professionals who provide top-notch, friendly care, and make the billing and insurance process easy.

You cannot go wrong choosing Bella Bridesmaid for your bridesmaid needs. Carrie - the owner - is amazing, honest and provides excellent customer service. The selection of dresses is to die for & it will be one thing you know will go smoothly during your wedding planning period.

They can never get a simple order right. 75% of the time my one blizzard order it's always wrong. I ordered a tropical blizzard which has coconut, pecans, and bananas. That's it. Last times had pineapple...today I said no pineapple..it doesn't come with it. what do I get? No bananas..just pecans and Coconut. At least I didn't get pineapple. 3 simple ingredients...manager had offered to replace it next time I decide to try once again.

Pros: Affordable rate for the area, at the center of several cool spots, walking distance to State St, huge community kitchen, clean bathrooms and rooms

Cons: Can't plug anything like a Fire Stick to TV (they're locked into the wall), small room

My wife and I spent 2 nights here on vacation. Would do it again. The pros far outweigh the minor cons.

There is nothing I dislike about this place except that it is a chain owned by an evil corporation.  

Setting that aside, the food is fresh and delicious and, more importantly, seasonal.  It's really my kind of food -- very simple, yet creative and tasty.  

We sat at the bar to avoid waiting for a table.  The bartender was very sweet and attentive and, even better than that, extremely knowledgeable about wine.  She helped us choose a red based on the meals we planned on ordering.  She was spot on.  

The atmosphere is very inviting, too -- very chic and elegant, but not pretentious or uncomfortable.  

Love this place!

Good but some problems. 

The ambiance in the courtyard is stunning. The tables are cheap and wobbly though. The interior space is tired and cramped. 

The service was not good. Everyone seemed disinterested. It's never a good feeling to feel like you're burdening the staff by being there. 

Their waitlist system was a mess. 

Expensive for what it is. 

Food ok but not standout in general, especially for a city like New Orleans. 

Won't be coming back.
So good. Had lunch and very good. Had cheesesteak sandwich with fries. Fries weren't as warm as should have been. Still good though. Someone also cauliflower tacos--excellent!!

DO NOT GO to this one if you have other option. This branch is franchise and different than other branches. The ACME loyalty card/program does not work there (they did NOT tell you that on website and in other branches). Its menu is not listed on ACME website and it offer much less items compared to others. 

Because it is inside the Casino, the smell of smoking is strong.
The bar has limited inventory and can not make the two kinds of classic New Orleans drink I ordered.

Remember - it's BYOB. 

Audrey Claire is an excellent place to host a large party. I have been to three dinners here where our party was 20+ people. One of the dinners was a 30-person party and they were kind enough to accommodate us. 

Their three-course meal pricing is also very reasonable. 

Standouts: 

- All the mediterranean spreads
- Mussels
- Rack of lamb
Great place, friendly staff and awesome food. One of my neighborhood staples that I always reccommend!

Another Manayunk recommendation from a friend that did not disappoint. 

We stepped inside during a hot and humid Saturday, and one of the co-owners, Chris, immediately greeted us and asked us how our day was going.

They have a nice mix of classic flavors and more interesting flavors. I tried the Strawberry and my partner went for the Honey Lavender. Both were very good, but the Honey Lavender was extra special.

The ice cream itself was probably more of a 4-star experience, but the friendliness of Chris at the counter made the experience really stand out.

Yeah the PCC gets the job done for the big conventions we all like to attend.  Be it the Flower Show or continuing legal education (really, how many more classes on ethics do I need to take...is this really going to make me more ethical?) or perhaps the Car Show or the always compelling Boat Show (nothing like a bilge pump to light my fire), this place can cram 'em in without feeling too...crammed.  The immediate proximity to Reading Terminal Market and the host of delectable stuff therein helps break up the ennui of yet another class on...ethics.

This place was recommended by our hotel clerk.  It was my favorite meal during our trip to New Orleans.  There was a line when we went for an early dinner, but it moved pretty quickly. The place was dark and noisy but who cares? Food was topnotch.  They have the best oysters -- whether raw, chargrilled, or fried.  We ordered so much food yet managed to lick our plates clean.  For under $100 without tip, we had 2 dozen oysters, seafood platter of fried shrimp, fried fish, fried oysters, fried soft shell crab, and a side of hush puppies.  We also tried the medley of gumbo, jambalaya, and red beans & rice, and we shared bread pudding for dessert.  My husband washed it all down with a glass of the local beer.  This place is a must try!

Great location for going to the theater! Fortunately we arrived early and were not in a hurry. Food was good and everyone was very nice but...
Took a long while for waitress to take drink order and we ordered an appetizer at the same time. There were 3 of us and 1 ordered just water. Appetizer arrived but still no drinks. Finally after another 5-10 minutes waiting 2 of the 3 drinks arrived but no water. Person bringing out drinks (not waitress) brought water upon request. 
Finally another person (very nice, not original waitress) took our food order. Order arrived in good time and food was good but salads on two of the plates got mixed up (arugula vs mixed green). 
Meanwhile at least 4 different people kept asking "how is everything?" which got annoying.. especially when asked by the waitress who finally appeared again near the end of our meal then asked twice (!) "how was everything?".

I recently took my diesel truck into A1 on a recommendation by a friend for a transmission issue. The tech (maybe one of the owners?)  took my truck for a quick drive (without me) and looked at the transmission fluid.  With this limited info he told me that the ONLY fix was to replace my whole transmission at a discounted price of $2500.  He said that he could rip the whole thing out and take it apart (the only thing he could do) and it would be the same price to fix it as to replace it.  I took it to Arrow Transmissions who fixed it for $360.   All that was wrong was it needed a new switch.  Really A1?

Great stay, excellent staff. Rooms were new and very clean. The only compliant that I could have is that the walls seems very thin, we could hear heavy footsteps and kiss running for a good part of the first day we stayed there.

3.5 stars is pretty much on point for this truck. I found them at the Livingston Food Truck Festival and it turned out the two rice balls I ordered represented the exact reviews I see here. The Philly cheeseteak ball was 5 stars - very flavorful, packed with meat and cheese, clean tasting without being greasy. The prosciutto ball was 2 stars - not very flavorful, couldn't taste the ham over the cheese, overall tasted way too light in flavor and with a heavy coating of grease on the outside. (5+2)÷2 = 3.5. I rounded up because I love the concept and the good balls are excellent, but they need to be more consistent.

We decided to try this place on a Sunday afternoon after church.  This is a home cooked soul food restaurant and the food was very good.  I had Turkey and dressing, sweet potatoes and mac and cheese.  The sweet potatoes were too sweet for my liking but overall it was all delicious.  Service was decent but not great until the end.  The lady that we paid at the end was amazing!  All of the employees in the kitchen were also extremely helpful and happy. I'd definitely visit this place again!  Oh....the spicy cornbread was amazing!

Very good food and a variety of health products. Fresh organic vegetables and fruits. Very nice wine and cheese selection. They also make some very good warm foods and sandwiches. In addition, go there about 8:45 and stake out some of the stuff in the deli area. AFTER 9 THEY REDUCE MOST OF IT TO HALF-PRICE!!! Why did I just tell you that?! Another plus: There are some hot Cougars who shop here!  Oh yeah, and City College students... "I love them [City College Girls]. I get older and they stay the same age." Ha!

Amendment: They have changed the half-price deal to 8:30 and now there is a huge line. There can be barely anyone in the store and then as you turn the corner you say out loud, "DOH!"

Ok so I have this little car, it is one I bought for my father in 1991 but since he is currently dead he does not use it much, nor do I. But this car does get dirty just sitting around so I took it down to where we get our other vehicles washed.

Even though this car is old, it is low mileage and looks as good as a new car. It is just not an expensive vehicle.

I noticed as it was surrounded by a lot of over priced vehicles being dried that those vehicles were worked on about twice the time as our poor little car. As we drove off I saw the result of this lack of attention, very dusty dash... you know like how we brought it in. the passenger side windows were never cleaned, in or outside and generally poor handling or care.

Folks give each vehicle the same care since it costs the same and the tip probably is the same... it is only fair.

Waited 1 hour to get our appetizer and another hour to get our entree. I ordered the pistachio flatbread pizza, and it was bland and tasted bad. Our wait staff was poor. The hostess began serving our table because we were not being served by the waitress. We left all of this!

As all other reviewers have stated, customer service is horrible. Expect to wait nearly an hour for a customer service rep that has no idea what he/she is doing. I couldn't wait to switch to Direct TV to cancel my cable with Brighthouse. Unfortunately I still have to use them for internet. Nearly $80 a month gets me highspeed internet. What that means is internet that has speeds barely above your high school AOL account and requires you to constantly reset your modem. Can't wait to cancel this company for once and for all.

This was my first "real" Philly cheesesteak from Philadelphia and it was delicious!!!  I had the Whiz with onions. I also like that i was able to see them slice and cook the steaks. Real Meat!!!
  I went on a Sunday and the line wasn't too long. It was only held up by tourists who got to the front of the line and still didn't know what they wanted, or had unnecessary questions. I could see the cashier's frustration every time they encountered 1 of these people LOL but they still provided good customer service! Luckily I was with a native Philadelphian who taught me how to properly order like I was from Philly!!!
  It was gone so quick that I wish I had ordered a 2nd one!!!  Or maybe that's just me being greedy!!

Love the freshness of the ice cream, and it is a pretty interesting process to watch! Just a bit pricey, but I think I would gladly pay the few extra dollars for the freshness of Sweet Charlie's. Will definitely be coming again!

Old fashion layout with small frying pan greens and old growth elms lining the fairways. Modest improvements by the city  have returned the course it to some of its previous glory when Ben Hogan shot the course record.  Far from the cheapest muni  in town, $45 w cart in April, overall a challenging and fun course. Four stars because not many munis left within 60 miles of Reno as this is the best in its category. 

Pro tip: When in doubt puts break towards the Peppermill.

As soon as I walked in, I knew this was a college dig, or what seemed like it at the time, the staff were rude and they thought they where 'cool', couldn't wait to leave that hole, wouldn't even let my dog in it! Much better bars in the magazine street area

I frequent Brown Sugar too many times to count. I usually get Cassave Pone, Currants Roll, Roti, Doubles, or Patties. The roti is great and I've had the freshest Roti on a Saturday morning. The doubles bara is a hit or miss sometimes but generally very good.

The actual restaurant is a bit small with the bakery counter and juice bar taking up much of the space.  There are two booths that you can sit at if you want to eat your food there. Then there are two windows. You order from the window on the right and pay at the window on left.

The roti prices are really good considering the amount of work that goes into preparing a roti. I highly recommend this place for take out roti or a quick snack on some doubles or patties.

Oh and they have a Mauby and Sorrel fountain.

Call ahead to order on busy days!

I love my massages from Reagan! When I moved into the Nashville area, I was in great need of a good massage. I've had the good fortune to have experienced some amazing massage therapists in CA, and I hoped that I would find the same here- Reagan is amazing!! I've been seeing her for over 2 years and I wish I could have an appointment with her every week. She is very intuitive with her technique and she always asks what my main areas of focus are. I would recommend Reagan to anyone wanting a fabulous massage.

I've been very pleased with the customer service and the quality of their work! For the record, I am a pretty regular customer with slacks, shirts and suits. I love what they are doing to reduce their footprint - recycling hangers and reusable bag. Thanks Team and will see you soon!

When my cheap ass friends came to visit me in Philly and told me that they wanted to eat a 5 dollar dinner, I thought it impossible.  Picking my brain for something THAT cheap, but still decent sized, I thought of all the take out places in Philly.  Two of them were vegetarian not to mention myself so fast food is not an option and is unhealthy anyway.  Then I remembered this tiny little place on south street that I remember visiting in New York years before.  
Maoz is a chain, but is delicious.  I reccomend the meal that comes with half a pita sandwich and fries and salad bar and drink for under 7 dollars.  It's a great deal, delich veggies in the salad bar (i love beets).  The place is TINY and a bit falling apart, but that is to be expected on South Street.

What a shame.  I bought an oven thermometer here that did not work.  It got stuck the first time I used it.  I tried to return it (after being coughed on by the rude cashier and scolded for grimacing after said cough.)  I was told that they wouldn't accept the return without the packaging, even though we had an itemized receipt.  I had thrown the packaging out assuming that the product would not be faulty.  "What am I going to scan?" was his response, along with, "there are receipts all over the place!"  (Not quite sure what that one meant.) A person with the least amount of pride in their business would take a minute, look up the product, and help a new customer at all costs.  Unfortunately, this is not that sort of place.  We left all of our potential purchases and future business at the store and are never going back.  We are new to the neighborhood and so sad that we can't frequent our neighborhood hardware store.  Avoid this place at all costs.

I came in because of my wedding coordinator, Kelly Villo's with Blue Sky Events, recommendation for alterations. On my way out, Janet had a gorgeous dress she was putting on display. She was so personable and kind! I ended up asking her to select a dress she thought would look good on me... she nailed it on her first try!!! She brought me the most gorgeous dress and for the first time, I felt like a REAL BRIDE!!!! I never knew this place existed and I definitely experienced the highest level of service and most certainly a TRUE EXPERT in wedding dress selection. I can be a bit of a perfectionist but that didn't stop Janet from completely satisfying my wants and needs for my wedding dress!!! Wow, the talent within this shop is INCREDIBLE! Never would have known! Ask for JANET and you will be speechless!! HIGHLY RECOMMEND!!!!

A woman named Monica, working at the reception desk, helped me tremendously. Her patience and attitude were both beyond welcoming and helpful. She not only assisted me with check in (as I had lost my VA Identification Card) but later in the day (while she was multitasking with helping other patients) helped me with submitting an additional  dd214, getting a new ID and even printed me off, all on her own accord, information regarding an award I was unaware of. Over the past few years that I have been seen at this VA hospital, I have had multiple troubles with being passed from phone line to phone line, and having to wait months to be seen for an appointment. So, with that said, I can definitely say that Monica was a wonderful delight and I hope she is working there the next time that I have an issue. It is never so much the personnel working at the VA that cause me grief, but the processes that I have to go through that make things frustrating.

You'll never believe that the best beer made in Philly is down this dark, dank alley in a homely tasting room in Kensington. But it's true. What the guys at Form have done is transform Philly into Brussels. I cannot recommend this place enough. This is how beer should be made.

I returned last week for more. This time had the jerk chicken wings appetizer, then jerk chicken platter. The wings were unbelievably good -- the skin was crispy as heck with a wonderful smoked flavor. 

The sauce for the jerk chicken platter had some sort of sweet/spicy flair. My entire mouth was burning after that episode. But OMG it was a heavenly evening!  Awesome, mon!!!

I booked with these guys over the phone for a one week stay for my dog, Minnia.  She is a vital part of our family as she helps both my wife and I and is our official ESA so we wanted her to stay at a nice spot. It seems that there are only 2-3 quality spots like this in the outer Philadelphia area. The girl on the phone was very professional and helped me pick the sports package. Minnia stays next week so I will update on the drop off. The nine day stay will cost about 700. I will update after her stay also to indicate the value of the costs and stay.

Drop off was an okay experience and the front desk manager took very good care of us.

I toured the place and my comment on that is that I had concerns about the number of young people working without what appeared to be enough supervision and the ratio of dogs to young inexperienced female and male (white kids mostly upper and middle class) worker seemed high. No job requires zero experience. Taking care of a lot of animals is a job that requires a premium level of technical expertise and expert follow through from dedicated and experienced technicians with a proven track record. I did not see a single supervisor while touring the facility. Based on my experience with running my own business and previously being a manager for a fortune 500 company, I bet they are not compensating their workers highly with an appropriate fringe benefits package to endear their employees to them creating business value through employee growth and longevity. Maybe I am wrong, I was only on site for a few minutes. 


Below are notes on pickup and afterwards.

On pickup it took them almost 20 minutes to reunite my dog with me which should have been paramount . I even had to ask why it was taking so long. 

On pickup I began noticing that she was coughing and throwing up all day long. She was also extremely fatigued like she hadn't been able to sleep in a week. She is at the end of the bed now and I am watching her cautiously and will be taking her to the vet as soon as possible.

We took her to the vet on Sunday morning and found out she had the dreaded Kennel Cough. We got her on meds and this afternoon she is doing better and not coughing much at all. 

I called to let Wagworth know and they put a manager on who did seem genuinely to care about the situation. She even offered to call us back and follow up with us. 

I have augmented my review because of the conversation I had with the front desk manager. I do not know if they view these comments or not but I did feel like there was genuine concern about  our puppy in the manager.  

The place is very expensive. The fees for 9 days were almost $700 which included a bath. This is almost as much as the median household income for the entire Philadelphia area for 2014.  (34 K per year) which is excessive in my opinion for the service provided. 

For that great of an expense, I would have appreciated near seamless luxury for my pet and expedited concierge service for me when dropping off and picking up just like staying at a 5 star hotel.


I moved my review from a 1 star to a 3 thanks exclusively to the front desk manager.

Great quick lunch, not so much dinner. This review is based solely on sandwiches. I've had the Testarossa (thinly sliced roast pork, pancetta, Claudio's sharp provolone, roasted long hots, and pepperoncini aioli) and the Italian Stallion (fennel salami, genoa salami, sweet coppa, Claudio's sharp provolone, house-made sweet pickled peppers & onions, and pepperoncini aioli). I've been to all three locations, and my most pleasant experience was at Rittenhouse (118 S. 18th Street).

This place is ideal for people who work nearby and want a quick, tasty lunch. I would not recommend coming here for dinner because they don't pack the sandwiches with ingredients. 

The Italian Stallion: This is not the best Italian hoagie in the city, but it's the best in center city (and if you don't have time nor want to go down to South Philly). There isn't (at least the two times I've had this sandwich) very much meat on it. Claudio's sharp provolone is better than regular old supermarket or deli provolone, but it's not that impressive, so I don't know why they're making a big deal and telling everyone that's where it's from. It always tastes fresh, so it's better than getting an Italian hoagie from the Wawa down the block...

The Testarossa: This is not the place to go for a roast pork sandwich. Again, there isn't very much meat, although it is still quite tasty, and the aioli adds a nice touch that other places specializing in roast pork do not. The pepperoncini aioli is my favorite thing, and I wish they sold it separately in bottles. 

The rolls (Liscio's) have tasted fresh the three times I've had Matt & Marie's, and having it toasted adds a nice crunch (highly recommended, although they don't usually ask if you want it toasted, so you might have to request it).

If you don't have much time, and you live/work nearby, come here.

It's been 70 days since I placed my $110 order on 12-24-2020 and I have gotten no response, no e-mail, no plants, no refund. No deal on plants is worth it if you have to bargain whether or not you get the plants in the first place. I'm happy there are some people that are getting their plants, but there is still a large amount of people who haven't gotten their plants or refund and are having to go through their banks.

So when is Yelp going to allow a zero star review?  It would aptly describe my wife's experience today (01/23/19) at this location.  

Her "stylist" didn't dry her hair all the way with the blow dryer, leaving it almost completely wet, and then asked "is that good enough?"  Who does that?  I mean, maybe it's normal practice to half-ass things for this person so they can churn and burn through more customers, but by definition a blow "dryer" is mean to "dry" hair.

My wife's hair is done in a very young, sporty, cool way, very short on the sides and then longer in a wave-like Mohawk on top.  She typically goes to Toni and Guy or another high-end salon, because she has been told by just about every person who has cut her hair that the cut is a challenging one.

This "stylist" said "oh this is an easy cut to do," and dove right in with clippers.  She proceeded to gouge a quarter-size hole in the side of her head.  Needless to say, my wife confronted her about it and was told "no, it's not our fault, your head has a bump in it which caused the clippers to cut it shorter."

No offer to fix it, no offer for a discount, no offer for a free cut, no validation or apology, nothing.  

Now she feels violated and has to either try to cover the spot with makeup, or spend another $50-70 to have someone who actually knows what they are doing try to fix it.

Manager, if you are reading this, you have some serious issues to deal with when it comes to your employees, integrity and overall professionalism at your location.  I fully plan on posting this review everywhere I can to warn others against visiting your establishment.

Went here on Sunday afternoon. The food was ok at best. The pork carnitas tacos were very greasy and the fish tacos were not fresh. I didn't care too much for the chips and salsa.  The kale salad was good except they charged me $1 extra for a few slices of avocado.  
The worse part of the meal was the bill.  This place is really expensive for the quality of food.  Not planning to come back.

We needed a quick lunch on a Friday afternoon and stumbled onto this small Vietnamese gem in the FQ. 

Get the steamed bun pork sliders. Delicious!  The pork was grilled perfectly- crispy and dark on the outside and succulent and well flavored. Can't go wrong here. 

Entree- we went with the yellow Curry gulf shrimp. Asked for no rice and double the veggie side- broccoli. They were happy to comply. The sauce had a peanutty goodness that made us want to lick the bowl- but we didn't for the sake of decorum.  Shrimp were large, tender and beautifully flavored. 

We ogled other diners food selections and plotted when we could return here. 

Service is fast and courteous. Decor is crisp and fun in a small remodeled space.

Pei Wei is your Fast Food solution to P.F. Changs, offering mostly the same kinds of dishes. 

I enjoy the food, but like others I feel the prices are perhaps a bit higher than they should be. 

The location is very nice with a reasonably decent amount of parking. You might run into problems in the evening, but for the most part you'll be able to find something. 

For my most recent visit, they kept trying to deliver the wrong food to our table, but we eventually got our food. 

I have no real complaints beyond price, and I'm sure I'll be back.

I had lunch here the other day. Note to self: Control is critical when it comes to the chips here. They are good, and it's easy to fill up on chips alone. 
The tri-tip burrito was great. While it wasn't huge, it was good...which is fine with me...I do not believe quantity is quality.
The server was attentive and prompt. Was it the best Mexican meal on earth? No. Would I eat here again? Yes, and look forward to doing so.

It kind of seems like they have a variety of legitimately otherwise hard to find spices and oils and the like but these are mixed with regular supermarket brands that they've just marked up by quite a bit. Since I get nearly all of my meats from Reading, Jonathan's Best is nice to have. In a pinch you can reasonably expect to find basically any pantry item you might need without having to leave Reading, that in itself can justify the mark-up since it's a time saver. The staff is courteous and laid back, which is great for me because I don't like people hoovering about me while I'm being indecisive. I mean, just because I'm being slow doesn't mean I need help. I personally don't like having people ask me whether I need help finding things and, worse, unsolicited recommendations. Ugh.

Unfortunately I got into an accident shortly after moving to Boise. But fortunately I chose Ray's Auto Body Repair to fix my car. It was a stroke of luck and I will never use anyone else to fix my car should I be unlucky enough to get into another accident. I was more than pleased with how quickly my car was repaired and my car looks like new! Ray was professional yet personable and went above and beyond on a couple of issues. If you are reading this because you are looking for a reputable repair facility, look no further. You've found it.

This is a good store, but I still can't give it a five star review. After recently remodelling, I will admit I was excited to see how this turned out. I love the new modern color scheme and the women's dressing rooms. The food/beverage areas are nicer and the whole store seems more streamlined. The reason I came here was for school supplies for my three children. When I walked in there were signs (hanging pencil trail) leading me to the back of the store, however, right next to the entrance there was also a school section. I thought we should try to get as much as we could in the front because they seemed to have most stuff there. This section did prove to have almost everything but my one complaint is that the notebooks and folders were strewn together like at the Walmart near me. The whole reason I came to Target (which is farther from my house) is so I could avoid the ransacked messy shelves. We ended up getting graph paper composition books instead of the regular kind because they were mixed with the lined ones. We headed to the back of the store and there was a much better selection with way better organisation. We also needed some back to school clothes and those new dressing rooms are beautiful! I remember cringing at all the dirt and hangers on the floor but now I feel like I'm wedding dress shopping. They had a nice selection of clothes that were priced fairly reasonably and I will be coming back with hopefully a five star experience.

Qachbal's Chocolatier is part of downtown Ybor located inside a convience store. I almost walked past it if it weren't for the sign on the sidewalk in the front of the store. 

None of the treats tasted appealing or made me want to take another bite. I was really disappointed because it looks good and appealing to the eyes, but once you take a bite, it transforms the experience into a downhill slope.  

There was nothing special about the Banana Bread. It's your standard banana bread with none of the ingredients jumping out and it was too dry!

The Croissant wasn't moist like traditional ones I've had and missing usual buttery taste. Sadly, I couldn't finish it.

The Guava and Cheese lacked authenticity and wasn't close to what I'm used to. It was just okay.

The Iced Coffee was the only good thing. The flavor of the coffee came through and wasn't  diluted by the milk. It was the best thing we got and it was nice walking outside with a cold drink.

Maybe we should have tried their chocolates, but it puzzles me if that is what they're known for, why provide other treats if they're not at the same caliber? I read good reviews for the chocolate and the pastries detract from what they're good at making. With it being so hot outside, we didn't want to eat chocolate that would be melting so fast.

I can't see them lasting much longer because nothing tasted memorable or made me want to return. I'm sorry to say, but I had high hopes. I wasn't impressed and the best reaction to describe my experience was, "Meh." I've definitely had a lot of better pastries elsewhere. It was unremarkable.

Excellent service and work. I have the outline of a bunch of flowers on my back and unfortunately, the artist that started them is deceased so I went in to have some color/shading etc. filled in and finished. Lester was awesome, did an excellent job, and obviously takes his art and craft very seriously. I highly recommend him. Of course I was nervous but he made me very comfortable and just seemed super chill. The people working there were also super professional, friendly and helpful when I was looking through the artists' books. I love it.
Great spot - the employees were so nice and welcoming.  I had the Cathy Caprese & hubs had the Pete italian, both really awesome. Might have also bought cinnamon bread too.... Really yummy

I had a bad experience with the Biospine Institute. I went there with numbness in my right arm, hand and index finger. When I went for my consultation, Dr. Ronzo assured me that spinal fusion surgery in my neck was the only way I would be able to get rid of the numbness and be able to things I did before the numbness. Not only is the numbness worse but now I have chronic neck pain. I wish I never had the surgery because at least before the surgery my neck didn't hurt. Make sure you research different places and doctors before going ahead with surgery. My surgery was in August and I still have not received a phone from  Biospine to see how I'm doing. I had to call them to make follow up appointments. The day of the surgery is the only time I've seen or heard from Dr. Ronzo. Once they get their money you never hear from the surgeons. My follow up appointments have been with other doctors. Be careful!!!!! It's not about care with Biospine it's all about the money.

I absolutely love this place and take all of my out of town visitors here when they come to Nashville. It's the perfect spot for drinks after dinner or an intimate date night. The drink menu changes with the seasons, so there's always something new to try. Choose the type of liquor that you want, and the drinks with that liquor are grouped together on the menu and sorted by potency. The food menu is actually incredible as well. Drinks are about $10-12 each and worth every penny. The staff are incredibly knowledgable and friendly, so I'd encourage any first time visitor to tell their server what type of drink they usually like and ask for recommendations. 

The only downside, which is completely outweighed by the benefits, is that they don't take reservations and can't really accommodate groups of more than 4-5. Go with your significant other or a small group, and plan on waiting a while if you're there on a Friday or Saturday night - it's definitely worth it!
Good pie. Especially like the three for $14 on Friday nights. Would recommend. A little on the expensive side but it's nice for a treat every now and then

Once again, another awesome experience @ SW. I thought my parents would love it here, and I was right. It was SUPER busy, but it was Saturday at 12, so what else would you expect. I am 100% convinced they were working as fast as they could, we were seated in 20 minutes, and it took a little bit to order and about 35 min to get our food. And the service was awesome, all the servers are always so sweet, it's kinda like going to eat in someone's house.

I have a bad habit of just ordering the same thing over and over, so I tried the breakfast burrito. Crap, so much food! It had everything you could've ever wanted in 1 tortilla - chorizo, home fries, eggs, onions, and was surrounded by green chiles and 2 types of cheese. Was absolutely stuffed, took the other half home and was stuffed for dinner. Of course, my parents loved the cornbread pancakes and their home fries and eggs were great.

Absolute best customer service I've received anywhere hands down. Stephen went out of his way to help us out making numerous calls to make sure we got our difficult to find tire and wheel fixed and back on the road as soon as possible. Would definitely recommend!! Great service and got us the best price around. Highly highly recommended!!

yummy for my tummy. can't beat the double charburger with cheese and bacon, damn good fries, too! My only complaint is the shakes are served in a thin, clear plastic cup and they melt faster than Michael Jackson's face. Go with opaque styrofoam, my milkshake is more important than the dumb old environment!

I really enjoyed this little joint with its exposed brick, twinkling lights and pretty pin-up bartender. A perfect hint of swank with unique handcrafted cocktails to boot.

The patio was reserved for a private party so we bellied up to the bar & were promptly greeted by a friendly bartender. I settled on the bourbon Old Fashion which was delicious while my fella chose Lady On The Lake, a fruity tequila based cocktail complete with a gummy shark garnish. It was a touch sweet for me but perfect for his sweet booze tooth. We ordered the appetizer special Mozzarella Burrata which was a perfectly tasty little snack despite the crostini's being slightly greasy. The drinks were on the pricier side so we decided to carry onto a new Mass Ave destination rather than have a second round.

Would definitely recommend!

This is one of my favorite places for dinner and drinks. Food is made in-house and always incredible. They also brew their own beers. I always recommend this place to all my family and friends. They have a great space downstairs to rent out as well. Go stop in!
Easily the best, fattest oysters I've had here! They were juicy, not gritty and tasted delicious.

Where do I even begin...

Check in for our 4 night stay was smooth... Until we got to our room. We noticed a very peculiar smell in the air. Checked out the air conditioning unit and found copious mold spores which can be found in the picture attached to this review. 

Went to front desk and showed the attendant the mold. Didn't say a word, just gave a blank stare and begrudgingly gave us a different room... Except...

We got to the second room, which looked nice at first... only to lift the lid of the toilet to find some sort of stain from a bodily fluid of sorts. Source: unknown. 

Went back to the front desk and informed the attendant once again of an issue, and we reasonably asked that someone clean it. He responded by telling me that he's never heard someone complain so much in their life. He also proceeded to tell me that he had just placed another couple in our original moldy room and that they had no complaints. Great customer service response right there. 

The only positive note was a very nice security guard named Jesse. He not only went to find supplies and cleaned the toilet, which was not his job, but gave recommendations of restaurants and bars to go to. 

Once we got all that sorted out, it was alright. Cheap cab ride into the city, which was our priority, and the only reason this review receives 2 stars instead of 1. However, looking back, we would definitely pay the extra $$ to stay in the city somewhere with a hotel that does not cut corners on cleanliness and hygiene. We will NOT be staying here again.

Bacchanal Wine is great if you're looking for a low key place to hang out on a nice afternoon. I came here on a Sunday for a few hours with some friends a few weeks back. We were in town around French Quarter Festival, and looking for a chill place with relatively few folks. This place delivered.

We showed up around noon, found that most of the tables in the spacious backyard patio weren't occupied. We ordered a couple of plates of food (the bread + salted butter, pork shoulder were my favorites), and a few bottles of wine from the store inside. The wine prices were great (you're buying the bottles out of the store to drink on the patio).

There's a small stage which was occupied by two low key bands during the three hours I was there.

Staff is super-friendly.

I'd definitely go back if I was in the area.

The place was packed even on a Thursday night but we didn't really have to wait other than circling around for parking. Unfortunately it was still deafening so it was difficult to have a conversation without almost screaming. That said, this review has nothing to do with the wait/noise. The food took a while to get to us but the Gnocchi with Italian Sausage I ordered still came at a reasonable time and was still quite hot. However, for the price point (as well as aforementioned factors), it was good but nothing exceptional.

It's a good bar to be if you want to sit down, enjoy the music and drinks and the good vibes. The bartenders are helpful and kind, and they have gender inclusive bathrooms which are hard to come by in Nashville. The rooftop bar here is definitely a hidden gem. We would definitely come back here again if we're pop ever back in Nashville!

I went here about 2 weeks ago with a friend for the first time. The staff is really nice and on point. The waiter we had was attentive but with the right dose to not be annoying. The food was made pretty fast and it was so so so good. I felt that the food was slightly over priced. When I got the food I didn't care because it was made to perfection. I ordered The Chicken Tostada for an app. to share. It was fresh and refreshing. I wish I had ordered my own. For dinner, I ordered the fish and chips. They cost more then other restaurants ( Mckenzie Brew House's) in the area but it was just as good and fresh. Whoever makes the food at Black Powder cares about what they are doing. At least that was my experience.
Fun clean atmosphere! Tara at the front desk was very helpful. Good food and drinks offered right on property! Close to Busch Gardens!!

UPDATE:  Customer service sent me a new cake,no better, no worse. I quit.

So very disappointed. After spending $54 on a SINGLE king cake($25 for shipping), my cake arrived smooshed to one side, folded over, will all the filling on that one side. I've had these cakes in Louisiana before, so I know they're good, but to spend that much on shipping for a ball of cake is ridiculous. I have emailed customer service to see if they will rectify this situation, but this is not their first bad review on any platform. Pics of my stupid cake for reference.

They have taken a former dump of a hotel (in the old Sears building) and made it fairly nice and a good value, just a block from the Quarter.  One odd thing if its important to you, very few of the rooms have windows that face the outside, most face the interior of the building. Ultra comfy beds, coffee maker, iron, hair dryers in room.  Attached coffee shop is open from early to late, PJs coffee in lobby.  Valet or self-park is kinda pricey, you can do better nearby.  Buying multiple nights on travel websites can produce real bargains for this close in location. (Safe neighborhood, too).

Pros: cheesesteaks served up without racism (I'm looking at you, Geno's). The condiments are great-- hot pepper bar is great. Got my order in about 30 seconds.  

Cons: Premade and cold, which is why I got my order in 30 seconds. The cheese on my provolone with was not even close to being melty because the whole thing was cold. All this for $9.50? Fuck that.  

If you're looking for a cheesteak don't go to a tourist trap. Little local pizza places are invariably better, though I will say that Jim's on South and 4th is quite good, fresh to order as you watch.
Great experience trying my very first canolli ever! They have their very own parking lot for customers which was a plus!

Finally tried this place out!

The concept is simple: a bowl or a pita! So many options to customize makes it a great place to bring everyone. The first time we went the employees were patient with us newbies and let us try sauces and toppings. I got a shawarma bowl with rice and the bf got a steak pita. 

We got different toppings but my favorites are the sumac onions and the Amba (spelling?) sauce! 

The bf "naffed it up" = got a drink and naf naf fries. They are not normal fries, some are crispy like chips and some are super soft and oily...not my fav and I wouldn't order them again. 

After our first visit I had a craving and ordered us dinner to pick up. The order system online is very strange and somewhat difficult to deal with- not sure I'll use it again, but my order was ready on time and correct! 

My one beef with this place is the inconsistency in amount/quality of meat. One time I had nice juicy shawarma the other it was many small burnt pieces with barely any "meat". There was so little I forgot it even had meat. 

Other than that- the pita bread, rice, and toppings are delicious! I'll be back but hope for more consistent quality!
My favorite day-date spot with the girl...the owner treats everyone like family and will sample you anything they are brewing up. Never disappointed!

Best steak in Indy and great service too.   

Valet is $10. Unless you park on the street you'll pay this in a garage. Use the valet.
The fastest service I've ever received for a new drivers license, I was out of there in 20min flat. Great staff friendly and fast!

I was impressed with the food....it doesn't remind you of a fast food joint but actually is...I ordered the half chicken with yellow rice and mashed potatoes with gravy and black beans...the chicken has a nice flavor to it! They have a table laid out for customers to get their share of onions, salsa, bar-b-que sauce, a cilantro garlic sauce (its the bomb!!!) and a spicy pollo sauce...the sweet tea is just perfect. I would definitely eat here again!!

First ever yelp review bc this place is that good. 
I truly appreciated the social distancing measures they had in place. Service was great and the food was even better.

I was so excited to see that I got 10% off my total just from checking-in at Oceana Grill so I HAD to give them a review.

It's hard to find decent food on bourbon street, so after failing miserably at a previous hole in the wall, this place definitely made up for it.

Oceana Grill was packed, but we didn't have a problem finding a table for three. I ordered the taste of New Orleans and it was great because it came with red beans and rice, jambalaya and crawfish étouffée. 

Good food, music and ambiance.
The food is always so fresh here. Great combinations of ingredients. Love the atmosphere as well

Nice little spot. 
Good beer. 
Reasonable prices.
No food but Sugarfire next door will deliver.
We had lots of fun! There's A wide variety of games plus yummy menu and wine, why ask for more!

Smells a bit funky here. This place seems to be willing to take any type of donation. I saw undergarments and a lot of shoes. 

With that being said, the prices are alright, and I found a Sol Alpaca sweater nwt! I thought it would be $5 since the signs said sweaters were that price but come find out orange tags are 15$. Either way it was a pretty decent find! This sweater seems to only ship from Peru and typically retails 120+ dollars. 

Prices are cheaper than goodwill but there's also more worn down super loved items here. Some of them aren't worth the 50 cents of 2$

First timer this past weekend and I'm wondering WHY I've never heard of this place. I'm addicted! They have about 5 different things to choose from with 5 different "fillings". I got a chicken quesadilla while my husband got the Ahi Tuna taco. DELICIOUS and QUICK! Small little taqueria with street style tacos. I suggest getting more than one taco as they're small and hubby ended up ordering two more. The Margaritas are amazing and the service is EXCELLENT! They also have a few beers to choose from. I'm so excited to have found such a fun little place. We sat outside where they only have 3-4 picnic tables and a window to order and pick up your food from. If you're a flan person, GET IT! Best flan in all of Edwardsville, promise! All in all we will definitely be back!

2nd Story is walking distance to a lot of great sights in Philadelphia, and it is everything I am looking for in a brewpub: great beer brewed on premises, a cool and interesting atmosphere, and a hearty food menu. My girlfriend and I both really liked our beers, the wit foe her and I opted for an American IPA. 

Though we only stopped in for a quick pint, we almost changed our dinner plans to eat here instead after we saw the generous portions, like the fish and chips, that the kitchen was serving up. We will have to come back with fierce appetites next time. 

The atmosphere is classy yet comfortably casual. I think I would describe it as rugged wood construction throughout the dining room and bar. I like the windows above that give a view of the brewing operations upstairs. I will definitely return.
So much fun for a cold Saturday morning! Looking forward to this years Crawl and maybe even visiting other cities for it as well!

Hooters as a rule isn't very good. This Hooters experience took it to a whole new level of bad. Service was horrible from the time we walked through the door. Waited five minutes before anyone spoke to us. Table service was atrocious and slow. Food was the same as it ever is at Hooters. The place had 20 people and most were at the bar. The bathrooms were filthy. I guess we need to be reminded from time to time while we don't frequent Hooters.

This company  is a fraud. They came to my home and said that they did a repair that was not done.  My machine is under warranty and now they are billing me for the service. They have no people skills. They are a  dishonest company.  As a stay at home mother I will be sure to let all of my friends and family in my various groups and organizations know how dishonest this company is.  Please save yourself the stress and don't use this company.

Food was great, seating was a mess. I showed up with three dogs and my parents and the patio/beer garden was packed. All the signs when you enter from the Monon side say to see the host for seating. I fought my way through the long line blocking the walkway to the host inside. He told me the beer garden and patio outside and upstairs was open seating. I wish the signs could have told me that. Maybe they did, but they weren't in a viable place. I found a table on the patio and sat there for about 10 minutes when a server came by and asked me if I had an order in. I told her I was waiting for my mom to fight her way back outside to join us. She then told us the table might be reserved. I argued that there was no sign and that we were told it was open seating in the beer garden, to which she then informed me that open seating was past the covered arrea in the "beer garden". There are no signs to differentiate and we were told by two different people that we could just grab a table. In the end, they let us stay, but the whole situation left me feeling a bit overwhelmed and unwelcome. 

The place was very crowded and hard to get in and out to get drinks, napkins, go to the bathroom, etc., so if you hate a crowd, don't come here, but if you love a busy, happening place, then this is the place for you. The beer garden had a party like atmosphere, and was both kid and pet friendly. They never offered me a dog bowl for water as most pet friendly places do, but you can go and grab a plastic cup and get the water yourself. Before we left, another server came by and offered us dog treats, which they make there themselves. The dogs loved them.

The bbq was really delicious. The servings, especially for the sides, were generous. The beer, of course, was great, and you can get it in the half liter or liter size. It's a wonderful place for large groups, and I think it'll be fun for family friendly dining all throughout the summer. 

I probably won't frequent this place when it's busy like that again. (It WAS a holiday weekend.) I hate a long line and I don't really look for a place where I can drink where children are running around, but I can see the appeal. Despite the large crowd, service was fast, efficient and friendly.

I understand that legally, staff does not need to wear masks, but they also do not need to be vaccinated.  If an immune-compromised long-time client, who has been coming to this restaurant for every happy occasion for more than a decade asks the young unvaccinated waitress if she will please wear a mask at the table just as she takes and delivers our order, that would have been a kindness on her part.  She refused, and the manager dismissed my request, as if our patronage was of no interest to him. I will Mystic Fish.

I was there at a normal afternoon appointment, to get laser hair removal. What a nightmare. Words can not explain my tragedy. She gave my face second degree burns and I spent 6 hours in the ER, also had to follow up with a dermatologist. All out of pocket by the way. I did not get so much as an apology, reimbursement on the $5,000 I paid as well. Don't ever go there, unless you want someone to destroy your face without so much as an apology. I've been depressed, can't wear makeup and have PTSD. I cannot even afford a psychologist. This has taken a big toll on my life. Please save yourself and don't ever go here!!!!!

This place is amazing! First time I came here was a year ago, had the best oysters in New Orleans. One year later came back again, still the best oysters in New Orleans. I've tried them at every place I ate, nothing even compares with this place. But what truly made my experience amazing was the bartender Sam. This man should get at least a raise if not an award of some kind! He joked around with us, made us feel very welcomed! Honestly the best experience ever! I've traveled a lot around the country and have never met such an amazing bartender. He's the reason I will be coming back every time! Word of advice, if you can go sit at the bar and ask for Sam, I promise you will not regret it! And order oysters and Crawish Queso Blaco. It's truly amazing!

I have been a D&Q customer since my father first started me skiing around 8 or 9 years old. I have watched their business grow and expand and change. One thing that has always remained constant at D&Q is the quality of their service and their knowledgeable staff. I mostly use D&Q for bicycle purchases now as I no longer ski, and their selection of cycling equipment is second to none in the area. They also of course have a great selection of ski equipment and clothing and also swimwear in the summer. D&Q runs several great sales and also has swap meets for used equipment on both skis and bikes. Everyone there is always pleasant and they go above and beyond.

Incredibly grateful to have an organization like this helping otherwise helpless animals.  Please visit, donate or, at minimum, sign petitions supporting state-level laws to ban ownership of wildlife.  The cats you see here have all got sad tales of being abandoned and deserve to have as good a quality life as Big Cats Rescue can provide.  They are compassionate in caring for the animals and in educating us on why Big Cats SHOULD NOT be pets.

I am updating this review based on feedback and returned phone call from the owner.  A few days after writing this, I get an email saying the owner replied to my review and to call her directly.  I called and left a message and within about 15 minutes, I got a call back.  The fact that the owner cares enough to respond one's review and a customer's concern  warrants a reply to this review and an overall change in opinion.  I was allowed to express my many issues with the admin problems I had over the past several years and she explained what went wrong and what Spavia is doing to try to prevent things of that nature from happening in the future.  Regarding my issue with the cancellation, I guess there was some miscommunication, but the owner did correct this.  I was given names of additional therapists to try, regarding my needs, so hopefully this will cut down on my wait time in booking appointments.  As with any business in south Reno, and the growing population, they will probably continue to experience some issues as any growing business would, but I am glad the owner took the initiative to make sure the client was happy in the end.  Sometimes it is rare in the service industry.
This place was mediocre, but after I had complained I was very impressed by the quality customer service.

I absolutely love China House. It is just a few blocks from my office, so my co-workers and I order it quite frequently. Yes, it is a true "hole in the wall" kind of place, but the food is excellent. The egg rolls are always crispy and delicious. I usually order the Curry Chicken or Chicken Fried Rice - both are awesome. You should definitely check this place out if you're looking for a new favorite Chinese food place.

Wasn't an upscale steak n shake! Small bun even smaller burger. No flavor.  Needed to be on a smaller plate so it didn't look so sad.  $6 with no side.  Place was really really loud inside and we were sitting outside.  Service was average at best.  Have heard there are lines get long.  Burger is certainly not worth waiting for.  Maybe breakfast?

PROS: Reasonable prices for delicious, authentic Mexican food.

CONS: Way too much plastic and Styrofoam here -- WAY TOO MUCH. Oh, and cash only. (Just be prepared.)

Five stars, but with a few reservations.

My review is only about the grooming experience I had with my dogs. I am new to the state of Florida. I had just rescued my second dog a few days early and brought him here along with my other dog. After I gave my dogs to the groomer and started to walk out she let one of them go and he almost ran out of the door so I grabbed him and brought him back. When I tried to hand him to her she said "oh I can't hold the dogs." How do you possibly pick them up and put them on the grooming table then? Then, I was backing my car out and I see that she had let him go again so I put my car in park and began to run after him when I saw a police man grabbed him. Finally, she brought him back in and closed the door so he couldn't escape again (he weighs 9.4 pounds so it's not like his strength made her drop the leash) so I thought ok I will leave them there but will call to check up. I called eight times in the two hours they were there and no one would answer. I finally called for the ninth time, thinking if they don't answer this time I will just go there and see what is going on, she answered and I said how is Kronnie doing. Her reply was "who"? (She didn't even know my dogs name) I said, "the small black dog you are grooming. The one who ran in the street twice. Is he fine or should I come get him"? She said, "oh. They are almost done you can just come get them in ten minutes. When I get there I see my girl dog, Eiffie, who I had asked to be down to a 1/4 of an inch barely looked like her hair had been cut and when I asked if their teeth were brushed she said no. I explained that I had asked for that when I made the appointment. In a tone that seemed like she was incredibly annoyed she said, "you have to ask for that when you drop them off but fine I'll go do it now." So she went back and it took like two to three minutes tops, so obviously she barely did anything to their teeth. I got the teeth brushing free but did not get an apology for all the terrible things that happened. When I told my grandma and mom they said they had never heard of anything so awful. I have these two dogs and a cat and am very protective of them.
