I had eiffie for over a year and a half and took her to get groomed every four to five weeks before this horrible experience when I was trying to find a place in the new city I lived in and with a new puppy I adopted. Please whatever you do, do not take your babies here.

My boyfriend and I come in regularly. We love the service, Jenna is our favorite bartender. She treats us like royalty, and is very detail oriented. The Avocado Turkey Burger is my favorite item on the menu. Everytime I come in I order it and it's cooked to perfection. This location in particular is very careful to make each order right. I'm appreciative of all the hard work they put in here, from the front of house to the line. 10/10 would recommend!

Professional craftologists are the bar. The staff here has somehow been transplanted from the city into New Hope. That said, everyone knows their menu and is friendly and courteous and doesn't make you feel like they are doing you a favor by serving you...which is pretty much par for the course at most NH spots anymore. Let's hope Stella attracts more of the same and forces everyone else to elevate their game. Thanks you Chef Graces!!
Great hair stylists and make artists!

The worst place ever. I bought a Groupon and they didn't accept it, they said that Saturdays they have "Glow", which I don't care, and it was not written anywhere in Groupon voucher. I just wanted my kids to spend 1 hour because it was already 8PM. They charged me $75. I'll try cancel the Groupon because I'm not coming back here, ever.

Wanted to like it more. 

The food was good, but nothing was stand out 'great', which I think you need if folks are hitting $100+ a head. 

Really enjoyed the salmon entree and the shaved pork 'snack' course. The white chocolate dessert though was very bland. 

Service was a little rushed - we got through 4 courses in around an hour, and by the end (9:15pm on a Saturday) were the only people in the restaurant and felt a little under pressure to finish our wine. 

Overall, a lovely space with great potential but not quite executing.

Gave it five stars because the food is genuine Italian and good, but mostly because it is such a great Mom and Pop restaurant.  And, Grandma is actually the cook.  Fresh made pasta, and you can hear them pound the veal tender for the salad that comes with your meal.  They are all charming.  Only drawback is the difficulty of locating it.  It is tucked in behind a shopping center, but worth the search if you want an authentic, cute place to eat.

Oku is great. Oku has the views, the central locale and the food to boast. I mean its the complete package and then some. There was, however, one thing that puzzled us. This is the only restaurant i have been to that refuses to tell you the wait time lol it was almost hilarious because person after person in waiting (its really popular) came who were walk-ins and the hostess said as politely as she could that they would be able to seat them but couldnt tell the time lol anywho after bugging the hostess twice, we did get a pretty sweet spot on the roof-top so i guess they wont make you wait all night with that mysterious response.
We ordered a bunch of stuff and everything was licked clean because duhh, delicious! We got the garlic spicy edamame, shinobi roll, shrimp and crab handrolled gyoza and the XO shrimp on garlic noodles *slurp slurp yumm* ughhh i wish i could fly back to eat some more of that delicious food and look dreamily at the beautiful Santa Barbara boardwalk.
The food here is delicious and flavorful. Kind and attentive staff. I have been craving their chicken and green sauce all week.  I'm so excited I found this gem!

Came here for beer and was blown away by the food. The wings were meaty and spiced just right and the ribs with wild devil bbq were phenomenal. They offer free brewey tours which are really cool if your into that stuff. Has something for everyone, would definitely recommend.

Beautiful building. This is the 3rd restaurant I've been to in the new LBMC Center that has a beautiful setting with food that costs twice as much as the same food elsewhere. The food was just ok. Not nearly as good as Hanabi a mile away. It had the virtue of being open at lunch on Sunday. I  really enjoyed being there. Great sake selection for another visit. Just know that better food is available cheaper anywhere else. This place has 2 tacos for $14 and $18 sushi rolls. Rent must be very high.

2 stars for food, 4 stars for service/atmosphere

Showed up before 6 on a Wednesday and there was a half hour wait - kinda surprised but when we were seated we realized why - Wed is all you can eat crab legs and it was packed with tourists. 

I tried the Beef & Guinness pie - gravy had a very sweet taste that none at my table liked and round steak was tough - would not recommend. Fries (aka as chips) were regular old fries, mixed veg were frozen - meh. BF ordered the Corned Beef and Cabbage - corned beef was thinly sliced sandwich meat variety and cabbage/potatoes/carrots were very bland (no salt/butter) - would also not recommend this. Others in our party ordered Bangers and Mash - sausages were very small but good and thankfully Guinness gravy did not taste like the gravy in my entree. Curry Chicken was not at all like the awesome Indian food in the UK, tolerable but would not recommend. The onion rings had a great beer batter and the fried Oreo dessert was awesome too.

Server was very friendly. Atmosphere is casual and warm, cute 2 man 'band' playing oldies - very much appreciated by the crowd.

While we didn't really like our entrees we'd give Cricketers another shot and probably order burgers or sandwiches.

Great fresh food! The falafel was great and the hummus was sooooo yummi! Don't miss out on tbe eggplant as well!

I took the platter which could easily suffice 2 ppl so either share or just take the small sandwich. 

The platter comes with 8 falafel balls, i had hummus, a bit tahini and chopped tomato and cucumber. Really recommended.

STOP LOOKING. BEST IN TAMPA BAY! I had my full length, three-layered (silk, full sequence embellishment, and tulle!!) bridesmaid dress altered by Daniela finished within a week!  In a time crunch and was worried finding someone who could not only alter it in time, but also not over quote me. She was by far my lowest quote too if I couldn't recommend her enough. Thank you, Daniela!!

I am not a fan! 

I have tried Chuy's three different times at three different locations. The star of the show is the jalapeño ranch dip they give you.

All the other food is is really sub par. Nothing I've tried is worth coming back for. 

When I go out to eat I like ordering things I can't make at home or that are time consuming. The things I've tried here I could most definitely make at home.

Skip Chuy's and try a real deal taqueria.

Normally I wouldn't ever give a 1 star review but the service I received tonight is absolutely piss poor. I ordered to go and called in my order at 6:30pm. I was told it would be ready for 7pm. I arrived at 7pm and parked in the designated space for curbside to go. I waited in my car until 7:20 and decided to go inside. After flagging down a staff member, I had to repeat my entire order only to learn it hadn't even been put in the computer. The staff member quickly snapped back at me and replied, "How should I know? I just got here!". I waited until 7:30 and was told the order was being "put up", whatever that means. After a 45 minute wait my food was finally delivered to me. I returned home, only living directly across the street (less than a 2 minute drive) to discover my food was ice cold. There was zero acknowledgement from any member of their staff and zero apology for the atrocious service. It's a good thing the Copeland's restaurants are slowly shutting down. This one needs to go too.

My family had gone to Oteri's bakery for a long time. Their strawberry shortcake was always our  favorite and go to cake. We stopped going after a while because the bakery just didn't interest us anymore and we needed something better.
After the revamping of the bakery my sister would go in and buy pastries all the time and tell me that it was a much better bakery. So when my nieces first birthday came around I thought that is where I should go and get her cake made. 
This decision turned out to be the biggest mistake I could have ever made in giving them a big chance like this. I went in and expected to speak to a cake decorator who specialized in cakes, but what I got was a counter person who seemed to have no idea how to hold a cake consultation. I told her every detail I wanted and even drew a picture of what I wanted to try and help her out. What I got was nothing what I wanted. To make matters worse they spelled my nieces name wrong! After me even spelling it out for her and writing it on the drawing they had the nerve to spell her name wrong. On top of that there was no delivery option so I had to hire people to transfer the cake and when I saw it, it was a broken crown on top, the colors were off, her name, and when I called to see if someone could over and fix it the wanted me to take the cake back. The cake was well over 300 dollars and not worth it at all. Never will I have a specialty cake made there because it seems as though they can't handle big projects.

- Cup Cake NAZIs - 

So, I order $50 of food for delivery. (2nd time this week)  Cup cakes are smashed. (didn't bring 1 other item) I call up and let them know. I ask nicely if I may have some unsmashed cup cakes. I'm told "that's normal" and it should be expected with delivery. Eventually they relent and send over some new cup cakes. Delivery driver comes to the door and gives me the new cup cakes (which are also smashed I find out later) She asks for the old cup cakes back?! I was nice and gave the old ones back. Now right there I should have said no. I was nice. Pissed but nice.. You smash the 1st set of cup cake goodness up then bring me more and have the nerve to ask for the old ones back? Do the cup cakes hold a high street value I don't know about? Maybe I could've EBAYed the old smashed cup cakes for $50 to cover the bill. Does Blackbird Pizza have a cup cake numbering system where all cup cakes need to be accounted for? I'm not sure what business model they are implementing when they ask for $5 in cup cakes back from the customer after they forgot 1 item in the order and smashed the cup cakes to begin with. I do not blame the delivery driver. My issue is with management. Maybe I hold their delicious, delectable, moist, fluffy, crack-like cup cake goodness with a godlike status. Maybe I should not salivate like a starving dog dreaming about the day I can taste their wonderful cup cakes. Is that wrong of me? Is it wrong to expect unmolested cup cakes? If you go through the trouble to create a masterpiece do not destroy it on the way to the museum!

As a Vegan who travels all over the country I can say their food is great. Their Vegan pizza is the best I've ever had. Mello Mushroom is a joke compared to Blackbird. 

Vegan or not you need to check this place out!

Great staff.  The Produce is not always the best in town but I can count on they 100% for all my other needs.  Personally think they have the best wine selections and goat cheese.  The family just loves this place.

Most importantly....my dog loved this place. He had a great time, wasn't stressed out, played great with the other dogs being groomed....and the haircut looked great. Will definitely be making another appointment soon

Don't think I'll be back here. We were lured in by $10 lunch specials, and were swindled into an $18 single meal. 

To start: food wise, the coconut shrimp was mostly just fried shrimp with next to zero coconut flavoring. Sushi roll was just okay...I've gotten better spicy tuna's for $3-4 less. 

What really left a bad taste in my mouth was the service. From one server to another: it is completely unethical for wait staff to nonchalantly offer different sides that are not included in the price without mentioning an upcharge. Granted, it makes sense that lobster mac & cheese would have been a few bucks extra...but would we have signed up for it knowing it would tack an extra $8 to our bill? Absolutely not. Though, I should have known after inquiring about the sushi and the server reporting that his favorite rolls were the two most expensive on the menu...talk about transparent. 

I digress. The correct and honest way to go about upcharging a table would go something like "the lobster mac is going to be an upcharge, is that okay?" It was simply a tacky move. I will say the lobster in the mac & cheese was delicious...and it better be for an itty bitty $8 side. 

Sneaky upcharging aside, the service was just plain slow from the get-go. Not surprised that it was nearly empty around prime lunch time. At an $18 lunch meal, I could have hopped across the sidewalk to Rocco's Tacos or Brio for a full on meal with better service. We'll know better for next time!

This place it top notch!  I have tried many corned beef sandwichs in my days and this is as good as any in NYC! Shared the gian special soup featured on food network.  This might be the best soup we have ever had.  It is giant but serves 4 people.  Theyt did wrap the rest to go for us.  It's bow ties, rice, chicken, carrots, fried onion and this fantastic delightful dumplings with beef brisket inside.  We shared that and shared the corned beef on rye with swiss cheese.   I wish I lived a little closer to this gem but it's closer than NYC or Harold's in Jersey.   The coookie everyone raved about was good but not as good as everything else.  My only regret is not saving room for the coconut cake.  Next time will get the beef brisket sandwich and the cake and get the soup to take home! Also the service was great too.

Good food, friendly service, clean, good portion size for price.  My husband is a lasagna fanatic and he enjoyed their lasagna. I had the eggplant parmesan and it was good.  This place was good but we also recommend maggianos in Tampa if your over on that side of the Howard Frankland bridge.
Their food is outstanding. Our relatives in Quakertown introduced us to this restaurant and we have been back a few times and have a positive experience each time. Their helping are very generous!

I'm only giving 1 star because its the lowest rate available if it were for me I would give a -1 star. 
The waitress was rude. I asked for avocado dishes recommendations, and she was already annoyed by the fact I've asked. She grab away the menu from me and point the veggie omelet. I order that one and a vegetarian sandwich. The result was a disaster: the veggie omelete was a pancake with no taste at all topped with argula salad nothing like in the picture which was covered with black beans. 
The veggie sandwich was basicallt a block of feta cheese super dry.

Water without ice, only one bathroom, etc. Only the hostess was nice.

4 stars?  Ah hell nah.  Drinks are overpriced, even for the neighborhood. True, you get your alcohol in your cocktails, but at what cost my friends?  At what cost? And the food, Food?  Eh.

The only thing this place is good for is shuffleboard, but there's always someone playing or trying to get you to stop playing.  The patio is alright I guess, but every bar in a two block radius has a patio so I'm not sure why this is better than the others.

The only reason this doesn't get one star is because if you're looking to get laid by a very generic looking "pretty" girl, or a unmemorable looking West Nashville dude with long hair or a nicely gelled abercrombie cut, this will serve thine purposes perfectly.  But for all the meat displayed here, I've yet to see many pickups.  Mostly people come here from somewhere else with their friends.  And don't think I have anything against fratboys either; I gave the Frayed Knot a better review.

Also, parking isn't nice, but I don't care much about that either.

I don't really understand these good reviews. This place is just so-so. The dining area is dirty, service is slow and food was nothing special. In all fairness, I've only been here once. I'll try again someday and possibly update the review.

3.75 for a  Kenzinger? Wow, Dirty Frank's you have won over my cheap, spenthrift heart of mine?  $5 for two shots of your special? I am SOLD!

Maybe the service was a little slow, but it seemed like a cool place to hang out on Saturday night for cheap drinks.  I definitely plan to hit it up the next time I am there.

A typical asian buffet with a sushi bar and grill.  Looks like there is alot of food, but not really.  They have a noodle station which was good.  That's the only thing I though was good about this place.  Everything was over salted!!  They use more salt then Penndot uses to salt the streets of Philly!

Happy Eatings!!
Real good duck and chinese broccoli. All their is good and well known locally. Don't let the modest strip mall location fool you.

Overall good pizza place, I would definitely recommend the 5 dollar slice deal; although you must pay extra for toppings. 7.50 for a Specialty slice, good Vegetarian options, as well as a meat lovers, good variety that makes everyone happy. Staff is nice, friendly, sometimes a little too fast paced when checking out

Little hole in the wall place on the boardwalk. Had surprisingly good food. I had the fish and chips which included some very fresh grouper. Highly recommended to eat here and then stroll about the other small shops in the boardwalk.

HORRIBLE HORRIBLE PLACE. DO NOT GO TO THIS UNETHICAL PLACE. I am vegetarian and i went here with 5 friends. Our food arrived and the server gave me my dish saying it was my vegetarian one. It ended up being LAMB meat and I accidentally ate it. She apologized later but thats such a huge mess up!! She didnt care to offer any discount or make up for it, but in stead she charged us gratuity without even mentioning it on the bill!!! By the time we realized it, we were in a rush to leave for class. The food came SO late that we had to take it to-go and eat later. Im surprised places like these are still in business.

I have to admit I didn't expect this transaction to go this smoothly. Ethan was very nice and did a good job answering all of our questions and explaining how the process works. The whole process took about 30-40 mins, signed a few papers, inspected the car, turned over title and registration. He explained the deductions from the cars value. Printed a check for me and I was on my way! I would definitely suggest we buy any car to friends and family! An did I say it was more than I expected .. yup!!

The saddest part of my review is that I will most likely never get the chance to eat here again. This place is the real deal! We ate at the bar since we didn't have any reservations but that didn't take away from the quality.  

To start, the popovers were fantastic.  
Filet was amaze balls!  Perfectly cooked medium with a nice crust. 
The Creme Corn Brûlée is a must and oh I wish I could have some now.

Very nicely decorated on purpose... they want you to know you're going to spend some money. The food and service are good but not great for the price. I don't mind spending money but take a place like Burns... it has next level steaks and almost perfect service at about the same price range. I expected far more.

We love Caddy's!  We have been several times and the food and atmosphere is the best on the beach.  Although we have had multiple servers, Amy by far is our fave!  She takes her time, gives us a lot of great suggestions and was the ONLY server to tell you about the REWARDS program after we went over seven times!  So do yourself a favor, make a reservation and request outdoor seating and ask for Amy.

We took the kids here and they loved it, we loved it, lots of fun. My kids loved the indoor playground, they had so much fun! We played a ton of the indoor games, we drove go carts, we played baseball. We all had a blast!!!

I've heard the phrase "When in Rome..." so we tried Philly steak sandwiches!
 Once again, Yelp never disappoints and pointed me to this place. Long story short, it was the best steak sandwich I have had. Would DEFINITELY recommend this place!

Clients keep giving me gift cards to Ruth Chris, and I keep accepting them!  I have received great food and great service across the board!  By this point, I've probably tried almost everything on the menu, and I have to say, I haven't gotten ahold of anything I didn't thoroughly enjoy!  I think it's weird that they have to bring everything out on plates that are like 1,000 degrees, but in the whole scheme of things, that is really no big deal.  I definitely recommend Ruth's Chris for a special occasion or business dinner.  I love the filet and potatoes au gratin!

Yummy! My first time trying the Thai ice cream roll. I wasn't sure what flavor to choose or what toppings to add, the employee there were so niceee and patient with me. I end up asking one of them to choose everything for me...and  I LOVE IT!! I would def go back and try the other flavors.
Great customer service, very knowledgeable staff. It was a refreshing spa day for my daughter & I.

Arrived for lunch during a very busy time, but were seated right away and received great service. Food was very good and prices very reasonable for the portion sizes. If you don't try the queso, you're missing out!

The food was very good, and the value for the amount of food we got was excellent, we got a bowl of hot and sour soup that could have fed all of us easy for 5.95.  The main dishes were also very good.  The only down side to this visit is the waitress could have been a bit more attentive to our table

This is the worst. The staff is oblivious, the floor is so slippery with grease that I have to use caution with every step. Dirty, dirty and might I add dirty. A staff member was laying down on the bench where customers would sit. Poorly managed was obvious. If this was my only time there I might forgive this poor example of a fast food establishment but...every time I have had the same experience. So...I will travel to other locations rather than experience this McDonalds. Ray Kroc would be ashamed.

There are far fewer options for good tasting delivery in the area, especially compared to what you can get across the bridge. 

With that being said, Dominicks is generally one of the better places to order from. I'm not from around here, so I don't have the "old owners were better" bias. I wouldn't know. 

The menu here is stereotypical of a pizza place - Pizza, Salads, Calzone, Cheesesteaks, wings etc. I don't like pizza so I'll talk about some of the rest: 

The salads are full sized and the lettuce is never wilted or warm. On a few occasions the chicken was "muscle-y" which a total turn off for me. I order the ceasar with extra olives and onions and haven't had complaints about that. The cheesesteaks were fine with extra peppers and onions and mozzarella - I refuse to consume cheese whiz. The wings always seemed under cooked to me but I order them fried extra hard and they always get that request right. 

It's better than mediocre, and probably not exceptional and Originals, the spot in the complex,  doesn't deliver. Dominoes across the street and Pizza Hut don't carry anything worth consuming - if that helps give you a feel for my discernment. 

Delivery from Dominicks is always super pleasant and they're always within the 30-45 minute window. 

I would recommend.

In November 2009,  I first ate brunch at Valanni with my best friends after a life altering moment and was comforted by the atmosphere, company, and most of all, the aromatic coffee served from a French press and the scrumptious brunch. I continued to visit Valanni a few time afterwards, to enjoy their coconut mojitos, a sample some of their dinner menu items.
 This morning a group of friends and I decided to go back to Valanni, craving their coffee served in a French press and their unique brunch items. From the moment we stepped into the restaurant, things went downhill. Since today is an unusually warm day in the city of Philadelphia, the restaurant was hot and stuffy. We were seated in the back, right next to the swinging kitchen doors. We eventually got settled and was greeted by our pleasant server. Two coffees, one tea, one iced tea, and one orange juice were ordered. I was highly disappointed when the server appeared with two cups of coffee with no French press in sight.  We asked the server what happened to the French press that used to be placed onto the table along with the coffee cups. We eventually found out that the restaurant had issues with patrons breaking the mechanism so there were not enough to go around.. Free refills were presented to us, after informing the server that our cups were empty. On the third refill, I was presented with tepid coffee that was not brewed properly.
A friend of mine waited about five minutes for a tea box to be presented to her...that didn't contain breakfast tea! When asked for more hot tea, the server informed us that she would be charged another $3 for a tea bag. NOTE: Bring your own tea bags next time. Our friend, who ordered an iced tea, was presented with a $5 bottle of iced tea, not even freshly brewed. The Orange juice was less then stellar, which also came with a $5 price tag.  The glass was filled with ice with a splash of orange juice barely covering the juice.
After waiting about 20 minutes for our food to arrive, and wondering if the kitchen staff had ventured out back to slaughter the cow to prepare the filet someone ordered, we received our food. The Huevos Rancheros was enjoyable, as was the filet, and the truffled provolone omelet. The cinnamon bun French toast was the topic of conversation at the table- and not in a good way. Two of my friends had ordered this "popular item" and was presented with two raw cinnamon buns topped with orange glaze and currants, swimming in a pool of syrup. If one did not have a cavity before, they would after consuming this monstrosity. The description of the cinnamon bun French toast was deceptive.  One of the plates were sent back, but not before the server telling us that we would need to talk to the manager first to see what she would say. Gordon Ramsay would've thrown a fit if he heard this! The customers' plate would be swept off the table with a flourish and immediately presented with a menu asking them to choose another item. The other plate of cinnamon French toast sat dejectedly on the table, untouched while another item was ordered.
After asking for salt and pepper, I was presented with a dusty pepper mill and a tablespoon of salt in a creamer container. I was thoroughly confused. I think that Kitchen Kapers sells mini salt and pepper shakers for about $12 for a set of 6. Maybe Valanni would consider invested in some of those?
 The bill was presented to us with 20% gratuity added for a less than average experience at Valanni. I am sad to say that I will not step foot inside this restaurant again. We dappled with idea that maybe after our brunch at La Croix two weeks ago, we didn't give Valanni a fair trial.  After careful consideration, we decided that this wasn't the case. Devil's Alley, Mixto, and  Marathon would be other choices for brunch in the weeks to follow. Atleast they don't try to cover their costs for their new décor by serving tepid coffee, charging $3 for a tea bag and $5 for bottled iced tea and watered down store bought orange juice.
 I think the highlight of brunch was the company and the upbeat music! And I think it's safe to say that Gordon Ramsay would NOT be pleased with Valanni.

This store has some nice stuff, pretty well priced considering.  I'd equate it to sales prices on new stuff, sometimes clearance prices (depending on the brand).  

What surprised me is the amount of stuff that was new, bought by goodwill to sell here.  It looked nice, but I was not paying that price (too high) for what appears to be seconds on a lot of the items.

Cute try-on area.

The food is average, but the customer service is really bad. We asked for pineapple curry ($15) with vegetables and tofu. But it turned out to be tofu curry instead with lots of tofu and barely any vegetables! When we asked our server he said that's how it is (we have eaten here before and knew that he was not being honest). Instead of offering to add vegetables or being apologetic he just rolled his eyes!!

There are better options available where the food is good and the eating experience is better ( example Siam Elephant in Carpinteria), but since it was not on our way we unwillingly chose this option. Realized it was a bad idea! Will never come back.

All time best gym to train at in NOLA!!! If you're in to power lifting, bodybuilding, or need a good personal trainer go here!!! I recommend this gym to anyone who wants to train to impress, get in shape, get that beach body, break power lifting records, get toned up, or just keep that sexy physique you already have!!! Come here and you will enjoy the atmosphere, there's always some kind of lifting phenom in the back part of the gym that lifts over 600 lbs!!!
The food was great! I really enjoyed my entire meal! The service was good. I parked on the street (no problem finding a spot on a Saturday night). I can't wait to go back.

Always a solid choice of places to eat! From apps to entrees to sushi it's ALL good!! I also have a food allergy that they are ALWAYS happy to accommodate!! Favorites(crab Rangoon, steamed dumplings, roll roll combo, JB tempura roll, sesame chicken)

Cool spot, got a cut from Ramiro. Super friendly, great conversation, great cut. He took the time to explain about styling my hair a few different ways. 

Moderately priced, but solid value for the cut. Friendly, busy atmosphere, happy people. 

There's a pool table, flat screen TVs with sports channels on, and free donuts :O

Definitely recommend!

Yes. The best restaurant in King of Prussia. Consistently delivers an excellent meal and service. This place is great for a special occasion, bites at the bar or just a really good lunch or dinner. My favorites and some of my family's faves are the avocado bomb, ribs, shrimp, any steak entree, burger, the carrot cake and key lime pie. The croissants before the meal are also excellent. The service here is also the best in KOP. Bobby Joe is probably the best of the crew. Super personable and accommodating.

Really nice place. Rooms are very nice. Had a lot of fun at the oyster bar and another restaurant there had a really good buffet. Can't remember the name but you'll find it.

Good for kids. Just not in the casino.
Very friendly owner. He was honest about pricing. Great work done. My car is running like new!
Food was good and the Hurricane is the best! Judit is awesome server! Definitely will coming back!
Service was nothing short of AMAZING, food just as good! I recommend the Reuben and cheese fries! Very clean establishment and the beer is nice and cold!

I would give The Choice a 3.5 if that was an option as it lands smack between 3 and 4 for me at this point.  It could change with more visits under my belt, but I wanted to get a review in on this place.  There isn't much out there yet, and I think it is a solid entry into the Benton Park area.  
What's a three?  The food, the table service, and the buffet/lunch area.  The Choice serves up southern style comfort food, the results are good but not great.  The standouts do far are fried chicken and the greens- both savory and delicious.  The chicken was spiced just right with the skin crispy while the meat stayed tender and juicy.  The greens are vegetarian, but you would not miss the flavor that bacon or other meat adds.  The many other sides we tried- red beans & rice, stuffing, corn bread, steamed veggies, and mac 'n cheese, were just okay.  Some were overcooked and mushy, some lacking flavor.  I don't like to add salt to meals at all, and when it's southern style comfort food, something is amiss.  BF tried the pasta alfredo with blackened chicken, which we thought could be a tactical error not being a typical southern dish, and we were unfortunately correct.  The sauce was bland and the chicken was just cooked through by baking or saute- not a trace of grilling or char to be found.  Our table server was a young, shy girl who spoke so quietly we could hardly hear her.  She did move us from the sterile, cafeteria like buffet area into the nice bar area, so that was a big help.  She didn't have a wine list or know what wines they served, so had to get the bartender's help.  Turns out they just have one house brand with a handful of varietals and one house special, so that should be easy to remember!  
So what was a four for us? The bar ambiance, bartender, and neighborhood location. The difference between the lunch and buffet area is like night and day.  The bar is upscale yet comfortable, spacious yet cozy.  It's beautifully decorated with soft, comfy seats, a gorgeous bar, and great lighting.  There are several flat panel TVs strategically placed for good viewing but minimal distraction.  This would be a great place to watch a game.  The bartender is apparently a local legend that knocked our socks off.  He is funny, professional, and serves up cocktails with flourish and flair.  While we were still at our bar table, he stopped by to top us off consistently but not intrusively.  Once we bellied up to the bar after dinner, he rolled out the charm and chatted us up.  We had a lot of laughs, but he was always on top of his game for us and other patrons.  A real gem.  It's nice that this is walkable from home as well, especially since the bar is such a highlight!  They do have a bar menu that looks good, so our next visit will be drinks and apps at the bar.

Here was my experience at Tiffany's:

1. I walked in, just after another girl, and watched as she signed in on a clipboard at the counter. Like when you get to the doctors office. I followed her and signed in, too.

2. Nobody greeted anyone? People barely even looked up when I walked in, and nobody even said, "hi" to me, or the 4 other people who walked in after I sat down to wait for my turn.

3. I had an appointment for 3:30pm. Originally, it was for 6:30pm, but I got out of work early and called to ask if I could come in earlier. I also knew my original appointment was just before closing, so I wanted to help them get out of work on time. I asked if that would work, if it would be okay, if there was an opening. The person on the other line said that was fine, and that I should come in at 3:30 instead.

I walked in at 3:25pm, waited until 3:40pm and asked how much longer the wait would be (the first time someone even acknowledged my existence..). I was told I could to go to a pedicure chair then, even though every tech was obviously preoccupied with other people. So I went to the chair and soaked my feet.. for TWENTY-FIVE minutes.

After 25 minutes passed, I watched someone be seated in the chair next to mine, who walked in after I did, and start her pedicure. The tech who was starting her pedicure turned to me and said, "sorry, she has an appointment." I said, "I had an appointment, too, at 3:30." It was then that someone was finally sent over to my chair to start my pedicure, too.

My question is this: if you're not going to greet anyone walking in or check who does and doesn't have an appointment and you don't even bother to ask, then purposely ignore someone in favor of someone else, why do you even take appointments and have that ridiculous sign in book at the entrance?

4. My pedicure starts, and I notice that they don't use disposable tools (like the pumice) and they all look considerably worn down. Then I proceed to get the world's softest "massage." She literally just runs her hands up and down my feet and calves, like she has vaguely/briefly seen a massage before, and is trying to imitate what she saw, but has also taken a handful of muscle relaxers and can just barely feel her arms and hands. To give you an idea of how bad this was, the lotion started DRYING IN CLUMPS WHILE SHE RUBBED IT BACK AND FORTH.

I'd wish for a 0-star option, but the polish job wasn't the absolute worst in the world, so I'll take the 1-star and just never go back.

I've been here maybe like eight times since I moved to Exton. It's the perfect spot to grab a drink, a flatbread, and chat away. The drinks are tasty and everything on the menu is cooked so well. It's also nice how they change their menu with the seasons. It's a cute spacious spot that never is crowded. 

The decor is welcoming and the wait staff is always sooo pleasant!

What an amazing experience it was at the Dali Museum this past weekend. The Museum was so enjoyable and elegant. The art pieces where sophisticated and intriguing. I never thought I would enjoy such a great exhibit till I came across the fascinating works by the famous artist Salvador Dali! 

His artwork and portraits were astounding, deep and perplex! There was a tour guide that explains the story behind his work which I found to be very interesting and worthwhile!!

For those that love the outdoors, there was an area which encompasses a maze, sculptures, creative art and a wishing tree. Although we were only able to stay for a few hours, there was so many parts of the Museum that can be visited and enjoy. I would recommend that a whole day is spent for art lovers !! 

I definitely recommend coming by the Dali museum as it is so worth the time and money!!

I don't really get over to university too city often, so I asked the waiter at the restaurant we were eating at to suggest a place to get some beers after dinner.  He suggested City Tap House which was less then a block away.  

The first time I went here was during the day at it was slow.  The bartenders were very attentive and knew a ton about beers.  They helped us each pick out a flight.  There's a ton of awesome beers to try here and a chill vibe.  

I then went back on a friday night and talk about a different experience.  You walk in and right away get why it's called "university" city.  Very college vibe, packed with Penn students.  We sat outside by the fire pits which are really nice.  As far as food, I've only tried the pizza.  

I probably wouldn't go here during the whole college scene again, but this is a solid spot I would return to for sure.

I went into here to buy some air pods. Not one person said or asked can I help... In fact I don't even think the staff saw me in the store. If you need something like a cable or headphones or a TV go someplace else! The customer service here is absolutely garbage!

I love the local ingredients and unique menu at Maynard's. If you are looking for something extra special I highly recommend requesting a chef's table here. I took my husband for his 30th birthday, named the price and specified a focus on sous vide cooking. We were delighted by a five course meal with wine pairings (non alcoholic Bellini pairings for me as I was pregnant) and all were excellent. Chef Addam came out to explain each course personally. The only slight hiccup in the evening was when my husband spilled his fourth glass of wine on me and started inviting everyone to come to our home for dinner :)

We went to Firebirds this past Saturday night and were so disappointed. The service was fine but the food was bad. We ordered the BLT salad, which was ok. Then we ordered the 7oz bacon wrapped filet with the $7 crab cake add on and mashed potatoes as the side. The filet had no flavor at all. 0. I could have been eating any other random meat, it was that flavorless. The bacon was both burnt black on the edges and rubbery in the middle, which is just gross. Half of the plate was filled with gluey, tasteless mashed potatoes. And the only part of the meal that was any good was the crab cake, which was slightly larger than a quarter. The $7 crab cake was a bite and a half of food. 

Expensive and bland. Never again.

Beautifully laid out and extra clean l am in love! Met the owner who was as sweet as pie and who believes in trying to make Willingboro a better place. Haven't tried the hot food but will be coming back this week to pick up lunch as everything looked appealing and fresh!

On July 10,2018 we purchased enough carpet and pad for four rooms, from the Sparks Lowe's.  The purchase price included full furniture movement , old carpet removal, and complete pad and carpet installation, miscellaneous items including transition strips.  The installation was not completed until September 1.  Even then not all the items in the contract were completed.  I completed the transition strip installation, door shortening, trap door and other misc. things myself.

The guys who put down the carpet were pleasant to have in the house but were always at least two hours late in arriving and did not always pay attention to detail and finish what needed to be done.  The subcontractors reps were pleasant when they came to check the work and identified several problems, such as the carpet, going into the master closet, being oriented the wrong way, but were not responsive in getting them taken care of.  The lady in Lowe's customer service department, with  whom we visited several times, was friendly and pleasant but again totally nonresponsive.

I could continue the saga but I hope the point has been made. If your going to spend several thousand dollars for carpet like we did my strong recommendation is to drive on past Lowe's and go to a true carpet store.  You just might get someone who knows and cares abut their business and knows the the importance of customer satisfaction..

If you want Mexican food, DO NOT COME HERE. 
I'd rather have some Taco Bell than eat here again. The food is not good, not authentic Mexican food, and their salsa sucks.

Lifeguards are a joke. They spend most of their time talking to each other. Have seen them on their phones, the Y relies on life jackets instead of making them do their jobs. They require all kids under 14 to wear one. How can they learn to swim with a life jacket on.

First time here and we had a good experience. We were a party of 4 adults and two 13 month old infants. They sat us in a rather crammed booth with 2 high chair attachments. One of the high chairs was not attached correctly and we were assisted by one of the hostesses who was very polite and helped fix it. Service was very good. Waiter was attentive and kept coming back to check on us and refill our drinks. The buffet had a lot of choices and everything tasted fresh! We left full but not disgustingly bloated like you do coming out of other buffets. The place was packed and had a good level of energy. My only gripe is the price point. I think it leaned toward more on the expensive side but it was for a special occasion and will definitely will be back.

I mean... YUM. Will you pay a bit more than your usual fast food joint here? Sure. Will it be worth it? Absolutely! Everything Shake Shake does is done to perfection. Their staff is excellent and will make sure you have a great dining experience. Its certainly a treat to splurge on every now and then!

Small cozy little place. Luckily when I got there the was a table available so I did not have to wait. The food was excellent. I had the BBQ Shrimp Po'Boy and the Gooey Butter cake. The BBQ sauce was both sweet and tangy and it was loaded with perfectly cooked shrimp. The cake was a little dry, except for in the middle. I think this would taste much better if it were brought home and heated up.

This is a great pizza place...far better than Pizza Hut or Papa Johns. The staff is friendly and they always make our pizza to perfection. They have been very accommodating. We like a variety of pizza so they have split one large pizza into 3 separate toppings on occasion for us!

This restaurant had been on my list for an incredibly long time, but yet I had never seemed to get to crossing it off. I am so glad that I finally did!

INITIAL - I made a reservation for 8pm two days in advance via OpenTable. Very easy process and there was a decent amount of availability for a Saturday night. Arrived and was assisted by the complimentary valet service. Once inside I was greeted warmly, and showed to the table. 

AMBIANCE - The atmosphere/ambiance of this restaurant is phenomenal with blush colored plush booths, a lit up sign saying 'irresistible,' subtle plants, and gorgeous art. My friend and I made note that the lighting was dim enough to be warm and comfortable, but not so dim that it was sitting in the dark, or gaudy 'too-romantic-for-friends" lighting. So pretty overall!

DRINKS - Lightly chilled water. Tried 2 different cocktails - The Mercedes and Essential Frivolity. Both featured grapefruit juice, which they squeeze fresh at the restaurant. Essential Frivolity was one of the best cocktails I've ever had. No strong overwhelming taste of alcohol, just a fresh, fruity, balanced concoction. The Mercedes had pear, cinnamon and grapefruit and your choice of vodka or gin. I chose vodka. The light cinnamon paired with the fruity pear and grapefruit was such a pleasant and surprising combination. I absolutely loved both. 

FOOD - We ordered the Bucheron Cheese, Shaved Bayonne Ham, Onion Soup Gratinee, and Le Burger. Each item was incredible and simple! 

The Bucheron was soft warm goat cheese, pair with thick buttery slices of sourdough bread and blueberry compote.

The Shaved Bayonee Ham was dijon mustard, thick slices of buttery sourdough, and thinly shaved cured ham, served with gherkins.   

The Onion Soup Gratinee was a classic French onion soup with warm, melty cheese and bread all baked in a ceramic bowl. 

I didn't eat the Le Burger, but my friend raved over it. It was served with Truffle Fries and she was in heaven!

Additionally, the service was incredible. Not campy or overly involved, but helpful, polite, and available! So great.

Will be frequenting this lovely restaurant. Next time will have to try the wine selection.

Love the design and layout of the new restaurant. Staff was friendly and attentive. Take advantage of the free pimento cheese hush puppies with Yelp check-in if they are still running that promo. Excellent starter. We tried several items and for the most part everything was pretty good. Unfortunately, I just had to try the Nashville Hot Oysters. Love the concept, but didn't love these.  Crust was a big mushy nasty hot mess. If they could crisp them up and ease up on the fire sauce they might have something. Looking forward to going back.

Wow, wide a slide from an awesome restaurant with wonderful service to below mediocrity!  I can hardly believe the change.  Just left - Sunday night.  Should have been a nice and quiet dinner but service was non existent, the food was either not avail, delayed, half served.  Let me give tangible examples - sat for nearly 15 min post ordering before bread arrived. Basket was about 1/3 full - 4 pieces for 3 people.  While it was warm and smelled wonderful, the dipping sauce was not brought at same time nor was butter?  Waitress indicated it would be coming and it was another more than 15 min before it finally arrived.  Salads came and we asked for more bread which never materialized.   The entrees came with the comment that we are out of vegetables so chef gave 2 helpings of rice?  Who needs two helpings of rice....kebabs had no vegetables other than onions....no tomato, no peppers?  What gives?  Asked for manager and he took 10+ min to show and no apologies, said he would talk to chef and come back and did not~  Will absolutely NOT go back and will shout from every forum to share this awful experience.  I and my family have been loyal supporters from their opening but this is not acceptable under any circumstances.

This place is so cute. Seriously had the BEST burger ive ever had here. It was so good it tasted like unicorn meat or something else equally amazing. I came here during Made in America Weekend, so the fact that this place was byob really helped with our drunken concert preparation.

Very crusty bread and bagel. Fast service. You can place your order on the ordering station.

Cherry bread has a weird artificial flavor. I'm not a big fan of it.

Canele has a nice crunch shell with moist filling but it is missing a citrusy flavor, it's quite bland.

Egg tart. I thought it would be sweet like those in Chinatown but to my surprise it's savory. Nice creamy custard.

Chocolate croissant and chocolate cookie are my favorite from today's haul. 

Bagel is relatively small.

In the middle of the bakery there's a shelf with bread as a display. You can also pick up some coconut pretzel shortbread and burnt toast biscotti. Against the wall on the right you can grab some flour.

There's no sign of the capacity so some people are waiting outside. I suggest that there's a sign indicating the room capacity for social distancing

I've gone to Lee Nail Spa for a few years now. Even after I moved closer to other Nail Spa's, I still drive back to go here. 

I've never had an unpleasant experience here. Everyone is very nice. Pricing is extremely reasonable. The facility itself is very clean. You can see that all of the tools they use to perform manicures and pedicures are clean and have been autoclaved. 

I must specifically give a shout out to Angela. She does the BEST eyebrows. She waxes my eyebrows and I've been asked on several occasions by friends if they're microbladed because they're so clean cut and shaped well! 

I highly recommend Lee Nail Spa. They do sometimes get very busy. Ive most often had luck walking in but you can make appointments as well.

When I am in Reno, this is one of my favorite places to be, besides Cabela's.  There is so much to do here that you do not even have to leave the place: Bowling, mini-golf, arcade, all the gambling you want, movie theater, driving range, swimming pool, shopping center, food places, spa, ect.  The newly remodeled summit rooms are gorgeous and are excellent for couples.  The normal rooms are hit-and-miss.  If I were to stay here, I would only go for the summit.  

What is also nice is that the prices for this place is reasonable, such as their awesome breakfast buffet, $9.99.  For the gamblers, sometimes they run $1.00 to $3.00 blackjack tables.  Their arcade is great for the kids, and the driving range is a lot of fun.  

The only negative things I have to say about this place is that sometimes, the staff is hit-and-miss.  Sometimes you get very nice staff, and sometimes you get very rude staff.  Also, this half-remodel look needs to hurry up and get to the fully-remodeled look.  This is a place I like to go to get away from it all!

Came here yesterday for happy hour. The server had an attitude up until it was almost time to leave. The spinach dip was sour and had to be remade ( wasn't done when I was there) The broccoli balls had no taste and didn't taste like the normal recipe. Overall bad experience , won't be back.

I've been to ZAMA half a dozen times since they opened last year. I love the minimalist decor, true to Zen keeping. I've only had their sushi, sashimi and designer rolls and I've never been disappointed with the consistency of taste, freshness & creativity. I'll come back. But, Umai-Umai is still my favorite,  by far.

They've updated again. Now along with the relaxing recliners, they have surround sound that vibrates right through the chairs. Very comfy but my other half says she wishes it reclined back more.  And I even think they may have updated the quality of the popcorn. It melted in my mouth. Maybe it was a new brand?!? Who knows for sure. But it was good all away around. AND OH YEAH the best part of all... they didn't freeze us out!

I lived here a few years ago, but still think very fondly of both the apartment and the company. Why? Since then I've lived with several other property management companies (in other cities) that are not nearly as responsive to maintenance calls or easy to work with. 

All of the apartments I saw were not only charming (hooray for older, rehabbed construction) but also affordable and close to downtown. 

Also, I cannot emphasize enough how much I appreciated (and now miss) their responsiveness. Maintenance calls were addressed, always, within 24 hours. If parts needed to be ordered or there was another delay, I was always informed of this. Anytime anyone entered my apartment, a note was left explaining what had been wrong and what work, if any, still needed to be done.

Great, job, Zender.
Good food, Onion rings and fried okra: best sides there!

The place looks funky...in a good way. It's very colorful, from wall arts to the menu. Definitely put you in a good mood. 

This place definitely resembles more of a hole in the wall place, so don't expect it to be super clean. 

I have had their fish tacos and chicken burritos. The price is reasonable and the food is very delicious. Especially after eating at chipotle or moe's regularly, th is is definitely the more authentic mexican food experience. The burrito offers more freebies, such as jalapeño, cucumber cubes etc. The burritos are more flavorful and the ingredients are better seasoned than chipotle. An add-on guacamole is alot cheaper than chipotle too.

It also provides four free flavors of salsa to go along with your chips. 

Overall, good food! Will definitely go back again.

I'm so glad mega-healthy places like this are becoming less and less rare!

Thanks Greenleaf for leaving your west coast land to check out the indy midwest vibe toting along some yummy greenery for us.

I had a salad that was very tasty. I think it was southwest.  The juice we got was great too.  One of the bowls was a little less flavorful but still enjoyable.

When I'm done with eating here I feel light and happy and i can't help but blame the healthy fresh food!

My only slightly negative is that on multiple occasions we've had to wait quite a while for our order.  For grabbing one juice or a salad, it might be ok, but I came once with 4 friends who all ordered juice and salad or bowl and we ended up taking over an hour for lunch.  I hope they can find the right balance for number of people working to mitigate this, but I do expect to wait a bit for something so freshly made! 

Great work Greenleaf! Now I only wish my work was closer so I could have this nourishing food more often!
Amazing fondu,  great food overall at reasonable prices.  Quaint atmosphere.

A must experience!
I grew up on German food and the sauerbraten will not disappoint !
Had fun listening to the oom pa pa band and drinking , my husband got paddled by the waitress Frau- too funny 
We will be back for more food and fun !

Jones, I may have judged you too soon.

I came here once for dinner with a friend and maybe it was an off night or maybe we just ordered some lackluster things.  I have heard people who I know and trust sing your praises to me.  I had always vowed to go back and finally I did.

Enter the elite event.

I saw a new side of Jones tonight.  I saw a polite and very accommodating staff.  I saw some serious mac and cheese that I kept having plates of.  I think I've seen enough to go back yet again.

I'm giving you a 3 star placeholder because I now vow to return and write an updated review.  I think you have potential Jones.  Now it's just up to me to find it.

Visiting from NYC and I have to say it's up there with the best breakfast I've ever had. Homemade gravy, eggs cooked to perfection, and those biscuits O M G! I see why Red Oak is the "go to" for breakfast. 

As a New Yorker we are very picky...I mean very. You know who you are. I'm scared to say this is the best breakfast I've ever had but man Red Oak is putting up a great fight. I ordered Mama's hash and a mini Smashing biscuit. I'll be back tomorrow.

Do yourself a favor and come visit Red Oak. You won't be disappointed

One of the older, well established restaurants in SB. Food is delicious and very unique. Service is S L O W, so if you are in a hurry, go somewhere else. Hopefully they will continue to escape the high SB rents and stay in business.

Pleasantly surprised by this location. Called ahead and was able to get my son in for an appointment 15 minutes later. Kat was amazing with my picky teenager and found a happy medium between what he wanted and what I wanted. Prices are reasonable, location was clean and we were in and out in less than 20 minutes. Happy teenager = big win!

I dined here with a friend, my treat. I ordered the chicken Caesar salad and he got the bay platter (or something like that). Drinks are too expensive at RL so I had water. Good service and food. Happy, happy, happy.

The food is prettty good! Bailey our server was AMAZING!! THE SERVICE is fantastic!!! The bowo wow drink has 80 shota of alcohol so be careful!!! But it was delicious and fruity! Pork chops were good! Potato salad is fantastic!!! Cornbread dressing tasty.. Ive come to terms that New Orleans people dont really know how to make mac and cheese.. It was basically spaghetti in some kind of creamy Velveeta like concoction. Ive noticee its really the same with all the restaurant's here. They dont know good baked mac and cheese like their Mississippi, Alabama, Chicago counterparts. But I guess that why they know red beans and rice, jambalaya, ETC. The jazzy wings were very tasty, fried!!! Redfish was overcooked and dry and the lemon butter sauce was sweet. The carrot souffle was tasty, tasted like a sweet potato pie! Would def go again!

Found this place on Yelp and so happy we did! My son had plain hotdogs, I had Chicago dog loaded and my husband had the beef with sausage peppers and onions. Amazing! Clown slaw famous. Service the best.  Highly recommended

Used to be great but meat quality and portion size has gone down over the years. The last pulled pork sandwich I had was super fatty. We've given them a few tries. 

The baked beans are Bush's canned baked beans. There's no way those are homemade.
Fabulous homemade bagels and donuts. Have not tried the sandwiches yet but hear  they are wonderful. Great coffee. Friendly efficient staff.

I'm not a vegan or vegetarian, but I like eating vegetables more than most places offer and salads are boring so this is a great place to drop by.
- Portabello burger: the bread is soft but hearty, the mushroom and everything inside is flavorful. 
- Curry tofu wrap: the tofu is medium, it's not firm like string cheese or soft like silky tofu and the curry flavor is quite strong. The other veggies in the wrap prevent the curry&tofu from tasting too heavy. It's not salty but does have a bit more spice than I like. If you are used to the amount of spice in Indian food, you'd like this.
- Udon noodle salad: this is my favorite. I wish the udon is more tender but everything works so well together. The "meat" is hot off the grill so it's crispy on the outside, juicy and flavorful. The salad and noodle are coated in a slightly vinegary and spicy sauce. It's the perfect filling salad for the summer!
- Cupcake: I had a peach and ginger cupcake. I'm a dessert snob and I'm impressed by the fact it doesn't taste vegan, but I can't really taste the peach or ginger in it. It just taste like a generic moist and nice cupcake with sweet frosting. If I have to nitpick, the frosting start to feel a bit greasy on the last bite, but at least it wasn't super sugary and is soft/fluffy.

I had another burger with crispy "chicken" I believe, the flavor was nice but it just taste like bland chicken and I would recommend the mushroom instead of fake meat if you are going for burgers. I had a green smoothie thing also, it just taste like a bunch of veggies and some fruits blended together, I wish they had either added sugar or more of....something so the flavor doesn't taste like it's been watered down. I do like the fact they have water provided if you don't want to order drinks, there's plenty of seating and it's a chill atmosphere. I wish it's a few dollarss cheaper, but the food is good overall.

Fido is a great stop for breakfast, lunch and coffee alike.

Located in the heart of Hillsboro Village, in an old pet store (the sign remains--I love it!), the space is ideal for nearby Belmont and Vandy students. While there's a ton of space, the restaurant is nearly ALWAYS full, from my observation--doesn't matter if you traipse on in at 7:30 a.m. or 2 in the afternoon... always packed.

I don't dislike their lunch options--I just prefer their breakfast menu. They always have a ton of great meals, as well as a case of scrumptious baked goods (muffins and carrot cake are my faves), beckoning to be snatched up. For coffee, the Bulldog is my go-to: espresso with coffee poured over. Before I moved to Nashville, my boyfriend and I would swing by, late at night and share a slice of cake. It's a sweet late night date night locale.

Service isn't bad--parking can be a pain... there's a paid lot behind the restaurant, just be forewarned that while the first 1.5 hour is free, you still need to go through the motions of obtaining a slip for your dashboard (I'm a small town girl--this is frivolous to me). 

Definitely swing by when you get a chance! One of my favorite coffee shops in Nashville.

Wow. I mean really just wow. 

My son has soccer by this location. Practice starts at 7:30; this location closes at 8. The location is about 18 minutes away. The last three times I've come here. 7:51 - drive thru doesn't answer 7:55 - "we're closed" 7:50 - were open but don't have hot coffee what do you want 

I mean this lady Stephanie is a customer service nightmare. This one guy said he'd brew my coffee for me and she came out to the window and said seriously?? Serve him and lock the window ASAP. 

Why do you work in customer service ? Get an at home job Stephanie. 

Question is obviously this location has been drowning for the last 2 years, do I even bother to call anyone? Clearly it's not worth it. 

This Dunkin' 1/10
Stephanie 0/10
The guy who is brewing my coffee against Stephanie's will 10/10. (Except for him calling me "bud")

Do better Dunkin'.
Best bubble tea in philly china town!!! nice staff and nice place to chill, hang out with friends.  great drinks for the great price.

This place seems to have quite the buzz. I sat at the bar and the service to me as well to the gentleman next to me was poor. The servers did not engage with us we had to reach out and be what I believe to be pushy to order drinks, get coffee, place food orders and cash out. The food was great, I'd recommend sitting at a table where I imagine the service is much better.

FAVORITE coffee place in Louisiana ! The Southern Pecan is to die for! Came here back in April when I was visiting my Family in Kenner and returned again as soon as we got in because I had to get My Coffee fix! Can't wait to come back in tomorrow- the girls Are super sweet and the coffee is always delicious. I got so excited I dropped my coffee lmao. Wish they had this  spot up in New Jersey. A must try if you are in the Kenner area.
First time at Luk Fu, and it was fantastic. Ada our servicer was polite, respectful and very accommodating. We shall return.
Totally outdated from the reception area all the way to the rooms. The whole hotel needs renovated. Bartender at the bar was not friendly and or attentive. Convenient location though

Service was not great. However, I believe that had more to do with the waiter knowing a group of people who came in. Dinner was very good, the wait from order time to table was not bad, and the wine was good as well. When it came to pay, once again the waiter was MIA. So it was a lot of waiting around. The food would definitely get me back, the service not so much.

Best in the city! My mother and I sampled pretty much the entire menu. This was my second time coming but I decided to take her because she's in town for the weekend. The food was even better than the first time! One of the head chefs was even gracious enough to bless us with a free dessert (the mochi donut holes). 

I really appreciate the thought of the different flavors that goes into the creation of these dishes. I don't know how they do it but it is as if they layer the flavors. I don't know how much sense that makes, but it's just an experience that you have to try.

Cavi Medspa is amazing! Heather is very knowledgeable and patient (I'm not a good tolerant of pain!). I had my last LHR treatment today, and am now trying to figure out other things to get done...just to go back. I highly recommend this place, you will not be disappointed.

I am coming back to this place!! real Peruvian food!
I went for lunch and ordered a Ceviche de Pescado, it was so delicious. My mom ordered the Seco (one of our personal favorites) it was so flavorful, just like how i remembered it in Lima. The staff was friendly and courteous. They brought out Peruvian corn, it was very good.

My husband and I had a late lunch at Parc today and it was amazing. The weather was pleasant and we set outside. Service was great and the staff was polite and friendly. We shared a niçoise salad and a mushroom tart for appetizer and had the trout for main course. Everything was delicious and perfectly cooked. The breads are to die for. We'll definitely go back.

We decided to come here for a work monthly happy hour and ended up having a great time! Our waitress was easy and go with the flow despite people coming and leaving all evening. The happy hour food and drinks specials were awesome - get the boudin balls! I also loved that they have a delicious and filling burger bowl if you're looking to skip the bread or saving room for onion strings that are dipped in Tabasco or crystal before frying... drool!

Even their drip is on point! I tend to get a double espresso shot since I can't make that at home... unless I buy a Slayer like they have. The carb water is a nice accompaniment with the espresso. Just ask what bean they prefer for your drink... I've never been let down on their beans. 

Great work spot. Minimal,  good music for study,  and great people who work there. They love what they do and you know it. 

I am a St. Pete native,  I have no personal connection with Bandit that makes me want to exaggerate, and if you want the best coffee in town as of this date... go here.
I always go on the weekend, and ask for Michelle. She's lovely. I also come to get my eyebrows and lip waxed. They do a really good job.

They seemed far overpriced for the service I received for my wedding dress alterations. I had to come back on two occasions - first because the used the wrong stitch on the hem - and then because they forgot to sew my cups in. They overcharged for the cups (based on what I have received from other seamstresses). The three bustles ($50 each) fell out of my wedding dress and had to be safety pinned into place at my reception. They also did an expensive and arguably unnecessary alteration to the back of the dress that involved taking the zipper out. When they put the zipper back in, it changed the way the lace detail lined up. Everyone said the dress looked beautiful, so it wasn't ruined, but the whole experience made me feel swindled. On the plus side, they were always friendly every time I went in.
Everyone here is absolutely awesome! Super friendly and helpful. Jonathan helped me get my tire changed in 15 minutes. Absolutely going back!
This is my go-to coffee shop in the morning. The home made biscotti is a great compliment to the fresh brewed coffee. Breakfast and lunch sandwiches are unique and delicious. Definitely recommend!

Rude staff. Staff is inconsiderate of the customer that pays wash there car. I had my audio in my car turned down I couldn't even hear my music. However staff still approached me and told me to turn down my radio down. Its kinda ridiculous i have a stock accord with no sounds system, so being told that is like saying you vacuum are to loud it just doesn't make since. Please place a sign saying no music at all before i enter. Anyway i will take my business else where and pay for someone else to clean my car. Do note that I would have not posted this review if my music was actually loud. I don't believe that is why the staff approached me. Overall I was very unwelcome

We went there last weekend on a date night.  The service was wonderful and the food was delicious! I had the scallops with the lobster mashed potatoes...it was amazing!  The view from our table was great...we sat right by the window and had views of the beach just across the street.  Lovely place, great service, great food.

Oh, I also need to add that I highly recommend the olive oil cake for dessert!!!
The pizza was good, but in my opinion not great.  It's thin crust...I actually prefer a thicker crust.  Plenty of toppings to choose from and pizza cooks quickly.

Great Spa. The best experience here is the customer service. They are friendly, polite, accomodating, and make you feel comfortable. They even provide beverages including wine and mimosas. They are super clean. Prices are reasonable for traditional services but provide other unique spa experiences. Only reason I only give them 4 stars is because the lady that gave me my pedicure was new and it wasn't that good. Otherwise, great spa. Have already recommended to family & friends.

I was having a crazy sugar day and happened to drive by this place, which I've been meaning to check out for quite awhile. It was around noon on a Thursday and they weren't picked over, their shelves are still quite full! But I have to say that the only thing I was really drawn to was the apple fritter. And it was only 70 cents?!? How crazy! I completely enjoyed my treat and totally inhaled it! This was a fun little pit stop on my cheat day!

LOVED this place! It was the most authentic Korean fusion place I've ever tried! Loved their sauce with the burrito and the tacos. The burritos was pretty big and so flavorful. It definitely was our favorite Korean fusion place!

Owned by a young business man by the name of Tj Miller..first impression was young preppy college kids as patrons.The ambiance is fine whisky and bourbon casks in the front foyer and a terrace to eat outside.farther in is agood size bar along the window .The menu is mostly craft beers and several bourbon whiskey and scotch drinks. THEY HAVE SPECIAL RECIPE DRINKS.these are unusual and many people werethere just for those..tThere is a small eating menu tapas appetizers and about10 choices for dinner.upstairs is more dining and a larger area for big groups to dine comfortably.
The place has only been open a week.stillworking out the kinks..but the young owners is very on top of it and trying very hard to give the best dining experience.
Worth a visit..
Different and unique 

I had the big bird.for instance.$12 pickled watermelon. Buttermilk fried chicken breasts on a sweetpotato waffle served with bourbon maple syrup..it is big enought to split (which we did) 

We also had cornbread pudding topped with braised bourbonbbq pulled pork topped with steamed spinach and a couple beerbattered onions..

Went on Wed for dinner-amazing! Fresh fish uniquely prepared. The crab fries app was delicious-wish we got the full order! Enjoyed the fried corn on the cob. Cam was the perfect server--gave recommendations and was attentive throughout the meal. Eat here!

Dr. Rita Bhakta is the WORST pediatric eye doctor I have encountered out of my 32 years of wearing eyeglasses. I took my 22 month old son here because he was going crosseyed. She didn't even introduce herself or try to build a rapport with my son. This doctor was asking him things like to cover one eye. He's not even two. My son didn't like the dilation so he was acting like a brat... I'll be the first to say! She stated she couldn't complete his exam because he wouldn't look where she needed him to look and was being uncooperative. He's a freaking toddler! Then proceeded to tell me his could have no vision in his left eye worst case and she would need to referee us out. I don't think she liked the fact he was having a melt down so she wrote us off! Her idea of a referral was writing a name on a post it. No notes... nothing! Then tells me there will be no charge. I do not think she's ever dealt with a patient his age! Do not take your babies here. The office was not toddler friendly. There was crap everywhere. Tons if open doors. This visit was a complete nightmare with the exception of the assistant! She was so sweet!!! The only reason for two stars was her!

I was hoping for more chips with my sandwich for the 12.00 price tag and I got 1 yes 1 pickle slice not a spear which was disappointing as well.  The brisket lacked flavor definitely needed some salt & pepper or something. Not smoked flavor, probably done in an oven not sure.

We got the vegetarian plate and foule and it was plenty of food for two.  This is not the best Ethiopian I've ever had by a long shot, but it was still tasty.  The atmosphere was nice and service was good.  I'd definitely go back to get an inexpensive meal; it's probably the best food at this price in this neighborhood.
Everyone from the call taker to Joe who serviced my unit was fantastic.  Joe diagnosed my problem and had my unit up and running at a great price!

The only thing that's keeping this review from going lower is the food deal. If it wasn't for the get an entree, drink, and dessert every hour, this place would be in the negative if it was possible. I don't know even know where to start with this place, this park is a mess! The map and signs are confusing, we've been lost multiple times throughout the day, locker directions are misleading with one sign saying 2 hours max for a day? Like what does that even mean? OHH and don't even get me started on Cheetah Hunt. If you want to read about my fun experience on the ride, find my review on the ride Cheetahs Hunt or whatever. The lines for the rides are the most atricitous/ridiculous I've been in for an amusument park. For 12 hours, I've rode a total of three rides with each ride lasting an average of about 60 seconds while I spent the majority of my time eating and waiting in line. Stay away from this park, unless you don't mind wasting your time and money waiting in lines. In my experience, the worst amusement park I've been to. Roller coasters are just cheap knock offs of other coasters, there's nothing original about their rides except for the safari tour and the tour isn't all great, because they didn't let me use the bathroom before we went and forced us to stand fpr 15 minutes throughout the tour. This park is honestly just a front for a zoo with fake stuff to fill up the park with lines that aren't worth it in the end. I mean 60 bucks for a quick queue?! Now you're just making it obvious. -50 or lower/10 would not ride again. Can't find anything good about this place so don't say I didn't warn you. This place seriously sucks A

Okay, okay....so I traveled all the way BACK to south street to try this restaurant again. Me and my wife decided to pop in for lunch. We walked in and was instructed to pick a seat. Soft Arabic music was playing. When I sat down, the table cloth was dirtyyyyy. Old food was on it. The guy changed the table Cloth but I honestly would've felt better if they just had plastic table covers or even if he took the fresh table linen out of plastic. I'mNot gonna lie, I almost left. I don't know if they washed the table Linen or not. You have no peace of mind. I just hope I wasn't eating over top of anybody else's germs‍ a sista was starving tho so yup I took one for the team and ate anyway! Lmao

But the food was all that! We were both left completely satisfied. EVERYTHING was good. Lamb was  sooooo amazing. I had lamb, shrimp, rice, fries, salad w/homemade dressing, pita chips &hummus and white sauce. The pic speaks for itself. I can't remember the last time I ate something so good.

I took two stars off because they reallyyyy need to clean in there. It isn't dirty at all. Just ALOT of dust and no tlc put into the actual store where the customers eat. That dust really messed with my allergies. And them table covers. I don't know if they wash them. I really suggest using table clothes that can be disposed in front of customer. It wasn't a good feeling to be seated at a dirty table. If I wasn't so hungry, I would've said nevermind and went to another food establishment. 

SN: Hopefully they'll step it up next time on the cleaning tip. Cleanliness is next to godliness  If you pop in, please update us about the cleanliness!


Don't hesitate to eat here!! You won't be disappointed at all folks. Oh yea, I had the fat boy (I think that's the name) and I paid $30. It was soooo worth my money
I will be back and will be updating again

Pro: Basically a local diner, serving breakfast food all day. Everyone here is super nice. The menu is great. There are some great gf choices. Its nice to have this restaurant as an option here in Tampa. 

Con: Unfortunately, they did charge me FOUR DOLLARS FOR ONE GF PANCAKE. This is a kind of discrimination against people with real honest health issues, or something. Seriously :(((

Con: And after I paid an insane amount of $ to have ONE gluten-free pancake, someone tried to give me a gluten-filled cookie. A would-be "we care" gesture turned BS because GLUTEN. An actual gluten free cookie would have been a true gesture of good will.

I went here yesterday for the first time because of the high ratings and I wasn't sure why people highly rated this place. It's very small and there were only 2 people working there. It was a Wednesday afternoon after 5 and my French gel manicure, regular polish pedicure and eyebrow/lip waxing took 2 hours which should be an 1-1.5 hour job tops. On top of that, they didn't know how to do any line designs on my big toe nail which is my first experience of someone not knowing how to do that at a nail salon. The lady doing French also had to keep going back to erase the French tip and re do it which added to my 2 hour time there. One of my tips on my nails also fell off only a day after. Gel manicures are supposed to last 2 weeks. The French tip lines are also very uneven. Although the workers were nice, I am not coming back to this place because of their sub par nail skills.

I really am questioning my self giving 2 stars. I do want to give only 1, however the reason for the 2nd star is for the mere fact the oysters were delicious and only $17 for a dozen. 

Where do I begin ?

1. My aunt ordered the sushi & sashimi combo. Her plate came out with just raw fish (sashimi only) and it took a while before they made this right. 

2. My wife and I ordered sushi and it never made it to the table. We noticed we were still charged for it on our check and when we mentioned this to the server he said it was forgotten on the window. He offered to box it up but we didn't want to take the risk of eating sushi that had been sitting out for over an hour. 

3. We also ordered fried green tomatoes which consisted of a thin sliced tomato with a massive amount of fried oily bread crumbs. The inside batter was uncooked. 

It seems from recent reviews that the thirty marlin has finally dried up.

RICHIE!!!!! He was amazing! One night I had too much fun drinking wine, catching up with my sister and the next morning I was hurting. I called Richie and within 15 minutes of talking to him he was at my Airbnb helping me feel better. He had the best bedside manner, the perfect amount of humor and chill, and made everything so easy! He is a dream!
