The only place to go for men's clothing. The selection of clothing is awesome. So many unique options. Both owners, Alan and Jackie are extremely helpful. Jackie is awesome at color coordinating the entire family. 


After the purchase is made, they are always available for nips and tucks. This morning my son tore the buttons off his shirt. I walked in without N appointment and Alan had them expertly sewn back on while I waited. He didn't even charge me. I challenge anyone to find service like that elsewhere!

This'll be brief....always the case when I wait too long to review a business.

What was memorable:

Ambiance - intimate, dim, good for groups 
Drinks - well prepared and delicious 
Food - took a while but it was pretty good
Service - not very personable but knew the menu & drinks well
Restrooms - nice & clean 

I went for a birthday gathering and there was plenty of room for our group inside however the outdoor seating is prime real estate being on the hottest corner in CWE. You can spectate the entire area from your peripheral at Euclid & Maryland.

Really a horrible experience. I came here 20 mins before my shift because i know it's busy. I waited 25 mins for my one drink before asking if they had my order. The lady said no, and then went back to making drinks. A different employee then approached me and asked if i needed anything, and i asked her also if my order was coming up soon. She said they did not have my order, even though i placed it in person. They gave me a refund, and after checking my account it appears that instead of refunding me they charged me again. I will not be returning to this location.
Just had the chicken and waffles for dinner, sooo good!  The crispy chicken wings,  fresh hot waffle and the warm syrup were perfect!  Can't wait to try the rest of the menu.  Thank's guys!!!!

I'm so glad Valhalla opened up in St. Petersburg. They not only have some classic desserts that they knock out of the park, they have some unique ones you won't find elsewhere that are also delicious. My in-laws recently brought over some of their donuts and they were a huge hit.

We've had Valhalla cater two or three events now I think, and everyone raved about the desserts. We keep going back to them because of their consistency and reliability.

Service: I got Shelac removed and a regular manicure. They scrubbed way too hard and did not do a thorough job before applying the color for the manicure.  The coat of color they applied was also not precise and I had to direct the manicurist. My manicurist did not speak English and it was difficult to communicate instructions.

Location: This salon is located 25 minutes northeast of downtown near several shops that you won't find downtown.

Price: Cheap

Probably won't revisit this place.

This place is a wonderful hidden gem here in Carmel. Whenever we go in we're the youngest by a few decades, and it's part of what makes it great. The tenderloin is fantastic and definitely one of my favorites in Central Indiana! The wait staff has always been nothing but pleasant and the service is always efficient. The restaurant itself is dated but it works. I love coming here for a quick delicious dinner with my husband!

Gem in old city. We made last minute reservations for Valentine's Day and couldn't have asked for a better dining experience. Ambiance and service was great! Food... even better. We tried two specials and a few things on the menu, all were great. That pork chop though! We shared a couple desserts at the end, but holy cow that avocado and coconut ice cream. Get it. Now. Not unreasonably priced either. Worth every penny. Ask for Amy. She's awesome, and gives great service.
Excellent brews and very comfortable setting.  We will return with the rest of the family soon.  Very chill brewery.
Great burger place! Lots of options and they will do lettuce wraps for gluten free people!
Cool lunch spot for exotic drinks and slice of Parisienne delight enjoyable lunch break!
Used to be so good when they had live b ands. It was the best place in South Tampa. Always a good crowd. Now they only have lame DJs during the week. So disappointing. It's killed the business.

Honestly worst Culver's I've ever been too. I get that this place is new but the fact that they send there employees to be trained and for them too be this bad of a place is crazy. First sign of bad place too eat was the fact that another costumer was say that her chicken sandwich was raw and when she complained the cook came from the back of the kitchen too tell her that's how it's supposed to be cooked should have been my first sign too get out and when I got my food the cheese wasn't melted on the patty but was freezing cold bad experience highly don't recommend

We worked with Kelly Marsh and her team at Cornerstone for our original mortgage and had a great experience. When percentage rates went down we decided to look into refinancing, and knew that we wanted to work with Kelly and her team again. We trusted them and appreciated how efficiently they worked. Communication was always quick and clear, whether it was to answer our questions or to get paperwork sorted and processed. We always knew what was happening every step of the way, and appreciate how fast and easy the whole process was!
Who doesn't love Target?!

I've only had beer here and was glad for it. The menu itself reads well, but I opted to stick with beer only as taps in this town seem few and far between.

The Staff was great! Welcoming and well informed.

Though I would hardly expect this on every visit, the balcony afforded us a front row seat on a building fire and 1st responders just up the street.

~m

I'm in New Orleans for a conference and it is my first time in the city. I had been trying restaurants within walking distance of the convention center but wanted to venture out a bit. I have to eat gluten-free, so some homework is needed. I wanted shrimp and grits and so I called ahead of time to verify whether they (and other menu items) would be an option. I had a pre-dinner cocktail (A Swell Disposition), my AMAZING grits, and a glass of chardonnay that my server (Yana) recommended. Excellent restaurant and service!

[bonus story: As I was attempting to pay, I realized that I didn't have my wallet in my purse. I was treated with respect and trusted to return. I am so grateful]

This was DEE-LISH! We are brand new to Indian cuisine.  The staff was so welcoming and hospitable.  They made excellent menu suggestions based on our preferences.  Shrimp tandoori and chicken tikki masala were our picks this time.  Was so good we mopped up every bite with fresh naan.  
Can't wait to return and try more adventurous  meals.

I was pretty smitten with dinner at Radicchio Cafe. I dined with a group of six for a birthday and we tried a lot of food, all of it eliciting raves from the table.  My husband and I are vegetarian and they happily accepted changes to our orders.  Their tomato sauce is already veg so nixing the meat on most entrees will immediately transform it into veggie goodness.  I opted for a chicken and broccoli cavatelli sans chicken, with mushrooms.  Everyone at the table coveted my meal.  Bread and a plate of bruschetta were complimentary and the appetizers were generous portions, as were the entrees.  They had a ton of special additions to their menu and they were delicious.  If you can get your mouth on the smoked mozzarella, mushroom and balsamic reduction do it!  It's byo and they don't take reservations.  We waited about half an hour for a six top to open, which didn't seem terrible to me at 8:30 on a Saturday night.  2 salads, 4 apps, 6 entrees and 4 coffee drinks set us back a mere $203.  They were also kind enough to bring out a birthday cake we brought, candle lit and then slice and serve it to us.  The service was a little slow, but in a good laid back way, not in an annoying way.  

Oh and we just happened to sit at a table inches from Chase Utley. So you know, we had that going for us, which was nice.

They no longer have Elijah Craig 12 for the Manhattans, but the cocktails are still great.

They have a new chef, and the food is improved.  The Duck Rillette and Chicken Liver Mousse (chopped chicken liver to us peons) were stars.

Excellent local sandwich shop. I've had the banh-mi and the cheesesteak and both are amongst the best I've had in Philly. I haven't had the burger but a few friends have told me it's phenomenal. This place is undoubtedly worth a visit!

Awesome place for music and drinks (sports too but that's not my thing). The place looks amazing, inside and out and the staff is super friendly and fast. This place alone changes the look and vibe of Ybor and has had a really good crowd the few times I've been in. Relax, play pool, dance, or watch sports... It's all here. This will be my new spot, for sure!

Reid park is a nice little zoo that is friendly, clean and pretty easy to navigate. All the "main" attraction animals are easy to get to, and they have great view points. I only took off one star because I wish they'd clean the glass to some of the animal enclosures a bit more. I've been here early once and it was still pretty foggy for the bears/lions. If you're a resident, or visiting during times where they do special events, I would save a trip and come during those. ZooLights, Brew at the Zoo, Summer Safari Nights, etc. Also, check the temperature for the days you come and bring your own water!

The sweet potato fries are thin and crispy and delicious, and definitely shareable! The Tex Mex and mushroom burgers were both tasty, although a little on the extra oily side. My go-to food place at the Tampa airport!

We had a great stay at this property. The staff was super friendly. The room and hotel were clean. They had board games in the lobby. And every time we walked in or out, we were greeted by the staff. Huge plus! 
THERE ISNT A POOL AT THIS PROPERTY. But you can go down to their sister property and use theirs. It's a bit small, but worth it if you want to cool down.

I've gone to Christian Brothers for several years...they've always been fair and honest, today my AC went kaput in Birmingham, after a dealer wanted to charge me almost $700, and Hallmark Hyundai wouldn't even give me a quote unless I brought my car by, Jimmy gave me a quote and was able to work me in at 1:00 pm.  By 530 my car was finished and CB charged me exactly what they quoted.  I really appreciate their willingness to help me out, and getting my car back to me the same day.

Located across the street from the definitely more dive-y, yet equally great in it's own way, McGlinchey's. Good Dog is always a good time. Downstairs has a bar along one wall and booths on the other. Pretty tight, but worth the squeeze. Dark and woody, just how i like it. The food is excellent, especially the burgers. upstairs has a dining room. A high class dive, i guess you could say.

Don't waste your money. The pizza tastes like cardboard with nasty processed cheese-like product, the "cheese bread" is literally sub sandwich bread, cut in half with the same nasty white cheese melted on it; no Italian seasonings, other cheeses, or anything. My small salad was iceberg lettuce & clumps of shredded mozzarella on the top-that's it. $26 wasted!

Cute neighborhood and shop. I was seated shortly after arriving. I had French toast which was excellent and I took home delicious croissants. The place is small but it enough room to suit the clientele and I'm sure the neighbors b

Currently sitting in the restaurant and wondering why our service is absolutely horrible. Our server, billy, barely spoke to us. Never shared his name (I got it from the tab), didn't ask us how our food was, didn't ask if we wanted more drinks, didn't ask ANYTHING. He actually cut my wife off while she was speaking -- about to order our food--and he ran off to go talk to another table.. he disappeared for ten minutes! He literally interrupted her mid sentence as she was about to order and then disappeared!! 

I can definitely say we felt judged. After observing him with his other 3 tables (all of which 2-4 people, so he was NOT in the weeds) we realized he was offering all of them 5 star service. He was holding conversations with them, refilling their drinks, laughing.. we had none of that. Just because we look young and don't dress like business professionals. My wife and I were here to celebrate our 6mo wedding anniversary as well as her annual bonus from work-- we gladly would have bought dessert and another bottle of wine, but our service was awful. We typically tip well over 20% but tonight we left 18 and I feel like that's way over tipping for the HOT MESS EXPRESS That is ocean prime!!!!


Aside from our server specifically, we were in this unusually bright room which didn't seem to fit the mood of the rest of the restaurant. It was like we were in some office meeting at at hotel while all the cool kids were out in the normal parts of the restaurant.


Most bizarre dinner experience to date, seriously.

I would have given a 5 star however customer service and attitudes with waitresses and hostesses seem not to work well together. 

Dem grits tho, Yum!  I enjoyed my cheese grits, fried green tomato, bacon, biscuit, and cheese eggs I had.  It was the southern breakfast.  Prices were reasonable as well. 

Now, the hostesses was complaining because she didn't want to seat people and then lied to some customers about seating availability. Smh. We all have bad days, pull yourself to the side and regroup. 

Overall it's a nice place to have breakfast, lunch, or brunch.

Had a reservation for one night for a visiting relative. The room was neat and clean, but the beds were AWFUL! Mattresses so soft you could not move in the bed. Comforters that must have weighed 20 pounds that made my elderly family member feel actually trapped in the bed. All four pillows were marked soft, if you like feather beds this is your place, we were not fans.

Other than the awful beds ( a HUGE draw-back when the ultimate use for the room IS sleeping) the rest of the stay was ok. The breakfast buffet was nice but they put out pots of semi-brown water and called it coffee (they must know it was a joke to call what they served coffee), it was so weak you could see the bottom of your cup, but the food was good. They ran out of eggs but we showed up kind of late so I guess that is on us. I want to say I would stay here again because I have always like Holiday Inn Expresses, have stayed in them for years, but if you can't sleep well the rest of the amenities really are just bs.

A true diamond in the rough. Honestly one of the best and extremely affordable. As soon as you enter you are being welcomed at the door. We sit where we like and the waiter is extremely helpful and inviting. This was our first time in here so the waiter explains how the menu works and suggest on a couple of the most popular sushi rolls. Right away we were sold one them. We get our home made sangria and automatically begin to love it. Sushi came right away. We left and  usually when we leave a sushi place we leave like why did we eat so much, not here. Here we left satisfied and happy. Hope this helps because this just became our favorite place.

I found and purchased a mattress that I love at this Mattress Firm location.

The salesman wasn't too pushy sales wise until we sat down to pay. I was talked into a 'free' bed platform. When the bed was delivered, the platform wouldn't fit my bed. The guys setting up the platform said I could use some bricks to stand it on and just left it in my living room.

I noticed the price of the mattress went down $200 a couple of weeks after my purchase so I contacted the store to inquire about the 120 day price guarantee. Turns out, Mattress Firm does not price guarantee a mattress when it includes the free bed platform which they value at $500. This also affects the return value of the bed - say you want to return it - they subtract the $500 value of the platform from the purchase price of the bed. Just something to watch out for since this all came up after my credit card was charged.

I've taken my car here for both oil changes and inspections: they are awesome! If there is an issue with my car, even if it is out of their wheelhouse, they will explain it to me and tell me where to go to fix it. There are ALWAYS coupons available online. 

They do take longer than they should to complete the work, but given their quality of work and their prices, it is worth the extra wait time. I recommend dropping your car off earlier in the day and picking it up around dinnertime.

This place was wonderful the food was all made fresh and the cocktails at the bar were also wonderful. I never go to the same place twice while I'm on vacation because I want to experience as many places as possible. I went here 3 times.,

At this location on Seminole blvd. TINA deserves a raise. She was very patient and informative of the current offers on which items were good and walked us through the flavors of the drinks so we could make a choice. Positive attitude!

I just don't like octopus.  I want to like octopus, I keep trying it, but it always seems to be just a little too rubbery, a little too fishy, a little too gross.  No matter what.

I LOVED the octopus here.  Little char on the fatty outside skin, perfectly textured throughout... SO good.  

I loved the rest of that meal - ceviche was fantastic - but I dream of that octopus.  Yum.

WISH I COULD GIVE 6 STARS!!!

Family run business that strives for your best experience!

Anthony called at 7:15am this morning and said he could come out between 12:30-2:30pm today and asked if that was good for me...Yes!
Anthony called me again at 12:20 (before the window of time) and said he could be at my house in 30 minutes and asked if that was good for me...Yes!
Anthony arrived at my house and was well-mannered, polite, courteous, and well-groomed. 
He pulled my dryer from the wall and had diagnosed the issue within 2 minutes! He grabbed the part from his truck and had the dryer fixed in 20 minutes! 
To top off this wonderful service, he answered my questions about the issues I've experienced with my washer for FREE! (No service done on washer today, but if it continues to act up or get worse, Anthony is the first person I will call!)

CUSTOMER FOR LIFE!!! 
Will recommend this company to ALL my friends!!!

We wanted to have lunch, few folks there..pulled in to asked where to park (not Valet) they said by Gil Dogs! So we came to one of our favorite spots Gil Dogs!! Sorry unless there is Reasonable parking I'm not going.  thank you for responding the Valet Guys
Didn't mention anything about Free Valet...?

Went on a Friday night. Staff was ridiculously in the weeds. Took 20 minutes to get a drink at the bar,while the bartender served everyone around us,after we ordered. Food was overpriced, but my ahi salad was delicious.
The poor staff looked like deer in headlights throughout our dinner. Will not be back.

this is our usual morning go to.  they are friendly, fast, and always get your order correctly.  bagels are fresh and they are really great about substitutions.  They know us by name and always greet us with a smile.  and their coffee is awesome!

my go to is the asiago cheese bagel and sausage, egg, and cheese.  that thing can carry me all day and I usually don't need anything until dinner.  my boyfriend is a huge fan of their bagel and lox and schmear. and his son loves the  hashbrown bagel with egg and cheese.  so there are options for everyone!

Worst bus company experience ever !
We booked our tickets to go to Atlantic City three days before the Sunday.
The bus was supposed to be at 9:30 am. We were there with my wife and my daughter at 9:00 am.
Unfortunately the bus only left at 10:30 and they let us board in while there were not enough seats.
So my daughter did all the travel seated on my wife's knees while we paid a seat for her !
Once at Atlantic City, we were supposed to get a voucher of $20 foe the casino but an employer your us that given that we printed our bus ticket at home we couldn't get this voucher.
It turned out that when you print your ticket directly at the Greyhound terminal you get another ticket with to exchange for a voucher.
So why proposing the very convenient solution to print at home if you cannot even get the same bonuses ?
Finally to come back during the evening, same with we came in the line but the bus was already full, although our tickets clearly displayed the 6:45 pm ride.
Another bus came 10 minutes later but due to traffic jams, we were at home around 9:45 pm while it usually take 1:15 hour !
We took this company for the casino voucher but it is definitely not worth it.
Too bad I couldn't put 0 stars.
Jérôme

Hate to do this to you guys. But when I call 22 minutes b4 you close and tell me you've stopped all to go orders, this is the type of reviews you will get. Never tasted the food because apparently, they're busy closing early. If I owned this spot I'd be pissed my managers were so lazy.
When the weather is nice, there is no better place in town to sit and drink in a peaceful setting while enjoying a cocktail.  Beautiful courtyard.

5 stars - cocktails
3 stars - food

Cocktails were the best I had this trip. Could have had something to do with the fact we were sitting on the stuffy patio in 90-degree heat at lunch time. My swizzle, not too strong, had just the perfect alcohol content and balance. My companion ordered the Elysian Fields Forever and it was similarly refreshing.

Starters were nice. Shrimp and grits was perfect except I could have done without the creole mayo everyone puts on everything in New Orleans (but calls something different). Fresh tomato soup was a triumph of good ingredients.

As for the mains... that's where things fell flat. I guess we'd already had a few good po'boys by this point in the trip, and we were disappointed that every entree on the menu except for one was a sandwich! All were too heavy. The catfish poboy, while tasty, was covered in that same creole mayo and leaked greasy drippings as I bit into it. Not really what I wanted to eat in the middle of the day.  Cochon de lait was a good sliced cold roast pork sandwich, but nothing more.

Definitely would try more of the lighter fare here - small plates, appetizers, soups, salads... and of course more of those refreshing cocktails.

Likes:
-bread served with sweet butter--LOVED!!
-arepa appetizer
-split-level, vibrant old cuban ambiance

Dislikes:
-ropa vieja: this beef dish came out a bit too wet for my liking and was drenched in tomato. I have never seen ropa vieja come out so soupy
-vaca frita: the beef was served in cubes instead of shredded
-Waitress spoke poorer Spanish than what I remember from high school!

Cute place, but in terms of quality and authenticity, look elsewhere. I suppose that's the problem with chains.

Have been here twice and enjoyed it. Get here early, especially on Friday's, as you may not be able to get close to the place. Everyone else evidently likes it as well. I had the best fish and chips that I have ever had; awesome! The deck is very nice, weather permitting. Looking forward to going back.

I have been going to this gym for years! 
Love the gang here , Theresa the manger , Jennifer the personal trainer. And the gang behind the counter.
Also great protein shakes as well !
The gym has all the equipment you could possibly need for a work out ! 
The prices are awesome and if you get the tanning package with it even better!  Why go anywhere else!

Rich A.

If tasty food is all it takes to make a place great, the Falcon Coffee Bar has it in the bag. The empanadas are very tasty and the bodega sandwich is delicious. I look forward to trying more! The atmosphere is trying to grasp onto feng shui but dying plants have it just out of reach, but hey, minor detail. The only thing holding me back from giving this eclectic plant based cafe 5 stars is the service. There was no greeting when we walked in. We sat at the bar and perhaps it was our own fault to not be aware that you can not order from there. Eventually we had to interrupt the conversation of the employees to ask where we order... just 3 feet away. We will definitely return for the tasty food with high hopes of better service and perhaps an extra star.

I finally had the chance to go into Burlington coat factory yesterday. never been there but always see there commercials on tv. When you first walk in the store looks clean and put together but as you walked more into the store I could not believe the dirtiness of it and clothes just put anywhere. the sizes were all mixed up in every department. I actually went in to find a coat for my daughter for xmas. needless to say I walked out without one because I got so tired of thumbing through to just find her size. all sizes were totally mixed up. It ended up just making me mad and frustrated. also I have never seen associates at the register move so slow in my life. I have been in retail all my life and that was pathetic. better pick it up for xmas. I do like there prices but its not worth spending all day in there just to find the sizes I am needing. the shoes were all mixed up as far as sizing. Very frustrating. I cannot believe all the bad reviews on this store going several yrs back considering not a thing has changed from what I am reading from several years back compared to yesterday

Would not go there again. For some reason they were doing construction next to our room. I thought it was very rude and Loud . 
Check your room first !!!!!

Went and grabbed a small order of the buffalo fries with a side of chipotle sauce 
Small-10.56(w/military discount)

What happened? Empanadas are soaking in oil, dough seems like it's the GOYA brand orange type you can buy at your local shoprite. Mashed potatoes seem to be boxed rather than freshly mashed. Under the Moon used to be this high quality food place, the past 3 times I've been there the quality has been barely acceptable. I'm truly concerned, Management, please do something about it!!!
I gave it 2 stars because of the Chimichurri sauce and deserts, they continue to be fantastic.

I would not recommend staying with Hotel Preston.  Let's start from the beginning. The front desk employees were NOT professional. There was a line to check in, and I was happy to wait. I noticed 2 of the 3 employees at the front desk were having a personal conversation as we were all waiting to check in. There is no air conditioning in the hallways and barely any once I got into the room.  Only one of two elevators worked so you had to stand in the hot hallway waiting for the one elevator to stop on your floor. I went out for dinner the first night and when I came back, neither of my key cards worked, and it happened again on the third night. I did not have soap or shampoo/conditioner in the bathroom. BTW, the front desk has them behind the desk and will just hand them to you when you ask (this tells me it is a common occurrence at the hotel). There was an ironing board in the room but no iron. I asked the front desk for an iron and never received one. Now to the bed - the pink blanket at the end of the bed was wet. There was a blood drop on the white comforter, and a piece of black tar on one of the sheets. Now to the bill - you just get a lump sum of charges, not an itemized invoice. So I have no idea what I paid for. I've called 3 times to get an itemized bill and keep getting the same run around, "We only can send them to you with the total amount." When I asked to talk to someone who could get me that itemized invoice, I was told no. The only saving grace to my experience were the two servers at the bar for the morning breakfast (BTW, it is not a free buffet - you have to pay for your breakfast). They were informative and very helpful.  I now have two new friends.

BOMB THAI FOOD!!!

Best curry ive ever had !!! best thai vibes!!

Delicious pho! I always order the rare beef pho with veggie base, very flavorful broth and the medium size is almost more than I can eat. The Bun with grilled chicken is another favorite when I am not in the mood for soup. Thai tea and espresso are also both fantastic. Have not tried dine-in, but I order about once a week and their take-out service is great- always ready in 10-15 minutes and never any problems with the order.
Great coffee, food and restaurant vibe. Owner was incredibly nice and inviting. Highly recommend

We really wanted some oysters and happened to be walking around downtown so we headed over to Lucky Lobster. My boyfriend asked how fresh they were and the waiter joked about having to shuck them for us they were THAT fresh. We also ordered a kid's chicken tender meal for the little guy ($9 is ridiculous for a kids meal btw) and the grilled octopus appetizer. The oysters were terrible, tasted old like they had prob been sitting in water all week, the chicken tenders were over cooked, the fries were hard and had probably been refried multiple times (old) and the grilled octopus came out already cut up it seriously looked like they just handed us someone else's left over plate. It was really disappointing. Service was slow as well and they weren't all that busy for a Saturday night. Way overpriced for less then average food. Won't be back!

What a great place to visit if you are looking for truly pet enthusiasts staff, genuine and friendly service. The person who helped us was very knowledgeable about the environmentally safe fleas  treatment product we were looking for.It is out of our way, but so worth making the drive.

This was actually one of our favorite places. But last night we had a really poor experience. And unfortunately it was a my wife's birthday.

Here's a tip. When you make a reservation, ask NOT to be put in "the cabana room"...ever. We've been here about a dozen times and I never knew this room existed.

Flamestone has great ambiance, but this room is like sitting in the garage while the dinner party is in the house. There's not a piece of art in sight with the exception of the big screen TV that's not on. Unlike the rest of the restaurant, this room has a tile floor and very bright florescent lights. We felt like we were in a Denny's dining room. We spent most of our time repeating our sentences because we couldn't hear ourselves with the echo. This room should be used for it intended purpose only. A corporate meeting, storage or to park a car.

Our waitress didn't bring any bread with our salads, one of the steaks was a very poor cut and never should have been served. Then the waitress didn't even offer us any dessert options. She just brought a complimentary piece of cake for my wife while the rest of us sat wishing we had something too. 

It was just one of the worst dinner experiences we've ever had.  Not sure if we'll be back.

PARKING: free parking on the side of the building, in a small parking lot but close and convenient to the restaurant. 

RESTAURANT: food was amazing, very authentic, so tasty and delicious!! Prices are reasonable, and the portions are perfect for me. Service was warm and friendly 

There's indoor AND outdoor seating :)

My extended family and I have frequent this restaurant since 2001.  Over the years, the restaurant has changed owners (for the better) and had 3 remodels to the interior (nice improvements).  The food has improved with the current owner.  My extended family eats here more frequently than me.  
I do not care for the location & it is not convenient for us.

Last night, tired from traveling for 12 hours, my husband and I decided to stop in for Pho & BBH (spicy beef noodles).  Soups always soothe a weary soul.

Pho- hot & flavorful.  Even without the hoisin sauce, the broth was really good!  If you must drown your Pho with hoisin sauce- to us, that means the broth did not have sufficient flavor.

BBH- I ordered mine without tendon & without blood cake.  Most of all, I asked for mild.  My BBH was excellent!  Very flavorful broth without overwhelming heat!

Neither of us got bloated or sick- so that means there is no MSG.  This alone would earn 5 stars!

So, why only 4 stars?  Here is where Pho Quyen disappoints us.  Our soups barely had enough meats & there were barely enough vegetables for 1 bowl of soup!  Hence minus 1 point for lack of meat & vegetables.

Service was prompt & the young waitstaff were polite & knowledgeable enough to serve most guests.

We have been to other Vietnamese restaurants in the Tampa Bay Area (Tampa & St Pete), which we will not go back to any of them.  One was so bad, I had to write a review about them to warn customers of their unethical practice serving rancid Vietnamese food to a Vietnamese person (me) & fought with me about it! 

I am really hoping that the Tampa Bay Area Asian restaurants will grow & expand as much as Orlando...

For the time being, Pho Quyen is the only decent Vietnamese restaurant in Tampa in terms of good food that do not make you sick & they are clean.  So we will continue to support Pho Quyen in Tampa!

I have loved Painting With A Twist since they hit the scene!!  I have gone to the events of the knock-offs and they don't have the same standards of PWAT!!  

My last outing was a surprise for my Mom & Auntie for Mother's Day--I told them we were going to see Art..they were so happy they were creating Art!!  Such a fun day!!  

I enjoy the BYOB, the instructors are usually patient and helpful.  I figure if I keep going, I will have enough art for a gallery showing!!
Great place. Emeril owned restaurant. Pork chop was great and split the gumbo with a friend to eat as an appetizer.

I'm an out-of-towner here in Mt. Laurel and therefore totally dependent on Yelp.  This isn't such a bad thing, because it brings me to places like Pho Eden and the magical strip mall it's located in.  I say "magical" because it has not just a pho place but a Thai place, a Chinese place, an Indian place, a sandwich place, a fruit store, and an Indian grocery.  If it had a good consignment shop it would be the shopping center of my dreams.  Seriously.

Anyway.  Pho Eden was yummy.  Not the best pho chay (vegetarian pho) I've ever had, but definitely good.  The broth was really what did it--delicate and just salty enough, it was a nice base for all the basil, sriracha, lime, and jalapeno I added.  The tofu was fried a bit too much, and I was disappointed to find that the only vegetables were cabbage and like two slices of carrot.  The flavors were definitely good, though.  I would return and have something else.

Note: There's a $10 minimum on your credit card, which is a hard number to get to when you're eating alone and the prices are so low.

Such a cool atmosphere, great coffee, and relaxing place to study. The staff is friendly and helpful. The coffee and lemonade takes a little longer than compared to a chain place like Starbucks but that means it's fresh and they care about the product they sell! As a student I love this place, keep up the great work!

I'm visiting St Pete for a few days and found this place walking along Beach Dr NE. Of all the coffee shops I managed to stumble into a gem! Cassis has great coffees but what stands out is their incredible staff and adorable shop! Shoutout to my girl Brittany for getting me caffeinated every morning! Despite being on a work trip, this coffee shop made me feel like a local! I can't wait to come back!

I really enjoy this place that being said I'm not a big chicken wing person but I love their salt and vinegar wings and their gar par fries are sooo good. Last time I went I ordered their buffalo burger and it was fantastic. I may have a new favorite. There's so much selection with wing flavors it's almost too much selection but that is their thing I guess. The service has always been great I'll definitely continue to come here.

We arrived on time with 7 of us. I had made the reservation thru open table and originally it  was for 6. I had gone on line and changed it thru their web site.Glitch somewhere, but the maiter d made it work for us. On a very busy Sat nite they just seemed to get it done!-Great food, super friendly service.. i felt the prices were fair and the portions were really good too! This was my husbands 60th b'day party dinner and they even brought out a whole cake and we sang and blew out the candles! OOOPPS, we did not bring a cake, but they quickly whisked it away, did some service recovery with the table it should have been for and all was well! -Quick thinking wait staff/.manager on duty made it all very smooth.We ordered desserts off the menu which we all shared...The cake story has provided us many laughs...and will live on! Keep doing what your doing Toma's its working!
Great for a date night or a family outing. I love their white pizza, I have had a lot of white pizzas and the one I had here was definitely in the top three.

Based on the yelp reviews and a recommendation from a friend I reserved a table for dinner this past Saturday night. We were seated right away by the very friendly hostess. Passing by the windows of raw meat hanging from hooks on our way to our table was a new experience. Love the atmosphere though. Cocktails were all yummy. Since it was our first visit we went straight for the mixed grille so we could try everything. My party and I agreed that every single piece of meat on that plate was cooked and seasoned to perfection! Just wish there had been a lot more. For a party of 4 hungry people it wasn't quite enough. So then we ordered the ravioli, more crispy potatoes and beat salad. Also very delicious. Our waiter was super sweet. I'll be back.

We booked this restaurant for a wedding dinner for about 40 people.  Danny, the manager, was wonderful to work with.  They set up tables in the back room for us, complete with burgundy tablecloths.  The food was delicious.  We personally tried the lettuce wraps and the Jazmine shrimp and both were wonderful.  Everyone was very happy with their food.  The wait staff was great, which wasn't easy accommodating such a big crowd with many young children.  They even cut and served the wedding cake (which was brought in) with no extra charge.  Everyone came away happy and stuffed.  We could not have been more pleased!

Went here our first night in Reno. Excellent vibes and no line. We ordered the braised pork bowl, string beans with minced pork, and egg wrapped pancake. The beans were very flavorful and go well with rice. Pork bowl was also very authentic. Pancake was really good, the egg was wrapped in layers around a scallion pancake! Highly recommend visiting here if you want good Taiwanese food in Reno.
Only had gelato but it was good. Wish I would have seen the cupcakes before I ordered the gelato. They were hidden in the corner. Cute place.
Small gem. Great wings w/ unique homemade sauces. Deep dish is great...better than Chicago. I will come back every time we are in town.

My review is just for the freshwater aquatics here.

I've been coming here for 20+ years just for that particular section and there is still none better in our area!  (I think this location used to be originally located in the strip center on US19 and 30th Avenue?)

Those other chain pet stores will gladly sell you a fish like a Pleco or Chinese algae eater.  I just need this type of fish in my tank for a few years to do their algae-cleaning work.

But when they grow too big?  It is cruel to keep them in my less-than-50-gallon tanks, so I need to turn them back in as a sort of "rescue."

The local pet chains are jerks and have NO morals about this common, freshwater aquarium problem!  The sell this species of fish, but will NEVER accept them back, even for free!

Those pet chains just leave me the option of flushing the fish, euthanizing it, or release it into a local freshwater pond??  (Yes, one of those chain store employees actually gave me those 3 options, but what do you expect!)

None of those 3 options are proper or ethical.  Plus, releasing non-native species into our wilds here is also illegal!

AH is the best!  When my algae eaters get a bit too big, I just bring them here to give them away for free so they can be passed on to those with larger aquariums or ponds!

AH have even offered me trade value in return!  So nice!  But I have always refused it.  They are doing me a great and humane fish "rescue" service.

I no longer spend any of my money on freshwater aquarium supplies outside of here.

The love all animals and have morals, unlike the big-box machines that just care about profit and the later animal welfare be-damned!

Simply killing an innocent aquarium fish after years of doing it's algae-eating job faithfully for so many years is damn-near evil to me!

So 5-stars to AH for having a caring heart and understanding...  THANK YOU!

First of all I frequent the casino and its restaurants weekly, I had not tried the Rez as Council Oaks is a favorite of mine but the other day I thought I would give it a try. This review has nothing to do with the bar staff who took care of us. They were wonderful, very attentive and helpful. The food however was another story. It was very disappointing. I ordered the chopped salad which was not chopped in the least and basically the same type of salad you could get at any pizza joint. The salad dressing had so much vinegar in it I couldn't even eat it and it came with bread that was so burnt it tasted like a bad grill. Not a great start to the evening. Next I ordered the ribs which came with fries and more of the burnt bread. The ribs were dry and tough and the fries were pretty dried out. They were busy but no excuse for serving this kind of food. I didn't complain to staff as I knew they would only offer to make the meal again and I didn't have the confidence it would be prepared any better given how busy the restaurant was. All in all a very disappointing experience and a waste of money.

I visited the Quest Diagnostics on Nightwalker Road in Brooksville, Florida. I have had plenty of experience in going to be poked, but this occasion was the most negative I have experienced in quite awhile. I walked in. Instead of a friendly face to greet me, there was an impersonal computer that did not work as expected. It looked like (and so I was told it was)an Apple, but the screen was temperamental. After a lot of frustration, I finally finished with this joke of a computer and sat down to wait. Shortly after I began waiting, a very elderly lady came in and had no clue how to use the computer. I felt sorry for her and helped her through the process. She got registered, but I couldn't help but wonder how others like her managed. No one in Quest was there to help those who needed it. What a joke of a business. 

When I finally got called back, the place looked dirty and the employees seemed disorganized. I just hope the samples I left will be correctly read and distributed. I WILL NOT go back to this facility. If you need to go to a lab, I would find another company. In the meantime, no more tests for me through this lab!!

Bait and switch all the way, unfortunately. 

I went to the store on Nov. 1st, made selections with a representative who placed the order for me assuring I'd have both pieces of furniture by Dec. 14th. After spending time on the phone for more than an hour on both 12/7 and today (12/16), I still do not have delivery of my furniture nor a clear delivery date before Christmas. 

Completely frustrated that a company can take nearly $2000 of my money right away, set expectations on delivery and then completely fail to deliver. The fact that one of the two pieces has been sitting at the delivery hub since Nov. 16th is even more frustrating. Why not deliver that piece right away if there were unforeseen problems with the delivery of the second piece? Customer services' solution was to "promise" a $100 store credit at the completion of delivery----so, why not just deliver the chair that's ready and waiting instead??? I already paid $100 for delivery that hasn't happened yet!

The topper is that I have company coming in for the holidays and according to customer service my furniture probably won't be here to accommodate. Had I known this on November 1st, I would have gone elsewhere.
Took my money for service and ran and then didn't return my emails and requests for my money back
Always in the mood for an awesome sandwich and fries. And not to mention the peanuts!

Not coming here again! I gave this location three shots and each time I walked away more disappointed. In this instance, I ask if they have nachos (it's not clearly listed on their menu), the young man behind the counter takes a moment from smacking his gum to look up at the menu, he asks an employee walking by where the nachos are.....that employee smacks the bottom of the menu board while saying "here"......hmmmm, not where he so appropriately said they were. Anyway, I l order nachos with asada. A lady called out our number I get the tray, I look her in the eyes and say "thank you", she replied "yeah" after smacking her lips together as if in total disgust that she had to say something to me. 
Carne Asada must be code word for cubes of gelatinous cold fat chunks because that's what I got on my nachos. The cheese wasn't melted, the food was cold. HOW IN THE WORLD DO YOU MESS UP NACHOS!! People PLEASE!! I took a few bites, deemed them not worth the calories and trashed the meal. As I walked out the same gal at the counter was emptying the trash. In an attempt to get her to smile, I said "have a nice night".....she just glared at me. Ok then, crappy service, crappy food=me never coming back! 
I hope the owner reads this, news travels fast around here!

I have been going to Todd for several years now. He only recommends the visits that are needed and nothing more. I like that he explains what he's doing and the reasons why. Times i have asked what I could do to help my situations he took the time to show me stretches etc. I have been having lower back pain for a year and half. It had been longer than that since I have been in. (I had a change in job and just didn't make the time to go In) A little over a month ago, besides my lower back pain I ended up having upper back pain. The past week it got to where the pain was running down to my elbow on the left side. Anyways, I finally called and even though busy during Holidays I was able to be seen. The pain in my lower back (that's been there for over a year) is completely gone!! My upper back pain isn't near what it was. He did explain he only got a little movement but expected it since it's been so long since I've had any adjustments done. YUP...got another appointment and so thankful!! I've had 2 nights of actual sleep. NO tossing and turning which I'm sure my husband appreciated too! Thank you!!

Ordered a mixed drink here that had pineapple juice in it. I watched the bartender make it & she used the entire can of pineapple and a little more from another can. All it tasted like was juice. I had to ask for another shot on the side to go with my juice. Lol. I bartended also so I know the pours and this was definitely under the standard. It also took a few minutes to get service because she was on her phone before realizing we were there.
horrible job not what i asked for, i came in asking for dip acrylic but she did gel acrylic instead , the males at the salon do good nails though...

*This is a Margarita Review!* I didn't have time to eat an entree but wanted to  bike over to try their house margarita since I personally consider margaritas a vital life essence. Anyway, I order the Festiva Margarita with Casa Pacifica Reposado, Caballero, Citrus and a salt rim. While they were making my drink my waitress, Lex, gave me a  little cast iron skillet of chicharrones with a generous helping of hot sauce - not a bad bonus. 

The margarita was in a somewhat difficult to hold on to glass with a very thick rim of salt. Turns out the salt rim was super necessary as this marg was v sweeeeet. I like my margs on the savory side so I was a bit taken aback. It was good, but if you're a margarita enthusiast like me, you like your margs true to form. 

Def coming back to try the food, but most likely won't make this my go to margarita spot.

Very Friendly, knowledgeable staff!  

I was very impressed with the selection and the knowledge of the staff member helping me.

I work hard for my money and Head Lines will get my business everytime!

If you want to be treated like a passenger going through security at the airport go across the street to the Magic Bus they will dress you down!  No charge!

We Have always had great experiences with Bazil's to go. The pizza is the best in town and the staff is always friendly. Definitely recommend if you're in the foothills, and they deliver! My mom was in town and I had to make her try the Bazil's special. She agrees- the best!

My visit to Outlaw Pizza on Mardi Gras day was super weird.  When we walked in the place was empty except for a couple guys at the bar.  The bartender/server told us that they're dead and sending home the regular servers but that we could still get pizza. While my first urge was to leave and go to the mexican place next door (that was packed), I decided to stick it out.  I ordered a personal pizza and a soft drink.  The food came out pretty fast but was overall the service was lackluster.  We were never offered refill or even silverware.  

The thing that saved this experience was that the pizza was really tasty and I enjoyed the crust.  I still couldn't get over the feeling that they just wanted to close up and go home.  I'll probably just skip this place next time I'm in the city.

Hattie B's is an above average fried chicken joint. 

Don't buy into the hyperbole of the Yelp reviews. I read these reviews beforehand and the image was conjured that some magician-turned-cook was reaching up into the high heavens and pulling down Fried Food Fit for the Gods and selling it for $10 to $15. The reviews are so hyperbolic that you'd think this can't even be chicken anymore - maybe Hattie B has discovered some animal in the Smoky Mountains that tastes like chicken yet is even better. 

Nope.

Lets start with the positives because you won't corral this many Yelp Elites without something special and distinctive. 

The secret sauce is the fried breading on the chicken. It's special and above average. It's thin and crispy in a distinctive way. The heat doesn't burn the tongue. It's a flare and then simmers - but still noticeable. Similar to Sriracha. You should probably get a drink. 

The sides are above average too. I got the black bean salad, potato salad, pimento mac and cheese, and banana pudding. That's a lot of food and only cost $17. I figured yolo. When you see this many Yelp Elite reviewers raving, it's kind of like a cosmic star alignment where you push the boundaries. 

The mac n' cheese is creamy without being too cheesy. Highly recommended. Black bean salad. Good with curious accents. Potato salad is just solid and good. 

To keep this in perspective, Hattie B's is a hole-in-the-wall restaurant that serves fried food fast (sans line). High customer volume, high turnover, with short preparation time. It's a good distinctive fast food experience. It's one of the better fried chickens I've tried. It's not a transformative experience that redefines your understanding of fried chicken. At least for me. 

There's a good chance I'll be back again, although I am in no rush.

Now here's why I give this place a B: My two main gripes: Outside of the breast, there ain't enough meat (that's why you should get the tenders or chicken breast meal). And although I got the medium it's pretty spicy and that distracted me from my enjoyment. The spiciness is also not a perfect blend with the fried chicken, where two essences mingle perfectly.

Here's what you need to know: Expect a wait, regardless what time of day you come out. Gratefully, I only had to wait 40 minutes. You have to hunt for parking so bring quarters. There is cheap metered parking but some parking lots charge $10. Don't use a parking lot.

It's good and worth the wait - but I don't think this is a bucket list foodie stop.

As a self-regarded high connoisseur of crap food (junk food), I appreciated the stop because it shed new light on fried chicken. But I don't feel it accomplishes the majesty and heights and full potential of what fried chicken can be.

I used to go to this grocery back when it was owned by A&P. Since Breaux Mart has taken over the prices have gotten higher and the quality of customer service has plummeted. 

Worse, when visiting recently I have seen Breaux Mart's contractor illegally booting people. When I get groceries I don't appreciate being spied on by some sleazy fellow staking out the parking lot. Although the law requires Breaux Mart to make the booting decision themselves, they have left it up to this dubious individual who isn't complying with various provisions of the city's boot law. I've complained personally to Breaux Mart but they refuse to take any action. 

 The prices are bad enough, but when combined with this kind of callous disregard for the law and for their customer base, it's clear that Breaux Mart isn't the type of place I want to shop.

This was our first time here. If the crowd had not shown me where to enter, I'm not sure I would have found the entrance easily. It seems like a back entrance, and is not well marked. It was a busy Sunday morning, and the wait for a table was about 15 minutes. Coffee is pricey, but you can serve yourself from a variety of choices. 

Menu has many interesting things I'd like to try next time. Lunch looked as good as breakfast! Our meals were very filling; neither of us finished. I liked the fries more than my " fried potatoes" that came with my breakfast. They would not allow a substitution for one side for another in the breakfast combos.
Staff is friendly but busy.

We approached dining at Stella with an open mind, a sense of adventure, and an open wallet. It's difficult to know where to begin with this review, as so many aspects of the food were mind-blowing, and the service was so seamless that the entire experience seemed to melt together into this blissful memory of a meal.

I'm aware that the menu changes frequently with the seasons and according to what's available, so your menu will vary. I enjoyed a gnocchi appetizer, which turned me from a "gnocchi is alright, I guess" to a "GNOCCHI IS MY ONLY FOOD GROUP" kind of person. For my main course, I had the filet, which had been expertly prepared by sous vide and then a quick sear. The meat was accompanied with an Asian-influenced brown sauce, as well as several vegetables that couldn't have been more perfect or delicious. The lobster dish on the menu that night was also incredible. We had dessert, too, which included a very inventive dish that involved pink lemonade. 

In fact, every dish was inventive and fun, as you've probably heard they are, and I'd recommend going in with a blank slate and an open mind when it comes to making choices, if you're a less adventurous diner. If you want the full experience, have more funds, and are really adventurous, go for the tasting menu, and maybe add the wine pairings. I don't think there's anything in New Orleans quite like this. 

Prices are incredibly high, but you get what you pay for in artistry, an incredible dining experience, and first-class service. It's truly romantic and wonderful for a special occasion. Reservations are a must, and I believe jacket is required for men. If you've planned to go to Stella, go, and make sure at least one diner in your party orders an iced tea.

We just walked in and the lady said they didn't have any empty tables. But there was an empty table in front of us but the lady didn't know if the people have gone to the other side of the restaurant or not even though they left nothing personal behind like a jacket. So she's told us to sit at the bar which was filled with dirty glasses and napkins. After sitting there for 10 minutes and being totally ignored we got up and left. Horrible first impression and we won't be back.

When my wife and I started looking for a paver company to replace our old concrete driveway, we started by looking on the BBB website for highly rated companies.  That narrowed down the field pretty quickly.

We asked three companies to quote, and as the prices were all pretty similar, it quickly became evident that service would be the deciding factor.  One of the three immediately eliminated themselves by not responding to a query regarding design.

We next went to look at some recent jobs in our area (Wesley Chapel / Lutz) done by the two bidders, and were told one of the companies had repeatedly promised to start the project, but failed to appear.  

Then we visited Bay Brick Pavers showroom to select the material for our job.  The owner and his wife were very cordial and professional, so we left a deposit and scheduled our installation.

They touched bases with us a couple of times, and reconfirmed when they would start.  The bobcat operator was prompt and capable.  He removed the old driveway and prepped the area so the installers were ready to go the next morning.

The installers were just as good, leaving the finished driveway neat and clean.  The entire job was completed in less than two days.  

The price was exactly as quoted, with no hassles or problems.  My wife and I can't speak too highly of Ted and crew.
Walked in looking to buy a new gun, showed they had a ton in the pics online of a large variety of guns, they had like 25 guns for sale,  I personally own more guns then this gun store. Lol

Love this place! I tried some tacos at the Johnny Sanchez in Baltimore's Horseshoe Casino and was excited to try more here. First of all, the space is really cool. One wall is covered in a brightly painted mural plus there are several Chihuly like chandeliers hanging in the middle of the space. 

Our group took over one of the largest tables and the server was able to handle our crowd and make everyone happy. We started off with chips and 2 kinds of salsa: tomatillo and grilled tomato. The grilled tomato was our favorite - just enough heat and a tinge of sweetness. 

I started off with the Tuna Agua Chile. It was fresh, spicy but not so much that it overpowered the fish. I loved the different textures of avocado and radish. A friend ordered the brussels spouts salad. Wow! What a dish! This would be fantastic for Thanksgiving. Crispy brussel sprouts tossed with a jalapeño vinaigrette then topped with cotija cheese and pomegranates. My goat tacos arrived with pickled cactus and queso fresco. I was in heaven. I thought the 2 small tacos may be too small, but it was perfect. I loved the extra heat in these dishes which I think the Baltimore venue lacks. 

Great space and great food!

I have taken my girls here for over 5 years now and we love it! We have seen it grow and switch owners a couple of years ago but it still remains to be one of local favorite restaurants. You pay a bit more for Pho compared to South Philly but it's worth it since it's only 5 mins from our home... plus it's a byo!   My girls expanded their palettes and love their fried rice, pad Thai and drunken noodles. Their green curry is also tasty! They have great service. You still see the previous owner helping out and greeting the patrons.

Average. I can see how this is a good place for a game. But the stars speak for themselves. Had the mushroom burger and it was ok. Sweet potato fries are not bad. The mango lemonade was the highlight!

This chain seems to be going downhill fast. They eliminated bussers to save a buck and forced the already-too-busy waitstaff to clear tables which looks awful and your table basically never gets cleared anymore of empty plates or glasses until you ask them to take them. I'm convinced the burger patties are getting smaller too as my burger this time around was more bread and toppings than actual burger. We used to be regulars but will not be coming here as much until Red Robin gets their act together.

My old Mercedes came due for the B Service. Instead of going to the Mercedes dealership, I took it back to Tony's Auto Repair. Not only did they perform the needed service but did more than that and charged me a lot less money than the dealership would have. I am very pleased with their service, their knowledge of Mercedes autos, attention to detail and their taking good care of my car.

I bought tickets to see the production of A Christmas Carol on a whim as something to fill our weekend in Indianapolis back in December. I wasn't sure what to expect, but after all was said and done, I was surprised at how much we enjoyed the performance.

The set, wardrobe, lighting, acting - everything was top notch. We bought value tickets for the OneAmerica Stage, and didn't have a problem seeing or hearing anything. Everything is pretty much the same in the mezzanine and there isn't really a bad seat in the house.

First of all, I am a big supporter of local independent business. Bono's is right up my alley. I did a quick search of pizza joints on Yelp and found Bono's. After scanning The menu I called in for a small Chicago style pizza with sausage, pepperoni, mushroom, and green peppers. 
The pie was ready in about 45 minutes and we carried out at the Watson-South City address. 
The pizza was hot and full of flavor. The sausage was spicy and finely diced. The mushrooms were plump and didn't taste tinny, indicating they were probably fresh. Chunks of tomatoes were on top of the pizza with a mild but sweet sauce. The pepperoni was sliced large and was a bit salty.. The green peppers were also diced and easy to digest without tasting overwhelming. 
We liked the pizza and a small was more than enough for two of us. 
We also order a small salad and...well... the pizza was very good. This is not a salad joint. 
I would have no problem recommending Bono's to anyone looking for a tasty Chicago style pie.
Delicious food, clean restaurant, and fast service. The best sweet tea from any fast food restaurant in the area!

Beautiful atmosphere...loved the prime rib sandwich at Harry & Izzys and thought I was getting the same thing here....noooo...no great slices of beef.just one hunk of fatty meat...sorry Lounge..know you're new...keep trying...

I love the NPR guy and his raving review. This place is a train wreck. It is one of the most awful dealerships I have ever been to and I have been to a lot of dealerships. I also have encountered the sales manager, Craig, and he lied to my face about why an employee wasn't there to assist me after the receptionist had told me the reason. I guess appointments mean nothing to these people and I don't think Craig could have been more rude to me. And lets not forget my lovely encounter with the toothless former Honda manager.  He's quite a jewel and couldn't tell the truth to save his life.  So what I am saying is YOU can go ANYWHERE and have a salesman or manager be rude to you, lie to you, manipulate you and care only about closing the deal no matter what it takes. So why go to this dealer where I guarantee you that happens.  So where did I buy from?  Ferman Mazda and Fitzgerald Hyundai. I love Fitzgerald Hyundai's pricing system, that buying experience can not be beat and I will recommend them all day, every day.  Mike was my salesperson-former EMS guy. And Jack was not what you expect from a sales manager...very friendly and pleasant.

First time there ...So let's start by saying their yellowtail sushi is NOT yellowtail...never saw yellowtail sushi with red dots in it! Can you say stingray?!! Also DO NOT get the combo of  yellowtail, salmon and tuna rolls! It was a bunch of  mush wrapped in rice!! "What the heck is that!" my dinner guests asked. Embarrassing moment right there. Do not walk, run as far as you can from this restaurant!! I've had better sushi at Publix, don't waste your money.

I've been associated with New Age realty for last 3 years. Awesome group. I'd would love to have them around but I am relocated a bit far away. Recommend to anyone but especially to College students who are looking for trouble-free place to live. 

Michael Park

This is a must do stop in NOLA whether you live here or are visiting. Lafitte's is a popular stop for tour group as it's the oldest consistently running bar in the US (or so I've heard). It's old, divey with great low brow specials and ghost stories to entertain.

I been here twice. The first time was for dinner and second time for brunch. 
The restaurant has a foreign country vibe, you almost feel like you aren't in America!! In a good way! 
I love their hummus and their latte! 
They have an outdoor space for the summer which is very romantic! 
Make sure to make reservations in advance because it gets booked up fast

I love driving new cars...I hate buying new cars. I visited recently and took a test drive in a used Mercedes. While I was doing the test drive I explained I didn't know much about Maseratis. My salesman Chance did a great job teaching me all about them. The dealership closed at 6pm which is about when we arrived. Chance never brought it up. In fact he got a car off the showroom for me to test drive. Long story short, I drove away in my new car around 9 that evening. I typically take forever to make a decision. Jim the finance guy also stayed the whole time and couldn't have been more helpful. Don't hesitate to stop by...be sure to ask for Chance.

The happy hour deals are really good and the food is great for how reasonable their prices are. The brewery/restaurant itself is huge and spacious with plenty of both indoor and outdoor seating. Good place to take a date or get beers and wings with friends and watch a game
Really good Mexican food! They give you two kinds of salsa a mild and spicy both were great. Service was really good as well!

I would not recommend anyone to this gym. I joined for the summer while I was home from school, and was appalled by how rude the management & customer service was. Initially I was mad about being charged for an extra month that wasn't able to be utilized, but now, I just feel bad for how miserable everyone woking there is.

Virtua in Voorhees is expensive, but worth every penny.

Grabbed a quick lunch here when I was in the area. Easy to miss when you're speeding down the highway, but it's right next to the Winn-Dixie plaza! Plenty of seating -- it wasn't too crowded on a weekday. I took it to-go and my meal lasted me 3-4 meals! Got the smothered fried pork chop dinner. Their portions are HUGE, and come with so many different things: rice+gravy, 2 large slices of pork chop, veggies, potato salad, and a huge piece of cornbread. They threw in some sweet potatoes as well, which was not part of the meal I ordered according to their menu, which was nice. All of this for ~$10, what a steal for some great quality food! I'm not from the area, so the food was a little salty for my taste, but I enjoyed the guilty pleasure. Two Sisters definitely did not disappoint! I will definitely be back to try the smothered okra and shrimp, which was highly recommended to me by the kind lady who took my order.

We knew we had to go on a swamp tour while visiting NOLA, and this is one of the few who don't use airboats(they scare me, lol) and they received good reviews so it was an easy decision. We chose to drive ourselves out to save the money, and just an FYI our GPS was telling us to use a different exit than the directions listed on the back of the brochure. We followed the brochure and had zero problems finding it. I was a little worried when I saw that the restrooms were outside, but they were kept very clean and well stocked. Inside the building they have a large gift shop as well as an adorable baby alligator! After checking in and receiving a wristband we had about 20 minutes to wander the store and then sat at one of several picnic tables in the shade. There is a large cat colony that appears to live under the building, and while some of the cats didn't let people get too close, others were happy for attention. It was great seeing that their ears were clipped-a sign that they received vet care and were fixed. While my husband and I both love cats, for those that don't they don't care for them, they aren't all over people-we had to go to them. Finally on to the tour... Because we were visiting so late in the year, we knew we would not see as many alligators as people who visit in the summer. When booking the reservation we were told that the tours in the middle, hottest part of the day would have the best chance of seeing the most, and largest alligators. As soon as everyone got settled into their seats, our tour guide Jacob got our boat off the dock and headed immediately towards the spot he found the larger gators on the previous tour, and luckily they were still there. In all I think we saw roughly 5 or 6, with one being hungry/active enough to come to our boat for hot dogs. The rest were just sunbathing and ignored us. 
After that we headed off to a part of the swamp where the boar lived. They definitely ended up being my favorite part of the tour. Each one had a name and Jacob(and likely all the other tour guides) knew quite a bit about each one of them and their individual personalities. The large mom of the bunch was very friendly and probably would have jumped in the boat with us if she could have!
After spending roughly 15-20 minutes watching the playful boars we continued on, and went by a family of raccoons who were also used to the boats and what they meant(food!!).
We saw a few large birds here and there, and also were shown some interesting fishing shacks. Jacob was a great guide with a good sense of humor and an enormous love of both the nature and the animals we saw. My husband and I were both very happy with our decision to go with Cajun Encounters and would definitely choose them again if we ever find ourselves in the area during the summer.

OMG i see results after my first session. I purchased a Groupon for the laser removal. I mean it was an amazing deal for a Brazilian treatments. I believe it is mid $300s for a huge area. I gave it a chance and it's working. I am African American so I'm always skeptical when it comes to spa treatments especially laser treatments since there are so many horror stories. I have 5 more treatments and will keep you updated eeekk!!!
This was a surprise! The inside was very nice and clean. The service was outstanding. The food was great! I will be returning to try all the different options they had on their menu.

I don't like writing reviews for less than 4 stars, I want to point out what's good in the world and leave the rest to take care of itself.

The circumstances are a bit special though with our visit to Carnivore. We went there for my wife's birthday, and she's been doing Keto. This place is ideal for keto - with a meat entree you have the choice of a starch, a vegetable or a side salad. No embarrassing having to ask for substitutions. 

We went on prime time Saturday night. It was packed and even though I did online check in we still waited about 20 minutes. NBD. We all ordered ribeye with side salads and also ordered a side butter to melt on the steak.

The choices are Worcestershire chive, blue cheese or garlic Parmesan, and they're all good. This is key because the meat is simply grilled without much hint of spice or even salt. Once again, keto-friendly. 

Now, even though she doesn't like any pink in her meat she ordered it medium, as did the rest of us. I suspect because it was packed, our steaks were undercooked and, thus, she got a medium rare steak with the blue cheese butter on top.

She said it was terrible. Well, her tastebuds are out of whack, I reckon from the ketosis she is going through. My ribeye with the chive butter was pretty good, I thought. 

We also ordered jalapeño poppers to start and they're not bagged, they're handcrafted and tasty even though there was only 3. Of course, that got a few grumpy grumbles too from the birthday girl who was probably wishing she could be face down into a huge platter of carbonara at Cunettos but hey, keto, dear.

Ok, I thought the food was good. They have priced themselves in the area between Texas Roadhouse and Capital Grille so that's the bar to be cleared, and two out of three of us thought they had. 

Or perhaps I was just swayed by the $9 Basil Hayden old fashioned which was outstanding and severely good value. 

I'd go again, just perhaps order it a bit more done than usual.

Btw: I just went over this review and found it substituted 'Leto' for 'keto'. So my wife was doing Leto. Hucking spellcheck.

Hotel was just ok. We will not be staying here again. We checked in around 11pm and literally just needed to sleep for our drive home in the morning so our drive was safe and we were alert. Well let me tell you sleep is hard to come by here if noises keep you up. All night you could hear people loudly talking walking down the hall then entering their room and letting the door slam. After finally falling asleep in the early morning hours we were woke up at 7am by loud Spanish talking, upon waking I stuck my head out the door to see what the commotion was and it was the maids conversing/yelling outside the room next to us. One looked at me and said morning and then continued talking. This is ridiculous and unacceptable for hotel guests to be woken by staff yelling in the hall in an argument Ive fashion. This yelling continued for over 30 minutes. We checked out a lot earlier than planned and upon checking out I asked for a manager, alerted her to the issue and was told she will speak to the maids and gave a half hearted sorry. Hotel cleanliness was ok, only surface cleaning done as the bathroom was dusty and had hairs stuck to the door. Complete waste of money, I'd have been happier flushing the money than giving it to a company for a service we pretty much didn't receive.

This place was horrible. We drove from about 3 hours from St. Louis and I was expecting better. Not a good atmosphere. NOTICE: If you don't like your order, they will not make it right. I ordered the jasmine milk tea with tapioca. First, the milk or tea itself tasted like it had gone bad. Second, the tapioca was so undercooked, it was still hard. I would have been willing to keep drinking if one or the other was good but both were horrible. I just read through the whole thing away after two sips. Never ever coming back.

I did not have a great experience! I will not be back! This is not what I expected from a "Jamaican restaurant". The food was not good. It was dry and flavorless. There was nothing Jamaican about the food besides the name. The service was okay. This is not a buffet by the way.

I wanted an authentic Philly cheese steak and some locals said this was the place to go..they were right.
The cheese steak is huge. I was only able to eat half and they were kind to wrap up the other half and I ended up flying back to Ohio with it (their wrap job was good and was still in one piece when I returned). I ate next day and still was awesome.
Of course I looked clueless when I walked in there and I must of had that "I'm not from here" look...the ladies running the register/orders were super helpful. I told them what I wanted and they made it happen.

Next time I'm in the area I will definitely be back!! So good!!

Yeeeaaaaaahhh  Pappy's!

I've been here the last few times we've visited St. Louis, and both visits have been downright awesome.  The first time introduced me to their great service (they gave my infant daughter a t-shirt!) and the second time reminded me how damn good their barbeque is.  "Burnt ends" isn't something on the bbq menus here in Florida, and it definitely should be.  Topped with some great sweet sauce, I felt I could eat 5 sandwiches.  Awesome potato salad and great beans, too.

Service was friendly again, and for a line 12 people deep, they sure got through them quick so I could get my sandwich.  Every time I'm in St. Louis, I'm here.

I had a wonderful experience with Christina. She made sure the wax was to my likely before I left. I am definitely planning on coming back for the next year since I purchased 12 more waxes from the nicest front desk person. She made sure I got the best deal.

I got there as soon as they opened and the service was excellent from the moment I got there. I wish I got her name but my nail tech did an AMAZING job. It's so hard to find someone who does a seamless acrylic set but she executed as well as filing them perfectly. The best acrylic set I've had. My pedicure was also amazing. He took his time to make sure my toenails were clean, filed, all dead skin gone which I appreciated so much and to make it better the massage had me passing out hahaha. They definitely do not rush your experience and do a quality job! I highly recommend.

The food was great but the service was lacking. I sat at the bar and my server was not very friendly. She actually left before I finished my meal and drinks.  To make things worse another server asked me how I was doing and when I said ok she said they were just trying to clean up the bar. I noticed that they seem to cater to the larger parties much more than a single guest. This place could have a great bar service scene for a middle end restaurant but not yet.

We have been bere twice and likley wont go back. We have had the past 2 times.  She isnt rude per say but is rather unpleasant.  Her manner of setting down the plates can be seen as passagressive .  Rushing to bill tables and not doing a second quality check gives a feeling of being unwelcome. 

With my allergies, I told the server that I had many allergies and when I ordered, I had asked for a modification  (sub one fruit for another due to allergies).  When the food arrived, there was no mention of the allergy,so I wasn't even sure she had noted it.  I had to open it up to be sure there was nothing in there that shouldn't have been.  In my experience as a server at multiple restaurants/bars, it is pretty standard to note allergies upon delivery.  Hence why I wasn't sure it had been clarified to kitchen. 

During our meal it was fairly quiet in the restaurant and the servers were chatting.  Their topic was customers that had just left.  They were talking about the customers in front of us, which only made us wonder if we would be their next discussion topic.  

Overall it was rather unprofessional. 

Our second visit was slightly better.  The reason for our visit was to use up a coupon we had from a fundraising booklet.  

There were a few people lined up when we arrived so we expected a wait.   The line moved quickly and we were waiting in less than 10 minutes.  Once we sat our server came by to ask if we wanted anything to drink.  We both wanted coffee so it came out quickly. Unfortunately we were not ready to order so quickly since the menu is laid out differently than the last time we visited.    

The waffle with strawberries and cutard wasn't as sweet as I thought it would be and there was a bit too much custard for my liking. If you like only a bit of natural sweetness and hints of vanilla, it would be perfect for you.   After scrapping some of the extra custard off, and adding maple syrup it was perfect.  Very filling as well.  The side of sausages were gross.  Very oily and tasted more like grease than meat.  They sat on the side with the 2 bites out of them.  One from me and one from my partner.
