Our server didn't come around for a second quality check so she didn't hear about this until after we paid.  

I tidied up the plates by staking them.  She brought us the bill and took the plates.  When she returned, she realized that she hasn't used the coupon.  No biggie, but looking at the sausages, across the table from us, should have been an indication that something was off.  She didn't seem to care that the quality was below standard on that item.  We paid for them even though they would have made us sick to eat.  We finished our coffee and left.  

We will not likely return only because of the service.  The food is good but if you want to feel welcome, go to cora's.  It's pretty much the same food anyway but the staff are sweet.

Really yummy! Green Thai curry with tofu is my favorite. Veggie spring rolls are crisp and fresh. FYI, it is much more enjoyable when you eat at the restaurant rather than take out. It tends to lose a little flavor and texture in the take out practice. One of the best restaurants around!

We used to go to Pappas Ranch but haven't in a while. We decided to go tonight because I hadn't had the fusion wings in a while and was looking frward to them. 
The first thing I noticed while waiting for a table was the greasy stink in the air; kinda like cooking onion rings in too hot an oil and forgetting to turn the exhaust fan on. While we sat there with our eyes burning I looked around and watched the staff at work. I am in the restaurant biz so I tend to observe the goings on at other restaurants. The first thing that stuck out was one of the waiters; a highly tattooed guy who went from table to table using very few of his smile muscles.  I watched as he went to three tables whose guests had gotten up and just picked up the credit card slips but didn't do anything to prebus. The busboy was running around quickly doing his best to get tables reset. On a side note, with four hostesses at the door chatting and running their fingers through their hair, why did we have to wait as long as we did for a busboy to reset Mr. Tattoo's tables? We wondered why there were four hostesses. Anyway, we were sat at one of the booths and were promptly greeted by Victoria. She seemed pleasant and gave us the impression that we were in good hands. She took our drink order and delivered them promptly. We weren't ready to order at that point so she went away. About five minutes passed and Mr. tattoo came over and asked if we were ready to order.  I asked if there had been a server change and he said that Victoria was his trainee.  We ordered some appetizers, wings and guacamole. I noticed that the fusion wings were no longer on the menu but I ordered the wings with the Greek fusion BBQ sauce hoping that it was just a word change. So we continued with the order process. My friend ordered the street tacos. Mr tattoo just looked at him and asked "which ones?" as if he was just annoyed at having to prompt for it. We finished the ordering process and discussed the experience up to that point. Then the appetizers arrived. The guac was really good. The wings did not live up to my hopes. They did not resemble those big, delicious, smoked beauties that I remembered so fondly. The entrees arrived and they were pretty tasty. Our server seemed to have a bit of a personality with the table behind us but when he came to our zone he seemed to leave it somewhere else. 
We felt that the experience this evening was memorable only in that it makes us want to forget knowing Pappas Ranch. Between the stench of grease in the air, the disheartening new wing preparation and the "barely give a crap" attitude of our server, Dan, (his name on the check was Dan), we can check Pappas Ranch off the list of go to places.  Oh, the experience didn't end at door but in my laundry room where I had to strip off my clothes that stunk of grease and then dash to the shower to degrease myself. Needless to say I am not returning.

I love Watanabe.  It's a nice, fairly quiet restaurant close to home.  The sushi menu (which is nearly identical to it's sister restaurant, Batter'd n Fried/Wave Sushi Bar) is my favorite in town.  The prices are about average for this type of sushi.  I routinely order rolls with special changes (a girl wants what she wants), and have never had an issue with my order being incorrect.  In the first years, service would often be slow, but I haven't had that issue in a long time.  They also do carry out, which is great after a long day at work.  I highly recommend this place.
I've been to Arby's before but my first time at this location. Service was good on this day and it's a clean facility.

Yesterday I needed to get away....but I was just having trouble getting going,  and I wanted to go somewhere new.  I did a quick search and decided to check out this park in Verdi. 

It was a quick drive and there was plenty of parking.  We forgot all our winter essentials, so Roxie and I just took a stroll around the frozen pond. 

There were signs up stating the trails were closed due to rain damage. Since we were unfamiliar with the area,  and it was coveted in snow,  we didn't venture too far. 

Had a nice stop at the river.  Then back we came. I would like to come back another time and explore some more,  then can give an updated and more accurate review. But from the little we saw. We liked it.  And it's really close.

Don't settle for the first bar off the Ferry! Keep those dogs moving and B line 300ft further to have the best gin and tonic of your life. It's a British "Proper" gin and tonic. It's a murky Mississippi River brown color drink which is quite befitting as you ride the ferry across the rusty delta over to Algiers. The vibe is always cool and seems to be mostly local regulars who chill around the bar mid day. They have board games, picnic tables and a cigarette machine. You'll be privey to random conversations,  great people watching, and the slow small town pace of the Algiers peninsula. It's only a five minute ferry ride but worth the adventure.
Awesome instructors and facility.  Highly recommended!  Perfect for families from children all the way up to adults!

This is probably the worst McDonalds you could ever go to. The staff is young and have no guidance behind the register. A young lady was taking my order and in the middle of our conversation a young girl screamed at her and told her not to use her register. There is really no structure here. No priority for customer service. I was so shocked when I ordered a crispy chicken salad and the chicken was not done. I get it, no one is perfect, so I went back and let the manager know that is wasn't done and the almost disgusted look she gave me after she looked at the chicken almost made me want to go home and talk to my girlfriend about the future of our relationship. To be looked at like a liar or criminal was cute at best, but to not even say sorry for me have to drive back to your store to have my food replaced, was the round of applause this location needed. Please fix the training of those young employees, so that they can extend that same courtesy to their customers.

Grabbed a late lunch here while in town. Okay food, average service, good beer menu, and reasonable prices. Place seems like it would be better to drink with some friends than to grab a quick bite. I ordered a side salad and some fries. Fries were too salty and the salad was basically lettuce, lettuce, and nire lettuce.

I got a coupon for a free entrée for my bday so I went and got a custom plate of a poached egg ona bed of spinach, roasted peppers, mushrooms goat cheese and pesto with pumpernickel bread and side of fruit.  despite the waitress being overly flustered by all my questions, I enjoyed my meal.

Then I went back the following sunday with co workers since I had a coupon for BOGO.  I got their "scrambled" egg sand (which really wasn't scrambled, it was more of an omlete on bread sort of disappointing.  I also customized that with pesto, spinach, peppers and goat cheese on pumpernickel bread,  it was good, but not what I was hoping for.  we also just beat the rush on a sunday at 12:30.  they were pretty cleared out by 1:15.  they do still give you a good value for your portions.

I ordered from Tal's for the second time last night. The falafel platter was delicious and well priced ($13) for the amount of food it came with: 5 falafel balls, a side salad, a ton of French fries, and copious amounts of hummus. It also had two pieces of their delicious fluffy pita.

Their hummus isn't the best I've ever eaten, but I will definitely order from them again.

We love their food! They're always quick and the food is very fresh (and warm) when it arrives. Tonight they accidentally left the rice from our order and they brought it back out to us which we greatly appreciated. I would highly recommend them!
Food was incredible. From the starters to entrees just amazing. The place itself is stunning. Would be awesome for a date night. Pricey but worth it. Service top notch

Went on Thursday evening.  The food was excellent.  We opted for the $100 7 course meal.  Small but excellent plates.  We did not care for the sweet breads and the duck was over cooked.   My biggest complaint was that our experience took 3 and 1/2 hours.  I'm all for being able to relax and enjoy but come on!  The plates took 3 minutes to eat and then we had to wait for 1/2 hour for the next course to come.  Way to long which wrecked the experience.  By the end of the second hour I had to get up and stretch as it was getting uncomfortable to sit.  Wont be back as lots of other great restaurants in the city. eg. Hardware Grill , Range Road, ect....

This place is really a hidden gem. An acquaintance reccomend this location to me, and told me how their service is fast and friendly. And they are! I dont know why I was putting up with CVS for so long. They never filled on time, not to mention the wait times are long.
But, here, I went in there, they got my name, insurance, etc,. And filled my meds. This all took about a total of FIVE MINUTES! I am so happy and relieved to have finally transferred over to this pharmacy.

We visited for the first time today and I was quite impressed. I had Shepherds pie and my husband had a steak. Everything was very good and the portion sizes were pretty good. I am usually overwhelmed with the amount of food on my plate but was actually able to finish my meal without wasting food.

Brent and Chrissy are personable and extremely professional.  They have consistently worked with our schedule to tend to our dog's high energy needs.  I trust them with the care of our cherished pooch and am grateful for the high quality service they provide.  I try not to abuse their good nature as they've been willing to work with me for last minute emergencies.  Their rates are on par with most dog walking services but the personal touch and excellent care sets them apart.  I especially appreciate that they leave detailed notes after every walk.   I can't recommend their service enough.

Mr Sweeney answered the phone and came to my house immediately when I heard a very loud noise in the bathtub when the washing machine was draining - I can only describe it as: "Glub, glub, glub."  I was terrified by the thought that sewage would come pouring into the house. I know someone who had that happen and it was a nightmare. 

Mr. Sweeney had the most up to date equipment available.  He ran a camera down through the toilet to see what was happening - and it was bad.  The roots of a massive cherry tree had gone into the pipe and clogged it. 

When the backhoe started digging, they found a full septic tank  the house was built in the 1950s.  Unbelievable.   It was just left there when the house was connected to the township sewer system. The full septic tank sat there for decades apparently.  We had no idea when we bought this house 3 years ago - maybe the previous owner didn't know - or maybe they knew and did not disclose it.  The sewer pipe zig zagged across the yard to avoid the septic tank!  It's surprising this crazy mess lasted as long as it did. The tank was pumped out and broken into pieces. 

Mr. Sweeney did not increase the cost !! So now we have a proper sewer pipe to the township system - and the full septic tank is gone. I am so grateful to Mr. Sweeney, his son and his crew for helping us.  And he is an incredibly nice guy! He briefed us every step of the way.   Sweeney's Mechanical is absolutely the best !!

This super cute cafe in the heart of The Hill captures the essence of this sweet Italian neighborhood. Great coffee (I got mine iced with a splash of cream) and selection of cookies and tasty treats. I got a chocolate drizzled macaroon that perfectly satisfies my sweet tooth. It was fairly busy inside and not a lot of mask wearing if that is important to you. And parking was tough on Saturday afternoon so you may want to give yourself a few extra minutes.

I lived in Bensalem for a year and came here multiple times a week. Never had any issues whether I used the drive-through or walked in. They have a smaller inside area but they have a decent outside seating area. Even though I've since moved, I still come here occasionally and have no complaints.

The ambiance is amazing. The music was great BUT I can't stop raving about the food and service to my friends. My date and I sat at the bar and Mattie (Matt?) was our bartender. This guy whips up some phenomenal drinks. He was so fast at helping people with their drinks but without making them feel rushed away which is important. I ordered the ribs and my date ordered the acme burger. They were so good I had to share them with the people (strangers) next to me because they would have never believed me. 

I'd give this place 10 stars if I could. I highly recommend. I am a fan and I will be back.

I have had a very good experience here. I broke my leg a month ago, and have been seen here 3 times as I have dealt with the injury. The medical assistants, cast techs, radiology folks, and Dr Wang have all been helpful and friendly. My only complaint (hence 4 stars) is that wait time can be long - I come for a 2:30 appt, and am not seen by the doctor until 3:15 - but I understand that this tends to be the norm in large, busy practices. I'm sorry that others had bad experiences, but I would recommend Dr Wang and his staff.
Slap yo mama goodness!  The yucca fries are phenomenal and so is the ham, mac & cheese sandwich.  This place is a winner in my book so get downtown and Check It Out!

Mention I'm from out of town. I pull up to the drive thru, getting our order all together with everybody in the car right? The person/window lady on the speaker says "can I take your order?" I don't answer right away because I'm still getting all the orders in my head to repeat it to her. Literally 2 seconds later, didn't even get me a chance to say ANYTHING, she goes, in the rudest fking way "HEEEELLLOOOO?!" Like everyone else should know if a customer does not answer right away then give them a minute to answer and be more professional with your job, aka taking someone's order. But other than that, our food is ok. Missing one of our Fries but that's not a big deal.

I grew up only about 5 minutes from this theater. I even remember when they were building it because at the time the only theater we had was in Cottonwood mall. Which was probably about the worst theater I have ever been in. Wooden Chairs just lined up in a room with a pull down screen. It was barely a movie theater.  Needless to say it is now out of business.

I was so excited  to see a weekend movie every once and a while in this Kerasotes theaters. Now it is an Amc Showplace but that means little to me. It is still a high quality establishment for viewing films in regular film, digital, or digital 3D. 

For me, being a college student, one of the best things is the deal they have every week Monday-Thursday before 4pm. Are you ready for it? I don't know....I mean this deal is pretty amazing......alright. Well ANY MOVIE that's right ANY MOVIE is just 5 DOLLARS! Not like the 5 buck club where only a few select movies were 5 dollars. This is a deal, especially if you are in college and are strapped for cash, that you cannot pass up!

Great food with excellent service.  We had the Calamari to start.  It was excellent.  My wife ordered the special, which was a blackened Mahi.  I ordered the Buffalo Chicken sandwich.  Food came out in a very timely manner, large proportions and was very tasty.  

We can't wait to come back.  Ask for Sean, he is an excellent server.
Our server Devon was awesome.....the Margarita's were very nice. We could tell they do not use sour mix.

We heard about this place from a relative, and thank God we did!  It's great to have a gourmet vegan restaurant located somewhere other than a major city.

We've had a multitude of dishes at Sprig and Vine, and all have been creative and delicious.  Appetizers we have enjoyed include Watermelon Gazpacho with Avocado Basil Sorbet, artichoke hearts lightly fried with a delicious sauce, french fries, lentil soup, and corn and quinoa stuffed zucchini flower with a green curry sauce.

Entrees we have enjoyed include a Tempeh Reuben, Risotto Cakes, and Blackened Tofu, just to name a few.

Desserts we have enjoyed include a cookie sampler and the PB&J French Toast. The PB&J French toast features three baguette pieces french toasted, served with a peanut butter powder and a port wine reduction.  This dish will literally have you licking the plate clean.

Service has always been prompt, accurate, and friendly.  We always joke with the servers how much we "hated" the food, because our plates are always completely spotless after each course.

Please note that they frequently rotate the menu so your favorite dish may be there one day and replaced with something else delicious the next.

So the 2 stars say "Could have been better". I can't even say it was "ok". I have been here twice before within the last year and it was much better. However, dinner was very disappointing this weekend. I want to say though, our waiter was amazing as was the hostess who seated us. But the food was really off. For anyone with an actual gluten allergic or celiac such as myself, the menu was very limited. Pretty much every other restaurant in St. Pete have more GF options. What was surprising to me was that things like I rice weren't gluten free - I was told the "seasoning mix has gluten". Why would seasoning have gluten? It shouldn't unless it's low end quality. But for an average price of $28 plate I would hope I'm not getting low end anything or any sort of spice mix or pre-packaged "seasoned rice mix". Yuck. Beyond that everything was just bland. I don't have dulled taste buds if you are wondering. The green goddess salad sounded amazing on paper, but was wilted due to way too much "dressing" on it yet had zero flavor! I read through some of the lower rated reviews recently and was surprised that several other people felt as though the food had come from some pre-packed mix rather than a chef created meal from start to finish. I have to agree this seems to be the case. Also as other reviewers have mentioned, it's possible there are some limitations to what is available due to the pandemic, but I find it hard to believe that this means everything has to have gluten and taste bland. Regardless, please add more options to your menu for those with allergies - we deserve nice meals too and shouldn't be told that the only way to make something GF is to basically take away any seasoning or sauce whatsoever and have a plain piece of fish and a steamed vegetable and plain rice lol. I can stay home for that!

Okay, I have a burger weakness and In-N-Out always hits the spot. I am always surprised as the price as usually pay more for a good burger! It was super busy but they seemed to move the two lines of cars through very efficiently. Everyone had masks and gloves that I saw and I was happy for the very rare outing to satisfy that burger crave.

Came here to get something made for a loved one who passed away.  They came through while I waited and put together a great piece for the widow of a Navy veteran who passed away.  The owner here has passion in his business and it explains why he's been in business for so long serving our community.  I'll be back for future orders.  Thanks again you've made this holiday season special.
Great food loved my bison ribeye . The waiter Andrew very knowledgeable and friendly. The cheesecake dessert was delicious with the blackberries. Will definitely be coming back

Great out of the ordinary beer here. A few classic flavors (jeffeweizen) and some unusual selections (dead Druid King and fuzzy pickles). My favorite was chicken and waffles. I didn't think that would be a flavor that could be well captured, but Earthbound did a great job. I can't wait to come back and check out their new space when it's done. Definitely recommend.

Came here today for the first time. Since I'm visiting from California (where they have the best açaí bowls) I didn't think any açaí bowl in Florida would measure up. I was wrong! First off, this bowl is huge! I get half the portion in Cali for the same price. I also always feel most places don't put enough granola, as I'm always left wanting more. This bowl has an abundance of granola and you get the crunch with every bite. The fruit was fresh and the peanut butter and Nutella drizzle topped the cake. Highly recommend the Grain & Berry bowl.

I went to Sang Kee today, and I must say, what's not to like?! I guess I wasn't in the mood for duck, so I decided to go for the curry lemongrass chicken soup. Sang Kee lets you pick the noodle you would like in your soup, and does not charge extra for using one noodle over another. The soup was a nice, filling, portion and was priced at $5. The chicken in the soup was off the bone, which made it far easier for me to eat. (Many places in Chinatown serve chicken on the bone.) My dinnermate ate the roasted pork with rice and was equally satisfied. The place seems rather popular, as we had to wait about five minutes to be seated. All in all, I'd go again.

For a copy of the menu, see: http://chinatown.adampowell.com/SangKee.htm

I had a crossbody bag that the strap had ripped off. I went to one place that was so expensive I decided not to repair it.  Then I found Rudy's.  I went in and received a really good estimate, so I said ok do it.  I expected to leave it but was told he would fix it right away.  Within 15 minutes it was fixed and the work was fantastic.  I already recommended this place to friends.

Took the city bus tour they have. Pretty disappointed with the tour. It was nice, but so much time for history of the city wasted on talking about the sugar bowl and the saints. This was a key city in the civil war and it was never even spoken of. I am glad the concierge at the @hyatt got a commission so someone got something out of it. And this was after sitting and waiting at least 15 minutes for them to consolidate with another bus load. As for the tour guide....stop laughing at your own jokes honey....they aren't even that funny.

After researching several other home inspectors in the area, I chose Crossroads Home Inspections because of their fair pricing, positive reviews, and professional service.

I worked with Chad - who I highly recommend.  He spent several hours at the house inspecting - in detail - every important factor of the home.  He was extremely informative - explaining what was wrong, what was right, and what to look out for in purchasing the house.

His turnaround time was impressive, given the amount of things he found with the house.  Overall, it was a very positive experience because of Chad's knowledge, systematic approach and follow-through.

OMG I echo Emily A.  I just discovered the Italian Market (I know, I know -what took me so long) and I went to Claudio's for home-made ricotta (so creamy, fresh and good - and CHEAP - $2.99 a lb.) and mozzarella.

Can't weait to pre-order a Burrate for later this month.  Burrata is Mozzarella with a creamy-buttery cream in the middle - so decadent.

I have FOUND my source for mozzarella.  Hooray!

I definitely recommend Veggie Village for a great vegan meal and a unique experience. We felt like we'd been invited to the home of a friend who is also a professional chef. The owner and the chef were so friendly and welcoming, and explained the menu. We had the vegan jerk chicken wings, barbie joes, greens, and coconut rice and pigeon peas. The portions were very generous and I thought we'd have leftovers for later, but everything was so delicious that there were no leftovers to be found! They also make teas and juices. Next time we're in Nashville, we'll be back!

Best Saladworks location. We come here regularly. They're nice people, who care about every single customer that walks in. The salads are always fresh, and their customer service is great. Every time we go to a different location, we're disappointed by some aspect of it. This is the only one where we feel like home, where we know we'll be satisfied every single time.

This place is always crowded, but I've never had to wait too long to get a table.  The decor is little strange and mismatched.  There are random items hanging on the walls and it's really dark in the dining area.  I don't really get it, but that's okay.  I come there for the food.  

     I'd been here once before for dinner and had the chicken entree with the mashed potatoes.  It wasn't bad, but was rather bland and not worth the price.  I wanted to give it another go though.  I started off with the mojito martini that has the right amount of sweetness and minty fresh goodness. I had the fried tilapia sandwich. The portion was generous.  The fish was breaded and crispy on the outside and flaky inside, but I think it could have used some more spices in the breading.  The toast it was on was rather plain.  I felt like the whole sandwich was lacking something.  The fries were hot and crisp and appeared to be fresh cut. Some people around us had wings that smelled and looked really good.  Maybe I'll try those next time.

This was the best pedicure I have ever had. This new salon is so clean and beautiful- I really felt like I was at the spa. All of the pedicure equipment was disposable so you can rest assured you're getting a clean pedicure. My technician was meticulous and my gel pedicure turned out absolutely perfect and they have TONS of gel and powder colors to choose from. I can't wait to go back!

I'd seen this restaurant tucked away on Westfield next to Baxbeaux's and had been wanting to try it, but just never had time to go in.  

Last night, my momma and I decided to try a new restaurant and we had a coupon to Mediterra, so why not? I would give 5 stars if I was just judging it on its food.  We had the wedge flatbread for our appetizer and then I ordered the Spaghetti Bolognese.  The flatbread was definitely unlike any flatbread I've had in the past - definitely enjoyed it. The wedge had several different types of lettuce, chicken, blue cheese.. all baked on the crunchy flatbread. I didn't know what to think of warm lettuce at first, but it grew on me so much that I ate all three of my generous sized pieces.

My entree was killer. In fact, I'm eating the leftover of it right now. I rarely order pasta at a restaurant, and even more rarely do I order pasta with meat sauce, but it just sounded good last night. And I'm definitely glad I did. It honestly tastes just like the Spaghetti Bolognese I ate while in Bologna, Italy. Very fresh. Not too heavy. Not too much sauce.. honestly, just great!

The service did leave something to be desired.  Not to be mean, but our waitress seemed to lack some professionalism that I would expect in a restaurant like Mediterra.  Also, for most of our dinner, we were the only ones in the restaurant. We did eat fairly early on a Monday night, so who knows what they're normally like on, say a Friday night.

Regardless, it was a solid meal. Yes, you can probably go to better restaurants in the Broad Ripple area, but you could also do far worse. I'll be back!
Love this place!  Drinks are fabulous... Especially the lime Margarita.   Food is stellar every time we go.
It never opens on time, you can't prewash the bugs off any more.  Typical south side bullshit.

I was super looking forward to trying a new nail salon for a pedicure.  I booked an appointment for my mom and myself, and it seems that a few days out they fill up pretty quick.
Things started out great, they let you pick from an aroma therapy powder that is dropped into your tub.  My technician was very sweet, and we had nice conversation. They even gave me access to the remote.  I want to say there is 4 total pedicure chairs.

I really liked the set-up, it's very bright and inviting and I liked that  the pedicure area was separate from the mani area.

There is a plethora of colors to choose from.  Things just took a weird turn 3/4 of the way through the visit.  After the toe separator was being removed it hit one of the big toes causing a big dent.  I would assume that the coat would be removed and polished over (to not have weird bumps).  But just a portion was wiped off causing weird texture to remain on one toe.  At the end of the day,  I noticed on the other foot the same thing happened where there is a weird texture on the other large toe.   

I know the next appointments were coming in and I'm thinking she was feeling rushed because I was feeling it too.  I was told that the top coat would be applied but it was only applied to two toes!

I did find it interesting that it is called nail "bar" but after reading reviews had seen that no one mentioned a beverage.  When I was walking to the restroom I saw there was a bottle of wine sitting.  I'm assuming that we have to ask for something to drink when it should be the other way around. It's always a bummer to leave disappointed since getting a pedicure is a splurge and supposed to be a special treat.

We went to have dinner here this past sunday, believing the yelp posting that it was open until 10.30. (That is the only reason why no 5 star, although that mayen't be their fault)           While perusing the menu, we were told the kitchen was closing at 9.45.  No problem. i just hate being THAT guest.  The one who is eating while all the waitstaff shuffle around glancing if you have an empty plate so they can go home or get on with their night.  
    My boyfriend and i each ordered appetizers: some sort of roll - his with chicken, mine vegetarian.  My lord were they good!  And they came with two radically different sauces that even tasted interesting when mixed together.  Since we realized they were closing, we told them to go ahead and bring the entrees.  Already we knew we'd need to-go boxes.  
    Lemme just say this: the staff were so accommodating and patient.  Two men were our servers.  Very sweet dispositioned and not intrusive. A very gracious host sat us and checked in on our experience.  
   D got the Flag of India and liked all the differing flavours.  I got the veggie version of a new item called 67 or some other number sauce.  The next day i was sweating at work and luxuriating in the aroma coming out of my pores. Sounds crazy, but that's how good it was! I want to learn how to cook this sauce and put it on every damn thing but know that i can never replicate their magic.
    Our next plan of attack?  Lunch Buffet- where we can try a whole load of different dishes and not have our eyes bigger than our stomach.
            Namaste!
I've actually never seen more rude people in my life. I understand if you hate your life, but don't take it out on your customers. Thanks.

Love it here! Ladies and dudes behind the counter are always smiling and very friendly. Pretzels are always fresh with cheesesteak pretzels or hot dog pretzels made to order.

Try the special cinnamon sugar dipping sauce.

This is absolutely a hidden gem of "The valley". I can't believe I just found it. I have met a new love. I did feel like I was cheating on All Ways Cafe just a tad. Despite it being located on the end of a strip mall, it's really surprisingly cute. There's a little outdoor seating area in the front and the inside is very homey and inviting. It has that cool, laid back coffee shop vibe. I expected Ross, Rachel, Chandler, Monica, Phoebe & Joey to pop out. 

I ordered the Spicy Moroccan Chickpea Salad for just $6.50! Chickpeas seasoned with cumin, coriander, garlic, and red pepper served with sautéed spinach, roasted beets, roasted butternut squash, and pumpkin seeds. First off I love that they serve on actual dishes! The salad looked somewhat small but was actual very filling and perfectly seasoned. I also got a Go Green smoothie which was yummy. Standard beginner green so it's a no fail. 

The baked goods looked to die for so I naturally had to get something to go. I got my girls a huge oatmeal raisin cookie and I got the breakfast cookie which was perfectly crispy on the outside and chewy in the middle with cranberries and oats. Yum! Should've bought more of them! 

I will most definitely be returning when I'm in the area.
Excellent food exceptional service, 50% of our bill was alcohol, it was substantial this will be an important topic at my next AA meeting

This is a delicious quirky place where the owner makes whatever he is in the mood to try. We started with a special kind of pastry which reminded me of what my great-grandmother created when I was a kid. Then we had a vegetarian stir-fry along with a sable platter. The stir-fry was totally modern and tasty while the sable platter was something I would have gotten at Miller's Deli in Baltimore when I was a teenager. All the ingredients are fresh. The atmosphere is also unique, sometimes featuring a Susan B. Anthony impersonator. You can also get a variety of food to carry out. The indoor tables have embedded parts from discarded computers. It is almost like dropping in at a friend's house for a meal or snack, if your friend happens to be a competent chef. The desserts include homemade rice pudding, pastries, and ice cream made in the store. The folks here are friendly and helpful.

TERRIBLE.  Very Rude on the phone when I called for towing assistance,  I didn't know the exact clearance on the parking garage (and clearly didn't have a tape measurer on hand). I gave an estimated clearance range, they smarted off to me, and then hung up on me. If I ever have to call a tow truck company... it won't be this one.

Cute interior/creative space. The location was a little off convention center Blvd hidden in a shopping center. I could see it almost being passed but you should definitely stop in! The food and service were excellent; we really enjoyed the menu! And the vibe was super chill.

Excellent locally owned and operated bakery in the Westchase area of Tampa. Owner is often on site, and she is busy baking all of the goodies in the cases! They have fresh donuts over the weekends, and lots of other treats during the week. Brownies, mini cheesecakes, cookies and bars. You can't go wrong. 

The ice cream is excellent, an Amish brand from Sarasota. Definitley recommend this little gem hidden in Westchase.
Yum yum yum owners wife waited on us & was awesome.  I'm def coming back. A cute place w good German food. I'm in Europe!

From California, in town just for some good BBQ (obviously not) and really enjoyed my experience here. I had ribs/chicken which were so tender it was beautiful. I also had the Mac and cheese/ the  chowder. Let's just say everything was licked clean. Great atmosphere and service! I also had the orange wheat beer from a local brewery, not too hoppy, nice light beer. COME EAT HERE

Unhappy to learn that fuel gauge, P brake, and lights not working when I arrived to pick it up.
Get things checked before you arrive if you're going to travel long distance.  Triple check the easy stuff before signing paperwork on as-is used cars.

Try the blood Mary. This was my first time stopping here. I used yelp to save me a spot in line since i saw that there is sometimes a wait. We were seated at the bar. The bartender stated to try the Bloody Mary. Sooooo good. This was my first Bloody Mary ever. And decide it was with a shot. The breakfast was delicious. I had the stone cake grits with the fresh catch. So yummy. My cousin tried the shrimp and grits and it was also delicious. The servers were all so nice. We also decide to be greedy and split a pancake. The pancakes are huge and so good. After eating all of our food we ended it by enjoying 2 peach bellies. So worth the trip.

Even though I don't particularly enjoy cigars or the smell of them, I really loved this cigar lounge. Being the Ritz, it's obviously extremely upscale and classy. The lounge is for members and hotel guests only.

I really felt like I was in the show "Boardwalk Empire" hanging out in a basement drinking during prohibition, especially since smoking inside is banned in most places but it's somehow allowed in this private lounge.

I loved the exquisitely fancy wall filled with cubby's and gold nameplates for each member. I was overall very impressed and hope to go back soon.
Kristi was a wonderful server and gave us great advice on the menu! Be sure to try the OMG grilled cheese and a great wine selection.

We went to smash burger with high hopes of tasty burgers and some interesting fries. What a disappointment.  My husband ordered the large jalapeño baja beef burger with smash fries and I ordered they goat cheese spinach cucumber  chicken burger and onion rings. 

The food came quickly but we were soon disappointed. My husband said his burger had zero flavour dispute the jalapeño peppers. The fries were okay - nothing to write home about. 

My burger was missing the cheese and cucumber. The girl was helpful and brought me some - unfortunately they were out of cucumber and suggested pickles instead (no thank you) the chicken burger was salty yet had no flavour. Bizarre. 

The onion rings were AWFUL! Nothing but salt coated onion. The texture was okay but the salt ruined it! 

I won't be going back. The 2 stars earned were only because the burgers came quick and the service was friendly.
Excellent breakfast menu. The pancakes were extremely fluffy, the biscuits, *mwah*, to die for. Furthermore it wasn't too expensive. I'll definitely be returning soon.

After having beignets, we happily strolled across the street for the gift shop! Coffee and beignets mix, plus oodles of New Orleans fun fare! A couple of magnets later and him-hawing whether I would actually make beignets at home, we easily checked out! This too is cash only!
Great choices of seafood. The downside is you can't preorder or prepay. You have to get in the line.

If you are going to this practice, go to see Marisa, the NP only!  Unfortunately, I was treated horribly by the billing office. I sent a letter addressed to Dr. Hruza. She, nor the office, ever replied.  That says a lot.  I had already been discouraged with the treatment course in addition to the hip-shooting and diagnosing Dr. Fraggle.
One of the best croissants I've ever had! The inside layer was very well structured and the flakiness was so perfect. I was so glad to find this place on my weekend getaway to Santa Barbara!
Love bennys. My kids love seafood and I do as well, we made a tradition to go every Easter. My favorite is shrimp linguini my kids love the mussels. Always satisfied when we go
What a cute spot! We came here for wine tasting before dinner reservations but will be back to try the food because the ambiance was awesome and so was the service.

This place is awesome!  It looks like a diner from the outside but the inside is first class. It' s casual dress with high class food. Our server said they are changing the menu to be more competitive. I think that's a mistake. The prices for our entrees ranged $23-$37. Believe me we got what we paid for. Fantastic presentation, excellent service from Tony, and the most delicious seafood in Florida! I can't wait to eat here again. Yum, lunch on Friday maybe?

I'll start by saying that this tour is probably best for people who have never been to Santa Barbara or are looking for something to do with the kids on a Sunday during daylight. 

We recently used this service to celebrate my friend's 25th birthday on Saturday. I'll give it to them, we were with a hyper group ready to party and had many people bouncing about wanting to dance to music and socialize. However, there was an issue with one of our friends who had an expired ID card that was not allowed on the bus. While I fully understand there are laws set in place I also know there is a polite way to approach such issues. After the issue with the id came up the woman checking identification immediately turned sour with a huge attitude. I walked on the bus sober and with proper valid identification and I felt as if I were being talked down to as well. Really rude service from the woman with dark short hair. I've bartended in the past and I know drunk people can be annoying, but come on, your company literally allows people to bring their own booze on a boat, lighten up. I sat in my seat most of the time because I felt uncomfortable being given the death stare by the hall monitor employee. We were celebrating a special occasion and instead it felt like sitting in the principal's office. 

On a more positive note I'm sure this would be more beautiful during the day. We ended our tour around nine and it was completely dark and kind of pointless because we couldn't see anything anyway. Honestly should have paid for a table at a club and got bottle service or rented a limo to drive us around for a bit. Wouldn't recommend for adult celebrations.

I don't know who does the cake decorating at Albertsons on Eagle and Mcmillian but oh my goodness are you AMAZING!!! Thank you for your attention to detail and for making all of our cakes with love. We cannot wait to order again... please keep up the fantastic work.

For the price this was one of the nicest hotels that I've visited.  I stayed on the 12th floor and had a great view of Lake Ponchartrain and the Causeway Bridge.  

The staff was very accommodating, but the room and view was really what made this hotel so great.

Great spot for a killer burger and a nice brew on a cold night with your favorite magazine (at least, that's what I did). The bar was filled with some locals in the 40/50/60 age range, all relaxed and classy people who clearly make some good money (but don't flaunt it). The bartender, Danny, was super friendly (but not pushy), and guided me (correctly) to enjoying the house ketchup. This place knows its vibe, and that vibe is 1920s Atlantic City. Live jazz was starting as I left. Can't wait to return. Wish they had a gluten-free bun so I could bring my girlfriend.

What a cute little Cafe and convenient to the Walnut St steps leading to/from Schuylkill River Park Trail. This place is pet friendly. They provide outside seating as well as a good sized indoor seating. Their menu is leaning more on the health conscious side. They will bring out your order if you seat outdoors. I will definitely stop by in the future.

Service here is hit and miss, as is the food quality. On good days it is outstanding. On bad days, I swear I will never come back again. Then I get hungry and hope for one of their good days and return...

The funny thing is in over 14 years things have not changed at Mt Ephraim dodge..I have been waiting a long time to write this,i wish yelp was around when i purchased a dodge quad cab back in 2003-04.I am reading all these negative reviews and i am laughing because Mt Ephraim dodge were scummy back then and it sounds like they still are..
 Back then i purchased a dodge quad cab from them,i put alot of money down on the vehicle(what a waste),Too bad is was the first car i ever bought at a dealership!!I was young and inexperienced,not that i would ever shop at M.E.D again even after all these years.The truck ended up not starting or breaking down 3 times within the first 2 months,i remember that no one and i mean no one at the dealership gave a shit or cared.I eventually got rid of the truck,wrote and contacted dodge several times over the years but eventually forgot about it and moved on to ford where i have been happy for many years.I even remember the salesman's name,it was a father and son team at the time,out of class i wont use his name but even after all these years M.E.D gave me the worst car buying experience i have ever had at the time and since then(14 years)!Maybe dodge is a good product,but M.E.D certainly is not,and by the reviews on here i am not the only saying this...I cant believe they are still in business.(i wish yelp had negative stars to give,1 star is being too kind)

$25 for all you can eat sushi that is made to order and fresh???? Yes, please! The establishment has a trendy decor and the welcoming staff. If you want the all you can eat option you have to sit at the bar and that was the only down side to this experience. If you have a large group it is probably impossible to pull off. The rolls were good size and not a lot of rice which a lot of all you can eat spots try to pull so you eat less. Fish was definitely fresh and was made right in front of you which was half the fun! Reno may not be a go to destination for most but if you end up their for one reason or another...try this place out!

I called Big Brand Tire an hour in advance on Thursday at 11am to dismount 4 tires off some wheels I was selling on Saturday.  They told me to come on buy and drop them off.  They provided great customer service and promised to treat my powder-coated wheels delicately like they were their own.  I came back almost 2 hours later after some errands (though they had said it would be done within 1 hour to 1.5 hours) to find them carefully placed right outside.  I inspected them and found no chips or issues with them, paid the $15 dollar fee per wheel ($60 total = labor/tire disposal fee) and went on my way.  No complaints for what I paid for.

Great local spot.  2nd time there. We Ordered the meat bowl (mocjate?).   Remarkable.  Chorizo was moist, sauce tasty.  Veggies cooked perfectly.  Chicken was moist (hard to do). Small issue with avocado taken care of easily and promptly (sign of great service).  Wife needed the dish without onions.   They made it.  Since we were there with another couple we got to compare dishes with and without onions. Both excellent.  Great job chef!    Always great chips.  Do again!

This is my go to Thai meal in the Castleton area. If you ask for the Thai heat, you will receive what you ask for. 

I have never been disappointed by the dishes served at this restaurant.

Food was pretty good. The BBQ nachos were awesome. It was long wait to get food and have no where to sit after. So many people were holding tables and haven't even ordered yet. There should be someone asking people to move if they don't have food. Highly suggest ordering to go!

Neopolitan pizza like you'd get in Italy. The crust is more than a conveyance for toppings -- crisp and chewy and a great balance between yeasty and salty. On the margherita, the flavors are all so mild that nobody overpowers anybody else, and you can taste the quality of the ingredients. 

We also got the large meat plate, which featured several of the restaurant's home-cured salamis along with a nice cheese and a dried fruit spread. Delicious.

I hate to do this people. But if it wasn't for Plum rewards cards I wouldn't have gone here. But if you meet, "Chantel" I was unhappy. I mostly am proud when I have the representative ask for my plum rewards at every transaction. But if the manager has to say "it has to do something about personal life" I don't think any should work there.

Hate to say....but NOT coming back. 
I know my review will be an anomaly....but I've been here twice in two months....not a good experience either time. 
Music awesome....people that work there...not so much.....

Nuff said.

I had a huge issue with customer service at this restaurant and asked to speak to a manager. I was told by the employees that there were no managers in the restaurant at the time. Are you serious? At 7 pm on a Thursday there is not a single manager/owner/anybody of authority in the building? Either they were lying or there is a serious leadership issue here. I tried calling back the next day and was told the same thing. I left my contact info and asked for a manager to call me and never got a call back. The food is decent enough, but obviously the owner/managers don't care about guest complaints. I will be eating sushi elsewhere in Tucson since they obviously don't want my business.

I love this place I have been going for such a long time with my family and it's a great place to go have fun doing mini golf. The arcade isn't there anymore but for the time it was there it was such a good arcade they are working on something else there but I'm not sure. The food there is very delicious and the people behind the counter are very nice. The mini golf course is nice and there are many different varieties of golf clubs and golf ball colors overall AMAZING PLACE!

I was really looking forward to this. The line was fairly long outside the restaurant, but it went quick. I had the mild chicken tender plate with mac and cheese and greens. Not a fan of the pimento mac and cheese. All in all, it was just okay.

We just discovered The Parlour last night after a great meal at Magpies. My daughters adore frozen yogurt so of course we had to give it a try. Very nice selection of yogurt and great toppings. I love the fact that they charge by the cup not weight. It's about time someone did this! First daughter got salted caramel yogurt, white chocolate chips, white chocolate syrup and, of course, sprinkles. Second got classic vanilla yogurt, chocolate chips, chocolate syrup and sprinkles also. The quality was far better than our usual place, BTO. The yogurt is a little icy, but still has a nice creaminess. I didn't feel like I was tasting ice milk as I do at BTO. We love pizza and we love frozen yogurt. I think my family has found a new favorite dinner outing!

I went in to check out their promotional deal - it involved taking a tour in order to receive a free 2-week trial membership. So I suffer through the pushy sales-pitch and tour and more sales pitch. And then they say if I do the trial membership and THEN sign up, the fees are much higher. I left without signing up for anything, not even the trial.

My entire experience with Stonebridge was great! It took a little longer than anticipated, due to weather, but it was well worth the wait!! My roof looks amazing! Looks like a whole new house. Working with Fernando, Mike and Simon was fantastic! These guys are a great team!! Simon Carlisle was our project manager and his customer service skills are on point! He was extremely responsive and worked closely with us on every aspect of the job. His team of workers worked fast and hard, even in the 100 degree days that they worked. The roof we had done was on our rental property. If and when we need another roof done, we are most definitely return to do business with these guys!! Thanks Stonebridge  Team

I ordered the blackened market Grouper entree with a modified Tuscany Sauce (crab meat, artichokes, capers in a cream lemon sauce) to have added garlic. The sides I had chosen was broccoli and yellow rice. The first bite out of the Grouper after a long day of Kayaking in the Fort Desoto area and I was in awe. The sauce went amazingly well with the blackened Grouper. The fish was very fresh and you could taste it. I will definitely come back here to try another entree or one of their pasta dishes when I kayak in the area. The server was very nice, if you are not sure what to order, they will be glad to give you honest recommendations.
Not great!!!! We ordered food from here after the game (we're from out of town) after waiting an hr for the food everything was ice cold we had to reheat everything. Never again!!!

The wings were okay up until I bit into a colder one. Cold chicken kills your appetite real quick, which is pretty odd since you'd think they are all getting fried at the same time for the same amount of time... 
Combine that with the quiet server who also stood ~4 feet away from the table (which wouldn't be too bad/awkward if the place was quiet enough to hear her), and you get a subpar place.

Quick service. Knowledgeable information provided in an appropriate tone of voice. Comparisons given. Related experiences shared. Suggestions and recommendations made based off of stated interest. I even got to sit down on a chair while filling out the required paperwork.

Best of all, I did not receive any form of attitude like I have received from DSS. 

Location was quite busy but we were assisted as soon as a gentleman behind the counter became available. My husband's lame response of "we're just looking for now," did not deter him to assist someone else. Instead, he inquired about helping in any way. This is when I informed him that we were currently comparing two guns. He did not hesitate to bring out the guns and share information about them. 

Clean restroom I was provided access to right away. By right away, I mean someone was told to wait so that I may be let into the restroom.

Great decor and ambience. This restaurant is tastefully decorated with tapestries, lanterns and elephants. When we first entered, the gentleman asked us if we had a reservation then left us for about 10 minutes to search for a table when 3 were immediately available from our viewpoint. We were seated at one of those tables.

From here, the situation improved greatly. Waiters were polite, prompt and attentive. The seating may appear crowded but once seated, there is ample space.

For appetizers, we had the Money Bags and Chicken Satay. The Money Bags are steamed shredded crab in a fried crispy shell, which were excellent with or without the sauce. The Chicken Satay was tender and the peanut sauce was delicious. The amount of chicken on one skewer was very generous, and there were three skewers.

The main courses were less impressive. The Pad Thai was too sweet and somewhat disappointing. The Green Curry was good but the sauce could have been thicker to make it perfect. Finally, the Drunken Noodles were excellent. Perfect spice level and flavor. All the main course had generous portions, and the cook on the meats and starches were just right. 

Overall, we had a great time and would come back again.

Yeah it's "divey" and cash-only, but it is a quintessential Philly bar & they likely don't care if you like them or not.  

Pros include no smoking inside, wonderfully varied clientele and cheap drinks.   Great location and a good place to end a night out on the town without breaking the bank.

I wanted to try a new place for breakfast thar wasn't along my normal route and I came across Chi Cafe.  I came on a Wednesday and this place was pretty noisy with activity and patrons, which is always a good sign for a diner.  The service is superb here; they left me with an impression that they treat everyone that walks through the door like a long term patron, which is nice and refreshing to see.  In terms of food, it is a standard solid diner menu and quality.  And the price is really reasonable too.  Definitely a worth a try when in the area.

Best Burger I have had!!! Locally sourced food cooked with great care and pride. Really good service. The home made ketchup is fantastic as is all their sauces. Great fries. I would go back every time I visit Tucson

We've only been in our 2 bedroom apartment for about three months now and the only problem I foresee is that if we ever have to move we will never find a place that equals Penn Treaty.  The apartments are spacious and beautiful with tons of storage(we have 9 closets, no really 9).  The amenities are as good as anywhere I have seen in the city. Maintenance is on the ball if there is ever an issue.  As another reviewer wrote, the management and staff are incredibly friendly and professional.  They knew our names from the day we moved in and  made us feel like family.  Nothing but praise for this property and I have been to numerous apartments in the city.

My new favorite bar in Philly  - without a doubt. Stephanie M took Jenny V and me here because she knew I'd love it, and she was right. Gay, straight, whatev...the bartenders here take good care of you and will make sure you leave feeling gooooddddd. I highly recommend the "Dirty Girl" or the "Pussy Galore," because not only do they taste good and pack a mighty wallop (as does the "Blue Balls," tee he he, which we also tried) but they also look cool. So whether you're a girly girl, boyz type gal, dude who is into other dudes or a straight up straight chick or guy with an open mind, come here. Not only is there no sign, which I am totally into, but the decor is lovely and the bartenders and clientele are beautiful. So go already! You're totally missing out if you don't. So much so that I went back with the boy on a Sunday afternoon. The STRAIGHT boy, btw, so that says a lot. ' Cause Stir loves everyone, and it shows. Can't wait to go back again, not only 'cause it's like my L Word fantasy come true on a Saturday night (seriously) but because it's great to support a new local biz with an awesome owner who gives a shit. So whatever you're into, who cares...go have a cocktail or a beer at Stir, 'cause they RULE!
Max Brenner's in Philadelphia is nothing like the Max Brenner's in New York City. First, the staff was rude and unattentive.

Delicious food! Had a Thai craving and was not disappointed! I got the "Spicy Noodles" which were not very spicy (but that was a good thing for me, and I asked before ordering) and my husband got the Pineapple Duck Curry. I tried some of his- typically I don't like duck, as it's a bit gamey for me. But it wasn't gamey at all- and it tasted AMAZING! I could have drank down the remaining sauce by itself if I had had any room left in my stomach! The noodles were a bit of a "darker" flavor, and the duck was "bright" (with the citrus of the pineapple, and the taste of lemongrass) so us sharing the dishes with each other was great! Only criticism I could give would be that the red pepper slices in the noddles could have been cooked a bit longer. Other than that, it was great!

Gave this place a 2 solely on my waitress, as she was very attentive and always making sure our water glasses and drinks were full. 

As for the food- started out with the nachos which were good, but for 12 dollars (without meat, which was an extra 5-7 dollars) it was pretty pricy for nachos. I was very disappointed in my burger. I ordered the "Pub Burger" medium rare. The first time it came out it was very dry and well done, so I very politely sent it back and asked if they could cook it less. To my disappointment, as I thought this was known as a burger place, my second burger came out even dryer and as well done as the first. Since it took 15 minutes for them to remake the burger, I didn't bother sending it back. 

All in all, the waitstaff was great and the atmosphere was really nice. However, for the price they charge of food, I'd expect much much better.
Omg best tacos I've ever had and the trio with all three sauces is amazing and a great deal. My mouth is watering thinking of the Baja shrimp tacos

My first yummy food korean experience in Philly!!  We all know that decent authentic korean food is definitely hard to find in center city.  It is a trek to get here but the food is worth it.  The soft tofu is yummy. Their menu is full of different permutations of what you can add to your soft tofu.  I love their short ribs and seafood pancake.  Banchan is a must in korean dining and they do offer a variety.  Good reliable korean food when you have a hankering for kimchi ;)

Scott Bentley gave me the best haircut I have ever had, in all of my 43 years. He talked to me before he started the cut. He really listened to me and my concerns. He explained what he was going to do and why he would do that, which made me feel so comfortable before he cut my hair. The salon is going the extra mile to be safe during this pandemic. The haircut he gave me is phenomenal. I love it and have been getting so many compliments on it. One of my friends I have known for 22 years told me, "I think this is the best hair style you have ever had!" I am so happy I used Yelp to find the Bentley Salon and that I was fortunate enough to have Scott cut my hair. I am feeling fabulous and very grateful.

I've been a loyal member since the early '70's-even keeping my membership when I moved away. But there has been an erosion of customer service over the past few years. First, they got rid of shared branching. This allowed you to use any credit union in Arizona for most of your transactions. I was told that the mobile app replaced all that. Well, with the lobbies closed to all but appointments, I can't use other credit unions that are open. Plus, if I want to avoid an ATM fee, I could go to a credit union and use a teller to withdraw money. Then, TFCU eliminated their drive through tellers and replaced them with virtual tellers. But the virtual tellers didn't replace all the in person tellers. So, with lobbies being closed, long lines are common at drive through lines. Then with the pandemic, they closed the lobbies to all but appointments. That was fine for a while but there is no reason that they can't be reopened with a limited customer presence. With a reduced number of drive through tellers, you have to wait in a long line for service. Walk up ATM's are gone and are now all drive up. I served on the board of a local CU for over 6 years and my email address and name was on the CU's website. There is no information about TFCU's board on the website-even though we elect them. After almost 50 years, I'm about done.
So amazing! Greatest roast beef po'boy ever!!

We were passing through Boise on our return trip to Denver and needed a place that we could stay with our two, big dogs and thus landed at Candlewood Suites.

Our room (single Queen) was pretty spacious and came equipped with a kitchen, a dining table, flat screen television, and Wi-Fi.  We had no need for a fridge or dishwasher, since we were just passing through, but I can imagine this would come in handy for extended stays.

While some pet friendly hotels compromise on cleanliness and feel (and smell) like dog friendly hotels, we found our room and bathroom to be very clean.  Plus, the bed was pretty comfortable, which was appreciated in the midst of 30+ hours spent in the car.  I did find it kind of odd that there wasn't a trash can in our bathroom, though.

My only complaint is that the walls/ceiling seem to be kind of thin.  We heard the guest upstairs walking around at night, which made our dogs wake up a few times to play watchdog.  However, it wasn't too big of a deal and nothing that cranking up the A/C couldn't drown out.

If you're traveling on a budget with or without pets, Candlewood Suites is a great option to consider.

I had my nails done with dipping powder and a regular pedicure. I was pleased with the way they both turned out as far as the shape of the nails, the colors, and how long they lasted. However, I'm a little concerned about the cleanliness here. The manicurist did my nails over a towel that had clearly been used and there was no liner in the tub for the pedicure.

Good enough. One of my main hobbies is training for boxing at Title Boxing Club in Glendale. So I wind up with a lot of tension in my shoulders from using those muscles all the time.

While my usual helper to get my shoulders worked out wasn't available this weekend, I looked around Indy for an easy chair massage. I don't need--or even want--much. I just want the kinks worked out of my shoulders and lats. So a chair massage is just fine for me.

Indy Massage is one of the few places that lists a simple chair massage other than mall kiosks. They were friendly when I arrived, and the office is well maintained and pleasant.

The massage itself wandered away from my shoulders and lats a little too much. Lots of scalp massage that wound up lightly bruising up later. I keep thinking I have bug bites on the back of my head and then remembering it's just the after effects of that scalp massage. I'm not sure what that was about as I've never had someone go above the neck with pressure like that before. Maybe that's normal. Almost got me to say "Uncle!" though as it hurt!

Overall, it was workable. I might go back, since it's one of the few options available for an easy chair massage.

This is the only reason my husband and I leave Philadelphia to dine. The french cuisine is delicious. We always start with the Country Pate and its out of this world. We fight over the last bite. We mix it up with the entrees, Tagine, Flank Steak and Scallops come to mind all exceptional. The menu changes so we have many yummy choices. Make sure to leave room for dessert because the Daffodil Cake is simply divine. The atmosphere is relaxed and inviting. The staff are all friendly and knowledgeable. Oh and I cant wait for Spring so we can set outside on the deck and take in the lovely flower gardens.

We always love Modesto and find it to be a great date night place.  My wife and I have been going for over 8 years once or twice a year as our special place.  The food is fantastic.  There are always new things in the menu that makes things fresh.   They have a great Sangria and a delicious Mojito.  The staff are pleasant and helpful.  Can't say enough.  The only negative is that they took Tres Leches off of the menu, on of the most delicious desserts I have ever had.  Please bring it back.

Ok so full disclosure, I did not actually have any work done here. The insurance company ended up totaling my vehicle after I had taken it to Fradella's. However, my experience with the Fradellas team was excellent. They were very patient while we waited for the adjuster to inspect the vehicle, and Chelsea was extremely understanding about how emotional we got saying goodbye to our car. I didn't need them this time but if I'm ever looking for body work in the future this will be the first place I go.

As a bar, this place is pretty cool. Decent prices, good vibe, not too loud, not too crowded, casual vibe. All things I really like. I'd give that experience 4 stars.

However, I've been here for live music a couple of times and it just didn't suit my fancy. That would get 2 stars.

First, the space is SO small that I feel like I'm on top of the band. I wish that was a figurative statement. You can't really see them unless you're in the front row (because there's people in front of you if you're not in the front). I like to watch the band and see how they interact with each other and how they play their instruments, as I'm a bit of a musician myself. I find that interesting. It's nigh impossible here. 

Second, because the space is so small, it gets really loud. Not in a pleasant way, more in a we're-cranking-up-the-music-so-loud-it-makes-your-ears-ring-in-this-tiny-space way. It's like they're trying to fool you into thinking you've got a real party going, when really you can't fit more than about 20-30 people on the dance floor.

Third, what I should have said was "dance" floor. Because it is not really a dance floor, more like a stand-here-squished-up-against-the-band-and-all-these-strangers-without-being-able-to-move-at-all floor. And maybe it was the particular show I went to (there were a lot of hipsters in the audience, and I tend to blame them for any- and everything wrong in life...sorry?) but there was really not much dancing when there really should have been. The band was rocking! Why are you just standing there? MOVE. YOUR. BOTTOM. PLEASE.

I would definitely give this place another try, especially because of its location directly off the St. Charles streetcar line, and because maybe it was just a weird crowd the last time I went. I would recommend it for an interesting bar that you probably can't find in a whole lot of other places on this planet.

I discovered some of your cool walking trails a few years ago that I love. It offers nice shade on a hot day. You also have a nice art fair you can check out. I used to love this park when I was a kid. I remember that zipline you used to have.
No security line when I came but boy is it a hassle getting around. Not the best layout at all.

Great store . Fast service to go in and out. Best fried rice and chowmein. I live closer to the Raleys on Robb dr. But when I'm craving some Chinese food I rather come here. Great costume service food is always fresh and delicious. Also love Pete's coffee so good.

A classy old-school speakeasy in a city of tacky, noisy bars.  Love this place.  I've never been disappointed with a drink.  Seriously the best selection of Old Fashions I've ever sampled.  And they even have a vegan meat/cheese plate!

The big booths are comfortable.  You can hear yourself think and hold a conversation.  And the wait staff is on it!  (You might want to sit at the bar and watch the drinks being made.  It's like watching artists at work.)

Don't forget to write out the post card that comes with the check.  Your waiter will mail it for you.

Alright, so get this - my First. Ever. Mani Pedi! With nice events coming up, spouse booked a date at Cozy Nails (keeper, yes? ;) ) I had no idea what to expect - so here goes.

We arrive for our appointment and are greeted immediately:
"Hello, here for mani pedi? Pick color!" The ladies helped me pick a neutral/natural pale pink for the mani, and a slightly pinker pink for the pedi. Spouse opted for no polish. Wuss.

Foot Station ~1H:
* Massaging chairs and warm foot baths: "This is great!"
* Callus Removing Gel: "... ooh, nice lotion!"
* Leg Massage: *passed out in sheer bliss*
* Callus Removing: "IS THAT A CHEESE GRATER?!"
          *****high pitched eeee's ... it tickles!*****
* Cuticle treatment: "ARE THOSE SURGICAL TOOLS?!"
* Hot Stone Massage. " ... I will totally do this again"
* Pedi

All Hands on Deck: ~10min
As one technician was working on my pedicure, another materialized and started working on my hands. (Guys, don't get too worked up on the all the girl action.)
* Fingertip Soak
* Cuticle Treatment

Manicure Station: ~15min (with foot fan to dry pedicure)
* Neck/shoulder massage: *zoned out with euphoria*
* Base Coat
* Two Color Coats
* Top Coat

Drying Station: ~5min (with foot fan also)
* Quick Dry Oil
* This part sucked the most ... I sat there with both hands under this fan thing, with my phone RIGHT THERE and I couldn't do much of anything.

I had no idea a mani-pedi was so involved! From start to finish, it took ~1.5H and ~$45. As the ladies were very attentive/efficient in getting me super relaxed, and both hands were being pampered ... I didn't get pictures. 

Now that I know what to expect, I'm now a fan of mani-pedis ... and will definitely be back!

Unbelievable. I can't even review the food because i never got it! Came with my friend and both ordered medium subs. She got hers in about 15-20 min and after continuing to wait another 10 min, I went up and asked about it. They hadn't even started it so told me they would immediately. Another 10 minutes, with my friends long gone, I went back up and STILL NO ONE HAD STARTED IT. There were at minimum 15 people that came in after us that were served while I was waiting.....So, I get it that mistakes happen...but this is ridiculous. 
So, I was offered a refund...nothing more for wasting more than my entire lunch break! Will never, ever be back to this location!!!!!!!!

Wonderful experience,great food and service....owner/manager? Excellent!!

Thanks!!

We split a bagel egg sandwich with the best breakfast potatos....don't forget the apple fritters....
Highly recommend them...

Our dine in experience was sub-par. The food wasn't particular good and reminded me more of airport Asian food - dull and overpriced. For the price vs quality you're better off trekking further north for the Asian cravings fix.
Friendly staff but a little too chatty. Arrived at 10pm to one person behind the desk and a line of 8 ppl in front of me
Called during business hours for information on consigning and got voicemail. Left a message - three days ago. No response. My phone number was clearly stated more than once. Disappointed.

It a dive bar and not much more. They hide the food behind hot seasoning. The food varies from day to day and it depends on who is working that day.  Even the wait staff said when asked about food "it depends on who made it today".  Dirty dinning area poor service and average food on a god day.

Great space about a mile + 1/2 from downtown Reno.  Lovely interior, long bar.  Very woodsy feeling.  I asked the bartender to make a special drink - Hemmingway Dacquari - and he was up to the task.  The place is fairly large, nicely appointed.  The ingredients were all freshly squeezed (lime, grapefruit + lemon) and the drink was very beautifully made.  They have both a large selection of liquors and many beer choices on tap as well.  Great place.

If you've never been to Mother's, it's definitely worth a stop. Big menu, great po-boys and classic NOLA dishes - all delicious. Had the Ralph, basically one of their signature Ferdi with cheese added, and a cup of gumbo; both were fantastic. If you can judge a sandwich by how many napkins are used in its consumption, this is the world champ!

Outstanding patio and the cheapest drinks you'll find in STL for happy hour and even have it on the weekends! Drag shows on Wednesday and Saturday are always a blast! The bartenders are good to look at as well! The owner Chad is amazing and a legend in the community.
Amazing walking tour! Our tour guide Anna wa fabulous. Her knowledge was incredible and made the entire tour enjoyable
Tacos were reasonably priced and delicious! Good frozen margaritas are VERY hard to come by, but we found them here. Service was great! Very pleased.

This place is a Gem - so cute and retro inside. It's great for kids too! The onion rings and burgers were very tasty.  The prices are very reasonable and service was great. We will be back when we are in the area!

Not one of my favorite places to go but it's close to the house it is a little chilly and I wanted something to warm me up and since I don't drink coffee they have some pretty good hot chocolate, The staff seems to be friendly but as far as the food goes I don't really care for McDonald's. But I must say my hot chocolate is pretty yummy!

On March 9, I made an appointment with BMCI to have the roof on my Florida room redone.  I went with this company because they were recommended by Vivint Solar and I was getting their solar panels.  Alex comes out a week or so later and making a long story short, walks around the entire roof before I stopped him and said I was only looking to have the roof redone on the Florida room.  I have zero idea how that could have been misunderstood.  The roof on the remainder of the house didn't need to be redone.  In hindsight, I should let him have it and try to convince me it DID need to be redone.  I took time off work on my second job to meet this guy and the company was recommended so I didn't have any misgivings whatsoever.  Alex was not capable of giving me an estimate at that point but told me after studious measuring that someone would get back with me 'shortly.'  I have no idea what 'shortly' means to BMCI but almost two weeks later, I called to get the estimate and Ken asks me what the measurements were on said roof.  I told him Alex should have input that info and I was assured that he did but gosh, could I confirm those figures?  Ken wants to apologize profusely and somehow managed to bring Vivint into the conversation.  He assures me that Vivint customers are treated 'special'.  Yeah, like I don't think so but anyway, I'm not sure what the problem is... The Florida room has nothing to do with solar panels and English sounded like it was the primary language of every person I spoke to at BMCI.  To add more hilarity to the situation, Ken wants to come out again and get me an estimate and I don't have to take time off work.  Really, kids?  Why did I have to take off work the first time?  What part of 'Florida room' gets confused with the 'entire house'?  What was Alex measuring if you need to ask ME for the measurements (which I knew btw compliments of the roofing company that did the job)?  I got an estimate emailed to me yesterday (3/28) which was approximately one grand more than the company that did the actual work with a 10-year warranty.  No, I don't think I'll be going back to BMCI any time soon.

Considering it occupies Scamps' former location, I should have expected tonight's mediocre experience for Quizzo and Taco Tuesday. The first three beers my friend ordered resulted in the waiter sulking back to admit that the keg "had just been kicked." At his suggestion, we ordered the $2 tacos, which were great until the undercooked rice revealed itself as flavorless bacon bits. The culinary highlight were the balls - wonderful cheese-filled donut-hole like bread nuggets that came with marinara sauce. The copious TVs, multitude of bad dates around us, and great company made up for tonight's lackluster culinary experience.

I can't comment on their repair services, but I'm always able to have questions answered and find a good selection when looking for a part or accessory. They seem to carry items in varying price ranges, which is helpful if you're on a budget, or really looking to invest in something. During business hours, they have a tire pump station set up outside. A great convenience to have in the neighborhood!

Also, I could walk in with my well-behaved dog, which is always a plus :)
Solid pizza, boring pasta, slow service. I can only really recommend the pizza for takeout. Everything else falls flat

Saw a fair amount of mixed reviews. Did my homework and found that they had a good deal on Falken tires with installation, road hazard, and lifetime balance. Talked to Mike, who was helpful through the process. Initially they said they only had 3 tires in stock and had to pick up the fourth, and it would take a few hours. I was short on time and told them I'd come back in the AM. The manager, Milken, dropped by, talked to a tech outside, came back and told me they could start now and have other tire sent over. Within 20 minutes, the car was on the rack. Talked to the tech who pulled my full sized spare and switched out the 15 year old tire for me. 

As the car was racked, the fourth tire arrived. They took the wheels to the back to mount and balance the new Falkens. They offered an alignment, which is typical for a tire shop, I declined since my previous tires have no signs of uneven wear and I have no steering issues. 

I came around 4:30p and tires were mounted by 6:15p. They brought out the torque wrench and tightened down all the lugs, which was nice to see; some places don't. 

Overall, had a positive experience. Friendly staff, quick service, a good deal, and no issues. Can't speak for maintenance, but would be back for any tire issue in the future.

Pretty nice Barnes and Noble. It is two stories, clean, and fairly quiet. Large kids section on the upper level out of the way which is nice for kid-free people like myself.
