Only complaint about the bookstore is the lack of seating. There is tons of open space upstairs that is being wasted. This is a problem on weekends when the store is very busy often making it hard to find a place to sit down.

There is a Starbucks inside of course. Lots of tables here and free Wifi. Only downside is there are only two plugs for computers in the cafe. If you are planning to set up camp here bring an extension cord or a full charged battery. Seating can be hard to find on weekends in the cafe as well.

Came here with 7 others from a conference. Food, drinks, service excellent. We sat at the bar and watched them cook. Vegetarians and omnivores alike were quite pleased. Skirt steak w/ trumpet mushrooms was very pleasant as was the butternut squash!  It was empty at 5:30 pm but packed by 6:15.

Has this location closed?

Wild West Wings location was not open when I went there at 1:26pm on Wednesday June 14th 2017.

Portions are so small. More like a side dish really. It's literally a very shallow small bowl. Looks are deceiving because the rim is wide. The contents would not make a full plate not even a half plate of pasta. I like the pasta, seemed fresh and homemade but  not so much the sauce. I thought it was supposed to be vegan but tasted off maybe it had dairy in it, I don't know. I had the Pomadoro and fusilli. 

The people are nice, the cannoli is hand stuffed to order which is great but for my friend but I'm vegan and not enough options to even get full on. I left and had to make some food and dessert at home ! You should not leave hungry from a pasta restaurant. That's a sin. lol 

While I like the environment the dim lighting, lots of space and nice people, very approachable environment I would only have reason to come here if I wanted one particular side dish of pasta and maybe a glass of wine.

My girlfriend and I were visiting Philly for a work trip and had an extra day to tool around the city today. We both love whiskey and found a Groupon for Red Brick Distillery and decided to do a tasting in between meals and historic sightseeing. 

Only 4 people showed up for the 1PM tasting, which made the whole experience intimate and really chill. My girlfriend and I shared an Old Fashioned at the beginning of the tour and got to bring it along throughout the entire 45 minute tour, which was really cool. The bartender (Nate) and the distiller (Brian) were both very knowledgeable and articulate and walked us through a 'how to' of distilling and tasting whiskey spirits. Honestly, it felt like we were having drinks with friends who were teaching us about spirits.

I asked a ton of questions throughout the distillery tour (since we were a small group) and was impressed with how patiently Brian answered them all. After the tour, we did a tasting that was easily the best 'sip-by-sip' walk through experience I could imagine for appreciating whiskey (2 types) and rum (1 type). We were told about flavors that would evolve in our mouths just before and while they did, with amazing accuracy. This was different from any tasting I've been on, and was extremely memorable.  We left with 2 bottles and 2 whiskey glasses to take back to California.

I'd totally recommend visiting this distillery, and I'd come back again if fate brought me back to Philly.  They are working on some new creations that aren't quite ready yet that sound exciting to try out.  5 stars, and thank you both for such a great experience!

I had 3 cats. The last one close to a year. He was a stray and my 2 other cats and him did not get along.  I am planning on moving to a smaller place and can't have more than 2 cats.  My older two cats started exhibiting behavior that was not conducive to me selling my place.  Upon research, I came up AWA in Voorhees.  I drove 1 hour on the NJ Turnpike.  I felt that this place would be good for Loverboy.  It was clean and they seemed very nice.  The professional yet caring female who helped me said they would contact me as soon as Loverboy got adopted.  If you have to surrender a pet, I wouldn't hesitate about taking it there.  I see them walking the dogs each time I have been there.  Was there 2 times.

Harvest is a great place to have a meal, whether it be for a special occasion or just w Saturday lunch. Their ever changing seasonal menu always makes it hard to choose what to get. When I was in last week for a birthday dinner, we had the scallops and strawberry chicken salad. Both were delicious. For desert We shared the peanut butter shooter and enjoyed it. Service is always prompt and the place has nice bar cocktails and great wine list.

A fab burger joint in Santa Barbara, they do their burgers right . They have the right mix of "menu" burgers and make your own, plus you can modify their "menu" versions which is what I did! This is a must stop whenever in Santa Barbara!

Waited 45minutes told it would be10-15minutes when I checked in, bar had three empty tables but apparently not enough servers according to the hostess.  The hostess didn't seem concerned in the least with their tardiness.  The final time I asked about my seat I was told "just wait, I'll let you know."  This was 30 minutes after they told me they were cleaning my table.  Won't be returning I wouldn't recommend it unless you bring a good book.

Came here for a haircut. Over priced but I decided to see how it would go. $40 for haircut. Nearing the end of my haircut I requested a goatee lineup. No problem. No one seemed to think it was appropriate to let me know that that would be an additional $40!!! Way way way overpriced at $80 for a haircut and goatee. Haircut was okay, goatee edges was just done with an electric razor.... sub par at best.

Go to spot for the latest and greatest cell phones. Too scared to buy online on ebay or don't want to deal with customs from the states? This is the place. 

Always great service from the Omegacell team.
The dumplings. Any of them are straight up delish! I'm jonesing for some again now as I write this review. Lol....

This place was excellent! The food, the service, the price. I had the flat iron steak and it was perfectly cooked. The price was very reasonable. Maybe I'm just used to California prices. We were there the night before the tornado. I hope they came out of it ok. I would highly recommend.

I never give 5 stars but this is a place that my best friend and I always mention and left a mark on us! We stumbled on this spot, not knowing it was vegan. It just looked beautiful and had nice ambiance. Sat at the bar the bartenders were wonderful and cocktails were phenomenal! Fresh ingredients... watching them make It enjoying their craft and it was just perfect. We got the Korean tofu and gnocchi. Staff was diligent, greatt hospitality and time efficient. We were so shocked at how flavorful and creative the food was... most restauranants that offer vegetarian options throw something together  but this was very well thought out. We then realized oh! It's vegan! What a pleasant surprise. In 10 years that I've been a vegetarian Charlie was a sinner. definitely left their mark on me, and have made a new standard! Food, presentation, quality, drinks, space... A+ !

No frills. No garnish. Just a good solid carne asada taco. I have tried a few other things on their menu but I can never go there and not have a couple of tacos...so I can't vouch for a huge amount of the rest of their menu.

But the tacos...slightly chewy corn tortillas, nicely spiced meat with cilantro and lime juice. Basic, simple food at its best! How can I make my taco meat like that at home? I just can't...

I'm wondering if I should try the head in my taco one day though. Curious.

The flat-out best cannoli I have ever had (I haven't had much).  They were friendly and knowledgeable about the cannoli, and I sample the regular with ricotta cheese and the chocolate flavored ones.  Doubles as a bakery, but I haven't had the bravery to go outside of the cannoli.  The best cannoli in Philly, if not the world!

Well, I have been to this restaurant twice within the last couple of weeks.  Once for a generous fundraiser and the food was awesome. I would have given a 5 star rating. However, we tried again last night and not nearly as impressive. I was told "at least a half an hour" for my food to be ready, but by the time I got there, about a half an hour later, the food was not hot, it clearly was ready in a shorter time. The veggie burro which I was so happy to have a good selection of vegetarian options from
the menu, this time was full of too many fajita veggies- onions and peppers dominated it and I really didn't enjoy it. I tore it apart and will try to eat the leftovers as nachos or on a separate tortilla since the one it came with was soggy and unappealing.  My son got a cheeseburger, first time he loved it- last night he even realized it wasn't as good. Maybe they have different cooks each night? Maybe Friday vs a weeknight the amount of business they get is  extremely varied? Idk, but I'll rate with a 4 due to one really good experience and one just so-so.

If I could give 6 stars, I would. I have been here 5 times now. My choice of appetizers never changed, neither has their preparation. Always delicious with great service, I can't say enough about this restaurant.

Instantly, the dim lighting and romantic decor stands out. No matter what floor you dine, the seats are extremely comfortable and the scenery perfect for a date.

I recommend the smoked Marlin tacos and sweet plantains as must have appetizers. For a main dish, the Vaca Frita has been superb 3 times I've had it. The new (as of the week of this posting) Arroz con Pollo was excellent, and their 16oz ribeye is perfect. I believe my favorite was the Duck, though. So flavorful.

For vegetarians, their scallion dish was absolutely amazing. 

I also am a big fan of their cocktails. I tend to gravitate mostly to the Havana Punch. Sweet, tropical, creeps up on you.

I'm not big on deserts. Their famed chocolate cigar is EXTREMELY rich even for the biggest of chocolate lovers. Their Flan, though, is hands down the best I have ever eaten.

My bill in each trip was about $120-150, depending on if I grab extra tacos and another Punch or not. Highly recommended.

First time ordering from this place online and received a missed call from restaurant.  Call back and the manager rudely asks me "are you going to pick this order up? We are not doing delivery tonight."  I placed my order an hour ago and just now got a call that i will not be getting my food.

Not a great first time experience and definitely my last!

Every time we go never any room to sit over crowed and no parking. A new parking lot is in the planning which will greatly help. 
The owner is having his problems with East Torresdale Civil Assoc. On improvements he wants to make. I live in the area and Frankly this place has gotten to big. I lived up here since 1980 when it was a small unknown bar now you can't enjoy the view way to many people.
They have quizzo Wednesday at 8pm
The food was excellent.  There was so many great vegetarian options that  it was hard to chose. The restaurant got terribly busy and they were unfortunately short staffed but well worth the wait
Ramen was very good. Took all my friends and they liked it too! I had original soup with egg noodles chicken beef and bean sprouts

I was visiting from San Francisco, CA on business. The warm welcome was very genuine. The Nica unique cuisine left me speechless, I savored every bite. I had to meet the chef and personalized how wonderful my meal was. This is far the best Nica Cuisine I have experienced! Please visit and allow your tastebuds pure pleasure.
Monkey Burger was great!  Funky atmosphere and service was friendly.  Kids enjoyed the kids burger and fried pickles.

I used them a couple of times. Nice Mercedes truck pulled up and off my fur babies went. Upon return my yorkie was shaved, head and all. All she needed was a trim. Needless to say, I was not happy. Evidently, my baby moved when someone was walking by and the razor slipped. I would have appreciated being told of the incident prior to shaving her down, since they were in my driveway. The groomer called the owner who refused to even give a discount. (I'm guessing they need to pay for that nice truck). I paid and never used them again. I continue to get calls from them reminding me it's time for grooming. Really?  Another Yelper made a very good point, they don't care if they have your business or not. Super expensive, with less than average services.

We went through the drive through because it's closed inside. But they messed up our order completely. The line wasn't even that long and they were completely incompetent. We ordered sides and never got them. We ordered biscuits and meals that came with biscuits and never got them. They gave us tenders instead of bone in. They obviously only care about drive through times because they just threw together a bag. My kids were disappointed and not fed all the way. I will not be going back and I will make sure everyone knows this restaurant is not somewhere to go during this time. Taco Bell and McDonald's and all other restaurants are stepping it up and Popeyes is not. Unfortunately it won't let me give 0 stars.

Run, don't walk run away. What a rip off. We paid $1050 for furnace repair, main board, and igniter. They covered $300 and it took them over a month to determine that. They did not send any determination letters, I had to request it.
Update GM named Lane contacted me about my issue and offered to assist. This is the first time someone competent is talking to me and explaining how this should have worked. He assured me that the price i received was incorrect. He checked into the claim and sure it was. I take it back dont run away, stay.  I am adding 4 more stars for the effort.
Unbelievable food made by unbelievable people. If you like bagels, then this might be your new favorite restaurant. They just opened today and already fully sold out. This is the real deal!!

Awesome service! The people are knowledgeable, friendly & professional, they not only service electronics but have a great selection of phones & accessories. The prices are compatible to the electronics stores in the mall. I definitely recommend this place.

Worst service Ever!!!

45 minutes and still waiting in the lobby for our pizza. No one else here!!!

Overall, I always have a good time at Dave and Buster's.

I ate at the bar this time since I was by myself.  Even though I was at the bar, Aty still noticed and greeted me.  Christian provided awesome service.

Within an hour of me eating, it got extremely busy, not just at the bar, but overall in the dining section in the midway.  I felt badly for Christian because it didn't seem he had the appropriate backup.  But he handled it like a pro.

Not long after I paid my tab at the bar, I noticed the midway filling up with people.  The Winner's Circle was EXTREMELY busy and usually there would be two people.  However, Symone was by herself.  She is awesome and always friendly.  I also felt badly for her because she didn't have the necessary backup when the line for ticket redemption wrapped around the entire Winner's Circle, which was the first I've EVER seen it that busy since I've started coming regularly.  However, Symone handled it with amazing composure and a smile.

There are great staff working at this Dave and Buster's Plymouth location and this is why I always come back.

Where did the quality go?   Drinks (iced tea and margarita) served in plastic cups, no plates for the appetizer...somewhat disappointing.  Food was ok. 

Waitress never asked how I wanted my steak cooked with the fajita so they cooked it well done.  It was like jerky.

We ate a quick breakfast here while waiting for our flight. The food was good, the service was terrible. We were the only people on our side of the restaurant and still it took forever to get served. The servers were friendly enough, but very very slow. There seemed to be some confusion about who was supposed to be taking our order or delivering the food. They also forgot to bring us cutlery and didn't top up the water. There were 4 different people I saw working on our side, but like i said, it took forever to be served and no one seemed to know what they were doing. One waiter actually said to us....I have no idea what I am supposed to do right now. Not cool. Airports are typically pretty busy places. They need to get their act together a bit more. But the food is good.

We hadn't been to Blind Onion for a while and we decided to stop in for a beer and pizza. We sat at the bar where we normally sit and had the absolute best service that we've ever had. Kerry helped us and was absolutely fantastic. He had a great positive attitude, was super friendly, and provided great customer service hands down. The food came out very quickly and it was so delicious. We had the opportunity to also meet Sarah who was an absolute joy to talk with. The dynamic of this store is on point. We will definitely come back more often now knowing that these awesome people work there. 

Definitely recommend this restaurant and this location above all the rest. Love our local store!

The quality of the seafood here is first rate  raw oysters and clams clam chowder entrees ranging from the lobster soft shell crabs scallops fish ask fresh and very well prepared. The joint is casual but the service is personal and responsive. Can't understand any of the mediocre reviews.
. The wine list is small but this is not a wine restaurant. 
We were a party if nine and well treated.  Four stars not five because if the casual nature of the place.

Check out the prices before! This was my first time visiting the salon and I will never come back. I came in at 11 for my appointment and left 4 hours later!! The cost was $485 for cut and colour. (Balayage) My stylist had a cancellation after my appointment so I felt like she took advantage of the situation. It felt like she was taking her time since apparently I was being charged "hourly." I have never paid that for my hair and left the salon feeling sick to my stomach. She did apologize for it running late and thanked me for my patience but I do not think I should off been charged for that length of time. I understand that what I got done is a lengthy process but after calling around to other salons, as well as my previous experience getting my hair done, the cost is no where near to what I paid. It would of been nice to have a heads up of the process and time it was going to take. My hair looked great but to be charged that amount is completely ridiculous. Absolutely disgusted.

A very well-rounded price fixe lunch menu. The oysters are great. Lovely service from Brandon and with such enjoyable food. We appreciated the 25-cent martinis for our group of seven. (Where can you get 23 martinis for under six dollars?) Great ambience in this historic place and highly recommend.

So great ! My overall experience truly amazed me and exceeded my expectations! The mash potatoes are zesty and the mac is perfect ! The chicken was flaky at the skin and juicy and tender with the meat. The okra and the gravy were compliments to the whole thing.

A high recommendation from me if you are in the area or just visiting!

Its Friday night and all I wanted was Chinese food and a good movie to watch. I called for a delivery order. Ordered Bourbon Chicken combo, with fried rice and eggroll. A small side of veggie lo mein and some crab ragoons (wasn't much of an order.) Before I got off the phone, I asked how long would the order take...they said 30-40 minutes. Okay, thats fair enough for a Friday. So in the meantime, I started the movie of choice...30 minutes passed, 40 minutes passed,... 1 hour passed. I called by this time, they say another 10 minutes. Well, they didn't get to my house until about 15 minutes before my movie was over. I was starving.... The only reason I gave two stars, my food was hot, and the delivery guy said "Have a blessed day". The bourbon chicken was alright.... Panda Express does it better.

Incredible service, from making the reservation to the check. Very nice setting, with super views over Philly.  The food was just ok. I had salmon, which was cooked perfectly, but came with a huge serving of frisée lettuce that was almost impossible to eat and bitter. The veggie sides were way too overly seasoned with salt, butter and  garlic.  These are easy fixes for such an expensive restaurant.

The great: Oreo beignets! Definitely great, but you only get 3-4 for $7. 

The rest of their food is mediocre and that would be okay if they weren't as insanely overpriced as they are, because rich people live here now. The chips are boring, the cubano is meh, as are most of the sandwiches. The service is hit or miss, the miss being a really dour by the young woman with a very round face, freckles and fro-y/curly hair, pulled back) or fine/sorta nice (most of the others).

Bottom line: The food is really not worth the money, and it hurts to pay this much (Especially when it's a choice between hangry-ness because I'm too exhausted or in too much pain to walk to Adams Street Market which is a way better deal and much more affordable.

This place is hip, cool, and "open" for business! It has such a cool atmosphere - very aesthetic and hipster. I went here and had one of the best lattes ever. While I love their drinks, they are on the pricier side. And you have to be a true coffee connoisseur to know what all the drinks are and what goes into them. I had to ask about a few. But they are creative and delicious!

The food here was off the chart good. We had the baked Brie appetizer and it was fantastic. It was served with roast garlic and stewed apples, yummy! The duck with the pepper jelly glaze was possibly the best duck I have ever eaten, if you are a duck fan, get it (even if you aren't seriously consider it). We ordered three of there desserts and they were the perfect size and the best desserts we've had on the trip. The almond and chocolate mousse is something really special and I highly recommend it. The beer expert in the family gives this place high marks on beer. The only reason this gets four stars and bit five is that the live music they advertise on Friday and Saturday nights is not great and we hardly noticed throughout the meal. Go here for the food not the music

My wife told me she was in the mood for Italian and wanted to try the place in our neighborhood. I couldn't imagine what I was about to experience. Pure culinary bliss!! From the moment we walked in we were greeted by the hostess and seated immediately. When you come here, be prepared to be amazed by the incredible service. The owners themselves constantly checked on us to be sure everything was perfect, and it was! My wife had the spaghetti with lobster and crab. It was sensational. I had the lobster ravioli. It was truly the best Italian meal I've ever had. My mom had the Chilean sea bass with champagne risotto. Superb!! I've dined in the best restaurants in Tampa. This is definitely in the top 5! If you love Italian food do yourself a favor and get here!!! You will not be disappointed!!

Gate 1 has quickly resolved my issue, and I am once again impressed by their efforts to work with clients. Once I was able to get in contact with them, they were able to explain my options and allowed me to select the one that best met my needs.  I'm looking forward to more trips with Gate 1 when it is safe to travel. Thank you, Jesse, for your excellent customer service.

Very delicious! I wasn't expecting to find such good food at a place like this. It doesn't look like much, but the effort these guys put into making a good bite is immense. Ask them about the food they serve and they are extremely knowledgeable as well. They clearly take pride.

I was truly impressed by every aspect -- service, price, food -- you name it. I even bought a 2nd sandwich to go for dinner!

We broke out! Our team conquered the 1776 room like champs. The game was challenging for sure, but we had 3 minutes to spare. Loved the theme of the room and how it transported you to a different time and place with the props and attention to detail. 

Our guide, Luke, laid the rules for us and was encouraging as we progressed through our clues. He gave us three free hints that helped us move forward when we were stumped. 

The only bummer to the evening was the "private" dinner beforehand. We were with a large group of 20-30 people eating a catered dinner in their main sitting/dining area. Other groups from other parties were finishing rooms and making lots of noise while we were trying to listen to presentations, and the staff ushered in new groups through our room - making it less private and I'm sure those groups felt odd, too. Which there was a separate entrance and exit so if a group is using the private space, it's exclusive.

Bland bland bland bland. If you want an omelette that has zero flavor, this is the place for you. I basically ate texture. Or if you just want a bunch of sweet crap loaded on a pancake. I was super hungry after an early flight and after wasting an hour and $30, I'm still hungry. What a mistake

We are big fans of this group, St. Elmo, Harry & Izzy, Burger Study...and were happy to see a new branch on the family tree. Food and drinks? The rubber shrimp had its familiar sinus searing cocktail sauce, the  egregiously over salted filet was accompanied by the delicious beans....so, good and bad. Overpriced drinks, but so what else is new. Pleasant, competent service, if a bit slow. That is not what the issue is. This is new building, custom designed. Clearly a lot of money was spent, and time and effort and more  money.....But....Whoever did the design of the space has either great contempt and disdain for patrons paying hundreds of dollars for the experience, or  has never sat in a restaurant before, or is just plain bad at design. Chairs more suited to a torture chamber during the Spanish inquisition face blank walls. Tables so tiny they barely held two dinners. Unbearable sound levels, on a  night that was not even so busy. Lighting that left us dazed, spots so bright that we thought we were under arrest alternate with areas of  near total darkness. Sorry guys, we expect a tiny bit of comfort at an upscale restaurant, even if you can't manage any real style. Unpleasant and  uncomfortable. With all the great steakhouses and lounges within a short drive, we will not likely come back here.
Great if you like overpriced (but fresh and *oooh* organic ::rolls eyes::) food and girls with hairy armpits.

All I'm going to say is... if you haven't been to Jazz Fest, you need to go!  It's a once in a lifetime experience and completely worth is.

NOLA,  I will be back for this again! :)

Delicious food and to top it all  they have great service. Will definitely be back soon. 
Very clean and always busy, it must be for a good reason.

Unbelievable service and unbelievable food at an unbelievable price. Genuinely impressed with this place I will be back for sure next time I'm in STL! Sean was our waiter and he was just a great guy! Fun activities to do too!

I went in to get a couple shirts tailored. 3 people working, but none can say hello or even look up. Finally after standing for a few moments, I was acknowledged but not helped. This place is not keen on professionalism or customer service. 

I turned and walked out, and they didn't speak a word. That told me exactly how much they cared. Take your business elsewhere if possible, you'll be happier for it.

Besides the obnoxious decor, the folks behind Mama Carollas pull off breakfast pretty well.  I had a traditional breakfast, consisting of an egg, some sausage, and a pancake.  Treat yourself to the fried biscuits/donuts/whatever they are called.  You won't be disappointed.  

As mentioned in other reviews, the coffee leaves some to be desired.  Patachou and Zest have spoiled that for us by serving some of the best coffees around.  

Service was acceptable,  and the food was served promptly.

I'll definitely be back around, but maybe with a pair of sunglasses on.  Or a blindfold.

Service was great but when you charge $5 extra for 10 chips for an appetizer that you originally only got 10 chips with we think pretty petty. Terrible move on management. We won't be back to eat here.
Great chocolate pie! Very good green beans and roast beef. Bus guy was very friendly and got me more tea. The rolls are divine!

Smooth. The way a martini should be.

This place is slick. Yeah, it's tucked into the Omni hotel, but it has it's own entrance, so it qualifies as it's own place. It just so happens that after 3 martinis, you won't have to drive anywhere.

And yes, I did order 3 martinis. A connoisseur of the vodka martini, I was putting this place to the test. 3 martinis, dry, all ordered at the same time. One was to be a Stoli, a vodka I consider good, but not top shelf. One was to be top shelf, and a third was to be a well vodka. The challenge: could I identify the vodkas, could the bartender maintain the same quality across 3 drinks (same minute amounts of vermouth, equally chilled), and would he cross contaminate, using the same shaker for the well and top shelf vodkas.

Olives is a short walk from the convention center, so if you are a conventioneer, this is a nice comfortable place to hang with your fellow con-folks. It can be a little smokey, as it is also a cigar bar, but somehow cigar smoke offends me less than cigarette smoke.

As for the martini challenge, the bartender was all aces. I could tell you how I did on my end, but that would be called bragging.

So I called this morning to reschedule today's appt due to inclement weather and the receptionist Mandy had the audacity to say well we are all here and there is a $50 fee. Are you kidding me? There is a fee cause of bad weather and I might not be able to make it and that little comment was rude. Not everyone lives in an area where it's plowed and maybe they all live areas that don't get much snow. Thank god today is not the day my mother in law also passed away cause I would of been charged a fee for a death in family. I think they need to take certain things into consideration with this fee. This is the only dental office I have ever been to that charges a fee to reschedule and I been to a lot here. This might be my last visit here today.

BLUF:  Updated review:  Still amazing.  I'll go back anytime and would give it 6 stars if I could.

Huge fan.  Awesome atmosphere in a good neighborhood.  This place has gotten popular so it takes a while.  Make a reservation if you can.  The staff was great at keeping us updated since they were so slammed.  Great staff.

We started with the yuka (like a potato) and choriso app.  it was really good with an interesting sauce.  (Update: just went back and got the fried plantains with mozzarella and it was pretty good but needs something else to give it a kick.  also got the ear of corn appetizer which was amazingly good.)

3 of us each got a different arepa.  i got the pork one and it was amazing.  huge too. very well priced in my opinion.  these arepas are definitely not standard arepas from south america.  they are served open and stuffed with a crazy amount of stuff.  very very good.  it would be hard for me to order anything different but i am sure everything there is good. a buddy got the soup which he really liked.  soup aint my thing though.

When i went back my sis got a tuna arepa and it was a nice lighter version of their arepas. I got the chorizo this time and it was out of this world.  

i really liked this place and cant wait to go back.

The food here was good, my biggest issue was the seating.  They had a booth bench at one of the tables that was entirely too low which made eating awkward.  It seems like they try to pack way too many people around each table and it results in a lack of elbow room.

That aside, the noodles and fried rice were good, as well as the chicken and steak.

Incredible pizza! Ordered a small build your own with traditional crust, red sauce, sliced tomato and spinach. Cooked to perfection. Fresh ingredients perfectly proportioned and the sauce, omg the sauce!!! First time here, a wonderful community feel pizza joint that also offered trivia tuesdays! Also dog friendly, great draft beer and wine list! So pleased, just may go back before the week is over!

Thai dinners were good... as stir fries. Curries could have more sauce but overall we tried different fish and vegetarian options and all were good! A good Thai place to visit if you are in redington area!

Thank god for Yelp. I just discovered I can leave a review. Note this review is from 6 years ago but I cannot and will not forget the service I received from here. I came in for a relaxer for my prom back in 2010 (18 at the time) and was attracted to the salon because they had offered the Phyto brand of relaxer at the time. Upon entering the salon, the staff was somewhat aloof, however I believe the owner of the salon was more cordial when my service was started. After my service however, I was largely ignored and while waiting in the lobby area to be picked up by my parent, I inquired about some products for hair upkeep. The front desk woman laughed at me and started to mock my word choice repeatedly asking what did that even mean. I remember being confused by her reaction and I explained what shampoo or conditioner would you recommend I buy. She just laughed and said "yeah, right. go look for yourself." and walked away to go to the back of the salon to sweep away clipped hair. I just remember feeling depressed after the visit and not knowing what to make of it. It was my prom coming up and I already was self-conscious and this didn't help with any underlying self-doubt young adults tend to have about their appearance prior to such a landmark event. I had initially liked my hair but that service left me feeling horrible inside and I never returned. I'm happy to see they have a yelp page up so that I can now share that story. They have probably changed some things in the past 6 years so giving the benefit of the doubt but I still look back on that day and wished I had spent my money somewhere else (I spent about $200 that day after saving up for a few months) where respect of guests is routine and the focus is on building up the confidence of the people who enter your doors.

The best bbq  ever!
Brisket is amazing. Very moist.  I am a regular local customer. Also the owner is very nice

Had such a great dinner there our first night in Santa Barbara, that we made reservations for the next as well. We were seated by Dennis Franz so that made it even more fun. The food and the service were great. We will be back next time we are in The area.
so glad I found this place! its super clean, staff is friendly. They use a plastic liner for pedicures which is great..manicures last longer than other places and they are very accommodating...

If they used something other than Styrofoam for take out, I would have given a 5.  The food was healthy and awesome!  Will be back with my own container.   Please consider switching to paper or some other recyclable container.

Quick and well done! I was looking for a tailor which these days it can be a difficult task. I called Willard's and told them I needed 3 coats altered. They told me it would be done in a day. Wow! 

Willard's offers quick, friendly, and professional service. I was very impressed that I could get my coats finished so quickly. 

I highly recommend that you go to Willard's for your tailoring needs.

This place is adorable! I love the cozy indoor furniture, and the outdoor patio's fountain and lights are so charming. I can't comment on the coffee, but the gelato was delicious (the Mayan chocolate was perfectly spicy). I would definitely come again for dessert or to spend some time studying.

Small and older looking store which is pretty clean. They have a lot of deals laid out to try and help them out against their competitor which seems to have a store on every corner. Staff is helpful enough and gets you to what you are looking for and out the door. Good meat from the beef people.

Stumbled upon Russell's today during my lunch break at work and decided to give a try.  It is an awesome inside and even has a mini fire pit!  Decided to go with the Chipotle Chicken sandwich and it was very good!  The service was very attentive and friendly. I am definitely going to try it again and try there amazing looking cheesecake

Would usually never go to a restaurant with only 3 Yelp stars, but went with a group of friends at their suggestion and to enjoy their company. My expectations were low, so I was not surprised. Only positives were that there was plenty of meat in the entree and it was edible. Service was awful. Food presentation was disgusting. Prices were certainly not in line with the food and the experience. Way too many decent-to-good local places for Mexican food, so no need to ever return to this chain. Do yourself a favor...don't go.
Good heating and cooling company. Exceptional customer service would recommend to anyone looking for specialist service to check them out!
Good spot near our home when we're craving Asian food. Sushi was decent and the chicken basil was delicious. Polite service and quick. Will definitely come back.
Service was pleasant even during Covid restrictions. I was seated fairly quickly. Order was taken and food arrived in a timely manner. The biscuits are very delicious!

Warm interior, and reliably good food. But something is amiss. The service is perfunctory. Dinner service is placed in front of the wrong people; wine glasses are not replaced for a new bottle of white. The server never asks about more water or bread (which is very good).  The woman at reception is sweet but seems new to the restaurant business--like an intern on her first day. We mentioned it was our son's birthday--ignored. 

The food good.  Duck perhaps had too much of the taste and flavor of accompanying bacon side dish.  The pork shoulder was yummy. Salmon--like it is everywhere. First courses--a bit small, but flavorful. 

There are just so many wonderful BYO's in Philadelphia--this one just needs to try a bit harder.
We love this place.  Everything on the menu that we've tried has been great.  Camerones a la crema is my favorite thus far.

Oh, Maxine's, I LOVE YOU. Seriously, I can't get enough of you. The fluffy vanilla waffles with the juicy, classic comfort of 3 jumbo wings, and the heavenly goodness known as PEACH BUTTER, how could I not love you? 

Oh yeah, your service. It's pretty hit or miss. As many other Yelpers have reviewed, one day it is great, very attentive service, but the next it can be pretty sucky. Also, sometimes the food wait is a little longer than I would like. (Once, I forgot I actually ordered my food because it was so long. But then, yesterday when I got there, my food was up before I got done eating my Corncakes w/ Peach Butter.)

Finally, the parking lot. That's one heck of a back up, go forward, back up, go forward ordeal.

Though you have a few flaws and you're mostly a hit or miss place, Maxine's, you're worth it to me.

Nice, convenient, clean, comfortable and the decor is beautiful. What more could you want? It was exactly what we needed after a long day on the road. Our check in was quick and I slept like a baby. Highly recommended !
This place is a perfect spot to chill out and do some work or just have fun with your friends. Their choices are yummy and not to mention quite beautiful. The decor is also stunning and fun.

So disappointed. We live in Tampa and have come to Berns on several occasions, typically a celebration type event. Always walked away satisfied and impressed. Last night though was a completely different experience. Treated my husband for a milestone birthday and were excited to score a table in the bar, as we were not able to get a reservation. We ordered our usual go to- Chateaubriand and upgraded to special aged cut. I do not eat potatoes so requested a different side.  From the beginning, things were just off. The French Onion soup was luke warm and bland. No onions in it, just broth. The salad was ok, nothing to write home about but fine. When the meal arrived, the presentation was very unappetizing. The meat was luke warm at best and there was no warm Bearnaise sauce presented with it. We had to ask
and it took a while to get. The meat itself was cooked as requested but was completely tasteless. I mean NO TASTE at all. The vegetables were cold and gross. And, I was given a double serving of what was was plated as my extra vegetables requested. Would have expected broccoli and something different. Was not told I had to order that.

As it was a birthday, I did not want to raise a stink and spoil the mood but as the evening wore on, and I was imaging the bill I was going to receive at the end of this experience, for a meal that was below mediocre at best, the more upset I became. I did finally tell our server and expressed how unhappy I was. He stated that I should have said something in the very beginning- which is true but, honestly IT"S BERNS- should never have been this way in the first place. In the end, he went back to his manager TWICE to see what they could do.The first time, they just offered to reimburse us for the vegetables.Yes, just the vegetables. $16.00 for shredded carrots and some uneatable greens. Finally, they did take a percentage off of the bill as compensation, but, the evening and experience and birthday was pretty much ruined at that point. 

Not sure what is going on over there but if you are going to spend several hundred dollars on a meal, you should get what you pay for- and with Berns that includes a relaxing and enjoyable experience. This was a far cry. Dont buy into the hype, There are many other great restaurants in Tampa. Had planned on a holiday party there but after this experience, will not be going back again.

I found out about stylist D. through a friend and had to try her. So happy I did because she is great! 

The atmosphere of the shop was laid back and relaxed but still professional. D. is very polite but honest. She also makes you feel very comfortable. It did not feel like it was only our first time meeting. All-in-all I love her personality! 

 To give a short back story, I hadn't been to a beautician in years due to horrible - balding - past experiences and then I just began to feel like paying money for something I can do myself is pointless. But now that my hair has reached this length just washing & conditioning has become a huge task that I have to mentally prepare for!

I really just came for the shampoo & scalp massage so the finished product was like the icing on top of the cake! I figured it would look nice but not this good! I loved the shine and how straight it was. Plus she even styled it for me without my asking. 

The big kicker is that she suggested I NOT get bangs. What beautician does that?! They are usually drooling for the opportunity to put scissors in my hair! I expressed my concerns with having my hair trimmed and her response was that she is not scissor happy. She definitely proved that to me.

Last but certainly not least, her prices are amazing! People usually want to charge me a lot based off of my length but her price range is very minimal compared to what I've seen. I will definitely be back and I'll be plugging her to everyone I know!

SO WONDERFUL! 
I've had plenty of traditional Japanese ramen to know that this place is truly a gift. The staff are very kind and helpful and the food is delicious. I had the spicy miso ramen - which I've had elsewhere - and it was so worth the order. The crab ragoons were good as well. They homemade their ice cream everyday and it sells out fast! 
The big paintings on the walls are blown up versions of the owners tattoos! They support local artists and the restaurant definitely has a unique and special flare to it.
Great fresh produce all year round. Very well priced and a great variety.

Not particularly crazy about Indian food and husband is. We had takeout from Curry Leaf  once. We both liked their chilli chicken and aloo ghobi. Husband liked the naan and vegetable samosa. The other dishes we ordered (vegetable biryani, chk tikka, chk curry, and chk tikki masala) were just ok. I thought the veg samosa was the worst samosa I have ever eaten and the food was a little oily. We wanted to give 3.5 stars but do not feel the food was a 4. We are not sure if we would order again.
Only Body shop I trust! Always very professional and great work! Always trust my car is in good hands with these guys!

This is the best Vegan restaurant I have been to. It has been years in the making but it's plant based AlIVE and oh so good. The ambiance is simple and stunning at the same time. The bar is a great hang out place for a large group of friends or you can hang outside and dine under the stars. I have to say this will be my new normal. Great job Oliver's , I'll be back over and over and over again.

Just had dinner at about 5pm.
I was seated promptly and provided a menu. My waitress, Sam, arrived to offer me a beverage before I could look at even 1/2 of the menu. I ordered a Mai Tai (plenty of alcohol in it) which arrived in less than 5 minutes along with a glass of water. 
Sam offered me the special of the day and I chose to order the 8 ounce New York steak medium  rare.  It comes with stuffed mashed potatoes and green beans. 
My silverware died not look clean, however, Sam apologized and immediately replaced them.  I have a feeling that it may be the water here that leaves a film on items rather than left over food.
My food arrived very quickly. The temperature of the meat and green beans were perfect. The potatoes were not quite hot enough to melt the cheese but warm enough to eat. The "stuffed" ingredients of the potatoes had obviously been freshly added and may be why they were not as hot as they should have been.
The steak was not the best cut of New York that I have had, but it was cooked as requested and had a tasty sauce on it. The green beans were cooked perfectly so that they were neither mush or hard. It all had good flavor.
Sam checked on me numerous times throughout my meal and was very pleasant. The other wait staff and bartender appeared friendly with the other patrons.
Sam arrived just after I finished my meal and offered desert which arrived promptly.
The brownie ala mode was yummy with a better than average quality whipped cream on top. My check arrived soon after finishing desert.
All in all a good dinner with wonderful service. I think the prices were about what I would expect to pay, although, I am from the South Bay in California and have not been out much locally to know costs here. Dinner, a drink and desert was $35 with tax. 
I was in and out in less than an hour and had plenty of time for a walk on the beach.

My review is based on lunch on a Thursday around 2p.m., while sitting outside. I didn't get to see the super busy and/or long wait times that other yelpers have commented about.

Stopped in for lunch on our afternoon out, seeing as there were people sitting outside enjoying themselves. We had a nice waiter (Harley) who was on top of everything too. I ordered a curry with a side of naan and my mom opted for the chicken pot pie. I only ordered a mild curry because I wasn't sure what they considered medium or hot, and I'll definitely bump it up to at least a medium next time. The naan was a little sad-looking (and tasting... almost like a pita); I'll pass on it next time. I tried a bite of the pot pie and the chicken was very moist. 

Our two meals plus a glass of wine for mom came up to something like $30 before tip, not too expensive.

French comment : Les different commentaires lus ne me donnaient déjà pas trop envie...j'ai quand même voulu tester pour me faire ma propre opinion...Le bar/resto est pratiquement vide 2 groupes et 2 filles au bar...pourtant il va me falloir du temps avant de pouvoir commander. Effectivement, le barman est très "friendly"...probablement plus facilement avec les filles ! La nourriture n'a rien d'exceptionnel et même si le prix reste abordable, la quantité et la qualité n'est pas suffisante. Même le dessert (une crème brulé froide probablement sorti du frigo...) ne vaut pas grand chose. Dommage le lieu est sympa mais j'en suis extrêmement déçu. Encore 20 minutes supplémentaire à attendre que le barman vienne prendre ma CB (alors que je suis un des derniers client...). Je suis alors passablement énervé et, alors que je lâche presque systématiquement 20% de tips lors de sorties lorsque le service est bon, je décide cette fois ci de rayer la case tips...Au moment de partir, le barman revient alors en me disant que je n'ai pas lâché de Tips ! Oui je confirme désolé, par rapport à mon repas, je ne considère pas avoir à lâcher de tips cette fois.
Great place to order delivery from. I've ordered a few times now, and it's one of the better Indian food places in Philadelphia. Would recommend the paneer dishes for vegetarians!

I was referred to Stratman Family Dentistry by Dr. Delasandro after numerous attempts to get a porcelain vaneer to work in my upper mouth.  After 12 attempts by Dr. Delasandro, I decided that I had had enough so I decided to try a new dentist.  My initial impressions of Dr. Stratman were weak because of something that happened on day 1 of my experiences in his office.  He noticed that my nose was crooked and made a point of calling several of his female assistants into the room to check out my "crooked nose".  I though it was most unprofessional and ill-mannered.

I received my new denture with three implants with snap-ons for $10,800 dollars.  They were great at first but then I had difficulty taking them out of my mouth one day and the denture broke in half while pulling it from my mouth.  I told the  assistant that helped me when I first took the broken denture to the dental office that they broke while pulling them from my mouth.  Somehow, the words became "dropped", instead of "pulled them" and Dr. Stratman said that my insurance would have to pay for the repair.  I insisted that they broke while pulling them from my mouth but to no avail.  After checking with another dentist, he said that Stratman was responsible for replacing and repairing my denture.

I haven't been pleased with Dr. Stratman so I've decided to go to a new periodontist, Dr. Cole to continue dental  services.

Dr. Cole did say that my implants looked really healthy.

I have always loved Chili's in the past.  Their food is very good, but I found the daytime Bartender to be rude to my face.  Making remarks to my husband about me, in front of me.  This was My Date Day and I was disappointed.  I'll never ask to come to this location again.  I'm sure this review doesn't mean that all Chilis are the same.  I know for a fact they are not. BYE!

My husband and I celebrated his birthday & enjoyed a wonderful dinner. Angela was our server, she definitely is a professional & made us feel right at home. She helped make the evening very special. We started with the calamari appetizer. We loved it! We had the Maui Shrimp and Jambalaya for dinner. They were both amazing. Wonderfully seasoned with jus the right spice. Then they brought out a dessert for his birthday, yum! But we couldn't resist having the banana bread Creme Brûlée. Yum!!
Sooooo tasty. Eat it. Be patient during lunch rush and eat the most tasty foods. Can taste the love.

Great hidden gem in St. Louis. All the food was amazing including the Mexican street corn not to be missed. The location leading up to this seems a little sketchy but once you get in the immediate area it improves quite a bit. Full bar is a nice option. The decor is modern and unobtrusive. Would definitely hit this place back up if I come back in town!

The food and sevice is always good.  Staff is friendly and wonderful.  Drinks are awesome.  My only issue is the steak I had there.  Although it was cooked to order, perfectly, it had a distinct lack of flavor.  It didn't taste bad and it was juicy, but it was really bland.  I got some demiglace that brought it up, but I shouldn't have had to do that.  That's my only complaint.  Otherwise they are 100%.

The program was a mix of new works & old fav. Expo by Lindberg composed 4 Maestro Gilbert when he started as conductor of NY Phil. Rouse's Oboe Concerto(1st x) played here. Beethoven's 6th Symphony. It was a very good mix of music. I like Gilbert's conducting.

We arrived on a Tuesday at 745, and were seated as soon as they cleaned off a booth for us. Love how dark and spacious it is. Didn't find the music too loud at all, but the table of 12 dudes next to us was pretty obnoxious. Brad was our server, super friendly and knowledgeable about all the beers on tap, of which there is 100+. My date enjoyed all the beers recommended. I enjoyed my Moscow mule. Our apps were decent, the seared ahi tuna had a nice peanut sauce, the guacamole was ok, small serving, and needed salt. Date got the fish and chips with sweet potato fries, great big pieces of flaky fish in a nice batter, fries were great, dipping sauces were on point. I was feeling healthy and had a small rice shrimp bowl. It was meh. Overall, the food is a bit overpriced. Drinks and beer and service were great.
Come for the beer. Stay for the Brad. And don't make eye contact with anyone. #hopsandcrafts4life

Yes do eat at Joes.  This spot is in the Philadelphia airport and if you need some grub this is a good place to get it (B terminal if I remember correctly).  

Joe (or whoever it was behind the counter...unlikely a man named Joe..but I digress....) was all business.  He did not crack a single smile but on the other hand how could he be expected to?  Line of 20 hungry travelers..check....one person to take orders, cook orders and to take money...double check. 

The man was a machine.  He got the job done.  And I was very satisfied with my bacon, egg sandwich.

Great people, fantastic food, AND dog friendly! You really can't go wrong here. If you're in the Belmont area and not sure where to eat, but you know you like sushi I would definitely recommend checking this place out.

Very good dinner generous portions, nice tasty salad comes with your meal. Nice bar with good crowd. All new inside, nice booths, great service in the restaurant, our waiter was really good. Great location. Only thing parking limited

This place is a hidden gem.  I really enjoyed this place and strongly recommend going. The quality of the fish was excellent and so was the variety.   The Bonito and Yellowtail sashimi were both very good.  

Ask for off menu specials.  

I plan on returning frequently.

Awful. Absolutely awful. First let me say that I had this delivered but that was not the issue, it's only important for me because I paid a tremendous amount for an entrée and rice (and a drink) after you add on delivery, tip, fees... etc. I kind of expected to enjoy it. 

I ordered pad Thai and pineapple fried rice (or Hawaiian rice as they call it). I asked for extra EXTRA cashews (I was fine with an extra charge) and chicken for the pad thai. What I got was a pad Thai loaded with tofu (that conveniently appears chicken like) . I cannot eat tofu and the entire dish is wasted. It was not listed on the menu through the delivery service either. I would never have ordered it. 

The rice tasted ok if not a bit bland but did not have any extra cashews and smelled strongly of burnt ingredients. It was really unpleasant. My kitchen still smells like burnt food. 

The best thing about this meal was the sprite and peanut sauce. So disappointed. For what I paid I could have had a lovely sushi meal somewhere else. Lesson learned.
Liam does an absolute amazing job on my nails! This is the only place in Reno I trust to do my nails   thank u for the beautiful nails for the perfect price!

I noticed my right front tire had a slow leak so I picked this place because all they do is tires and nothing else. They were very busy the day after Easter but they got to us right away. It took an hour because they were backed up with lots of customers. They had almost every bay filled up with a customer's car. They found a screw in the tread of my tire and patched it up for FREE! WOW! I will make sure to go to them when my car needs new tires. Their prices are very competitive and they say they will match any internet price you bring to them.

Excellent little shop. You can watch the conveyor belt fill cartons of milk through the viewing glass. This shop has fresh milk, cheeses, yogurt, salty snacks, ice cream, bread, tea/coffee, and cookies -- all at great prices. The place is always busy, and it's no wonder.  So many varieties and flavors of ice cream, and all at great prices. Pint sized cartons allows for anyone to try many flavors. Their Halo Rich milk, is a fattier version of homogenized milk, and it is great. Give this place a try, and you will find yourself coming back again and again.

This place is such a joke. Last call at 10:30 on a Saturday night, in Reno no less. The bartender was a jerk and gave us a lame ass excuse that he has to abide by mall hours. Whatever, BJs was open. I am done wasting money at this place.

We talked this place up to our out of state visitors, only to find out you have to pay a membership to play in there now. We would have had to of paid $24 for an hour and a half until they closed tonight. At 25 cents a pop to play a game, this is pretty pricey, and I think ridiculous. I was wondering why the parking lot was practically empty. Hopefully they will switch back to opening this to the public.
Great fish and small intimate atmosphere. If you like fish and a down-to-earth feel for your restaurant, Little Fish is a good pick
The food was excellent and made me feel like I was having a meal at home, service was great, atmosphere was comforting. Everyone should definitely try this place
They've truly thought of everything! The 2nd phase has been completed! This space is huge and beautiful! The food selections are unlimited and everything is well spaced out! It's a beautiful maze

Don't let the name fool you.  Crust on the pizza was "doughy" and below
average. Nothing good about the dough and the pizza for $22 was AVERAGE.
They need to step up their game.

The place does not have steak sandwiches. They do have a "rib eye" steak
sandwiches---however they are served on a round roll (????)

C'mon guys!!!

The easiest answer a sales person can give you is: " we do not have it in store". I was looking for a key board cover and no one was able to assist me. I even told them that these covers are advertise on their web page.

Cake Batter Doughnut: I've wanted to try "Donut Bar" for over a year now and I finally got the opportunity on my vacation to Tucson last week (I was planning to go to their main location in San Diego back in March 2020, but we all know why that was canceled). As my loyal followers know, cake batter/birthday cake is a favorite of mine when it comes to treats. The cake batter doughnut is a behemoth yeast doughnut with vanilla icing and decorated with confetti dots & dollops of yellow buttercream. Like the other doughnuts from Donut Bar the dough is fresh, chewy, thick, sweet, and buttery. This doughnut was a terrific doppelganger of yellow cake with vanilla frosting and I couldn't quite place what sold it to me at first. I then had an epiphany that it was mostly the icing. This is going to sound a little weird, but it reminded me of Betty Crocker Rainbow Chip frosting (minus the chips of course). The Betty Crocker frosting was synonymous with cake in my childhood and this doughnut brought on a large amount of nostalgia. While it was a fantastic doughnut, it did seem to be missing a little something extra. I wish it would have been filled with cake batter filling like Krispy Kreme's Cake Batter Doughnut. This was still as tasty as a slice of cake and is Doughboy approved. Donut Bar is listed as the #1 Donut Shop on lists from USA Today, Thrillist.com, MSN and many other nationally acclaimed news organizations and blogs. Donut Bar has also been awarded consecutive annual Best of San Diego awards for Best Critics' Choice and Readers' Choice from San Diego Magazine.
My Favorite Doughnuts:
1. Hypno-Henry, The Cake Batter Monster (Krispy Kreme Halloween 2019)
2. Oreo Cheesecake Doughnut (Hurts Donut Co.)
3. Nutella Overload (Donut Bar)
4. Peanut Butter Buttercream (Cranky Al's, MKE)
*Honorable mention: Nutella Filled Doughnut & Cinnamon Roll (Cranky Al's, MKE) 
Doughboy _Food_Reviews

Our visit started out shaky. We were seated and forgotten. When I flagged the waiter to let him know, verything turned around. The waiter apologized and took our drink order. We ordered 2 glasses of house red. For dinner we ordered the village burger (rare) and the Cobb salad. The burger was incredible. Not sure how a simple burger could be that good. It melted in my mouth. According to my wife the Cobb salad was excellent. The best part of the evening and what made me want to acknowledge Village Whiskey was what the waiter did at the end. He brought our check, once again apologized for waiting on other customers who had come in after us while we received no attention and then told us to make up for the slight he had removed the charge for our wine. This was unsolicited by us and most appropriated. It seems to be the rare occasion when a restaurant does the right thing and whiteout having to even ask.

RJ and I stopped here for lunch today on the way to the Phillies Mets game in NY. Our waitress was friendly and fast. RJ had a chicken Caesar wrap and I had the buffalo chicken wrap. I requested cheddar instead of bleu which I'm always afraid to do at a restaurant because it's usually screwed up. Not this time, it was made right and was delicious. Grilled chicken and hot sauce, yum! I wasn't crazy about the fries, they were the kind with the weird crunchy coating. We split a piece of strawberry cheesecake which was light and fluffy and amazing. We got 15% off our order for checking in on Yelp.

The coolest place of all cool places. I'm a horrible golfer and this place is great for me and for people much better than me too! The food is great. HIGHLY recommend the injectable donut holes! So fun and overall an amazing and enjoyable experience. I personally love being on the second or third floor.
Best Indian food in Reno! Garlic naan, samosas, tandoori, paneer, and all the currys...it's all so good.

The food and service are good, and the space is pretty nice (although that hanging sculpture of metal shards in the main room is a bit threatening when you're seating under it). I had the petit filet a couple of times, and also the chicken, and they were all quite tasty. But...the wine list is RIDICULOUSLY overpriced.  When we were there, there wasn't anything in the wine list under 60 dollars.  Not even the OK (just OK) california wines that they have on their list.  In general it seemed to me that everything in their restaurant was overpriced by about 20%. So, minus 2 stars.

I visited Core de Roma last night with my parents to celebrate my new job. It is very nicely decorated inside, with a wonderful ambiance and candles on each table. Romantic! However, I felt it was a little cramped. 

Our server was attentive but we felt rushed, to be honest. It seemed like they wanted us to get out of there so someone else could have the table when we left. We did have reservations, though!

Food was good. I had linguine alla pescatora which had lots of yummy seafood including tender calamari. The pasta was al dente which is A+ but they used spaghetti instead of linguine which was weird. My mom had linguine and clams and enjoyed it thoroughly! Dad had the same as me and the only complaint he had was that he wish his dish had more broth (he likes to dip his bread into the broth!). 

Overall, a nice meal and a nice setting.

I have been having my hair cut, colored and styled at Salon LaRue for over two years with consistently excellent results. The quality of work is superb, the consultation to assure that Rudy and I are "on the same page" is excellent. The Salon is professional, organized, always running in time. I have and would recommend Salon LaRue to anyone.

I got a spa facial here by Maureen. I heard about the salon from Groupon. It was the best facial I've ever had. She took her time, was very gentle, the scents of the creams and concoctions were delightful, and I really felt relaxed when I was leaving. The extractions were done sonically with minimal discomfort, if any. I'm 37 wks pregnant and I'm pretty sensitive, so that's saying a lot!They also had the cutest jewelry that was very reasonably priced, of which I bought a few pieces. The owner finished my transaction and she was lovely as well. Overall, I will definitely be back and highly recommend Wave Reviews! Good vibes when you walk in, friendly atmosphere and great services.
Well done, as usual!  Great location, very friendly staff and great food!  Hopefully outdoor seating next spring?

Miss Rachel's Pantry catered our wedding at the German Society and we cannot say enough good about their food, service and kindness.  Rachel and Nichole (event planner) are awesome people who truly cared about us and our day.  People, both vegans and non vegans alike, have been raving about the food since the wedding.  Check our their hearts of palm and artichoke "crab" cake bites - they are our favorite!  The gnocchi are also delicious.

We love Rachel's new storefront too!  Sticky buns, coffee, spreads - everything is delicious.  Thank you to the team for putting so much love into our day and the food!

Very good, was expecting a little larger pizza for the XL since they're NY style but wasn't disappointed with the taste. I do believe there are more authentic NY style pizza places in Clearwater but this place won't let you down

No idea why this place has such high ratings. 

This place is super expensive for not very good food. The hot and sour soup tasted fine but had developed a film from not being stirred recently. The steamed dumplings did actually taste good, but they fell apart too easily to eat without a fork. The lettuce on the salad bar is too large, and you are not provided a knife. The honeydew on the salad bar was so unripe that none of us couldn't eat it. The vegetable lo mein was too greasy. The stuffed mushrooms were bland, which I didn't think was possible to do. They had some kind of roasted beef with au jus on special that was... not what I would call special. 

Meanwhile, we were sitting in a back area with a skylight. Seems like a cool idea... if you ignore the obviously taped up areas where I'm assuming it leaks. The booths were so close together that two people cannot pass unless both go sideways. The tables have built in heating elements that mean there is no where to put your feet. I had to sit with my feet turned to each side while my husband (whose feet are 5 sizes larger) sat at the far edge with one knee half in the aisle. 

The service is so slow that I tried to refill our drinks myself and got yelled at by the staff. We only got a second refill because I got up from the table and went to find a staff member.

Holy moly! We were visiting Tampa for a Busch gardens trip and ordered China Wok for delivery to our hotel. It was delivered fast, the portions were humongous and delicious! Would highly recommend them!

I love this place! The pupusas are awesome. It's like a corn meal hot pocket, I get it with beans and cheese. The cabbage that you put on top is really fresh and has just a hint of spiciness. I've been to a few other places in town that have pupusas but Guatelinda's are the best. You don't have to spend a lot of money to leave full.

What a fun little gem! An old gas station turned into a local brewery serving hotdogs, soft pretzels and chips oh and of course yummy beer! Have to go inside to order but there is ample seating inside and outside for lots of hangs!

I have ordered the food for delivery. When you receive the food it's great. That being said when you try to order they put you on hold for periods as long as 30 minutes. I will never order food from them again. I'm from out of town and I had better service from Dominos!

Best place for live music! Cool venue, neat shops, good food, try the mussels!
Mango Margarita Rocks!

My former officemate was Indian, and a darn good cook. I lived in Ann Arbor for six years, and that town has an Indian restaurant on every block. With these influences, I became an Indian food snob, and I think I know good when I see it.

Nirvana is not it. I went a few years back, and the food was OK. But my most recent visit was really disappointing. I had Aloo Gobi, a simple dish by which to judge Indian food in my opinion because 1) Indian food is the only cuisine built around vegetarianism, and 2) there are only two kinds of cauliflower: a) amazing, and b) terrible. The cauliflower was terrible.

We also had malai kofta, another veggie dish. There were two tiny dumplings, swimming in a large casserole dish of gravy. If we hadn't eaten most of the gravy, we'd have left hungry.

Add on a $3 order of naan, tap water to drink, and we spent over $30 for two, at best, mediocre vegetarian dishes.

Not exactly the steal of Magazine Street.

Excellent. Different from a lot of the burger shacks that  have popped up lately. Apps- spicy pork eggrolls - for the money, not a very good deal. Overfryed, with too little filling. If it had been a few bucks, fine, but not at that price. Not enough to share.
Onion rings - perfects. Excellent, light, not "refried" in terms of texture/flavor  Obviously cooked to order. Main burger - had the lamb burger as a recommendation of the "morning bartender A". Awesome. Don't see that on a menu frequently, was as good as expected. Chocolate shake - could have been a hair thicker, but I didn't ask either, so... Still a good flavor. Unknown "famous ice cream" brand but didn't matter. Waitress was excellent, just the right divide of watchful but not overbearing. Definitely recommend. Not as cheap as a 5 guys/smashburger/etc., but a much better burger (not in same class)

We showed up without reservation; we may have gotten the last available room. This place was jammed; cars packed the lot. We ended up getting the only available spot right in front of our bottom-level hotel room.

Be careful with the sink water; it comes out of the faucet at about 1400 PSI and splashes back when hitting the bottom of the sink bowl. Raise that pressure gradually until you get what you need. The water pressure of the shower was lacking. The pressure is there they just need to pull some it from the sink and put it in the shower. Poor balance of pressure.

Nice big king-sized bed; it was huge. Pretty comfortable and warm. They have a nice soft, cushy top layer. 

The room itself was nice. Lots of space. We watched some crappy Adam Sandler movie, one of the new ones(I strangely liked it though), on the nice flat screen they provide; the microwave blocks the infrared TV sensor for the TV remote. I had to move the microwave to be perpendicular to the TV to get it working again.

I haven't been here in a while. But the place is nice it used to be Route 56 restaurant. 

Decor is beautiful in the parlor room is where the jazz session are held. Menu options are short, you pay extra for the parlor. But overall very nice $$

Came into town to visit the family...since my brother lives around the corner and he didn't have to work the next day we hit up Thursday karaoke at Jonnys.  We actually didn't know it was karaoke night but boy were we pleased when we got there!  The place was packed yet not everyone was singing so you could get your songs in fairly quickly.  Age ranged from 21 year olds to a 75 year old guy called "Blue"...as in YOU'RE MY BOY BLUE!"  BTW that guy made the evening, he told jokes before he sang and was just in general HILARIOUS.  As for the food, it was better when it was Jonathans, my quesadilla was soooooo salty....my brother got the cajun wings and commented that they were also sooooooo salty.  But it apparently made the drinks go down SO GOOD.  Great times!

Over a number of years, I have a number of friends who I've gone out with to try and learn to surf, but  not with a lot of success.  They were great surfers, just not great teachers.  Two years ago, I took lessons with a local adventure business that does surf lessons, and while it was good, it just wasn't quite thorough, and I still just wasn't quite getting it.   So a couple of weeks ago I decided to try again with a friend who had taken lessons with me before.   I found Pike's School and we made plans for the next day.  From the first moment you know you're going to have a good time, he is just a great down to earth, friendly guy.   He kind of baby steps you through the whole process, which isn't a bad thing.  I assume as he's used to dealing with kids in his skate camp that he brings that same teaching style to his surf school for older kids, like myself.  Everything is very A-Z...which is great.  So often we want to rush right into things without knowing the basics behind it.   With Pike there is no just rushing into the water with a board.   I would say a good 45mins was on the beach learning fundamentals.  Once we were in the water he was very patient and encouraging, before we knew it we were both getting up and standing.   We had such a great time we went looking for boards and suits later that day.  But I know we will definitely want to take a couple more classes with Pike in the future.  Can't recommend Pike's Surf School enough!

Genuine Amazing New York pizza.
I eat pizza all over town.
You could say im a pizza expert of sorts.

This is my favorite place in all of tampa bay.
Yes better than Eddy's and equal to, really a bit above New York, New York.

I just got a plain cheese pizza, 
I am still thinking about it.
The sauce, cheese, crust, taste, it was truly amazing.

This is my favorite breakfast spot!  Not only is the food amazing, but every one that works here (including the owners!) are friendly and personable.   Great food and great people- perfect combination!

Why see a movie anywhere else? Ticket prices are comparable to standard theaters, but here you can recline fully, eat real food, and have an adult beverage. For the price you would pay for popcorn, candy, and soda at a standard theater, you can have a dinner for two, while watching the movie. We're hooked.

Last time I ate at culinary dropout it was nothing short of an amazing experience. Other than the great food, my service is what made it worth it. My server, Madyson, was outstanding. She was very attentive, friendly and knew the menu very well. She recommended the pretzels and fondue and the hot wings as an appetizer and they were AMAZING! Overall, everything was fantastic.
Just around the corner from the Fox Theater, the Dark Room is the perfect place to go before a show. Great menu, good drinks, friendly service at a decent price.

This is a great, authentic Mexican hole in the wall.  Everything is delicious and reasonably priced.  They have beer, so that is always a plus.  I love the Al Pastor, but they other great meat options.  They also have amazing Albondigas soup, which is my favorite.  The sopes are fantastic.

Food is authenticly Mexican, portions are NOT small and the wonderful surprise of being serenaded by the Owner/Server, and (yes, there is more!) the delight to hear the Chef singing by the end of the night. BYOB, cozy seating, reasonably prices for portions, and don't leave without trying the cake!

If you have a party of ten or more, reserve the back room or don't bother coming. The tables are high which made it exceptionally difficult for my grandmother and other older family members to sit at. It was also hard for everyone to chat with each other too.

Love Tibbys! Haven't been disappointed with any choice in food. Go for lunch at least once a week. Staff is amazing. Take all friends and family from Wisconsin  here. The lemon chicken and 12 napkin beef poboy are some of their favorites.

The Skinbar is my happy place in Brentwood. 

Last time I went in for a facial, I thought I was just having a cup of coffee. I'm still new to this area, and found myself wandering in circles when my GPS died. I was getting hangry, and since it was almost lunch time, I figured I would stop in to the Perch for coffee and a crepe. Lunch was very tasty, and after I was finished I realized that the Skinbar and the Perch were connected. I started browsing the adorable little boutique filled with fancy skin creams and cute clothing, and a lovely woman named Danielle mentioned that she had an opening for a facial. Perfect timing! 

Danielle suggested the exact services that would benefit my skin, and customized the session to my needs. What exact services? I couldn't tell you because I was so chilled out I fell asleep halfway through. All I know is, when I woke up, my skin was gorgeous. 

I am a big fan of the Skinbar, and will be back to let Danielle work her magic soon!
I will be returning. We had brunch, sat outside and enjoyed watching for the airplanes come and go and even the helicopter. The food was good and the staff was friendly. great atmosphere.
Great food and equally good service. Where I go to celebrate. I only wish the Bird Refuge were somewhere else!

I really enjoyed this place. Loved the local cheeses and meats. Great beer selection and a knowledgeable wait staff. 
It's nice to have something in the Old Hickory/ Hermitage/Donelson area that's not a chain.
The only brewery in NOLA proper.  Puts out some good brews and the tour will fetch you free beer.  Can't complain about that.
The staff is not friendly and the fish spread is ok, but it is certainly not the best in town like they advertise. Overall, we were disappointed with our experience.

I have nothing but praise for Maria and her team!!!  She has been cleaning my house for several years and will continue to be my choice for many years to come!!!  She is reliable, efficient, trustworthy.  She goes above and beyond any other house cleaner I have ever had!!  Keep up the great work Maria!!!

margaritas that come in those foot-high,  generally-wrapped-in-jesus/mary-motif, skinny candle jars. that's all anyone really needs to say about the Baja Room.  i will continue, though, because i would like to relive it just a little bit...

to continue on the margarita subject, we had several choices on that particular evening: watermelon (holy crap. not what you expect from watermelon flavored anything... so delicious!), apple-ginger (also, but not as, good), and traditional.  the waitress told us that they change up the flavors all the time. how exciting!! 

and by the way, the maitre d' (what is the term in Spanish?) was super friendly.  he led us right through the labyrinth that is the Baja Room and into the cool, air-conditioned dining room containing the most awesome church-pew seating i think i've ever seen (outside of Mexico).  and bonus, our waitress was a precious princess with at least a handful of helpful hints about the menu. 

so, the food... not as exciting as i would have hoped. no complimentary chips and salsa at this joint.
