(total bummer.) and my tuna tacos were more than just a little bit boring. my friend ordered a monstrous succotash and bean burrito. it, again, was a bit of a borefest.  there were multiple other items on the menu that sounded more exciting, so i will come back to give it a more fair shake on that end.  the drinks, however, are worth coming back for!!!

Free things to do in Santa Barbara

Located on the second floor of the Paseo Nuevo mall near the intersection of Chapala and De La Guerra, MOCA Santa Barbara is much smaller than expected.  I like museum browsing and I especially like it more when it is free.  Stop by if you are in the area and check out what they are showcasing.  You might find something interesting.

Cowboy Rhett and Ed were the best leaders! I had a great a great time with my team at this location. It is also a good workout. Come to have fun and sweat just a little. Also the facility is nice and clean.

The food was absolutely amazing with very large portions at a reasonable price!!

The only shining star beyond the food was our server, Ponce!!

Was here on a 70 degree sunny day and got to enjoy the back deck seating and watched the trains come through. Food was top notch. Had the black bean quinoa burger. Also had their IPA which was a great IPA. Their beer list isn't too extravagant. They seemed to stick with the basic craft styles. Loved the whole decor of the place. Kind of makes you want to hang out there all Day!

I wish I could pick less than one star but the food is good.  Customer service is a whole other issue.  DO NOT get anything delivered because it will either not come or they will bring it when they feel like it.  And if you try to complain about what is taking so long you will get nothing but attitude from the person on the phone.  It has happened more than once, twice and three times.

Decided to try this place rather than the donut experiment on Clearwater beach and that was the worst decision we made. We were one of the first customers and the donuts were cold, the toppings were stale (the captain crunch & fruity pebbles). The only thing I really wanted was maple bacon but they didn't have bacon which seems odd being that is was an early Saturday morning. We asked for suggestions and the cashiers had none so we had to go to the Facebook page to get ideas. The donuts are extremely expensive and this was my cheat meal. I threw half of it away, it wasn't even worth the calories and sugar. I will never go back here and I don't suggest you waste your money.

Came here because I heard about this place from so many people.. then read the lady's comment below saying she was offended by no masks so I had to come support a local business who's standing strong on the values that our great country was built on! And guess what the food is amazing!! The service is great and we will be regulars here!! We tried the raw oysters (amazing!) grouper fingers (perfect) , calamari (great) plus a few rolls and other apps.. everything was amazing 
Struggle Jennings

Came here because someone was having a birthday party here. Service is horrible. Difficulty to get a drink. You have to fight your way to the bar to get an order in. Over crowded. Obviously no covid restrictions here. The live music was entertaining but that's about it.

This is a great little (very little) local breakfast and lunch place. It used to be The Hut. 

Most recently I had a bacon, egg and cheese on a kaiser roll and a small cheesesteak. Both of them were delicious. Hot, with plenty of cheese and meat on each. And they use excellent rolls, which we all know is very important. 

They also have great coffee and a surprisingly good beverage selection. 

Fast, friendly service consistently make it a pleasant experience as well. 

I'm looking forward to more visits.

From start to finish the service was fabulous! The pickles and bread starters are yummy. I LOVED the pickles. Caesar salad was one of the better ones I have had. The salmon and mashed potatoes is the perfect combination -can't go wrong there! My boyfriend had the New York strip and scallops potatoes those were delicious as well! We also had the mad hatter wine that my favorite wine too.

Accidentally stubbled upon this place a couple of days ago while staying at the Hilton Garden Inn on business.  I almost walked by, because it definitely wasn't the normal atmosphere I am use to in Scottsdale, AZ.  I am so glad I stopped... the food was amazing, probably one of the best meals I have had in a good while.  I had the blackened chicken, it came with grilled vegetables which were cauliflower and zucchini, with a special sauce over all.  So yummy!!!!   Also came with a side salad.  The staff was very friendly and made me feel right at home!  We will definitely come back here when we return!  
The owner also mentioned that they have been there for 54 years!

It's a Barnes and Noble .. There's not much more to say! Nice big store that also has a Cafe / Starbucks inside as well as a DVD / music department. Definitely one of my favorite past times to come wander around, look at and buy more books to add to my ever growing reading list. All employees I have ever encountered have been pleasant and helpful as well.

I just spent $26 dollars on a delivery from this place. I got the 6 buffalo wings and pizza. This is the definition of AVERAGE FOOD. It was mostly okay to eat, but I couldn't help thinking that I could have bought a better meal for $26.

Great food and service!  Traveling West and stopped based on reviews and a desire for Thai food. I had the pineapple stir fry with chicken..mild. It was delicious!  I'd like more pineapple and raisins but that a preference. There were plenty for most. Husband had chicken fried rice medium. He likes hot food and he quickly redden and smiled. It too was great!  The wait staff was friendly and we were greeted when we entered and thanked when we left. They must have been reading reviews . Portions are huge!  Shareable or take some home. Enjoy!

This is by far the most superior nail salon in center city. Don't been fooled but their unassuming exterior- Young's provides a luxury experience from start to finish. They honor appointment times and I've never had to wait when walking in. The technicians are detail oriented and all of their services include extras that any other salon would up-charge for: exfoliating scrubs, paraffin wax, shoulder massage. The pedicures are on point, gels last until your nails grow out, the chair massage does not disappoint and cleanliness is a top priority. Today a heated pack was placed around my neck while I got my manicure- and I opted for a ten minute neck massage while my polish soaked off- needless to say I left feeling relaxed and rejuvenated. I even left with a holiday gift today! Skip the other nail salons in center city- Young's cannot be beat.

Hands down Best Cheesesteak in Philadelphia. I'm a Philly native-born & raised and I eat cheesesteaks at least once a week. I do eat other cheesesteaks but Campo's is definitely my favorite. If I had to pick "who's the best"... I wouldn't hesitate to say Campo's followed by John's, Pat's, Jim's & Geno's.
loved the doctor but when I had a problem with my bill they were not accommodating or helpful at all

Full disclosure: I would never buy from Ashley Home Store. The first time I entered the store, a chair leg broke in half without anybody so much as breathing on it. A salesman and I saw it happen. He went to explain it to coworkers. I left the store. (Welch Rd. Ashley Home Store) 

My sister insisted on looking for a California King mattress in the same store, even after my telling of the broken chair leg story. It took Ashley nearly 3 months to deliver the mattress, after changing the delivery date 3 times. They eventually delivered the wrong size (a standard King) mattress. 

The delivery guy convinced my sister over the telephone (sister had a neighbor accept delivery) that the mattress "could" fit inside her waterbed frame if she "simply" repositioned the brackets to make the frame wider. 

Of course the delivery guy was wrong and just wanted to get out of there without returning the mattress. 

The saleswoman who sold my sister the mattress recalled a conversation about a California King vs a standard King mattress but didn't recall my sister distinctly. The saleswoman told my sister she would follow-up with her later that day. She has never called, texted or emailed.

I would never spend my money at a business that cares so little for their customers. I recommend other do the same. Ashley Home Store is the worst!

Okay this place years ago had the best cheesesteaks and home made rolls ever but now it's not the same. I've ordered over and over and gave them multiple chances but EVERY time I order  I dont get what I asked for. Its good... just never what I asked for.

I usually run in to return packages- which they help pack and tape. They shred papers securely fir $1.50: pound. They also laminated my COVID CARD for me. They're patient and helpful, since I'm usually running in at the last minute.

This place is by far the best, not only in Mayfair but in the surrounding area. I would frequently travel from Jersey to get food from Shef's. You really can't go wrong with any of the choices on their menu. They are the only place I know of that you can really get WHATEVER you want. Their menu has so many delicious items on it that the only problem you will have is figuring out what to order. Trust me that you will not be disappointed, their wings are amazing, the pizza is great, cheesesteaks to hoagies to pasta, you can't beat this place. In addition, this place has great greek food as well, if you have never tried it you should! The owners are extremely friendly and I have never had a delivery driver that was not polite.

I'm not sure where the negative reviews are coming from.  I went to Cornerstone Dental from a Groupon I purchased and was really pleased.

My family and I were truly impressed at how modern the office was, how friendly the staff was, and how professional the dentist was.  I was not pressured into any services other than what I discussed as options with my dentist.   They gave very detailed explanations and were very accommodating with my scheduling needs (I have several other medical issues that make my schedule very difficult to plan).

They had WiFi while I was waiting in the front (which was only a short period), a modern TV in my room, and a very comfortable atmosphere overall...especially for nervous dentist-phobes like me.  My hygenist noticed that I was nervous but was so friendly that she put me at ease. 

Again, I'm not sure why the reviews I read prior to coming to this place were so negative, because that was the TOTAL opposite if what I experienced.  I think that the prior negative reviews were really unfair.

What a great place, fantastic comfort food for cheap! Very reasonable prices with big portions! The drink prices are great, pretty much everything is about 8 to 15 bucks. For the price and the service,  it gets 5 stars. Keep in mind there are fancier places , but I feel like cheddars is competing against other like minded restaurants like Applebees and chili's . This being superior than both of those establishments.

Not sure why the reviews are so high here. The service was ungodly slow an we came around 5 on a Friday. It wasn't busy at all. All I could taste with the food was salt. Very salty. It was to the point where my wedding band barely fit by the end of the meal. I would not recommend this place to anyone.

I'm also 99% sure my dinner came with a desert, yet the waitress failed to tell me.

Both times I visited this place it was in between hours on a long day of drinking so I have clear and definable memories that I will now recount for you.

It was really dark. The menu says it's Nashville's best burger. The burger was okay. It was not Nashville's best but I wouldn't throw it on the ground or anything.

The music was a nice mix of punk and megadeath. That was an odd combo with the $11 drinks, but who am I to judge? I happen to like all three of those things.

The bar seating situation is crazy oversized. My friend and I felt like toddlers in a high chair. Or maybe we were drunk? No, it was definitely a strangely proportioned bar. If you have short friends, maybe you should get a table.

The staff was crazy nice. I liked these people and wanted to drink with them. So I did.

I went back for the QDP Halloween party ('13) and yeah, that was good too.

We continue to come back...over and over..trying new dishes and new drinks! It is never a disappointment, for two reasons: the food is delicious and Olivia!! Let's talk about food, the Burnt Ends Mac & Cheese is the bomb! Sorry I didn't take a picture! But we sat at the bar....which is fun & comfortable, that's where Olivia comes into the equation!! 
Olivia is hands down the BEST bartender! She never misses a beat and is super attentive! Highly recommend!!!

came out on a friday night and were able to get in free with only about a 15 minutes line to get up to the top before 9pm! couldn't get out on the patio part because it was pretty packed due to the cardinals game, but were able to get a good seat over looking the arch! service was right on (and maybe even a little OVER bearing). drink prices were VERY reasonable... coming from chicago, where you're paying upwards of $50 for 4 drinks, this was a breath of fresh air (a round of drinks for a table of 4 cost us about $30!)

This location had a coughing patron waiting on a takeout at the counter over other people food orders.  When I requested waiter to move hime to another waiting area so I would not get sick with his coughing germs, they did nothing.  This place is too undersize to manage this magnitude of people without proper hygiene codes.
Awesome as always.  I hit this place for lunch and peach sangria outside after the beach.  Never disappoints.

Great food - been here several times and the food has always been amazing! We had a little trouble with our service last time, but all of the other times have been fine, so hopefully it was just an after the holiday glitch.

I usually go to Chipotle for the food. The service usually is mediocre, less of a human interaction and more like a robot interaction. Now I've worked customer service for 11yrs now so I know that heart sinking feeling when an hr before close someone walks in. I want to be cleaning up by now! So I felt bad but I had just left the gym so I needed something good. They were out of fajitas so I had to wait but without making me feel like the jerk who is making them cook more I felt like my happiness mattered more. The kid told me it would be a couple minutes so I sat down in assumption he would call me over when they were, unfortunately he may have forgotten slightly so about 20-30mins rolled by and I returned. He was more then apologetic in the most genuine and positive way. He was getting ready to head out but his human interaction with me is what really made me less irritated. The two girls to help me after this gentleman had the same profoundly positive and welcoming attitude. Wish I had grabbed names but the closing crew on 9/1 was on point! Outstanding customer service and going forward I will go to this location for the awesome service I received.

One of my favorite places to eat in Nash. Everything is so yummy from the homemade sodas, to them burgers, to the sides. I get the orange creamsicle soda (spiked is even better) and build my own black bean burger with tater tots. The black bean burger is one of the best I've ever had, and I've had a lot, tater tots are crispy, portions are huge and my personal favorite topping are the aiolis and bbq onions. Other people I've been there with have raved about the white oak bbq burger too. This place is a must go

We had lunch on a Thursday afternoon.  The place was 90% full, yet not noisy at all.   Mostly a senior crowd from Sun City Center.  Food was very good, as is the atmosphere in this small restaurant.  Going for dinner might be a problem due to size limitations.  Never felt rushed.  Our server, Delayne, was very accommodating.  Will definitely return.
Average food, nothing special. Placed order for boneless wings and 2 minutes later they're out...damn good microwave!

This store is just lovely! I was looking around at various local jewelry stores to try and find a gift, but everything in most shops tended to look pretty generic. Not so here! The necklaces and earrings are beautiful and unique, and I found a turquoise and seashell piece I really loved. The prices were also reasonable.

The ambiance of this place deserves its own paragraph, I think? Because walking in I immediately felt happier and more restful! It's just very well-designed in layout and color scheme; there are gorgeous handmade lights, and tons of cute little finds tucked away in odd corners.

When I was checking out, the woman at the register said she was glad that this necklace had found a home, as she had made it and some other pieces using parts of one of her grandmother's necklaces a couple of years ago-- this is one of those small but very positive interactions that make a place stand out. I haven't been using Yelp much, but wanting to review this place made me start again, because I want other people to go and support it.

Excellent service. We were a large group and our waiter went above and beyond to ensure a great meal.
I had the tortilla soup and it was excellent, the small pieces of avocado made it quite nice.
The red  chili shredded beef was a hit as well as the taquitos!
In a previous visit I had the posole' it's a bit more of a stew here but quite good.
The fact that they limit the free chips to 1 bowl is kinda of sketchy, no Mexican restaurant I have ever been to has ever charged for a refill of chips, So there's that as a negative.  .
But overall the food is good and the service is great!

Easily in top 3 best pizza I've ever had. I got the Bourbon Special Pizza. Crazy simple to order off here and pay for and showed up on time. The front desk loved the smell so much they ordered there own. If the world comes to an end in New Orleans and you haven't had this pizza you'll regret it. And completely reasonably priced. 2019 ...Christmas Review.

Originally written on Google. 

Way to much to write on a cell phone. Want to see her REAL reviews check out Yelp or healthgrades.com. She can't alter those. Spoiler alert 1 Star on Yelp 1.7/5 on healthgrades. Where to start??? Hmmm well my story is similar to the other negative reviews. Went for MARRIAGE counseling and in the second appointment (after I walked Out because of false accusations SHE, the fake Dr. doctor made about me such as saying that I said certain things and used it as an excuse. Things I NEVER told her. Something generic she could tell anyone and not be wrong. Like the way a "psychic" words something to MAKE IT apply to you) she told my wife there is no hope and handed her a card for a divorce lawyer (We we're seeking advice on communication and me being better scheduling while being a full time student AND stay at home parent. If we were seeking divorce we would google that)Must be bitter ever since her husband petitioned the court and was granted divorce from her in 2003 (public records ). Anyway back to this "doctor" that's the equivalent of Dr. Pepper being called a doctor. Extremely EXTREMELY unprofessional. Brought us back late for appointment, spent the first 5-10 minutes talking about a previous client and their issues (while you might not know their name but you definitely pass them as you come and go) and her Dog. I kept Track of how much time was actually counseling and how much time I was Paying her to do her personal crap. I will be finding out from the insurance company how long she tries to bill for and will make them aware. Her dog, while friendly, is a MASSIVE distraction. It's a large Golden retriever and it likes to chew. I mention this because I had A $40 fitted hat on my knee, her dog took it to which she replied "he won't hurt it". Ummmm I don't care. It's mine, not his/ yours. Well hat was destroyed to the point she had to throw it away. Then the dog goes INTO my wife's purse and takes her keys out and starts chewing on them. Same response. I get Florida is a pet friendly state but seriously?!?!?! I will ABSOLUTELY be contacting the governing board that oversees her "PhD" and/ or her license to be a counselor.

This was the closest authentic New Mexican food I have experienced since living in Santa Fe and southern New Mexico. Unadulterated authentic New Mexico green and red chile, and puffy sopapillas fried fluffy and hollow the way they should be. Pork red carne adovada comes close to Chimayo red, but being in Arizona must be difficult to duplicate. Excellent experience with my work family on 07-02-2019. Our server was a vivacious delight as well.

this place is so good, the food is so well made and so fresh. The consistency is always the same and it is always so delicious everytime. the staff are so kind and welcoming. highly recommend if you're in the area!!!

Phenomenal!! Fantastic! Not sure how many more adjectives I can add that do justice. Nashville hot chicken and waffles were on point. $5 bloodies! Courteous staff. Will absolutely visit again when traveling to Nashville

Please please please do not boom with this company! Worst party bus experience ever. The bus broke down leaving us stranded in the middle off the hwy. The owner only offered us an additional hour to make up for our missed wine tasting reservations. Worst ever. They will it take care of you.
Top tier Sando. Great bread options, great taste, great size for the price. Highly recommended haven't had a bad sandwich there yet and I've had about 5 different onesZ

The pizza we ordered was sausage and mushrooms. He said that he didn't have any mushrooms. First time that ever happened to us ever!
The sausage wasn't very flavorful at all. The rest of the pizza was greasy, mushy, soggy and salty. Very disappointed! Don't let the name of this joint reel you in like it did us.

I will probably not every stay here again and it is pretty much based off of my observations and general feelings about Towne Place Suites as a whole. 

However, this IS one of the better TPS I have stayed at because of the staff. I felt very welcomed and appreciated by the staff but I attribute that to the good old southern style I am accustomed to.

Another great thing about this location are that it is within walking distance to a great pizza place called Reginelli's and I highly recommend going there to check it out. Or, check out my Yelp review on it.

All in all, I probably would not select this hotel again.

I can't begin to tell you how great it was waiting just under a half hour for two very simple burritos when I was there yesterday. The experience was dismal from the start. In front of me in line were two dope sick men who smelled like a mixture of the inside on an anus, and a fermenting carcass of roadkill on a summer day in Fresno. They took about 10 minutes to order a dollar and mixed change worth of food, and the guy helping them seemed like he was just over it. I don't blame him; that area is filled with dregs and he probably puts up with them daily. Upon sitting down to wait for my two burritos, I noticed that there were only 3 people working there. Which gives me the impression that it must really be a terrible place to work. The drive-thru was at least 8 cars deep the whole time, and I've come to accept that you're going to be sitting there for at least a half hour to get your food. I wish I could say things like "they are doing the best they can", or "maybe they were just really short staffed that day", but its an anomaly when you actually get your food quickly, and your order is correct. Go to Betos or Robertos- it's better food and the human bacteria of Reno stay away from those places also.

I was having a great night, enjoying some food and drinks. I paid money to the juke box to play a few songs, and when it finally got to my song, someone (bartender or some other staff member?) SKIPPED MY SONG THAT I PAID FOR! I paid money to listen to something of my choosing and they decided to skip it?! Not cool! I will not be coming back here.

I've only been to Rosalitas for dinner a few times, but I've never been disappointed. The food is fantastic and the wait staff is very attentive. We made a reservation  each time we've eaten here, which I recommend because the dinner rush will have you waiting for a while. I ordered the shrimp quesadilla on our last visit and really enjoyed it. The margaritas are fantastic--we ordered a pitcher of the house margarita on ice and they are so good! The chips and salsa are very tasty and you can tell they're made fresh. I can't wait to try some more items on the menu.

I have given this place 5-Stars up til now. I just found out that they're now closed on Monday nights going forward.  Ridiculous!  I travel a lot for work and have been staying at hotel next door for past few months. I really enjoy being able to walk over to Tavola's 3-4 days a week after work for the great food, ambiance, and watching sports after a long day at work, especially now that Monday Night Football is back on. Ron and Alex always take care me so well at outside bar. Not happy!  Don't look forward to having to find another hotel and restaurant for Monday's.

Went there this AM for an issue with my phone that I bought there last year. A young man, I believe his name was Vaughn was very polite and helpful and even applied the replacement protective shield on my phone.
He checked my account, and phone that this was a covered repair under the protection plan.
Very helpful staff and has a good variety.
The chicken udon is a great cold weather warm-up. Nice portions for sushi too. I've been a loyal customer for 20+ years at this reading terminal market gem.

Affordable Mowers is a reliable and truly affordable place to service your lawn equipment or purchase new.  We recently took our law mower in for general blade sharpening and service. It turns out it requires additional attention. We paid $25 for the service and just $15 for the other needs.

I mowed the lawn today and was pleasantly surprised at how it turned out. I had no idea how sluggish the mower had become all due to a loose drivetrain cable.  She runs like a brand new mower!

If you live or work in St. Pete and need a trustworthy place to service your lawn equipment, I highly recommend Affordable Mowers. They are kind, considerate, and knowledgeable. You can trust their advice.

Cute and quaint gem. We live very close to the winery which makes it convenient. The owner Ben and his staff have always been warm, welcoming and enjoyable to converse with. The environment is clean, upscale and beautiful. Gorgeous deck to watch the sunset or to view the French Creek. Would highly recommend for a tasting, a few glasses or for a venue to have an event. Would love to see non-alcoholic options as well.

Came here with big expectations and left somewhat disappointed. 

Service was very good, the setting was very nice with gorgeous oaks in the patio. We ordered the fried black fish, fried chicken, smoked pork loin, and marinated chicken. The pork had excessive amount of fat, though the meat was very tender that literally dissolved in your mouth. The marinated chicken was rotisserie-style, good but need great. The fried chicken was the best. Very tasty, perfectly crunchy, not greasy; really a winner!

Our main problem was with the sides, specifically the cole slaw. This was not from "today" for sure. Tasteless, cold like ice, and stuck. Who knows how many days it spent in the refrigerator.

Have been here twice- a great AYCE sushi place!  You first start with appetizers, and boy do I love their list...The angry bird is my favorite!  I've ordered a few different rolls both times I've gone and they have a good selection and even on very busy Saturday nights food comes out fast.  I've tried the banana tempura dessert and mango mochi and both are delicious.

Awesome! Service was 5 stars, no doubt! Went out for a happy hour with friends from work on a Tuesday. Who knew it was free nacho night??? The nachos were incredible (some of the best I've ever had) and the margaritas were THE best I've ever had. SO SMOOTH... a little dangerous. :) Our waiter was HILARIOUS! So much fun! Totally made the experience complete. Will be back for sure!

Sorry for my English, I am Italian.

One Saturday night I decided to give it a try after a local friend of mine said it was a good Italian restaurant.

I give 1 star only because I cannot give 0. Trust me, 1 star is even too much.
I do not want to talk about the location and the structure. Only of the food and service, that at the end is what matters the most.

1. The service. 
The waitress who served us forgot to bring the bread as start. The one they give everyone as soon as you sit. (Ok, we wait and order the drink) After about 10 minutes, the bread arrives, but the soda went forgotten in the waitress' meanderings mind. We pointed out the missing drink and she asked: Sorry which drinks did you ordered? (Seriously!!!)
Then we ordered the food. This time I was really afraid she would have mixed up the meals and I re-confirmed it after I ordered. The meals arrived and were the right one (and I relaxed that she did for one time the right thing....) but then she tried to remove the bread that clearly I was still eating. I was actually chewing a bite in that moment (....and the confidence in her vanished even more). Because we left most of the food, explanation in point 2, we decided to take away a cake. I ordered with the slightest confidence I ever had in a person. But I did not mind because I liked the other choices as well.

2. The food.
There should be the legend that Italians eat 5 cloves of garlic (or more) per dish to fight vampires. We ordered a carbonara and a pizza. Two dishes that do not require the garlic at all. Neither the faintest smell of garlic should be present in those. In the carbonara we found dices of garlic cooked and uncooked. As if the chef, afraid he would have been too stingy with the garlic, thought that some more as garnishment could improve the taste. It was a disgusting sensation!!!!!
We put the carbonara aside and took the pizza hoping it could purify our taste buds. It was infested of garlic too. (WTF!)
After that, I could have killed all the Transilvania inhabitants only stepping in the city border...faster that Van Helsing did :-)

In summary, if you want Italian food, go to Gino's. The best in town. Otherwise, in the same street, Tango's makes the best Empanadas ever.

absolute favorite place for parisian macarons. 
they take bulk orders, too. one of the most reasonably priced in the area.

I had a great day. Hung out at ocean viewing area adjacent to Sam's before having lunch. Hostess was very friendly and asked me where I would like to sit. Inside or outside.  Sun or shade. Nice! Most hostess just don't go to that much trouble.  Patrick was my server and he was great! I only order a bowl of lobster bisque,  which was delicious,  but he didn't skip on service.  A lot of times when you don't order much you don't receive very much service or you're treated as if you're wasting their time.  Not Patrick.  He was prompt,  polite, I got a refill on my drink with out having to ask and he offered me dessert.  I plan on going back for the key lime pie. Just a all around great experience.  Thank you.

This is one of my favorite restaurants in Indy. It's consistently tasty. Not once have I been here over the last 7 or 8 years and not been completely satisfied with my meal. As a vegetarian, there's a variety of options on the menu (both appetizers and main dishes). The service is solid, ambience is fine (it's generally pretty quiet / relaxed). Honestly no complaints. Glad to have such a good Turkish restaurant on the north side. I'd suggest it to anyone to give it a try!

I love pizza and I think this is the best pizza in the valley.  The wait was responsible for a Saturday evening but we choose to sit at the bar anyway.  The bartenders Alex and Brad helped us through the menu and did I mention it is the best pizza in the Valley? Loved the place!

Tentatively I decided to try this restaurant having seen the 3* rating.   I guess I was hoping for at least average,  but it doesn't even rate average.   You get decent size portions of food I think they are just trying to get rid of.  The best part of the visit was the German beer selection.  I ordered saurbraten with sides of sauerkraut and spaetzle.  None of it could be any worse.   Everything tasted out of a package and severely overcooked.   The presentation was all together unappealing as the spaetzle was a mushy mess with the texture of rubber.   The saurbraten was tender but topped with a sauce that tasted like it came out of a foil envelope.   The sauerkraut appeared and tasted like it was out of a can.  Foolishly  after an unappealing dinner I went all in and decided to try apple strudel hoping dessert could salvage a bad dining experience and this only made things worse.   The strudel was not the least but flaky and sadly I began to wonder how many days ago it was prepared as it came across as a stale spongy pastry.   I don't know how much was paid as the tab it was picked up by one of the people dining with us, but he surely paid too much.   The entire meal came across as being prepared by a cook that is no longer inspired to cook food but is just going through the motions.   I cannot in good conscience recommend this restaurant.   I suggest you pass and find other options.

For convenience and a because I thought Popeye's had better quality, I spent almost $25 for a meal of 8 tenders, 4 biscuits, and about 10 Oz of mashed potatoes with a tablespoon of gravy. The tenders were mostly batter on a stringy, dry piece of almost invisible chicken. No real meat included. Then too, the biscuits were dry and potatoes tasteless with greasy gravy. Disappointing. One star is a gift. 

I will not return. I will drive further next time for quality meat and juicy tenders at Cains or Chick-fil-A.

Love this place!! I've been all over Tampa looking for someone good to do my nails and I finally found the place! They take their time and do an awesome job shaping my acrylics. I can be a bit particular. Polish always looks amazing. Everyone is super friendly and the place is clean.

Red Garter Saloon is really special. You'll leave feeling full with yummy food and wonderful vibes. Everything we had here was fantastic. Crew is very polite and definitely will help you with anything. Red Garter Saloon is hands down the best.

These are novelty empanadas.  I knew that coming in.  I thought for the price it would be larger or maybe really well crafted.
Doesnt seem to be hand crafted, maybe pressed, extremely oily and the shell itself reminds me of the huge egg rolls they give you for free with Chinese food.
These itty bitties were far from free though. Felt like grand larceny.  With so many great, cheap authentic spanish food around, I didnt find this funny.
Has everyone lost their minds?

Great auto shop!  I have been coming here with my family for years since they opened. Mr. Kong and his team are very efficient and honest with the repairs needed for the car. They never charge extra and are very forefront with their prices. I had my car finished within the same day to replace a broken side mirror and they even took the courtesy to buff out minor scratches on my car.  Highly recommend!

Fantastic dinner on our first night in Nashville! 6 of us ladies came in for our first time here, and Steven was AMAZING. took great care of us & was attentive the entire visit. He kept our drinks full throughout the entire dinner and kept us entertained. Next time in Nashville we will definitely be back to see Steven!!

Delicious! The pho broth is amazing. Spring rolls are a bit chewy, but add chili paste to the peanut sauce to brighten the flavor! Fried rice is good, but needs more vegetables. Service was wonderful! Definitely coming back again!

They left multiple scratches on the hood of our car. When we went to make a complaint, the owner immediately went on the defensive saying there was no possible way it happened there. He was so dismissive he didn't even let us talk and had an awful attitude. We will not be returning and don't recommend it at all.

This pizza was absolutely amazing! I was in Boise for a work trip and a coworker and I decided to try out Spitfire upon recommendation from our hotel. So glad we did! The service was great, the beer was cold, and the pizza was delicious!! This friendly staff and clean establishment make everything that much better!

I'll start with the positive: Cool view (we saw string rays, dolphins and fish jump out of the water). Didn't have an issue parking. Cleanish rooms. Cafe breakfast and drinks were good(but overpriced). Deep pool, not overcrowded.

The negatives: They charge you a 'resort fee' of $20 per day but then you have to pay for everything still? The cabanas are only open Thurs-Sunday but you still pay for them regardless and don't get to use them if you aren't there thurs-sun. Pool furniture is nasty. Very misleading marketing. Sunday is a loud ridiculous pool party that you still have to pay to get to if you want to go (not for us). Smells like pot EVERYWHERE you are.

To sum it up, we won't be staying here again nor will we recommend to anyone visiting the area but the view of the bay alone was the only reason they're getting two stars haha.

My dad told me about this place that they have this amazing sandwich that I must try, so here I am trying his uh-mazing sandwich. This place is right on a broad walk. The inside was kind of cramped and noisy, they have only a few tables to sit outside. I sat outside for the ocean view and breeze and to people watch. Our server was really nice and was helpful on recommending items on the menu and explaining what had what. I order the fried grouper po boy, which was actually not that bad. The sandwich was pretty filled, so I was only able to eat one half of the sandwich. The fries, I didn't think fries could taste that good. They were perfect. My friend ordered the lobster roll, it was okay for being one of the popular items. It was more like a lobster salad on bread. We felt that it lacked flavor, taste bland. I would come back again.

I've never eaten here before, but I did come for their bar and Cuban night club. It was so much fun! The drinks are not expensive, and you can either watch the people dancing or tear up the dance floor yourself! The inside is just amazing. They did a great job of decorating to make you feel like you're in Cuba, without being too tacky at all!

Every half hour or 20 minutes or so, they clear the dance floor and a professional Cuban dancing couple comes out and dances to a song. They even change their costumes for each dance!! It's beautiful and very entertaining.

Love this place and will be back soon!

After moving to Tampa, I bounced around from barbershop to barbershop until I stumbled upon this place; that was over a year ago. Since that time, I've been going back every 3-5 weeks and I don't foresee that changing any time soon.

Everyone there is great at what they do, but Jackie is my "go to" person. She's very professional and I always leave feeling good with my cut.

The greatest supermarket in the history of the world. It's like Whole Foods with a better deli (yeah, I said better), add a little Trader Joe's feel, and put it in a great location where you can sit down and eat one of the great pizzas or pastas or sandwiches you just bought. 

Whatever kind of esoteric cheese or unusual fruit or brand of wheat germ you're looking for, they probably have it. They have a wide range of organic options, and things that are theoretically better for you are always available. But it's not all just good stuff either, as the sugar cane soda aisle is my weakness. 

The parking above makes it very convenient, but it's walkable for a large portion of SoHo as well. And yes, they have the world famous Publix fried chicken and sweet tea. I'm a converted Southener, but I know amazing sweet tea when I taste it. I write a sports blog, and someone once posted in our comments that if you don't buy your tailgating supplies at Publix, you lose your Florida citizenship.I certainly agree with that, and this place gives you the best of both 

If you need to get shopping done, or just grab a quick and awesome bite to eat, it's a perfect stop
It's just okay. The food is good but a little over priced and the Service is awful every time. If you sit outside be prepared to be ignored and not know who your waitress is.
Excellent all around. Great atmosphere, service and food. From the salsa to the view to the enchiladas and margaritas!  Five stars all around. Highly recommend.
A favorite! The backyard dog is so good, and love the chili mac  Can't wait to try more!

So disappointed! First time here ordered the half-and-half ribs and they were so dried out it was like jerky. Most of them are going to go into the garbage. After all the great reviews I really thought this would be my go to. The only reason I am giving them two stars is because the sides are really good and the young lady who took went to go order was amazing. Probably won't be back.

They do henna here!!! Yayyyyyy! Walked in there and learned something new. An older Indian lady does it. I hope she is good...because I plan to come back. Two hands cost $15. Didnt ask for the Arabian style one. I am not sure which is which. But yayyyyy for henna.

Absolutely the best car buying experience! After going to dealership after dealership, my mom told me she bought her first car here so I went out on a hunch and made a trip to Flammer Ford of TarponSprings. I had the pleasure of meeting Mack the man who helped me each and every step of the way. He not only was nice and actually cared about my situation and what I was looking to get out of a vehicle he gave me advice on buying cars and credit I had no idea about. Mack took the time to show me every car I was interested in, thoroughly went through the vehicles, explained many things about them and made me feel at ease. All of the flamer ford team members are wonderful too, you are always greeted with smiles the atmosphere is welcoming and the guest coffee is good lol if I had to recommend anywhere to go for a car, I will always recommend flamer ford of tarpon springs. I had experienced a lot of car dealerships and sales men, all those times were not easy, and not a happy ending. But I promise if you show here you will walk out happy. Highly recommend. Thank you flamer ford of tarpon springs. You guys are truly the best!

After a long day on a Friday, I decided to head to old Chicago for dinner since I never bothered to make a reservation anywhere. My waiter after asking me if I wanted my margarita blended or iced and with salt and lemon, apparently forgot about it completely. Then when he got my drink finally (after almost done with my food), I asked him for dipping sauce for the fries. Again forgets it.!  I understand it's Friday, but I went there to celebrate something. So I did call out to tell him that he forgot my garlic sauce for the fries (anyways, I finished my fries by then). I decided to write down that the service was bad when I got the check (though these things tend to happen, I was mad that I din' get to enjoy my food,with me being a foodie.) But guess what, when I was about to leave, his manager came to my table and gave me a coupon for next meal. The waiter might have as well left it, but he did tell his manager about it I guess. 

Bottom line: lots of courtesy, good food.

We love Mexican food and were apprehensive when this opened. Now it's a regular weekly stop for us. Their arroz con pollo is so cheesy and the chicken is good on it's own. They have great drink specials and the staff is amazing. We highly recommend coming here if you love Mexican food, you won't be disappointed.

This was the first restaurant that my wife and I came to when we went on our honeymoon to New Orleans and I have got to say that this is the place to go if you're in the area! The service was incredible and the servers were so nice. The food was beyond compare to anything I've had. If you want great confort food and good service come to New Orleans Hamburger & Seafood. Try the Alligator Bites. They're outta this world! Free icecream if you order from the kids menu or the beverage bar. You won't be disappointed.
Amazing food, amazing service, and the atmosphere is incredible. The pizza is to die for!

I have been to the Fuzzy's location on Watson Rd. about 5 times over the last 2 or 3 months.  While the food is generally great, the service is always less than stellar.  Specifically, the employees working the front desk appear to have no sense of urgency or motivation to quickly process orders and move the line along.  The last time I was there (today), there was a line to the door and only one person taking orders.  There was another sweeping floors and another who would occasionally hop on a register, but then moved to other tasks like changing TV channels, etc.  There never appears to be a manager or other strong leader present to ensure guest service is paramount.  An overall disappointing experience.

Good service but overpriced menu for 'just-ok' food. You're paying for the  upscale atmosphere to dine here because the food really wasn't anything to get excited about. Do yourself a favor, seek out a smaller local Asian restaurant, spend a fraction of what it would have cost you at PFC's, support a local business, enjoy your meal and leave happy!

This studio is the best! I love the space, it's a large room so it doesn't ever feel cramped and there's lots of natural light. I've had a few different instructors and had a great experience every time. They lead challenging classes, but always make sure to offer modifications if it's too hard. There are people of all shapes and sizes so no need to feel shy!
Well organized and reasonably priced. Captain Bishop is a riot and extremely knowledgeable. We drove out there rather than take the bus, and it's an easy drive and the place is super easy to find.

First time brunching at the 3 Monkeys and I was super impressed. I've been a fan of 3 Monkeys for years and have been there many times for happy hour, dinner, drinks, rehearsal dinners, and other random get togethers. Service is great and the food never disappoints. There were 4 of us and 2 members of our party were taking the train from center city and running late. They still seated the 2 of us, and didn't make us sit at the bar or wait for the others to join. That was really nice - most places don't do that. I have to say, too how much I love the decor of the 3 Monkeys. It's so eclectic and welcoming. I ordered the crab-adict which was basically eggs Benedict with a crab cake, it was phenomenal. It really hit the spot. The portions were big and I think the price is great for what you get. I really recommend the 3 Monkeys overall. I've never been disappointed there.

Feel bad for the girl who was working apparently had whole bar and service alone during a big world cup game it would of been fine if she didn't snap at my gf who asked for check cuz we had to wait for bus. I should of checked reviews b4 going in doesn't have even 3 stars and I see why.

I am writing a review for my mother, who isn't familiar with computers but was so impressed with the service she got at this dry cleaners that she wanted me to write a review for her. She put in a pretty substantial order and there was some kind of error on one of the articles of clothing. When she pointed this out to the owner, not only was he polite and apologetic, he even refunded her money. He just seemed so intent on giving good customer service, and the overall quality of cleaning here is so good, that my mother counters herself a loyal customer and intends to do all her dry cleaning here. A warm thank you.

Oh! They got pretzels, a hobby shop with Gunpla and Transformers, a GameStop that had Gundam capsule toys,  and plenty of custom t shirts I could pay and print out for my own sake. I wish they had a Yoshinoya with all the items though, then I'd go there nonstop.

I absolute love anything resembling a farmers or flea market, so of course fell in love with this place. I stuffed my face with Amish donuts and bought some herbal tea from this organic shop. I doesn't over an hour going up and down the aisles. I probably shouldn't of went hungry. They had everything to eat you could ever want. Food coma!

Excuse me! How dare you be this good?! The soft serve is perfect. One of the best in the city. The shop itself is small but accommodating. The staff is beyond amazing. We only ordered two sundaes and they presented them on a tray and handed them to us. They even offered to bring them out.

Even more impressive, they have been extremely vocal about covid protections of customers and staff . Something I'm really focused on is how places treat employees. I can tell this is a healthy working environment. This is what I love about Philly businesses. 

Go to East Passyunk and just enjoy a memory on the street in these summer nights.

I'm from out of town but had to get a good yoga day in. Found this place on yelp. It's not like other  yoga studios  I've been to or normally practice at. This one is located upstairs and shares a hallway with other offices I think. There's no reception desk so when there is a yoga class in session the door will be shut and you wait in the hallway until the class is over. It is not a heated yoga room. But with all the bodies you can create a lot of steam and heat in the winter lol. It is very tight space as you walk in and find a cubby or spot to place your shoes and things. But it's a cute little place with wooden floors and window views of outside, the zen like decor gives it a great feel. I was happy with the instructor and the class flow she taught. I would come back again :)

After reading other reviews, I am glad that other people know Famous Fat Freddie's as well as I do.  The food is terrible.  Whether it is the pizza that is undercooked and covered with a tasteless watery sauce or the mediocrity of their steaks that somehow get served cold or the hoagies that have warm lunchmeat in them, it's all garbage.  The only thing worse than their food is their delivery service.  When you place an order and are told that the food will be there in forty-five minutes to an hour, you have the right to expect it in or around that amount of time.  When you are kept waiting for two to two and a half hours, that's another matter.  To top it off, when you call to complain about the amount of time the order is taking and speak to a manager, they respond with, "what do you want me to do about it?"  Here's what I did about it....I am spreading the word that Famous Fat Freddie's Pizza is a complete waste of  money.  The food is terrible, the shop dirty, and their service is a joke.  How this shop manages to still do business is beyond me.  The fact that this filthy establishment hasn't gotten someone sick yet is a miracle.  Look elsewhere for food.

I went into the Broadway store and ask for the 3 for $30.00 One guy didn't even know he had the flavor I asked for on the menu. The other flavor he didn't know how to make. These were on the menu mind you. I had to leave with only one flavor. You could tell guy that was running the register did not like the guy making the juice. The guy making the juice seem like a complete tool.Customer service was lousy. I like the flavors they have there if they could only make them. OPV is way better. At least they get along and know the inventory.

Not even worth it if you're absolutely desperate. THIS IS AN ABSOLUTE SHITHOLE. The sheets were not clean and they were ripped. The whole place smells like urine and old people. And not the good kind of old people...and urine.

One of my favorite places to go for lunch. They have amazing lunch specials that range from $6 to $10. As well as some of my favorite chips and salsa. (Chips always warm) 

I have been here quite a few times. I have had fajitas, burritos, tacos, and more. All were great. Only thing I don't really care for is the chicken quesadilla on the lunch special. 

Service is on par for a place like this and so is the ambiance. They do have outdoor seating which is a plus.

Pretty much the only authentic, non chain-conformed Chicago cuisine around here. And my favorite hot dogs as well, beating out Stan's, Costco, and any hot dog stand roving around downtown on a weekend night.

Taste of Chicago is a small hot dog stand next to the Rite-Aid on the southeastern corner of Main and Fairview (the address displayed in the business info is actually Rite-Aid's, as their menu doesn't show an official one other than "Fairview and Main").

Hot dogs and hot sandwiches are what are served here. They use Vienna Beef dogs and get them shipped in weekly from Chicago along with some of the condiments. The buns have poppy seeds and are steamed. And of course there's the trimmings involved with a Chicago-style dog: celery salt (put on both my dog and fries), the neon green relish, sport peppers, mustard, onions, tomato wedge, and pickle. It's the real deal. He also has footlong, jumbo, and BLT dogs. All come served with crinkle cut fries. The best value and my constant order is the daily special-- two Chicago-style dogs, fries, and soda that ends up being cheaper than just ordering two dogs.

The four stars are for the dogs. As I don't make it into that area often within the limited hours the stand is open, that's what I go there to eat when I do make it there. My only other excursion into the menu was his Italian beef sandwich. I love the Italian beef from the Portillo's chain, and Steve the owner swore that his was better. Sorry Steve. Perfection denied. The roll was subpar and the taste was lacking. He also serves meatball and sausage sandwiches.

By the way, Steve is very chatty and personable and has some good stories to tell if you're up for them.

Now, when can we see a restaurant pop up that will serve some REAL Chicago-style stuffed pizzas a la Giordano's?
Excellent and clean.  But the ran out of things I liked.  Like the new fans.  New hooks ! The stylish stuff is selling quick.  Staff very attentive and friendly to us.

Sister ordered a veggie pizza and two types of buffalo wings- hot and BBQ. 

The pizza was alright. Yes, not bad or anything but the toppings were a bit off putting in  my mouth. 

The wings were good- the hot sauce just tastes like Frank's Red Hot to me. The BBQ sauce tasted good but I wouldn't be alarmed if it was from the bottle as well.

Overall, alright food but left me scratching my head a bit.
This shop is beautiful! I could spend my entire pay check here! It's  must go to spot, Sarah is amazing and goes above and beyond for her customers!

This one is very interesting! Daughter hates this place did not like anything except the dessert. Wife who does not like barbecue loved this place. And I was OK with it. I thought it was extremely over priced. Wanted the tri-tip but were out, so had to settle with the brisket. Their BBQ chicken nachos were great. wife loved their chicken, and pulled pork. Their brisket was not that great. Did enjoyed their ribs. But what saved the dinner was their apple cobbler, and pudding bread with cinnamon ice cream. For just the four of us and we shared the combination plates. Dinner was still pricey.

Beautiful location on the Boise river. It had the traditional fun Joe's crab shack vibe. The servers were very nice and helpful when it came to ordering. Our food was very nice for a sea food chain and the drinks are fantastic.

Let me preface this review by saying that I am from Texas and I have much experience with authentic and genuine Mexican food. Cinco de Mayo definitely lived up to my expectations and even exceeded them as well. The outside of Cinco de Mayo is very plain and bland, it kind of looks a little sketchy on the outside, but once you go inside, the interior decorations and lighting are comforting and inviting. My girlfriend and I went there for Friday night date night and luckily we only had to wait about 10-15 minutes before getting seated (it gets pretty busy so get there ASAP). Our waiter was very kind and attentive and he even gave us suggestions on how much food to order. Therefore, we ended up with the grande shrimp quesadillas  and the Fajitas Texanas for our entrees. Another perk about Cinco de Mayo is that they give you free chips and salsa as well. Anyways, back to the entrees, the portion sizes are huge and most entrees are around $10. The grande shrimp quesadilla was one giant quesadilla stuffed with shrimp, cheese, peppers and onion. The cheese blended well with the dish and the shrimp were tender and flavorful as well. My only complaint is that the quesadilla itself was a little bit oily. In regards to the Fajitas Texanas, it was a savory dish and came in a giant skillet with a side of tortillas. There was a pretty even ratio of chicken, beef and shrimp, but definitely too many onions and green peppers. The meats were cooked to perfection and the tortillas tasted pretty fresh as well. Lastly, for dessert, we tried the sopapillas (only $1.99). Honestly, it was not the sopapillas I had before because the one at Cinco de Mayo is flat and crispy instead of puffy and soft. In addition, they drenched the sopapilla with honey and sugar, which my girlfriend didn't like, but since I had a sweet tooth, I was able to tolerate it. Overall, if you want some good quality and authentic Mexican food, I highly recommend trying out Cinco de Mayo.

Went the store in Arnold. Ordered chicken wings. Wanted half in one sauce and half in the other. THEY WONT DO THAT. Asked if I could get sauce on the side. YES YOU CAN DO THAT. The first sauce is free and then 50 cents for each additional. Ok.....I can live with that. Oh did you want ranch or blue cheese with your wings? Automatically comes with hot wings right, in any other restaurant Ive been too? Ordered ranch. Was told the sweet and spicy wasn't as hot as Sybergs sauce. WRONG! TASTED LIKE STRAIGHT HOT SAUCE. 

0Asked the waiter if they gave me the wrong sauce. He said did they? I told him it tasted like it was strsight hot sauce. He smelled the sauce and said. We make our sauces, looks like someone put too much of something in it. He said I'm sorry and just stood there. I Just looked at him and he finally said can I do something for you? I HAD TO ASK TO HAVE THE LAST 5 WINGS REPLACED. 

Got the wings back finally and they bring them with NO sauce! Had to ask.

Had to ask for extra napkins
Had to finally after passing us at least 5 times ask for refills of our drinks.
Asked if we could get our drinks in a go cup. Waiter gave a story of they were out of lids, he didnt know why they just were. Only thing they could give was a kiddie cup.

Finally get the bill. Charged me for two sauces. One was the ranch dressing. Even Imos gives you ranch or blue cheese automatically with hot wings.

Asked waiter why am I being charged for two sauces when one was free? He then informed me that they hand make their own ranch so they have to charge. I asked why didn't you say that when you asked me ranch or blue cheese with my wings. He said I was just being courteous and  assumed you knew.

He asked if I wanted it off my bill and I said yes. You should let people know when there are extra charges. He said will in my mind ranch is a sauce. I told him, well I'm sorry but I cant hear what you are thinking.

He said I'll take it off and left.  Then comes what looks to be a manager asking if I had any questions? I went thru the whole situation and she says well Sybergs doesn't give ranch along with wings like everybody else. We sling alot of wings and beer here, so we must be doing something right. What the hell was that supossed to mean. She said what it was, was a communication problem. I said yes, he failed to tell me when there was an extra charge. She thanked me for letting know so she could let the waiter know and would take it off my bill. Very condecening woman.

The waiter comes back with the bill. I looked at it and its the same..NOTHING CHANGED. ALL THAT, AND IT WAS LIKE OH WELL....WE DONT CARE. I asked the waiter why he didnt take the sauce off and he whined.....oh I'm sorry.....I forgot. I can take if off now. I said NO its pretty much a mood point now. 

Missed on two tips tonight. If he went back and changed the bill.....he would have charged me for it. 

I hope Corporate reads this. 

Denise
314-605-4715

Friendly staff, great atmosphere and delicious food!    Had the tenderloin and the grilled cheese and both were delish!  The coleslaw was good as well as the chips and guacamole!  A very nice patio area as well as indoor seating!  If you're in downtown Indy check this place out!  Good prices and good portions!  We'll be back when we're visiting Indy!

The original Century 21 in NYC is the only Century 21 store worthy of 5 stars. All the other locations, even the newer locations in NYC, are like this one in Philly. You won't find the very high end designer goods that are in the original NYC location. But once you get past that disappointment you can come to appreciate what they do have, a large selection of mid-tier designer goods at steep discounts. 

I can only speak on the men's section. With the Lacoste store in Center City now closed, this is the spot to grab Lacoste shirts discounted cheaper than at Macys. They also have a large selection of Tommy Hilfiger, Calvin Klein, Michael Kors, and Izod, and Perry Ellis shirts. The shoe section has some Nike sneakers and a nice selection of both dress and casual shoes. The store is large, so that's only a sample of what they have. You can always find something good in your size, which you can't say about other Outlet department stores, and puts it a step above the others.

A new addition to the great Tarpon Springs food scene. Brian and Heather Healey, with their wonderful friendly staff (Shannon and April took care of us), reworn with their great food and reasonable prices - on the water! Two friends and I stopped in for Sunday brunch and sat inside as it was a bit chilly outdoors. The space is charming with local artwork. Peg and I split the lobstah omelette which was jam packed with Maine lobster chunks with fresh spinach and gruyere and came with delicious potatoes. Opt for their amazing homemade biscuits served with their signature homemade jam. I was stuffed with the half portion. Can't wait to go back to try their other offerings.
The best service in NOLA!! Perhaps we went in with expectations that were too high but our brunch was memorable and the service and jazz made for a great NOLA experience!

This place is really good!! If you have ever been to CALIFORNIA and been to In n' Out, this place is very similar! We just moved from California to Tennessee and it was amazingly refreshing to have a place that is so like In n' Out! One of the best things about this place (that's exactly like In 'n Out) is the "Shack Sauce" which is like a sort of 1000 island dressing with grilled onions. They put it on burgers and you can have it on fries too (phenomenal!) The people were also really friendly here, and it wasn't too busy when we went (Thursday at 12). Our friends were telling us that it can get pretty busy. Also, it should be known that it is CLOSED ON SUNDAYS.

Although I usually really love my visits to milk & honey I needed to reduce my original rating due to how much the service has declined. This morning I waited for about 15 minutes to get my order in which was fine because it seemed there were only 3 people working and they were moving around quickly. I was pretty upset though when I finally got my order and my large smoothie was only 3/4 of the way full. If I'm paying for a large I should at the very least get the cup filled. I didn't want to make a big deal about it because everyone was already waiting ridiculously long and it wouldn't have been fair to them. There is no way the guy who made this could look at it and think this was acceptable though.

The Hermitage was worth visiting. I had first heard about it from a coworker who was a history junkie and wasn't sure if I was wanting to go. Once here we decided to give it a try and it was totally worth it. 

There were two tour options when we went a $20 tour including a movie, mansion tour, and audio tour. Then a $28 tour where you get the above but with a multimedia tour (not sure). They had additional carriage rides and other add ons. 

The video was a good way to get a more historic overview about Jackson and his presidency for those who need a little bit of a history update - I sure did. 

Then you head out on your own walking tour of the grounds. They give you these "remotes" where you punch in the corresponding number and listen to the audio. I wasn't excited about it at first but it was actually really easy and I liked it. They offer three listening options - about Gen Jackson, his wife Rachel, and a Polly tour for the kids. I listened to the Jackson and Polly ones. 

The mansion tour is guided by people in costume. It was cool that 90-95% of the place is all original - so they, naturally, also want to protect it by keeping you on carpet and not touching anything. The entryway has the original wallpaper and the rooms have the original furniture in them. The tour guides do a good job taking you through it all. Then they kick ya out and you're back on your own walking tour. 

Overall, this place is worth a visit and is a good reminder about Gen Jackson's history. The grounds are kept up well and are pretty much original. It's a great trip!

Monster Boba Latte and Thai Tea with golden boba. Perfect drinks on a hot summer day. Rich Carmel taste with the Monster Boba Latte.  Always served by a friendly staff that welcomes you when enter and wishes me well when we leave. Make you feel that there is genuine care for how you're doing. Every experience has been pleasant. The reward club sends discounts often. #ShakeForLifeReno

Phenomenal!  I'm very picky when it comes to gumbo (no one ever makes it as good as my nana) and my nana would be proud of this place!  I got the Mumbo Gumbo and a fried chicken po' boy.  I left so stuffed, but I wanted to keep eating!  I'll take anyone visiting the city here for sure!

But , Get the right thing or you're losing stars fast. I love Shanks. It's the best cutlet sandwich I may've ever had. It's thick properly pounded cutlets breaded just-right. You'll never worry about a question mark in these cutlets, they are good eats. The Rabe is so good. It's crunchy, bitter, and reminds you of a fresh batch. It's a major deal for me to have my sandwich on the proper bread. Shank's uses Liscios, which I think sucks. I always call to check and make sure. I don't recommend anything else. I am extremely satisfied with the Cutlet italiano, and I would put it up to any best sandwich.

Modern , sleek atmosphere, cold and loud. Way overpriced $17.50 for a martini $9 for crappy wine..$16 for a chuck hamburger. Next time I will go
Into Nashville or cook at home. Williamson Co rents are high and the liquor tax is unreasonable.

Great food, okay service. 

I sampled the iceberg wedge salad, such an indulgence with bacon and creamy dressing. The hot wings were the best I have had in the city, seriously. The hummus plate was better than I expected, everything was fresh and it came with a tomato, cucumber, red onion and feta salad that was outstanding. The crispy chicken sandwich was very good, the bread tasted just like my mom's and it wasn't too greasy. Fries weren't great, not terrible either.

This is much better quality and variety than the bar food we have in Fairmount. I'd kill to have North Third in my neighborhood.

From the outside you'd really have no clue what you're in for. It's in a shopping plaza that has a Publix Grocery Store and some other small businesses. You wouldn't expect a large restaurant behind it's dark tinted windows.

Inside you get a 70's Italian restaurant vibe. Big glass ball lights in blues and reds hang from the ceiling. The place is huge, the lights are low, and usually it's pretty busy. 

The food is delicious. Especially the pizza. Although a little bit spicy (they must add some funky seasoning) the crust is thin and crisp. They have a large menu, prices are moderate,and they do allow take out. It's a great place for groups, I know they set up large tables to accomodate people and it's very kid friendly.

I have been here 3 times and have seen Dr. Liliquist who seems to be knowledgable and takes time to listen and explain clearly.  The only complaint I have is the incredibly long wait times!  Each time is has taken well over an hour from arrival to departure.   Today it has been 50 minutes and I have still not seen the Doctor.  I would suggest trying to reduce the long wait times.

My mother and I took a super shuttle from Sarasota to the Tampa airport on Wednesday. I had taken one down a few days prior and had a great driver but our trip back was absolutely horrendous. The driver reeked of cigarettes, was swearing, going 30-40mph above the speed limit (for example on a 10mph trim ramp he was going almost 50), had gone over the white and yellow lines 3-4 times, was complaining about his boss and the company, etc. I'm not a nervous passenger but his driving was completely unsafe and erratic. My mother submitted a complaint and emailed but received nothing back.

This is our second time coming here and only because we are from out of town. If we lived here this would be our weekly spot..  Love their parrilla and their yucca fries are to die for. On our next trip back from California we will for sure need to stop here again. Thank you Diego for your service.

I've had the chance to try this place for breakfast and for lunch and both times i was very impressed. I have to say it's nice to have a sandwich shop that is above the simple JJ or Subway with options of a plated meal but all in a sandwich. What a brilliant idea the owner had when designing this place. The flavors are like no other you'll find in a sandwich shop. If you get the chance to try it do go! I'll be back!

First off, let me say the food here was very tasty and I don't like giving bad reviews, especially to small businesses. However, they have some kinks to work out service-wise that really colored our visit.

My parents, who are snowbirds, had been here before while hanging out in Dunedin, and they have been here with friends on other occasions so we knew what to expect. Their food on every occasion was always good.

However, this weekend we went while the art festival was going on so it was pretty busy, but it wasn't like there was a line. There were plenty of tables when we arrived. It looked like they only had two servers yesterday and probably needed a third because it took quite a while to get our drink order. However, my mom also went to the rest room right away so I can cut some slack on that. The server probably wanted to wait for her to get back.

We were there around noon, so you could either get breakfast or lunch, which was great. They both ordered omelettes, I got the pork roja soup because I expected it to be a little spicy and hardy (it was cool outside so that sounded great.) 

Once we ordered, we sat there for almost 40 minutes. That is a really long time to wait for two omelettes and a bowl of soup. I think they may need more help in the kitchen on busy days. When it finally arrived, the omelettes looked great. My soup, however, hardly had any pork or vegetables in it. Also, no crackers. I complained. I do have to say that the server brought it back and tried to get more meat in it and the owner came out an apologized, and when I asked about crackers he offered something else. The soup was still pretty thin. I asked for half a sandwich and he brought out a whole sandwich. He also comped the whole meal and really wanted to know what they could do better. They are new owners so I appreciate the fact that he was so nice and trying to make things right. I will go back because like I said, the food was delicious and I think they will work out the kinks.

Water Street is definitely worth a visit. We loved all 4 of the cocktails we tried! For an appetizer we had the bruschetta trio which was awesome and for dinner we had steak and trout - also great choices. The ambience is nice and the quality of the food and drinks is great. My only complaint would be that it took a while to get our drinks. Probably need another bartender in there on busy weekend evenings. Overall, a solid dinner :)
This place is amazing! Go and order a Venezuelan arepa with green sauce! You won't be disappointed! Love love this place. We go regularly.

Had a lot of fun at this casino the other day! We were cruising through town on our way to the beach for a few days and decided to stop in here. 

It's pretty big. There was ample parking to chose from, as well as a parking garage, and valet. All bases covered. 

We walked in a side-entrance and were in the non-smoking section. We wandered around a bit and found the larger smoking section with more machines. They had a really nice assortment of machines to choose from. The place was fairly busy, yet it wasn't hard to find a spot. 

I ended up walking out ahead, so I have zero complaints! Definitely worth checking out if you're a gambler and in the area!

Every year I go to Dickman's to get Lobster for V-Day. I usually go to the one on Oracle. This year I went to the one on Broadway. Boy did they drop the ball. 4 Lobster for $88.00. One was rotten. What a disappointment, to me and my guest. Called the next day and they are refunding me my money. So I am happy again.

Yo dude. This food is awesome. I had the chicken strips with xhot sauce. It was too hot for me but delicious. I love hot sauce tho, so the xhot is really hot but if I figured that going in. My problem with this establishment is their service. The first day I waited about 30 minutes to get to order and was told that it would be another 45 minutes to 60 minutes for the order. AND I was told that there was no beer. I tried again the next day with much less of a line. I got to order in about 15 minutes, gave my number so they could call me as they said they would. After about 30 minutes of waiting I went back to see what the situation was and found that the food was under their heat lamps. They never texted/called me. That said, again, the food was awesome. Their service sucked but their food was the s&_t
Owner is hilarious and food is delicious!! Definitely take advantage of $10 lunch buffet!!
A return visit for sure!  Owner is from Sicily and it showed from the time we walked in the door.  Food was over the top and the long hot peppers is worth a plane ride anytime.

Not a family environment at night.  This is the worst Hooters I've ever been to.  My wife and I both had buffalo chicken sandwiches and they were burnt,  so were my son's chicken nuggets, and other son's burger.  We should have left as soon as we were seated at the bar section and a drunk chick at the bar made a scene. Next time we are going to wing house.
What a great hidden gem!! Definitely my new favorite bagel place! Such a warm & friendly atmosphere with excellent bagels & coffee. Totally coming back here!!!
G & E has the best prices on dog and cat food. I like shopping here because of their knowledgable and professional staff.  Great parking

Great wings, Great burgers, Great chicken sandwich.
Dog Friendly.   Great outdoor seating space.  Good selection of beers . Downtown palm harbor  golf cart ride away.

I'm not sure how to order a steak form there. I asked the waitress to have it between med and med well. I like just a tad blood. Mainly to keep it moist. She told me I had to pick one or the other. I've cooked steaks for a living when I was young and she acted as if I had no idea what I was talking about. I told her give me medium first and we'll go from there. It came out rare. Still cool on the inside. Dark red from top to bottom. I told her,this is not even close to what i want. She says so you want medium well. I guess if this is your medium. It came back out as a hockey puck. Well done. Cooked from top to bottom. I guess they don't know in between. They offered to recook it,but,I just suffered though it.

My coffee was good and came with an adorable little icecream cone. My friend enjoyed the gelato. The atmosphere is spunky. Not super modern but fun. It's in a neat little plaza. The employee was nice.

Upon getting locked out of our house after a 3 mile run, my girlfriend and I knew that our landlord had a key on file at our local Home Depot. We swiftly ran over to Home Depot 20 minutes before closing time. After several technical difficulties while setting up the KeyHero app, the staff (Lauryn, Anna, and Robert) at the key making department were extremely patient and helpful getting through this stressful night. After finally getting the keys printed, Josh in customer service, was extremely generous in letting us take the keys for free because we had locked our wallets in the house. We are very lucky to have such an amazing Home Depot close to our home. Excellent service, would recommend!

A friend told me that the owners have taken care of the service and attitude issues that I experienced last time I was here. She was correct! The young woman at the counter was very friendly, and my Beauregard sandwich arrived promptly. The salad or bagel chips are included again too, but I miss the pickle. Thank you, Rounds! I will be back.

We needed about 10 pages Notarized for our Trust and Wills - The service and knowledge for those documents was impressive. We didn't have an appointment and were about 5th in line - but they are so efficient, that the wait was minimal and we were done in no time. Also the price was really reasonable for that much notarizing!  We recommend them highly!

Attentive and very friendly staff every time! Food is always delicious and moderately priced. Ahi tuna is as good as any 5 star restaurant around. Thanks for all the hard work, your food is delicious! More people should know about this awesome joint

DO NOT RENT FROM THIS COMPANY!! My experience is similar to what others have written. We booked with them because the price for the week was great. After a plane ride that arrived over 2 hours late, we went to get our car and learned they charge a $250 deposit. We were asked if we wanted to pay $10/day for their Sunpass which we declined. At no time were we told that we had to avoid Toll Roads; we planned to pay cash for the 1-2 times we would use a toll road. When we tried to pay cash, the toll collector told us the Sunpass had paid. Every time we called Customer Service, we were told something different. We have not gotten our $250 deposit back although it's almost 3 months. We ended up disputing the claim through our credit card , and the Executive Rental responded that we are not entitled to the full amount due to the tolls and their charge for the tolls. We told our credit card company we would accept the refund since it's over $200, but the reality is they are stealing the rest of the deposit from us.
