And I don't believe we would see a dime if we hadn't filed a dispute with our credit card company. STAY AWAY FROM EXECUTIVE CAR RENTAL!!

Six of us went here for an early Saturday lunch. The waitress could not have been nicer, the food was very good.  Several people had the French onion soup and enjoyed it. I had the soup of the day which was very good and a delicious burger.

Nice little pub with a pub menu.

After my boss was late to our meeting he decided to buy us all breakfast. I've never had first watch and typically to-go breakfast food is soggy eggs and over cooked bacon. But then I saw on the menu Nutella French toast and I knew I had to have it. The most delicious French toast I have ever had with strawberries and Nutella! I can't wait to go back for more.

Great Sushi in the Desert 
Friendly staff and a good atmosphere 
They have a specialty Jazmine Roll as well as other local favorites. Golden Gate roll, crystal shrimp etc...

Recommended sushi stop for Reno Residents as well as visiting friends

This is one of those must-see places in New Orleans; both historical and hysterical. Do yourself a favor and keep this spot on your list; stay for a drink or two or three and enjoy the "atmosphere". Now, it is a few blocks walk from main Bourbon Street but don't be discouraged. Piano bar, patio, main bar, or outside: all entertaining. 

Enjoy!

I travel frequently and was in NOLA and looking for a studio. I found Swan River yoga on Yelp. What a beautiful studio and wonderful class!!  I was delighted from the moment I drove up. Very important if you are not from NOLA...this studio has a parking lot and it was easy to find a spot and free!!  When I walked in, the vibe was warm and friendly and the people were all very kind (employees and other students). The instructor (also the owner) passed out booklets prior to class so we could join her harmonium playing and beautiful chanting. The flow was simple yet challenging and I LOVED it!!!  I would highly recommend Swan River!!  I will definitely be back on my next trip!
Cory & destin were truly amazing! Took absolute care of my car like it was their own. Expert customer service & knowledge

Ive been to this place a few times as I travel to Clearwater Beach every 6 weeks or so.  The Service is horrendous.  The waiter we had the other night had no clue what he was doing.  There was a party of 10 of us and we advised there will be split into 4 checks.  When it was time to pay the bill he had everything on one.  Then he began to ask people what they ordered to redo the bills.  A good 20 minutes goes by and I see him inside serving other tables.  He finally brings out 2 of the bills and says he can't figure out the computer for the other 2.  We wait another 15 minutes I go inside to inquire as to whats going on, he was confused and acted as if he didn't see me before.  He then stated we could pay the bills that we received and we didnt have to pay the other bills as its a computer problem.  The place was not even busy, he just was clueless.  The tacos are pretty good if they come out hot, the margaritas are inconsistent and the service is VERY SLOW.  If they could fix the service issue we would give this place another.

Heard the buzz about this place and figured I'd give it a go. So far, have been here twice.

Service consistent both times at the bar. Raul is an awesome bartender and his drinks were excellent. Only had food once - the Tuna Tartare - which was also pretty decent. 

Views are good and the combination of the indoor/outdoor "rooftop" space makes it versatile. 

It's a bit on the pricey side for what you get, but also a good time. I'll be back and recommend you try it out.

I did not have a good experience here. It is very far from my house and the only reason I went here was because I heard that Dr. Tenase was more holistic minded regarding pediatric medacine. There were a few times where, without a word of explanation, she just gave me things for my newborn daughter to take (for instance, vitamin D drops with artificial color/flavor in it). I would have appreciated some discussion about vitamin D drops, and I am certainly not giving those specific ones to my daighter! I also didn't get the feeling that she was very understanding regarding alternative vaccine schedules (she just kept saying we would revisit it at the 2 months visit and offered me pamphlets.)
The kicker for me was that they recorded her two week check up as a sick follow up (I had taken her in a few days prior for a lump on her head, determined by the doctor to be totally normal because of the use of a vacuum at her birth). I had no idea that I would be charged another $25 for the follow up, which in my book was her regular check up that she had already been scheduled for that week. Since it was in the doctor's notes as a follow up, they refuse to change it. Again, no one told me anything about this visit being a follow up! I am beyond frustrated. I will not be going here again.

This place has the most awesome pizza. And their meat and cheese platter is to die for. The mixologist downstairs make the best drinks in downtown Tucson as far as I am concerned.  Always enjoy drinks here before heading out downtown.

My wife and I ate here 2 nights ago. We were celebrating our 52nd anniversary. I ordered the Ribeye and she the Tenderloin. 

The good: Service was excellent and friendly.

The bad: My Ribeye was tough and flavorless. Tasted like a steak from Walmart or Winco. I sent it back and got the Tenderloin. The Tenderloin was tender but had very little flavor. 

On our last visit here I had the Halibut which was very good.

If you want a good steak I would not recommend The Cottonwood Grille.

We ordered the Cotton candy Bacon but were served Pork Belly fat piled with cotton candy. The chard broccoli was cold and hard, the Brussels sprouts were also cold, the steak had too much pepper and rolls were fried flour.  Help me now!  We were also expecting  4 Star meal. So disappointed.

Went to We Dat in New Orleans Convention Center during Essence Fest and the wings were very very small. $10 .00 for 6 wings that looked like they killed the birds in City Park. I have brought wings from We Dat several times and never that small. They are wrong for treating visitors like that and only serving wings no fries or bread. I will not purchase from them again. What a rip off. Shame Shame Shame

I ordered a large spicy green papaya salad, and champagne. The salad was extremely spicy, and tasted like it had a lot of fish sauce. The shrimp were bland. I tasted it, but I couldn't eat it. So I sent it back. They have a policy where you can't substitute dishes. I asked if I could have ramen instead, and the waiter said I would have to pay for both the ramen and salad. I decided to just drink my champagne. So I paid $35 for a couple glasses of cheap champagne. I am not going there again, and I would advise you to stay away.

I just moved into a house literally right down the street from this coffee shop so I was SO excited when I found it. I've been in there 3 times since this past Saturday. My low review is for the price of just regular coffee. I know it's a boutique shop and a small business (which I would love to support) but all I get is a regular brewed coffee with a splash of almond milk and it's $4.48. That's insane. It's not a latte, Frappuccino, or a speciality drink. I get the exact same thing at Red Bicycle for $2.75 and 8th & Roast for $2.80. When I went to pick up 2 coffees for me and my husband it was $10. Unfortunately, I will be finding another small shop to support. Don't mind paying this for a special drink but I need something more affordable for my everyday morning coffee run. Hoping maybe they drop the prices (even just slightly) as it would make a big difference.

Worst experience ever! Expect to be delayed 2-4hrs from your departure. The employees dont have the courtesy to announce when a bus is not on time and they won't refund or change your ticket for a later date or time, you can expect a voucher for free nachos.

Great food at a reasonable price. I absolutely love this place. It's my new fav! The toasted ravs were easily the BEST I have ever had. My entree was HUGE. I had leftovers for sure. The only thing I didn't care for was the cheesecake. But I'm still giving it 5 stars because everything else was so good!

Came for the burger, returned for the sausage sandwich (the "choripan"). I'd seen the burger written up as being either the best new burger in town or best overall. First visit was for the burger and it is indeed a fantastic sandwich. It's served on bread similar to Texas toast (but significantly better) loaded with pimento cheese and drizzled with a super savory dark sauce. Not the typical American cheeseburger and downright enjoyable for its unique flourishes. But then on the way out, I saw the chef cooking up the sausage sandwich and I literally fantasized about that thing until my next visit. 

I think I overlooked it because on the menu it just says "sausage sandwich." And in the land of hot sausage poboys on every corner, that simple text didn't draw me in. Seeing it in action was a different story. An insanely good marinara sauce with a nice heat tops a link of some of the best sausage I've had. Ever. And then some fresh shaved cheese on top. Just wow. 

 I've had the other items, the pork sliders, the chicken, the ropa vieja, all good, all excellent value and generous portions. But now that I've had that Choripan, it's going to be my first choice in my many future visits to Shank.
Helen is awesome!...I called scheduled an appointment and was seen at my appointment time..my nails looked beautiful and lasted! I will definitely be going back

I ate here a few months ago and ordered BBQ Spare Ribs. The salad came out warm and wilted. My ribs came out cold and nasty. The service was far below par. I then met a gentleman tonight Ed who dined there tonight. He ordered one of their best steaks a Rib Eye medium rare. It came out completely rare!!!! So one of the so called managers said he would personally cook the next steak to get it right which was a new york strip that cost more. He brought it back and it was well done. Ed had wanted it medium rare. He ate the steak anyway. The only thing the two managers did was to say I'm sorry. It is obvious they have never heard of a comp when they are at a fault. The tall manager was hostile to Ed! Neither of us will ever go back there!!! We will tell other locals and tourists to avoid this place at all costs!!!! You would be better getting a burger at Burger King!!! We are regulars at Dunkin Donuts so you can be sure myself and Ed will make a strong effort to pursuade people to not go to this place.

I will take all of my cats here in future.  Took cat here (cat with kidney disease) after a weekend of horrible vomiting and thinking I would have to put him down (even made calls).  As a last resort I made an appointment at Cat Hospital.  They saw him right away/same day.  Dr. Keyes knows what she is talking about.  I believed that any vet would/should know as much about cats, but that's just not true.  As a cat specialist, Dr. Keyes has the answer to everything regarding cats.  My cat is doing much better and is happier and more comfortable.  There is no high pressure or judgement about your decisions regarding your pet.  The staff is amazing, the doctor is amazing and the follow up is amazing.

LOVED our tour and tasting at Nelson's Green Brier! IF you're in Nashville, take a cart over to this distillery and get your tour and tasting!

My boyfriend and I were staying outside of Nashville for a few days and through some Google searches and an Uber, found ourselves outside this little warehouse on a Thursday morning. We arrived just after they open at 11am and were the only group there, meaning we were lucky to have a somewhat private tour of the distillery.

This distillery has an awesome history and is worth visiting for that alone. However, if you're planning to go ask for Mary. She was our tour guide and was absolutely fantastic! She's knowledgeable, funny, and articulate. Some of the early history could get boring at the start of the tour, but she sprinkled plenty of little jokes and anecdotes into the tour to make it entertaining as well as informational!

The tasting was a real treat at the end of the tour. My boyfriend is a big bourbon drinker and really enjoyed all of the flavors. The sherry-finish bourbon was easily my favorite. This is a new business that is still developing its flavor, but they definitely have a distinctive style and a really cool background. Can't wait for the company to grow a little more so their bourbons are more accessible! (Right now, they're only in 13 states)

Food is pretty mediocre. You can get much better sushi and ramen noodles from other Japanese restaurants with the same price plus with  good service and good ambience. This is a self service restaurant. Door is also broken and there's no sign anywhere to inform people. Feel bad for other people who walked away because they thought the restaurant is closed.

Pluses: Atmosphere. Service. Quality of food.
Minuses: Somewhat limited menu selection. Also somehow the cider was oddly mediocre.
I had a great dinner, enjoyed the eggplant. I would definitely order it again.  This was my first time there and will go back. The service was great and very friendly.

We came here on a Thursday night around 9pm (since our restaurant.com certificate is only valid Sun-Thurs) and the place was definitely dead. We were pretty much the only ones in the non-smoking area and the waiter seemed a bit annoyed to have to serve us.

Regardless, this place is one of the few with a menu where my boyfriend and I wanted to try EVERYTHING. They had sandwiches, lots of different kinds of ravioli and pastas, and beef/lamb shank options as specials. We ordered the polenta fries with chipotle-lime sauce and I thought the fries were far too bland but my boyfriend loved it. It reminded him of grits and comfort food, go figure.

I ordered the butternut squash ravioli which was beyond delicious. I loved it. The portion size was perfect too, not too large but enough for us to take home and finish as a snack. My boyfriend ordered the lobster/shrimp ravioli but we were both far too enamored with the butternut squash ravioli to notice anything else on the table.

Our meal turned out to be really cheap and quite tasty. I've heard this place is livelier on the weekends and I wouldn't mind checking it out again for the bar, although I'm not sure I'd eat there without a coupon.

Over the past year the service and quality has gone down.  Dip nails only last 10 days and they chip.  Most of the girls are sloppy with filing and just try to get to the next client.  Try somewhere else... Like Dazzling Nails on Haddon Ave.
This place is the best!! Adorable but spacious, ridiculously amazing Mac and cheese, with an all around delicious menu. The drinks are also banging. My favorite spot in New Hope.

Super yummy
Menu changes periodically. Great wine list and the atmosphere is relaxing and easy.

We had prepaid a room 7 days in advance for a "sweet" discount (pun intended), and when we arrived they had trouble locating our reservation, even though I presented the email confirmation. There was a delay of about 25 minutes and a few phone calls to their IT Help Desk to verify the reservation. Normally I would be impatient but seeing that it wasn't busy (Saturday at 4 PM) with check-ins, I just figured let them take the time to work it out.

We were visiting for the weekend and had our two teenage grandsons with us. For our inconvenience, they upgraded us from a 1BR to a 2BR suite and that worked out great because we had a queen in one BR, and the boys each had a fullsize bed.

As the other reviewer noted, the kitchen is quite good. Applances are small, and there is no oven ex. a microwave. There is a ShopRite nearby. All the major chain stores are in strip plazas or a full-fledged mall (Moorestown) within walking distance, making it convenient.

Our room faced I-295, but we didn't notice as the building is well sound-insulated. 

They have a great indoor pool and whirlpool. The breakfast is also quite good.

I would not only recommend this property, but would stay here again if I can get a decent weekend discount. We usually look for the best offer on suites with breakfast included and have also stayed at Summerfield, Embassy, Candlewood, Amerisuites, and Staybridge in the local area. 

This is at the top of our list.

Who does not like HPB! I like going here because they also offer a great selection of books. I'll usually in the bible, christian, and self-help areas. I also love coming here if I have missed one or more of my favorite magazines, I looking checking here to see if they have it. Coming to HPB's always makes me happy. 

I like this location of HPB, but I have seen better. I was a little disappointment that the clearance area for the kids sections was VERY disorganized. Also, why are teen bibles in the teen/kids section and not in the bible section...that was confusing. I was also disappointment in the small collections of journals. I've just seen better in other states. 

But overall, I was able to find four good baby books to read to my godbaby at night for cheap. I'll be back and hope that they get a bit more organized.

I came here after a coworker recommended Sherry for a great haircut and she was right! Sherry did an amazing job with my hair. This was my first time working with her and she asked my opinion and requests every step of the way. Couldn't be happier. I will definitely be coming back. It's $65 for a hair cut and style, which I think is the right price for the service and amazing job that they do.

Terrible service today. The waitress did not know what kind of soup was being served. She said " Umm... I think we have chicken soup and something with beans and uh something with spinach, I think." She could have said " I am not positive but let me go check." Also, I asked her where the meat came from that is in the French Dip sandwich because in the menu it says "farm raised beef." I asked her if it was local and she said " Umm... I don't really know." Again, she could have said "I don't know but let me find out." She was very clueless. I also noticed that when she was bringing out our appetizer she showed it to the other girl who was working like there was a problem with it and they both had confused looks on their faces and just kind of shrugged their shoulders. Then after she brought it to us and went back to the other girl they started to laugh. I don't know if they were laughing about the appetizer but it made me a little uncomfortable. This young waitress also had a very low-cut shirt on that I don't think is appropriate for a waitress and also because this girl looked like she was still in high school. Besides the young waitress being clueless, on of the female "chefs" who was at the soup and salad bar was not sanitary. First, we had ordered the soup and salad bar with the baked potato and when we went up to get the food there were no potatoes left except for one so we had to ask the female chef and she said she would have to make more. She didn't even apologize and was not friendly at all. While we were waiting for the potatoes back at our table we observed her go on break and leave the cafe. after about 10 or 15 minutes, we saw her come back in with a drink in her hand and walk back to the salad bar. I followed her back to the salad bar to see if she was going to wash her hands. She did NOT wash her hands and I saw her start to stir the soup. I went back to the table because there were still no potatoes and I was also disgusted that she did not wash her hands. A couple minutes later I went back to the salad bar to see about the potatoes and I noticed her cutting up tomatoes and touching them with her hands. I do not know if she washed her hands before she started cutting the tomatoes but I sure hope so. I also had a problem with the salad at the salad bar because most of lettuce looked like it had been sitting out for a while because they were soggy and a dark green color kind of like cooked spinach.The spinach and artichoke soup was also disgusting. The soup was overwhelmingly salty. We ate our salad first and then when we went to eat the soup, a strange layer of film or something had formed on the top of the soup and did not look appetizing or normal to say the least. Overall, I was VERY disappointed about the service and the quality of food. I do not think I will ever come back.The only nice thing I can say is that the location and view are very nice and the sign and general idea of the cafe is good but it doesn't live up to what it is portraying. I was truly very dissapointed and I really hope that they  were just having an off day but they should really take a look at their employees, the quality of your food, and sanitation (some of the utensils and cups were dirty.)

The food is very good very filling. The staff however, needs a make over. I sat at my booth eating, While people stood at the door waiting to be seated. I watched the staff walk up and down the leaving tables and counters filled with food and plates from customers that have already left. The floors was filled with dirty. They need more staff to keep up with the traffic of people coming in.

Although very small, the food is FANTASTIC and the service is great. I start with the Queso. So creamy, so good. Then I get the beef Fajitas. The best ever! The atmosphere is AWESOME. The decor, paintings and food make you feel like you're in Mexico. Keep wanting to come back!

First visit to BrazBQ and had the "Big Burger" which is filled with churrasco steak, ham, egg, potato sticks, lettuce, tomatoes, and corn and a very tasty sauce.  Bring your appetite.  I went as they were videoing customer opinions so got to enjoy a fellow customers review of their burger and he raved about it. 

Will be back for more.

Love this place. The plate was hearty compared to other restaurants in the area. First ambience, we chose a window view which faced the French Market. We were greeted by the friendly wait staff, which being in a strange city is always welcome.
Second, our waiter Justin was a sweetheart. We instantly recognized each other's regionalism accent with origins in NYC. Justin was helpful in pointing out the great choices on the menu. I chose only coffee (which was constantly being refilled) while my partner chose the omelette with bacon, spinach and gruyere.. His plate was overflowing and of course I had to taste it. The bread was exceptional and was coined as French toast and it was sumptuous. The potatoes and egg omelette.was cooked to perfection. Too bad I did not have much of an appetite but will return tomorrow to sample the menu.
In conclusion if you want great food, reasonable prices AND GREAT WAIT STAFF (go JUSTIN) come here and you won't be disappointed!!!

Butchertown Hall is a great restaurant in Germantown. It's got plenty of outdoor and indoor seating and a pretty cool bar. Get the burger, it's amazing. The whole chicken is pretty awesome too. Make sure you try the beers from the left side of the menu, those are (apparently) exclusive to just this restaurant. The drinks on the menu are almost exclusively tequila drinks but they will substitute other spirits.

Okay I guess. Quality ingredients, but just very bland and lacking in flavor when I asked about that they said they don't season the meat before cooking. 

They did say I could season it after, but that just makes it salty. Nice people but clearly lacking in culinary skills. 

I'll try again. It's clean food and good atmosphere, but it will be a while.

Everyone in my family including myself has been coming here for years. They are always busy because they are THAT good. Everyone is friendly and hard working. I recommend getting here early (opening-9) on the weekends or the line backs up to street. Totally worth the wait though. I live 15-20 minutes away and I always drive out here because the service is exceptional. Highly recommend

I love this place so much I have gone once or twice a week since it opened. I've tried every variety of croissant, tarts, baguettes, bread, sandwiches - everything has been outstanding. The owner is delightful, and the space is charming and very pleasant to sit and enjoy your food. I have visited France three times and can't express how happy it makes me to have a little piece of France just a few blocks from my home.

Dr Wilderman and his staff are so warm and friendly.  They make you feel right at home.  All the steps in every procedure is explained in detail so you feel comfortable with what is being done.  I have to say Dr Wilderman could be called an artist is many ways but what he can do with a new smile is simply amazing.  I put off having veneers for way to many years and now all I can do is smile.  Thanks a million Dr. Wilderman

I'm reporting this location as it needs to be shut down, but until then, please do not eat here if you value your health. I'm not sure but the dumbass girls working here might have had their tongues cut out or may have possibly been lobotomized...someone might want to check on them because no one here can talk or do anything but stare. Sitting for 30 minutes in the drive-thru does not constitute as Fast Food. It should be called Slow Slop.

Went here twice while in town for business and everything was great. I had the ribs the first time as I kept smelling them at the hotel next door and they were awesome. I also tried the chicken picatta and it was a great lighter dish with great flavor. Trying to figure out how to get back here for lunch again tomorrow.

Second time I've eaten at Carlitos and second time I've been impressed. My family went for lunch this time and got great service, huge servings and there was live music to boot. 

We started with chips and some really fresh guacamole. 

For main courses we had a grilled chicken sandwich, chicken enchilada, fish tacos, and steak fajitas. The servings were gigantic. We couldn't finish and took home large amounts of leftovers. All the food was delicious as well.

Will be visiting here again next time we're in town.

Met up with some friends here for lunch. The selection was pretty big, The place was nice and big.. it lacked something at 2:00pm.. AC. Come on, Leave the freaking AC on after 2! It was soo dang hot in there and hard to eat. I guess that is besides the point... the food was good better than i expected and it wasn't as spicy as I  thought it would be. The Naan was pretty good, thin and not thick. If you ask they will bring you a basket to your table. I will be back around 12 when the AC should be turned on full blast!

Wanna play like an adult?

D&B's has the games, the grub, the brews, and the crowd for any occasion.  A group of co-workers and I went to D&B this week for some fun and libations.  Lots of private rooms which allows plenty of group celebrations regardless of the size or noise level.  I mean this place can get noisy, but in a good way.  You can smell the competitiveness in the air from the pop-a-shot free throw, to the Nascar race cars, to the beer pong (which is harder than it looks).  I was really impressed by the cleanliness of the restaurant.  No empty glasses or dishes, large fully stocked bar with chairs placed as a welcome mat to a good night, and a surprisingly expansive game floor where, imagine this, every game actually worked!  I can't tell you how many arcades I've been too with "Out of Order" signs throughout the available machines. Not here!  In fact, there was a mechanic on the floor making sure that all problems, which there were few, were resolved quickly.  

The food is overpriced and mediocre as mentioned in other feedback.  The chicken had a vinegar taste to it.  The focus isn't clearly on the food.  They make their money on the drinks and the games.

The staff was on-top of their game, prompt, eager to please, and happy to be there.  Our group took on the Company Challenge.  We competed in racing, basketball, trivia, and beer pong.  Not that it matters, but my team took home the grand prize!  We each got a D&B full-size basketball, $20 of game money when you spend $20, and $5 to a vending machine (i.e. the photo booth).  And, did you know you can recharge your game card using the D&B app?  I'm going to keep that one a secret as my family drops some coin when we go to places like this.  

Overall, great times with friends, clean and energetic atmosphere, and service that isn't a distraction or a disappointment to the purpose of going--- playing some games!

Everything we ordered was amazing except the sweet and sour pork could have a bit more flavor and cooked a little bit longer. Other than that, the dinner special items and the chicken broccoli are amazing!

Probably one of the worst dining experiences I have ever had.. Came here for Sunday brunch and was very disappointed, we came in and we were sat at a booth outside that wasn't even clean the table was still dirty from the last occupants that sat there, the waiter proceeded to get our drinks without even wiping it down. The brunch menus that were given looked like the had been from hell and back, they are printed on paper and have tons of stains on them and look like they were crumpled up and straightened back out. We ordered some mimosas and they took forever to come out and the orange juice was super tart and unflattering (even refills on the mimosas took a good 15 minutes). The Benedict and the chilaquiles were decent, there was definitely nothing about them that was over the top by any means. The food took a long time as well just for some breakfast items. The bathroom walls also have paint peeling on them which makes it very unattractive. When we came in we saw the restaurant was really not that busy, at $30+ per person (food+beverages) I expected a lot better cuisine, environment, and service.

hands down the best yoga studio in Philadelphia. Never have I found such knowledgeable and caring professors. Here is where I learned to love yoga! You can get a range of classes, from more intense workouts with Mark and Daniel to relaxing winding down with Betty(I love Betty!!) 

The space is lovely, as well. I feel relaxed from the moment I walk in, but might be the conditioning. 

They also have great $$ deals.
Convenient to highway north. Starbucks a few steps away with patio we could sit with our dog. Perfect.

I really like Sofra. My last visit there was exceptional. My boyfriend and I went for our anniversary. We ordered the Sultan's four-course meal for two. It was incredible and a great way to sample the menu if you're not familiar with Turkish fare. It started with stuffed grape leaves, a duo of dips with Sofra's delicious handmade pita bread, then a platter of meats, bulgar and fresh greens. The dessert is perhaps the most amazing Baklava I've tried, and it was the perfect finish to the meal. 

The chef and owner of Sofra is Turkish and went back to his homeland to be trained as a chef. The food is always perfectly cooked and perfectly seasoned. If you go, be sure to try their pita and dips, washed down with a Turkish beer. Incredible.

I've always had great service, and the restaurant is unique and cozy. I highly recommend Sofra.

I'm sad to report that SoBro has been consistently disappointing me and my friends over the last couple of months. The last 3 times I went the service was awful and the food was sub par, a bit over cooked, a bit wilted and overall blah. The ingredients are the same but they seem to have lost there magic. I hope they work through whatever is causing their issues.

This outlet mall is the prettiest one I have been to, with a fountain, open space and benches u can enjoy relaxing on. The stores are well maintained both inside and out. All the standard outlet mall designers are represented here. There are plenty of deals to be had. A Yelp check in gets you a coupon book in return. The food court could be better. This is a great place to shop going to/from the Jersey shore.

Delish! Stumbled upon this because I hear gooey butter cake is all the rage is St. Louis and this was the place to try! Bought a cake to take home and ordered an iced Americano, and it was the best Americano I've ever had!! Wish this was closer to me so I could enjoy this coffee always!!! Cory was my master barista!

This picture speaks a million words. Yes I have eaten pizza here more times than I probably  remember. This is the worst it's even been so burnt on the bottom that it tastes like nothing. Unfortunately the "supervisor" had nothing to say about this and said we should come back to exchange it even though we are on the other side of town. V disappointed

Great experience!! I highly recommend SK heating and AC!! Saki arrived on time and was extremely professional. He explained everything to me and was very upfront with any fees that would be incurred. He didn't try to up sell at all and was very honest about the good aspects of my heating system and the ones that may need attention in the future. He is much more reasonable than the larger companies and is a pleasure to do business with. I own a small business as well and the highest compliment I can give is that Saki knows how to treat the customer correctly and wants you to be a customer for life. Highly recommend!!
the best place I've found in Pinellas

Really enjoyed the food. My friend really liked his pork burrito and the fish tacos were fantastic. Perfect mix of fried fish, sauce, and vegetables. I only give four stars because the cashier had his sauces mixed up. Green was the most spicy but he told me it was mild. Only minor confusion but it could've gone smoother. Restaurant was clean and atmosphere was good. Food was done quick.

This address is incorrect. I'm not sure if they moved or if it's just wrong on here, but the actual address is 9987 E Washington St. It looks like a newer Aldi. Everything was clean and bright. 

I absolutely love the concept of Aldi. It's smart and efficient. It's perfect that you put a quarter in to retrieve a cart and once you return the cart you get your quarter back! 

Make sure to always bring a couple bags OR grab a couple cardboard boxes they have in the store so you can carry your groceries out easily. 

The staff at this location was very friendly. The products were neatly displayed and they had everything from Living Lettuce (which is my favorite!) to trash bags to wine! I love the produce here too! I always get the best avocados from Aldi. They're usually hard as a rock, but after a couple of days they are perfect. 

Everything is fairly cheaper than most grocery stores and the quality is still there. They even have name brand things like Tide and Kellogg's. And no, they aren't things that are about to expire. 

Remember that they don't take credit cards, but they do take debit cards!
They have great traditional sides here. The meats are good too. It's a local classic. People come here for Mama's great BBQ; only she cooks the meats.

The Coil is always so well maintained, quiet, and clean. It's very modern and in a great location. I also like that it's so dog friendly and the front office staff are always so welcoming. I'd highly recommend living here!!

Great place.  Excellent friendly service with good food.  I love to sit outside and enjoy the Reuben sandwich with an ice tea.  It's a great sandwich because they use thick corn beef brisket, cooked for hours, everyday.  Home made French fries are special too!

I love going for lunch with fellow employees and friends to this little hidden gem. Looks can definitely be deceiving when you first spot this delightful diamond in the rough, but I highly recommend you check them out.

I've had their lasagna, Wor Wonton soup and pizzas. The food is really quite good and very reasonably priced. The customer service is exceptional in this relaxing atmosphere.

Drop by for drinks and a pool game in the lounge area or enjoy a full meal with friends or co-workers. You won't regret it!

Thai food is my favorite and I am so glad I found this gem of a restaurant. The owner and chef is so friendly and what amazing dishes. Everything I have tried is fantastic. Pork pad thai, pumpkin curry. Customer service is top notch.

Quality of food at dinner was fine..I had very good minestrone soup  and fish n chips while the wife had the "wreck" fish special. The portions were on the small side.
Issues: entrees took at least half an hour to come out and the place was not overly busy at this time. . For the two of us the bill before tip was $55, which I think was too high given we had no drinks, appetizers, or dessert.
A real person answered the phone, gave the correct info, I got what I wanted and was on my way.  Thanks.

Low-quality merchandise for high prices and rude customer service. I visited this shop with my boyfriend and mother. The salesman was a little pushy to get me to look at this dress and try it on. It was adorable, and I was thinking of buying it. I'm a picky buyer, and I try only to buy things that are both high quality and made ethically. It felt a little cheap, and I went on my phone to look up the brand to see where it was made. As I was doing that, the salesman saw I was on my phone and came back over and rudely said, "do not take pictures of my clothes." I wasn't and thought that was not polite. My mother said, "she wasn't; she was trying to look up to see where the brand was made." As he walked away while she was speaking, and said, "I'll tell you," but did not do so. I have no intention to hurt a small business; it's crucial to keep them in business. But that behavior was unacceptable and made us uncomfortable in the store. Oh, and as for the dress itself, it was significantly cheaper online. He was selling it for a high markup. It was also made in China, where the child labor laws are laxer, and many work in poor conditions.

The BEST pizza in St Louis hands down. Delicious Traditional wood fire pizza. Also the wings were awesome and a solid local beer selection. The service was also great. Would highly recommend this place.
We went for happy hour and we had the best martinis and crab & cheese dip! Our server was super sweet and she attended to us well.

Reached out to Lucky Strike 3 times, once by phone call, once by text and finally via email. 
NO REPLY on any attempt. A simple no would have been appreciated.

Josh sold us our engagement ring this past weekend. Both him and the other employee there (Jessica, I think) were extremely kind and patient, allowing me to try on a lot of rings and giving me great information about each one. They weren't pushy and they had endless patience, and they helped us find the perfect option within our price range.

Typical chain restaurant that offers a simple, limited menu without ever being too creative. We ordered a shareable appetizer plate with fiery chicken wings, honey chipotle wings and some southwestern eggrolls. The honey wings were too sweet for an appetizer and the fiery red wings were not fiery at all. One of those restaurants that's an OK bet when there's nothing else around.

The beer was decent and the atmosphere was cool, kinda an old industrial look. But I was charged $74 for one beer!! They changed my tip to $68. Be careful with this company, they seem dishonest. I tried calling them several times and left messages and no one will call back. Chase is investigating charge! Beware!

The Italian sausage is good, the Italian beef is great, but the Italian Combo, beef and sausage , is Out...of...this...world. Cheesy Italian beefs are great too. But do not, under any circumstances, ever, ever, ever order a dry sandwich. Always get them dipped. 
The hot dogs are on point. I heard some people go there for salads. Why? Why would they do that? Portillo' s is a Meat Emporium. Don't go there for salads. 
And there's not enough space to talk about about the Chocolate Cake Shake. It's food porn, but in a cup. If your eyes don't roll into the back of your head after the first sip, or two, you're doing it wrong.

The ambiance was great. The lost barrio is a perfect setting for this trendy-yet-original spot. My hot tea was very good, my friend's coffee was great. I had a Mexican wedding cake for an appetizer & it was light and lovely.

I really would love to give this place 5 stars, but the food was just not that amazing. I ordered the turkey sausage platter and it was just meh. My friend ordered a lunch meal--it came with tortilla soup which was very good, but her entree was just meh like mine.

I'll go back, but only for snacks and tea/coffee, not for main meals!

It's pretty clean for a Waffle House (of course it is still new, and hasn't yet accumulated the thick patina of grease and lint most of them have...), and the food is as expected. Loses a star for a loud jukebox blaring Gospel music at top volume.

When M&M (the owner) is in the house, you are in for a treat. What kind of a treat ? A treat like you have been friends forever (which to us, that was our first visit), a treat like you are the most important customer to him. I understand sometimes, a restaurant can have a not so good review about their customer service on some particular day, but during our visit here, customer service is everywhere, even the Chef (M&M's wife) came out to say hello to us and to make sure we are satisfied with our meal. 

We have been driving by this place for a few times and always say let's give them a try someday ! Well, we finally did on a Saturday morning. It is located on Wells and some calls this "A hole in the wall" place, and it is exactly that, if you don't know it is there, you will pass by it everytime! They were just open and getting everything ready for the day when we arrived, place is very colorful, cheerful, very clean, and spacious. We picked the table next to Wells. Browsing through the menus while we listened intently to M&M about his story. It didn't take long for our breakfast combos to arrive, it is first time for me to try fried plantain and it was good, even Dad loved it too! My Huevos Rancheros was saucy and got characters, I almost cleaned the whole plate which I rarely do. Most of the ingredients here can be used in Mexican cooking, but our meals were served with El Salvadorian love. 

I already look forward to coming back here to try their lunch or dinner !

The Steakhouse of Western Village, Sparks NV is the model for what fine dining should be. I am not going to spend time repeating what the other 5 Star reviews said. To that I will say "Ditto".

Servers are all in black and white jackets with ties and give a very clean professional appearance. The restaurant is very well staffed and everyone makes sure your requests are met.

The giant prawn shrimp cocktail is amazing, soup and salads are perfect and the steak is what it should be.

Since this is an upscaled, fine dining establishment-the prices reflect that-but worth every penny. It is not what I would call bring the family and kids type of restaurant, although everyone is welcomed.

Make sure you make a reservation in advance, as it is usually very busy.

The meal ends with a photo to remember the evening. I HIGHLY Recommend this restaurant!

Had a great time here! Love the ambiance. There was a live band playing on the lower level, sounded great. We went to the bar upstairs for just drinks, extremely friendly bartender, very attentive. Nice clean place, drinks are reasonably priced for a tourist place. I had jack daniels so you can't really screw that up, my friend got malibu and coke again you can't screw that up either. Would definitely come back to have a few and relax. :)

Went here today for lunch. Had the basil chicken. It was flavorful and good. Wasn't expecting the bones in the chicken as the photo didn't appear to have bones in the chicken. Also the waiter said it came with rice which ours didn't. We also had a noodle and beef dish. The noodles were good the beef was not. I bit into a piece that was fatty and that kinda turned me off. The parking is absurd. I had to park across the street in a dirt parking lot. I would go back again if asked but wouldn't pick it if I had a choice.

We found this little Bistro on our way to Sam's.  We were excited since we've had such a hard time finding a good place to eat Chinese.  Unfortunately, we were very disappointed.  We arrived at lunch time.  We were surprised when we looked at the lunch menu.  The specials came with only a spring roll for $6.95.  Soup was an extra 4.50.  Most places we've eaten always include soup as a lunch special for around $6.95. So OK I ordered the kung pao chicken.  It was fair, just not what I'm used to.  My wife ordered the chicken with garlic sauce.  When she started to eat it she noticed the chicken was still very pink and not fully cooked.  We called it to the attention of the server.  She looked at it and said she was sorry but, she didn't offer to do anything.  No I'll get you something different, I'll take it off your bill or I'll give you a coupon for a free meal on your next visit.  Nothing.  She then brought the bill and disappeared.  It wasn't like she was busy as we were the only ones in the place.  Obviously, we will never go back.

We had a reservation for the restaurant at 7:30pm. We got to the restaurant at 7:10pm to check in and the hostess was less than accommodating. I have been to many Capital Grilles and never had an experience like this. We added another guest and she then told us the wait would be another hour without even checking or looking. We left and went to another restaurant that accommodates our party and will not visit this location again.

Dumplings were as BOMB as they say! 

The wraps were absolutely huge! See the photos for the wrap-to-head ratio. They definitely were extremely messy, because they fell apart once you start munching but they are so worth it. I got the Big Thai Chicken Wrap which was a good amount of tangy, and my sister got the Spicy Tofu Wrap, which had an excellent peanut sauce.

TLDR: the giant wraps are definitely worth it!!
My go-to cheesesteak spot. Some might be put off by them not putting their meat in a roll but don't knock it till you try it. This place is a must especially if you're coming from out of town.

Not sure this place is going to make it cause it was pretty empty on a Friday night and the drinks are pushing it on the exotic range.  These characteristics, however, made this place a perfect, 5-star experience for me.  The drinks were edgy, a little pricey, exciting and never bland.  Not sure you can even order a straight martini here.  The food menu is impossible to decipher but the 'lettuce wraps' and 'pizza' that came out were tasty.  The waitstaff were friendly and welcoming.  Cool theme-bar to start the evening off...then go to a cheaper beer/cocktail mess when tasting your drinks becomes less important.
If you come in and eat a cheese slice of pizza WITHOUT any Parmesan or garlic powder, you're doing it WRONG!!! So good!!!

I've eaten here before, and the food was good. However, today, I encountered a very rude and pushy cashier/manager! First off, the menus that one picks up are not quite the same as the menu above the cashier. I ordered a veggie burger, and he said that I wanted a veggie club. I said yes, thinking that there was only one veggie burger as was shown on my menu. I later found out that I could have gotten a classic kind of veggie burger for less. Since I went along with my order, I couldn't complain too much. It was a different story for my husband however. He ordered a small salad, but when he sat down and looked at his bill, he saw that he got charged for a larger salad. When he approached the cashier about it, the cashier insisted that my husband ordered a large salad. So when my husband asked to see the manager, the cashier replied that he is the manager, and  he's too busy to bother with him right now. We left in disgust, but I do remember being there the past few times when I saw some people leave this place and say something like "I'll never come here again!" I thought they were isolated cases until now.

I think the people on here love them selves. All morning its all about them. I cant believe Mike J. Keeps his job. Apparently they think hes great. Him and his facial expressions. Please. He always walks into the center of a story. Hes gotta be number 1. I hate watching this channel, they dont tell the news. Again its all about Mike. I think hes an azz. They are supposed to tell the news, they are not entertainers
Food is delicious! We came in without a reservation & were promptly seated at the bar. Mimosas are delicious as well!

Decent Mexican outside of Philadelphia.  They make guacamole fresh at your table.  It could use a bit more salt and onion, but you can't beat the freshness.  The free chips and salsa are decent.  I was fairly impressed by the fish tacos.  Surprisingly flavorful and well balanced.

Great for groups.  I went here for my friend's 28th birthday gathering (or 7 people).  The environment was great.  The food was excellant.  Service was very good, too.  It was a bit pricey, but I guess you get what you pay for.
Nice Asian market out here in Chester County, decent enough selection. Does anyone know why the county closed it down?

Just visited tonight from milwaukee and loved this little dive bar. It's simple, beer, wine, some seltzer's, and some games. We had a good time playing pool and honestly I didn't smell any cigarette smell.

Just not a fan.  But I'm not a big vino drinker either.  Food was all mediocre at best and the place had a very "corporate" or "chain-y" vibe.

I wont be back.

Very cool place to eat and have drinks! We met some friends here for dinner. We all enjoyed our meals and its moderately priced as well. We had drinks, appetizer, a meal and dessert all under 100 dollars.  Staff was nice enough, could have been friendlier. But since we were too busy talking with friends it really didn't matter. Highly suggest the bleeding orange cocktaill ! YUM I will definitely make it back here again.

When I went to speak to the Receptionist at the front desk to ask fora sales person,  she as with another female employee and did not have face masks on. As I was walking closer and closer, I thought they would put them on, but No. Finally, when I asked them to put them on, they liked at me like I was inconveniencing them and gingerly started to put their masks on. 
Very disrespectful of customers. I have friends who have had the Corona Virus and one died. I won't be buying another Mercades any time soon. Once my lease is up - I am going to Reeves BMW.

Buffalo Wings
Placed an order thru yelp and Grubhub. I received an order confirmation, I waited and waited and waited no food arrived, call Grubhub my order was cancellled because there was no driver. No one called me and they should have! Then they had the balls to tell me I have to wait 5 days for a refund.

If you're out on Mass Ave for a slice make sure you stop in for a Hot Buttery Whiskey in the lounge! It's the perfect cocktail for this quick, cold fall weather. It's definitely a must try for 2019! Brennen, Danielle, and Keith are absolutely amazing and will always make sure you're taken care of. They so knowledgeable on their product and can whip up an amazing cocktail on the fly. You won't regret stopping in.

Customer Service: (5/5) As a tourist traveling during the holiday weekend, they were very responsive with all my inquiries via online, phone and during our dinner.

Food Quality:(4/5) As a Bay Area consumer, it was definitely fresh, great quality and unique in presentation.

Ambience:(5/5) Awesome ambience, clean, chic yet relaxing.

We came on a Saturday night, and after a brief wait outside, we were allowed in. We didn't want a table, just a drink. Upon entering, we were welcomed to people dancing on their seats, someone receiving a paddle to their rear end and music. It was great! Within a minute of sorta checking it out, a server approached us asking if we'd like a drink. Absolutely! I thought it was great they had servers wandering around serving those who didn't have a seat at a table or the bar.

We went upstairs for a view of the whole place and ended up staying there to people watch. The band was great and played a mix of things. It was fun watching people dance. The food looked good but we didn't order anything. 

I really enjoyed the dark beer, while my wife liked the 'original'.

Can't wait to go back!

Amazing food. Our server Paul was prompt and really knew the menu. He recommended a lot of great food. The bacon lollipops were delicious. Avocado toast, impressive. Press brewed coffee, my favorite. First time there as well. I'll be here a lot more often.

Their wine is not good! Their vinegars are. I'd recommend a gift basket of pastas & vinegars/ oils from here, maybe some wine accessories. Get the actual wine somewhere else. More bitter than the vinegar. Taste everything first. If u buy a bottle of vino & wait until you get home to taste it, you'll be very disappointed !!

Dave the waiter was great he earned the star, food was not. Veal parm with bone grizzle, ouch. Pasta Sauce was like water, cheese bread was so hot from the microwave I burned my mouth.
I won't be back.

Went here looking for an alternative to PJ's (sick of that place and its never-changing menu) and the Pour House (after two cases of food poisoning), but "the mile" doesn't measure-up. The service sucks. They are ALWAYS under-staffed. Too bad.

Hidden gem. Took me a minute to find it, I almost gave up, but really glad I didn't. The service at this establishment is top notch, Chuy himself was behind the bar serving but that did not stop him from holding the door for incoming/outgoing patrons and tending to the needs of the restaurant. 

The food was fresh and flavorful. 4 stars because I felt it was priced a bit high for Mexican food, however it was prepared well and the service left a memorable experience. Guacamole was delicious as was the skinny margarita. 

I love that they are dog friendly also!

Excellent melt in your mouth pralines and candies. Wonderful staff, convenient location. Im on an extended stay here with Fema working and wanted to send my kids a piece of something special from New Orleans. They made gift boxes for each of them and sent them to home to florida for me. That was nice. They sell mixes for pralines, bananas foster, and even king cakes. One of the workers explained to me the king cake tradition which was interesting and made me want to bake a cake and participate in the king cake tradition.

I cannot recommend these guys enough! They were thorough, professional, and did a complete 180 on my very messy Honda CRV. They ran out of daylight on my first appointment, and came back the next day to finish the job. There is such pride in their work. You will not regret taking advantage of their services.

The second star is for the  nice layout of the restaurant. The lack of the other three stars is because of the less than mediocre food. 

The sausage and bacon were not of a decent quality. They were the same quality I would have expected at a Denny's or IHOP, not a beautiful restaurant overlooking the river. The eggs were the worst part. The scrambled eggs were dry and over cooked. Looked like eggs that were poached in a bag that are served at continental breakfasts in cheap hotels. It's also clear they didn't even attempt to put a bit of salt in them. The omelets were burnt and dry. The canapés lacked any intriguing creativity or quality products.

The coffee was excellent but the rest of the meal would only be acceptable to people that frequent cheap motels. 

Burlington deserves better than this place.

My car was hit by a deer and my insurance sent me to A Pro Collision. When i arrived everyone was so nice and Enterprise Rent A Car is there on site! I dropped my car off and left the office in a matter of minutes (didnt have any questions). Ken the manager bent over backwards to meet my concerns with my windshield. They brought my car to Safelight for a safety inspection, and it passed! When i came to pick the car up (after a week not having it), i was pleasantly surprised! My car looked amazing and the washed and waxed it and even put shine on the tires!!! And because one of my headlights was broken and then replaced, Ken offered me to bring my car back on Saturday so they can buff out my old headlight for free so both headlights would match!!!! (The old headlight service wasnt covered under insurance because its wear and tear)
I am glad i brought my car here! No complaints at all with A Pro Collision Center!

I enjoyed the plantains, yucca, black beans and rice here. Workers were very friendly. However my partner did not enjoy his food and I later spoke to my mom and her partner and they said they did not enjoy their food from here either. Maybe I'll come back alone if I am ever in north Saint Pete again lol.

My family and I was craving for BK @ 9:00pm tonight..while we was headed that way it seems like it was closed looks like the lights was turned off but decided to take a chance and turned into the drive through surprisingly they were still open! The cashier guy sounded like he didn't want to be working tonight. As we wait for our food for 5 minutes he hands over our bag. We ordered 4 chocolate chip cookies, 2  Jr. whopper cheeseburger meal and 2  extra jr. whopper sandwiches on the side. All 4 burgers was COLD!!!!! Our chocolate chip cookies was not fresh or warmed. It was hard like a rock! Never coming back here again! We are so disappointed!
Great breakfast, but just went there for lunch and definitely a thumbs down!! Sure knew how to ruin a cheesesteak and ham and cheese wrap.

The setting is amazing, the food was pretty good. Went there for dinner. 

Service was poor mostly because of the servers - as mentioned in another review the support staff were great and are much more helpful and knowledgeable than servers themselves - should be promoted indeed!

I don't know where they get their servers from but most of them seem to have no idea what they're doing. We were watching one of them describe with lots of confidence, to another table, what a parpadelle is... And he was describing pretty much a spaghetti. At least that was entertaining to watch. 

They got our order wrong, brought the starters and the mains at the same time, on top of a bunch of little things that added up. Then everyone kept apologising all night. Just a bit much.  They were very kind to adjust our bill. 

This place has amazing potential but they need to sort out their staff!

Wow, what a place!

This is my boyfriend and I's "go to" place for all holidays/special occasions.

It is a MEAT LOVER'S dream! 

Start with the salad bar. There is a great selection, but be sure not to fill up! Definitely get some of the fresh mozzarella.

As soon as your ready, flip the coaster on your table to green. The servers will immediately be at your table offering you some of the finest meats in the world. Once there is enough on your plate, flip your coaster to red and begin enjoying.

They also put a few side dishes on your table. They refill them as you eat. 

The atmosphere is wonderful and rather "fancy." Definitely a place to dress up. 

Service is always great. Only downside is that sometimes the cut of meat you want does not come around frequently. If this is the case, you can always request it and they will bring it right over.

A little hefty of a price (about $50 a person), but it is WELL worth it. 
Definitely not a place for vegetarians. You can just get the salad bar, but they will still charge you about $30 for it. 

Definitely head here for your next special occasion!
The most amazing and original food. Garlic bread cheese steak and raspberry peppercorn wings is a must try. Very addicting. And the ownership and staff are awesome and curteous

SERVES A PURPOSE
Perhaps the least memorable of the museums on this quadrangle.
Well not perhaps but more a definitely. 
However, it's good that the CAC promotes local talent and provides space for topical lectures.

Great food and nice people. Maybe not the most original ramen, but who cares! It tasted great and it was not too expensive. I also really appreciated the updated decor. We did a carry out order, it was quick and they offered us water while we waited. The miso dashi was yummy!
Amish Doughnuts. Get the Amish Doughnuts. They're life-changing. They're the best I've ever had.

I really like that they always have my favorites, but I wish the market would just carry Coffee Coffee, Buzz Buzz so I wouldn't have to pay a premium just for what I want. Probably the only reason this is 3 stars admittedly. The perk is that I feel like this place is never crowded so I can always get in and out!

Super fun tour! Josh was very informative, told great stories, and was really passionate about his city and the history in it. The stories can be scary and graphic so definitely keep the kids home but it's great walk through the city for adults.

Well this is by far the worst experience I've ever had at any restaurant. My husband, my daughter and myself visited this Ruby Tuesday last night. 
When we first walked in my daughter needed to use the restroom, before I even got to the door of the ladies restroom there was this toxic stench, I couldnt figure out where it was coming from so I proceeded to take my daughter to the restroom. 
When I opened the door it was the worst smell ever. My daughter had to go really bad so I allowed her to use the restroom while in there I had to cover her nose with her jacked, the smell was nauseating I started throwing up immediately. 

We left the rest room,  sat down and ordered our dinner. 
While eating I could not get the smell out of my nose it felt like it was on my clothes my hair my skin. I couldnt even swallow the food.  It was really the worst thing I've ever experienced in a restaurant. 
I order the steak which was more fat than meat. 
My husband ordered the tilapia with shrimp which was dry and tasteless. 

Right after finishing our dinner my daughter needed to use the restroom again.
My husband had to stand outside the mens restroom so that she can go. 
I told the waitress about the stench in the ladies restroom and its then that she informed me that there was an issue with the sewage. It made me sooo sick to my stomach. I immediately paid the bill and left. 

On my way home my husband had to pull over two time for me to throw up. When I got home I immediately took a shower and gave my daughter a shower. 

I called the restaurant and spoke to the manager on duty the only thing she had to say was "sorry". The manager was very non chalant as if this situation was normal. 

  I truly believe that the restaurant should be shut down until the issue with the sewage is resolved. 
I believe that this is a serious health hazard and I hope that anyone who reads this will believe me and not visit this restaurant. 

I WILL NEVER EVER VISIT THIS  CHAIN OF RESTAURANTS AGAIN!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

My husband and I live a few blocks from this restaurant and dine out often, we were excited to try a new place.  

We walked into CB around 6:30 on a Saturday.  The restaurant was at about 75% capacity and we didn't have a reservation.  Although there were about 5-6 tables open the hostess looked displeased and just looked down until another girl came up and whispered "No reservations?"  (We saw them turn away people later)  We suggested that we could sit at the bar, which was completely empty.  If we had not suggested this, I think they would have turned us away, even though some of those open tables were never seated while we were there.  

We were the only people that were sitting at the bar, but the bartender made us feel uncomfortable.  She was nice, but seemed like she was in the weeds with two customers.  We had to ask for water, appetizer plates (coffee saucers?), napkins and silverware.  My $10 glass of rosé was a very small pour.  I usually order two glasses of wine with dinner, but I did not feel this was a good value.  

The cheese plate was great.  There was a nice variety of cheese, but only 4 pieces of bread for the cheese.  We ordered a galette which was a twist on the croquet madame: cheese, ham and an egg on top.  There were not a lot of fillings and the crepe was soggy.  

After ten years of eating at La Creperie in Chicago, this place fell flat for me. 
The decor of the restaurant did not remind me of Paris, but yet a themed party or a Paris themed homecoming dance.

What's 25 miles between our favorite spot. Just picked up salad, rolls and eggplant parmigiana. As usual good food, picked up on time. Can't wait till we can eat in. Thanks Claudio and Jen for staying safe and feeding the public. If you haven't tried the food do so, Mangia Mangia.

Dim Sun Garden opened their new location today (you'll understand why if you know what the old place looked like) and I was eager to eat their famous xiao long baos in a nicer setting. These are my thoughts about DSG:

THE DECOR:
Interior design plays a big role in getting customers for restaurants along with the food and service and also these days the competition does almost everything to get a piece of your customers, you have to try and one up them. Well, DSG gets an A+ in this category. Undoubtedly, it's the most beautiful restaurant in Chinatown. The modern design is impressive, as beyond the seating alone the color scheme and angular paint design makes it a pleasure to the eyes. 

THE FOOD:
If you're unfamiliar with XLBs, welcome to the wonderful world of soup dumplings. A perfect XLB has to be thick enough to hold the soup, but thin enough to break  at the slightest pinch. Unfortunately, the XLBs here were on the thicker side. However, they had an extremely flavorful broth that spills into your spoon and leaves you with a tender pork dumpling that goes perfectly with a dip of vinegar.

How to eat XLB:

1) Eat right away while the broth is hot
2) Lift dumpling by the topknot via chopsticks
3) Dip into vinegar gently
4) Place on spoon
5) Nimble a tiny hole 
6) Wait a moment for it to cool down
7) Eat it with your chopsticks or right from spoon to mouth
8) Forget what I just said- You can eat it any way so long as it gets into your tummy

THE SERVICE/PRICE: 
Surprisingly efficient for their first day. Whatever service issues occurred, I was quickly to forgive (it's their 1st day!). CASH ONLY, of course. Fairly inexpensive. BYOB for now.

But seriously. Order the soup dumplings here. DSG and their XLBs is actually one of famous chef, Jose Garces' true loves in Philly. Another stamp of approval that these soup dumplings are awesome. It's also nice to know that two of my favorite Chinese restaurants in the city are now right next to each others!

just now
Strap in for this one. I ordered a little Mac, but received two hash browns instead and unfortunately Didn't notice until I arrived back home. I proceeded to head back to the restaurant to get my correct order. I informed the staff of the mix up and they promptly remade my order. I thanked them and proceeded back home. Once at home I opened the sandwich to find a double cheeseburger with Mac sauce. I again traveled back to the store and informed the staff it was incorrect again. The staff remade my sandwich on the fly and asked me to inspect it before I left, this time in front of them. I did and again it was made wrong. I was shocked!  3rd time wasnt a charm. They took the sandwich back remade it again, this time The manager John brought the new sandwich back to me, i opened the sand which,  again it was wrong (4th time)! John then proceeds to aggressively tell me why he made it wrong, stating they don't use a seeded bun for the little Mac. I turned and pointed out the picture directly behind of him of the little Mac made properly, (with the seeded bun) I stated you are advertising a sandwich in this picture but then giving a cheeper version. I asked him to use a seeded bun from a Normal Big Mac or any seeded bun so I would get my sandwich as advertised. John says he can't, but he will Make an exception. John storms off into the back and proceeds to make my sandwich for the 5th time. This time He uses his bare hands (During COVID) tossing lettuce and onions with said unwashed hands. (This now the 6th sandwich) I get the attention of Christina another manager, and she observes John using his bare hands and apologizes. Christina washes her hands gloves up and makes my sandwich the way it was supposed to be made with extreme professionalism. I thanked her, and asked for Johns name so that I could contact the owner and corporate for his unprofessionalism. Having a manager like John does no good for any businesses brand. John creates a terrible example of how to properly deal with customers when a mistake is made by a restaurant. I too work in the industry as a Manager of a restaurant, and managers like John can be a direct reason why restaurants fail. On the exact opposite of the spectrum we have Christina. Who is the type of manager that helps restaurants thrive and create a more positive experience ensuring repeat customers. I truly hope this review finds Frank Collison, the owner of this McDonalds, and the proper professionalism can be retaught to John.

We took our dog here at 11 am today. As of 9:15  this evening NO ONE HAS CALLED US about our dogs situation. We have called several times through out the day and no one can even tell us if he had been seen by the Vet yet.  As of 3 he had not been per one of the staff. I am so angry about how we have been treated. The level of unprofessional behavior exhibited by this business is appalling. I will never ever use their services again.I wouldn't recommend my worst enemy use them.

I've had a pedi and also a mani here on separate occasions and was really impressed. They took the time to paper and my nail polish (French style) lasted a long time on my hands and feet. I've been searching for a spa like this in the area and have been to almost a dozen different places. This is the best I've found!

The food was mostly awful.  The hamburger was like shoe leather.  The soft shell crab legs were like little twigs.  The French fires were overcooked.  The service was mediocre.  Very disappointing considering its general rating.

Amazing view ,super sunsets ! Right on the water came by boat. Live music and a pool to cool off in. Staff very friendly and pretty especially Kelly she made sure our drinks where perfect and Mmm yes they where.

Use to love this place. Seems like some recipes have changed. Over priced for quality of their food. My favorite part is the atmosphere and their tea. Everything else has gone down hill. Hopefully the next time we go back it improves to our old standards.

I have a handful of shops that I could spend hours in just looking at everything they have, dreaming about what my closet, kitchen, or living room would look like if I had an endless supply of money. Anthropologie is one of those places. Everything in here is beautiful. The tiny little tea light candles? Gorgeous. Window displays? Perfect. Coffee mugs? Darling. Coats that cost as much as my rent? Oh my gaaaash, I'm drooling.

Aside from the endless amount of day dreams that this shop supplies me with, I love them for their sale rack. I like to think that one day their swanky coats will fill my closet, but until then, their glorious little sale room is where I live. You wanna talk about hidden gems? You'll find 'em in here! Purses, hats, accessories, you name it, they've got it. Thank you anthro for jumpstarting my Christmas shopping. Because of my most recent to their sale room, I have almost all of the shopping for the ladies in my family done. Huzzah!

So go wander around the store, appreciate all its gorgeous furnishings, buy a swanky coat, or score a sweet deal in the sale room - you might just find me here doing the same. :)

This store is open to midnight so it's our go to spot to purchase breakfast items and alcohol after a late dinner. They have quite a few choices of canned and bottled beer and wine. They even have a large selection of gluten free snacks and many fresh baked goods, sushi and nice cheeses. Additionally, they have household products like coffee makers which we had to pick up on one of our visits. The restrooms are stocked though not necessarily clean. It was in working order and didn't have tissue or paper towels on the floor but the toilets needed a good brushing. The staff also needs a pep talk because they aren't very friendly. I get that it's 11pm but you chose this job and you're getting paid to be a "courtesy clerk" so be courteous at least. The clerks don't even acknowledge us. They just scan items like a robot and hand us a receipt without the pleasantries which is very odd for a store in the south.

This is our new favorite spot!

I'm ALWAYS in the mood for Mexican, while my wife is always in the mood for Chocolate. Someone decided to answer our prayers with Cafe Y Chocolate.

The nachitos, and the "veggie" chicken burrito were both amazing. I wasn't crazy about the fish tacos, but can't wait to try their other taco options.

We also got some kind of chocolate espresso coffee drink that was delicious - not too sweet, just perfect. 

Great prices, cash only.

Went out on a cold front and a windy day, so I knew going out it was gonna be slow. We still managed to snag some decent sized fish and had a great day on the water. While Im not as big of a fan of inshore fishing as I am offshore, this was still a great experience and I recommend it.

Just went here with my roommates after a yummy lunch at Midway. Sign up for their loyalty program via their Facebook page, it's so worth it!
They had a great array of flavors, and a "bottom bar" which I thought was cute and unique. I got vanilla, red velvet cupcake, and cookies and cream. I was lost in a sugar frenzy and finished my 7 oz. cup in about 5 minutes. The lady working (i assume the owner) was very nice.
Their toppings selection is OUT OF CONTROL. Better than Yogurtland, better than Pinkberry. I will never go to either of these places again. Why go to a chain when you can support a local business and get the same quality product? I really hope that this business sticks around, it's so nice having a local froyo place close to home.

BEST FRIED CHICKEN EVER
I've have some of the best fried chicken spots in the south and this is deff #1
We waited about 40 mins
Got sat and put in our drink orders,
Then ordered shortly after. 
Got to say the food was on point and the chicken has the wow factor. The service sadly does not. 
After receiving the food we were not checked on once, nor got a refill for my sweet tea. Even after we finished and made eye contact with multiple people it took a good 10 mins to get the check. 
Ambiance was as expected
food for portion was good
food is great 
Service no

One of my favorite places in Philly! I love Vintage Wine Bar for many reasons. As soon as you walk in, you will be struck by the beauty of the place. It's full of interesting art and a lovely chandelier. Floor to ceiling give a warm, vintage feel to the whole place. You truly forget you are sitting on one of the busiest streets in town. 
The service (Mark) was helpful in making recommendations, friendly and quick! We told him we were from out of town and he quickly made different suggestions of things for us to see and do!
The Happy Hour was superb, offering lots of treats for $7 or under. We tried the pear and brie bites on a crostoni and they were to die for! 
I will be back and highly recommend you do the same!

Cozy venue for a spirits distillery and I'm happy to watch their success since opening. They probably now need a longer bar or 2 bars to keep up with the guests i think. The drinks are really good and fancy. The bartenders are friendly and helpful with navigating the menu and your preferences. The menu changes seasonally and decently priced at about $8-$10/drink. There's only 2 restrooms and sometimes it's a little uncomfortable to wait when the need hits. The Fletcher Place/Fountain Square area is fun to explore, especially during the warmer seasons. Whenever we're in the area, we find ourselves stopping at Hotel Tango.

The food was good, but probably the worst service I've seen in a restaurant in a long time.
It took them at least 20 min to just get us water after we asked for it for it twice and they never refilled it. Also, the bread took forever to get there. 
The portions are pretty small for an Italian place.
The only people that got treated well were the locals and the regulars.
Don't waste your time and money on this place.

I just received my glasses yesterday and long behold my glasses had a defect in them so I had to take them back and get a better frame hopefully the frame that I picked out this time won't have a defect. The glasses are picked over overpriced I'm so sorry that I ever went here and it was hard to look around the whole store for another pair that I really like for one pair of glasses it was $279.. dollars. The glasses start-off $69.00 and and up. All I can say is consumer beware check your insurance check your glasses before picking them out and some of the people in there is not knowledgeable so they're giving you anything again hopefully this pair is right but I don't think I'll be returning back to visit once I just need to pick up my glasses because my insurance pay for them and me. oh and I forgot the first day I was in there someone brought back glasses they had for a while and they broke which didn't make any sense. But today a little girls glasses the lens popped out and her father was very upset all they wanted to do is pop another lens in and let her be on her Merry way. some of the people there needs training a lot of training they want your money once your insurance so you're in you're out and you're looking crazy because if you walk out the store you like what did I just do. and at this point is a little too late because they have your money and it'll took your insurance so again consumer beware.

No doubt this is the best place to get a steak on the Illinois side if the river. This location blows away the Ofallon location and the prices are better that less quality places to eat. The bread is awesome and the best thing on the menu is the prime rib. The bananas foster is off the hook as well. Again Andria's Countryside in Edwardsville has it going on and you will not find a better steak than here. I have eaten there at least 20 times and have yet to ever have nothing but fantastic food and service. Don't waste your time trying other places just stick to the best.

Every dining experience there has been awesome. My last trip there I ordered the penne pasta and shrimp in vodka cream sauce and it was fantastic. So much on the plate that I had to take some home. The Wednesday special of prime rib and shrimp is one of the best deals around. I have never been disappointed with the meals or the service. Absolutely great!!

To Start, service was poor i called in advance in regards to selling my gun and the guy on the phone said he could give me $350. i got there let them know i was there to sell a gun one gentleman was nice an notified another employee an the other wasn't so nice he argued and snapped at the other. Just a little unprofessional in my opinion
