Solid breakfast place. We first went to another spot hard on Yelp reviewed, but there was a line and did nothing sell the standard sandwiches we were expecting! K&a had all the usual listings, with a few twists like the homey moon which replaced the sausage patty for a chorizo patty and had avocado.  I enjoyed the fun mix, and would order again. We got a side of hash browns, and would have preferred they fry them harder. Overall would definitely come back!

The best Mexican restaurant in Florida, staff is friendly and margaritas are fantastic.   I go almost every week.  And the new location has a Great authentic Mexico environment.  And the owner always is a pleasure to see and talk too.

I have been a costumer for a few months. Today as I was drinking out of the cooler I noticed debri in the water. After looking closer I noticed there was a frog. A FROG! 

I don't know what kinda company provides filtered water with frogs in it. 

I would never recommend this company to anyone. 

Horrible! 

Note: they were completely insulting over the whole incident.  Suggesting I put the frog in. Typical low life response.

Great experience! Stopped in and 3 with my parents and boyfriend. Every employee we injected with was super nice.  The server who took our order accommodated every request my parents had.  We asked about thin crust and he said "i can make your pizza with think crust if you'd like" is just one example.  The (what I think were complimentary) garlic knots were great and the pizza was delicious! Will def be back!

This is the second time we've eaten at Ms. Hazel's Kitchen.  So delicious and so much food!  Home cooking indeed!  The best part is that it's not too salty.
Actually, the best part is Mrs. Hazel!  She is a sweetheart!
We ordered 2 fried chicken, medium size meals.  One could've fed both of us; great value!  The morrel (hibiscus) drink is one of a kind!  It leaves a kick in the throat.
Minus a star because the chicken was a little dry.  The rum cake is good, but I think a little scorched.
We will continue to visit Mrs. Hazel!

The car wash shut down operating while i was in the wash. It really freaked me out but I was not trapped by any machines. I did want to get out of my car but the attendant insisted I get back in my car. System started and then stopped again. I again asked to exit my vehicle but was sternly told to remain in my vehicle. Eventually the car was washed and I was on my way. However, after waiting about a month later I went for a wash and I freaked out when the machine came around the car. Major panic attack that it would shut down and I would be unable to get out of the car...Cancelled my "over a year long" membership 3/19. Very disappointed as I really liked keeping a clean car. I would say 7 out of 10 car washed were a decent result, other times hit or miss on quality washes.

How have I never been here before?! The service is top notch and better than most fancy restaurants I have been to recently. Another review said that the low ratings come from people that want lower quality cheap food. This is authentic and delicious!
This is a bod McDonald's because almost every time I get food here, they don't get the order right!

I was so disappointed with my stay. My best friend and I booked a room for Mardi Gras. We checked in at 9:45pm and were greeted by security at our room at 11pm. Apparently me and ONE girlfriend were being so loud that we were threatened to be kicked out on our "first offense". We were talking at a normal volume, did not have tv or music on, and were not drinking. I was absolutely appalled that they would not try to work with us to meet our needs. How could two sober girls be loud enough that we were threatened with being kicked out on our first offense? This is my second time staying here, as well as my friends, and we will never be back. I can provide a very detailed and thorough list of hotels that provide an enjoyable and worth your money experience within a 3 mile radius of this hotel.

This is a very nice park that has something for everyone. A Zoo, paths for hiking and biking, pool, golf, tennis, stables, etc. I like to walk around here and just relax. The park occupies a large space and you can spend several hours or all day here. They have parking and it is also right on the Streetcar line.

This place is wonderful!

Awesome deal for a mani-pedi. Super luxe. Hot stone massage and parrafin dip come standard.

My tech, Judy, was awesome. Super precise and one of the best manis I've had in a long time.

Oh- free 'mosas and smooth r&b of the 90s playing? Couldn't have been a more perfect/relaxing experience for this girl.

So glad to have this awesome high-end nail salon around the corner from me!!

Visited for the first time last night; staff was friendly and the venue clean. Parking was super accessible, although it didn't appear to be monitored at the end of the show. Only things I could complain about was the bathroom stall being pitch black (I think the light was just out, though) and the fact that I got hit on by the lot attendant from inside of my car and was made to feel very uncomfortable. I agree that the no-re-entry policy is annoying, but whatever. Overall a good venue; would definitely go back again.

I had a great experience at this salon.  You do have to make an appointment,  but the Yelp ad states this.  He did a great job on my nails and feet and I appreciated making an appointment because I didn't have to wait. Prices were reasonable and I will be back!

I mad a yelp for this place. Best burger I have ever had. I got the cheese burger that was made with cheddar swiss and Munster cheese. The homemade sodas were fairly priced and were really rockin.
Anyone traveling to Nashville should check out this rare gem of a burger place

This place is absolutely amazing. Food is beyond delicious and the service is top notch, especially Brandon. He's always polite and great with suggestions. My wife and I randomly tried this place one day a few months ago and we've been going multiple times a week since. It's THAT good
Authentic vibe and delicious food! Be sure to get the Street corn and several tacos to enjoy.

Slice of life has been our go to delivery since we've been in Port Richey. I do not have high expectations from delivery and the food has been fine this far. But I must say that tonight's meal is completely unsatisfactory. To the point that I can not eat it. I ordered a pasta dish and found the pasta has a weird mushy texture that is not even normal for delivery pasta. I do not normally review businesses but this was just that bad. The better half ate her pasta do to not wanting to cook so late but I could not follow. I'll cut the review short as I have to go cook something for dinner

There are a lot of bad jokes that can be made about the optimist and its name but its simply not a good restaurant. The place is nice and the service was good but thats it. First we ordered champagne. The waiter puts two wine glasses on the table.  When asked about champagne glasses he said they dont have those.  He then put a plastic bag of ice on the table to put the bottle in. So all night we slurped champagne out of a wine glass while staring at a wet plastic bag. The oysters were very good but then the burnt octopus came swimming in a vat of some red sauce. The halibut was gross. Frozen and undercooked with a green moss on top. Horrible experience.

I had dinner there this evening with my husband. The food is remarkably bad. The curry I ordered had a thick layer of grease and looked and tasted as though it was made over a week ago. The calamari were rubbery. the shrimp coconut suit had more rubbery mushrooms than anything else. And some of the other appetizers looked like they came straight from Trader Joes.
The food is delicious and the service is super friendly. We will be back for sure! this is a perfect spot for a girlfriends get away lunch!

I went back to GW Fins last night, and it was as wonderful as I remembered. Amazing food, excellent service, fantastic wine list -- just awesome. 

We had the pork belly and watermelon dish (holy shit, so good), the tuna tacos (also fantastic), and the lobster dumplings (just straight delicious). For my meal, I had the pork chop, which was cooked perfectly, and some tasty collared greens on the side. My dining companion had the scallops, which she loved. 

What a fantastic meal!

You can open the menu, close your eyes and put your finger on anything on the menu. Chances are it is good. The prices are unbeatable,  food is delicious and rich in flavor,  the staff is very friendly. I am looking forward to warmer weather so I can enjoy the outdoor seating.
Best of the best!  Please see my review under Carib Carpet Cleaning Services.  Kwame has expanded his services, so now he is Carib Carpet Cleaning Systems.

Amazing! Growing up in a town that raves about its bagel & cream cheese, I'd have to say I'd take Proper Bagel any day. First of all they let us try 4 cream cheeses before deciding anything. The strawberry shortcake was off the charts- things dreams are made of. 
I ended up getting the blue cheese chicken salad on a spinach everything bagel with pickled onions, tomato, and arugula- so delicious. Full of flavor, fresh, and crisp! 
He got the everything bagel with lox cream cheese, capers, tomato, and arugula. The lox were so fresh and silky.
Both were amazing, were traded halves of each sandwich to get a taste of each. Best idea!
We then shared the s'mores bar- it's a must!!

I attended a work conference at this resort. Well that was the plan anyways. I ended up having so much fun at this hotel I actually skipped the conference. 

Check in was a breeze and my room was comfortable and quiet. I spent most of my days on the beautiful stretch of beach and by the pool. They have a cute beach bar where they served a dangerous drink called a? Well it was something turtle. It had lots of rum and was delicious! 

I was traveling alone (something I hate doing) and I felt so safe here! I walked around the area and enjoyed many of the bars, restaurants and shopping. The food in the hotel was good (overpriced, of course) as well. 

I'm planning another trip back here soon and this time I won't let a silly conference get in my way at all. The beach is one of the best in Florida (IMO)!!

I placed my order Jan. 12th. and it is now March 3rd! I still haven't gotten ANY of my items and now they tell me they're estimated to ship out Mid May???? Do yourself a favor and spend your hard earned money elsewhere! They are a sad excuse for a business and even their customer service center was no help, it was one excuse after another!!
Worst experience. Both me and my children left in tears. If I could review zero stars I would. Please don't expose yourself to these terrible people.

in and out.  Lead dentist guy is super nice.  The rest of them are all business. No frills.  Dumb receptionists with no customer service skills.  Tiny waiting room with a tv.  Don't go here if you care about ambience.  Cute, funny murals.  Like the dentist version of every other south philly business. Kinda ghetto but they get it done.  Funny dental assistants.  Attitude problems galore.  Really helpful for the insureance-less.  Cleanings for a decent price.  I like it here.

Old town coffee has easily become one of my favorite coffee spots in town after just one visit!

Let me preface by saying I've been searching for the PERFECT coffee shop in Santa Barbara and OTC has fit all of my categories.
1) good coffee, I love a good dark roast and a good latte
2) food, they have local pastries and donuts and are soon getting sandwiches!
3) parking, I don't want to have to pay for parking for staying an extended amount of time
4) workspace and outlets, they have a plethora of seating inside and outside and a long bar that allows for working/studying with outlets!
5) ventilated air, the weather is amazing in SB, but oftentimes stale air attracts fruitflies which for some reason is a pet peeve of mine!
6) dog friendly, I like sitting in coffee shops but sometimes I want to bring my pup and the option to do so is wonderful

There's PLENTY of seating all around and a great place to meet with friends or have a coffee on your own. The decor is so hip and inviting. The back patio is huge and they have a ping pong table, cornhole and a huge jenga set for those who want to hang out. 

Rachel and Tim (the owners) are so kind and so thoughtful about their shop and coffee. They have been married for 10 years and decided they want to work together every day so they opened up a passion project. Their happiness shows in their products and this is a great addition to old town Goleta.

SYOY,
Jess H.

Awesome for any age! This practice is excellent on many levels. The staff are very welcoming and the office is highly organized with almost no wait. Dr. Gemmi planned my Invisalign program and gave a timeline, which actually was completed ahead of schedule but you have to be consistent with the trays. I have two trays left and will have a beautiful confident smile for my daughter's wedding. Drs.Gemmi and Middleburg saw me during the one and a half years and they are so very nice, and more importantly, great at their profession. I felt the price was appropriate. My daughter's had their braces at this office and have beautiful teeth.

Had a sweetpea and gram gram lunch at Convivo today and sat outside with our beautiful beach views. I live here and it was so nice to walk 10 min away to such a Beautiful Hotel to have lunch. I ordered 2 kids size pizzas to share and they were huge. Boxed up the other pizza to take home. My gram baby had CHOCOLATE GELATO and she loved it !!

What a cute shop. Almost has a little bit of everything. Cute stationary, bags, and just cute STUFF. 

The lady that was working here was talking very loudly to another customer about her run in with a celebrity. I thought that was humorous! She seemed nice and quirky in a good way. 

Overall, I had a good experience. The prices aren't too bad. I found these airplane shaped paper clips that I thought would be cute for my dad (he's a pilot) and it was $8 for the pack - a little more than I would've liked to spend on paperclips, but it's my father and it was just a little something I picked up to show my appreciation for him. So, hope you use them, dad!

Nice little casual taco spot by Garces! Hubby and I went on a Saturday night, and while busy, it wasn't super packed. Short wait for food. Between the two of us we tried 5 different kinds of tacos, which were all fantastic. The fish ones were definitely our favorite. Guac was good, albeit a bit pricy. Margaritas were awesome and it was a nice touch for a quick service place. All in all food was fantastic but deducted one star for value as we thought it was a tad pricy. Would definitely return though!

We only came here because everywhere else in the hotel is closed. Our whole group was too tired to get a taxi and go somewhere else.

But the food here was not bad at all. I loved the fillet Mignon. It was tender and juicy!

The sauce was a rendered down Cabernet sauce which was so good and rich.

We went for a cleaning, x-rays, and exam thanks to Amazon Local. It was the most awful experience. The assistant claimed to find 3 serious cavities and wanted us to signup for a payment plan. Based on the earlier reviews, I wanted to get a second opinion or, at least, talk to the dentist himself. No dice. The hygienist just kept saying I was making a mistake and being cheap. The proposed bill was just shy of a $1,000. I said we'd think about it and get a second opinion and she just kept bad mouthing other practices! She said anyone else would use mercury which would cause health problems later on and we'd have to wait a month for another appt. In the end, they refused to give us the x-rays, much less show them to us so we could see just how serious the cavities were. Unprofessional, greedy, rude. I wish yelped allowed negative stars.

I love MacNastys but there is a ratchet woman that hangs out there and she is super prejudice so watch out for Ratchet Sandy! She's mean and everyone in this town knows her and they don't like her.  she is racist and homophobic! This place means a lot to me, my husband's name is Nasty and the bartenders are great but Ratchet Sandy will still be there. Do not talk to this ratchet woman if you go there. Racist, homophobic and doesn't like to share!

Had an early supper after touring the Tucson Botanical Gardens.  We decided to have some appetizers only, but decided on an early lite supper.  Yelp helped us find a healthy place nearby - Zayna Mediterranean.

The rating are well deserved.  The atmosphere, service, and food were all well above average.  The modestly decorated place was teaming with revealing pictures taken by the owner's of his homeland - Damascus, Syria.  Our server, Corrina?, was also a delight.  Lets get on with the food.

We ordered the following, Baba Ghannuj, two combo plates with lamb skewers, and a grill sampler also with lamb skewers, along with beverages and pistachio and walnut baklava for dessert - all for $65 bucks.  What a bargain since every dish was very large and full of unique flavors.

The Baba was some of the best I ever had - creamy and smoky.  The two combo plates had 2 very tasty lamb kifta skewers, 2 falafels (best ever - lite with a little crunch), 2 dolmas, hummus (creamy and tasty), pita and taziki along with a mediterranean salad.  My grill sampler also had 2 lamb skewers, chicken shawarma, and chicken shish tawook, some garlic flavored stewed green beans, lightly flavored curry rice, and some pita and a med green salad.  The lamb was very good, chicken shawarma was also very good, but the chicken tawook was very dry and lacked very much flavor.  The green beans were soft and nice and rice was okay, and the pita bread was a weak point - I have had much better pita.  But whose to comment on a place due to its pita?

If you are looking for a healthy alternative to American food, I think you will like this place and keep coming back.

The quality of this family run restaurant is second to none. My wife and I try sushi in nearly every town we go in and the quality is important. From the tightness of the roll to the freshness of the fish and the diversity of choices and Willingness to make something custom. We will be back next time in this area for sure.

I've been here 4 times and each time it's very hit or miss. 

1st time- ordered the vegetarian soup (can't recall the name and their menu is ever changing so I can't find it on their website either). It came out very cold. Date had the lamb which was under seasoned and a pretty safe dish. It didn't live up to the "Janos" expectations. The cocktails were very good. 


2nd time- Went on a Friday night with a friend. The restaurant was packed so we squeezed into the bar. The bar seating is so small that you really are squeezed in there. We ended up sitting next to a pillar and it was very uncomfortable. Neither of us could back out our seats or else we'd hit the chairs of guests behind us who were very very close. The bartender let us try a few wines and was talkative. I ordered the Griddled Trout (with Golden Raisin/Almond/Honey Vinaigrette). The trout was once again under seasoned and bland. It was a bit overcooked in some areas and well cooked in others. The toubbeleh on the side didn't match at all with the flavors of the other items. It seemed to be on the plate just for a pop of green color. The fava bean puree tasted like a bland, dry hummus. The beets on the side were fantastic though. I cant say they were unique or cooked with a special glaze or anything. I just love beets and haven't ever found a beet dish I wasn't a fan of. The mint wasn't pairing well but who can turn down beets? 

3rd time- we ordered the Hamachi Crudo + Foie Gras Snow and Black Mussels as appetizers. The mussels were so tasty and perfectly cooked. Not tough and chewy at all. The broth was spectacular. Perfect amount of seasoning and salt. The Hamachi crudo was also delicious although a bit on the small side of portions, even for being an appetizer. For the entrée, I ordered Moqueca de Peixe which is a Brazilian seafood stew. The portions were large and filling. Once again, no complaints. The chocolate bomb for dessert was very rich and can be compared to a "snickers" ice cream but WAY better. The waiter was attentive and I was very pleased with the service and experience. I was sooo pleased with this visit.

4th time- Went last night with a friend for dinner after our class let out early. We started off with the bread. The server gave us a bottle of olive oil but no dipping tray/saucer. There was also no salt and pepper at the table like usual. We both ordered wine (I ordered granacha and he ordered pinot noir). We ordered the Beet Carpaccio with salmon gravalax. The dish was tasty and salmon was well smoked. I don't remember any horseradish that comes paired with it. We put it ontop of the bread and ate it like bruschetta. The waitress came inbetween and asked if she could clear the table while it was obvious we had lots left on the plate and were still working on eating it. Odd. Anyways, It's an appetizer I would definitely order again. For an entrée we both ended up ordering the same dish- Shrimp with a Sense of Place. My dish came out very cold and the shrimp were chewy like rubber. Not enjoyable at all. 



Summary of my long winded essay: This place is very hit or miss. Go for appetizers and a drink. Skip dinner. If you must eat dinner, check in on yelp so you can get a free dessert with an entrée order.

(Skip to the last paragraph for a summary)

I have been on the hunt for the perfect baja fish taco in Tucson for some time now.  Based on some glowing Yelp reviews I decided to catch an early lunch today at 9:15am at the Taco Fish truck.  :)  I actually showed up there around 8:30am, but the truck wasn't there yet despite the website claiming opening time for both the restaurant and the truck is 8am.  This posted time may change soon as I informed the young woman it was listed at 8am and she seemed surprised and said she would tell the owner.

I ordered one fish taco and one shrimp taco.  From other reviews I expected the price to be $2.50 each, but this has changed since those reviews were posted.  The price is now $3.00 each.  Due to the amount is fish (4 pieces) and shrimp (5 pieces) and the fact that you get more than one tortilla, this is still a good deal.  It is basically 4 tacos for the price of two.

Service was quick and friendly.  I cannot speak to how they well they handle rushes here though as I was the only patron at this early hour.

The tortillas they use are of the retail variety.  They aren't exceptional, but they serve the purpose without detracting too much.  Fresh tortilla always win it for me, but I won't skip the establishment for this reason.

The fish was fresh and tender with a nice crispy fried exterior, although oilier than I like.  It is possible that, as I was the first customer of the day, the oil was not yet to full temp (food gets slightly oily when fried in a low temp fryer).  The shrimp was slightly overdone, but not to the point of being unpalatable.  

The flavor was a little lacking on both.  I think the seasoning was a bit light.  This was remedied mostly by the condiments.  With most fish taco restaurants/trucks the cabbage/carrots/dressing/pico is put on the taco before you receive it.  Here this is all outside for you to garnish as you please.  I liked this.  Some places put way too much cabbage.  Others use too little dressing.  Here you doctor it up as you please.

The garnishes that I remember from here include a cabbage/carrot slaw (dressed), pickled red cabbage, radishes, pico de gallo, limes, a creamy chipotle sauce, a red chile salsa (I think arbol), an avocado salsa, several bottles of retail hot sauce, and a white dressing.  When I saw this bottle of white dressing I thought it was the tangy creamy dressing I have become familiar with on my fish tacos.  I like my tacos with a bit more dressing than comes stock in most cases.  So I proceeded to squeeze out some extra goodness.  What I was met with was a thick dressing that was just pure mayo.  Not good IMO.

This was a good, not great, fish taco.  For the quantity and customization options it is a bargain at $3.00 a pop.  For quality, it remains average.  Improvements that can be made are to up the seasoning on the battered components, provide the more classic Ensenada-style baja dressing, cook shrimp to proper doneness, and fry at the correct temp (or solve the oily issue).  If these changes are made, this could be the go-to place for fish tacos in Tucson.

12th  and Irvington location:
Don't waste your time or money shopping here. I came on a Friday at 5:46 to see if they could replace my leaking valve on my rim. I was told no, and that they were already closed. Their posted hours say 6pm!
I went a little more North on 12th to Wheels & More and was taken care of immediately and was in and out in less than 5
10 mins and they took excellent care of my rims

Too many tire/rim shops around here to skimp on service!!

Had a great experience today after finding this place on Yelp. They fixed my glasses for only $20 and had them ready within half an hour. They explained the risk they were taking by attempting to fit the lens back into the frame, and once they had fixed them, they showed me what to be careful with in the future. I will definitely return here.

Best authentic Italian-American restaurant in Tampa hands down. I'm Italian originally from New York, and it exceeds my standards. The chicken parmigiana, and tiramisu make me feel like I'm home. I've also lived in Florence Italy, and I can't say it enough this place is amazing. Go on a Monday, they have a half off the food menu, as well as happy hour deals during the rest of the week!

We ate there this past Saturday and ordered the beef hot pot.  In fairness it had two chili peppers as a symbol on menu. We specifically asked for it to not be made so spicy and our waiter agreed and seemed to understand.  It was extremely spicy..and extremely salty.  I could stand one chili pepper for spiciness but this was a four-five on my rating scale.They still put tons of chili peppers on top, I spent time picking them out and leaving them on the plate.  The waiter and manager said nothing to us when they cleared the table with nothing eaten and a plate of red hot chili peppers on the side :-(

I seldom eat burgers out, but decided to break my hiatus recently.  I was pretty excited to go to a joint that is all about burgers.  Sadly, I was underwhelmed with the Tru treatment.  The burger was a bit shy on flavor but still too heavy.  I thought the fat was supposed to be what makes it taste good.  The strawberry shake I had was even more disappointing.  It had a plastic, full of stabilizers, soft serve quality and very little flavor.  I'd give the burger another shot, but the milkshake needs a serious overhaul.

After waiting at one counter (the order entry doesn't work), had to go to bar to order.  She said why didn't you take their order?  Back to order counter and finally got ordered.   
Bad service, small portions, and burnt food.

A brand-new, super-clean, SORELY needed, Starbucks with a busy drive through in "Brookheaven".  Brookhaven had nothing like this shopping center - until now.  A new Giant, a wine/spirits (State) store, Smashburger, Hand & Stone - and finally - a real Starbucks.

My advice?  Skip the drive through - especially if there are more than 4-5 cars ahead of you and just go in and get your stuff.

Hallelujah,

I have only been here once but apparently it was on the soft opening. The staff was friendly and helpful. I like coffee but I don't usually find one cup different from another. I had an Americano with coconut oil and it was the best cup of coffee I have ever had. I can't wait to go back!

I had a flood in my bathroom Thursday night!!! The first thing I did Friday morning was to call Greenway Home Services. I had several meetings on Friday but had to get this fixed as it was my daughters 13th birthday party with 10 13 year old girls!!! My hardwood floors meant too much to have that happen again much less with a housefull of screaming girls. When I called Greenway, they were very accomodating. They actually called me to let me know when they were going to the appointment before me and when they were heading my way. I was able to get my work done and be there when they got there. Then I got a couple of follow up calls afterwards to make sure the job was done to my complete satisfaction. I would reccomend Greenway to my friends, family or anyone that needs quality service at an affordable price. While talking to them, I learned a couple of things. #1 Greenway has same rate service 7 days a week (they do not knock your head off on the weekends). #2 They have technicians that are certified in either appliance repair, plumbing services or heating and air conditioning repair and replacement. This keeps them from having to know everything about 3 different services and they are really good at what they specialize in. Call them, Call them, Call them

The only real issue I had with this course was the lack of effort by the staff to maintain proper pace of play. We spent at least 10 minutes sitting in our carts between shots. There was no starter(even though we were told upon arrival there would be) and we didn't see the ranger until the 16th hole. Other than that, course wasn't in bad shape at all. Tee boxes were a little sandy but other than that I would absolutely play it again.

Excellent food excellent service. This quaint establishment is located a short drive away from UCSB and the Santa Barbara airport and is a prime destination for grabbing a bite to eat with friends or eating a quick meal during the lunch hour. The interior connotes a feeling of comfort and familiarity that brings one back to a simpler time in one's childhood and creates an atmosphere that makes it impossible not to enjoy and savor your food.
Best Lash Spa in South Tampa!  Well trained lash technicians and fantastic customer service.  I wouldn't trust my lashes with anyone else.

Family BBQ joint in Brandon. The chicken was soft and fell off the bone. The portions are large. The ribs were good. Just a Smokey joint like the places of old times. 
The key indicator for me was the owner seated and enjoy a meal himself.
I will be back to enjoy again.
I'm a big fan of this restaurant -- every time I go the food is fresh and tasty!  (It probably helps that my fiancee always orders our food in Mandarin!)

I ordered 12 dozen donuts for my wedding and they were a big hit. They gave us a decent variety. 
I'd never had their donuts before, but they were definitely worth the trip. 4 stars because that's all I've ever ordered.

Whenever my family visits me at school, we get lunch here. We can never go without ordering the beef carpaccio and calamari for our appetizers. I always get the pasta alla vodka-it's to die for. It's a ricotta-filled pasta w tomato parmesan cream sauce and a little crispy pancetta sprinkled on top. Staff is highly experienced and always so sweet! Definitely go here for a nice meal w family/friends.

I just can't help but keep asking WHY IS THIS FOOD SO GOOD?! Not gonna lie, it looks a little sketch from the outside, but the prices are insanely good for the quality of food! Such good food, great service! Also, they are playing some serious jams over the sound system HAHA!

Not a huge fan, it smelled good from afar, they have a wood burning stove that gives off a great aroma.   That's about where the good vibes end.  I ordered the flank steak, it was absolutely disgusting.  It tasted similar to freezer burned very old low quality steakem meat.  You know the crappy meat they would serve back in the day at school lunch and put it between 2 stale peaces of bread.  It was bad, not really sure what was on the top of it but it was the greasiest nasty thing I have ever consumed.  I'm probably going to regret eating as much as I did because I'm sure it's going to be as gross on the way out as it was on the way in.  I would have stayed for a few more beers to wash down the vial aftertaste of what was served but when I asked to have a channel changed the lack of quality service was lowered to substandard.  Not a single person was watching any of the 10 TV's and I was told they would ask but the answer was no.  I watched, the only no was the effort the server put in to even ask.  You guessed it, he didn't, since there was nothing of interest to watch on the TV (just trying to get a ball game on) I made a point to see if he made an attempt to ask his manager.  The only thing he asked his manager was if he could go on break.  I was about 5 feet away when he finally approached his manager.  Find a different place to waste your money and down time at the airport.  I know I'll never stop here again..
Love this place! Amazing family style Korean BBQ at a very reasonable price. Everything I've tried has been excellent.
THE BEST OF THE BEST,they literally make you feel like family and take such good care of you  they completely restored my smile ,it allowed me to gain my life and confidence back,im so grateful !!!

What a disappointment ! It's hard to have a sub par experience in an area that has so many amazing Mexican eateries.  This was by far one of those places that you experience and wonder how the heck they keep the doors open?!   We walked in for a late lunch on a Saturday afternoon and wished we had walked right out.  The server was also the bartender and the only person waiting on tables.  Needless to say, service was slow, atmosphere was the equivalent  of a library and food was meh. While in the restaurant, I saw business being turned away because the server was too overwhelmed to take on more business (she had 3 tables to help). 
Do yourself a favor, the name is deceiving, this is not your resort town Señor Frog's experience.  I don't actually know how they are able to use the same name.  It's misleading and false advertising.

After being a long-time fan of the Lucky Dill, today's experience, after several other disappointing ones over the last few years, has driven me to review here. Our server took so long another server had to step in to greet us, take drink orders, and bring a complimentary cup of soup, which turned out to be the best part of our meal. The bottom half of the bun on my son's chicken sandwich was actually wet (like from water, not the chicken), I received a different (half) sandwich than what I ordered, and the size of the apple streudel dessert was an insult. About 75% of the COVID-limited menu is $14 or more now. To make a long story short: one half Cuban sandwich, one Metro chicken sandwich, a chicken parm dinner, and a chicken rustica dinner, two soft drinks, and two waters was over $67 before the tip. So sad to have seen the inside of a (former) Palm Harbor favorite for the last time. We're done. Should've gone to Chipotle across the street for 1/2 the price, or Glory Days about 1/2 mile north.

David Kim helped my husband and I purchase our home in Santa Barbara and then sell our condo in town. We felt our interests were well represented in both transactions. We were pleased with the level of professional guidance we received. 

David always responded quickly to our calls, texts and emails. He was patient in answering our questions and explaining the process. We could always count on David to follow up on the numerous issues, big or small that would arise. We also found that David was well networked and respected by his fellow realtors.

David was aggressive during negotiations when needed and very calm otherwise which was very much appreciated. 

David Kim is courteous, ethical, knowledgeable and a pleasure to work with. I highly recommend David to anyone looking for a professional in real estate.

Pho Street is very unassuming. The decor looks a little sterile and weirdly corporate. It reminds me of one of those corporate office cafeterias with fake "ethnic food" because of the largeness and lack of "asian" decorations. I suppose it really caters to the business lunch crowd. I came after noon on a weekday and it was not super busy - maybe being newer. The appearances are deceiving. I was very surprised at how great the pho was! 

You order at these little screens which take credit/debit in the back part of the place.  The food gets out very, very fast in 5-10 minutes. Then you get your order at a raised area further back in the place. The pho itself looks great and the first thing I noticed was how MASSIVE the bowl was. I ordered the Pho deluxe ($11) but looking around you can see that the bowl is normally that size. It can seriously feed two people. It was great value so much that I felt a little wasteful because I couldn't finish it.

Each of the ingredients was plentiful and they do not skimp on the beef slices nor the tripe. I had those beef ingredients AND beef meatballs. It was really satisfying. The broth itself was great too. Very, very savory without being too salty and heavy - and even a slightly sweet note. The nice sliced onions were perfect too. The veggies on the side were pretty typical and again given in large portions. The noodles themselves were great too - though a little bit clumpy.

Overall I would love to come back again and try the other menu items.

I met some friends at Craft Street Kitchen for dinner. We did outdoor seating due to Covid. We were seated quickly and our server was friendly.  We ordered the beer cheese everything please and the short rib sweet potato tots for our appetizers. The everything pretzel  and the beer cheese were huge and shareable. The beer cheese soup tasted good as well. We loved dipping the pretzel into the beer cheese soup.  Our friends enjoyed the tots with the short rib. The appetizers were well portioned and shareable. We would order both appetizers again.

For entrees we ordered the farmhouse grilled cheese, the angry pig sandwich, and craft burger. All were enjoyed. My friend out in specific requests for his burger and the kitchen kept messing up his requests. The waitress had to go back to the kitchen twice to rewrite the issue. She was great and fixed the issues promptly. 

While we were at our outdoor table we witnessed two different waitresses dumping leftover water and beer into the bushes adjacent to railing of the out door seating section. I have never seen any server do this at a restaurant. It grossed us all out and I believe it is a health code violation. I would urge management to fix this issue. This disgusting and makes me not want to dine outside at Craft Street Kitchen because I do no what to see staff dumping leftover water and beer into the bushes when I am eating. That should be done inside in the kitchen.

Overall, we enjoyed our experience and food. We all had a good time here!

I come here on the regular with co-workers and it used to be very good. Lately, overall quality has slipped. On our most recent visit, our food wasn't hot, just barely warm. One of the members of our party found a piece of cardboard in her food and when she told the manager, he insisted it was a mushroom, but mushrooms aren't corrugated (she was eventually refunded her money). The outdoor dining area was dirty; tables hadn't been wiped down in a while and were sticky. I wonder if management has recently changed and that's why things have faltered. We used to enjoy coming here for lunch, but it has fallen out of our regular rotation.

Wahappen?? Haven't been in like 3 years and just went for a group dinner. The cheese plate was a joke. And literally every dish was disappointing - like nearly diner food quality. 

This place used to be really good. Not anymore.

Cocktails were still good.

Here is a tip for whoever is running Ava's:  this town is full of local Italians. We feed our dogs under the table bigger portions than you serve for dinner!  The fennel sausage... Are you serious?  Where's the meat???  I ordered the small portion of the cavatelli and fennel sausage, thinking it would be adequate as I am a woman and this is, after all, PASTA.  The fennel sausage, while tasty, was two crumbs. I paid 12 bucks for a smaller portion than you get in a Smart Ones frozen entree. Shame on you!  I will NEVER go back and neither will the other 10 LOCAL Italian women that were at my table.  If for some reason I actually find myself back in that place, I will order the pizza. At least I won't have to go stop and get a burger on the way home so I don't go to bed hungry. Pay attention. Even if Pope himself made that cavatelli, no way in Hell do you have any justification to charge 12 bucks for such a measly portion.
The bar itself was average but the the band that played that night had amazing energy and music that helped to create a fun and upbeat environment in the bar.

I couldn't tell you why I haven't been back to this Yats in over two years. Quick service, good cheap food, NOLA decor and chill vibes - what's not to love? You walk up to the counter order off the chalkboard behind the cashier and I kid you not the food is out +/- by the time you're done filling your drink. I like that you can get two half orders on the same plate, which is great for food mixers like me! I spent only $7.75 and got the B&B a rice, black bean and corn mix with the chicken and red sauce (can't recall the name right now). Plenty of flavor but not crazy spice. My plate came with a piece of French bread which is perfect for dipping! I am definitely going back for more of the French bread and to try the peanut butter pie, chili cheese étouffée, drunken chicken, etc. Expect plenty more check ins at Yats in the future!

Was there for the clubbing. Not really impressed. But then again, maybe this is just Tampa's fault because Tampa is not a very happening place. 

I hope I never go back.

The food was great and the place was busy. I sat at the bar. If you want a sit down dinner, I recommend calling ahead. I ordered the special - ahi skewers with rice noodles and loved it. Beers are awesome, too!
Fast service, excellent communication every step of the way, and most importantly received top dollar on my scrap gold and silver.  Highly recommend !

I love this little taco truck. It's a hard working family business and they turn out great tacos at a great price. We've tried the Atul Tacos with every available meat, quesadillas and nachos. All were very good. I think the only place that really gives them competition is Capital Taco on US 41 in Land O' Lakes, but Azul is both closer and cheaper. This is our go to taco place whenever no feels like cooking.
Came in for a slice of pizza. Very, very good. I like the thin crust. Service friendly. Prices very reasonable.

What a fun way to see a different side of Nashville!  The crew was fantastic...especially with the dumping of water on the heads of people who got too hot.  Being able to bring your own food and booze on the boat was great too!

I usually like to budget but it was a friend's night out so I couldn't say "NO" when BJ's was suggested. Lets face it no one likes to be the debby downer. For one entree, and a third of a dessert plus tip I payed $17... Eh... Is all I can say, by no means was the food bad, but for the portion size, and the overall quality I'd rather take my change somewhere else. One thing that they can boast are their pizookies. Those little chocolate chip cookies (which come in varying flavors) serves as a throne that the ice cream sits on and it melts away both in your mouth and on the cookies, and sometimes on your face as you shovel it in as fast as you can. One of my favorite fat kid fixes. For a chain it isn't bad but that's all that there is to be said.
Food was great, but the service was pretty slow considering the restaurant wasnt busy at all. Staff was pretty nice and was helpful with the menu as well. I recommend the lamb chops and mucver.

I would call this a MOtel, not a HOtel.  We had their standard room (no frills here) and room is very small.  Too small for additional furniture, so there is no table top or dresser to put your things on.  The bathroom is even smaller - one of those where you have to stand in a specific spot just to be able to open and close the door!  Room has a (3 blade) ceiling fan, no air conditioning.  We came in October and it was still uncomfortably stuffy at night.  The only thing it has going for it is the location (and Aveda products).  But there's still a noisy street in between.  

It's storytime:
After we checked in, we had trouble opening the door to our room.  We figured the key card didn't get swipe properly and went back to have them re-do it.  Instead they said "oh yeah, that room has a door problem, it sticks a bit, just use some muscle".  Ok...  So, we went back to try again and I watched in amusement as my husband is now body slamming the door so that we can get in.  Still didn't work...  We had to have the staff show us the trick to get inside our room.  Atleast we don't have to worry about anyone breaking in to steal anything!  Unrelated - the next morning, the hotel lost power.  Had to shower and get dressed in the dark.  Very fun.

Not worth it - go somewhere else.

I was allured in by the beautiful complex and glossy brochures. One year later, my lease is almost up and I'm furious with the leasing staff and management. My main complaint revolves around an air conditioner that barely works and has caused me to have electric bills near $200. For living alone and rarely being home during daytime hours, I think this is astronomical.  It is consistently 80 degrees in my apartment at 9 pm AFTER the sun has gone down. The air coming out of the a/c is hardly cool at all and you can barely feel it blowing out.

It took me a lot of fighting to get them to supply me with a window unit for the summer, so at least I have some relief. However, I was promised that the central a/c would be fixed at the end of June, and now we are approaching the middle of August and not a thing has been done. After following up several times with the front office, I was having no luck. I decided to call the manager. On FOUR separate occasions, I was told by various members of the front office that the manager was "in a meeting" and each time I gave them my name and phone number and told them to have their manager call me back. I still have not been called back by the manager.

The office staff is pretty rude as others have mentioned. At first I had no issues with them, but it's almost as if I have my move-out date stamped on my forehead every time I walk in there and they don't want to deal with me. I'm a good tenant. I pay my rent on time. I have kept the apartment in good condition. If this place wants to keep good tenants, they shouldn't treat them like garbage.  Return phone calls and repair what needs to be fixed.  The place is pretty outdated and I knew that moving in. Popcorn ceilings and some worn down cabinets don't bother me, but not fixing the problems that need addressing does.

The ponds are beautiful and the pools are nice, but don't let that suck you in. You will have to constantly keep on top of the staff to fix your problems.  The actual maintenance staff does work hard, you just have to hope the leasing office actually relays your work orders to them.

Madison Ridgelake, now called Bellevue West, is one of the cheaper places I found in Bellevue. But renter beware, you get what you pay for.
Was very impressed with the service.   I didn't expect them replace my tire so quickly!  I highly recommend them

I'm a meat lover through and through, but I'd eat vegetarian more often if I could have all my meals here!
My boyfriend and I had been eager to check this place out since we've never been to a Chinese vegetarian restaurant. It just opened and is very nice and clean with cute lighting. They take reservations but there was ample seating when we stopped by.
They have a paper menu where you can check off the items you want, as well as a standard menu with the same items, just with more pictures for you to peruse. When we went a few days ago, they had a promotion where you could get a free bubble tea with any purchase above $10. Take advantage of this deal, our taro milk tea was so delicious!
They have many of the typical dim sum items you would expect. My favorites were the sticky rice shumai, pumpkin tarts, and fried taro cakes. The real winner for me was the General Tso's seitan which was crispy and flavorful. I'm planning to come by again and try more of their specialty vegetarian items.

Had high hopes, as this was highly recommended by locals.  Long wait for a table and a long wait for food, but there are highlights:  four of us ordered the barbecued chicken.  Very tender and juicy, but reviews of the sauce ranged from too tame to meh.  The brisket and ribs both drew thumbs up.  Barbecue Salad with brisket was declared okay and the fried fish got a passing grade.  Reaction to the sides was mixed as well: cheddar grits were scrumptious, cole slaw nicely crisp, good potato salad, great dirty rice and ok BBQ beans.  Sweet potato crisp may as well have been dessert.  Cornbread was tasty, not too sweet. 

Biggest criticism of the menu:  limited fish offering (fried or shrimp and grits), only one vegetarian burger, no fresh veggies (other than salad), no cooked greens. 

Overall, pretty good food,  but be prepared to wait for both your table and your meal!
Good Food - lovely place - we dined outdoors - enjoyed service and food - good selection of beer.
Best sandwiches I've found in Florida, and the people working and making my sandwich were very friendly and helpful! I will definitely be back here another 5 to 5000 times!

I had been growing my hair out and decided to go back to a short cut. I had never been to Atelier so I looked it up on Yelp. It had excellent reviews so I booked a same day appointment online for after work (easy!). I met with Lexi and showed her a picture of what I wanted and told her that I was very low maintenance. She said that the cut I wanted was high maintenance. She suggested a stacked cut all the way around, which we did, and she gave me a blowout. I have curly hair and had never seen it straight -- it looks fabulous!!! It's an excellent basic cut and will look great curly as well. Lexi is very knowledgeable and I had a great time there. I never book a second appointment at salons before I leave but I did this time. 

Highly recommended.

We had another venue cancel on us last minute for a party of15. This location opened up their back room for us and rocked the house on service, taste and presentation. Our guests were wowed by the food and spirits. Tyson was a fantastic server and kept everybody happy. A great evening!

Spent a long weekend here with the family. The 1 bedroom suite we booked was huge with 3 LCD TVs. We didn't watch much TV though. Hung out at the pool a lot. The separate kids' pool is fantastic with little toddler/baby areas too. Service was exceptional. All around this was the nicest Ritz to which we've been (Orlando, St Louis, Maui).

i won't ever waste my time with this company again. they play games with your time and act like you should be glad if they return your call. they have a nasty attitude on the phone and then don't call you back. call roto- rooter instead they are very nice and send out someone the same day. he fixed the problem with no unpleasant attitude -- never deal with Budget- Rooter unless you enjoy hassles.

This place is such a dive but I love it!!! The burger may be...hands down...the best one Ive ever had! It is so yummy. Always perfectly seasoned and prepared. The ambiance aint much but its a cool spot to grub on a huge burger and fries while having a drink and watching the game. You gotta try it! Promise you wont just go once.

One of those classic South Philly institutions that I'm surprised that I hadn't reviewed a few years sooner! But it took enjoying New Year's Day dinner with good friends to realize this as I reveled in delicious pasta!

Ralph's is thee timeless Philly Italian restaurant that has been around for over a century in one form or another. And while there may be a few Philly Italian restaurants that may have hit higher marks in one way or another, Ralph's is that classic spot that influenced them in that direction, while managing to still have some new tricks up its sleeves.

Both floors of Ralph's are great for enjoying old world eats. But the 2nd floor dining room has the added benefit of dining among some fantastic murals of iconic Italian landmarks. For drinks they have a solid wine list, and it doesn't get much better than an ice cold Moretti or Peroni for beer. But they also have an impressive cocktail list that's worth a look over.

For food pasta is very much on the menu! And it's bound to be a good decision whether you order it as spaghetti, ravioli, manicotti, penne, cavatelli, or other! But they also have plenty of old world classics like saltimbocca, braciole, linguini pescatore, chicken cacciatore; and their famous and HUGE veal parm! But for me I also like the selections from the portion of their menu that adds new twists to old recipes. The fettuccine alfredo with shrimp, pancetta, and peas I ordered was as phenomenal as it looked! But no matter what you get it's sure to be a solid Italian meal!

So happy to have tried the new restaurant Oori in Chester Count, PA. A friend brought me to the restaurant for a lovely Sunday lunch. I ordered the House Ramen and it was delicious, the fried pork was perfect and melts in your mouth. The broth tasted fresh and spicy. The atmosphere was casual and the decor monochromatic and tasteful, but it was loud, they could use some sound panels on the walls. The service was good and the host was friendly and remembered my friend from the night before. 
I will definitely go back, there's so much more to explore on the menu than just ramen.

Bf wanted me to check out Ybor City last weekend. Ybor  is like Bourbon St. in New Orleans minus the people puking on the street. Since most of the bars/clubs had a cover charge, we settled on JJIP, which had no cover charge, live music, a pool table, and 2 dart boards. Service was good --no long waits for a drink. You can smoke inside. Overall, we had a good time.

With the Epidemic, Reservations are necessary at The Chart House on Delaware Ave. at Penns Landing.... So I made a single reservation on Open Table for 7 pm... I arrived 6:55 and had no problem parking in their parking lot, nor was there a charge... I checked in with the Hostess, who was expecting me, and was seated by a window overlooking the Delaware River... I had a perfect view of The Battleship New Jersey ! My server, Roe, introduced  herself to me and brought me a Split of Korbel Brut. I often like to drink Champagne with my appetizers... I Started with The Lobster Bisque.  It was WONDERFUL !  Thick and creamy concentrated with lobster flavor, and lots of lobster garnishing the Bisque ! I then ordered  the Coconut Crunchy Shrimp... Large Butterflied and split Shrimp fried in a batter rich in Coconut and served with coconut cream and citrus chili + soy dipping sauces. I liked The Coconut Cream and asked for another... It was a bit like a pina colada sauce... When I arrived at The Chart House, I had every intention of ordering Prime Rib, but something on the Menu caught my eye, so naturally I asked Roe what she thought... She told me that she didn't like fish "at all," but she LOVES "Snapper Hemingway"  That was enough for me ! Snapper Hemingway it was !!! Red Snapper encrusted with panko and fried to a golden brown topped with Lump Crab Meat and a delightful lemon Caper sauce. It was accompanied by a 3 potato mash topped with a piece of deep fried carrot...  A nice touch.... Roe Recommended a Mezzacorona Pino Grigio that worked very well with my meal... I was grateful...I was also stuffed and didn't order desert or Capaccino... Another GREAT adventure ! Thank you Roe, and Thank you Chart House !  https://www.chart-house.com/.../chart-house-philadelphia-pa

Over priced!! Paid $13.95 for a burrito and it came with a side of potatoes. I ordered to go, my burrito fell apart and was soggy. I was starving and this place is across from the college, so I thought I would walk over and give it a try. Great location but was really disappointed.

We always get an amazing steak here.  The service is always lovely and competent.  Try their Greek salad dressing.  Only downside: there tends to be a lot of noisy kids in the dining room.  The bar room is a much better choice.
Nice small place. Everything is nice about it except for one thing. They must not have very good kitchen exhaust fans, cause you will walk out of there smelling like you were the cook!!!  Hahaha

Got the munchies? if so, head on over to Dave's dogs! Almost everything they have on the menu will hit the spot. This place is definitely Comfort food heaven, Seriously can't rave about this place enough. Also, You can tell the restaurant is well-taken care of and it is nice seeing the local owner there all the time.

Food was fantastic.  No reservations so we sat at the bar.  When you get your salads and the. Your appetizer is slammed down right after it is disappointing.  Had to ask for bread 3 times and water refills.  Great atmosphere and I will try again with a reservation.  The food is worth it, I'll go again.   .

Great Peruvian food served in huge quantities enough for two normal people or one monsterous appetite. Prices are very reasonable considering the amount of food served and it's quality. Tucked away in a corner of Fiesta Plaza, it is much roomier on the inside than it appears outside. I saw it as a good sign that it was crowded even at 3 o'clock in the afternoon.  The servers and the owner were very helpful and accommodating. If you like or would like to try Peruvian cuisine, put this on your list of places to go.

We had stayed at hotels across the country but nothing matched the Drury Inn. They had an extremely helpful and nice staff, free breakfast/dinner, and a nice room with great rates.

We stayed 4 nights and had nothing but great experiences.

I would definitely recommend this hotel.
Great boba concept. Well executed, nice variety of flavors and concoctions even for those, non boba or non tea  lovers

This dunkin donuts really takes the cake for being a bad experience for a fast food joint that really doesn't have an extensive menu. The drive thru experience was absolutely terrible. When I pulled up to the speaker, all I heard was mumbling, something like "hwcnhlpyou". That's what it sounded like. The one taking my order was talking so fast that I didn't even understand what he was saying. It took me about 4 times asking what did you say before I realized he was trying to say "how can I help you?" and he didn't even try to speak any clearer. Anyway I ordered my coffee and then again, he spoke really fast and told me the cost and pull up to the first window. Still had no idea what this guy was saying when I pulled up to the window. He spoke the same when we talked at the window as well. I mean I know it's just a donut place but at least use your words and act like you care about where you're working at and if you don't just find another job. Definitely the last time I take this drive thru. I am still debating going inside because I might have the same experience.
It was my first time here and I will be coming back! The massage and facial were amazing. I have already booked another facial. My skin felt and still feels amazing.

I love Pei Wei because they have online ordering and a separate door/cashier for take out orders. It's perfect for a night when you want something quick and inexpensive. It's also a great alternative to the same ol' Chinese takeout menu. Once I discovered Pei Wei had pad thai, I've been hooked. The location in Tampa (Westshore) has some new sushi items and I'm hoping this location adds them soon.
Had the charbroiled oysters appetizer (amazing) and drumfish meal (great) highly recommend.  Have returned each time I've been to New Orleans since my first visit.  Can't get enough of the oysters!

We love the pizza here and think it is among the better pizzas in center city and surrounding areas.  Our favourites are simple: a few mushroom pies and a few margarita pies.  The dough is superb;  thin, light but with just the right amount of crunch to it here and there along with a nice "bubble" or few.  The staff is super friendly and while it can get busy, you can either go on off hours (which is awesome), or just know that it is worth the wait.  We sometimes just do take out... Pull up right in front and pick it up.  We are super happy to have Vetri's Pizza in this part of Center City.  Though my wife doesn't fully agree, I think there is sometimes a bit too much salt in the dough, but of course, she is correct :)  Vetri's Pizza:  100% worth checking out.

For the 2020 holidays we got some cakes and goodies here.  Excellent.  Nice big eclairs with semi crisp shells hours after purchase delicious plain butter cake that remained delicious a few days later, lovely cookies and pastry oh ms delicious chocolate cream pie wonderful thin crust delicious chocolate filling and dreamy real whipped cream on top.

Clearly this establishment uses top quality ingredients no bad aftertaste no heavy chemical artificial flavor.  We've been getting stuff here forever but dahm this place is (insert four year old slang phrase that old white guy uses to try to hang onto his rapidly disappearing middle age, sigh). Still, in my many years in Philly I'll say this place is perhaps better then terminis and is certainly at least eye to eye with Isgros.  That said Terminis does a cannoli TO FING DIE FOR.  There is a bit of apples to oranges here all three are great.  Pretty good company for some place in Rockledge huh?

None of the ice blended drinks come with espresso and the coffee and latte quality is not good. The only reason why I go here from time to time is because it is convenient. The happy hour pastries are an awesome deal. But I really wish they would improve their coffee and espresso quality.

Do not order delivery from this place. I put an order and literally waited for 2 hours and it still didn't leave he store. I have ordered take out from here. So annoying. Either hire more delivery drivers to deliver your pizzas or stop delivery if you can't live up to expectations.

Very friendly staff.  Good place for locals to take a break while at Clearwater Beach.  The food is reasonably priced and the drinks are good.  Tap beer is $3.00 a pint or you can get a whole pitcher for $7.95.

We had a great experience with Damian and his crew.  He was very consultative and made a huge effort to trim the trees exactly as we wanted.  They were also efficient so great value (and craftsmanship) overall.

We made a reservation for 7:30. We arrived and were quickly seated. We were given menus and I selected a filet mignon while my husband selected a porterhouse steak. We also ordered sautéed mushrooms. We asked for the fried ravioli appetizer and it arrived in good time.  I took one bite and did not like it. I was expecting cheese filling and it was a meat filling. In fact it tasted like a meatball. My husband said they were very good. However I did not like the taste and did not eat any more of them.
My husband ordered ceasar salad and shared it with me.  It was delicious. We both felt it was the best ceasar salad we had ever had.
Next came our meals.  I had asked for rare with the butter sauce on the side. Instead it was poured over it.  It was very heavy on the garlic side.  Our waitress asked how I liked it and I told her.  She took it back to the kitchen. The owner or maybe his wife came by and apologized and asked if there was something else she could get me while I waited.  I said no because I had a beautiful baked potato in front of me.  I had maybe taken 2 bites of potato and my new steak arrived.  It was perfect.  My husband said his steak was very good. He also had the baked potato. We both ordered the potato with butter and sour cream as well as the bacon with cheese toppings.  All of this was very good.  The service was wonderful.  We ordered desert to go - tiramiso and whiskey cake. They look yummy. I could only find one very tiny flaw and that is some of the foods were a little too salty. One interesting item is the light in the middle of the room.  It is a Dale Chuhuly  piece according to management. Very beautiful.  There is also an extensive wine cellar, which we did not take advantage of. It was a nice touch to this restaurant. Overall, a very nice dinner.

Without going into all the gory details allow me to say that it has now been over five weeks since my first contact with Xfinity.  I have had the same promise  three times, and three canceled install appointments.  They are now willing to reschedule me for February 8th with the same assurances that I had three previous times.  Do yourself a favor and go to anywhere else or just update your library card.  Xfinity has the worst customer service department I have ever experienced.  They should not be allowed to operate wihout cable competition in all of their markets. Where are our political regulators at and why do they allow Xfinity to hold us hostage?

We had an absolutely terrible experience from start to finish on the lease of our vehicle. After many years of experience in purchasing and leasing cars from other dealerships, it was shocking to us how dishonest the sales staff is at Sloane Toyota. I cannot stress enough how unprofessional and misleading one of their sales people (Rick) was in how he handled his business. He will say anything to get a deal done. Unfortunately for us, even though we knew the type of salesman he was within minutes of meeting him, we still believed we were getting a good deal and now know that we were basically scammed into getting into a lease that would inevitably put us under water by the end. Even after speaking with a manager who said he was willing to make up for what we went through, he in the end wasn't even willing to help if it didn't help his own bottom line.  My advice is to make the extra drive to Del Toyota or another dealership if you are looking to purchase a Toyota.

This was my first visit to Cancun Mexican Restaurant.  My friend Marty and I decided to check them out.  I had the fish tacos, which were okay, but I'd have preferred them not being breaded and fried.  My friend had the Burrito Del Mar and he seemed to really enjoy it.

HUGE margaritas on the drink special and we were quite buzzed and stuck around after we finished eating so we could get home safely.

We'll be back.

i think bomb vegan places are hard to find...or maybe san diego just lacks the amazingness of such places.  ergo, my idea of a "bomb" vegan restaurant may not match that of a vegan restaurant connoisseur.  

beyond the high prices, this place was well, bomb.  the fact that the joint is an old house with an old gas oven being used as display art makes it even more bomb.  the lounge-like seating adds a bit more bombness.

then comes the food.  still bomb.  now, as a meat eater, i like my animal proteins.  the vegan kfc fried gardein was spectacular; yes, i can tell it's not really chicken, but gosh darn it was good.  the "beef" sandwich uses jackfruit as the meat replacement, and the barbecue sauce smothering the jackfruit pieces was awesome: sweet, slightly spicy, and peppery.  

although we didn't have any, there was a bunch of vegan cookies and pastries.  if only i wasn't so full off of vegan goodness.

I had stayed in Circus Circus Hotel at Reno a few times the only thing, I like about Circus Circus is the free Circus Shows that they have all day & all night. I also like the a big selection of carnival games that they have at CIrcus Circus.
Some of the best Chinese in the area. Solid Rangoons, General Tso's and really any other dish is good.

Love seeing shows at this venue. Wish the bar and food situation was better. Small plastic cups of expensive mediocre beer served by not so friendly bartenders. This place has so much potential to be awesome. Hint..... 
Hire music lovers or musicians as bartenders.... a lot of super friendly musicians also bartend to pay their bills.

First off this place is gorgeous. I love the design of the inside and the vamped patio. It's a great space to enjoy a drink with friends. We went during a week night and there were about 7 tables. Only 1 server so we sat waiting about 10 minuets to order drinks. 

Many items were not available the pies advised and unfortunately the menu changed and not for the better. We got the devour menu. I had the shrimp and my husband had the fries as an appetizer. 

The shrimp was extremely salty and I love salt. The fries were what you'd expect but came with three different dipping sauce. I ordered the hot chicken sandwich which let me tell is actually spicy. I did enjoy the actual chicken breast but found the honey was too sweet on the sandwich would've been better on the side. My husband got the grilled cheese which lacked cheese. He didn't enjoy it. Unfortunately we didn't get dessert as they were all out. I really wanted the sugar cream pie. Service was really lacking. We never got silverware plates or napkins. Went the whole meal without a drink refill.

Five stars. At first I was a little suspicious, but I had vegetables and chicken and it was amazing. It was a little bit of a longer wait, but I can't blame them for having business. Overall, Food was great, Price was cheap, and the female waiters were hot

Dawn Baker! I hsv3e made many check-Ins for this establishment...however,  Dawn Baker made me bring out the review. Now, sure this is a chain restaurant and the food is basic  and etc...but Dawn baker made our experience here more than regular.  Ok let me say this...it was not as packed in here and honestly i was not expecting  this GREAT of service to my kid and I here. She had soooo much patience  and she was knowledgeable  about  the menu. However,  she spoke to my kid like he was an adult  and even msde great suggestions  to him. She knew how to handle all her customers.  She had me at $5 margaritas and even managed to get in a side conversation  about the weather and flying  cockroaches that terrified her since moving down from michigan. She checked on my food constantly and made sure  our drinks were full , food was ordered and that was comfortable.  She was honest  on what they did not have on the menu today and she sold me on stuff they did. It's  been cool to be here with a Superb waittress. She really made my time here enjoyable!

This little place is really nice if you want simple, easy, and tasty food and coffee.  The owners are really friendly and make a lot of the cream cheese spreads themselves. The salt bagel on Sundays with lox is worth is great!

Went to The Buck Hotel tonight to celebrate  my mom's birthday. Our server, Brooke, was very attentive, friendly and professional.

The meals were delicious, however,  I was unable  to cut the steamed asparagus with  my table knife. Also, they do not offer free refills on the soda which in my opinion  is unacceptable when the price for my meal alone was $46.00.

Nicely decorated for Fall. (Inside and out)

Best pizza on planet earth! There are a tremendous pizzas shops that say authentic NY Pizza and 9 out of 10 LIE! This place is the REAL DEAL! First one in Brooklyn and they have a franchise in Tampa! What sets them apart is the coal fired brick oven, thin crust, fresh buffalo mozzarella, sauce, coin pepperoni. A must for any pizza aficionado or novice.
Authentic Chao Zhou Restaurant in St. Louis

Morning coffee and a bagel here is the best! $1.99 coffees and bagels!  They offer coffee on tap every morning with the following milk options: soy, oat, almond, and possibly cashew. 

Other breakfast options include breakfast burritos, pancakes, waffles, eggs (most styles are offered), donuts, and more! My favorite on the menu includes their brûlée grapefruit and donuts. I believe the grapefruit is the same dish that's offered upstairs. 

The service is consistent. They seem to always be understaffed on weekdays and especially on weekends. It may take more than 15 minutes to get served, but I'm usually not in a rush to get anywhere when I'm here. 
They have online order options - if you're in a hurry that morning, it maybe best to order ahead for pickup.

Our intrepid group of 12 women on a bachelorette party greatly enjoyed our Manchac Kayak tour with Mike. Mike was a fantastic guide and taught us so much about the ecology and history of the area. Pickup was so easy and we had a great time. Highly recommend!

Good basic noodles and rice dishes, nothing special but nothing bad either.  

Had to work a late shift and went to the Bakery for a let night dinner.  The crowd there is an eclectic mix of people from all walks of life, there were a few trendy young partiers there, a few families with young ones, a few gentlemen who have seen the bad side of the tracks, a few city workers who just got off work...  

Service wise, those ladies who work there are super fast but may be a tad on the rude side.  There was no "hi, how are you..."  you walk in, they motion you silently towards an empty table, generic tea and menu got placed on your table.  They reappeared when you are ready to order; food were brought out quickly but dont plan on seeing them again for the rest of the meal.  Do not expect fine dinning service there.  Avoid using bathrooms too, the non working fixtures and foul odour can be a little offensive.

Ordered the $7 Beef Brisket Lo Mein and the Seafood Chow Mein.  The Beef brisket Lo Mein came super fast, the beef was nice and saucy, tender and tasty, the boiled lettuce... not so good.  The accompanying clear broth was pretty tasty.  I would definitely give this dish 4 out of 5 stars.  Some better veggie would bring this up to 5 stars for sure.

The $12 Seafood Chow Mein came later.  The noodles were very chrispy, a plus.  Lots and lots of bok choi...  should have called in bok choi chow mein.  Seafood was scarce, 4 shirmps, half a scallop and 2 pieces of squid.  The sauce was blend, needed soya (rare that I add soya to anything) and chili (I always add chili to everything  :-)).  Definitely not the best chow mein I have ever tasted, only gets 2 out of 5 stars here.

Portion wise, both dishes were huge, great value for the money.

The average for me is 3 stars out of 5 for food.  Would I come back, yeah, great place to come for a late night snack or late dinner.  But I probably wont order the Seafood Chow Mein again, I will try something else.

My wife and I had lunch here this week while entertaining a couple from out of town. And while I've been here many times before, on a day with wonderful weather, the patio dining was truly phenomenal. The staff has always been friendly and welcoming, and every time I've had a meal here, the food has been superb. Tammy and I shared the escargot and the French onion soup as appetizers and we had the soft shell crab for and the blanquette de veau (braised veal medallions in a wild mushroom and white wine cream reduction with baby carrots in a puff pastry shell - sorry little cows!) as main courses.   The food was incredible and the best part was the bill. Since Café Degas is a participating restaurant on the Restaurant.com web site, I was able to purchase a $50.00 off of $100.00 gift certificate at restaurant.com's annual 90% off sale for $5.00. With dessert, and the cost of the gift certificate, our $132.00 bill (inclusive of tax and a 20% tip) was reduced to just $82.50 (just over $20.00 per person)! And while I certainly don't eat out like this every day, it is great to have such a wonderful meal at such a cozy romantic venue for a really competitive price. I think the best compliment I can include here is that our friends from out of town who'd been eating there way through some of the city's most notable eateries for four solid days both stated that their meal at Degas was hands down the best food they'd eaten yet in New Orleans.

Our water heater died on us yesterday and today, before 11:00 AM, we have a lovely new one.  That's how fast this company is.  The owner/plumber showed up early.....that's right...EARLY for our appointment...oh and by the way...they didn't give us a window of "we will be there anywhere between 8:00 AM and 6:00 PM" we were given an actual arrival time of 9:00 AM and he was early!  Sorry but I had no hot water so this was a big deal for me.  He was very clean, professional and very personable.   He got to work right away and got the job done quick.  He left the area cleaner than when he started and even upgraded some things for us and included it as part of the installation.  The price I paid was the price that was quoted (which was very reasonable and lower than some other quotes we had received).  What a great experience...so much so we will definitely be calling this company again for some other jobs we have planned in the future.  If you need a plumber, I highly recommend Joshua Tree Plumbing.  Alright bye you guys I gotta go take a shower.

One word sums up my visit to dining at the Buck Hotel: PUTRID.  That word would apply to service and especially the food.  To say the food is mediocre would be an understatement.  Everything that we ordered was awful. First, our waitress had a nonchalant attitude, showing hints of impatience and surliness. Whose fault is it that you're working there, missy?   

The Happy Hour menu consists of $6 items, most of which are normally priced at $7 anyway so no real deal there.  The food was unbelievably awful and I can't believe any restaurant would try to pass it off as being edible, much less tasty.  Each dish was worse than the previous one.  The pizza was tasteless and seemingly made with those 4-in-a-pack make-it-yourself pizza kits with the dried out pizza crust.  Even pizza made in a lightbulb-cooked EZ Bake Oven tastes better than this!  

From the tavern menu, we ordered lobster egg rolls which were obviously cooked in too-low heat and were crunchy on the outside but were dripping in oil with each bite.  As Clara Peller would say about the beef, I wanted to shout "where's the damn lobster?!?"  There was not one shred of lobster present in these oily, soggy and tasteless egg rolls - perhaps they were still in the kitchen or at the store?  The overall taste of these egg rolls suggested that they were fried several times over.  That $10 cost could have tasted better spent on Mrs. Paul's frozen seafood, something that I normally eschew.  

Clams in white sauce seemed promising.  We asked to buy extra bread to sop up the sauce and the waitress retorted that it would cost extra.  WTF.  Um, we didn't ask her to make, knead and bake the bread herself, we asked wanted to BUY extra bread, for chrissakes! 

We also made the mistake of ordering the $16 appetizer sampler from the tavern menu which was quite a scant dish for the price.  The chicken fingers were tasteless and bland, the buffalo wings were of the frozen variety and the onion rings were a joke - not a single onion ring had a complete layer of coating.  Rather, the coating was all broken and in pieces, an obvious sign of being freezer burned.
