The french fries were scant but the most edible thing on the plate, albeit ridiculously expensive given the other crap on this dish. 

Our food was ordered from the happy hour and tavern menus.  I wouldn't dare order from the ridiculously expensive dinner menu given the quality of the food at this establishment.  I'm surprised that they're still in business!  Their calendar shows dance nights and music during midweek, ostensibly to attract the cougars and geezers who most likely care more about landing their next fling than the food.  

The Buck Hotel is definitely not a place I would recommend spending/wasting my hard earned money. The only thing which could have made this dining experience even worse would have also been afflicted with explosive diarrhea or other gastrointestinal distress, which didn't happen but I was definitely afraid, very afraid.  Save your money and eat the week-old leftovers from your refrigerator - believe me, it would certainly taste better!

My previous lunch experiences at Chateau West have been good. I especially enjoyed the fondue options, quiche of the day, and the onion soup. However my son and I went to Chateau West on Mother's Day for dinner. the first thing I noticed is that the prices have gone up significantly (I know that dinner prices are inflated but, even the options from the lunch menu have gone up.)
We got escargot and onion soup appetizers which were both good. I ordered whole Chilean Sea Bass as my entree and my son ordered lamb chops. The lamb had a sauce that was strange but we decided that we liked it except for the hard shells in it. The sauce had dates but I was puzzled by the "shells" that looked like the outside like dates. It was puzzling and they were hard enough to break a tooth. The bass was de-boned at the table and it was clear from the get-go that the person didn't really know how to do this. The head was cut off, the fish split in half length-wise, then the trouble began. The flesh was scrapped off the backbone into a mushy pile on both sides then heaped on the plate. There wasn't much; it was unappetizing, and had pin bones in almost every bite. Both entrees were served with a veggie combo which was tasty but just dumped on the plate. We ordered one glass of wine and one beer and the bill came to over $100. Not sure that I would go back even for lunch.

This place is not the worst option..BUT wouldn't be my first choice.

Came here for a business launch with coworkers for Cinco De Mayo. They did a great job accommodating our group of 10. The service was slow in the beginning but later picked up. They started us off with the typical complimentary chips and salsa. Chips was fresh and hot and the salsa was great. It tasted authentic with the right amount of heat. I like that they gave each person their own salsa bowl.  

I ordered the Fajitas Mariscos. (tilapia, shrimp, and scallops)  I really didn't enjoy it much. They over sautéed the veggies making them all brown and mushy. It's was also saturated with the "sizzling" oil on the hot skillet causing it to cook further and even burn the bottom pieces a bit. This place was given a solid 3 star consensus around the the table.  The group did order a few pitchers of margaritas that seem to be a hit.

3 (excellent) F's: fast, friendly and super filling.

Adel himself works super hard behind the counter and has always been in a good mood to see me. He even joked and laughed as we checked out, complimenting us. We ordered on the phone about 10 minutes before we got there, and the food was ready to go! 

The food is delicious - hot, freshly cut beef and lamb on top of a warm pita with cucumber yogurt and fresh veggie sides. There's definitely enough to fill you up for an entire night. I had a traditional gyro for dinner, attended CarmelFest and woke up the next morning full (and craving more Adel's). 

As mentioned in previous reviews, the interior could certainly use some work. But don't let that deter you! While this isn't the restaurant to go for a nice dinner, it's a quick delicious and cheap place for lunch and friendly service. Plus, to go is always an option!
Great zoo. The apes have nice exhibits. The Insectarium is awesome. The grounds are lush. Lots of places for kids to play. All for free!

We had a great waiter named Tony. I was excited about the calamari, but it was diappointing. There were only about 5 rings and a bunch of thin fried carrot strips. The lobster claws were also disappointing. They had no flavor and in the future I'll stick with shrimp.

Nothing fabulous about the place. Prices are a bit higher than any other smoothie place. I order a small smoothie that cost me 7$ - a bit pricey for a very small size. The service is decent, took them long to prepare the smoothie and they were not busy. It's vegetarian friendly.

I ordered a very simple large cheese pizza two hours later after two calls I was told it would not be coming. I was told that the driver was there which was baloney cause I left the front door open with the screen door closed I was sitting in the living room and I have a dog so if anyone came to the door I would've known about it very easily. A major disappointment,  I was home sick I couldn't leave to go out and get anything it was a nightmare and all I got was excuses and lies I won't use them again.

I like this place. Would go there more often if parking wasn't such a bitch.

Lots of options to choose from. Some low-fat, some high-fat, etc. If you like smoothies, you should find something that you like here.

I've always found the service to be pretty quick. They usually have 2-3 people working behind the counter.
Super friendly, knowledgeable, and affordable. Plus, they accept Careington 500 dental discount!

We got a burger here with jalapeños and my fiancé got bbq sandwich with Mac and cheese and beans. Everything was tasty any my fries were delicious. Service was quick and my waitress was pretty funny kept us entertained throughout the evening. I would certainly come back again.

Food was bland. I asked for the "special" which was curry. I got shrimp and vegetables. I will say kudos for the yummy shrimp and the portion size of shrimp was great. However, the curry was bland! I had to add salt, and spicy sauce just to add some flavor to eat it. It didn't really taste like curry either. I would have gone to Thai or Indian if I were to order curry again. Also, my meal comes with soup, egg roll, and chicken wing. I don't eat meat other than fish, so when I asked if I could substitute, I was told no. They could have just switched the egg roll with a spring role in my opinion.

The reason for a star up from a one, is that the service was friendly, and the place was clean and nice.
Felt like a dream. Environment is so luxurious and fancy, you won't even notice your food.
Friendly staff and good food! I like their salsa it's better than any I have found in the area. The nachos are HUGE!
Great Indian.  Tops Tiffin in my opinion.  Better flavor, larger portions.  Definitely my go-to spot.

I called Fidelity several times about a work order over the course of several weeks, and they kept saying they would call me back. They never did call me back. Now I am thinking of closing my account that I have had for over 15 years, and am very disappointed. Please do not do business with them.
Best happy hour in town. Great food. Awesome people. All the bar sports including volleyball! The day wilhagans closes is the day i move outta Nashville!!

We love this spot for incredible fresh and delicious egg dishes. Their baked goods are great and their coffee is sublime.

I highly recommend D"Angelo's Bakery!
Great to find a nice family-run restaurant with pride of their home country. Pork was outstanding
Great service, tasty latte and croissant. Nice music, perfect volume. Wish there were larger tables available...
I agree with everyone else.  This is a great addition to Fishtown.  The sandwiches are high quality on fantastic bread with fresh and tasty ingredients.  The owners are very nice and friendly.

Discriminates against paying LGBTQ+ customers and paying homeless customers. Video proof a couple reviews down. Disgusting way to treat customers. 
Not to mention, food often comes out incorrect and/or cold. Not worth the money. Can't attest to the drinks since I've always been the DD while visiting here, but apparently they're about the same quality as the food. 
Don't bring your business here if you value your money and morals.

Owner Laurie Petrolino just made the most 
Beautiful and delicious 50th birthday cake. 
It was a hand sculptured Buddha on top
Of a triple layer cake with custard and strawberry filling. She delivered it to a packed house at the employees lounge 
At Kamran & company and what a wonderful
Surprise. This cake was 5 stars!! 
I would use them again without hesitation. 

Erica Salda 
805.679.3776
SBTSR.com

The food is good but they were out of a lot of stuff. They didn't tell us that they were out of the kind of chicken we ordered until after we paid which was kind of annoying but the employee was there alone and very nice. He gave us a few extra pieces of the one kind of chicken they weren't put of and apologized for the inconvenience.
I was also sad that they were out of their edible cookie dough bites. I have had them before and they are so good!

Overall the customer service and the food is good, it was just frustrating that they were out of so much so early in the morning

After hearing about this Deli from my German friends I was finally able to check it out.
Very happy they opened this place. Now I can get my specialties from them. They have Rollmops ( marinated hering ) one of my favorites. They also have quite a good selection of bottled beer and groceries.
I tried their cold Leberkäs which was really yummy. I had the Hackfleisch ( basically a larger spiced hamburger patty ) with potatoes and mushroom gravy. It tasted really good  but I was expecting the Hackfleisch to be made from mixed pork and beef, this one was only beef. I my opinion the pork would make it tastier.
Tomorrow is ribs and sauerkraut day, hope I can make it.

We came in with our family circus and the staff could not have been friendlier and more accommodating. They brought fresh cut strawberries and bananas for the kids!  Food was outstanding and the service was even better. Added benefit if you're there for happy hour $5 margaritas hit the spot!

Good to see this Walmart is STILL CRAPPY. 
Utterly mismanaged and truly a disgrace. 
It's 8pm on Sunday and every checkout line is 10 people long and only 5 open. Managers standing around joking with each other like no care in the world and not about to open any other lanes??

The self checkout is an 18 people long line.

Why does this Walmart suck so much every time I go??

Friday's = time to try a new restaurant for lunch. Bonus, new lunch buddy! We decided to check out Cerdo Tacos + Tequila, sister restaurant to the uber popular Jack's Burger Shack because Mexican options are few and far in between this oasis we call Edmonton. Okay, technically Cerdo's is in St. Albert but come on, it's pretty much the same.

We arrived around 12:15pm and the place was packed. We had to sit in the bar area but eventually were able to snag a table as the lunch rush slowly filed out. Man, I don't know if this is normally the case but service was slooooow. Took forever for us to order and even longer to get our food. I don't normally complain (that much) about service but we only ordered two dishes!

Here's a breakdown of their menu along with prices (starters, huevo bowls and tacos). Seriously, I really, really, REALLY don't like when restaurant websites don't list their prices.

- Nachos ($13.50) with the Saltroom's chorizo ($3.00) - corn tortilla chips, jack cheese, tomato, green onion, jalapeno with sour cream and salsa as dipping sauces. The chips were crunchy and had that satisfying bite to it but the cheese could have used some better layering skills (it pretty much was all clumped into the middle). The jalapenos weren't spicy at all but gave the dish some colour. We added some chorizo - a tad expensive for a few pieces of chorizo, although it was tasty. The nachos were decent but we felt like you could make these at home pretty easily. Factor in the price, it's definitely overpriced for what it is.

- Chicken al pastor ($9.00) - guajillo marinated chicken, pineapple, cilantro and radish. The tacos come in pairs, topped with cabbage and onion and with your choice of either corn (gluten free) or flour tortillas. I don't understand their choice of offering two tortillas over the standard three. 

Given that, the tortillas were stuffed with a good amount of chicken. Cilantro was fresh. Pineapple was itty bitty pieces - a few small sliced pieces would have gave it a nice contrasting sweetness. The chicken wasn't bad. Overall, the taco was missing something - a kick to it, a Mexican hot sauce or something like that would have helped. We didn't see the hot sauce bottle until after we ate the whole taco though. Oops, our bad but they should have had something in it originally!

It's nice to see a more diverse selection of food options available in St. Albert. I know they have mad love for chains over here so I applaud the owners for bringing something new and different. I will have to try a few more dishes to see if my opinion changes of Cerdo's. As for now, decent spot but not my first choice in town when I'm craving Mexican food.

Franklin Manor is the ONLY fun place is Tampa! There is always great music and perfectly made cocktails to enhance the experience. I always try new places around town but ALWAYS end up at Manor. I highly recommend trying it out day or night. It never disappoints. 5+ stars!

I have been visiting the area for 4 weeks for work and have ordered from here once a week so far. China 1 is easy to order, online or by phone, and the delivery is VERY fast, usually about 20 minutes. Have had Beef & Broccoli, Sweet & Sour Chicken, Wonton Soup, Fried Rice, White Rice and vegetables. Everything has been delivered hot and delicious. With enough for leftovers for the next night. As often I travel, and try foods across the country, this is a great fast Chinese restaurant.
The food here is AMAZING!!!  The service is exceptional.   Don't miss out !!!!  This place has service, price, drinks & ambience.

Disgusting place, workers are trashy and disrepectful. The employees have no manners and are extremely unwelcoming. What more can you expect from a place employed by new port itchy scum. Will not be back here!

I had some friends recommend Thunder Mugs as a nice, local coffee joint. The type of place you could pop in for your daily coffee.

We wanted to try somewhere new and you're able to order online. We did an order of some coffee and pastries. I had an iced mocha, a cheese danish, and a croissant. Coffee was delicious. Nice chocolate flavor. The croissant and cheese danish had a perfect pastry. The cheese danish was a little light on the cheese, but so good! Definitely going to be coming back!

I'll begin by saying that I used the mobile service, not the repair shop.  I have used this service in the past and had great results.  Unfortunately, my most recent experience was horrible!  The technician came and spent about 20 minutes supposedly repairing a circular crack caused by a truck kicking up a bolt.  In the past, the crack was invisible after repair.  In this case, it looked as if he didn't repair it at all!  The crack was still completely visible with the exception of one small section.  The technician also punctured the windshield with several "entry" holes and there were globs of resin visible in various spots all around the crack.  When I questioned him, the tech said that he had to make the holes to access the crack and the resin residue was part of the process.  I told him that I could still see the crack and that this would never pass inspection.  He stated that if it doesn't pass, I would receive a credit of the cost toward a windshield replacement, which is about 6x the cost of the repair.  The areas around the "entry" holes that the technician created now have multiple cracks feathering out.  They guarantee that the crack will not spread.  When I called customer service to complain, I was told that I could have my windshield replaced and they would credit the repair cost back.  When I reminded them about their guarantee that the crack wouldn't spread, the woman I spoke to said because the new cracks were near the old ones, there was nothing they could do.  HUH?  So my initial crack was roughly the size of a quarter and located on the bottom of the windshield near the dashboard--not requiring an entire replacement!  But now my only choices are to live with this mess, or replace the entire windshield now, proactively, or later once the cracks they caused spread and get much worse.  Buyer beware!  If you have an incompetent technician, you will wind up paying a fortune for a small repair that turns into a windshield replacement.  I will NEVER use them again!  Too bad they lost a repeat customer.

I have been coming here for years! I've seen three different owners.

The individuals who do the pedicures take their time and don't zip through it like there's a prize at the end of the finish line. I love the massage chairs. I usually get the sea salt pedi.

I usually go to Chow or her husband. They take their time and listen to what I want and sometimes I don't even have to tell them, they just know. 

Although I have great experiences here, I didn't give them a full 5 stars due to how I have seen other customers get serviced. I have seen people walk in for appointments and are waiting 15 mutes or more after their scheduled appointment-unacceptable. I have also seen people walk out because they don't always get greeted when they walk in, sometimes I wanna say hello to the customer myself.
Had an Asian chicken salad with peanut sauce. Big ups to the young lady that made it.

My girlfriend & I had a really nice time together at this winery. The weather was wonderful so we sat outside & ordered a bottle of wine, got cheese & crackers & finished with a crab cake sandwich from the food truck! 
Our wine was almost gone when we were told that our time was up. We knew we had 1 & 3/4 hours of time allowed but there were many tables empty & there was no line of people waiting, so we didn't rush...until the manager came by to remind us that our time was up. Kinda weird that we were spending more money as we sat but I guess a time limit was more important. Tons of empty tables there!
So not a big fan of this policy. Will try other wineries to see how their policies are.
Food has been great every time I've been here. Love their margaritas. Great service. Nice atmosphere. Will visit this restaurant regularly!

My visit to your restaurant on November 9 with a friend to celebrate our birthdays was a perfect spot. Everything we experienced  from start to finish was fabnk  for a wonderful place to have a celebration!

One of the worst places I've ever been to watch a movie!! My party of 3 went to the movies on 7/30/17, my partner & I got the two for $25. The first item that came out was popcorn, the popcorn was not even hot at all. Like how do they serve popcorn in a movie theater that not hot? We sent the popcorn back 3 different times the final time I asked if we could substitute the popcorn for a different entrée the server said yes, I then asked to speak with a manager before the movie had started because it was super crazy that they could not deliver hot popcorn. The manager came out during the the first half of the movie and tried to ask what was going on I asked if we can settle the issue after the movie was over  he agreed and the rest of the food came out (after over 30mins of the time the order was placed), my partner asked for another honey mustard with the chicken tenders that came a hour after we received the food. The movie ended and I was looking for a manager to speak with about my bill and how the food was,the manager had us in the back of the theater talking to us about the bill he was not apologetic and was not trying to rectify the bill. People where leaving and looking at us like we tried to skip out on our bill  which was very embarrassing. This place needs new management, the hallways smelled horrible the restrooms are very unsanitary. I will never go back to this "place of business " ever again and I hope you as the reader won't either!!!

Have you ever gone to Medieval Times in N. Jersey or where ever the hell else that place is located and have reenactors massacre each other  to vie for your attention? 

Yea, ... City Tavern is just like that, but without all the bloodlust... or fun.

You basically eat and drink and watch people dolled up in colonial garb NOT murder each other.

Honestly, I felt shame walking out the door, did you feel shame?
Came to Amanda needing a major color correction and she fit me in right away even though she was completely booked. All the girls in the salon helped "fix" my hair and Ill keep coming back

Took my wife here for her birthday this past Sunday with my two daughters. First, the food is outstanding but there are several things that separate it from either a four star or a five star (which, I might add, they could certainly achieve). We both had the Lasagna with Lamb and it tasted as if it were made in a restaurant in Italy (if you've been to Italy you know that food is incredible every where you go). I believe it was the best Lasagna I've had. We paired it with an excellent wine from Italy (my apologies for not remembering the name). The appetizer was excellent as well. This is where it begins and unfortunately this is where it ends.
The presentation of the food was not the best (there was this very good sauce poured over the Lasagna but it looked like someone just poured over the Lasagna if you know what I mean). Food was still great but the presentation was - meh. Next the noise level was just too difficult to carry on a decent conversation. I know we had a large party near us but it's the brick walls and the narrow seating area that bounces the sound back and forth. This is not a place for an intimate dinner conversation. Next the service - wow, they need lessons in service. Not only were they not attentive, they didn't want to be. One of my daughters was running a bit late and we had some questions about the specials. The waiter curtly addressed this issue by firmly stating he didn't want to waste his time and repeat himself so we would have to wait. Then he went away. Huh?! Is this a restaurant or a garage where I was getting my car fixed? The person who seated us took our payment and acted in a very similar manner (they looked to be related so perhaps the waiter was trained this way). 
Great food is the beginning of a great restaurant but there are a lot of restaurants that have great food in Tampa. What it now needs is a more caring approach to serving the customer from the presentation of the meal to the acoustics to the service. Then this might be a five star restaurant. I'm not sure if they have takeout but I would encourage you to try this route as you can supply your own great service and ambiance.

Took my son for graduation and the rest of the family. I was impressed they had Habachi bar made to order,Sushi bar, carve your own BBQ ribs and carve your own Prime Ribs all the seafood you ever want shrimp Blue crabs,mussels,clams,oysters and more. The best thing I liked was BYOB would go back and would recommend it. My family enjoyed it as well.

This was my first and last time going to red lobster. I ordered my meal for curbside pickup (took 20 min for them to come out) but this wasn't a big deal. The lobster and steak quality was so bad I could only take two bites from the lobster. Truly disappointed with this experience.

We've lived down the street from here for months now and finally decided to stop in. I'm not much of a bar person, or really a fan of drinking, but my boyfriend is and they do have the one drink I really like (Ace Pineapple Cider) on draft, so it wasn't a hard sell to get me to go. 

The parking lot in front of and around the side of the bar is really small, and the spots out front are super tight because of the parallel parking spots behind them. I was lucky to find a spot on the side. They do have overflow parking down the street according to the bartender, but I'm not exactly sure where. There's also a bike rack. 

The inside is spacious with lots of seats at the bar. There's a pool table, darts and board games. They had a handful of TVs around with hockey and basketball, so its probably a pretty good place to catch a game. There was definitely a super laid back vibe. 

They let you sample any of the drafts, which is good because there are a lot on the menu that I'm guessing are either from local breweries or just not well known. A lot of weird names. 

They don't have any real food at the bar itself, but they do have snacks. The people next to us were obsessed with one of the popcorns they sell - something with chocolate and caramel, I think. We bought some cookies to go, which were just alright. They are vegan, which wasn't advertised. 

We didn't venture out to the patio, but there was a food truck when we went on a Friday night, so if the popcorn and cookies isn't enough, you can grab a bite on the patio. 

They're also a pet friendly bar, even inside the actual bar instead of just on the patio!
The best barbeque ever!  Their Brisket Mac is to die for.  Thank you Wilson's!!

Saturday night, 5/2/15, 8 pm -
They "lost our reservation" after THEY CALLED TO CONFIRM our reservation this afternoon.  Party of 6 and a very special occasion.  WE watched a lot of people come in behind us and be seated right away. They really weren't the least bit concerned.  This is what happens when your business is turned over to a bunch of posturing young people.  Finally offered us a table OUTSIDE.  No one else was seated outside after the temp began to drop.

In all fairness, the service was excellent and attentive.  It's as if the waitstaff sensed our disappointment.

But, no apologetic manager appeared.  And there should have been one.  

Caesar salad very small and ordinary, worthy of a fast-food place.  Calamari very good, but really didn't need to be drizzled with 2 other sauces..  12-oz. prime rib really no better than Outback at 2/3 the price.  Definitely not in the same league with top steakhouses.  Won't be back, ever.

11/21/15
Returned for my birthday (surprise from the kids) and everthing was terrific!

We have been ordering pizza on Fridays from this Dominoes for several years.  Two weeks ago we did NOT have a great experience... slow delivery, cold pizza but decided that it must be a fluke.  This week we ordered and it was a HORRIBLE experience.  First order was late so I called and asked about it before it arrived.  It arrived COLD and WRONG. My daughter is GF and that pizza wasn't there.  Called again, manager was kind but would not hear that anything had gotten worse and said food will be right out.  An HOUR later food was not delivered called and it was sitting at the restaurant as a carry out and the manager had left. When we picked up our food (again this was suppose to be delivery) there were no kind words, and the order was wrong again.  We did get my daughters gluten free pizza finally. We are done with you GV Dominoes.

The food here was over priced and poor quality. They nickle and dime you for everything. An alleged combo which included 5 small dry wings and hard wedges with flat fountain drink was $9.38. In addition the lady at the counter appears to hate her job. She actually discourages customers. I asked about the sides and saw that it had sweet corn on the menu, she said"just to let you know, its canned corn so dont get your hopes up thinking its on the cobb". This was my 1st and last time here.

I brought in a car for a pre-purchase inspection.  Things they missed after inspecting the car:

Broken motor mounts (how do you miss that!!)
Warped front rotors
Leaky A/C compressor
Leaky (front & rear) struts

Never would have bought the car had they actually done a thorough inspection.

I've been seeing Kim Kelly regularly for almost 20 years.  I've recently had an appointment, and I must say my face feels glorious after her treatments.  As importantly, my entire being feels refreshed and radiant.  She and Erin are the best of the best!  Not only are they the best aestheticians, they're very passionate about their work and they treat their clients with great care.  My skin feels heavenly.

Nancy G

My bf and I just moved to Santa Barbara a few months ago and Finneys is our Go-to! Their food and beer are ALWAYS great and we are never disappointed. Their prices are very reasonable compared to the rest of SB restaurants and we love that they have happy hour. Fun, great food, great beer and I would definitely recommend trying it out.

I have come into this store three times. Every single time I have left upset. 

Today I came in looking for a specific Eyeshadow palette. I didn't see it so I asked an associate if they were sold out or if they simply were online only, or had not gotten it in, and the employee shrugged at me and didn't say anything. I asked again and she said she didn't know. 

I then went to check out with a few small items I didn't really even need, and the associate helping me used my ulta rewards points without asking me if I wanted to. He said something about not having the birthday gift and threw "next months birthday gift" in instead. 
It is literally the second day of the month first of all. Second of all if they had said they were out I would have told them I didn't want the item and would have waited til the end of the month to see if anything else came at the tail end. I had my card on the table and he swiped it and was talking a mile a minute about it being the end of the night. When I saw he used the points, I told him I didn't want him to do that. And he got really disgruntled and then spent a few minutes returning and repurchasing the item, and after that said I would have to call customer service "to fix my mistake". Ok. 

Everyone has a bad day, and it was the end of the night. I get it. He seemed really nice just desperate to leave and there were still like 6 people in the store. But there is no reason to be pushy and act like your customer is putting you out when they are asking for something the store itself offers. I don't know it just really rubbed me the wrong way. 
From now on if I have to order from ulta I'm sticking to online orders. Every time I visit this location it stresses me out, but maybe someone else will catch them having a better day.
Good bar food!! Great location with outdoor seating and water views! Pricing is great too for the amount of food you get!

Went here in October 2008

They have a very knowledgeable waiters here. One thing which stood out was how much the waiter actually knew about Spanish culture. In conversation with him, we found out he had actually spent some time in Spain before working at Tinto. His knowledge lead to some excellent wine pairings and also some much needed explanations of what exactly we were eating.

The food was top notch. This is a restaurant by Jose Garcia, so the tapas style menu was in full swing. We were a group of 4 and we all got the tasting menu selection. This included 5 or 6 courses. The girl I was with happened to be a vegetarian who ate fish. It wasn't a problem to have fish substituted into the chef's tasting selections at no extra cost.
I did include a pork entree in with my selections, and I'm glad I did. It was phenomenal. 

I greedily ordered some squid in black ink after dinner. (not much of a desert guy) I'm sorry I did too. It was sub par, and it looked terrible. 

For the 4 of us, the meal ran a little over $400

pricey , but definitely worth it if your a fan of Jose Garcia

Ashly or something like this name, 6: 20pm at counter 8. 
The worst  cashier service I ever met. I dunno what was happened for her, but she hit so strongly on my foods, juices, waters, and vegetables. She also forgot count the  bottled water even I told her 2 times b4. The third time she take the bottled water out and forgot push them back, all of them fall on the floor when I went out. 
What's the basic skills of employees needs, that's attentiveness skill, Ability to Handle, and positive attitude. 
What's a nice person! 
Never come back!!!

Have been coming here for years. However this last visit the all you can eat chef was terrible. Pressured into to dinner all you can eat. Asked for a few special rolls and said it wasn't on the menu and it would cost extra. Blamed it on management. Other guests in our party experienced the same issue. Poor customer service. Hmmm...will NOT return.

The Tango has the best Bloody Mary! Being a Terrapin this Old Bay encrusted libation hits home. Garnished with olives lime and a slim jim makes this worth a trip downtown. If the Bloody isn't your thing the Cuban Missle or the Lemon cocktail will delight.
This Target is just awful. Might be worse than the one on Bustleton Ave. The merchandise seems to be thrown just anywhere. Have some pride, people.

Beautiful place to eat. I had the Cobb salad for lunch which was delicious! The salad was huge! I just wish they went heavier on the salad ingredients - especially the avocado. The service was great. 

I'd like to come back for dinner next time! The menu looked amazing!

I read nothing but great reviews online, and heard great reviews from one of my coworkers. So after not being able to get my nails done for FOUR YEARS because of the job I was working at, I made an appointment for 3pm on a Sunday, and I called and made the appointment on the Friday before. I got there about 30 minutes prior to my appointment, the lady at the front desk was super friendly, but I didn't get told where to pick my colors and what was where. So I didn't pick a color. At about 2:55 the guy calls me over and I asked him about colors. He took a deep sigh and said "you need to pick out your own colors." I explained to him that I hadn't had my nails done in years and I wasn't exactly sure how it worked. I went and picked my color and asked a few more questions about shape, whether or not they could be matte, etc. and he seemed bothered by every single question I asked. So I just kept my mouth shut. Then towards the end I asked if he could put a single rhinestone on my ring finger on each hand as an accent, to which he responded "I don't know. I have to go. I was supposed to pick up my son at 3 and now it's 3:30 and I'm still stuck here doing your nails." So I asked why someone else couldn't have done them, and he said not many people can do acrylic nails so he HAD to do them. Anyway, he finished them, and immediately left. I went and paid, got to the car, and was EXTREMELY dissatisfied with how they looked. The acrylic was uneven, the color was inconsistent, and by the time the evening rolled around, one of them had broken. So the next day I called the shop and told them what happened and asked them to just remove them for free. They agreed and told me to go in the next day. So I went in the following day. They welcomed me and asked me what happened, so I told them exactly what happened and showed them my broken nail. They said "okay sit down and we'll remove them." I waited for 25 minutes and watched as he took other people. Finally I just had to leave because I wouldn't have made it to work on time. I called again and told them what happened, and just asked for my money back, which they refused. It's disappointing because I waited a long time for this to happen and was left extremely unhappy.

Started off poorly as we called and the gal who answered had an attitude - "what do you want", lots of negative vibes in every detail of our simple order for two hamburgers with fries.  We picked the food up in the little van out front which was convenient, and at least the pick up person was pleasant.  The food.....not so great.  Just OK food, but it was hot and the fries were good.  Wouldn't go back.

Stopped in for a late snack or dinner, depending on your perspective. Salad with tea leaves was delicious and good to share, as the slight bitterness of the tea leaves builds up the more you eat.  Burmese noodles hit the spot and weren't too sweet. The thousand layer bread seems confusingly named, as it is really just a single layer fry bread.  Nonetheless tasty.  The curry sauce was way too spicy for more than a small taste, but your mileage may vary.  Prices made this a great value for the money.

 Service was friendly but informal.  We came in to a mostly empty place around 9pm and were told it would be a few minutes minutes. But then the server filled two water glasses at a table right in front and invites us to sit. No complaint here.

This place is awesome! GREAT food! GREAT service!

It's only been open for 3 days, and they already have it down! Had beers, nachos, and street tacos. Everything was fabulous! Our server, Adam, was friendly and fast.

We have enjoyed the State Street location for years, but this is much closer for us. We will be back over and over! Thank you for a great afternoon! Great job!

If you want the best, stick with someone who makes the best.  In my opinion, Ruth's Chris has perfect the steak.  Start with a Crabtini, it's the perfect combination of lump crabmeat, remoulade and a hint of lettuce, we weren't above eating every single bit of it, it was incredible.  I followed it up with a Petite Filet, cooked to perfection and melted in my mouth.  Be sure to add the broiled tomato and a side of asparagus, both are must haves whenever we go.  I had a house Cabernet and it was exactly what I was looking for.  Although we arrived later in the evening, the service was spectacular.  If you like to eat, you will love Ruth's.

I honestly have no clue as to how this location has 4 stars. The customer service is very unprofessional! 
I came into this place on 4/7/2021. The staff is super duper unprofessional, they are literally kids who do not know better and apparently does not see anything wrong with eating ice cream in your face as you are checking-out. The little girl did not even offer me a bag for the Quart of ice cream that I purchased, I had to ask for one... Who in the hell hired these people?!!!

okay, so the drinks are cheap and if you like to people watch (point and laugh) then this is the place for you. i don't know what it was but man, there were a lot of bachlorette parties going on. if you want to get trashed for cheap and have a bunch of nasty boys try to rub up against you one last time.. this might be the place for you.

oh.. and i don't know what what up with the DJ, seriously, if the record keeps skipping, you might not want to play that song.

meh.. i had a good time, but that's only because i spent most of the time attached to my boyfriend's hip. it help close out the nastiness around me.

This is a MUST Eat Place!!

Sure there a lot of people and noise. But they will welcome you up to the counter and make you feel at home. You have to try the chicken pot pie. It felt like it was made just for you. It a hard place to find in the market but you should seek this place out. You will find yourself wanting to come back for more.

Great selection of local crafts. Nice food menu. Brief menu, but a nice wide variety. Very chill. Friendly wait staff. They have live music too. Industrial wood/metal interior. Nice outdoor seating in the front and side. Would like table side service. This place is on the regular rotation.

Lack luster food and service.

Ordered a cobb with steak.  Oddly they ran out of steak.   Came out soaked in dressing with the chicken substitute that I agreed to but missing egg.   

Basically, get your booze here but avoid it for food

Dinner on a Tuesday in January with 2 others...we had perfectly made martinis at the bar, and then seated ourselves in the dining room. All three of us were excited to try Tod Wentz' newest project. It was a slow night, but the service was still very attentive. Food was great, I'd call it elevated bar food? We had the wings (actually ordered a second round toward the end of the meal bec they were so good), fried chicken sliders (2 per order), mussels in red curry broth (excellent), Agnolotti (only ok) one other item I'm drawing a blank on. For dessert, we tried the butterscotch budinos and beignets. Both were the perfect sweet ending. My main reason for only giving them 3 stars is that the atmosphere wasn't anything remarkable. I think there's a lot of potential for this place (which took over the old Irish Pub location), perhaps some improvements to aesthetics.

I feel anything I write here would not do my multiple experiences here any justice. Delicious. Delicious. Unique. Delicious. If you have not been to Helm, just go. Really. That's the only advice I can give... Oh wait, make sure to get a pasta and whatever pork dish they are serving. Personally, I would avoid the carrots. THE END.
Great service...thanks Brandon! Food was delicious.  El Camino margarita on point! Beautiful setting. We will definitely be back.

My family has visited Walden's several times for an afternoon treat.  The espresso has always been fresh and the cookies are some of the best I've had (and reasonably priced).  The staff has always been courteous and attentive.

only internet i can get where i live.
really sucks.
constantly dropping me!
i am selling my house, and moving to a high speed, reliable internet for stock trades.
they are costing me $1000s

anybody but...
be warned.
good luck.

This spot is functioning great during COVID, and the service from the employees goes above and beyond. The only reason for my low review is due to the food. I love seafood tacos, as they are almost impossible to get here in Tucson. However, this place has only had untapped potential. I thought the Stuffed Chile Poblano Taco was rather lackluster, and the cheese was not what I had hoped it to be. The fish tacos also felt bland and unexciting. I truly hope the restaurant improves in the future as it is a fantastic concept that is truly needed in the area.

Out in the land of high end and over priced lunches- the main line i saw a big sign for a $5.95 luncheon including brown rice and there was no question where i'd dine today.
There were several items for 5.95 but most ran around 6.25 including my choice Hunan Beef. Hunan beef was many thick slices of tender beef seasoned with red pepper flakes in a brown sauce with standard Chinese vegetables. Alongside was a generous portion of brown rice. 
The meal included wonton soup and a steaming pot of tea. Portions sizes were such i have a leftover for lunch tomorrow. Very tasty and the service made it a pleasurable dining experience

Just joined. Really love all the employees and overall it's a great atmosphere. It's definitely a very busy gym. Weeknights I find myself getting a workout just walking to the door after parking so far away. They need about 10 more treadmills and a lot more space for you to do like abs or stretch. I just think it's ridiculous to pay $45 a month for a gym membership though. WIll definitely be canceling when I can just run outside again!
Great food! Great views! Love this place. Definitely try to dine outside rather than inside.

WOW!   Could not have been better.  I have eaten here many times for seafood, but went with the steak tonight.  Outstanding.  Everything was great Including the salad dressing and the sides.  Will be back soon.  Sat at the bar and service was also top notch.  Don't miss the warm buns with house spread....

Had lunch upstairs, great view, server very friendly ( and we had two young kids with us ). Prices are more than reasonable ( I live in LA and can tell you that you can't find seafood platters that cheap down here ). Will go back for sure .

First time I've been to this pizza place, hands down, loved the pizza, the serve, the owner (I'm assuming that man is the owner) he is top notch, made a point to greet everyone, great customer service.
Don't advertise that you're open Christmas Day when you're closed, annoying and wasting people's valuable time.

Your average chain restaurant. Always packed with people who are obviously not picky about food. Go around 3 p.m. to avoid a wait. More of a scene than a place with good food. I recommend the mahi and shrimp lemongrass bowl. Not cheap.

First of all, its a zoo at this restaurant during the need to social distance. Way too many people in close quarters and no sense or order to managing the crowd. 

Now on to our order. We ordered our meals online for carry out. When my husband picked them up the hostess told him to check he has everything but she walked away before he had a chance and was practically pushed out the door by people waiting for a table. When he got home we found that we either had someone else's order or they just decided to not make my half of it (we got 2 BBQ omelets but we only ordered 1 and I ordered a waffle combo). We tried to call the restaurant to have the order corrected but their phone line is busy. I kept calling and calling but can't get through. I feel like they intentionally took their phone off the hook because they can't handle the traffic. Now my husband is on his way back and will have to wait in that crowd for our order to be made properly. 

Keke's just lost us as customers. I would never recommend an establishment that has complete disregard for public safety and poor customer service.

Update when my husband returned: The hostess confirmed we were given the wrong order # (maybe she should have confirmed the order ticket before handing it off?) and had the kitchen remake our meals. My husband said he was going to wait outside because it was too crowded in the waiting area. 20min later he went back in to find the waiting area was empty and 3 hostess standing at the booth staring at him. They were in the process of asking him how many in his party when he interrupted with "you guys are making my food right?". Finally at that moment they realized who he was and said they're working on it. Really? 20Min later and still working on it?! Finally the manager came over, pointed at my husband, confirmed our last name, handed the bag and walked away. No apology, no "have a nice day". One of the hostess did apologetic tho. 

Both my husband and I used to work in a very busy chain restaurant, so we understand breakfast rushes. This restaurant is not equipped to handle rushes. Their hostesses are clueless and forgetful. Their management doesn't care. Don't waste your time with this place.

I love the food network, so of course I had to visit multiple food destinations on every trip. We started at Geno's and I was a bit apprehensive that there was only one option for sandwich, no add-ons like mushrooms, or pizza stuff like Pat's, pretty much take it or leave it. But after the having the sandwich I understand why they only serve one variety, if you got something good, don't mess with it ;). After Geno's we went to Pat's, then back to Geno's because we were so much more impressed with Geno's (and yes disgustingly enough this was all in one afternoon). What made Geno's stand out in my mind was the steak and the bread, both had more flavor, and the quality of the steak was better.
Really like this place! Delish Cubans, ruebens and sides! Definitely a must try! Owners very friendly! We will be eating here a lot!!

Father and son work here. I dealt with the son. I came in with 3 storage containers of PF69 or better proof coins. I had quite a few high grade buffalo nickels and walking half dollars. I had many high grade coins. The first thing Mike said was "you don't have the quarters do you?" So obviously he didn't want any of the silver proof state quarters I had for every year. I then showed him a PF69 buffalo silver dollar I had. I told him I had about 25 of these. He said "I already have a bunch of those in the back" So again no interest. NICE. I also keep all my coins in dessicant to keep the moisture out and the coins in fine condition. He then picked out one PF70 gold coin I had. He made me an offer so I agreed. He was "cherry picking" He didn't care one bit about the customer or the coin collecting community in general. He was only out for himself. I think he was too lazy to go through my collection. He showed no interest in any of my coins except the PF70 gold coin so I very GLADLY took it back. He then recommended me to another coin shop. I was treated horribly at the shop he recommended and I was offered close to spot silver for my high graded coins. I talked to the Father at Causeway Coin about his son and his recommendation. He told me they NEVER recommend that particular coin shop to anyone! Don't do business with Causeway Coin!

This was my 2nd choice on a rainy Sunday... our first stop had over an hour wait and I didn't have time for that. As I was headed to Mississippi, I used my handy Yelp app to see what was available as I headed out. And found this cute little spot on Yelp.

LOVED the family feel and that it was clear most of the people were regulars. Did not love that they were out of so much that morning. My friend wanted the meatball sandwich but they were out. They were also out of fries. I think he ended up with a burger and I had fried shrimp.

The Sunday brunch menu was not in effect that day and I'm not sure why because it looks amazing. Would try it again if I was in the area.

Pretty good local eatery that the Indy government reps we were meeting with recommended for lunch.

I had the grilled chicken sandwich with chipotle mayo and a serving of fries. The portion was large and the chicken was cooked nicely but there was barely any mayo on the sandwich.

Service was friendly and attentive while the prices were reasonable.

It takes a lot to shake me and this place did so and more. Unbelievably rude bartender and manager and sketchy stuff going on here. That's all I'll say about that. Should have known this place was a bad idea when we walked in on a Sunday afternoon around 5pm and not a soul was in the place besides the DJ and bartender. Not a soul. We had wanted to check this place out because karaoke sounded like a fun idea. 

First, the bartender had her back to us almost the entire time and we had to say excuse me each time she was on her phone texting to get her attention. Asked her if she had an outlet behind the bar to charge my phone and she snootily said, "No, we don't take responsibility for anyone's personal belongings." That's actually fine with me but she was hella rude about it. We did some karaoke up on the stage and the place again, was sketchy as hell. People were going in and out of this door and some weird stuff was happening. Their bathroom is connected to Tequila Cowboys so you had to go down a hall and around the bend to access it, it was just straight up weird. Couldn't find my coat at one point so asked the bartender if anyone had turned one in and she tried to make it seem like I was accusing her of stealing it. She went as far as to get the DJ or manager's attention about the coat matter and the DJ made an announcement over a loud speaker and spoke, "Our bartenders don't steal people's coats so stop accusing them of doing so and no cursing at our bartenders." You've got to be kidding me. Cursing? Not once did a curse word come out of my mouth, we were livid. Livid. She couldn't have been more rude and unwelcoming and she was causing unnecessary drama that put a huge damper in our day. Never ever ever would I recommend anyone coming to this place. It's disgusting with some real winners for employees. Plain and simple.
All I can say is AMAZING!! Best burger ever!!! Made my night !! Peanut butter stout by tailgate brewery a local was awesome too.
Best breakfast in New Orleans! Everything I've tried was good. Especially love the giant pancakes!!!!

Despite a rocky booking process, hotel monteleone more than made it up to us. After trouble over the phone, an apologetic manager not only upgraded out room to a beautiful top floor room, but even sent us a wonderful tray of fruit and water as an apology. The room itself was spacious and clean, no complaints. Courteous staff, an ideal location, and historical quaintness make this hotel a great place to stay. 

Spa Aria, the hotel spa, is excellent and does a top notch mani pedi, massage, facial, and more. Great prices and friendly service!

In the ideal location in the french quarter, I can't see staying at some large chain hotel over hotel monteleone for the price, and I would def return for their very affirdable summer discount deals!

This was my first time at this Kaldi's location and I was happy to find a welcoming neighborhood shop. Many people were sitting outside enjoying their coffee which the patio overlooks the park. For a small shop they have a nice variety of pastries available for breakfast. I opted for an iced latte this day. Ordering was easy and I was able to distance among the other patrons while waiting for my coffee. It took a little long for a simple drink with only two other people waiting ahead of me but they had drinks with a full presentation. My drink tasted great and was refreshing in the nice weather. Will definitely be back to this location again in the future.

I was here for lunch during the week day, so I can't speak to the atmosphere when the bar is in full swing, but the food is mediocre.  I had the french onion soup and a portabella mushroom sandwich.  Nothing that I couldn't have gotten at a run of the milll chain like a bennigans or houlihans.. the beers on draft were weak, but they did have some good bottles for the winter season (troeggs mad elf, rogue yellow snow, etc).   Bottom line: this was a one and done place.

Truly a hidden gem! Kristen at Herban Skin Therapy provides the best facials and waxing I have ever experienced. I can't go anywhere else now! She truly pampers you from start to finish; it's so much more than a facial, with optional massage for arms, chest, scalp and feet. Kristen customizes every treatment to your skin and current needs, and she hand-batches organic products herself. I'm always picking her brain on the best skincare ingredients. She is just a joy to be around, really takes care of you, and her space is a little sanctuary that I love visiting. I cannot recommend Herban and Kristen enough. Pure magic.
Very friendly staff and great pizza ! Abby really makes you feel like part of the family ! We love this place !!!

Got the classic grilled cheese and tomato soup - JUST what I was craving. I really like this place! The soup is nice and thick, not just from the cream but from the amount of tomato they put in. I felt like the flavor was very concentrated. The Grilled Cheese was also very good. The bread was nicely toasted to balance the cheesy goodness. 

I did not like the wait and I was bored - read as available cashier/kitchen staff did not try to make conversation even though i was the only one there - but I think you can order before hand so if I had done that, there probably wouldn't be a wait. Bonus, they included these cute goldfish with my meal. Random but cute,

I have always loved this place until today. Our server Chelsea T. was terrible she does not have an ounce of hospitality in her. To Chelsea defense she did not know that my husband and I had just experienced the worst week of our life and just wanted to come an enjoy the food and some alone time at our favorite restaurant. I explain to her that I had a mushroom allergy and She advise us that they did not remove the mushrooms from the appetizers. ((So what do you normally do for customers with an allergy?)) She was rude to my husband when he questioned why his tostones con queso was soupy instead of crispy And nacho like. She forgot to place my husband entrée for  paella. And when all was said and done she praised herself that she had her manager remove $5 from our bill. We did not go to your establishment for $5 off our bill. We went there to have a great experience and eat good food. Neither of those things happened tonight.

Great cafe that gave me exactly what I was looking for this morning: a bacon, egg, and cheese, with a tomato slice on an English Muffin. My brother went with the burger special. Total came out to $8 and that's just awesome. Definitely a great spot for a quick hot meal, especially when calories don't mattah.

I love this place.  Best pastrami I've ever had.  I love the thinly sliced flavorful pastrami on a soft roll.  Yum...  Makes me hungry just thinking about it.

Also, there's a batting cage next to it so after a nice meal, you can hit some balls to work out couple calories...

Chuck was trustworthy and gave me the best rate compared to others. He did a good job and was very nice. It's hard to find such a mechanic these days and I will definitely get back to him when I need other repairs.
The food would have been good... Had it come warm. It didn't. It took forever to get drinks and we definitely saw the bottom of bottomless mimosas.

Wow best ribeye we've had in Tucson. Chicken and brisket combo was perfect. Very tender and full of flavor. I love the rubs and all the sauces. The salad bar was ice cold--perfecto. Can't wait to come back with more family so we can try the appetizers and desserts!

Had this delivered. Brown and black avocado, flavorless beans, salty potatoes, dry Cuban sandwich, polish sausage instead of chorizo. Spent $81 for food I'm giving to the dog. Except for the rotten avocado. I already threw the avocado away and gave the egg to the dog, but this is what's left. Not sure what the disc is, tastes like hardtack. Empanada columbiana was the best thing and it was greasy.
Service and food amazing. Only question is as many times as we have stayed at the Siena why haven't we eating here before? Jovanny's service makes it a six star.

I didn't have a complaint until now. About 3 months ago, I stopped receiving important mail. I ONLY receive advertisements and junk mail.

I'll be retiring soon and my job has mailed me important documents that are time sensitive; I never received that mail. Also, I found out from other companies that my mail was returned to them as undeliverable.

 - I tried calling (I work during the hours of operation), the call either goes to a busy signal, after ringing forever OR someone picks up the phone and hangs up.
 - I called the 1-800# and was sent through a loop (didn't get anywhere).

Has anyone else in the King of Prussia area experienced non-delivery issues, particularly in the Valley Forge Towers? If so, could you please share your experience and if you were able to correct this issue?

Chicken was the same as the other Helen's I've eaten at.  Good enough, nothing special.  My issue is the near half hour wait for a single piece.  I was told 10 to 15 mins which I could have dealt with.  The extra charge for the soda was irritating.  They seem to just tack on a change because they can as it's contrary to the sign on the wall.  I was already irritated because it made me have to rush back to work.  Skip this dog and if you like Helen's, there's another one close by.  Prince's sleep easy, you have no competition here.

Good, but not great. Have been going for years and the food has been going downhill.  The pork nachos used to be my favorite, but it no longer worth the price tag.   My favorite thing lately has been the fresh onion soup.   The beer is what brings us back, but we usually eat before we go now.

Location 
Parking 
Staff 
Halal Food 
Cleanness 
Atmosphere 

Paprica is our number 1 family favorite Restaurant in Philadelphia.

We go there almost every weekend to enjoy the Food with the Friendly & Lovely Staff.
Thanks to the Owners who won the trust of their staff and customers.. The owners always make sure everyone is happy and content before leaving Paprica

P.S Love the Te

Thank you from our Heart  

Can't wait to try the Turkish Breakfast on September

This is the worst planet fitness ,rude manager "Carlos " you ask him to  enforce company policy on mask . Then you inform him it's been several times at this location they allow anyone to walk around  doing what ever they feel .Do your job "the promise "by the corporation and protect every single customer that walk  in to use the gym ! ,we should all do our part to fight against covid .  Then you ask  Carlos  manager to speak to the owner he refuses to give you a number . Then he tells me he will review the tapes ilater ! What will reviewing a tape  a day or week later?  idiot  if you are not stoping it currently . New management is needed at this location that enforce company policy's !!!

Food is always wonderful here and portions are huge. The grilled chicken torta is my go to meal. They have great lunch specials as well and you can't beat their happy hour deals. Service is always pretty good as well.

You scalped my husband. When I called to schedule an appointment I specifically asked for someone to use Scissors NOT shaver. Not to give him a military cut. He has shaved sidewalls. It's the SHORTEST haircut he's had in 20+ years. No wait. Stylists said he just got out of Barber School. Used a shaver and then scissors. What the heck! Husband said all of the guys walking out of there had the same buzzed haircut. What happened to styling men's hair with texture and length? I'm so mad  - feel bad for my "executive" husband / he looks like he just signed up for Bootcamp. Ugh.

Spacious indoor rock climbing gym, awesome for the kids and experts, but not for adults who are not experts.
They have many rock walls at the front and boulder climbing without ropes at the back.
It was a very warm muggy day in June, we headed there with some friends, the space was nice and cool and a large birthday party was leaving, it was not too crowded on a Saturday early afternoon. It was the perfect place to escape to get out of the hot and humid weather, and have some exercise and fun.
We were three adults and three kids, we bought the kids a 15 punchcard with 5 climbs each for $60 which we split 3 ways. We had a belay expert that worked solely with our group (since they were not too busy), normally they told us the belay expert will take up to five kids. She was nice and good with the kids.
The kids had an awesome time, BUT us adults were not permitted to climb. Here at this gym adults must take a class in belaying and do it themselves. It was a shame, because all three of us adults wanted to climb and the place was not too busy, so they missed out on some cash and we missed out on some fun.
Go vertical is a rock climbing gym in the city and they do allow non expert adults to climb. We will have to go there next time.

We dine at Donatello rather frequently and it is my favorite restaurant in Tampa.  I love the authentic cuisine and it's fine dining atmosphere.  You can enjoy fine wines and impeccable black tie service.  It's not the place for someone who is only accustomed to a lower price point "Carraba's atmosphere."  The price parallels the quality and service you receive.  Donatello is a family owned restaurant with 30 years of business and you will always have the opportunity to meet the owners who greet you and seat you.  I don't think there's any other restaurant in Tampa with that old school Italian charm that you will receive at Donatello.  It is a higher price point but you get the fine quality you can't get at the local "Italian-ish" chain restaurants.   This isn't Olive Garden and it's not the Macaroni Grill.  This isn't the everyday pasta mill restaurant.  Donatello is the real deal. The Osso Bucco is amazing, the grilled lamb chops are always perfect, the Barolo is always served at the perfect temperature and the Napoleon is absolutely to die for!

What the pho!

I'm somewhat green to Vietnamese cuisine, but I was turned onto pho by my cousin from L.A.  She came to visit, and by no surprise, the conversation lead to food.  Not all that uncommon in my world!  She raved about the pho (pronounced "fuh" not "foh") that she had had in the City of Angels and demanded that I gave this cuisine a try.  I knew about this little joint down in Tower Grove.  It became top priority!

I tried it for the first time this summer after a long day of walking around in the sun.  My friends were at a festival, but I needed to get out of the heat for a while.  It seemed like a good time to check out Pho Grand.  The hostess saw how sweaty and dirty I was and promptly sat me in the back corner.  

Nevertheless, I tried their Pho Dac Biet:  rice noodle soup with medium rare beef, meatballs, and tripe.  The meatballs were so tender, and the slivers of round beef were a nice touch.  However, my favorite part of the experience was the broth.  What a soothing moment.  It was relaxing, and I could actually feel spirit slowly rejuvenated.

My wife and I came in last weekend, and I had much of the same experience.  We tried the spring rolls, fantastic.  She had some sort of spicy chicken dish and was in heaven!  The staff is wonderful there, and I would suggest this place to anyone.

Have a little piece of tranquility.
This doctor is the worst one I have ever encountered. He has ZERO bedside manner, is rude, and insensitive. He should not be delivering children nor deal with pregnant women.

Cool place - not packed like some vape lounges, but not empty white walls like others. This place has personality! Juices are some of my faves, and they are very reasonably priced. Staff is very nice. 

The only reason I don't go here often is because on 3 different occasions I waited out front for them to open late.. 15-25 minutes after they are already supposed to be open and still no one there is extremely annoying. Especially if your on your way into work and pass other vape stores just to go to this one! I just don't chance it anymore! 


I suggest going in the afternoon!

Ate at Pastaria last night on our way to TPAC. I was really looking forward to a good oven fired pizza. I ordered the pepperoni. They claim the pepperoni is made in house. It was tasty as was the cheese but the thin crust was soggy. The house red Wine was quite good. My boyfriend had the pasta with clam sauce and was underwhelmed. Home made bread a little too moist. The best part of the meal for us was the gelato, I had the coffee, cream and cookies and he had salt and caramel. If we go again it will be for the gelato.

Great coffee that changes daily and brunch. Always has a few daily specials. The eggs Brunchery Benedict is really good. They tend to be busy on weekends so allow for a 20 minute wait. 
Very family friendly.

We couldn't be happier with the job that they did! The job was for interior painting of multiple rooms, some with incredibly high and staggered ceilings. Jeff and his crew arrived on time each day and were incredibly courteous and professional. Jeff was helpful in recommending paint brands and did exactly what we asked. Incredible attention to detail and very responsive when I nit picked things - they were on them right away. A three day job was completed in two and we recommend them absolutely for any job. We liked the job they did so much that we've already hired them to do our exterior painting as well!

First let me say that the food was good, not exceptional...just good.  For a neighborhood restaurant, you might want to walk a few spaces to West End Grill if you are hungry.   Got a two dollar upcharge for 2nd piece of chicken white meat and ended up with a chicken wing with no meat on it.  Asked for a diet coke and ended up with a 3 dollar Mexican soda.  Be careful what you order.  If you want to pay 25 dollars for a meal that will make you walk away hungry, then this is your place.

So I'm relatively new to seafood.  Prior to my adventure at Little Fish, I only knew bivalves (or cooked bivalves more specifically).

I had a colleague in from the land-locked midwest, and she wanted some good seafood.  I know Little Fish is known for good seafood, so I made a res.  I was shocked, and sweating buckets when we sat down and the menu looked like a bookmark.  It had 2 types of oysters, 3-4 starters, 3-4 entrees, and 0 cooked bivalves.  I could tell my coworker was nervous too.  I began to rationalize- the menu is small because everything is so fresh.  Everyone loves this place!

WINE!  Can someone open the wine?  We bought 2 bottles along; a red and a white.  

My colleague was thrilled to see oysters.  I told her I'd never eaten one before.  She told me that was about to change.  She ordered 2x of each kind. Turns out I like oysters!  We ordered more.  Then, we ordered the rock shrimp and pasta starter to share.  It came out, and it was delicious.  Turns out I like rock shrimp!  Then we ordered the lobster dish and again, turns out lobster isn't half bad!  I felt like He-Man with all of these new proteins coursing through my veins (and the 2nd bottle of red wine... oops).  

Bottom line- Little Fish rocks.  The food was delicious, the service was friendly, they didn't care that we sat there all night and drank wine.... this place is where it's at... and I'll be back to continue to expand my underwater eating skills.

Sat at the drive thru forever how do you forget that you have people in the drive thru? Wtf horrible service dirty inside employee always outside smoking instead of inside working no wonder it's forever this place needs to get it together it's fast for a.

Staying in a nearby hotel and walked over. So happy we did! Everything was delicious and beautiful. We had salads which were large, fresh and tasty. Pizza is a very nice thin-crust that had plenty of toppings and nice sauce and cheese. The calzone was very nice, large and filled well. Everything was cooked well and served with a smile. Very friendly service. We will be back the next time we are in town.

Dr. Kim has been servicing my family for over 4 years I have had two encounters with a nurse that is very disrespectful and racist, I complained about her a year ago and the doctor said he would address my concerns however yesterday she was rude and disrespectful towards my children and I and no one addressed her behavior. I was completely offended and will never refer anyone here and will never return due to the unprofessionalism and ignorance.

Let me start by saying that I would never recommend this Hampton Inn to anyone. 

I am a former business traveler with Hilton Diamond status, and have stayed at my fair share of Hampton Inns. While the age and decor of properties can vary pretty widely, the customer service is typically, consistently top notch. 

While this Hampton appears like a nice place, the service is absolute crap. I stayed here in February 2016. We arrived, checked in, and the interactions with the front desk were fine, though certainly not up to typical Hampton standards. The room was clean on arrival, but after an hour or so, it was getting incredibly hot. I checked the thermostat and confirmed that it was set appropriately, but the heater continued to blow hot air. I turned the thermostat off, and it continued to blow hot air. I called the front desk and they said someone would be right up. So we waited. And waited. And waited. I walked down to the front desk, expecting there had been an evening rush and they were busy. Nope, empty lobby. The front desk staff (playing on his cell phone) admitted that he completely forgotten about us. I asked if we could simply switch rooms, but he insisted on coming to look at the thermostat. So he came up, fiddled with the thermostat, and explained that they break pretty frequently, and this one appears to be broken. I stated that we would need a new room, and he agreed to move us. 

All of that, while not great, would have still garnered a 3 star review. 

Flash forward to April, when my in-laws stayed at this property. They reported that the front desk staff was rude, and that despite their requests for service, the room was not cleaned during their stay. They wrote to the manager, who failed to respond, and eventually contacted Hilton corporate, who gave them a voucher for a free night. (Whatever happened to Hampton's money back guarantee??) 

They're back in town this weekend and booked two rooms, planning to use the voucher for one of them. The first pair of their party checked in and went to their room. In the meantime, my in-laws presented their voucher, and were very rudely told by the front desk that it couldn't be used since they had prepaid for the room. They asked to speak with the manager - the same manager that was there during their last stay - who maintained that they couldn't use the voucher, as the room had already been paid for. My father in law asked for their names (so he could reference them specifically when contacting Hilton corporate), and only then did they finally offer that they could void the transaction and take the voucher. 

Meanwhile, my brother and sister in law, already in their room, found only one towel, along with a used bar of soap and shampoo in their shower. Clearly, it had not been cleaned. 

The rooms were refunded, voucher thrown away, and they are now at the Holiday Inn.

Prompt service. Roughly 350 dollars to do an assessment. Nice peace of mind to have their specialized equipment to assess if there was a potential for a leak. Two gentlemen came to the house, wearing masks, and were polite and on time. Would be a great service to have when purchasing a new property. Very thorough.

If I could give this place zero stars, I would. 

After having a strong Chinese craving and reading good reviews on beyond menu, I decided to place an order for delivery with them.

I really wish I never did.

Not only did it take them a hour and a half to deliver, but the food was cold and bland. The egg rolls were horrible.. soft and no flavor. The chicken low mein had barely any chicken and no veggies. I literally threw everything out after the first bite.. it was that unpleasant. 

I didn't think it was possible to mess up Chinese food this bad but boy was I wrong! I will NEVER get anything from General Tso's House again and I highly recommend saving yourself the time and taking your business elsewhere.

Not coming back. Pedicure was less than mediocre. The technician did a very poor job with scrubbing the bottom of my feet and completely neglected the back of my heels. Had to finish the job myself when I got home. The polishing was sloppy with traces of nail polish on the area close to the nail itself. Barely said two words to me while I was there. Not sure if they sanitize their instruments between clients,but it looks like they just stick the tips of the tools in some blue gel. Not worth the $28 memo pedi.

The gardens is beautiful, and the restaurant good but pricey.  The cinnamon flavoured butter with the complementary bread was delicious. We ordered the tea lattes and the salads which were fresh.  A great place to come for a meal with great ambiance and good food

I admit, I have walked past this place about 20 times thinking it was a hipster/pretentious hang out. Finally the lady friend, convinced me to give it a chance (despite the sidewalk dj trying way too hard). The first thing I noticed was that every table inside had a reserved sign on it when we walked in at 9, yet no one was seated at these tables (when we left at 1030, two of those tables were occupied).

The bartender(s) were very friendly. We happened to be nerds on an old-fashioned cocktail pub crawl so ordered one here. Our bartender made us some hybrid between a bourbon based old-fashioned and mojito, and I will say this was one of the best drinks I have had. We had to continue our crawl to The Canopy so I couldn't have a second (as the crowd seemed to arrive), but I probably would come back here.

My only complaint (I can't comment on food) is that the dj, while live, wasn't very good. We later walked on over to The Canopy and enjoyed the music much more. That being said, I realize everyone has a different appreciation/taste in music. I love anything on Beach Dr and being close to the bay, and everyone working here seemed nice enough, so I plan on visiting again eventually.
I enjoyed the food but the service was a little slow if you weren't eating from the buffet.  The servers were friendly and the food had Good Dominican flavor.

Mr. Jimmy Shaw was great. I started looking for a car 2 years ago, just looking of course. But Mr. Jimmy Shaw kept in touch reaching out to me to see if I wanted to test drive different cars and models. He left a lasting impression on me. I will send any and everyone I know to Rivergate Toyota, to see Mr. Jimmy Shaw.

I learned about this place at school and it has become my go-to place to eat when i don't want to drive anywhere. I typically get the gyro platter, but the rest of the  food I've gotten there has been great as well. 
The only issues I've had are that the menu is extremely overwhelming, and i've had a couple times that i've had trouble ordering by phone because the person on the other end wasn't very clear.

We love Flamestone! The servers are great, especially at the bar. The chef is always creating new dishes and will often accommodate a few changes. Atmosphere is great, restrooms clean and the kitchens is not behind walls, so you can see how the food is being handled. The menu, we were sad to see has changed....everything else stays same! Thanks Nick for a great place to hangout any night of the week! A+ for Beso's as well.

This is just based on one trip and one technician here since I am from out if town. 

I give it four stars based on the price, cleanliness and how friendly they are. 

I got a gel manicure and it was mediocre. The massage part was almost non existent and didn't even do my fingers.

The food is good, especially the "crack chicken" - a must try.  My issue with the Lingnan isn't with their food but their service.  If you are a regular and have been going there for years, you will be treated as an  honoured guest.  However, if they don't know you, you get at best average service and it will be at a deference to regular diners.  We were waiting to be seated one night and had the opportunity to view this in action.  There was a confusion about the take out orders, with later orders being ready while a gentleman started voicing his concerns about his 1 hr over due order.  The matron of the family quickly went and got "gifts" from the office.  When we were seated and ordered, we observed several tables who had arrived and ordered after us receive their food.  We waited 1.5 hours for our food and were on the verge of leaving when the fellow came with the hot plates (that were cold by the time the food arrived).  We waited even longer for the food, which as stated was good.  We like the food but now do exclusively take out.

Great food and a cute little spot. A little pricy but delicious. Our waiter was a bit new so at times he was a little stiff and unprepared for questions such as ... How big is a small salad. He replied with a sarcastic answer ..."I mean what do you want to know how many ounces..." Lol I was a bit taken back but later became a bit friendlier as we joked around with him.
Good food but we've had really bad service the past few times we've gone. Disappointing because we like to go to local establishments.

Went out to watch the Fiesta events on Friday night with my husband, sister in law and brother in law. We decided that we really wanted pizza before we drove home an hour away.
