We rushed over the the location with 5 minutes before closing time thinking if they turn us away its ok, we would go somewhere else, but we get in and nothing is said to us, so we order and go to sit down. Our order was about $60.00 keep in mind...so after a few minutes the man in charge (didn't catch his name) told my brother in law that they were closed and that we needed to take it to go. There were still two families in the restaurant eating, but we decided not to make it a big deal, and asked for a refund. At that time, I told the worker that he should have told us when we walked in instead of waiting until we ordered and sat down,  his response...he started yelling and pointing his finger saying "I toll you, I toll you, I toll him over there," and started pointing at my husband who only tried to order a beer and was told that they were no longer serving beer, nothing else. I told him that he didn't need to yell, and that he was being very unprofessional. If he would have told us when we go there then we would have gone somewhere else! No problem. He was not listening, just yelling. It was embarrassing, since the other families were watching this. Anyway we left, went to IN and OUT and got GREAT service from them. For much less at that!

I do love Rusty's pizza, but will not go back to this one again.

After an extended stay at Disneyland we planned to stay a night in SB and my wife found this place.  Our check out time in Anaheim was 10am and knowing we were going to arrive early in SB, we requested an early check in the day prior to our arrival .  The gentleman on the phone said there was no guarantee but that shouldn't be a problem since it was a Tuesday night and they may have vacancies and to call the day of.  Even if our room wasn't ready they could put us in another similar room.  The morning of, we called and asked again and a gal at the front desk said they couldn't promise anything but would do their best.  Called again when we were 15 minutes away around 1pm (normal checking is 3) and the gal said that the cleaners were not back from lunch and the best they could probably do was 2pm.  Then we arrived and spoke in person to her and after explaining our conversation with the gentleman  the day before, and she  abruptly interrupted my wife, saying, "Excuse me, if you let me finish, I'll explain to you how things work around here ......blah, blah, blah", she had lost us with the intro and we didn't hear the rest except for the ending of, "the best I can do for you is 3pm".  I told her that we'd be back at 5pm and to take your time! After checking in and throughout the evening we noticed that many of the rooms were empty so not sure what the power trip and push back was all about.  The reason I didn't give this place a 1 star is because the room was clean and the beds were comfortable.

Great spot right off Bourbon! Definitely a nicer atmosphere. The food was fantastic, and the entire waitstaff was very accommodating of our various allergies (dairy, gluten, etc). They had plenty of options for us to choose from, and everyone loved the entire meal. Would definitely go back. 

The restaurant did seem pretty busy, so I would recommend a reservation. We had one and we're seated exactly on time even though we were early.
So far all the  people who work here are very polite and upbeat! In my opinion, it's a big plus!!! Keep up the good work guys. It's making buying sadwiches a lot better.

My family and I went to lunch here and waited 10 minutes to get a menu and drinks.  Then after 20 minutes with know one helping us we walked out!   There was no reason for this  there were only three customers in the restaurant  and plenty of employees none of which would acknowledge us sitting in a booth!   When I tried to flag someone down it was like they looked right through us.    We have eaten here before and the service was slow but the food was OK so we thought we would try again.

Treated my boyfriend and some girls on my team to lunch while we were in town for the Beachbody Summit. One of the girls I was with is vegan, and not an "annoying" vegan, but someone who is conscious about what they ingest. She asked the waiter about whether the hash browns were cooked in oil or butter... The waiter began to speak to her condescendingly debating about how the hash browns were cooked. She asked a simple question and he decided to talk to her like a fool. Aside from that, there weren't that many healthy options... I got a grapefruit salad which was inky dinky. The food wasn't that spectacular.

Pho 1 did not disappoint! The spring rolls were amazing and had a great balance of veggies and meat. The pork chop was prepared very quickly and was very savory and delicious. My family that ordered the pho said it was almost perfect. Fast and friendly service.

Second visit to Tito and Pep's and it did not disappoint. New restaurant in central TUCSON and every dish we ordered was exceptional. Recommend you check it out. Wonderful cocktails and excellent vibe. We tried the foie gras, T& P salad, charred broccoli, pork chop, charred trout and panna cota. Lucky to have this new spot in central Tucson!
Great food!!! Went there for lunch & dinner for many times during summer. I won't go there again because it's too far away, but I will find something like this in St. Louis!

We came in to see a Steely Dan concert here.  If you drive in a couple of hours before the concert you should be able to park in the local neighborhoods. We chose to uber in and out from our motel to the bowl and that worked quite well.

Most important.....there is a long uphill walk so good shoes for walking is important. And as in any bowl there are a lot of stairs. They offer wine and beer for sale. We went with a Pinot noir for $9 a glass and it was pretty good. 

They do offer food but we had already ate so we didn't try it out. I did see people with pizza, so they have pizza for sure.  The seats get hard quick so if you have a cushion bring it.

The sound system and lighting was first rate. The closer you are the better the view. It's  always a good idea to bring binoculars.

Definitely a great venue and we would definitely come back!
Very helpful as this was my first time with this area. Food was great but a little busy in the takeout area.

An Israeli place in New Orleans isn't exactly what you would expect, but this James Beard award-winning restaurant was worth all the hype! We had a giant breakfast so we didn't order a ton of entrees, instead got all 5 salatim (types of dips) and a few small plates to share. Everything was so beautifully done and the pita was a fluffy cloud of bliss. I loved that all the salatim were different flavor profiles too--there was even a fish one with caviar! Fancy. And the mushroom hummus was sooo tasty. Definitely worth the trip!!

Another visit another disappointment.  This place is border line criminal.  This time I had to replace the appliqués that broke.  They broke in their car wash!!!  It's their poor design, in their car wash, but I pay almost $1000 to replace?

Then I find out my peeling rims were just out of warranty.  Now they make me aware and want to charge me 3-5k to replace? 

Do yourself a favor and find another dealer.

Went on a Tuesday for half price burger night and was not disappointed. Had the turkey burger (no roll) with avocado and pepper jam, paired it with the seasonal veggies and broccoli, and everything was outstanding. The service was also great. Will definitely be going back!

OMG!  The Twisted Tail Totally Rocks!  The food is incredible (i've tried everything on their menu) and who knew--I now have a new found love of bourbon.The decorum is really cool with a sharp eye for detail--clearly spared no expense on furnishings and decor.  Even the restrooms are nice and the wallpapers are decked out with a juke joint vibe. The overall atmosphere is fun and inviting. They have great music gigs on weekdays and weekends. I love to play shuffleboard (billiard-style table--not the old people game) so that's a big draw too (they have Monday night tournaments--what a hoot). I'm here two to three times per week and it just keeps getting better and better. There's a huge selections of beer, bourbons, good wine selection or whatever hooch you desire. Their holiday events (Halloween/Christmas/New Years) were really well planned and so much fun. This is a must eat/drink and drink some more place. The bartenders and other staff are very friendly and helpful. I've had the pleasure of meeting the owners are they are so nice.The twisted tail is the best thing to happen to this neighborhood in a long time. I highly recommend it whether you stop for a quick drink with appetizers or a nice dinner.  The twisted tail is a great hangout.

Sher

I can't believe it took me so long to finally visit Eckert's!  My fiance was incensed that I had never picked peaches before, so we decided to make the long trek out to Belleville to get some supplies for sugar-free peach cobbler.  Yum.  We picked way more peaches than we expected - almost more than we could handle - so I'm glad peach season is just about finished.  I won't want to eat another peach for at least a year!

The country store is cute.  I love their selection of jams and jellies!  I got some tasty sugar-free apple butter and fig jelly to bring home with us.  

We'll be back again to pick some pumpkins in the fall!  Can't wait!

Have been two times now and I love this place. Cute seating area, great prices, friendly staff. Chicken and beef pho were both delicious and HUGE. Only $1-2 more than the baby bowls at Bamboozle Cafe, but you probably get 4x as much soup. I will definitely be back to try the other dishes and the teas. Also, good music is always playing!

This event is growing and getting better for sure.  It is now much easier to get beverages and food to enjoy while spending time here.  There are lots of warm alcoholic beverage options that are great considering it is an outside event in December.  This is a German event, so pretzels, sausages, and German pastries are the main selection.  We did not try any on this visit, but they smelled amazing!

There are still a few things to improve on for this event.  1. The mud.  Half of the event is rubber mats on soil and today everything was covered in mud.  2. Sitting area.  There are only about 30 stands.  So if you are not skating, you can easily be in and out of this event in 30 minutes.  If there was a sitting area to enjoy the drinks and food, it may make it more of a destination event.  3. Some cheaper vendor options.  Most of the vendors are selling handmade German items.  Which is really cool to see, but unless you are coming prepared to spend $100 on a stein or nutcracker, you don't have many shopping options.

First of all this restaurant is very new to the area.  With that said ,there was just a small temporary sign above the restaurant because as the server told us that they didn't get all the permits they needed.    A  lack of  pictures on the wall.  The soda machine broken. I only drink water anyhow.  So all this is really unimportant and quite moot.   To let anyone know going here, the restaurant is right next to Fairfield Motel on the east side of Oracle Rd.  We do have our favorite Chinese Restaurant but because this opened we decided to venture to new grounds.  I of course looked up my fellow yelpers and mostly 4 and 5 stars so that really gave us the impetus to check ou China Valley.   One of the yelpers loved the egg foo yung which I never order because I usually like more exotic dishes.  However, this was awesome. Four very large patties sautéed with a wonderful brown sauce.  Loved this dish and even if you don't like egg foo yung it is worth the try.  Then one of our favorite dishes is chow fun either chicken, beef, tofu, or house. We chose the vegetable and it was awesome.  Not seeped in oil which it sometimes comes like that.  The veggies were zucchini, carrots and onions, bean sprouts.  A little on the smaller portion size so every bit was gobbled up almost instantly.  Made with rice noodles as a side note to this dish.  I just loved it.  Prices are varied.  Some of the more exotic dishes were $13 to $18 .  We had the vegetable rolls as appetizers and for $2.95 we got 4 small ones and they were super good.  We will go back again for sure.

Definitely the best wine-tasting deal in town -- $5 for 6 wine tastings (2 whites, 4 reds), and it included the glass!  For every other tasting room, I shared a tasting w/a friend, which worked out great - got to taste more wines, and still remain standing.  But here, we each did one, so we'd each have a glass to take back to our room. Cute stuff to buy there, too.  And in the afternoons, they have free olive oil tasting (we got a bottle of the mushroom/garlic one - so good - the basil one is great too).
The charbroiled oysters are the absolute best in the city. The shuckee duckee is my favorite entree. However, any fish on the menu is outstanding.

Ran into this place as we were looking for something to quickly eat. Was very happy leaving!
Definitely in the Chipotle category. Very easy and no fuss. Fresh ingredients and will add whatever you like. 
Street tacos were about $2.50 each which is pretty good. I think the burrito bowls were 6.75. 
The do have street corn (elote) which was pretty tasty.. could have used a smidge more spice but good. 
My friends got bacon wrapped jalepenos. They said that they were got but 2 of them were INSANELY spicy and the other 2 weren't. 
Restaurant was clean. Line went quick. Saw some beers on tap too.

I would give less than one star if possible. Some friends and I decided to head to Jerry's for Sunday brunch. Our table was a total of 6 and we did arrive two at a time, though we were seated before our whole party arrived. The waiter seemed attentive at first, getting the two of us our drinks and delivering them timely, the restaurant was not crowded at this point. Two more from our party arrived and the waiter came back to fill our waters but did not take additional drink orders, he also spilled my water all over the table an walked away with barely an apology and no attempt to get us more napkins or help clean it up. We waited about 20 minutes for him to come back and during that time, the final two arrived. This time, we had to initiate ordering drinks and discussed ordering food at that time because of the delay, rather than wait and take our order, he said he would get our drinks and come right back. Another 20 minutes went by before we saw him again.  Once we finally put our food order in, it took at least 45 minutes before the first 3 of six dishes came out. At this point, the waiter realized he forgot to put in our appetizer and asked if we still wanted it. We waiting another 30 minutes for the remaining 3 meals and were asked no less than 6 times what we were waiting for because the servers and kitchen could not get their tables straight. It was clear other tables were having the same problems. The manager came to our table a few times and apologized and told us one or two entrees would be comped, not the three we had been waiting for for over an hour. We finished our meal and could not leave fast enough but couldn't find the server anywhere to get our check. After another 30 minutes of waiting once we finished, we finally asked some one else if we could have our check. That server told ours and he clearly looked annoyed, got us our check, and not one item had been comped. The service we received was appalling. The manager seemed to be trying but was clearly overwhelmed and the staff is grossly under-trained and not attentive.

We thought that since it's Thanksgiving day, this place would be closed, but according to Yelp, it said it was open. When we arrived, the sign said, "Open" and the door wasn't locked. We walked in, but no one was there! We kept calling out and even checked the bathroom, but no sign of anyone!

I hope this review will let the owner know about the absence of his/her employees. The whole place could have been robbed. There's so many electronics (sound boards, game consoles, televisions, etc.). This place is lucky that we didn't take anything -.-'

My husband and I were there for Feast of 7 Fishes on Dec. 19.  The seating was more suited to sardines than people.  The servers had to have us pass the plates and wine from one table end to the other.  They were generous with the wines, but the dishes were mediocre.  I learned they do not make pasta fresh anymore, and you can certainly tell the difference.

My boyfriend and I recently visited and we had an amazing experience! Our waitress was so on top of everything. I think her name was Taylor? She gave us our dessert for free because of our wait for our food. We honestly did not have any wait in between getting the soup, salad, breadsticks and our app! We will be putting this into our date night routine!

This is now hands down one of my favorite places in Tucson. Spectacular service and the best ribeye I had ever tried in my life! Also, I love the atmosphere and lighting of the place, very soothing and relaxing. I also loved that they had holiday drinks! Such a great way to get festive!

I love coming here after a long day at work...The family pack is a great value for the family.  My husband likes the fried green tomatoes so I usually pick some up for him.  The staff is always friendly.  If you are not looking for fried fish they have grilled options.  They will substitute the french fries with veggies.  My kids always eat everything on there plate when we come here which is an amazing feet in itself...

I dine out 12-15 times per week. I can't think of worse service than this place. 30 minutes for the first drink...downhill after that. I don't know what else to say nor do I want to waste anymore time.
What a great place. Great location.  Simple. Fast.  Everything you need. For a small market, they have what i need.  I go here at least 2x a week.  Its historic in glenside.

Breakfast was disappointing. It took 45 minutes for our food to be served. There was no explanation for the delay and the restaurant was not busy. The food was decent but the timeliness was ridiculous.

I had passed by this place a million times and finally stopped in. It was ABSOLUTELY delicious. My husband and I shared the ox tails and the curry chicken. Very tender with great flavors! I wasn't even that hungry and I almost licked the plate clean. The staff was so, so nice! Will definitely go back again!!!!

I've had some good times (in the drive thru) and some bad times (in the drive thru). Some times my order is perfect, other times they forget to put chicken in a chicken quesadilla, or no fire sauce in the bag. I've had times when the food is great, other times the rice in my shredded chicken burrito is like little pieces of rocks. They start serving regular food at 9am, well supposed to. I've pulled up at 9:15am before and they said that they're not ready for lunchtime food. Who eats Taco Bell breakfast anyway? This place is always full of surprises. The staff is always friendly though in the drive thru.

When we visit family in Reno this place is one of three places that we like to frequent for brunch. Every visit we add another sign to their wall to basically show that we've been there. They have a rewards program and consistently send you coupons for $ off and complimentary champagne for your special events like anniversary and birthdays.

The menu is just like many of the other brunch places with benedicts, omelets, and things a like. The food is great! The portions are huge! They give toys for the kids to play with which is a plus for those with families. 

We highly recommend Squeeze In as their service is great too!

Thanks much.

This is your typical greasy spoon chain restaurant that is best served just after massive amounts of alcohol, in the middle of making bad decisions, right before you go to bed. Maybe not your bed, and maybe not to sleep, though after a night of poor decision making after hanging out with friends, you should go straight to your house, alone, and go to bed, making this place not be one of your bad decisions.

Seriously though. It's not really that bad of a place. This location is on the west side of Elysian Fields between I-10 and I-610, just east and north of the French Quarter. For some reason, they tend to be filled with families Saturday and Sunday mornings. This location has opened in the last 5 years or so, which is probably a good thing. The food is not healthy, they don't serve anything that isn't slathered in butter and cooked on the grill. And not the good butter.

Some visits are good, the food and beverage order taken quickly at tables that are clean, and everything runs smoothly. When things of bad, they are stuck mid-spiral near the very end, and you are more than likely to get dirty and feel disgusted with yourself and everyone around you. The current management seems more intent on not managing, but cooking.

The best thing I can say is usually the floors are clean. But I will probably be back. I am into making bad decisions once they have started for the night. Or if I am in the area with a friend. Or wanting breakfast while I am in the area. If you want a cheap meal with no frills and no pretension, come here. Do not have high expectations of anything wonderful.

Give it up for THE best sushi spot in Broad Ripple! The once-violin shop has grown into a thriving sushi bar on the village strip.

- Minimal, modern, and comfortable interior with a small sushi bar and dining room. 
- Quiet. Great for independent diners, romantic dates, or professional lunch outing. 
- Lunch special - two rolls and soup/salad for $6.99! Pretty much everything on their lunch menu is under $10. Me likey.
- Fresh, clean, and very yummy! For Indiana, this is up there. 
- Staff is usually pretty quiet. Not sure why. Attentive though.
- HUGE selection of rolls! Like a kid in a candy store...except the candy is raw fish and seaweed.
- Parking does suck in the area. I would suggest parking near the canal and walking over. You'll need a walk after you stuff your face full of sushi.

If you're living in Broad Ripple, it's nice and easy to walk here for some rolls and a beer. If not, sad face for you. Miss you, Sushi Bar!

This is by far one of the best Middle Eastern restaurant I've dined in South Philly.  Great service and food. Price was very reasonable. Also free wi-fi and the place is clean. They serve lunch and dinner. Highly recommended.

Such a gem. They make the croissants fresh. The woman at the register is delightful. My husband and I both had the pressed croissant sandwiches and we went back for another, they melt in your mouth. Highly recommend for a cute and relaxing breakfast experience.

Star did a great job with sugar waxing my underarms. Very thorough, making sure she got every hair before she was done. It was my first time with her and she was super friendly and accommodating. Loved the ease of booking an appointment too. Would definitely recommend!

First....small local. I did that. Outstanding people that own and work here! 

Second...We drive 30 minutes one way to go here, passing several other pizza joints. It's that good, and that cool.

Third...they have homemade custards. And a big pic of Bourdain on the wall. Whats not to like?

Back from when I was on tour with the Trio:

This is a true find.  I've eaten BBQ all over this fine country, and this is some of the best I've ever had, certainly for Idaho (no offense, Idaho).  

It's a Mom and Pop store in the best sense of the word.  If you want an haute cuisine experience, go somewhere else.  If you want a high value BBQ experience, stay here and get the Monzello.  The ribs are great, and the pulled pork is even better.  Tri-tip is just OK by me (although very tender), but I'm partial to brisket, therefore biased.  

--------

Small sidebar - there BBQ philosophy is a little nuanced for me. I like a very concentrated smokiness and overall flavor (I think Hickory is great); their approach is more delicate and balanced (for instance, they smoke with Pecan only).  Whatever.  Even though I'm more of a Syrah guy, I know a killer Pinot when I taste one, so I've "gotta respect."

No comments on the Vegan fare.

Great ambiance, great haircut, great experience.  I will definitely be back.  I got the exact haircut I was looking for with styling advice and some product.  Nick definitely knows his craft and we had a great conversation while he was practicing his craft.  Sports on the TV in the leather furnished waiting area, and good music in the background.  The barber chairs are beautifully restored historical masterpieces and super comfortable.  Hard not to fall asleep with a hot towel on your face and that chair all the way back!

We have purchased three Acuras (new) from Chapman.  Our adult children also have Acuras.  Cars are sold not needing add on acce$$ories.  Chris and Steve have recommended service(s) that were needed and advised when service was not needed.  Our daughter's Acura (125k miles) needed an alternator.  Chris recommended an Acura replacement part with a longer warranty and better price.  Price is fair and reasonable for the service received.

I was just there on Friday night. Josh Arnold was fantastic! Jake Baker had a hard act to follow. He was good, too, though. Wait staff was good. Pre ticket sales: Whoa! Posted price for General Admission tics was $10. We were charged $13. I went to the ticket window and told the guy that he overcharged me. When I told him that the price posted was $10..he rudely replied...WELL THEY'RE NOT! He asked me where I saw that price. He then stormed out of the office, went over to the posting, tore it off the wall and trashed it with much flourish, and then went back to his little ticket office. No apology, no refund, no offer of a free drink. On the upside, it gave me a crazy story to tell the rest of the weekend.

We moved to the area during COVID and have only gotten to get takeout, which I am traditionally hesitant on in general. They have beyond exceeded our expectations and changed my mind on takeout being not as good as in restaurant.

They always have great service. The quality of food is great and the prices are amazing for the quality. It all feels fresh and we will continue to order from here.

Worst service place I've ever been. I'm even upset with myself for buy my jeep there. All the people are rude and act like they are doing you a favor. Customer services is horrible. Would not recommend going here.

This is place has the best sushi I have ever tried. Nothing will ever top it. I've gone 3 times this week and I am not ashamed. I leave full and within two minutes, I crave the sushi once again. I highly recommend the Crunch Spicy Tuna Roll and the Bonfire Roll. Amazing place with amazing service and amazing food. 10/10.

So good I have been here several times. Everyone is so nice and goes out of the way for you. Idk if it's family owned but everyone treats you as family. Recommended to all my friends. 
 You can see them prepare it right in front of you.
In from out of town and passed this place. Decided to give it a shot. Great authentic food and service was great. If I lived here, I'd definitely go back.

Delicious food! I saw them at Beardfest in Hammonton, NJ. Their food was so yummy, I came back twice! They even offered breakfast sandwich specials as they were open for the entire duration of the festival (24/7). I highly suggest the grilly dog. Best damn dog I ever had. Thanks Grilly Cheese!!!
Amazing food for a great price! We got the tofu spring rolls, shrimp vermicelli bowl & pork 5 spice bahn mi. The boba was also wonderful

Terrible customer service and management. Arrived at 5:00 on a Tuesday night with a party of 8, 4 tables were seated in the entire place, and we were given an hour and a half wait time.. After a discussion on seating and reminding the hostess we had called and were told "we don't accept reservations, but it won't be a problem," we were then told maybe closer to 45-60 minutes. Needless to say we left, and all the while the spineless manager watched as we tried to make things work and let the hostess deal with everything.

Loved the bed and the fact that the linens and towels don't smell like chlorine.

Like the suite aspect, nice large living room and bedroom (with a view of the harbor).  The bathroom door opened awkwardly though.

The reason for only two stars?  The entire hotel was infested with gnats which I found floating in my coffee, dead on my hand soap, crawling and flying everywhere.   In the ballroom where the meeting was being held, people were swatting them away from their face.  Needless to say, I found eating there very unappealing.  I just resigned myself to the fact that I'd eventually be digesting a few.  Ewe.

We found ourselves in Metairie and wanted to stop for lunch.  Bobby Hebert's has a generous drink/food menu.  I got the red beans and rice with a house salad.   My husband got the shrimp po boy with fries. 
The food is delicious and generous, and there is great sports memorabilia inside.

I decided to come in one morning when the drive thru line was long thinking it would be faster, seeing as the place was empty. 

Boy was I wrong. They were so busy trying to fulfill the Drive thru orders that it was almost 15 minutes to get my food. I stood here for 8 minutes before I was even acknowledged.

I wish I could give this bridal salon 10 stars. They were wonderful with me! I ended up purchasing my beautiful lace wedding dress from here. I can say with confidence that all 5-6 dresses I tried on were like nothing I had tried on before, certainly not in Indianapolis. The staff was extremely sweet but also professional. They knew exactly what dresses to pull for me without me having to shop which I know from another experience I had with a salon in Indianapolis is extremely hard to do and can't always be accomplished. 

All of their dresses are truly one of a kind and you will not look like a ''cookie cutter'' bride. If you are really looking for a dress that will make you stand out and feel beautiful try Beloved Brides.

Was a great place went because it was right next to our hotel.  Actually just went in for a coffee but stayed for breakfast because everything looked and smelled so good. Good service and very reasonable

Blue Monkey is an awesome little neighborhood bar.  It's never overly busy and the service is always great.  They have a TON of beers on tap and even more bottles.  They also do beer specials that they rotate every week or two.  The food is also great!  I've had numerous appetizers, salads, sandwiches, burgers and a few entrees; I definitely recommend giving it a try!

Go for the cheese omelette and home fries with onions, the cheeseburger and fries, freshly roasted turkey and cheese sandwich, freshly roasted grilled ham and cheese on rye, rice pudding and GYROS! They put the Boardwalk to shame.

This has got to be some of the best Thai in the STL area. Worth the trip up north. I appreciate being able to request different heat levels so I can get what I like to order. I also like the decent craft beer selection. Definitely one of my stops when I am up that way.

3 star food, 1 star service:

I went here with an out of town friend due to a recommendation made by a friend--we were pretty dissapointed with our entire experience.

While the gentleman at the counter was friendly and said hello--he was the only one.  The entire wait staff seemed not pleasant, did not say hello/goodbye, did not interact with us at all.  In fact, the first engagement I had with my waitress was "BEHIND" when she was coming behind me with plates...no "excuse me" or "thank you"...I just found that very rude.

When we were seated, our waitress again did not say hello or welcome us to the establishment.  She just took our order and left.  The food came out and it was alright--nothing to write home about.  We finished our food and paid in the front of the restaurant.

They make you pay on an iPad app, which is OK, but very awkward because the same waitress checked us out and said "feel free to leave a tip for how much you want" and so she would clearly see what she would be given, instead of it being a more confidential process where we can truly give what we feel our service deserved.  Also the app itself is deceptive about only showing 18%+ tip options.

At least the waitress said thank you after receiving the tip and giving us our receipt...no have a nice day.

Ultimately, based on this experience, I would not come back again.  The entire staff needs to be better trained in good customer service and better engagement with their customers.  It was just not a good experience, and compared to the other breakfast/brunch spots in the area, it just doesn't compare, which is a shame because their food is good.

Great overall environment very good selection of beers on tap. They have probably the best fried pickles around and with check-in with the yelp app they are free. Every burger that I have tried was really good, the only thing is when I order them ask for medium doneness but come out more towards well done

The stay here was amazing. The rooms were clean and nicely sized. The wifi was free, which is a bonus. The breakfast was great both mornings. However, the hotel guests in the breakfast area were very rude and would actually try to block you out of areas. It was the strangest thing I have ever experienced.

Huge plus, we never heard any noise from the airport, ever!
RE: The Dry Cleaners: Good quality. Reasonably fast service. Easy location for pick-up and drop-off. Prices are average and maybe a bit high, but they do good work. Family run business.

Competent, if mediocre.  Many of the 'hot' items were lukewarm at best.  Flavors were good, but signage had not been changed since the previous night.  Pricing can also be a bit off-putting, so ask before eating.

A perfect tattoo afternoon from Andrew Parker and Sanctity Tattoo. My friend and I dropped in on a week day to get some arm pieces. They got us both in and chatted about our design. While they drew up our designs we chose an awesome Greek restaurant among many others. Andrew was able to finish both of our tattoos quickly and both were done perfectly to our loving. 
I am coming back when I'm in AZ again. Thanks Andrew and co.

I just don't get it...went here for the first time today because I was craving a burger and wanted something higher quality than fast food but less expensive than Engine No. 9. I had the World Champ Cheesburger and added the lettuce tomato mayo onion ($5.35). Not a bad burger but I was completely underwhelmed. Given how small the burger was, a person would definitely need to order fries or something to even touch your hunger which would put the meal around $9 before drink and tip. Reading the reviews, them being in business forever, and the best burger in St Pete award several times, I don't know, I guess I was just expecting more.

I came in on a recommendation from a friend specifically for the Banana's Foster. And was so pleasantly surprised by our delicious lunch.  We started with the crab claws, so delicious... then the palace pot pie was scrumptious. And the Banana's Foster did not disappoint.  Food was delicious but the service was . Ashley our server was awesome and Alison her captain made a stellar team.

I was a pretty frequent guest at Kelliann's before I burned some bridges, and then well Kelliann's burned down...no connection... But this spot is pretty solid as long as the crowd isn't whack, that said on a good night you could pretty much dictate the vibe, which is always a good thing.  The owners a drunk too, so talk to him and he'll throw you a couple of drinks.

Warning: watch out for egocentric bartenders.

I have been dining at Mr. B's for the past 12 years, and try to get there whenever I am in New Orleans. A bit more elegant than the Brennan owned Bourbon House, Mr. B's is decorated with warm dark woods and comes equipped with a small bar area which is where my party and I ate. It has become a little bit of a tradition for us to come to Mr. B's to chill out, pig out and have a few drinks. 

Gary and Rich were our bartenders and they set us up at the bar with some Abita beers, bloody Mary's (a crowd pleaser), and easy conversation.  Now, on to the food. For starters I had a bowl of their Gumbo Ya Ya which comes with chicken and andoullie sausage. The flavoring is delicious. My only complaint is that I wish there was a bit more rice served with it, but that is personal preference. My friend had a special oyster and pea soup, which he enjoyed but noted it was lacking the oyster flavor he was hoping for. My other friend had a greens salad, which he seemed to enjoy. Moving on, for my lunch entree, I had the shrimp and grits. The shrimp was wrapped in bacon and they were salty, plump and juicy. They were very tasty. The surprising star on the plate, however were the grits. They were insanely good. I could just eat a plate of them and be perfectly happy. The consistency and buttery taste was absolutely heavenly. I had to defend my plate from my friends attempts at stealing them. Likewise, my friends BBQ shrimp was also fantastic. Rich sauce and plump shrimp are well worth the effort in peeling them open. I swear I thought my friend was going to chomp down on his plate he enjoyed the sauce so much---buttery, spicy, perfection. My other friend had a petit fillet that was wonderfully prepared accompanied by some eggs which I did not try. For dessert we split an addicting bread pudding that was sweet with cinnamon and raisins and soppy yet firm. Man it was good. I also had a special coffee called a "Bob" which had Kahlua in it and it really hit the spot. 

It should be mentioned while we were eating at the bar there were a lot of people enjoying the Sunday Jazz brunch that was being held at the time were were there. Having some live Jazz music while eating such good food, enjoying some delicious drinks and watching some sports is a real treat, especially because the Jazz trio that was playing was quite good. As usual, Mr. B's just always delivers for me and I plan to return as soon as I am back in New Orleans.

We came from NY and randomly chose Phantom St. Louis 1 cemetery tour from a Groupon deal. We were not disappointed!  Not only does the VooDoo Bar have 2-for-1 hurricanes and a New Zealand bartender that truly enjoys his job. The star of the show was Blaise, our tour guide. She was knowledgeable, funny, intelligent and reverent of the resting places. Take this tour!

I came to Ingrid for my first bikini wax ever through recommendation of a friend. I honestly had no idea what to expect. Ingrid immediately made me feel comfortable. She's got good energy and just makes you feel at ease (especially considering the positions you're put in). I got the "deep" bikini wax. The waxing was quick and pain minimal. She had me take a breathe for the more sensitive skin. She finished quicker than I expected (which was a good thing!). My skin looks super beautiful, clean, and smooth! Honestly I don't think I could have had a better first time experience. Definitely see myself coming back to Ingrid when I need another wax.

What a bad idea it was to come here with my kids on a saturday night. Oh well, was worth giving it a try. The moment I walked into this place I had instant regrets. 
So I took my kids here to play downstairs for a couple hours. I will start with this- they had a decent time. But it's only because I was in a generous mood.

This place sucks cash from their customers, that's it. It's a mediocre arcade- not even a great arcade. That's it. There's no joyful shared experience you have with your kids; it's watch them blow cash quicker than at a slot machine in atlantic city. Some games, no lie- lasted 5 seconds. 5 seconds! Many games are just games of chance, where the goal is to win tickets, so you can pick out some crap prize in the end. 

The games weren't very good. I don't know what they're idea is of cutting edge games- maybe arcade games just aren't that great anymore. It's either machine guns, or race cars here. Honestly, give me an arcade filled with 80's and 90's classics, and it would be 1,000 times better than this junk. 

After an hour and a half, I was done with being packed like sardines with people of questionable character, and wanted to just be done with the place. So we went to a carnival type game, where you try to throw a ball into a milk jug, and we just burned up the rest of our credits there, picked out some cheap junk in the prize store, and got the hell out of there. 

Forget eating there too- what a zoo that was, and the food looked like snack bar food at a roller rink. 

Also, the entire place (adult area upstairs too) had an intentional aroma of bad cologne. It's the way they want it to smell there I guess. I have no clue why, it was repulsive, but I guess they need something strong to mask the scent of some of the characters that frequent the place. 

Needless to summarize... Never again!

The grilled oysters are UNREAL here! In my humble opinion ACME was good but- this was even better. The sauce on these oysters ... divine. And we had two shrimp dishes that were excellent. Can't recommend this enough for the grilled oysters. I will be dreaming of these things long after we go home.

Food was great!  I had the cedar plank salmon, squash soup, and an old fashion.  The soup is a total pass, but the rest was great.  Really solid old fashion!!!!

It was a little loud overall but I could hear the people I was with perfectly fine.  The service was also great.
We were traveling on I65 and found this gem. It was totally worth the drive in the country.  Excellent service, wonderful food, and tasty beer.  Brisket was particularly good.  We'll be back!

Excellent company to reach out to meet your lawn care needs. Really customer focused as it should be. We all know even with the best effort/intentions,  issues with service happen. What matter most is how a company owns the issue. Lawn Pride exceeds my expectation in terms of  addressing any issue with my lawn care service. I never felt disrespected or lack of care and belief about the credibility of my concerns. A human being with a passion for customer service picks up the phone and tries to solve the problem in a reasonable way that always meets/exceeds my expectation. Lawn Pride  will continue to get business from me. Highly recommended.

They accept medicaid (it's hard to find that info without just calling around so i hope that helps someone). The wait was a bit long, but there were a lot of patients (maybe fitting people in same day). There's a little area for kids to play which was very helpful. The staff was very nice. I saw Dr. Cecola, he was very attentive and took his time with me. My problem was something that was involving the inner ear, something that he couldn't help me with. However he was familiar with my condition because of a seminar he attended. He gave me information on it and a website I could use to find more about it. He was probably the most helpful doctor I've seen about it. I'd definitely go back if I needed to.

This place is lovely: the atmosphere and vibe is so calming. They had a lot of drink and food options -- there were too many things I wanted to try! I ordered the milk cap peach oolong tea and it was soooo good. Some boba places make their drinks either extremely sweet or super bitter but I think Mr deer the camp hit a good balance between the two. 

It took them about 10 minutes to fill our orders, which is pretty good! If I stop by Philadephia again, I'd love to come back here.
Great food and friendly bartender.  will love to come back again.

Keep trying to make this darling restaurant work but service is SO bad, food is ok, once you finally get it (forgot we order food), wouldn't prepare it the way we wanted it, they said all Pre-prepare it, therefore can't change anymore, sliders ...

THEN

Overcharged......when we brought it up they said, "your right it is on this menu for $42 not $53"......well the menu we had it was $20 and 2 of us sawit at $20, but, they just said no you are wrong we are charging you $53!  Well you are wrong, we paid the $53 and for the 1st time in my life no tip 

 SO, after tonight, it is official, I will NEVER return...strike 4.....DONE.

Ruth's Kris PLEASE come back!

This place   was my go to in Philly every Thurs with my co-workers. I had a ball on New years Eve  def a fun atmosphere, good drinks  appetizers . I  have been there for lunch and  never had a problem . Service and good  sandwiches. I splurged and had some special hard rock burger which was delish and I don't do heavy carbs as I am always dieting and doing the low carb but sometimes u just have to splurge but I think if ur looking for a fun place , good food , and in the heart of center city its def worth going they do have specials and it's just cool place 2 relax after work or if ur in the city. 

Btw, I  also have been 2 Hard Rock in Orlando, FL and the it was on vacation and I have a great time , fun, relax and enjoying appetizers and fun drinks . So really surprised at the low reviews but both Philly and Florida were both  great . I really enjoyed all the cool memorabilia and ended up buying my ex a $400 leather jacket, as a gift . So, def a fan. Would def recommend.

This is probably one of the best experiences I have had in a cafe or restaurant ever - the man who took our order was so polite, friendly and welcoming!  He was warm and my friend and I felt very comfortable - this is a super nice cafe, homey and intimate without being overly kitschy or trying too hard.  Our beverages were expertly made and brought to our table - super nice cafe, and I look forward to visiting there again!

Tried this place twice before. Wanted to like it since it's so close to home. However the fish options are mostly frozen or low cost options.  For example tilapia,farm raised salmon, butterfish, and cod are their main entrees.
Their food is overpriced and tasteless. Their wait staff is also mixed.  Some are really helpful  and others are pushing all the options like " you want bottled water, and wheat garlic bread" which are all up charges.   Stay away from this pathetic attempt at a gourmet restaurant.

I'm traveling on business and needed a quick spa day. I saw good reviews for The Nail Lounge so I decided to try it out. Best manicure and pedicure I've ever gotten! Clean atmosphere, friendly staff, and reasonable prices!

Amazing! I would highly recommend Maynards to anyone. I came to Maynards on a  business trip and was blown away by the food, drinks and the professional staff. Our server, I believe her name was Alex, was extremely educated on their drinks (which they do a fantastic job of adding their own signature to) as well as the food menu. Simply knowing the menu is one thing- however, she made me feel as if I was talking to the head chef! If you are looking for a complete dining experience and a friendly, professional staff then look no further than Maynard's Kitchen.

Five stars for the food quality, flavor, and service.  Minus one star for accuracy and website ordering. Website ordering is very convenient and sometimes is the deciding factor of where to go. The website always has issues. Any mods to the standard and the order cannot be placed. Wanted no cheese on the salad. Would not accept order so entered salad normally and then added a note in special instructions for no cheese.  Salad had cheese. Requested ranch dressing with the wings instead of blue cheese. Got the blue cheese with the wings. 

The food was excellent minus those issues. The wings are excellent. They were not as large as previously and I got all drumsticks, no wing part but it was tasty. 

The salad was very fresh and plenty of the great ingredients. I just had to pick out the cheese. 

Got a thin with carmelized onions, zucchini, garlic, mushrooms. There weren't a lot of mushrooms but it still was great. Carmelized onions is not available everywhere and it's good, just take care in ingredient selection so the onions don't make the pie too salty. Crust was crisp and delicious. 

Meal was good, just wish for the price it would be more accurate.

Food is reasonable. Family friendly, can bring a toddler which is usually and automatic 5 star for me.  Beers are interesting.The place is unique and is a good find for a neighborhood that has a lot of duds in terms of restaurants.

I am new to the area and needed to find a new stylist/ salon.  I tried Bleu based on most of the other reviews and location.  I had a great experience here, from the front desk to my stylist( Caitlyn).  Caitlyn did a half head foil and cut on me.  The results were great!  It's probably the best foil treatment I've had.

Most of my family and friends don't consider me your typical 'manly-man'. 

Can't say that I agree with them; but it's unlikely you'd ever hear me arguing the point...mainly because I don't like confrontations.   I guess I just consider myself more 'polite' and 'respectful'...while they've been known to use terms like, 'pushover' and 'dim-wit'.  Make of that what you will...

But when it comes to automobiles:  I can hold my own with the best of 'em. 
 
First off, I've probably seen every episode of Home Improvement.  They were constantly working on hot-rods and other cars on that show.  Plus, I always paid extra attention during the special segments of Tool Time...so I practically earned an honorary degree from LincolnTech just by watching all those episodes.  

Secondly, I've seen every Fast and Furious movie...and I think that speaks for itself.  

So when my car started acting up the other day, I just assumed I could handle it myself.  Boy was I wrong!!  

I keep a toolbox in the car, so I started by grabbing a screwdriver and hammer.  Then I popped the hood (gear head lingo for 'opening the front compartment where the engine is located') and began to check things out.  Well, let me tell ya:  If you haven't done this before, be prepared to see a lot more stuff under there than you realize.  There were things spinning, vibrating and making a whole lotta noise.  I didn't know where to start!  Maybe I should've been taking notes during Tool Time.  Tim Taylor would disappointed...
Anyways, even though I didn't know where to start with my car, I did know where to start on Yelp.  I quickly did a search for 'Auto Care' and located Firestone.  I called and they said I could bring my car right over and that they'd be glad to take a look.  

That earned them star #1.  

When I arrived, I parked and walked up to the counter.  Before I could even introduce myself the guy said, "Looks like you got yourself a flat tire there."  I was blown away!  This guy wasn't even a mechanic (I don't think so, anyways) and was already diagnosing the problem.  So I gave him some basic info (both on me and my car), then he said they'd take a look.  I handed over my keys and he informed me that I could have a seat, watch some TV, read a magazine and even help myself to a cup of coffee!  In all my amazement that he'd identified my car's problem from behind the desk, I hadn't even noticed the incredible waiting area in the lobby!!!!  They really have a TV, lots of magazines and coffee!!!  What he failed to mention was that the coffee was specialty.  Not exactly sure of the make (because it wasn't Keurig) but it was a machine that makes those individual cups of coffee from packets.  There were a few different choices but I went with 'French Vanilla'.  Oooh, la, la!  

Anyways, this earned them the second star.  

After about ten minutes or so, I was called back over to the counter.  They told me that they were almost finished with my tire (they were replacing it...said it was beyond salvaging with a patch job) and wanted to know if they wanted me to do a basic check on my vehicle.  I said, 'Sure'.  

Then I sat back down to drink the rest of my coffee and watch a little more TV.  It was tuned to one of those cable news channels, so it didn't keep my attention for very long, and I wound up drifting off into deep thoughts about how 'ironic' it would've been if they were airing a re-run of Home Improvement while I was there.  

Anyways, after they had finished, I was called back over and given a full report on the work they had done.  They replaced my tire and said that otherwise, my car was in 'fantastic shape'.  They did say they topped off my windshield wiper fluid (free of charge) because I'll need it with this harsh winter weather.  So I paid my balance and then asked the cashier if I could say 'thank you' to the mechanic.  I winked at him when I said the words, 'thank you', and he said, "Sure, I guess.  I'll go call him over."  

So he called him over and I slipped the mechanic five GW's (George Washington's for anyone not on the DL).  (DL is for down-low for anyone not on the down-low).  After that, I felt like a 'manly-man', so I grabbed my keys and headed out the door.  All of that earned them stars number three and four.
  
So you might be wondering how they got that 5th and final star from me, huh?

Well, before I could make it out of the parking lot, I saw the cashier waving at me and then motioning for me to hold on a second.  Apparently, while I was too busy trying to be something I'm not (a 'manly-man'), I'd left my cellular phone on the coffee table near the TV.  So he had spotted it, grabbed it quickly and ran it outside before I left the parking lot.  Talk about phoning a friend!!!  So I thanked him and told him I was grateful for all their help and would be back again soon.  

And that's how you earn FIVE stars!

Incredible!  We lucked out on a snowy night and got a table upon arrival (no reservations accepted).  It is a cosy, comfortable restaurant with maybe 30 seats. Extremely friendly and courteous staff, encouraging you to take your time and enjoy. We had the roasted cauliflower (very tasty) and Tuna tartar as starters. The tuna was phenomenal - expertly prepared and incredibly flavorful. For mains we had the rib eye (very large, but perfectly cooked - perhaps in the top 5 steaks) and the Icelandic cod was tender and moist with just the right amount of mashed potatoes and veg. And for dessert - banana bread pudding and Franklin Fountain ice cream. We left feeling so content and happy with the entire meal!  Oh yes, and it is BYOB - it could not be better!

This is by far the fIlthiest place I've ever seen. I havE called Chipotle corporate numerous times about the horrible conditions of the kitchen, serving area and the eat in section but all they do is send free burrito coupons. Staff is unfazed by food on floor, dirty pots and  pans everywhere. I have teenagers and would love to come here several times a week but have now walked out 3x in a row. Shame on you Chipotle we're done with this company.

Last couple of reviews were concerned with lack of Covid-19 protocols. Seems they got the message. All staff was masked up correctly on our visit. 

We were greeted as we walked in. He let us know there was a store-wide sale through the end of the month. Store and outdoor lot seemed pretty well stocked. Prices were "okay" with the discounts. 

We didn't buy anything at the time because we needed to go home and take measurements. (We are in the market for larger pots for the front of our home.)

Cutest little shop I've been to in Summerland! I was invited by a friend to attend a jewelry event here. I was thrilled because I've been wanting to check this place out for the longest time. 

The staff was very welcoming and helpful not to mention the lovely spread of cheese, crackers, fruit, wine, and dessert to munch on while shopping.

I was very overwhelmed by the endless choices of charms, chains, rings, bracelets, and so on. In the end I didn't purchase anything but I have my eye on a few items that I will definitely be going back for! 

They carry unique items that you won't find anywhere else. If you happen to try on one of their beautiful handcrafted rings and you need a different size they do have more behind the counter so be sure to ask.

Be sure to sign up on their website to get a 15% discount on your first purchase at:

http://www.waxingpoetic.com/?gclid=CPbXs_XH0s0CFZNafgodw1QOfw

Many thanks to the lovely ladies working here for being so kind and welcoming. Can't wait to go back!

Favorite date night spot! This concept was genius and well-done! The theater is nice and the staff is always friendly. The best part is the food is actually really good! I had low expectations since it is a movie theater but I've loved everything and I've tried almost everything. The craft beer selection is great and a selling point for my boyfriend. This is the only movie theater we attend anymore. 

I love that they show older movies again as well!

My only complaint is that they do not have every movie and the ones they do have only show for a very limited time.
I lived here 4 years. Everyone was always very nice. Maintenance was fast. Community was safe and quiet. Price was great. Highly recommend.

Prices were decent, cost us about $30 for two. I got sweet & sour chicken, beef chow mein, and chicken fried rice. Starting with the sweet & sour chicken, it was tasty however I like my chicken to be a little crispy and this was a little soggy :/ the beef chow mein was good, the beef was a little fatty though. And the chicken fried rice was bomb. If you want Chinese food that's fast at a good price then come here!

Every time I go to NOLA, I must come here first. Always a regular spot for my wife and I.

Gumbo soup is extraordinary.  The Gator sausage was great.  After visiting 4 times, always ordering something different, I can say that this place is really good.

The service is really good as well, very attentive and if you haven't ever been there, the servers are real good about going over the menu.  A must visit for sure!

Beware: Shady Business Practices

I was in the New Port Richey store on Florida 9/29 trying to buy a lamp that was a discontinued piece.  I know it was the last one they had and I told the sales girl that I wanted to buy it right away. We were at the cash register, the order had been written up and totaled, and she was checking to see how she could proceed with the transaction even though their computers were down (how can your business be open when you can't legally do a sales transaction?) when she saw two warehouse employees carrying that particular lamp down to the customer service desk. She ran over and told them that she had just sold it. I stated out loud that whomever customer service had on the phone was in line behind me and I was there to buy the lamp first. The manager of the store, Robert Leingang, came over and told me - to my face - that the lamp was being given to another customer who needed a part from it.  I told him no, I was there first. He then told me that I would have to order it and walked away. So unprofessional and disrespectful! I couldn't believe it! He was even ignorant enough to not know that the lamp has been discontinued by the manufacturer.  It can't be ordered!  I left the store and called the corporate office and asked to speak to the customer service manager, Shannon.  She wasn't available so I had to leave a voicemail message. I was very detailed in my  reason for calling her. She called me back hours later and left the most generic, unspecific voicemail message as if both she and I were just robots and not  human beings. I have spent thousands of dollars at Kanes in the past, but I will do so no longer. Take my advice and go elsewhere.

We were too scared to go in here last time. We are stupid. This place crafted sandwiches that epic songs should be sung about by men in festive garb. The meatloaf was a magical experience- the homemade pickles were so good my dining companions asked me to stop ranting about them. Oh, the grilled cheese. They do something to it that would probably get you arrested in Utah. If you are in New Orleans and don't get a sandwich here, you are doing it wrong.
KILLER  & CHEAP got a walk in on a Thur. mornin!  top notch service & bangin tunes
They do the best dip mani in Indianapolis area. I have been looking for years and this is my favorite. You will love it

so. good. can't even begin to explain how much I love this place! i went last night and danced every time I took a bite if that tells you anything! the nachos are DELICIOUS-large portion, so definitely share. the quesadillas are not your typical quesadilla but for sure one of my favorites! the chicken enchiladas are so good also!!! the beans are a MUST. so good I would have licked the bowl!!! the classic margarita is so good!!! lastly for dessert, the mexican chocolate ice cream is a MUST! I can't say enough about how amazing this place is!!!!!!!!! it's all so so good!!!!

Wow, came in for the special prices (awesome), and actually loved the two or three meals i've had there so far.  I had the 1 lb T-Bone one Night for $8, it was awesome, only issue is they made me eat potatoes and didn't offer to substitue some lettuce instead - weird.  Also had the Ribs one night, wich were surpriseingly better than most the Rib joints in Reno, see my Rib reviews - fav so far was famous dave's.  Good price on beers here too.
--RibChamp
Just fast food sushi. Lose of quality. Walk-in hungry with more money walk-out hungry with less money. Novel concept only!

My last stop before getting to LA!  The website pictures of this place seemed grandiose.  For the most part, the entire premises had a good layout.  There was a restaurant with a bar, outdoor pool with hot tub, and nice landscaping.  My check-in packet had a voucher for a free beer, but I passed on heading to the bar downstairs (there were only 3 patrons there when I walked by).  But if I needed to catch a game and grab a drink, I might have stopped by. There were a couple of flatscreen TVs scattered around.  

The bed was very comfortable, pillows fluffy, and everything in the room was clean. There was no musty smell or any other residual smell for that matter.  Everything was what I expected of this chain however I do have one big complaint.....and that was the footsteps that could be heard above me. It didn't just sound like it was the unit above me, but coming from all areas. Maybe the adjacent room?.....hell it could have even been from the above hallway....the creaking and thumps were just coming from everywhere, with different harshness, longevity, intonation, you name it.  Maybe my neighbors above me were having a stiletto runway fashion show.  Who knows. The sounds weren't super loud....just that it was there and I figured that a property like this should have had better insulation.  Good thing I was exhausted from my 10.5 hr drive so I slept through most of it.  If i remember correctly, there was no wifi internet, but they did have internet via ethernet cable which was plenty fast.

The area is right by the entrance of the airport so the convenience factor is there.  To the hotel's credit, their complementary shuttle was always outside, waiting to take a customer to the airport.  There are a few strip malls around also so grabbing quick food isn't a problem.

Do not use this car wash. Horrible!!!! They ruined the finish on my car. I had it washed and a clear coat late in the afternoon. I got home and realized that they had not gotten some sap off and instead there was now a gooey finish on my car. I went back in the next morning and they would do absolutely nothing about it. They said I had to bring it in the same day. I asked where that had been posted? They said when I went through the sprayer it was posted. But couldn't see it plus I was expecting them to do a good job. They would not even discount another car wash. Do not use this car.

Later....I also submitted a complaint to Crew Carwash Customer Service and got a quick response. They told me to go back in and give them my name and I would get a free Ultimate car wash. I did that and there was a different Asst. Manager on duty who was very kind and I got another car wash. This time, they took double the amount of time, and used a brush, and all of the goo came off. I did not get the clear coat again. What a contrast in service!

After experiencing a 2nd run theater in Upstate NY, where my brood and I paid under $15 for admittance and all the popcorn and soda we could ingest, coming to a regular theater, where 1 ticket is $15 is a bit of a bummer.  However, I'll hate the game and not the player and say that I always enjoy coming to the Regal at Edgemont. 

And there's even a better reason to love this place, with the installation of recliner seats in at least one of the theaters. Perhaps to combat people leaving in droves to Movie Tavern and the like, Edgemont now gives you the option of watching a film in the utmost comfort possible with seats that recline to an almost bed-like position at the push of a button. Actually, right now it's not really an option. You have to be seeing the film that is in that particular room, but I'm sure they are putting the most popular film in there. 

Overall, if I have to take out a second mortgage to fund a trip to the movies , at least I can do it in comfort.

What could be better than sitting outside with wine and cheese, listening to music? Get there early to get a seat...this place is slammed by 5! Great cheese and wine section. Hidden jem for the locals in the Bywater. Casual and relaxed.

Can't wait to attend my first show here. You literally just feel the history when you walk in, a true Tucson Gem near Hotel Congress. I came to see the Cats game on a big screen and was shocked how spacious and open the main arena was. The staff was friendly and upbeat, a rare thing in Tucson if you ask me. I got to meet the manager Mark, who was really nice and told me the building was 100 years old, I love historic buildings. All the servers and barstaff were attentive. Wendy, Olivia, Sam, and Justin are my jam. Guest service literally oozing out of 'em. Gio Taco appeared to be supplying food for the event which was awesome. Will definitely be back.

I have literally never in my life given a yelp review but i've been here twice and both times it was fucking terrible. First time, I waited in the drive thru for so long and when it was time to order the guy was so rude. He also forgot to turn his mic off and he started cussing and saying how stupid his job was. I was still very nice and I pulled up to the window and he was still so rude. Didn't give me any sauces I asked for even though I asked him twice ( which he didn't even answer me he just looked at me like I was stupid ) 

Next time I went, there were about 2 people in the drive thru, and like 3 tables sat inside and a couple of pick up orders. It took 40 times for me to get my food after I parked and waited. There were a lot of staff there and barely any customers.
Shut this place down or hire new staff to get the job done.

I've been here multiple times, and it's sad that they believe they have any right to know anything about my medical status.  It used to be a fun place to go.  However, I'll never be back.  For the record, I am vaccinated but that isn't any of their business.  They are clearly anti science because at this point everyone in America has had access to a vaccine.  It's not my concern about anyone else bc the vaccine protects me.  The vaccines are for personal protection.  If you choose to be foolish and not get vaccinated, you have to live with those potential consequences.  It doesn't concern me.  It's too bad that 5 spot thinks they get to make medical choices for their patrons.  RIP Motown Monday's...I'll never come back.

One of our favorite local Thai restaurants. Really friendly staff with reliable quality. The pad see ew and massaman curry are two of our favorites. The portions are large enough to leave with leftovers. Easy to miss as its located in a strip mall. Once inside, there are about 15 tables. The restaurant has been open for as long as I can remember, which always tells you something about quality and consistency. Recommend
This restaurant needs to be shut down. F- on food quality, service is abhorrent. Do not go here if you want edible food!!!

Worst Starbucks ever. Problems with their order system supposedly. Caused all the girls that ordered after me and some other guy to get their drinks before us. Like we didn't exist the barrista kept calling out what the newest people waiting wanted. Finally we went up to the counter and made a point to demand our drinks

I attended a wedding last weekend at The Oasis and was so impressed! This is an awesome place to get married! The location is so pretty and has several areas where events take place. The ceremony was in a nice grassy area with a gazebo. The bride had a gorgeous flower arrangement decorating it, which I saw staff taking down as we were leaving the party. Staff working at The Oasis were excellent and always appeared attentive to the bride and groom! The food was the best! Best wedding dinner I've had! Steak done perfectly and chicken Marsala! Everyone that attended was raving about the dinner and the sorbet that was served after! If my husband and I hadn't eloped, this would be our place to get married! And that's saying a lot b/c we really haven't felt that way at other Tucson weddings. Highly recommend this venue. It felt like old time Tucson in the best possible way!

The server April was so great, and the older Spanish man that was assisting tables was also so friendly. I ordered the huevos rancheros special, my husband ordered the tiger breakfast. The mimosas were great as well. The kids loved their breakfast. This was walking distance from la playa hotel and will come back the next time we visit Santa Bárbara!

Every one that I interacted with was extremely friendly and helpful. Kudos especially to the agents at the Frontier counter! Other than having to pay $50 for an unaccompanied minor, the process for dropping off and picking up a minor is extremely easy. The agent even remembered us from a previous trip and part of our conversation with her.  Love the personal service!
A good spot any time of the day.  Good place for coffee, cafe food, breakfast, dinner, baked goods, people watching or just to chill.  What a coffee shop should be.

I did not have a great overall experience using this company for my new countertops in my kitchen. This was my first experience purchasing granite countertops so I was unfamiliar with this process. When they installed my countertops, they did not make it clear that I paid for a 4" in backsplash when I really wanted backsplash throughout the kitchen wall. Also, they did not inform me verbally that there would be no faucet provided or that they could provide me a faucet for an additional charge. When I voiced my concerns, I was told you should have asked about any confusion aboutthe faucet and backsplash when I placed my order. The lack of customer service in addressing my concern was horrible and makes me regret purchasing anything from this company. I would recommend any potential buyers that are purchasing granite countertops for the first time, take your money and go with their competitors because if you have an issue, it will not get resolved and they will claim it's your fault for not asking questions.

Took my new 2018 Escape with 1300 miles in this morning because I'm having problems with the electronics.  I got up late so I didn't expect to get it taken care of today, but at least I could set up an appointment.  I was told someone would be out to help me, but it didn't happen.  I saw service writers helping people who arrived after I did.  I got tired of waiting and left.  Unfortunately Tucson only has two Ford dealerships and while they have different names, they are all part of the same family so I'm stuck with using one or the other family dealerships.  I'm guessing that if I take the car to Jim Click they'll want me to take it to Holmes Tuttle since I bought the car there.

Went in for my rapid pick up, got back to the office and items were not in the bag. Chicken on my sandwich is SUPER dry. Asked for delivery of missing items, literally down the street said they couldn't get ahold of a driver? So instead of getting refunded, I get rewards and not all our food??

First time here and purchased a 6 inch cake for $15. A little pricey for such a small cake. It was very dry and the icing fell off the cake once I cut into it. The part that put me over the edge was that I found not one but two strings ( maybe coarse hair) in the cake. Very disappointing for my husbands birthday. I give two stars because the employees were friendly.

It was a mixed review kinda ordeal for us. Took the family out to eat for our daughter's upcoming 13th birthday.

My kids are simple & loved their food - but they also love McDonald's, so we don't rate places based on their opinions. ;)

I had the Napa Chicken & (upcharged) carrots. I enjoyed both, and commented that I'd have actually been happier with more artichoke, tomatoes & mushroom vegetables instead of the extra cut of chicken (even though value wise, I know the chicken is better). I just love my veggies, what can I say....  The carrots were really good, but I think charging almost $2 extra is a bit ridiculous. Taste good, cost no. 

My husband, however, was NOT so happy about his experience. Mind you, he's quite the steak conesour being that he was lead broil chef at Bern's for over 10years, but he was WAY LESS  THAN HAPPY with his steak experience. He ordered mid-rare and bottom half was completely uncooked rare. He even gave specific instructions to the waitress to send it back to the cook & have them "put it in the hottest part of the grill, this side up, for ONE MINUTE". Steak came back after about 10-15minutes, still raw in the middle.... Just now it's been covered with butter to "cover the issue". 

I heard about this fiasco for a good 45 minutes after we left. I'd have gladly paid double the $25 I did for his steak to be cooked by someone who knows what they were doing just so I didn't have to hear him complain about how horrible it was for almost an hour. 

So, yeah. There's your review.

We've made two visits to the Copper Pig and will definitely head back! On the two visits we've had both burgers, wings, beet fries and bahn mi.  The flavors were outstanding.  The presentation of all dishes was spot on.  The cocktail list was small, but well done.  They do a great blood and sand, and I enjoyed the boozy lemonade as well.

The reason for four stars instead of five is because of service. On our first visit, the service was outstanding.  Our waitress was attentive without being pushy or overbearing. She was attentive to our needs and took excellent care of us.  Our second visit was several weeks later with a slightly larger party.  We had a different server who seemed to be newer to the restaurant world.  She didn't know the menu well, and we had to ask for the specials.  Even with asking, she didn't tell us all of them.  While the service wasn't so bad as to complain, it was definitely a detractor.

Updating my review after speaking and empathizing with the owner. It says a lot about a restaurant who understands flaws and strives to work through them! We will be back to give Bridgeview another shot.

the bread and butter is always good. can't really go wrong with that. also, their croutons are super delicious! 

i ordered victoria's filet mignon and mashed potatoes. my cousin had a combo that included crab cakes, and those sounded pretty good. so, i added that to my entree. it cost $6 for two not-so-tasty crab cakes.

the service was alright. i was slightly thrown off when our waitress sat down at our table to take our order. i thought that was a little unprofessional. but, hey, what do i know?

over all, not that bad for the price. drinks during happy hour seemed really reasonable. the pictures on the wall were a bit creepy.

Really enjoyed our visit to this new gem. Soon you won't be able to get a seat as word spreads....delicious food at a good price... Nicest people working there.. Omelets are wonderful.. They even have a shrimp one!! Pancakes are light and fluffy... Kids meals are also a good price  .. Closed on Tuesdays 7-3 the rest of the week....

Ahh! I really want to give them 4 stars, but my food wasn't even hot!! And the whole ambiance was just eek...

- - s e r v i c e - -
I walked/drove by here several times throughout my stay in New Orleans and there was ALWAYS a line outside. Don't be driven away by this, they go through the line pretty quickly. Just make sure you put your name on the list at the front before getting in line. It was only about a 20 minute wait so not bad at all! Once we were seated we were greeted with the most upbeat and happy waitress (oops I forgot her name...sorry!) But she was great! Super attentive and friendly! Loved it!!

- - a m b i a n c e - -
How do they keep their line moving? Well that's cause this place is PACKED. Seats are back to back, not the most spacious setting. Also since the restaurant has multiple windows that stay open to the outdoors, you get that humidity from outside. Lastly it wasn't the cleaniest or freshest smelling restaurant. So overall with the crowdedness, humidity, and dirty smell...ehhh not my favorite environment.

- - f o o d - - 
Overall, the food was mediocore and prices were ehh a bit on the steep side for what I got...

[ [ fried alligator ] 
FREE WITH CHECK-IN! Well you can get any appetizer, but I mean when else would I be able to try alligator (you usually don't see this in California). This was good! I didn't know what to expect...so they tasted like popcorn chicken bits...not bad!

[ [ raw oysters ] ]
Good! These weren't the best oysters I've had but they weren't bad! Size wise, they were pretty small. But they fresh so you didn't get too much of that raw fishy taste. 

[ [ blackened shrimp & jambalaya ] ]
This was sooooooo good!! They are generous with the shrimp....packed with flavor (for both the shrimp and jambalaya). I really wanted to get some authentic NOLA food and yumm this was it! The jambalya was absolutely delicious! The shrimp and jambalya complemented each other great and I really liked it! I would've loved it but ughh my food came out on the cool-warm side! Why?!

[[ crawfish etouffee ] ]
Again, I loved the flavor and everything but it was cold! The gravy was great and again it came with lots of fried crawfish tails. You definitely want to eat this fast before it gets all soggy! 

Overall...I'm just torn. I loved the food, but it was cold which is so depressing. Ambiance was also just not for me, but great service. This was recommended to me by many locals so it was definitely worth the try.
Bob, the ribs and chicken were SLAMMIN' this week!  Excellent smoke on the meats and mom's mac n cheese rocked!

Cheap drinks, nice folks, and a vibe that is neither pretentious nor obnoxious. A truly great Uptown bar. They also have an internet jukebox that takes plastic, plus pool, ping pong, video poker, and most importantly pinball. Don't forget to check out the specials!

I always try to make it every year,  I've missed the last few years coming to this wonderful event, but you can take your whole family. especially if you like to see bright colorful lights.
It's a wonderful display of Christmas lights,  everyone loves to look at them from the young to , the very old & even a baby or teenagers it's beautiful.
