Amazing to see how the neighbors all come together to display set beautiful arrangements of lights. 
With so many different things it's great to walk around in or even get on the hayride or on the trolley to see the lights. 
Throughout the neighborhood they sell hot chocolate or my favorite, kettle corn. Don't forget play some non-perishable food because they do have boxes where you can drop off food it's always great to donate

LOVE that i finally have a local bagel place now in nolibs, great location too

i ordered my bagel and matcha latte all online and went to go pick it up in about 20-30 minutes. there's some tables inside as well as one outside

the bagel is perfectly priced and DELICIOUS! i got the cheddar habanero bagel with bacon and scallion cream cheese. holy shit - so delicious. great flavors and that cream cheese to bagel ratio IS WHAT IT ALWAYS SHOULD BE

the matcha latte isn't cheap but it's good matcha - i could tell bc i couldn't sleep (i drank this at 12pm) and had to take melatonin at midnight and only good matcha does that to me 

all in all, i will be back and can't wait to try the other cool bagel and cream cheese flavors they had!

I had a hoot. If you're coming in here expecting the choicest cuts of the freshest fish handled expertly by a craftsman who has dedicated his life to nothing but the noble art of the sushi forge you're probably out of luck. But it's good.

In their AYCE selection, deep frying and cream cheese and mayo and crunchies and cooked things are all there to make a non-sushi person feel comfortable. This is the place you take your cousin from Wisconsin who's vaguely scared of raw fish. It's a Fischer-Price My First Sushi experience. It's sushi on training wheels. And it's also a guilty pleasure for those of us who *have* had a taste of Jiro's Dreams (or close). 

Sometimes you really want to savor a bite of perfection: to experience a lifetime's dedication to one trade crystallized in the form of a single maki. 

And then sometimes you want to shove 40 rolls down your food hole and have a good time. Which is what I did.
Wonderful place! Wine, cozy atmosphere, hot stone/mask/creams/scrubs/campus remover, and all the trimmings included . Great massage

Tchoup yard is a great new business. I had the pleasure of hosting a small event with them and it was a hit. Robert (one of the co-managers) was super easy to get a hold of and great to work with. The bartenders took extra care of my group and even went out of their way to run drink orders for people seated! We had ~50 people turn up and there was no shortage of space. Their drink selection was really good too and well worth the price.

This is a nice, small restaurant.  We went at 2:00 p.m. on the Monday after Christmas.   There were only 3 parties seated, which isn't unusual for this time of day.  There was 1 waitress and one chef (I think).  Our waitress was lovely.  She was gracious, helpful, and attentive even though there was one couple that was mostly monopolizing her time, asking a lot of detailed questions and switching tables mid-meal (?).   They constantly were waving her over but she handled it well and managed to meet our needs and the other large party (6-8) very well.

My companion ordered the meatball sandwich and I ordered the Mediterranean Veggie Sandwich.   We overheard the waitress mention how much she liked the soup of the day, a grilled tomato soup.  As it was cold outside, we both ordered the soup.   We were so glad we did.   The soup was outstanding!  The soup was clearly homemade, using very fresh ingredients.  I think the tomatoes are roasted or charbroiled as there were tiny bits of roasted tomato in the pureed soup.  My companion detected hints of roasted red pepper sweetness, and there was clearly a don't-worry-about-counting-calories amount of cream.  The soup is served in a very small bowl with a toasted garlic baguette slice on top.  It was stupendous.  Had I known how delicious it was, I would have skipped the sandwich and had a big bowl of soup.   As it was, I finished the soup far before I finished the sandwich and I just wished I had more.

The sandwich was absolutely delicious.  It included roasted peppers, feta, crisp red onion, and artichoke hearts on a sandwich roll.  It was lovely, and just the right amount of food.   I also had hot tea, which was lovely.  The waitress offered 4 different varieties including lemon, earl grey, and green tea.  I forget the 4th option. The Meyer lemon tea was by Stash and was yummy though I wished the water were a touch hotter as it had cooled significantly by the end of the meal.  The waitress did offer more hot water so really it's my own fault I didn't take her up on it. 

My companion had the meatball sandwich.  He said it was yummy.  The meat was finely ground and well seasoned.  There was exactly the right amount of homemade (!) marinara sauce.   My companion rarely brings home leftovers but definitely asked for a to-go box for the remainder.  Between the sandwich and soup, there was plenty of food.

The food is served on orange and gold fiesta ware, which seemed a bit odd since this is an Italian restaurant, but for me it was a quirky detail that seemed charming.  There are italian posters decorating the walls.  The restaurant is on the small side but seemed very comfortable with only 3 parties being served on a late Monday afternoon.  There is one co-ed restroom.  For gatherings, there is an alcove sort of area near the restroom which seated a big party of 6-8.   If the Stuffed Olive ever moves to a larger location, I recommend a separate room for large parties
INCREDIBLE food!  The staff is so hardworking and the food is absolutely exceptional.  The shrimp quesadilla is my go-to.

Honestly, I don't get the appeal. Truly one of the most annoying places to go to. Every time I come back to try and give it a second chance, I'm always reminded why i always say I'm never coming back here. Whether it's the bitchy hostesses who act like their job is more important than curing cancer or the wait staff who couldn't find their way out of a wet paper bag. Two nights ago I ordered a steak dinner (medium rare) and the food runner just dropped it off and went on without giving me a knife. Took about 10 minutes before the server came back where I had to ask her for a knife and then an additional 5 more for her to come back with it. Do you think my medium rare steak was still medium rare?

This new place took over the Crispers  spot next to TJ Maxx.  They have 10 banh mi sandwiches to choose from.  We chose the grilled pork.  It came warmed on a french baguette with fresh vegetables-cucumber slice, carrots, jalapeño, cilantro and mayo.  The pork was seasoned with teriyaki and was nicely flavored.  They also had a nice selection of bubble tea.  We had the peach black tea with strawberry popping boba and peach green tea.  Both delicious.  On to dessert...they had too many to choose from!  They had a refrigerated case with everything from tiramisu, to chocolate cake, to cheesecakes, to macaroons.   In the bakery case, which they claim is freshly baked everyday,  they again had a wide variety of croissants, all flavored danishes and other pastries.   We chose a refrigerated dessert, Love Heart and 3 different pastries...delish!  Along the wall with the danishes, they offered all kinds of packaged snacks. They staff was welcoming and friendly.  Only down side, I wished I could have had the pastry warmed with a cup of coffee...other than that, me and my daughter will definitely return to try other items on their menu.

Very good food. Had the medallions  of fillet and it was perfect. The following night I had the salmon, also very good. Both highly recommended. 
The bisque soup was tasty and also the house salad. The only reason I don't rate 5 stars is that the desserts weren't as good as the other dishes. I had the lime pie and the coconut bread , both nice but not excellent. The service was awesome, very friendly and welcoming. Very good recommendation from the waitresses. 
Overall I recommend this place, very clean, beautiful and great food.

This place is so adorable. I love the decor and atmosphere. It seems like a newly opened small business and the woman working the front was so sweet. I had the cinnamon roll which is like a mix between a cinnamon roll and croissant and I loved it. I also got a vanilla iced latte. The espresso was perfect. Some coffee shops have espresso that tastes burnt but this place knows how to do it. I will definitely be back!

We went for a quick dinner as we're traveling and  are leaving in the morning.  We both ordered the brisket tacos with sides.  The good is that the baked beans, cole slaw, and broccoli were good and typical sizes for most bbq places.  The bad is that the four tacos were extremely skimpy - barely a tablespoon or serving spoon of brisket in each.

If you're hungry, I wouldn't recommend this place.

Loved Burger Up! The best service I've experienced anywhere in awhile (shout out to Hope, our server). So friendly and welcoming--definitely added to the dining experience. 

The burgers were delicious, a perfect juicy patty and great toppings. The ketchup and aioli both taste homemade, and the truffle fries were fantastic! My friend ordered a salad and it looked massive--and really good. You can order a salad and have a burger patty as a topper!

I can't wait to return to try more on the menu!

I've been a loyal customer of this restaurant for over a decade. Like many Covid has taken its toll.  Servers could work at Waffle House , manager is the same .  There is no more personal service.  It's very sad. It starts with degradation of management. I need to find a new "go to" restaurant .  It's a super spreader for Covid as they pack as many in as they can fit. Don't pay the prices for a top drawer restaurant. I'll be surprised if they make it through 2021 .
Super friendly and welcoming during my 11 pm run to pick up 3 fairly random items. So happy to have a 24 hour option so close to home!

Nice place. The Make a grown man cry breakfast was superb.  Highly recommend 

It was hard to pick a meal it all sounds so good. Tyler H our server answered ALL my questions and gave recommendations based on my likes. He was attentive and a genuinely nice guy. Ask for him.

Let me preface this by saying I'm rating Bierstube on the German food.  If you're looking for beer or a tavern, I think many would be happy.

We both had the wiener schnitzel plate --- there were 2 cutlets per order, so we could've shared.  The schnitzels were not pounded as thin as I was expecting; they were thick.  It was more like 2 breaded boneless pork chops.  The potato salad is not German potato salad with German vinegar --- it has mayonnaise and paprika.  The blaukraut was not bad, but it was not sweet/sour like I'm used to.

The place is dark -- and what you'd expect for a pub or tavern.  If you're expecting a German restaurant, this is not the place.

Oh Yelp, why didn't I check you first, like I always do. $6 will get you a small sad pathetic breakfast wrap and another $6 will get you two artificial looking chicken sliders. Skip this place for breakfast and go to Chic fil A.

while I was there, I asked one of the male employees if I can try on some clothes. He rudely said fine and grabbed the keys to the changing room. After 3 minutes he knocked on the door and yelled "hey! you need to hurry, there is someone else trying to use the room." Wow, I did not know 3 minutes was such a big deal. Whatever I got out and told him he didn't have to be so rude. Again, with attitude "I don't have time to talk to you. If you want to buy these I'll be more than happy to help you check out at the front." Needless to say I didn't buy anything and left.

Hotel St. Louis, one of the Marriott Autograph Collection hotels, is a beautiful renovation of an older-style downtown St. Louis building. It's not quite as refined as I'm used to Autograph properties being, but the in-room bathroom is absolutely to die for.

Do be aware that parking is at a premium in this area (especially overnight), so you'll pay a decent chunk for parking. They offer valet parking as well (for a very small fee).

PROS:
-THE BATHROOM. Seriously, I could talk all day about the bathroom. Heated toilet seats and built-in bidets? Ugh. Love. A shower that's so large and beautiful that I can stand in it and not be able to touch the wall on any side? Amaaaaaazing. A TV built into the smart mirror? Ample shelves along the shower walls for all the stuff? The best part of the room, by far, is the bathroom. The rest of the hotel room is still amazing, but that bathroom is going to stand out for eternity. 
-Lots of natural light. Your hotel room is going to look gorgeous with all of the curtains open.
-Instead of water bottles, Hotel St. Louis offers free reusable metal bottles with a sign that says St. Louis has amazing tap water. Appreciate the conservation effort - and the tap water IS actually really good. I'd appreciate a note on the signage that said whether the bottles were clean or not, though. 
-Free in-room WiFi is able to handle streaming shows decently well - as long as you give it a little bit of time at the beginning to buffer the show.
-Bed is super comfy. It's definitely on the firmer side, but it feels so comfortable to sleep in. 
-Air conditioning and heating work fantastically and cool the room nicely. Perfect for those darn warm summer days. 
-The main lobby offers four elevators. I can't say I've waited more than 30 seconds for an elevator - and that's with a packed hotel. 
-Staff was all super friendly. Valets (and there are a freakishly large amount of them working at any given time?) always opened doors. Front desk staff would greet you as you walked by. Housekeeping staff would say hello if you were walking down the halls at the same time. 
-Speaking of housekeeping, they always leave the room spotless. They try to come once earlier in the day and actually will come back later if they're unable to clean in the morning. 

CONS:
-The Hotel St. Louis doesn't feel quite as "refined" as other Autograph hotels we've been to. It's still freakin' beautiful, but it lacks the beautiful themes and unique offerings that we've seen at other Autograph hotel offerings - such as the starry/planetary theme of the one in Columbus or Cleveland's only indoor dog park at their Autograph hotel.
-The hotel is not in a bad area nor in a good area of the downtown. Go down one street and things will start to look pretty updated and kept up. Go down another street, and it's only abandoned building after abandoned building. Depending on your comfort level in downtown city areas at night, I'd be careful if solo. 
-A lot of the buildings near this hotel are abandoned, gutted, and not-that-pretty to look at. Depending on what room you're in or what hallways you walk in, you'll get the "privilege" of looking down at tons of garbage and molding walls in other buildings on the way to your room. Our room had views of an 8-story parking garage.
-There is not a SINGLE vending machine in the entire building - nor a downstairs 'stuff you need' hotel shop like some hotels tend to do. This is frustrating for a couple of reasons: a) if you're looking for food/drinks without leaving, you're forced to use their $3-$8 in-room beverages or $5-10 in-room food snacks or b) if you're looking for medication (Advil, etc.), you're forced to head out into the downtown city, probably get lost a bit, and try to find a drugstore. This is silly and seems like an intentional oversight on the hotel's part.

My wife and I ate here this afternoon. Food was fantastic. Wonderful service as well. The young lady who took our order was very nice. Explained to us how the food was made. Another employee stopped by our table and asked us how our food was. Very impressed and will definitely be back. Would definitely recommend.

To start with we reserved a place for 20-25. The bartender is our server. The music is great but the cover was a surprise. The food is not authentic and my meal and many others was actually lukewarm if not chilly. This lady has no since of timing or urgency and apparently can't multi-task/delegate. She even had the nerve to say; sarcastically mind you ~" this is only my second day". No it's not. Having been in the industry a loooong time I know a newbie.  I'm hoping this was just an isolated incident but I won't be back to find out.

I love Panera and I have said it a thousand times! They have great sandwiches, salads, soups, and bakery items that I routinely order for my Medical offices when doing a breakfast or Lunch training. 

My most favorites are the Winter squash soup and the Fuji apple salad add avocado.

But today I saw this pineapple, strawberry, etc., salad that looked fabulous and had to try it. It was good, but not as tasty as I was expecting it to be.

This place FAILS on so may levels.


They DO NOT HAVE the NBA League Pass or NHL Center Ice!


despite the lit-up sign to the contrary out front.  They will even say they do if you call them on the phone but THEY DO NOT HAVE either.

Some nice ass cheeks here & there but do not go here to watch an out of town game.

Strike two! 

I keep trying this place and they just can not seem to get it right, or even close.. which is sad, b/c I LOVE Smash Burger.  Today, I thought I would give this place a shot using the Grab app (which is a great app).  I kept it simple, a vanilla shake.   The app said it would be ready in 20 minutes.. I figured it would be a long line.. I showed up a few minutes a later and there was no one in line,  just one guy waiting for an order. 

A staff person was loading up a shake to mix. I was encouraged. Then he went back to cook , leaving a well meaning , but inexperienced person alone at the counter.  Ten minutes later my shake was still whirling away...a manager type came out and I had to ask her to pour the shake.. She said it was "almost" done.. we all were amused I told her I had been watching it whirl for nearly 10 minutes... she reluctantly poured it.

No surprise, it was barely a milkshake and not even cold anymore .
This place has everything that Vegas does not have, to bad I can't find anything over there. I have found that I was looking for and so did my wife and she wants everything that she can carry.
This is the best Sushi in Tampa. Always fresh, great atmosphere, friendly and efficient staff.

Would be 0 stars stay away thieves steal ur money do not refund it don't even give you temp tag tell u u are approved then change the story weeks later after you have the vehicle then send u threats saying u r goin to report it stolen when u have insurance n ur name and proof you paid them your dwn payment please .....i never do social media these ppl pretend to be good they post there own reviews on internet saying they are good u can tell on posts all the ones with a by it are them under Nicholson reviews most of ppl show there pics by they reviews gave them bad posts I wish I reviewed this before I went there now I gotta do all kinds of stuff to get my money back  so be warned .....

Our family loves Mama's Pizza! We have never had a bad experience at this location. We have attended in big groups as well. We enjoying sharing this place with friends & family. Food comes our quickly & hot even of busy nights! 

The breadstix are one of my favorites in town! Always come out so hot you can touch them for the first few minutes. Cheesy garlic bread also good. 

The pizza this is so good! They have a family size pizza yes it's around $34 but so worth it! We have had 10 people eat off it!  1 slice more than fills you up! I have to have ranch with mine. The ranch is so good & thin. 

The Cannoli's here are also delicious. Honestly, I haven't had anything here I haven't enjoyed.

Edomasa is a great sushi place in Santa Barbara, especially for a late night meal! The fish is fresh and delicious and the service is wonderful. The staff is friendly and even the sushi chefs are pleasant to speak to with.

 It can be a little crowded at times and the fact that it's a relatively small restaurant, it can get LOUD and HOT. 

Some of my favorite Special Rolls would have to be the Deluxe Buffalo Volcano, Rainbow Roll and the Santa Barbara Roll. For just two bucks more you can have almost any roll deep fried for some extra mouth watering goodness! 

My buddy is vegan and he really enjoyed the Vegetable Combo Roll (excluding the miso soup) as well! 

If I ever have to work late and crave sushi, this place always comes to mind. One of the best sushi restaurants in town!
2nd time there..it's great.  easy take-out option is a plus

My wife ANITA set up my 70th surprise birthday party at Cafe Antonio. Maurice is the manager she spoke to and he arranged a private room for the event. Upon my arrival I was shocked to see my relatives and close friends. The service was great and the attention to detail was great. The cuisine was excellent, salad hot bread and butter and the main entrees were very tasty. Everyone love the restaurant and the great service they received. Many of the guest said they will come back to Cafe Antonio in the near future. My wife received many accolades for her choice of the restaurant.

Located in the Ace hotel with a warm and welcoming feel about this place. We waited about 4 mins to be seated with a great window spot and we were very keen to try it out. 

We started with the cheese plate which about 4 types of cheeses, chutney, candied pecans and crackers. We tried the green salad and although not my taste, my wife enjoyed it. Next up was the pasta, we had the lamb pappardelle and the cacio e pepe. Both were on point and full of flavour. 

There was a slight mishap with the pappardelle so they brought us a cherry ice cream on the house which was delicious. 

A great spot with some equally great & delicious food. Check them out for yourself.

Gang this place is legit. There are not that many Indian grocery stores in Center City so I had to go out to West Philly for it. It's a no frills grocery store that has everything you could want for indian cooking, and has a dhaba restaurant in the back. Imagine a home-cooked indian meal, without the creaminess of more Americanized restaurants. I've searched long and hard for authentic chai, and they make a fantastic one. A cup only costs $2! If only other places closer to me picked up on the recipe.

My favorite place to hang out in the summer. Games, food, activities, and more....it's great. They had a beer garden one night which was a lot of fun. Great food trucks every night. Glad that Philly is adding more great things for residents to do here.

We had an awesome stay here. Very updated for such an old hotel. They had a nice variety of coffee and teas downstairs. We were disappoint by the fact that they only had valet parking, we had to pay each night. Also, when we checked in, they didn't mention that there were bikes for us to use. We would've had a much better experience if we new about those bikes. Overall, it was a good choice. Very close to everything and lots of places to eat.

My husband LOVES this place! When it was in Hudson, a 45 minute drive from our Palm Harbor home, he would find any excuse to get his hot dogs here. During the pandemic, he would get his hot dogs and drive 10 minutes to Hudson Beach to eat - savoring every bite. In their new location, Roger's is only 25 minutes from home, and he is ecstatic. He took a friend here yesterday, and they both enjoyed their lunches. We went together Monday to get two pizzas, on the way to a friend's house. 

I can't comment on Husband's experience yesterday, but I CAN say the pizzas were very good Monday night. When we got to our friend's home in Spring Hill, we had to warm them up and they were fine. Better than fine! 

Roger's was not accustomed to making a white pizza without sauce (cream and tomato sauces upset my stomach), but they made it with just the mozzarella, garlic, oregano, olive oil and spinach. My friend and I loved it! I took the leftovers home, and last night I enjoyed the last 4 slices. My husband enjoyed his sausage pizza Monday night, and his leftovers last night. 

Husband will continue to come here, with or without me, but after Monday night's pizza, I hope to accompany him more often.

Fat Dans is a great atmosphere with lots of good sports and Cubs memorabilia . And I give it in A for the fries and bbq sauce. Wait staff very friendly and attentive! Suggest to anybody traveling or in the area! And my son loved the root beer

Not sure why this restaurant can seat 750 they weren't even full and turned people away saying it was a two hour wait to be seated then 25-45 minutes longer for your food to arrive because their kitchen could not keep up with the volume.  Those words came out of the manager's mouth.  Sounds like they need a new manager and a lot more staff.  We walked out because it was a total mess in there.

Dealing with Defender Security has been an absolute nightmare.  My Pulse arming/ disarming feature has stopped working, but the camera feature works just fine.  I've had two (one a lead) technicians come to the house to take a look at it- and both techs said that the issue is not something they have the capability to fix.  Both the techs, and other service members they called while in my house, agreed that this is a one-off issue that they have no resolve for.  

I've been calling, and calling, and calling, Defenders since June trying to get this issue resolved.  Since I've been paying extra for a feature that is not fully functioning, I asked Defenders to provide me with an outdoor camera to make up for the payments toward Pulse I've been making- since the camera feature works just fine on my Pulse, this would make up for the non-functioning arm/disarm feature.

The manager I spoke to on the phone was less than helpful, giving me the run around, trying to troubleshoot (even though I suggested she look back into the records to see the millions of times I've already tried troubleshooting).  Then, tells me that their Defenders provider in the area "temporarily does not have outdoor cameras in stock".  Okay great, so my next question was, "Well, can they order one from another branch"- and the answer was "no, they cannot, they don't have outdoor camera service yet".  Okay so, wrong use of the word "temporarily" if they've never had the capability to begin with.

Defenders has been taking my money for a service that they are not providing due to whatever the communication error is between my Pulse app and panel.  They are also offering no real solutions to the problem.  The manager (still not understanding I've been dealing with this issue for MONTHS) said they'll send another tech out.  Great, another tech who will most likely not be able to help (since the other two couldn't do anything), and I'm right back at square one.  

I would never, EVER, recommend Defenders to anybody who cares about their money being put to services that actually work.  Talking to the Defenders staff has left me with many questions about how they were hired in the first place (sounding confused, saying they're transferring me and hanging up, offering no real solutions to a persistent problem).  ADT should be ashamed of themselves for contracting with this company, it makes the ADT brand look absolutely terrible and has me looking to transfer to a different home security company.

Service (hostess staff) was very slow (absent) for nearly 10 min upon arrival, but once seated and we got going, things were great. Good service after that and great food. Loved the calamari and grilled crab cake dinner.

I am a retired occupational therapist. I have suffered from spinal stenosis for many years.  I went to Action Therapy for physical therapy since I had gotten recommendations from my colleagues.  Action Therapy was mentioned being one of the best places to get Physical therapy in the area.
From my experience with them, I am now referring my friends and colleagues to them. I found them to be extremely caring and knowledgeable .I was complaining about my hand. The therapist questioned what my pain was from. She questioned if it was from the Spinal Stenosis or something else. She wanted me to get an evaluation from a hand therapist to make sure that the pain was from my spinal stenosis .She helped me not only as a diagnosis but as a whole person, Action Physical Therapy also followed up by sending me helpful information to remind me of specific exercises to help me with my problems. I highly recommend them as the best!

Long story short: Philly cheese steak is good, roast pork sandwich is terrible. 

I really don't understand how the John's Roast Pork is considered this place's specialty. It's a strange combination of lukewarm pork, cheese, and spinach where the flavors are nothing but salt and the textures are odd. Frankly, this mix doesn't mesh well together.

The cheese isn't melted and it's chunks of cheese thrown haphazardly on top of roast pork. Maybe this sandwich used to be good? But the customer volume is now so overwhelming that the quality is rushed.

While the roast pork is disappointing, the Philly cheesesteak is actually quite nice! The cheese is melted well among the cheesesteak and the best part is the seeded bread-- it's soft yet crispy, very good. The Philly cheese steak is almost able to balance out the lackluster roast pork sandwich.

Food was only ok, but atmosphere and beer selection was great. The good seemed to be too uppity for my family's tastes... Nothing really down to earth.  Honestly, I can get good beer at a lot of places, so we've never gone back...
Torani syrups, 2 for $12.00. Good deals on some good looking leather furniture, too.

Never really stop by Nordstrom's Cafe, but WOW! The food and ambiance is fantastic. The patio has a phenomenal view of state street as well as the shopping center. The customer service here is just as great! Our server Victoria was so sweet and darling, definitely recommend to everyone!

Always busy there but the last couple of visits my car interior just isn't getting clean. Dirt left on the doors, carpets and seats, very disappointed.  For the $25 I spent (including tip) I expect better. I won't go back anytime soon.

Went with the house salad, the porchetta pizza with arugula and the side cars.   Everything was amazing.   The lemon vinaigrette was perfect.   The garlic fondue side car was heavenly.   You could taste the roasted garlic, the salty flavor, a little sweet.   I could eat that on anything.   
I was in and out within 15 minutes and they were quite busy.   
Highly recommended

Boy oh boy! One of the greatest lunches I've had in my gosh dang life. Amazing ambiance, stellar service from my man Matt, and food that will send your tastebuds on the rollercoaster ride of their lives! This is a meal you do not want to miss!

I don't know about their Hibachi but the sushi is inedible. I've had all-you-can-eat sushi in NYC for $5 more then what this place charges and it was excellent but this certainly wasn't worth it.  The server was nice and the environment was fine but I should have known when the menu didn't list unagi (eel, which I've never not seen at a sushi place) that this place was "a little different." My wife and I just kept ordering, wanting to get our money's worth, so we tried pretty much everything, thinking it would get better and it didn't. I ended up eating most of the stuff since my wife couldn't stomach it -- still hungry, she decided to get something "safe" which was supposed to be sweet and sour chicken. This is what she got. YIKES!

Let me start by saying I have to give this 1 star to write a review. 0 isn't an option or else that would absolutely be what I would give it. Next let me say you should scroll through and read some of the most recent negative reviews. They are all spot on. To sum up our experience briefly, when we arrived for our 7:15pm reservation we were told by the hostess that per management they could not seat our party and that they were on a "hold" for the "foreseeable future" due to "back of house issues". However, they DID offer us availability to order food and drinks at the bar. Makes no sense, wouldn't ordering food from the bar still impact their "back of house" issues? Less than 5 minutes later they were suddenly able to seat us. We sat down and it took 20 minutes for a server to arrive, an hour to get a bottle of wine, 2 hours for dinner to arrive and when it did come we only got 3 out of our 4 meals. The GM Curtis did speak to us briefly but it was clear this situation was not new and frankly he didn't really seem too bothered by any of it. He didn't go out of his way to make any of it better, instead just whispered to our table that he was "sorry." We spent $400 on that experience. After leaving I glanced at the recent reviews and saw that this seems to be a weekly occurrence for them. Save yourself the money, go anywhere else (literally anywhere) but here.
Great place near home if you need gas in a hurry!!! Oh, and cheap beverages as well.  That's all I got folks!!

I hired Buzzettas to cater my rehearsal dinner BBQ at Mercer County Park West Picnic Area. They did an amazing job -- the food was plentiful and very delicious. All my guests loved it. Everyone was very friendly and helpful in correspondence leading up to the event and during the event. I highly recommend Buzzettas, especially for a Mercer County Park event, as they know seem to know the setup there well.
A nice quiet tucked away place that serves breakfast lunch and dinner, the breakfast is better than average, with possibly the best French toast in St Louis.

Some of the best queso and guac in town. If you enjoy fancier Mexican food with a trendy setting, this is your place. Taco Tuesday is very clubby if you arrive past 8 and good luck getting a table. Regardless, it's still a blast!  I enjoy brunch which starts at 11 on Saturday and Sunday.  They get busy for brunch too so reservations on open table or by text are good.  They also have a cute room you can rent for private parties.  I love the Green Lemon but it can be loud and I would say it is not family friendly because there is not a lot of room to park strollers or high chairs.

Very Nice Place...  I had the 60 minutes deep tissue massage and I can't remember a time when I felt so good... he was super amazing and thoughtful. I would highly recommend the deep tissue massages expecially for mothers with small children like myself. I'm so relaxed and ready to take on the week. VERY happy with my experience here

Jason did a great job on my hair! Great color, low lights, highlights and cut. He gave me a consultation first then came up with something I love! Highly recommend this place! Has a great atmosphere and everyone was very friendly and highly talented.

Cost of Work:
- $2500

Reason for Work:
- Roof leaking around chimney

Work Performed:
- Chimney repairs
- New gaskets around pipes extruding from roof
- Front steps repaired and coated
- Front porch coated to match steps

The good:
- Price: Did a fair bit of work for a fair price and threw in some extra things for no charge
- Friendliness: Crew was friendly and the quality of the work look good on cursory inspection
- Punctuality: Showed up at agreed upon time and completed it that day

Too Early to Say, will Update:
- I cannot assess the quality yet as it is early, and only time will tell. The primary problem was a leak that is hard to assess as it only occurred during a heavy rain. Hopefully, they fixed the root problem and no more leaks.

Opportunities for Improvement:
- Payment: Cash only. I prefer to pay with a card even if that means paying a few % extra for CC fees for piece of mind and security.
- Urgency: The act now, I can get you in tomorrow, must be available strategy creates unnecessary stress when the repairs are not an emergency.
We got the steak and eggs and the sage apple French toast. Both dishes were delicious. The service was awesome. I would recommend this place for brunch.

Everything was great! Appetizers, entrees, and beer all delicious. Service was on point. I remember moving the last of my chili fries to a smaller plate and no sooner then when I'm done someone came by and asked to remove the plate. My Banh Mi was delicious and my fiancée's turkey club was as well. Thank you!

Excellent service!!  The customer service is wonderful! I had trouble designing my husband's promotion party cake.  They had fantastic ideas and created a beautiful cake that also tasted amazing.  The cookie suggestion was a huge hit!!  I definitely will refer this Bakery to everyone!
The menus have pretty much everything you need to satisfy your fast food hunger especially if you are a vegetarian.

I would not suggest moving here. I lived in these apartments for about 2 years. My AC broke maybe 10 times a year, the apartments are not insulated well, so in the summer it would reach 85° in the apartment shortly after the AC would brake. The place is also INFESTED with roaches. When I say infested I mean INFESTED. I was killing upward of a dozen roaches a day. I always kept my place super clean in hopes to keep the roaches away but it never worked. I called the front desk to complain several times and all they do is send some guy in to "spray" but that never was effective. They were in my food, my pots and pans, dishes, you name it. Not an hour went by when I didnt see a roach. It got to the point where my partner and I decided to brake our lease 3 months early because of the state of disgust we were in. Everyone in my building complained of the same issue and 3 tenants (not including me) all moved out in the same week ALL FOR THE SAME ROACH ISSUE! The roaches were so bad I was considering calling the health dept before quickly finding a new place to live. The dog park is also infested with flees and ticks so unless your pet is on some good flee/tick prevention medicine I suggest you avoid that area.

The management company changes every 3 months and so does their payment system, rules and FEES! You cant get a hold of the staff and we even had to message one of them on their personal Instagram account to get them to call us back after leaving many voicemails and waiting almost 4 days to hear back. When we went to brake the lease the office had no idea where our lease was and to this day we have not yet received it. 

The maintenance staff also threatens the residents with fines when they put in requests. (Usually repeat requests that they never fixed in the first place) and if they told you theyd be at your door in an hour, go ahead and just wait 2-3 hours because thats how long they usually took.
 
The space and layout was perfect for me and my partners first "solo" apartment. However, this place is not the same as it was when we moved in two years ago. The place now looks horrific (amenities, yard maintenance, etc) and the management LOCKS ONE ENTRANCE on the weekend. Yes, thats correct, they forbid their paying residents from using a side gate for no reason whatsoever, adding an extra 5 minutes to my commute home.
Excellent, knowledgeable service and great prices.  Check out the redesigned website too! Looks Great! http://www.recycledcycles.net

I guess I was expecting an epic sandwhich shop when I went here, but sadly everything was just very blah. I will commend posh nosh for having a variety of sanwhich offerings, but none really wet my palate. 

I orderd a turkey sandwhich with lettuce, tomato and onion and my bf got the veggie sandwhich. Mine was bland with very large and thick pieces of turkey and my bfs was uninspired. The veggie sandwhich was totally an afterthought (just random veggies like greenpeppers and onions on some lame bread). 

I prefer Pickles in the CWE over Posh Nosh any day and for that, I will not be back here anytime soon.

Best froyo in Reno, hands down.
The staff here rocks.  They're always super helpful, and very friendly.  They have such an amazing assortment of toppings and yogurt flavors-- which is probably why my yogurt always ends up being over $5, ha ha.

Without a doubt my favorite nursery in the Tucson area! Not only are the plants stunning but the staff are incredibly knowledgeable and go out of their way to be helpful and make sure that you are successful in your gardening. Being new to gardening in the southwest, they helped me navigate all of the gardening differences.  They have delivered and planted trees for us as well, always doing amazing work! I highly recommend them!

Horrible service, inexperienced nail techs, do not do what you ask them to do! The male that works there is very very rude! Saw him screaming at another girl in her face because of a mistake he made when I was getting my nails done

The check-in experience was fairly simple even though we had to park on the street and walk past a cloud of smoke from some of the workers.  The agent went thru the rules, said to bring the car back with a full tank but never once mentioned bringing the actual receipt.  Ok fine, we are in the habit of keeping receipts anyway but when we did return the car, the woman already had an attitude when we walked in the door.  She was rude and inappropriate and complaining about another customer to us (apparently, he didn't have his fuel receipt).  She charged us twice and issued a refund that she said couldn't post right away.  The car itself was fine.  Overall, it was less than lovely experience, and we will not be back to this location again.

We have been coming here for Salsa Lessons on Thursdays (our instructors call it Street Salsa because they are not too strict on formalities). It costs 8 bucks to get in and you get about an hour and a half to two hours of instructions. There is a beginner and advanced course. THE DANCE FLOOR IS HUGE! 

My fiancee and I are in our early thirties, but the all ages, young and old, are welcomed at the lessons. There is a cash bar, and the bar tenders are so friendly, they already know our drinks. More importantly, having a drink or two definitely makes dancing the strangers more fun. I say strangers because they make your switch partners during the lessons. DO NOT BE SCARED AWAY BY THIS! I was nervous at first because I am a terrible dancer, but now I love it! 

i am sitting here at my desk typing this review and doing some sitting salsa steps, just thinking about it has got me going! Seriously, this is great fun and anyone not coming is missing out! I believe there are other types of dance on different days. GET YOURSELF DOWN TO THE GULFPORT CASINO AND DANCE YOUR PANTS OFF... well maybe keep your pants on, but yeah, it is just fun to say dance your pants off! Maybe bring that guy or gal you have your eye on and the dancing... well... YEAH! 

Also, we know some people that have had weddings here and everyone raves about this unique venue, it would make for a great wedding. 

As a side note, supposedly there are some pretty rare pokemon around the Gulfport Casino, so after you get done dancing, you can go outside and try to catch em all. Or if you don't want to nerd-out, then go to O'Maddy's and get the BEST RBK (BEEF ON WECK) EVER!!!!!! You have been advised!

My boyfriend and I always crave Vietnamese food (mainly me). However, we don't eat a lot. I can barely finish my meal and I end up wasting it. This place lets us get one entree and share it!! It's really really fills us up. We order something on the stir-fry section and get an extra side of chow mein. Its dang good food for lunch. There's also 20% for locals and students, so it helps out as broke college students. We will be coming back :)
This place is always packed and is always loud. Some people enjoy that so however you take that can persuade your visit. The drinks were very delicious though and the food is eh...okay.

What a find! Located in a strip mall that would be otherwise nondescript, this little cafe does a great business with tasty food, friendly service and outdoor seating. Offering just about anything you would want for a substantial breakfast including fresh OJ, the cafe also has a nice array of tacos for lunch - with homemade tortillas to boot. I was impressed at the flavor and quality - would definitely stop by again.

Definitely one of the better China buffets in town. Their chicken, beef, and shrimp were all hot and fresh. As usual I avoid the deserts at these restaurants, so if you stick to the main dishes, you should enjoy your meal. Their Sesame Chicken is very good The reason it isn't a 5 star is because the layout of the restaurant does not give you any breath a privacy from the other tables. I would also add avoid their pork unless you are really in the mood for it. I don't eat pork but others said it's one to avoid.

There's almost too many Biergarten style spots in CC but they are so fun tho. This one is niiice and the food was good too. Met one of the coolest engineers i know here after her young women professionals seminar or something. We left all her docs on the table to further inspire the wonderful ladies of Harpers garden

Yum, yum, yum! Oh St Albert, you have got it going on!! I am so in love with this Burger Shack (and soooo happy I don't live close, 'cause my bathroom scale would bust). Lets do the check list:
Decor: Adorable.
Patio: Decent. 
Shakes: Phenomenal. Try the Darth Vader, I loved it!
Fries: Crispy and delicious. 
Burgers: Crazy good - oh, and I had the chicken. 
Dessert: Ya right... I'll never have room! But good for you if you can do it. 

Jack, whom ever you are, you should be very proud of what you've created.
Excellent food, excellent service and beautifully decked out for the holidays! Everyone in our group enjoyed their meal tremendously.

OMG, the Kona coffee! I was transported back to my 2012 vacation to Maui! I also love that they are willing to accommodate the customers with whatever they want or need. Food was excellent and service was attentive and friendly. I will definitely be back, and specifically to this location.

Saw the good reviews but really disappointed the way the place looked, didn't look like a real pizza place with the showcase also didn't like that I couldn't sit down and eat my slice of pizza. The cheese and sauce were ok but the dough was so spongy... Should of went with digiorno

I'm a sashimi kind of guy. Never been a huge white rice guy, and I love the honest taste of the fish I'm eating. But we decided to try a couple of rolls while we were waiting for a friend who had recommended Samurai. And they were amazing. The one name I remember was the Oops roll. Delicious. The place is clean, although my wife says the bathrooms are a little dicey.

I'd go back in a heartbeat. Fast, courteous service.

TIP: Arrive early, this place just keeps getting busier as the night goes on. Lots of food taken out, too!

My family and I decided to get a carry out from this restaurant. We've drove past this place so many times and decided to check it out. When we arrived there was a short line and it gave us time to look over the menu. As I looked around I noticed they have a bar to the left and the dine in area is to the right. There was already a decent amount of people sitting down eating so I figured it would be awhile before our food would be done. When we made it  to the front of the line I ordered the 4 whole wings dinner and I got collard greens and yams as my sides. My mom and sister ordered 4 whole wings dinners as well. They got mac and cheese plus yams as their sides. We got my dad the fish dinner and I can't even remember what sides he got. All the dinners came with drinks as well. The total came out to about $60 or a little over. The lady at the register told us it should be about 30 to 40 minutes before the food would be ready. We decided to go to home depot to give it some time for the food to be done. After about 45 minutes we decided to go back and the food wasn't quite done. The lady at the register said they were running behind and to give them more time. They seem to be understaffed. There was already two other people sitting at tables inside the bar area and one lady stated she was still waiting on her food to be done. Luckily after about 15 more minutes our food was done. Before we left we decided to stop at a table and check that all the food was correct. Everything was in the bag. I hope they are able to get more organized with the carry out part. As far as the food I really loved the greens. First let me say I have never had greens cooked with banana peppers. The taste is so explosive and I can tell they add vinegar to the greens as well. The chicken was okay. It wasn't super hot so I feel like it had been sitting out awhile. Now when it comes to the yams I was not a fan. I am very picky when it comes to yams. It literally just tasted like nutmeg and I could not taste any cinnamon or sugar. My mom and sister thought their food was just okay as well. My dad loved his meal. I'm not sure if I will come back or not. I have heard great things in regards to the live band they have here. I may give this place another chance one day. The staff is nice!

The primary criteria for any Pho restaurant is the broth. Noodle City's broth is flavorful and aromatic, and it's probably the best you'll find in Santa Barbara. The broth is served hot, so the meat cooks nicely. The noodles are never clumped together (a good thing).  I recommend the #7, Pho Tai, for beginners.

I love the spring rolls because the rice paper is moist, not dried out like other places.

While they have Chinese food on the menu, you're better off getting Vietnamese dishes. Why would you go to a Vietnamese place to order Chinese food?

After a tour of the new Mormon Temple in Center City I was taking my Mom home and she asked if she could treat us to breakfast.  I was hesitant because I wasn't sure where she would want to go.  We were near the Made In America concert and the traffic was awful with many street closures.  She suggested we leave Center City and eat close to her home so we agreed and that's how we ended up at Four Seasons.  I haven't berm here since they first opened so I was curious what it was like.  Certainly nothing exceptional.  My wife and I both got a Cajun omelette.  Their omelettes are made with four eggs and are definitely bigger than most other restaurants.  It came with home fires and toast.  The food was good, no complaints from me.  My issues were the coffee, very weak, and all the noise.  The place seemed so chaotic.  It was like I was having breakfast at Chuckie Cheese!  The music was way too loud and the staff seemed to be screaming when they talked to each other.  Twice during our breakfast a Happy Birthday song was played over the loudspeakers.  It made my ears bleed, too loud and too long.  Why does the entire restaurant have to be subjected to this. What ever happened to your waitress singing a little ditty while she brings the birthday person a cupcake? We couldn't even see the two people having a birthday from where we were seated.  Also, we had a bad experience with those little creamers they bring with the coffee.  Both my Mom and myself had bad ones.  We went to pour it into our coffee and it came out like white snot.  All thick and clumpy, disgusting.  Our waiter was nice, replaced both coffees very quickly and overall provided good service.

This is the new chinese super buffet restaurant that opened recently that is attracting a lot of local big eaters.  There are people waiting outside for 30 minutes, so I decided to check out the hype in spite of the shame of going there knowing full well that it's not really Chinese food.  The cost was around $15 a person.  

The food was what you would expect from a super buffet.  Trying to be all things to all people, mediocre quality, & questionable sanitary conditions.  

This actually happened:

Me:  Do you think sushi at buffets are safe to eat?  I can't imagine that the fish is sushi-grade.  

Bro:  Don't know.  But you know, I think we've eaten worse.  

Me: WTF? (after taking a bite into his crab rangoon and pulls out a staple).

Still wondering how the heck a staple found its way into that crab rangoon?  Suffice it to say, that's the last time I'm going to this place.

Really great vibe. They have some great beers that were all wonderful to try. I cant decide whether their pizza or calzones or better but TRY IT. (I had "The Roni" I honestly did not expect to enjoy the dining experience as much as I did.

We went to Field House P & G for dinner before the evening show at the Fox.  I got the Grouben (Blackened grouper, creamy cole slaw, remoulade and Swiss cheese on marbled rye) with Cole Slaw.  The attentive server reminded me there was Cole Slaw on the sandwich, but I can never get too much Cole Slaw!  Excellent meal, fantastic service and a warm & friendly atmosphere.  The numerous TV's showing any sporting event worth watching was a nice bonus.  I will definitely return!!
Ordered to go. Was told 15 minutes & it was ready in less than 10. Dining area looks clean. Bibimbop was good as was the kimchi jigae (quite spicy). I'd have liked a bit more banchan selection.

So good!! We stopped here on our move across the country when it was a scorcher! So yummy, my husband and I had milkshakes and let not forget about the dog! She had he own pup cup of ice cream. She's so small she couldn't even finish it. It was so nice for her to have on a hot day. I can't wait to visit again.
Peet's coffee, friendly staff and good modern design (crisp product design and an interior design that is very open, light and uplifting) will keep me coming back.

I really like these apartments and the people who run it. Everyone is extremely nice and helpful. Maintenance is responsive. The only thing that bothers me is the fact that they won't let the dogs use the grass to go to the bathroom. My dog is very picky where he goes and the grass is 2 feet away from where the "designated pet use area" is. He's very strong and will pull me down trying to get to the grass. Mostly pet friendly other than that hiccup.  Overall I think they're good apartments. They're built very nicely and well thought out.

On of my favs!

Marsha is from New Orleans, so this was a must for Mardi Gras. She got her start with Ruth, of Ruth's Chris steakhouse in New Orleans. I visited that same steak house in the early 80's in college in New Orleans. 
We started with a couple of craft cocktails. 
Bourbon Apple Crisp with Bulleit bourbon was sweet cinnamon apple goodness. Cinnamon/sugared rim. 
Cherry Blossom tasted like sweet dark cherries. 
Their crusty French bread is served in a Marsha Brown paper bag which holds the crumbs as you tear off new pieces. Served w/sweet whipped butter and garlicky red bean hummus(I need to make this!) they bring out a bottle of New Orleans Crystal brand hot sauce. 
Shrimp cocktail. Giant U-15 shrimp. Sold by the each. You decide how many!
Gumbo. Traditional smoky andouille sausage and okra and filled w/veggies
Beet salad w/spinach, goat cheese, beets, orange slices and a tasty vinaigrette. 
Church salad. Basic salad w/balsamic vinaigrette. 
Filet tournedos. Perfectly cooked filet medallions w/peppery cream sauce, grilled asparagus and mashed potatoes. (Great as a leftover on a Caesar salad)
BBQ New Orleans Salmon(less the normal shrimp due to allergy) served over cheesy Southern grits. The New Orleans bbq sauce was buttery, spicy and yummy!  It was like it was meant for salmon as well as shrimp!

Pecan pie to go (work night). Sweet. Just a bit of salt and that caramelized molasses is almost smoky. Great buttery crust and crisped pecans.  

Sooo good!

$180, but so worth it if you are a fan of fine dining and great food and drinks. Our server was great. The Manager told great stories. He found out we were fans of New Orleans and Mardi Gras and brought us some beads. I'll be back!

Delicious! Our sweet waitress made a recommendation- and it was so good! We made a second trip and it was just as good! Again our sweet waitress took great care of us- offering food recommendations for us. We will eat here when ever we are in town.

Such a great experience with this company. I tried two local plumbing services prior to redcap and wish I went with redcap first. They're prompt, reasonably priced, and my technician (Daniel R) was very knowledgeable. 

If you're having a hard time finding a trustworthy plumber, I definitely recommend this place.

I got my hair and make up down here before my wedding. They made me feel and look like a movie star. I don't usual wear make up but though I'd get it done for my big day. I was worried it would look over done, but I turned out looking amazing and very natural. She was able to make my hair look just like the picture I gave her. She even put some lavender essential oils on my wrist to keep me calm. Would highly recommend this place.

The worst service beyond the hostess. Took forever! I ordered medium steak, got rare. The waiter was downright mean! An asshole! No smile, no how is your food? Wow! The gnat flying around our table seemed to be enjoying itself though.

I went in and ask for a recommendation for nice steaks to celebrate my 31st anniversary with family.  The butcher recommended the bone in Ribeye at $23 per pound.  Tonight I cooked them based on the directions and seasoning recommended by the butcher.  The cuts of meat were extremely fatty and lacked the flavor one would expect from a fine cut of Ribeye. They were however tender and cooked perfectly.  If I would have received this from El Corral, I would have sent it back.  My son in law brought over T-Bones purchased from Safeway at a third of the cost.  They had a much better flavor and far less fat.  I was very disappointed with my first experience from Dickmans.  There were cuts of meat in the cabinet that looked better but I went with the butchers recommendation.  Of course what he recommended was the most expensive cut in the case.  If you go into Dickmans, make your own choice as to meat.  Also, as a "specialty" deli, I was surprised to see that what they carry is Boar's Head deli meats.  I am familiar with the product and can tell you it is far below what I would expect from a specialty butcher.  I never do on-line reviews but this was so bad, I had to warn others.

I have a lot of reasons to like this gym: high-tech ish machines, safe area, yoga habit right next door, clean, awesome space to work out with an awesome staff. One of their signs read, "if you see anyone feeling self-conscious, give them a smile to make them feel welcome," How many gyms care about community that way?

The price of membership seems worth it to me; I think they do a good job building community and maintaining the place. The only downside to Anytime Fitness, Fairmount is that it is very small. While the gym doesn't come off as cluttered, there are fewer things they can accommodate. 

I must have gone at a time that everyone needed to use the bathrooms (they only have 3), and I was in a bit of a rush so I didn't feel too good about that. However, the  bathrooms were single person use, and looked absolutely great. It's almost like having your own bathroom there -- something I wish I could have experienced the time I went! 

Their "locker room" area is also on the smaller side, with a 5x2 shelf for things, and little combination lockers for more valuable things. None of these are real problems, neither are do they affect your gym experience too much, but sometimes it's nice to have a little more space. Anytime Fitness also doesn't seem to have an area for core workouts. 

If it were slightly cheaper and bigger, I would have definitely loved to join this gym! I loved my experience there and would definitely recommend it to anyone who wants a space to gym that's tidy, and without judgment.

The lamb chaat is exceptional. Ask for it spicy and expect it to be so. They will happily make your meal vegan or gluten free if you need it. Small place, but speedy service, they make everything to go and they deliver via uber eats and postmates. Worth a try.

This place is so good. The beef brisket was incredibly tender and delicious. It's served with a potato, apple, and horseradish salad that blew my mind. The pork cheeks were some of the best I've ever had. Highly recommended.
Last night was mine and my wife first time and we loved it. We both ordered the lechon sandwich and was not disappointed at all. Will definitely go back!!!
fantastic service and fantastic food...Never disappointed with The Jud... Great place to get your burger fix.
It's a surprisingly clean park. Very refreshing. I wish they had a weeee tiny bit more bench's around places. But they already have good ones. Perfect place to go for a walk

Just came back from brunch at this very British watering hole. Brit beers abound. I love pub food, as I lived there while attending University. I had to have scotch eggs, and sausage in pastry. Not as mushy as the bangers of my youth but a very nice and pleasurable alternative, yum!!! My husband got the full breakfast and it delivered, exactly what it should be. Well here is to the local in Philadelphia, with a wonderful new spin.

Fellini's is a great place to go if you're looking for cheap pasta.  The menu has a lot of options and the portion sizes are huge.  The only negative is that the seating area is tiny!  You will often be sitting elbow to elbow with the patrons of the adjacent table, which can be awkward and uncomfortable.

Meal was perfect, just as I asked!
Haven't had my eggs cooked liked I wanted in a long time and the potatoes were just liked I asked!
Wait may have been long but it was worth it.
Brandy at the counter was most attentive and couldn't have been more charming!
Love the dimples!
Thanks for a great breakfast!

Very nice brunch spot in Goleta.  I really enjoyed the grilled chicken and fried brie sandwich -- although I do think some vegetables on it would have been nice.  I had a side of tortilla soup which was very good but nothing exceptional.  The service was good and I felt that the price was good for what you get.  They definitely have a very large menu for brunch with everything from standard breakfast foods to filet mignon.  I would definitely recommend this place for a nice brunch with friends.

Went to buy a cup of coffee and I got the we don't take debit.  We have a minimum. If you don't have cash there is a ATM behind you.  So, I am supposed to be a $3 ATM fee for a $2 cup of coffee.  My response was here is the coffee.   I won't be back ever again.  You have a Whole Foods next store. WAWA a block away.  You are committing business suicide and will be out of business in a year!

Best sushi place in town hands down.  Osaka has the freshest and brightest fish, carefully put together for a roll that looks as good as it tastes.  My favorites include the Cris roll and the Diga roll.  Shumai and Gyoza appetizers were amazing.  The decor is interesting and relatively modern.  Occasionally smells a little strong like bleach if you show up pre-noon but overall it's always a great experience.

I've been bringing my children here for years. I'm very satisfied with the professional but laid back atmosphere of the office and staff. They are very friendly and accommodating. I would defiantly recommend this office for your children if you looking for a dentist.

I dined at this establishment one evening for dinner with my two young children, after having passed by  many times. I was very disappointed in the meal. The waiter, while good intentioned, was very inexperienced, and service was poor overall. The real issue, though, was with the food. I ordered chopped sirloin, which was dry and flavorless. The gravy, which I ordered on the side was of a strange consistency and light color, and after a quick taste test, I found it, too, had very little flavor. The choice of sides left much to be desired, so I ordered mashed potatoes and fried eggplant, both of which looked terrible, so I declined to eat it. My children ordered from the Kids menu. My daughter simply had pasta in butter, but complained it was too greasy. My son seemed happier with his hot dog, but they served it with an overpowering Italian roll, so he ate the hot dog without it, as the bread was too hard for him to chew. The bright spot was that our meals included dessert. My kids had ice cream, and I had chocolate pudding, which helped to end the dinner on a positive note.

I found an older digital Casio watch out of a grab-bag at an antique shop, it was so adorable and still worked!!  The only issue was that the clasp was somehow put onto the band backwards, which is probably why someone decided to get rid of the thing to begin with.  So I just hopped on Yelp and found the nearest jewelry store of any type, and of course the name of the shop caught my eye. So we went in and showed the guy what I just bought and what was wrong with it.  He was very helpful and turned the clasp around for me so that the watch fit and closed just right.  Best of all, he charged me nothing!  Couldn't be happier with the customer service here.

Beautiful, clean rooms; friendly and courteous staff. Lots of lounge space with various setups (some relaxing, some tables, etc)- great space for working or meeting with friends or colleagues. Breakfast buffet was hot, tasty and a reasonable price.

I like that you can reserve your seats when you buy your tickets. The movie theater was pretty clean when we watched a holiday movie. I didn't like that the lines were really long and employees didn't greet me when ordering from the concession stand. The area was not that clean when I ordered food, but I'm not sure if that was because of the holiday rush of Christmas or a normal thing. Self serve pop machine was a nice idea as you can mix your own beverage.

it was terrible I got kung pow chicken it was a what looked like all celery 4 mushrooms a few pieces of diced carrots 6 pieces of shaved chicken for 835 what a rip off ! the chicken lo mein had no flavor do yourself a favor and don't go there .

Remy is the man! Awesome staff and even better food. The butter chicken is by far my favorite and the garlic naan is killer. A flying horse beer is delicious with some spicy curry. I've been to Tamira twice now and wouldn't think about getting Indian food anywhere else!

As a student living in this expensive town I try to get a good deal where I can so I saw that this salon offered a groupon deal and once I saw the amazing yelp reviews I was sold. Angie is the only hairstylist there that redeems these groupons so I made an appointment with her. I asked for a 1 inch off the length and layers for volume. I got an OK hair cut. She went very fast and seemed bored. I got a blunt haircut with no layers and a freehand blowdry with a very quick straightening job.

My biggest complaint is that the food as drink here are pricy. BUT everything you will eat and drink will be delightfully tasty.

Definitely have some of their sangria. Our table split both a red and a white sangria and they are both great tasting and well made.

My favorite of the tapas was the empanadas-- YUM -- followed by the steak one we had (do not recall its name from the menu). The tortilla espanola was decent though nothing to write home about.

Best friend chicken ever! Moved out of our Alton area for almost 18 years. This restaurant was one of the things I missed the most. So happy to be back. Great food, great service, fair prices. Always more than I can eat but toggle bag is great for the next day.

I had no idea where I was going when my friend decided we were eating here. She was in the mood for Pho and this was listed as open and good. So she drives us and imagine my surprise when we pull up to the old Bob Evans near where my childhood home was. It's not a Bob Evans anymore!

The food at Saigon was great. I ordered Pad Thai and she got her Pho. Both were well prepared and the ingredients were flavorful and filling.

The decor is a little odd as it's fairly obvious inside that you're in the remnants of an old American eatery. Maybe a Big Boys or IHOP, but something along those lines. But I would't say that really put me off the place. The waitstaff were friendly and attentive and the food was good.

I dont understand the bad reviews, maybe an off day or bad employee, but our experience there on a Friday nite without reservations was outstanding. We were seated promptly at a nice booth. The decor is typical oriental, but pleasant. BYOB is nice, we came prepared with libations.  Our waiter, and actually the entire waitstaff was on call for whatever was needed. The petite friendly manager stopped by several times to check on us. We had sampled many items on the menu and were pleased with everything. We will be back.

I've been here four times and every time I leave happy but not satisfied. I love ciders. Been drinking them for years and have had them around the country. I think their ciders are good but not great. They have interesting combinations with various fruits or herbs in their beverages but many of them are semi-sweet as opposed to dry. I prefer dry cider and they usually have only one option. I don't think their ciders are bad, just not what I prefer. 

Also, every time we've come in and sat we are never sure if we are being served or need to go to the bar. The last time we went we sat down at a table, ordered drinks, chilled and drank, but as we were finishing, there was no server in sight for a solid 10 minutes. We didn't know if we were to go back to the bar to pay or not. Then another server came over, asked if we needed anything, so we just said the bill. She gave it to us and while we were waiting to pay our original server finally returned and had no idea we were paying. It's always confusing so hopefully they can figure that out within their staff. Usually I stand at the bar but since there were 4 of us we sat down, but we never know what to do when we're sitting... so I'll keep coming back and hopefully I'll see more dry options and better service.

I thought the atmosphere was terrible for dinner  in this place.  The tables were all so close together that I'm pretty sure at one point the guy next to me ate from my plate and didn't even notice.  Ok, not really, but he definitely could have.

The music was so damn loud in this place I wasn't even able to hear my boyfriend speak from across the table, and it was so dark I wasn't even sure if he was still sitting there.

The table we sat at was too small for our plates, so we had to constantly be shifting things around.

I can't even remember what I ate, so I imagine that it wasn't that good.

I would go in, go the bathroom to say you've seen them (be sure to lock the door), and then leave.

Nice outside area to sit. Fun menu. They are definitely trying to be fancy with the food. The presentation was great. The taste however... They seem to overcook some of the meat. 
I got the tugboat with fried chicken. Looked great. But underneath the eggs, the chicken was completely black. Dried out. I paid extra for it, but I wish I hadn't.
I had a similar experience with their burger. Excessive burnt char on each side of the patty. Not my thing.

Always an excellent experience...great food and service....I have never been disappointed here. Don't pass the cheesy bruschetta...the best in town! Mimosas made with fresh squeezed OJ...yummy! Liege waffles were very sweet. Turkey pesto sandwich delicious.

Yesterday I had my ombré brows done with Jay. 
Seriously he's one of the nicest people I've ever met. We did a FaceTime consult where we went over the procedure and desired results and I felt so comfortable with him after that and felt like
I knew exactly what to expect. 
He spent a while mapping and shaping my eyebrows until we agreed on a perfect shape.
He really loves his job and took the time to make sure I got exactly what I wanted. 
I can't wait to see them completely healed.

I had a $25 gift card for a nail salon that had really bad Yelp reviews so I decided to forget about the gift card and pick another salon. I looked at several places before deciding to give this one a try. I'm so glad I chose Natural Nails!! I'd give them 6 stars if I could. Their attention to detail is impressive. The staff is kind and friendly. The gentleman who took care of my pedicure is amazingly meticulous and did an excellent job. I mostly interacted with Diane--she's a really nice lady and a real professional. She took her time to make sure I had a perfect and long-lasting shellac manicure.  I'll definitely go back!

My wife and I recently had our wedding reception at the CAM. When we first visited venues, the staff at the CAM was quick to provide information on reserving the venue, and held our date for us until we made the decision soon after.

There are some caveats to the contract (no red wine, limited set up times) but those were non issues. We also did not know what pieces would be up since we booked almost a year in advance. Since it is a contemporary art museum, there may at times be exhibits with adult themes, so keep that in mind while booking.

The night of our event went great. The staff was super friendly and approachable. The bartenders were very outgoing. When we realized we had left some chairs there after our event, we returned find them secured for us to pick up.

Over all, the CAM was a great place to hold our event. It was a terrific venue for 150 guests, room for a band, and a dance floor. I would recommend this as a spot to hold a reception or gala in STL, or as a place to spend a couple hours admiring frequently rotating exhibits of modern art.

We were definitely underwhelmed with Sylvain. The night kicked off with them not having our reservation in the courtyard, as requested and confirmed, and only continued to disappoint from there. We were celebrating my wife's birthday, something I told the hostess when we making the reservation, yet was never acknowledged. Certainly not a huge deal, but surprising to me for what's supposed to be an upscale restaurant.

The sever was definitely not professional and at this price point we expected more. She wasn't knowledgeable about the wine list or the specials, nor did she check on us even once after our meals were dropped off (by the manager) my wife wanted to order another glass of wine with dinner and I wanted to order a cocktail and was never able.  I would have been more understanding had there been a rush and she was in the weeds, however it was early, she did not have a full station and we could see her standing around chatting, never even glancing in our direction.

My wife was disappointed with her meal, something that could have easily been corrected had the waitress ever appeared. The braised beef cheeks were good, nice flavor and cooked well, they melted in your mouth.

The shining star was the mushroom bruschetta. I can't speak to dessert, although we had been looking forward to trying the Root Beer float, we were so disappointed we opted to move on to The Pelican Club instead. With so many amazing options in the city, I certainly would not recommend Sylvain, but if you do decide to dine here, get the bruschetta.

This is my 4th time visiting the SMG, the first 2 times everything was excellent! Service, drinks, and food! The 3rd time the service slipped and last night, the service was awful! Employees were rude and childlike. The employees were playing and yelling at each other in the "lobby-hostess" area..  I'm still waiting for my salt and pepper... SMH... The locations service is slipping a great deal.

over the years, I'd only been to Central Grocery for a muffaletta.  This year (Nov. 2012) I decided to try Frank's.  The sandwich tasted pretty much the same between Central's and Frank's.  Except that I found the service way better at Frank's for my 'to go' sandwich (not pre made like at Central) and boy were they friendly and helpful at Frank's.  I'd definitely go back and for my next plane ride home, this will be the sandwich I'll be bringing back with me from NOLA.

I'm not one to visit cemeteries often, however, I did willingly visit this one. My visit wasn't to attend a service or to visit a family member's grave. I visited to admire the history and the beauty. The grounds are clean and clear. You can walk through and read names and dates and some of the tombs are absolutely breathtaking. I felt like I was walking within an outdoor museum. It was eerie or uncomfortable. Instead, there was a steady peace amongst us. Definitely a must-see if you're in NOLA!
They messed up my services 3 times..... I kept giving them chances but I changed my mind finally. Too bad

First time at a Japanese steakhouse was a great experience! The food was incredible and the portions were huge. The chef, Fluffy, was a great entertainer on top of being an excellent chef. I loved the sirloin steak, the fried rice was exceptional, and everything that came with it complemented the main items well. Service was great and prices were reasonable. A very cool dining experience worth your time
Loved everything about it! Friendly staff, clean, large portions, and good food! The pineapple fried rice was delicious! The takoyaki was great!

I'm going to be harsh, especially having lived with ramen in the West Coast:

I was particularly disappointed with the service when I walked in today with my family. Girl at the cashier was completely dismissive, almost lost in space until we got up to the register. We had sat down at first mistakenly believing they'd come to take orders. Absolutely no reaction from her despite the fact we were sitting there until I asked if orders were at the front. Very curt with our orders, and it's pretty unacceptable to not know items on your own menu. I ordered the hiyashi chuka and her co-workers had to tell her what it was. She later brings out the bowls and just drops it on the table. I understand this is a minimal service kind of establishment, but really, this kind of lack of professionalism by the teenagers working the counter/kitchen isn't going to cut it.

The food:

The ramen texture is decent, though honestly, I would venture to believe it's the packaged ramen. I don't think $8 is worth that.  Broth, though, is decent: strong tonkotsu flavor, but it's just a bit too salty, even for shio ramen.

Miso ramen tastes muddled as if it were actually a shio ramen with miso paste in it. (that might actually be their intention)

Hiyashi chuka - absolutely not worth the $10+ price tag, even if it's a "summer special." Decent use of sesame oil in the tabe sauce, but there wasn't much in terms of veggies and the three pieces of meat seemed almost like a second thought. 

The fish cake in each bowl looked like shavings. Not very generous with the ingredients per bowl, chashu especially.

What an amazing find - love the food. Didn't know what to eat -  got a recommendation from the sweetest waitress (difficult name to spell started with a J) - on what to eat. May not be on the beat and path but look for it - was worth every bite.  We went back again the next day.  The grits were so creamy and sweet best I've ever tasted.  Wish they were closer to New York!!

My favorite Indy bookstore (and my husband goes for the records, my kid for the old toy trains). Great vibe and selection here of new and used everything, funky earrings, friendly staff, good conversation, cozy couch + cat. I just love it. And if you can't find what you're looking for, they can order it for you!
