So after two weeks, I've finally had the chance to come back. This time I came in literally 5 minutes after they opened instead of coming at dinner time. And just as I expected, everything was wonderful--just like the last time. I'm already craving for more and I can't wait to come back.

Again I must say: try their beans. They're to die for.

I really enjoyed my lunch here with my wife the other day. The location is absolutely perfect, towards the south end of the Eagle Creek reservoir. You do not have to pay to get into the park in order to get here, which is a plus. They have a lot of outdoor seating, whether you'd like to make a reservation or look for a spot in the bar area. The bar area is first come, first serve, and was great when we came. However, I can see that it can get a little chaotic when very crowded due to how close the high top tables are to each other.

I went with the mahi mahi sandwich, and my wife went with the salmon roulade. Her fish was delicious, and I could not get enough of my fish sandwich. The blackened seasoning on the fish, as well as the spiciness of the fried onions, was a perfect combination. The red pepper aioli had a nice kick to it as well. I really enjoyed it, but heads up to those that don't want anything spicy; it is hot! Overall, they have a fantastic variety of food items available, for both lunch and dinner. I can't wait to come back.

One of the best meals I've ever had.

Went here with my friend Samantha and we both thoroughly enjoyed our experience. The biscuits were awesome, and the lobster dumplings were also delicious. 

Our entrees were even better. She had the salmon and I had the wood-grilled butterfish. During this entire meal I was going through flavor heaven; the attention to detail was astounding. Very pleasant and professional service. Some very passionate and talented minds came together to create an experience that will intrigue and satisfy your most discerning tastebuds. Om nom nom. The chefs are truly experts. I was fully impressed. 

For dessert we had the Salty Malty Ice Cream pie. PERFECTION. 

We both fell in love. I don't have a single complaint.

Urban diner is now my go to brunch place. My friend took me once & ever since I been taking my friends ( or dragging them with me ) . My favourite is the poffertjes ( mini pancakes ). That's mainly the reason I go but everything I've ever had was amazing.  The bennies are tasty as well. I was never a big fan but urban diner changes my mind. It's all I crave now.

This is the only restaurant in Indy my husband enjoys, as he is a bit of a food snob. The dishes are always adventurous, and we can trust that everything will be cooked perfectly.  We went last night, and were not disappointed with the food; however, the service was surprisingly sub-par.

We were seated in a booth between two parties of couples whose average age was about 60.  I'm not ageist, but I feel I need to mention this, as these parties received much better service from our server than we did (my husband and I are in our mid-thirties).  We were planning on ordering a third drink each, as well as dessert, but our entree plates stayed on our table for roughly 25 minutes after we were finished, even as our server had to wedge himself in between our table and the tables on either side of us.  I finally had to flag down another server to remove our plates and bring us our check.

We've been coming to Oakleys for years, but based on our service last night, and the ever present lack of respect for anyone under the age of 50, I don't think we will be back.

A little pricey for the common selections, but you can't get them served any better. Add all you can stand to that and it's definitely your monies worth. Don't eat for a few hours before you go there.
I've never met anyone that wasn't pleasant, for at least an hour.
This is a great store with skilled staff. A woman named Alex did a fabulous job fitting my new progressives. I highly recommend this store

I wish I could give a full 5 star review. After trying takeout 3 times and dining in once with friends, I sadly must be honest.
While the owner was SO kind and even bought our first round of apps, the food and drink was mediocre. The margaritas were weak and didn't have much flavor, and the chorizo burrito bowl was more like ground turkey than actual chorizo. The service was great and the guacamole amazing though!

ITech were awesome.  They replaced my breaker box.  They were extremely nice, knowledgeable and did a first class job.

I would recommend them to my mom.
Quite simply the best pastrami in Tampa bay. This little gem is pure homemade, Jewish, magic..Deliciousness! Do yourself a favor an GOOOO!!!!!!!

When looking for a hotel in New Orleans - for me, location and service are my most important factors.

Location: Right in downtown New Orleans; 4 blocks to Canal St. and the French Quarter; 5 blocks to the riverfront; St. Charles Streetcar stop is right across the street.  Easy bus lines everywhere.  2 great restaurants in the hotel (Trenasse for dinner; Pete's for breakfast/lunch).  It is quiet at night; just far enough away from the French Quarter hustle and bustle.

Service:  Customer service was top notch.  Staff was accommodating for recommendations on where to go; what to do; how to get there.  Easily added stuff from snack shop and restaurants to room.  Cabs wait outside the hotel and are always available.  Our room was quite large and very clean, the bedding was perfect for sleeping.  The bar in the lobby has a happy hour with $5 drinks and 75 cent oysters.

Beyond that, on the top floor is a gym and pool (which I used twice) with machines, free weights, a medicine ball, treadmills, an elliptical, and a couple of bikes.

This place has great made at the table salsa, 
in-restaurant homemade tortillas, good Margaritas and great food.  It's fun with the mariachi band walking around table to table. The atmosphere is lively and loud.

Cassius arrived and immediately evaluated the job: remove/install a water heater.  Cassius described the work to be done and listed the parts that would be required.  Cassius had me sign the authorization for the work, which specified a start time of 8:43 AM, which I assume was the time he arrived on the property.  The good: Cassius is fast, really fast.  The job was completed at 9:33, or earlier, since that is the time I saw after walking inside the house.  Extra good: Cassius knows his stuff and installed the heater to code, adding items that were absent from the previous heater: Gas drip, 3' pipe to from the pop-off valve, plus a new cold water supply valve, because the existing one did not work.  Included in the work was removal of the old heater.

The bad: The work took less than an hour to complete; however, Cassius charged for the 10+ minutes that he spent completing the invoice, adding an additional $25 to the bill.  I complained and Cassius was kind enough to supply a $15 coupon which dropped the labor from $122, to $107.  I was happy.  So why do I bother describing this?  Because looking at the invoice later, I discovered that Cassius changed the "in" time on the invoice, after I authorized the work.  He changed 8:43 to 8:38.    He should have noted this and had me initial the change.  The "out" time on the invoice reported 9:43 AM.  Is it just coincidence that the change resulted in an extra $25 in labor??

Lesson learned: pay attention to the start and stop times, as a few minutes can mean a big difference.

The service was excellent.

The atmosphere was relaxed and we didn't feel rushed.

The food was outstanding! 

Bread basket: My GF loves bread so I knew that was going to be a part of our meal. The homemade bread with whipped butter was perfect. I only was allowed 1 piece. There was a variety of bread and we were able to bring a good portion home. 

Fish Tacos: The fish was seasoned and cooked to perfection. The slaw and the fish were a marriage made in heaven. You get two tacos with flavorful Cuban rice.

Short Ribs: Tender, tender, tender... No need for a knife. The garlic mashed potatoes and fresh spinach just enhanced the meal. 

Calamari: Flash Fried and lightly battered. I didn't like the Asain sauce that accompanied the calamari, however, it was still exceptional.

Plus two Tito's drinks and a soda.

The total: $95

It was reasonably priced for the quality of the food.

I will definitely return! We came at night so didn't get to enjoy the views outside. Next time!

The duchess definitely bakes excellent products. We tried autumn tart which was delicious and excellent texture. We also tried key lime tart, which was way too sweet. And a milk chocolate scone. 
The setting reminds me of high tea places. We always went on the afternoon of weekends, so tons of people lining up, hard to find a place to sit, very few selections remaining. 

The prices seems quite outrageous. It is hard to justify to myself spending 8$ per item at the end of the day. Don't get me wrong, the quality is great. But it seems I am paying a lot for the ambience and the brand name. 
Just down the street there is another bakery called the Dutch bakery. Very low key, no places to sit, but everything is made fresh and at 1/2 price of the Dutchess. Unless I have friends I want to impress, I probably will go to the Dutch bakery from now on.
You get what you pay for. These people are putting out some high quality doughnuts. King Kong
We went on a Friday night it was busy. It was 1st come 1st served we had a party of 8. Server was attentive and polite. Food was good but mixed drinks wasn't good.

"$2.25 Cleaners" is only $2.25 if you print out a coupon and bring it with you. Since I assumed the sign was true I came in without a coupon, so my items were varying prices over $3. When I picked everything up there were stains that should have come out with even the lightest cleaning. Very dissatisfied.

I've been here several times but have never written a review. This place is consistently good. I love the Italian Kitchen gourmet pizza. Great burgers, cheese steaks, and wings. The turkey hoagie I ordered once kinda skimped on the turkey though. The service is always good.
Love the drip coffee and the baked goods.  Great atmosphere too.  Day old pastries are usually half priced.

This hotel is very appealing to the eye, it's very modern and clean. The staff is friendly which is great. That's about it for the pros of the hotel, on the downside there is much more which is why I feel I couldn't really give much more than 3 stars. The bathrooms are very pretty and would have a great shower if it came with instructions on how to make the rain shower head work. The door was the biggest thumbs down for the bathroom it's a frosted glass sliding door which pretty much doesn't work it took two people to open and close it every time in our room. Speaking of doors, the entrance door for the room was way too lifted off of the ground and didn't work well with the hallway noises you could hear everything from people's conversations to footsteps. Now although the staff is friendly and welcoming, the housekeeping service feels entitled to walking into your room at whatever moment. On several occasions we had called down for toilet paper, towels, etc. and the house keeper after knocking once decided it was ok to just barge into the room, first off if we REQUESTED these items obviously we are in the room and do not need you barging in to bring it, one could possibly be undressed or doing something. The TV is great cause it has a lot of channels and smart tv features but other than that it is so dark that you can barely see anything and there is no way to change the settings for the brightness. Also like everyone else has mentioned the cologne smell in the lobby is a bit much but didn't bother much cause I just walked past it to get in and out.

Omg!!!!!!! The best trip to Nashville ever!! I have been drooling to visit this store when I saw they were building it around Sept '14!!! Finally made it and let me tell you my heart skipped a beat as I walked through the door...racks upon racks of designer items!! I took me 6 hours to go through everything and I got some great deals!! My favorite item were these Kurt Geiger Pumps for 550 dollars, but the steal price of 189!!!!! The best place everrr! Have to pace yourself or you will have past due rent and car note!!!!!
Regular of their grilled cheese and home fries, hummus and tabouli plate, great cup of cafe americano and open any time of day and night.

We ate there for the first time this weekend.  
We had a veggie supreme which was outstanding.  
Love the thin crust pizza, the toppings were perfect, and the price is great.  We still have half the pizza left over and I can't wait for lunch when I can have the leftovers.
Great place for Vegans!

I'm not sure there are any words to truly express my gratitude to Lyn and the rest of her staff. I have only been in Tampa for 3 weeks, but I am SO incredibly particular about my nails. I absolutely dreaded having to find a new place to go because normally there are 3 or 4 train wrecks before I find somewhere I like. However, we walked in to schedule appointments today and were pleasantly surprised. We were greeted immediately upon entering the shop by several people. I explained to Lyn, the owner, how nervous I was about getting my nails done. She comforted me and asked me what made me nervous. They don't take appointments on the weekend, but she told us to just come back when we were ready. She was so upfront about pricing and the process that everything went through, so you knew exactly how much you were going to pay. From the pedicure, to the beeswax dip, to the sangria, to the nail shaping... every single thing was perfect. I absolutely LOVED the experience. In fact, going to the nail shop IS an experience. My nails have never looked better, and I have never felt better. All I have to say is GO! You won't regret it. Every single person was charming and personable. I'm honestly ready to go back, and we just barely left. Not to mention, my mother hadn't had her nails done in nearly 16 years and left beaming. She was so happy, and I was so incredibly grateful for the entire team that made her feel so special. Thank you SO, SO much. Can't wait to see you all in 2 weeks!

My experience here was on the border between okay and disappointing.  First, I like to be greeted immediately upon walking into a restaurant, and the delayed greeting came just the second before I was about to turn and find another place.  There are posters all over advertising blended juices that are fresh made daily; when we asked for one, we were told that the juicing had not been done yet.  At 10:30 in the morning, at a breakfast restaurant, and they had not started any juice?  The server did not offer to make any, or say yes but there is a 20 minute wait, or any other suggestions that ended with me getting juice.  She seemed pretty disengaged the entire meal.  I had to ask for a water refill, and I should not have to ask when my glass is clearly empty.  The restaurant was not busy.  Everything about this just made me feel complainy.  My eggs benedict was enjoyable; the english muffins were a bit blackened on the edges but the ham, eggs, and hollandaise were decent.  The pumpkin waffles were great.  However, you'll see in the photo that there is a giant scoop of cinnamon butter on the waffle, which tasted great but was cold and the waffle was not hot enough to melt it even a little bit.  We ended up slicing it into pieces to eat with the waffle bites.  My dining partner got one of the egg skillets and said it was meh just okay but pretty boring flavor-wise.  Our server dropped off the check and did not mention whether we were supposed to pay her or go up front, which typically is a good thing to tell customers.  (pay up front is the answer).  Overall this is a very generic breakfast restaurant that could easily be swapped out for any other, and there is no particular reason to choose this one.  The juice could have brought it that extra step up.  If you're going to advertise a differentiator, it is important to actually have it ready for customers, or it becomes a bigger disappointment than not having advertised it at all.

I've been regularly going to Causeway for almost 10 years. Why? Above all else, it has all the things you are looking for from a fish store: the tanks are exceptionally CLEAN, there is a good stock/ rotation of HAPPY & HEALTHY FISH, the staff is very KNOWLEDGABLE & you are shopping LOCAL!!! 
Thank you Causeway Pets for being my trusted & dependable choice for fish & supplies!

This is my new favorite lunch spot and it's a quick walk from many downtown locations!  They have a really stellar little salad bar with a lot of items and, most importantly, everything is kept FRESH.  At lunch you can get a personal Chicago style pizza and a small plate of salad for under $10.  The pizzas are very flavorful and taste just like the authentic thick crust pizzas I've eaten in Chicago.  There are many different flavors to choose from or you can make your own.  They also have unlimited salad bar for ~$9 or you can take some salad to go.  The service has always been fast and friendly.

Delicious pasta dishes cooked with love and from the heart.  They take their time to do it right the first time ! And they get it right each time !  Not a beer drinker but if you are they have a huge selection for your enjoyment!

I love coming here for special occasion hair and make-up. Gianna did my hair and make-up for my wedding and everyone thought I looked flawless! Nicole did my hair as I was a bridesmaid for a wedding and she gave me exactly what I imagined. Gianna and Nicole are warm, personable, and get the job done right!

A "hole in the wall" in all the best aspects of that saying!  A real neighborhood joint, food will surprise you in a really good way! Drinks are priced very reasonable, food as well. A usual late night stop for us when in town, nothing fancy, small overall.  Very consistent in food quality, drinks always good.  Solid 5 stars... Good luck finding it, is borderline hidden local secret even with bars / restaurants all around it.

Beignets were outstanding.  The place was packed and it took a long time to get my beignet, but it was worth the wait.  There are 3 per order, so it's best shared with a friend.  FYI: for those not in the know, beignets are not a good "walk around and eat" food.  Too greasy and the powdered sugar makes a mess.

Service and parts are very good with the exception of the service manager. He has an arrogant attitude. Told me to take my business elsewhere if I didn't like his prices. Fortunately the service writer stepped in and explained the value.
Regarding sales...worst experience I have ever encountered. My fiancé and I have been looking at a ultra glide... we are the perfect demographic with spendable cash. Retired early 60's... we were never approached by a sales person... we kept showing interest in the 
floor model. After 45 minutes we decided to leave. 
We decided to try again a couple of weeks later. Same outcome as before. I was not pleased and asked the greeter for the sales manager. She was sympathetic to my situation. A 1/2 hour later the greeter said that he was tied up and it would be awhile.
We left and no one seemed to care.

THIS IS BY FAR my favorite goodwill in the area! I have tried others but this one for me always keeps me coming back. The store is always clean and organized on the 1/2 off day. I can spend hours at this store and when I say hours I mean 3-4 hours! Just looking at all the different clothing they offer. I have told friends about this location to get them to come and try it and they too love it. The 1/2 off day is not for the weak minded. lol It is very crowded and people can be somewhat aggressive! But you just can't beat the price! I have furnished so much of my house with things I have found at this location. 
My favorite things to look for at this store are the dresses and coats! You can't beat $2.50 dresses, which some are brand new with tags, and $5 coats! 
Goodwill has spoiled me to the point that I can't really go shopping anywhere else because I don't want to spend $20 on a shirt when I can get one for $1.50. 

The only downfall to this location is the wait time in line. They do announce to take the hangers off the clothes but you can still be in line for easily 20-30 minutes of your visit. I recently visited another location where they do the style of line similar to TJmaxx where its a single line that goes to the next available cashier. It really sped up the wait time in line. 

I will continue to go back to this store. I will always consider this my home base goodwill store!

First time here and I will definitely be back again. Almost all of the pasta is made by hand, which you can definitely tell. The bread, the desert, just everything was great! Also, our server Anthony made our experience even greater. He went through the ENTIRE menu with us and described popular items and just details about the entrees. He was also very attentive, funny, and just a really nice person overall.

Made a reservation and got the table I wanted on a Saturday night!

Had the calmari and shrimp imperial apps.  Both were good. Calamari were generic (I like to have a mix of rings and legs). Entres were crab cakes and strip steak. Both excellent!  Strip steak was ordere med rare and that's how it was served. Crab cakes were full of flavor and crab meat. Shared the creme brûlée for dessert and it was good but I have had better. All of the food was brought to us piping hot including there delicious asst of bread.  

Wait staff very friendly and informative. It was busy, therefore, service suffered a little. 

Lighting could be dimmer for an extra cozy feel. Wide range of clientele.

Super small portion! I had the Spicy Tuna Tempura, 6 pieces total and $12.96 plus delivery. The tuna in the roll was chopped and super cooked. I thought it would be raw tuna, like in a chunk and then deep fried. Wouldn't recommend, for the price there is better.

In Phoenixville for the night, so we decided to walk around and try some other local haunts.  Two different people at our restaurant recommended Molly's, so we stopped in.

Traditional Cookie-cutter "Irish" bar, it looks familiar when you walk in, although it's a little brighter than these types of bars usually are.

The Pretzel was all garlic, no salt. It needed sometypemof spicey mustard, but it comes with a bland melted cheese and a ranch dressing.  Neither went well with the flavors of the overseasoned pretzel.

We happened to be there for Bingo night and most of the bar was participating.  Other patrons around us all made it a point to say something to us and we soon we feeling like a local.

If you're in town, it's a friendly place to grab a drink and be apart of smalltown USA...just make sure you grab dinner up the street first.

I tried The Naked Monkey for the first time today. I will say my esthetician was very fast but not very thorough. It is a little disappointing. At first I noticed that I had a lot of wax residue left on me but figured I would take care of it at home. When I got home and got a good look at everything I found that she did not do a very good job. I had some small areas of hair left and some of the wax left on me was still connected to hair that I had to rip off.

The best moving experience I have ever had. Tom was able to set me up with an appointment the DAY after I called. Kyle and his team were absolutely amazing, courteous, efficient, friendly, careful, and thorough. Nothing broken, scratched, anything. Can moving actually be...relaxing??? The whole process took 2 hours less than it did with a different local moving company just a year earlier (same # of stairs, distance, same furniture, etc.) I hope to stay in my new home for a good long time, but will come here first the next time I have to move.

This is a somewhat pricey activity but worth getting to ride down the Mississippi river in a real steamboat(it's like Disney Land but with no track or fake Indians).  The captain narrates the entire journey filling you in on the historical sites you pass, and there are two full bars on board.  We didn't bother with the food, as you pay almost twice as much for the meal and we were glad that we didn't as it looked pretty mediocre.

We went for the food time yesterday and Diesel and Lucy loved it. It was nothing like I imagined it would be. We met the owner as he continued to make rounds to insure all was well and that everyone was enjoying themselves. We will definitely be returning

Decided to finally try Shula's before heading to the monday night football game between the Colts/Bucs. Like the concept celebrating the Dolphins undefeated season in '72. Presentation was good by our waiter. Oversized tomatoes on my salad was a little much. Had the filet as my entree. I have to say, everything tasted average at best. Baked potato was bland. They messed up my friends order and did nothing about it. Don't worry, we'll just chalk that one up. Overpriced for the quality, or lack thereof. I had a better meal at 3 in the morning down the street at the Waffle House. They need to bring some new management in and revamp the menu.

The short- great service, but a little frenetic, excellent location, great hangout atmosphere. BUT the food? Its what matters and we were very disappointed.

The long- 
I had two tickets to see a show about a block away, and I did the ol' " last minute" groupon search. I found this place.
Its inside the historic Bank of Delaware building. Super high ceilings, great bar area, and a well separated formal dining area separated from the Swillers in at the bar. Very big.
Our server was extremely busy, but still made us feel important to her, very personable. She knew the menu and didn't rush us. It did take almost 25 minutes from ordering before the appetizer came out, so the restaurant needs better organization and turn times.

The food.....man I wanted it to be so much better than it was. The location and atmosphere just yearned for some bangin' food.
By the numbers-
Appetizer - 
crab and artichoke dip with pepper jack cheese. The dip itself was above average in flavor, but what really made it pedestrian was literally a square slice of deli cheese melted on top, like from the grocery store deli counter. Plus the crab and artichoke was not loosely mixed chunks; it was over processed, it was so over mixed that there was no distinct flavors of crab or artichoke. When I eat "crab anything" I expect to pick out a little shell.   Not memorable.
Entrees- 
1. pan seared scallops with some sort of field peas mixture. The scallops were tiny farm bay scallops, and if they ever saw a hot skillet ( pan sear) it was with binoculars. White, no color, just lackluster. And forget whatever they came with.
2. Bone in pork chop, with smashed potatoes and green beans. This was actually cooked really well, the pork chop cooked to a medium, well seasoned, moist, and really enjoyable to eat. The green beans were still crisp tender, and the potatoes were properly made. The gravy, however, was a little watery and not notable in any way. If they had actually made a reduction instead of a buffet table gravy, that dish would have been almost 5 star.

Bottom line, the seafood and protein plates, when being charged at 25-30 per plate, need refinement and better execution. The pork chop plate, as a whole was definitely good. That scallop plate....a quote from Spiderman, "back to formula!"

Appetizer came out quickly but we were waiting for our meals for over 45 minutes when a different server let us know that the kitchen switched our tickets and they were making our food now. Finally got our meals after waiting for over an hour. No one came to apologize about the mistake or the extremely long wait. Will not be back.
To start off not the cleanest place definitely below average. The service was poor, very rude workers, would not recommend to anyone. Not worth the trouble for the cheap price.
After a few hours of live music and drinks, the sweet and savory chicken and waffles at Sun Diner were the perfect way to end the evening. Great diner vibe, fun music and friendly staff.

Let me start by saying that all the food was very good, but that was over shadowed by the extremely poor service we received before being seated. Since there seemed to be an extremely large group in the bar area my party who arrived before I did were seated down by the chef's table for their cocktails. once I arrived it took several minutes for the server to even acknowledge that there was even an additional person there. When he did eventually notice it took several more minutes for him to come and take my drink order and correct the wrong drink that a member of my party received. shortly after the replacement drink and a drink for another member of my party arrives but not mine. Then 10 minutes no drink, 15 minutes still no drink. I'm starting to fume at this point and the person whom I assume is the manager keeps walking by but not making eye contact. Appetizers which were ordered before I arrived have not arrived either. Said manager stops by to ask if the replacement cocktail was correct still no mention of my cocktail. The server was asked 3 time where my cocktail was and all he said it was coming (I ordered the punch which the menu states it's on draft) at this point I am ready to leave but since we are there for a monthly dinner date with friends that is not an option. my drink eventually comes delivered by said manager after a member of my party having a conversation him and we are ready to be seated for dinner as our appetizers arrive and the manager tells me the next one is on him( that was a relaxing cocktail before dinner shot to hell). we get out table and proceed to carry our appetizers to our table( yes I said that WE carried the appetizers to the table not a server, not the hostess, not the manager). the service during dinner was better but you have to think was this because we complained to the manager about the earlier issues. it is sad that because of bad customer service I was not able to enjoy my evening.

Great location at end of C terminal, but the food was hardly worth the wait.  Used to get crab fries with your breakfast sandwich, no more.  Ordered the Bordentown (pork roll, egg and cheese) and the roll was at least a day old.  It's Philadelphia for crying out loud!  If you don't have a fresh roll, don't serve it!  Thanks for kick starting my Atkins diet.

The prices are reasonable, and most of the room was clean. However, the shower was nasty. No hotel room regardless of price should be that nasty. It was surprising to see a safe in the room after seeing the nasty shower. I guess they're trying to help people hide their drug $ LOL.

3 starts for the environment. The food is pretty bleh. Tampa has a lot of taco places so this place had its work cut out for them. What I will say though is the atmosphere and drinks were pretty good. Taco Tuesday was packed (but in a good way). Music was great too. I'd come back just to grab a drink and enjoy the ambiance.

This place is wonderful. The husband and wife are so nice, and are always genuinely happy to see you. The prices are reasonable, and the quality's great. 

A bit out of my way, but well worth the trip. Also, there's 20 minute loading just down the street, so it's easy to drop off/pick up without having to pay for a meter.

Accepts credit only over $30.

Stopped by here after having lunch because I heard their stuff was good. I ended up getting this little cake like thing, I'm sorry! I forget the name of it but I posted a picture. It looks like a dome of chocolate with almonds on top. It was alright tasting.. I thought the inside was going to be more cake like and soft but it wasn't.

After taking another look around the store, I noticed the front wall on the left (if you are facing the door) had a shelf full of items made from chocolate. The chocolate heel caught my eye because my friend really like heels. Got her a dark chocolate one & ended up having a funny conversation with the chasier about "old people likes dark chocolate" this is a quote from my little cousin because I now like dark chocolate. The lady was also night enough to go get me a different heel after looking at it & said the other one was starting to come apart.
I've never had a bad pizza there.  I love the flavor, I love their rewards program.  I've recommended this place to a lot of people!
Dennis- the owner of the shop is friendly, very knowledgeable and helpful. I'm a fan of Bike Friday's folding bicycles and Rincon Cycles is one of the few dealers in the country.

My experience at Dickey's was mediocre at best.  My party of three each ordered our meals at one end of the counter.  Two of us ordered side items that weren't immediately ready (waffle fries and fried okra).  The  rest of the orders were added to the trays and slid down to the other end of the counter, where the cashier entered them into the computer. Empty paper bowls were marked with the missing side items.   Drinks, silverware (nice plastic ware), and condiments including BBQ sauce were self-serve, and we carried our trays to a table. I had a 3 Meat plate and chose pulled pork, brisket, and spicy cheddar sausage.  All three meats were barely above room temperature. The brisket had good smoky flavor but was very dry. I am used to smoked brisket slices that you can cut with a fork, but this was actually chewy.  The pork had good flavor and did not need sauce, but again, it was cold. The cheese in the sausage wasn't even melted.   My sides were "baked potato casserole," which is actually mashed potatoes topped with cheese and green onions, and jalapeno baked beans, which were the best thing I tried.  After tasting all of my meats and sides, (this was about 10 minutes) I realized that my kids' fried sides had still not been brought out.   I walked back to the counter to ask about them, and the staff seemed very confused.  5 minutes later, fresh hot sides finally came.   Free vanilla soft serve is a nice touch at the end.  All in all, you can get much better barbecue and service at City Barbecue or Squealers.  I'm in no rush to go back.

Is this the best restaurant in the city? YUP. The tastiest AND most affordable vegetarian/vegan cuisine on the planet. I go crazy for the spring rolls, but the vermicelli bowls with fake meat are to die for.  Owned and operated by two complete sweethearts, MAKE time for Veggie Garden - I promise you won't regret it.

5 star service at really reasonable prices!

I was tired of paying steep prices at the mall salon I had gone to for years so I tried a cheaper place near my house and they promptly dyed my hair purple by mistake - I was looking for a new place so I searched around my work and found Lather. With only a few reviews to go on I took a leap and make an appointment with whomever happened to be available the soonest. 

I showed up for my appointment and was amazed by how cute the shop was. It was so modern and clean and to be honest I was a little worried that I won't be able to afford this place but I went with it - had a fantastic experience with Michelle, in fact the only person to ever get the exact red color I was looking for. Also super friendly and I felt very welcome and comfortable the whole time. 

I was so happy with my color and cut that I was completely blown away when she told me the price and it was literally half of what I had been paying before. I was so happy I have a 30% tip and booked my next appointment before I even walked out the door. 

I've recommended it to several friends and will not hesitate to go back!

AMAZING!!!!! Bridgette is more than accommodating, and her prices are great! She recognizes the military and offers a discount as well. My fiancé and I had her do our engagement photos. She is very nice and has a great and spunky personality. She made us feel like we were having a friend take our photos. Bridgette crafted something very special for us to use as a prop and we were able to keep it after the photos were done. Our photos were ready for us very quickly and her customer service is just awesome!! Whenever we send out cards, we will always go through her to get the perfect photos. I highly recommend her!!
Great food. Freshly made & decent prices. I had the curry, calamari & fried rice. The fried rice was to die for. My brother had a sushi dish. Beautifully made!
Had a great meal with my kids...great selection and quick service. Food tastes authentic and generous. Must try!

After years of wanting to go I finally took my kids. I'm glad I made it early because it got so busy people were waiting for tables. The selection of pottery pieces was insane. It took me forever to try to decide on what to paint...was a little more expensive then I expected but we will just be better prepared the next time. Staff was super helpful and really took the time to explain the process and the paints. Was a really fun thing to do with the kids. We had a great time and can't wait to pick up our pieces.

Not as good as they used to be.
A few years ago way better now not so much.
The last few times I went was not tasty. Frozen fries.

My first time was perfect. Small place but excellent food.  The bruschetta was delicious. The crepes were homemade and perfect thickness.  I will definitely come back. 
Today I came back and the sauerkraut perogis- were undercooked and had no sauerkraut.  They still had excellent cappuccinos.

Shannon at Rescue Spa saved my skin. After spending two years visiting dermatologists and other medispas in the city, while my terrible skin progressively worsened, I finally found Shannon. My acne and rosacea (embarrassing even to write) had gotten to the point where I didn't want to face clients at work or leave home for social events. This is not an exaggeration. I was only 35 but could see my skin toughening, reddening, breaking out in huge boils, scaring, and developing brown spots. I was scared that I was going to prematurely age. Shannon started me on a regular schedule of biolift facials, peels, microderm, IPLs, and even a Matrix treatment, as well as Biologique Recherche and Valmont products which have literally changed my life. Shannon never let me get a treatment I didn't need, even when I got excited at the improvement and tried to overdo it, and always explained what I did need. I never felt like she was just trying to sell me something, but instead fully trusted her expertise and it paid off because now my skin is clear, even toned, and healthy. The scars and brown marks and redness are gone. The treatments and products are not cheap, but they are worth every penny. Going from never wanting to leave home out of mortification to regularly being asked, "Who does your skin?" is one of the best feelings ever, and I love, love, love my healthy, glowing skin.  Shannon is an expert at what she does.

This place is EXCELLENT! The staff was super nice! Everything was super fresh and flavorful. The portions are huge! I got the bbq chikn and crakerony wrap as well as the Korean bbq wings. Both were delicious! The wrap was more like a burrito and it was HUGE, melty, toasted and packed with flavor. The wings were so realistic and delicious! The Korean bbq sauce is to die for!!!! It was so filling but did not make me feel sick or bloated after like a lot of more indulgent food sometimes does to me. I will definitely be back!

I first came here a year ago, and the young woman doctor, Tabitha, was AMAZING, THOROUGH, AND EFFICIENT!! As a mom with a demanding career, time is scarce. So, I can appreciate the superior level of quality and overall speed of service.  From the moment you walk in, the young woman at the front desk Voni meets you with a smile and tender caring demeanor.  Then Karina takes your vitals, she is so nice. Tabitha, the doctor is top notch and the reason I drive and extra 35 minutes out of my way to see her when I'm sick. Marcia and Consuela (Sway) are also keen to ensure that you have everything you need before leaving.  The staff is top notch, all so invested in seeing you feel better.  And the office is so clean!! BEST URGENT CARE IN TAMPA!!!

Food was delicious (as always), service was very good. All aces. The only negative I could say is this. A patron brought a puppy and was waiting for a table outside. No big deal until a waiter feels the need to scratch and pet the dog. I'm not a germophobe, but he handles people's food. It's just not right.

My wife and I got the Bulgogi bento box, bulgogi fries, and a boba tea. 
Everything was fantastic. Very flavorful and fresh.

Wow. This used to be my favorite place at Armature Works. But the last few weekends they have had this older gentleman working the lines on a Friday night. He sometimes looks confused and is definitely not helping with the line that is 15 people deep. I love the food here. They have got to figure out how to serve their customers in a more timely manner. This older gentleman is not doing much and taking up valuable space and time.

We are up on Mission Ridge and have Ligne Roset furniture from France.  Ben came and did a great job cleaning the couch and outside Sunbrella couch and chairs outside.  So great.  Looks like new.  So reasonable and good for fabrics.  And I keep him on my home exchange list of home instructions so if there is an accident the client can call Ben and he can come and take care of the problem without clients worrying -- or me worrying about them doing something wrong.  Just so good to have this kind of service in SB.  nan b.

Just moved into the neighborhood and couldn't be happier for a neighborhood coffee shop like this! When the weather gets warmer the open garage doors are great. Friendly staff (with a great taste in music, too!), good coffee and tea, and chill atmosphere.
I don't care for their coffee as much- but nice big location, plenty of parking, big windows...

The hoagies are just as good as the ones at the Conshy location.  I tried the turkey schwartzie, which has turkey, swiss, cole slaw, and dressing.  It was delicious, especially on a sesame seeded primo size roll, but I will admit that I also put some french fries from Wendy's in it.  Because I miss Hoagie Haven and Primanti Bros.  Try it and you will see.  It may be a heart attack in a roll but I will die happy.  A billion times better than Subway.  Service is A LOT better too.

We are not the typical reviewing customers of Battleborn Cleaning. We didn't get our carpets cleaned, We aren't renting a house and needed our deposit back or moving into a new house and wanted it cleaned. My husband and I are expecting a baby any day now and I can't get down on my hands and knees and scrub like I used to and I really didn't want to come home from the hospital to a messy house.

I contacted the owner Matt through Yelp. I'd contacted multiple companies for quotes on cleaning our furnished home. Matt responded within an hour. Usually I'd go with the cheapest company for something like this, but after hearing everything they would clean and with Matt promising that they don't "spot" clean and their Yelp rating, we went with them.

I've never had my house professionally cleaned so I don't have anything to compare their work to, but I was very pleased with the job they did. They cleaned places I would have never thought of. We had hard water stains and soap scum on our glass shower door I've never been able to get off. It was no problem for them. Our tile floors were literally squeaky clean.

Both Matt and Dylan were very professional and Dylan in particular was very personable which I appreciated. I also appreciated the fast service. From the time I requested a quote to the time our entire house was cleaned was about a week and half. If we ever need our house cleaned again, I would definitely trust Battleborn again!

El Sarape is quickly becoming a go-to restaurant for me when I'm in town visiting family.  The menu has something for everyone: my boyfriend who is usually in the mood for some red meat, my dad who likes to stick with the classics like quesadillas, my mom who is not a big eater and generally just gets the chicken tortilla soup while picking off everyone else's plate, and me--I love refried beans and mole sauce on my enchiladas and El Sarape happens to do both very well.  

If I had three wishes to change this restaurant, they would be: better acoustics so I don't feel like the people around us are screaming in my ear, free and bottomless chips and salsa for every table, and no automatic gratuity since we forget every time and end up tipping double.

I love love love PF Changs China Bistro.  They have varied foods Chinese, Mongolian, Szechuan... but all more or less under the Asian Food umbrella.  But no matter this place ROCKS!!!! 

Keep in mind, for whatever the reason, the food will vary from town to town.  I had PF Changs in Houston, New Orleans, Baton Rouge, Destin and Maryland... all good, but different in taste.  Apparently they send different spices to different places (for the taste of the patrons), go figure... but this is STILL a happening place!

I love going here for breakfast. Today, we thought we'd try going for lunch. We don't necessarily live close by and it's a good 45 minute drive to this location. It wasn't busy since it was around lunch time & we usually get greeted but maybe today wasn't a good day for the lady who say us to the table. Maybe she was just getting over the morning rush. Anyway, we already had our minds set on the fried okra and fried chicken but, once we got our food I knew that I had made the wrong choice. Nothing really seemed fresh except for the salad. The fried okra was soggy and it seemed like it was microwaved a little too long. The corn bread was hard as a rock and the fried chicken...well, I was hoping for fried chicken. A little bit disappointed with this visit. Next time, we will just come here for breakfast.

I've been going to the little nail shop for 2 years and it's amazing! They offer a large variety of different services and it's SO clean and well kept.

I've tried a few different nail techs; however, Carmen is by far my favorite and I continue to make appointments with her. She works quickly, does a fantastic job and is so sweet. 

The salon overall is large and I've never had to wait very long. There's tons of nail and pedicure stations. One additional benefit is they allow large parties and let you bring it food and drinks! While I haven't taken advantage of it I've seen parties in there multiple times.

While I've tried a few different nail shops in Indy, I'll never go anywhere else!

Amazing! Our party had fillet, rib eye, NY strip and chicken for the 7 yr old. Every steak was cooked to perfection and juicy! We also shared sides of potatoes au gratin, creamed spinach, fried onion rings, macaroni and cheese and broccoli. I recommend every one of them....and the desserts- coconut cake, sweet potato cake, and chocolate cake; all fabulous. It's also very kid friendly
It was a slow weekday morning and service was slow. Hash browns barely browned, coffee stale. My daughter didn't complain, but I sure did.

Just want to give a big shout out to Famous Tate in South Tampa.  When our Samsung refrigerator, along with hundreds of other Samsung refrigerators right now, failed to continue to work, we purchased a GE Profile refrigerator from Famous Tate.  Old fashioned service is not dead, and you DON'T have to succumb to buying large appliances at Home Depot or Lowes!  You deserve good service, and you definitely still get that at Famous Tate.   We got a better deal than what was offered at either one of the others.  They have my continued vote to keep shopping at famous Tampa icon stores.  They have been around for many years, and STILL giving fantastic service!  Thank you, Famous Tate, we love our new GE Profile!

Icky, dreary evening and had to run to Chalmette to take care of some errands. I was really craving seafood gumbo with garlic bread. I was cold, wet, and in a mood that only gumbo could fix. Husband asked around and we were sent here where he got me a huge bowl to go. He was a bit worried because y'all were closing but he shouldn't have been. Oh my goodness!  It was AMAZING!!!  Like seriously the best gumbo I have had in forever. I couldn't possibly say enough great things about it. Your gumbo rivals ANY in NOLA!  Thank you!!!!  Bad mood cured!

I have been a fan of the Atlanta F2O's for awhile. Excellent variety, quick, child friendly, fresh. Granted this location JUST opened up. I felt I knew the menu better than the workers. Everyone was friendly and there was a lot of management present from other locations. They were all still training. Had the Asian salad and added the almond chicken skewers. While delicious, it was drenched in dressing. Definitely ask for it on the side. They most definitely have to work out the kinks, but this is a solid concept I've been dying to see in Nashville for years now. I'm not sure the location is the best in town since this is a great place to take kids and this area just isn't the most kid friendly. Seems as if they are targeting working professionals. 
Give them a chance. Hoping they'll be hitting the mark next time I come back.

Came here after a couple drinks so this review may be offset towards the positive. That being said Kin is great. Definitely the best ramen I've had in New Orleans, and I'm glad that New Orleans is finally getting some good Ramen spots. 

I went with the classic Tonkotsu and the broth, as expected, was awesome, very flavorful and creamy.  The brussels sprouts were a nice hipster touch to the otherwise traditional tonkotsu. They cooked really well. The enoki mushroom I also enjoyed, although it was a bit hard to eat. 

The place is small and quaint - the perfect place for a cold night when you crave ramen (since we get so many of those in New Orleans), and the service was nice.

Also kudos to these guys for doing a vegan ramen, which is uncommon, but I'm sure is what I'll try next time. 

Kin is a catch. Worth checking out for sure.

We had a wonderful experience working with Melissa Morfin. She made the home buying process a breeze. Melissa looked through all of the paperwork closely and contacted us immediately whenever any changes were made. I would highly recommend working with her!

Truly one of the best places to get breakfast in the morning. The staff and the management team here are all very friendly and upbeat. A very welcome environment. Also lunch and dinner is this as delicious as breakfast. You're truly feel like you're at home but then this establishment.

What's a gal from BKLYN gonna do on a Sunday night?  Put on her Lucchese cowboy boots and take a line dancing lessons at Gilley's.

The original Gilley's was a bar/honky tonk founded in 1971 by country singer Mickey Gilley in Pasadena, Texas. Gilley's at the Nugget is to the Original Gilley's as The Olive Garden is to Italian food.  Don't get me wrong it was a good time but it feels like a chain restaurant.  

The food was ok.  Sampler appetizer plate and the nachos are gooey comfort food.   The Prime Rib looked good and was enjoyed by my friends. 

Yes there is a mechanical bull available for riding or photo opts.  Two bar areas with video poker machines on the bar; it's in a casino after all. Two dining rooms with plenty of tables to accommodate parties of all sizes.  

The dance floor is in a pit between the 2 bars with seating around the top. Yes people will sit and watch you dance.  Sundays and Wednesdays the dance lessons start at 630 PM.   We learned 2 dances.  The group included newbies like me and some seasoned line dancers.   Everyone was there to have a good time.  The more seasoned dancers even helped those with two left feet.  

It was a lot of fun, I'd do it again but I might get something lighter to eat before hand.

Just getting around to figuring out Yelp, so I apologize for the late review. I was in town for a short weekend and managed to sneak a private city tour in with Will. I was pretty reticent as I've lived in New Orleans before, but some of our group wanted to do it so Livery set us up on short notice. What an amazing experience - it takes a life-long resident to really show you around somewhere and Will's knowledge was truly "insider", taking us to cool places I'd never heard of. Very informative and fun, I'd highly recommend that you ask for Will.

We recently met some friends at Bing Bing for happy hour/dinner and it has now wedged itself on to my list of favorites.  We had a wonderful experience.

The decor is cute and energetic, the staff was very friendly and helpful, and the food was amazing!  They have a few things on the happy hour list but honestly, none of the prices are too high so you can basically order whatever you'd like and get a good deal.

We ordered primarily small dishes with one noodle dish and split everything.  We tried the spicy cucumbers, turnip cake, pork bao, soup dumplings, lamb dumplings, pickle plate, ribs, and the soy pork noodles.  Everything was crazy good but particular faves were the pork bao (sort of philly roast pork sandwich meets bao bun), the turnip cake (with a great fried egg on top), and the soy pork noodles.  I also really like the cucumbers as a lighter palate cleanser. 

Beers and cocktails were solid accompaniments but the highlight is obviously the food.  Cannot wait to go back.

The tacos are delicious and authentic compared to other places. The people that work there are very nice and make sure you are enjoying everything. I'm going to recommend this place to all my friends that love tacos as much as I do! They have a bacon wrapped burrito that I'll be sure to try next time it looks amazing. Thanks you Samano's for coming to fishers!!

I am a loyal customer at Johnny Londoff, this is my 3rd Chevy.  I like to buy them and keep them in good shape for years until the next best car is out.  I searched all over the car market for a 2015 trade-in and was not impressed with any sedan until I drove the 2015 VOLT.  Very nice car.  Efficient internet shopping link --but --be sure all the internet deals apply to your situation lest you waste your day not getting the  deal published on the internet.   I appreciate getting a fair trade in value for my used Chevy.  For 8 years that I have been going to Johnny Londoff their service center has been efficient and accurate with trouble shooting. They sell a  nice car at a nice price & service professionally.  I'm a fan.

This had been on my list for a while but could never ignore my frugality to give it a try.... Until now!

Good ambiance and attentive staff. I always like trying something different so I went with the lobster fried rice Shanghai style. I was not disappointed. VERY tasty and filling. Rounded the meal off with some solid creme brule. Its not your everyday place but a go to option for date night

Burger Up, Burger Up. One of those places that is always packed but probably doesn't deserve to be.

Okay, the burgers are pretty good. So are the fries. And yeah, the decor is nice.

But these employees! Seriously?! What is the screening process for these guys? I've never been served food by such a consistently put out group of people in my life.

If it bothers you to take my order, get a different job. Oh yeah, and put on a real shirt. I'm pretty sure that see-through v-neck tissue-t you've got on violates some sort of health code.

Was lost in the area and found this 
Place thru Yelp. Food was pretty good nice portions. Had shrimp scampi. Prices very reasonable. Will come back whenever we're stopping at the Columbus farmers market.
Amazing food. Tons of space upstairs. Healthy food options. WiFi and outlets. Like Panera but better.

Waited in line for over an hour to see the main collection, and once in was told by a member of staff we could exit to the bathroom and when we went to re-enter was told we had to wait in line again. One member of staff said 'I'm sorry', another muttered 'I'm not'. Such a shame this experience was completely ruined by contradictory, rude staff. Obviously wasn't going to wait in line for more than another hour so left and will not be returning.
I wish they had a clearance rack like other health food stores. But other than that we love this place for organic items.

Prob the most extensive Organic grocery in the Tampa Bay area.
Love the selection, the smoothies are all real fruit! The Cafe is gourmet all the way! Try it out. You will become obsrssed, like ME!

In my opinion, Fiesta Mexicana is easily one of the top Mexican restaurants in Reno. They offer a wide variety of food/drinks options that are always full of flavor.

While I usually indulge in chile rellenos or enchiladas, this time I stuck with a classic burrito. If you love the sides of rice and beans you will love the burrito even more as it is full of more of the same! I personally can't get enough. Rice is such a deal breaker for me and Fiesta Mexicana could not do it better!

Fiesta Mexicana is a must try in Reno!

Me and my husband went here for the first time a few weeks back. Never knew it existed but I am very glad we found it! The burgers are just delicious. Couldn't ask for a better place, nice set up. Great customer service, great job guys! Will be returning!

I was having French friends over for terrine de foie gras and cassoulet so I stopped at two Philly bakeries for pastry and baguettes. J'aime French Bakery has a great ambiance and the happy ghosts of the long gone Millenium Coffee (and Afterwords) that used to occupy the space.
Bright and cheerful, simple yet elegant - the visual appeal and feel is great.  I arrived early and my server was nice and cheerful, very helpful.  He seemed a bit chagrined when I purchased most of the stock that was set out - 3 each of: eclairs, pear tarts, orange tarts, blueberry tarts, 6 chocolate croissants, 4 sugar brioches, 3 chocolate brioches and two regular French baguettes.  It was too early for a few of the other items on the menu like the chocolate tartlet or the Paris Philadelphia.
Service was good and the pastries were overall pretty good and the baguettes were excellent.  One of our guests oversees the baking of croissants in France so he felt the chocolate croissant were a bit sloppy and as a baker of croissant and eclairs myself, I have to say I agreed.  They looked a bit hastily made but they tasted fine.  I still think that J'aime French Bakery was pretty good overall in spite of a few issues and so I still give them a 4.
I love this place.  My husband and I go here when we walk the dog on the weekends.  The yogurt is delicious, and I always feel welcome.

Enjoyable place for mexican food. The atsmosphere has a authentic mexican feel with the dark wood, lighting and colorful flooring. I started with their classic margarita and it was very well excuted and taste great, then as appetizers we got salsa rojo and fundido. Both very tasty. Everything here is ala carte so I was able to try several items. I love they have elotes it is often hard to find. My wife had the kale salad and really enjoyed it.  The big thing I liked was how friendly the staff was, great at answering questions too. The location is slightly off the beaten path for those of us not from Broad Ripple but it is still easy to find. They have free parking on the side of the building but only a few so get here early. But must important just get here.

What happened to this place?! I am shocked to see any good reviews!

Most likely, the good reviews are from those who ate here after some heavy partying in the FQ &/or listening to music on Frenchmen Street. Or maybe tourists who have no idea what real NOLA food tastes like.

Well save your money and a long wait unless you're so loaded that you won't remember eating here. Seriously, Taco Bell would have been a lot cheaper and tasted just as good. 

I was mortified that MB was my choice b/c the food, to put it simply, sucked and I was embarrassed that I bragged about how great NOLA food was to 4 people in our party who were from the East Coast.

The spices tasted like they came from a Zatarains box that you buy in the grocery store. The crabcakes had NO crab in them. The oyster BLT had 3 oysters. The cocktails (Pisco Sour, Bloody Mary, Caipirinha, etc.) were WEAK. 

Oh, and our service?! Let's just say that I'd prefer my servers NOT to be stoned because it impairs their memory and ruins the dining experience when we have to ask for things, like silverware, 3 times before its brought to our table.

I could critique each dish and cocktail but I'll save time and simply write that there was not ONE dish that anyone liked nor one drink (excepting a Diet Coke Zero) that was half-way decent. Come on, I'm from NOLA and watered down cocktails in the Marigny on Frenchman Street?!

Next time when I'm in the Marigny with friends from out of town, I'll take them for mouth-watering burgers and kick-*** cocktails next door at Snug Harbor, which is consistent and worth the wait.

If I could give this place ZERO stars (or .05 stars for the delicious Diet Coke Zero) then I would.
A must try!! Spectacular service and phenomenal food! Bar tender extremely knowledgeable and on point. Well worth the trip out of the immediate Tampa Bay Area. Brussels are the best!
Get the burrito bowl or you're ingesting like 2000 calories. Everything is fresh and I like that's it customize able. Overall good quick food.

We ended up here after trying to get into Coop's to eat. I'm sure that happens with some frequency to this place. We were starving and they had food. 

This was one of those places that reminds me of Rush St. or Division St. party bars here at home. The main reason one goes is to get tanked, it doesn't really matter where you are. Thursday, Friday, and Saturday this place is open 24 hours. Pop, there blew my little Chicago mind again.

Everyone was pretty friendly. I got the chicken fingers, nothing fancy. He got the rib eye special, a mere $15 for a steak, creamy garlic potato and garlic bread. Good deal, he thought! He said the steak was actually really good (and he's a steak snob). The potato had ranch dressing in it so neither of us cared for it. 

One of the bartenders told us to try their fried raspberry cheesecake dessert, so we obliged. Fried raspberry cheesecake?  Huh.  It was surprisingly good. Little wonton-like wrappers with cheesecake and raspberry sauce fried up, dusted in powder sugar and chocolate sauce. 

In all, not bad, the whole thing. They all seemed a bit amused by us for some reason.  So, it was an ok stop.

We stopped in for lunch and loved the food. I had the hot chicken with turnip greens and fried green tomatoes. My husband, Robert, had the meatloaf with mashed potatoes and macaroni n cheese. Chocolate Cake finished a delicious meal. Owner Hattie came out to check on us. Nice place we will me back.

This place was wonderful!  Atmosphere was very pleasant. The service was great! .  Almost too good. :) But maybe it was because it was a Monday night and they were slow.  The buffet was a meal in itself.  Will definitely come back!

A great experience overall! Everyone we interacted with here was very helpful and friendly. We had the boneless jerk chicken and curried goat, and both were delicious! The beans and rice were also good, as were the fried plantains. We sat out on the patio, and really appreciated the relaxed setting. We are looking forward to going back and trying more menu items - already planning for the wings and oxtail next time!!

Great band tonight -Ceramic Animal

Beer options were numerous and fresh
Lots of room and outside patio area

Only downfall is food is mediocre
Perhaps too fancy

Went here a few months ago, good food but not GREAT, if anything I give them props for a pretty creative menu/style of their food....kinda gourmet country cookin. I got the chicken cornbread salad and I thought it was tasty but not worth the $16 I paid for it; in our group people ordered the veggie pot pie and loved it, my bf got some kind of fish (I forget) and wasn't overly impressed. Its a cool place but a tad overpriced.

My pleasure to be the first to review this awesome coffee house.  The owners are very friendly and recently parents.  This is there 2nd location and I couldn't be happier for them.  They supply really good quality coffee from Verve Coffee Roasters but they have intentions to get to roasting their own at this new location.  Make sure to try some of there pastries and order a shakerato on a hot day!

Safe enough to send your children there, but there are some problems...

Nurses...good and an oops .  Everything was mostly fine until I had to go home and the nurse in charge lost my home care paperwork.  Now, how am I supposed to chase that down when I'm hopped up on drugs to numb the post-surgical pain?  I complained about this and they made excuses about how I seemed to be alert and oriented and that the nurse notes said she reviewed the paperwork.  BS!  It's not like ANYONE sane would have allowed me to drive a car, so I couldn't have been that alert and oriented, right?  Then again, they brought me a popsicle or Jell-O basically whenever I asked for it.  They even looked for Cherry, which is my favorite.  Thanks.  

Accommodations...good.  The food was good - not that I had much of an appetite.  My family really enjoyed the cheap, delicious, and nutritious eats in the cafeteria.  I've eaten there a few times since myself, and it's rather pleasant. However, the seating area could use a deep cleaning, some repairs, and someone to sweep and clear what's left behind by messy folks.

Upgrades...some oops.  The new forced air system is so strong that you practically have to wear a windbreaker and hunker down under the covers to stay warm.  Ear plugs would help drown out the noise produced by all this air whooshing around.  It was seriously a few decibels quieter in the old wing of the hospital.  What gives?  I also ended up in a ridiculously small room for the amount of visitors I had, which was only 3.  I was lucky to have a private room, but more seating would have been nice to have.  If they would just fix a few of these things, it would be a better place.

ER..oops.  When I was a student at UCSB, I went to the emergency room on a Sunday because I had an ear infection and dizziness - like falling down dizzy and having my best friend drive me there and wait with me there dizzy.  My doctor's office was closed and there were no urgent care clinics that I knew of.    I walked out with antibiotics and got better over the next week.  The report I read years later had an aura of condescension to it which I didn't enjoy.  I'm wondering "what the heck?"

Billing...oops.  It took some time to get some issues straightened out between insurance and the hospital.

Patient concierge..good.  We had to go twice and ask for printouts of all of the interactions the hospital had with the insurance so we could stick it back to our insurance.  I mean, we were even asking for the most minute details to track down any notes of calls or messages left with our insurance.  Thankfully, they had everything in the system and our insurance finally caved on the penalty fee.

Word to the wise -call your insurance or have someone in your family call the insurance to let them know what's going on just to make sure everything is covered and sent in by your doc in advance of a surgery.  I don't think my call would have counted, or even anyone in my family.  They want to know doc actual orders.  But they SAID that we ought to have called them.  Whatever.  Insurance is nasty - and I don't mean Obamacare insurance either.

I've tried out several dentists over the years and Dr. Brunelli is easily my favorite and the best!  His office and equipment is very modern and his entire staff is very friendly and helpful.  Dr. Brunelli and his hygienists are very open and tell you what they are doing and why.  I always appreciate when doctors are willing to spend time with you to answer any questions you have and explain your treatment options and everyone in this office does that!

Not impressed. Those are words that I don't normally use for a place that serves what I want, but in this case, it fits. 

My husband wanted a place to sit and watch the Rams/Packers game last Sunday and after asking around the office, nearly everyone said Sybergs.

"Great food!" they said. "Lots of friendly people!" they claimed.

The food was okay, and I mean okay. As in the onion rings were supposedly dipped in batter that I can only guess was supposed to be tempura like. Uh, tempura like batter does not have flour inside. Not appealing at all. 

The extra dip we asked for the mozzerella sticks were forgotten. The only good app we had were the pretzel bites, though I would have liked the option of a not so spicy cheese dip, or maybe even a mustard dip. 

Also, the claim that they have wifi is not true. They say they do, but we had to ask our server several times about it before we found out that 1) it's hidden 2) it does not go to the entire bar and that we can just piggyback on to the hotel next door. Uh, no you can't, because they have their's hidden too. The only time my smartphone picked up wifi was when I was in the lobby heading to the ladies room. 

So let's review - okay food, they need to work on the onion rings, offer better dips for the pretzel bites and make sure that their customers get what they ask for. 

Wifi needs a booster - they're not that expensive. I'm sure if they priced one out at Best Buy they could get one for under $40.

Let me start by saying that I love going to the Aces games!!  Great fun. 

However.....  the last two nights the women's room by section 116 was woefully under stocked. That is just nasty. Two nights in a row zero soap in ANY dispenser. Two nights in a row no toilet paper in many stalls. Two nights in a row no toilet seat covers in many stalls. 

Come on folks!!!  These are public health issues I'm talking about here!  Clean up your act, please.

As a woman who takes average shirt sizes and plus sizes in pants I often find it interesting and yes even a little entertaining to go into store that only stocks average sizes and see what kind of service I will get.

I have a few tops from Banana Republic that I have gotten at thrift stores and I love the material,quality,and colors of their clothes. Admittedly though I have never been in an actual store despite the fact that the WEM location has been around as long as I can remember. I had a little time to kill before bowling yesterday however and saw they were chopping off an extra 50% off of their clearance prices so I decided to take a look.

I walked in and an older lady standing at a table folding a shirt made eye contact with me but quickly averted her gaze back to her pile of shirts and I knew right in that moment it was going to be one of "those" kinds of visits. No greeting,no offer of help,no helpful reminder about the sale....nothing!

It actually does get worse though as while I was checking out the clearance accessories a young lady walked up to the older associate to ask a question but seemed to be struggling a little with her wording and had a fairly pronounced accent which lead me to believe english was not her first language. So they finished their exchange and the young lady walked away but before she was reasonably out of earshot another male associate walked up to the one folding shirts and commented on how weird he thought that young lady was.

It was at that point I became disgusted and walked out. If she was displaying some sort of "weird" behavior that made him think she might be shoplifting you definitely can follow them around the store inconspicuously but you certainly don't broadcast it to your coworkers,other customers,and the person in question while they are still in earshot. I really believe it had more to do with her struggles in talking however versus any behaviors she may have been displaying.

Banana Republic is a beautiful premium brand and I would really encourage this store to seriously look at hiring new staff that can create a beautiful welcoming environment that anybody can feel comfortable walking in and shopping at.

Very creamy and delicious cookies'n cream ice cream. 
However, why do they have 1 girl working the whole store full of people? It's lunch time during tourist season, there needs to be more people behind the counter. 
Don't get me wrong, the ice cream is worth the wait, but there shouldn't have to be one at an ice cream shop.

This is a great place to take your family or go out on a date. 

They have a lot of food options here. It is pretty expensive though. I might bring my own food next time. I feel for the Chickie's fries. Save your money, don't order them. I woke up today and my wedding ring was tight on my finger because of all the salt from the night before. Order a side of their cheese sauce and buy your fries elsewhere. 

The crab Mac and cheese balls caught me off guard at first. I bit into one without the sauce and I was extremely disappointed. I thought they were over cooked and dry. ($20 for fries and over cooked balls.) That was until I tried the balls with their sauce that they come with. Whoa, big game changer. It really tied all the flavors together. 

As for the ice rink, it's a decent size. They offer these little things that kids or adults could push around the ice to help them learn how to skate and or not fall. 

$10 for skates and ice time. ($14 if you don't have blue cross health insurance). 

They have a nice heated area indoors where I spent most of my time while waiting for my husband and daughter. They have heat lamps and fire pits outside also.

Ps.. wiz wit is a real thing.. so for all the hate about having my steak with cheese wiz... worry about your own cheese ;)

This is the spot to go the people are friendly,the magic guy he's really good what he does turning one dollar bills into one hundred dollar bill.he got card game's tricks of magic I give the man a 10+ also you should check out jewelry place they have beautiful hand made jewelry bracelet etc the lady that works there very nice and helpful perfect demeanor and respectful. One the steak  is beautiful inside old cowboys Western style the chairs are cool ass hell never seen a restaurant like this one it amazed me all the pictures they also got John Wayne suit his cowboys movie days in a glass container the customer service is great and with good attitude I ordered the cowboys streaks comes with a big steak and baked potato how ever u want it side comes with cowboys beans and salad and bread on the potatoes they give on the side the chesse and green Chinese Onion and crumbs of bacon and sour cream Soo good the BBQ sauce very good .the gun show was the shit lol really the Mexican guy really funny character and side kick they funny they all put in a great show I would watch over anytime Soo if u from out of state like must see and go they got a I hunted house very scary they say they say they mess with your mind they put in a room bye yourself pinch black they professionals in this type of stuff one was an actor before I just forgot to tell what movie he came in but anyways I'm coming rite back and bring all my family in too check out this spot I give this place a 10+ everyone has a great positive attitude all the store open up cool place to come with your family and kids..thanks pinnacle's peaks
Best guacamole and best fajitas around.  Salsa is always fresh.  Great margaritas and great service.

There's a review here that says about long wait times "if it wasn't any good, people wouldn't wait"

We came, we were told 15min, waited closer to 40, seated in a section that was EMPTY except for us (why were we waiting??) and then were served food a starving dog would think twice about. So while the opening statement may be true, for me, it will only happen once.

No return planned

I stopped by this small gem in my neighborhood and I was pleasantly surprised! The breakfast sandwich was fresh and flavorful. My oat milk latte was tasty as well! The staff was very friendly! Can not wait to go back!

Very disappointing.  Over priced for good, but low quality food.  the eggplant used to be 3 pieces now only 2 ... but still $24.  Ordered the baked ziti as well, but it was not really baked ziti.  asked for extra sauce, as there was nothing on it and charged $3.00.  Even with a $20 coupon it was over priced.  I used to like this place but not going back.

The new owners of the sunflower cafe have now brought authentic Japanese cuisine to Valrico. The octopus balls or takoyaki were delicious with a sauce that reminded me of my time living in Japan. No need to go to Temple Terrace for ramen noodles anymore. This is a go to place for the Brandon Valrico fishhawk area

Ordered take-out. Good asada torta that is filling, and can be two meals... Creative salsas: Avocado, Pineapple, Hot One (Habanero?), and a subtle and flavorful yellow/green one. I ordered chips and tried the salsas with chips. I recommend ordering a side of Pico de Gallo for the torta, and spraying yummy lime juice in torta to counteract heaviness, some shredded lettuce might be nice as well. The torta was good, and the meat quality and taste of asada (steak) were better than other places, which I appreciate! Horchata was refreshing as well!

Have now been here twice-- once midday on a Wednesday, out of existential frustration, and again last Wednesday evening. I certainly prefer the place empty by the light of day, with the chill bartender and lone patrons. Light allows one to appreciate the design of the walls and the absence of unnecessarily scantily-clad waitresses. 

Evenings, the waitresses are out with exceptionally short lederhosen (even though they're traditionally menswear) and lace tights, a self-congratulating bro grins for a photo with das boot, the lights are low, the music is a somewhat- incongruous playlist, presumably the result of staff compromise. 

The beer list, from what I've skimmed and imbibed, is a nice collection of a fair number of quality beers that could satisfy most people. Bru also has the Crabbie's ginger beer, of which I am in full support. The Brussels sprouts have a satisfying consistency, although there is more parm and bacon than sprouts. Somehow they avoid being too salty, and I enjoyed them. The Bavarian pretzel was also delicious, diminished if anything by the smoked gouda fondue. That dip was a tad gritty and lackluster. 

tl;dr -- I'd give Bru another go.

I used to hate icecream.  Now I'm a Jeni's addict.  

I'm not sure if I should be excited or mad at them, but it really is the best icecream you'll ever have.

The staff is always really friendly.

The WORST place ive ever been to. the girls working there were so unprofessional and completely ruined my eyebrows. The owner is a super B***** and had the biggest attitude problem. when i complained to her about my service she said i am welcome to go somewhere else. i was shocked. I would definitely never go back here again and do not recommend this to anyone who wants to get good eyebrows or any other service done. Ah ..so Disappointed

What's important to me for a service business like this is quality and value. Safe Lube delivers on this. How you do that consistently relays on good management. Tim has been there 16 years and will see to it things are right. All the guys there have a good attitude. I'll definitely be back.
