12/22/17 Just wanted to say that I thought the business' comment below was very well worded. Perhaps someday chance will bring us back...

11/23/17

I would say that if you're looking for a local's oyster bar in middle of the most non-local area, being the famous French Quarters, this would be it.  The decor looks like it hasn't been touched in half a century.  The shuckers totally have that I-don't-give-a-crap attitude mixed in with various half-smiles of indifference.  And of course, the entire eating area smells like bleach, but though quite strong still cannot cover the fish smells.

So we sat down at the bar and proceeded to order some raw oysters... or at least we tried.  I said what I said, then he says, "It'll be a while."  Then... sure enough, the dude just walks away, not to appear for another half hour or more.

But that said, looking around, the place had many locals who clearly knew all the servers and bar men.  Everyone was laughing and happy, about what I would not know.  Before we left, they all also insisted to grab our ol' shucker to take a big group photo along the back.  All friends here.  Like a bigass Cheers reunion.  Guess this was all how they liked it.  Everyone knows your name.

1. Big Easy IPA $8:  Crisp, but no body.  4/10

2. Charbroiled Oysters Half Dozen $12 "fresh, shucked oysters grilled over an open flame with butter, Parmesan cheese and herbs, served bubbling with French bread":  Well, definitely fresh oysters, so that's good.  But the cheese was hard and overpowering.  At least the oysters were still juicy themselves, but overall wasn't great tasting.  4/10

3. Raw Oysters Half Dozen $9 "fresh Gulf oysters shucked to order and served with horseradish and cocktail sauce":  Yup, definitely fresh.  Cold too.  Unfortunately, not the most flavorful oysters I've had.  Mostly just that juicy slimy texture is all you can taste.  Just a tad bit of mud around the rim for that authentic feel.  5/10

Well, it wouldn't be my first pick, but clearly there are people that love this plce (like the whole crowd that was eating with us).  Anyway, in my opinion, there are better choices for an oyster bar around here.

Also note, this score is just for the oysters and our experience sitting at the bar.  I realize they have a lot more on their menu, so who knows...

2/5

#neworleans #frenchquarter #oysterbar
Fun trendy breakfast place...long lines which is pretty typical for this type of restaurant. Food was good but not great! I'd still go back and think you should give it a try!

YUM!!

I was on a pizza kick during our vacation.  I found this place on Yelp and decided to give it a try.  

OMG, Grimaldi's is amazing!  Love, Love, Love the flavor of the red sauce, love the taste of the crust.  The whole restaurant smelled great!  To top it off, we were there on a Tuesday night which just so happened to be 1/2 off wine night yay us!.  Our server Kyla was very helpful and made some great suggestions since it was our first time there. 

We even ate there again the next day for lunch before we left to continue on our journey.  On the second day, we were pleased to learn they had lunch specials.  That was a great deal.  The lunch deal came with a personal pizza, a salad and a drink for an incredibly reasonable price.  We decided to try their white pizza this day, which was tasty, but I prefer the red sauce over it.  

Even more exciting news, while reading up on this place after the fact, I learned that there is one in El Segundo, California which happens to be very close to me.  I can't wait to try it.  I'll definitely be putting on some weight, I can already predict.  

Thanks Grimaldi's for serving up great food and great service - 

 - A Very Satisfied Customer :)

Ordered the duck ramen at $16. There was literally no duck in it (also compared to other reviews here). It wasn't busy, but we waited an hour for 4 ramens. We used to come here years ago, definitely downhill. Make sure you have a smart phone for the menu.

Super slow service this morning. Waited 50 minutes to eat once we ordered. However, the manager paid for our meal and apologized.  Brioche French toast was great along with salmon avacado toast and their BLT.  The three of us enjoyed the food. The Americano... the creamer... not a good flavor at all.  But, the teenagers liked the hot chocolates.  Our waiter was very nice and apologetic which was appreciated.  Seems like they have a few kinks to work out in the kitchen.  We will certainly be back at some point to try them again. The food was worth it!
One of my favorite restaurants in the city. I always love eating here. I like to go on weeknights because the wait is a little shorter. Great place for great drinks and great food.

Friends and I come here for trivia sometimes on Wednesday nights, which is always a lot of fun. I've tried a few entrees there which are pretty good. I tried the steak tacos there this time. Good flavor, good portion size. Came with chips, salsa, and guacamole, which were a little sub par, but still enjoyed it overall.

Note...make your reservations in advance.  I'm glad that we did.  Arrived at 5 til 8 and wee were promptly seated at 8.   I overheard the hostess inform a couple that the wait time will be 45-1hr.   Whew!!!!
Our meals were excellent.  The crab lobster bisque is awesome, I highly recommend it.
Not too good. Not much sauce and the crust wasn't very nice. It was the closest place to the hotel

Checked in around 3:30pm on a Sunday for a 2 night stay to celebrate our 14 year anniversary.  We stayed in room T1211 (which is a suite with a fancy European bath tub), after asked the woman at the registration counter for a room that didn't include a view of the parking garage.  Our view is now of a nice pool and some mountains in the distance, not bad.

The Tuscany tower suite is very nice - I'm serious.  The room is decorated in fancy shmancy Italian details with what looks like Venetian glass, gold lines, textured wallpaper, dark woods, and special attention to scroll/swirl details. The bathrooms hosts his and her sinks, separate room for the toilet, a full size shower, and a fancy tub.  Did I mention there is a flat screen Tv in the bathroom? The room itself hosts a large slat screen Tv, work desk, sitting area, bed, and 2 night stands.  They also have a snack tray and mini fridge - all managed by weight sensors and time limits or else it will be charged to the room.  I appreciate the note letting us know about these details.

We were in our room for literally less than 10min - didn't have a chance to unpack - when there was a knock at the door.  A plumber!  He tells us he was asked up by housekeeping for a clogged sink.  Well this sucks, I think.  I did manage to arrange my toiletries and makeup along the sinks in the bathroom in the few minutes we were here, so I'll move that.  30 minutes after some splashing, plunging, and really black water everywhere, the plumbers job was done and he would send housekeeping up.  15 minutes passed and no housekeeping.  We were kind of anxious to get out of our room and check out the hotel, so I called down to housekeeping, and they sent someone up right away.  After some questions on what we needed cleaned and what happened, the housekeeper very nicely cleaned up the plumbers mess.  Unfortunately, this unwanted and unexpected plumbing/housekeeping situation kind of put a damper on the start of our stay - especially because we had no clue; why book us in a room that needed some extra maintenance and wasn't ready for guests?  I called the front desk and they comped us $50, love the customer service. 

The casino itself reminds me of the Bellagio in Las Vegas; it's very heavily Italian themed.  Everything seems of great quality, it looks like a lot of money ha been pushed into this place to keep it fresh and nice, something I notice in details like shot machine chairs, extra added details to the columns and structures.  I also like that the casino floor is lit up well, isn't dark, and there are air purifiers everywhere.  It's the little things that ad up for me.

Gambling wise - I won $82 on a THE first a lot machine I sat down to play after playing for 3min.  Go figure!

Overall, the stay was great, the hotel is faamazingly well kept, staff was kind and hospitality was great, and I appreciated them making right on the inconvenient plumber needed to come to our room within minutes of arrival.

ok, i know all y'alls hatin on sisters, but if you go in a group, and are not trying to pick up women (i guess that's the point), it's fine! the drinks are cheap, the bartender was a hottie, and lesbian courting can be fun to watch

Miracle worker... I had a muscle spasm where my entire neck was locked in place and excruciatingly painful. After one session with Roger I was back to normal. Been going to him ever since for any little injuries I've had.

Got my hair cut and styled by Macy. She was so nice and did an amazing job on my hair! After a string of bad haircuts, I was so happy to be in the hands of someone who knew what I needed and liked even without me having to say it! I love my haircut and I'm definitely going back!
Heard about this place and went tonight with the family. Great food! Loved it all and great people! Some of the best BBQ I have had!

Had a great stay at India house hostel! Staff was friendly and helpful. The pool was fun and had a great vibe. They offer a super affordable breakfast and dinner for $5 that was delicious. I would definitely stay again!

This was my first time at volcano and the sushi was not appetizing at all! I ordered a spicy tuna roll which wasn't too bad but the shrimp tempura and eel avocado roll were not good to say the least! I will not be back especially for the price.

This place truly lives up to its name. I had the Spanish omelette (the best) and the biscuit comes with a maple butter spread that's to die for.Don't forget their signature Bloody Mary. I must visit if your in the area!

This was a visit we were definitely looking forward to but were extremely disappointed. The lobster roll came in a box with a small side of cole slaw and a pickle. The lobster roll was tiny and resembled a kids size portion at best and it was very bland, tasted absolutely no seasoning at all, it did have a mayo pack on the sideThe bisque had a tiny piece of lobster and it was definitely not worth the 9.50 price. Two rolls and two soup totaling 58.00 and I did tip, ouch. We needed to go to dinner after dinner. First time and unfortunately last time.

Service was top notch...food was incredible. The French pull apart bread they serve was amazing. My SO and I split an entree of steak and scallops. Steak was awesome, scallops were average. Expensive but worth it.

It's the spot for a cheap drink. Food is below average...plenty of other places with similar pricing that has way better food. It all tastes like something you would cook The first time you moved out of your parents house.
Very unassuming and almost missed it.  Fantastic trip through history and renewed my passion for genealogy.
Very helpful staff.  Good location and security seems good. No problems at all and when we decided we needed a bigger unit they made the change effortless.

I mean, it's the northwest side of town. I'm not expecting luxury living here, but I do have standards. We've been here since May 2013 and haven't had any major problems. When you decide to live in an apartment complex, you accept certain inevitabilities. Noise, parking, and pets. About that...

Noise - All we hear are neighbors either slam the front door or stomp down the stairs. Whatevs. No issues with loud music or people yelling. Bass music really drives me up a wall, and I would say that I heard more of that when I lived in Broad Ripple than I do here.

Parking - We made an investment in carports since there isn't a lot of parking near our building. Depending on where you live, parking can be limited. I would suggest scoping out the parking BEFORE choosing a unit to rent.

Pets - There's a dog park on the property and pet stations around the complex for residence to clean up after their dogs. I haven't had any issues with people not picking up after their pets, and the office sends notices to remind people to clean up after their dogs. Always see dogs on leashes, and the owners are usually really nice.

When we were looking at this place for an apartment, we came in with a list of "must haves". Since this place met our location needs, we spoke to the property manager, Rhonda, about what units would be available for our time frame. After looking around the grounds many times and talking with the office staff about the pros and cons, we finally decided to rent here. Unfortunately, we couldn't see the unit until it became available, but it was good enough for our needs.

So far, the only cons have been some of the shady neighbors. Thankfully, my husband installed an alarm in our apartment, just in case. What a guy!

First of all couldn't believe when I went to get service on my Jeep today that Nobody was wearing a mask not in the service area or in the Sales department. As for the service I was having done , it took 3 phone calls and 2 weeks waiting for the part to come in only to find out they had it in stock. 
Please have your employees wear face mask especially for the safety of your customers.

Johnny's is the quintessential Italian restaurant- with a marvelous ambiance, service and is plenty of menu options to satisfy anyone with discerning palette. 
As you walk in, you find a beautiful bar on the right, with stone walls adorned with photographs, mostly autographed, by celebrities whom have eaten at Johnny's. It is impressive and is a warm-up to the guest that one has entered a special place. The staff are so kind, apologetic when it is busy, and not merely matter-of-fact that you've come at a busy time so it's too bad for you; they are welcoming and seem to take no guest fir granted. It is recommended that one makes reservations, it just makes things easier for everyone. 
Having gone here 5 or 6 times in 3 years, the menu is fabulous and there is just no going wrong. I'm a fan lasagna and ravioli, both if which I've had here. Those are simple foods, but on the simple things Johnny's was extraordinary. The fettuccine Alfredo, option was with chicken, is pretty amazing....as one whom makes a very good Alfredo sauce myself, I am truly impressed with their sauce, which is not bland and commits to a flavor (so many places won't firmly commit to a flavor so the customer can salt/pepper as they please. The spaghetti bolognese, am option for younger folks in our group was so good- and it was delicious as a midnight snack. 
Desserts are just a must. 
The mood is set wonderfully with the Rat Pack, Sinatra, Tony Bennett and big band playing in the background- certainly quite intentionally to set the mood, but very appropriate for an extremely nice Italian restaurant. 
The service is on-point, no worry about having dirty dishes or going thirsty, the staff are very attentive and personable. 
Reno residents should know this spot, but anyone visiting and looking to go out for a dinner for two, in a group or even alone on business travel, try Johnny's and there will be no disappointment whatsoever.

Solid Chinese take out place! I call and pick up the order and it's always correct, even when I do large order for my family. Quick service and consistently good. I like the vegetable lo mein and the vegetable mei fun. My family also loves to get the appetizers like crab ragoon, shrimp egg rolls and wontons and the combo plates at dinner are great. Lots of options at this spot.

Woo Office Products...OK they're kind of boring but necessary. This store is small but has the essentials. And sometimes you can find someone to help who may know what he or she is doing. Bonus that you can buy a book of stamps with no mark up. Although the stamps are locked up in safe (or something) so a manager has to come retrieve them for you.

There is a parking lot and also an NOPD officer since the bar/daiquiri shop around the corner has had...issues, let's say.

Office Depot opened up this tiny store so that it comes up as the closest delivery location to the convention center. They make most of their money delivering to out-of-towners so us stop by shoppers are not their bread & butter.

We would recommend Lisa to anyone who asked us for a real estate agent recommendation.  She's amazing and so knowledgeable.  She helped us purchase out current (and forever) home back in 2009 and then helped us sell our previous house in 2010 after several issues with an unruly tenant.  And through it all, she maintained her professionalism and her kept soothing our worries.  She was patient with us when we wanted to see about 100+ houses prior to finding "the one."  And she would always give us her honest opinion on the house and what issues we may need to think about.
She has kept in touch with us over the years giving us advice about investment opportunities and also contractors for projects around the house.  I would recommend Lisa to anyone looking for real estate information in a heartbeat!
Fantastic place with variety! My favorite place is Steelbach, large portions of from scratch comfort food. Armature works is made up of a variety of vendors to cater to diverse crowds
my husband and i just had the best dinner here !! The staff is on point and the food - just wow!!!! Can't wait to come back for dinner and drinks . Thanks for everything Little Its !!!!

The place is unique in that Argentian resturants are not the most common.

One issue we had with this place is although it's kind of funny in retrospect is how cramped the place is, as we could literally hear everyone's conversations that were next to us.

Aside from this, the Croqueta's with codfish were really impressive, and not as skimpy as I expected although definitely not filling.

The picahna really was amazing for the price, and my partner got the NY Strip and the same can be said. Definitely packed with flavor and as someone who doesn't enjoy steaks too much, I really liked the food. Great yucca fries too.

Only reason I can't give 5 stars is due to the insanely cramped feel, and this is a ~6 month delayed review so it was when COVID was much worse, although I understand they only have so much space.

Aside from that, the food is 5 stars and I great date place, would highly recommend.

I only wish I could give this joint 6 Stars.  If you like Pizza - REAL PIZZA -  this is your place.  However, if you call these "we'll deliver our crap product to your front door" type pizza places you see on TV during the ballgames, then stay away!  Your pallet is conditioned to cardboard with sauce on top.

Delorenzos crust is like a circus in your mouth.  It's thin and crunches from the first bite.  The texture is perfect 100% of the time.  I add the pepperoni and my wife tell's me "you're happiest when that pizza is in front of you". It's the best!

Pandemic got you down?  Go get a couple of pies and take them home.  You'll be a rock star to your kids for 15 minutes.  

I stumbled upon their Trenton location while dating a young lady in the late 80's  when I lived in Bucks County full time. Twice a week I would drag this girl over there and we loved it!  These days I spend 60% my time in Newtown & NYC (& the rest of my time in Chicago where the deep dish "famous" for being nothing special).  I feel blessed that Delorenzos opened this location nearby.  It's a treasure to this community.  My only complaint?  I wish they had a loyalty club so I could get some free grub once in a while.  But you know what?  The product is so good they need a loyalty program to bring us all back!

The Service?  A+++
The Product?  A+++
The Price? A++++++  I have NEVER left this place and felt as though I did NOT get my money's worth.  

As I said earlier, if you're a rube who enjoys Domino's, Poppa John's or this other trash pizza, you are probably better served staying away Delorenzos.  There's a reason people line up for this pie EVERY NIGHT -  it's worth it!  The dining experience is great but I am just as happy eating this in my car with my kids and listening to their dopey music.  Good times.  No Burgers.  No Fries.  Just the best Pizza (and salads for the wife) I have ever tasted! 

Treat yourself right.  Go over to this place in Lower Makefield and get a couple of pies.  Life is short!  Enjoy.
Today I learned about the Vanilla Sweet Cream Undertoe. Amazing! The friendly staff was very helpful and enthusiastic. They went above and beyond by showing me my new favorite coffee drink.

Overall the food was tasty and the portions were large - great value! 

However we notice a ton of gnats/flies flying around the whole time we were eating. It was a cold, winter day so it was quite unusual to see that amount of bugs... and we were certainly not sitting outdoors! It just kind of makes me wonder about the hygiene in the back of the restaurant, so I removed 1 star.

On a good note: we had a large party and they were very nice about catering to us even though we had people arrive over a 2 hour time span. The server was very sweet even though she was incredibly busy! 

I would like to come back, but worry about the bugs - hopefully they can do some spring cleaning!

This is the third Morton's I been to and have to say the worst one of all. The service could have been better. The food was not good. It was my b day the only good thing about that was they decorated the table and I got a free  dessert.  Besides that, would not recommend to go eat to Morton's in New Orleans. The steak was not good the Mac n cheese lobster was not tasty, the drink was all right. Was not pleased at all. When u have good prior experience u expect it all around, I was very disappointed. To say the only reason I went to New Orleans is to eat there that won't happen ever again. I rather take a 4 hr drive to Houston than a 2 hr drive to New Orleans. However Houston, Tx and Miami,Florida  LOVED IT.

Had a great cut and color here by Alecia.  Both she and Timothy (I think that was his name) too their time to ask me a lot of questions before deciding what we should do with my hair.  The color has lasted forever and I love it.  Very warm atmosphere.

Something for everyone here...great food, great drinks, and great people watching. Expect to see a bunch of southern bombshells, and a bunch of musicians who love good food. Really cool vibe, yet friendly. Love it! Try the chicken waffle

This place has the highest of standards. They never let me down! The staff is always 110% professional. It's impressive! 

In addition to the great staff, the food is incredible! TRY THE MONKEY BREAD! It redefines monkey bread with the perfect balance of sweetness and salty.  Today, along with my required monkey bread, I ordered chicken soup and beet salad. I'm posting photos of both. The flavors are unbelievable! As you can see in the photo of the soup, there is plenty of chicken and vegetables. If you like beets, I high recommend the beet salad. 

They have good measures in place to keep everyone safe from Covid. It's carryout only right now but they did have some people eating at the tables outside. With nice weather around the corner, more people can take advantage of the outdoor seating. 

I love this place and look forward to a full reopening after Covid!

I used to rent -religiously- from Enterprise until yesterday.  I picked up my full size car on 10/8 for a 2 week rental.
FIRST- the agent put my in a very funky smelling car with  a pint of windshield wiper fluid.  Upon returning it on 10/23 I left a note in it describing my issues with the car during the rental:

FOB would not unlock the doors consistently- had to have AAA  unlock it 2 times
the infotainment system was shaky at best- blue screen of death appeared 2 times
the lighting for the infotainment system would work sometimes.
The gas door would not release - 
the back up camera engaged when you are driving -FORWARD
the check engine light kept coming on and going off the entire time

What surprised me the most was that no one at Enterprise cared about my sub par rental experience.  I was/am not looking for $, since I'm sure they already charged my cc full price for the sub par rental, but they made no effort at all to contact me to discuss the issues I documented and left for them.  I am in the customer service industry on a senior level and could not even fathom leaving a paying customer with such distaste in their mouth. 

Very disappointed in Enterprise, at least it's for the last time,

Brian Jones

#-7botfj-

If you love country music, you can't go to Nashville and not the visit the Grand Ol Opry.  The show has such a wide variety or acts that even if your main act is kinda lame (like our was...an American Idol contestant who isn't even really that country? Really??? Oh well, beggars can't be choosers), you will still have a blast.  If I lived anywhere near Nashville, I would be at this place a couple times a month.

This place is nothing like I imagined.  It's massive with  beautiful landscaping out front and great photo opportunities.  They also had some neat items at their gift shop to commemorate your experience.  As a country music nerd, Nashville is my mecca and this was a dream come true. (All non country music fans, please don't judge that statement.  It's beyond my control).

I took my first suit to get tailored here, they did the full gamete of alterations; took the back in, sleeves up, pants up, and legs in. They finished it within one week and it looked amazing. Price was $100 total for all 4 alterations, which was an incredible deal. Suit has held up to multiple dry cleanings and weddings since then. I will be back after I purchase my next suit. It looks phenomenal and holds up well.

After having bought 3 new Jeeps in over 20 years in both Reno & SLC, I needed another car from having my last Jeep being in and out of the shop so much. A friend had suggested that I get a used car instead of keep pouring money into the old Jeep. I listened to her, but I paid $4k to fix things. A few more things started happening to the Jeep recently so I tried to get a new car loan from one of my banks. I was declined for the amount I had asked for. I thought about getting a used car from CarMax. I finally called my friend's contact at Reno Buick, Laura Epperson. Laura had a salesperson, Brian Karsok call me back to explore my options. His manager Josh, listened to my needs and from what was available in stock, he suggested I apply for a lease on a new Buick Encore GX AWD. I signed the paperwork with Ivan. The staff & customers all had to be wearing masks due to COVID. One of my favorite sayings is "They put erasers as standard equipment on all pencils because EVERYONE makes mistakes... ". I was able to drive away the next day in a new car and they took my old Jeep in as a trade-in at Kelly Blue Book. They had checked my maintenance records of it in CarFax. Bryan, my salesperson, showed me my credit rating as we went through the process and he helped me learn the new technologies of a 2020 car. The sales floor is clean and the sales staff all were helpful to each other. The bathrooms that serve for both the sales and service area was clean as well. I look forward to working with the dealership in the future. Lance and Brian helped answer a question I had about registering my new vehicle in a friendly way.

Nice rooms, good size and clean upon check in.  Lobby bar area is nice and not crazy expensive ($14 for a pint and a glass of wine).  Great location!  Walking distance to everything we wanted to do and see.

Sorry, I just didn't enjoy the experience at Ichicoro.  We were excited to go to a ramen restaurant during our recent Tampa trip.  We arrived about 10 min after they open for dinner service and were seated immediately at a small'ish table right underneath an air vent.  Not enjoyable to have cold air blowing on my head the entire dinner, so I asked to be switched to another table.  I can't imagine really anyone wanting to sit under that vent unless the AC is not on, which in Tampa, probably kinda rare.

Anyways, we got moved to a very small table, a bit uncomfortable, but is ok since, well, we are having ramen... one bowl in front of us each should be sufficient.  We ordered the chicken karaage as starter, which I was not a fan of.  The breading was a lot heavier than what I am used to with this dish.  

As for ramen, I tried the Champon Ramen and hubby tried Spicy Kimuchi.  Unfortunately, I really didn't enjoy the flavor.  The champon seems to have a lot of extra stuff that didn't make sense to me.  The shrimp and the raw'ish onion especially stood out.  The price point was high to start with for ramen, then to add $2 for half an egg, just didn't do it for me, sorry.  Hubby didn't like the broth flavor of Spicy Kimuchi, but said it was better than mine after trying my bowl.  He did enjoyed the pork belly, but the big piece of pork rind that quickly became a soggy mess, didn't make sense.  We were looking forward to be wow'd by an upscale'ish ramen place for a nice hearty delicious bowl of ramen, but walked away disappointed.

First I called to ask about their vegetarian options. The manager answered and told me about their fish plates...read online that they have a vegetarian paella so I booked a reservation. Reservation was for 8 that was booked 2 days in advance. We got sat at 9. Ordered straight away and our food was served at 10. Our waiter did not check on us between that time. The paella was a $23 plate for rice and vegatable that were obviously pre frozen because it was served cold. Do not go this place is over hyped. Maybe other locations are better but it is not worth the wait or horrible food.
Staff is abysmal. Untrained. Zero care. No asking what we would like.  Brought out milkshakes with no straws, spoons, napkins. Bad service. Milkshakes are more for photo taking than taste.

This place has the best macaroons in Philly! I highly recommend trying any of their macaroon flavors. Their other pastries and deserts are delicious too! I gave them 4/5 because service can be a little too slow and disorganized. I've been going here for years, and have seen more and more people crowding the small cafe. Time for another location!

Bistro 112 deserves some credit for staying open across the street from a Second Cup and a couple blocks away from Starbucks (and another Second Cup). The biggest draw for me here is coffee and baked goods for less than across the street, with the added perk of supporting a local business over another chain store.

The food here is alright, nothing spectacular. The coffee is good, but again, nothing earth shattering. Still, I often head here when I need somewhere to do some reading or writing. The staff here are friendly and super laid-back (and amazingly patient with technologically challenged folks using the computers there). If you're looking to procrastinate, eavesdropping on their conversations is often pretty entertaining.

Free wifi and giant people watching windows too.

Finally made it over to Dulce and I'm glad I did!  I got a trio of cupcakes, the Elvis, strawberry shortcake and samoa.  The Elvis was awesome, peanut butter and chocolate (aka gods gift to this world).  The strawberry shortcake was so light and  I loved the cold icing from being refrigerated, finally the samoa was just a great combination of coconut, chocolate and white cake.   
I will definitely be back!

Food was excellent and the service was fast and efficient. Mr. Sutton was our waiter and he was patient and gave good suggestions. The drinks were good as well as the music selection, chill vibe overall.

One of my favorite stops on our trip. The wait staff were very attentive. Our waiter explained in detail the different entrees on the menu to help us make a decision for the indecisive group. 

Pork chop - Enormous and delicious! How do you eat a massive piece of pork. Very carefully. The flavors of the grilled seasonings were tasty. The potatoes were cooked to perfection. 

Gulf Fish - yum! For someone who is eating on the lighter side, this is for you. Under the fish are grilled veggies and potatoes. Loved it. 

Since it was my birthday they surprised me with this sweet treat. I have no words. It was just the right size and full of flavor. 

For an appetizer, this bite size ham and cheese but was a burst of flavor that was unexpected. 

Make a reservation and enjoy!

I preface this by saying that I only came to this acme because it was close and I needed to grab something really quick and I knew that there are never any lines at the supermarket. I was in need of dinner one day and decided to pick up some of the fried chicken in the hot food section. Be that it may, the chicken was absolutely tasteless. It was as if they put flavorless flour and decided to fry it. I was so turned off that I decided to grow the rest of the 8 pieces away. It was too later in the night when I realized this so I didn't even bother to return it. It's safe to say that I won't be coming back here with the exception of utilizing the redbox machine to watch movies.
This is my favorite thrift store by far. They have my taste in home decor at a good price.

Love the daily specials menu, which seems to change seasonally. Fried chicken night is worth checking out and I look forward to the day they bring back their deep dish pizza night! Had an awesome burger the other night and I always like getting a salad because they have delicious house made dressings.  Definitely check the $1 day old pastries out at their counter. You never know what amazing baked goods you will score!

I love the Leg. I love walking around the beautiful, well-maintained groups, I love cycling here through the River Valley, and I love splashing around int he wading pools in the summer months. As a political activist, I have taken part in numerous demonstrations on the steps of the Legislature building itself - what a rush! This is the place where democracy is supposed to happen, where free speech reigns, and people should feel free to express themselves. I take pictures here, attend events, and just generally think it is one of the most picturesque places in Edmonton. Guided tours of the Legislature building are available, and in another structure leading to the LRT stations is a gift shop, washrooms, and all kinds of displays about Edmonton. I would definitely recommend a tour here for anyone visiting the city.

Awful!  Service was slow, and stupid. 
Can't toast bagels, asked for butter they said ok. No butter in bag. Asked for butter again. They were out. Told me to go to another restaurant and ask. REALLY!?!  I would have spent my 25$ at the other restaurant had I known. No cream for coffee had to wait another 5 mins for them to refill it after asking and they made it sound like I was asking for them to donate a vital organ. Ugh. Not a great way to start a travel day.

I've had multiple problems with Papa John's and not just this location. 

However, Papa John's has an annoying habit of not telling you the wait time when you order online until AFTER they charge your credit/debit card. 

On multiple occasions, including today, the wait time is over 2 hours, which is unacceptable. To add insult to injury, when you cancel the order, it takes 2-3 business days for the money, which was taken out immediately, to be returned to your card.

I have made several complaints to Papa John's customer service and have never received a response.

I am an out of town guest and I wish I lived in the area just to eat at this amazing place. The food was phenomenal, the customer service was outstanding, and I would recommend this cute joint to anyone. I got the chicken and avocado sash which, there was a full breast and LOTS of avocado.  Also you have to go just to get the pecan bread pudding!
Food was super good !! Made my parents try it and they loved it !! It was my second time coming and it was so fun trying more things off the menu

This restaurant has revamped the interior over the past year. The food is rich and heavy. I went here for brunch and ordered the turkey sandwich and nutella cronut. The fries are excellent. The sandwich was also good. The cronut was delicious. My only complaints is the price of the burgers and the lack of healthy sandwich options.

Ozzie Jr. was our tour guide and he was amazing. You get to see ALL of New Orleans and stop in a beautiful park. The guide answered all of our questions, was very personable, and has a great love for the city. He even shared so Katrina survival stories.  Definitely do this tour. It is so worth it, and if you do it early in your stay, you can probably avoid most tourist trap places.

I had lunch with a client in Ybor City this afternoon and we chose to meet at Bernini's on 7th Avenue & 17th Street. It had been a while since I had eaten at Bernini's, and I had honestly forgotten about their excellent Italian food.

The restaurant sits right on the corner of 7th & 17th, and there is plenty of on-street and metered spaces to be found during the lunch hour. At night I usually park in the nearby garage.The Baroque-style of architecture and the artwork of Giovanni Lorenzo Bernini add a real touch of class to this establishment. Bernini's is a very elegant stop along the main street of Ybor, and the locals flock there for an excellent Italian meal. 

We asked to sit upstairs this afternoon since it was a bit chilly, and there was a breeze blowing through the open front door. Normally in our Florida weather the cool breeze would have been welcomed, but I certainly appreciated being able to sit upstairs along the windows and watch the people scurrying by below us. The pace of service was just right with our waiter offering several menu suggestions, and then letting us relax and have our business discussions without too much interruption.

My client ordered a delicious-looking Greco Roman wood-fired pizza which was made with green & Kalamata olives, red peppers, capers, grape tomatoes, Feta cheese, Mozzarella & plum tomato sauce. It looked great, and he seemed very happy with his choice. He boxed up some to take back to the office, but later confessed that he had a "late day snack".

I ordered the Cigar City Chop salad (affectionately known as "Bunny Chow") and this delicious leafy-green treat contained provolone cheese, marinated artichoke hearts, Romaine lettuce, shaved carrots, toasted almonds, artfully-sliced pieces of salami, red onions, asparagus and grape tomatoes with a drizzle of Italian vinaigrette. The salad was quite large, and I thoroughly enjoyed eating something a bit lighter for lunch.

The lunch menu has lots of Bruschettas, pizzas, salads, sandwiches, pastas, risottos, and full-on entrées for very affordable prices. The dinner menu has many of the same selections, but the entrées are meant to be enjoyed with an excellent glass or two of wine and some lovely company. (Great spot for date night just in case you need a recommendation.)

Great tempura shrimp and delicious cocktails. They were slammed so service was on the slower side and the bathroom situation is downstairs and shared with other businesses so not great for the older folks I went with.

I've checked this place out a couple of times now, and I'm looking forward to the office opening back up so I can introduce my colleagues to it. Very happy with what I've tried here. Definitely going to be going back often.

If the burgers were always this bad, I'd give more stars. But to go from that to this is a slap to my Fin face. What once was thick juicy meat is now a thin frozen patty. You can keep your corporate decisions. I'll keep my money.

- A guy who used to love the monster burger. RIP monster burgerz

We had a really great experience with Alistair. He went above and beyond when we found out there was a manufacturing error with one of our products we bought. He went out of his way to fix the issue so we wouldn't have to ship the product back. He also hung a tv that looks really clean with no wiring. He was quick and efficient and we will be using him again.

This review is for happy hour: nice selection of food at $8 each (ahi tuna, crispy lobster, steak sand, crab blt). Not my fav place for HH drinks; prefer Sea Salt HH deal. Get there right at 4:30, bar fills up quickly. They don't offer HH on Saturday, but Sea Salt does!

Cash only, long lines no matter when you show up, limited menu. But: delicious. So delicious. We went there with a friend on his birthday- it's all he wanted to do on his day- and oh man. I sort of haven't stopped thinking about it since then. Huge portions, low cost.  Yes please !

Good Karma Cafe is one of my all-time favorite cafes in/near University city. It's secluded enough from campus that you don't have to deal with the hustle and bustle, but close enough that a 10 minute walk will bring you right back to University City. This place is the perfect cozy cafe to get some work done or catch with someone over coffee and I definitely see myself coming back when I'm back in University City and need to grind.

Tip: Their seats are metal and can be cold. Take advantage of the available seat cushions if you plan on spending a few hours here!

Absolutely great service!! Fair prices!! Very knowledgeable staff!! When my husband and I first started going to VIP Game Store we did there monthly plan, pretty much unlimited video game rentals for about $20 bucks a month, and we loved it, the only reason we stopped is because the games we were buying from them were not getting played at home. 
I have also traded a lot of stuff for credit with them and was amazed at how much I got. 
You can pre-order with them, sometimes they get stuff on the release date and sometimes they don't but they let you know that before you put money down.
I really don't buy video games anywhere else now!

I had a really good roast beef po-boy on Monday. It was dressed with lettuce and tomato. The meat was tender and flavorful, and the sandwich was not overstuffed with too much meat. The French bread was perfect. It was crunchy on the outside, soft on the inside, and the right thickness. The roast beef gravy was the right amount to complement the sandwich without making the bread soggy.

Our party of four shared an order of fried pickles. The batter was thin, and fried to a good crispyness. The ranch dipping sauce was a nice contrast to the sourness of the pickles.

More laid back then the Mad Mex in University City but still a good place to go for a bite to eat and drinks. The service was good, Frozen Margaritas were great, and the Honey Chipotle BBQ wings were delicious!

They might be open until 8 o'clock at night or so their Voicemail says but they have very limited staff to answer the phone. Very frustrating to try to call and set an appointment. I'll take my business elsewhere or it's not such an effort to try to get an appointment set

Literally the BEST escape room I have ever been to. I'm a HUGE 1980s fan and I literally felt like I stepped into heaven. There are 4 rooms total that you'll have to breakthrough in 1 hour's time. I did the game with my husband and I, just the 2 of us. We made it out with just minutes before we hit the 1 hour mark. If I could live in this escape room, I would. It's the closest this 1993 baby will get to living in the 80s. I highly, highly recommend this fantastic escape room- if you are a 1980s fan you have GOT to get yourself over to this escape room ASAP. You are missing out!!

The restaurant is currently under construction so unfortunately this has tainted my first experience.  I called to order take out. Was told it would be ready in 30 minutes.  An hour and a half later,  we finally got our food. That said, it was delicious. Surprisingly, a simple spicy salmon roll wws my favorite thing.

I am so grateful to have a Massage Envy in south Reno. I really appreciate their membership options which help someone like me who needs frequent, quality massage therapy but doesn't want to pay for extra frills at some fancy spa. I don't need fluffy slippers, a robe and a latté, I just need someone skilled at getting my knots out! 
The interior decor and helpful staff make it easy to begin your relaxation process the moment you walk through the door. I love that they have a quiet waiting area where your therapist meets you before the session, then walks you to the prepared room. It's also really nice to have private rooms, bed warmers and linens as opposed to some of the cheaper "foot massage" places where you're sitting in a chair often without much privacy at all. That's not my cup of tea.
I've had two massages at this location and both of my therapists were fantastic! They asked questions about my needs before we began, then checked in frequently during the visit to make sure I was comfortable and to ensure they were using the right amount of pressure. 
If you're up for it, the membership provides a discounted rate not only on the one hour session but also if you want to add on 30 minutes or more. They also offer stretch sessions and facial appointments and have quite an extensive menu of services to check out. Scheduling over the phone is easy and their hours of operation should work for just about everyone. Go take care of yourself and get a massage!

Dropped my new couch and bent it. When I asked about when it would be replaced, I got the run around on the phone. Once I came to the store for face to face conversation, the run around continued. I would give zero stars for customer service.

What ever happened to customer service.  Drove through kinda late (11pm) and was charged three times for a 4.39 meal.  "Oh sorry Sir, we are training a new employee and didn't realize I swiped your card three times.  Can I get you a free apple pie for your troubles?"  HMM 14.25 Charge and they are offering a free dollar product.  Yeah I think not.  still have not received a refund to date.  What ever happened to customer service?

Overpriced and underwhelming.  This hotel had the feeling of a motel in a parking lot.  It felt old and poorly maintained.  The security door was broken and wedged open all night with an athletic sock. There was either dirt or mold in the gasket of the refrigerator.  The room's popcorn ceiling added to the feeling of being closed in.  Breakfast was a choice between a small packaged muffin and a granola bar.

Would not return for the price.

Another night out with Trenton Eats Local led us to Casadeluna. Apparently, the only Venezuelan Arepas place in town and NJ.
Casadeluna is now where Tatonni's Italian restaurant used to be. And the interior has not changed, but we came for the Arepas!
15 different kinds from Pernil, which was delicious, to scrambled eggs...all for$5 each.
Casadeluna also serves many other options like fajitas and tostadas. 
They have a full bar however this is not the place to come to for a cocktail. Most patrons are doing shots or having beers or both. 
The lovely proprietor really wanted to please and made F her version of a gin and tonic (without tonic. Although she was going to run across the street to try and get some!).
I was able to get a vodka martini, made by one of the people in our group, lol!
Trenton is one of the coolest, quirkiest places in NJ. Our restaurants, cafes, shops reflect that vibe. A lot of people don't get us and probably need to stay in the sub-urbs.
This is another local spot we will be coming to often!

Not open. Not sure if permanently. But today Saturday, at 10:42am it should be open. 
Done with trying to guess their schedule, the Union st. And the Market St. location.

If you go to Belle Meade, I would recommend purchasing a guided tour of the mansion. That gives the only verbal information (and access to a tour guide that can answer question). The rest of the plantation is self-guided and you can't see the inside of the mansion without purchasing the guided tour. 

I found the plantation very interesting, but I would have liked to hear a more balanced approach of the history of the plantation from both sides. I noticed that they glossed over the negative history and likely mistreatment of slaves and servants. They mostly focused on the story of the plantation from the owner's family's perspective, ignoring the ugly parts of it's history. (Which I think would be healthy to hear about and talk more openly about).

That being said, I thought the tour was interesting. I really liked our tour guide in the mansion--he had a good sense of humor and was very knowledgeable. However, the guide for the tour after ours sounded like a cranky old woman without a sense of humor. So I imagine the quality of tours vary, based on who you get. 

The wine tour after was included in the price, and the wines were fun to sample. Although it had some drawbacks, overall I was happy with the trip.

The Palace Grill came out as highly recommended by one of my colleagues who went to school in Santa Barbara. We decided to check out the restaurant on a Friday for an early dinner. We reached the restaurant a few minutes before they open for dinner and were shocked looking at the long lines that were already formed even before the restaurant opened!

There were two lines, the one to the left was the one with people who had reservations and the one to the right was the one with people who did not have reservations. We were lucky to get seated soon without reservations.

We were immediately served complimentary muffins, they are fresh out of the oven - warm, fresh and fluffy. There were different varieties of muffins and one of those was jalapeno. I have always thought muffins are supposed to be sweet and was enthusiastic about trying  a spice one. Loved it. My friend who has no resistance to spicy food found it to be too hot.

We ordered Cajun Crawfish Popcorn and Gumbo Ya Ya for appetizers. For entree, we got Jambalaya pasta and their signature steak (House Specialty Stuffed Blackened Filet Mignon). The steak came with veggies and mashed potatoes, was phenomenal! 

As my friend raved about their Louisiana Bread Pudding Souffle, I couldn't wait to try it. Ohhh, freshly prepared and sooo delish! The dark Chocolate Souffle turned out to be amazing too.

They sing songs every hour or so and the waiters handed out the song lyrics to all of us. All the waiters clinked wine glasses for a toast with each of the restaurant patrons. The vibe was so energetic.

The decor was in line with the Cajon/Creole style in Louisiana. Playing cards were strewn on the roof.

Fantastic service; great food. Love to return!

This was a fabulous experience!  Check in was very quick and the office staff was very pleasant.  The doctor, Dr. Keith Graham Williams, was wonderful! Friendly, knowledgeable and resourceful. I got my prescriptions right on site quickly and out the door!

I went into BGI North this morning because the right brake lever on my Cannondale Trigger (that I bought from BGI) was broken from a collision I had with a tree. I saw how busy it was and, knowing that Covid-19 had caused a lot of people to take up cycling, I expected the turnaround time to be weeks.

When it was my turn, the gentleman at the counter looked at it and said, "Let me look up the part. It may be out of stock at the supplier." At this point, I was thinking, "I'll be lucky to get the bike back by Halloween."

One of the managers - Mark - walked by, took the bike, and started to work on it. After a few minutes, he told me to leave it with him and he will call me later in the day. At 4pm I received a call saying my bike is fixed. Mark was able to use a part he had and just charged me labor. I got my bike the same day.

It is this level of personalized service that gains companies like BGI loyal customers. I am very grateful for the service that Mark and the team provided and am a loyal customer for life.

Stephen is a fantastic Magician. He is very personable and
 I have had the pleasure of seeing him perform his magic on several occasions and would definitely recommend him hiring him for a party.
Watch for sale items and you can get what you need at a decent price.   Grooming prices are reasonable and they have great hours.

I usually find this bowling alley the best in the area, but I couldn't have had a worse experience tonight. My ball continuously got stuck and wouldn't return every three throws, the pins didn't reset properly throughout the night, and to top it off the ball return machine scratched my ball on my last throw of the night. I'm highly disappointed and would not recommend this place to anyone. They need better upkeep!

Absolutely abysmal. The first time going to the doctor, I scheduled an appointment for only one thing, a particular shot required for my child to enter school. Before going, the school nurse called the office to confirm that he was only getting the one shot. 
When my wife and mother arrived with my son for his appointment, they said, "so he is here for his 4 annual shots". They both promptly corrected the nurse and told her that he was not there for 4 annual shots but only for one particular shot to start school. They said ok and then when my child went into the doctor, the doctor proceeded to give my son 4 shots he did not need and we did not want, and neglected to give him the one shot he did need. 
So my child could not start school on time and when I asked for an explanation, they said it was my fault for not telling the doctor the correct shots my son needed. The doctor said, "well, he should have those shots anyway, so it is ok", but it turns out they did not retain all of the medical records and shot records we gave them so 3 of those 4 shots he received were ones he already received 2 months prior. 
Then to add insult to injury, they lied to cover their mistake and said that my wife was required to sign an acceptance letter for each of the 4 incorrect shots he received. I asked for a copy of these signed letters. They could not produce them. I later asked a different nurse for the signed records and she said "Parents are not required to sign an approval form, I do 't know what that nurse was talking about". She then apologized. So out of the 4 awful nurses and one negligent doctor there was one good nurse. Thanks to her because she was very nice but not enough to save this awful pediatrician.

A group of us went to the Washington Street Ale House.  We waited 80 min for our food to come.  We ended up having to take our meal to go and eat it at a business meeting.   They had the audacity to charge us full price for our meal.  We notified the hostesses however they said only mgt could deal with the issue.  After waiting 10 min we left.  I left an email using their website but have not received a reply from them after waiting over a week.  

Update:  left 2 emails.  Still no reply.

We had a family dinner here and the staff and food was fantastic ! The rib eye steak excellent ! The service  was very very good ! The manager  couldn't have been nicer and very accommodating ! We went back tonight for a burger ! Wow! One of the best I have had ! What a really good place ! Highly recommend their food !

This will be my third and final visit to this restaurant. We walked in on a Friday at the lunch hour to a full house. Out of ten tables, every one of them seemed to have empty drinks and no food. The service of the counter staff was lacking in that they seemed more interested in what the kitchen was doing than about what questions we may have. The kitchen staff was loud and at one point yelled "Don't worry about the recipe, just get over here." In observing other patrons as my party waited for our food, several got up and down to check the status of their orders. The prices speak to a higher end market, the menu itself speaks to office workers and college students looking to grab a quick bite (it's all served in To-go containers, but the wait times speak to a retirement home -- a group of people never needing to be anywhere. Groups of people who were before us still have not received their food or have partially received their orders: after over twenty minutes of our being here. Parties that came in after us have received their orders. The food isn't terrible. The lotus chips are pretty interesting when the fry oil has been properly changed. As a Saint Louisian who's proud of my city, I'll buy anything with the name on it. But please, do not get the STL philly. It's bland. It's greasy. For a place that speaks about its passion for travel in their menu and motto, I simply expect more.

Oh. And my boyfriend ordered the Chicken Adobe Phatada with lotus chips ($8) and by the time we got to where we were going to eat, he opened his box and realized they gave him a quesadilla ($6).

If this place wasn't so expensive, it'd be five stars for sure.  They have gourmet sandwiches, salads, and soups.  There were so many amazing things on their menu it was hard to narrow down my search.  I ordered a sandwich off of their daily specials menu, which had garlic aioli, chicken, pesto, goat cheese, and whatnot.  It was soooooo GOOD.  I also had a cup of their broccoli soup, which was similarly fantastic.

My husband had this gigantic pulled pork sandwich, which he really shouldn't have consumed in its entirety...but he did because it was that good.

It's hard to find food this good in Goleta, and the prices keep the undergrads at bay!  A peaceful retreat with delicious cafe food...I'll be back!

If you have a car produced in the last 5 years the part that seals out air on the gas pump is too small so you have to hold it and it clicks on and off the whole time. It's inconvenient at best and frustrating in general. I am waiting to see if I get an extra hold on my card for using this station as well.

I've been here 3 times now and after my last experience I will never go back. The first time I went with my boyfriend and his parents to try the place before he had his graduation dinner there. Our waiter was very polite and helpful and the food was pretty good. The grilled artichoke appetizer was delicious however the shrimp cocktail had only four so between the four of us we each got one shrimp which was not exactly what we has in mind for an appetizer. The entrees were good but nothing special. We came back a couple weeks  later for graduation an we had a great time; no major issues. About a week ago, my boyfriend and I went to try there new happy hour and it was the worst dining experience I have ever had in my life. We ordered 2 draft beers which were advertised as 2.50 a pint and the pulled pork sliders appetizer. The beer selection was alright. My boyfriend ordered the Figueroa mountain IPA which was delicious but when we got the bill they had charged us 4 dollars for it. When we asked the bartender about it he took our bill to change it but while he was changing it the woman manager was trying to argue with us that it does cost $4 but the menu advertised ALL draft beer for 2.50. After I pointed that out to her she's just started ignoring us. After that I felt so uncomfortable I didn't even want to finish my beer or food and just leave.

This place is really as good as it gets. Has lots of great memorabilia and even a special section to 9/11 and Katrina. Have contacted the GM of the Hall of Fame, Mr. Trahan and he came to open the place just for me. Southern hospitality at it´s finest. If you´re a Saints fan you have to pay a visit!

I am just not there with all the other reviews. I have been here once for dinner and then did takeout. And it makes me sad because this is not how I remember other Sahm's and this location is a death trap, so it has to be good to survive. I had the salmon with broccoli and buffalo cauliflower for sides. The broccoli was like it was just nuked for 3 minutes. But ok, it's broccoli. The buffalo cauliflower was so salty it was inedible. Hubby liked his salad. Takeout on Friday, I had the pork chop with mushrooms. First, the pork chop was dry and 2) there were only about 5 mushrooms (exaggeration) and I thought there might be a sauce, but no. My baked potato suffered in the drive home. My side was chili which I just ate and it was passable. Nothing here is remarkable but it's so close I'd love to see it succeed.

For full review please see
 http://eternity-ply.blogspot.com/2011/03/nomiya-restaurant-review.html

My first impression about Nomiya is very positive. The decor is very nice, new, and the color scheme is quite relaxing. The place is quite small (sit about 30 people or so). I got there are 7pm on Tuesday night and the place was still packed. Luckily my friends were there already, and I didn't have to wait to be seat. The music in this place is very subtle and is not overwhelm the conversation we had. The waitress was quite attentive and the service was good, except that our food didn't come at the same time, and certain item took really long (see below, Saru Soba). One of my friend came late, and the waitress didn't rush us at all, she was very friendly also. Their menu can be a little bit confusing. Food are categorized in type (appetizers, tapas, sushi, and rice), however, ramen menu was on a flimsy piece of paper. I wish that they could have done better than this. The price was not bad considering big bowl of noodle only cost 8-10 dollars, some might find that their sushi can be expensive compare to other place around town.

.... see website for full review :D
The service was good. I ordered the Crispy Duck with fried rice and a spring roll. The sauce that came with the duck was in a red curry sauce which was really good.

Went in today my new favorite burger place this needs to come to jersey asap! Food is fresh they grind up the meat for the burgers. Good selection and the spiked shakes are amazing. Manager Katie went above and beyond for our table and it was much appreciated she is an amazing asset to that store and the company.

Absolutely wonderful! Yesterday I met my husband for lunch here. I have been twice already but this was his first time. It was fabulous as usual. I ordered the Angus Beef Melt as a wrap and my husband had the Turkey Pesto on whole grain. I had quinoa and hubby had coleslaw as our sides. Everything was fresh and delicious. There is a chili sauce on the table that really kicked up the flavor of my wrap. Not that it needed it, though. I asked the waitress, Melissa, where they got it and she told me that Gino makes it in house. Gino was feeling generous that day and came out to our table to tell us how he makes it. The service in here is always fast and friendly. The place gets really busy at lunch time so I always try to go around 11 or 11:30 to beat the rush. This is a very health conscious restaurant and they support local businesses.

Amazingly good! Better than most Pinellas Thai restaurants. One of my new favorites!

I came here because my usual local favorite, Thai Bay restaurant, doesn't open until 4:00 on Sundays, and Yelp had such good reviews. So glad I tried it!

I arrived around 1:30 and there were a couple of other tables occupied. Service was fast and friendly. I ordered off both the lunch and dinner menus. The Tom Kha Gai (chicken coconut soup) was delicious. I also got Chicken Nom Sod (savory chicken and peanut salad) and also quite well executed. I selected Basil Chicken for my main course, substituting broccoli for the eggplant. Another excellent dish!

I order everything Thai Hot and the heat was just right! They provided me with the spice tray - just in case - but it wasn't necessary. I did however notice that the red pepper was very nicely ground, the way I prefer, unlike most Thai restaurants that serve it coarse.

I can't wait to come back and try more of my favorite Thai dishes!

Joe's is my favorite Pizza Place! I am a huge fan of their pizza with pepperoni and green olives. Their sweet red sauce, buttery mozzarella, and salty toppings cant be beat. I also love their Spagetti Sundays--all you can eat Spagetti, along with salad, garlic toast or sticks, and a drink for a low price. Count me in!

Padmanadi is such a nice vegetarian restaurant!  I thoroughly enjoyed the food here.  The focus of the menu is very Asian with food from China, Thailand, Indonesia and perhaps others, giving it a sort of eclectic feel to Asian cuisine.  The restaurant seems to promote a healthier form of eating, which it exhibits well with bright colors and semi-urban feel to the interiors.

It's worth mentioning that most of the items on the menu are actually imitation meats.  In fact, I had a glance at the brunch menu online and they also have imitation eggs bennies!  Now that's pretty hard to pull off. (and I'd have to try that to convince myself) 

The imitation meat is reasonably good and I will go out on a limb and say that you might be able to satisfy a beef craving with this imitation beef!  The ginger beef is pretty good, though I found that the sauce was a little too strong, but then again, I'm not a ginger beef expert.  

The next time you come here - DO YOURSELF A FAVOR - try the Iced Lychee drink ..... it is soooo good.  I took a sip of the iced pineapple drink too, but given that Lychee is so exotic - I'd say, go with it!

Can't wait to try the hundreds of different items on their menu!  Unfortunately (or fortunately) the serving sizes are quite big and might end up having to go 100 times!

I cannot recommend AODetail enough! This was a long (very, very long) overdue detail for my 2014 Countryman. The car does a lot of sitting at times and living in the city does not help the cause. Nick was great about helping us to schedule my service and the hand off of keys was easy. The employee for the walk around was thorough and clearly laid out what could and could not be "fixed." My expectations were not high for some of the marks and scratches I thought were deeper and just a part of city living. When I picked up my car a few hours later the work was impeccable and I was happily shocked at how they were able to buff out and shine off so much of what I thought was damage. My car was honestly in cleaner condition than when I bought it from the dealership. I drove off feeling like I had a brand new Mini! Reasonably priced and well worth the drive across the bridge to NJ. I hope more Mini enthusiasts take their cars to AODetail and I know I'll definitely be back!
Had a Manipedi with Pang yesterday, and she was meticulous. I'm very picky about my nails and she was more than happy to accommodate my requests. Very happy!

The crew from American Arborists completed the work as promised a did a great job of it. They were on time every day, courteous, cleaned up well and kept me in the loop with any changes they thought might be helpful. I can't recommend them enough.

Wow - I don't know that I have EVER not left a tip at a restaurant/bar but this experience was horrendous.  Was there on the night before Thanksgiving and we sat at the bar where we were ignored by the barender who was NOT busy.  After eventually catching her attention, she honestly threw the menus down in front of us and went back to texting on her phone.  When we were ready to order we had to tear her away from her phone again and she was short, impatient and overall unpleasant.  We tried to make conversation, ask her about different items on the menu, etc but to no avail.  I had the burger which was okay - the fries were nothing great.  At one point I ordered a beer that tasted nothing like the way it was explained on the menu.  I very sweetly told her I didn't like it, and could I order something else.  She said it was my fault for not asking her about the beer and they don't "exchange" beers.  She also basically refused to clear our dirty dishes away once we finished eating and put off a vibe of hating us and hating her life.  Will never return nor recommend.

Stay away from the buffet!   They lured us in with a two for one price.  It was a joke.  We paid 33.00 for an overdone slice of prime rib and a small serving of Asian food.   I had such stomach pains an hour later I thought I was going down.  
We stayed in a nice room, loved the shower.  Visited the pool but one drink was freaking expensive!

Now my largest complaint is the lack of class regarding the clientele.  This casino used to be nice and attracted a good group of patrons.  Now I was in watch mode from the many apparent meth heads to young men just scoping out the place
In addition to being dirty, this place took 30 mins longer than it was supposed to. And they only gave me half the sauce for my wings. Do not go here.

I love this place! Their chicken udon noodles are my jam! I just found this place a couple weeks ago and have ordered from them 3 times since. Every time I have to get the udon! I haven't yet been inside the restaurant(Chinese is usually when I'm too tired to cook) so, I usually DoorDash it. I will have to update after I get a chance to go in.

I've made it a point to try chicken wings across four continents and these win hands down. 

Not for the grease-averse.

I started knitting when SlipKnot opened.  While I still consider myself a beginner, Claire (the owner) has inspired me to challenge myself on several ocassions and I've been thrilled with the results and my new found skills.   She, Barb, Cindy and Jane have not only supported and taught me, they've  actually talked me down off the ledge more than once.  I find the staff kind, supportive, friendly, professional and extremely talented.  I'm always warmly welcomed when I come into the shop. I recently learned that they stopped carrying a particular yarn line because it moved it's production to China...Thank you.  I prefer to buy American and/or support smaller, artisanal companies.  This is not your typical yarn store, nor is it's staff.  I love them.  Cindy

I tried Baekjong for the first time last night. My friend recommended it. I have to admit that I was pretty impressed with the service and the food. One thing that I am glad we did was make reservations. We booked for a later dinner, around 7:30 p.m. yet the place was pretty packed when we got there. I believe for people that did not make any reservations, the wait was about 30-45 minutes. When we got there, we waited for 10 mins for them to clear our table and got seated immediately. The menu was very good with many Korean grill options. This place is legit!!!!!! We ordered C1 which contained various slices of beef (brisket, short rib, rib finger, rib eye steak) with steamed egg, beef sausage and tofu soup with the P2 which was pork shoulder
 off the menu. When the meat came it, it looked amazing and you could tell the meat was very good quality. I loved that there was a call button in each of the tables and booths so that you could get service right at your table if you ever needed a refill or service. The staff was very quick and prompt and courteous. Overall the experience was positive and the food was delicious with great variety of daikon on the side to pair. One thing is if you're planning to go around dinner time, make a reservation. You will not regret it!

Todd, Aaron and the crew are awesome. They want to make sure you are happy and do great work at a reasonable price. I bought wheels, tires and have had service at this location and always experience perfection. Everyone gets along great and you can tell this crew and management like each other and really care about the customers. If you have to deal with the drama of a flat, they make it easy. And Todd and Freddie are really funny....I may be the only one that thinks so.....
Absolutely the best food. Gluten free bread option for sandwiches is amazing. Such a great set of options
Really wonderful breakfast food. But quite expensive compared to Michael's or Bonnet Lane. It is extra good, but just be prepared for the prices

Don't come here for the customer service, come here for the incredible selection of fabrics.  You won't see some of these fabrics anywhere else.  When I want something fun and unusual, this is the place to go.  The owners are quirky, sometimes friendly, often bizarre.  Don't ask a lot of them, you are lucky if they cut your fabric for you.  It's always an adventure!
Fun little shirt shop with a lot of local designs. Quality shirts with quality designs.

I ordered a Moana cake 12 days in advance for my daughter's birthday party and paid in full to make sure there was no hold up in picking it up the morning of the party. We excitedly talked about the moana cake for a week. Less than 24 hours before the party they call to say, sorry, they can't make a Moana cake after all. When we arrived they gave us  a generic cake with flowers on it that looks like it came from a grocery store.

It's not hard to find a good cake--do yourself a favor and order from a bakery that knows how to run a business and won't disappoint you and your family.

Love, love, love this place. It's a pretty authentic Vietnamese place. The restaurant itself is small. It can get pretty busy during lunch hours but the staff works fast and you can get seated pretty quickly. 

I'm vegetarian so I usually get their egg banh mi--it's not on the menu so you have to ask for it. I also get their iced coffee with condensed milk (cafe sua da)--super good! Vietnamese iced coffee tends to be stronger than your typical DD or Starbucks iced coffee so don't be surprised if it gets it wired.

Never had their pho but my friends and family have said that it is the bomb.com. The broth is flavorful and definitely has Pho Quyen beat. Their combo rice plate is also a good pick. It comes with grilled pork and grilled shrimp. 

I would definitely recommend this place to everyone. It's not a elegant type of restaurant---no fine dining here. It's more of a quick lunch spot to grab a delicious and satisfying meal with the homey atmosphere. 

The staff is also nice and courteous. It's run by a Vietnamese family that speaks fluent English. They also have pre-packaged rolls (goi cuon) and desserts (che, banh Bo) that you can buy if you're on the go! 

And what tops it off is that the prices are cheap for such a great meal! Another gem of Tampa!
Had THE BEST TIME here!  That outside garden area is just made for communal enjoyment.

Pops is awesome! Their bagels are huge. We've had basically ever type of bagel they make and they're all just so good. I tried the "Philly" breakfast sandwich last week on rye toast and it was ... incredible! My husband had one of the platters which he loves. The potatoes that come with it? Delicious. 

We can't wait to keep trying new things at Pops!

workers are a lot nicer than in those in springfield. this location is also much cleaner and does not smell like cigarettes like the springfield location. workers were pretty nice and bought a good amount of my stuff. would recommend.
Love the quaint ambience and their sizzling rice is awesome.  Love the Chicken Basil Green Curry dish as well.
I'm a huge fan of thai food and this is amazing! I had the whole fish and it was tender and delicious. I'll be back soon

One of the best plain pizzas I've had in a while. The buffalo wings were cooked well and a little spicy. Cheesesteak was also delicious. This place is under the radar for sure and deserves higher ratings. Would recommend to friends.
Love the boudin balls. The fried chicken livers with pepper jelly. The gumbo and deviled egg was good. So was the rabbit and dumplings.

THIS PLACE IS AMAZING! 

I grew up eating here. family ran and the food is great. as for vegetarian options ... your decision to be the vego... complaining isn't an option. The pork here is great. everything is great

The Pivirotto Wellness Center is my favorite break in the middle of a work day. I go there to row, ride a stationary bike, fight osteoporosis by lifting weights, and in general take a breather from my day by breaking a sweat. Membership is very reasonable ($18/month) but is limited to UA Health Network/north UA campus employees and family members. However, the cool thing is that family members of inpatients staying at the hospital are eligible to purchase a temporary membership. In addition to the exercise equipment, the staff offers personal training and body composition analysis, among other services. I always appreciate it when a staff member gives me a tip on my form or on a new exercise they think I may enjoy or benefit from.
Had lunch here a while back... It was just so-so... Nothing really stood out for me, but I would give it a try again sometime.

Love The Nugget.  Been coming here since I was a baby.  Its one of the best casinos and hotels in the Reno/Sparks area.  They always have their bloody mary bar on saturday and sunday! Its soooo good.  I love their tournaments also.  If you ever get a chance, please sign up for a red white and blue tournament.  You will catch me and my family there.  Its also great to see Mr. Ascuaga and his wife at rosies cafe on sunday mornings.  They are so cute and down to earth.  Great place.

You must do this! These guys have a fun, well designed game and absolutely top notch customer service! I played VIPERS Nest. It is wonderfully logical and creative! The puzzles are really fun! I especially love the unique element of making a big choice, I've never seen that before and I've played 175 escape rooms nationwide. Highly recommend!
Excellent food and the live music by Joe Rodgers is a blast!

I highly recommend trying if you're in the area looking for a great date night, birthday surprise, or just something different to do. Even if your not the best painter or artist, the instructor goes step by step on how to create the picture. They serve one complementary glass of wine, beer, or a non-alcoholic beverage during your session then leave the option to buy more. They also have great music playing to make it fun and exciting.

It was love at first sight. Girl met Pastaria. Girl lived happily ever after. From the unlimited bread (heart be still) to the iced tea served in a mason jar, this place was a dream. I ate the Bucatini Amatriciani (pronounce it Bugatti Americana if you want your server to give you the "at least you tried" look). My friend and I also split the risotto balls which I consider to be one of my best life choices thus far. I will definitely be going back as everything tastes authentic and yummy. It's a very cute restaurant with a familial, comfortable layout. I definitely can't wait for warmer weather to try out that pretty patio. 

Also props to Natalie for being an amazing server. We loved each time when she came around and actually engaged in conversation with us.

Even though I only pay $19.99 a month to work out I would still prefer my experience to be good.  I have been at this gym for 2 yrs now and it has only gotten worse.  If it's not staff flirting with other guest, or favoring their "regulars" you get a mumble of a hi or just a stare.  I don't know how much they get paid but it's obviously not enough for them to check the tanning beds when someone is done; in my last few times of tanning I have had to clean up after the person before me and it's gross.  The manager has talked to me about it and said how sorry she was and put up a sign but that hasn't helped at all.

DO NOT STAY HERE!! Motel 6 right next door is better than this dump. Bobby the manager has no idea how to treat their guests. Their front desk gave fake names so when I complained to Orbitz about my stay they said no one by those names worked there. No A/C, they charged my card a pet fee after the owner told me herself that they would wave the fee waved since our dog was ESA certified.
