I left the hotel with a $15 parking fee on my card...Days later BOBBY charged my card without contacting me for a pet fee anyways. After it was already agreed upon when I checked out that that there would be no fee. Have to leave windows open because there is no Air conditioner.  A smoke alarm was going off with a low battery...after listening to it for two hours I called the front desk to ask them to check which room it was in, could not sleep with it beeping every 30 seconds. They told me it was the utility closet and there was nothing they could do. So I walked around the entire hotel at Midnight by myself searching for it on my own. Low and behold there was a smoke detector on the ground outside my door sitting in a box beeping. I brought it to the front desk myself. When we cleaned up and checked out the next morning I found dirty men's underwear in the corner of the room. Terrible customer service, STAY SOMEWHERE ELSE!!

My experience at Pacifica has been great... I graduated with a master's from a traditional setting and am already licensed and work in private practice. I chose to attend Pacifica's clinical Ph.D program because I wanted something different from my Master's program. I honestly am not sure why the previous yelpers were so harsh on the school. My experience has been nothing but positive. The instructors are well published and the best in the field. One thing I did notice though is that this school does sometimes attract people who want something a little different, with that being said, some students (not the school) in my opinion need to work on some issues before attending. I feel like some students have a lot of unresolved issues, and if you are looking at studying depth psychology without having had your own stuff resolved, you will show emotionally unstable and externalize your issues. I recommend this school, however, make sure you deal with your own stuff before applying...

Yao is the BEST. My gel lasts three weeks, never chips or peels. She takes her time during pedicures and knows when her clients want to chat, and when then just want to sit back and relax. The salon is clean and I'm sure everyone there is great, but I will ONLY go to Yao for all my nail care needs while living in Spring Hill.

I was in town for a conference near the convention center and ended up walking down to The Heights Public Market on two different nights with some of my fellow conventioneers.  It was about a 30-40 minute walk down the Riverwalk, which was beautiful.  We went twice because I was super impressed by the design and concept of the Market.  It was like an upscale, adult food court.  

Both nights, we tried local draft beers from Bar AW.  It was near the entrance we walked in and had a great brick warehouse atmosphere.   I tried the Graffiti Orange one night and the Guayabera the next.  While both were good, the orange was tasted stronger to me, and I preferred the lightness of the Guayabera from Cigar City brewing.  I love trying new things, though!  

It was totally worth the beautiful stroll down the Riverwalk to keep returning to this gem.
Everything is good here. Tortilla soup from here is my favorite soup of all time! Bright some people from out of town and all they could say was wow!

This In-N-Out is consistent with all the other locations.  The buns had a nice toasted crisp.  The lettuce was not wilted. We had some protein style burgers and the lettuce was generous. All the veggies were fresh.  Patties had a little juice to it. There is nothing bad to say about the burgers. The food was exactly as expected from In-N-Out Food was presented well and staff was courteous.  On top of that, if you order inside, the service is waaaay faster than the drive-through.  They also did a pretty good job in social distancing by taping up seats with cones.  Overall the whole restaurant was super clean.

Brunch. 
Yes, you have your obvious dishes, but just adding something special elevates those dishes to greatness. 

Like - The Veggie Eggs Benedict made more tasty by adding caramelized red onions. The sweet potato fries sparkle with a little grilled sweet chills on top. 

Nice interior and friendly staff to boot. 

Go there - you'll like it.

First time at Jeannine's! Did not disappoint! The dinning experience at this point is all outdoors. They have plenty of seating available and it also appears they share outdoor space with the restaurant next door. I had the chilaquiles with green sauce (original comes with red sauce. I asked if they could sub for green sauce and they gladly accommodated my request). The sauce was spicy enough for me but a little too spicy for my wife, but enjoyed them too. Came with fresh cut avocado, onions and cotija cheese. They were very good. One of my top three I've ever had. My wife had the Banana Kahlua French Toast. Wow! DEADLY! The toast was thick and fluffy. The bananas were cooked perfectly and the sauce...wow! We were fortunate enough to have front row seats to these amazing carolers! Overall, great restaurant. Wait staff was friendly and service was fast. Will definitely be back!

I love the vibe of Meril's! It's casual but also fancy so good for just a dinner out as well as if you want to go out to celebrate a special occasion. I had a VARIETY of things off the menu and nothing disappointed. 

I did recently learn that their happy hour is DAILY. So you'll be seeing me stuffing my face with fries and flatbreads there more often

I typically come here when I'm going to the movie theater next door.  They apparently don't have wings anymore which is a travesty, but they still have fantastic flatbread pizza, cheeseboard is lovely and the extremely fresh salads. I've also had many pinot noirs and cabernets that were crisp & delicious. Service is great - the bartenders are always very engaging and attentive. Food service can be slow, but I haven't been late to a movie yet.

Went to this restaurant after seeing a description and hearing rave reviews. What a huge disappointment! First bad thing, we were walk ins, but the restaurant was maybe 1/4 full and were told we had to wait 15mins. They were out of crab, lobster and cod. Well thats half the menu and my friend and I wanted the seafood cobb. ended up getting a chicken salad in a seafood restaurant ! Oh and the corn, outrageously priced. At least give me fresh corn, not frozen for $4! sadly we will never get to try the seafood cobb NEVER going back, and yes the portions are small.
Oh and when we left, the restaurant didn't even get any fuller than when we entered...okay maybe 2 more people...

My brother recommended this place as a must do for our trip and I am so happy he did! Everything was so amazing! The chicken? OH EM GEE!!! The crunch was light and the chicken was juicy. Everything was so well seasoned. Ashley, our waitress, was very attentive to our group of seven. Next trip down to the Big Easy will DEFINITELY find me here!
Excellent food with lots of options, nice decor, great price for all you get, and good service!

We stayed here for our trip to NOLA. The decor is very modern and beautiful. The room was obviously a little small, but that is fine with me. The staff were pretty helpful and kind. The bedding wasn't very clean. I found a person's snot on my bedding. My sister found a lot of hairs in her bedding. We asked them to change the bedding, but she still had hairs in her bed. Along with booking the hotel room, they gave us complimentary 3 drink from the bar and 2 tickets for a Jazz Brunch at The Court of Two Sisters. The parking across the street was pretty expensive. We ended parking in another parking lot a few blocks away for a much cheaper price. The pool is amazing at night! I loved coming down there to soak my feet after a long day of walking and bar hopping. It is also so close to bourbon street! You can't hear any of the noise, but you can easily walk down there at night. You can easily walk down to the French Market as well during the day. I would really recommend this hotel.

FYI: We paid about $200 a night.

I have been going to Ashlee Sexton for all my hair needs for four years. I live in Georgia and travel to TN just to have my hair styled! Her colors and cuts are unmatched in the industry and anyone who makes the wise decision to go to her for cut/color/or make up will not leave disappointed!
Excellent food. But you can get same quality at much lower prices at other brunch locations.

I attended a wedding reception here, and was very pleased.  The food was delicious, and the ballroom was beautiful!  I was less than impressed with the room we were in for the cocktail hour, however...it was too small for a party our size, and there was not enough tables to accommodate everyone and their plates of appetizers.  I had to carry around my plate and glass because there was nowhere to set it down. In any event, there was a vast assortment of food and beverage to choose, and the banquet room was tastefully decorated, not at all dated or garish as with other generic party halls I've been to. The staff was also very attentive.
Delivered to us hot! Super delicious. And not greasy! I love the Kona with the cheddar cheese, and the white pizza with oil and different cheeses. Drooling.

Great service.  Came in looking for someone to repair a - 15lb? - frame of mine.  The guy at the counter mentioned effectively what he would do to "repair" it - really just moving the screws along the frame (perhaps I should've come up with this myself, but hey, I'm not all that handy).  He spent a good portion of the conversation explaining how to do things, and why it would be cheaper to do X over Y.  Just wish I bought more in the end than a simple premade frame, since he saved me some money, hassle, and explained everything well enough that I could take care of the rest myself.
Great beer and a nice atmosphere if you're just wanting to hang out for a bit and drink but not feel like you're at a bar. I'll definitely visit again

The boys and I tried Rae Rae's cafe this morning for the first time. Reading the good reviews about the food and bad about the sound problem I wanted to give it a try. We got there about 8:45 on a Saturday and found a few parking spots. We were greeted right away and told there was a short wait. After a few we were seated in the larger VIP table right by the kitchen and waiting area. We had great service from Garrett. After talking to her she is the owner's sister. The lady can hussle and has a nice pair of legs to show for it. We all enjoyed the fresh ingredients used. Nice bright fruit on all of our plated as garnish. I really liked the bread. You can tell was made locally from scratch. We didnt have a problem hearing each other but it was a bit loud. The place is small and when full you would expect that. With good food and prices i'm sure it's full just about every day.
There's not a lot of good pizza places in this area but Brothers is definitely the best. I love their pan pizza, and their they aren't cheap with the toppings like a lot of places. Fast delivery too!

Great food here.
im in town for the indy 500 and i asked one of the guys who was working the front desk of the hotel i was staying in and he said this place was Good. so after a couple of trys of trying to find a place to park i got in and got a table within seconds and then the server was awesome. i had the very interesting shewman special burger and it was GOOOD! when i saw the burger and i read Peanut butter was on it i had to stop and look at it again and i just had to ask our server how was it and he said it was good but odd. the burger was very good with the odd combination of peanut butter and jalapenos but it was good.

after the very good burger i had the peanut butter pie which was amazing!

overall this place was great and i wished i had this place back in California

Visited the restaurant prior to a concert across the street.  The experience was very disappointing starting with a menu advertising a steak special that no longer was offered to a barely warm prime steak that was clearly not prime.  For the price point there are much better values.
Love this place for a great basic diner breakfast. Servers are always great. We have been here several times. Free coffee before 11am!

Being located in an old jail building, it has an interesting ambiance. There's 4 cells in one area and they've installed long tables in there so it would make for an interesting dining room. They also have other tables in the building. They did a nice job on the remodel. We live 90 miles north of St Louis so whenever my wife and I come down, we try to checkout new places to eat at. We were going to be in Soulard, so this seemed like an interesting place to eat at. We've done Pappys and Bogarts, so again trying something new was a good idea. 

I really wanted the burnt ends, but when we got there on Wednesday, they didn't have any ready to serve. So my wife ordered the corned beef sandwich and I had the brisket platter. First off, there was plenty to eat. At least a half pound of corned beef and 4 thick slices of brisket. The corned beef was tender and juicy. Not a lot of smoke flavor, but it was very good. When I smoke a brisket, I like a lot of course pepper as a rub, but that's me. Both us would consider ordering it again. 

The brisket was very tender and pulled apart easily with my fingers.  My two complaints with the brisket was that it luke warm, not quite cold, but not warm either. Secondly, I found it to be dry. It had a great smokey flavor, excellent smoke ring and good spice in the rub. If it was warm and not as dry, I would have given it a higher score. We had the baked beans as a side, which were different, but very good; Cole slaw was good but needed a little more flavor; and also had the potato salad which was warm but I found it to have a lit more vinegar than I like. 

The various sauces were good and many varieties which seemed to be home made. 

We would certainly go back and hopefully get to try the burnt ends.
Food is great and the new management has that place humming! Customer service is a priority for the staff and their commitment to community and our public servants is outstanding!

I had the hot tenders...heat index not quite as hot as Princes, Bolton's, or Pepperfire, so it was just right for me, since I get medium elsewhere. They were nicely cooked, crisp, and seasoned. The place is clean, small and not fancy but it's perfect for what it is. I like that the drink station and napkins are handy to dining area. The girl behind the counter had next to no menu knowledge, that shouldn't be. The only reason I knew to get the hot, a guy waiting for his order knew the place and I was able to ask him about how the heat runs there. No one taking orders shouldn't know that. Especially since some are more sensitive to heat than others. It worked out fine for me in this case.
I was nervous about getting my tooth pulled and everyone made me feel comfortable and relaxed. And it was done in a timely manner I didn't even have to wait

First time at L&L in Gilbertsville, had a manicure with Melissa, she was wonderful with a lovely/friendly personality.  The location is perfect and salon is nice & clean.    Look forward to returning!

Spoke to Maria this morning about some questions I had about a pair of glasses. Right away she was rude and condescending. She has no business being in a customer service sort of job. Not going to that location!
Amazing food!!! The spice is true, medium is medium and HOT is HOT. Best Thai food in Indy!!

While having our dinner I was watching the waitresses roll up silverware. It wouldn't have got my attention normally but 1. They were not wearing gloves. 2. They were picking it up by the business end. And 3. The grossest part one of them dropped a fork in her crotch area picked it up and wrapped it up anyway!!! Don't know about all of you but this seems disgusting to me. What else could they be touching with bare hands?

I met some friends for Happy Hour one day after work and the music was BLASTING so loud into the courtyard that we couldn't hear each other.  We went for nice ambiance  and conversation and I was disappointed in how "casual" the staff was even though they were open for business.  Just because the place isn't packed doesn't mean the staff should make the few customers there feel as though they aren't worth professional behavior. Tighten it up, New  Feelings.  The ambiance was Killed by this miserable display of childish, unprofessional behavior.

Traveling on business always brings me into and around tampa.
I make every effort to dine here one night on my stay over.
The pizza is always the best. The wine selection is good.
The craft beer a bit sparse but a few good ones.
Got to try the pie!!
I was staying with folks who prefer to have a healthy liquor cabinet sooo we stopped by here. Lots of choices to be had. They got rye and wines. It was interesting to browse the selections.

Food was great, service- not so much. I understand that English is the second language of most of the people that work there, and definitely of our female server, but she could have at least smiled! She also acted like it was such a hardship for her to bring out the dessert tray?! Everything was great so I'll probably be back but I'll ask for a different server!

I really like Sher-e Punjab.  Their dinner is better than the buffet, but the buffet is a great deal!  My only complaint is that the buffet is a little americanized - creamy and mild dishes being the predominant offerings - but what can you expect?  Tucson is predominantly white people without a tongue for spice.  

I've sat in and ordered take-out several times and have always had a pleasant experience.  All of the naan is good, I've had several dishes that I liked and when I've asked for it spicy (8/10), it comes with a good kick.  Once I was adventurous enough to order it as spicy as it came and it cleared my sinuses out.  Score! 

All in all, I would recommend this place and I definitely consider myself a regular.  I tried to branch out and try another place in town and was so disappointed... Sher-e Punjab sets the standard in Tucson!  Just do yourself a favor and get dinner there before trying the buffet.

BEST PIZZA IN THE CITY! Now people let's remember this is a pizza joint NOT 5 star dining!! I've eaten here for years! The pizza just gets better! I definitely recommend trying the "bourbon" anything is good but this is my favorite! And I'm a local! And I'm telling you this place is the best in New Orleans!!! I hate seeing people write weird reviews that expect 5 star dining with a 1 star budget! If your looking for a great pizza spot! This is the very best!!!

I liked this tour a lot. It lasts 2 hours including waiting to board so finding parking can be rough since most meters are 2 hours maximum. Fortunately, we did not get a ticket since we got back to the car 15 minutes late and yes we ran as soon as the tour ended to the car. The duck shows you almost all of the must see places in Philadelphia and the tour guide was fun and energetic. He has a hard time going off script because we ran into traffic and he complained he did not know what to say while we sat there. I am not sure if he is allowed to go off script or not so I did not hold it against him. I did wish he would warn us when places were coming up so we could take pictures. There were many times he told us at the same time we passed it that it was there. We circled around the same streets a few times which would shave off tour time if they mapped it out better which would be better for parking. The low point was the Will Smith moment. He pointed out where Will Smith's father had a condo then played a Will Smith song. He spent 5 minutes talking about how once upon a time he saw Will Smith and played his song for him. Looking at previous reviews, he must have seen him years ago since older reviews mentioned it to. I do not care about Will Smith or any member of his family! I think the Coast Guard captain who drove the duck in the water got sick of working because towards the end of the water portion he tried to floor it. We could hear the engine revving really loud as he tried to hurry the duck back to land, even the tour guide brought it up. Overall, it was a lot of fun and saw almost all of Philadelphia's must see places. Philadelphia's parking situation was brutal so the duck tour was a great way to deal with that and see all you want to see.
Love this place.. Unpretentious, good Italian food.  Great staff too!

The best kept secret in Smyrna!  Hands down the best Greek place in Tennessee!  Everything freshly made and proportion sizes are so forthcoming you wonder if you can finish it all. All good is made to order. If you haven't been to this place you are missing out. Friendly staff and owner, 3 big screen televisions if you wanna watch the news or sports. There's lamb, beef, chicken, salmon, cod...and so much more...tzatziki sauce, fresh cucumber and tomatoes...banana peppers...,ok I have to stop but I don't know where to end. Just visit Deez Greek Cafe. You won't be disappointed.

RISE is one of the great coffee spots in STL. The lattes are artfully made and presented, the espresso is rich and flavorful, and the vibe is chill and neighborhoody. If you're up for some brunch-ish food, try the quiches -- mushroom and spinach, bacon and sausage, all nice, fluffy options.
The food here is inconsistent. At times I have been very happy with their flavor and spice level whereas at times the food has been mediocre- no flavor at all and chicken is very chewy

Thank you Frist!!!!!!!! 

We just went to see the Fairy tales, Monsters and the Genetic Imagination exhibit this past Monday. I have to say I was thoroughly impressed. The pieces evoked a lot of feelings because fairy tales are very dear to my heart. I felt disturbed, intrigued and inspired at the same time. I also enjoyed the Phillips collection, O'Kieffe is one of my favorite artists and it was awesome to see some of her pieces. 

We are very lucky to have this little museum in Nashville and I hope to continue supporting them! 

PS I finally got a good look at your beautiful wood floors, they're lovely!!
Inexpensive tacos that are fresh and yummy. It's nothing fancy and service is friendly but far from attentive.
Best service I've had. Great server who knew the menu back and front. Good atmosphere for a family and very well priced!
Happening Place!  This was one of the stops on our Brew Tour!

I'm going to be honest and tell you right now that I didn't read 90% of the things in this building.  There's TONS to read and look at, which is great as far as I'm concerned, but we were on a walking tour and we needed to get in and out.

The Liberty Bell is at the far, south end of the building, so take your time reading the signs.  You'll have to wait a bit to get a really good picture (there's lots of people hanging around doing the same thing) but it's worth it.  There's a lot of people willing to take your picture, too, so don't be afraid to ask.

It's a very modern facility and it is kept in tip top condition.  The Parks Department really does a nice job with this facility and it's worth a visit, especially since it's FREE!!!!

good christ almighty - the burger here is unreal. 

I didn't go crazy and get the foie gras burger - just the straight up village burger with bacon - and it was unreal. First off the meat is seasoned to perfection - then the way they shape it and cook it is perfect. I mean it is the exact shape of a hockey puck - perfectly flat and smooth it is crazy. 

Then we have the fries cooked in duck fat. With a side of cheddar dipping sauce. Again - these were ridiculous. My gf had the pulled pork sandwhich and that was amazing as well. 

Prices for the burgers and sandwhich were reasonable - drinks are pretty pricy. All signature drinks are around $12-$14.

For 7.50$ I am able to get more than full here. I ordered the general chicken with crab rangoons and fried rice every time. The chicken does not have the second layer of breading that gets all gooey that you often see when ordering general chicken, and the flavor is awesome. During my two week stay I have eaten here many times. The building may be a little misleading but the food is excellent.

Walked in today with a recent power problem on my iphone 5C.  20 minutes later I was out the door without breaking the bank.  All seems to be working fine.  While there I peppered the 2 techs with a bunch of random questions about my phone.  They answered without hesitation and didn't seem to be bothered by me.  Would highly recommend them to others.

This place use to be the best for gyros. But upon my recent visits it has gone down hill. I get the same thing all the time and made me smile and here is what I'm now sadly eating. the spinach rotini that was robust and Tangy is now dull bland and unsavory. The gyro deluxe that was the epitome of the deli is now sad and disheartening. The lettuce use to be crisp and crunchy is now soft and cabbage like. The feta was pungently great that made your mouth scream "YES" is now feeling like they bought it from the cheap store and left in the car for a few days. The pita once was airy and filling now feels like smashed bread that isn't even cooked. And now for the meat it was the best in St. Louis every bite was was delightful and you could taste the lamb with the longest after taste even an hour later you felt like you had just eaten now tastes 3rd rate and wanted to quickly drink water to make dull the after taste. I'm am saddened to write this and hope it's just a food slump they are in and soon explode the flavors they once had.  I will eat there again in the later months and update this if it's better. The next time I'll make sure to eat there so I can ask them what happened.
This is a great place for tea and Raman. Good prices and good atmosphere! The pork ramen is one of my favorites.

Great experience from start to finish! We went in a couple of weeks ago looking for a new gas range. Jeff greeted us and was able to help right away asking questions to show us what would be best for us. He never tried to oversell us or sell us anything we didn't need or want. He was super knowledgeable on all the brands and ultimately helped us choose the best fit for our needs. It was delivered this morning, on time. The delivery guys were very friendly, helpful and made sure everything was exactly the way we wanted it before leaving. Wonderful experience, can't say enough good! Thanks Famous Tate!

The Ruby Slipper wasn't on our list and was recommended by another local brunch favorite as we had an extra large party (12).  

They were busy also late Saturday morning and about a 1/2 hour wait estimate ended up being closer to an hour.  

The Ruby Slipper Mimosas definitely helped as we waited.  Excellent & they top it off with a splash of Pomegranate juice that gives it the Ruby color.  

We ordered a good variety of dishes and all were happy with their meals.  Highly recommend the Bananas Foster French toast-a local favorite and amazing.  The Chicken St. Charles (Fried Chicken eggs Benedict) was perfect and the 3 little pigs Omelet & Migas didn't disappoint.  

Again, we didn't plan on Ruby's but ended up here and, besides the longer than communicated wait, all in the party of 12 were very happy!
Food is awesome here! The food is an excellent value. And the workers here are the nicest ones among all the mall court restaurants. Would highly recommend.

Really nice place. Quite a selection. My friend has an Isis fixation & loves cats, so this place is Nirvana for her! Whenever we go in, we just ask for what she's looking for & the gentleman there works very hard to find it, but never with a hard sell. It's a bit out of the way, bit worth the walk to explore.

Delicious food. Great restaurant in New Tampa. Recommend the Citrus chicken or Tuna.  Robert does a great job running the restaurant. Been here many times and always a pleasure. Service is always excellent!!!

I was pleasantly surprised with this! Timmy did my full set of acrylics with a regular polish. He was very quick (took about 70 minutes, which is good in my opinion) and double checked everything--shape, color, length, etc. He was very good about making my nails look exactly how I wanted. He didn't lay the acrylic on too thick; chunky nails is my biggest pet peeve.

I have a scar above one of my fingers from an incident as a child, and every other salon I've been to have made comments on it (asking if it was a wart!) He did not mention it at all and acted as though it wasn't there. Very professional.

The pricing was incredibly fair; $25 for a full set plus polish.

Definitely ask for Timmy when you get a full set plus polish.
Great live bands and cold beer. If you are looking for a historic dive bar you found it.

I was so upset when I cracked my screen yesterday, but it is like new now thanks to myPhoneMD! In less than the amount of time it took me to have lunch at Lilly's Cafe, they fixed my screen for me, and while it was a little bit pricey, it was still cheaper than the cost of a new phone through my carrier EVEN THOUGH I HAVE INSURANCE. I was happy to give my money to myPhoneMD instead of AT&T.

My first trip to Little Spoon Cafe was good. At noon, there were plenty of tables available and no wait to order. Water glasses were brought over to the table and I went to the counter to place an order for myself and my dining companion, Simone A. 

I love that the tables have everything you could possibly need - flatware, napkins, salt, pepper, ketchup and Cholula. All my usual staples! 

Simone ordered the grilled cheese of the day and some tomato soup, which she said were both very good. I opted for the BLT, no mayo, add avocado. We also shared a side of the baked parmesan fries. Both sandwiches came with awesome dill pickles. 

I was really surprised that the bread for the BLT was not toasted - I would have asked if I had known. Otherwise, the sandwich was nice, but I did have a little regret for not going with my other choice, the Cuban. The fries definitely had that distinct baked texture - not a lot of crispiness to them - but they came with a lot less residual guilt than if they had been fried! No one came around to refill waters so we had to take our cups back to the then-busy counter to ask. Maybe some carafes on the ledge by the window for people to self-serve would be helpful!

Definitely looking forward to round two and trying that Cuban.

Summary of our experience:
1. Arrived with a group of 10, asked how long the wait to have tables together would be. Was told it would be 30min.
2. After 30 min, asked how much longer and was told it would be easier if we sat separately. We confirmed that would be okay for the group and waited an additional 30min.
3. After waiting for an hour, was curtly told that it would not be possible to seat us for at least another hour because of a prior reservation.

We left without eating. Looks good but difficult to get a clear idea of wait time. Main complaint is staff didn't seem to communicate well with each other.

A St. Louis gem, for sure! I was lucky enough to stop by this super hip coffee shop to meet any old friend. Needless to say, we were both quite pleased! We try a new coffee shop each time we meet for coffee, so we were excited to try The Mud House (formerly Mississippi Mud House.)

From the pastry selection to the coffee, everything looked wonderful! The blueberry muffins are certainly big enough to share with a refined sweetness that makes them a special treat! The pairing of the muffin to my iced mocha was absolute heaven! With the coffee plus kakao mixture on ice, my taste buds were doing a little dance! 

Seriously, people, if you haven't tried The Mud House plan to make it date with an old friend or significant other!

I was definitely disappointed in this place. It's really close to our AirBnB, so we stopped in to get drinks. 

It was 6:45, and the sign on the door said they closed at 8. When we went up to the register, the employee said, "Our kitchen is closed." I replied that we were just stopping in for some drinks to which she stated, "well, we're closing up in 15 minutes, so I guess I can get you drinks." We each got a glass of wine (we knew we could drink these in 15 minutes), which were really expensive for the brands of wine they were. 

The café itself is very welcoming on appearance, but the atmosphere was very odd and not something I'd probably come back to.

Our favorite place in town to swing by to grab a sweet treat! There are soooo many choices though so it's hard to choose what to try! Staff is super friendly and their ice cream cakes are delicious too

The word EPIC is often overused and used out of context. I can honestly say that Scheels is EPIC. 

This is by the far the largest and coolest sporting good/outdoors store ever. Their selection is ridiculous. Everything from your regular everyday sports like basketball and hockey, to fishing, camping, archery, water-sports, cycling, and much more.

Each department has a big selection with a lot of cool demo displays. Although the store is big, it is very easy to get around and find the area you are looking for.

They also have a their own food court and ferris wheel for the kids.

Any sports/outdoors junkie can hang out here for hours, if not days. 

Giving this a 4 star as most items are expensive and almost nothing is on sale. In addition, the snowboarding section was very small.

If I could give zero stars I most definitely would. I ordered 5 frozen dessert cups an Oreo milkshake and only received 4 of them. We triple checked to make sure that we didn't miscount any of the orders. After we compared our receipt with what we ordered, we confirmed that we were indeed missing part of our order. I contacted Just Cravings to tell them that we did not receive a part of our order and the worker on the phone was giving me an attitude and continued to rant about how she was the only person working at the time. She said that she wouldn't be able to make and send the missing order. She said they will pay me back for the ice cream we didn't receive. However, I still haven't received the money. Adding on, the Oreo milkshake I ordered wasn't really good. It was half Oreo crumbs and half milk like split down the middle of the cup, as if she filled the rest of a vanilla milkshake with leftovers. Not really a milkshake I'd expect to get. I most definitely won't be ordering from Just Cravings next time I am in Philly. Also there was hair that obviously didn't belong to me because the cap was unopened.
Still one of my favorites.   Great gumbo and tonight the oyster Rockefeller bisque with spaghetti and meat balls for my little guy and fettuccine Alfredo for me.  So good

My experience with this place is amazing .. yesterday I had party of 14 people and I couldn't be happier to celebrate my wife's birthday here ... the table was already set food was brought on time..Praveen our host/restaurant manager was on point on taking orders and bringing food on time .. lastly the food was on point freshest cooked, flavorful and right quantity. I will prefer everyone to try this Turkish cuisine.
Busy busy location.  Love dunkin donuts.  Fast service.  Open 24 hours

Wonderful date night had.   The drinks were well prepared and the horseradish steaks were amazing with just the right amount of food presented.    Who needs the fancy places when you have this place? The atmosphere was second to none.   We requested to see the water and the hostesses were happy to do so.   We were fireside with water views.   The whole night was memorable.  
Side note: there were very loud pretentious paterons but I know Wolfies can't control that.  People are just arrogant and unwilling to be curious these days.

Their pizza SUCKS. it was burnt, tasted like cardboard with some tomato sauce and a sprinkle of cheese on it. If that's how you like your pizza, well then, be my guest. I tried giving this place a second chance but I won't be ordering from here again. I wish I could get my $17 back.

When you're looking for consistently good southern cooking, look no further than this Cracker Barrel. I'll start off with the store part - the store side has a pretty large variety of items to buy, from Tate's Cookies and Moon Pies, to classic TV shows on DVD and a talking parrot toy. The staff members at the store are always nice and helpful. Moving on to the restaurant, I'll start off with the biscuits, and boy are they good! They have a slight sweet taste and once you have one bite, you want to keep eating and eating them till you're out of biscuits. They don't even need any kind of jam, jelly, or butter spread, they're good to eat even plain. The pancakes here are also very good. They're medium sized and then with the 100% pure syrup, you can't go wrong with those, but of course you can't go wrong with their crispy bacon and scrambled eggs either. I've mostly tried the breakfast items on the menu, but for the few times I've had a lunch or dinner menu item, they're good too.  The service here is usually pretty good, except sometimes the food can take a while and the most recent time I ate here for dinner (as of writing this review) we weren't given the breakfast menu, which I thought was odd that we had to ask for it even though breakfast is served all day. Overall, my experience has been consistently good here and I will of course return.

Hired Express for a cleanout.  Steven was totally professional and did what he said he would do and for a solid price.  At first I was hesitant cause he's not a corporation but I'm glad I hired him because I got the same quality at affordable prices.  I would defintely recommend him to friends and family.

I was looking for a place to get my hair cut and dyed. The reviews here don't lie. Very friendly people (although not very talkative). Got a great dye and she listened to what I wanted with my hair. Libby is sweet. I will be back!

I love the layout of this airport. The way that the terminals are organized just feels very natural, like I would walk to the right place even without signs - if that makes sense. There's also a decent amount of interesting restaurants to try. I would encourage them to consider restaurants that have more plant based options but regardless of that, this is one of my favorite airports to fly into!

I never leave reviews, but, I'm so disappointed I want others to know. I used to go to Aqua Stone all the time. One day, they didn't put down my appointment and they booked someone else and couldn't get me in until the next day. Mind you, the next day was a big event i had and wanted a nice manicure and pedicure before hand. Shouldn't have went back. So, here I am, this passed Sunday. Wanting a good pedicure. I go to Aqua Stone and pay FOURTY NINE dollars for a leg massage and a bad paint job. Don't get me wrong, she did great on the massage and cleaning my feet/nails, but I had two messed up toes and 3 others are now chipped. I don't know what happened to this place but I'm so upset it went down the drain.

My food at Cafe la Maude was very good.. but the wait was not. As a party of 2 we waiting nearly 2 hours to get a table. 

The food was excellent (as it is in many other philly brunch spots) but nothing that completely blew me away. I can't see myself spending another 2 hours of my Sunday morning waiting in line to eat here again. I guess I just don't get the hype!!

I will however get a latte to go because it was the best one I've ever had!

Yeah outdoor seating! My three positives and a negative (because the world needs more positivity):
+ calamari appetizer was one of the best I've ever tasted. Crispy, sweet with a touch of heat. Delightful 
+ my seasonal short rib and Mac n cheese was scrumptious. Apparently it's the third year they have brought it back- for good reason
+ the peanut butter cookie dessert was so worth the calories
- it is a tad pricey ($40/pp for lunch w/o alcohol)
Hidden gem with kids of indoor and outdoor space

The food is amazing and the prices are very reasonable. Jimmy Hula's is a must! I brought my family and they all loved it. The music and ambiance are spectacular. Great restaurant after a beach stroll!
Very good, ambiance is a bit lack luster. Here on a Saturday for lunch. Will have to come back at night.

A great combination of great food and great atmosphere. I went for dinner on a Monday night with my family and the place was hopping. A live musician gave the restaurant an energy that made me feel like I was on vacation (ok I'll admit that I was but even so!). The menu consists of the fairly typical modern Italian dishes, which everyone in my party enjoyed. A good drink and cocktail menu rounded out the meal. Recommend!

I actually can't believe what just happened. I went to go get AirPods and a screen protector replacement and the sales associate Emily was on the phone with her COLLEGE COUNSELOR having me waiting for 20 minutes. I actually had time to go ahead and take a video of her on the phone with her counselor. To think things wouldn't get worse, while in the middle of pandemic, Emily was both not wearing a mask and coughing and whipping her snot on the screen protector making me request that she doesn't install it. I've actually never been more disgusted in my life. I wish I could attach the the video but can't cause yelp doesn't have that feature. I will upload however a screen shot of the video with her clearly on her phone on her college website while not wearing a mask per Santa Barbara policy!

Really wish I had read the Yelp reviews before staying here. The one and two-star reviews do a great job of conveying the experience of this dump. 


I had emailed the hotel with some basic questions a couple of days before our stay (which thankfully was only one night). No response. That should have been my tip-off that this place is a dump and poorly run. 

Why 2 stars instead of 1? The staff was great. Helpful, polite and really wanting to accommodate. Also, the proximity to downtown is nice.

That is where it ends though. This place needs about a half billion dollars in improvements and renovations. If it were priced at $30/night, it would be a fairly priced hotel. 


Specifics: peeling paint, dead light bulbs, one channel on the TV, broken concrete, stains. That is EVERYWHERE you look once you leave the lobby. My sheets had some red substance on them (ketchup? Salsa? Blood? Uncertain). It scraped off and then I could use a washcloth to remove most of it. 


Also, the train. I blame myself for not noticing the hotel's proximity to the train tracks. At least twice an hour....all night...the train horn gently awakens you from your slumber beneath the filth of your room. 


This place is ideal for 20-somethings to engage in a drunken brawl at 2am. Which also happened. I don't blame the hotel for that, but those are EXACTLY the experiences one should come to expect at a dump like this. 


The photographer who took the pictures of the rooms should get some kind of award, because he captured the lipstick on this pig elegantly. 


Avoid this place at all costs.

South Street Barbers (SSB), I'm really impressed by this establishment, I've been a regular patron of SSB for the last 8 years at their 1302 South St. location, now I'm a regular at their second location at 1316 South Street location. 

These two shops have amazing barbers, they are very knowledgeable, whether you go in needing advice on what hairstyle to get or even if you need help deciding how short or how long you want your hair to be, SSB can help, they give the best haircuts and whenever i go in and can't get with my regular barber Will, I feel comfortable enough to utilize any of the other barbers in that day. One thing I really love about SSB is the structure that's in place at these shops, all staff members are always courteous and respectful and it's also kid friendly, whenever a child enters the shop the staff is always guaranteed to make sure the conversation in the shop and the movie on the television is safe for a child's ears......I've been to a lot of other shops in my life but SSB has them all beat by far. The owner has done a fantastic job catering to the public and making sure his staff is up to par. One other thing I love about SSB is that all of the barbers try and make sure customers get in the chair at their scheduled appointment time slot and they are always accountable for their actions so if the customer is not satisfied for any reason they will work with you and try and make things right. They always take their time and cater to each customer and focus their energy on satisfying their clients. 

I live in Delaware, but I come all the way to Philadelphia to get my haircuts because South Street Barbers are awesome. If you are looking for a barber shop that cares about you the client being happy, SSB is definitely the place to go. I highly recommend them. Everything about SSB screams customer satisfaction.
This place has the best pizza I have had in Philly. The buffalo chicken pizza is rockin. This pizza reminds me of NY Style pizza. It is not the real thing but an excellent substitute

Great stay. We got a package which included parking and breakfast for two. Parking is expensive in Philly, so the package is the way to go if you have car. The hotel staff was very pleasant and friendly. The hotel building itself was awesome. The five stars goes to the historic all marble hotel. The lobby bar reception area welcomed us in from the cold weather.  It's hipper and younger than what I would expect from a Ritz. Another reason for the five stars, the freakin view from the room including from the tub was fantastic. Best city view ever (William penn on top of historic city hall).
Great food and service! Their general chicken is very crispy and tasty. We usually drive 45 mins every time to get to this place.

Let's be real, the Nat Caf is great, but it's not that great. There are plenty of other restaurants that will give you much more heart and soul than Nat Caf. This place is really for old people that have problems with their digestion so they need boring food with a sprinkle of cheese. 

I've had the chicken stir fry and the fish tacos and both times I walked away feeling very full, but knowing that they were multiple other places that would have been better. It's a fine meal, but if you're looking for something that will make you happy with intense flavor and creativity instead of just a full belly than this is not my jam.

This was recommended as one of the best Chinese places by someone coming out the door.  The server was very nice, the food, on the other hand not so much.  They had just ran out out hot and sour soup and settled for egg drop- was cold.  Rice was burnt and the egg roll was over cooked and greasy. Would not recommend to anyone.
A funny name that does not describe the business but an amazing rooftop bar with friendly staff and great food and drinks

Great service. Rented a bike to ride around the French quarter. Bikes were kindly old and the chain was rusty but it got us around. They provide locks which is perfect in case you want to go into a shop. Price was very reasonable $6 an hour.
Great place with "local" feel.  Close to Butler University.  Stop in for a great meal before the next Butler Bulldogs Home game or watch the away games in the Bar area!

For starters, there are better options in Edmonton. We stopped by for lunch because of the Yelp reviews. However, the service was almost non existent. 

We were seated right away and menus were provided. Tea was dropped off but the tea pot was never refilled. Our server mistaken an order and we corrected her so one person was left waiting for his food while everyone else was eating. Food was okay. I ordered the vermicelli bowl with grilled beef and spring roll. The spring roll was made with the wheat wrapper. I always prefer the rice wrapper instead. The beef was grilled alright. There was a good amount of veggies. I tasted the pho broth and it had a strong star anise flavour. The broth was just okay. There was a good amount of meat and noodles.

We never saw our server once food was dropped off until we got up to pay the bill. The restaurant only had a few tables of customers dining during lunch. They accept credit card payment which is always a plus. There is ample parking available on the plaza.

Awesome!!! I can't say enough good things about Kay and her grooming! I tried preen mobile pet grooming due to connivence and previous bad experiences with "chain" groomers. Our yorkie "hank" has never looked better. We re-booked  right away! His coat is so soft and shiny and looks fabulous.

This place is awesome. I highly recommend the cajun turkey sandwich. They don't short chance you on anything. You'll get a pile of meat, cheese, lettuce, mayo, other things I can't even remember....it's all good!

The food is awesome! The service is great. The whole atmosphere is welcoming. The staff greet you with warning smiles and hospitality. The must try is the fruity pebblets waffle. They allowed me to personalize it as a chicken and waffle plate and I must say it was the best tasting thing I have ever had. My husband had the waffled French toast and can't stop raving about it! It's a new establishment and a MUST TRY! Also the vanilla latte was from the gods. The hands of the kitchen staff are truly blessed!

My last months in Tucson I promised myself that I would go on a mission of trying as many restaurants I could. Tucson, although a weird place, has great food per square mile. El Charro however, is not that great. I don't get what the big deal is. They are supposed to be the classic Tucson Mexican restaurant but there was nothing to love about El Charro for me. The food was only ok, chips weren't even fresh.  I have had much better carne asada. I mean, if you want to go to a restaurant because you are "supposed" to when you visit a city then fine, do it. But I have to say, I didn't miss anything in the 3 previous years of not going to El Charro.

Fixed our washing machine in a quick and efficient manner. Reasonable price for the parts and repair. I was told the price before he did the repair. 4 stars because he left all the stuff he moved out of my laundry room in the hallway after rather than cleaning up after himself. But other than that I was satisfied.

We have not been out to breakfast in over months with the pandemic.  A couple drive thru experiences at the local McDonalds did not fulfill my breakfast experience.  So decided to try Meghan's.  I was able to place an order and pay easily on their website.  Pickup was easy and server was friendly and had a smile.  Food was good.  I got an egg sandwich, French toast for the wife and pancakes to try.  Will repeat.  They seem to have curbside contactless service down pretty well so I, as a germaphobe was comfortable with the experience.  Will definitely return.
The best banh mi in town !!!!!! Omg I want to try the whole menu ... but I only can try two cause I cannot eat it all plus it's better when it's fresh !

Chifa was on the top of my list for Center City Sips.  I absolutely love Sips because it's a great way to get an introduction to a restaurant without committing to spending a bunch of money.  BUT at Chifa, I was denied the Sips experience.

If I can say one thing about the service and general feel of this place it's MILITANT.  As soon as I walked in, I got a spiel - "Sips is ONLY downstairs."  In the basement.  Where there's not nearly enough seating for everyone.  The UPSTAIRS, mind you, is almost completely empty at 5:00 and I would think a newish restaurant could benefit from looking crowded upstairs where people outside can see.  

When my friends arrived, there was no seating and virtually nowhere to stand, so we decided to head upstairs and eat like civilized people with a table and space and service.  The bartender gave us a hard time about ordering sips drinks and taking them upstairs.  Lame.

We go upstairs and ask for a table.   The server says the table is only for the full menu - we accept these terms.   At this point one friend has wandered off to make a phone call and the waitress says "You do have 3 people right."  3 seconds later "Where is your third person, does he know where you'll be."  These servers need to CHILL!

Finally, they take their food way too seriously.  The server's description of the menu was right on, but quite a few times things were over explained.  Honestly, though, I don't think it's the server's fault.  It's the food's fault.  One of the things we ordered was the Anticuchos.  It was delicious, but the skewers basically came with 6 (yes, six) sauces - 3 on the side and 3 already on the meat.  And it was about 12 pieces of meat total.  That's totally overwhelming for any palate.  Fun, but kind of pointless.  The night was a blur of sauces and tastes and nothing particularly stood out accept the chimichurri.  It was just too much on every dish.   Also, when I order fries, I expect there to be a bunch, not just a handful.  

The rest of the night, we basically had to shoo the servers from taking our dishes prematurely and then we had the banana-pumpkin funnel cake.  Sorry, but this dessert looked like a turd.  I KNOW there have got to be classier ways to plate funnel cake.  And it was greasy, not crispy.  Definitely not worth $8.

Finally, I can say I won't be going back here because I feel tricked.  I wanted Sips, damn it.  The restaurant knew that if people really wanted to stay, they would head upstairs and spend more money.  But they could have just been nice about it and opened Sips to the whole restaurant.  The people would have left by 7 or so anyway!  Instead they made the Sips patrons feel lowly and acted really full of themselves.  The upstairs really wasn't THAT special.  

At a restaurant, you don't get to treat your guests differently depending on what they order  (especially when you are OFFERING things at a discount).  I think Chifa could benefit from a slice of humble pie.

To sum everything up for you, the experience we had at Le Cheri was nearly perfect, if not perfect.

There were ten of us on a busy Friday night in June and the meals came out promptly, were still hot, and delicious - something that does not happen very often at a restaurant. Not only was the service on point, the food itself was enough of a reason to go back.

I had the steak tartar, which was one of the better tartars I've had. The accompaniments were well done and I had no complaints. Some of my table mates also had the foie gras. Now, I've had my share of foie gras and I must say that it may have been the best foie gras I've ever had.

For my entree I had the scallops which were large, fresh, and tasteful. Again, scallops are hit or miss - you either get fishy tasting rubber, or you get an epic mound of buttery-like goodness. I'll let you guess which version they plated.

The restaurant itself had a nice neutral atmosphere, nothing to trendy or impressive, but it fits the bill for a French cuisine restaurant. 

Of you're looking for an authentic French experience, look no further. It's not a Stephen Starr restaurant trying to recreate a French menu (cough Parc), it's a restaurant with French owners who know French food and fine ingredients. Can't wait to go back.

The garage door opener at my house in Incline Village broke. I have a home warranty so called them and they gave the job to Thompson Garage Doors. I was worried at first since Thompson is way over in Sparks. But they called to schedule appointment, Kenneth Mickey showed up right on time, he had all the necessary parts, work was completed really fast and efficiently. They get 5 Stars for sure!

I worked with Mike Manguso on the purchase of a new Highlander.  This is the second car we've purchased at Weiss Toyota with Mike and he truly made whole experience great and easy - from selecting the car, to purchasing and  driving off the lot.  We would highly recommend Weiss Toyota to anyone.

Paid almost $900 for THREE nights only and haven't slept due to the motorcycles and other loud cars outside. Insane to me that you pay so much a night and the windows aren't even sound proof. 

Everything in the sundries is expensive. 

The rooms aren't even that clean... they basically made our bed and left all the trash which had left over food in it. 

On the plus side, rooms are huge, love the bedroom having a door. Love the location, loved the free breakfast.

New favorite diner in the area! It's brand new and super nice inside. Say goodbye to the grungy diners that smell like cigarette smoke and haven't been renovated since 1960. I mean this place was sparkling clean. Now onto the food, the pancakes were so fluffy and delicious! We also had fries which were awesome well done. Our waitress was the sweetest lady ever and made sure we had everything we needed at all times. We actually tipped her 60% because she was so sweet. I honestly have no idea what people below are talking about as far as service goes since I had such a great experience. Maybe it's because we came during the late night shift.

This place has potential.  There are some definite areas where improvement is possible. 
I ordered the smothered fried pork chop.  The breading was crisp and tasty, the mushroom gravy was good, but would have been better if there was more of it.  The pork chop was overcooked. A bit dry, and slightly tough. It also needed some seasoning before being breaded. 
The mashed potatoes were buttery and chunky, but once more, gravy was skimpy. 
The collard greens were ok.  Large chunks of pork, but the flavor was not obvious. 
The wife had salmon croquettes, and they were also ok.  The only real issue with them was some small bone pieces (vertebrae, not pin bones). 

We will probably go back and give them another shot, as we really want to support locally owned small businesses.

The manager, Jeremy Smeltzer, has ruined Tesla for me. I'm selling my plaid, and cancelling my two Model Y orders; as well as my cyber truck order. 

His team promised a Dec 31st delivery date, confirmed on my Tesla app, I bought my plane tickets to pick up the car, and Jeremy called me this morning and said that unless I am there by Christmas (december 25th) I will lose the car and my deposit. 

He then lamented about tesla's policies and took no responsibility and offered no help. 

Very poor service.

If I could, I would give a negative -1,000,000 rating.Please never ever go to Ashley.  Summary, I bought a sectional couch on 3-22-16 and it is 7-26-16.  I don't have a couch or my $1500 refund.  (plymouth meeting, pa) Ashely advertises quick delivery, its been 4 months!  Gary promised I would have the couch in 7-10 days.  I called after 2 weeks (was placed on hold for 10-42 minutes each time I called any department) they said it was there and they had forgotten to call me. I scheduled delivery-they didn't have openings for 10 days, then only had one or two options.  This was dishonest-delivery is not 7 days.  I stayed home all day on a Saturday.  I am self employed and am paid per client hour.  I did not work and I missed my son's soccer game.  I had a morning window for delivery.  I sat home all day.  They do not use Ashley delivery people.  The delivery guys explained they rent a truck on Saturdays and deliver furniture for Ashley.  The guys did not come until 8pm!  The truck they rented broke down twice.  A day later, I realized the couch was torn. I called a distribution number (22 minutes on hold.)  I was calm (not realizing the horror to come).  The woman was nasty, accused me of tearing the couch, asked why I had signed if torn, said their items are not torn, etc.  She actually hung up on me.  She told me to email photos to a 'furniture doctor' email.  She did not speak clearly and was frustrated bc i asked her to repeat email.  She said I would have a reply in 1/2 hour.  I cancelled a client.  Again, reducing my income.  Well,1/2 hour turned into 2 weeks!  I spoke to a human after 31 minutes on hold after being disconnected  as well. I was given different numbers and passed around.They said they had ordered me another piece, but no one told me.  I thought they would repair this piece.  I waited another couple weeks for the new section. I called them after not hearing from anyone.  Again, they said the couch was there, but had not been scheduled for deliery.  John,the manager of Plymouth Meeting store, promised delivery that Saturday at 8am.  I then talked to Mary Rose at corporate.  When I talked about frustration of the long phone wait times, Mary Rose laughed and said she waits on hold.  She was rude and asked why I was calling her when the situation had been resolved.  It was resolved, bc John had scheduled a Saturday delivery.  At this point, weeks have passed.  No surprise, I took off work and they no showed.I called the delivery center and was told I was not even in the computer or on the schedule.   I was unable to get in touch with John or Mary Rose.  I finally got in touch with John Tuesday.  He apologized for not returning my calls. He shockingly admitted that he had "lied" to me.  He acknowledged there is no such thing as an 8am delivery. Deliveries are later in the morning and you get call the night before with a four hour window.  Other companies allow you to pick an AM or PMwindow.  With Ashley, they tell you the night before if you are AM or PM.Now,I asked for the couch to be returned.  John gave me a hard time about this and questioned my ability to get  a refund. I called VISA and they  started a fraud investigation for an item over $1000.  John promised all 3 new pieces and I again scheduled another window for delivery.  Two nice men showed up (they rent a truck/deliver) and replaced damaged couch with new. Three days later, I tried to put the couch together,they were the wrong pieces, the couches did not connect!  I was placed on hold over and over at the offices.  Mary Rose at corporate spoke to me again to no avail. John dodged my calls.  We then tried a Saturday delivery to simply bring the new piece.  After they no showed again, I drove to Raymour Flannigan and bought a couch in 20 minutes.  It was in my house in 3 days. I begged John to pick up the couch and refund my money.  I scheduled for the couch to be picked up on a Wednesday. I took off work-they never came. over 3 months have passed. The lady at store said I was moved to thursday.  Even tho I was on wednesday.  John then said, he did not know why i was moved.  again, no one told me--but regardless-i had changed my whole work schedule for wednesday and could not simply take off thursday too.  We then went with a sunday pick up.  Well-that Thursday, i had a work cancellation-and came home. Ashley contractors showed up!  These poor guys were never told my pick up was moved to sunday.  However, I was home and the nice men picked up the couch. The couch is gone after 3 months! I tipped delivery men three times for this couch.  I had given my old couches away and had no where to sit for a while.  when i got raymour couch, I had to pay them to move the ashley couch to other room. so tips 4 times.  one time, the guys scratched my wall. so I tipped 4 times, I have a scratched wall, no couch and they owe me $1500. It has been an absolute disaster-like some sort of humorous cocktail story---Please never ever go to ashley!!

I can't believe I'm just trying this restaurant. Very quaint, cool vibes. Great spot to bring the family. Definitely recommend getting a few apps for the table to start. We had the chicken dish which was fab, the steak was so tender and delicious, and the burger was simple yet so tasty with the meat being from Porter Road Butcher. We loved our server too! Very funny and engaging and not to mention attentive! Will definitely be back.

My hi index glasses broke, and where my optometrist couldn't fix, Eyeglass Repair did!  Grateful that I didn't have to throw my expensive glasses out, and can still have a back up pair.  Even got my other back up pair adjusted.  Highly recommended!

We visited Bistro 44 for the first time last night, and I was impressed enough to say that I will give it a second try.  However, I wasn't impressed enough to give it more than 3 stars.  We ate in the bar area, and I liked the atmosphere.  Dark, but not so dark you couldn't tell what you were eating.  Four or five tv's, but none of them had the sound turned up.  We only had appetizers, and they ranged in quality from excellent to just ok.  First up, the steak skewers on homemade coleslaw was excellent.  Great tasting slaw and tender meat made it a real treat.  The pineapple shrimp were huge, and tasted ok, but the side sauces needed more flavor.  The one that was supposed to be tangy wasn't.  Finally, their much talked about sliders were just ok.  Not a lot of flavor, and I was underwhelmed.  They had many beers in bottles, but only three on tap.  Overall, I enjoyed the atmosphere enough that I will go back and give an actual dinner or lunch a try.

Gorgeous studio, spacious, large bathrooms with showers! Two rooms for classes, including classic 26&2 Bikram style, mat and towel rental. The heat was perfect and the timing and dialogue were excellent. Great place for yoga! Ample parking, too. Easy to find.

Just had my first cut w the shop's manager, Tim Cook, and all I can say is WOW! I came in w a sub par cut from an unnamed establishment and Tim came through. Sick pseudo fade and even tapered my beard to match. Big ups to the establishment as well. If you're looking for a quality cut and a safe (Covid precautious) establishment Derby Supply should be high on your list.

Anyone that has donated to this place quickly understands that they are worse than telemarketers when it come to asking for donations. We started getting one or two phone calls a month. Went to once a week. Now as many as three times a day. All my donations and buying will be elsewhere from now on. Very rude when I asked them to take me off their call list.  Never donate to this place unless you are lonely and/or just want phone calls. Last time I checked, I was donating because I wanted to or could. Not because I was forced to.

Great venue.  Even better when YELP is involved :)  

We arrived as YELP guests at 6:30 pm, got inside at 6:45, a full hour and 15 minutes prior to the general public.  As you walk in the door you are greeted with a Brew Ha Ha  / Sierra Arts logo beer cup then off you go to a variety of represented breweries.  Sock Eye, Drakes, Alibi, Sierra Nevada, High Sierra to name just a few.  No shortage of beer here folks.  Lots of choices, styles and flavors.

First stop, Dust Bowl Brewing.  Theses guys are located in Turlock, California and brew a great variety of beers.  All are excellent.  I have been to the Turlock location a few times so I know this beer to be good.  They did not disappoint.  2nd stop was Alibi Ale Works.  They are from Incline Village, Nevada and are about to open a tasting room in my hometown of Truckee.  Lucky me!!!!   Again, a great variety of good tasting beers.

I do not I believe a missed any of the breweries represented here.  Tasted them all!!

One place of note.....Record Street Brewing.  These are the new kids on the block.  They have not even opened the location yet, which will be on 4th street in downtown Reno across the street from The Depot. The beer was outstanding....be sure to check these guys out when they open later this month.

Revision Brewing....another not so new kid on the block had a table with brochures but no beer or rep.  Of course they haven't even opened the doors at the Sparks location on Rock Blvd.  If you know Knee Deep Brewing in Auburn, CA, then you will love Revision.  Jeremy was a founding partner in Knee Deep before venturing back to Reno to open Revision.....stay tuned!!!

Overall this is a great event benefiting a great cause.  Plenty of beer swag and an abundance of art to look at and purchase.  If you get the chance next year, be sure to attend, this is a well worth it event!!!   Thanks YELP for the great time!!!!

                                                                              Cheers..............................................

After hearing several recommendations from friends my husband and I came here for brunch at 1pm on a Sunday. Parking was easy with a large lot behind the building and we were seated right away which surprised us a little bit. Maybe it was an off day, I really wanted to like this spot but things just did not add up. 
We were seated at a table for two that had a booth on one side and a chair on the other, when we sat down there was no one sitting at the tables on either side of us. As soon as these tables filled up it was extremely awkward, they were so close we could hear what everyone else was saying word for word. There was no music being played in this area of the restaurant and you could hear every part of the conversation at the tables next to you. 
It was also extremely hot in this part of the restaurant, it did not feel like they had any air conditioning on which was unfortunate considering it was in the 80s outside. 

Now onto the food...I ordered the Keg Omelette which had Serrano ham, Swiss cheese, tomatoes and came with home fries and a biscuit. The best part was the biscuit. My omelette had almost no filling, I think I had one bite that actually had ham and mostly it was just plain eggs. It took quite a while to find our server and ask her for some ketchup, it was not severed with the meals and not on the table. 
My husband had the 1/4 Keg Omelette, he said it was alright but nothing special.

Drinks from the bar were fine, but we had to beg anyone to refill our water glasses throughout the meal. 

The bill came to $28 for two omelettes, one mimosa and one manmosa. Doubt we will be back.

Meh. I really really wanted to like this place but it just falls short on so many levels. 

Food: Bland, tasteless and boring. Do they know how to use some simples salt and pepper even? Doubt it. I had the calamari and really all I could taste was half cooked white flour, I have never been one to eat straight flour soooooooo needless to say I did not finish this and I did NOT ask for a box. My boyfriend had chicken tortilla soup which he specifically ordered because that type of soup generally has at least a small amount of spice and he wanted to clear out his sinuses. The broth was pretty much watered down tomato sauce, not a pinch of seasoning of any kind to be found. 

Service: We were seated right away but then I think the waitress just kind of forgot about us. Many times. 

Atmosphere: It's pretty inside, but that's really about it. The atmosphere itself was kind of dreary and again, pretty lonely since the waitress kept forgetting we were there. 

I really don't think I will ever go back unless it's just to grab a drink or 2 before the game. I can guarantee I will not waste my money on food there again.

This place is bussin!! I frequently order from here when I come back to West Chester to visit my family. I live in Pittsburgh now and also lived in Maryland during the pandemic and I have never-not once found anything similar to Bon Bon Sushi near me

It's sushi and a burrito...it's the best of both worlds

The sushi burritos are really delicious and they're super filling. The cost is under $10 and it's such a unique dining experience! I like going into the shop and sitting at the window. But you also can't beat the take out from here!

Walking around downtown Franklin, I wasn't sure about this place because I saw other places nearby with better reviews - but I'm really glad we stopped here for lunch.  

The space is a converted Pharmacy, really cute, with some of the walls wallpapered with old prescriptions.  They had just rolled out a new menu a few days beforehand.  I had the zuchinni and squash arugula salad.  It was great!  We also tried the banana  pudding and dark chocolate brownie - both were great depending on whether you wanted to top your meal off with something light or rich.

As of 5/16 the new menu does not look as though it has been updated on yelp, so it's a bit different in the restaurant, I uploaded a photo of the new menu.

This was such a nice find on our recent trip to New Orleans, recommended by our Lyft driver. 

We made a reservation right away for that evening, a Friday, and the earliest they could take us was 9, but that worked out fine.  After a hurricane at Pat O'Brien's and sone time in Jackson Square admiring St Louis Cathedral a night, we headed over.

Everything was great -- we did have to wait a few extra minutes for our table, but on a crowded Friday night, with a last-minute reservation, that was understandable. We had a place to sit while we waited, so all was well.

My drinks (I tried their house Manhattan) were outstanding, as was my scallop-crusted halibut (named Scalibut here).  We also had the lobster dumplings as an appetizer based on the reviews I'd read while sitting on a bench people-watching in Jackson Square, and they were outstanding.  I could have happily made a meal of just these!

The shrimp risotto that came with my dinner was delicious.  I think the only change I'd make is with the pea butter -- I'm not a fan of peas, and the pea flavor of the butter was stronger than I'd anticipated, so the next time I'll ask for plain clarified butter. 

Everything was lovely, including the dining room, and service was gracious and attentive. 

This was truly a find.  We'll be back in our next trip to town!

The best part about coming to any chain restaurant is friendly staff. The staff here was great! As for ordering, I love their barbacoa burritos with corn salsa, lettuce, sour cream, fajita mix, and guac. It is a big burrito so I say an okay bang for your buck. Now I know some people have cited inconsistency here and I get it! I also ordered a meal in a bowl and the bowl was FAR smaller than the burrito. All and all, it is a good Chipotle for a yummy, fast meal!

My first time here yesterday. It was clean. Service was very good. Nice diverse selection of craft beers. Craft beers were $5-$7 each. Happy was until 7pm so we got the fries pickles. They were breaded spears and tasted very good! We also got the special for Taco Tuesday. The chicken tacos were not very good. It was like a ground chicken that was very dry and lacking flavor. The beef tacos were good though. The chicken enchiladas were better than the tacos but still dry and a little bland. $2/taco $3/enchilada. I would come back again to try something else off their overwhelming menu, particularly because the service was very good.

For those of you who know me, you know I don't take reviews lightly.
It's hard to tell you how pleased I am with these guys!
I called Robert and he was very pleasant. He was also very flexible when I had to change the moving date last minute due to weather forecast. The irony is that it ended up not raining at all!
Anyways, they arrived on time and ready for the huge job we had. We had to move our super heavy 70" TV and our sectional couch down a very narrow staircase. They worked nonstop for 8 hours straight. They truly impressed me!
They were very professional and careful with our stuff and with our kids running around.
At the end of the move we realized the movers that moved our stuff from Oregon forgot to bring the "hookups" for the washer and drier. Robert stopped by Lowe's the following morning and bought it for us and came back to hook it up.
Wow! Talk about service! 
I hope I don't have to move again any time soon, but I will definitely contact AAA Soto if I do.
Last words: Go with these guys if you want a stress-free moving experience!

Great beers all around and a killer local spot to escape the crowds. ....surfing sucks don't try it cough cough....

Recent expansion is great , as are beers

They rep the avocado beer a lot which correlates with local geographics being abundant to avo's...but personal favorite is the Paradise Pale Ale , photo attached accompanied by partial patio view

Excellent place.  The food is delicious and authentic Italian....freshly made and the sauce is some of the best we've ever tasted.  

I highly recommend this place to everyone.  It's now my family's favorite Italian restaurant.

Clementine's is quite a treat! I've had ice cream but not on this level. I had the strawberry-balsamic ice cream and a scoop of the Indian Summer. The strawberry-balsamic was good but paled in comparison to Indian Summer. Just thinking of it now makes my eye lids flutter and stomach growl. Simply salivating. 

I look forward to trying out all the flavors and revisiting the Indian Summer one. Hopefully this weekend!

I got a VIP Bottle Service for me and my daughters Birthday Celebrating in the same month. My first time there. The Director Joe was very nice. We had a wonderful time and loved the D.J. The Music was all that. Kept me and my friends and family dancing throughout the night. I wish I knew my waitress/server name. She was very sweet and helpful. From reading the reviews about how the waitress and servers are rude. I guessed I luck out! I was very fortunate to get a VIP in the back corner thank God we did bc it got super crowded around the other VIP Sections. The only issue was I was not aware of how the Bottled prices were calculated into the VIP Bottle Service bill. I was told up to 12 people liquor/Juices was included. I was shocked when I saw the menu that a regular size Grey goose is around $400. The bottle VIP should have been explained that if your VIP Bottle Section is up to 12 people and that the price is around $850 so therefore you can only get basically two regular bottles for that price since it's close to $400 for each bottle. At least they could have larger bottles! And I was not told over the phone that I would have to share a VIP Section with only two small tables. A few of us went outside to get some air and came back just to find a crowd of people sitting in our VIP Section I Thought!!!  I asked if we could have pizza and a cake. The Director Joe said yes not realizing when it was delivered that we had nowhere to put these pizza boxes bc the other crowd was still hanging around. Eventually they left and we enjoyed the rest of the night. A beautiful experience with the D.J and selection of music.

I was supposed to come here to get a colon hydrotherapy, but unfortunately because I had severe anemia he said he couldn't do it. Which I'm glad he told me that because I wouldn't want something bad to happen to me. But even though I wasn't able to get it done I really liked how kind and professional he sounded over the phone. He seems to really care about his clients. Some people would probably just tell you it's up to you if you want to get it done or not but he was kind and caring to tell me he couldn't do it. I know that when I get my anemia taken care of that I will definitely be coming to get it done here.
More like Pat's Crap O Steak ! You should have seen the cashier and cook, no teeth one was picking his nose and the cashier asked me for my wife's number wtf!!!! This place is a sh&%$ hole

Sat at the bar on a Wednesday-met a couple girlfriends for dinner/drinks. Joeys is a solid 4 for me all the time,  but it is worth noting that the food/drink suggestions and overall service from Jordan(in particular) and the rest of the staff was truly exceptional.  We had a great time. So thank you for doing it right!
