Brought my not so nice cane corso in today. I was so worried he would not behave. Dr. G and his staff are fantastic!! Very caring. Brandi at the front desk is awesome,  confident and reassuring.  We've been bringing our pets here for care for years and they never disappoint!

We had a wonderful experience here for a celebratory dinner/date night. We were able to make reservations via Open Table and were sat as soon as we arrived. Service was prompt, friendly, and thorough. We had the crap dip to start, which was excellent, but a little salty. I didn't care for the chips it came with, but that is just my personal taste. I had the Mixed Grill, which included 3 pieces of fish, shrimp, and scallops. Very tasty! I wish the fish portions were a tad bigger, but it still filled me. My husband had fish and chips. Holy fish and chips! Biggest portion ever and the flakiest, moistest fish this side of the Atlantic. The best he's ever had! For dessert we tried the special bread pudding, which was recommended by our waitress. We were waffling between that and the lava cake, but she was nice enough to let us try the pudding and if we didn't like it, she would bring us the lava cake instead. Well, we didn't need to return it. It was so good!  We had a wonderful night here and will definitely be back for another occasion. 4.5 stars!

Not a good experience. Up all night because the drapes dont close and a bright light shines in the window. Not a good neighborhood could not sleep, kept thinking  my car would be broken into while the bright lights kept me up. Very small rooms, especially the bathroom. Had to use your room key to get into the breakfast room.  Hmmm something is wrong there. No regular coffee. Finally asked for some and they brought to the room full of waiting guests desperate for coffee. Scary place.
Low quality ingredients.  I was very dissapointed.  Although the service wasnt horrible I dont intend on ever returning .

My best friend, her mom and I had breakfast here (local restaurant for her mom who is a Brownsburg resident. The building is a old Hardee's - and the new owners haven't done much to the place to make it not look like a Hardee's. 
Otherwise waitress was very friendly and attentive. My best friend had corn beef hash, she said it was the best she has ever had, I had scrambled eggs and hash browns and it was very good. 
I would definitely recommend this restaurant.

My first time at this tiny jewel of a spa, inside of the charming Monteleone. Lance worked some magic to get our party of four accommodated in less than 48 hours on a holiday weekend. 

My full pedicure was expertly and lavishly administered by Millie. I'm no stranger to pedicures, but this was several levels above the usual fare! 

Also, I received a one hour massage at the magic hands  of Trudy, during which my soul left my body at least twice. Trudy's skills are beyond relaxing and highly beneficial; I felt ready to take on the hot NOLA afternoon with renewed vigor. 

Thank you to all. HIGHLY recommend.
This place is dang good. The owner of the shop is a real Greek person. Knew the exact area that my fiancé family is from is Greece and he is a diehard poak fan. Def give it a try!!

I was having a hard time finding a place that did gel manicures in my area, until I found this place by my job. Kayla did a great job on my hands and feet, and I'll definitely be returning for future services.

La Tia and Pancho's is our go to Mexican spot! The waiters and waitresses are so friendly and accommodating. The  chips are warm, crispy and delicious. We always get queso! The salsa is fresh and addicting! We have tried the chimichanga, fajitas, street tacos, and burritos.
Got the colorful tea!! So amazing!!! Fresh fruit is delicious!!! Definitely a must try!!
The best restaurant in Indy by far!!! Loved the wine pairings with the food, great service, couldn't ask for a better place to go to for dinner.

I called this company for trash removal of yard debree. After sending a picture of trash via text message I was told they can come tomorrow. A time was never given and I text them my address. I waited all day and no one ever came. I don't understand in this economy how a company can give u p good money. It was taken care of quickly by another company.

Nice guys. I bought in my iPad mini 4 for repair. Due to on side of the screen pop up a little bit. It was out of warranty. But instead of ripping me off at the repair. They suggest me to give apple a try to see if apple will replace it for free even it is out of warranty. And apple did replace it for me. Basically I got the repair for free and these guys did not rip customer off

I took a friend for a birthday dinner. The cocktails were delicious as was the food. The crab cakes were to die for as was the pear salad. My guest said the chicken was fabulous. A little disappointed that wwe did not receive a birthday dessert to share. Most restaurants at this  price point do. We ordered the apple dessert but were disappointed. Service was excellent.

Was looking for a Brazilian restaurant in the Ybor City area, found this place instead. It's a small quite restaurant two blocks away from 7th Ave. I ordered the Churrasco, 22 ounces of tender juicy skirt steak, fried yuca and beef empanadas. I ordered it take out and took it back to the ship I was on. It was delicious, fried yuca was good, I prefer the Brazilian aipim but the yuca was good, the empanadas were large and stuffed with beef, but the steak was great, tender, juicy and perfect. I had enough meat for three meals. The restaurant was small and quite, no loud conversation or music. I would definitely go back to this place next time I'm in Ybor City.

Great salesmen and women.A place that feels like family.Service department very honest.I would recommend this dealership to family and friends. They go out of their way to make sure your needs are met.They don't give you the run around. Our sale man honest about what we could afford.

A bright, trendy environment with a fun menu and my nail tech was very sweet.   I bumped my foot and damaged a nail as I was leaving.  Another tech fixed it and rudely told me to be more careful.  However, two hours after my pedicure, the polish was still not dry.   Because of an appointment, I had to put on shoes, which ruined the polish.    While the "gimme some sugar" pedi was relaxing, it was totally not worth it.   IF I return, I will not be moving from my chair until I'm sure the polish is dry.

How cool is this? You choose the meat, pasta, veggies, sauce and watch them cook it on a giant stone (I think) griddle. Everything was fresh and the choices were almost too numerous. Friendly and attentive service. Yum. I will return.

I have wanted to check out this spa for a while now.. The first time I called- a busy gentleman answered the phone, was kind of rude, or that's just the way he talks, I guess they weren't open yet.. He told me to call back. I never got around to it, but today I got the nerve again.. We need a good steam/spa facility, I was excited to get the "corrective wrap" service for $125 as well. Holding my breath, I dialed the number, the same busy gent answered the phone, 
he said "No, impossible, we don't have time for that today.. Well here, you talk to the masseuse and have it out with her, ok?" 
Excuse me- Did I say something to insult this guy? Sorry I'm not Russian speaking!. He passes the phone to a woman who could not understand which package I was asking for.. I'm a kind, patient person.. Finally I was able to help her understand the package I wanted from their website. She was able to make time at 3 pm I was so excited til she told me I would have to pay an extra $40 bucks to use the steam room and hot tub facilities. WHAT?!!! I have never been to a spa that makes you pay an additional fee to use the facility! It's absurd! Especially if I am already paying for a service! That's just business foolishness! Unfortunately for them, I cancelled the appointment, my advice to this business-- get someone ELSE to answer your phones and take appointments properly.  Someone pleasant and friendly with all people not just Russian speaking people, as I am very friendly with all people, even Russian speaking non English speaking people. Be kind to all your customers. 
Also, you would probably get more business for your spa treatments if you don't charge this extra facility fee. Don't get me wrong, I can understand a fee for those coming in just for the facility, but not if you buy a spa service!!! I wanted to love this place, but I'm disappointed, I didn't feel welcome from the first phone call, I am not going to call this place again.
UPDATE- ok so I finally went because a friend bought a groupon coupon. I did enjoy the steam room the best, the saunas were very relaxing but too hot for me to lay down. I loved to pull string showers, it was fun to just let the water drop on you.. The shock it refreshing. I didn't have the nerve to totally plunge in the cool pool but I managed to go up to my thighs. It was very refreshing and an interesting experience. Drink lots of water! I would go again but $40 seems too much, I would go a lot more often if it was only $20 bucks. Still, I did have a good time, it was fun.

This was my first time axe throwing and it was so
Much fun!!! This bar is actually in the back of Fergs so it was kinda hard to find we had to ask someone. They are taking Covid precautions which is great too. When you arrive they give you the rundown and you sign a safety waiver. You can't wear heels and they prefer closed toed shoes. Then they spend some time teaching everyone how to properly throw which is awesome because it was def harder than it looked! Then they have different games on the score sheet for you to play. It was such an awesome experience I can't wait to go again!
This place has been closed for months. They haven't updated their website or the message on their door.
Service was good. Food was ok. Had a chimichunga and taco salad, nothing to write home about. If I lived in the area, I'd give the place another try.

This hotel/casino is awful!  I received a free nights stay Sun-Thurs for teacher appreciation week and have now tried to use it twice this summer only to have been told that they cannot honor it.  I would stay at the scummiest motel in Reno over the Atlantis.  It's far off the strip, the pool is awful, there is no club and the staff is rude!

Really enjoyed the meal here. Started it off with a couple of cocktails, Sazerac and a light pink drink which we enjoyed. Had the Shrimp and Tasso Henican appetizer. It was good, a bit spicy. The Commander's Crisp Romaine Salad was very good. We also had the Praline Lacquered Texas Quail. The glaze and the cabbage was excellent! For dessert we had the pecan pie and the bread pudding souffle. I enjoyed the pecan pie, but disappointed that the crust was extremely hard. My husband enjoyed his souffle. Complained to the waiter about the crust, but all he said was, "Sorry." His name is Sway and was otherwise nice and attentive.

Not sure why my Oct 2014 review was removed?  Try again:
Have been here several times previously, and no major issues worth noting.  Stopped by last week (~10/2) around 5 PM. Place smelled strongly of restroom cleaner, we were told to "seat yourself," and waited 15 mins.  No one showed up at our table.  We left.  The place was empty, save for a few gents who got bar service and sat outside and one other couple... sitting and undoubtedly soaking in the overpowering scent of bathroom sanitizer. Wow. What happened?
On prior visits we found the food so-so, but entirely acceptable with some creative salsa usage. We've had some of their house specials, combo platters, and a la carte items, as well as a margarita or two. My dad did like his tortilla soup, and my daughter enjoyed the shredded beef taco. We live very close by and had hoped for a really reliable, tasty Mexican option. This isn't it, sadly.
When one succumbs to the fears of government propaganda it will look like this place/ They've fallen for every scare imaginable. Masks required at all times! No thanks, bye.

Ted Drewes was the perfect stop on a really hot day! They have really yummy frozen custard, tons of flavors, and the prices are great! I love that there's a variety of sizes to choose from. I ordered a mini raspberry pecan custard and It was just the right amount for me--not to mention delicious! 

This location looks a little run down, but don't let that stop you from coming. I already want to go back!
Love this place.  It gets busy and trendy, but I have never had a bad meal or bad service.  It's different sushi, can be a little fussy, but it's so good.  One of my favorites.

I'm totally into SSS.  Good authentic Greek food.  My roommate is 100% Greek and loves this place.  They have take out or sit down, and you can't go wrong with either.  Sandwiches (gyros, kebobs, etc.) are only $6 and definitely fill you up.  Try the flaming cheese appetizer too... it's warm gooey goodness served with pita bread.

I like the no-BS attitude of the place.  The sign that reads, "Help Wanted: Experienced servers only, don't waste our time and yours" is classic.  So is "If you can't control your children, don't patronize our restaurant" printed on the menu.  You get the feeling the owner serves great food and doesn't need to charm the pants off you.

Snag the table by the window if you enjoy South Street people watching.  It's a fabulous pastime.

P.S. Beware of the sneaky sodas - they are only $1, but no free refills.  Just because they refill your same glass doesn't mean you aren't paying for another soda!

Reno Fly Shop is now my 'go to' place when I need to stock up for the season.. They are not just employees they know a lot about fishing so they can give you advice. The owner personally takes time with you. This is a smaller 'mom and pop' shop, and although I still shop at Bass and Orvis for hunting, there are some things I'd rather pick up from these guys.

Good food. Reasonable prices. Speedy services. 

The lady owner does not like to smile, appears like she's angry all the time. But deep down, if you chat with her, she's friendly in her own way. 

Still ... that kind of angry face takes away two stars from this review. 

Recommended dish: shrimp fried rice. 

Also: Pho soup is delicious, and I don't even like Pho in general but I still find it delicious here.
Pretty awesome place staff is friendly makes you feel at home went for a cleaning and came out feeling clean the doc is awesome straight forward highly recommend this place

I had lunch here with a colleague yesterday and this was my first visit to this restaurant. I'll begin by saying I give the restaurant decor and service  a rating of 4-stars and the food that we ordered a rating of 2-stars. I like the design of the restaurant very much. Chic, clean, sharp, and attractive. Our server was very friendly and attentive, as was the server who brought out our pizza, salad, and sandwich.

However, the food was rather disappointing. This was, by far, the worst Margherita Pizza that I have ever eaten. First, the crust had a section that was burnt. Second, the pizza sauce had a very poor flavor that was not tasty at all. Third, the toppings were very skimpy. It was just rather poorly done and very disappointing. I also had a half of one of the panini sandwiches and it was acceptable, if not bursting with flavor or having any distinguishing culinary characteristics. 

So, I'd say they really need to work on their menu quite a bit. All fixable issues, but definitely requiring some attention.

I'm not sure if I'm jinxed at this place but the last couple times Ive been there the service was terrible. Took forever to get a waitress and even longer to submit food orders. No one knew who our waitress was. Other than that it's a good place to eat. 

Food is what it is. A typical Red Robin Franchise. It's great food for a decent price.

I would not recommend staying at this hotel. The cons: screwed up our reservation
Homeless hangout out front (next to McDonald's)
"City view" room had one tiny window that mostly looked over a roof with air conditioning units
Thin walls meant we heard all the drunkards in the hall. 
The only pros were the inexpensive price, proximity to French quarter, and an awesome staff person named Kie.
We had a great experience!  Be sure to ask for Paul Wheeler!  He is extremely helpful and best of all, honest!

Shocking to see how nice it is down stairs, upstairs is more of a diner vibe. The ambience is very nice, the cocktails were great,& the food was also good! Recommend the chicken parm, gnocchi, and shrimp scampi!
Wow, second time here in a year, and it doesn't fail to satisfy.  Really great food, very fresh fish, beautifully prepared.  Coming back every time I'm in town.

Had an engagement ring made here, I provided my own diamond and Russell provided the band and setting.  He did a great job at a very reasonable price.

At his recommendation we went with a slightly smaller size.  My fiance wore it for about 2 months but felt it was too tight.  We took it back and Russell resized it free of charge and gave it another cleaning and polishing, all free.

We felt very comfortable working with Russell and would gladly go back to him in the future.
Service was great and hotpot and KBBQ were tasty. We went with a big group of seventeen people and all the food came out very quickly.

I'm a Jersey Girl at heart, so of course, I love me a good diner. BSD has a nice menu. Fries are delish, so are the wraps. My friend loved her huge omelet. Staff is on top of things most of the time. Fast service. Small parking lot is always nice.

Dr. Tad Suzuki is an excellent endodontist. He performed an apicecotomy on a very complex root canal that had failed twice in the past and I was able to keep my tooth.  So very happy! Eternally grateful.  He is gentle, kind and meticulous. Highly recommend him :)
Marleen V.

My boyfriend and I went to dinner here for the first time, Saturday February 18th. We were about a half hour early for our 745 reservation. To our surprise, there was no record of our Open Table reservation. My boyfriend had screenshotted our confirmation as we have had this problem once before. He showed to shot, only for he and the hostess to both realize the reservation was for Friday, the 17th.  My boyfriend was clearly embarrassed and apologetic. 
Without skipping a beat, the Hostess told us we could enjoy our meal in the liunge at a table with a lovely view and no wait, or we could have a drink abd wait to see if she could fit us in. She suggested grabbing an app as it was a very busy night. 
Knowing our situation, the Hostess went out of her way to find us a great table and seat us within minutes of our reservation!! Well 24 hours and minutes from our reservation. Our apps were sent up to.our table and our night was FLAWLESS!!! 
The food was out of this world!!! We had shrimp cocktail. I had Ahi Tuna with lobster and crab. He had Prime rib. 
I have nothing but great things to say about the food, cocktails a VALUE. 
If a manager reads this, PLEASE, give your Saturday hostess a raise!!! She was clearly the lead, dark hair. She MADE our night!!!

April 11, 2014 Friday - dinner 5:45 PM
4 adult

Atmosphere - 5 stars
Food - 4 stars
Service - 5 stars 

Background - Getting dinner before TMCC production of Spam-A-Lot off of Keystone.  The group wanted Mexican food for dinner.   Figured this was close.
 
Atmosphere - Older building probably has not been updated since it was built except for the promotion items from booze companies.

Food
  Drinks - They have a good selection of Mexican beers and tried Sol.  It was happy hour and the girls got a pitcher of strawberry margaritas.  They said they liked it; $12 got more than three glasses worth.

  Appetizer - Free chips and salsa, the give two heat levels.  I like having choices.  We also added so guacamole, which was good.

  My Main Course - I went with a chimichanga, a deep fried burrito, gotta love that.  I also added the side of beans and rice for a little extra.  Nothing really grab my attention, it was done well but not much in flavoring.     

  Dessert - none for me.

Service - What you need from you waiter.  The drinks came fast and so the food. 

Future - I would like to try their other locations.

So we decided to try out a new spot for take out and my girlfriend found a place near the hospital where she works. Very healthy alternative to the everyday humdrum of take out food. Unfortunately, we put an online order at 4:30 for a 7:30 pickup. Not only was the order not ready when we arrived, but we get home only to notice that the Hummus Tahina salad had no falafel in it. You work hard all day to have to stand in a long line for an order that should have been put together before you got there and you get home all the way to Manayunk just to find out it's not even complete. Thanks Sweetgreen. Really appreciate it.

If you're looking for southern comfort food in Indianapolis then you've come to the right place! The Eagle is a transition family style restaurant/bar where they serve large portions to share. The food is delicious and features fried chicken with a homemade spicy honey sauce that is to die for! You can get a quarter chicken, half chicken or whole chicken.  In my group we have 4 people and got a whole chicken plus 3 sizes. It was the perfect amount! Some of our favorite sides include Mac n cheese, biscuits, and fries (all the carbs!). 

They also have a full bar with average priced drinks. If you looking for a deal though, they have a chalice of Miller high life for $3 so you can drink like a king or queen all night 

Now for the not so great...if you only have a couple of people in your group, its likely you will have to sit with people you don't know. Their tables seat 6-8 and they will put two smaller parties together at one table. Some people could find this awkward but I say why not make some new friends! Also it can get pretty busy on weekend night so be prepared for a little bit of a wait. They usually tell you the wait will be longer than it really is though so you're pleasantly surprised when they call you early. 

Overall, one of my favorite bars on mass ave to get some tasty grub and enjoy a few drinks with friends!

Oh My God! I just ordered take out of their Gnocchi and they were fast than Jimmy Johns having it ready for me! The inside was cute but very slow for a Tuesday and I hope this review helps pick up some business but my gnocchi is beyond amazing! I wouldn't say it's the most traditional Gnocchi shape but wow it is so good!

I was on a time crunch and needed to get my nails done before vacation. I went to TWO different nail salons near by and was turned away because they closed in an hour. I stopped in to Britney Nails and was seated right away. I just needed a gel manicure...nothing fancy. It turned out great and I was in and out in 30 minutes. Despite the other reviews, give this place a try if you're close by! The staff was super friendly and I will be back! Thank you for the great service!

We love this place. We live out in the sticks and while everyone else refuses to deliver, this place always happily takes care of us. The food is amazing and we have never been disappointed. Today we visited and ate in for the first time while running errands. Not only was the food good as usual but the customer service and how kind we were treated was amazing. These guys know how to take good care of their customers.

Soccer Fans - American Outlaws Tucson Chapter location for watch parties.  For the USA-Curacao Gold Cup semifinal. There was a light crowd. Normal bar service and prices, the waiters was present but not pesky, came by about every 20 minutes to ask, and security quickly and respectfully attended to a situation, I think it was resolved before most patrons were even aware.  Top marks.

Great cup of coffee! Comparable in price to Starbucks. Superior in flavor when compared to a black cup of coffee at Starbucks... sorry Starbucks. Coffees we had were bright with nice acidity and great aroma.
Loved the atmosphere and decor,  amazing service and friendly staff.  Great delicious food in a fun environment.

The food here is okay but we typically eat in about once or twice a month because we live down the street and it's super convenient. Yesterday we went to the take out window and ordered a shake and cup of water. 40 minutes later I returned to the window and asked for a refund (they obliged). The girl--who spent five of those 40 minutes greeting my dog and another puppy in the area with the benches--seemed surprised that I hadn't received my shake. In some ways I blame myself as I am all too familiar with their poor service, particularly at the window. 40 mins for a shake is next level incompetence/disregard though. I am never returning. Metro Diner on Kennedy is infinitely better.

Who doesn't love a good grilled cheese! These guys are doing it right. My BF was in 7th heaven with the MacDaddy...come on Mac & cheese and grilled cheese. He just missed the loaded tots as the people before us got the last order but they were so sweet and hooked us up with some extra original and some chipotle dip to try. I went fancy Caprese and paired it with the pesto dip. Oh. My. Word. Needless to say we're fans.
Very authentic, fresh, yummy and tasty Chinese family tradition dishes. Staffs are professional and polite.

As a complete newbie to yoga, I can say that Moksha is definitely a great place to get started. The studio is very welcoming, and there is no feeling of judgement towards a newbie. So if you are considering starting yoga, specifically hot, this is a great place.

The intro membership at $50 for one unlimited month is a great option. They also have $5 Karma classes on Friday evenings that might be a good way to wet your toes a bit, but honestly go to for the $50 one because you shouldn't just try it once. Different instructors have different styles of teaching, and there are also different types of classes which the website gives you a good description of. I have tried the Yin Yoga class and Moksha Yoga, and both are great in their own way. 

I can honestly say, after only one week of practicing yoga, it really is a zen experience. My body just feels so calm after. And it's still a great workout, as long as you really listen and give each pose your all!

Some tips for newbies are:
- the MindBody app is great to see class schedule.
- You can buy your passes online and even register online (although registering for a class is not necessary). 
- Arrive 30 minutes before to lay down your mat and you can either stay in the room and acclimatize or wander out and come back. 
- Bring a towel for your mat, and a towel for your face. If you don't have a mat, rent one for $2.
- Keep your valuables at home as there isn't much for lockers, although if you really need, they do have tiny little ones for a cell phone/wallet.
- Drink plenty of water the day you are going, and bring into the studio with you.
- Remember, you can always lay down, or even leave the room, if you need to. 
- You will sweat buckets. 
- Just listen to the instructor's voice.

Good local feeling place, friendly staff and good bar food. Had the wings. I would rate them a 7 out of ten. I'm a business traveler and this is close to hotels in Birchwood area. I would recommend for a casual meal.
I've been here a couple of times, once for dinner and once for the lunch buffet and the food has been pretty good. Biriyani is excellent! A little pricy but I would recommend.

The pulled pork was good. Unfortunately, they were out of brisket ( a main feature on the menu). after we ordered they informed us that they were also out of baked beans. My meal was average at best - very disappointed.

I'm not sure what they think makes this place a reasonable substitute for a NY establishment. Lox? Matzoh ball soup? The atmosphere doesn't scream Nashville, but it doesn't shout New York either.  I went in on a Sunday afternoon, post brunch crowd and the overall feeling was, in a word, sleepy.

Delis and diners aren't quite the same thing and Noshville strives to be both. For decor, you have the diner aspect, vinyl booths, wraparound counter, fluorescent lighting. For deli, you have the menu with plenty of sandwiches, bagels and smoked fish, and a bowl of pickles on the table--the one I sampled could have used a little more snap.

I started with a salad, which was very ordinary for the price. Iceberg lettuce, a shave of carrot, two rings of red onion and a slice of cucumber. The rosemary citrus dressing sounded tastier than it was, I found it bland and lacking more than a hint of rosemary.

The scrambled eggs with smoked salmon and onions was a tasty dish, though if I order it again, I think I'll get a side of capers. The potatoes that came with it were deliciously crunchy and were easily the best part of my meal. 

I was disappointed in the lemon meringue pie I ordered for dessert. The filling was way too sweet, the meringue was skimpy and half of it was covered in coconut, which I don't like, so I ended up leaving most of it. Half a star for having several tea options--three flavors from Tazo, so my dessert was not a total loss.

In short, the meal was good, but nothing knocked me over with greatness. The service was very good, but the prices were a little high for what was delivered. I'd go back, but I don't see Noshville becoming  one of my favorites. I would, however, take NY friends here for a good giggle.

What a great pizza place to come to Sparks Nevada! It has excellent service and the pizza is made fresh. I truly appreciate the quality of the ingredients, how fresh everything is prepared and the simplicity of the menu. This one rates one of the highest pizza pies that I have tried. It is thin crust pizza, so the style is a bit different but it all works out. All the salads that we have tried are excellent, made with fresh ingredients and perfectly dressed. This place is going to be one of the top places we think of when we are in the mood for some good pizza. Definitely a must try!!

Patricia P.

I rent a condo through Western Nevada Management and I've had a great experience living here. The property manager Jodi is one of the best I've ever dealt with. If I ever have a situation that needs to be taken care of, she gets it taken care of right away.

Imagine this, you ask for Togo silverware when you place your order for delivery, they say okay. When they deliver your food, you find no silverware in the bag. You call up the place and the lady says "you have to remind me" what?!? How am I supposed to do that? Call back 10 minutes later and say "hey I just wanted to remind you to put silverware in the order I placed 10 minutes ago. Anyway the food is alright. There are better places that don't forget your silverware.

Any place that can make my ugly ass pretty is amazing. the customer service is top notch. Everyone is super friendly and fun.
They are masters of their craft. Seriously, I look like a supermodel because of them right now.
I blame them completely. 
Highly recommended!

My wife's first mother day dinner! Place is nice very rustic! Parking is sufficient. Our server was absolutely horrible not a single smile through out the service, slow service! Food was good would have definitely gone back had it not been for the server!

Unlike the experience I had at a local Labcorp office the day before, this office visit was about as good as it gets.  I was called up almost immediately after I signed in, waited only a few minutes to get my blood drawn and was on my way.  Both the receptionist and the nurse who drew my blood were very pleasant, smiling as they did their work and actually seemed to enjoy doing their job!  Thanks to both of them for great service

Place is very nice. Good for conversation.  I ordered the Herbie burger mentioned that i like medium but last time i was there it was way under cooked.  This time it was burnt as you can see in the pic. Wish they would get it correct

I love this place. We went to the Cirque Dreams Holidaze show. This is our second time going to this show. It was amazing once again. We will definitely check it out again next time we are in Nashville!

My sister and I came here for dinner and drinks while on vacation last week. We honestly came for the sunset view, but the drinks and food ended up being fantastic. Although a bit of a splurge for dinner; the nachos and blueberry mojito were to die for. Our service was friendly and quick! We will definitely return once we come back into town. We have already recommended our relatives who live in Clearwater to check this place out for dinner!

I visited main line optical this morning with a pair of eye glasses I purchased at another optician. My glasses have not fit properly since purchasing them in January of this year, even after several attempts to fix the problem at the other optician.  Richard made every effort to adjust the glasses to my face and said if they still do not fit properly, to come back.  He could not have been nicer or more helpful. I cannot remember the last time I have been treated so attentively.  Based on his expertise and service, I will be back - not only to adjust my current glasses, but to purchase my next pair.

Terrible customer service skills from every employee here. I was trying to complain to them before i wrote this review and complained to corporate. Unfortunately, they didn't want to hear anything I had to say. When I told them I had an issue with the service here and the fact that my key didn't work at 1 am, she cut me off and asked me could I get in now? So I started again, and then she started speaking over me again talking about maintenance issues. Never once apologizing.  

The main problem was that when we checked in at 1am, The keys didn't work. They turned red as if we re in the wrong room. After I walk all the way back to the front desk, she gave me another key and and acted as if it was my fault. She also gave me a master key . Only the master key worked.

The fish is terrible. I understand not every chef knows how to cook fish but if that's the case you shouldn't serve fish. When we got here, we asked our server if the fish is fresh, he assured us that it was and it had arrived yesterday. When we got the fish, it was very soggy and mushy on one side. Also one side was warmer than the other side. We complained to the server and again he assured us it was fresh. He showed us the receipt showing the fish had arrived yesterday. If he is telling us the truth, then I have to assume that the fish is prepared ahead of time and microwaved for customers or the chef has no idea how to cook fish. I can prepare fish better than the chef and at a lower cost. Also most of the food was average. For the cost I think they can do better.

You know urban sprawl isn't the worst thing in the world.....it's may be ruining the character of small towns, destroying wildlife habitat, and altering the landscape, but screw it....let's look at the positives: Who actually wants to drive another ten minutes out of their way to spend your hard earned bucks? Convenience is the downfall of America and I'm buying right it......
So I bet you spent the last paragraph wondering why I'm preaching about B/S instead of talking about fresh baked rolls, and you're right to wonder. The answer: I'm an arrogant jerk off. Now for the review:
So They developed a large track of land by the AC expressway in Sicklerville and the economic downturn has kept it largely vacant, but Bertucci's and Texas Roadhouse have opened. I was a little stoked because of all the Italian chains I generally find Bertucci's the least offensive to my heritage. My girlfriend and I used to make the drive to Marlton, but now we're almost walking distance........almost as in if all the cars, taxis and buses exploded I could walk there. They're not going to kick anyone Mom Mom out of the kitchen, but they throw together some decent dishes. This past Friday my g/f and I made a pre-movie trip. I kind of wish I hadn't....it definitely cost them a 4 star review.   I've never had the Chicken Marsala before...I consider that a pretty basic dish. I usually go with a more interesting menu item, but I diverted from the game plan and paid the price.  My confusion/dismay started when the dish came out. The penne was the first sign. It was supposed to have Marsala sauce, but appeared dry. When I told the waitress that she told me it was at the bottom, but she'd get me more. So while I waited for the sauce I dove into the chicken. The chicken itself was prepared well and very tender. The Marsala sauce on the other hand was terrible. Bertucci's is chain so I assume the sauces come prepackaged and they just reheat them. It appeared to me the sauced wasn't reduced, nor did it have any Marsala flavor....just a brown watery mess. When the waitress brought the side of sauce it was the same mess. Dinner did not impress me one bit.  I started with a Cesar salad which was enjoyable. It was a little dressing heavy, but I will take heavy dressing over not enough any day.  I should also mention the fresh baked rolls. I'm sure by fresh baked that means they took them out of the freezer and put them on a baking sheet, but they're still warm and delicious. I'll mop a plate of olive oil with one any day.
A couple of positives: we showed up 8:30ish pm on a Friday and were seated within 5 minutes. Our server was very attentive, almost to the point of annoyance, but she took care of us and was always close. We were shooting for a 9:55 movie and I was nervous about making it, but we were out of there before 9:30. 
I do like Bertucci's and hopefully next time around I can drop the 4 star on them.

If I could give a zero star review I would. I am on the last month of my second 1 year lease agreement. In my first year, these apartments were great, our previous property manager took pride in these buildings, they were well maintained, clean and we saw regular parking lot maintenance winter and summer. However for my second 1 year term we have acquired a new property manager. (Ramona, Cortez Manor/Terrace Fort Sask) it is actually amazing her incompetence, we are sitting here after the SECOND major snowfall since the beginning of this winter everyone's vehicles are getting stuck and we have yet to receive a lot cleaning notice. The hallways are ALWAYS dirty the outside doors only lock about half of the time meaning these buildings are not secure what so ever. If you try to bring up any concerns at all
to Ramona she either cry's and says she's 'overwhelmed' or says her 'English isn't good and she doesn't understand' 

This is not my first complaint about Ramona. I have taken these concerns to Mayfield head office in Edmonton and spoke to Eileen, Ramona's boss on Dec 17 2018. There clearly hasn't been any reprimand because these buildings still have the same issues almost a month later. 

1. Dirty hallways, entrances 
2. Burnt out lights in stair wells and hallways. 
3. Zero snow removal in the parking lot. 
4. Outside doors don't lock properly 
5. Emergancy lights in stairwells are unplugged presumably because they don't work. 
6. Maintenance requests don't get filled in a timely manor. (Ex; my mail box was broken for so long the post office charged me a fee for not being able to deliver for weeks, my neighbour moved in with promise her cupboard doors would be fixed, the still haven't) 
7. When maintenance is performed she provides contracted workers with keys to the units and does not supervise the work. (Tenants sink backed up and flooded, plumber used a key to enter the apartment Unsupervised, Ramona wasn't even on the property)  

Watch out for these buildings in Fort Sask. as I have exhausted all resources trying to get regular maintenance or a new property manager and all has fallen on deaf ears. 
Cortez Manor Cortez Terrace Fort Sask 
210 & 214 Westpark Drive Fort Sask.

This is probably the worst Mexican food I've had. No flavor of any kind. I ordered the grilled fish
Tacos. The should have been advertised as cabbage and sour cream tacos on cold flour tortillas with bland beans and rice. I had to search for the fish. 

They charge for chips but tell you about their salsa bar. However they will not give you a small bowl for the salsa. They were out of their tiny cups so they has 2ounce plastic to go cups - they said they would not let us have a small bowl for our table of 4 to share because "they didn't want the customers to over do it". Really?  They were washing the normal 1-2 ounce containers....

Taco Bell would have been gourmet compared to the food here. 

GCR - you can do better. Where is your quality control? This was a huge waste of $125.

From the minute you walk into this little quaint bookstore you feel right at home. It's very neat and tidy the books are lined up The walls are  a beautiful color with some hand painted trees. The owner seems to have curated a wonderful collection of books. Even though I don't live in the area if I did I would be here every day

This is a special occasion place. On the river, watching planes come in at the Philly airport and beautiful view. It's a great venue also. We celebrated my gfs bday. We had a lovely, wonderful time. It was busy, we had drinks at the uncrowded bar then ask for our table and we were sat. Everyone's food was great, not wonderful for the little fortune we pay. I think they should get rid of the obnoxiously huge menu....and have a much smaller menu so the chef and concentrate on the important little things that count. Like: I had the appetizer muscles, sause was not good. Needed a lot of garlic! 
But over all, if your not a LITTLE picky, like me sorry, you will live this wonderful place! I did give 4 ****

Stumbled upon this gem of a bakery that offers amazing cakes, cookies, brownies among other delicious treats. We are sharing a very lemony lemon bar and a chocolate cupcake with buttercream icing and terrific coffee! 2 thumbs up!

We went there tonight for the first time. I hear great things... But when we got there a little after eight for dinner they said they were going to be out of food by time we sit down. We had to walk right out of the bar. Hopefully we will try again.... I'm just confused as to how a restaurant turns you away bc they ran out of food at 8pm on a Saturday.

We were in Santa Barbara for the day and choose Moby Dicks - big mistake.  We all had he fish and chips and if we weren't in a hurry would have sent it back.  The fish tasted as if it had been cooked earlier and had been reheated.  Very chewy and rubbery.  Would not recommend this place

I have eaten here twice since it moved into our building, and I am very disappointed by the quality of their food, especially for the prices they charge.  I have ordered the grilled cheese sandwich and the bacon, lettuce, and tomato sandwich.  Both sandwiches had very little content.  The cheese sandwich had a small glob of goat cheese in the middle, with most of the bread uncovered by any type of sandwich filling.  The same was true of the bacon and tomato sandwich.  There were about 3 half slices of bacon in the middle, two small tomato slices that were the hard tomato core.  I tore off most of the bread from the middle portion.  Also, the outer crust of the bread was tough as though it had been microwaved.  I'm usually not given to complaints about food, but because the price of their food is high (over $10 for a sandwich), these sandwiches deserve attention.  Very disappointed.

Came in on a Saturday night around 9pm with a friend. Large selection of ice cream (with Covid - no testing of flavors). 2 girls working greeted us as we walked in. 
I got a half scoop of their triple play (chocolate ice cream, brownie pieces and chocolate chips) & a half scoop of mint chocolate chip. My friend got the cookie dough & triple play. $11 for both - I'd say that's average for the "craft" ice cream places.  We both enjoyed our ice cream. 
They also have sundaes and ice cream flights on the menu.

The staff is very nice and the store itself is beautiful. I have ordered two books though and never got a call for either. One of the books I had to call in myself and ask if they had ever gotten it. The other one was supposed to be released the day I went in but they had not got it yet. I placed the order, never got a call and just ended up using amazon to get it. Oh well. I love supporting book stores but if they want to stay in business they have to make a little more of an effort.
It was pretty good, speaking as a Chinese American who has eaten alot of dim sum in her life. The rice "jook" was pretty good with thousand year old egg in it. I will be back.

Don't order a phone through the mail; only take one if they have it in the store. I ordered a new phone (to replace my broken one) over a week ago. FedEx seems to have lost it and Verizon says I need to wait another week(!) without a working phone until it is officially declared lost.

I can't say how much time I have wasted trying to get this resolved.  Everyone at the store is very polite, but suggestions like "don't you want to go to the FedEx facility and pick it up" are not helpful.
i ordered chicken andouille, and the taste was not that good. it kindof tasteless.

This Hilton was a little disappointing because the location is so nice. I was on the sixth floor and my room smelled damp the second I walked in. I went to look at the view from my window and WOW. There was mold all around it! They put me in a room on the 9th floor (no upgrade or complimentary drink like they normally do) and that room was fine. 
My new room was near the elevator so all day and night I heard the elevator pinging which got annoying. Their AC units are loud and make it sound like it's raining.

Very impressed with this place. Cheesesteak was great, good selection of sides and drinks. Overall a great experience. Will definitely be coming back. Wish it was a little more accessible but thats the downside to downtown Philly.

We walked in at 9:00 and the waitress yelled across the room they were not seating any more tables , we drove from ashland city to come to this ihop location , i run a retail store and seeing that place empty and the way we were treated , i wouldnt have a job anymore , the management in this store really needs to be trained in customer service this was unexceptable

Came in with some friends, because we had heard a lot of hype around this place. It had originally opened, closed down, then opened back up again. Being a halal Mexican restaurant, this spot is pretty unique for this area. 

Upon entering around 8PM one night, we were seated promptly by a waitress. The restaurant was fairly empty, and we took some time to look at our menus. The waitress took our orders within a few minutes. She had talked about bringing over some fresh tortilla chips, and she brought them in about 15 minute. They were pretty fresh and hot. We had some friends arrive about 10 minutes late, and it took almost 20 minutes for their orders to be collected.

As the earlier arrives waiter for food, the restaurant owner was very friendly to us and struck up some conversation. I had ordered Steak Fajitas, and once they arrived they looked decent. They came with 3 side tortillas and some toppings. I do feel that toppings were a bit skimpy, and I could have used more. Additionally, they needed about one extra tortilla with the meat. As for the fajita meat quality, it was OK. I've definitely had better quality fajitas, and the meat tasted like something I could make at home. It did lack a bit of flavor. 

After almost 30 minutes, the friends who arrived later were given their food. After we finished eating, the owner was kind enough to give us 2 free desserts, because I was fasting. We received Fried Oreos as well as Churros with ice cream for dessert. They were both pretty good.

Overall, I would probably revisit. I just think customer service speed needs to be improved as well as fajita quality. The others in our group who had ordered fajitas were also not too heavily impressed. I do appreciate the owner's personal contact with his customers, and halal Mexican food can be hard to find.

First off I'd like to start by thanking the team at Kaufman Eye Institute for an amazing experience during my appointments. I had scheduled my appointment to do Lasik eye surgery follow up appointments with Dr. Jonathan Kaufman. 

It was my first time at this location and it was also a very busy day.  I was called back for my appointment 5 mins after checking in. The technicians are wonderful and very sweet! I saw the doctor within 20-30 minutes of my visit.  Dr. Kaufman is one of the best eye doctors I have ever been to! His impeccable knowledge in this field is outstanding. He thoroughly explained everything I needed to know about Lasik and helped me make my decision for the procedure. He is very personable and trustworthy.
Great spot to eat or just hang with friends and have some cocktails while listening to great music.  awesome food and cocktails

The only question is what was worse, the service or the food. A group of us gathered for dinner this past Saturday at 7. We were all on time. The waitress, who did not seem like she wanted to be there, eventually brought water and then bread. Neither were ever refilled, except when water was requested. The Appetizers were average at best, bruschetta out of a can, but the long peppers were good. After several requests to place our our the waitress finally showed up---almost an hour after we arrived. Dinner took for ever, despite the "yours should be the next one out". Other parties had come and gone in the interim. Mine was certainly not worth the wait. The chicken and shrimp with penne was AWFUL. It was like they saved leftover chicken then cooked it until dead, and what must have been frozen small shrimp was also over cooked. Sauce was terrible, but the penne was cooked well.....just nothing to eat with it. I understand the special veal and the scallops were good, everything else was fair to poor. 2 hours and 40 minutes to "dine" on a pretty awful meal with poor service. All of those who complained about service are correct. Not even worth a second try.

I ordered a pizza online to be delivered. Aside from being 20 mins late, which I can deal with bc it's a Friday night, I paid for extra cheese and the pizza barely had any cheese on it at all. A small pizza delivered to the house was $21.00. For that price I expect my pizza to be made exactly as ordered. The only time my pizza seems to be right is when I order 2 slices. Any time I order a whole pizza something is always wrong lately. Don't think I'll be trying Jet's again for a while. There are too many alternatives in the area to have to deal with constant dissatisfaction.
Had damage from hail, and then a week later, an attempted theft of car. Reflections worked quickly, the quality of the work was excellent. We were very pleased with the service they offered.

This is where I get my nails done. Period. I come regularly for manicure appointments with Van, who incidentally, also does an excellent job waxing one's eyebrows. No uneven or surprised face from her. I find that my polish lasts up to a week and I usually take it off because of the grow-out gap. They use high quality basecoat (Orly Rubber) and their polishes are all OPI, China Glaze, and Essie. I'm not a fan of gel polishes in general, but in the past I have gotten them done here and when it chipped/peeled before the guaranteed 2 weeks, they invited me back to have it fixed for free. 

I always ask for my cuticles to be pushed back and not cut, and I have had far fewer hangnails then before. They will actually listen to your requests, even to what TV channel to put on while your nails dry. 

As mentioned elsewhere, what I like best is how clean and hygienic the entire shop is. I don't feel nervous about getting an infection at all.  Every piece of equipment is put in one of those UV-ray toasters that kills germs, and after pedicures, the tubs are immediately scrubbed out. 

I have never gotten my hair done here, but they offer cuts and styling for both men and women. 

Don't forget to get a stamp card! 10 services and then you get one free. She'll even keep it for you in her desk if you go regularly.
The fluorescent green skylights make for an alien atmosphere. Decent food, but a little pricey. Effusive service.

I heard this place was a staple in the area... so, when in Rome. I stopped in for a quick bite and tried their cheesesteak. Service was great, food came out quick. The cheesesteak was huge, had a white cheese and I added fried onions. This staple did not disappoint.

3.5 stars is more accurate. I plan on coming back to try some more menu items but this location is super close to my work so I love coming here to try some Taiwanese food. From the food I have tried, I will say I've had some okay items and some delicious ones, too. It's a quaint and quiet spot, there's no music playing so if you're talking, it's easy for everyone to hear your conversation or if you're slurping noodles, it'll be heard by all patrons. Things that I liked were the Super Spicy Wonton Dry Noodle, fried rice, and lo mein. I also love the miso soup that comes with your meal, so salty and warm and I get a lot of tofu sometimes. However, I did mention that I can handle medium heat and they did not hold back on the chili - I was chugging water and breathing fire - so I would've loved if she took note of my heat preferences. The noodle are nice and soft though and it came with several wontons in it. The chicken lo mein is not your typical dish, it has a hint of a sweet sauce but it's the correct texture and greasiness that I love in lo mein. The fried rice was cooked well, too. My friend ordered potstickers and I am not one for fried potstickers that are crunchy. A little seared but still soft is my go-to. I do love the sesame balls though - I dream of those little things. Super chewy, warm red bean paste, and fried to perfection. $4 for 7 of those delicious little things!

I came here today to get some chicken breasts and turkey ham, the line was very quick, no more than 5 minutes just after the weekday lunch rush.  I got 3 large skinless chicken breasts and 1/2 pound of turkey ham for 14!  The friendly person at the counter sliced the chicken breasts for me because they were very large, and tonight I ate the chicken for dinner, it was delicious!  The chicken was moist, juicy, tender, flavorful and it's free range chicken, I believe!  I will definitely come back here, this is a great poultry shop with friendly service and great chicken!  I'm eager to try the turkey ham tomorrow for lunch.

Came here after some shopping on a Sunday afternoon and the first thing that got my attention was the "fresh" in Doc Bs Fresh Kitchen. I sat at the bar and ordered the Tiger Shrimp Salad with an avocado dressing, feta, peppadew, and red onion served with arugula. Omg this may be the most fresh and delicious salad I've had in a long time. It was a little pricey at $15 but well worth it as there were lots of shrimp in my salad that made every bite delicious and I didn't want it to end. All the flavors paired so nicely and it was very well balanced. I was also surprised by the great happy hour that started at 4pm on Sunday which all of their wines and wells are $3! Can't beat that when all the wines are from $8-$15 a glass! I really enjoyed my experience here. Fresh and delicious food! Service was also excellent!

Super nice staff. Ok it goes downhill from there. As I have German blood, I was disappointed in this restaurant. I heard from friends that this place was great, I think they were talking about the Beer choices. I had the schnitzel, it was too thick, the crust was soggy, and the veal was chewy. It was a cross between chicken and pork. My wife had a dish with three different sausages, bland no flavor. But it seems that tampa residence are satisfied with mediocre german food? Ps the sides were better!

This is an excellent restaurant. I've been here 3 times and got the Mediterranean Wrap each time because its quite awesome. Try it! It's a cozy little restaurant with not much seating. I like this place a lot more than Apollonia which is right down the street from them.

My only complaint is that this place does not accept credit cards and I'm going to get preachy about it right meow because I find that very annoying. I'm a small business owner so I understand them not wanting anything to eat into their margin. I definitely sympathize with that thought. But... did I somehow accidentally time travel back to 1985 when buying lunch with a credit card was a new and novel concept? They do accept checks which is quite rare nowadays. More proof I'm a time traveler. Call me McFly. And if you don't have cash, you can make like a tree, and get outta here. 

Today when I went to Gyro Co I finally asked the cashier why they don't accept plastic and was told exactly what I assumed I would be told... that card transactions are pricey for the business and they want to keep their prices low for customers. I was told that their customers are used to using cash so it's not a problem for them. The cashier is probably correct that their customers are used to having to use cash. That's because they've pushed away the customers that want to use a card so... they are no longer customers.God bless this place and I want them to succeed, but someone needs to get a business degree here. Of course not accepting credit cards is hurting their business. Tons of people don't even carry cash. A couple months ago two of us from my office were about to go there for lunch when we both realized we didn't have cash, so we went somewhere else. Say bye bye to what would have been roughly $15 from two patrons. So, rather than have that sale and give the card processing company their cut of less than 75 cents they missed out on the sale completely. And that was just one occasion with only two people. Gyro Co can't see physically see how their policy is harming their business which is why they have this cash policy, but this archaic mindset is indeed hurting them. Running cards should be considered a necessary cost of doing business and they are making a big mistake by not factoring that cost into their pricing. There is a reason that roughly 100% of their competition is also accepting cards... it's because accepting cards is GOOD for business because customers want to use them. Perhaps they should try to negotiate better pricing with their vendors so that they can run cards and not raise their prices. They're missing out on a ton of business! If they ever do begin to accept cards it will take them a long time to see the benefit of it because they'll need to work hard to get the word out that they've caught up with modern times. If people don't realize the policy has changed it won't do them any good. It's such a simple concept I'm amazed they don't understand how bad their cash policy is. Gyro Co prices are pretty low, and charging roughly 20 to 30 cents more per menu item would cover the cost of card processing, depending on what kind of deal they receive from merchant services. They also don't have a website or online menu further proving their refusal to leave the dark ages.

With all that being said, I will continue to eat here when I have cash on me, especially since its so close to my office. I should probably explore some more of the menu finally!

Poor and rude customer service... Including management who wouldn't even give the courtesy of speaking with us after I specifically requested to speak to management but instead they passed conversation between the front desk clerk and myself. Due to catastrophic flooding and storms that passed through Houston the day of our departure, our flight along with 500 others that day were cancelled. Our hotel reservation for our first night stay at another hotel happily refunded our stay. Hotel Preston (which was booked through Rocket Miles) and stated on there that we could cancel within 24 hours of stay refused a refund. We provided flight cancellation details from our airline and Rocket Miles requested the cancellation more than 24 hours but Hotel Preston still declined a refund. We've stayed there before and planned to stay many more times as we travel to the area often for work. Needless to say our company has decided to remove them from our list of hotels. Let's hope the $120 they made off of that non-refunded night is worth the loss of business that they will no longer receive from our company.

Came here for a drink and was a little disappointed. Their drink specials are way over priced for what they are, and especially because they come in a plastic cup. It's the middle of the day on Monday I don't know why they wouldn't serve things in a regular glass. Also our waitress wasn't very attentive or very welcoming. The only upside to this place was the really awesome musician they had, he made the experience better.

We arrived on a Wednesday night without a reservation. We were told we would have a 25 minute wait but in a short 10 minutes we were seated. The service was a bit slow but the drinks and food were worth the wait. My New York strip was prepared to perfection. I recommend Weber's for your next visit to Indianapolis.

The worst customer service I've ever had. Never return calls. They act like they are doing you a favor. Rude all around. They did a good job onmy fence but left trash and large pieces of cement from my old fence in my property as well as my neighbors. Would not come back to clean up. Then recently a tree fell and broke sections of my fence could not get them to come out to fix it. They had my deposit for 4 weeks. County line fence act like they don't need your business that's fine you will never get it again

Mimi is kind, prompt, caring and truly a gifted stylist. Luce Salon is a welcoming salon with a beautiful quaint patio located on the Mesa. You will leave with beautiful results and a lifted spirit after an appointment with Mimi. Ive worked with her at a salon in the past and she has done my hair many many times and in my humble opinion I think she is one of the best hair stylists in SB. She is such a gem. I love her, and you will too!

If you have small children, this gym will "change your life," as many parents told me.  The kids academy is fantastic. The facility is clean, the employees are happy to be there and kind to the children. The free (most are included With the membership) kids classes are the highlight of the academy. If you are looking for a little time for yourself, the membership is worth it for the kids academy.  You can't leave the facility, but they have several lounge areas where you can sit, if you aren't working out that day. 
Overall, the gym is very nice. 
There are two yoga instructors in particular who are great. 
The marketing after you join is a bit intense, but manageable.

I really like this Starbucks because it is located in the hotel for easy access, and the player card money can be used here to purchase drinks. The workers are always really happy and outgoing when talking to customers.

Buyer be ware. Ashley is a franchise and each store is operated independent. If you decline to have your furniture delivered and go to the distribution center on Green park road to pick up and something happens during the one year warranty for cushion's or frame in order to enforce the warranty you must bring the product back to them in order to have it fixed. What kind of business practice is this? have a warranty but in order to have it fixed you have to bring it to them. But if you pay their delivery fee during the one year they will send their service to you. Scam.

Whoa! Don't judge this place by the store front. It should be a Diners Drive-Ins and Dives feature. Absolutely loved it! Jambalaya, Seafood Gumbo, Shrimp Scampi and Chicken Jefferson were all phenomenal. Met Chef Ron himself. My wife has celiacs and they were great with GF options. My son said this is a definite stop next time he's in New Orleans.

Indy needed a restaurant that specializes in Mac & cheese.  I placed an online order for their Philly Mac & Cheese.  Huge portion!  Very creamy and tasty.  They opened recently and I urge everyone to give them a try.

Had a wonderful classic pedicure done this evening with the lovely Marjorie. She's beautiful, professional and a delightful conversationalist. She was patient with me for my selection of colour. I can never make decisions so I really appreciate this factor. I try and be courteous but there is always so many great colours. Hahah She also gave me the best tip ever on another amazing coworker there, who does other services that I cannot wait to make an appointment with based on the recommendation. These little tidbits always play big on my experience. 

I added the shellac gel to my pedicure as I am going on vacation and like to keep it at its best for these occasions. This was an extra $15 fee. I feel the price is pretty standard of a higher end salon and spa with the total running around the $80 mark. 

All around great experience. Called and fit me in that same day as I was travelling and needed a quick but nice pampered pedicure before leaving again. I will definitely be returning to see both Marjorie and a fellow coworker. 

Thanks, Icon!

I am a massive Patsy Cline fan, so this was #1 on my Nashville stop. 

I got nervous when I walked in and saw so many people for the Johnny Cash area, but once I went upstairs- I felt better. 

I truly enjoyed the detail and personalization of the museum. I learned things I haven't seen previously. 

If you're a fan, this is a must see.
Great food. Great service. Great atmosphere. I love this place. I think it is very authentic and makes me feel like I have stepped into another city. It is a hidden gem.
Pretty good. Deep fried pork bacon was tasteless to me and plum salt is average. Salted fish chicken fried rice is my favorite. Pork belly pao is good also. The waiter is very nice and friendly.

I have not yet tried their ramen but their chicken katsudon was delicious! Went here on a Monday night (MLK day) and it was busy. Had many people waiting to get seated. The way they handle the crowd was very professional and efficient. Would have to come back here and try their curry udon noodles.
Luckily got the best bargain rum bucket on the island with the amazing Cruzan rums. Bartenders were friendly. No food service Sunday night due to rain which was a bummer.

This is my new favorite pub in the West Chester area. Super pizzas, burgers, steaks, duck, wings, divine crab cakes,  excellent lobster rolls and much, much else that's yummy, plenty of free parking and a smart selection of craft brews on draft. Service is friendly and prompt. Seating is comfy. Architecture beautiful. What's not to like?

We stopped by Tomasello's on our way to Atlantic City.  Tastings were 5 for $5 and you got to keep the glass.  In addition, if you bring the glass back again for another visit, you continue to get 5 free tastings.  The setting was gorgeous--it had a hall used for weddings and events connected by a beautiful patio setting to the tasting room.  Our pourer was a very nice older man.  He was very knowledgeable about the wine.  He also was nice enough to give us huge pours because we chose 10 different wines to share.  There was a HUGE selection of wine--the paper that they give you for the tastings is almost overwhelming!!  As per usual, we usually try the reds and sweets.  Most of the reds were delicious--especially the Sangiovese which we purchased.  We also purchased the  American Cape May Red--a very light table wine which was only available in the 1.5 L variety.  We also enjoyed a few of the semi-sweets--but many of the sweets were overly sweet for us.  We purchased the American Ranier Rosé (also in a 1.5L)--which was sweet, but had a nice cut to it.  The blueberry-pomegranate was definitely interesting as the pomegranate cut some of the sweetness of the blueberry.  The wines were very fairly priced.  We will definitely be back with our glasses!!

Lunch buffet for $7.95? Sold!

It was a crappy Saturday afternoon & we wanted something fast but tasty, so when we passed by the restaurant, we made a u-turn & headed in.  The buffet was simple, only six or seven main dishes, but everything was delicious. I love raita, the yogurty lemony sauce that I generously topped on everything. The curry was good, but the standout dishes were the chicken tikka and masala, plus the fragrant rice.

Other than the grumpy staff, we enjoyed the quick meal and will certainly return.

I take my boys here to get haircuts. I highly recommend Kelsey or Sam. Both have done a great job with my boys and always check with me to make sure I like the cut before finishing up. It is best to make an appointment. I typically call the day before. As stated in other reviews, find someone you like and request them each time. The boys have gotten a few jacked up cuts from other stylists. Now we only go to the girls I know do a nice job.

I stopped in here before the Yelp Elite event recently and I'm glad I did.  The place has a very hipster vibe and seeing as how I'm not very hipster I did feel a little out of place at first, but once the smells from the kitchen hit me, I didn't care.  

I had the cochinita pibil quesadillas, which were three small quesadillas with cheese and the pork.  They were deliciously cheesy and the pork was tender and spiced perfectly.  I'm glad there were only 3 to an order because I was full when I was done, but I think I would have kept eating more if they were in front of me.  I tried a little bite of the black beans and they were spicier than I thought beans should be, but tasty nonetheless.  I kept attacking the chips bowl too which were still warm and salted perfectly.  There were a bucket of sauces on our table, but nobody told us which sauce was what so we had to just kind of taste test them.  Thankfully none of them were mouth-scorchingly spicy.  

Definitely a great meal and I'd love to get back here soon for happy hour!

This pizza is tasty, thin, and sliced large.  Of course at over $4 a slice with no additional toppings added, it better be!  There were four different options for slices on the given night I visited and at first glance, they all certainly looked great.  There didn't seem to be options to customize further.  I asked for an original to go, it was heated up quickly and I was on my way.

First impression, this slice is very, very thin.  While I appreciated the crusty-ness of the slice, there was no body or chew to the dough, which is not what I like.  The ratio of cheese and sauce was good, and there was a sprinkle of fresh parm on top.  

I'll be back to try other slices.

We went there yesterday for lunch. There is a nice cross breeze through the restaurant, compared to most restaurants in town. The cupcakes are very good. My huevos rancheros, however, was almost cold, but I was hungry so didn't send it back. Also, the portion size was small for the price. My husband turkey sandwich was very good.

I went here  on Christmas Eve with my husband and mother in law. This was there 3rd time going so, some comments made are also based on their experiences.

First of all, the outside is absolutely stunning, they had small bonfires lit leading up the mountain to the restaurant that was super cool...the view and landscaping at the top made me feel rich and fancy..and then walking in - Holy shit, it's amazing.  Being from the midwest, perhaps I have not been in a lot of Hacienda style places, but every detail was to perfection.

The food.  Well, my husband and I do eat seafood but don't eat other meats such as chicken, beef, etc.  Though there were 3 fish dishes on the menu, only one was only seafood.  That was really disappointing, as my husband had to get the risotto, which ended up ...  well adequate.   

My mother in law had the duck, which she said was really chewy and just okay.

I had the swordfish which was really awesome.  The pureed  cauliflower kind of made the dish.

The wine list is intense, and though it's really impressive to have a catalog of wine, it would be nice if it were organized somewhat better, or even had a featured wine list of the night.  

The service was okay,  but as my husband, whom is a waiter at a fine dining restaurant pointed out, the food runners had their thumbs all over the inside of every dish.  yuck.  

The desert was pretty good, but the espresso was super sour tasting.

I was so impressed with atmosphere that it overshadowed the mediocreness of the food.  But in the end..it was really expensive.  The night before at Wildflower the food blew me away, and was much less.  They also said that the menu seems pretty much the same as it has the last 3 years on Christmas Eve.  Unfortunately, it sounds as if the tradition will be broken next year, as they will be looking for a new place for their special holiday dinner.  Both my husband and mother in law are pretty intense foodies, and I think really wanted to be blown away by the food, but it just doesn't cut it.
Friendly greetings, nice staffs, great food with good portion. So worth it for the price!!! I love coming here at least once every 2 weeks for lunch.
Most authentic Szechwan in Tucson. The execution and ingredient quality were a cut above the usual too. Definitely.

Kim has done my Botox twice. I am extremely pleased with the results and will be back for more.
In addition I have had two Clearlift treatments and am already seeing the fine lines diminish.
There is absolutely no pain and no downtime. It is a warm heat on your face. My skin was slightly red,but,not enough to be noticeable to others. I was able to go right back to work after the treatment. I cannot wait to see how my skin looks after my nest treatment.
As for the office,it is very clean,decorated beautifully and provides a very relaxing feel.
I highly recommend!

Went on a Tuesday to the newer outside bar, bartenders were attentive, and they had good food specials such as 10 wings for $5 bucks.  The wings were not small and they had a variety of sauce choices.  Overall it was a good experience and I will be back.  The TV set-up in the new section is a huge improvement from what was in the older section.  There isn't a spot in the new section where a TV isn't viewable.

I re-found Jason's Deli for lunch today and I am sorry that I forgot how great it is. I ordered online and had the food delivered right to my office! No traffic or parking nightmares encountered. The food was delivered promptly and they even called later to ensure that I enjoyed my experience. I highly recommend them for great fresh food with wonderful service... The only thing missing was the free ice cream, but I am sure that it would have been a messy delivery! ;-)

Its fried and its yummy....their chicken is good...fries are okay....can't really mess those ups. I got their fried fish - really bland and tasteless. Was not impressed by this. My sis in law got their fried chicken in sweet chili sauce. She liked em though will get the sauce on the side in the future. 

I'll be back to give it another try.

The food is fresh, fast, and delicious, just like a food truck should be. I had black bean patties on top of tostadas topped with a fresh salsa. My friend got a sort of chicken wrap with a bunch of goodies inside with sriracha. Both of us were very happy with our orders

For the price and service, this place was not worth it.  Would not recommend this location. Food felt like it was re-heated and not fresh. Half of our food was cold.
If you read this review and still go, don't say I didn't warn you.

I am a big believer of membership in credit unions. I have never had an account at a bank.

Trumark Credit Union was formerly Philadelphia Telco Credit Union (a spinoff from Bell Telephone).   In person, I have dealt with their Center City and their Wayne branches.  Nothing but nice people work for Trumark.  I think that customer service is a priority and a significant part of their training.

Their main office is in Feasterville, and I have had to call and/or email with a question or problem with my account.  Trumark is quick to respond and provide you with a complete answer to your question.  

I have also worked with staff from Feasterville on loan processing and Trumark staff do not follow the "banker's hours" style.  They communicated via email, phone call and the entire process from application to closing was simple and problem-free. 

Trumark's requirements for membership are rather vast: live, work, worship, or go to school in the 5-county SE PA area, and you can open an account.  Their rates are some of the lowest I've found for loans and you aren't nickeled-and-dimed with fees for check writing or online teller services.

Ordered takeout, and they gave us fries that were so undercooked that they fell apart when you tried to pick them up. I couldn't even call the fries soggy, they were literally dripping. They also gave us about two bites of mac and cheese (the already small container was maybe 1/4 of the way full) and messed up one of our items.

Honestly I used to love this place.  I was there since day one.  Opening day and every year after for their anniversary.  I referred all my friends and even people I didn't know claiming this was the best pizza I've ever had.  I travel about 45minutes just to eat here. I'm a huge pizza lover, so I'd like to think I know my pizzas.  They used fresh ingredients, even catered to your health style.  They made special pies too.  The owner worked long, hard hours to get his business going and he made it.  But ever since he stopped going in every day and hired other people to work for him, I feel they've lost what was important to him.  His vision in a different kind of pizza.  Unlike all the rest.  He actually put his time and care into making something enjoyable for everyone.  Now their pizza is nothing but burnt crisp.  I try to give them chance after chance, I'm on my15th chance with them by now.   Each time I go gets worse.  Nothing but hard burnt pizza.  It seems like they don't clean their ovens because every time my pie or slice comes out with burnt ash from whatever was left in their that day or week.  They've stopped taking care of their pizza and seem to only be popular by their name.  Please go back to your original style of the pizza I used to love and want to eat every day. I probably won't be back any time soon.
Not as good as miss Saigon's broth :/ chicken was gameier. Service was better. Clean interior.
Courteous, quick, punctual, and reasonable! Great communication. Highly recommend. thanks Guys!

Excellent. Authentic Taiwanese cuisine. I asked for bitter melon and was told that that is not something on the printed menu but they would check to see if they had some. Unfortunately they were out of it. But the waitress asked if I like authentic Chinese dishes. Very attentive service. "Yes! Of course" was my reply. So she recommended sauteed pork with fresh hot peppers. "A little spicy" I was warned. But that's right up my alley. It was (well, actually more than "a little"), and it was great. Also tried the Crystal wontons after reading so many reviews. Also great.

This place succccccks! Especially if u get Will. He sucks the life out of people and acts like you are trying to win a competition and need safety goggles or something.
