God forbid u walk u for the rope. Bury the Hatchet has always been fun. We didn't even want to finish our round here. The only reason we did is because we were with family visiting from out of town.

I love this place..  I love their chunky fried dumplings, sesame balls, n more... never disappointed when I go there...  you even call them in advance if you have a  party , they can even make extra fresh bakery for u!

Modo Mio, you are amazing!  This restaurant has set a new standard for me for Italian dining (and perhaps for 5 star yelp reviews).  The turista tasting menu is the way to go.  And with 4 delicious courses for $33, you would be hard pressed to find a better deal in this city.  Highlights of my meal included the bucatini (thick, hollow spaghetti noodles) with pancetta, and the lamb.  The lamb was amazingly juicy and succulent!  As great as the first three courses were, the best part was dessert.  I have a dairy allergy, so none of the dessert options were an option for me, so they brought me a poached pear with a sweet and tart sauce (cranberry, I think) and walnuts.  In addition to this dessert being unique and tasty, I was just so happy to actually be able to eat dessert!  (Dessert was also accompanied by some complimentary sambuca.)  

You can expect some of the normal BYOB qualities:  It is crowded and loud, they only take cash, and you usually need  reservation.  But unlike many BYOs, at no point did I feel as thought they were trying to rush us along.  I had adequate time to enjoy my meal and service was top notch!  I would highly recommend Modo Mio.  Just be sure to make a reservation and eat a small lunch that day.  You will get a lot of food!

We came in Saturday after dinner and felt like we were a nuisance to the girl working.  There were very few cupcakes left, and they were still open for 1.5 hours longer, but she wanted to be out of there and was not friendly.  To the owners - hire people who are grateful to represent you and who want to be there.  Such a negative experience we won't be back.

I've used H&H for almost two years. They replaced my heater/air conditioner and they've done a few maintenance checks. All of their techs are amazing (especially Brian!) and super friendly. They're very outgoing, explain what they're doing and keep you in the loop. Highly recommended.

Have been wating for a Metro Diner to open on the west side - did not disappoint!  Had chicken and waffles, and a couple of desserts to go.  Food was great - and the service was fantastic!  Will definitely be back!

This is the locatiion of my very last first date. CBP is good. Sunken field, good beer selection and helpful staff. Very romantic.

My favorite seats are the VERY last row up in the 400 section on the first base line whet it's hot. If you're lucky enough to get that last row, not only can you see the entire field, but you also get a bitching breeze coming off the river.

First game? Swing by guest services to get a certificate signed by the skipper.

Now two kids deep havent seen much baseball since our little birds like the playground in the Phanatic Zone. I also hear hot dogs in tat section are ALWAYS $1. I keep forgetting to test this theory. Check it out and hollar at me.

This place is alright.  They have plenty have staff so you really dont need to wait long if you need to get your nails done.  

Let me clarify that statement above. My friend and I coordinate our schedule that we get there as soon as they open.  Thats why we dont have to wait.  I came here once on a Friday afternoon, and waited 30-40 mins to get my nails done.  

Its location is perfect on 4th St, and close to my house.  

I like the nails they do, but I have not been impressed with the pedicures.  They are always packed, but I've had 2 pedicures here, and they do a quick "scrape scrape" on your feet and its over.  

Soooo....I come here for my nails when I want a quick in and out service or hang out with my girlfriend.  

****Prices here are about an average of $7 higher than most salons


Update - came her for a quick lip & eyebrow wax.
Holy smokes the wax was too hot and I actually let out a scream when she put the wax on me. 
Never, ever, ever will I come here for a wax.
I would rather have caterpillar eyes. 
The woman actually had the balls to ask me if I needed a bikini wax while I was here. 
Yeah....NO!!! Hell no!

Wonderful Chinese food when I arrived I only came in as it was right near the bus stop. The decor is not the reason to come here,it's the food. The hot and sour soup is one of the best, and could easily be registered as the cure for the common cold. Lunch special was filling and since I had a bad cold on my visit I was quite thankful for that soup. The sheer numbers of local business people coming here for food underlined it's quality

Nothing but a positive experience! Usually car buying is a stressful experience full of pushy sales people trying to get you into the most expensive car on the lot, but that isn't the case with carmax! Wonderful and helpful staff that are truly interested in getting you into the best car for your needs. I didn't end up buying a car from them this time, but I will certainly remember the pleasant experience I had while working with them. I wouldn't hesitate to send friends and family to their dealership!

The only reason things took time, was because I said I wasn't in a rush. And honestly , I'm still not. But I know they have a lot of work to do, so I brought my van home.

Bernie and his staff have been very very helpful!!! My dad's 1988 Ford van has been sitting for a long time. It has 60,000 ORIGINAL miles, so I want to keep it. I want to live the van life. But I know this van needs help. I refused to take it to the mechanic my dad had because I think they were a joke! So I found Nevada Auto Diagnostics! And I am pleased with them!

There is a new battery and starter got a boost, but Bernie informed me what is a deeper problem. And he gave me a quote that is pretty close to what I figured.

Whether I keep it or not, I trust Bernie and his staff with all my vehicles, from now on!

I had the corned beef hash omelette and it was over cooked.  Had a brown scale on the outside.  The hash browns​ were soaked in greese; if you like that this is the spot. My wife had scrambled eggs and they were good. Service was curt and didn't keep the water or coffee full; had to request it.  There are much better places to choose from in the area.

Today we showed up for an appointment that we made weeks ago and even had an appointment card with today's date and time.  Apparently, they screwed up and deleted the appointment somehow.  They refused to see us.

The "girl" at the counter was both defensive and had attitude when I asked why no one was answering the phone.  She said she was "busy" and wouldn't keep people on hold for 5 minutes. I said ok and asked if I should just leave my order on the answering machine and she said no it was better to just come in.   

Considering I work in the area and do not have a long lunch break, this wasn't great on the customer service side and may cause some people to go elsewhere.   

That having been said, the place was nice and well maintained. There were about 8 other people in the place and the food was very good.   I will come back as long as I have the time.

My rating would have been higher but the wait time was a little long and the desk left me in an off mood.
Great little neighborhood bar/grill.  Eat here often, the food isn't anything fancy but very well done.  Burgers are good, wings top notch and decent beer selection.

Mediocre at best.  The burger was okay, nothing special. The fries were put in the bag upside down and they weren't even that great.  I should have wasted my calories at Burger King.  At least they carry Coca-Cola products.

Worst Chinese food we've ever had. My boyfriend and I tried this place for the first time yesterday. Everything we ordered was horrible. The wonton soup had a sour taste, the chicken in my chow mein was fake and rubbery. Crab Rangoon's were sub par and my boyfriends general tsos was horrible. Even the rice was bad- dry and tasteless. We ended up sick after eating it too. I never write reviews but I do read them. I feel misled by all the positive reviews so I felt compelled to write my own. Try this place at your own risk.
Great authentic Thai food! Big portions and great prices! Recommend the chicken pad thai and papaya salad.

What a cool place to stop by and eat if your in the area or driving through! We got the yellow brick road burger. Macaroni and cheese, nacho cheese, and bacon. It tastes amazing!!!  We also got the beauty school drop out shake! Its definitely a shareable dessert! Was also very good! Our server Lynn was amazing as well! Very friendly and helped us out in deciding what to eat on the menu!!
Don't let the strip mall location drive you away. This spot is solid authentic and affordable. We are coming back.
Good service, good buffalo shrimp, clean environment! Shells seafood restaurant never lets our big family down.And will come back!

No STARS, not the manager or the server expressed concern when I told them that my mac and cheese had chunks of burn pieces, they replace it and the side came the same, BURNED. The manager went ahead to explained that was the way it was cook, signature if Cracker Barrel. Beware nobody cares at this location.

I love this place, i've been coming here since I was a kid.
The burger/fry option is delicious, and for kids they have cute dinosaur chicken nuggets, and everything kids get comes in a retro cardboard car.
Great family place, and you can pick your background noise on the jukebox.

Five Star Painting of Boise can't be beat! Let me tell you why.

Actually,... where to start? Doing a reno and the GC brings a paint company in to bid our exterior paint project. We get a completely ridiculous quote after I'm also told a professional paint job should last 3 to 5 years after asking the painter (one of the owner's of the company) how long I can expect the paint job to last. No joke!

...Next! 

The GC then hires what he claims is a professional painter as his employee. When I saw the minimal effort put in to what was called prepping, I pulled that scope of work from the project and decided we'd take care of the exterior painting when they finished our reno. They never finished! Look for their review in the future:)

Enter Five Star Painting... a home down the street looked like they were getting ready to paint. As I walked past each day, I'd check it out. These two dudes were killing it with the prepping of this home. This house is huge and there was no nook or cranny these guys weren't getting. After a few days, I met the owner of the home and asked whom they were using. I was in complete awe at the prep that was going into this job. I was given Craig Jones' contact info.

I may have called on a Saturday and left a v.m. and got a return call shortly after on the same day. Craig came within a day or two after that initial conversation, looked the job over in detail and printed me a quote out on site. Damn! These guys are efficient with and without the painting. I requested the same two guys that did an astounding job down the street.

Job starts,... btw, the price I was quoted was completely fair for the scope of work involved. Joe and Andy start working at my home and in just two hours their results of prep were superior to the b.s. of two days/two guys our past GC guaranteed me. Needless to say, Joe and Andy kicked ass! I couldn't even believe the prep Joe & Andy did with the 113 year old cedar siding and all the trim of my home. On top of being two of the finest painters I've ever met, they are both as cool as they come. 

If you're thinking of painting your home, pick up the phone and call Craig today. I am easily entertained, but not easily impressed... me being impressed with Five Star Painting is a complete understatement.

My wife and I had our wedding here Saturday night. We were excited to have found a local place reasonably priced for our small family wedding. The night went great up until the bill came. I was charged for 89 dinners when we only had 80 guests. The owner insisted that the kitchen sent out 89, attempted to blame guests for ordering more than one entree, and eventually blamed his own staff. The room was set for 83 people, one guest could not make it and 2 people came for the ceremony but did not have dinner. I tried to explain that seeing as how nobody ate standing up and no extra places were sat, it was completely unreasonable for us to pay for more seats than the room was set for. The owner insisted that the chef said he made 89, so I had to pay for what they cooked. It was incredibly unprofessional when he told his server (who did an outstanding job) that her mistake was costing him money and he would take it out of her tip. He did not actually refund me any of the money. I told him he should not take a dime out of the server's money because he was the owner and it was ultimately his responsibility. He got incredibly defensive and continued to blame his very hard working staff. His manager who told me in person, with the owner present that if we paid cash they would knock off the tax, told me he "never got official permission to do that" and he basically stood by and did nothing to correct the situation. After all was said and done I was overcharged by about 700 dollars. I hope it was worth it for them to make an extra 700 cash and lose a customer and his family for life. I looked forward to spending anniversaries and special occasions there with my wife, but we won't be doing that. I just hope that the staff was not punished by the incredible cheapskate who owns the place. The staff was outstanding and we actually tipped them a little extra on top of the 800 dollar gratuity. The food was also good, so it really is a shame that a wonderful night was jeopardized by the owner's desire to make an extra 700 bucks.

I wrote this review on my way to the airport...in a taxi. I have never gone as far as to join a website to write a review...but I am so angry.

I had a car scheduled days before my trip. I received a voice mail 15/20 minutes before my pick up. The message said  they are running a 1/2 hour behind. Well that's an issue...this isn't a doctors office w good magazines to read. Can't really call my flight and say hey, sorry going to be late. So I called them back in a frantic state. Mr. Casual & confused picked up the phone. I told him my issue.  And after a few awkward sounds he said, "we aren't going to Make it". Was he high? Is this really happening?

The hate and anger I feel towards this company is unexplainable. I am not really the angry type, more so the upset and tearful type. But not today! No apology, no explanation...NOTHING! This car service is garbage...I should have stuck with dial 7. 

Update...I made the flight...and took another car service home. :)

Eating here last night was nice.  The atmosphere is definitely Italian, down to the music being played.  Was quiet when we arrived, but filled up quickly.  It is a small place...cozy.  The wait staff was good, but we waited far too long for our check.  Not a big deal, but worth mentioning.  The food was good, but the sauce had a background taste of too much dried basil, which ruined the wonderfully garlic filled tomato marinara.  I know it was dried basil because I use it in my sauces, as well, however I don't over do it so the taste doesn't overwhelm as this did.  This restaurant claims that everything is made on premises by the 2010 Tucson Iron Chef winner, so that led me to believe it would be all fresh, including the spices.  The salad was good, the veggie soup was also delicious.  The garlic bread was good.  All in all, a pretty good meal, but my hubby and I both decided we prefer Carrabba's (I know, it's a chain, but it IS good), where they use fresh basil in their sauces and we get dipping oil for our freshly baked bread.
I've been doing orange theory for over a year and I love it! It's nice to come to a group class to hold you accountable. It is a very good workout and for people of all shapes and sizes!

If I could give this bar a -10, I would.  This is by far the most overcrowded, hot, noisy, and annoying bar.  Everything is filthy, you can't move inside and you feel like you're packed in like sardines, it smells awful, etc.  There are three stories to this place, and each story is the same thing.  I stayed there for about 10 minutes and the whole time I got bumped into, couldn't move, and couldn't hear any of my friends. Oh, and the drinks are small and overpriced. NOBODY should be spending their time here while in Nashville, to put it nicely.

If you plan on going during the weekend you should make reservations. This place gets booked a week or so in advance.

The food has great flavor. Everything was fresh and juicy. I had the MC burger with mac & cheese. The burger was so filling I had to take the mac & cheese home. 

It's a bit noisy with live music and full tables, but it's an exciting atmosphere.

Be patient w this little gem of a place. Authentic Food from its Italian owners. They bend over backwards to please you. On our visit, they were terribly short staffed. But their heart & desire to please, was so appreciated in our "rush and demand" culture. The food was good, the place adorable. The owners the BEST. Support this place. Don't rush your visit and don't expect Italian like you make at home.

WOW!!!!  What a show!!!  The Grand Ole Opry has been running longer than Forrest Gump and it has more country than a sweet potato pie! I saw that the Steep Canyon Rangers were on the schedule so I bought tickets immediately. The night filled up with other great stars of counrty music, Steel Magnolia, Jim Ed Brown, Dierks Bently and a little girl witha  big voice named Carrie Underwood. The Del McCoury band played for us as well as Connie Smith. I really enjoyed the show from start to finish.

For those of you that haven't been... GO. We are lucky to have the Opry so close to us and they have regular shows so there's no reason not to go. Go now before it's gone!!!

This place was awful. Ordered for delivery. They answered the phone with a simple "hello" I had to ask if this was China Delight. 

The Szechuan beef had no taste at all. This dish should have been spicy but instead was BLAND! The absolute worst Chinese food I have ever had! Can't give zero stars but wish I could.

The National Park Service could really learn a thing or two from the staff at the Old Barracks Museum.  I've been to nearly 50 NPS sites and while at some the site itself or its artifacts are  enough to make it great, at a lot of sites it's the rangers who either make you glad you came or who make you wish that you stayed at home to watch lint collect in your belly button. 

When you come to OBM the first thing that strikes you is that none of occasional NPS lethargy applies here.  Everyone you meet is highly appreciative that you came and the tour that we got from Bob was nothing short of fantastic.

Let me back up for a moment.  

The building was built in 1758 during the French & Indian War as the largest building in Trenton.  Originally there were around twenty rooms for soldiers with each room housing twelve men which equaled around 300 at any given time.  

During the Revolutionary War British & Hessian soldiers used the Barracks along with some residents who wanted to be protected.  After Washington's improbable Trenton raid at the end of 1776 the Americans eventually used the building as a field hospital until the end of the war in 1781.

Eventually the Barracks were divided into individual homes until being bought by the Society of the Daughters of the American Revolution who bought the south section of the building in 1902.  The state of New Jersey bought the north section in 1914 and the middle section was rebuilt to look as it did in 1758 with new corridors, windows, balconies and landscaping.  The Barracks became a State Registered Landmark in 1970, a National Register Landmark in 1971 and a National Historic Landmark in 1972.  A second restoration of the Barracks began in 1985 and ended in 1998 giving you what you see today.

Getting back to the tour.  First you can watch the short movie about Washington's 1776 Christmas Crossing and the battles of Trenton and Princeton.  It is NPS/History Channel (back when they actually showed history pieces especially the good ones with Edward Herrmann narrating) quality and it is necessary watching.  After that you are taken to a few different rooms showing where the rank and file soldiers bunked, the officers side of things and the little hospital-ish area which dealt with how the Army overcame Smallpox.

We had Bob and he was nothing short of a beast.  Both his knowledge of the subject and his delivery of that knowledge rivaled anything I've ever seen at any NPS site, period.  Even if the Barracks weren't such a neat place to visit he made it fantastically interesting from beginning to end which was around 45 minutes.  Also worth noting is the museum on the second floor which is well worth the visit although photography of that area in a no-go.

All in all, especially if you get Bob for your tour the Old Barracks Museum is a winner as it was not only great, but was far better than what I thought it would be. Even though I've read/heard the story of Washington's Crossing many times, it's my favorite and this place helps to retell it perfectly.

If you go:  The Barracks are open Monday-Saturday from 10am-5pm.  Tours start at the top of the hour and the last one obviously starts at 4pm.  Admission is $8 adult, $6 students/seniors and under 5-active military-members are admitted free.  Check their website for various special events that take place through out the year.

Parking is a bit of an SOB if you come during the week when the government is open so come knowing that you'll be parking at least a few blocks away in a deck or if you're lucky maybe you can shark a metered space slightly closer or you can just come on a Saturday and park for free right behind the barracks in what looks like a State Police Lot.
It's nice to see a smiling, friendly staff.  They were busy and still gave attention to detail.  I would recommend this subway.  It's also very clean and well maintained.

If your in mt. Airy and looking to eat this is your best option. Good pub style food. Their specials are usually pretty good. The best part is the beer selection. A good variety of beers for all tastes. Like many bars if your in for the food and not to drink be wary of ordering soda. It's always flat and not very good. Oh and keep in mind they use RC cola not coke or pepsi. I have been here several times and rate it a 3 because of inconsistancy, mostly in service.

I consider myself a chocolate connoisseur and happened onto The Candy Jar after visiting another large area candy store, waiting in line and finding out they no longer made dark chocolate covered sugar free pretzels. The Candy Jar is a chocolate experience you don't want to miss. The Candy Shop is the essence of why you should support local small businesses. The proprietor greets you as you walk in asking you what kind of chocolate you want to sample while you wait. She has a passion and enthusiasm for chocolate and for her customers. She sells all her chocolate with a money back guarantee. I don't think she'll have to worry about returns. She also offered to make my husband any kind of chocolate in a sugar free version. I'm sure we'll be visiting there often.
Great food and amazing sushi!  Wait staff was on top of refilling drinks and asking if we needed anything.  A must if you are staying at this hotel!

Incredible, awesome, fresh, hit the spot seafood. Yes, this place is the secret. 

Hidden in an unassuming strip mall you would miss it if your passenger isn't looking as you drive by. The store is very small with limited and uncomfortable seating. Its a quick lunch in and out or best for take out. That is what might be a deterrent if you didn't feel venturous. It so worth sitting on a bed of spikes to eat here.

The menu is limited but very specialize with the best of what this team has to offer. Crab Cakes is where it is at, but dont stop there. Everything is mostly a la carte. We ordered two orders of crab cakes, shrimp, and hand cut fries with two drinks came to $43, not bad when most fancy places charge you twice that, and dont taste as good as this place.

We sat outside at one of the aluminum table and chairs. First came out the shrimp on paper plate. They were beautiful, plump, in all of its shrimpy glory. They were cooked perfectly. The sweet shrimp with garlic, butter, and herbs (6 to the plate) were the perfect starter. Following were two plates with a baseball size crab cake (not cakes as advertised, more later). They were golden brown and smelled like heaven. We also received our old bay hand cut fries and they were also golden with a dusting of old bay. 

The flavors were amazing. The shrimp was scampi style and so sweet, perfectly cooked you couldn't ask for better. The crab cake was 99% crab with just enough filler to keep it together. It was sweet with flavor and smell of the ocean. A flavor and smell you will not soon forget. Finally dont forget the hand cut fries, perfectly cooked, crispy outside soft and buttery inside. They do not need anything. The food was awesome and I cannot wait to eat here again. 

There are a few things that most people would question why. Seating choice is made for one cheek seating. Almost thought about sitting in my car. Paper plates were the cheapest Ive seen, almost see through. Crab Cakes implies more than one. I am however ok with one big one as it really was two in size together. This is minor stuff which you will bypass once you taste the food. They are working hard to make the best they know how,  so maybe overtime some comforts and presentations will change with feedback. 

94% chance you will enjoy your meal. Recommend the cakes, shrimp, and fries. Oh and I cant wait to try the roast beef looks incredible.

Nothing makes my tummy happier than an "All That Jazz" po-boy from Verti Marte.  It's a layer of tomatoes, ham, turkey, grilled shrimp, cheese, and mushrooms all covered in wow sauce, which is some sort of creole mustard/ mayo hybrid--but better.  I think that every single layer of this sandwich is integral.  The shrimp philly, also lathered in wow sauce with a melty layer of cheese, mushrooms, and peppers comes in as a close second. 

They don't use the traditional Leidenheimer bread here and instead use the sesame seed Italian bread, so I kind of put their po'boys in a class of their own.  I've had a lot of the different po'boy combinations here and they're all pretty good, especially the ones with melted cheese and mushrooms!  And if you're looking to enjoy some food porn up close and in person, just stand in front of the glass counter that displays all sorts of hot and cold entrees and that will literally send your belly and mouth into foodgasm. 

Resisting the urge to eat your po-boy right there on the street is hard, so make sure you have a fast route to your eating destination or you'll end up dining right there on the street. It's cash only, so be prepared, but there is an ATM in the front.  I will literally speed walk here from the Marigny/ Bywater limits and can get here in about ten minutes; it's that good.

Does Noche mean night? Either way, Noche served as a relaxing end to ours.

Heading upstairs, we flashed our IDs (no cover!) before seating ourselves. After ordering, a server brought a couple redheaded sluts to our table. They tasted delicious. (Shots, not ladies. Not that time.)

Their $3 beers were crowd-pleasers, and the crowd wasn't absurd, even for a Friday night. Someone queued rock on their jukebox, and we chatted, staring out at the rain.

We were hungry a few nights ago and Mama's is very conveniently located to my house. I inquired about the size of pizzas and was told the small was a 16 inch pizza which is plenty for 3 so I ordered a half cheese half Mama's special and got a antipasto salad. My total was just shy of $30 which is WAY TOO MUCH. My pizza was 12 inches, not 16 like I was told. They took a small cheese pizza that was precooked and added toppings to half and threw some cheese on the top. The lettuce in my antipasto was not fresh and had brown edges on all of the romaine. We were all incredibly disappointed and for what I paid for a small pizza and salad was even more disappointing. We will not go back.

I wasn't impressed with the food or service. We were the only table in the restaurant and the food took a long time. The $18.99 shrimp and catfish platter should be called a sampler because there wasn't a lot of food at all. My expectations are attainable...this is New Orleans people.

While the selection is nice the quality is horrendous and the people that work there know it. That is why once you walk out of the store, even if the product is defective no returns or exchanges. My cousin tried to warn me when she brought home a "beautiful" cabinet that has terrible craftsmanship. We are stuck with some crap stools that wobble. Also I just saw the same ones (slightly different height) at home goods for $20 less. Just go there. Nothing is worth the hassle of this store.

What a perfect way to end our trip to Boise. Mom and I needed breakfast before we got on the road to go back home and we saw this little 50s diner when we had stopped at the store the night before and after checking reviews decided to stop in on our way out. It didn't disappoint! Perfect atmosphere. We used to listen to 50s music together all the time so the decor was awesome! The booth was super comfy and the staff was beyond friendly! From the person opening the door as we walked up to our server to the staff cleaning the tables. Everyone greeted us and thanked us for coming and told us to have a great day on our way out and thanked us for coming in. 5 stars for service alone.

The food was on point! I came in expecting to order breakfast but when I saw the melts in the menu I had to order lunch! Mom got 2 eggs over easy with hash browns and sausage with rye toast. A simple breakfast but this simple breakfast is so easily messed up at other restaurants. The eggs were cooked perfectly and while I prefer my hash browns a little more on the crunchy side my mom loved how they were cooked and the sausage was delicious and a totally different type than she was used to and not too spicy. I had the turkey melt and it totally hit the spot! Bread perfectly grilled and buttery, cheese melted to perfection and the tomato and turkey were fresh and delicious! The server was honest in her suggestion when I asked whether I should have the fries or tater tots and I went with the tots. Perfectly crispy and crunchy!! I was in heaven. Even the pickle slices that came with the sandwich were crisp which usually are slimy and limp at other places. Everything about my meal was perfect and the prices were reasonable as well. 

The final test is always the restroom for me and they passed with flying colors. Neat and clean! 

Thanks for a great morning send off we will be back next time we visit for sure!!

The best meal I've ever had in my life was here. I wish I could actually get another reservation at this point b/c they are booked months in advance at this point. So worth the wait if you can get a reservation!!
Friendly waitstaff, nice interior, and tasty food. I went in for breakfast and the food was typical breakfast fare. But I will added that everything was absolutely yummy!
Fixed my cracked screen in about 15 minutes and it looks and works as though it's brand new.   Extremely courteous and the price was great.   Thank you!

There are only two redeeming features: the fries and the $5 shot and beer combo. Imperial now offers both- so we would rather go there and not deal with the jerks that work at Our Bar. The front door bouncer has the worst attitude and obviously isn't trained for his job because he can't read the birth dates on ID cards. He let's everyone with a fake into the bar- he probably thinks it's cool to be from Florida or New York-- but if you're from Nevada and actually 21 you don't make the cut.  My advice is to experience real Reno nightlife, which doesn't include the yuppie scum that works at Our Bar
.

Not great. We go here sometimes for breakfast or dinner, Worst coffee EVER!. I can't believe that other reviews stated they have good coffee! If you like your coffee to taste like an ashtray, then yes, they have good coffee. Food is mediocre but it's reasonably priced so you get what you pay for.

Picture a idyllic Sunday afternoon stroll along a grande 150yr old oak lined grande southern avenue, electric trolley cars passing by. Street scene straight out of a model train museum. This neighborhood is a beauty. The ambiance of the area is a must see. We stepped in and were warmly greeted by the proprietor. She was super friendly and apologetic about the brief wait. Moderately sized dining area with decent mix of seating including single seats, hightops, and a handfull of low tops, approximately 12 tables total. The young waitress was also super friendly and polite...all smiles(I'm going to guess she may be the proprietor's daughter..? We chose 'bun' dishes, chicken and pork. They come with lots of veggies, vermicelli, protein, and a bowl of fish sauce (more like a vinaigrette)  The portions were VERY generous, as in I couldn't finish it... the only bad for me is it doesn't come with a chopped up spring roll. That's an extra..hmmmm? Overall a great deal, I highly recommend especially if you dig the pho craze of late...

Great dogs on delicious rolls firm enough to stand up to the toppings.  The Texas Hold'em had the right balance of bacon, cheese, BBQ sauce and hot sauce for a satisfying meal to the end.  Bronx bomber with onions, mustard and onions very flavorful but could have used less toppings as it fell apart towards the end and I just ate the dog.  Staff was friendly and knowledgeable. Nice addition to South Street.  I'll be back for sure.

Terrible food, terrible service and management for such a great location. Who takes 30 minutes to re make soft poached eggs for benney? And then asking if we would like an over easy egg instead as it cooks faster.... They couldn't even get my over easy egg cooked correctly for my meal. What manager doesn't come out to speak to the customers regarding the issues we had with our food? Why asked how we like our eggs if you don't cook it the way we want as we are paying customers! It wasn't just our table that was having issues with the food, service and the management. Good to know as both our tables discussed how the place was not busy, food took forever to come out and manager avoiding customers. Both tables agreed we will not come back, let our friends and family know so that they won't waste their money and time.
I like the other location better but that doesn't change the food. The cheese steaks are excellent and they have WaWa coffee.....You know what it is if you've been in the Philly area.
BEST IN TOWN!!! Hands down one of my favorite places to drink coffee, relax, do homework, or watch the game. Before finding this place i could never find a consistent good espresso.

My husband and I just wanted to be together forever.  We didn't want to spend a fortune on a wedding and going to the courthouse seemed more complicated than we wanted considering you have to make an appointment and those appointments are only during working hours.

We picked up our license the day before our "wedding".  (This is required so don't try to wed without it).

We drove to the Nugget, the limo picked us up, took us to the chapel where we got married and then drove us back to the Nugget.  Done.

Seriously, it was super simple to set up, the staff was very nice, no hassles, no frills, no 5 year long debt.  

Check them out - for $15.00 more than the City of Reno charges you get a chapel and the "Reno quickie wedding" experience.  It was awesome.

The only suggestion I have that would have led to a five star review is a better back drop for photos.  Right now they have a purple curtain up that doesn't really cover the whole wall.  (No biggie since after you leave the Arch of Reno you and your sweetie have a whole lifetime to take photos).  Just a suggestion.
Great meal. Came there on a Saturday afternoon and it took some time for my party to be seated and waited on. Everything there was good. Flaming cheese is always a good idea.
Mahi tacos were great but the roles were the stars of our dinner. Waitress was very nice. The owner came out and spoke with us. Everyone was so nice! A prefect dinner in Meridian.

The panna cotta gelato is phenomenal!  Tastes just like the real thing. I have tried to find something similar locally with no luck. This became a nightly must when we were in New Orleans for vacation.

Chilaquiles Verde! Who turns down pulled pork for brunch? Not me. I would compare this to maybe a nacho style meal and I loved it. The veggies, poached egg, and a little crunch made for the perfect meal. I never think to explore brunch options, but on this Saturday I'm glad I found a new spot to visit in Indy. Shoofly isn't in the most popular area of Indy, but it is the up and coming area! My friend also ordered the biscuits and gravy. Seriously, some of the best biscuits around!! 

Great spot for Saturday brunch!

Just had a repair done at villanos music, was promised by the owner "Leonard who worked on the guitar" that it would be my satisfaction guaranteed or money  back. He came highly recommended from an another music shop, but now that I have my guitar back I've seen much better work at my usual repair shop was really disappointed with the work coming out of villanos music. Also feeling taken advantage of because he promised that I would be satisfied or money back. Now he says that he never said that. which I wouldn't have given him the guitar if he didn't. It seems like he does a whole lot of making excuses for the reasons why the guitar is not fixed properly. My usual guitar repair shop is The Guitar Repair Shop of Tampa, these guys literally do the impossible. Leonard did offer to keep working on the guitar but with the kind of work it was and what was done already, I did not feel comfortable having him do anymore because I do not think he was equipped for the job like my other shop was. If you're picky about your setups and your guitar work and craftsmanship I suggest not going to villanos music for a repair because he will make promises he can't keep and excuses why it's not right. Out of $100 now and i will have to spend more then that to get it right. Very disappointed with my experience at villanos music and dealing with Leonard the owner.

The pizza was soft and mushy but the flavor was good. Sauce was surprisingly good but needed more cheese. The ferrentini pasta was really sub par. It actually made me sick. Been here pre Covid and it was much better. Cocktails was decent.

Doesn't really compare to the great chicago deep dish spots.  The crust is too crunchy and lacks in flavor. Not enough cheese for deep dish.  sauce had good flavor and ingredients tasted fresh though.  I would ditch the salty kalamata olives and get black olives on the menu.  Couldn't believe they weren't available, such a standard ingredient. The kalamata tasted terrible on their pizza. While I wouldn't say the pizza is bad, I really wouldn't call it good either.  I was very excited to try this place and was extremely underwhelmed.  I doubt I'll be returning.  I wanted to like it, I really did.

Came in with a group of friends for dinner.  As others experienced, wait staff was slow, disorganized and couldn't answer any questions about the food.  Also had to ask for complimentary bread.
The house wine was $16/glass, my $40 steak was dry and well done (ordered medium rare).  
The space is beautiful, so if I was going to return it would be for a drink at the bar.  But I won't return.

Visited on a Saturday around 11.  We waited nearly 40 minutes for 2 crepes. Multiple tables were left with dirty dishes sitting for a while. It seems they need more staff on. 

You also tip when you order so you're not able to tip according to your service experience. Kinda backwards. 

Food was pretty good once it finally made it to the table.

What a ridiculously awful experience.  The restaurant was freezing.  Our waiter was barely around and we waited a good ten minutes for someone to notice that we needed a box and finally gave up and asked someone. I am beyond disappointed.

These folks offer a nice flight/sampler of their beers - I think it was something like 4 or 5 selections for a reasonable price. All their drinks can also come in 8 oz or 16 oz glasses in case you want to try a bunch of stuff. The service is great and the vibe is nice. It was relatively empty on a Saturday around 8pm. Foul Weather Jack was what I settled with, and I liked it a lot, so I'll be back for the beer.

I can attest that the food was just absolutely horrible, however. My friend had a nice french onion soup, but the Pork Pibil Tacos were dry and had wayyy too much pickled onions on it. The fries were horrendous too - soggy and super thin.

They apparently have free tours on Saturday afternoons.

So yes, back for beer, but never for food.

I love Catch 23. Been coming here for years. The food is delicious!  The service is just as fabulous. We sit at the Bar and Zander always takes great care of us. He's very friendly, attentive and makes awesome drinks. I highly recommend coming you visit Zander and get some yummy appetizers and delicious drinks. You won't be disappointed.

I came here back on 7/17/10. I was visiting my HS friends that live in St. Petersburg. WFLZ the radio station was there that night and they were doing birthday shoutouts so my friends birthday was on the 19th and I got him on the radio with the radio personality. I'm pretty sure her name was Katie. Overall the club was good. The people in general were really nice. Lots of pretty women that night.

Renovated interior and the food was great.  Bacon was crisp and toast was fresh.  The waitresses were nice and quick to refill my coffee.  I recommend the french fries - crispiest fries I've ever had.

I was very impressed with the quality of food, but even more so with the service. The server was attentive and even grabbed Naan from the back for me because the ones on the buffet was a little dry. I will definitely be coming back!

Very good food and good service. If you are in a hurry this probably isn't your place as this place is always busy!! To me that is a good thing!  Great crepes and biscuits and gravy is very good also.

I really loved this adorable bagel shop my sister brought me to when I stayed at her house a couple weekends ago. If I lived in the area, it would undoubtedly be the place where I would set up shop a couple times a week. It's cozy, with the brick oven cooking bagels and lots of comfy seating and coffee tables. Looks like they have lunch options in addition to breakfast.

Love that they serve La Colombe, and loved even more that they had bags of beans for sale as well, since I needed to stock up. While I didn't get my own bagel that day, I did try a bite or two of my sister's, which was delicious. Can't wait to go back next time I visit and eat all the carbs.

Simply had to share our recent experience attempting to dine at Commanders for Thanksgiving. Background; we live in NOLA and dine here frequently. In fact I have maintained a standing reservation for Thanksgiving for decades. 
On this Thanksgiving I requested outdoor seating, confirmed same 3 times, and was advised 2 days prior to Thanksgiving that we would be accommodated as reserved unless it was raining. My family arrived on a sunny Thursday at 12 noon for our reservation. It had not rained any that day, and indeed did not rain until late that night. 
Essentially we were turned away for outdoor dining by the maitre'd. He advised us that the management had decided to cancel courtyard dining for that day pending inclement weather?! He implied that those holding a reservation were contacted, but I assured him that we were not notified. Indeed their representative had confirmed our reservation the day prior to Thanksgiving. 
No offer to accommodate our outdoor party on this sunny day was offered, ie a solitary outdoor table in their empty courtyard was NOT an option! People in our party felt uncomfortable with the risk of indoor dining during the Covid pandemic. An inadequate, "We are sorry" was given. Finally, we were advised that take away food would be unavailable to our party as well. Commanders essentially sent us home with NO holiday meal options, and never notified us of their decision to descend all outdoor dining reservations! I am certain this will all be blamed on Covid, but in reality this crappy outcome had little or nothing to do with Covid. Rather someone at the vaunted dining "Palace" dropped the ball.

Love the location. Friendly people. Beautifully decorated. 
And for this location to be OPEN on a Saturday, sweet!!!! I've found other big banks to close some of their larger more remote branches on Saturdays due to the lack of customer flow. But not these guys; afterall it's only 4 hours. And your focus IS customer service, huh? Hello, ok, winning!!!

Walking in you're in the lobby/foyer area, with grand sitting chairs, round throw rug & marble tile, hello,#imlovingit. 

Greeted by the tellers in the booths, I explain what I need & am immediately assisted. Then once my business transaction was complete & upon exiting, I am then greeted adieu. 

Prompt service. No waiting (maybe because it's a Saturday, but still worth noting). Friendly & courteous staff. Beautiful decor. Pleasant atmosphere. 

Street & private parking. 

#iloveit

We spent the Friday night of Labor Day weekend in Ventura so we could specifically visit here on Saturday.  We arrived at 9:30am and every single parking spot was taken.  Wowzer.  I didn't expect that.  Thankfully, a car pulled out and we were able to snag the spot.  The beach is set up with two areas, off leash and on leash.  The on leash area is super close to the off leash area.  The beach was loaded with flies too.  Super annoying. There are no shower areas either by the restroom.  I was not super impressed with this beach.  I guess if you live in the area, its awesome, but considering we drove 84 miles it wasn't that great.  We left at 12:30 and it took over 3hrs to get home.   Oh and a dog rammed into my wife, knocked the wind out of her and gave her multiple abrasions and bruises.

Stay away! The bridal garden made my wedding planning process far more stressful than it should have been. Their lack of communication, professionalism, and customer service is unacceptable. I ordered a Martian Liana gown that I tried on at a trunk show. Kye was my consultant, and she was super sweet and helpful. She recommended I pay an additional $200 to have the designer hem the dress as there was a lot of detail in the lace and having the designer handle it would ensure the gown would be seamless. My measurements were taken, my deposit was paid, and I was happily on my way! A week or so later I received a phone call asking me to come back in for additional measurements. I work over an hour away from cherry hill, this was a huge inconvenience, but I left work early and got remeasured. Approximately five months later I received a phone call that my dress had came in. I couldn't have been more excited! I went in to try on my gown, and I cried. Not only was it SIX INCHES TOO LONG, but the alterations were horrific. It looked like the gown was cut mid calf and sewn straight back together. There was a distinct live through the dress, it was bulky, and it changed the flow of the bottom of the gown. Donna (the manager), kye (my consultant), and the seamstress all saw my areas of concern. The seamstress explained to me what they had done and what she would do to try to fix it. Although that was appreciated, I paid $2700 for my wedding gown. I didn't want a gown that needed to be "fixed", I want a gown that is perfect. 
Additionally, I tried on a size 14, but I ordered an 8. The back of the gown I tried on came to a pretty, modest v in the back. The gown I received came to a wide open u shape that completely exposed my back. I now understand the difference is due to the original gown being significantly larger and having a clip placed in the middle to hold the dress snug. However, that should have been explained to me. Key, and the other consultants who were working when I tried on the larger gown at the trunk show all made comments on how beautiful the back of the gown was-no one at any point insinuating it would look completely different in a gown my size. It may be "common knowledge" to some, but it wasn't to me. The back of the gown was a huge selling feature for me. I would NOT have chosen this gown had I known the back would be totally bare.  I've went to multiple fittings in a sad attempt to get my gown to the point it should have been when it came in. FINALLY, at my fitting that was two weeks from my wedding date, my gown was acceptable to me.- NOT perfect, not what I ordered, and not enough to make me glow, but to the point that it no longer brought me to tears. I wanted nothing more than to be excited to show off my fabulous gown. I not only payed FULL PRICE, but I was also charged AN ADDITIONAL $500 for alterations! I was sold a botched dress, and instead of being reimbursed for it, I paid extra for someone else's mistakes. 
BUT WAIT! The nightmare doesn't end there!! When I picked my gown up I was told it was pressed and ready to be worn- I was not to try it on until the big day. I was nervous because I hadn't tried it on after the last alteration, but I made the sorry mistake of trusting them. The day before my wedding I was feeling unsure, and on a whim tried my gown on... I absolutely melted down. The lace that was added to the back of my gown had almost completely fell off!! Now, not only was the back horrific and unwearable, but the front was also hanging off of me due to the lack of support. Once I calmed down enough to speak, I called the bridal garden and  as given the contact information of the seamstress- not an I'm sorry, but contact information. It was 1pm the day before I was getting married. The seamstress gave me the address of her shop and told me to come in. I got in my car and drove the hour to get to her. She was shocked when she saw my gown and said she's never seen anything like it before. Fortunately, she stopped what she was doing and corrected my dress to the best of her ability in the short time span she was given. My mom and I ended up being an hour late to my rehearsal, Un showered, and not dressed in what we purchased for the event because we didn't have time thanks to having to get my gown fixed for the 635272th time. I arrived to my rehearsal late, upset, and stressed beyond belief, but at least my gown "fit".

After reading the Yelp reviews, how could I resist? I ordered the five and dime which was massive and delicious. The sausage gravy was the best I ever tasted. The fried green tomatoes were crisp and bright with a raunchy sauce. Staff and manager were friendly and helpful.

I wasn't blown away by the food at Spasso Italian Grille, but it was good. I bumped up my star rating from 3 to 4 because of the nice ambiance and service. The atmosphere at SIG is relaxed and unpretentious.

We had the caesar salad and calamari to start (just average tasting, but nice portion size). I had the puttanesca which was very tasty but a little too oily. The linguine was cooked perfectly though.

My husband had the gnocchi in gorgonzola sauce, definitely worth going back for. Although the sauce was quite rich (in a good way), the gnocchi melted in your mouth! I've never had gnocchi quite so delicate before. The portion was huge too, enough for two since it was so rich.

Next time I'll try the escargot, since that seems to be a fave.
Great store.  Been doing business at this location  here for over 5 year and never have any issues. They have a great supply of different boxes for any size item that you need

I've been going here for 5+ years and have never had a bad experience. The staff is really friendly and they always help me pick out frames that honestly look good on my face. I feel like they don't try to oversell me and I genuinely like going here. The optometrist is great and as long as I'm living here I'll go to Glasses Galore.

Not sure where to begin about the experience tonight. Food was good. Service not so much. My friend and I walked in around 6:50pm for dinner in the lounge area. The hostess sat us right when we walked in. Around 7:15pm, the waitress still didn't come over to acknowledge us.

The hostess walked by and I said who is our waitress? She told me she would let her know. Another 5 minutes goes by, so I got up and saw the hostess and manager just sitting down on chairs of where the hostess stand is. I said the waitress still hasn't come over and we've been waiting. The manager said oh this is the first I'm hearing this and I mentioned how I told the hostess, but clearly nothing was done about it. Instead of her going over to get the waitress again, she could of said oh sorry let me at least get you drinks right now. The fact they have the bartender do serving in the lounge area makes no sense to me. I couldn't even believe this. 

Finally the waitress did come over. All was better until I had to wait to get a soda refill during dinner. Then after dessert, we waited for her to come back over to give us the check. It was like she completely was not paying attention and coming to us on her own time. 
 
We waited a while before I got up again to go up to the hostess stand, the manager was there and I said can you just give us our bill like we've been waiting and she hasn't come back. The manager got nasty with me and brought the bill to my table. 

I asked who the manager was and she told me it was her. First off, why didn't you come to check on us the first time I complained about the service? I said this was by far the worst service and instead of her trying to discount the bill or do anything remotely nice, she kept saying why don't you talk to me instead of looking at your phone. She said she could of care of us, but clearly didn't from the first time it even happened when we sat down.  Really? Is this woman serious?? She really needs managerial skills. Doesn't she know the customer is always right? I just wanted to get out of there after two hours. Didn't even discount our bill for this awful experience like a real manager should of. My friend and I couldn't believe this woman. Will not be back.  Hopefully the owner makes changes.

Came into dealer for an oil filter. Was charged $40 i thought it was expensive but i needed it. I called around this morning and found the same part number at other area bmws to be around 18-22 so being charged 40 must relate to inconvenience shown on the parts man face that i was waiting at the counter. In the future I'll go to competitor's or order online. With all this being said the dealer is beautiful and id give sales a shot in the future but will stay away from the parts department at all cost.

Just disappointed.  First, we were the on ones in there (4:30p) - they got 3 of our 5 orders wrong.  4 of us got the sampler - the pulled pork and chicken we're dry and part of the tenders way over cooked to the point they were not eaten.  The sides we're obviously fresh out of a package.

On the positive side - the staff were friendly and attentive, the ribs were tender, flavorable and delicious and our other friend enjoyed his sandwich.

The bill was $75 which we don't mind paying, but with all the bbq places in Sumner County I think it would have been better spent elsewhere.
The seats are nice and roomie.  They have the new seats that recline, theater temp was just right not to hot or cold.

This place was great.  I walked in at 7:45 on a Saturday night to place a to-go order and was immediately handed a menu.  The bar waitress took the time to explain the different rolls to me and offered me a drink while I waited.  Once I got my food, it was AWESOME.  Everything tasted fresh and the portions were great.  I got the rock n roll and the yellowtail sushi, and both were exceptional.  I will definitely be making this a regular spot.

Ordered burger and sweet potato fries.  The fries we perfect, right size cut to cook through and be crispy on the outside, right amount of salt.

The burger however disappoints, if they are going to overcook the thin patty, it needs to be a higher fat content to still stay juicy.

I do like the toppings, pick what you want, load it up, no more cost. The crispy onions are something 5 Guys and the others should add to their menu. I would suggest Mooyah add a soft fried egg as a premium topping option, they are perfect on burgers.

Though I won't be back, I'll keep going to 5 Guys and Phil's Grill.
We've been coming here for many years, almost since it opened. Fantastic food, friendly staff, and great atmosphere!

Ok i must be honest, this place really blew us away !!! Quite possibly one of the best places we have eaten at in a long time. I must also admit we're beginners when it comes to Szechuan cuisine, and we're finding it might be one of our favorites. First off the restaurant is really nice, spacious and has a little bar for drinks. The staff are super friendly and the restaurant is very clean as well.
We skipped apps and just ordered 3 entrees which worked out perfectly. One of the things i like about Szechuan cuisine is that is always seems to be served in a more family style set up where a big serving of each entree is placed in the middle of the table and everyone can dig in for a taste of everything. 
First we had Dry hot pot with beef.....absolutely incredible !!!! this comes to the table in a mini wok sitting on top of a small flaming heat source to keep it cooking away. Secondly, we had the Mei la Chicken which i would suggest should be ordered as part of every meal no matter what as it is just that good. The spicy braised beef soup was incredible too. The soup broth was savory and perfectly spiced!!!!

I can't believe I haven't been here sooner!  Healthy paninis, salads, smoothies and coffee bar... yummm.  My smoked salmon panini on rye was delicious and filling.  And so many other delicious-sounding options with moderate calorie counts.

I also had a coffee with sugar-free peppermint syrup - didn't realize what I've been missing here!  You know hot chocolate with peppermint schnapps?  Obviously this wasn't as good since it's lacking chocolate and alcohol.  But the taste was comparable and wonderful, and won't pack on pounds or give you a DUI.

My solo dining experience was a little awkward, but that was my fault.  Apparently the options are to-go or full service (not fast food counter style).  Not realizing this I inadvertently sat down at the nearest table to the counter to wait for my food, which then became my private dining table... awkwardly right next to everyone ordering and me basically facing the wall.  Oh well - still great!

This place is great! The pasta was very good and filling. The covered patio is really nice, there's pretty lights hanging out there and there's fans so you won't get hot. The prices aren't outrageous and  the waitress that we had was super sweet.

I love Pita's Republic food but I suggest you go to a different location. First, their customer service is horrible - we had an incident where they gave us the wrong meal and wouldn't make it right for us. Second, the restaurant is not sanitary - the last time I was there I saw 2 cockroaches in the bathroom (which is right next to the kitchen area). Plus, their kids are always running around all over the restaurant (including in the back where the food is). Go to the Bloomingdale or Causeway locations. It's worth the short drive for a clean restaurant with outstanding customer service.

I went on a Sunday and kept reading that the lines get crazy as this was true, in my opinion the lines move so fast that it doesn't feel like a bother. They have stations set up that will prevent getting lost. There are a lot of eateries and I am def coming back to try more. Only thing is next time I will bring wet wipes because it can messy. I was fortunate to try 3 different places here. I got the Olympia Gyro's, Basset's Ice Cream and than to finish the food coma I got some cookies from The General Store. 

Olympia Gyro's- They were so good, soft warm pita bread with freshly sliced lamb. The Gyro sauce compliments the wrap nicely. Nothing over powers another its just the right blend you want.

Bassets Ice Cream- Massive scoops. I bought some for my sister and she was amazed when she seen her cone. I got the Butterscotch Vanilla in a waffle cone. It was both creamy and sweet and the waffle cone was satisfying. It was a big cone.

PA General Store- I got Top 3 best cookie I ever had in my damn life. I got the Lemon and White chocolate, The Turtle, and the Original Chocolate Chip Cookie. I would def shop by the pound next time. Don't make the mistake and not bring back extra for home.

The place seems to be run by kids. After attending an event at the CC on Sunday, I asked for the draft beer list and she rattled off what turns out to be only half of them, so I picked Sierra Nevada. What I got was a very full glass of carmel colored something with absolutely no head and it tasted watered down and had a distinct metallic taste. Undrinkable for $6. When I spotted the beer list on the wall, I asked for a substitute Guinness which actually arrived with a head and tasted good. We were there for apps and a few beers to watch the Eagles lose again, and got cold, chewy calamari and reasonably decent but lukewarm buffalo wings. Our server was prompt but unapologetic for it all and charged for the awful beer. Oh well. Just because it is a sports bar shoudnt mean lousy food. This place has its ups and downs; this time it was a downer.

We happen to find this place on Saturday and a little surprised it was so quiet. But quickly realized that the business crowd wasn't there on the weekend. I was excited to see avocado toast on the menu so of course ordered that with a latte. My breakfast was excellent! A perfect blend of flavors added to the avocado on big piece of toast! Not too full, just quite full enough. I have struggled finding a good coffee bar that wasn't soooo expensive and under 8oz. But not at The Well. My latte was extra hot, which is how I ordered it and a good size and price. It's a 2 mile walk from home, but now I'm motivated! See you soon!

Wanted to love it, but just can't find the love for Taste of Portugal. 

My Pork and Clams (Carne de Porco à Alentejana) was just fair. The pork was tough and chewy, as if it had not been braised enough, the four clams added little clammy goodness to the dish and the french-fried potato cubes where a strange substitute for braised potatoes cooked in the juices of pork and clams. I did like the salsa verde that bathed our shrimp and scallop dish (Camarão e Escalopes con Salsa Verde), but both beef dishes we had (Bife à Portuguesa and Bifinhos a Madeira) lacked depth of flavor and the Portuguese bread tasted days old. 

The service was good, but the nearly empty restaurant and the dated decor suggested that Taste of Portugal had seen some better days. It appears that it is coasting into its twilight years. A shame, since with a bit more determination and some more TLC in the kitchen, Taste of Portugal could be dynamite.

My front tooth has been needing treatment for a long time. I was very fearful of how the treatment and cost until the staff walked step by step what would take place. I would reccommend anybody to come here who has dentist anxiety, the staff is very friendly and try to make everything stressfree as possible.

Please know up front this is not me blowing hot air as I rarely write reviews and the few I have, are positive. This genuinely merits a review and is not COVID related.

It pains me to write a bad review about a local business but I simply must as the service was absolutely terrible.

I first tried Goodness in October and had a rather neutral experience - while the patio was not shaded and it was rather hot, the food was overall good (although I would agree with other reviews that the açaí bowl was too runny). 

I then went back this past week with my family and left rather in shock as to how our experience unfolded. I will summarize with this statement: be prepared to work for yourself. 

1- Do not expect the tables to be clean. And when they are not, you may surprised at the response when you ask for them to be wiped down. We started at a table with jelly and crumbs all over it and moved. The new table had such a dirty film on it, it was clear they hadn't been wiped in awhile. The employee said they cleaned them each morning and they're just stained and not dirty.  IRONICALLY he stated this as he lifted the white rag and it was black from where he wiped it. Yeah, don't try to back out of being caught in a job not done; clearly the tables where not cleaned that morning as we showed up 10 minutes after it opened. Just own it, apologize and move on. It's your job at ANY time in history to wipe tables. And especially during COVID. 

2- Don't expect to get cream, sugar, utensils or anything of that sort with your food. Instead, be prepared to go in not once, not twice, but 3-5 times to ask for these things as you discover you need them. And then be prepared to have the employee get clearly annoyed at... wait for it... having to do his job.
We went back in and asked for cream and sugar, painfully he got it. We went back in and asked for a stir stick, he grabbed with bare hand in the middle of it (a napkin is what is commonly used to grab those; even in non COVID times).
We went back in and asked for a booster seat- nope, don't have them- sorry. "We used to but got rid of them."
We needed a bit more cream- he was clearly annoyed. 

To feel GUILTY as a customer for then needing a fork for my child to eat his food and not wanting to go in and ask, well, that's bad. 

3- The REDEMPTION is that the food is great. Reasonably priced, good quality. Good stuff. 

In summary- so much focus is put on the customer supporting small businesses during this time. And I agree we should; as is proven by how we spend our money. But know that when you are an EMPLOYEE of a small business, you represent that business. Do it well. You're paid for more than a transaction. Say hello when a customer enters (oh that didn't happen either). Anticipate what a customer will need and if you don't know, ask them at the table. Smile. If you make a mistake (like failing to wipe tables before opening), own it and do it with no excuses (because when you lie and say it was done as you heap black grime on your cloth where our child is eating it looks lame). And for heavens sake, don't look annoyed when the customer NICELY comes in for the 4th time to ask for something needed and basically is doing your job.

I give it two stars because the food is food.

4 stars for food. 
No stars for service. 
2 stars is the median.

What a charming out of the way little gem. Delicious, fresh and generous portions, everyone in our family of 4 enjoyed their meals. LOVED the Indian fry bread and homemade buttermilk biscuits. The omelet with Bacon, cheese and avocado was perfect for my more restrictive keto diet.

Wow! This place was great. I would recommend making a reservation though as it is pretty busy.  The portions are great and me and my friend left feeling very satisfied. We started with the small antipasto and ordered the 20 layer lasagna and the family jewels pizza. Everything we ate was great and I would recommend this place to anyone who likes Italian food and a up to date atmosphere.
I stumbled across this place and so glad I did. Great food and great service.  I thought the portions were well worth the money. They also have a great craft beer selection.

We went to the El Cisne Restaurant website and hit "make a reservation" which sent us to "Open Table".  We confirmed a reservation for Thanksgiving Day, 2019.  We received an email confirmation as well as a reminder about our reservation.

When we (a party of 3) arrived at the restaurant on Thanksgiving, we found the restaurant closed.  This was a disaster!  No where to go to celebrate the holiday.

I emailed and called El Cisne after the holiday  and after several  attempts finally got a response from MR. PHIL FERRANTI, restaurant owner advising me "we are not open on Thanksgiving, we don't deal with "Open Table" any longer, and ended the email with "SORRY FOR YOUR INCONVENIENCE".

I"m sorry but "sorry for your inconvenience" in my opinion is not the way to handle this situation.

So, if you really want to eat there, I suggest you don't make the reservation on line...but in all honestly - their customer service stinks so why bother even going there?

Thank you Dustin for a quality cut! Just moved to the area from Northern California and he made me feel right at home! He took his time and made sure I left satisfied, I will be back! Also love the free coffee, BEER and soda they offer! Dustin is appointment based, however they do accept walk-ins! Cheers I will be back!

Hans cleaned my place until it sparkled like sunset at the Pacific. The attention to detail was amazing; I felt like I walked into a different house. I'm so, so grateful for his services.  I'd recommend hiring him to refresh your home space, and revitalize your living place.

Guac is the best Mexican restaurant in Bellevue. Elegant atmosphere, affable staff, and fine food contribute to this restaurant. Their salsa was beyond standards and their handmade tacos were decent. Definitely coming back soon!

This is a place that you go more for the show than the food.  And they do put on a great show that had my entire group involved and laughing.  Everything from onion volcanos, flipping food and spatulas around to a birthday rice cake.  This however, was the best part of dinner.  I had the Hibachi grilled shrimp and while okay it was on the blander side.  The soup and salad were tasty.  But it really went down hill from there.  Everything off the grill had about the same flavor.  For a dinner over $20 I expect more.

More like 3.5, if I could give halves.

Cesar's got diversity going for them. Breakfast, lunch and dinner menus, specializing in Mexican seafood, plus fish and chips.

Also, a spacious dining room and sturdy tables. It does not quite feel like a hole-in-the-wall.

A flat screen tv on either side of the room means you can watch Mexican wrestling and/or soap operas in any direction. Or, you have to watch Mexican wrestling and/or soap operas no matter where you look. This all depends on how much you like Mexican wrestling and/or soap operas. On this day, the guys in the masks were getting a pounding, and then the midget manager would get the last punch in from the third rung. Ouch!

Most of the clientele on a hot Saturday afternoon were a mixture of latino plus a few hipsters and everyone was ordering the seafood cocktail. I can take a hint.

Shrimp cocktail $10
Octopus cocktail $11
Shrimp, octopus and baby clam cocktail $11.50, that's what we got.
It comes with lime wedges, some packets of saltine crackers and is topped with about half an avocado, cut into chunks.
Yeah, it was yummy! Next time, though, I'll be perfectly content with the shrimp. But it was all good.

Also ordered was an adobada soft taco, about $2.50, and chilequiles with green sauce.

The taco was fine, with 2 factory-made tortillas, a pile of meat that was good to dish out between the two tortillas. They'll add onions and cilantro to order.

I really enjoyed the chilequiles. There was a choice of red or green sauce, the red being hotter. And for the first time in my chilequile enlightenment, there was an option on how the two eggs should be done. Over-easy, please! 

Chips with two salsas were complimentary, coming straight to the table with some glasses of water. 

The prices were a smidgey higher than some of the cheap eats elsewhere on Milpas, but for this you get a less divey atmosphere and waitresses who won't struggle with English as much as you're struggling with Spanish.

Grew up eating the Montecito one which is sadly now gone.  This is the closest approximation and seems to be same recipe, but the quality is all over the place.  The time before last I swore would never eat it again, then broke down and got it again subsequently and it was a damned masterpiece.  That last one had fresh garlic on it and I requested it well done or "crispy".  Anyway give it a shot.  Eat a piece in the car as you leave, you can almost hear your dad cursing at you.

Tried to call to order dinner while visiting from out of state and the girl acted like she couldn't hear us and was rude. Decided to just order online. Went and picked up the food about 8pm the got back to the hotel to cold food, wings that weren't even covered in sauce, nasty tasting ranch, and not even the extra ranch we paid for! Tried calling the manager for over a hour! Called home office and they said they would just refund me and have the location call me no phone call or refund yet... while checking to see if I have been refunded yet and looking at my bill I realize I was charged 2 times! Called by American Express to dispute the charge and they said they swiped my card while in the store too! The email said to bring the credit card in on pickup to verify order and they decided to be sneaky and charge me again when we thought they were just verifying the order! Called home office again and they don't seem to concerned... we will never eat at another Hooters again!

Convenient location and pleasant staff.
Got the "roast beef poor boy" and the Caesar salad.
Virtually no vegetarian options - even the "sizzling tofu" comes with pork and shrimp (?!?!). The sushi is not fresh, either. Bummer :(

If I would have rated the pizza from here 2 months ago I would have given 5 stars. Sense then we have gotten 2 overdone pizzas in a row. We get take-out  every time. I haven't looked at the pizza before I leave because it's always been excellent and worth the price. The first time it was burnt was about a month ago, the second one was about 3 weeks later. The quality hasn't been there either. I do like to support local, but I may have to find another local pizza place if this happens again.

If you had to goto one bar and one place only this place is a must. Its located smack in the middle of Bourbon st. and inside the atmosphere is loud/wet/sweatyshots. You can always find people dancing and drinking on and off stage. We went mostly during happy hour 1pm - 7pm and all of the bartenders were extremely hott. 

Pros: Funny people/ cheap shots $3, 3 for 1 drink specials $20 will get you 3 shots of whatever in 3 huge cups.

Cons: Gets extremely packed during weekends / late nights. The drink specials were only half-filled during late nights.

Part retail, part museum. If you've seen the Pickers TV show you know the interesting kind of things they have here. The selection is small. If all you want is Pickers logo merchandise, I'd give this 4 stars.

Finally! A good restaurant in downtown Clearwater!  this is their 3rd location and i really liked the decor and set-up.  I had the tuna with mole sauce, an interesting concept and was very good.  I also had the key lime pie, yummy.  Service needs improvement, only on doing what was requested.  My friend asked for no ice in her water and she got ice.  We asked for split checks and got one check.  Also the hostess seemed to think it was ok to shove a table right next to ours for the next party while we were still sitting there.  If the waiter had told us it would have been fine but didn't.  We explained this but I think this went over their heads.  Anyway I will go back for sure.

I walk down to Clementines all of the time! Every time I have friends over they expect to go to Clementines. The staff are always helpful, I suggest asking them for recommendations! It is always tough to choose a flavor, so frequently I will ask the staff for advice between a few different flavors. They have not steered me wrong yet! They have rotating seasonal flavors, and just last night I ordered the hot chocolate with marshmallows and the Boston cream pie flavors (based on staff recommendation), both were delicious! I will probably get them again, if I am not busy trying another new flavor!

We had a lot of fun at Recess over the weekend. I found the staff to be friendly and prices to be reasonable, although I will say they seem to have a light pour, so beer is probably the best value.

I enjoyed all the games and all the space to move around. I agree with the sentiments others have left in that a bigger variety of games would be nice. There are SO many air bowling setups and I think that space could be used for something else. I imagine / hope they'll continue to expand their game selection as they continue to grow. Either way, I plan to come back!

I love this place! I have been here twice, once with a bachelorette party and once by myself, and the service is always incredible! The staff are so friendly and the amenities for what you pay to have even one service done is so worth the price. I recommend taking advantage of the pool and saunas, and make an afternoon of it by ordering some food from the restaurant downstairs while you lounge. I wish I lived in Indy so I could come more often!

YUM! Friday night seafood and prime rib. YUM!

I am a fan of crab anywhere its king crab and endless. It was a bit salty thus the minus one star but the other food made up for it. I never see mac n cheese an mashed potatoes and bbq chicken anywhere! There was a great selection and there was sugar free cheesecake that was actually good! I think the price is very fair for what you get. Seating is good and the crowd wasn't very loud for a Friday night. We got seated right away and service was excellent. Will be back if in this area again!

This was our second time eating here. It was a long drive from Spring Hill but worth the trip.  Our appetizers were cucumber Raita.  It was more shredded cucumber than chunks but still delicious. The Samosa Chaat with chutneys was also amazing. A pinch of kick but not too much. For reference my husband usually orders mild and I order medium spice. 

I ordered my standard chicken tikka masala.  I always love it and have been craving Indian for a while. My husband decided to branch out and try something new so he ordered chicken chenndai (not sure on the name spelling but that is close). He found it to be amazingly delicious. I was very happy with my choice as well. Of course we ordered our favorite garlic naan to go with our meal.  

I highly recommend this Indian restaurant and we will be back soon.

I love Michael's! 
I love their frequent sales!
I love all the coupons
They send me in my emails!

The store is very well organized 
You'll easily find what you need. 
But if you need to ask for assistance
The salespeople will help with speed!

I've been doing lots of paintings
I get all my paints and canvases here. 
With all the Christmas sales
They were practically a steal!
