No matter your craft
Whether making jewelry with beads,
Painting, scrap booking, or cake decorating
Michael's will fulfill your needs!

I made an account JUST to write a terrible review for this place. First, I showed up 30 minutes before my scheduled appointment in case there were paper work to be done. Another patient showed up the same time as I did, except he was one hour early. The front staff helped him first instead and kept me waiting in the front store for 45 minutes. The three staff members there claimed they were too busy so they cannot tend to my needs when I spoke up. The doctor there suggested I move forward with the appointment without checking my insurance because I was "late" for my appointment. How unprofessional and disorganized! When I said, I prefer to have my insurance checked to see how much will be covered, the doctor's face and attitude changed. The way she treated a patient (me) was rude, unprofessional and not worthy of the title, "doctor". When a staff finally walked toward me to check my insurance, he said everything is fine and my eye exam is covered. However, after the doctor rushed through my entire exam, the staff member who checked my insurance handed me a bill for the eye exam. Not only was my eye exam full price, he also claimed that my insurance only pays 50% off my eyeglasses and 15% off contacts, IF I get them together. Despite saying my eye exam was covered by my insurance, I received a full priced bill and was "strongly encouraged" to order contacts and frames with them. When I asked about my frames, the staff rudely told me I pick them out next week because I get a bigger discount when I order contacts and glasses together. HOWEVER, their contacts are TWICE the price of ordering independently from Walgreens or any other place WITHOUT insurance. When I refused the offer, the guy flipped out on me and asked, "why would you order from somewhere else without insurance when you can order from the store using insurance?". This is place is most likely a scam, trying to get paid twice, by the patient and your insurance company! I was here two years ago and had a great experience which is why I came back, Unfortunately this place went downhill very quickly! All the staff there are untrustworthy, rude and unprofessional. I will never be back here again! Giving them one star is being nice.

Pat O'Briens is an institution in new orleans, and still a lot of fun.  The problem is it used to be a reasonably priced place to go imbibe.  I've noticed that their prices continue to climb and most recently I paid $6.25 for a manhattan in a very small glass full of ice.  It is no longer a place you can park it and party at a reasonable price.

Found this on Instagram before we came to St. Pete for vacation. Lovely little place to get some really good evening bites or lunch. Staff are very nice and down to earth. The dole Whip is to die for!

I found Detail Pros through the Santa Barbara Axxess card and emailed Bijan, the owner who quickly responded and scheduled an appointment for the Deluxe detail.  I was very happy with the results; my car was cleaned and waxed fastidiously and looked fabulous!  Merrick was the friendly young man who performed the detail work and he did a wonderful job.  I will definitely return.

Sylvain is a hidden gem for tourists surrounded by typical New Orleans tourist traps in the French Quarter. 

The restaurant inside is very cozy and it also has a beautiful outside patio. We opted for the outside patio and learned that the back wall is the second oldest wall in the city which was pretty cool. 

The food was delicious and the service was perfect.

This is one of those restaurants I wish existed in my home town!

Wow, what a great find. After a long hike we were deciding where to go and found an absolute gem at Devil's Acre. The wait staff is great, very knowledgeable and friendly. We started with deviled eggs and pretzel bites, both great choices but the pretzel bites were incredible. My wife had the grilled swordfish and I had the pork schnitzel for entree, we agreed each was a home run. 
If you are passing by or looking for somewhere special I say give this place a try, you will not be disappointed.

They're not even on the barrier islands, yet they charge on the beach prices.  There's no redeeming quality for this place; even the drink coolers are kept outside so you can sweat while you decide what you want.  Just do yourself a favor and go a couple miles to Wawa; you'll save as much as $0.50/gallon depending on how bad this place has the prices set on any given day.

Just left from a fabulous dinner at Rexys!  Dined with a group of three. My prime rib dinner was the best I think I've ever had. Prices were very reasonable. The  atmosphere was conducive to having a conversation. Our waiter, Michael, was always there for us and accommodated every request. He was pleasant and very personable. New York cheesecake was great!

They really do try to work with you on a transaction it's just that one of the staff members was young, probably new and not knowledgeable on the currency so they had to call the boss a few times so it took a while. The other staff member didn't seem to know as well or just didn't want to help. I'd expect the staff to know more about currency types than me but I guess not this time. Overall they got me sorted out, it just took a while.

Not a review on the pizza, which i would love to taste. Unfortunately, their posted business hours seem to be loose guidelines rather than actual business hours. I write this in the hope that the business owner cares enough to check yelp reviews.  Went by last night at 8:15-the hours say 11-9-and they were closed. So, on a Saturday night you close when? how can anyone plan to get a pizza from you? I've seen 4 pizza joints come and go from that location. I'm getting a suspicion that we are about to see another one close. Too bad.

I would sincerely like to revise this review, and will give Taste of New York another chance, because I would like to see businesses succeed in my neighborhood.  We'll see.

Great place to watch the game. It's not too busy. I will say parking was terrible since the building is attached to apartments. The only parking available seems to be just for the tenants. The bartender was very interactive as well as the other staff. If you are looking for something low key, not that big of a crowd. This would be a great place.

Found this little local business when we first moved to the Wesley Chapel area. My daughter has very narrow feet and they carry Sride Rite shoes that make wide and narrow shoes for children. Have not been disappointed with the wide variety of shoes they carry. They even have Sanuk's for women. If you have never tried on a pair of Sanuk's you must give them a try. So very comfortable and made out of yoga mat. Little feet carry athletic, casual, dressy, and sandals for boys and girls. Also have, hair bows, socks and accessories. If you sign up for their email specials they send out great coupons for sale items. They have a buying reward program. Buy 8 pairs of shoes over $25.00 and receive the 50% on your next pair. The owner Diana Ciccarelli is extremely friendly and always willing to help you find the best shoes for your children. If you are in Wesley Chapel stop by and take a look at their selection. Conveniently located in front of Sam's Club on State Road 56 in a strip mall.

Had my first experience at MedExpress this past weekend. I usually go to a different urgent care type place but this one is closer to my house and members of my family had been pleased with their visits here.

The check in process was super simple, I liked that you signed the papers at the desk on a signature pad instead of  having to bring them back to your seat. I also appreciate that they e-mailed me after the appointment. 

There were a few people in front of me so I had to wait maybe twenty minutes before getting called back to the first room where the nurse took my temperature/blood pressure/ wrote down my symptoms. 

Next, the nurse led me to a different room where she gave me the flu and strep test. The doctor then came in shortly after and was very friendly and checked out my nose/throat/ears. 

After my diagnostic of the flu (meh), the doctor sent me on my way to the checkout area where I received care instructions as well  as my prescription. 

All said and done, I was in and out within an hour. This location was very clean and all the employees were pleasant. I'll be back here next time I need to go to the doctors quick!

Very sad place of business, manager is very rude, very Combative, and will refuse service to you in a heartbeat.   I would not recommend anyone to shop here, my family and I felt like second class citizens at this shop.   I would take my money else where.    They should put a sign up you are not welcome here so others don't have to be treated the way we were treated.   I would put no stars if I was could.   

Please avoid this place!!!!!!!
I had high hopes but there are few choices and flies are EVERYWHERE. Kind of a put off having to swipe flies every few seconds.

We will highly recommend Lucatelli's. The prices are great for the city. It really tasted incredible. Team are incredibly lovely and service is really quick. Certainly worth checking out if you're in the area.

We got delivery via grub hub and everything was great! Got here within the projected time, all was as ordered and we loved the food! I got the beet feta salad with a green juice and hubbs got banana bread French toast. We'll definitely try them again because I want that breakfast burrito and shrimp quesadilla!

Exceptional hotel, called me by name and asked if I needed anything every time I returned during my stay, Breakfast was great, rooms were very spacious and clean, had a live band on Thursday night. The shower was very large and had a light inside. The housekeeping staff would make towels into swans, which I thought was very nice. There are plenty of restaurants nearby and a shopping complex within walking distance.  I would highly recommend this hotel.

Wings where great, I got the garlic parmesan with plain fried rice. Rice portion with combo was huge, and Winnie where big also. Would definitely recommend this place. 

Great prices also... 6 wings, fried rice with drink combo plate with order of egg rolls (3 to order) ONLY $12 and some change...

Well, Cowbell just lost a few regulars today due to their rude staff.  I showed up at Cowbell to meet up with my friends who had a table with 4 extra empty chairs.  Instantly, the most awkward waitress that works there tells me I am not allowed to sit in the empty chairs at my friends' table because there is a wait...  A wait that involved 2 whole people.  These 2 people could have sat at the completely empty bar or could have sat at my friends' table with the 4 empty chairs.  But they didn't.  So therefore, I was told to leave because they don't allow "joiners".  Seriously?  I used to eat at Cowbell frequently, but not anymore.  I'll be sure to "join" my friends elsewhere where the service is nice.  

Two thumbs down!  

On a side note... way too many flies in the restaurant.  Last time I ate there, I was swatting flies every couple of minutes throughout my meal.  Ya'll should spend more energy getting rid of the flies than former-loyal customers.

After having my late night flight canceled and having to be at work early the next morning, my only option was to rent a car to drive from Philly to NYC. It was very late and I was very tired. After getting my car, I noticed there wasn't a USB in it so that I could charge my phone since that is what I used for my GPS. I went back inside and spoke to Margaret and asked for a charger that I could use with my phone charger (she said they were out of them as many people had already taken them) . She came out with me to double check and see if she could find one. After she didn't, she made it her goal to find one so that I didn't have to pay for a Garmin. About 10 minutes later, she came back with one, making my already stressful night much better. She was extremely friendly, patient and and helpful and my 5 star rating is for her. If it wasn't for her, I would've given 4 stars, but the fact that she went above and beyond to help me was why I gave a 5.

Ordered a large pie with two toppings, two veggies on half for my vegetarian boyfriend and bacon and olives on the other half for me. We were charged $27 for the pie, so for four toppings. I order this same exact pie wherever I go, from a half dozen places in my hood and it's like $12-15, we called back to explain they were trying to charge us for 4 toppings as a plain large pie is $14, but the guy didn't understand.

Pie arrives and the dough is so chewy and tough it actually hurts my mouth to chew, bacon is chipped up fine and over every sq inch of half the pie, same with the olives but instead of being regular black firm mild olives you would get on pizza or nachos, or in a can, they are super salty overwhelming Kalamata olives.

I've never had to pick a pizza topping off a pizza in my life to make it edible. But this was a nasty salt bomb. Mediocre pizza. Sky high prices.

I'm never a fan of all-you-can-eat-slash-buffet-style Sushi places.  Bottom line, the fish is never really as fresh as a traditional sit-down type of restaurant.  

With that said, food here is meh-mediocre.  One dish that stood out among the rest, the Special Scallop Handroll.  The all-you-can-eat is optional, but considering the price, why not?  This place can get packed, so the wait might be awhile.  So be prepared, especially if it's during the winter... standing outside might not be an option.

This place ranks 3-stars purely on their service.  The guys that run this place are pleasant, cheerful, friendly, and fun.  They make the experience worthwhile.  

I'd say come here to get ok-sushi served by good company.
The staff care about getting you the best medicine for your specific needs. The deals are always good and the halo brand shatter is fantastic.

Main Line Auto is the best. They have a nice selection of cars. If you're looking for a vehicle Main Line is your place!! From start to finish the process was smooth and seamless. Make sure to ask for Gee. He was helpful throughout the entire process. He was not like most cars sales associates, pushy and rude. He asked me what my budget was and worked from there. Needless to say, Gee was the best! 

Thank you, Gee and Main Line Auto!! You guys rock!!

Do not use HBK Appliance Solutions or First American Home Warrany companies. We have been wait over 4 months now for a dishwasher repair. These companies keep telling us the parts are on back order...yet I can find all of these parts for less and $20 each on GE's site and 3-5 day shipping less that $6. HBK techs also do not know what they are doing. Everytime HBK is at my home they call their manager, or lead tech, to figure out how to perform the attempted repair; ie how to remove the door. Do not waste your time with these companies. Better off just finding a reputable local repair tech. The insurance companies put customers through hassle and I feel are part of the delays with just getting a repair done. Stay away from #hbkappliancesolutions and #firstamericanhomewarranty #FirstAmericanTitle

First of all- on this visit, I did not try on any dresses & did not make an appointment. I wanted to talk to someone about setting up an event so that my out-of-town maids could put in their dress orders at their local David's Bridal. I had to wait about 10 minutes, because as usual, the store was packed full of bride-to-bes and their girls and prom dress shoppers. It was a definitely hectic inside. But surprisingly, I spoke to the manager within 10 minutes and had an event set up and all my questions answered. So I must say, I had a rather pleasant experience. However, I have been to David's Bridal on several occasions on behalf of brides and for my own dress shopping and have a few tips: 

1. Don't give them your primary email and phone number, because they sell your info to other businesses and you will get spam.
2. Always make an appointment when trying on dresses, because you will be incredibly frustrated otherwise.
3. Bring a friend who can help you when the employees go MIA.
4. If you're interested in renting tuxes at Men's Warehouse, ask for the coupon that gives you $40 off per tux
5. They will pressure you to purchase your dress right then and there. Sleep on it! Come back later if you still love it after looking at the pictures of the dress on you. Don't let them talk into a dress you don't love.

David's Bridal doesn't have to be a nightmare, just be smart and prepared. You can get some incredible deals here if you're interested.

It's not bad for being next to a gas station.  Bathroom situation is a little sketchy being you need a key on a rope and then go into the gas station.  The Thai food however is good.  The Phat King is yummy and the Thai iced tea is good also.  Pad Thai is well prepared.  Nice spot for lunch with the crew, don't take your girlfriends parents here though, it's a little too sub standard as far as settings go.  Safe bet for good Thai food and the service is good.  Friendly people.
Stopped by today and had so much fun. Made some new friends and was able to escape the Mardi Gras room. Nance is the best!!
Breakfast Cuban is pretty darn tasty here and the B.E.S.T. is named perfectly for me. You can't go wrong with bacon, fried green tomato, spinach and a runny egg!!

Why did I come here without reading the reviews??? I am so disappointed. I saw their $2 Tuesday special and got excited. I should have known discounts aren't always what they are cracked up to be. My drink tastes like dollar store juice. Its kinda dirty in here and the service is lackluster.... What a frickin waste! And the crazy thing is everyone here seems quite content with mediocrity... I like Chilis but THIS location SUCKS!!!This will be my last time at this dump! Thumbs down!

First let me say that I really enjoyed the atmosphere, outside and in. The inside is dim, like a pub, but not so dark that you can't read your menu. Our server was polite and attentive. They were busy, but I never felt forgotten. 

The food was good, not great, but very satisfying. My friend had the pasty, which is one of his favorite dishes there. (He's visits there regularly.)

I had the tabouli with Mahi Mahi, blackened. I liked that it was lemony and fresh, but I did wish it had chunks of cucumber in it. It came with hummus and pita, which was also tasty and lemony. 

The beverage menu, I was a little disappointed in the beer selection, the only dark beer offering was Guinness. I also thought it was a little pricy in contrast to the food, which I thought was reasonably priced.

Finally, my friend and I don't like smoky bars. I don't even know if it's allowed there inside, because neither of us noticed. 

All in all, I'd do it again. I'm Looking  forward to trying the curry.

So, we have been waiting on our meal for about 50 min now. Waitress isn't too friendly, but hey, maybe she's having a bad day. There was a family who waited this long too that just upped and left because they were tired of waiting. To be honest, it's not that busy here. Also, the shakes were in little styrofoam cups, not glass, how they are supposed to be. Also, hair in the food. Overall, slow service. Very slow.
Just got back from Naples Italy last week and happened to drive past Bavaros. Gave it a try and it is the Real Deal. Great Mozzarella, authentic Naples Margarita pizza and teriffic tiramisu.

I had high hopes for this place but it fell short on all ends. We made a reservation for our bachelorette party to come wine and dine. Our table was not ready (or thought of) when we arrived. After 15 minutes we sat down to eat. The truffle frites held us over before our entrees arrived. The flatbreads were HUGE, definitely shareable. I enjoyed the margarita flatbread to kick things off. I also ordered the burger which honestly sucked, it was bland & blah. Though we were sorely inconvenienced, the server and manager offered nothing to offset the burden. The server asked several of us if we wanted change after gratuity had been included on our bill. Asking if a patron wants change is an industry 'no no'. The restaurant was freezing cold and hard to sit in as well. I wouldn't return or recommend City Winery based on this experience.

Today I dropped my Honda off to get my lock repaired. I felt very assured that the work being done would be satisfactory. I initially spoke with Mark, Service Manager on Thursday April 20th who was very professional and caring. He gave me the utmost respect as I had many questions and concerns about the work being done. I arrived at the Dealership this morning and Mark, Service Agent was just as nice and professional. Mark got me connected with Enterprise Rental and now I'm home waiting on a call to pick my vehicle up. I'm sure the work that's being done will be satisfactory. Please give this dealership a chance because they trult have the customer service experience down. What a great feeling of ease I have being from the South. Not all Philadelphians are rough around the edges.
Thanks Piazza Honda, you guys rock!!!
Love love this salon ! Lindsay & Tara are the bomb ! Always happy with results. Been going there 10yrs .
Amazing sugar cookie.  The double pie inside the double cake thing - totally not worth the money.  Just too weird.

I had a business lunch here on Monday February 20th.  If your in the mood for cheap Mexican food at higher prices,this is your place.  I would call this fast food served slowly, I guess.  I do love Mexican food, but there are plenty of quality Mexican spots on the north side that are much better than this.  Abuelo's is to Mexican food what Red Lobster is to seafood.

A must try in New Orleans! I dragged my wife all over to try different fried chicken because it is one of her favorite dishes and I wanted to make sure she enjoyed the trip-tried coop's place, mother's, and willy mae's .  We kept coming back-the chicken and the sides were excellent! We were lucky we never had to wait in line that long and the servers were very friendly and helpful! The first time we thought we would take the trolley car out there and the walk was adventurous-when they say it is not in the touristy part of the city they are not lying.  We managed fine, although the are seemed a little deserted. The subsequent trips we took our rental car,  this was one of our trip highlights-we even packed some for home in our suitcase!

horrible...I know they are busy at 12:30 but...I got 2 whopper jr.'s.with fries..one whopper without onion or cheese...one without sauce,onion & tomato..the lettuce was missing...the burgers were overdone..as I waited for 10 min...I heard her tell him 3 different times how I wanted the burgers..[what to leave off]...3 times...I heard her repeat it 3 different times while I was waiting....really...and he still got it wrong...so ...special orders do upset them...lol .I could of done a better job & I am 68 ...won't go back there

My son is a student at UT and since taking a car there he has had to have several things done to the car- brakes, battery, oil change, broken door handle, etc.  Every time they have been helpful and the work has been great. Recommend them for your auto needs.

I've recently gotten into Vietnamese food and decided to give this place a try. I cant say that this place is better then any other as this was my first time going non pho which right next door Pho75 has the BEST pho i have ever tasted in my short career eating Vietnamese food. 

I would like to say that the woman that helped me was very helpful as I asked questions. She was very patient with me and a pleasure to speak with. I ordered the #2 BBQ pork hoagie without the cucumber. FANTASTIC!!! only $4 too. I also wanted to try something  else so I ordered the #20 Roast pork egg noodle soup. I'm in love. 

No need to think twice, just go. I'm looking forward to trying more from this place.
Honestly the best damn chicken parm sandwich I've had.  Hands down.  Make sure to ask for long hots if you like a little spice.

Brand new place!  Brand new faces!  That's what you get when you enter the new Kroger off of Franklin road.

They have almost all of the awesomeness of the Marketplace Kroger with exception to the clothes and home goods.

The feel is light and earthy since they put the organic and greens at the front of the store.  The walk through feels very natural and easy to navigate.  Having all of the non-food items like dog food and hygiene stuff at the far left side, makes going through your shopping list so much simpler.

They also have a Starbucks for your caffeine boost while roaming and doing your essentials.  Yum.

And if you're feeling hungry, they have their new food area (like the one at Marketplace) with Asian, American, Sushi, and some Mediterranean choices.

Definitely worth checking out if you live on the southeast side.
This location is nasty. Smoky. Not clean. Gross bathrooms. Decent daiquiris but get it to go. Ownership needs to spend some money and clean this place up.

My wife and I have been in Louisiana for about 3 months due to her having a medical procedure. We've been eating out the majority of the time. The Saltgrass Steak House has by far been THE best place we have been to. The service was outstanding. The whole staff was busy and engaged and seemed to really care about the customers. I had a surf 'n' turf and my wife had a filet. The food was outstanding. It was served hot and in a timely manner.  I highly recommend the Saltgrass Steak House. You won't be disappointed. We're looking forward to going back before we have to go back home. Thanks to the entire staff for a wonderful experience. 

R.H. (California)

I! Love! Old City Movers! We will never move with another company. We used them last year to move to a house and unfortunately our landlord sold it so we were on the move again. As soon as we found out we had to move I contacted Old City Movers before we even found a new house. They gave me a quote and were so prompt with responses. We were given the estimate of 8am-8:30am on the day of the move and the morning of we got a call at 7:45 saying they were 15 minutes away. They handled our belongings with care and made sure they understood where everything needed to go. They finished 2 hours faster than the estimate! Thank you Alex, Aaron, and Chris for a smooth move.
Very good. And the service was even better. Finally a place where people acted like they cared.
Usually great and relatively quick, but almost 2 hours for delivery is unacceptable. Food arrived cold, not even a little bit warm....  My delivery needs will be met by other places in the future.

Asked for a Pink French tip. Received dented unfinished nails. The nail artist seemed to be rushing the process and it came out just messy. All of my nails are uneven and dented. I do not recommend!

Edit: I went back. And they re-did them. They look much better. Free of charge.
Awesome service and employees. Very polite and professional. Never leaves a mess. Does leave me with thick green grass.

They rarely do anything right at this location. They can't even include the gravy (that comes with the chicken strip basket) in your order. They have horrible customer service... I would recommend spending your money ANYWHERE but here. It's a shame too because their chicken is good...but if you don't even get what you're supposed to get in your order, well then why bother??

I took a family heirloom ring to get resized and I'm happy I choose them! First off, the staff is extremely knowledgeable and friendly. Second, my ring was ready the next day!! They did a beautiful job and were able to buff out scratches on the ring as well. I found the price extremely reasonable. They also validate parking which is a bonus. I will definitely be returning for any jewelry needs I may have.

Right in my backyard and I admit I haven't been here in maybe 10 years.  

Recently on short notice I was invited to have sushi here for lunch. Pass up sushi ? No way !   And I'm so glad I didn't. I'm in and out of the Peppermill property all the time but never seem to hit Oceano. That will have to change.  

Located in the north front end of the property, this restaurant has been updated with a bright open atmosphere. The sushi bar is in the back and very large.  Whenever possible I always sit at the bar, I love watching the chefs work and can ask questions about their recommendations and where their fish comes from.  That day I had Resty as my chef, as well as 5 others at my end of the bar. Service was spot on. And he still had time to answer all our questions about the fishes and preparations. 

Wow, the mussels are probably the plumpest ones I've see in town with great flavor and tender.  The menu had some of the usuals from around town, but new and very different creations.  I tried the Baked Alaska and Salmon BTLA rolls. Loved the Alaska, the BLTA was interesting, I did dislike it but would have like more crispy salmon skin in it than bacon.  The nigiri I tried was fresh and on small thumbs of rice. Even the rice had a nice flavor to it.  

If you haven't already, add Oceano to your Reno AYCE sushi list.  Lunch $21 and $30.

I really like the concept here. Endless lunch was just what I needed. The steak and chicken hibachi was delicious. The steak was cooked just the way I like it, soft and juicy. I've definitely had better sushi but everything else was on point. Service was quick  and friendly will add this to my eat everything off the menu. Looking forward to my next visit

Cool experience. Terrible service. 

We went to Elliston Place Soda Shop with my brother when he was visiting Nashville for the first time! It's such a cute, fun, historical place to visit. And I did enjoy the soda counter options (we didn't eat). I had an egg cream - yum! My brother had a milkshake, and my fiance had a malt. Everyone liked what they ordered! But the service was terrible! We waited forever to order (even though it was pretty empty), and there were lots of staff around just kind of hanging out! 

It's a fun place to visit once - but it's definitely not a place I'd frequent! A place to bring people to when they're visiting Nashville!

Parkway has built a reputation over the years that still stands. The prices are high but the food is definitely worth it. After 25 years living in this crazy town I'd have to hand the Po-Boy trophy to Parkway. The variety and quality of the food here is second to none.

Rarely am I served PERFECT over easy eggs.  Crisp on the outside, liquid gold on the inside.  Most breakfast chefs will cook them on a grill too cold or hot, resulting in slimy or hard eggs.  These were absolutely the best eggs I've had in a decade.  Thanks Benedicts... your attention to detail, friendly service and charming atmosphere made my day!  I'll definitely be back and will bring family/friends with me next time!  :)

Ummm our first place to eat in Tucson.
It was okay. 
Walking up it sounded like a skateboard park was inside. So loud! It turns out it's a open room. No walls. (Half of it was) with games and fire pits and heat lamps. 
Very awesome set up. Very relaxed atmosphere.

Menu was very limited for the hubs and his gluten allergy. Price a little much. 
Drinking selection was okay, still not a lot on the gluten free side of things. Tried my first sour beer, Arizona brewed.... is it gluten free? It was really sour! (Tummy hurts now.....beer?) 
Took a A while to get our food. Maybe that leaves room for playing games and and talking. Had a back porch, front patio feel to it. 
I had the honey chicken, it was good. Normal mash potatoes and gravy with an okay slaw. 
Hubby had ribs, he said to much sauce on them but the flavor was good.
No dessert, because there were no options for him. Though what they did have looked yum.

Ok people, make sure you get the Ajiaco soup. This may be a Columbian restaurant but in my country, Guatemala, we make it too. And this was just like my abuelita's cooking. 5 stars on their soup, 4 stars on their ceviche (too tomato-y for me) and 5 stars for their ambience.
I got my first handgun from them 3 years ago and helped my father get his CCW too. Everyone i've dealt with is kind and helpful. Great used prices on all sorts of firearms.
Food is subpar and pricey they hire such young unexplerienced pple get more pple that are experienced.

Just ate here for the first time after hearing all the amazing reviews. Well lemme tell ya... It's worth it. The atmosphere is fancy but friendly, which is good because we didn't care how we looked (spent all day at the WWII museum which is awesome). Well first thing we ate was the BBQ oyster and that is a must. I've had oyster stuff before but it had a nice combination of spice and cheese and not overly cooked. 
I personally had the yellow fin tuna (medium rare) with a soy like sauce with it. The serving size was perfect and not overfilling at all. I was very satisfied. My parents had the red fish and lemon fish. Everything  was very delicious. 
Our service was speedy and Joe D. was excellent. Awesome at answering all questions and giving us suggestions. He deserves a big raise.

Super vegan restaurant in Broad Ripple that's located in a cute, hidden away spot. I usually get the smoothie bowls to go and have never been disappointed. The ingredients are always fresh and if you have questions just ask!

The parking lot is tiny, but you can also park on the street. I was a bit intimidated the first time I went, but it's super down to earth! 

I haven't tried the savory items yet, but the fruity meals are awesome!

I have been hyping myself up for John's since I started my cheesesteak hunt way back last fall. Their schedule is a bit hard to work around being that they always close at 5pm, but I was finally able to make it just in time before they randomly closed at 2 on a Friday. 

Despite an excruciatingly long wait (they were short of chefs), it was entirely worthwhile. The steak is by far Philadelphia's best. The roll is delicious, the meat is perfectly cooked, and the provolone melts beautifully throughout the sandwich. 

I took my family here after hearing all the great word of mouth, and they could not have been happier. If you're looking for the best steak Philly has to offer, then go to John's. There's simply nothing better.

2.5 - Came here when they first opened and felt the broth was clear but a bit soapy (lots of bubbles) and friends said they weren't feeling the broth for some reason or another. 

 Thought Id give it another try today and to my surprise, there were more than 3 patrons in the restaurant. (Everytime I come, about 5 ish times now or walk by, it's always pretty empty) i got the P11 and the broth was very brown, muckled (maybe I got the end of the pot) but the soup tasted funny! I left with half my bowl intact and hungry. How are you gonna call yourself a pho restaurant have have funny tasting pho. 

Also, service was kinda Slow. Maybe cuz we sat at the farthest booth but we werent checked on once after getting our food and didn't get our check. We just opted to pay at the counter and leave. I believe there was only 1 server for lunch. 

I may come back for the rice platter but for pho? Id go elsewhere.

Wanted a blow dry just because.     Oh, and on a Saturday morning.    Left a message through some form of electronic magic late Friday and got a call first thing on Saturday ... No problem!   Beautiful Blue washed my hair with an awesome head massage that removed all stress and Tommy blew it out giving me the volume I needed.     Good conversation during, a warm and welcoming vibe throughout this clean, modern and wonderfully air conditioned salon.    When I'm back in NOLA I'm coming back!

I tried to call them to reach a human being in several departments and couldn't get through for 10 minutes.  I finally decided to drive to Lowes which is moderately far from my home to get some help to find out what day and time my new washer would be delivered.  Customer service was very busy and I had to wait 10 minutes for help.  They found my order and I was told that they'd call me between 9 and 11am to tell me when they'd be delivering the washer the same day of the delivery.  Turns out though that they only had a 9:30 to 11:30am opening so I had to miss my yoga class.  They showed up on time and quickly removed my old, leaking washer and installed my new washer in no time.  Then they explained how to use it and told me to call if I was having any problems.  (Oh, please, please, don't make me have to call them)!  So far it's running super.  The installers were really nice and courteous.
It's a Denny"s. not much to say about it except moon over my hammy is the greatest.

This is a GREAT Mexican restaurant with super nice servers.  We used to go to places in Carmel where we live, but now drive out of our way to eat only here for Mexican food. The food is fresh and made from scratch.  They have a very broad menu and they only negative thing I could say is that it is hard to pick which of my favorites to order.  My husband loves the fajitas and the shrimp in garlic.  I love anything that includes their signature shredded beef, especially the Mexico City Pizza which is something unique . Their guacamole is superb.  Their margaritas and ice cold mugs of beer are a must.  We eat here regularly and it is always wonderful.  The portions are big and we enjoy the leftovers for lunch the next day.  Stop wasting your time and money at any other Mexican restaurant.
This is a Washington Redskins bar located in Ybor. They show all NFL games as well and have plenty of TV's. The wings are outstanding...all drums!  FYI they do allow smoking inside. HTTR!

I have always Loved QVC & order from them all the time! I recently had the worst experience ever. From web site issues, chat disconnecting me then not answering to calling customer service 3 times! I ordered 7 things that were lite & shipping was $30. After talking to customer service who was a girl with an attitude & no care way of speaking, I am so angry. When I called I was so frustrated so instead of diffusing the situation she met me with above nastiness.  I totally lost respect for QVC. Their shipping is ridiculous when u place an online order of a few things. Their customer service reps used to be so nice, now they don't even try to give you any satisfaction or even speak to you appropriately.  Like I said I lost respect for them now. Good luck if you run into any issues with your order.

Excellent meal, excellent service, so I am saddened to report one negative development that sort of puts a nasty dent in the whole experience for me. A short, dark haired employee (kitchen help or bus boy, I think) used the men's room and left  without washing his hands. Now, if he's not taking hygienic steps with a customer right in the room, do you imagine he ever washes his hands when alone? This may not bother others but it was very disturbing to me.
Crumbl is so worth the hype. I've had several of their flavors and they have all been amazing. My favorites are Churro and the classic iced sugar cookie!!

I followed Shannon here and I'm so happy that I did.  Everyone at the salon is so nice and make you feel welcome.  It's a very relaxed atmosphere.  It's evident that everyone enjoys being there.  Shannon is fantastic and knows exactly how to deal with my hair.  Her coloring skills are amazing.  I always leave looking and feeling like a million bucks!  Shannon is well worth every penny, and she is very reasonably priced for the services offered.  The salon offers a great variety of "must-try" products.  I would highly recommend paying the salon a visit.  You will be happy that you did!

well here we go again few months back they had the same problem can't find any decent employees. 
at the time I even called customer service and we're going to look into it and they admitted the app that store doesn't have enough people to open up the dining room I just laughed my ass off I said that just a joke the reviews really do coincide the last year or so...I guess some are locked up some don't want to work who knows but the lobby was closed in the summer time for at least a week straight..here it is Super bowl Sunday walk up there from the hotel at 6:30 in the afternoon there's another sign lobby is closed drive-thru only you can't walk up to the drive-thru cuz they won't serve you this is become a shittier and shittier problem with this Krystal.. yet they don't seem to want to do much about it however I can say over the last year the quality is gone right down the toilet and that's a real shame..you can't even walk in to get something to eat because they don't have anybody working sorry when I have enough people to work to open the dining room it's a small little dining room which is an absolute joke how you can if somebody walk out and take a look at it you keep the other door locked all the time anyway that's a cop-out and stupid..that's why I lots of other crystals when you tell him about this lousy one in lavergne just sit there and laugh they were not surprised especially given the main manager here nobody wants to work for her anyway I could see why..

Great place to go for happy hour! $3 drafts and even though the food wasn't discounted, it was totally affordable. The tapas are definitely worth getting, especially the veggie quesadilla. Will come back again!

The New Mexican a style cuisine here is damn good. I love the breakfast, especially with a bloody mary. Yum! The green and red sauces are delicious but SPICY so if you don't like spice consider the sour cream green chili sauce, or half and half. The staff are pretty great too.

I used to love New Samosa, until it broke my heart. Last year, I had a habit of popping by for lunch once a week. It provided a whole buffet and menu full of options, which as a vegetarian, came as a rare treat. Also, the salad bar was always fresh.  In recent months I'd found myself too busy to keep up with my New Samosa weekly ritual. When I found myself in the neighborhood yesterday, I had to stop by. What a shocker! They now serve meat and half of the buffet items I had previously enjoyed had been nixed in favor of meat dishes - which were NOT clearly labeled. There was not even any veggie pakora. The lettuce on the salad bar was starting to wilt and brown and the tomatoes were also beginning to go bad. The raita didn't look too hot either. To top it all off, they raised the price of the lunch buffet by $1. I don't know if they are under new management, but this wasn't the same restaurant I made my weekly lunch spot last year. Unfortunately, I don't think I'll be going back. I need to find a new Indian spot with decent veg options and a fresh salad bar.

Bottom Line: Overpriced 
Mediocre Service
Above average food!!

On a number of occasions, the wait staff have been rude and unfriendly. For $60 a person (without wine), you would think the waiter would know who ordered what when serving the food. In fact, the waiter misserved not only the entree but also the dessert!!! INEXCUSABLE! 

The Lucky Burger, the Onion Rings, and the potato skins are excellent. The mac and cheese, salads and soups are not far behind. 

Overall, could be better considering the price point.

NewsomEye is a group of top professionals.  My appointment was thorough, timely AND I was offered a very conservative approach to care.  A big shout out to Dr. Ochs and his staff.  The optical store was not in any way pushy when I chose to delay my purchase.  Super-impressed by the whole experience.

This place never disappoints. I had dinner here last night, with a party of 10, and the staff didn't miss a beat. We were able to get a private section with a short 10 minute wait. Our server was quick on her feet and made sure all of us were taken care of. Portions are so large, I was only able to eat half of my meal! Oh, and the cheesecake was of course the highlight of the evening.

If you like bacteria with your roast beef, look no further! Don't try any sauces here. There's a fly infestation, probably from no one ever actually cleaning where the sauce comes out. Same goes for the soda machine, where the lids and straws are too. Imagine what the grill looks like in the back!
I won't even get into how ignorant the cashier was, after walking in and every employee was hiding in the back for five minutes. How does that even happen?
Anyway, go get Taco Bell or something next time. Sheesh!

Went with my wife to enjoy several rolls off their regular menu. Cheaper priced and cheaper quality sushi. 

Pros: friendly staff, quick service. Decent shrimp tempura.

Cons: Sushi tasted and looked old (crumbling imitation crab and falling apart spicy tuna). The raw sushi was room temp which made it seem like it might have been laying around for a long time . The cooked sushi was also served room temp - if you're all about that warm cooked sushi or cold fresh sushi, this isn't the place for you. You could tell it's all you can eat - you sacrifice quality for price. 

Gluten: If you are gluten free, they do not offer gluten free soy sauce (at least we weren't) and the server did not know bread had gluten. Eat here with caution.

Didn't make it to ordering when outdoor seating is easily able to clean off seeing as its plastic. You make my dog uncomfortable that's no hospitality for me. Seeing the establishment right next door does not question the pet friendly environment. I did like the courtesy discount at check in but nothing can evolve from poor hosting. Newsflash, there is a dog park around the corner which is a big avenue for marketing sales. But it all starts with great staffing and hospitality.

always a great reputation, well deserved, and a really nice indoor restaurant. The location is awful unless you work on Tulane nearby Charity - perhaps that explains why it's never crowded - which helps when eating inside. Street parking is available, again because there are few patrons. But the food! Awesome. Definitely among the very top when it comes to Vietnamese food. and the service is super too. Bring a mask!
Great place to eat! All of the tacos, burritos etc.. contain fresh ingredients. Also love the chipotle hot sauce.

I was staying at the hotel for a work trip and ordered the half chicken and was blown away! Cooked to absolute perfection: juicy and savory with a crispy skin. So good I ate the whole thing.

I also ordered an Old Fashioned with a Canadian rye the bartender recommend. It was great.

The whole experience was fantastic and far better than I'd expected from a hotel restaurant. I'll come back for sure.

Why, B. Hughes, Why? I really wanted to have an amazing experience here! My wedding is going to be in Nashville in the fall so I figured I should look for a bridal shop there. I searched online and found that B. Hughes was the only shop in town that carries couture wedding gowns, so I guess this was automatically the only place to look.

I called a few weeks in advance to make an appointment for when I was flying out for the holidays. During that phone call I asked to see if they had a specific Oscar de la Renta dress I was dying to try on infront of my family, and she said "no, but if you pay a fee (either $100-$200) we can have the designer send a sample for you to try on." Are you kidding me??? So then I ask well, would you credit me that money back if we bought a dress at your store, only to hear the response "well we credit it back if it's the specific dress they send, otherwise no." Obviously that rubbed me the wrong way...

Being from Los Angeles, I had already gone to try on dresses at the actual designers' stores (Oscar de la Renta, Vera Wang, etc) but this trip was more about sharing the experience with my family and possibily going ahead and purchasing the dress. I bring my parents, who live in Nashville, and two of my bridesmaids that also flew in for this experience..... From the moment we walked in, it was all wrong. I don't believe the girl helping me was much older than 21, and I'm not knocking age but she seemed very inexperienced to be solo-working with people who are purchasing a $10k+ gown. We weren't offered water, coffee, champagne or anything. Before even asking who the bride in the group was she told us to start looking at dresses and that she would pull the ones I wanted to try on,. Then she proceeded to walked out of the area and left us..... Really? Okay, whatever but by doing this, this girl doesn't know if I have a budget, she doesn't know if there is a specific style / look I'm going for, she doesn't know my venue or if I'm getting married inside or outside, let me state this again, SHE DOESN'T EVEN KNOW WHICH ONE OF US IS THE BRIDE.... And she just let's us loose with no guidance? That's your only job! I don't work in bridal wear, but I can tell you the first rule is to ask the budget because you don't want to put a girl in a dress that she falls in love with only to find out it's a $20,000 dress and her budget was really $2,500. Am I the only person whose seen "Say Yes to the Dress"?!?!?

I found a couple of dresses to try on, most of them were from 2008 - 2013 collections. They still had the ball gown from Bride Wars on the main rack.... hello 2009! At this point I knew this wasn't going to be where I found my dress, but yet again I wanted to share this experience with my family and friends, so I went back to try on gowns. I came out in the first gown, my mother was smiling ear to ear and pulled out her phone to take a picture of me in the gown. Then you guessed it, inexperienced girl very smuggly says "I'm sorry, no photos allowed, it's store policy and our designers don't allow it." I took hundreds or photos in the actual Oscar de la Renta store, pretty sure that's just your policy. There are no signs posted anywhere in that store, so how are we supposed to know? I get it, you don't want your clients taking pictures and then going out and finding a better store to purchase the dress at, but come on, they have a monopoly on the whole Nashville / middle TN market place! 

My advise, if you're spending this kind of money on a dress anyways, go to Atlanta, Chicago, NY or LA and buy. They have hundreds of more dresses to look at and try on and your experience will be 100x better. And remember ladies, when buying your dress you are also gaining a bridal consultant that you will have to be in contact with for 6-9 months before your wedding and they are in charge of making sure the most imporant dress you will ever wear is perfect. I would never trust these people with that responsiblity and suggest you don't either. I'm so happy this wasn't my first experience trying on and that I knew better. If not, I would never want to try on dresses again.

I have gone to this nail salon many times but I will never step foot in this place ever again! I came in with my mother in law to have a few pedicures and at first I was really surprised that they didn't even say more than four words to us and continued talking among themselves in their language which really doesn't bother me. What does bother me is that they were talking about me, and laughing! I am allergic to mosquito bites and my legs are quite scarred. My pedicurist actually pointed at my scars and turned to the pedicurist beside her and said something in her language, he even stopped what he was doing and looked at my scars. She then laughed hysterically! I have never seen anything so rude in my life! I may not understand what you are saying but I can read body language and I'm not fucking stupid!

I am sooooo happy we finally have a CFA close to my house!! They are new, so the drive thru was out of the question when we pulled up, lol. It was literally stretching across the kmart parking lot. But the inside was moving right along, business as usual. Customer service was absolutely excellent; I am praying it stays that way!! The employees were in the dining area constantly cleaning and asking guests if they needed anything.
Very good food. Big fan of the Nachos all Carbon...steak, chicken and shrimp on a bed of chips with melted white cheese!

This Waffle House was so nice. I have never been to a cleaner Waffle House. The staff was very polite. The service was fast. We were able to get in and sit. The place didn't have dirty floors or tables. We even asked our server for Togo plates while she was sweeping and she politely said yes and washed her hands with soap before touching anything else. 
The food was cooked very well and tasted delicious.

The vermicelli bowls are very good. I have had better Pho in Metairie. The fried egg rolls were good, as well as the spring rolls. The staff is very nice and the place does have a good following. All in all, the food and experience are ok. Nothing great but not bad either.

It's been a while since I stopped by and had a bite and had heard someone talking about how they had remodeled a while back so I thought it was time to take a peak. The remodel turned out good! Basically the same layout and a different color scheme. Today we tried the street tacos and finger steaks. Both were good but a little lacking. The chicken was just diced chicken without a ton of flavor but it was tender. The rest of the taco was really good with some good spice and flavors. The corn tortillas were warmed up and crispy which was a nice touch. The finger steaks were super tender but a little on the batter was undercooked and on the soggy side. Service was amazing and friendly!
Be careful when dealing with these guys!  They will not honor an advertised price or the listed price on the window.  Shop somewhere else!
This is a delightful spot! Good coffee, wonderful atmosphere, and delicious treats (try the Samoa cookie!).

Oh what a sweet spot total pun intended.

The two baristas that were working in the morning of Sunday, July 2 were phenomenal. They were just chatty enough, very friendly, and just had a nice welcoming warmth to them.

Their pastry selections for gluten free and vegan options were plentiful.

Of course, it being New Orleans, alcoholic coffee drinks were on the menu as were your basic coffee pours. Justin and I loved our lattes and enjoyed sitting in the chairs that opened up to the street to watch the slow morning movements on the streets.

It was a great vibe inside and out. Very charming.

Tried out LOS while at the Night Market at the Italian Market.  Since the Night Market is an awesome, gluttonous affair, I only had room in my belly for some french fries with the bacon mayo dipping sauce.  

The portion size was plenty for me and my husband to share- although I can see how someone may think $3 for the portion is a bit expensive- I thought it was fine.  The fries themselves were very brown, crunchy, and piping hot, which I enjoyed.  The dipping sauce was super delicious and tasted 100% homemade.

Next time I see LOS, I would like to get one of their burgers- esp since they use grass fed beef

Mediocre at best.  Nobody around to seat us (daughter actually walked in back after 5 minutes of us listening to employees laughing / goofing off).  Once we got seated our waitress seemed "offended" that we interrupted them so her attitude was blasé the whole time.  Messed up my cheesesteak order (requested no onions - got onions - theyre chopped into the meat so can't remove them) - food was average to me, but my daughter REALLY liked her cheeseburger so rhats why my "average" will lean to 3 stars instead of 2.  Won't go out of my way to go back.
Good and great. The pho made with beef broth very oily...the hush puppies burnt but such interesting things gs on the menu and great staff that I will try it sgsin.

Food and music was good, but our waitress was horrible and not friendly at all. Every time we talked above a whisper or whistled after a singers performance we were reported by the table behind us for a noise complaint. Then our server would come over and threaten to kick us out. Had a bad over all experience because of the table behind us and the not so friendly server. If you're planning on coming here don't plan on being able to converse with your table, and quite literally just listen the entire time.
The service was excellent. Mushi is an prompt and courteous server. She was kind and patient (I have a hearing loss.) I'll be back!

This store is massive.    It used to be A Sam's Club, but they had no problem filling every book and cranny with stuff.

It wasn't too busy before Christmas and the store is laid out well enough that you won't spend an hour in here finding what you need and checking out.

This car wash is a joke. I wrote a review about the poor quality of the 3.00 car wash but gave them some credit as to getting what you pay for. Today I decided to get the top 15.00 wash to get all the winter salt off my SUV. The wash was crap!!! Back hatch door not even touched by water and the wheels were suppose to be cleaned but they were still a mess. Areas of the car still looked dirty considering it was just washed!!!The vacuum is also crap suction and filthy hoses. Look at your hands after touching one of their hoses. You get dirt all over your hands after using their vacuum hoses. Its free but you get what you don't pay for as well. Its tight between cars using the vacuums as well and since people are selfish  by nature they keep their car doors wide open so its hard to move around vehicles. Not worth 15.00 at all.

Yes, yes, a hundred times yes. This place warmed my heart. My friend and I sat at the bar facing the kitchen and everyone was so friendly. We got to talk to chefs a bit and both them and our server were wonderful. Even with it being incredibly busy everyone was relaxed, professional and even had fun with each other. Great atmosphere and prices were true to what you get with most plates being under $20 for high quality/not the biggest portions.

We got the sheeps milk ricotta app, pork ragu pasta, and the star of the night... the octopus. The ricotta app was delicious (duh) and a perfect amount. Pork ragu was not the most spectacular and portion was a little small but still enjoyable. The octopus I could eat every day for a year and still be happy having it that much. It came sliced up in this flavorful lemony mediterranean salad and a tasty white bean puree. The presentation and portion was great on this and the octopus was cooked perfectly. Everything else that was going out of the kitchen looked wonderful and I cant wait to come back here sometime. Seemed like an ideal date spot.

AMAZING!!! 

We showed up with a group of 6 adults, 3 kids under 4 and no reservation and they didn't even flinch. 
Someone quickly arrived with 2 high chair and colorful plastic place settings for the kids. Perfect!!

We were hungry and distracted with the kids so our lovely host recommended some items and placed the order for us, we really had no idea what was coming. 
Throughout the meal there was a team of people making sure our waters and tea were never empty and made sure we had everything we needed. Instantly clearing the empty serving dishes. 

It was delicious!!! Enough that all of us left with full bellies and had one little box to take home.  They also had the cutest little coconut bunny desserts for the kids. 

We got the bill and could not believe how reasonable it was. 

This is must go and will be a regular destination for us.

We have stayed at Maison Dupuy on our last 2 trips to NOLA. The staff is friendly. We will be travelling to NOLA once per month for the next year and will probably stay at Maison Dupuy. They have been kind enough to cover our parking cost for us so far and I'll be calling to see if we can get a better rate since we'll be there so often. It is just off N. Rampart, so be careful when walking at night, and don't go any further toward Rampart after dark. 
Love the hotel--it's a home away from home.
Extremely good food and great service!! We will be back next time we are in the area!!

0/10. Do. Not. Go. Took about 6 years to order my food/drink and another 12 years to get the items. There are only the two of us in the place. And the came out wrong. If you are going to take forever to make the food at least make it correct. Please go to The Dunkin on Old York rd in Abington near the McDonald's. This place sucks

These guys got themselves an extra star for being able to handle a large group, such as mine. I was in a group of about 24 people. They had to split us up into three tables, but they were able to handle the tables nicely and somewhat keep up with the drinks being full.

Other than that huge compliment, I will say that I was not too impressed by this place. It could have been the time of day, but most of the food that was out had been made for a little bit. Which wasn't bad, but once they were out they were out. We went at a later time of the day so there was no reason to make another batch in their mind if they had to pitch half of it. The food options were also similar to other Chinese buffet restaurants in the area. So, I was not overly excited about the selection. The one bonus I guess would be the hot pot. However, I did not have that. Thus I cannot tell you what it tastes like. 

I will say. If you would like for some good buffet food, go for it. It wasn't bad at all. Though, it's nothing super amazing to write home about.

I used to love this place.  The pretzel bites were amazing and kept me coming back!  Just recently visited for a rare dinner out and they changed the way they make them.  It is basically fried dough that resembles something you would get a the fair.  Made me and my husband sick.  Probably will not be back if this is the new norm.

Great bar service, even when packed.  They always seem to have a great band or singer playing as well.   Have only tried a limited amount of food but it was good each time.    This is a one of the places I look forward to when I visit Nashville.    My only advice to the management is to try and keep the bathrooms cleaner.
Glad we tried this place -a little slow to get our food, but that's because they cook to order and was well worth our wait. Philly cheese steak was delicious!

I needed a new nails place sense the girl I've been going to for years moved out of state. I found this place on here and saw the great reviews and decided to give them a try. I had my nails done by Pam and she did an amazing job!

This place is not same without former bartenders the new ones are cool but just not the same experience. Shani and Tameka had the bar moving getting orders in and making drinks with quickness. 

I am sure I am not the only one missing their customer service and professionalism find them and bring them back ASAP.

I've had my fair share of spicy food. Bolton's was truly a unique experience - outrageously hot dry rub that will leave your mouth scorched and your entire body feeling like a bubbling volcano for the next few hours. 

On recommendation from a friend, I decided to go for the "hot" chicken leg. Normally, I'd be a little adventurous and go for the extra hot, but decided to listen to the reviews and stick with hot. It definitely did not disappoint. In fact, it was MUCH hotter than I expected - the instant the dry rub hits your tongue, the heat kicks in, makes its way to the back of your throat, and stays there as you whimper, re-evaluate your decision, and decide to go for another bite because it's really just that delicious. 

This place is a must do for the spice enthusiast!

Delicious! It's about time Madison, TN gets a decent, affordable, authentic place! The staff (shout out to our waiter, Frank) are extremely friendly and the food is incredible!! The phone number is now programmed in my phone...We go every week and if we don't make it, we call in!  I highly recommend this  place!!

I'm usually a fan of warrior because every time I've been the staff has been laid back and fairly nice. Today my service was terrible. I came in just to buy another bar for my conch because I had lost the screw on ball. The guy behind the counter kept pushing me to buy other items and I politely refused and stated multiple time I just wanted to buy the same bar (gauge and length) he then proceeded to force me to have the piercer put the jewelry in for me. It's a nice free service but I don't see the problem in letting someone leave after making a purchase. Neither the piercer or the person who sold me the jewelry showed me what the actual piece looked like so I assumed it was what I asked for... the same bar with the same length and balls on each end. It was dumb of me to trust either of the people because when I came home and checked the piece had a flat back and a shorter bar.. exactly what I told the guy I did NOT want. So I'm pretty pissed and disappointed since I came to warrior over infinite because I thought it'd be more laid back but I was wrong and it wasn't worth it.
The best Po-Boys . I go there almost every week . Love it! Best corn beef around , could compete with NYC's Katz Deli . Prices more than fair.

FIVE stars for GleamClean! I have been trying to clean certain spots in our house for years with little luck, and GleamClean got them sparkling like new. They literally cleaned every inch of our house from the inside of our toaster, to the outside of our hand-soap dispensers, and everything in between. We could not be happier with how great they did and we definitely plan to have them return to work their magic. And it truly was magic how they got rid of some spots around our sink handles and not a speck of dust remained on our blinds.  We have tried many other companies and the results were not nearly as good as GleamClean. Also, Arturo was extremely easy to work with on scheduling and payment. He was very professional and scheduled a cleaning for us with a few days notice.
Gorgeous. The sand is the softest there is. I am a go resident and I love this beach. My favorite time to go would be in the evening because it's significantly less busy.

All four of my children have gone to Flanagan's Preschool in Plymouth Meeting. The staff is great and they have a really high retention rate with their teachers. They are very accommodating to every child's individual needs and have a great curriculum. My two older ones that graduated entered into kindergarten already reading.  My kids are always happy to be dropped off in the morning because they have so much fun.
Decent food and solid service. Nothing super outstanding as far as Philadelphia dining experiences go.

I called Automotive Sound and Protection in Norristown PA to inquire about installation dates before I purchased my Groupon.  They informed me of install availability and told me to purchase through them instead of the Groupon site and took a $50 deposit from me.  They then informed me the price for my vehicle would be significantly higher than the $119 Groupon price due to the vehicle type.  I told them to cancel and refund my $50, Heather who is one of the owners then told me "to go F*** myself".  To this point there has been no refund.  I wish I would have read some reviews prior to calling them as it is easy to see the consistent pattern of complaints.  As a business owner myself I always want other businesses to succeed but I think it is time for Automotive Sound to look in the mirror and make some serious changes if they wish to survive in a competitive industry.  I would run from this business as it is obvious they have serious issues!

Best Italian in the area......'nuff said!

Best to visit on a week night - weekends are a bit too crazy.

Deep dish is an overrated concept period. It was nasty when I had it in Chicago, and it was merely tolerable at Pi. Doesn't matter where you make it, deep dish is just super-sized and super-greased pizza with stuffing that falls apart too easily.

Other than that, Pi is a decent pizza place that charges too much for being nothing-too-special. I prefer California Pizza Kitchen better.

My favorite part of the meal was, of course not the overpriced pizza, but the salted caramel ice cream they import from Serendipity.
Sushi bistro is great!!! My favorite sushi restaurant in St. Louis by far! Fantastic quality fish!
Food is excellent but the service is terrible they disappear on you and don't check up on you. I had to go upfront and tell the manager

I tried this last week just because my friend got a new job there and has been working there for a couple months now. Honestly, it was really good. No complaints, they just put a little bit more rice around it to fill you it.... which I guess is a good thing because it's a little more expensive than some places. But all in all, I would def go again, but most likely on a date. lol

This is my second time here. First time was for dinner second time was for brunch on a Saturday. First and foremost I should note that valet is not complimentary during dinner hours it's a requirement to pay 5dollars for parking due to not having anywhere else to park but the 5dollar valet. Personally, I feel the 5$ valet is rediculous and should be complimentary no matter what hour of the day. It's out dated and down right appalling for a place to REQUIRE you to pay 5 dollars to park in order to eat at there establishment. How dare you!?  

Dinner was ok- over priced did not at all feel farm to table. 

Brunch was once again just ok. Drinks were over poured which to an alcoholic or a regular drinker might be appreciated but to someone who doesn't drink on the regular - I just want a normal pour. A well balanced specialty cocktail would be greatly appreciated. It seems as if the bartender on both night did NOT know how to mix a drink and for dinner I had to send back 3 drinks and just settle for water that's how badly mixed the drinks were. 

Over all this place has great potential- these are just growing pains- it's there choice to learn from there mistakes or not. I'll come back in about a year to update this review with hopes of it improving immensely.

A little behind on the review as I was in a few weeks ago.  An old friend and I always like to try something new, or old in this case as the place has been around longer than I have.  We were in for lunch and it was pretty busy but service was good.  We both had a burger that had a nice char grilled taste and was clearly hand formed there.  Toppings were fresh too.  We both agreed we will be back again before trying something new.

While I was traveling in New Orleans, my son called from his work and urgently needed a notarized authorization letter from me.  I found this UPS store which offered notary public and FAX.  When I walked in there were 3 people busy working. I stood in front of the counter for more than 5 minutes before a man (whom I think was the owner) finally waited on me. 

I got the letter notarized and they FAXed it for me.  After several minutes, my son still didn't receive the fax.  The man showed me the FAX log to prove it was successfully sent and asserted the receiving end was the problem.  However my son said the machine in his office was working because it had several FAXes came in during this time.  After 15 minutes the FAX was still not received.  The UPS man retrieved the FAX job from the machine's memory and resent it.  Another 10 minutes went by, my son still didn't receive the FAX.  Now the owner said nothing he could do about it.  I decided to take a picture of the letter with my mobile and texted it to my son.  Fortunately the picture was accepted by the staff my son was dealing with.

Later my son figured out what was going on by checking the sending phone #'s of the jobs coming into his machine.  The UPS store did FAX but they sent wrong file (someone's inventory) from their computer.  When they re-sent, the same file was sent again.  Both my son and I wasted 30 minutes of our time and $2.50 FAX fee for nothing. 

Also I think the $20 notary fee was pretty high.  The several UPS stores in my town charge only $2 per page!

Went for a family brunch. Two guests ordered eggs benedict, food was cold. Eggs overcooked. Not at all what we expected after reading good reviews. Guests were happy with their breakfast burritos. Said they were " okay" and they were served hot.  Definitely nothing to return for. We were disappointed.

Dropped by this place on 7/20/13 to check out the new ownership and just to have beer on the deck. I have to say that I will NOT be stopping by any time soon. This place has no idea what they are doing. I orderd a half Guinness /Half Cider located on the bottom of the drink menu, afte about 5 mins the waitress came out and asked if I wanted Two drinks? I showed her the menu and explained what I wanted again, when she came out I had a glass of just Cider! I sent it back and for the THIRD time told her what I wanted. About 20 mins into ordering these drinks she finall got it right. Another couple sat down next to us and waited over 45 mins for their dinner and had to cancel cause the meal didnt show and they were going to a movie in less then 15mins. THe four top of individuals on the other side of us was complaining out getting there desserts and they had been waiting over 30 minutes????? Not a single table around us was happy. It took more that 25 mintues to get our check and the waitress said you can just leave your beers are on me. Needless to say we did pay our bill and left. This place needs some management skills. Its a great location, the outside patio is awesome but service is poor!
Easily my favorite nail place in Philly. Be sure to make a reservation: the shop is small so they do fill up. (And this is seemingly the only place that gives a back rub while your nails dry!)

Boise is growing up! Island Sushi definitely met my ramen craving tonight. 

The miso ramen with chashu (pork belly) was delicious. My husband had a lobster roll and a shrimp roll and both were delicious. 

All together, we ate for under $35, so you get a great deal for your money.

Food is good. Waiters are friendly.

The one suggestion I would give the owner is to split the four seat tables into 2x two seat tables; most of the four seaters were occupied by only one or two guests.

Ricky and his crew re-routed our kitchen/washing machine lines in 3 days!  That is allot of digging, grading, leveling and the whole crew did a great job!  

We have used Ricky and his crew for all our rootering for many years now -  we would never call anybody else!

Just call them - they are the best!

Though the food is decent, and the drinks are strong, the service is horrible. The only time we saw our server is when the food came out. We tried catching her later on to order more food, and it seems she forgot about us. By the time she came back by us it was like 30 minutes later, by then we were done. The food seems to be good and the service needs some work. Been a long time since I tipped below 20%
Great salsa bar. Spice it up to taste. The tacos are worth the 3.50-.75. They are medium sized. Tortillas were fresh.

My boyfriend and I ate here on Thursday (12/27/18) around 8:30pm. Service was ok, the bar tender messed up his drink, he ordered a Manhattan and it was brought to us in a tall glass with what seemed to be st germain or some floral liquor added to it. My pasta was over cooked, they messed up my boyfriends steak so, he had to wait even longer for a new one. Once his steak came out (med-rare) it was so fatty he ended up cutting away at it and having a tiny piece of maybe 4 bites left? The waiter was apologetic and very kind, however, the manager never came by to apologize. We saw the manager walk by a few times and seemed to hang around the bar talking to the bar tender more than actually working. The waiter did offer us free cheesecake. The only good thing about this place is the cheesecake. It was excellent. Everything else was absolute crap. Never going back for dinner, maybe will stop by for some cheesecake, but never at this particular location.

I attended the Nero show over the weekend, and the bartender pretty much killed my night with her rude attitude, and complete lack of bar-tending knowledge.  i wish i got her name, but i was too upset to want to deal her. 

Don't get me wrong, the venue is amazing, the sound was great and the food was pretty good for a concert hall.   But... when it comes to the drinks, the place was a complete rip off.  I order a double jack and coke- yes, a DOUBLE jack and coke- because the main act was coming on and i didn't want to have make several trips to the mini bars located upstairs.   I payed $14.50 for this drink; which is reasonable for the order.   What I got was a piss-ant 3 second pour of some watered down Jack Daniels.  The female bartender, located by the bathroom upstairs, who made the drink claimed that she gave me a double jack, but i couldn't even taste the jack.  I asked for a little more seeing as i paid FIFTEEN DOLLARS for the drink but the bartender claimed she gave me a double pour. I bar-tend in DC, and I don't know how things work in Philly, but a 3 second pour is barely even a shot.   If she is on your pay roll as a bartender, you should demote her right away and make her a dishwasher.     

I don't know if this is a company policy to short the living hell out guests or not, but please take into consideration that this can ruin peoples nights, and certainly keep them from coming back.    Besides the disgraceful bartender, the venue would get 5 stars.
Clothes were okay, but the staff was extremely rude, just like Plato's next door.

As a member for many years I canceled my membership today!! My bad customer service originally began when an attendant hit my car door against the brick wall chipping my paint. I didn't notice the missing paint until I got home. I did think this was an accident and would touch up the door myself. When I went back I asked to have my car go through the middle bay, to be vacuumed, I explained to her why I didn't want to go through the first bay. She said that it wasn't them who chipped my paint!  She was very rude!! I wanted to leave, but I had a car behind me so I went through. After speaking to a manager that day in person, he apologized.  I still I felt uncomfortable going back when this attendant was checking me in. She obviously knew I spoke with the manager. 

I have had spotty wash jobs from time to time and just thought, well I will come back and have it redone .  Never a reason to complain especially since I had a membership. 

I recently purchased a newer car. The last time I was in the attendant put a new sticker on my windshield. Today, I asked if the sticker could be moved down and was told it was their policy to have it that high (above the black on the windshield) I told the manager it wasn't that high on my last car, again he stated it was their policy. I asked if there was anything that could be done and was told no. Yes it's a sticker, but I do not pay a monthly fee of $70 to be a driving advertisement for Mister! Needless to say I will be taking my business down the road!

Ok I'm usually not one to update reviews or change my mind but in this instance I must. Let's once again start with service, Danielle was our waitress and she was very attentive any communicated with us very well. My wife ordered the Mediterranean chicken and was very happy with it. The manager even came out and told us that they had run out of the sauce for that plate. He was extremely polite and respectful I was very impressed.
