I had the turkey burger and it was delicious the fries are exactly how I like it. I'm sure we will visit again and I appreciate the night we had thanks to the service and food served to us today. Thank you very much.

Food was delicious. The manager was very helpful in getting us a seat when it was very busy. This place is always packed since it's new so the wait times for tables are a little ridiculous but I really like the place. Fun vibe inside, cute, old fashioned diner style decor. Overall, had a great experience eating there.

Not like I remember back in the day. The cheese steak wasn't to bad but the cheese taste fake.  The Bronco Burger is real good but the fries and Onion rings where soggy. I wouldn't rush to go back but maybe in a few months I would. Kinda pricey as well.

I saw their Groupon for a $20 haircut so I called before purchasing to see if they could fit me in the same day.  The woman I spoke with was friendly and accommodating and told me not to buy the Groupon- that they would honor the price.  The space is small but clean.  I had a great shampoo, quick but efficient cut and a great blowdry.  It's only $20 for first-time customers , after that it's $38.  I was skeptical and usually pay 10x that for my hair but this worked out great!  I definitely recommend them.

We booked this hotel solely based on its location. 
We were there for a week for a very difficult situation. They did not know the reason for our stay.  We had 3 rooms (5 people).  We were always congregating in the lobby breakfast area morning and night.  The staff at this hotel, primarily Katie in the mornings and Victoria in the evenings, could not have been more AMAZING!!   They overheard one of us getting up to get an extra chair and before we knew what was happening the chair was already coming to us.  They were always offering us waters, and sometimes just bringing them to us because they knew we needed them.  There was even a takeout pizza brought over to us because it was late, we were tired and hungry and Victoria must have known.  As the week went on we opened up to them about the reason for our stay... but before they knew why we were there, they were already amazing.  Every staff member that we spoke to from the breakfast lady to the cleaning staff and beyond was so pleasant.   As a family, we can not thank everyone enough for making the worst week of our lives a little more bearable...sometimes even a little fun!  I HIGHLY recommend this hotel because of the amazing staff that they have.

First off, let me say this is one of my favorite places to visit because of their spectacular outside dining area. The bay breezes, even on the hottest summer nights, make for a wonderful experience.  They have the largest assortment of yummy cocktails with speciality drinks including many mojitos, margaritas and more. I enjoyed their house margarita and it didn't disappoint; it was perfect on a warm summer night! I shared some of their nachos which had the perfect amount of cheese....I am all about the cheese!!! Beef, beans, jalapenos, and sour cream deliciousness.  For my main entree, I ordered their fish tacos with plaintains. I love the carmelized, smushy goodiness of a perfectly fried plaintain!!! The fish tacos were large and filing. The fish was battered and fried to perfection. The taco wasn't over done, you enjoyed the fish with every bite. Prices are good, I felt you got your money's worth.

Lee is the most wonderful cobbler ever, and just generally an awesome person to be around! Taking my shoes in usually also means gossip time with Lee. So happy to have found someone trustworthy and kind without having to trek all the way Uptown.

The kitchen needs an update... My chicken sandwich was bland and the roll was stale.  I couldn't finish it..

The service and decor is nice... But the food is not up to par with typical irish pub fare in the city.  If you are a tourist only come here for drinks not food.

I come here often and I can't help getting the quart of water ice every time.  They always have rotating flavors and I gotta get my frequent buyers card stamped to look forward to that free quart.  I don't like that Rita lady anyway.

My wife and I enjoy the food from Tiffin, and would like to be more frequent customers, but the service is regularly terrible. I realize they are busy on weekends, but all I ask for is a realistic wait time for pick up. They consistently state pick-up times of 20-30 minutes, and then the food isn't ready 50 minutes later, but they're working on they last item. 

That said, both the Butter Chicken and Tikka Masala are delicious.
Still the best place in Reno to eat if you have food allergies or need to eat clean, well-made DELICIOUS food.

I know.  In 2012 I made a case for the fifth star.  I can see the logic of my argument, but after last night's experience I have to say that it is not all about the oysters.  It is also about what accompanies the oysters.  And in this case what accompanies the oysters is just too much: too much noise, too much salt, too much waiting, too much change, and too much of a bill at the end.  I appreciate that we get $12 off of the normal $22 parking costs across the street.  But if it comes with a $30 hike on the price for our typical meal, it makes the trip downtown too much effort.  

Now we only get down here once every year or so.  We are not exactly regulars. We were not surprised to find that their oysters are spectacular, as usual, and in a world where there are no other oysters with the same freshness and the same adept preparation it would be well worth the trip.  But now there is some competition, and, while it is just as expensive, it may be time to check it out.

Why?  Because the rest of the meal was inferior.  The Southern fried oysters, a classic and my wife's favorite, were overcooked and dry.  The arugula salad, which looked healthy and low fat on the menu, tasted strongly of bacon.  The brussel sprouts with bacon came with way too much bacon (and, yes, you can have too much bacon).  But saddest of all, they changed the recipe for the snapper soup making it far too salty.  Shame on the chef.

The whole meal?  One salad, two cooked oyster small plates, one bowl of snapper soup, three beers and one dozen oysters on the half shell cost well over $100.

Good oysters, though.

I did try to like Miga. In fact, I was quite excited about it. Going into the restaurant, I was also at first pretty impressed. The space was large and clean and the waitstaff were courteous. But for some reason, the food just didn't measure up (part of me just really misses the Korean food in LA).

The dolsot bibimbap is pretty average. None of the BBQ meats really tasted right. They definitely did not satisfy my Korean food craving. The soft tofu soup (soon du bu), also did not really measure up. 

Unfortunately, I'd rather drive out to the outskirts of Philadelphia (Cheltenham or Upper Darby) for Korean food than dine at Miga.

This is the second time we have used champion pools and spas for parts and service. First time they did not carry a simple pool filter gasket that I bought up the street from there competition. The second time they came out to work on my older pool spa heater. They claimed it needed a new electrical part which they ordered and installed. After installation of new part they claimed that new part still didn't fix it and I should just replace the heater with a new 2,500 unit. Ya right!! What a bunch of BS!! 

Here is where it gets good! I had a heating and air conditioning friend come look at the unit and he discovered the part was wired completely incorrectly and they had also bypassed a built in safety fuse. These guys are hacks! They are a joke! Bonus!!!! They left there dirty rubber gloves on the ground near my unit which blew into my pool!! Stay away!!! They are small time! Order your parts online and go to Leslie's pool supply in Rita Ranch for quality service!

I was lucky to find Dr. C by attending a very informative trigger point class with a friend who was a current patient. After knowing how much she helped my friend, I was very open to speaking further with Dr. C. I have pretty rare neurological problems so am overly cautious about who I allow to treat me.  Her initial evaluation was incredibly thorough and she took a lot of time going over all of her findings and plan to help improve my problems. She impressed me so much with her extreme knowledge of so many different conditions.  Her approach to treatment is even more impressive and expansive.  My experience with chiropractors is just one modality of treatment, adjustments.  Her approach is multi faceted,depending on your individual needs. Dr. C is one of those amazing doctors that you know genuinely prioritizes patient care first. She goes to any lengths she can to help people get back to better health. She has a passion for what she does and it shows.  She has helped me and my spouse immensely.  I consider her the best chiropractor I have ever gone to and highly recommend her

This place is to DIE for! Definitely come hungry, and don't expect yourself wanting to leave.

For starters, the staff is incredibly sweet and accommodating. We were running a little late on our reservation but they sat us in no problem at a GORGEOUS table (we kept commenting that we wanted it in our own kitchen). The decor is beautiful. It's clean cut yet at the same time homey.

I loved how there are two options here. The first (and cheaper) is all you can eat of the hot and cold bars, and then chicken, and only a few meats. We did the version and it was honestly perfect.

For the cold bar: the cold cuts were AMAZING. I could've eaten the roast beef and I think buffalo chicken for the rest of my life. I loved the yuca fries and the sweet plantains as well.

The meat... oh my god the meat. My favorite was the red sausage. I don't even know what it was per say all I know is I kept stealing everyone else's. Close second is definitely the garlic beef. Cooked perfectly and truly melts in your mouth, delicious.

For dessert, we had fresh fruit and some of their fresh juices! I tried the passion fruit juice (14/10) and my boyfriend got the limeade. He liked it, but I have more of a sweet tooth than he does.

Overall, I want to come here again and will in the future, and splurge for the all you can eat because they were bringing out some SERIOUS meats to other customers as we were leaving. This place is so amazing and I cannot stress enough how nice the staff were and just how great they treated us, a party of 7. Come here wearing your stretchy pants, and consider fasting all day!!

After having shopped at Matter Brothers other times, I am extremely disappointed and angered about how I have been treated for the last several months. We bought Capri furniture at Matter Brothers, and a few months later, noticed a bug infestation in an end table. We were told by Judi at customer service that the bugs did not travel from piece to piece.  But they did. Since last fall, we have replaced a coffee table, a dining room table, and four chairs.  I had to keep providing "proof" of bug residue each time.  Delays occurred constantly.  In February, I tried to get four chairs replaced.  I'm still waiting. Three have arrived, without chair covers.  Judi told me she has "bent over backwards" to help me, but she has done no such thing. Our sales associate at the Tarpon store has retired, and no one has stepped in to make things right for us. Judi also promises delivery soon, but it never arrives when it is supposed to. She never called me to inform me that only three chairs were on their way and without the cushions.  I will never shop at this store again.  This is the first negative review of a store I have ever posted.
We visited the Keel and Curley Winery over the weekend and enjoyed a wine tasting. Great wine and atmosphere! The BBQ out front is the bomb, too. Try some gator sausage!

I just don't get it. 

I just don't get why this place has the level of popularity that it has. The food is alright. The drinks are fine. The dining room is big and loud. The service is competent but not special. The pricing is mid. I would never recommend El Vez to anyone, but whenever someone suggests going, I shrug my shoulders and say "that's fine," because it's just fine.

Super cute place located on Machester that I have been waiting to try! We got two waffles to share- one sweet and one savoury and they were both delicious. We got the Goat in the Grove (goat cheese, avocado, eggs) and the Nutella and Strawberry and they were both great choices. The service was fine but we checked in on Yelp and got a 20% off but they did not honor this for us because they said it was meant to be for the week before (only on 08/24/2019). I feel like they should have honoured this discount or just take it down but this wasn't worth fighting over.

This is not my first time dining with Piazza Gavi.  We ordered through ubereats and the delivery took about 60 minutes to get to my door.  We ordered pepperoni pizza, cheese ravioli with a side of meat balls, garden salad, and minestrone soup for about $71 with tip.  You can't go wrong with ordering the pepperoni pizza.  It was our favorite, but this is not the best pie in Tucson for the price.  The meat balls are huge balls covered in red sauce and pair nicely with the cheese ravioli.  For some reason, the garden salad did not come with dressing.  Luckily we had some Italian dressing in the fridge to keep the theme going.  Overall the food was good, but can be pricey for the amount you get.  Pair this meal with some hard kombucha.  Cheers!

10/10 would recommend to anyone. He was absolutely incredible AND he taught me a little bit about why my body hurt in the places it hurt. I had covid in March and my body has been just a ball of pain since and he actually gave me some relief from that. I found this on Groupon, but I'll definitely be back.

Pros:  Super great location and access to the beach, walking distance to the piers and State Street.  We also got early check in and the staff was efficient and friendly. 

Cons:  No cookie upon check in and I was too embarrassed to ask for something so petty.  We stayed in the Bogendelia wing with an awesome ocean view.  The room was very outdated and the tub looked like it was patched with bondo.  The matteress seemed like it was outdated too.  

Over all, we had a good time, but I think we could have gotten better money's worth somewhere else.
My favorite ice cream/dessert place in the city. Is there anything more than I need to say? It's a great casual after dinner date location, or if you're just getting together with friends!

The food was a let down, but the service was even worse. Came with my boyfriend for our anniversary. Hostess told us they were cleaning our table, but didn't follow up for another ten minutes until a manager, I'm assuming, asked if we were being helped and showed us to a table himself. We weren't asked by our waitress for our drink order at the beginning of the meal and, by the time we got a hold of her halfway through our meal, she told us she couldn't get us anything due to the bar being closed, which we weren't made aware of until that point. 

They were out of everything we wanted to eat. My meal was $20 itself, which was simply dry, fried chicken. I could've gotten better at KFC. 

It's clear the staff just didn't care.

I've driven by a bunch and was hungry yesterday about 11am.  I knew my favorites would be packed so I brought my daughter and headed in.  It was busy, but tables were available.  Nothing special.  Typical (Greek) diner with expansive menu and breakfast served all day.  You can tell it is a family place.  It is not dirty, but the tables are formica and chipped there are some stained ceiling tiles, but you are not there for the ambiance.  We had breakfast, it was ok - nothing fantastic and nothing disappointing.  My daughter said the fries tasted like they were bought at Walmart and cooked there, I thought they were fine.  While I would not rush back I wouldn't not go again.  They have a turkey reuben on the menu and I might try that for dinner one night.

I used to be a Super regular but the last visit was so awful I thought I must be dreaming. I even tried the downtown location. Nope. The magic is gone. They have changed the menu. Took my favorite thing off.  Tried to improvise. New owners Perhaps?  Prices have gone up too 
The food was great and now. Half the portion 
Half the ingredients.  54-C with crispy noodles is no more. Must find a new Saigon Style noodle house.                                 

 YIKES!!' and the service was the worst as well
No utensils even after the blandest plate of food was delivered. Had to ask three times. Water the same. I used to go there 1-3 per week and drop an average of $30 per visit. Why change the menu?

Unfortunately, I need to give this establishment 1 star due to the inability to reach anyone about an item that I left. I was in for business and out the next day so unable to just go back to pick up the item.

A friend and I have called their listed number continuously for 3 days/nights - no answer. I posted 2 requests on their Facebook page - no comment or reply. Today,  I submitted a quote request for a private party hoping that someone would respond or call me. The auto response to the form listed a different # of which I left a voicemail - NOTHING.

Unbelievable!
I am so glad there is a sushi restaurant so close and even more glad that the food is great.

Speechless. Ordered the Debris Po'Boy and was blown away! I'm from the Midwest and we get the pleasures of meat sandwiches from the famous Portillo's, originally of Chicago. But this sandwich, with it's masterful blend of the flavorful, tender beef, paired with the crunch of the shredded cabbage, along with the subtle tangy taste of the pickles, all stuffed into a soft and generous cut of delicious French bread and then served in a pool of palate pleasing au jus sauce, has exceeding my standards for the ultimate beef sandwich! Next up, I'll see if it can compete with another iconic beef sandwich, Geno's of Philly!

This was one of the first cafes I've been to and I loved it! The workers there were very nice. The coffee was really good. I went around 4:30/5 on a Tuesday and it wasn't too busy which was nice because I got my coffee pretty quickly. The inside is cute. The food was good. Everything about this cafe was good. You can expect me to go back again!

I have been going to Ninfa at Strands for almost 15 years now and I can't say enough positive things about her and the salon in general! Ninfa is amazing with color. She has serious talent and a great eye when it comes to helping her clients try something new. And when it comes to cuts, she is an absolute perfectionist! She will spend the extra time and effort making sure your new style is exactly what you've been looking for. She is so creative and always has great suggestions when I'm thinking about changing up my look! In addition to her many talents with hair, she is an unbelievable makeup artist as well. When I got married almost three years ago, it was a no brainer to choose Ninfa and her team at Strands to do my hair and my bridesmaids' hair. She and her team made us all look and feel like a million bucks. Nothing but great things to say about Strands - they are seriously the best in the business!

The service was cheerful and attentive without being overbearing or insincere. They were very accommodating to an ever-expanding crew of females on a Saturday night. My sister-in-law got a beef dish with sweet potato mash that she said was very excellent. I loved the spring rolls. Thank you for a memorable evening!
A bit dark in the restaurant but the food makes up for it!! Highly recommend & come hungry bc you get tons of food!!
Awesome service. Everything was great, from the scheduling to the plumber (Steve) who did the repair. Very reasonable pricing IMO. Would definitely use them again!

We were in the area and looking for a breakfast place.  This place was amazing.  We had the beignets.  They were delicious.  As was the fresh fruit french toast and eggs combo.  Make sure if you are in the area you give it a try.  We live 30 miles from here, but will definitely make the trip next time we want an excellent breakfast.

Better than Pat's or Geno's by a long shot. In addition to having a better product (i.e. Meat that hasn't been overcooked and hacked into a million tiny pieces), there's also a degree of patience (at least at lunch time) that the more famous cheese steak places lack. Oh, and the location is also much more convenient. Hard to argue against a more convenient, better tasting cheesesteak served to you by more courteous servers...

Delicious food. Good vegetarian, vegan and gluten-free options. Great service. Decent prices. 

I went twice during my visit to New Orleans because the first time was a hit. 

The Broken noodle salad is pretty much their take on a vermicelli bowl that you'd find at any Vietnamese restaurant. 

The Rico open-faced sandwich is good, but I really enjoyed the BBQ seitan special they had. Very filling too.

I'll be generous and give them 3 stars, because I don't believe a restaurant's reputation should suffer because of 1 waiter who doesn't know &$*(# about customer service.  SO... that being said, the food is very, very good.  Lots of selection and the prices are reasonable.  I got a steak and shrimp dish.  The steak was a bit over done (I asked for medium well and it was definitely well), but the shrimp were absolutely perfect.
The waiter, however, was a different story. It took him 20 minutes to get our order, 4 times being asked to get a water that he forgot in the first place, and then not knowing that a salad ordered separately comes out BEFORE the meal... hot mess.
Overall, the food is very good and I'd recommend it to someone if they don't have a hard time sitting on flat wooden benches (cafeteria style). Fair warning. It's the price you pay for succulent shrimp.
5 stars for the cocktails and the environment. 2 stars for the coffee. Great location if you want to explore the area too

Really delicious food, great options and multiple combo options. Area is what it is, parking can be difficult depending on the time of day. Prices are reasonable. When I visited there was only 2 people in front of me but it took about 15-20 mins for them to complete my order. I could see staff on the back but am unsure if they were completing to go orders or maybe on break. By the time my order was complete there was a line of 8 customers and I was done eating before the last costumer had even ordered. Be prepared to wait a bit or maybe order to go if you're in a crunch.
Cool place to be and be seen.Staff moral is high,business is good.Chill atmosphere.Surprised at lax security late afternoons,never been there late though.

There's a vast selection of food, where the quality is tolerable. This is a place where you go to load up and fill you. It's not place to go for quality food. I've been here a handful of times. Most of the time, their food is a rushed job. Example, their corn on the cob. The first time I had it, it was raw. The second time it was overcooked and mushy, like it's been sitting for a while. The most recent time. It's cooked right, but it was a little bland. Dinner for $12.95, is worth it if you go and fill yourself up with the high ticket items, like the seafoods.

Really authentic Mexican food too many time new restaurants start off well then change where they get there ingredients to save money and the you can tell in the taste of the food but not this place it is consistently good every time I've come over the years.

Awesome! 10/10 service.  5+ stars, 2 thumbs up.  I can't remember her name but the lady behind the bar/ counter was super nice and knows her stuff. If you like vegan/ raw food then u will looooooove this.  They make these raw chocolate bars that are the best thing I've ever had... Crazy good!!  Like I didn't know chocolate could taste so good.  I bought a bar. 
Get ur vegan on!!

I love that this is a vegetarian restaurant, but they totally downplay it. The tacos were AMAZING. My boyfriend got the burger and he loved it. The drinks were overpriced, but the food was well worth it. We will definitely be back!
This is my boyfriend and I 2nd time here! We love it! Service was great! The Mongolia beef was like no others. it was delicious!

This recently renovated Target is a convenient place to stop for a quick shopping trip.  I love how clean it is inside and how many staff members there appear to be everywhere.  I always find what I need when visiting this Target.  

The prices are reasonable, the staff friendly and the location very convenient.

Went here for lunch with my co-workers and manager 7/19. I am happy to report this place is awesome! Tried the chicken skins and they left a smile on every ones face as they had such great flavour and the right amount of spice! For my main dish I tried the blue steel burger and boy that was great too! just enough blue cheese and the perfect cut of bacon! It was great! I am only disappointed I wasn't able to try more because I was so full haha Great atmosphere (a little warm but this was during a heat wave) Great Service and GREAT food! I will definitely be back for more!

If you ask me what my favorite food is, i'll tell you it's pizza. If you ask me where the best pizza place is at, and you live on the north side, i'll guide you here! This place surprised my expectations...really really. 

I had The Trap pizza (seafood) and it was SO good. Get extra of their OG Trap Buttah sauce on there too if you really want to get the food coma effect. It's a 12" pizza (their only size) that you'll probably end up taking home. It's huge.

They also have a desert that is basically cinnamon rolls with icing..I can't think of the name (someone help me out!) but they are super good as well if you have a sweeth tooth.

We arrived when they opened so it obviously wasn't packed on a Thursday but you'll have the option to post up outside to eat or head inside...regardless you can hear the music!

Overall i'll give this place a 5/5...nothing rubbed me the wrong way and the food is definitely fire

This place is great! The staff is very friendly,  and happy to meet any request asked of them.  They even accommodate off menu requests.  This is the new lunch spot for every one of us at my workplace.

We recently at this hotel for three nights. First they put us in a smoking room even though we didn't ask to be. They hardly had anything out for breakfast and it wasn't that great. In the room there blow dryer didn't work. The sheets had blood stains on the side and when we asked them to change them they didn't. We told managers they said we don't change stay over... It wasn't even our blood!! We kept having to go by the trash can upstairs which had a ton of trash in and around the can which they never took out while we were there. No dirty clothes bags, no nose tissue and no extra blankets. It took them two hours to bring us toilet paper. We come in town often but I seriously doubt we will be staying here again.

I work in the area and my co workers and I go to lunch here every Tuesday for Taco Tuesday. The servers are always nice to us and the food is typically correct, hot and prompt.  They do have a bit of a lunch rush that keeps them rather busy, so you can get in and out even faster if you go for an early or late lunch. The salsa that comes with the complimentary tortilla chips is even better when you mix it with the hot sauce on the table. The place isn't anywhere near fancy but it won't cost you much to leave full (especially if you're into tacos on Tuesdays).  Sit on the patio when the weather is nice, it had a great cover that keeps you from getting too hot in sunny days.

At first I was skeptical about going in, but we went for a bride's bachelorette party and we had the best time. All of the dancers were very friendly and greeted the women as they came in. Each dancer takes a turn on the stage and even brings the brides to be up on stage. Private dances are available for $40 and you can select your dancer. This was my first time and I was very nervous. I asked Carmine and he led me upstairs. He was so professional and kind, making me feel right at ease. The best part was that he was very gentle and slow and read my body language; but, he also was exciting and encouraged me to relax and let loose. He has a soft laugh and sweet smile. I highly encourage any first time lady to ask for Carmine and this place deserves a second glance and a walk inside.
Eric brews some great beer and takes the time to do it right.  The staff is always friendly and knowledgeable.

YUM! We ordered delivery, which included green papaya salad, pad prik king with beef, mo yang (thai bbq pork), and the pad kee mao (drunken noodles). The salad was tasty and I enjoyed the pad prik king, which is a regular spicy favorite of mine to try at any new-to-me Thai restaurant. I'd never had Thai bbq before, and it was a very pleasant surprise, and I found the drunken noodles to be almost impossible to stop eating. Highly recommended!

I love the food, and always traveled to the east side to get it. Glad they opened the second location. They have the best, and longest list of Eggs Benedict I've ever seen. They have a weekly special Benedict and pancake that aren't on the menu every weekend (you can find it on Facebook). Now, if it is your first time at the restaurant, let them know. You will receive the best pancake you will ever find on the planet. Snickerdoodle!!!!! I don't really care for pancakes, but order this every time I go. The only reason I didn't give them 5 stars is the ambiance is lacking. It's very loud, and at times you feel like you are sitting on top of each other. The service can be a bit slow on weekends, because they are always slammed.  So arrive with you patience and you will be rewarded.
Finally!  A good wing spot in Riverview!  Definitely our go to wing spot!  Food is tasty and reasonably priced too!!!

I'm not a fan of fancy donut since it doesn't seem worth it but we decided to come and try it since we were so close.  Well, I'm glad we came! 

As soon as we got in line there was a big crowd behind us.  A wide variety of choices and unique combinations of flavors.  We just wanted to split 1 bc we had more places to eat later.  We got the blueberry cheesecake and a cold brew.  Absolutely wonderful.  1/2 of the donut was filling and I loved the cheesecake filling.  . 

Cold brew was also smooth and tasty.  Not enough time on vaca to eat everything.  

Yum.

LOVE the atmosphere here! Love their flights as well. 
Their sours are the best! And I even loved their IPA's (I usually am not the biggest fan of IPA's). 
I have not seen or been on their patio yet, but I will definitely be coming back!

All you out-of-owners are idiots! This is by far the most AUTHENTIC, flavorful Mexican food anywhere. The fish tacos are to DIE for! My husband and I have eaten here dozens of times and have never had anything but perfect, attentive service. I think you northerners come down here with a sense of entitlement and typical rudeness. Stay up north!
I love the owners here!! You get expert service quickly. I bring in my crazy nail designs from the internet and she can usually create it or something better!!

I can't say enough about how this restaurant exceeded my expectations and just completely blew me away. I came here opening night around 9 o'clock with my bf and friends and I think I'm safe to say on behalf of all 7 of us, it was AMAZING. The menu is small which, in my experience, tells me that they took a lot of time and care into perfecting each dish. Every aspect of the restaurant was on point. 

Decor (for those who are picky/pretentious enough to judge a restaurant by its cover) - Has that Chinese restaurant look without all the tackiness of the gold coins, dragons, bamboo trees, phoenix, and Buddhas at every turn of the head.

Service - The host was most gracious and friendly. I liked him immediately. Our server was very sweet as well. As for the wait time? Surprisingly short for the amount of food we ordered. Opening night and no kinks thus far. *mental note* must Yelp review.

Food/Drinks - My bf and I both ordered the (please excuse me for not remembering the name) first drink on the Cocktails menu...something Cobbler. I can't even describe what it tasted like but the only words that come to mind now are interesting and refreshing. Everything we ordered from beginning to end was just superb:
Starting off with the Red Ginger Shrimp bao and B.L.T. bao (steamed bun variations), Vietnamese noodle salad, Kim Chi Fried Chicken, and Scratch Dumplings.
Moving on to the Wise Boar (ramen with pork in every way imaginable).
Ending with the Snappy Ginger Lemon Bars.
There was not one bite where I was disappointed.
Quick FYI: everything is super inexpensive. I love seeing that $ when I search on yelp.

After we paid and got up to leave, Chef Neal approached our table and asked how the meal was. Uh-may-zing! Duh!! He was such an enthusiastic and wonderful person. You know the food is boss/top notch/great quality (however you want to say it) once you see the chef just gushing about how much he/she loves cooking. 

Bottom line. Eat here.

**Lunch hours don't start until the following week, so for now it's open from 5:30-10:30(?). Before you go, check the hours. Idk why people get so mad when they come to a restaurant and see that it's closed. There's a sign and the site said they're closed...why are you so surprised?**

I'm so happy this opened near me . They have one in Palm Harbor . MUST TRY No BS 
Thank God no more d u n kin ' Donuts
This is a real deal donut  shop.  ................ edit my review since the business owner wanted to reply to my great review. I would like to add that the location in Palm Harbor looks the same pretty much has all the same stuff and the more they serve breakfast sandwiches and cheeseburgers also sorry for the inconvenience of your business but most customers would know this.

Love this place, I have multiple pieces and enjoy them all. The quality is excellent - worth the price. Treat yourself - you'll be glad you did.
The sales professionals are top-notch, they are friendly and make a pleasant shopping experience.
I agree with one of the other reviewers, I wish St. Louis would get more of the latest releases.  However,  you can always have it shipped to the St Louis store.

Beds could be a bit softer but that is personal preference.

Overall, nice hotel, clean, friendly staff.

If you forgot something, you can call the staff for numerous things including slippers, woolite, make up/nail polish remover, and hair spray.

I am LITERALLY addicted to the chicken lettuce wraps. I will NOT share, so don't even ask. Their edamame is AMAZING so I won't share that either. I just don't understand the delivery of the lettuce wraps. I'm doing homework and I need food. I ordered PF Chang's 2 nights in a row because I needed those lettuce wraps. The first time they forgot my fortune cookies and I was heartbroken. And then how can you have lettuce as half of my FAVORITE dish, but send me broken and torn lettuce, or pieces not even a magician could figure out how to stuff?! TWO nights in a row??? The cold lettuce COMPLETES the dish with that magic blend of chicken and AMAZING ingredients. Lettuce may be a natural ingredient, but it's still half of your dish and should be monitored as such. When I order again this week, I'll expect better lettuce with your magic chicken.

Great find ... Though I grew up in Tucson, we have lived in San Diego for 30+ years.  When we visit a few times a year, we try out local diners in the area for breakfast ... So we know that part of the Tucson landscape pretty well, and were very pleasantly surprised with this great find ... Definitely toward the top of the pack!

Good breakfast food (between the three of us we had the biscuits and gravy combo, a scramble and a walnut-banana pancake combo), great atmosphere and very nice staff.

Will definitely be back...

Tucked away in a small pocket of New Hope is a brand new restaurant filled with really big surprises.

Owned and operated by Horizons' former Sous Chef, Ross Olchvary, Sprig & Vine is truly an amazing dining experience. 

Our party of 4 was greeted with a warm welcome by their host and we had arrived just after the big dinner slam, so we were promptly seated.

The entire restaurant was glowing with warm ambient light shining from very unique cork light fixtures suspended from the ceiling. After being seated, you can't help but feel absorbed by all of complimentary natural earth tones and the independent track list. 

Our server immediately greeted us with a smile and opened our bottle of wine and poured everyone a glass.

We started with the Fried Baby Artichokes, Edamame Falafel and Tofu Satay....all amazing. You almost feel guilty as your fork disturbs the pristine presentation of each dish, but the flavors and textures are truly out of this world. 

Ever have black sesame tahini? i certainly hadn't. The edamame falafel was delicately placed ontop of light drizzles of the tahini. It was perfect and garnished with pickled radish.

the tofu satay was accompanied by pickled mango and had an amazing coconut-peanut glaze.

We ordered every entree except for the cornmeal-crusted tempeh (only because we were a party of 4 and not 5).

The slow roasted root vegetable pave was like a sunburst on my plate. Featuring local soft polenta, red wine braised shallots, and grilled asparagus, you forget that what you're eating is "100% Vegetarian". You forget because it's kind of hard to believe that such complex flavor is the result of purely vegetarian ingredients.

We also tried the Cauliflower Three Ways, Seared Coconut Risotto Cakes and the Moroccan Grilled Tofu. We had to try one of the side dishes, so we went with the Kennebec Potato Fries.

I really can not say enough wonderful things about Sprig & Vine. After 3 bowls of soup, 3 appetizers, 4 entrees, one side, one dessert and 2 cups of coffee, our bill was only $122 dollars!!!!!!

In the past, i've  paid literally triple this amount for half the quality!

Bring a bottle or two of wine, bring your friends, but also try bringing someone who has never had the opporutnity/privilege to try an all out vegetarian/vegan experience. it will totally change their perspective on food.

Whether you're a vegan, vegetarian, or all out carnivore, do your taste buds and spirit a big favor and stop by Sprig & Vine for a wonderful and reasonably priced dining experience brought to you by a true artisan.
Ok ?? Is the district manager looking at these reviews and the district manager myself I would be embarrassed fire everyone and start from scratch

We were all pleasantly surprised by how good the meal was, the options, and the atmosphere on the back patio. The waitress was patient with our party's many questions. Our group had a wide selection of appetizers and main courses including curry with tofu (happy for vegan option), shrimp & grits, salad with chicken, lamb Shepard's pie, crab cakes, Bavarian pretzels, hummus, and more. Special shout out for the gin cocktail with almond flavoring.

Came here for brunch for the first time on a Sunday. We had reservations but the place was pretty empty for 11am. We chose to sit outside on the new deck. 

We took advantage of the all-inclusive $12 bottomless mimosa/Bloody Mary special & I had the Crabby Benny. The crabby Benny was a typical eggs Benedict with a southern twist; a biscuit topped with lump crab meat, poached egg & hollandaise sauce & a side of asparagus. It was delicious but I would have like a little more sauce on top. (If you're looking for a great crab eggs Benedict in conshy go to the Conshy Cafe!) 

Overall, food was good, atmosphere is adorable & prices were average. I'd definitely go back.

Food and view were excellent. Loved all the healthy menu choices and the kids buffet was a hit. Black grouper was great. Would like to see a few more fresch catch choices on the menu and for some reason we never heard of any specials although I heard them being read at another table. Service was friendly but a little rushed given the restaurant was not crowded. Would like to also see a better wine list. Doesn't match up with the great food. I'd go back for sure as this is not your typical Clearwater touristy joint.
Great, incentive menu. Vivian is an attentive wait staff and treats customers like family. Dies get crowded at times, which slows service a bit; otherwise great brek/lunch venue!
Fun walking tour. Our tour guide Scott was awesome! Definitely would recommend to any AHS Coven fans.

Beware. They are 'family owned' but  have no ability to help out a potential new guest. We booked through expedia, Granted the terms don't officially allow a same day change but we called to move the date 3 hours before check in with an illness. Best they could do is charge us the full rate and suggest we book a new reservation with them.  That new reservation will happen...never.   Inflexible+smug= never getting my business.

Always great service always great food. came for the pizza today ended up with the grouper sandwich special with home made hush puppies oh and I ordered one slice of pizza for desert. If you want to experience Detroit style pizza you must come here.

Espresso made from beans aged in former New Day's breakfast magpie mead barrels?  Damn amazing and don't dare add any flavoring or sugar to it.  I still dream about that espresso, and its complex, vaguely alcoholic berry taste.   Bee Coffee Roasters offers you the chance to step out of your caffeine comfort zone...taste a cardamom and shagbark hickory syrup latte, freshly roasted espresso or coffee, or other creations (I happened to witness the menu remnants from special Gencon offerings, including Doctor Who and Groot lattes). But, overall, I recommend anything with those breakfast magpie espresso beans...hopefully it was not a temporary offering.

Hometown Family Restaurant was formerly O'Grady's Family Restaurant until it changed within the last year. It's still in the same building as O'Grady's was in Souderton Center and has roughly the same layout too. It's been years since I've last eaten at O'Grady's so I don't remember it too well and I was certainly shocked when it changed over so I knew I wanted to give Hometown Family Restaurant a shot. Today I got my chance and I certainly wasn't disappointed. We found decent parking, we were able to be seated right away, our server was friendly and the food came out quick and it was delicious! I was very impressed by the bacon, egg and cheese bagel and homefries and enjoyed every bite!

I will admit it is a shame O'Grady's Family Restaurant is gone as I did want to eat there again but honestly Hometown Family Restaurant is awesome and is able to fill that void easily. I look forward to seeing what else they have on the menu!

One of the best restaurants in the area! Authentic, flavorful, and well mannered staff. They also cater to vegans. Popular items are their deluxe pho, pork vermicelli, shaking beef, and spring rolls. Everything on the menu is tasty!

Small but to the point. I was very pleasantly surprised by the selection within the store. They cater not only to the runners out there but they also carry triathlon equipment, sports nutrition supplements, other knick knacks for sports enthusiasts, and more! I was psyched to find that they carry a wide range of Tifosi sunglasses which I have been searching high and low for. Very cool store (with recognition from Runners World to boot!) and the staff were friendly and helpful.

I like complete nutrition much better than GNC. 
Never once when I asked about a product did they say, "I'm not sure" or "let me ask someone who might know." They really took pride in their product knowledge. 
Another thing I loved is the fact that they care about your success. Your first time in, they take your measurements and as you keep coming in track your progress. 
They have a club that's $11 a month. That money builds up so you can use it towards supplements in the future. I like it because I'll always be needing something.
Unreal...ordered an orange tree for them to cancel the order 2 months later which I've been waiting for that whole entire time. Don't order anything from them
Excellent food, and the service is phenomenal. Definitely an awesome place for Sunday Brunch!!
I've used Bryan before and he's about as real as it gets. He's local, down to earth, not all corporate. Price is about 1/4 what the dealership tried to charge me.

Although I'm from an hour away, my brother and sister-in-law live in Mt. Holly, so I finally got them to go.  They will now go back even without me because they enjoyed having a place where you can enjoy fresh beer, sit for a long time, and not have food pushed onto you.  They also have a really nice outside area, so when the weather is nice I will sit out there and enjoy.

On the way out, I grabbed a six pack of cans.  Canned beer really has come a long way.  They canned it just a few hours before I picked it up.  Boy, did it taste fresh when I got home!

I've lived in Rofo for over a year now and always made the assumption that this place must be a dump due to it's location. While having drinks one night with some friends in KoP, a few of them had asked if I'd ever been to FQB. They all said it was a great hole in the wall and that I had to try it. So I did the next day. I chose to dine with my friend at the bar rather than the restaurant. Bartender was friendly which always makes me happy. We ordered gumbo as an appetizer which was very, very good. I'm a Southern native with family/friends from Louisiana so I've had my fair share of amazing gumbos, and although this one was "different" in flavor that I'm used to, I was impressed. Only thing, they don't serve crackers or bread with it, which they should. My friend ordered a burger and I ordered the craw fish po-boy with voodoo sauce added on (I like it spicy). I couldn't believe how good both of these dishes were! So flavorful and just simply delicious. How have I passed this place up for so long? The other plus, maybe even the better plus, the craft beers they have on tap were sooo good. I'm quite the craft beer connoisseur and was elated to find beers that are often difficult to find. You can also take these great beers to go. Go here! It's goooood!

When I first moved to Philadelphia 35 years ago, I was taken to Dalessandro's and thought the cheesesteak was great.  I have had at least a hundred more at other places.  After not visiting for at least 5 years, I returned for a taste that I remembered as terrific.  Sadly, this was not the cheesesteak it used to be.  Actually, it was terrible.  The meat tasted "off", and nothing I would eat again.  There was also much too much of it, making it hard to eat.  The provolone cheese was much too sharp and it overpowered the sandwich, and there were too few onions to round out the flavor.  I will never return again, because I think any decent hoagy shop can make a better cheesesteak that really.satisfies.  Having a well-earned reputation is fine, but you cannot coast on past glories.  Stay away!

Where do I start?!? This place is wonderful. The food was amazing. Try everything! I had the shrimp and lobster with risotto.  The best part of your experience was our waiter Mathew. He was phenomenal. Informative, attentive, social and hilarious. I'm sure he's reading this. Thank you Matt. We love you! Keep up the great work.

I was here with a group of five this past weekend. We were seated quickly and with a smile from the staff. The menu was good, though a bit limited for the vegetarian in our group. But the BYOB aspect is fun as I always love a mimosa! 
It was pretty loud, which just means people were happy and having a good time, which is another good sign. I thought the food was fun and different, without being too unusual. The prices are very reasonable too. I'll be back!
Phenomenal!  Jay Dee did my first tattoo ever and he was amazing.  Professional, welcoming, and so detailed and thorough.  I will definitely be back!

My friends and I had reservations for tomorrow and we called to switch the time and the manager said they don't have our reservation so now we have nowhere to eat New Years Eve.  My friend even has the record of the phone call on her call log from when she made the reservations.  And before she hung up after making the reservation she double checked to make sure she had the correct location.  
Ok, cool. HOOK 'EM

Not so good, they were out of everything I truly wanted. I had to ask them what they did have then I chose from their availabile food. I decided on something I'd never tried and it was overcooked...straight up Bo-Bo like the clown...and yeah, not coming back here!

Excellent experience. I made an appointment after a less than stellar experience at Firestone and hearing the NPR interview with Patrice on Fresh Air. I think the SHEcanic helping me was Colleen- she was excellent, did not talk down to me at all, and after looking at my car sat down with me in the waiting room with a binder full of diagrams and explained what needed to be done on my car in a language I could understand. She was extremely friendly and thanked me for my patience after a little bit of a wait, which was a 180 from Firestone who kept my car for an entire day without a mention of the inconvenience to me. This is a phenomenal operation and I am happy to support it!

Good food. Good atmosphere. Friendly people. Parking sucks but then again parking issues is the way of life for these folks. Loud /noisy. Not to busy but I guess that's due to no place to park. Even pay parking is hard to find

Geez.  There are too many reviewers on this site that are far too easy to please. I now take restaurant  reviews written on yelp with the proverbial grain of salt.  This particular eatery is just plain ordinary at best. Totally unappealing and unprofessional in appearance/atmosphere, this doesn't even have the ambience of a fast food place. A t-shirt with holes and a pair of dilapidated cut-offs with cheap sandals would match the decor and food.  Two beers, one appetizer, and two meals--all mediocre--cost $55!!! That for greasy food served on plastic plates by waitresses that must have learned their craft at Mel's Diner. They couldn't have cared less about any part of their job (ala, "Kiss my grits"). When the salads arrived, I knew this would be an awful experience: anytime it comes in a small black plastic bowl with iceberg and the blandest of ingredients, you can be assured this joint doesn't care about the food it serves. Sure enough, the fried oysters and fried calamara tasted exactly the same--of breadcrumbs and oil. "Where's the Seafood?" The portions were very small considering their high cost.  I wanted to order the baked potato as a side but was informed it wasn't available until 4 PM. My request came at 3:30. Upon leaving, another diner was happily plunging into one. I felt further disrespected--they should have offered me one to go if they really weren't ready at 3:45.....

Until diners insist upon better we will be stuck with glorified crap like this. I'm never going back and urge you to avoid it completely. A total waste of $55.  Remember, I'm the one you should listen to.

You know you've got a great vegetarian/vegan restaurant when your customers have no idea they're not eating meat. I came to Yelp to rave about the super friendly service and yummy Al Pastor and surprise! Great job, Guys. Delicious and we really appreciated the warm welcome and hospitality.
Charming Main Street locale with amazing ice cream and hand made chocolates. Prices are reasonable and the girls working were super friendly.

What a great spot to "hang out" . Great food across the board.  Drinks are very reasonably priced, generously poured. Lunch here always. The owner, Chuck, is always there cheerfully escorting patrons to their table. Very hands on. Numerous rooms for private parties and even a beautiful banquet room for up to 200 . They just hosted a reunion for any football player who ever played for my high school in the banquet room. A great event!  Bottom line is the GAP offers anything your looking for in a go to spot. Thanks to Chuck and staff for always providing patrons with a great bang for their buck!


Nick d.

Took my wife here for a birthday dinner last night. The staff was fantastic and the food was excellent. I highly recommend the braised rabbit but you can't go wrong with any choice from their menu, we were staring at all the dishes coming out of the kitchen.

Stopped by on a recent getaway in SB. Came right at 11 a.m. on a Saturday before heading up to Santa Ynez.  We ordered the Huevos con Chili Verde and the Cochinita Pibil and enjoyed both immensely.   The owner was very kind and helpful and gave us some nice winery recs. The salsa bar is definitely worth mentioning - my favorite was the tomatillo salsa.  Prices are a little high for Mexican food but the presentation, ambiance and quality make it worth it, in my opinion.

Would definitely return if I was in the area.

This AMC is pretty on par with other major movie theaters I've been to. This location offers 3D movies, comfy recliner seats, online ticket purchases and a selection of movie theater concessions (including a bar). However, the movie options are pretty limited: they only ever offer about six options whereas the Regal UA will have about 12.

This place was recommended to us by our dog trainer.  It's a fairly new business having opened in the past year and we've taken our dog here a number of times and have been extremely happy with it.  They do doggy daycare and boarding.

The owner and his staff are extremely trustworthy and knowledgeable about dogs and I have no worries about leaving my dog here for the weekend.  I feel 100% confident that he'll keep my dog safe while at the same time giving him the attention and playtime he needs.  Also he keeps the place very clean and monitors the dogs to ensure nothing bad happens.  What I like most about this place is that there's really nice emphasis on playtime and exercise.  

He has a very spacious facility with both a large indoor and outdoor play areas.  There are separate spaces for large and small dogs, and they're happy to do any special requests such as feeding instructions, medication,  etc.  Also it's staffed 24 hours a day and has mini suites for the dogs that are boarded or must be crated.

The best part about taking my dog here is the 3 hour nap my dog takes when he gets home because he had such a fun weekend!

First time just out driving. We live in Lutz but we're always trying to find mom and pop restaurant. 
There a little out dated , the place is nice and clean and the food was amazing. 
Had 2 flap jacks and bacon had to send bacon back but there's only three place that gets my bacon right the first time. 
Will be back for sure

No need to make a plan to go see the Reno Arch because it's hard to miss if you take any time to walk around the town. 

I've been to Reno many times over the years, walked by the sign many times, driven under it many times, etc. but I never get tired of it or bored of taking new pictures every single time, as if they're going to turn out anything different.

I love Reno and the sign is just one of many little, simple things I get excited over but it is definitely something special in its own way. :)

This place appears to not be in business anymore.   The website link takes you to a porn site.  When you call the number it is for Fulton Bank.  After reading the reviews we were looking forward to trying this place. :(

We took our white minivan here, purchased the top wash package and left with dirt on the lower panels and the back unclean.  The sales attendant with green hair was rude and was bothered to explain how I went from a $24.00 wash package to $47!  This place is a complete rip off.

A great hotel in Holiday, Florida!  This is our 4th visit to this Fairfield Inn and I really cannot find anything wrong with it!! The staff is fantastic....very friendly and will help with anything they can.  Rooms are pretty spacious and very clean.  The housekeeping staff does a great job.  We usually stay for a week and everything is pretty consistent!  The breakfast in the morning is pretty nice and saves time and money.  Coffee, eggs, toast, fruit, oatmeal.....the usual.  The staff who runs the breakfast area are fantastic and keep things well stocked and are very pleasant to talk with.  Plenty of parking and the hotel is located in a good  area....not too far from golfing or the beach.
No wonder the rating is so low. My nuggets were saturated and gross. The person that i paid just put out her hand like didn't say hello or anything at all... not going back

Five stars if it wasn't for the tamale. Got a combo and the tacos were awesome, beans and Mexican potatoes were all on point. Service was good and they were busy. 

Tamale was terrible. Avoid.

My first time here and I will definitely be back to try some of their other sandwiches. I got a tuna salad wrap. Great flavor but the mayo in it seemed a little watery because my wrap was dripping as I was eating it.  I would get it again bc it was delicious.

Went for my birthday and it was a great experience.  Being a local beer enthusiast, I was pleasantly surprised to visit two breweries I had never been to.  My only complaint is that at least one of the breweries didn't include any free tastes, and I would like to have a few free tastes before ordering, and there was really short time to drink the volume of beer in the taster for purchase. 

If you think you'll get hungry during the course of the tour based on timing, be sure to get something ahead of time or bring something with you because there wasn't food at the breweries and not even really enough time to eat it.

A great time for me and my group of friends which included locals and out of towners!
I can't believe I have never eaten here. Ate here yesterday. Ordered the taco, refried beans and rice meal, Togo. It was AMAZING. Hands down delicious. One of my new favorite places to eat.

The place is very cozy and beautiful. We ordered mix plate and it was adorable. Chicken and beef kebab are very good. 
Dishes are perfect, and Kunefe is the best in town

We were heading out of town and stopped at Big Al's for breakfast because of the great reviews on Yelp. We were very hesitant when we pulled up and debated whether we should go in or find somewhere else. Thankfully, we didn't judge a book by its cover. We ended up having a fabulous breakfast and considered ordering lunch to take with us. The lunch menu was unique and sounded delicious. Because of time, however, we passed up lunch and vowed to go back next time we're in the area. I had the best omelet I've ever had. My family enjoyed the French toast, biscuit sandwich and pancakes. The homemade biscuits were delicious! It wasn't just the excellent food that will bring us back. Al was cooking out on the open and was talking to everyone. The waitress, who I assume was his wife and the server, possibly his daughter, were very friendly. Impeccable service! We also lost our credit card and when we called, Al went outside to look for us. You sure don't get that type of service just anywhere. Wish we lived closer so we could be regulars. We also found that they were featured on Heat Seekers, a Food Network show. One of our goals when we travel is to find restaurants that have been showcased on that channel. We highly recommend Big Al's. We can't wait to go back!
Coolest little bar I've seen, my favorite in west chester, but i dont go often because its more of a chill spot and my friends like to dance and all that. Cheap beer great atmosphere

WOW. The food here was delicious - the shrimp and grits would be my last meal that's how good they were. We came here for my 25th birthday brunch and were not disappointed. I realized when planning my trip to NOLA I was picking restaurants based on food, reviews and recs, and not necessarily based on their proximity to the French Quarter. But with that said, it's only about a 15 minute Uber ride and SO WORTH IT. Don't be afraid to get out of the quarter for some good eats! Everyone got something different on the brunch menu - the crawfish Benedict was also really tasty, but those shrimp and grits were to die for. They have bottomless mimosas and our waitress kept them filled! They also do champagne toasts for birthdays instead of dessert which I thought was so cute and really appreciated because I would much rather spend my calories on champagne (: the manager also gave us another round of champagne because he heard we were going on a swamp tour after brunch - how sweet!! Our waitress was super great and answered all of our questions - we had a vegetarian and a vegan and she was able to recommend some yummy choices for them. The next time I am in New Orleans I might just come back here for every meal. Thanks for making my birthday so special in the Bayou, DTB!!

July 5, 2021
My daughter, grandchildren and I ate lunch at Mama's Greek Cuisine. They were understaffed and we waited forty-five minutes for the server to wait on us. It took another forty-five minutes to get our meal. We made a complaint to the manager, who was very rude. The server was rude as well. The food wasn't that good. It was very busy and no servers for the customers that were sitting and waiting. I know one thing, this restaurant I will never go back to.

If you are in a mood for pancakes, this is the place. I don't understand what's up with all the bad reviews here. I experience great friendly service each time I come. The variety is incredible for breakfast.

I keep seeing reviews call this a dive bar. Has the term "dive bar" lost so much meaning now? This is NOT a dive bar, even if it's dressed as one. If you think this is a dive bar you haven't been to someplace that is actually sketchy.

Other Bar has some of the decorations of a dive bar but is actually a great place where you won't feel like need a shower when you leave. It's conveniently located across from Dat Dog and just down from Liberty Cheesesteak, so you've got drunk food options for dinner. Unfortunately chances of a late night snack are slim.

Drink wise there's a solid selection of beer (both domestics and craft stuff) and cocktails. Prices are low without being rock bottom. They even have Pine Belt, which is a beer that doesn't have local distribution. The Dark and Stormy in particular seems to be a favorite in house.

Lastly, no review would be completely without mentioning the free to play Ski Ball machine. What better way to settle a drunken argument or bet than ski ball? What' s your high score?
Great local brewery. Make sure you go on Fridays for $5 growler refills. The entrance is on College not Ohio St

What a fantastic new spot!! Everything ordered was absolutely delicious. A group of four of us shared the ricotta appetizer and the pub board, and the mushroom pizza. Ambiance is excellent....Great addition to Boise ! We

Seriously a GREAT spot for sushi AND Thai food. 

We were up bear 54/Vet and decided to look on Yelp for some options for an early dinner. We chose Bangkok Thai and were so happy we did. 

It's located in a section of shops and a Residence Inn. In the plaza is a Mexican place, a beer garden, Carrabba's and various retail shops. Upon entering you realize just how great this place is going to be. There are linens on each table and the inside is ornately appointed. Each table is adorned with fresh flowers. 

Our server was extremely attentive and brought out our drinks very quickly. 

We decided to split a sushi roll and order an entree each. Our server was kind enough to bring out our roll while our entree was cooking. 

The roll was spectacular-the shrimp was fresh and adult warm from the tempura frying. Our entrees came out piping hot and were decorated with a fresh orchid. The vegetables were FRESH-crispy fresh, which I like. The sauce on my cashew chicken was spot on and even the rice was shaped into a heart. 

If we are ever in this part of town again, I would definitely come back. It is a great addition to the Odessa area.
Steak frites are amazing! All the different sauces are incredible. Not a bad item on the menu. Careful on the Tripel de Ripple. That will kick in faster than you think.
Ribs and brisket was awesome... No sauce needed.. Very busy but food still came out fast and hot...we will be back...

Love this restaurant!  This is the second time we have visited. Food was phenomenal! Ambience is wonderful. Server was kind, courteous and beyond prompt. We loved everything we ordered. HIGHLY RECOMMEND baguette & olive oil, spinach and beet salad, salmon as well as  macaroni and cheese (YUM). My only critique is the music that was being played. Otherwise I give this restaurant a 10 out of 10 and highly recommend!!!!!

I took my car into Eddie's because I was tired of feeling like Firestone (et al) was taking me for the proverbial ride.  First off, it is obvious that this is a mom and pop place.  I would much rather give my money to a place like that than Firestone.  Secondly, everyone I talked to, in person or on the phone, was extremely courteous and thorough with explanations.  I thought the bill was fair.  I will definitely be going back even though it is a drive for me.

Working on getting new artwork.  Hubby's ex-wife had atrocious taste.  Seriously bad.  Like expensive and bad.  I want to meet her and see what her clothes are like.  'Cause it is BAD taste.

Sorry, I digress.  So, I have been hunting down interesting prints and other paintings in a mid-range price point.  Freeman's is great because you get the pretty catalogue and start wandering around.  There are estimates of prices and you can ask lots of questions.   Yes, there are those things going for the equivalent of a nice house or condo, but you can still admire them up close.

Over the years, I have gotten a fabulous French desk, settee, and old Oriental rug for a song (like $250 for a 10x12).   Plus, the clothes are great at the couture sales.  

It's my favorite weird way to spend an afternoon to watch the bidding process.  You can wander in for a few minutes at lunch and see the phone, internet, and live bidding go down.  A great piece of Philadelphia living history- first auction house still going in the United States.

Dark lighting, and being jammed in with others isn't my thing. The food took forever, our drinks took forever, the check took forever. We got our appetizer 4 minutes before the food came out. The chicken was cold when it came out, and so was the steak. The margaritas weren't mixed well either. Over all pretty on par with a chain restaurant. I'd rather go eat at a Nashville original restaurant.

I LOVE, LOVE, LOVE Anthropologie!!  I can literally spend hours there. If i'm feeling a bit down, I instantly feel better once I step inside.  I always imagine what it would be like to live in the store!  It is absolutely beautiful.  They do a great job with all the displays and its always changing. Also, I always love when old buildings have been well preserved and the original features maintained despite being used for a different purpose. As well as clothes, they have really lovely items for the home.  I always find myself drifting of into a dream world where I live this charmed life and money is no issue.  In this life, I spend my days thinking about interior design and entertaining guests in my beautiful Rittenhouse townhouse where I would be serving tea and cakes on funky plates with matching napkins and wearing one of those fancy aprons they sell.

I really do love Anthropologie, and before they opened in London last year, I always brought my friends visiting from home there.  I think I felt at home there as it's one of the few american chain stores that had that eclectic european feel that i'm used to.  The only reason i'm not giving 5 stars is I think some of the things are a bit overpriced.  Also the prices don't seem consistent, some items are quite reasonable and then others are, "Seriously?! You expect me to pay $200 for that?!"  They have a large sale section downstairs that is always being restocked so it is worth checking regularly.  You can get some good bargains in there.  

Today was their holiday shopping event which is always great.  Same shop with all the things love but with free wine, snacks and gift wrapping.  Amazing!
They are the best! Jenny takes such great care of Charlie and always makes him look so awesome!! I highly recommend them!!
Tried this for the first time tonight it was great! Prices are good and food is tasty

Out front of Mafioza's is a neon sign that reads "Sorry, we're open 'til 3." I couldn't put it much better myself. The food isn't good, per se, nor is staff all that friendly, but within Nashville's shameful spread of late-night options Mafioza's has made a name for itself simply by keeping its doors unlocked. Prime location between sprawling suburbs and the city's nightlife ensures that Mafioza's is never deserted, but there is no standout attribute that explains why so many people end up there. 

Then again, the pizza isn't bad, the staff isn't awful, and it's a nice place to sit and relax on the way back from a night downtown. If you're looking for one.

I was in nerd heaven.  So many games (board, cards, puzzles, interactive...) that I couldn't decide on just one.  The staff were super friendly and helpful and even opened a game to let us check it out.
Every time I come here we receive exceptional service and the sushi is the best in town. I also recommend the soup. Fantastic.

I will never go back to this nail salon. They offer SNS on their menu, but obviously have no idea how to use the product. The owner removed my previous SNS by scraping it off my nails with a nail file, instead of soaking in acetone. He ruined the condition of my natural nails. The SNS that he then applied chipped on the tips of multiple fingers within 2 days. This is a product that should easily last 2+ weeks. Furthermore, the shop looks sloppy - they keep supplies and tools in shoeboxes and plastic shopping bags piled on windowsills and on the floor in the corners of the room. This is a place where you definitely get what u pay for, if that much.

We stopped in for a quick bite to eat.  Visiting our son in Tampa.  We ordered the Pork Nacho's which we ate them all but the beer cheese was a little off putting.  I ordered the Disco (Poutine, brown gravy with cheese curds) and it was good.  My son ordered the Blackened Chicken and it had a lot of flavor and was juicy.  I had two Blue Moon drafts.  The inside is not overwhelming, so it makes it inviting.  There is outside seating if you choose not to eat inside and yes mask are required to go inside.  I would give it a 3.5 if that was an option but it was not a 4.  I would go back but its not a destination for me next time I am in Tampa.  Waitress was fast and friendly.  Overall the inside was clean as well.

Don't waste you money in this ripoff of a strip Club. $22 admission for a terrible "show". Dance-corners are right in the middle of the club and staff looks at you like hawks. Even the girl who was dancing on me was embarrassed because they were so looking over to us. Never again. She was a cutie tho, and the only one who was pretty. All the others are below the standard of what you'd expect in a strip joint. 
Spend your money elsewhere.

We had a bad experience the first time I went to this newly opened restaurant, but had to go back and so glad I did. The food was good the music was excellent as we went upstairs Liz Hollister is on the weekly Saturday night entertainment schedule which includes other musicians Who joins in and what a great time for all.

Food was good. Fried asparagus was yummie, and a large portion. Halibut slider was tastey. Butter cake was sinfully delicious. Service was very slow, never got a drink refill. Would have been 5 stars, service knocks it down.

If this was based on food they'd get a 3.5.  

5 Guys with a BS message.  Fact is its still burgers and fries.  Wrapping them in health conscious buzzwords like "organic" and "grass-fed" does not make it health food.   Theres even a link to that tripe on their site called Better for You.  At least 5 Guys, or even Great Burger down the road spares us the act of trying to make you feel like downing a bacon cheeseburger, fries and a shake is some sort of health-conscious decision.

Note:  I understand that a burger and fries isn't as healthy as a garden salad.  But there are plenty that count, and want to know how eating there will fit into their diet for the day/week.

Don't get me wrong, I love cheeseburgers, maybe a little too much.  And Elevation does a good job on that front.  But hiding behind a health halo (mind you they don't publish nutritional data) doesn't make french fries good for you.

I like this place; as a frequent travel to go to a lot of restaurants and this place has some pretty good food and it's the overly expensive. It's a very casual and relaxing atmosphere and they have pretty good viewing setup for TV's if sports is your thing. 

I like the nachos and they have a reasonable beer menu.

My first time for actual Dim Sum and I had a great time at China Pavilion! I am such a fan of small eats and you can find that here and so much more! 

Their Dim Sum menu is separate from their regular menu as Dim Sum is only available on the weekends from 11:30-2:00pm.

Our server was awesome and the overall service was excellent! She even threw in a few lighthearted jokes as we ordered our meals! Pan Fried Pot Stickers, Chicken Shu Mai and Steamed Soup Dumplings were my faves! 

Thank you China Pavilion!

My husband and I initially liked this shop for all our vehicles. This changed after many unnecessary repairs were made and repairs that didn't resolve the issue at all. My husband did his own personal work on his own time to help advance his company, but he kept being taken advantage of and wasn't appreciated. Since all this has occurred we have stopped bringing our vehicles to this shop. It is unfortunate since we initially did like the shop. They have never reached out to make things right, after I've tried working with them. If you have repairs needed that they recommend always get a second or third opinion. I promise this will save you a lot of frustration and money in the long run. If resolved in the future to satisfactory I will do a new positive review.

So we made a reservation here for my birthday. When the hostess called to confirm our reservation, she was less than delightful... Skip to the day of, we arrived right on time, but were told our table was not ready and to have a seat at the bar... Which would have been fine, but all the seats were already full. We stood around wondering what to do next and luckily someone else left. Okay, fine, so we had a cocktail while we waited. Since we weren't given a time frame, we decided to request menus so we could get a head start on our selections.  Fast forward 25 and we were on our way to a great meal. Overall, the starters and entrees were amazing, the dessert was sub par. 

What we got: Ham croquettes (juicy inside, crispy outside. amazing), garlic shrimp (sizzling, lemony , fried garlic. yum), aged manchego with AMAZING truffle honey, cheese with a tart cherry/fig marmalade, scallops perfectly cooked, short rib flatbread cheesey goodness, creamy baked goat cheese, and chicken with an egg (very simple, our least favorite). Dessert was a custard of sorts and a chocolate cake... Not our favorite. Oh, and even though I made a note on Open Table and mentioned that it was my birthday when the hostess called to confirm our reservation, it was not even acknowledged... Disappointing.

We will return to enjoy tapas and just skip dessert in the future!

We have been a few times now.

I like the open feel of the place, but it can get noisy when busy.

Always greeted and served by very friendly and efficient folks.

Having had a variety of foods and a pho or two, all I can say is we have yet to be disappointed.  All very tasty and some things one does not find on other menus.

So when we are in that part of town I assume we will be back.

I went here for lunch while visiting with a friend. The place was very busy, and after eating there I can definitely see why!

All of their sandwiches are named after cartoon shows, which makes the kid in me think that they are more fun to eat! Both of us ordered the Speed Racer panini. Fried chicken, mozzarella, garlic scented tomato and vodka sauce on a focaccia. Delicious! She ordered a chocolate chip cookie and I had the chocolate chip peanut butter. The peanut butter cookie had little chunks of peanuts in it. Both cookies were perfection. 

Hoping to make our way back there the next time I visit so I can try out one of their other delicious sounding sandwiches or salads!

My girlfriend had the Country Fried Steak and I had the Country Chicken Fried Chicken.... Both were delicious, ordered the dinner portion... It's comes with 2 chicken breast. Yup..! So good I polished off both patties and the sides, mashed potatoes with green beans cooked in bacon. 

My girlfriend who usually has leftovers finished her entire plate... Only thing we recommend is get an extra side of gravy. At least for us we wanted to have more gravy to dip our food into.. 

If you want comfort food and like these types of food. I recommend you order either of these plates. If you have a significant other, order both and split the plate so you each have both types. 

I apologize for not taking a picture but when I return I will take a pic and post it... 

Now on the dessert... Chocolate Croissant  Bread Pudding... Was so, so... I would recommend the Monster Cookie Sunday..!
Worst customer service ever! I spent an hour on hold and they did not know how to fix the problem with there own devices. I had to call family member to fix problem, turns out device was bad.

This is an incredible car service. I've used them many times for trips to the airport, but recently my mother needed a daily ride to and from Penn Hospital (she can't drive in city traffic). She's confused by things like Uber, and we wanted a service that would be reliable. Avalon Limo was such a gift during this hard time! They were kind, flexible, and just went out of their way to take care of my family. Honestly, I know we could not have gotten through the past two weeks without them. Every driver went out of his way to help my mom, one of them even helped her pack and unpack medical equipment. If you call, no doubt you will get Sara--and she is such a pleasure to work with. She answers the phone immediately. There is never any waiting or wondering with this service. If you want the anxiety taken out of a trip, just call Avalon Limo. I can't thank them or compliment them enough.

A nice overall experience but nothing out of this world. Guacamole, tuna tiradito and shrimp and fish tacos were good but nothing memorable. Plantains were yummy but could have been better. My favorite was actually the chopped salad. Service was fine. The location is great and the restaurant itself has a nice feel to it.
