I think it's a little bit expensive for what it is (especially, as others have commented, when it comes to the guac!) and with a slight improvement in the kitchen this place would be much more interesting.
I have used their service for years and always reliable and uncanny how they make the proper measurements each time.

I was hoping this was going to be great because of how close I live to it, but it wasn't :( My nail tech seemed rushed and wasn't very thorough. My nails came out so streaky at first and I asked her to do it again and she seemed very annoyed. There are bubbles and fuzz stuck in my nails and I was charged $95 for a gel mani/pedi. Not to mention she got polish on my skin. I was irritated by the time she was done that i just wanted to leave. Won't be coming back

I went to Pizzology for the first time for lunch during the week with coworkers. It wasn't very busy at the time. We sat next to windows so we had a great view of Mass Ave. I'm sure during the summer/weekends it is a fun view.

Most of us ordered the lunch special which consisted of soup or salad and also a personal pizza (of which you could get any of their pizzas). Most of us opted for that; one person ordered a full size pizza (which was enough for 2-3 people, so nice for leftovers for one!). 

I had the salad (chop salad), and it was delicious. The ingredients (gorgonzola, grape tomatoes, olives...) were very fresh, and there was an ample amount of toppings so you didn't get a lot of bites of just plain lettuce.  Others who had the soup also enjoyed it.

I ordered the Baby Cheesus pizza (lol). It was seriously a cheese lover's dream pizza! Local Foxglove cream cheese, parmesan, and mozzarella make for a delicious and uniquely flavored plain cheese pizza. I've seen others complain about burnt crust, but it is like Blaze or any other oven-fired pizza. 

Our lunch took a while, so I guess that was the only downside (although I was not in a hurry to get back to work ;)). 

This isn't the best pizza I've had (Chicago homegirl here!), butI will definitely return when I'm in the Mass Ave. area.

The very definition of mediocre. I have personal pet peeves and this seemed to hit them all. The plates weren't dry. The salad section was sloppy. The dessert section was dissatisfying. But the worst was the carving station. I have never eaten drier brisket in my life, it was cremated. The prime rib tasted really strange. I didn't even bother with the sausage. I may not have eaten $20 worth of food, but I definitely threw away $20 worth. The pastas weren't bad. The macaroni and cheese was good. If you are mobile you will find better buffets at: Atlantis, Peppermill, and El Dorado.
Old style walk up, with some outside seating. Tried the Cajun Chicken Sandwich (Good), Steak Fingers (Good) and Chili (Ok - not a fan of beans in my Chili). Overall a good meal.
An hour and 30 min an the pizza never showed up. I live 6minutes away. Not even a sorry.

Show was fun. Cash bar was very slim pickings and no garnishes for the two beers they had (corona and no lime?) 
The food was just ok. The portions were incredibly small we had to stop somewhere on the way home bc we were hungry. 
We did think the show was a good time though and would probably return for that. Don't pay full price though. Definitely try for a groupon or through goldstar.

A Quaker City Tech was sent to my home via my to diagnose a problem with my Kitchen Aid dual fuel range.  a few days after the repair call, a rep from Quaker City called to tell us that he hated being the bearer of bad news but we needed three parts totaling about $500, one of which was no longer available...my range is 7 years old and Kitchen Aid stopped manufacturing the part--they really did!!!  We were is a panic thinking we had to replace an $8000. range.  We called AJ Appliances on 15th and Passyunk Aves in South Philadelphia for a second opinion. Joe Pinto, their tech came over, looked at our range, replaced a $30 part and it's working perfectly!  I called Quaker City and told them about the misdiagnosis. They said: "we stand behind our technicians and your range is probably going to break again!"
such professionalism...call AJ Appliance Repair instead.

Bought a Groupon for this place and saw that it had lots of good reviews, but I have been trying to make an appointment for 2 weeks to no avail. I've emailed, called, left multiple voicemails, but no one has ever picked up the phone or gotten back to me. Not sure if the Groupon was some kind of scam or what's going on. There's no indication that the office is closed. I don't understand what's going on.

I keep coming back to Zaytoon's! I'm from the Mediterranean and the food tastes like home. Also, the owner is heartwarmingly welcoming. My favorites are the babaganoush, the hummus, the tabouleh salad, chicken shawarma, the falafel sandwich, and the fries (probably the best fries I've ever had). When you go there, don't forget to ask for the spicy green sauce - it's freaking delicious. 
The place is a small business venture that deserves to prosper, because it is truly good. So please, go give them your business and enjoy the delicious food!

Small local eatery. Friendly staff. Good shrimp. 
I'll return to try more dishes throughout the weekend.

It was a Saturday night around 7:30 pm the restaurant was packed.  There were four of us in our party 2 adults and 2 children.  

We ordered as our appetizer the cheesy fries and little did we know that this was going to be the best part of our meal.  The fries were hot and ooey gooey cheesy delicious!

Our main courses included two sirloin steaks with a baked potato, Broccoli with cheese and a side salad.  The children ordered a cheeseburger with broccoli and ribs with the fruit cup. Everything was average and couldn't stand up to the appetizer. 

The real problem with our meal was no one ever came to remove all the dirty dishes from my table it appears outback no longer has busboys they only have a waiter to take your food order this is pathetic. Who wants to sit at a table with dirty dishes all around you. 

The waitress was extremely forgetful she forgot a drink, she forgot to bring butter, and she forgot an order of shrimp that went along with one of the sirloin steaks.  

This place was not memorable and I won't be returning.

Have been going there for 6 years as the food is terrific and the staff are always friendly. You won't be disappointed! We add grilled jalapeños to the fajitas. Always heavily decorated for the season which I appreciate as it adds to the festive yet casual atmosphere.

My husband and I flew into NOLO yesterday and immediately went looking for a good breakfast. Not wanting to visit one of the old classics, we decided to try this place. It is excellent!  It has great gluten free options (for me), and is casual (for him).  The food was excellent, and a little unusual.  Service was prompt and friendly, but not overbearing. So, we are back today for lunch!

I asked if there was a minimum about of hours you had to rent it for a Saturday night and this was the response I received    

"While you MAY be able to reserve a request for as few as three hours on a Saturday night, there is a strong chance that on VERY LITTLE notice ( an hour or two ) you will receive a phone call explaining that the vehicle was "damaged, delayed and / or defunct" and that no other vehicle is available. This last-minute cancellation will be the result of a better request  ( $$$ ) for the company. While Profile Transportation will accept a 3-hour booking ( perhaps a day or two in advance of service ), I am not able to commit a vehicle on a Saturday night two-plus months in advance.  Please remember that you need a vehicle which is specialized...not the standard sedan or SUV. Be careful. Thank you. Philip.".

The good: It's located directly across the street from Reading Terminal where you can find everything your heart desires - including vegan hot dogs! It's also connected to the convention center if you're there for an event.

The bad: I was literally greeted with "Next!" as I waited in line to check-in and was treated as if I was a bother to the person doing her job. I suppose I could have waited in the Elite line had I spotted it, but if this how they treat guests without status, count me out. The neighborhood is kinda sketch too. 

The ugly: Stains on the carpet and a run-down feeling.
The best massage I've had in Tampa was by Kaita! All of the staff members were very friendly and helpful. Such a great experience!

I use to come to this location years ago but my last recent visit my food was horrible, every item was cold, my pancakes were over cooked and hard and stiff, I could tell the batter was not fresh... but the service was good.. however I will not be coming back as I felt like I paid for trash bcuz that's where my meal ultimately ended up

This is a nice theater, even if it is lagging behind the other area theaters. It has a couple screens with fantastic quality. Theater 9 and 13 (or is it 12?) have big screens with good projection. Unfortunately, all the theaters are standard seating. The movies generally don't get too full here. If you go at the right times, you can get some bargains on movies, but the RPX theater is never price reduced, which is a disappointment. 

The patrons here aren't always the best, some leave quite a mess, even though the theater does a great job at cleaning, especially after you notice how disgusting some of the other theater goers are. You'll also end up with the occasional talker/phone user/seat kicker/child in an R rated movie.

The biggest negative of this place is the poor quality popcorn.

The fried oysters were great and the rest of the food was really good. Our server was amazing as well. My complaint: I feel that they make the servers work a section upstairs AND downstairs. Which makes it impossible for them to keep track of the food is coming out on time or if anyone at the table needs anything as simple as silverware. So my lack of 5 stars lies on the management. We saw multiple people in the seating area waiting on something and that's due to spreading their staff thin

This restaurant is a terrible excuse for a restaurant especially in New Orleans where there are many other excellent choices for dining out. Hibachi is fun and usually tasty, but this place is dirty and the food is not fresh.

Food was really good. Obviously paying for a high end restaurant you expect high end quality and service.  My 3 star rating is based from my expectations of a high end restaurant like this. The food was good but no excellent and the service was.....well just alright. I'm not suggesting it was poor but it wasn't over the top either.  This was not a busy time as the pacers game had already began 30mins prior so the rush was over.   With the great selections in the downtown area I can say I probably won't return here anytime soon.

I was in St Louis on business, looking for a knitting circle to attend.  The group was very friendly, funny, and interesting.

Nicely varied yarn selection with good color range (found a wonderful orange and green in Hempathy) - great selection of books.

Personal favorite: the Hello Kitty stitch markers.  Love it!
It is very good food but no alcohol is here if you like margaritas and others drinks a lot of bottled drinks no tap drinks over all it is a five star

Wonderful food and quick service! Everything was fresh and delicious. I had a Turkey Club, potato salad, Cobb salad and Macadamia  nut cookie. Everything was incredible. Will definitely be going back often. The staff was super polite and helpful. I would recommend highly!

This place is pretty good! I always do the drive thru, and it's not that long of a wait. I usually get a sandwich there, and its good. My vegetarian sister gets the "impossible" whopper there, and she likes it. My only complain is that our server, Basil, put half the amount of onion rings I ordered there. Overall, very tasty sauces and food!

We stopped by the Blue Willow for lunch before a visit to the Desert Museum. The staff was welcoming and helpful, as well as being fully-masked. The food was delicious and quick. I had a super fresh beet salad with chicken, while my boyfriend had the Mac n Cheese with broccoli and chicken. Both were large portions, enough to take home. They also had a variety of tasty adult beverages, and my prickly pear margarita felt perfect for the location. We picked up a few trinkets on the gift shop on our way out as well.

I came to David to get my haircut in layers. I have had traumatizing haircuts where too much length had been cut in the past and David even asked me about them so I assumed that from that alone he would understand that I didn't want too much of the length cut. I also told him length wise I was maybe willing to lose 3-4 inches at most.

After the haircut I noticed that the shortest piece (which was  to my hips before) was slightly past my chin. I was literally in shock and trying to just stay calm during the rest of the experience.When I came in my hair was past my hips at least 4-5 inches and I left with a SLIVER of long hair up to the middle of my back. 

After a few days of literally crying and being extremely depressed ( I have been growing out my hair for 10 years and trimming it every 2 months to keep it healthy.) I worked up the courage to send him a message Via Instagram telling him how I felt in a civil manner and hoping to feel some form of closure from the entire situation. He expressed that he was sorry and appreciated the feedback and I WAS going to leave it at that. 

Just today I noticed that he blocked me and posted my photo on Instagram with the caption saying " let that shit go. " 
I feel like this is extremely unprofessional and even spiteful towards me.

No one should be made to feel the way I did by someone you entrusted to care for your hair, something sacred to women. I feel violated and I cannot get my hair back for it.

I share this painful experience in hopes that it doesn't happen to anyone else.

My wife and I went here based on a local recommendation ---our valet at the hotel. We walked there. The restaurant is gorgeous. We went for lunch and it was pretty empty. We went there for peel and eat shrimp, which was not on the lunch menu. We opted for Po Boys. Having been to Parkway and a couple other places, the Bourbon House's Po Boys are okay. Their seafood, spinach and artichoke bread appetizer was very good. Their grilled oysters were too salty and each oyster was different --- one had too much Parmigiano, one had none....poor quality control. I paid way too much for the quality of food we got...so I was kinda salty about the dining experience. It was nothing I could complain about to a manager. Again the place is beautiful and a nice respite from the busy goings on outside, but it wasn't worth the price to me. I was underwhelmed and wished I hadn't gone there.

Cigar City is the ONLY gold medal winning brewery in the state of Florida and for a very good reason: Everything they make is fantastic!  Don't believe me?  Go to the many beer review and rating websites and you will find that Cigar City makes several of the best beers In The World!  From the more common Jai Lia and Florida Cracker to their limited edition Hannuhpuh Russian Imperial Stout, everything is amazing. Add to that the fact that this is a working brewery, something Tampa sorely misses since Budweiser pulled its Brewery our of Busch Gardens, and you get a special experience. Tours are only $5 or so and you get a beer and glass to keep. Talk about a bargain. If there is any negative it would be that Cigar City's popularity can make it crowded at prime time. So i tend to come at off hours like right after work or thursday evening. Also this is a brewery only; no food. So to remedy this Cigar City has food trucks come at busy times. Yesterday i was there and they had one called Hot Mess which was very good comfort food like fried mac and cheese. So hurry over because this is the type of local creativity that we want to reward and that can help Tampa build a reputation for GREAT beer.

Yeah.  It's a 5.

You should know...I don't drink alcohol, so I can't speak to their beer selection of the quality of the beer.

I CAN speak to the atmosphere, service, and food, and it was all stellar.  The menu is limited...food isn't their "main" thing, after all, but it was very good.  The service was stellar, and more than that, from the servers to the bookstore clerk to the owner himself, they all seemed like they actually appreciated  the fact that we were there.  That goes a long way.  We'll be back, and soon.

It's good ramen no doubt. But the noise of the speakers are off the chart. I think the purpose of this is to keep people moving I'm sure. I'm just not paying these prices to not have a conversation without shouting. I will say the steamed pork buns are so good!! I'll go back during the earlier hours and hopefully won't get blasted out again.

Relaxing atmosphere, great food, awesome drinks and excellent service, what more could we ask for. We will definetly return on our next visit to Santa Barbara. We were very impressed by the level of service provided to us by the staff. Big "Thank You" to Daniel O'Conn for being so attentive and making us feel so welcomed. Congratulations to your establisment for understanding how important great service is in this line of business.  Keep up the great work. 
Very Happy Customers,
Mr. & Mrs. Yang

Love the sandwiches but had an awful experience today. I tried to order by phone but it goes straight to voice mail box that hasn't been set up. I ordered online and messed up the order. I made a second order online for the correct sandwich expecting to pay for 3 sandwiches instead of 2. My fault but I should have been able to call the store and correct the order. Not happening since only voice mail. I ordered the correct sandwich. My daughter went to pick up and staff were inside but the door was locked with a sign saying they had no bread. Great just great. Now I wonder if I'm going to find 3 sandwiches on my credit card that we never got. This store is a joke. They're always out of something and not being able to call is ridiculous. Love the sandwiches but hate this store. I wouldn't give them any stars but that's not an option. You can't even call to talk to an employee or manager.

13$ lunch for a gut-bomb flavorless grilled cheese and cup of soup that actually tasted bad to me. The soup was lukewarm and I'm guessing old. 

I wish the server had asked why the food was mostly uneaten, or offered to bring something else. Would've made the difference between disliking the meal and feeling ripped off and kind of hating a restaurant. Other people've had better luck here. But I am resentful of a bad meal carelessly prepared and served.

Terrible customer service.....Manager Randy treats people like dirt... Very disrespectful.....He not only was rude he doesn't care other customers are right there listening to him rip me over my bill...Very embarrassing for the Dean Team......

Really good pizza.  We got a large cheese, regular chicken artichoke, and a portabello goat cheese.  The cheese was good, a doughy crust and lots of mozzarella, my son's favorite.  My wife and her friend favored the chicken artichoke, tons of toppings on a thin crust.  I really like the portabello goat cheese, thin crust and just the right amount of goat cheese.  A little on the spendy side but a nice treat
Jeff & Chris at SB Auto Group BMW found the car I wanted and worked out a good lease deal.  Very pleasant to deal with and responsive.  Good guys.

Great place, wonderful atmosphere ... The bartenders/mixologists are very helpful and generous !  The food is equally amazing !! The crab cakes are amazeeeee !!! The steak is perfection !!! Oh and the shrimp and grits are pure Perfection !!! Great gem for CWE !

Disappointed. I wanted to like this nail salon so badly. The salon is beautiful and they have happy hour which I think is very innovative, however, I hate my nails. I got a dip powder manicure and the color is inconsistent, they weren't filed and buffed down evenly and I was cut. I will say that the manager offered to fix my nails if I come back within a week, however I was in the area for work only for the day so this wasn't an option for me.

Horrible dining experience. Trial run to test the waters if it is really back to normal per se to dine in at our favorite restaurant. Low and behold. Worst experience ever. Floor was filty dirty. I posted a photo to prove my case. The QR code to see your menu, not very cooperative. All we see are ads. When we informed our server, she gave us an attitude and informed us to just view the menu on the website. She also said,"we have no paper menu so you will need to view the menu online." What ever happened to great customer service? Did it disappear and this is the "new normal?" Then a few moments later, old folks walk in with white colored paper menu. Nice one! Busted big time!!! Took so long to give us our order but I am used to that. Always short staff. I still left 15% tip eventhough she doesn't deserve it. I guess that will be our last time dining in. Back to take out since service is better.

I wanted to love it here, truly. I will preface by saying the staff is amazing and the only reason for three stars. We ate on the 5th floor, and other than the hot chicken sandwich, the food was mediocre at best. The pulled pork was somehow dry and greasy at the same time, with no sauce and a few shreds of slaw. It had to be sent back. The fries were the only saving grace for the under-sized burgers. The calamari was extremely greasy, but not in a yummy, fried way, but in a heavily over- sauced way. Quality control needs to be enforced, I doubt the famous owners would be okay with lack of care and finess coming from the kitchen.

Such a unique and fun venue to play some mini golf! The staff is very friendly and helpful. They're even dressed up in costume to match the monster theme. Decorations in the building are "spooky" without being too scary or weird for little kids. They've got an arcade to play in while you wait to get golfing, as well as prizes you can collect tickets to win. 

The course is made up of 18 holes, each with varying levels of difficulty and obstacle blocking your way. The staff will occasionally call out challenges for you to do while golfing, like swinging the club between your knees (granny style!) or behind your back. Tons of fun! Things usually move along pretty smoothl. There are certain holes that bottleneck up so don't be surprised if you have to wait a little while to get onto the next hole. Luckily the music is awesome so you can jam along while you wait.

The mini golf is also super affordable! I think I paid $25 for 4 games (groupon!). They also hand out free play vouchers to the loser, which is the cherry on top!

The food was good. Good pub food, we had a couple different burgers and a stuffed chicken sandwich. 
The service was bad. The kind that gets a bad review, the restaurant was mostly empty but still only saw our server a couple times ( not really sure she was a server or a manager). 
I had the Frisco Melt with the tots, the burger was good and the tots were crispy and delicious .

Simply amazing!  My wife and I were after some good shrimp and grits...and boy did we ever find them!

We did not have reservations on a Friday night, so we had to sit at the oyster bar (or wait 45 minutes) and were a bit apprehensive at first because the oysters are right there on ice.  But, we were quickly made comfortable by the two gentlemen behind the bar.  We chatted for a bit, since this was our first time here, to get some insider information about the menu.  One of them told us that the andouille sausage won some awards, so we decided to try that as our appetizer.  Delicious and such a generous portion.  

My wife ordered the shrimp and grits and I ordered the halibut special of the night; our waitress Megan was extremely knowledgeable of the menu and made my choice extremely difficult.  

The shrimp and grits were, without a doubt, the best we have ever had.  The grits are ground in house and cooked with gouda and a hint of chipotle...so creamy!  The shrimp was on point as well.  

My halibut had a nice taste to it with thinly sliced grilled onions to top it off, along with a couple of sauces (that I can't remember what they were) but the whole dish was topped with crushed up peanuts.

We drove down to Nashville to catch a movie and found this gem of an eatery!  We will definitely be back!  Great job guys.

Worst breakfast I've ever had.  Absolutely no flavor at all to my waffles or eggs.  Our waitress was barely around so the drinks were never refilled.  She did notice that I didn't touch my meal and apologized and took that off the bill.  An appetizer with sausage gravy was ordered, don't remember exactly what it was( it was that unmemorable and terrible) and that also had no flavor.  This is the 2nd time I've had lousy food there.  I'm afraid to go back.  Their grilled cheeses are the only thing I would get if I ever did go back there.

This place is a great place for business conferences, weddings, club meetings, and the like.  It is also a great place for lunch with a friend or business associate. 

I didn't score it five stars just for the food.  Although you cannot fault the food for prep, taste, and presentation.  The food is excellent and worthy of five stars.  However, unless you can guarantee a pearl in every other dozen oysters, thirty dollars a dozen for oysters on the half is insane. 

The BIG payoff in this treasure of a Tampa Bay restaurant is the scenic view of Tampa Bay and the overall ambiance.  If you want to romance the wife or partner, make an impression, thank someone for their business, or you've won the Florida Lottery and want to treat yourself; this is your place.

My pictures just say it all YUMMY!!  the sushi is fresh and the customer service was amazing.  Sushi one after another - best part its ALL YOU CAN EAT SUSHI!   very affordable for how much you can get!  Also i recommend the mango ice cream and why not do some saki bombs while youre at it.  :)

Awesome food. Awesome beer selection. Always kind staff. My friends tell me the sushi is some of the best they have ever had (I'm not a sushi eater so I take their word for it)I love love love this place! Come here as often as I can :)

Watching a movie in a reclining position is a luxury all people should be allowed to experience and enjoy. We're so spoiled having a Galaxy Theater here in Reno-Sparks and I must say it has renewed my love of going to the movies. It's still rare that we go, but each time we do, we leave saying we should do this more often.

Last night we saw Jurassic World, an epic CG fest filled with badass dinosaurs and all sorts of amazing carnage. Indominous Rex? Are you effing kidding me? This thing was a serious contender for scariest thing in the history of the world, and managed to consume half the staff of the park through the course of the movie.

Speaking of consumption, why does Galaxy have to offer the skyscraper sized popcorn bucket? Every time we go my wife gets it, and we only eat half. I always ask why she gets this monstrous bucket o' corn and her answer is "It was only $1 more!" It's hard to argue with a deal like that, but buyer beware. That extra buck will cost you popcorn in your teeth, possibly a tummy ache from over indulging, and the annoyance of having its silhouette obstruct your view of the film until you succumb to it and place it in your lap further contributing to your over consumption and closing the deadly loop of the extra large popcorn. 

Fortunately the tummy ache won't set in until after the movie is over, so it won't interfere with the comfort you're enjoying in the luxury + seats at Galaxy. I'm looking forward to the next blockbuster to come out so I can do it all over again!

==Service: 5 Star==
when you buy a ticket on the ticket booth, you can ask them for an information. The staff there are very nice. It feels that they are willing to help you. This staff characteristic shows that they have good customer service.

==Food: 4 Star==
I believe the food was good, and the cost is about normal as going outside and eat.

==Atmosphere: 4 Star==
Typical normal zoo, nothing fancy nothing to get excited about.

 ==Cleanliness: 4 Star==
Zoo cleanliness is hard to maintain but so far it is clean.

==Cost: 3 Star==
25 Dollars entrance fee. I believe that is too much, but it is worth it. You can really see the animals close up. Which is better than other zoo, where you can only see the animals in a distance. Food is affordable.

Note: This is my opinion and it does not affect others. You may agree or disagree, it is not my responsible for your actions if any circumstances occur. Thank You.
I got a massage here with my mom & when we walked in it was very serene & quiet & in the massage room it was dimmed & very peaceful with Japanese zen music, best massage ever!

I absolutely love the Reno area with all of it's parks and trails and Galena Creek Park is one of Reno's highlights. A hidden gem,   Perfect day, warm weather, well defined trails, wildflowers in bloom and rushing water.  There are trails fro everyone - for the strollers and for those who want more of a challenge with higher elevations and more incline.  Great place for a picnic.  Lots of picnic tables and places for bbqs.  The blue jays were in full force, along with the squirrels.  Fishing at Marilyn's Pond was great - you can get trout there.  People fly fish there and also fish from the bank.  I think the limit is 5 or 6.  Check the signs and check with your nearest game warden.  I also love manzanita bushes and there is plenty of it there.  It is endangered in California so I would just look and not touch in any of our NV forests.   I love the fact that  Galena Creek Park is right up Mt Rose Highway in South Reno  and easily accessible if you want a piece of nature and fresh air, a break from the biggest little city.

Oh yeah!  I'm talking about 'the fish market' at Kona Jack's.  Where in Indiana can you go and trust that the seafood was swimming the day before. I do here!  The reviews that speak of over priced in the restaurant, maybe so.  -- try buying your own and preparing it in your own home.  Call and ask what came in that morning.
There are a couple of places that I think you can trust the fish monger's dates on seafood in the city and there are many that you can't.  -just do the research!

Don't listen to your buddy who lives in the Main Line and thinks he knows "the best spot for a steak" and sends you to the place across the street. Listen to me .... I've lived here my entire life and this is the spot. 

If you want a real steak, this is where you need to go. Fresh rolls and made to order sandwiches. The meat isn't left out on the grill all day like the place across the street. 

Try the smoked peppers on your sandwich as well. A wonderful kick of flavor.

I don't know what some people expect these days...you can go down the street and get a mediocre burger and a couple of mass-market beers for the same price as a 2SP TBA burger and a pair of craft beers.

Whatever.  If you're cheap, don't go on a Tuesday...don't order the set of 7 tacos, 4 of which change week to week...stay at home...or better yet go to Taco Bell.  Save the table space for those of us that appreciate creativity, variety, and absolutely FABULOUS food.

And please stay away on Thursday, when one of the random craft beers is infused with a different flavor from a device called Randall.

Keep up the great work 2SP!
The best customer service ever! Brian is fabulous and this is a wonderful Yardley business.

Excellent brunch! We had the blueberry waffle, a side of biscuits and gravy, bacon fried rice, cinnamon toast French toast, omelet and pork steak breakfast. Literally everything was spot on. The only complaint were the guys who ordered the pork steak breakfast said it was Judy ok. The rustic ambiance was welcoming and the bar options were just about limitless. Can't wait to go back and try out their lunch or dinner menu!

This store really needs to re-think hiring the mentally ill and the mentally inept.

Yesterday, we stopped by to pick up a few things (we should have went to Rouses but it was rush-hour and we just wanted to get in and out). We were greeted in the parking lot by the person who collects carts as he was screaming ," Fucking people!", Assholes! Assholes!! Assholes!" and other really weird things as he went about his collecting.Then he stopped right in front of the Girly and stared at her. I stepped in front of her and guided her by.

The store itself seems to be decently laid out. Nothing was too hard to find and their prices, while not as low as Rouses, surely weren't in a league with Mardi Gras Zone or convenience stores.

Our cashier, however, La Joya or something like that, never greeted us, never made eye contact and never said a single word to us. She was too busy either being on the phone or yelling across the checkouts to her friend (who she then invited over and chatted with her while checking us out, never once acting like humans were standing in front of her).

A business is only as good as the people working there. If you hire people who just don't give a shit, then you risk injuring your business. And when you are across the street from a competitor, you might want to revisit your hiring practices.

Stayed here for two nights, which was two nights too many.  We've stayed here in the past and thought it was a wonderful hotel.  That was not the case this trip.  Upon arrival the staff was less than friendly which I can overlook.  The room was another story.  There was exposed electrical outlets, stains on the couch, stains on the floor, the plumbing was exposed in the bathroom - not bad just weird - shower curtain did not fit the entire shower and minimal counter space in the bathroom.  I kept my shoes on the entire stay and did not put any bags on the furniture or floor.  The door joining two rooms had an obscene picture drawn on it.  Placed 4 calls for housekeeping and never got an answer.  Also called the front desk 4 times with no answer.  Overall, the place was not nearly as nice as it was on our first stay.

Recommended by Javier at the local Marriot hotel. A great call by Javier!  Attentive service, excellent Margaritas expertly blended and delicious food.  The salsa cart was absolutely muy bien!  I have never seen this in the US or Mexico or anywhere else in the Americas. Fantastico!

Brunch was an added bonus to our 1 night in NOLA. 
The bottomless mimosas was the way to go!!! Food was rich and tasty. I wasn't crazy about my Cobb salad that's the only reason they got a 4 star. I am a Cobb fan and their spin was flat.

Some of my favorite tacos I've ever had as well as in the Santa Barbara Area, where there are great tacos and Mexican food in general. A nice shop with a classic feel, it is always a treat to eat here. The staff is very friendly and the food is excellent. I always get the tacos al pastor, which are delicious. I definitely recommend this restaurant to taco aficionados and people looking for a hidden gem.

Empanadas are amazing! Their food is delicious and you can tell it's made with the best ingredients. Love eating their food along with the ambiance of Black Circle brewery. Get the empanada sampler and you'll be quite happy!

Stopped in for take out one day. We ordered a lot, as there were a lot of us, and we were hungry. As expected, their menu isn't crazy expensive, but don't expect the world if you stop by China Inn - it's just typified Americanized Chinese cuisine. In particular, I liked their vegetable lo mein noodles.

Find it in the Northtowne shopping center, right near Game Stop. There's actually seating there, in case you don't feel like take out.
Allison's classes are the best. High energy, aerobic punch the heck outta them classes that will leave you feeling dead, but in a good way.
Floor was dirty. Food lacked taste, not a lot of selection.  Service was good nice waitress. Glad it was only 15.95 only had half a plate and I was turned off.

Would highly recommend Huntington Learning Center.  Signed up to get my son help for the ACT exam.  They did a "walk-in" exam to evaluate his level.  He attended 2x per week for 3 months.  His ACT scores improved monthly.  He now has a score that will enable him to attend the college of his choice.  Could not have been this successful without the help of one on one tutoring!  Of course it requires the commitment of the child to be successful as well.  Lastly, they were accommodating in that if your child does better with some tutors and not as well with others they will schedule your child accordingly.

UPDATE 03/16/2019: 
Owner replied (no name given) to my posting asking what they can do to make it right. While I appreciate the reply, all any business has to do is answer the phone. This voice tree, computerization, leave a message bull going on in business today is simply a way of saying "my time is worth more than yours". Want my business? Simply have someone with a heartbeat answer the phone. 

Do not know what is happening with the service department of this dealership. I have used it for my car maintenance several times in the past and was OK. I received a "Product Improvement Campaign" letter in the mail advising that I call right away and set up an appointment to have the my car CPU updated. I called Jim Click Hyundai (Ford) Green Valley four times trying to get through to the service department. Each time I received a voice mail prompt wanting me to leave a message. On the fifth try I finally reached someone with a heartbeat who promptly transferred me to a service representative who in turn put me through to their voice mail. I gave up and made an appointment at a different Hyundai dealership.

While the food here is great and the restaurant is lovely, the services is poor and it's just not a comfortable place to eat. The employees are not nice or helpful. The way you place an order, then have to go get your own drinks that are always ready before your table is is all very confusing if you've never been there. I don't like that you order at one place. Your drinks get ready at another place. You have to go grab your drinks and stand there waiting for a table drinks in hand. It makes it hard to get to your stuff coats, bags and kids together and get to a table while holding drinks. Or your mimosas warm by the time you get to a table to enjoy it with your food. I advise to order food and get to your table then go back up and get your drinks then they will bring them out to your table and they'll still be hot or cold. It's also FAR too cramped. They try to cram as many tables in there as possible so it's very hectic and chaotic and hard to move around. That's probably why the workers always seem annoyed. There's no good place to stand and wait for your table. Your always right on top of another table while eating and it's always stuffy inside. I'd say it's probably a great takeout place. The food is spectacular it's everything else that makes it hard to enjoy it.

I've heard so many awesome things via social media and word of mouth and they all we're true! The place is so beautiful on the inside and the walls are decorated with art and everything was clean. The employees we're very friendly, patient, and energetic. I ordered the "Wuzzam" wings the first visit and "BBQ" the 2nd visit. Both were awesome! The owner was also onsite and made sure everyone was satisfied. Every bite was extremely delicious and satisfying.

Welcoming and friendly staff from the moment you pull your car into the parking deck. The hotel was exceptionally clean and the room (we had a suite) was very modern and spacious. The restaurant on site had typical pub food, not amazing but god for when you don't feel like leaving the hotel. The pool was small but clean with warm towels waiting for you straight from the dryer. Excellent location with lots for families to do a short drive away. Would definitely recommend.

I think Primos hoagies are fantastic. Regardless of the location, it's hard to get a bad hoagie from this franchise. However, this location is especially great. I have been coming maybe once every 3 months for about 5 years and every time I go in the staff at this location is extremely courteous and friendly. The location is very clean and the food always comes out quickly and very fresh. Most times I am in there the owner is working but even when I have been in and he is not in the store the staffs demeanor is no different. I have gone to many primos locations and most are great but this location stands out due to the people who work there. 

Keep up the good work Ardmore Primos, your efforts are not unnoticed.

Loved this place. Great atmosphere. Great service, great food, good wine. Our server was married to the chef at merchants and we loved both restaurants. Fettuccini was not traditional but noodles were cooked perfectly!

We stopped here for lunch the day after Thanksgiving and the food was outstanding. We were even treated by a man who sang an old traditional Irish song. This place reminded me of an Irish pub in New York City. The tables were close together, but no one seemed to mind.

Brisket is horrible! save your money and time. It's dry, but with a greasy taste. The taste overall is nasty! They may offer better choices, but I'll never know because you couldn't pay me to eat there again.
Tastes like it was soaked in dirty dishwasher. Before you get skeptical,  I'm easy to please.
Waitress never asked how my food was and had to wait 10min for a straw. Restaurant was completely empty at dinner time, kinda know why now
Brady himself I think was sitting two chairs away and read my comment on ticket, yet did nothing.
There brisket is, well, gross and they don't seem to care what you think of it. Walk down to Barfly and get something good to eat!
Pinewood is a great place to unwind and hangout on the weekends. Great food, great cocktails, friendly service and a very cool atmosphere. I would highly recommend checking it out.

My friend had a horrible experience here. The order was to be delivered by DoorDash. After an hour she called to find out if the food had been finished and picked up by the delivery service. However, the girl who answered the phone just kept repeating that delivery was not their problem.My friend tried to ask if the food had even been finished and the rude girl on the phone just kept insisting that deliver is not their problem and if she wanted it she would have to come and get it herself. She did do that and when she walked in she found that the DoorDash guy I've been sitting there for an hour waiting for the order to be ready. It was ready, sitting in the back but no one told the DoorDash Delivery guy.  When my friend asked the manager, "If you're just going to let the food sit there, what were you going to do with it?", he said "none of your business". 
Even though it was all Taziki's fault, DoorDash apologized about the delay and compensated her. The delivery company showed themselves to be a class operation, but this restaurant revealed how rude and poor at customer service they really are.  
Based on my friend's experience I will NEVER patronize this restaurant and I cannot recommend it to ANY else either.
Awful and Slow Staff. Who has given the right to speak to the customers condescendingly? Never again.

Amazing atmosphere, great menu and delicious Philly Cheese Steak! My friend got the Turkey Rueben, which was also pretty great. The only downside? They didn't come with fries, just a baby bag of chips... And I do love my fries. 

But all in all we loved the cute atmosphere and great food!

We tried to get in on Friday night and gave up out of frustration. Back again on Saturday before 5 and it was manageable. Parking is terrible, if you go on a busy night I would probably park at an Thoroughbred and walk. Our wait time was reasonable about 20 minutes and we were seated. They have the screens, plenty of views throughout and the picture quality is excellent. Andrew was our server and he was attentive and moved quickly. We started with Garlic Bread and it was excellent., hot and the marinara sauce was quite tasty. I enjoyed my pizza and look forward to other selections. Hot and served in a timely fashion. Once again Andrew did a great job keeping our glasses full and asking if we needed anything. I do know the place just opened and they probably had extra staff but I was pleased and will be back. They do have a Lunch buffet and I intend to try that. Special shout out to Charlie, one of the servers. They do a good job working other tables and he stood out with his concern for all customers. A great first visit!

DO NOT finance your new car through Flagship Credit Non-Acceptance. They have such a strict policies for changing making payment arrangements or changing payment dates its insane! I imagine they do this to generate revenue.

When I spoke to a customer support "manager" he offered and confirmed a payment extension. Funny thing was the payment extension I requested WAS NOT accepted and furthermore did not receive a notification. 

Extremely unprofessional financial institution so watch out! They will not do you any favors over the course of your loan.

Great little German Biergarten! With a little more business, this place could be a nice spot. We sat outside, so I'm not commenting on the inside decor. The potato pancakes were awesome and my Hungarian Ghoulash was Yum. They also kept the beers coming:)
Excellent bagels! The everything bagel and jalapeño cheddar are on point. I make a weekly or biweekly trip for bagels and breakfast or lunch sandwiches.

My boyfriend and I needed a quick bite and wanted some solid pizza. We were not disappointed. This place was so good. The pizza slices are HUGE and they put so much toppings on speciality slices you will not be disappointed. Oh and the mozz sticks are perfect !!! I recommend x10 for some solid pizza etc in philly, cute little place, love the local feels.

I chose Rack Room to look for a pair of dress shoes as a gift to surprise my husband. They have a great selection. I was able to find his size and style he likes easily. I do think the staff could be better about acknowledging customers and being helpful. When I did go to check out there was only one girl working the register. The poor thing had a really long line of people and was working hard to get everyone through while being extremely nice and pleasant. There was what I assume to be a manager or supervisor standing right beside her doing inventory, ignoring the long line of customers and the clear help his fellow associate needed. It was pretty ridiculous. When it was my turn I did tell her what a great job she was doing and that it would've been nice if she could have gotten some help from the fellow associates just standing around. She said it hd been like that most of her shift. I used to work retail and understand inventory is important, but the paying customers keep that inventory coming in. Hopefully that manger/supervisor realizes that down the line.

We travel frequently and so visited this gym for the morning.  It was not bad.  The stair climbers were at the front door, so I felt a little self-conscious.  It wasn't super dirty nor super clean.  I take my own precautions wherever we go.  The saunas are in each locker room, which is good, warm but not hot.  I would go back if in Boise again. I asked for a towel but it was unclear if I could get one.  Cleanliness 1-5 scale, 3.  Equipment quality 3.5.

I walked in a few weeks ago after moving to the area and needing a new nail salon. First off, their color selection for SNS dip is amazing. I've never been to a salon with so many colors for the dip. Sophia took such great care of me! My nails looked fantastic and lasted 3 weeks (which is really impressive for me!). I went back a few weeks later and Sophia also did my eyebrows. They look fantastic!!! Today, I went in for my nails. Once again, fantastic! I was torn between two different colors and Sophia picked out the perfect blend of the two. They've def made a customer for life!

We were not country fans but we might be now. We new nothing About the Grand Ole Opry.  Didn't even know it was a live radio show.  A way cooler experience than I thought it would be. We saw a collection of old and new recording artists like Ashley Cambell and the White Family. Not terribly expensive either.

The wax pot is amazing! Everyone is friendly and they greet me by name as soon as I walk in. It's a small company which is what I love! They love what they do! Joan is who I go to. She is so nice and makes you feel comfortable. Amazing company!

I used to love this place, but I just picked up take out from there and I am really disappointed. It seems like they are trying to cut costs and my salad had very little in it besides spring mix! I asked for dressing on the side, salad was covered in it. Very little cheese and no kalamata olives. Just disappointing! The worst part is that the tablet that processed my visa asked for a tipping option and I tipped 20 %, as I assumed it was going to the lovely girl who brought me my order. As I was sitting and waiting. I saw a sign that said " only cash tips go to employees" which means that I just gave the business an extra 20% ! Why even have the option to tip? Seems dishonest and creepy. I heard this place switched owners, and that seems evident. Won't go back there. Too many delicious comparably price options in the area.
Some of the best dishes I've ever had. Amazing twists on Japanese food. The sauces are ridiculously good. Top of my list of restaurants to revisit.

Since we knew we'd be visiting Santa Barbara we laid down money on a couple Groupons a while back to go stand-up paddling with Channel Islands Outfitters.  We bought 2 Groupons but 4 of us ended up going, so we weren't all on the cheap.  None of us had ever done this SUP thing but we didn't sign up for a lesson, just a rental.

The shop seemed well-equipped, although the SUP boards are stored in a locker a little ways away by the beach.  That would have been fine except there was one fewer boards in the locker than we needed so I had to go back to the shop and schlep one  a quarter mile down to the beach anyway (the beginner boards are extra long, wide and heavy!)

Aside from that little bit of disorganization (it was the end of a busy summer weekend) we had a very good time playing in the waves and being chased by dolphins for a couple hours.  The CIO folks were slightly overwhelmed but when they could get to us they were attentive, informative and enthusiastic.  Money.  Well.  Spent.

Aki has good lunch specials ($11-15) that make this an easy choice during the week. Various roll combos or sushi/sashimi platters also arrive with your choice of miso soup or salad. It's usually not very crowded during lunch either, so the chefs are able to serve you quickly. They also have a full bar, if you're feeling rebellious at lunch (or just want to go to happy hour).

If you are a sushi fiend and can't get enough, get the all-you-can-eat special. $26.95/head allows you to have unlimited apps, sushi, maki, and some special rolls (some have an extra surcharge). A notable exception to this is the sashimi plate, of which only one can be ordered per head. Totally worth it, so make sure you eat it. You have to eat EVERYTHING you order or you will be charged a la carte, so take it slow and only order when you're sure you can finish the dish. Drinks not included.

The food itself is solid, with a good ratio of rice to fillings and lots of fresh fish. I especially love their miso soup, which has a really thick broth and minimal tofu cubes. On the weekends, the sushi chefs have to rush more to serve the crowd, so the technique slips a little (but then again, when you're scarfing down all the sushi you can eat, does a loose roll really matter that much? Not to me!).

Epic failure.  Scary as they come.  Convoluted bureaucracy with messed up computer systems & call center, bizarre policies & processes.  The list of issues myself and colleagues have had over the years is right out of a SNL skit.  Express Scripts should get it together or shutdown.
This is the worst groomer I've ever been to! Don't even waste your time and money! Plus they were mean to my dog!

I have driven by the Lodge so many times over the years, wondering what was behind the walls.  Now I know, an absolute GEM of a rustic, warm and comfortable lodge/hotel.  We decided on a little staycation this past weekend  here booking a double fireside casita room.  We could not have been happier with our stay.

The staff were all incredibly friendly, helpful and nice from check in to departure.  You feel as though you are away on a retreat almost and can't hear any of the traffic from being right in the middle of town!  The grounds are absolutely beautiful and clean.  There is a pool and hot tub, although we had forgotten our suits, so did not try.

Our room was spacious and comfortable.  Sleep quality excellent.   I am happy we splurged for the bigger room.  

They did offer a free breakfast to overnight guests at Cielo's.  I was not sure what to expect - they bring juice and/or coffee and a little plate with fresh muffins and fruit.  You then have your choice between two 'daily' breakfast options - we had scrambled eggs/sausage and a slice of a french toast bake.  

A bit surprised by the less than stellar reviews.

They were great!  We were in and out in no time at all. I was a little nervous about her first hair cut but they were very kind and gentle with her. She has been loving her new hair cut. So glad we choose Kim.

Tucson needed a bar like this! The beer selection is insane, and they have one of the hottest and best bartenders in Tucson. Do yourself a favor and sit at the bar and ask for Dee to help you. She is very knowledgable about all of the beers they serve at World of Beer. The couple next to me mentioned that they were very impressed with the service she provided and her beer knowledge. The food portions are small but tasty, but who cares when you have a bartender that will serve you a different beer that you will like more than the last one you had?

Pure Heaven. Came here while on our honeymoon in November 2014 on recommendation from the owner at Municipal Winemakers. On a Tuesday night, a reservation was easy to come by. The ambiance was romantic and laid-back. Interesting and fancy food, but not fancy-weird, fancy-delicious! Great service, too.

According to my husband, the ravioli tasted "like a sunset." He's not usually a poet but the food was downright inspiring! I would LOVE to go back. 

(Also, just a note... it was warm inside, the kitchen is kind of close to the dining area, so if you wear a sweater be prepared to take it off! Street parking was no problem.)

I was a little apprehensive about getting a prenatal massage in COVID times but my husband booked me a massage and it was fantastic. Thank You Mama did a great job making me feel safe! Everyone was in a mask and covid precautions were taken. I was able to comfortably wear a mask my entire massage. Belinda did an amazing job and was kind and professional.
Really great tacos! Had great flavor, and the tortillas were very great in texture and taste.

Really awesome place for breakfast! Open floor, nice bright light inside! Customer service was superb & unique! Food was amazingly yummy & worth coming back again and again! The drinks wears amazing too! All prices fair & reasonable!

Excellent espresso, my husband enjoyed his macchiato so much he immediately purchased a bag of the Gibralter beans to take home. My soy latte was smooth, delicate and mellow, and it was gone way too soon!
I love to bowl here on weekends. And I joined a league and its fun so I HIGHLY recommend bowling and participating in a league here!

Went for the first time last night for dinner with friends. Service was friendly and never felt rushed even though the place was packed. Food was delicious - I had the meatloaf sandwich. My only complaint was that seating was tight because they were so busy and those of us seated on the bar side of the table felt a little in the way of others coming and going. 4.5 stars.

Great lunch. Had the chickpea and farro burger on Scalia (sesame bread) and sangria. So good! We sat on the full-serve side, which was the only place we could find a seat. The other side, more of a Panera-style - where you order your food and it's brought to your table - was totally packed. Not a single seat. I have heard that lunch is the busiest time, though.  Will definitely be back for brunch.

The portions are generous and the sides are great.  I was reluctant to get anything hotter than "medium" for my tenders but it turns out it could have been much hotter for me and I would have been fine.  Go for the black eyed pea salad!

I don't get it.  It's good - but that ain't ribeye.  Personally don't like soft roll's.  Sonny's seemed to have more of the italian bread with a nice crust as we walked by.  Nice staff, but I don't know.....I'm going to keep looking.
Super hard to find vegan food here in nola and this place has many super good options!
I love this place. The food is yummy and the staff was so nice, especially the manager. Will definitely be back for my fav wrap

This is a great place for some tasty food at a reasonable price even when it's not taco Tuesday($1.50 tacos). I had their tempura fish tacos. They were wonderful. The brisket tacos were very flavorful also. The house hot sauce has great flavor and just enough heat. it was a great addition to their tacos. At our table we shared their shredded chicken nachos. Huge portions with flavorful toppings.  The employees were very patient with me being a newby. I would like to go back and try some adult beverages next time. Seemed like pretty reasonable drink specials also. It was a very pleasant experience overall and will be going back to try some of their other menu items.

I used to love going here for my late night pizza... and then I went on NYE and saw the bouncer throw two chicks to the ground around 1AM.   Two separate incidents of the bouncer making these girls bleed.  The person working the register finally sent him downstairs and basically was like "what can I do about it now?"  It was scary and left a really bad taste in my mouth.  The bouncer's anger problem and staff's lack of concern was very upsetting, and I won't be returning.
Delicious Indian food. We got a lamb and chicken dish and they were both very good. The stuff was also really friendly and helpful. Very simple takeout order, we'll definitely be going here again.

I love First Watch and I have been to so many of their locations.  I went on the weekend and I expected it to be crowded.  I was deciding between going to the Villanova location and the Wynnewood location, so I looked at the wait time on the app.  The wait in Villanova was almost and hour and Wynnewood was 15 minutes.   This seemed like a no brainer, until I tried to park there.  They have such a tiny parking lot, which they shared with Starbucks.  On the weekends Starbucks and any breakfast place is mobbed.  I don't know why they would put them in the same area.   There were a ton of people waiting in the lot for people to leave to get a spot and few empty 10 minute spots.  Obviously, eating at First Watch was going to take more than 10 minutes.  I circled the lot a few times before I just parked in another lot and walked. 
The food was the same as the other locations, which is good.  I ordered the special, the flank steak and eggs and it was tasty.  The service was good too. They only have one person checking people in and out, which can make it a little bit crazy.  

I don't understand when businesses open locations in the suburbs with not a lot of parking.  Thankfully, I didn't get towed, but it was kind of annoying.  If you are coming on a weekend, just be warned.

Beautiful place, tastefully decorated and intimate. This is the second time we're having brunch here. After last year's great experience we made it a point to make reservations for my birthday weekend in lovely New Orleans. The staff is great and the service is without flaw. The food... Is not from this world! Had sweet and savory menu items but I couldn't make up my mind on what I liked the most. You have to try their Grits though, I think they're the best I've ever had. Simple but amazing! The place is small and the sitting is close with the next table, so be sure to be friendly with your neighbors if they're open for a light conversation. We got great tips from other diners and found it interesting to interact this way.

Really enjoyed my cold pizza dough with splotches of pizza sauce. Oh, don't forget the toppings for my cheese steak pizza - AKA, cold meat and some cheese. And of course, i appreciated that I was given the opportunity to burn some calories going down 8 flights of stairs to get it, and then being told that my 20% tip wasn't enough. Hoping that I don't get food poisoning from this experience. To be continued.
I love this place more then the sound of slapping noodles soundtrack while you and the loved one you've deemed worthy to share your $6 noodle feast are sucking up broth like it's the antidote.

I have honestly never been a fan of this place, but recently visited again with a co-worker. I do have to say that I was pleasantly surprised with my meal (lunch). My co-worker has several severe food allergies, so I always leave the restaurant choice up to her. They seem to have plenty of options to meet her restrictions, and are willing to prepare the food properly for her to enjoy. While I don't require the same restrictions I can appreciate their attention to the situation. 

The lunch menu was decent, and relatively new from what I heard. I ordered the Asian Grilled Salmon with fried rice and a cup of Egg Drop Soup. The salmon was absolutely delicious - I loved it. Like I said, I was pleasantly surprised at how delicious it was. 

The service was okay.. I felt bombarded by the numerous servers coming to the table (one brought drinks, one took the order, one brought food, another cleared the food, etc). I appreciate their work ethic, but it almost felt rushed. The food also took a while to come out, but that may be due in part to the dietary restrictions/prep of my coworkers meal. 

All in all it was okay. I probably won't go back (unless dining with her again) but it was a good lunch.

We are here Valentines night and had a great meal!  We started with the crawfish rigatoni as an appetizer for the group as well as frog legs and the Boudin buns.  We enjoyed them all but the rigatoni was the star!  We had a few different entrees that we all shared all of which were very good.  We decided to try a few desserts as well and the pecan pie and king cake bananas foster were to die for!!  My only reason for a 4 star review rather than 5 was the service.  On our section we had three different people serving us which can be good but the team wasn't on the same page and we had to keep saying she's already taken our drink order or he's already taken our food order.  Of course then when it came time for the check we couldn't find any of them....

If I could rate with 0 stars I would! Placed an online order for pickup and when it was time for pickup none of the burgers were even ready and the fryer was down because they were installing new fryers in the middle of lunch time! It would have taken an extra 20 minutes to even get the fryers installed. Then we had to wait for the burgers to be made. What's the point of placing a pickup time 45 minutes early when it's not going to be done on time. Very unorganized and bad managing. I really wanted to keep giving this place a try even though they have given us the wrong orders before but this is ridiculous. I like Five Guys but do NOT order from this place!

One of my stops while I was in Florida over the Thanksgiving holiday was Sloppy Joes at the Bilmar Beach resort on Treasure Island. After a day recovering from our Disney trip, my sister and I headed out to the beach. We grabbed breakfast at the Waffle House, which I have already reviewed so be sure to check that one out, and afterwards, explored the area. I was scouting out potential gems to stay at and also did some gift shopping. We spent a lot of time walking on the beach and were getting tired and thought a seat and a drink would be nice. My sister suggested Sloppy Joes because she and my brother in law had eaten there before and loved it. Honestly, I wasn't that hungry but knew I had better eat something. Sloppy Joes is on the beach and has amazing views of the gulf. I'm a sucker for a good view!

We followed the signs to the front to check in and get a table. Being it was a fantastic day, we asked to sit outside. We were quickly seated and given the food menu as well as a drink menu. Being in Florida and at a quirky beach resort restaurant, the drink menu was exactly what you'd expect. Lots of Rum and fruit... otherwise known as beach drinks! All of the drinks came with a souvenir glass. I wasn't that interested in a souvenir glass and asked our server about other options. She told us that all of the drinks were available without the souvenir glasss so it was Rum Punch for me! I haven't had a beach drink in a long time and the Rum Punch from Sloppy Joes was just what the doctor ordered! Cold and delicious.

Since I wasn't hungry, most of the menu wasn't what I was looking for so I settled on the sticky, sweet, chili wings. They were highly recommend by our server and she was spot on. They were sticky and sweet with just the right amount of heat, oh... and nice and crispy. I hate wings where the sauce runs off and creates a pool of liquid underneath the wings and doesn't stick. The wings were exactly what I needed and I am glad we stopped.

If you find yourself in Treasure Island, Sloppy Joes in the Bilmar Beach Resort is worth a visit!

Aside from a nice neighborhood, what's then point of having a hot tub when it's cold as heck after 40 minutes of warming up...also a little frustrated with the one working treadmill, a squeaky secondary treadmill, and a dysfunctional elliptical.  Aside from that, all is well.

No better quality fried chicken in the state. Wish there was more options for toppings. The tatter tots though... Fire.
Ps. The ketchup on the tater tots was not my move lol.

My wife and I ordered take out yesterday and was very disappointed. We ended up paying $66 for 2 burritos & 2 sides of guacamole! Yup...RIDICULOUS! Well at that price you would expect to enjoy an amazingly prepared meal, but that was far from what happened. The steak burrito was a big slab of steak that was barely cooked. We enjoy a rare steak, but this wasn't edible since the steak was still cold in the center and wasn't cooked. The chicken fajita burrito was soggy and has more flour wrap than chicken and veggies. We eat out 2-3 times a week and usually go to El Limon for Mexican. We decided to try this place and it was a mistake. We could of eaten at El Limon 2 times for the same price and the food is much better!

Terakawa used to be Ramen Boy and I suppose owners change and along with it came a name change to avoid the bad reputation from Ramen Boy. 

First off, improvements in customer server went up with the new ownership so this is really good. The waiters are prompt and the food arrives quickly, so quickly perhaps that I think it was already pre-made... Like lighting quick. My dish came out in like 2-4 mins after ordering it. 

I had the original Terekawa Ramen, I can't recall the name. It was good. Not spectacular, not bad, just good. Like I wouldn't write home to my mom about it, but I'd probably confirm it's existence if someone brought it up. The Ramen was a little on the salty side for me and in comparison to all the other Ramen joints in Philly, I'd say it's about in the same league. So far, no Ramen in Philly has really stood out compared to other cities, but I believe Philly will reach that point eventually. 

I also tried the Takoyaki, which was 90% breading and 10% squid. I'd say this is consistent with all the other Takoyaki's I've tried in Philly which is disappointing. 

Overall, going to give it a 3, which is much better than the 1 I gave to Ramen Boy back in the days. Things are better here, but not all that much better where I will make a trip out here. But if I'm in the area, sure I'll grab a bite if I am really craving Ramen. The issue is that this is in Chinatown and in Chinatown, there are so many other better restaurants that you can go to.
Beautiful restaurant, and the food and service are always fantastic. Will recommend to friends!
Best chicken sandwich I've ever had. Piggyback fries are amazing. My wife loved the catfish sandwich. We will definitely be back.

This was probably my favorite meal out of my stay here!  Goat cheese crepes.  Need I say more?!?  I could have been happy just eating this!  Ok so the full descrip....Goat cheese and shrimp crepes in a butter sauce.  Slurping the drool from my mouth.  I will put a fat rock(a wedding ring) w/ anything involving goat cheese!  Next had to go w/ the turtle soup, which Ive never had and didnt know much about it.  It was very tasty, not my favorite kind of soup, but it was really good.  The entree ended up w/ the vegetarian of flavors.  You can just see the creativity in a chef when you receive a plate w/ 7different piles of veggies all good various ways.  I was impressed w/ just the plating on this.  You had your raw, fried, roasted, steamed, and I think thats it.  Not one veggie was alike the other.  The roasted spaghetti squash was def my fav.  And really it was a tie for 2nd for the rest.  All of them were marvelous and even though I steered away from a tradition n'awlins dish, this was a pure treat to eat.  This was all consumed for lunch hours.  I ate everything and when you eat this much food it wasnt bc I was hungry, it was lunch time.  Only reason it was bc the food was soooo flavorful.  The service was decent, but the creativity of the food all around deserves a 5.

Overall, I would recommend people avoid this dealership. I bought a used car here about a year and a half ago. At the time, I was a 23 year old white female who was looking for a used car because my sister needed mine after she totaled hers. The salesman I was with was very good at acting like he cared and felt bad for my situation, but kept pushing for me to sign papers for a car that was on the lot for about $26,000. I'm not as dumb as I apparently look so I obviously refused to sign the papers. I went home to check online and saw that the exact same car was listed for $17,000. At the lot, the salesman kept going to "talk to the manager" and "convinced" him to drop the price down to $24,000 for me because of my situation. 

That should have been warning enough, but the next day I called them out on what they tried to do and ended up going back and paying exactly what the car was worth with an extra warranty. I thought getting an almost new used car would mean I wouldn't spend harley any time getting it fixed, but within the first month of buying this car, I had to take it back to the shop two times (maybe three, I can't remember) due to issues with the radio and then the speakers. THEN the battery died a couple months later. Oh, and when I bought the car, they originally forgot to put the license plates on it so I had to go back to get them after getting almost all the way home.

I took my car here for an oil change and waited over TWO HOURS because they "forgot" about my car. Out of the 4 times I've taken my car here, I've only once waited less than an hour and every time they reassured me over the phone that it would be a "quick fix."

Now for what REALLY bothers me. I was angry one day for waiting so long for something to get fixed and one of the sales managers called me over to his desk and said "Hey sweetie, you look upset, you should cheer up." Creepy middle aged man with over greased hair "sweet talking" a young female - I'm not into it. I told him why I was upset and he offered a free tank of gas but my tank was full so apparently he thought I'd prefer talking to him during my wait. He asked what I do for school and I told him I'm in medical school and eventually we got on the subject of military psychology. My father served on a special task force in Iraq and Kuwait. He came home with PTSD before people knew what PTSD was and unfortunately committed suicide. I was 12. THIS manager had the nerve to say soldiers are "crazy" and he doesn't get why they're so "messed up." He told me a story of his friend who had returned from overseas and had a meltdown in a club because it was too loud and overwhelming and the manager just laughed at him and called him names because "it was just a club." I didn't tell him that what he said was so incredibly insensitive because I didn't and still don't respect him enough to tell him my story. It is DISGUSTING that this "manager" who is supposed to be representing a brand and leading other employees can speak so insensitively about something he clearly knows nothing about. 

So between the fact that they tried to rip me off, take very long times to fix anything in the shop, try to sweet talk young females, and this experience with a "manager," I'd say no no one should buy a car from here - avoid the hassle, even if it means spending a little more money somewhere else.

*I will say I've had one good experience here. I had to bring my car in for a second oil change and told the man at the shop counter how my car is consistently "forgotten" or that I typically wait over 1.5 hours and he made sure my car was done quickly. That was my most recent experience and I was surprised because it was the first time I didn't leave the dealership wanting to pull my hair out with frustration.
Would definitely recommend this restaurant! Very good food and amazing service from Martin. Will definitely be back soon!

I'll start by saying i've been going to this location with my family at least twice a week for years. Last week, I ordered a sandwich before work and when I got there the guy working was rude and apparently my order had been lost. After they made it I went home and found a short curly hair on top of my sandwich. I didn't have time to call and get another one so I threw it away and went to work. I decided that it wasn't a big enough deal to complain about it then, because accidents happen, but that next time I called I would let them know so that maybe I could get a replacement. Well today at 12:45 I called and let them know what had happened and asked if they could do anything for me. The guy said yeah I think I could give you a small discount. I'm in the service industry myself and any time a hair is found in food we immediately give them a replacement for free and apologize. So I was taken off guard and asked if they couldn't do anything more since I threw the whole sandwich away. He gruffly put me on hold and another woman answered. After I explained what had happened, she said that for all she knew I could be making it up just to get a free sandwich since I was calling a week later. I said that I had been busy with work that say and she said "okay then why didn't you call the next day then" with a rude tone to her voice. I've never been accused or talked to so rudely by customer service before, which I told her and hung up. I feel so disrespected and disappointed in my experience, not only because of the failure to resolve my issue but the attitude and accusatory way I was spoken to. I will not be returning to this location, and I'm really disappointed in the standard of service this location has and has had recently. In the past there was a sweet woman who worked there and knew me by name and always made me feel special, and I have not seen her in a while.

I started having a strange little "cold" on November 30th.  10 days later I tested positive for Corona, luckily, my symptoms were extremely mild. Tested positive again a week later, not feeling sick at all. That was 10 days ago.  I had used home tests by Pixel Labs. I am trying to get another test before Christmas so I can be with my kids, so I just went to the medcenter, wearing my mask, keeping at least 10 feet distance, not touching anything.  The guy who greeted me asked if I had symptoms, I told him I tested positive last week, he immediately moved away even further and told I was putting a lot of people at risk and I shouldn't even be there.  When I asked him how I can get tested he told me that I was going to test positive for quite a while.
This makes NO SENSE at all! He didn't even give me a chance to let him know that my symptoms started November 30th, so there is a good chance I am now negative! How can he give a "professional" opinion on my status????
Obviously some of the people who go there are positive and might not even know it and not take the pre cautions I do, isn't that the reason they get tested????? My daughter went there 10 days ago and told them, mom had tested positive, they had no issue with that even though there was obviously a good chance she was positive too!  Luckily hers turned out negative.
They need to get someone at the door who can ask the appropriate questions and maybe even show a tiny bit of care and kindness!
I guess the only recourse is to not tell them, which I won't do or to get another home test.

How can you not give 5 stars to a place with $1 steak street tacos and $3 frozen margaritas at happy hour? The tacos were good and so were the margaritas! The service was great. I will be back for sure!

Had a really great experience here with a server who was very attentive. She recommended and let us sample some great beers. We shared a couple appetizers and the pork schnitzle entree which was delicious. A bit crowded and commercial but good for what it is.

Terrible service.  Seated by a waitress only to have the hostess tell us that we can't sit there because the it was reserved. Um, ok.  We moved and were promptly ignored for 10 minutes. Ordered and then waited for refills forever.  The rest of the meal took forever since we were ignored for a good portion.

The food was bland and standard hotel fare.
I always get deliveries from here and the food always come fresh and still warm. One of the best places to get sushi in my opinion
Enjoyed going to Santorini for a quick lunch today. I had a gyro sandwich combo and a drink. Quick, tasty, and a great price! Will go back again.

Visited this location of PDQ (People Delivering Quality) after enjoying the chicken at another location. Unfortunately the manager at this establishment didn't take well to being asked for fresh fries when the order we received tasted twice cooked and were just warm for a sit in meal. He was dismissive and reluctant at best. We received our fresh order and then watched as he questioned a minor with a $10 bill who attempted to buy a milkshake without adult supervision and then five minutes later preceded to embarrass another man who returned part of his order. We won't be returning to this location and not because our food was over dressed and cold, but because the manager insisted on embarrassing customers and staff when the quality was wasn't what the brand stands for. What a shame!
