One of the best restaurants in king of Prussia.  Quality food and quality service. Worth every penny. Very reccomended.

Love, love the food my all time favorite is the tomato bisque soup and the small wise guy sandwich....mmmmm!  I also love the italian soda:)

The only thing I am not in love with is the order at the counter.......it just seems so weird and impersonal in a way, as they have people serve you drinks and bring your food?  So that is why the four stars :(

I lost my wallet in the hotel lobby on Thursday night and alerted the front desk to put a note on my room. Every time I walked in and out of the hotel I asked the front desk if they found it and each time was told no. Come to find out on Saturday that they sat on it for two days and now dropped it into a safe that apparently no one has access to. When I told them I would miss my flight they let me know that I still had to wait. My ID, credit card and cash are literally being held hostage. The manager was no help she immediately said nothing could be done and refused to reach out to her counterparts to even try. I would never stay here again.
What it is lacking in atmosphere it more than makes up for in flavor. Vegan friendly.

Just had a roof put on my house.Was very pleased with the company. They went above board to be helpful.They did all the paperwork that my HOA required and worked with the HOA. Saved us a lot of time.
The crew that did the roof were very pleasant and did a great job.
We will certainly use Tristate again should the need arise.

These guys were fast, professional, and extremely respectful of our home. They all wore masks the whole time (covid) were friendly with our dog, and cleaned up every day. They even finished the job a day early. Highly recommend for central air!

We were rather surprised to walk into a downtown restaurant on a Friday night and find that we were the only ones in the place.  Some friends told us about Comida and we all decided to try it, excited for a new restaurant and one with great ratings.  I was a little confused by the signage and menu to walk into an Asian-Mexican Fusion Coffee shop, but I'll try anything once.  We were early and decided to order the elotes and meat empanadas along with a couple of coffee drinks while we waited.  Checking out the decor, it was quirky and cool, but obviously still "under construction" as you could see some areas that just looked like they haven't been touched or organized yet.

Our drinks arrived first and were pretty decent.  I took the suggestion of the bar staff and tried one of their house drinks, good suggestion.  The only complaint here is that they serve everything in styrofoam or plastic, they don't have any cups or plates (though the flatware is metal).  Our elotes and empanadas made it to the table shortly after.  The empanadas had clearly been made days ago and reheated which was a pretty big disappointment, but I brought it to the counter and they both refunded my money and made me a "fresh" order that they just microwaved longer so that it wasn't cold in the middle.  My wife tried the elotes with flaming hot cheetos.  Corn is out of season, so that was a big flag for me, but she gave it a try and was pretty disappointed.  I'm not sure that one really had a shot with us, so I don't know how fair it is to criticize the flavors.

Once our friends arrived, I warned them about the empanadas and they tried the elotes, so we decided to give the nachos a whirl.  The bar staff recommended the nachos above anything else.  The flavors were pretty interesting as a mashup of bulgogi, queso, refried beans, and some other toppings.  Not terrible at all, but unless you are ordering for carry out, you'll spot that all of this is pre-prep and based on their lack of volume, likely not from that day.  The meat was rubbery and chewy--some decent flavor, but not a great experience.  We decided to cut our losses and head elsewhere for mains.

I think there is some real potential here.  The flavors in the bulgogi nachos had a lot of upside, but the execution was all wrong.  Are they cost-cutting already since they don't have any customers, trying to salvage food that was pre-prepped?  Given the prices and the downtown location, I'm paying a premium for fresh, and preferably local, ingredients and a great dining experience.  The styrofoam and microwave have to go.  Good luck to the team there and good luck to any of you.

It is fine to be a burger snob, but you can't turn your nose at an Awful Awful. When you tell yourself "I could eat a whole cow"...PLOP here it is in all its savory meat(wad) goodness. The place is nothing to look at...dinky and always a line.  They are as quick as they can, and boy do they deliver, good food that is.  I wouldn't rate them on their bed skill. ;0)

The Conshohocken IKEA probably will always be my favorite since it was the IKEA that took my IKEA virginity. hahaha. I just the atmosphere in here while browsing this " furniture museum." By the way great stress reliever walking through here when it is less busy.

Excellent food with full flavor. Workers were knowledgeable about the cooking process and very happy about serving the food. 

The sauces are heated! Great touch!

Pedicure was decent. But my manicure... My poor gatdamn fingernails. But do. Not. Let. The old asian guy do your nails. He will fck you up. Please just look at the pictures. 

He tried to tell me the fill color wouldn't be visible and initially insisted on a bright ass fire truck red to pair with the white french tip. I refused, politely, and explained that I wanted a natural looking french tip anyways. Fck the color I picked I guess, because the white tips themselves were decent but the fill color job he did saltily??? Sir are these ur nails also I didn't want my nails looking like I got done fingerbanging bob the tomato's asshole from veggie tales

This is like 2 restaurants in 1.  A BBQ joint and tex/mex. Both types of food at ECR are usually very good, especially for the price.  Generous portions (chicken cheesesteak weighs about 3 pounds!).  The mac & cheese is truly dynamite.  Some people like a dryer, rub-type BBQ, which is featured here.  This really focuses the flavor on the meat, and the brisket, pork, and chicken are very good.  Mex side, check the machaca burrito and the Gringo special.  Great salsas, too.

If you're considering getting your windows tinted and don't know where to go, look no further than Ziebarts! Absolutely amazing service and work, I couldn't be happier with the work they did. They are extremely friendly and have amazing customer service as well which makes the experience twice as amazing. I 100% recommend them to anyone that needs work done ! Thank you for my tinted windows !

In-N-Out makes the best burgers ever! 

The place is clean and the employees are friendly! Their menu is simple. Three burgers to choose from. Hamburger, cheeseburger, and the double double. They have fries and shakes on their menu as well. They do have a secret menu. Animal style fries and burgers, which consists of grilled onions, cheese, and spread. The animal fries are OMG good! I love animal fries! There's other items on their secret menu (4x4 etc.) but their simple menu is perfect. Their burgers are my favorite!

We usually go to the one in Kenner, but decided to stay on the Westbank this time. Minnie was our servers and she was great. Very attentive to our needs and she had a great personality. My husband loved her attitude which says a lot cause this man is picky. We will definitely be back. Thanks again Minnie

This couldn't possibly be the same restaurant that the reviewers below experienced. I don't know how this place is in business. We used to eat here a few years ago until it got so bad we couldn't stand it. Last Saturday I decided to check it out to see if anything had improved. It was disgusting. Have these reviewers ever had good mexican food? I love tacos al pastor and asada. What they serve is something like sloppy joe meat on corn tortillas, Same with the Asada. Sloppy Joe meat. My kiddo ordered a chicken cheese quesadilla. What he got was chicken meat in a tortilla folded over. The service was just as deplorable. There is Mexican food available in Franklin. This isn't it.

Fine for lunch, not worth the effort for dinner. With a name like Yellowbelly, a restaurant raises expectations for excellent raw fish experience. It fails to deliver. Last time I went for lunch and got Caesar salad. It was barely edible. It'll be a long time before we are back.

The staff and location are amazing. The amenities are good not great. The restaurants were either closed or limited hours - due to COVID. The pool was nice but not fun - lack of a party - so could be good for some. 

The rooms are large and clean. The showers are hot. We upgraded to a balcony, however we moved back to a regular room because the balcony is shared with the adjacent room. My wife was not having that. 

They have an entrance right off Bourbon Street, so you are close to the action without being immersed or overwhelmed.

The venue is beautiful and it accommodates large groups very well. We were here on a Tuesday evening at 5pm to beat the dinner rush.  I really wanted to love this place so I ordered the simple fish and chips with a coconut shrimp side. The strawberry lemonade was lovely. I thought the fries were cooked perfectly and the coleslaw was perfectly fresh and flavorful.

Unfortunately, the fish was dry and over cooked so much that I could not eat it.  Hopefully the manager works with the kitchen to prove the food quality.  Since the seafood at this restaurant is the main feature I have to take off points. They did comp me for the $17.99 entree since I could not eat it.

Our server was wonderful. Her name was Stevie. They really need to give her a promotion for being so friendly and providing refills with our prompting and followed up with the kitchen when the food was slow.

We rent 15 passenger vans for national travel from this Enterprise all the time! They are always incredibly accommodating and friendly. Thom is the man! 

#teamfreedia

It ok but nothing special. It all about pounding out a chicken breast and coating it and sauté it in some olive oil. I had the parm. The sauce was just average and so was the cheese. It was very lonely on my plate for 11 or 12 dollars. The sandwich we had the bread is too much and too dry. We also had the tot nacho for 9 dollars and none of the ingredients provided any punch to the dish. The milk shake is not the thick eat it with the spoon shake. You could drink it easily with a straw. 

A lot of options on this street!! Maybe I will try it again someday.

We live downtown and try different places all the time. We have eaten there before. This time was different only because of the manager. There  were 4 of us. We all ordered burgers. The burger we ordered was medium well but the center was bloody red raw. We asked to see the manager and finally 20 mins later and then we told her. He response was sometimes they are bloody? What? Med well? She said here is a free breakfast pretzel. What? She was the manager on Sunday around 3pm, Short buzzed haircut. We all looked at her because of her response. We all agreed this was not right. We will NEVER eat there again and NOW after her response well advise ALL of our friends to not eat there. She was rude when she was talking to us also. Never Again. We have heard of her before from friends. NEVER AGAIN......

Food is awesome, service has some room for improvement though!!!

I have been to Chi twice and both times have had solid sushi.  I recommend the mango roll (when mangoes are ripe)...it is hard to beat!

Now onto what pissed me off about Chi...I was their during happy hour and since appetizers were half off I figured I would order a cup of miso soup.  Easy, right?  Not so easy for the waiter that was serving us.

He brought me my bowl of miso soup, but the spoon I am used to seeing accompany the soup was missing.  So I thought to myself 1. He just forgot the spoon or 2. They want people to sip the soup.  Now this is a personal preference of mine, but I like soup better using a spoon as opposed to sipping it.  I figured it would be easy as pie to just ask for a spoon and be on my merry miso way.  BUT when I asked for spoons, I was reprimanded that miso soup is traditional sipped.  I told the waiter I understood, but if they had spoons I would just prefer eating my soup with a spoon.  He gave me a snarky look and handed over the spoon (a soup spoon nonetheless so obviously someone at the restaurant bought spoons to eat soup with!!!) and I enjoyed my soup.

Now if this spoon vs. no spoon dilemma ended here, I would have brushed it off my shoulder and forgot about it completely.  But the douche bag of a waiter proceeded to bring miso soup to the table next to us and very loudly proclaim that this was the TRADITIONAL miso soup they ordered (no spoons included).  This was obviously a dig to me for preferring to eat my soup with a spoon.

In the scheme of things, this is a very small issue but it definitely rubbed me the wrong way and gave me the impression that keeping customers happy isn't a high priority here.

The sushi is tasty, but with many others places offering great sushi as well...I will probably take my business elsewhere from now on.

Not the best Italian BYOB in the city. But it does the job this was my latest CC Restaurant Week spot. TBH - didn't really want to come here but it was the only place with a dinner reservation on a Friday night. (I was booking a last minute date night). 

The food was just ok. It's 4 courses for restaurant week. An app, a pasta second course, entree and dessert. All just okay nothing to write home about. 

Service was great and the staff is friendly and accommodating. Also in a great location right in Rittenhouse. I would come back, but I doubt it would be a first choice.

Mesa Verde is one of the best restaurants in Santa Barbara! If you are ever visiting Santa Barbara, Mesa Verde is a place you must visit. The food is always super fresh and really good! The deserts are definitely something you need to try! All the staff are super friendly from the second you walk in to the moment you walk out! 10/10 for Mesa Verde!

BRUNCH here is greater than Everywhere Else. There is a reason this restaurant is on Yelp's Top 100. IT WAS FANTASTIC. The atmosphere is very beachy and fun, and they have a lovely patio...and all this is just seconds away from the beach. I ordered the square meal (a basic breakfast platter) and was VERY impressed. The drinks were strong and delicious also. Our server was named Maryanne and delivered excellent top-level and friendly service. I'll definitely put this on places to stop on my next visit. Very good value and very friendly staff. Can't wait to go back.

2/13/20 was my 6 th visit with cousins visiting from the East Coast. The food and service were flawless as always and our server Dani gave us the time we needed to chat -LOVE BLUEWATER GRILL !!! 

Wow my 5th visit and the best food I have had in Santa Barbara in 18 years! REALLY! Blackened ahi and blackened swordfish- our waiter Chris was great and I just learned that the chef is a woman - Chanel and she is so talented and as always the Manager, Colin, makes everyone feel so welcomed and appreciated THANK YOU EVERYONE!!!

Now that the hype has died down and lines are normal, GO!!!! As some one from Chicago, it taste like 87th street, which is great. The chicken is fried fresh, which explains the 10 minute wait. The mild sauce is on point. It's sweet and tangy. It's a tad pricey compared to the house (Chicago), but worth it. GO!!!!

I inquired about injections, I have received daily harassing calls from their call center soliciting me all hours. I told them specifically I work ft, your Calling during work hours and then I go to school full time the calls were ridiculous. Finally had the chance to answer these fkers and the woman was rude and condescending trying to make me go for a consultation only and unfortunately it's not going to happen right now. She starts to laugh on my phone and ask me when's a good time then I hung up. Weirdos ----- bad taste in my mouth before I walk in the door. What the hell do u need a call center with morons for a medi spa? What's next? Get ur sht together.

I've been back a few times since my original review and decided it was time for an update.... Nothing's changed... I mean that in the most positive way possible. Everything is still fresh and delicious. Service is still friendly and awesome. The prices are still reasonable.  And the place is still small, busy, quirky and yet somehow inviting and comfortable.  Change is good for some people. I don't particularly like change and I'm certainly glad Süp hasn't changed a bit!

This is easily my favorite liquor store in Indianapolis.  Rather than giving a rambling review that will end up being more of an ode to how much I love booze than how much I love Kahns, here's the breakdown:

Beer

- In the coolers they have a large selection of local micro-brews, and also micro-brews from all over the country.  You can always find a six pack of something you haven't tried yet that is cold and ready to drink.  They also have a cooler of single bottles so you can get a little of this and a little of that.  There is a very nice cider selection as well.  They carry Strongbow cans, Samuel Smith, Original Sin, and at least five other varieties that I can't think of at the moment.  They have a pear cider that is above the $20 range that I REALLY want to try.

- Out of the coolers, they have a large selection of single bottles arranged by country.  They also keep a row of sale beer in the middle of an aisle.


Wine

- HUGE wine selection.  If you want it, they likely have it.  They have one of those fancy wine rooms full of bottles I cannot afford.  Don't mind the face print on the door looking in...

- Sale wines are often really good deals, keep an eye out!
- If you have piles of money and want to turn that money into cases of wine, Kahn's has a big wine cellar and will happily store it for you.


Liquor

- Very nice liquor selection, prices a bit better than what I would find shopping at my local Meijer.  

- The best part of the liquor is tasting bar.  Yes, that's right.  The liquor tasting bar.  I once left here a bit buzzed because one of the employees went on a mission to find a gin that I would like.  As gin is one of the vilest substances on earth, that was a tough task, but he did eventually find one that didn't taste quite as much like ass as all of the others.  Anyhow, when venturing into the realm of new, expensive liquor, it's really nice to be able to sample it before shelling out the cash.

The employees have always been very helpful whenever we've gone in, going out of their way to answer questions.  In general, I've found the prices here to be comparable to other liquor stores, though they do have some really good sales. They also have a room full of stuff you need for drinking - glasses, wine screws, decanters, etc.  They sell a small selection of fancy cheese, but you'd be better off going to Trader Joes or Whole Foods for that.

As a new student, I am in love with this studio. The place is clean, the teachers MOTIVATE you, and you want to keep coming back. The Hot Room offers a changing room, showers, bathrooms. They also have a small shop with yoga wear/accessories, supplements, water, coconut water, etc. 

I have taken classes led by Hye Jin and by Kalee and both are excellent at what they do. I have to say that I feel The Hot Room really cares about its students, and aims to help them by providing guidance inside and outside The Hot Room.

This is truly a place for beginners and if you are considering trying Bikram Yoga, I would highly suggest coming in and checking out The Hot Room!

Nice food, not too far from 101, service is only ok.
We ate at the Beachside Bar when driving back from L.A.  The restaurant's just off the 217 connector from highway 101, and is right on the beach - it made for a nice sit-down meal after some hectic driving. 
Food was decent; we had the fish tacos and tri-tip sandwiches.  Both were decent; the only problem was that our waiter confused our order with another table, and our entrees apparently sat for ten minutes waiting for that table to finish their salad.  The food was lukewarm when it arrived.  On the plus side, at least we were looking out at the Pacific Ocean while we waited!

Came here with a group to watch college football. The staff was very accommodating to changing the TV's to our game and waiting on us for four hours during the entire game.
I ordered a Jamaican Joe beer which was very tasty and a Reuben which was greasy (what do you expect?) but good as well. I wouldn't call the food 5 star, but its good for bar food.
The atmosphere is perfect for game-watching and I didn't notice any dirty looks at our occasional yelling at the screen ;)
This place is so close to my house and am always impressed with everything I get. My go to for quick takeout!

Very disappointed in this luxury car service center. I have different levels of expectations of dealerships based on the type of car they sell. I absolutely love my BMW. But I HATE when I have to bring here to be serviced! I tried to give them time to get it right, but I have been here 4x and they are still effd up! If I'm waiting for my car, I expect ATLEAST hourly updates. Not me having to go up the counter every hour asking. And let's not even talk about the hour long car wash. I gave 2 stars, because the facility is nice and the shuttle service is prompt. It's extremely hard to get a loaner car  and even harder to get a call back. I called everyday 3 days in a row, left voicemails, and had to send an email to a manager to finally get a response.  But overall disappointed in the poor customer service. I won't be returning.

Took a bike ride to the loop and decided I would treat myself to a small portion of the delicious mac-n-cheese here--I could justify the calories after huffing and puffing to get there....

It was just as delicious as normal and I even had a free smoothie on my card so I got the low-fat strawberry with ginseng and that was a nice refreshing  treat to my day.

They have some outdoor seating but I decided to cool off inside with the air conditioning.  

I didn't feel like this location was as clean and shiny as some of the other locations so they need to step it up a little and wipe the tables a little more frequently and run the sweeper.
"Great food, great service AND they offer rewards when you download their mobile app!"  The Ribs were amazing!

Love the chocolate stuffed chocolate with extra cream! They don't skimp on the juice and when we ask for a medium in a large cup, they ask no questions. (we do this so it doesn't have a cone top - one person picking up 5 snowballs is a task)
Staff is friendly and they have quick service.

A must stop if you're in Boise. Been here 3 times, exactly as many times as I've been to Idaho. Great for dining outside in summer. They just added a DJ inside. I swear every year I go to Boise, the girls get better looking. I recommend the sturgeon when they have it, the scallops, and the lobster corn dogs for appetizer.
Quaint family owned restaurant!!! Excellent for catering  and just grabbing a taste of Cuba! Be prepared to pay $1 extra if you are using your credit card.

My husband and I strolled from Center City to China town on a recent friday night to satisfy my regular asian cravings. If I do not eat some sort of rice or rice noodles for 1 month, I turn into a hungry Panda.
The only thing that disappointed me was the Cash Only policy. I had no idea until we got there and by the time I read through the menu and saw the roast ducks hanging at the window, I did not want to leave. So we had to do some math when deciding what to order to make sure we had enough money. Sort of like when I was in high school lol. But it was a good exercise to learn to budget right?
I ordered the wonton egg noodle soup, stir fry snow pea leaves, half a roast duck Hong Kong Style (didnt have enough cash for the Peking duck) and beef chow fun dry.
Everything was good but not spectacular to my husband, and it would be the case for me too if I was not super hungry and suffering from this craving. Of course we have all had better in NYC or CA china town or back home in Hong Kong. But it was really yummy to me! 
The soup for the wonton dish was not too salty and comforting. The wontons were big. I think there were 6 of them? The price was good at about $5. The noodles seem to be cut up so I didn't get to twirl my chop sticks around it. Seem strange that they would cut it up but maybe they thought my husband is not good with chop sticks or I looked like a child? haha.
The roast duck was greasy as expected but sweet and had crispy skin. It tasted and looked fresh. The meat was tender.
The stir fry snow pea leaves with garlic was mediocre and there was nothing wrong with it. I can make it myself at home. I always order greens to counter balance the carbs and greasy meat.
The beef chow fun with flat rice noodles was stir fried well. It did not have as much beef as I had hoped but the grease factor was just right.
We cleaned up everything except for the snow pea leaves.

There are other noodle places in Chinatown. I will probably try those first before coming again. At least I know this is a safe place to go for my cravings.

Service was prompt and nice. It was also interesting being sat next to a table of high school girls dinner night out. Interesting outfits they wear nowadays!

I came to Nashville for a fashion show. I stayed at this hotel with a king size bed in a high level. The staff was courteous and responsive. I requested new towels and toothpaste and they brought in less than 5 minutes. The sheets were clean and I have no complaints. It is an older hotel but it has been updated. The only sign of its age is the AC units. I would definitely recommend this hotel for anyone staying in the area. It is close to the highway and close to malls and lots of restaurants.

First time here for brunch. We had 15 people and arrived at 1245pm. There was no one at omelette station...there was no carving station just some old meat in a pan...no bagels or bread of any kind. The shrimp were floating in a bowl of water??????

Great for lunch or dinner, casual or formal. Pasta made fresh in house and the octopus appetizer is amazing, step outside of your comfort zone and try it! Sometimes parking can be tough but there are a few spots behind the building too.

What a pleasant surprise. An elegant bistro with a full bar! I had a terrific whole wild bass, filleted (not that tasteless pretender, 'Chilean Sea Bass' otherwise known as common Toothfish).

My wife had a spicy pasta dish, and we started with some lamb sausage and hummus.

Thanks, Yelp users for such a nice little find only 25 minutes from home!

Had a horrible experience here.  I wanted to buy my boyfriend a couple of cigars for Christmas. As I do not know much about cigars, I was hoping to get some information and recommendations for good cigars. The sales girl could not even come out from the behind the counter to help me. She would just point and say, "Yeah, those ones are pretty popular." My boyfriend smoked the cigars and said that they were not even that good.  Will definitely not be returning to this establishment.

I went here to get my passport photos taken because it's the closest place to my house.
I was asked to wait for a few minutes, because there is only one staff member who does this service.
She took four photos and decided that my face was too shiny.  She sent me to the cosmetics counter and told me to get the beautician to powder me.   This lady was very serious about prepping me just right.  It was kind of funny.  I felt like I was going on a high end magazine shoot.
My photo was taken again and was perfect.  
When my passport arrived, I was thrilled to see that I did not look like an escaped criminal as I have in my past passport photos. Ironically, my previous photos were taken at a professional photo studio.  
Thank you lady at Shoppers Drug Mart.  
The reason for only 3 stars is that they shelves are not always stocked well and I have purchased expired items.  This area needs a little more attention.  They also need to have all registers open because it is insanely busy all the time.  
I also find the pharmacist unfriendly.

I was in town visiting my son who's car was overheating.  We took it to Jack Furrier's and after waiting a bit they kindly checked out the problem.   It was not actually overheating but a bad gauge in the car.   They could of very well had me change a thermostat and then a fan clutch to eliminate the obvious things that might be wrong but they took the extra time needed to test the car and eliminate those problems.
Go to these guys...they are a rare find!!!!

I have lived in this town my entire life and once finding Ivette I won't go anywhere else. I've followed her from her old salon. She does exactly what you ask and beyond. Not to mention she is so sweet!! Won't go to anyone else!!

**BEWARE** I do not recommend this place to anyone. I initially took my car to have the shifter replaced. They charged me 500.00 for this service the first time.The repair didnt last a week and I was back with the same problem. This time, they charged me 1100.00 for the same job. Upon retrieving my vehicle, I noticed there was a crack above my blowers. I asked them what happened to that area of the car, Doug one of the repairmen told me he didnt notice anything damaged to that area. My car never had damage to that area as long as,I had been driving. Of course they passed the buck and didnt know who did what. Covering eachothers asses. Bunch of thug repairmen. If anyone decides to forge ahead and do business please take pics of the inside and outside of your vehicle before releasing to their care. I have learned a hard lesson and that is to check around before wasting money.

My first burger joint in Indianapolis. There is a variety to choose from and I love their waffle and sweet potato tater tots! 

I was thinking the burnt cheese option would be all sorts of nasty but instead adds a nice salty crunchy texture to the burger.

I would not eat here everyday because my arteries would clog but I recommend  this place for hamburgers.
Best Pizza in Harleysville by FAR .. worth the extra 5 minute drive
Roxy is amazing and my absolute favorite waxer ever! She is funny and witty and will help you forget about the horrendous treatment (waxing) you are getting done.
Food was so delicious I will tell my friends and family about east coast wings there are awesome thank you very much

The food here is generally pretty decent but the prices are way out of control.   The worst thing I've ever had was the clam chowder.  Avoid that at all costs.   Bland and stale tasting.   They also don't have a happy hour and the drinks are all atleast $6.  You are basically paying for the view.

I returned after 4-5 years for lunch.  My first visit was remembered as being poor but the guys in the office get takeout and looked good so I gave it another shot.  The sampler platter left me with a full belly and it was good.  Service was decent with attentive waitresses and the price was reasonable, atmosphere perfect for BBQ.

Mid-day lunch. You order at the counter and pick up when your number is called. I like it here because they have different types of meats. I ordered the lengua tacos. It came with cabbage and salsa. The lengua was in nice chucks and very tender. Very good. My companion had the pollo tacos. She said it was good and flavorful. The 3 tacos with rice, beans, pickled carrots, pinion and limes was filling. Great service and clean.

Food and drinks are good.  We had a party of 5 and we all enjoyed the appetizers and dinners.  Not sure I would agree with the negative reviews.  We've eaten here several times over the yrs and was never disappointed.

Moe's is great for a quick lunch spot. On a strip of shops next to Vanderbilt, it can get pretty busy here. There's a large menu at the front to help you make your Southwest option--they have nachos, tacos, bowls, burritos, quesadillas, and lots of other options. 

I went with the quesadilla, which I have been craving for weeks. I normally just eat a cheese quesadilla, sometimes with chicken if I'm feeling crazy. Their quesadilla menu options offered beans, rice, lettuce, tomatoes, and other ingredients. I decided to try out some with my usual quesadilla and I was pleasantly surprised. It was absolutely delicious!

I love tipping for great service, and the staff was king and helpful throughout the dining line. They rind a bell if you tip, which is so fun!
Good pizza is hard to find in philly and this is not the place, very very avg.  Only if you are very hungry and need fuel to exist.

Nice place good food and friendly service. However, last night while visiting, my friends and I decided to look for a new place to go. If you like loud music that's the place to go. If you want to have conversations with your guests you'll find it difficult yelling over the loud band.  Admittedly it was a very  decent band, however, my friends and I wanted to chat and catch up on a few things but we found it nearly impossible.

the hotel was a dump. surrounded by porn stores, casinos and liquor stores. The sound of sex was coming from multiple rooms.  I'm a us Marine and didn't feel comfortable staying there or leaving my car in the parking lot. I was given a $10 voucher to eat in the dirty restaurant in the lobby as compensation for the broken elevator. when I told them I didn 't want to stay the owner became biligerant and threatened to call the police. when I told expedia they wouldn't refund the room with out the hotels authorization (which wasn't given). I was also charged for a deposit they claimed was just a hold for incidentals. DO NOT STAY HERE.

I'm sorry, but a pizza place that makes a TERRIBLE pizza, only gets one star.  I called ahead to place my order, got there after 20 minutes, and the pizza wasn't even in the  oven yet.  There we lots of people behind the counter milling around, so why did it take so long.  The tossed salad was decent, but the pizza was really bad.  The crust tasted like it was a frozen crust, the sauce tasted like plain tomato paste, the pizza was SOGGY, the the toppings were skimpy.  

There other food might be great, but a pizza place near philly that can't make a delicious pizza doesn't deserve to be in business imho.  Skip this place.
Amazing buffet very clean and staff is happy to explain any questions about the menu and or ingredients.

Have been having my eyebrows done here for awhile, threading and tinting- she does a wonderful job and is so pleasant!Today I had my first facial and it was superb! Lots of face massaging to get the blood flowing along with steam, exfoliating, and a mask.  While the mask is on you get a head massage, and at the end of your facial a back massage.  Only wish I had gone sooner and will definitely be going back!!

I felt judged here, we didn't get no complimentary chips and salsa, the lady told us we won't be able to finish a bottle of champagne, which we did, and she kept coming back and picking up our bottle to see how much we drank. The food was not that big portion for what we paid either...
Unfortunately overrun to the point of horrible service, bad odors outside, little acknowledgement by servers, mediocre food. Will not return.

The food was just ehh. I ordered the tofu soup, beef bulgogi, and the spicy Korean rice cake. 

My tofu soup had no flavor. It just tasted like broth with flavorless spicy powder. I even threw in the Kim chi but since the Kim chi also had no flavor either..it didn't really help. The weirdest part of the soup was that my raw egg never cooked. It was just sitting there because my soup was not hot enough to cook it. So that was quite a turnoff.

The bulgogi was also kind of strange. It had noodles in there and slices of bell peppers and onions. More like a stir fry to me. (Good thing I like noodles.)

The rice cake was okay.  Nothing too special. Did not care for majority of the  side items though. The crunchy (turnip or daikon) and fishcake was the only ones I liked.

I was left with Korean food cravings after though. I think I'll stick to cooking Korean at home or go to Korea house.

I really like the ambiance here though. Maybe I'll try eating here again if they change some of the recipes. I wish they had more Korean food places down here like in Los Angeles.

I was out in philly for school a couple of years back, and this is my favorite cheese pizza by the slice joint ever.   Perfect for your 2am drunken cravings.  They only have cheese pizza if you do it by the slice otherwise you have to order a whole pan to get one with the toppings of your choice.  For two bucks you get huge slice of pizza that's so big 1/3 of it is folded onto itself!  So, if you're drunk, stop by and have slice two three or four you won't be dissapointed.
Excellent customer service.... hands down the best burger  I ever had in my life. Very friendly and what a delight. I recommend anyone to go and enjoy what I had. Thanks stephon....

These are hands down the best doughnuts I've ever had.  We got these for our clients while we were working in Reno.  We bought at least one of each kind, and all of them were said to be fantastic.  I tried the strawberry frosted, maple bar and coconut doughnut.  They were all totally amazing.  I'm kind of glad I don't live here, because I would get addicted to this place, haha!  Definitely a must try if you're in the area.
Hamburgers are out of this world. Served with a fully loaded baked potato. Dark but they play great music.

The quesabirria tacos here are really, really good. Definitely the highlight of everything I've eaten here thus far. A couple of the other meats are solid too, but make no mistake what you're here for. The ordering process can be a bit confusing/chaotic, and the wait can be rather long. Those are my only drawbacks; the food stands on its own.
Just opened a few weeks ago. Staff is super nice, bagels are great, and there's a lot cream cheeses to choose from. They allow you taste the cream cheeses before you pick which is cool.
Doesn't communicate good with deaf people, and won't reply to my email asking when my refund check will come in mail.

In the past when I imagined La Quinta, I pictured a cheap motel. Let me tell ya, this place definitely exceeded my expectations. We had a two Queen room, and a two queen with a pullout couch. The rooms were big and clean, the beds were super comfy, the bathrooms were clean and renovated. The coffee downstairs was out of this world. However, the breakfast was just meh.

Andres and staff are excellent and make this one of the friendliest coffee places in town. I love their iced lattes. They also have live music that provides a lovely atmosphere for the neighborhood. Esplanade wouldn't be the same without The Flagpole.

Twice now the maintenance male receptionist has been rude to me, told me he couldn't get me in, and that all Subaru's were backed up. Called Subaru in my town and got an appointment sooner and they ensured they saw my car.  They used to have great customer service but looks like I'll go to the other Subaru, pay a little more, but at least not he talked to appropriately. Not to mention not buying my next Subaru here.

Can't get enough of this place! The selection of premium quality olive oil and vinegars can't be matched in Santa Barbara. Great customer service and knowledge of products. Makes the PERFECT gift. Family and friends think I've become an aficionado of olive oil, really I just let the staff school me for a few minutes and pretend I'm an expert. Nobody questions my expertise, they are too busy eating.

Pickles was right next door to the hotel we stayed at in St. Louis so I stopped in before our conference one morning for a quick breakfast option. I ordered a build your own breakfast sandwich and it was pretty tasty. The bread was a soft sourdough, not toasted at all. I didn't mind it but the bread was pretty squishy. You might want to try requesting it toasted if that's your preference. The egg, sausage, and cheese were all hot and was a decent basic option. I could have used a little bit of hot sauce for more flavor but it did the trick. Note that they are not open on weekends which I thought was strange. There is quite a bit of room inside to sit down and eat and they have a large sandwich menu for lunch options.

Good food and good deal of $20 for a large cheese pizza and 1 lb of boneless wings. BUT...

Service was TERRIBLE (albeit friendly).

We called ahead twice,. Phone was picked up and just there, unanswered, as we heard aan everyone training through the phone for the 5 minutes we waited. Hung up, called again.  Was told to please hold... same thing. Is there no actual hold button? 

We gave up and decided to come in and order in person as we are just down the road. 

35-40 minutes later...

I guess they were short staffed? Many orders in the kitchen with no names so we had to constantly be asking whether our order was ready -- and it seems like it had been there for a while by time it was given to us.  

Several couples came in to be seated while we waited for carry out, but left unhappy as they were blatantly IGNORED (?!) and never seated -- even though there were empty tables (that were not cleaned for the whole 40 so minutes we were there).

Btw, this place is SMALL. And CROWDED. 

Food was surprisingly good so will be back, BUT not if we're very hungry aand probably never for sit down on a rainy day (or high season. Or ever).

So 3* for carry out due to price-quality ratio and the bad next door competitor Britt's. Up to 4 stars if Feola's ever gets its act together. 

PS. MILD wings were nothing but. They were more if a medium low spiciness, which I loved.

Haven't been here in 15+ years. Originally discovered this place when I went to the chiropractor down the avenue. Went after the lunch rush and originally wanted to get breakfast. Saw the $7.50 specials and focused on the chicken coquettes, eggplant parmesan and homemade fish cake. Great deal. Substantial fresh salad or cup of soup, main course and one side plus coffee. My bleu cheese dressing was 50 cents extra. Got 2 wonderful croquettes, moist, filled with real white and dark meat, celery, carrots and potatoes with good gravy and a dab of mashed potatoes to hold them on the dish. Coffee good and strong. I had deep fried potatoes as my side. Ice cream additional. Service excellent. Very clean. I'll be back with Senior Management to see if it meets her high standards.

The atmosphere is transformative. The lights set the mood and the music is bumping. This place is so fun and totally worth a try. The marshmallow skillet is a gooey mess but otherwise the drinks were good.

First time dining here. Celebrating anniversary and a birthday.  Gods was excellent. Ahi tuna  cooked to perfection. Our waiter was Phillip. EXCELLENT service. Very knowledgeable about the menu. Will request him next time. Thank you very much!!

My cousin introduced me to this place. I could tell she was letting me in on a neighborhood gem, and probably wasn't thinking I could be as into it as she was, 4 months later and I've pretty much been there at least twice a week. Amberella is all over the place and it has an equally chill vibe. No one is pushy or rude and in a super rush. Everyone is there to chill or read or get their work done( mainly upstairs) but downstairs there are mostly tables of eaters. The drinks are creative and seasonal, my personal favorite is the lavender coffee ( you have to ask for it now, 2 pumps or you won't taste anything). Eggs are always good and the carrot cake steel cut oats is bangin. Healthyish but more so sweet and filling. Desserts are amazing, they even have cronuts, which I haven't seen anywhere in Philly or any city outside of New York. Don't bring a big group because it's not really the type of place. More chill and good for 4 people or less. Most people are by themselves which is nice I think. I go there with an without one other person. But wouldn't bring 3 others or it would be annoying to find a seat and be comfortable unless it's outside

Hands down the most flavorful seafood in Nashville. I usually get the Alaskan snow crab or crawfish platter. Only reason I give 4 stars is because we had a super long wait to get our food during one visit. My friends and I devoured the platters in minutes. Seating is limited.

Went on a Monday night and there were not a lot of ppl. Service was still slow. This was my first time at the broad ripple location and I was upset they did not have chihuahua cheese. Margaritas were still great though!

The Omni Majestic is truly a three-star boutique hotel, but the excellent service my mom and I received while staying here bumps this place up a star.

We both had been flying from opposite ends of the country (me since the night before) in order to attend my grandmother's wake and burial. The funeral home was close to the Omni Majestic, and from the reviews I chose it as our home-base while we were in St. Louis.

I had phoned prior to our arrival to let them know we were coming. When we arrived Mark and someone else who's name I cannot remember (but she was the sweetest woman ever) greeted us. We were early, but they got us into a room quickly. They stored our bags while we got some food in our stomachs and off we went upstairs.

The hotel is rather dated. The elevator is old and the smell is not that great. It still smells like it's legal to smoke indoors-that's how long it's been since the carpet was changed out-or perhaps it was never changed out.

The room was nice. Bathrooms are tiny and the televisions are the old tubes. They also offer complimentary shuttle service within 5 miles.

The $25 a night parking fee is a bit steep considering the location and city. I'd expect that in Chicago, Miami or NYC, but not a boutique hotel in St. Louis. 

If you want nice, centrally located, clean-though dated and great service I'd recommend the Omni Majestic for your stay. If you want big and roomy with some glamour, keep looking.

All in all, such a pleasant and positive stay. They were very kind and accommodating during such a difficult time.

Food was perfect. Service & ambience was ok. We didn't receive the bread service we noticed on the way out on the other tables. Wine pours were small. I still think it's worth trying if you have an extra day. It felt like southern bbq to me. It was my 3rd choice next to Peche & Galliano. Our tab was over $100 for 2 so I think service should be better.

You get great value for your buck. Haircuts are $12 before tip, and Patty is amazing. She is very skilled, quick and super friendly. I have had nothing but great haircuts here! This is the place to go in Nashville for an excellent haircut.

Well guess what?   Buying a washer & dryer this weekend and I will not even bother going to RC willey. All the appliances break . Going to good ole Sears and buying  a kenmore.  My last disaster with them was,a couple of years ago and there is no way I would ever go back. Bought my front load washer from RC willey three years ago and that has also busted. Also my dishwasher busted and have been without for a couple of years because they refuse to take care of customers. Oh well you lose RC Crap.

I am definitely not one to write reviews on a fast food joint since the experience and food is usually pretty standardized.

I ended up at this location because I was getting my car detailed and had an hour to kill. Didn't really see another coffee shop around so figured why not. As soon as I opened the door, another patron who was exiting quickly told me, "Don't waste your time here. They need to get their act together." Disgruntled customer? Whatever. I'm just getting a coffee. Shouldn't matter, right? 

I was wrong. So wrong.

The staff was super loud and I could overhear the manager yelling about people and their breaks. The girl that was helping the guy at the cash register dropped the "MF" and "F" bomb several times. I ordered a small Iced Caramel Macchiato and it took them almost 10 minutes to make it. I had to remind them TWICE until they brought me my drink and it tasted like straight up milk. Had to get them to remake it and it still tastes terrible. Like watered down sugar free syrup. I've had this before at other locations and have never had this terrible aftertaste with it. The cooking area also smells TERRIBLE, like BO mixed with urine. I caught whiffs of it as I was standing by the cash register and can't imagine why it would smell like that. I also wasn't the only one they messed up the orders on. During my 10 minutes of waiting, they had to redo the orders for THREE different customers. All in all, I don't trust this place for sanitation reasons. Find another location.

I have since returned to this establishment at the right time. 
A friend of mine is a regular here and it's not to shabby. 

The bartenders were excellent and service was pretty prompt and pleasant. 

It was late about 6 Saturday's ago when we accompanied by friend for a few drinks. 

A lot different than my first experience. 

I will be returning. 
When I am not sure but I definitely will be. 

Cheers 
Dino.
Atmosphere was pleasant but the food took an hour to arrive with a half full restaurant. The food was average, I ordered Coconut Shrimp, and I've made better at home.

This airport is smaller than some others I have been in. But I will say it is laid back.  When dropping off, the police are not standing there rushing to hurry and get out the car like in some other cities.  You can take your time and say good bye to loved ones.  TSA is quick, efficient and friendly.  Had to be one of the best TSA experiences ever.  There are tv screens everywhere so you always can keep an eye out on your flight.  

When picking up, they have people directing traffic and you can only pull over to the curb if your person is ready to go. If they are not out there, they will make you go around the loop again.  The greatest idea ever??? In the cell phone lot, they have a giant screen in the parking lot with the times of arrival, delays and everything for every air line.  It keeps you from driving around that airport loop a million times. BRILLIANT!!!!

I live downtown so I was super excited when a hair store with a familiar name opened up! Supercuts definitely did not let me down! I've been to this location 3 times since it has opened and it has never disappointed. The stylists are extremely polite and carry decent conversations. The reason they lost a star on my review is because, even though they cut my hair in great time (usually 20-25 minutes), it's always just a tad uneven. Granted, I have wavy hair so most stylists have trouble cutting it but I feel like a big name like Supercuts should be able to handle wavy hair. Great place and will continue to get my business!
What a nice addition to SoBro: Delicious ice cream from a select Michigan dairy, wonderful friendly owner Kirstie, and great location. This is one local business you'll definitely want to support!

My GNO group finally made our way to Rolf & Daughters.  We were not disappointed in the least.  We started with cocktails (all were fabulous and I personally recommend the Buon Appetito) and noshed on the bread with seaweed butter and the chicken liver pate.  The sprinkling of salt that came with the seaweed butter added a wonderful crunch to the dish and livened it up.  We needed extra bread to polish it off.  But the highlight was the pate.  Oh, the pate!!  Who knew that a green tomato jam and a dusting of cacao would off-set this enchanting dish?  Obviously the chef because I was quite leery of cacao with liver.  It worked and delightfully so.  There were "oohs" and "ahs" and those weren't just from me but also the self-proclaimed haters of liver that I dined with this past Saturday evening.  If I were not in a public venue, I would have stuck my finger in there to get the last of this delectable dish.  Next time I think I will order it just for myself and revel in the glory of liver.

For our main course, we all opted for a pasta dish.  Thankfully, we all wanted something different.  Between the four of us we had the garganelli verde with heritage pork ragout and parmesano; the farro gemelli with hen of the woods, cavolo nero and parmesano; the spaghetti fra diavolo with octopus, rad lardo, and calabrian chili; and then the squid ink canestri with shrimp, squid, rad pancetta and chili.  As is our norm, we all sampled each other's dish and proclaimed them all ah-may-zing!!  None of us were disappointed in our on personal bowl of happiness.  I was the one who was fortunate enough to have the squid ink canestri.  I would be hard pressed to go there again and not have the pate and that dish.  It was sublime and the pasta was like heavenly little puffs of deliciousness.  

For dessert, we again shared.  Our favorite was the bombellini filled with vanilla custard and a side of a warmed chocolate sauce for dipping.  They were pure bliss and truly an enchanting end to a memorable night out with friends.  Our group enjoyed our evening so much that we are already planning on a return trip.  If you haven't been, call now and make a reservation so you too can have a food night to remember.

All soda jerks are fun to go to as it's a modernist version of a 1950's diner. The food is very tasty but a little expensive. My fries were a little stale but the burgers were great. Our server was really good, his name was spencer.
Had some difficulty getting a table but I look forward to trying husk again sometime in the future! See you soon, Husk!
Love this place.  Proper Irish Pub!  Darts, good vibe, free popcorn, cold beer.  Very casual.

I was very disappointed with Dr. Quigless's practice. I actually saw one of his associates rather than Dr. Quigless himself so these are comments are broader in nature. I scheduled a cleaning but instead got a new patient screening. This is understandable, but not what I had specifically asked for. I floss a couple times a week, brush regularly, and use mouth wash. I have never had a cavity. I was immediately told I had several cavities and needed a deep cleaning. I was then referred to an individual in his office to be scheduled for these procedures which cost several thousand dollars. I just felt like they were trying to bilk money out of me rather than see me as an actual living and breathing patient.  

I have since scheduled an appointment with another doctor. My insurance only allows me to get the comprehensive x-rays once every three years. I called repeatedly to get the x-rays sent to my new dentist. Nothing happened. Finally they agree to send my x-rays to the new office. They sent paper copies. I am not even kidding. All of their x-rays are kept digitally but they refused to send them. I am not actually sure it is legal for them to deny me access to my own health records like that. Overall I just have a very bad feeling from the get go. I would avoid this practice.

Good and clean. No bedbugs! These days that's important to know for sure. The toilet had an issue where the tank wouldn't fill to flush which we reported upon checkout. Traffic noise and planes flying low from the nearby airport can be heard. Hot breakfast was good. Had a good corporate rate.

Service was solid and Isaac was awesome. Prompt delivery, super nice and made our dining experience great. Loved the food as always and can't say enough about the service. Thanks Isaac! You're the man! Dabs*
The owner eventually contacted me after discovering the mishap. The item was delivered as described.
It's just ok. I wish that the naked burrittos were in a larger bowl. I also wish that they offered shredded beef (like chipotle does). I do like their green salsa with chips. Yum!

11/23/21 
Workers do not wear masks and even when they do they have them only covering their mouth, not nose. 

Restaurants are a high traffic area. If a patron is required to wear a mask then why aren't servers wearing them and doing so appropriately?

As we waited for a table I called the actual
Restaurant (didn't want to make an in person complaint because well...I hear stories about what happens to food when someone complains) and a man answered- stated he was a manager. I told him the issue about the lack of masks and he did NOTHING. We left. 

We will not be returning to this location. Absolutely unacceptable that a manager (maybe his name was Brian?) would hear the concern from a patron and not address it at all. We waited 20 minutes at the bar to see if anything changed with the staff not in compliance. Nothing changed.

Reiterating so many reviews here, the BBQ shrimp is out of this world. Similar enough to traditional NO's creole BBQ to be familiar, but unique enough to stand out. They're heavy on black pepper, almost to the point of being overwhelming, but if you can avoid getting too many in any fork full they add just the right amount of kick to the sauce. Once caveat, they're head on peel yourself which with this sauce equals a big mess. Thankfully the servers are aware and bib you up. 

The other items are good, but unlike the rest of New Orleans dining were strangely under seasoned. It was actually the first time/place in 8 trips to the Big Easy that I had to reach for the salt shaker. The Pecan pie is awesome as is the bread pudding. 

The room is all dark wood, wrought iron and open kitchen. There is a decent sized bar to have a drink or wait for a table. 

The place is always packed so reservations are strongly recommend and mine was promptly honored. It wasn't my favorite in town but its is another strong performer from the Brennan food dynasty that I'll definitely go back to.
Meh.  It's just a Taco Bell.  Be prepared to wait 20 minutes for your food at times.  This one is pretty inconsistent.

I have ordered from this place a few times now. Never disappointing. Their menu is mostly vegan, but they always have something for meat lovers. My go-to is the buddha bowl. So good! We try to get something different as their menu rotates as the chef comes up with new ideas. We also have done a pre-made Mother's Day Brunch. Food was delicious with easy heating instructions. Oh, and their baked goods are out of this world. Always something different, fresh, and yummy.
This place is very good.  I wish it had more inside seating.  Great for lunch when you want something out of the ordinary with out having much time.

Okay here is a cool spot for everyone. We came in for the Maine Lobster at $9.99. It's a special on Monday and Tuesday for a limited time. This place does not look like anything from the outside or inside. I do wish it had a little bit more atmosphere but we sat in the oyster bar area which had a Louisiana flare to it. Beers are ice cold just the way we like them. We also got the fish spread which I highly recommend. It even has a little spice to it so don't add the hot sauce until you taste it. It might be too much and we felt like it didn't even need any hot sauce. Lobster definitely worth the $10 and service was very friendly. My boyfriend got the lobster roll priced $22. I thought it was a little steep. I say it had too much butter it actually felt like it was soaked in butter and the bread was limp. I like it when the bread is gently toasted  and the lobster is brushed with butter . My boyfriend said you can never have too much butter. You decide.  We also saw the soup come out and I definitely want to try that next time. I even think you can make a meal out of the fish spread and the soup. Can't wait to come back with some friends.
I am amazed by the quantity and quality of food sold here.  I stop by here sometimes for the meals ready to eat and the hot food bar.  Love the food selection.

The Appalachian Pork you DO NOT need a knife. It was so unbelievably tender and juicy. The seasoning had a BBQ twist which was perfect.  I accompanied my dinner with green beans and a side salad. 
The service by Nicole was very attentive and she has a perfect customer service aura about her.

If I could give no stars I would. I didn't even get to try the food but I did in the past and it wasn't that great. Wanted to give their breakfast a shot but I hit the button multiple times in the ten minutes I waited and never even had someone come on the speaker to take my order. Horrible customer service. Please do yourself a favor and never come here.

Food was Very good. Only critqie would be "medium spicy" was a bit too much, still delicious! Very nice inside. Dont be afraid by the location. Service was great. Highly recommend checking this location out.
I love this store!  They carry Tommy bahama, Robert graham, vineyard vines.  The owner Isaac and the staff are fantastic.  A truly  personal shopper shopping experience.

Food is great, but nearly an hour wait for saturday brunch? They have a ridiculous table policy. No flexibility!! If there are 2 two person tables next to each other, then TURN IT INTO A TABLE OF FOUR!! Instead, they just keep seating tables of 2.

Living Social deal that did not deliver. Website no longer working and phone calls not returned. No good reviews or any reviews for that matter recently. I think I read there was a bad rating from the BBB.

Ooka is exactly like Benihana (teppanyaki restaurant) when you can't find Benihana anywhere in the area. Dining here is definitely an experience. The chef prepares your meal right in front of you, performing food tricks to keep you engaged. It's definitely a treat to see these talented chefs perfect their craft. With that said, the service is excellent. Everyone was friendly and you feel welcomed.

The restaurant wasn't too crowded and there is plenty of seating. If you don't want teppanyaki, you can sit on the other side of the restaurant and opt for a more traditional sit-down seating. The menu on that side is more of your typical Japanese sushi. We dined on the teppanyaki side and marveled at the great chefs!

For teppanyaki, there are a bunch of different combinations you can choose from. If you want teriyaki chicken only, you can have that. If you want a little chicken with steak, you can have that too. You can pretty much have any combination imaginable between chicken, steak, fish, lobster, etc... I would definitely suggest ordering a combination so you can try multiple things. I decided on getting a combination of the chicken and NY strip steak. Personally, I don't think the food compares to Benihana but the ambiance certainly makes up for that. The chicken was a little on the dry side but the beef was good. It was prepared medium-rare, just the way I like it. It can get a little pricey depending on what you order.

Ooka is a decent place for teppanyaki. I wouldn't say it's the best teppanyaki ever but the ambiance and cleanliness of the restaurant is great.

First time at Monterey Court and instantly a fan.  For starters, came to see Darden Smith.  After listening to his music for 25 years, I'm happy to get this chance to see him live.  And he didn't disappoint.   Awesome singer/songwriter!  Opening act was Eric Schaefer, a local Tucson favourite.  Really nice songs.  And let's not forget the venue.  Outside stage and seating.  Quaint village atmosphere.  Great food and drinks, too.   We need more places like this.  Keep it going.  Glad to support local food and musicians.  This is why we live in the Southwest.

For the business traveler forget it. While the offer free wifi as a guest, they provide for only day one. Wake up the next morning and no connection. They require payment for another half day at the hotel. For what they charge for a night that is absurd. Using the lobby wifi is not an option as it is so slow that it is useless. Nice place to visit but not if you plan to conduct business via Internet.
Service was perfect.  Food was unbelievable.  I had the lasagna and it was the best ive ever had. I am recommending to everyone.

I am so angry at this place first off let me say I really think offering free vacuum is super super cool to great customer service tool that is why I paid $6 for my car wash today but the guy that sprays it when you first go in spread it for like 5 seconds and not even at full power and I looked at him I said that's what I get for a six dollar car wash so needless to say I'm driving around with the damn s*** from the birds still on the roof of my car so I tried calling and all I got was a busy busy busy busy signal and I'm mad so that's why I give you 1 star because the dude that spread my car in the beginning should have sprayed the bird s*** off my car

大盘鸡拌面也太好吃了，味道很棒，不是很辣，大块土豆软糯，拌面配底汁太好吃了，根本停不下来。凉拌土豆丝太赞，刀工不错，切得很细，调味也不错，酸辣适口。最重要的来了，包子，包子太棒了，皮薄肉多，配的醋汁，尼玛太好吃了，一定要点哦。
All my order is good, delicious, fantastic and bravo. Sautéed spicy chicken is tender and soft, spicy flavor is medium hot, potato tastes good,  melts in your mouth. Pork bun is damn good, meat is filled 100%, dip with vinegar sauce, man, it's heaven. I recommend you guys try those in the pic.

I'm currently waiting to be seen, but I called in yesterday to make an appointment for 2:00pm today to have a painful tooth pulled. It's now 3:30 and I still haven't been seen. I went and asked how much longer it will be and the lady at the desk says that I'm next but she cannot give me a timeframe because if just depends. This is ridiculous. I'm sitting here with a debilitating toothache and I was supposed to be seen 1.5 hours ago. This is unprofessional and I will NEVER come back here. What's the point in making an appointment when they don't stick to the appointment time?

Edit: I finally got seen -- 2.5 hours after my appointment time.
Ordered takeout and Ryan was super helpful and professional.   Food was delicious.   Don't forget to get a cupcake too.  They are SO GOOD!

They never answer the phone I bought a dryer online and within a week it stopped working tried to call them to find out what steps I had to take called multiple times over many days and let
the phone ring over and over finally had to take time off from work to go to the store in person when I mentioned that I had been trying to contact them, the only reaction got from them was one of clear annoyance with me for bothering them
Home Depot from now on!

Wonderful store devoted to pet birds and their owners.   The owner is very knowledgeable and caring towards all the animals under his care.  They offer boarding, which is wonderful since it is often hard to find someone to watch your pet parrot, and take time to socialize with the boarded parrots so they don't feel like they were abandoned.

The store sells several kinds of well known food brands as well as their own mixes, many different toys (several have been new favorites for my own fids), a large selection of cages that are appropriately sized for the parrots, some unique odds and ends including dishes and millet.  They also breed certain kinds of birds.

Of course, if you go by there you have to say hello to their friendly Caique Calypso - she really can get wound around your fingers or hair or purse, and is an absolute delight to interact with - it's also a testament to her owner (who runs the store) that she is so friendly. 

Just a wonderful store to pick up things for your favorite bird as well as to interact with other parrots and their owners.
Happy Hour Review: Awesome Happy Hour specials  ($5 bubbly or sangria, $4 glasses of wine and $3 beers) Our server was awesome. The dipping sauces for the fries are amazing.

One of my top fave cupcake shops in Edmonton! It's not the #1, but certainly second best :)

The have peanut butter flavour, which I think is essential and so many shops here don't have it.  I also think they have a better icing, not as sweet as others.

Highly recommend it!
I ordered the taste of new Orleans and it was nothing but watered down rice. Waiter was very nice and accommodating. For a seafood place the blackened grill chicken sandwich was bland but ok.
Raw Oysters are awesome. Escargot excellent. Great service. Good people watching spot. Cajun Cuban sandwich ok. Bourbon selection nice.

Beware of fraud. I went in for an inspection and I was aware that one of my tires had a slow leak. During the inspection I was told my brakes needed to be changed soon but the vehicle would pass inspection. I was informed that there was a nail in the tire. I refused the $600 brake job. I was charged for the tire repair. The next day when I did the brakes I noticed the nail was still there. When I called the dealership the receptionist said tech said he had no time to speak to me after I explained my issue. I even sent an email requesting a refund for the tire repair that wasn't performed. I never heard back from anyone.

Didnt experience the zoo and walked around for a bit before finding the "dog park" (what this review will focus on}

Brandywine dog park has no water, no fences, and no dogs. It's a field next to a somewhat busy street. Theres two picnic benches and nothing else. It's hardly a dog park, it's more just a stretch of grass.
New to East Nashville and visited here with our friends. They were vegan and the kitchen took special care of our friends. My Amberjack fish was amazing. Highly recommend this spot!

Posting this in order to help future customers of this establishment.
It's my fault for not asking for the price I guess, but never in my life have I been charged $19 for a double shot of Makers Mark.

If you love coffee and want a good donut to go with it you can't beat this Dunkin.  The staff is friendly and quick.  I have never had to wait long in either the drive thru or inside.  I usually get a medium or large Cold Brew.  I personally prefer the cold brew because of the smooth mellow taste. Currently you can get the Sweet and Salty Cold Brew which I have had and if you are a fan of savory and sweet you will love it.  The donuts here are hard to beat. I am not a big fan of yeasty donuts and the cake here are great.
Excellent brunch! And like most places, the earlier the better.

New to area so we relied on Yelp for a place to go for Mid-week Anniversary quick dinner! We were very pleasantly surprised because it far surpassed the ratings. Food was delicious and loved the atmosphere. We sat outside but could hear good live music inside. Nice servers and friendly management! Will be our go to place!

Havilah's selection impressed me because my teen daughter actually bought skirts for school!  Brand names, very current, excellent condition.

Great spot to find practically new trendy shoes/boots, too.  Fun jewelry - found brand new necklaces here at half the price of mall kiosk.  Brand name jeans, too.

Amazing selection of wedding dresses and formals/semi-formals.  Everything is in pristine condition.  Spacious dressing rooms - more than one, so less waiting.

Owner very helpful and friendly.  She offers frequent sales to keep inventory up to date.  My daughter found a cute semi-formal dress that was marked down to $8 -wow.

Being that this is my local Home Depot I'm in there quite a bit. the only possible way to make the store any better is for the operating hours to be longer. I am consistently going into the store at 10 minutes to 10... But with the time change in the fall they now close at 9 so I really don't go in there at all, due to the hours I keep. Some days, although,  I do manage to get in there before my bedtime.

I've never had a problem in the store with availability of an item... The things I want they always seem to have in stock. the sales people are always on their best game and they can generally answer my question and if not they can refer me to someone who can. One of my favorite things about Home Depot is that I very very seldom... Maybe not ever... Have heard that well you can get it on homedepot.com. for some reason that always hurts me because I know that before I even leave the house so I don't come to the store to shop online. So again, that's one of the good things I rarely hear that which means that they usually have what I want in the store. so that in a way is a compliment to inventory control. and hence I am a repeat offender at this store, haha.

I love a good brazilian steakhouse and have been known to pull a homer simpson and have the gauchos actually avoid me to save a few bucks. This one was just ok. The service was really slow on drinks - we didn't get our first drink until after we finished the salad bar - good 15 minutes.  As soon as we flipped the cards the meat started flying - i know thats always the way it is but they were coming too fast so really couldnt enjoy the meal. Every 30 seconds someone was whispering in my ear about their meat..... wow that came out a little odd,,,

The meat all seemed to be overdone, and the cuts seemed to be cheaper than i typically see. Overall it was just an ok experience and probably would not go back

I wish I could give them negative stars. I've never experienced such a combination of bad customer service and frustration with a company. I ordered a new car stereo and remote start as a Christmas present, and installation was included in the bundle. I set up the stereo install for two weeks out, and the remote start about a month out. Two hours prior to stereo install, they inform me that the installer called in. Luckily, I contacted the Greenwood store (who were great) and still got it done. Then, on the day of remote start install, they call me a few hours before and cancel my install. They then tell me they can install it on Jan. 13, which is 21 days later. Well, that kind of ruins it as a Christmas present. The biggest problem is that I now will have to take time off work, as these installs can take 4 hours. I had set aside time to do this when my schedule permitted it. To top it off, it's nearly impossible to get someone to answer the damn phone at this store. I completely regret purchasing anything from Best Buy, and especially choosing this poorly run excuse for a store  for the installs. Never again. Screw you Washington St. Best Buy.

Best. Detroit. Style. Period.
Delicious Neapolitan best piping hot out of oven.
The surprise box? Fantastic mix of wings, smashed, onion rings, pizza etc.
Staff? Fantastic 
Best of Philly select status with just a few others. That's rare air to share.

I walked in for a pedicure and was told wait would be 20 minutes. They had me soaking my feet for over 45 minutes. Even by that time none of the techs looked nearly done with their current customers. The entire time there was no acknowledgement that I was still even waiting or how much longer the wait would be. I ended up leaving with no service completed. Very poor customer service. I wouldnt recommend anyone come waste their time here like I did.
The food and service are consistently excellent. I love this place. My favorites are Drunken Noodles with beef and Mango Sticky Rice but really everything I've had there has been great.

Being genuine pays off. This ice cream dive was a great idea for us as a family. Stopping at the "sketchy spot" paid off!! We moved the ice cream and the couple working were fantastic. We felt welcomed and felt like family when we stopped by. Five stars from us!!
The oysters here are divine.  Freshest we have had  short of gathering them ourselves! The service was excellent.  We will be back.  We had oysters Rockefeller,  charbroilled oysters, and raw.

Being in the spa biz it PAINS me to pay retail - but for certain items that I must have NOW (and can't or won't wait for shipping online or ebay) I get at Douglas.

They even carry Bond fragrances.  (Love 'em.)  Also my 3 year old is a girly-girl and runs straight to the CHanel display to play with lipstick whenever we are there.  She adores Coco Pink.
Heard great things and we had high hopes. It was just okay. 5 of us ate here and none of us felt our food was above par. We left disappointed. Staff seemed great. Food not so much.

I remember Sambo's from back when my Grandparent's (who lived between Goleta and SB line near In and Out [before that spot even opened] were alive).  I am now a resident and am still yet to go here, I do live right by the corner of Milpas and Cabrillo  My dad told me about the "dark past" of this restaurant, but after reading so many positive reviews I have to visit ASAP!

I recently renewed my lease for my apartment at the Adelphia House, and this year would make the 5th year of staying here. I love staying here and couldn't imagine moving elsewhere unless it's a lakefront home with five golden retrievers and a garden in the back. Okay, I digress. 

Here are five great reasons why I love it here:

1. Maintenance has always been very responsive to my work order requests.

2. I don't need to be home to receive packages; the front desk signs for them and keeps them safe. I never had an issue, and I order a lot. #dontjudgeme

3. My floor and neighbors are quiet. A little too quiet, but I'm okay with it.

4. Friendly staff! There is nothing worst than dealing with nasty attitudes when you're trying to rectify an issue.

5. Location, location, location! Post office, Target, Reading Terminal Market, Trader Joes, great bars & restaurants, and let's not forget the Wine & Spirits stores are within walking distance.

If I had to think of things that I don't like, it's the occasional fire alarms set off by people who fill the dryers with too many clothes. Like, c'mon folks, is that complicated.  Several times last year, the water had to shut down due to pipe upgrades. Although it was a minor inconvenience, I understood the need. Okay, that's all. Thank you for reading. :)

Overall, very happy with the sizeable portions, reasonable pricing, and interesting tastes.

Started off with the shrimp summer rolls - these were your typical vietnamese rolls, with rice paper wrapping around carrots, cabbage, noodles and shrimp. Good taste with the peanut sauce (they also provide a honey vinaigrette which tasted pretty good). One order is also enough for 4 people!

Next were the thai sweet chili wings and spicy curry wings. These were a hit with our table, and definitely something we would order again the next time we come! The chili sauce and curry mayo tasted awesome, and the chicken itself was fried perfectly while keeping the meat tender

The lemongrass pork belly banh mi is also a must-order. One sandwich is enough for two people. The pork comes in thin slices (like bacon), and is rich with the lemongrass flavor. 

We decided to try both the crab fried rice and lobster fried rice. Each one comes with plenty of the respective seafood meat cooked within the rice, as well as a fried soft shell crab and lobster tail, respectively, on top. 
My only complaint is that the lobster tail was probably overcooked and too tough.

For dessert, we decided to forego that fried cheesecake and tried an interesting dish where sweet bananas were wrapped in spring roll dough, and then fried and paired with ice cream. Nothing mindblowing, but fun to eat and a nice way to end the meal!

I wanted to update my review and add a star for the Chef. He invited us back for a private tasting after we had shared our previous experience.  We really enjoyed tasting some new foods and the origin behind them. The kitchen staff were so nice and entertaining too.
