Businesses that are transparent and open to feedback make all the difference and we really appreciated the entire experience! It was a treat getting to know all of them.

Visited for breakfast, and we enjoyed this little mom and pop donut/pastry shop. I got a bacon egg and cheese breakfast sandwich on a bagel and my boyfriend got a ham and cheese omelet. My breakfast sandwich was great, and it was super affordable at $2.99. The omelet was average, and a little more expensive. I would stick with the breakfast sandwiches and donuts if you visit. The donuts are fresh and they offer a variety of interesting flavors.

Their pastries, donuts, and bagels are all made in house and the staff if very friendly and accommodating. Great place to try if you are looking for a low key, cheap breakfast in the st. Ann area.
Our go to for tacos and Diablo enchiladas great green salsa, and the best jalapeño guacamole ever. My favorite, carnitas super taco, one and I'm full.

My 5 star review is for Heather, the amazing Nail tech!

I recently fell in love with a new product called Axxium by OPI, Beauty Brands advertises it as a stay perfect manicure.

Stay Perfect? A manicure that stays perfect??? Yeah right I thought, as most women who read this review will also think. Because as a woman you know damn well that no manicure stays perfect. ever.

Guess what? Stay perfect is the closest manicure I have had in 26 years of staying at all!!!

It is not fake nails or any of that crap that ruins your nails. It is a polish by OPI that is cured under UV lights. 

For $34 I go pick out a great OPI color and then have a mani that lasts 3 weeks. And the kicker is if it does not stay perfect, they fix it for FREE!!! The bonus here is that for $17 you can get a fill, so you can extend the life of your manicure! I love this option, I have never had a nail polish last so long that I am like take it off!!! So I usually get one fill and then change the color!

My first axxium service was done by a very nice gal that did not specialize in the nail service so it did not stay perfect... so Heather fixed it. An entire manicure for freee! I have been going back religiously for 3 months ever since!!

I am a mom, full time Nursing student who waits tables and occasionally tends bar. What does this mean? It means my nails break, split, and look like crap 24/7! After having my nail done by Heather they look great 24/7!!!

I cannot say enough what an amazing service I have discovered and I adore how professional and timely Heather is at every appointment!!

Thank you Beauty Brands for giving me gorgeous nails! And for those of you who want nails that will make your friends envious give Heather a shot, you can thank me later!!
Main Street pizza and eatery is by far the best food and service in Hendersonville, would recommend to anyone who wants to have friendly service, cold beer and great food
sign outside says something about "accepts donations"  but the store is not a non-profit entity.  that may be legal but......i will not shop there. i have a social conscious & i hope you do too.

Came here with a school group for gospel brunch during FQF. Linda wright and the band had us all dancing, and the food was remarkably good. Would definitely return! Thank you for being so good to my kids!

I was in need of a parking space for the coming school year and Lydia in the office went above and beyond helping me have a secure place to park every day.  She tried to find a spot for me that someone else didn't need and when that didn't work she had the trash dumpster moved to create a parking spot.  Lydia is kind and attentive to us, she always responds right away to our emails and finds solutions.  Want to feel like you are more than a rent check?  Move to a Sierra Property and try to get Lydia as your property manager!

Delicious first visit! The kind of perfect meal you want til the very last bite. The vibe was very pleasant - it was packed but didn't feel uncomfortable. The staff was ideally friendly, laidback, helpful and on point. Can't wait to go back for more.

It's our second time there and we have not been disappointed. The first time we sat at the bar and got beers and their short rib pizza. It was so delicious. This time we did dinner there. I got a Caesar salad and the spaghetti with meatballs stuffed with short ribs. The boyfriend got the NY strip. Everything was delicious. I love that they have "uno" and "duo" sizes because some places give you so much pasta it's ridiculous. I got the uno size and it was the perfect amount of food. We also got the chocolate peanut butter tart for a dessert, it was very good but the milkshake shooter was weird and was not mentioned in the dessert description. Great spot for date night or a special occasion!

The seating hostess actually carried out a nearly-five-minute conversation with someone who appeared to be a friend prior to seating us.  She was also not very friendly - maybe if we were friends outside of the restaurant she would have been more pleasant.  The food was just okay - nothing great.  My wife had a ribeye steak sandwich and it looked pretty awful.  She said it was just okay.  I was hoping to leave a better review for them!

Horrible customer service. I paid for a pick up order through the website and when the delivery guy arrived, I could not receive my food. He said my order had not been paid for. I paid through the app online. I also showed the delivery guy my bank statement showing the amount for my food being drafted out my account. The delivery guy then puts me on the phone with someone who works there. I thought she wanted to verify my card info but instead she just wanted me to pay AGAIN. NEVER will I order anything from this place again. I never had this problem before when placing an order online. I was on the phone for at least 5 minutes trying to get her to understand the food was paid for. I even gave her the shopping number for my order. She then proceeded to act like the phone was breaking up and she could not hear me. Extremely disappointed in how the situation was handled. She refused to refund me as well. Never order anything online or from this business. They do not appreciate their customers.

I usually eat here all the time. I love their Tuscan tomato soup. I called today to order  to be greeted by a rude lady on the phone. She put me on hold for 10minutes and didn't have the one and only soup they served. Very displeased with this service !

We've been here twice now and it's totally wonderful! The Shrimp Fettuccine Alfredo is the real homemade from scratch stuff and totally amazing! The waitstaff is great along with the atmosphere! Every time we come to NOLA we eat here for the Alfredo alone!

I was excited to try this place when I heard that Nhu Y had been resurrected as a such.The bahn mi was better than I had remembered . The chicken (B4)$4.75, was superb! I do believe the search for the best bahn mi in Philly has ended.Chicken was moist and well- seasoned. Nice amount of mayo, carrots & daikon. Just the right amount of jalapenos for some heat. The bread was the real star. Dense and chewy; a cross between a baguette & ciabatta.

They also offer, sweets, steamed buns, spring rolls, croissants and more!The veggie spring roll ($1.25)  was great! Not greasy and the filling had so much flavor, dipping sauce(not provided) was unnecessary. For dessert the steamed banana cake ($2) served w/ a side of coconut milk was a taste and texture sensation.Gotta try a steamed bun($2) next time, for which I cannot wait.

It's good , but i get the usual ( The Turkey over Italy I think it's called ) it seems to take a while to get food to go here . It's a busy place and has been around for quite a while and it's an Old building . Would definitely recommend at least trying .
Closest thing to NY pizza you are gonna find in St. Louis. Great guys who run it and even better pizza.

I cannot even count how many times we have eaten here. Big Fish Grill is one of, if not my favorite restaurant! The food is always fresh and flavorful, and the staff are always kind and attentive. For the longest time I was stuck on ordering the Big Shrimp Penne. I would get it every time we came and was always satisfied. However, I have started to get more adventurous and am now trying almost everything on the menu, and am getting something new every time I come. I have the confidence that everything I try will be delicious, and so far I have been right! Along with the main courses, the appetizers are also great! We usually get the shrimp cocktail, and the shrimp are always super meaty. It is also impossible to dine here without getting dessert. We always go for the peanut butter pie. It is very big, creamy, and overall wonderful. The atmosphere is another plus, both inside and out. The big sea creatures hanging from the ceiling are always fun to look at, and the river behind the restaurant is also some nice scenery if you are dining outside. If it is a warmer day, I also recommend taking a walk along the river after dinner! Overall, Big Fish Grill is an amazing restaurant in all aspects of dining and I highly recommend it to anyone and everyone!

Sponge docks definitely worth a visit. Great educational experience for kids and a fun reasonable gift shop. Also in Tarpon the Replay museum is great. One price and all the free play on old pinball and electric games you could want. Be prepared to be there awhile. https://www.replaymuseum.org

This place is a classic. With witty signage, a concession stand with trendy bottom shelf beer, and floors sticky enough you may want to wear close toed shoes. 

With a world full of reclining plush seats, $15 bags of popcorn this theatre is a throwback to the way movies used to be seen. Carrying only a small handful of titles at time with a friendly and knowledgeable staff this a great place to go see a flick. 

Tickets are affordable and so are concession (relative to other theatres) and although I've only been a couple times there's never a line out the door. 

Hi Pointe is always on my list of theatres when looking for a good movie night location.
Changed an inner tube on my wife's bike in 5 min 'flat'.  The guys were friendly, give them a try!  They sell cool t-shirts too.

I've been using this Jiffy lube for the past year, but never again. I didn't recognize any of the staff members and everyone was rude. I was in there for 40 min and finally knocked on the door to see what was taking so long. I was told that my dip stick wouldn't even read and that my tank was empty and corroded so the plug wouldn't come out. I get oil changes regularly and stop in for the free top off service all the time. I was very upset and they said it would be a few hundred dollars to fix. I left without service and later called to speak with the manager, who asked me to come back in. While waiting two other clients said they told then the same thing. I would not recommend this location ever! DO NOT USE THIS LOCATION!!!

Life saver! My mommy has been going to Terri for her pedicures for years now; so when I realized I had a chipped nail and was overdue for a gel manicure while home for the holidays, naturally, I decided to try this shop...beyond happy I did! I called on Saturday (before Christmas) around 6:15 pm and asked if I arrived by 6:45 pm did they have time for a gel mani, she said sure, come on in. I got there right at 6:45 pm and I was greeted and seated immediately. They were friendly and once they realized who my mom was, they raved about her and how they'd heard all about me. Warm and clean environment and very reasonably priced! Lighting is also a plus, as it is adequate but not too bright (I get migraines). I had gel on my nails and she was gentle when removing that polish! My manicure and polish look fabulous! Definitely recommend trying this place and this will be my go to when I'm in town!

Wow - we are 1 week into a 7 week trip from Socal to Banff in our 89 Westy. While in Reno, we noticed antifreeze leaking from underneath the van. It's a Friday before a 3 day weekend and we are in unfamiliar territory. Put the word out in a VW Facebook site and was referred to this place. I called and spoke with Spencer who advised that he could take a look at it around 9:00...it is now 10:00 and we are back in our hotel with a completely fixed vehicle. Spencer and his son were so friendly and helpful!!! Also, very knowledgeable about vanagons and VW's in general - had a beautiful Porsche in his shop as well as other VW types that he was working on...I wish we lived in Reno so he could be our regular mechanic....Can't thank you enough Spencer (and son) for getting us back on the road - a big shout out to Matt for the referral!

Good food, great prices. I have had the tacos on a couple of visits and I've really enjoyed it. Nothing fancy here, it's really like a fast food atmosphere. The food is good quality and when I'm in the mood for Mexican food, this a go to place for me.

I tried this "cute little" place 2 weeks ago and let me tell you, I felt like I was VIP.  I am serious.  I am not one to throw around compliments if you are mediocre and TRUST ME, the word is not applicable for this husband-wife team.  For this visit to be my first with them, I was already planning on my next appointment...fully abandoning my now former nail artist.  Ann and Andy are top-notch professionals.  They gave me one of the best services I've ever had!  They do manicures, fill-ins and even complimented me on my color choice.  Relaxing atmosphere.  No stress...and did I say that I got the best pedicure/leg massage ever?  Yes, indeed!!!!

Mothers Day Fail:
Online ordering system is down so no curbside.  Put mask on and go inside.  NO WORKERS wearing masks.  Cashier complaining about phone orders.  Maybe if your online system was working you wouldn't have to take orders over the phone.  Too many slow moving hipsters.  Too slow...

Such a pretty place to see! Loved the set up! 

Got a mini lemon meringe tart and a piece of their chocolate flourless cake. The lemond curd filling was spot on with tartness! Crust and meringue weren't as memorable. The chocolate flourless cake was definitely rich and reminded me of chocolate truffles.

Their ambience and set up of the menus is what I am most impressed with this place. I'll be back to dine in more than to have their baked goods. They have a pretty small assortment at the counter but a decent enough amount to choose from. This is a cake place where you can get a slice of whatever cake you see in the display case. Lots of cakes to try!

Next time I will try some other items!

I don't care what anyone says.  I grew up here in Tucson and this place is the best.   I still stop here every time I drive up from Phoenix.  The strawberry lemon eegee mix is my favorite.   I love the ranch fries and stinky pretzels (with provolone).  I will continue to come here for ever.   

Ps I have been asking for 14 years now please build one in Phoenix.

Went on a Sunday night and it was pretty dead. The sushi was good, nothing special but definitely a place I would go again. The plate presentation was fun with three different sauces on the plate. We ordered the Crunch roll, Dynamite roll, Rainbow roll and hamachi nagiri (bill was $37). There was a mirror above the sushi bar so we could see the sushi chefs preparing the rolls, which was unique.
The restaurants style was eclectic and fun. The server was awesome and it seemed the staff all worked together nicely (picking up our empty plates and making sure everything was going well).

I just moved to Indianapolis from California and was really nervous about finding a dress here. Side-note: I have recently gained 30+ lbs because of stress and was feeling really body-conscious about the whole experience. In fact, I was DREADING my appointment. Once I got to the salon I was still freaked out, but Celeste helped me find a dress that was magnificent. It made me feel beautiful. I ended up picking out something TOTALLY different than what I came in for but felt truly beautiful and became excited about the wedding despite my personal issues. 

Celeste was wonderful to work with, she was so fun and I felt like I was working with a friend who was honest but tactful and aware of my feelings. I didn't feel pressured or rushed. 

I did go over my budget but that was of my own choosing, they have a wide variety of dresses and can work in your budget. 

Now I can't wait for the big day! Since they found me a dress that already looks fabulous I have also been more motivated to get back into the gym. Working with Marie Gabriel was a great choice for me!
I love sushiiiii. I'm on a mission to try everything here and so far, I highly recommend the Mexican roll. Yummm, I'm in the mood for sushi now.

I went to Maxwell Johnson almost two years ago and had a horrible experience that is still going on today. I went in to have a crown put on. He put a temporary crown on. When I came back to get the perminent Crown on, in taking off the temporary all of a sudden he Says uh-oh we don't have enough of the tooth left to Crown it. Are u kidding? So then he proceeds to tell me that my options are a post and and an implant and something else. I cried in the chair. So I decide on an  Implant. And he proceeds to tell me I don't have enough bone to do the implant so I need a bone graph. He does the bone graph tells me to wait 3 months and come back so he can do the implant. I come back in 3 months he says that the bone graft didn't work I don't have enough    bone.  To that I left to go to another dentist. Actually went to a well  Respected  periodontist in Tucson who did a scan of my   Mouth.  He agreed not enough bone to do the implant.  he did a bone graph after taking out the mess that doctor Johnson did. But before doing it he measured everything and showed me how much bone I needed for the implant and how short we were. doctor Johnson never measured anything . So I had the  Bone graph.  He told me I had to wait 9 months for that bone to grow back. Quite a bit longer than the 3 doctor Johnson recommended.  Thousands of dollars and quite a bit of pain later I'm going to get  The implant Thursday.  Needless to say I was not happy with the care Dr Johnson provided. Also while I was having treatment there, he and his assistant conversed about their weekends as if I was not even there.

Went for the NYE party. Food was great, bands were entertaining, the event was well planned and definitely worth the price of the tickets. We booked a table and our server was very attentive making sure we had drinks and that we didn't need anything. A really great time. The portapottys outside for the lounge was really great since it was so crowded and the lines could get long for the bathrooms. Everything was clean despite the crowd. Would have been fun if there was a way it could have been planned to also include a fireworks viewing. Would definitely go back.

Not the nicest environment but lots of tvs. The prices for specials aren't that great. Pitcher of beer is $8 instead of $9 during specials which doesn't seem like much of a saving. The food is alright too but not great

first time trying this boba place out! It seems that this is one of the few boba places in this area. The menu is huge, there are so many different flavors of drinks to choose from. There are also fresh desserts on display for customers to choose from as well. The aesthetic of the boba shop consitists of a panda mural, lots of k-pop band posters and different plants around the shop. The only thing I would have to say is that the boba was a little hard.

Food was good, waitress was super friendly,  interior was sleek and cool, and I got a complete and filling breakfast for about $10. While the food isn't mind-blowing it's cheap and decent so I will definitely be back.
Fair prices. Had my car in and out within a day with no hassle. It's hard to find a good mechanic so we are thrilled.

Not impressed. Went in this evening for a pedicure for myself and my 7 year old daughter. They seemed incredibly annoyed we were there. We arrived at 5:30 and were out by 6, when the door clearly states they close at 7. My pedicure was rushed, she didn't even remove any of the dry/dead skin around my toes. Then she charged me full price for my 7 year olds pedicure.. We will not be back. Ever.

Order from them every week using GrubHub.  My favorite is the Seafood Drunken Noodles.  Spicy, and fulfilling.  I've had the sushi a few times, and like the creativity of the rolls.  Staff is nice and the actual restaurant is small, but cozy, with a nice ambiance.  I dislike that they don't accept AMEX in person, but since I order delivery from GrubHub anyway, it doesn't really matter.

Great neighborhood coffee shop!  I normally stop in 4-5 times a week and get whatever is on "Tap."  Normally the works is a great bet for taste and a caffeine jolt!  Also, check out their sandwiches.  They are made fresh and pretty good and will pack it up "togo" if on the run.  

I recently asked the owner, Neil, how his coffee is always so fresh and flavorful.....he informed me that he roasts his own beans and is one of the few coffee bean roasters in the area.  I was pumped to hear all of this and now understand why the coffee is so great!  

Check it out for yourself, but Im an advid customer!

This restaurant is not wheelchair accessible because they have one door that opens. There are two separate doors with a very tight transition to the next door which will not accommodate turning radius of wheelchairs. I got stuck between the two doors and it was humiliating. It took 12minutes to get the second front door open so I could back out, I could not turn around. Additionally they had a lot of crap in the area between the two doors which further complicated this fiasco.

I was pretty excited to go here, and super miffed that it is not actually wheelchair accessible.

My hairdresser is the best hands down. They need to get some shampooers that know how to do their job. 
Place could use some updating.

Well.. we thought we will try something new and it was disappointing. We waited for 15 minutes before we got drinks and it took over and hour after we ordered to get our brunch which was now lunch. The food tastes okay but only go here if you like to pay double what you get everywhere else for less. Extremely overpriced. My chicken and waffles where $22!!! For one waffle and 3 tiny chicken strips. Man...and in addition they pit no logic to cleaning. Put fresh napkins and silverware on dirty table and come back to clean the table after the fact. Also reused napkins that customers that only were there for a drink on the table. So guest were all over them....

The quesadilla was delicious! They make it with an awesome garlic sauce! 

The decor and atmosphere were weird, but the staff was friendly!

Everything thing I need in a gun range.  The staff has always been extremely pleasant and helpful.  The range itself is comfortable and in great shape.  The rules are straight forward.  They have many targets available for purchase.  The "Battleship" target is my favorite which makes for some friendly competition.

The gun rental is also a nice option.  They have quite a variety from full autos to semi auto pistols.  The few guns I've rented work as advertised and are nice to try out.  The full auto AK was a blast to shoot and put a huge smile on my face.

I highly recommend this range!

I am not typically a believer in holistic/natural medicine, so I was a little skeptical.  After taking tons of Rx & OTC medicine to shake off a cold before vacation, I decided to see Dr. Evans since my co-workers rave about their experiences after the injections.  I made a same-day appointment easily online and, including paperwork and payment, the appointment was less than 10 minutes.  A half-hour after getting the Immune and Mega B shots I began to feel more energized and within an hour my sore throat, sniffles, and cough were almost completely gone.  Now, a day after, I am completely surprised at how great I feel and I have absolutely no lingering cold symptoms!  I only wish I would have gone to see Dr. Evans sooner!
I came here because of Groupon, I will return because of the food and friendly service. I had the sloppy roast beef and it lived up to its name:) I only wish they were open on Sundays.

One of Philly's great BYOBs.  Reasonably priced and excellent service!  Our waiter was very friendly and helpful in making suggestions.  The pasta is made fresh -- enjoy this authentic Italian restaurant.

You know how to tell if a place is great - is count the number of times a person from 200 miles away returns.  I nearly stop every chance I pass through St. Louis.  The hamburgers evidently received high marks among the city burger joints at one point back in the 90's.  A friend turned me on to the place and I've become a raving fan ever since.  It's simple - it's almost like a "Cheers" atmosphere.  Just a local friendly pub with great burgers and fries.  Give this a try.

Do not go here for ice cream unless you have a LOT of time and patience. If you want bubble tea you are good. I had the unfortunate experience of going with two bubble tea drinkers. We waited 1 hour for my ice cream after they got their bubble tea. With that said it was just okay. Watching the 3 people preparing the ice cream was excruciatingly painful. They moved as if they were wading through their own vat of ice cream. Drink bubble tea!!

This is where you take that special someone for an amazing meal! It is on the higher end of the scale as far a pricing goes, but certainly worth every penny (I mean, dollar)

Cam here for a work dinner (so luckily I didn't have to pay the bill). Between the 6 of us, we tried a large portion of the menu.

The outstanding:
pepper crusted steak (from the center of the menu, it's a combo), so juicy and flavorful!
roasted brussel sprouts (from the gotta have it section of the sides).. these are sooo amazing! even the person who doesn't like them, loved them.
twice baked stuffed potato (yes, it's a potato stuffed potato) and it is heavenly and large enough for two people to share.
steak diane (the fancy one where there is fire involved table side)
ice cream (made table side with liquid nitrogen)
lemon tart (it's tangy and perfect end to a rich meal)

The fabulous:
the lamb chops (I didn't taste this, so I cannot describe, but the co-worker was very happy)
shrimp scampi 

the bartender was delightful, and even created a special shooter for our team based on a couple ideas from the boss 

The wait staff was fabulous, she went along with our jokes, and helped us to push on one of the coworkers who couldnt make up his mind.. great teamwork! :)

I would definitely come back any chance I can (and have the extra cash to lay down) for this extravagant dinner.
This is the best car wash around. They are a little expensive but they always have a coupon on the website. They clean every nook and cranny even the trunk. I highly recommend it.

So glad I finally had a chance to visit this gem! Right now they have seasonal fall flavored drinks.  There is patio and indoor seating and enough space between the tables.  Service was good and the drinks were fantastic!  I had a caramel milkshake and hubby got the pumpkin latte both were excellent.  The latte was quite strong actually.  We also tried the caramel truffles which was very good. Will def come back!

This place was awesome! Dinner was absolutely amazing and we tried a little bit of everything. The staff was very friendly and had good insight on each beer, wine and food choice. The owner has rotating artist displaying  their pieces on the wall throughout the restaurant. We highly recommend this place

If you have not tried this place, you need to go. The menu changes with the seasons so if you're a regular, you don't end up eating the same. The food is supreme and the service is great. It's BYO now so bring libations of choose with you. Been coming here for about 15 years and it never disappoints. Keep an eye on their FB page by the same name for weekly specials.

This place is super super cute and I had no idea it was there!! Stopped in for my free coffee and left with a chocolate chip cookie and bacon and cheddar scone . Service is super awesome and I love the large table and window seating where you can get work done.. along with comfy chairs and outdoor space to relax after a hard day. I appreciated the host asking me for my milk preference as many times, I forget i'm lactose intolerant 

The pumpkin spice latte is  and I am not a PSL fan.... but omg.. thee hint of cinnamon... the flavors really pull throughout. Definitely a fall treat! Also: get the TREATS! Scone was sooo. buttery delicious and the chocolate chip cookie melted in my mouth. Cheaper and BETTER than any chain! PLUS, 100% sustainable.. what are you waiting for?!
Today i had the lamb chops with green and brown rice with a salad this food was so good and well seasoned.this place is a hidden gem if you visit Deleware you must try this food.

I found Sharon! Formerly of Reds Salon in Yardley/Morrisville Gaslight Village.  Experienced with all kinds of hair. I can always count on her knowledge and understanding to give me the cut and style that I am looking for. RELIABLE!!!

A favorite for brunch! Super crowded on weekends so we try to hit up during the weekdays when possible. Huevos rancheros is awesome and the bf is in love with the hot sauce it comes with. Pulled pork tacos get two thumbs up!  Seriously Yum!! Delicious coffee. Wonderful service! This place makes me happy :)

Please do not use this company! It was the worst grout and carpet cleaning experience ever. His cleaning machine left a 4 foot stain on my carpet from his machine and is refusing to pay to replace it. He left my floors soaking wet and did not replace my furniture and told me to mop it myself the next day. I had to hire another company to come and re-do all the grout because after Sunshine "cleaned it," it looks worse than it had before.  DO NOT USE!

It's an arcade, a restaurant, a bar... and it does them all pretty well if you're looking for that combination, but it gets expensive quickly.

They pride themselves on being "primarily an adult establishment" (no, not *that* kind); under 21ers must be accompanied by someone 25 or older, which may cut down the crowds, but sometimes it's pretty rowdy with kids playing games earlier in the evenings, despite the higher cost of food, drink, and gaming that probably exceeds their allowances.

Crazy Hibachi has been here so long. This used to be a Hometown Buffet.

The building is pretty spacious with lots of tables and booths. The buffet area is located in the middle of the restaurant. The inside doesn't feel clean and is sometimes humid.

I am not a fan of the food here. Not fresh and too oily, even for Chinese buffet standards. Also noticed the buffet selection has shrunk throughout the years. I would avoid the sushi at this place, but that's my general rule for Chinese buffets.

The service was basic. Workers came and refilled drinks and cleaned plates.

There are better options elsewhere for Chinese food in the area.

Mother's Day dinner was so good. I ordered flautas as I do at every Mexican restaurant that I try, theirs are excellent. We thought the salsa, carnitas and rice were yummy also. Excited to have a great choice so close by. Wish we had discovered them sooner.
This is a small joint.  The cleanliness is terrible and parking is terrible.  Food over all is average.  Not out of this world.  Staff are friendly.

Made a quick stop in here on a very rainy Saturday lunchtime. I missed out on the fruit Kolache because it was later in the day. I had their coffee, and we ordered the brisket taco and the  sausage, Turkey, and sweet cheese kolaches. They were all good, but I would definitely return for the super sweet fruit and cheese kolaches and the delicious brisket taco. Everyone was super sweet, friendly, and helpful with the menu items (none of us had ever had a Kolache before). Will definitely be back!  (But will be earlier so we can get the fruit Kolache.)
No seating inside, but there were a few outside tables.  Street parking only.

Wow, talk about amazing customer service! I dropped my car at Wheel Works and decided to take a walk on the trail next to it. I did not realize that Wheel Works closed at 5:00, and when I returned at 5:30 there was the guy sitting there holding my key in his hand with a smile on his face! I was mortified, but he was soooooooo kind! They now have a new customer for life! And then of course the car wash was terrific as well! Thanks Wheel Works!

Great Pizza awesome setting!!! Food was great along with the friendly staff. Places like this makes New Orleans so unique from anywhere else in the world! We had the Margherita pizza along with the fried Brussel sprouts as an appetizer. Everything was great and would highly recommend if in New Orleans.

DUCHESS ORDERS - DON'T STAY HERE...I'm so thankful I never have to sleep here again! I'm kinda embarrassed to be the duchess here! 

The walls are thin, the doors have a one inch gap between the floor and bottom of the door so you hear everything in the hallway. You also hear the neighbor snoring, sneezing, talking on the phone, you name it! I thought it was an issue with the connector door, nope its just they're too cheap to put any insulation in their walls! 

Not good for business travelers. They have one lamp with a plug in it and they have something plugged into it! There's barely any plugs in the room which is the most annoying thing when you're traveling for business. I have two cell phones and computer that needs plugging in! There's no plugs anywhere around the bed either, they need to purchase some of those lamps with one outlet on them and put them by the beds. This place is so outdated, the carpets are disgusting. The "renovated" rooms are a joke, they kept the same nasty carpet! 

Everything bad that could happen at a hotel has happened here on the 3 different occasions I have stayed here! Thank God I found out that this is not one of the hotels that our company prefers anymore so now I get to stay somewhere that I can actually get some sleep!! 

Here's some of my quick tip that sums up the hotel: 

This morning the shower head was facing out, not cool! I prefer my showers in the shower! Officially the worst hotel that I've stayed in!! 

Switched to a more "quiet" room and a worker this morning (6AM) is standing outside my door with their walked talkie on full blast, wtf? 

Ask for no connector door, you can hear everything including the alarm that goes off in the empty room at midnight! Not a fan of this place 

One desk person with a big line... This always happens here! 

One good perk: Found out you can work out at the Philadelphia Sports Club for free when you stay here!

This is one of my favorite restaurants. The Spaghetti and meat balls is terrific. Pizza is yummy. Calzone is very good. And I love their Greek salad. Never had a meal that wasn't great here.  Good service and the price is good.

Good service, good prices, but subpar seafood. I ordered one of the seafood tostadas and it was loaded with meat and had two tostada shells. It was basically two tostadas for the price of one! 

I was anticipating fresh, flavorful seafood and got the opposite. The seafood was dry and flavorless. It felt like pre-made seafood they just scooped onto a tostada and called it a day. The dish lacked color and you could tell before you ate it how old it was. I really want to like this place, and our waiter was fantastic, but I wouldn't return.

Perfect pastry! The kringle here is the real deal- totally authentic. We sampled a variety of pastries that were delicious. The staff was quite friendly. Our only complaint is with the cinnamon roll - believe it or not it was much too sweet. Nonetheless, we are looking forward to our next visit.

EDIT - This shop is INSIDE the mall on the NE corner of Plumb and S Virginia. There is no sign from the street but it is the same place as Tuesday Morning.

This skate shop embodies the true heart of skateboarding. If you want to experience what skating was like from the 80's to today, this is the shop for you. These guys have been skating since the 80's and this shop is an extension of their love for the sport, lifestyle and culture. No false representation or pretense here, they're the real deal.

They know all about the classics. They know the modern. They stock only the best. They stock new designs and reissues from only the best in the industry.

They know the area. They know the skateparks. They know what works for street, ramp, or pools. 

At my age of 41, my son has taken to skating. This has rekindled my love for the board and these guys get it. I felt welcomed back into the fold and this shop is where I will spend my time and money. They told me to bring my son in next time so they could meet him and welcome him. He's 8.

If you want to feel part of the skateboarding family, come to this shop, get to know them, embrace and love of the board. You will not be disappointed!
went for lunch. bottom of the earth house. had the navy bean soup and lentils on toast. pretty solid.

This funky flower shop meets home decor shot meets cappuccino cafe is a fabulous little place located at the beginning our our (very little) little Italy. Upon first walking through the doors of this beautiful shop, your nose is greeted with a wonderful aroma of flowers of all different varieties. The ones that really stood out for me were the orchids, but maybe that is because I love orchids. They really do have wonderful ones here. They have them in all different colors for about $30 a "set."

The other thing you are greeted with when you first walk in is the smell of fresh coffee. It may seem like an odd combination but it is deliriously wonderful. There is certainly something special about the smell of Italian coffee.

After your nose gets used to the wonderful smells wafting around this store, your eyes are enlightened by all the different colors and cool vases in the room. This place has some of the most unique vases I have ever seen in Edmonton. They also don't JUST carry vases. They also have tablecloths, napkins, wine racks, coffee mugs, pots, glasses, and tiny art pieces. Zocalo is filled with beautiful gifts for yourself and others.

I came here in the winter and saw that they also have a little patio outside, so I will definitely be checking it out come patio weather!

Prices are a little higher for some things, but I think the quality is also well matched so it is worth the extra penny.

Great octopus appetizer. Short rib entree good but not great.  Nice oyster selection and good martini - very dry.

Not worth the food poisoning we got though

Came here during the lunch hour rush and was still able to get seated right away. Everyone was very friendly, even though I was waiting for a bit before my friend arrived. I ordered the al pastor tacos and he ordered the mahi mahi. I can't eat deep fried fish for some reason, but he told me they were good and the al pastor weren't the best I've had but still good enough. I like the small side of oranges topped with spice. We also ordered the churros at the end--which were super freshly made and came with two dipping sauces. The spicy chocolate was a favorite, though I didn't taste much spice and the strawberry jalapeno one had a tad bit of kick to it but I felt like it could of had more.

Ok, if you need movers, these are your guys. I have used them twice now and they are on time, efficient, hard working, friendly and trustworthy. Just a great group of guys who make teamwork look effortless and demonstrate real Idaho customer service. Jimmy, John, Joe and Bryce moved me from my BIG storage room and a third story apartment and worked so very hard. To top it off, I have an antique Steinway piano that is so delicate and they put it together for me like pros and repositioned the monster several times. They took great care to ensure they didn't touch any of my new home walls and placed everything right where I needed it. I've never seen guys with these kinds of big muscles treat everything they touched so gingerly. Even had to send them ahead to the apartment after dropping the first load while I had to wait for deliveries at the new house. These guys are a blessing and will move you with care absolutely anywhere you need to go for a reasonable price. Thanks guys! You're the best!!

Elevation Coffee Roasters is a nice coffee shop, brand new to the Carrollwood area. The staff is extremely friendly and personable. The shop has a nice inviting vibe to it when you walk in, as it is tidy yet inviting.
    Elevation's coffee is delicious. Their roasts are perfectly blended, an excellent cup of coffee. As President Teddy Roosevelt said about some other roaster, "It's good til' the last drop." My Americano this morning was good til' the last drop and left my tastebuds begging for more. This is good coffee and would make TR take back his words and replace them with "Elevation Coffee Roasters - good til' the last drop."
      The food here is great too, I had one of their coffee cakes - absolutely delicious and freshly made. This went great with the Americano. I look forward to trying more of Elevation Coffee Roasters delights in the future.
       What I appreciated more than the coffee and cake ( and this was a lot) was the sense of community that this independent coffee shop has. Most coffee shops either have a "hipster" vibe or a "we're trying to be an alternative to Starbucks" vibe. Here people were meeting each other, studying, and reading. This is the perfect place to come with friends and enjoy a coffee.

Is it quick? Very. One degree less than lightning.
Cheap? Six bucks for lunch - I'd say so.
Lots of food? OH GOSH YES.
Token cute elderly Asian woman? Checkaroo.
...but is the food good?...

...not so much. The idea of take out Thai food is so great in theory but doesn't really work so well in practice (unless you are that place in Reading Terminal which is so good it doesn't make sense). The food tastes like it has been sitting there for a while (because it has). The pad thai noodles are soggier than I would like them to be, and because it is not made to order, everything is severely devoid of spice. The red curry was also quite bland, lacking in flavor with pretty low grade chicken and vegetables that did not taste fresh.

But if you don't put a ton of weight on the quality of your Thai food and just want a lot to eat RIGHT NOW, then Thai Singha House To Go is your spot.

I, personally, love a good salad bar.  Theirs is about as good as any that exist anymore.  They also make a decent soup.  The rest of the fare is pretty standard, but there is something for everyone.  The salad bar is the reason I head here.
We went on a Wednesday night and the service was slow..... but definitely worth the wait for the Thai curry wings.

Parking for the game is great. There isnt much going on outside of the stadium. There is a section where kids could play and also some shopping places. I went to the Eagles/Panthers game on Aug 14th. There were various promotions going on but not much entertainment besides a group of guys singing old songs on stage. 

I thought inside the stadium was pretty dull. It reminded me of a airport, grey and dark. I guess I was expecting it to be more like the Phillies stadium with status of players and more color. There was nothing to look at but more stores and places to eat. 

The field is wonderfully lit. I had 3rd row seats so I didnt have a problem seeing the players at all. It was my first football game so I was excited and so were the rest of the fans. Even though it rained, I had a great time. The fans were energetic throughout.
I was following this restaurant on instagram and finally got time to go there. Food was really really great love chicken Shawarma plate and loaded fries.
Really like the place but for what you get the prices are too high.  There is too much competition in media now for a ok butcexpensive breakfast.  Coffee could be better.

Cool ambiance. Lots of dogs at outside dining. Everything here tastes fresh, food came out quickly. Scrambled eggs were so good we ordered more. Swedish pancakes were a tasty alternative to crepes. I ordered the spicy chicken and hummus wrap and the chicken was incredibly tender. Perfect meal, attentive server. This is a definitely a GO!

Cool little spot in downtown Santa Barbara. Some interesting bars shops museums artwork wineries and Figueroa mountain brewery and a small distillery.Definitely come and check it out plus your two blocks from the beach.

FOOD IS AMAZING!!! The service is very, very kind and super attentive! My coffee cup was never even close to empty! The pancakes are huge, so only get one if you don't have a huge stomach. The French toast is also super good and so is the fried chicken.

The pizza was very good and the wings were also very good. We walked here from the condo we were staying in on vacation from Buffalo. Being from Buffalo we were curious how the wings would turn out, they were a good standard wing that we could get back at home. We got them hot which for most people would be hot but they were more on the medium side. We got our pizza with pepperoni, sausage and black olives and we enjoyed every slice. The service was surprisingly fast for being busy and short handed. The staff was on top of things making sure all were taken care of, they were all working great together as a team.

I came to the establishment at 4:34 and the employee told me that they only have sugar donuts because they close at 6:00 pm. What? then she said that they stop doing the donuts sauce at 5:00 pm again what the hell? They do not want to work? Sale donuts? Completely unacceptable and extremely disappointing!

Five star? Keeping the price and location in mind, yes! Flavor, spice, variety - all are here and well priced. Everything I had came with a good base seasoning, and a choice of salsa and two different hot sauces, you have the heat scale covered. 

This is my first time, but I shall become a regular if this is the standard. 

Oh, and their beans are not the typical bland fare. They have a really nice hearty flavor. 

I'll be back!
This place is a waist of time. From the ugly decor to the worst home fries and eggs I have ever been served. Do not waist time or money!

First  let  me  say ,  this  company  has  a Facebook rating  with  the  better BBC , second ,  they  towed my  Daughter's  car in a place  that  has a  4th hrs visitors  parking  pass   she  was  only  there  24 hrs.. billed  her  $155.00 then reduced  it to   $100.00. The  apartment  complex  said  they have towed several  they  should  not  have . They  will  not  return  phone  calls  neither  will  the  apartment  complex . This  does  not  speak  well  of either  company . I looked  up  the  rating at the Better  Business  Bureau.

My sister and I were in town for a half marathon. We were a little bit hungry when we're at the exhibition center and decided to find a place to eat and stumbled upon this hidden gem. 

Inside the café is quaint. Although there were quite a few people waiting outside, we are seated within 20 minutes.

We were expecting much, but we were surprisingly very impressed when we drink their coffee and ate their sandwiches. The first time we came here we got a avocado and turkey sandwich. It was very light and flavorful at the same time. The coffee had a very unique taste to it. It did not taste like burnt, bland diner coffee. 

We had such a great meal that we decide to come back again. This time we got biscuits and shrimp and grits (things as they are known for). You can definitely taste the butter and both dishes, without the dish feeling way too heavy.

If youre in town, 11/10 a great place to visit.

Nice atmosphere, great service,the food was outstanding. Highly recommend checking this restaurant out.
Thanks for a great evening.

They serviced MUC (squid)! I'm amazed! Winner over here. LOL

$10 per hour for the pool table. Decent food that they served and pretty quick service too! We also got a few buckets of beer and a bottle. Smoking is allowed inside. 

Overall, it was a chill experience.

Made a trip out to Villanova so we decided to give this place a shot.  Food actually not bad (ordered the pizza & a burger) but the service was just horrible.   Not very attentive, didn't have silverware when apps came out (calamari) just not a pleasant experience.  Just constantly had to wait for service. Would probably not return
My first time at the salon was earlier in the week.  David did a great job on my color and cut.  I will definitely be back!
Biscuits were stale, omelette didn't have much flavor, eggs Benedict were alright, just overall not super impressed.

A really nice addition to the Carmel arts and design district. The space is two levels with a bar, food vendors, and bathrooms on each level. Seating is first come first serve. It's kind of like a city market. You get your food/drink at a vendor station and grab an open seat or table. Sun king beers and merchandise  are also available for retail sale. I'm not a big booze hound so I will not speak to the spirits, however there is a large assortment of sun king beers on tap. The service was friendly and trying hard to keep up on a busy night. Despite the crowd, the tables and space overall remained clean. The bathrooms were also clean. We were in a large group and had a blast. 

The vendors include La Margarita(Mexican food), Pi Pizza, and Smoking Goose. The Goose also has retail sausages available, I highly recommend you pick up an andouille next time you make jambalaya or gumbo. 

If you are looking for a sit down and be served restaurant, then this is not the place you want to go to. However, if you just want a few drinks and a quick bite to eat, then check out this place.  Sun king spirits will only improve as the construction around the building is completed and the outdoor space is not overlooking bulldozers and gravel. Plenty of free parking in the area.

Superlative customer service! The entire operation was efficient, polite, and a delight to work with. They got me an appointment on the exact day I needed it, accompanied me to get my rental car to make sure that I had what I needed before taking mine to be repaired, and finished all the repairs in a matter of days. I would recommend this place to anyone.

Came here for restaurant week, and got the mussel appetizer, Branzino fish entre, and tiramisu. Overall very delicious, but I thought every course was a tad bit bland--I would have liked the mussels to be more acid, fish to be more savory, and tiramisu to be more... liquorific? Cappuccino was delicious. Recommended for BYOB!

First time here and we have a great time eating an awesome ice cream!
Santa Bárbara, thank you!
First time to Tucson. Found this place on Yelp and was not disappointed.  The food and service was great. Love the ambiance of the restaurant as well n

Dark meat on the rotisserie chicken is dry. How do you make dark meat dry? The "whipped potatoes" are definitely instant potatoes from a box, saffron rice doesn't have saffron in it- there's like a vague amount of flavoring and I can't even figure out what it is, and the spinach- man put some onions in it or something! Anything! Did you forget where your spice rack is? The prepared foods at Winn Dixie are better than this! And to top it off, your coke/soda ratio is off. You only get one star because they won't let me give you no stars.

Came in with a large group on a reservation so you'd expect there would be a plan to serve everyone.  Such was not the case.  Once food was ordered,  it took a full hour for us to start getting fed and then it trickled in over the next 20 minutes. When it arrived my mashed potatoes were luke warm and had likely sat in the kitchen for 15 minutes before coming out.  Waitress never returned to refill drinks. Food could have been great but execution was atrocious.  Service was beyond bad. Waitress had to come back and walk around the table and ask how many beers people had because she had no idea. 

Will not be back.

Posted this almost 30 minutes ago... Still waiting on checks.

Worst hair cut I've ever gotten. I walked in and showed the Stylist a picture of long hair with face framing layers and walked out with a shoulder length "U" hair cut that looked nothing like the picture, super short. At one point he said he was done and I had to mention I wanted layers. Zero regard for what the customer wants, worst hair experience of my life. Hope my hair grows fast. 

The other reviews were right, this place is bad. Only good part was the shampoo lady I guess.

Nasim and his team are seriously amazing. Not only is the process for inquiries about whatever yo u need painless and honest but it's quick. I was referred to Nasim's by my good friend who has been going to him for years. I got my brakes done for a great price, he gave me a great key ring when they were done( and I needed it) as well as vacuumed my car and offered me food while i waited. I came back for damage due to collision, perfect experience! Came back a third time with some bad luck, someone broke my side mirror, got a quote the very same day and it was just beyond the price of my deductible, not only did he fix it, do my inspection and my brakes, and make my car look gorgeous inside and out with a nice wash but he saved me hundreds of dollars. I literally could not stop referring him after that. Located on a quiet street in the North Philadelphia area closed to everything in the city, I will never go anywhere else for a repair.

First and foremost I wrote a review earlier this evening on their Facebook page and they deleted not only mine but the 4 other not satisfied reviews from other previous patrons!! They only left the TWO five star reviews! Shady!!! So guess I'll do a review here so it can't be deleted!
So gist of my review is we had a party of 6 people, almost nothing came out correct. The worst was we had chicken that was served still pink! We sent it back and when the "manager" came out to ask what had happened we were explaining all the things that came out incorrect and when explaining the color of the chicken, trying to keep the situation light, said it was the color of the ketchup. The manager then said "it's not that pink". Well, obviously it wasn't red, but are we really going to argue over just how pink chicken is? It shouldn't be pink at all! She said she saw the chicken back there and after talking to the chef, he told her he cooked it himself and it was fine. But when we asked her if she would have eaten it she dodged the question and started back pedaling as to whether she even saw it. After she left, another "manager" came up and tried smoothing things over, but honestly the last lady was so rude we were done. Only positive about the place was the bowling was fun. They did comp us a round and we enjoyed that. If you go, avoid the salmonella and stick to just the bowling!

Overall, it worked out for us despite having to pay the full price $299/night when we couldn't find a deal on Priceline due to the NCAA tourney games. It's been so long since we've paid full price.

The good: i enjoyed the breakfast and the evening happy hour. There were some lines for drinks, but not bad for a free drink. i could do one of their omelets every morning (jalapeno, mushroom, ham, and cheese...delish).
The location worked for us. We were in town for the NCAA games at New Orleans Arena. It's a walkable distance (about a mile or about 20 minutes) to both the arena and the French Quarter. There are a few good eateries in the area like Lucy's and Mulates. Decent view as we walked out our door seeing New Orleans.


The bad: i thought some of the things in the room could use some updating, improving. Our shower head kind of sprayed the ceiling and dripped from there. The doorknobs were old looking and loose, and the bathroom door didn't lock.

It pains me to write this review, but I need to give other families a heads up because our family was allowed to adopt an aggressive 2-year-old  "retriever mix" from down south. When we met the dog he was very calm and resting in his cage, beautiful red fur. In the beginning, when he was weaker, he was manageable. But as we treated him for worms and fed him, he grew stronger (a good thing). Then we took him to training, 5 group lessons and a private lesson. I thought we could fix his jumping, lunging and what I thought was play-biting for attention. I truly think the dog had two personalities. After he jumped at my son's face and daughter's neck (after 6 training classes and taking calming treats), bit at my ankles and hands, we sadly had to return him. I took him to the vet, muzzled, for part 2 of his Lyme vaccine and the vet told me they had to label him as aggressive in a database.. This was very difficult emotionally, bonding to a dog that had a different personality than when in his kennel at the spca. One last thing, study the markings and facial features of dogs before you go to the spca. Though labeled "retriever mix," the dog had Doberman body markings and a Pitbull mouth.

We went the the National WWII Museum with our family.  The museum exceeded our expectations. Our sons are history buffs and this museum did not disappoint.  All the various exhibits and artifacts of the different campaigns of the war were very well laid out and very informative.  The guides were exceptional and very informative.  Our guide Gale put everyone at ease and was very helpful and informative.  We talked to a WWII veteran, Leo who is a great storyteller.  We learned about his life and the man he is.  Kudos to all the volunteers and staff working at the museum!
Late night buffet gone, not the same pizza, wings, place, or even location anymore. This place is cheesy looking now and after 10p.m. no one under 30 is allowed in, this place sold out hard.

My cousin wine and dined me at Zaytoon on my trip to Santa Barbara, and it was fabulous, darling! We splurged and ordered the chicken schwarma, taziki, labneh, cheese sambosik, haroumi cheese, and batata hara. You MUST get the batata harara and the cheese sambosik. I was obsessed with both. The chicken schwarma was unlike any schwarma I'd ever had, probably because it is Lebanese. It came with a garlic spread and a salad, both of which were great and complemented the meat well. Nothing was disappointing. The ingredients were fresh, the dishes were healthy, and the food was delicious with that beautiful exotic kick. 

The restaurant is also especially gorgeous - there's lights and trees all over, with fire pits in the middle of the bigger tables. Perfect for dates, family gatherings, friend hangs... really anything. And don't forget about the hookahs available too! 

If you're in SB, make sure to dine at Zaytoon!

Love visiting Dunedin and the shops and restaurants.  Was there a couple of Sunday's ago and was craving breakfast. Was too late in the day, so figured I'd try for coffee and some pastries.  Passed by Cappuccino's and read the "Worlds Greatest Coffee" claim on their signage. Entered the shop; very cute.  Asked for a large coffee and asked if they had any bakery goods.  The employee snapped back, only coffee, nothing else, no bakery stuff. Employee went to a back room, returned with a large coffee in a Styrofoam cup, and snarled $3.75.  I thought, for the price, this must be great coffee.  Pulled out my credit card and was told - no credit cards, just cash.  Was thinking that I should politely decline the purchase, but I continued.  Searched my wallet for money, gave her cash, and asked for a receipt.  No, no receipt. Turned around, dressed up the coffee with cream and sugar.  Said good-bye and left.  This is absolutely not the "worlds best coffee" and was so bad that it made instant coffee taste good.  The coffee was as bad as the customer service.  No credit cards, cash only, $3.75 for a Styrofoam cup of coffee, no receipt - hmmm - you can guess what happened here.  Won't return to Cappuccino's.  Hope you have a better experience.

I am now in the best shape of my life thanks to Malcolm!!  I came to Malcolm to help me lose weight and tone up before my wedding.  He spoke with me about my wants, needs, hinderances, and advantages and customized a personal workout.  Since then, he has tweaked these workouts to hone in on what I want and need the most.  Furthermore, he is very accommodating; he is flexible with my often busy schedule, but he never lets me get away too long without checking in and pushing me to work out.  He has also helped me with nutrition, coaching me on proper meals and portions, even providing recipe ideas! Probably for the first time in my life (and I'm 30), when I look in the mirror I am happy.  Malcolm has become a good friend and he is an EXCELLENT trainer.  I highly recommend Malcolm for all fitness needs and levels!

We came here with a larger group for ISC and still managed to have good service. Our drinks were always filled, the bread came out warm, and there was no awkward pause in between courses.  The mood was friendly and light throughout the entire restaurant.  I had the risotto with lobster and prawns. The risotto included corn, spinach, and tomatoes. It was cooked perfectly. Great flavor, great texture. My only complaint would be about the awkwardness in regards to removing the shells. I wish they would have done it for me in the kitchen and added back into the shell (for presentation of course). Their menu is very decently priced unless you get a special...mine was $25 but my boyfriend's lasagna was only $12. My wine was $9 and it was one of the lower priced options. All in all I would recommend this place to anyone to loves Italian or just looking for a new spot for old friends. We will be going back and bringing company with us as well!

A friend of mines recommended this spot to catch a Saints vs. Chiefs game. We arrived about 30 minutes before game time. They were already at capacity and starting to seat folks in their outdoor overflow section. Which was set-up comfortably, I'll give them that. 

My friend and I were able to snag a seat right near the TV. It was a decent size for the space. The waiter was on point, food and drinks were good + it's dog friendly! 

The Saints fans kept it classy and we also ran into local Chiefs fans :)
Best ice cream so far in Philadelphia. They offer a lot of flavors. Large cup with 3 flavors is about $7

Nice spot.  Spent the better part of the day here drinking and watching baseball.  Service was excellent - very attentive, friendly - food was good with a solid selection, as well.

What makes this bar 5-star: first - a full listing when you get in of what games will be on which of their 20-some-odd TVs.  Nice - sets your strategy fro the whole day if this is what you are planning.

Second:  in the bar area, there are high tables w/ checker boards imbedded in them. Nice - a little checkers and beer - now we are talking.  The full fifth-star comes in when you get bored w/ checkers.  We were screwing around w/ the checkers for like 2 hours - rolling them across the bar, shooting them at eachother...the only reaction we got from the staff was laughter.  We then looked at the other checker area, and the guys over thee had covered their whole table with in order to make the checkers slide better...excellent.
I'm not normally a chain pizza kinda gal, but decided to try this place because of a groupon j found.  We had the deep dish Detroit pizza and it was so yummy and greasy!  We will be back!

Memo to the residents of Tampa: We need to make this thing work.

Urban mixed-use development only works if an entire community buys in, and this area was designed to integrate a posh, upscale shopping district with a real neighborhood where people live. It's just beautiful, and sticks out architecturally from everything else around it. 

They also do a great job with events like art festivals, and their famous Hyde Park Music Series the last Wednesday of every month over the summer. It's also as dog friendly as the SPCA, and there are plenty of great dining options like Irish 31, Timpano's, and even CineBistro if a movie is what you seek while eating. Plus with two free parking garages, the usual parking chores of South Tampa really aren't an issue at all. 

So why only 4 stars? Because so much of the area sits vacant because of the outrageous rents being charged to potential tenants. It seems like they've started to give a bit of ground on this front, but it's hard to encourage people to come walk around when so much retail space sits unused, and it devalues all the folks already there. There are multiple shops and restaurants opening currently, so let's hope this great space gets to 100% occupancy sooner rather than later. It's a true treasure, but needs to be filled with shops, and supported by the community.

The only reason I went here is because this is where Home Depot orders go through and I only needed a very small piece and home depot has a $500 minimum. They are clueless and horrible in every way shape and form. It's taken 4 visits and at least 6 hours of time between myself, my husband and our contractor to try and get a lousy $200 piece of granite.   

STAY AWAY
Great old southern BBQ. Had thee brisket with beans and vinegar based slaw very good. My wife had donkey Ed turkey tho and said very dry. Still a good place tHose a smokers are doing their job...

Thanks to one very convincing review here, we made our decision to finally come here for lunch one day.  Why did I wait so long to try Viviano's?  I have missed out for so long, but I intend to make up for lost time.

Everything I have tried has been great.  The salads, The Festa, the pizzas, but one sandwich and one pasta stood out.  Go there on a Tuesday for their Reuben Tuesday.  It will blow you away.  The bread, the kraut, and the melt in your mouth corned beef.  It is amazing, probably the best Reuben I have ever eaten.

The Tortellini is surprisingly one of the best I have ever tried.  I didn't have high expectations for it, but wow, very fresh and flavorful.  It isn't a gooey cheesy mess, but rather a rich cream sauce.  

So the food here is just amazing, one of the best Italian places (for the price) I have ever eaten.  Thank God I live so close, I will be going here often.
Very nice people and they do a great job! Reasonable prices. Took my husbands dress shirts and they came out great.

Absolutely amazing!  The jerk chicken was to die for. My husband had the schnitzelwich. The pork was the best and most unbelievable flavor. Our friend had the cod and grits...I'm from the south and we know about fish and grits...it was crazy good!!!  5 stars hands down!

The food was awful. The wings were mushy and not nearly enough sauce. The sicilian pizza was cold in spots and not cooled off but cold and the crust was stale. Like it was a day old. Delivery took over an hour. If i didn't have company i would of returned dinner and addled for my money back.  I ended up throwing away more than half of dinner and $65
Slow service, food was typical. Very slow. Wrong order and wrong drinks. It took nearly two hours to get in, seated and check paid.
I own a pizza shop in another town and I love this place.  Great skill and care taken with my pie.
My fav sushi place in Newark. I like to sit at the sushi bar. They know you. Friendly. Quick service. Quality sushi and clean. This is the place to go for sushi.

I am a native Philadelphian and LOVE pho, especially when it is cold or as comfort food. Since I do not want to drive 30-45 min down to Philly for the pho I decided to try this place since I am now situated in langhorne. Boy was it disappointing! Since basil is in its name, I would think they would at least give you more than two sprigs of it! The bowl was smaller than I am accustomed to from Philly pho bowls and the meat was chewy and not tender. The broth was also lacking. Sadly to say but I will not return.

Heard a lot of great thing about this place and it really lived up to everything! 

We called ahead to get our pie for pick up. I would recommend this if you're just looking to experience the pizza! It only took about 20 minutes! For to go orders you go to a small shop where you can order slices or whole pies. There is parking in the back but you have to pay (another reason why pick up is great, one person can stay in the car and another go in!) 

It was pretty packed in the shop when we picked up on a Saturday evening. The service was quick and really friendly as well! 

As for the pizza...I can barely explain it, it's just SO good! When I try to isolate the parts...the crust is pretty mediocre and the toppings aren't anything special the pizza is soooo above average! It maybe is the sauce?!? Really not sure but it's working!!! 

It's also pretty fairly priced about the average price for pizza!

I love Sassy Nails!! They are very affordably priced and they do an amazing job. I have tried many places in town closer to my house and I am always disappointed so I keep coming back to Sassy Nails. Everyone that works there is great, Angie is my favorite, she is so sweet and does great pedis. They can do great nail art, I have brought in pictures from Pinterest and they have duplicated them. They have 5 big massage chairs for pedis so it's fun to bring your girlfriends along. They are always busy but if I'm in the area they can usually squeeze me right in as a walk up.

Hard to pronounce, but easy to eat and love! At least for me ;)

A winner in my cookbook. From the moment my family and I walked in, we were courteously greeted by the hostess and soon after our waiters, (there was also a waiter who promptly served us water before our main waiter arrived) and there were no bad vibes from start to end. I called a few days in advance to reserve a table for seven guests for brunch and they were able to happily accommodate us! We were pleased with the private room they offered. With three kids under 10 in our party, we felt comfortable and safe in the roomy space. 

I will vouch (and gladly eat again) for the:
1.) Eggs Atchafalaya with buttered crawfish tails
2.) Duck Hash
3.) Oyster Dressing Benedict
4.) Praline Waffle

Overall, a wonderful and memorable experience for my family and me!
SLOW AS SHI*. They couldn't be more curteous and friendly. But they need to hire more than one person to take care of all car problems. Took 30 Minutes To Tell the Lady I Needed an Oil changed.

So, being the healthy, financially responsible person that I am, I often pack my lunch at work. Some of my coworkers don't always pack theirs, which is perfectly fine. Being the great coworker and teammate that I am, I occasionally like to join my coworkers when they go out for lunch. I believe it is great for commraderie, and it's nice to get out of the office for a bit. Recently, I joined my coworkers at this cafe for lunch. This was my first time here. Upon entering and seeing the menu, I thought "oh, this place looks great. I'm definitly bringing my family back here this weekend". I proceeded to sit at a table with my lunch, while my paying customer coworker ordered his food. The tables were all empty at lunch time, which should have been a warning sign. There was maybe one other customer there.  A gentleman, whom I believed to be the manager approached me. He seemed very angry that I was there, and had a very rude tone with me. He told me I had to leave. He actually said "I'm trying to run a business here, this isn't a cafeteria. You need to leave". I apologized and explained to him that I was there with my coworker who had just ordered food. He again said, now seemingly and unjustifiably angry "this isn't a cafeteria", as if I didn't already know that. I said ok. My coworker packed his food to go, since he had already paid, and we left. Needless to say, I will not be returning to this place, and I will be letting coworkers, family, and friends know the same thing. It's no wonder this place was empty at lunch time if this is the way he "runs his business". Side note: my coworker said the sandwich was small and overpriced. He will also not be returning.
One of my favorite boutiques in Nashville! A nice, constantly changing selection of men's and women's clothes and accessories as well as homeware. A nice range of prices too - highly recommend.
Great service, attentive hosts but they have the most ugliest buffalo wings. Yes, wings can be ugly....Red Lobster proved it!

I love Coffee Bean normally, don't get me wrong, but the service here was nothing to write home about.  Service was slow, even though there were only 2-3 people ahead of me.  Also, the store itself is pretty small, so don't try to study or hang out here.

The lentil soup was bland and not worth it, but the rest of the meal was quite good! I had the fish masala, and while it was a bit sweeter than what I'm used to, it was still incredibly good. Oh, and their "spicy" level is by no means spicy.

We just moved from Hawaii in October of 2008 and bought a car from Carvision. It was a 2004 Chevy Trailblazer that nickeled and dimed us from the moment we bought it until the we traded it in to somewhere else. We  needed brakes a month after buying it, brakes were bad, they should have been new when we bought it. We needed a new transmission, transfer case, AC compressor, all the expensive parts. Everything broke down. I see there is new management and I am looking for a new car for me back and forth to work but just leary to try them again. Do not want to get screwed over again.

I tried this place a couple years ago and it was pretty good. Tried it again tonight and it was better!  It seems they're always on Groupon, so check if you decide to go. Could save you a little $$.  https://blainerestaurantreport.wordpress.com/2018/07/07/sushi-village-metairie-la-report-108/

Rather having lunch from the conference buffet, I thought I would abandon that ship and sail off on Amuse. This single experience at Amuse made me want to go back and try everything.

After placing my order, the server starts me off with their Chef's Welcome Palate Cleanser, sliced radish with supposedly salted butter. They could have saved this and would have received a five-star rating on the sandwich. This was a disappointment. I don't believe it offered any kind of cleanser. Presentation was nice, but it was just a raw radish slice with unsalted butter. Yikes.

The Pulled Pork Sandwich gets five star, no questions asked. The pork is deliciously marinated and extremely tender, served on a brioche bun. It doesn't have any of those fried onion strips that other places use to add texture. They use house made giardinera vegetables that are in a vinegar marinade.  These vegetables alone make the pork and the whole sandwich sing. You'll take a bite and you'll get the perfect marriage of vinegar crunch from the vegetables to the bbq tender pork. Seriously, I wish this sandwich could come in a foot long version, I would eat the whole thing.

The sandwich is served with Pommes Frites. Although presented attractively and spiced adequately, they were a bit soggy. Either they sat in the back, it wasn't cooked to order, or under cooked, they didn't have a nice crunchy outside. Too bad, after the sandwich, I had extremely high hopes for everything else.

I had lunch today at the Dale Mabry location . fries were cold and undercooked and i think the burger patties we're frozen, bun was soggy and the beef was undercooked. Was not happy. I might try again, but seriously dont think so......

Tried this place because it had good ratings and reviews but it's way overrated. 
The crust had no crisp nor was it golden. My favorite part of eating pizza is the first slice because of the crispy bottom crust...Black Rock Pizza was limp and warm with the crust tasting under cooked. Not mention served on a freaken plate. The toppings were somewhat gourmet but but sounded undesirable.

This was probably the most hole in the wall type restaurant I've ever seen. My partner and I ordered alligator bites for our appetizer, the blackened duck, and the red fish for dinner. The alcoholic cocktails  are served with dry ice making them look awesome and feel consistently cold. There's nothing bad to order here. 

The paintings and hangings around the restaurant made it feel incredibly authentic and filled with culture. Our waitress was helpful in deciphering my hesitancy with fish. She also came several times to check in on us even when we were seated in the corner.

Would highly recommend this place. It's close to Bourbon street without all the noise. After dinner, only you could decide to go right to Bourbon or left to the hotel.

Checked in approximately at 3pm, the Starbucks was closed, gift shop/convenience store seems to be independently run, shelves were empty (had cheap flea market kid toys) with a bunch of open floor space.  2 restaurants-one is overpriced with a view, the other was closed(only open for breakfast & lunch). 
Now for the BEST part!!!!!
The SEX in the next room. Uh hello dumb sh-t, not everyone needs to advertise their junk.  The room was separated by a door but there was an inch gap you could feel a breeze through.  Apparently someone or something in there had her hacking all night too.  
I did call down to the desk, they said security would go right up but when that didn't work I had to go down in person and request a room change at 11:30 at night.  
GET RID OF THE SHARED DOOR THAT SEPARATES THE ROOMS.  They're paper thin. 
This was a waste, I thought I was staying in an upscale property.

Tried this place for lunch. The specials are reasonably priced with an average of 9.00 that comes with 2 sizes. I chose chicken pad Thai with salad and egg roll.. I was surprised to find the salad dressing was strong with a cinnamon bite to it. The egg roll very tiny but good ! My lunch date had the chicken fried rice with soup ( chicken rice) and an egg roll. Food overall tasted fresh and hot!!! I would recommend for lunch definitely!!

Score!!! Finally coffee beans that are not burnt!!! What a treat!!  Thomas made me an iced latte with oat milk that was amazing.  I had to try a lemon cupcake too.  What a treat!! Can't wait to come back!

Great pedicure! Walked in and they seated me ASAP. Had great color choices. The guy that took care of me spent so much time on my feet, it was great. He also kept great convo with me the whole time! I will be back for more!!

First of all the coffee is so addicting, I got the horchata cold brew and it was perfectly sweet, and I got the corn tamales since I don't eat meat and it was bomb as well as a good value plus the employees were super sweet and helpful ! Green Tamale Bowl

Rumor has it that this location will permanently close tomorrow, January 26. I am a lunchtime regular at this location and to me that is bad news.  The food has always been good and the staff equally wonderful and attentive. That will leave no Boston Market locations in Tucson. They will be missed.

It's been quite a while since I've visited this theater, which is by far the closest to home for me.  I was pleasantly surprised on this visit, as it was the first time I'd been since they redid the whole theater some time ago.  Much to my surprise they have reserved seating like some more pricier theaters and the seats even have electronic recliners, which is a great touch.
