The overall movie watching experience was great here and I'll definitely be back in the future.

Wish there was a 3.5 option! Heavy on the small town bar feel, this is the joint your family's been going to for generations! Even for an out of towner, very friendly service. Just not quite the kind of joint for  a mid-20s dude from out of town. Food was average bar fare, but if you're planning on eating  or watching a game get the wings, and ask for naked. Everything is better naked. Usually.

Oh, and the men's bathroom was clean. Impressive.

A great course, ALMOST! (I played the General's course)

The course was in, overall, REALLY good shape. The greens were fast but true. Fairways were very well taken care of and the rough (which I saw a LOT of) was good, although "sponge-y" in places. A couple of tough lies from errant shots, but hey, that's my fault.

The practice area was fantastic. It sure was nice to have solid turf as well as a few different areas to work on your game.

Inside was very nice. Pro-shop staff was professional and courteous. As a single, they put me with a group and the starter was great, introducing all of the people in the group. A nice touch.

Reasonable, and very tasty lunch in the clubhouse was a salad and soft drink. 

The only issue: lack of on-course amenities. There were a couple of events, so perhaps the beverage cart was near them, but in 90+ degree heat, a bev cart was sorely missed. So were cart towels, sand/seed mix, and club washers on the cart. GPS was not to the pin, and seemed to be "off." No Marshall out there to keep the (slow) pace of play.

That all said: it's nit-pickey. I'd rather NOT have that with all of the positives I mentioned. The course was fair, the green complexes GINORMOUS, and conditions were great. If in the Nashville area again, I would definitely play again and strongly recommend to all golfers.
Just okay! A bit pricey and bland. Great customer service. Neat building with a lot history, glad they restored it and tried to keep its original structure and fixtures.

Our first time at this Italian restaurant in Belmont Hills, PA but not the last. Menu is your expected offerings. 
Milan Salad, ziti & Asparagus and a couple more 'Jimmy's Milan' & Mel's' classics. 
Some nice basic appetizers in the contemporary styled room with oversize tables. 
The stuffed Hot Peppers were good and spicy. Mussels, clams, meatballs, sausage, grilled artichoke heart and all good appetizers. 
Milan Salad and a couple other salads.
Entrees include pasta, fish dishes, chicken and several pasta dishes. Selections galore with dependable service attendants. The chicken and veal cutlets were wonderful. The Chicken Parmesan was, as expected, nicely made. Thinly sliced chicken is old school South Philly style...thin chicken beautifully breaded. Of course the veal and chicken available as Marsala's and Picante. Both were very good. Portions fine. 
A few well prepared fish dishes. Like rest of menu the food is prepared in a basic and non-concocted fusion or reinvented dishes.
A few sauces abuvegetahave.s spaghetti sauce too sweet but vegetable's. Spicey' sauce is attention getting. The usual vegetables.
We didn't have dessert.
BYBOB is fine since they had ample setups. 
Service fine by friendly and competent staff. 
We enjoyed all including bread.
Make reservation and enjoy.
The place is clean and friendly (even when it's pretty darn late). I had breakfast food and it was really great! Everything was flavorful and cooked exactly as I asked. Highly recommended.

My experience at spa Atlantis today was one of the most relaxing times I've had when receiving a service. Sami is very skilled at what she does and pleasant. I would return to her for a facial and recommend her to anyone. The spa was very clean and quiet and safe considering these troubling times.

Although it is too far away from where we need to be, this public storage facility provides good prices, good service, and the one and only Nikki in the office who is truly the icing on the cake!  She is hilarious and outgoing -- a Nashville treasure.

After going to Nail Dior in Odessa and having a terrible experience with them me and my bridal party headed to this place and they took us all in!  They're phenomenal I 100% recommendation this place!

My son is attending to Tulane University and I was looking for a nail salon close to campus and Cindy's was recommended. It is everything I was told and more! The service is friendly, attentive and refreshingly appreciative. They offer you a beverage, assist in choosing colors and are very committed to attention to detail. As a bonus, a regular mani/pedi is just $38! The massage chairs are the best I've ever enjoyed. All in all, a great find and my new home for nails in New Orleans for the next four years!
Old food stale and nasty  food small portion steak not worth it don't eat here at all

Mrs. Williams and Gary gave me some advice that I will remember for a lifetime. They helped me overcome my fear of being behind the wheel of a vehicle, and simplified things so that I could easily learn the mechanics of driving. Kudos to this wonderful driving school.

The employees are always rude and treat you like thieves. I went in Walgreens one night in July 2019 after I got off from work and there were two employees standing in front of the store as if they were the police guarding the entrance and they demanded me to tell them what I came in there to buy. What I came in the store to buy was none of there business and they had no right to ask me that I could have been getting tampons or yeast infection medication for all they knew. Their excuse was that half of the store was blocked off and they said that's why they had to ask, but that was bull shit. When the finally unblocked the entrance I heard them talking about how someone recently stole out of the store. So they basically try to control who comes in and out the the store by violating customers rights and blocking people from coming in to avoid thieves. I have had people follow me around at night when I've gotten off from work at other(now closed down) locations. No wonder why a lot of the 24 hour Walgreens are no longer 24 hours or have closed down completely. Look at how the employees treat the customers. Again, these types of incidents always happen to me in the middle of the night or the wee hours of the morning at Walgreens in the New Orleans area. I strongly don't recommend this location or going to any Walgreens location in the New Orleans area.
Delicious! One star knocked off due to limited seating & lack of juice options. The turkey fig crepe was phenomenal & the coffees are prepared with care. Friendly staff!

Anthony was professional from start to finish and did a great job on the glass in our two remodeled bathrooms. He is honest and delivers on his word. I'd use him again and recommend him to anyone looking for glass cutting and installation.

I was recommended to this place by a family friend. My boyfriend and I came here not knowing what to expect except for good Mexican seafood. It was a Tuesday during happy hour. Jackelyn was our server and was very kind and responsive to my requests. When we ordered something off the menu that we had misinterpreted, the staff had no problem customizing our dish to our liking. All staff was very friendly and personable. I would recommend this place to anyone looking for good food and service with a smile!

Spot on. Food is delicious, price is reasonable, and we are always full and happy. 


Personal favorites are paper wrapped chicken, chicken and broccoli and the chow mein.

I had a serious hankering for some pho before we hit the road back home to Louisville. I looked this place up and we were SO happy with our decision. 

First, the ambience is super chill in there. They were burning incense (which may bother people who are sensitive to smells--keep that in mind) and there was music softly playing in the background. Our server was so friendly, and I heard him accommodating someone with food allergies at the table next to us.

I got the Pho Tai and it was perfect. I don't think I'd change anything about it. It was the kind of satisfying where you get to the end of the bowl and a deeply felt sadness sets in. If I lived in St. Louis, I'd probably be here weekly. Maybe more than weekly?

Really nice. I've been to there location in Wayne. This is in the center of town. It's cute , the servers are cute, the menus is cute. I really liked it although it's on the very high side. I got an excellent ice coffee and Two biscottis . All excellent but like $11 plus tip. Oh well the servers were super nice, and I don't mind paying for quality and a really warm experience. Absolutely will go back if for nothing then the great attitude. Smiles count, people forget that with masks on.

I finally decided to dip my first toe into the Botox and filler world and went in wanting the most minimal changes.  I was feeling sunken in around my eyes (I am 33) and lips so thin, but am not into plumping too much.  Dr. Lloyd did a perfect job and I waited even 5 months to leave a review because I wanted to see how it settled in.  I received Botox in my forehead and sides of my eyes and filler under and around my eyes and in my lips.  I honestly love how it looks so much in pictures and it was the perfect little touch I wanted.  Thank you for doing A LOT by doing a little.

My bride and I have visited this Red Lobster location many times. There are times that we are thrilled to be here and are treated with the utmost dignity and respect. Unfortunately, today was not one of those days. We were NOT thrilled to be seated in the bar area - we would have preferred the dining area but were not asked about our preference - this after waiting for the hostess for over 5 minutes in a NON-busy lunch-time arrival. The food was the typical good servings, but the service was lacking. It was as if, since I was using a coupon for a free birthday option, that our server felt that the tip would be small and her attention to us was not needed. I tip based on the service and the size of the check PRIOR to any coupons/discounts, etc. We enjoyed the food and each other's company, but that is all.

Pinefish's happy hour is great! You get some awesome deals for food and drinks. You also have buck a shuck oysters, which are great. The best thing is that their happy hour still happens on weekends! 

We ordered a variety of small plates. My favorites were the truffle mashed potatoes, brussel sprouts, and calamari. We also had their chocolate cake for dessert, which was great!

Don't come unless you'd like to stay for an hour or more! 30 minutes in line, 30+ minutes to get our shakes. I got an cotton candy ice cream cone, and there was nothing remarkable about it. The ice cream is not creamy- it's not super soft, and the ice pieces make my tongue feel like it had rug burn, lol. Everything is expensive, and not worth the price for mediocre shakes and ice cream. It's supposedly good for an instagram photo op, but my boyfriend's shake looked like somebody just spilled half the shake down the sides of the glass. He asked for a to go cup and never got it. Will not return.

Two small plates filled me up! Surprisingly good.  Had the salmon which was combined with interesting attendents. Including being on a potato chip. Mmmmm. Tasted my dinner mate's spedini and it was tender and perfectly seasoned.

Fine place to eat!  We stopped for lunch on the way thru St Louis and really liked everything we ordered: delicious cornbread in a skillet with honey butter,  fantastic smoked meatloaf, my grandson loved his smothered fries (pulled pork, onions, cheese sauce), excellent pulled pork sandwich, tasty mac & cheese. An etch a sketch provided for the kids. Nice atmosphere. Highly recommended.
Delicious fresh Vietnamese in the heart of NOLA. Be sure to try their pork dumplings and their delicious duck 'cole-slaw' (their term- not mine!).

You want to get away from the French Quarters and have some great food and drinks,  this is definitely the place.  We had the tacos and wow they were good.  We took the Trolley from the Quarters and we were here in no time. Well worth the ride up.  Certainly more family oriented than the craziness of the French Quarter's.

Came here with a decent sized party of almost 20 on a Saturday evening, boy will I never do that again.  My brother got there at 5:30pm to put his name on the wait list and was told that it would be an hour.  We were taken to our bay at 8:00pm.  I kid you not!! This is not an hour.  If you plan on doing something at Top Golf please know that they get EXTREMELY BUSY.  Pricing also depends on what time you go.  The bays at the time we were there ran $45 per hour, but 6 people can play and share, which isn't too bad.  The waiter and manager were very attentive. Since we waited 2.5 hours, we were there for a few hours so we decided to get some food and drinks.  The prices are not too bad considering the environment that you're in.  Since I'm trying to be healthy, I went with their turkey wrap which was full of turkey and yummy avocado -- with a side of fries, haha.  Top Golf has 3 levels and we were in the second one, all levels really look the same, so to me there was no preference when it came to which we were in.  Also as an FYI, there is a $5 ONE TIME fee to get a card that allows you to golf. This keeps track of your history should you decide to come back, you can just keep using it.  It's a great environment; however, it was so busy that there was almost no walking room.  The bays themselves were clean and had enough clubs to use, no matter if you're a righty or a lefty.  The bathrooms were also clean and good sized...and they had a changing table.  That's a bonus.  Of course, there was a girl that was "sick" from all the alcohol she drank in there, but staff actually handled that.  They waited while she was done to escort her and her friend out.  Parking lot was really big and even with the amount of people there, it wasn't too hard to find a spot.  Overall I'd definitely come again, but it'd plan for a time when it isn't so busy.  I've heard that you can reserve the bay for $50, but you don't get this back nor does it apply to your bay fee.

Going to bed hungry. . I usually like this place...for their ala carte sushi. Tonight we were more adventurous but our sushi order was incomplete and the 
shrimp Pad Thai we wanted to try had an odor and seemed as though it had been sitting a very, very long time. I only had a bite and hope I don't get sick.

Went in for the first time and was in search of jeans. I currently wear a size 12 and have a curvy shape, which I mention because consignment can be more difficult for folks with a body type like mine. They had many options for me to try on and great brand recommendations that I hadn't already been familiar with. I quickly found two that fit great, match my style, seem high quality, and were so much less expensive than my usual retail finds are.

I was also very impressed with their selection of tops, blouses, sweaters, and coats. I had great luck with these, too, including a gorgeous cashmere/wool blend coat that was priced very reasonably.

I am so glad to have found this place and will definitely be back!

So I go in to the Wawa to get a couple of Sizli's and some altoids the bill was a little bit more than I thought, I looked at the price for them and I was charged $2.59 when the price says $1.19. I tell the cashier no problem (rushing to work) so I had no time to argue, but I do tell him to change the price because someone may lose it.
So when I go back there a couple days later what do I see the same incorrect price.

Two stars EARNED.

We no longer needed the reservation and canceled way in advance of the 7 days required on their website, back on January 3rd. If you need to cancel with the Montecito Inn, please ensure that you receive a cancellation number from the agent you speak with, prior to expecting an email, as they did not send us an email and we are now on the hook, on only for the deposit, but also penalty for "no show, the nights' reservations, taxes, and more [ed. they charge a fee to park, not clear if they will refund this or keep it, too]. I have sent evidence to the manager from our system, but they request the information they did not provide in the first place, which has left me in a very tough spot. Please perform your due diligence with the Montecito Inn or there are other hotels are nearby with whom we have not had a problem.

Added after reply from Jason:

Dear Jason, when one calls the Montecito Inn to cancel ones reservation, the agent confirms that your cancellation number is your confirmation number, I have provided this number back to you. If your agent took no further action, i.e. generating the "actual" cancelation number, logging the call, sending the email, then that reflects a shortcoming on his part. I understand I called early in the morning (after searching for the Inn's phone number), before 8:30am, as I have explained to you in our messaging, but that is no excuse for his inaction that brought us here now. I understand the compromise, but I really want you to do the right thing and please provide us the cancelation as we followed the Montecito Inn's policy of canceling prior to 7 days. Thank you again.

Ok first off, a drive-thru for pizza? I'm here for that. However, the pizza itself is less than exciting. It stinks because the dough is actually very good, the cheese is good, but the sauce threw it all off.

Convenient to Vandy Hospital; friendly and clean. The food was ok. I ordered the polish sausage with sauerkraut. It was served with little to know sauerkraut - I think if it was measured, maybe 1/8 cup...rather stingy. Came with a pickle. That's all I can say

The good? Fresh and delicious oysters, both raw and chargrilled. Bloody Maries are made with care and can be had nice and spicy.

The bad? Shrimp and grits had great flavor but were a bit on the salty side. Oyster Caesar was also good with anchovies in the dressing, but the fried oysters were a tad salty as well.

The ugly? Inexplicable neglect from our server Mike. He took a while to take our entree orders. We saw him make touches at all his other tables, and we still had to flag him down to place our order. He managed to miss the fact that our menus were on our table for the entirety of the meal. And our water glasses remained empty as well despite seeing other tables receive some resemblance of hospitality. We spent the whole meal wondering what we could have possibly said or done to deserve the neglect, but to this day, we still have no idea.

This is a hidden gem, people. If I lived in Nashville, I would probably be addicted to this place. Not even exaggerating. I highly recommend the falafel plate. The yellow rice that it is served with is incredible. I'm not sure what they do to it. This is not a fine dining restaurant by any means, but the food is so satisfying. You will be full for days.

Good food, great value.  I can't believe it's taken me three years to try this place (it's right around the corner to a house we moved into).  It's not much to look at from the outside (it's in an older strip mall) and some of the decor inside is dated....but I don't come to look, but eat.

Our family of four each enjoyed our meals.  Boneless wings were excellent, the blackened mahi sandwich was a generous portion and tasted great, and the kid's menu was expansive (and good).  We had four meals, two large beers, and the bill was $32 (actually, $27, as they advertise a $5 off $30 coupon in a number places.)  You just cannot beat the value here.
Good place for a special occassion dinner - the garden dining room is so pretty!

A friend did the research and booked 3 rooms for our stay. We arrived late in the day and were greeted by a wonderful staff member by the name of Kerri Sue. She checked us all in in record time and even gave us some great suggestions on where we could find dinner. 

My room (#13) was very small but I was the only occupant so it was perfect. The room shared by 2 other friends (#12) was a bit bigger and the third room (#23) for the last couple was large with a HUGE bathroom! 

The courtyard was super cute and filled with tables and chairs for hanging out. There is also a hot tub should you need to soothe aching legs and feet from walking the French Quarter.

The location of the Inn is awesome. Only a few blocks from the French Market and the water. Canal Street isn't too far and Bourbon Street is the next block. If you don't want to venture far from the French Quarter, this is the place to stay.

The only drawback is the noise from people in the courtyard at late hours. I wear ear plugs at night during hotel stays, so this didn't bother me. But my friends said they were kept awake for awhile by loud people. 

All in all, if you like charm and old style buildings, this place is awesome. Kerri Sue was so helpful with food recommendations and such a pleasure to deal with. You can find me at the Inn on Ursulines on my next visit to New Orleans for sure!
Love this place! The staff and Chef James are awesome! This is always our Go-To Sushi place  :)

This place has amazing salsa! Their guacamole is also very fresh and you can still see chunks of whole avocados in it. We also love their tacos and chalupas. The service is always fast and friendly. Overall a great option for good inexpensive Mexican food!

Decided to grab dinner and drinks here for dinner our first night out in Nashville. We were staying next door at the Velocity so this was very convenient.  The place looks amazing, they went all in on the look of this place to give it a very "Pub" feel. They have a pretty good beer list. The happy hour deals were pretty solid. Some of our favorites were the buffalo chicken sliders, nachos, Fish n Chips, Smoked gouda chicken pasta. The wings and buttermilk fried chicken not so much. Service was okay, maybe a little understaffed.  Overall the place looks great, but like other people said theres something missing. This place has alot of potential to be something special, but in the end has a very "chain " feel. I would assume this would be a great place to watch some soccer games, but other then that there are better options.....

Once again we had a perfect dinner here.   Ceasar salad, Rack of lamb and Grand Marnier souffle.   Our wait staff was Kendell and Blake.   They could not have been any better at their jobs.   How about opening a restaurant in Seattle?

Endless sushi was good- $15.99 for all you can eat. I got the volcano roll and the top of the world roll and they were both tasty. However, the service was below average. The waitress was very sassy and not very friendly at all. We had questions about the menu and the food because it was our first time there and she seemed frustrated and annoyed that we asked a simple question. She seemed to be the only waitress working while the other servers were sitting around, so maybe that was the reason, but still, their service could use some cleaning up. Aside from the awful service, the atmosphere and ambiance were nice and relaxing. I might go back if I were in the area and wanted sushi, but I would not drive out of my way for this place.

I've wanted to come to Ducky's for quite some time now and I finally had the opportunity to do so. I came in with a friend on a Tuesday evening and it was about 25% full. I was surprised to see how empty it was considering how I always hear that it is jam-packed. 

We sat down at a high top near the bowling alley area and ordered a drink and some food. The menu is a little strange as it's kind of all over the place. The prices are a little high on some items, but the food was worth it. The BBQ Chicken Pizza that I ordered came with a good amount of chicken and red onion. They didn't overpower it with BBQ sauce and actually kept the sauce on the lighter side, which I actually prefer. It came with eight small slices, which was plenty. 

The corn dip sounds weird, but is very good. Not sure what exactly is in it, but it is something I would order again. It was hot with a nice consistency, and had great flavor. 

Our server, Ivonne was terrific and on top of everything.  

My one complaint about Ducky's is that there really isn't much going on inside. They have a ton of TVs at all angles, but they lack sport memorabilia. Usually a sports bar will have some pictures, autographs or anything on the wall consisting of local sports teams. Ducky's had the bare minimum. 

 One would think with Evan Longoria owning Ducky's, he could at least get some Rays stuff on the walls, but with all of trades they make it would be hard to keep up.

Ordered delivery through Grubhub the other day. I ordered garlic naan, vegetable samosas and an eggplant curry. Unfortunately, they were out of eggplant, but I was able to change my order to a mushroom curry at no additional charge. When the food arrived, I was happily surprised at the portion sizes. It was about $20 for everything and it was enough food to last me 2 meals. Everything was DELICIOUS. Was very happy with my meal, the price, and their customer service. Will definitely order from here again.
Third appt in two years.  Great location and office. Very friendly staff.  Always on time.  Dr Sutton and   Dr journagan are prompt and professional.   Highly recommended

So why not 4 or 5 stars, Will? Why are you the first schmecklehead to afford this new and vaunted eatery anything less? Well...I care about public health and selfishly, my own health. The lack of PROPER mask wearing by staff is a great concern for me: our server and the busser did NOT have their schnozes covered by their cloth masks, rendering them useless as far as "slowing the spread." I did see one other server wearing the ubiquitous light blue procedure mask with a fully covered face, so kudos to him. While I'm an old soul, I couldn't help but notice the age of our fellow diners skewed older and thus higher COVID risk. 

As far as the food? Everything was truly good at worst and wonderful at best! 

Appetizers:

-on the advice of Yelp friend Vince, I pulled the trigger on the rice balls/Arancini. They were of perfect consistency: fork tender, a poke of the fork unearthed a center of peas, cheese and I don't know what else, but it was wonderful tasty gooey goodness with a crispy breaded shell. Not much "Sunday gravy" was provided, I wish there was a little more. 

-squash blossoms: my girlfriend LOVED these and this skeptical critic did as well. The oil in which they're fried is nice and fresh, the inside had ricotta,  perhaps even burrata based on the rich creaminess. 

-Bruschetta: if you dined at the former inhabitant of this address (The slightly pretentiously named Oliver, a Bistro), you'd likely agree that Angelo's iteration of this Italian classic isn't quite up to previous snuff. That being said, the bread on which the fresh mozzarella and tomato rested seemed fresh and had just the right amount of crunch. 

-Bread + compound butter: I cannot comment on this. It wasn't provided as we "had ordered a lot of food already." Yes I could've asked for it and taken it home or something, but I didn't wish to appear gauche in front of my date. Whatever happened to (as they say at Olive Garden): Hospitaliano!??

Mains:

-cacio e peppe: pasta was a perfect al dente and tasted as it should. You'll often find this dish atop clickbait listicles of "what not to order at the Italian restaurant" due to the perceived ease of preparation which in turn lowers the value for the money, something I'm typically all about. However, it's a restaurant, if I wanted a workaurant (shout out to Steak 'n Shake) I'd procure heavy creme, black poivre and Parmigiano-Reggiano to make this at home.  It was a winner here. 

-Short Rib Ragú atop pasta that was like fettuccine but probably another name: this was a special of the day and among the most popular. I can see why! If you love bouef bourguignon (shout out to Julia Child and my Dad) you'll LOVE this and I hope it becomes a menu staple here at Angelo's.  Rich flavor of red wine, beefy short ribs, perfectly cooked pasta and creamy cheeses (I believe Parmesan and ricotta). Half of this went home as it's very rich. 

Dessert:

Budino: will definitely order again! My baseline for this is Barbuzzo in Philly. Theirs is a salted caramel flavor, while here at Angelo's there are notes of espresso topped with mystery cookie flakes and a sweet cream. Perfect for sharing! Your stomach won't feel like lead either, a nice plus for dessert

The espresso had a nice crema and paired perfectly with the aforementioned Budino. Angelo's passes this must-have of a trattoria with flying colors. 

We arrived 15 minutes early for our 5:30 reservation on a Thursday and being seated was no problem. No, the outdoor tables aren't all 6 feet apart but that's simply not a feasible reality in areas with limited real estate and I understand that. Angelo's also can't control the numerous mask-free pedestrians walking by on the sidewalk. 

What can they control? For one, remember drink refills? Especially when it's tap water? It's a nice thing to keep an eye on and a fair barometer of service quality. I had to ask for more water after making a point of passive aggressively leaving my empty water glass at the tables edge. 

Additionally and more importantly: My hope is their staff can be properly trained in correct mask usage to ensure everyone's safety. Is that too much to ask? Should this even be a controversial thing to say? Unfortunately it is in these times. Let's hope they end soon! I think we all can agree that the "old"  normal beats this terribly clichéd "new" one. Total for 2: $100 before tip.

My wife and I did the curbside dining tonight.  Service was great and food was ready on time.  I got the prime rib sandwich and fries.  My wife got the beef medallions.  Food was hot and tasty.  We will be back!!
The salon is beautiful and I always get a great haircut from Vini . Definitely  top notch., Liz
1st time here & was fantastic. The bartender, waiter & food was amazing. We'll definitely be back.

I went to this nail lounge for the first time, it's very clean and well lit. It was also my first time trying a dip manicure and I loved it. They give you a discount if you pay in cash, would go back again!
The sushi was delicious! I ordered the shrimp tempura roll and the spicy tuna roll and both were made well very and tasted great! It only took about 30 minutes from order to delivery.

Where do you start when everything on your plate is great?  I had the brunch buffet on a weekday.  There is salad which looked fine, but I didn't have any because I wanted to leave all my space for the main course, besides I can make salad.  What I can't make is the best fried chicken in the world, which is what they have at lil Dizzy's cafe.  That said, the smothered chicken was even better.  It's fall off the bone chicken in a brown sauce.  There is green beans which were very tasty and made me feel less guilty about the rest of the food on my plate.  Which brings me to the gumbo.  I haven't had every gumbo in New Orleans, but it's hard to imagine there is any better than you find here.  Man it was great.  Then there is desert, bread pudding.  Don't know what's in it, but man its the best desert I ever had that didn't have chocolate in it.  All this for $16 including a drink?  That's insane.  If I lived in NOLA, I would be here every week.  This is a must restaurant in New Orleans.
Room was clean, humid/odd smell when first entered. Bar and staff were great. Would stay again.
Hamburgers are delicious! The grilled chicken sandwich was one-of-the best! The indoor dining room is very noisy which makes conversing very difficult.
Charbroiled oysters the best I've had!  Louisiana Crawfish Bread is unbelievable!  Excellent atmosphere

Loved the pies, we had the great white pizza and a bowl of mushroom soup, which wasn't too bad either.  The service was really good and the band " the Mexicans"  were really good. They played Pink Floyd, Santana and a host of 80's and 90's rock and pop songs in the land of Honky Tonks lol.

I came in for a facial on a Saturday when Christina was in town. I had terrible discoloration and broken blood vessels and my skin has just been off lately. First, it was more than a facial, it was informative and insightful into what I'm doing that would cause broken blood vessels -- like hot yoga. She also asked about my routine and gave me some suggestions on how to adjust what I was already doing to help alleviate my issues. She didn't scoff at my Accure grocery store brand face wash, just simply suggested I add an oil to my routine to help with dryness and use my Clarisonic 3x a week to clarify pores. All that to say I didn't feel judged or shamed by what I was doing, it was real life advice. Secondly, it was massage+facial-- no just laying there while the serums work their magic, I also got a massage that was heavenly. Thought I was floating kind of relaxation. Afterwards, I did end up buying some products that Christina suggested. I emphasize suggested because she wasn't pushy or insinuating that I should or my skin would wither away kind of end to the whole experience -- it was refreshing to not be pressured into buying something. But holy cow I am glad I did. Been using them for a week now and I was able to not wear makeup (besides a tinted sunscreen) yesterday (4 days post). My hyper sensitive skin has done a 180 and I'm glad I already have an appointment on the books with Christina when she is back at Osho in August. Can't recommend it enough, easy to get to, easy to book an appointment, and you feel a world away once you are there.

Don't quite understand the rating here. This is a "chain" average burger at best. Tasteless. Was expecting more. Was going to ask the two other people that were in the restaurant at 8:00 on a Wednesday but...

I would give this place 0 stars if it were possible. I generally give restaurants the benefit of the doubt; however, there were 5 of us and we barely touched our food. My mom and sister went to speak with the owner who was rude and did not comp anything off the bill even though it was clear most of us barely ate. The waitress then apologized later, letting us know the regular chef was out sick. Terrible service. Terrible food.

Great food, the atmosphere is a little cramped, but good for a casual night. I ordered the Blackened Alligator Po'boy. It was not on the menu, but suggested by the bartender. Definetely full of flavor (calories too, I'm sure). Try it out. The prices were not too bad for a tourist area.

3.5 stars. Bleepin Yelp.

I went here last night extremely hungry after working late. First they gave me chips with TWO bowls of salsa. So that was nice and a change from most of the other places here that charge you. The chips were allright, pretty much equivalent to those I've had at Qdoba with some lime flavor crystals/salt. The red salsa was very fresh and had some good flavor to it, though it was on the mild side. The green salsa was very sweet, maybe a little too much cilantro and/or lime. 

I ordered a Super torta, which has carnitas, chorizo, cheese, refried beans on a bun. When they say super, they aren't kidding. It was humongous. And I"m not talking about the Beyond Thunderdome guy it was that big. The meat and cheese and refried beans were so so good. And they perfectly toasted the torta buns. Total heaven.

My service was decent. My server did pay attention to me but she wasn't hovering over me at all. I think she could have come around a bit more, but at the same time I was the only guy indoors at that time of night.  And she did refill my water glass while I was visiting the Men's room.

Its not real mexican, but whatever it is it was tasty overall.

Oh yeah, if you are female, do not wear high heels and drink in this place. The bathrooms are located in the basement so you have to traverse a series of wooden stairs. I thought the drunk woman behind me was going to fall on her ass. Thankfully she made it.

I'll be back.

Oh yeah the name of this place had me singing the lyrics to that A Tribe Called Quest song "I left my wallet in El Segundo". Evil, evil, evil.

Been here twice recently, once for dinner and once for sunday brunch.  The food and service was great both times.  Would give it 5 stars but even after an hour and 20 min wait on sunday still would not have made a difference on a great experience.
The only complaint is that on both times it was so DAMN hot inside that it made it hard to really enjoy good food and craft drinks.
Someone needs to crank down the A/C!  I will be back in the future but never again during the summer or on a hot day.

Very nice 5 stars in Clearwater excellent service there .On the return to Elmira Corning in NY two stars lines were very short as was the service. I would give them one star but the ticket agent was so nice and courteous I gave two stars

Cool rustic decor. Indoor and outdoor seating. No bread or anything given at the table. Unique menu items. I had Espresso seared tuna was cooked nicely. On rice with asparagus. My friends loved their entrees of shrimp and grits, duck and steak. Loved the large portion of chocolate cake. Service was friendly and good.

We hired OCR to help us with stucco remediation. in South Philadelphia. They removed the exterior stucco on the rear and roof deck of our home, removed and repaired any damaged wood underneath, wrapped the home, and put up Carolina beaded siding. The new siding looks fantastic. 

OCR were extremely responsive in setting up a consultation and providing a quote. This responsiveness continued throughout the project. The team was really courteous and communicative about the stages of the project. The work was high quality and completed on time. The price was very reasonable compared to other quotes we received. The team takes pride in their work and were willing to come back a few times to fix little things that we wanted to be touched-up (like a little extra caulking here or there). Most importantly, we felt supported and listened to throughout the process, and felt that the team operated with integrity, which is hard to find in contractors sometimes. 

I would highly recommend OCR for any stucco remediation or siding needs you have!

Great food.. Sushi is good, fave dessert here is the double crem brûlée. Ambiance nice for a date night.  Negatives.. It can be tight depending where you're seated. And if don't make a reservation, be sure to plan for a long wait time.

I haven't tried much Indian food, but one of my Indian friends recommended this place. I have been here multiple times now, and it's really yummy each time. I've tried the chicken tikka, chicken biryani, and some all veg dishes. They were all really tasty. Plus the garlic and cheese naan are amazing! I also tried the mixed veg pakora, and it was just okay. The sauce it came with definitely made it better. 

Kind of slow service, but it may be because I went during the lunch rush.

I come back to this place time after time. The food makes you feel like you are "home" and I'm not even Russian lol.. my husband is! Everything in the menu is delicious and we never had inconsistency in the plates. I much rather eat there than order to take out I believe nowhere is the food the same when you get it delivered. Will always come back.

Second time in over a year to Salty's and have had a great experience every time! Patricia was our waitress last night and she was awesome! Very thorough, nice, and attentive! Food was amazing also! Definitely check out Salty's!

Fantastic place. The food and fresh brewed beers are exceptional. We have been here twice, first time was great, this time better. They just have it, menu, food, drink and service and great ambiance. We live in the neighborhood, so it is really cool to have what we think is an exceptional bar/brewery/ restaurant in the area. We will certainly be regulars if it holds the course and all that work there are very nice and fun to deal with. A real 5 star!!
By far the best cheesesteaks and hoagies in the country and I have lived in 40 states. You will not find a better roll!

Get ready to spend an easy $50.00 if your thinking about eating here with a family of four... The burgers were really great but the fries were over cooked and a bit crunchy. The atmosphere is themed around the 1950's era with jukeboxes (not working) and other novelty items that take you back in time...

St. Louis as a city I found....uh, how do you say....shitty. 

Nonetheless, the Saint Louis Sculpture Park did not share in the St. Louis essence. 

The sculpture park was huge, and quite impressive. The park had many different trails, which all seemed to lead to a different style. 

The huge clump of deciduous trees found everywhere make this place best visited in the Fall.  

If you are in St. Louis, well, then I'm sorry...but, hey! Go here! 

This place is free and awesome. Impress your significant other: take them to a park AND to an art museum.

oooooooo

I've already written once before about how much I love this place but recently I took time to experience their amazing bar/lounge.  If you haven't had a chance to come here and just sit with a really good cocktail, I recommend trying that soon.  They are open until 12am on Friday and Saturday nights and they provided one of the best dirty martinis I have ever had.  Their bartenders are competent, helpful, and seriously talented.  I'd recommend coming here after dinner if you are of mind to chill with some friends and have stellar cocktails.

Great restaurant. The decor is rustic industrial! The food was deliciously seasoned. I just wish it would have came out fresh off the stove. My food was a warm not HOT! The staff members were very pleasant and helpful in suggesting some popular dishes. 

The manager came around several times to make sure everything was going well. Great serious!

Keep it simple and you'll be alright.  Fortune Teller knows what I'm saying. They keep it simple in the vein that there's no pretentious bull to be found here.  What you WILL find here is a great selection of craft beers, wine and spirits along w/ a very nice craft cocktail menu.  Decor is simple and low key.  Service has always been friendly and prompt.  Last night, my girl and I popped in for drinks and a bite.  Together we had a portobello rueben, side summer salad, bowl of house made chili and a delicious cornbread muffin.  The rueben exuded a great flavor and had the perfect amount of mushrooms and melted cheese.  The salad was bursting with flavor and was definitely way above anything you'd consider "pub fare".  Sliced fruit w/ crumbled cheese, nuts and a great vinaigrette. The chili and cornbread hit the spot for this cold, fall weather.  Chili was flavorful and meaty w/ subtle heat and the cornbread was accompanied by a jalapeño jelly that I would spread on a roof shingle and eat cuz it's so delicious.  Cherokee Street is lucky to have Fortune Teller amongst its limits.  Well done.

A fun and entertaining place to eat! This place has a calender on their website for live music. I love variety and the tapas are excellent to order several to share for the evening. The atmosphere is relaxing and enjoyable!

Holy crap! Their Mac and cheese is amazing! I got the buffalo chicken and the only thing was I could have had her go lighter on the chips on top - otherwise it was perfect. The portion size was awesome and the noodles were creamy with cheese but not mushy. And it's so fast!

My new favorite pizza place. Love it here. Really thin crust. Freshly baked in really hot oven! Closest to  Venice style pizza I've found so far. Although I haven't tried Wood pizza yet which promises to be just as good.

Absolutely the best Oysters and wonderful Seafood!  The service at the bar by "Al " was totally top shelf.

Will definitely be back!

This place has amazing fried chicken!! Sunday nights is all you can eat fried chicken and it comes with unlimited mashed potatoes, green beans, and chicken and dumplings for the side. I have been a longtime fan on Rabanelli's but had never vised this location in O'Fallon. The location was very nice and the booths were quite spacious to my suprise! 

I was also very glad that they had bread pudding available (occasionally they will sell out). If you are a bread pudding fan.... definately check theirs out. It is covered in a thin carmel sauce and the portion is HUGE (enough to share). 

I also really enjoy their salad and tortellini soup that comes with most of the meals there. Their house ranch dressing is fantastic. And while the fried chicken is amazing, they also have great pasta dishes as well. I had the cast iron skillet tips recently and they were excellent.

The service was very good and the owners seem to take pride in ordering high quality servers. Of all the locations I've been too, I've never had bad service!

They also do catering and to-go orders. Would definitely recommend this place!

This place has so much stuff crammed into this store front. It is what I image a shop owned by people who buy storage units looks like. A lot of miscellaneous stuff. They had some nice antique/vintage furniture, and the prices were okay.

Just had the worst experience at a nail salon. I simply asked for black French tips and as you can tell from the pictures they are uneven. I simply asked for them to be cleaned up and the lady in charge came over was immediately rude. She started asking what the deal was and I explained to her the issue. She was not professional and her tone of voice was so rude like I was the issue. My friend was there with me defending me, the lady in charge was yelling at her and put her finger in her face and started calling her a b*tch and to get out and never come back. I'm honestly appalled at my experience. I never write reviews but I felt like this was warranted. We immediately left and went across the street to get them fixed.

Love Pizza opened its doors last week in the Canterra Shopping Centre on 109th Street and Jasper Avenue, much to the excitement of the downtown crowd. Goodbye Planet Beach Tanning, hello Love Pizza! It's a good trade for me as I'm about to make the transfer to work in the area in only a couple of weeks. I'm going to have to resist the urge to buy a pizza every lunch hour but whatthehey. #workproblems.

Who doesn't love pizza? It's one of those questions that when asked, you would never respond with "I don't" for fear of being labelled as the weirdo of the group. That's a true story for me, my elementary-aged self much preferring burgers or chicken nuggets. As I grew older though, pizza really did start to grow on me. I've found that I prefer a thin crust, but not into flatbread territory, a nice sauce with a zip to it, and simple fresh ingredients. Nothing funky like olives or pineapples please!

Co-owners Braede Harris and Gavin Fedorak have a different story about pizza, their history on the wall for all to see. The "mom-and-pop" shop (because it technically is, they just welcomed their son into the world only seven months ago) is a modern, hip pizzeria that guaranteed will grab your attention. I'm already dreading the weekday lunch crowd. As a co-owner of Press'd Sandwiches, this isn't Gavin's first rodeo but as the couple's first space together, Love Pizza is in it's own way, their baby.

The space is gorgeous, a simple black and white scheme with fantastic usage of geometric patterns. The floor, the walls, even their stacked pizza boxes are an Instagrammer's dream. Local photographer Randee Armstrong's work is featured in large scale proportions on the feature wall. If you're a caffeine or sandwich addict, you might even recognize one of her subject, Justin Benson, co-owner of Farrow. Completely unrelated to the Love Pizza project, but it's just cool to see local faces and artists together in Edmonton!

Once you get over looking at every nook and cranny of the amazing interior, you can start thinking about what kind of pizza you want to order! First, choose your crust: their classic Traditional Caputo "00", their 100% Organic Whole Wheat, courtesy of local Gold Forest Grains Heirloom Park Wheat & Red Fife flour, or a Gluten Free crust (+$3). Then choose a Love Pizza signature or go nuts and build your own, $11.95 either way. Three minutes into the oven and your thin crust pizza will be ready!

Joe and I made a quick mid-afternoon visit last Saturday and ordered the Meatatarian: Tangy Tomato Sauce, Fior Di Latte, pepperoni, Capicolla, Spolumbo's Italian Sausage from Calgary, and Shaved Parmesan on a '00' crust. Delicious! It's definitely going to be a fast favourite for many, Albertans like their meat! Loved the '00' crust, mine was crisp and held up nicely to all the toppings. Joe enjoyed using the tableside chili oil to kick it up a notch as well.

A second visit earlier today for some take-out pies had me trying the Holy Shiitake and The Q. The Holy Shiitake (Truffle Parmesan Sauce, Fior Di Latte, Roasted Mushroom Blend, Caramelized Onions, Fresh Arugula) is one of two vegetarian options on the signature pizza menu, and was enjoyable despite the fact that very little truffle flavour came through. I also noticed the absence of shiitake mushrooms, perhaps the roasted mushroom blend not completely mixed, and only white button mushrooms to be found. Sad face.

The Q quickly made up for it, a Chipotle BBQ Sauce punching a lot of flavour and meshing well with Creamy Gorgonzola, Caramelized Onions, Chipotle BBQ Pulled Chicken, Cherry Tomatoes, and a Chili Oil Drizzle. I opted for the 100% Organic Whole Wheat crust for this one, a fantastic alternative for those who are more health conscious!

I have no doubts that I'll work through the entire menu by the end of February, the Ode to Ukraine (Roasted Garlic Spread, Shaved Potatoes, Irvings Farm Fresh Bacon, Caramelized Onions, Medium Cheddar, Creme Fraiche, Green Onion) next on my list. Or perhaps the Old MacDonald, featuring a spicy tomato sauce and farm fresh eggs! Or maybe one day I'll build my own pizza, the combinations endless with seven different kinds of sauces, six different cheeses (one of which is Daiya Mozzarella as a vegan option!), and over 40 toppings. Love the fact that they're supporting local producers and farmers when they can and the quality of Love Pizza's ingredients!

Did I mention they also serve wine and beer (but are also kid-friendly!)? And Pinnochio Ice Cream for dessert? And open late? It just keeps getting better. I'm looking forward to creating more memories with Love Pizza and with my new workplace. It's going to be one heck of a year! And OMG, I almost forgot. Sign up for their loyalty program and get $5 off your first pizza! Collect points to earn free pies and make sure you put in your date of birth. Birthday pizzas for the win!

Why would I go anywhere else to see a movie? Online seat selection, great food, better in-house brewed beer, and elite sound and picture equipment. Fix is the answer to my movie theater needs. Carmel was in dire need of a state-of-the-art movie theater and man did they deliver. They have a special theater called MX that is the Flix version of an IMAX style theater, huge screen, loud speakers and great seats. The pizza is great, the house made IPA and Wit are great (especially on Mondays when Indiana pints are $2.00 off. 

I've had nothing but great service when we've ben there, both at night and during the day but I have heard that on opening nights of big movies the service does slow down but I think thats more than reasonable since the food is made fresh and the beer comes from the tap. If you love movies you have to check out Flix!
Delicious ramen I get the spicy with pork belly and black garlic oil. I've been back three times and I live an hour away
Ugh.  Still trying to grow out this horrendous do.  Maybe they're better with long hair but my short pixie cut is looking terrible 3 weeks in.

MTJ is my favorite taco place, but I usually visit the one in the CWE.  We love their queso (minus chorizo) as well as their duo of salsas.  My husband and one kid are huge fans of the side of street corn.  My favorite?  The shrimp tacos...by a long shot.  But this visit my server informed me of the special seasonal Cajun shrimp taco.  I was trying (sort of) to be good and just order 2 tacos, but then that sneaky little server twisted my arm and I added one Cajun shrimp taco to my order.  I decided to eat it first because you just cannot top the regular shrimp tacos and what if this one was underwhelming.  Drop. The. Mic.  The Cajun shrimp taco was AH.MAZING.  Put it on the regular menu right now.  Seriously.

Run, don't walk and try this taco before it's gone....or MTJ could just add it to the regular menu......

Local neighborhood grocery store  Has most things you need. Parking lot is a good size. Large over hang for rainy days. Fish department is not the best but it is available Good variety of fruits and vegetables Good stock of hard liquor alcohols  nice wine selection It has a little bit of everything but not a lot of everything

Ups and downs, gutters and strikes.  Overall, it was a good brunch, but I'm sure the unlimited mimosas had a factor in my laid back mood right now, that and the ridiculous view that every table in the house has the privilege of enjoying.  I do love a good view of the bay, and this is a great one.  None of the dishes really stand out to me even after a couple visits.  They were going back and forth, and last time, they had a small a la carte menu which was cool but again not impressed by the quality that came out.  It didn't seem like it was made to order, more like they made a bunch and let them sit in the back until "made to order".

There were as many positives as ehhhs along the way.  My across the board gripe would be nothing was actually HOT.  Warm...  Sure, but hot nothing.  When it comes to mashed potatoes and grits, that's kinda a must.  

There wasn't one dish that will have me running back to this place, but nothing so terrible that I would warn anyone to stay away.

Service was great, I sat at the bar this time, so I never was in need of a drink or to order one of the small plates they offer.

I was very excited to visit since this place has great reviews, but found it to be a very disappointing experience. The wait was two hours long. My husband and myself were finally seated. Received bread and butter immediately which was great, but took 25 minutes for someone to take our drink order. After we received our drinks the waitress left and it took another 20 minutes  to comeback to take our food order. After this the salads arrived in about 15 minutes and consequently the food arrived about 25 minutes later. This is too long to wait for service in my opinion. My husband ordered the Road kill, medium well and it came rare, I ordered Rib-eye medium rare and it came well done. We asked to speak with the manager. To our surprise he was very short and rude with us. ( I guess I expected more from the manager)  My husband and I came to celebrate his birthday but we were very dissapointed. What should have been an enjoyable dinner was a very unpleasant ordeal.  If you are looking for good service this is not the place to go.
Fantastic Cocktails. The vibe of Square Root makes me want to hang out often. The bar selection is impressive, and the cocktails are top-tier.

We have eaten at this Panera Bread many times. While we enjoy the food, this store has become neglected & could use some upgrades & cleaning. Also the surrounding homeless population congregates inside the restaurant, takes baths in restroom, uses Wi-Fi, charges phones, drinking coffee (free), takes up booths & seating, etc. On 11/20 at 6p.m when we ate dinner, there were more "street people" in restaurant then customers, & security guard for the shopping center was socializing with them. It just isn't a clean environment for eating. We will choose another restaurant or possibly another Panera Bread location.

Best candy I've ever had! This store is fantastic. It has a beautiful setup. The candy is so delicious and fresh. So far I've had sour gummies and regular gummies. It's beyond the best candy that I've had. They have a lot of chocolate as well. So many gifts and candies for Easter available.

I read the reviews carefully before bringing my 6 1/2 year old son here for his first real hair cut.  We've been doing it at home his whole little life and pulling it off because of his thick head of curls, but the kid wanted to go short and try something new.  
This is NOT a kids place.  I was aware of that going into it.  There are no kid accoutrements.  Jimmy did accommodate us by turning the news off and putting on golf at my request so the kid didn't need to watch bombs exploding in the Gaza Strip on tv.  
I kind of had to lead the pre-haircut conversation regarding what we wanted.  Jimmy didn't give much guidance here in terms of helping us explain or determine our end goal.  
BUT the dude gave a phenomenal haircut.  Seriously he did a very very nice job and we will definitely be back.  We made an appointment and didn't have to wait. (Big Plus.)  He was fast and to the point.  (Plus.)  The price is super fair.  (Plus.)
The cut is killer and it wasn't straightforward and easy.  Jimmy put in alot of special touches.  Used a few different instruments.  Tons of hair everywhere.  I could not have imagined a better outcome.
And the kid was really happy.  A nice mellow experience.  Not fluffy.  Not fancy.  But just what we needed.  Next I will try to get the husband guy into see Jimmy.  I'm pretty sure Jimmy could do anyone's hair and made it look fab, and I even found myself asking if he does women's cuts.  (Turns out he does, not so much here but at a salon in Bucks County a few days a week.)
So that's the deal.  If a great cut is what you want for a boy or a man, and you don't need a lot of fluff, there's a high chance of success here.

Very well mantained and friendly. Covention hall is spacious and well lite. Been there for car shows and CON shows. People are upset with entering these shows because of the precautions involved in door security. Hey ! Ever watch attendies trying to bring in REAL arrows. Real guns. " Oh I painted the nose of the real gun orange " so it's ok. Or swords that are sharper then your razor.  If these items had stayed in the car or at home the lines would fly through entry. Most shows don't cater to weaponry. Those shows we all enter in record time. Parking is always a problem. Especially if there is another event downtown. Plan ahead.... A little secret.  Use the DOWNTOWNER and leave your car on the outskirts of the area.

This place is great only been here a twice but definitely will be back again. The staff is great and so welcoming each time I've been here I had the duck and apple pizza it's to die for!! Also I love how the beers are always changing my favorite so far is the sweet potato IPA. Would definitely recommend stopping in or getting to go.

Oh my goodness. I couldn't even take a picture because I couldn't put my food down after the first bite. The food overall is incredible. I went with a large group and they were very accommodating to us.

This was the first time that we did an escape room. It was so much fun!! There were 6 of us. We were all able to complete the room together. The staff was friendly and helpful when we needed a few clues. Highly recommend going here!

Applying for my passport, the gal, was rude, inconsiderate, mumbled and was even more awful to deal with after asking her to repeat herself because i couldn't hear her. when I asked her to hand me the birth certificate, she rudely said she couldn't tell my birth certificate was "real or fake" then told me I'd get my birth certificate back after a week. At the end of this flustering process anyways, I then am told I'd get my BC back after I get my passport in 4-6 weeks. I will never return to this branch. Wish I would have gotten her name. Blonde, 20's-30's about 5'7".
Great meal. Excellent service from our waitress, Julia.  Wingers never disappoint!  We will be back soon!

We haven't gone to China garden in years and did tonight for convenience.  Now I know why we don't go there - it was horrible.  The sushi (rock n roll maki) was fishy tasting and the green curry Thai chicken was disgusting - mushy eggplant and veggies and overall not appealing at all. I feel badly giving review - never do it but this was a really bad meal

Ordered delivery as a quick easy meal after just moving here and was thoroughly pleased with everything we got. We ate the appetizer sampler and the build your own sushi bowls and everything was incredibly fresh and well made plus the portions were huge! Definitely going to be regulars here (:

I recently stayed here with a friend and chose this hotel based on location and price. Yes, the elevator is small and slow and reminded me of the ones in Europe. Take the stairs when you don't have your luggage. The front desk staff was very friendly. 

We were in town to see my cousins band play so we had a late night. Before 9am I woke up to construction in the room next door. This included persistent hammering and screwing and cutting right next to or into our shared wall. They mine as well have been in our room. I called down to the front desk 3 times asking about it and requesting it to stop. Although the man apologized and claimed he went up stairs each time asking them to stop, they didn't. Just when I thought they did, I would be jolted from my bed again. 

This place would have gotten at least 4 stars from me if it wasn't for the construction. I understand they need to improve the building or rooms but then you shouldn't be placing someone so close to the work. 

I am traveling to PA very soon and would have definitely stayed here. Now, I would only stay here if they gave me a discount and assured no unwanted wake ups.

The food was GOOD...not exceptional like i've been reading on the reviews...but what really sent us over the edge and will make us not ever go back is the fact that we brought 2 bottles of wine for our group of 4 and Juliennes charged us $50. corkage fee ($25. each)...Too ridiculous for this small casual little restaurant...we won't be returning!

Dial One Broussard Appliance was very courteous and professional.  Dave Martin, our technician, was courteous, efficient and very good at diagnosing and fixing the problem with our clothes dryer.  He called ahead to let me know he would be at my house soon and inquired about the dryer being gas or electric so he could have the part he thought he would need to repair it.  He was very nice and was able to fix it promptly, clean up the lint from inside the dryer and clean all the mess it created on my floor.  He even stayed to allow the dryer to run through a cycle to make sure it was working properly.  Dave previously repaired our ice maker and freezer and I am thoroughly satisfied with his work.  Thanks Dave!

Jolly's is the best! Cordial staff, delicious food, and amazing music! And special thanks to Jacqueline for being a great bartender! 

5 Stars!

Great hospital, the staff is very attentive, the nurses are fantastic and friendly, and the doctors are one of a kind. My name is Juan I spent 8 days there with sepsis, DKA, Blood clots, stomach infection you name it, and Dr Appezato left me walking out of there like a new man. Words can't describe how much I appreciate this place, they literally saved my life and I'll be eternally grateful. Thanks Dr and all the staff, God has a good spot for all of you in heaven one day. God bless, and may continue blessing you all. Thanks again.
This place is great. Smaller selection of entrees prepared to perfection. Definitely will be back

A large "All that Jazz" (aka Jazz) Po' Boy, a bag of Zapp's Spicy Cajun Crawtators, and a pair of Abita Amber made a perfect cheap ($10-$12) lunch for my girlfriend and me.

The bread was nice. There were brown paper bags of Leidenheimer bread stacked on a shelf, so I'm assuming that's where it came from. The meat and vegs were tasty too.

Verti Marte, as the name suggests, is not a sit-down restaurant, but a store that sells po' Boys and  lots of other basic grocery items. So if you are staying in the area it's an efficient place to pick up sandwich and whatever you might be needing while away from home.

It's open 24 hours.

ANOTHER WARNING for parties of SIX OR MORE - the TWENTY ONE PERCENT MANDATORY GRATUITY is REAL! Though the food was fine, I found it extremely inconsiderate NOT to be notified of the charge by the front desk and to have that gratuity warning on small print at the bottom of a page in their menu. Teams and friend groups, just please be careful - don't even bother going in groups of at least 6 especially if you can get less expensive Indian food of comparable quality elsewhere.

I needed to some junk taken away and requested quotes from yelp. Junk Gladiators replied right away and provided a very reasonable quote. The came the next morning and 5 minutes later they were on their way. If you need junk removed, give Junk Gladiators a call. They are fast, reasonably priced and reliable!

Excellent massage! I was already planning my next massage as soon as it was done. Lizzy listened to my descriptions of my aches and pains, then tailored a customized treatment to address them. And it was great! Really loved the options of cupping and aromatherapy. The hot towels and heat lamp made the experience even more relaxing. Very calm and comfortable atmosphere, too.

I made an appointment on 08/16 for 08/17, the 4 hour window promised. I received a text and email reminder so I could be available. I did not hear from the company until after the window of time passed and no updates were given during the 8-12 pm window that was reserved.

I shop here every time I am visiting from Illinois, as we do not have a Kroger here. The last time, I had a huge shopping list from my daughter with meats on it that I do not normally purchase in strange  sizes. The butcher came out and completed my whole list for me and even cut the sizes that I needed.  The remodel of this store made such a difference! I think it gave the employees pride in their store. Wonderful customer service!

Nothing that special here. Not sure what all the pretty good reviews are for. I could have had Long John Silver's or Captain D's and felt I got a better deal and been fine with the taste. Just to expensive for what it was. Which I should mention the menu listed via YELP is incorrect price wise. The seafood platters were $5-10 more than menu price. View their website for correct pricing. Only thing better than standard was the mixed drink I had. 

With only 1 mixed drink the cost for my wife and I was $100+ and even she admitted the filet mignon she had was more like a sirloin.  While it seemed slightly bigger that was probably the cover up for lack of quality. 

Not a picky eater but we could have easily saved $50 eating elsewhere.

I like this bay looking restaurant. It is on the water. They have plenty of waiter and waitress. The time we have visited were Sunday lunch time at 11:00 am. It is a bit cold seat at the outside next to water for December. This is our first restaurant for progressive lunch. 

I got the grilled grouper sandwich with pretzels bun. The side we have the coconut rice. It was great. The grouper is fresh. They do give good pieces of grouper. We also has the lobster roll with sweet potatoes. The sweet potatoes were crunchy. The lobster is chunky and has less mayonnaise which show more lobster meat in the rolls. I like this way better. 

Our service is good. Food come out quick. We were able to finish our meal and paid less than 50 minutes.

This place is great. It was busy, I had zero issues with service. The food was excellent , the margaritas were huge. I will definitely be going back!! My wife and I went had 32oz margaritas and both had meals all under 40 bucks!!!
I lived in El Paso, TX for a few years and have eaten my share of authentic  Mexican cuisine; this place is great.

It has been a little over 20 years since I have done any business with Larry Miller. The recent visit gives me hope, but they have a long ways to go to earn my trust in them. It is a car dealer after all.
A long time ago I got this Dodge Neon. It was a 95, so you'll probably understand because it was the first of it's kind. When I took it in to be washed their staff sped around the lot in my car. In addition to this, I was always taking it in to be fixed only to be told "I don't know what noise you are talking about," nor would they do anything about the check engine light. They antagonized me when I complained and it lead to me not being allowed there. My transmission soon wasn't working correctly.
That being said I would be surprised if their plan that we paid $3,000 for a couple weeks ago will be honored. I would be very impressed indeed and Larry Miller will earn my trust back...kind of. 
I want to mention that the team at Larry Miller today is patient and willing to make a deal with their customers. They work things out. I did not think that they would approve of any loan, but I was approved for a new car. John Smartt worked hard to get us into a car. We got a nice car at a decent rate.
What I dislike about the entire sale is the fact that there was this sense of, "Nothing to see here, everything is okay!" When we asked about the details of the offer on the car John Lile (I think that is how you spell his name; he is the finance person) waved at the question like it was a fly, "It's all in the paperwork." He did print some documents for us, but there was much information that was still not disclosed. 
Even though Carfax indicates that the car was never in an accident, but I think it says that about all of their cars. The car we bought was clearly rear ended because there is damage on the back bumper. They were clearly aware of it because when we brought it in for the clear coat (I think that was what it was) they stated that pre-existing damage is not covered under what we enrolled in.  
Larry Miller has improved since 20 years ago, but could still use a little more work. Would I recommend them? Yes, because they will work with you. Just keep your eyes peeled when going because evidently cars just go through the shop without any safety inspection. One car from the lot had a bad transmission and another car from the lot had sketchy looking seat belts in it. Pay very close attention when shopping at Larry Miller.

The store was stocked well. It took me a few minutes to figure out the layout of the store. Once I did, I found most of what I needed. I couldn't find chalk. Lol The cashier I went to was on her cell phone while checking customers out. It took a few minutes to get her attention to ask where chalk would be located. It felt as if I was bothering her. I hope Howard, who she was talking to was her boss. The other cashier's register was locked. She seemed to resolve the issue to move customers through her line. It still takes getting used to using a quarter to get a cart. Overall, a decent shopping experience. Poor customer service.

My husband and I first met Jean-Paul when we rented an apartment two years ago. As a landlord's liaison he was friendly, replied to our needs quickly, and facilitated communication excellently. There was something very appealing about his honest approach, love of the city, and friendly demeanor and when it came time for us to start looking for our first home purchase we went to him to guide us.
Working with him was great. We didn't know what we wanted and he helped us through the whole process. Even though we had a modest price range, he never suggested that we exceed it nor did he ever make us feel like we weren't valuable clients. He was funny and charming and the least sales-man like real estate agent I ever met. I would absolutely recommend him to anyone I know and would use him again in the future if we are in the market again.

I'm in love with this store. I have this store to thank for changing my wardrobe for the better and not breaking my wallet!

This store changed my thrift store experience. I love the selection offered here, the friendly staff, and the great prices. I found my first pair of vintage black Salvatore Ferragamo shoes here for only $20 and since then, I've been addicted to designer shoes. Bad because I now have have a collection of vintage designer shoes, but good because I will never pay full price for any thing designer after my experience from shopping here. 

The store set up is very shopper friendly. You don't have to waste time rummaging through racks of clothes to find something in your size. Everything is already labeled not only by size, but by style and color! Yes....you heard me right?! If only every store was set up this way! They have different sections for shoes, dresses, coats, skirts, and tops. 

Why you should shop here:

-shop for a good cause
-great prices on designer clothes, jewelry, and purses
-friendliest staff on board
-well organized
-Holiday sales can be up to 50%-75% off
-Senior citizen discounts 25% every Tuesday

This place looks pretty old from outside, but it surely worth a visit when you want a good breakfast! Our waitress was friendly and fun, brought us food pretty fast. We joked with her about how we were thinking about going to Camden, NJ for dinner later, her face just completely turned pale and expressions froze, thanks for warning us and serving us yummy omelettes.

Arrived back in Nashville to pick up my car and the lot was closed. I called several times and left emails but nobody will return my call. I'm stuck in Nashville without my car and can't get to Jackson,Tn. Somebody please contact me so I can get my car

This was my 1st last lift and I think I love it! My appointment was 2 weeks ago and my lashes are still dark & curly. I skip mascara most of the time now. Will definitely make this a part of vacation prep & skip mascara at the pool. Surprisingly relax, too.

This place is AWESOME!  I cannot say enough good things about the staff and the doctors.

In January, I was in a bad accident that injured my back and caused severe whiplash.  Tests showed damage that required PT and my doctor recommended I see a Chiropractor as well.  I was referred to Health Goals by multiple friends and I am so glad I chose them.

I credit this place with giving me my life back.  Chronic pain can bring you to a very dark place- not being able to get through my day without severe pain, not being able to work out and not being able to take part in normal activities was having a very negative impact on my general health and well-being.  Because Health Goals offers so much more than just chiropractic care, I was able to get into a program of adjustments, heat therapy, massage and PT that has helped me regain strength.  In turn, I am getting mentally healthy as well.

It has been three months; my neck is completely healed and although I still experience back pain every day, I have come so far in my recovery and continue to get better each week.  I completely PT and now only go once a week for therapy massage, heat therapy and an adjustment.  I was recently cleared to start light workouts again and I feel like I am on the road to a full recovery.

I would highly recommend Health Goals for chiropractic care, massage care or anyone looking to get healthier!

My very old Gateway needed to be replaced.  I spoke to a technician who ordered the new Gateway and advised me NOT to return the old equipment because it was obsolete.  So, I received the new Gateway in a few days and it worked fine.  It came with a shipping label to return the old equipment, but I recalled that the tech said to ignore the instructions and NOT to return it.  I then started getting emails following up for the old equipment to be returned.  I called and again was told to ignore them since my equipment was outdated.  Then I got charged $150 + $10.61 for equipment not returned.  I contacted 4 different ATT reps with my issue and every one of them disregarded what the other employees had advised (to throw the old equipment away and not return it).  Because ATT customer service has declined in the last 5 years, I kept the equipment and the mailing instructions, anticipating that this might happen.  NO ONE would credit my account. When the equipment was brought to Fed Ex, they advised to keep the receipt because frequently customers have told them that they never received credit for returned equipment.  How can a company continue to survive with this lack of service? This business model (of using cheap oversees labor) has got to come with a cost of lost business.

We decided to stop in and cool off. Our server was friendly and attentive. I would argue that the 'flat bread ' was flatbread. It was a pizza. And the restrooms were pretty scary. Dark dank and hot. And not handicap friendly.

Definitely the club to take a girl to. The dancers were all beautiful. Seriously, I was shocked. They were polite and friendly, the cover was only $5 and dances were only $8. 

The whole club felt clean, lots of seating, chairs, couches, etc. 2 stages w/girls all the time. The women on stage were dancing really well, no one looked bored they all seemed to really enjoy themselves. I would definitely go back.

Wow so delicious. We didn't get to order many things but what we did try we loved. We also were only able to do take-out due to COVID. However, their rice bowls and their spam misubi were amazing. At first I wasn't sure if the rice bowl was going to be enough because it was in one of those small compostable containers, but it was so much food just jam packed in there, It was delicious!!

When we went by to pick up the food, they did have an outdoor seating area which you could help yourself to if it wasn't too busy. The staff were also very friendly and helpful. Will definitely be back if we are in SB.

Although I like that the sandwiches are bigger than your head and they are certainly delicious, I find this place gets slightly more esteem than it deserves.  It's certainly good but essentially a deli - no more magically than any other I've seen.  

I will say I find the people that work there friendly and helpful.  Lunchtime brings a big crowd so be prepared to wait a bit.  The line moves fast and you'll be rewarded with a huge sandwich for a completely reasonable price.  I only wish there was a place to sit after purchasing - even if only a few more tables outside.

Great food, and good variety. Authentic middle eastern food. I will definitely be back again.
I got the mixed grill platter, and falafel on the side. Meat was cooked to perfection.

I was lead to Manda by whatever power leads me around by the nose and I'm so glad I went along for this ride.  I stumbled upon Manda while lost looking for the head shop in the same cute little shopping center, " Monterey Court".  I popped my head in for directions and got so much more than I ever expected. She has a gold light about her that glows, she is friendly, gave me directions and I was off.  For reasons unknown I couldn't get the shop off my mind.  I realized shortly after that she was the one to do the restoration work I've wanted done for over 20 years and finally put a name in the banner. ( I sobered up midway through the 1st time ) 
When I reached out to her, she was fully booked up ( you must book early) but said she would see what she could do and she found space for me within a day.
