She's warm, super friendly and makes you feel at home in her chair.  She consulted with me extensively before the work began. I'll admit it is a simple tattoo but she went about her business as if my tattoo was the most important thing she had going. Long story short, I am unbelievably grateful for her fine work which I got to see a few excellent examples of.  She is amazing!   We travel full-time and get to Tucson 2x a year, when we are back next time I am so visiting her again.  
She took my 30 year old, unfinished and very faded heart tattoo that you could hardly see and restored it better than the original. I would definitely check her out!  Thanks Manda!

I've taken my standard poodle puppy here twice now for grooming and it's been a fantastic experience. The staff is very friendly and accommodating and this is the first groomer I've been to where I've had a complete, un-rushed consultation with the groomer prior to starting. Both groomers that I've used (Sheila and Erika) are very experienced and did a fantastic job. Five stars is really hard to get but they deserve it. Highly recommend.

I stopped in randomly to ask questions about the different blood donation processes. Amie, the donation clinic manager, was incredibly helpful and knowledgeable about the different types of donations. She answered all my questions with a smile and without judgment. I highly recommend this donor center.

DO NOT GO HERE!!!!  Service is terrible. Was sunday night around 7:30pm. Not busy at all. We were consistently forgotten by our waitress,  Sharon G. Everything took forever. From coffee and water to being able to order our food. Once we were finally able to order it took 40mins for food to come to the table. I asked our server how the steak there was because I wanted steak and eggs. She raved about how good it was then after it came out severely overcooked she complained about how bad the cook was. Our sizzling skillet was cool to the touch by the time it got to the table. Over heard 2 waitresses calling a customer a retard. Got to listen to a different waitress complain very loudly about not getting the tip she felt she deserved. Watched as 3 different groups of people were walking around the resturant with empty glasses in hand trying to find someone for a refill. To finish off the evening, less than 20 minutes after leaving and I'm nauseous from the food. The worst dining experience I've ever had. Save your money and literally go anywhere else but here. Wish I give this place negative stars
Great food, they mix their alcohol. So when you are ordering a hibiscus marg, it's more like tequila + hibiscus margarita mix. Not a fan of that.

Meril is one of my recommendations anytime someone asks "where should I go eat in NOLA." The food is delicious and perfect sizes for sharing! The atmosphere is great- one of those places where you can dress up or dress down.

There used to be a Qdoba on 23rd Street in Manhattan that I liked to frequent; however, it closed about eight years ago.  I hadn't been to one since, until I tried this place in Tampa International Airport.

I had a chicken quesadilla and it was very good. The price was low and the portions generous for an airport. 

One isssue: When I asked for sour cream in my quesadilla, I was told that I could have it on the side, which I thought was odd. (Moe's Southwest Grill lets you have sour cream inside the quesadilla.)

Although I like Moe's better than Qdoba, Qdoda is still good, and I like Qdoba better than Chipotle.

The Beach on Bourbon is such a fun bar! It's got 2 bar areas and a dance room. We had a blast all over. DJ and music were awesome, drinks were good, atmosphere is poppin. It can be trashy or "ratchet" but hey you can see that anywhere, who cares, as long as you're with your friends and enjoying yourself. Stop by here for some fun!

This place is the perfect little spot! It really gives me true Japanese flavors and vibes. As soon as you walk in you have to place your order like most Japanese restaurants which I loved! It reminded me of when I visited japan this year. Everything from the sake to the coconut shrimp roll was very delicious. I will be coming back !

This is a terrific store in a dead part of the mall.  I hope a new department store opens soon, because many shops at that first phase are dying.  
De Serres has everything.  (except beading materials) 
I needed a paint pen.  I couldn't decide among 3, so I was given very helpful demonstrations on tester pens.  How nice.  
Very cool staff.  I really hope they succeed.

This restaurant is pretty much the definition of a hole in the wall.  Located next to a barber shop that has probably caused dozens of car crashes due to epileptic seizures.  

Ordered the Honduran grill for two and the horchata.  Horchata was tasty, if a bit overly sweet.  You receive a very large portion size in comparison to what you pay for.  The grill for two was pretty good, but really nothing to write home about.  I would've preferred the salsa verde to be spicier as well.  Pretty good experience overall.  

Bonus note: If you are afraid of dirty restrooms, go beforehand.

As a peace officer, I look for smart, capable, and no-nonsense counsel to guide me on critical issues.  When it came time to deal with my own legal issues, I knew I had to get the best legal advice possible.  Tracey Rangel Cruz and her team have proven to be all of the above and so much more. They told me exactly what was happening through the entire process and never left me in the dark. Thank you does not seem to be enough for the sanity and peacefulness that Tracey and her team have brought to my life. At the end of my first meeting with Tracey, I knew that I was in the best possible hands.  Every interaction since that first meeting has shown that to be true. Tracey and her team truly care about you. They will work unbelievably hard, and utilize their extraordinary experience to get the best outcome for you. If you need an attorney, do your due diligence and contact as many lawyers as you want. But when you give Tracey and her team a call I can bet you will not be disappointed with the expertise, service, and attention you receive. I will remain forever grateful to the Law Office of Tracey Rangel Cruz.

This is a super cute park that offers multiple play areas for kids and adults alike. There are basketball courts and a playground near the front entrance. 

The playground had three little areas, one specifically for very small children (1-3 years old). The other two areas gradually get more challenging for the kids and can be enjoyed by all ages. There are also two swing sets, three of which are toddler seats, two regular, and one swing seat that appears to be a 5 point harness. 

Behind the basketball courts is a little trail, swing and splash pad. The splash pad is super cute and had lion statues surrounding it. It's fun for little kids to play in but you can still smell the chlorine in the water, so keep that in mind. It appeared to be clean and we let our little one run around and play for a bit. 

There were a few kids that made our experience less than perfect; they insisted on swinging from the palm frans over our bench and even after asking them to stop they continued so we moved. They climbed a fence and jumped onto the roof of a small shed and stood on the swing rocking it back and forth. Eventually they left...and so did we.

Ripoff!!!  $15.00 for a "special" that was basically a pile of rice with two shrimp in it.  I told the server i was unhappy with it.  she didn't offer to take it back or take it off the bill.  I will never go there again.
Got gipped on the weight of my Rugelach. Also found numerous dog or cat hairs on my desserts :(
Excellent ceviche and chile relleno. Strong  margaritas, appealing setting. Efficient, patient service. Worth a stop!

I'm Italian and from South Philly, so I know good pizza. However, living in the NE now, I've tried out a lot to see what's up here. My daughter came home and said, Try Pizza Roma! So, I ordered their family special by using their website.

The pizza was good, sort of like a NY style. The quart of soda advertised for the special on the website somehow became a 22 oz soda in a cup. Hm. I called to find out why. the girl said due to the new Philly soda tax, they reduced the size instead of raised the price.

I was told it would be 85 cents EACH for the cheesesteaks to add wiz as the cheese (I know NO other place that does this)...when I expressed shock, she said, "Its bc its the GOOD wiz" (I can attest after tasting it that its not...Philly Pretzel Factory has the good wiz.)

I sent my husband over with what I thought was the right amt of money, only to find out something not mentioned on their website: they don't take credit cards. WHAT restaurant in this day and age doesn't take cards? I mean, REALLY? So they jack you with the ATM in the store that tacks on an additional $1.50? For food that's not even anything special?

Our first and last time there. (I still can't believe it...no credit cards?! Come into the 21st century! Unless you're doing it to avoid taxes, lol)
Have eaten here several times for breakfast, lunch and dinner.  Always good food.  My daughter and I love eating breakfast here.  Staff always friendly and service is good.

Are you hungry? Do you like lots of food for a reasonable price? If you answered Yes to both questions then this is the place for you. I've had about everything on the menu, and all of it has been good. 

I highly suggest the large gyro combo if you want something for lunch and dinner or you need to feed a small village.

After scrolling through Grubhub I came across Grand Lux and decided to try the Pot Roast. 

The dish was about $22 with taxes. I feel it was a good portion size, but I also can't eat a lot so, I'm not sure how sufficient it would be for someone else. 

The Pot Roast was tender and flavorful. The mashed potatoes were creamy, I think they should have included some pan drippings with them. The carrots and potatoes were fresh and were a great accent. I could've done without the pearl onions they were bitter, almost as if they were spoiled and didn't go well. I ended up throwing them all out. Overall great dish that I would recommend!

Honey got the the Ribs and wasn't happy with them. They were about $20 as well.They were a little dry and he didn't like the bbq sauce. I'd definitely go back for the pot roast or give something else a try.

I don't usually eat alot of bread, and it was my second time to NOLA so unfortunately I haven't had many poboy experiences--- however, It was delicious. It's in the back of the dive bar Erin Rose-- all the way at the back. Tiny space, with only one table, but I didn't mind standing. I had the grilled shrimp poboy and my brother in law had the grassfed beef. I would totally recommend splitting it like 2 or 3 ways-- It's huge. I left over half... Cash only!

I love the jewelry. Great stuff. I've already purchased 2 pairs of earrings there. I understand the store is filled with tons of tiny pieces but some of those people working there need to back off a little bit....i felt a tad uncomfortable while shopping. My bag is in plain site and so are my hands. Jeez! The awkward feeling though doesn't hold me back. The stuff is well priced, and I love their 50's styled jewelry and will continue to go back for new inspiring jewelry for outfits and such.
Chili's is always a great go to. Food was fresh and hot. Drinks were amazing and strong. 2-4-1 never fails.

Small menu. Out of items. Portions need to be larger for price point. The taste is good. Ingredients seem quality and healthy.

I frequent quite a lot of organic, healthy, pure, veg/ vegan restaurants around LA and wherever I travel. While this has a great thing going for it, I'm really not sure what all the 5 star reviews are for. I went and had a ceremonial matcha latte. I think the fact they offer sprouted almond milk is great, although I wish they had more alternative milk choices (hemp, coconut guys.. these are standard for vegan joints.. not everyone likes almonds, they consume a ton of water and some people are intolerant to nuts). THE PORTIONS need to also be larger. I also ordered an orange smoothie and it was too much money for the size I got. The matcha was made perfectly. I order matchas most places I go and it's rarely made this well- no chunks, nicely frothed a little, slightly sweetened. It was a good matcha and that portion was pretty good. The guy mentioned below made it.

The customer service: the guy with dreads or braids was really sweet and helped patiently answer all my questions since all ingredients weren't listed on the board (I'm thinking of the flavored chais and maybe a couple other things) and it was my first time in. The menu was extremely small and limited and most items contained things I don't eat. There were a couple items that looked really promising but they were out (and it was like 10am). So all in all it was kind of a bust for me.. not sure I'll return. There was another girl with tattoos and she was cold to me, seemed like I was unwelcome there.

9-17-18
Gave it another try and the same thing. The girl who rang me up (same girl again) was slightly less chilly but acted so annoyed that I was even there it looked painful for her to act polite. Smoothie was again very small and this time too runny. For what is charged, it's not worth it. I don't plan to come back.

Great clean place. I order the 1/2 chicken, frys, mac and cheese. My wife ordered the chicken mashpotatos and biscuits. Chicken was hot and fresh. The mac and cheese was great. My wife said it was just ok but since this isn't her review we're going to go with my opinion. The biscuits looked and tasted homemade. The hot honey they serve is great. If you haven't tried it, you have to. I live in Chicago but anytime I'm out in Indianapolis I definitely will be stopping in.
Food was tasty; however, hard to get past the dirty, dingy atmosphere. If they fixed the place up a bit, it would help.

Nothing really special about this place. The decorations were different and catchy but with the restaurant part not really a place for a nice sit down dinner. This place is more of lounge than a restaurant. The music is too loud for you too enjoy your food. Nothing special about the menu. I ordered chicken wings (mild) and Hawaiian BBQ Flatbread. The wings were not good at all. It does not taste mild at all it tasted just a regular wings. The chicken were not that cooked as I bite down my third piece the meat were still red. And even one of my friend noticed it too. I did not finished the wings after that but offered it to the rest of the group. The flatbread is good as I love pineapples so that's a given that I will like the flatbread. 

They also have sushi in their menu so if you like a place that offers ceviche, sushi, flatbread, wings, sandwiches at the same time then I guess this is a place for you. The service is okay.

I went here for restaurant week and it was wonderful!
The waiter was very attentive to us but not in an annoying way. Plus he was frienWe happened to get there in perfect timing right before the dinner rush came in. 
The food was so delish! 
My first course was the Caesar salad. My second course was the max brenner burger. Oh my gosh it was so good! No burger will ever compare now. It had cocoa dusted fries on the side. Oh I also got a dark chocolate spicy hot chocolate which was of course very good. For my last course I had the dark chocolate fondue. 
I really liked how for all chocolate items you could pick dark, milk, or white chocolate as the chocolate.
Obviously on a regular day it would be pretty pricey so I'd date a date there haha! But if I'm ever having a need for chocolate day this is where I'll go. :)

This is a cute mom and pop shop in the heart of Santa Barbara. When you walk in, you get the authentic French feel. 

We stopped here on our way out of town, and I am glad we did. It was not crowded and the service was prompt. 

Everything in the showcase looked fabulous, but we chose the Croque Monsieur and the cinnamon brioche. The Croque Monsieur was cooked to perfection and the bread was absolutely delicious. It came with a side salad. More so, the cinnamon brioche was AMAZING!

I stopped here to grab breakfast before a flight out...my gut told me I should've gone to Au Bon Pain, but Guava & Java was convenient so I decided to try them out. Bad decision.

Good:
-They offer Siggi's yogurt! I've never even seen this brand outside of Whole Foods or Wegman's. What a nice surprise and a great way to start my day.

Bad:
-Service - one of the two workers was REALLY slow, even as the line was growing. The other worker seemed pretty fast and super friendly.
-I ordered a toasted whole wheat bagel with cream cheese. The bagel was warmed, but not toasted, and there was a TON of cream cheese. The most I've ever had on a bagel. I ended up wiping most of the cream cheese off and only eating about half of the bagel. Such a waste...
-Most of their bananas were really brown - way too brown to eat. I had to really dig to get one that was edible...not good. I feel bad for anyone else after me who wanted a banana..
-The fruit cups did not look fresh
Really good experience! The "Thai hot" is delightfully hot, very delicious and the service is fast and friendly. Great dinning atmosphere. I would definitely come back to try more.

Their pizza is always a favorite of mine, but at this location I find myself in a situation that required me to ask for more help than I deserved. Manager Stephanie was very accommodating and eager to provide excellent customer service.

One of my favorite spots to pick up some good soft serve on a hor Florida day! They have changed the menus around a little, and made the flavors very small to see recently, and their drive thru traffic tends to block the front entrance/exit.
You can still go up to the window and order, and the coolest part is that you can mix whatever flavors you want in a swirl. I suggest trying the coffee/black raspberry swirl (ads chocolate sprinkles if you like a little crunch)
Great portion sizes, decent pricing, and friendly staff. Oh, don't forget to ask for a pup cup if you bring your dog along (just vanilla mix)

Used to go here all the time in high school (9 years ago). Loved it then and don't know why it's taken me so long to come back. I'm just never in the Milpas area. It's my birthday weekend and I took my fiancé for the first time tonight, it was even better than I remembered. It's hard to find good reasonably priced Chinese food in Santa Barbara, this restaurant does it right. But what really tops it off is the service. I wish I got our servers name cause he deserves a raise if the owner sees this. Saturday night August 17th. The girls working were sweet too. But our main server was funny, professional, prompt, and personable. Great experience!

On a drive through Boise we needed car supplies and those include cookies. If your road trip doesn't have cookies, well, maybe you should re-evaluate. 

If you need cookies and baked goods and sandwiches and coffee and a grilled cheese for your kid, this is ze place! We pulled in and followed their Covid guidelines of masking and social distancing while we waited in line to order. Breakfast sammies, aforementioned grilled cheese and some snickerdoodles were rang up for me by helpful and kind staff members and I was on my way in no time. All the nosh was delicious and my toddler thought a grilled cheese for the road was genius. 

If you're not in a hurry or need to stock your pantry the freshly baked loaves of bread are plentiful and impressive. Very recommended! Thanks Boise, support local.

Our first time playing here. Awesome course. We used a Living Social deal to play. Absolutely beautiful scenery and the grounds are immaculate. Very challenging course with lots of sand, water and elevation changes. Got windy in the afternoon and could use more info on each hole either at the tees or score card. A very friendly, polite staff. They even cleaned our clubs after. Some construction going on while building a modern, elegant clubhouse to be completed early next year. We just moved here and absolutely love the area and the golf course. Can't wait to play here again and for the clubhouse to be completed. Now, if I can just talk my wife into becoming members?

I went to Carrabbas's Restaurant yesterday with my friend Maggie. I ordered a Johnny Rocco salad that had shrimp and scallops in the salad . By the time I got my salad the shrimp and scallops were cold.  I was very disappointed.  The server Toni should have served me my salad right away. My friend Maggie ordered a sirloin steak which took awhile to cook.  Toni should have served my salad right away instead of waiting for my friends steak to be cooked and serving both together.
Not good service. I don't like cold seafood in my salad. The salad should have been served hot. Then bread was served in plastic wrap instead of the wire bread basket. Table that came in after us got a wire bread basket for their bread.  Excuses the wire basket needed to be washed. This was at 2pm in the afternoon I give this visit rating and of a two. Disappointed with visit this time.
P.McGorman

One of the best BBQ joints I've been to in a while! Meet was so tender and moist! Sides were great! (Salad and mac and cheese) salad dressings are homemade.. the BBQ sauces at the table are all so good!!!! And to top it all off this places supports our vets!!! I will be visiting this place more often!!!!
Disgusting not a Sonoran dog they aren't even from Mexico they have no idea what a Sonoran dog is worse thing I've eaten ever!

Four words: Build Your Own Burrito. Do it soon.

Their house chips and salsa are excellent and the margaritas are yummy too!

I loved it here! The food was filling and delicious and very reasonable. The only drawback was the fact that it was a cash only enterprise. I am a person who never carries cash. But there is an ATM out front. I had the seafood noodles. They were great. Next time I will try the shaved noodles.
I have gone here a few times for some celebratory drinks, nice back patio, nice staff, friendly atmosphere, I like.
Very good.  Reuben was outstanding.  Sweet potato fries were good.  Wings were good.

When we arrived today around 12:05, we were told there was a 15-20 minute wait for our party of 3. As we waited, we noticed several empty, dirty tables waiting to be cleaned. After almost 15 minutes, I approached the counter to let the host know there was a table close to us that was still dirty and empty, yet they had several parties waiting for tables. Shortly thereafter, the bus person cleaned the table and it still sat empty for another 5 minutes before we were seated there. Right away, we noticed the water glasses were not cleaned well, as there were lipstick marks on them. We alerted the waitress who brought us different glasses with our water and tea. Those glasses also had lipstick marks. We informed her again, and she finally brought clean glasses with our drinks. We began to order, then she informed us they were out of potatoes for a side item; this is their signature side item and one of the only reasons I like First Watch is their potatoes. She gave us the option of a side salad or fruit. Instead, I ordered French toast. My table mates ordered an omelette and a breakfast chimichanga. The omelette was tasteless. My French toast was also tasteless. When I poured the syrup, the lid fell off into my plate and syrup drenched my toast. The French toast was a little soggy in the middle as well. I have had a tummy ache since eating it today. I will not return. Very poor service, unclean dishes, and poor food quality as well as not being able to provide signature items from the menu has left me with a bad taste in my mouth.

The coffee God's put their hands on this place and said "Let there be light, airy, foamy, delicious coffee." And so it was. This stuff is addictive and I feel like if my boyfriend didn't love it as much as I do, I'd be cheating. But I'm not bc he loves it too. Vanilla latte all day.

I started living here four years ago and in the last three years the office staff has become worse. Let just tell you i have had a number of problems from security of the build doors to find needles in the launrdy room. Well kromer investment corp is like we don't care. Now they have been acting as a utility in collecting a extra fee on top of rent for water, gas, sewer, grabage. Been pay like between 40 to 49 dollars extra ontop of rent now since they were told that they could not collect those moneies they are going to use a thrid party utility company to try and collect those moneies. Be aware of how your infromation is used. We do not have individual water meters here so how you cacullating my water useage and I have no gas to my unit so why do I pay gas? More people should ask more questions. The investment corp owens there realestate site http:\\www.aparmengeni.com and Riverwood Apartment.
They are not a good company to get involved with

The first time I've been to Sasha's was many many years ago when I was in the habit of going from neighborhood to neighborhood, look at some condo for sale, walk to lunch, and maybe be that creepy guy who tries to have a conversation with a total stranger.

Places like this make me almost want to immediately find some half assed ok place to move into.  This makes an already great neighborhood even better.

There are very few places that actually do crab in a salad well.  Sasha's is actually the only place in St. Louis where I haven't regretted ordering one.  The crab chunks were of good size and not slimy at all.

Aside from that, the crepes are excellent and the only thing I wish they had were more syrups like hazelnut for coffee.  The guy was like oh wait.... yeah... I thought you meant a latte with hazelnut liquor... sorry...

That would have been pretty freaking awesome if I didn't have the rest of the day with the mother-in-law ahead of me.

GREAT FOOD AND GREAT SERVICE!!!!  Our burgers were cooked to perfection.  Lots of sauce, toppings and sides to choose from.  Everything was very fresh and tasty.  Thank you Emily for the great service!!!!

Pro: Everything you need for your ethnic culinary tastes is here! I enjoy being able to get any Goya product needed for Puerto Rican dishes, most (if not all) ingredients for Thai food, and access to goat/lamb/oxtail any day of the week! 

Con: Closes at 8:00, wish it was 9:00

Bottom Line: Wanna shop "off the grid" for a meal "off the continent"? Start here!

I loved the atmosphere of this restaurant. I also thought the fresh juices were delicious and a nice touch. We ordered the chicken tostones nachos, and fish, steak, chicken, and pork nachos. The nachos were soggy and a little too sweet. I was disappointed with them, but I like the concept. The fish tacos were my favorite of the tacos we ordered. The fish was fresh and flavorful. 

Would I go to this restaurant again? Yes, but only for the happy hour. I also would only order the fish tacos.

I love going to this location because of their drive-thru! It's a close drive to campus, I come here all of the time.

Everyone working is always super kind and thoughtful. I have never had a bad experience at this location.

The drive-thru line is quick and they hand everything to you in a mug so it's keeps germs away. They also had you the credit card/chip reader so the employee doesn't need to touch your card. They are doing the best the can to keep everyone safe during the pandemic!

Had the best time here on a Friday night so I had to come back Saturday night and get some merch! Super friendly and knowledabe staff that were more than able to give me a recommendation. They allow outside food as long as you keep buying beer from them. There is a huge bottle and can selection as well so you're guaranteed to find something you like. Easy 5 stars!

YES YES YES! A must do in Nashville! I found this by accident when we had two very disappointing "BBQ" lunches during our stay. It is in walking distance from downtown and was just amazing! The restaurant itself is no frills and has that homey feeling from the moment you walk in. We absolutely could not stop raving about it! Any BBQ in the future is going to have a tough time beating this place. We got to see a pig on the BBQ, which was also quite the experience!
Just about every year my family host our annual family reunion picnic and every year its always a great success and this park plays a big factor in that thank you

The only saving grace was the bar tender- I visited April 29 and took a friend to show something that reminded us of good times- I was served raw chicken, bloody raw, and they replaced it with some of the worst bbq and beans ever- couldn't finish the nasty crap served. Biffs was great for decades- now no longer- sad. 

Bikers won't even eat the food... and that's bad!

Went in on 12/23 a Tuesday night. The service was sooooo bad! They have these new tablets to take your order and get refills but that's no excuse to stop giving service. Food took forever to get to our table and came out cold. You could tell it was sitting in the window for a while. Nobody came to check on us to see how our food was or clear dishes. We waited to see how long it would take for someone to come by but it was taking so long we had to give in and push the stupid tablet to call for service. When we said something about it the server was not very apologetic and the manager who was standing behind her while we were complaining didn't say a word to us. We will stick to the to go portion of this place cause the service sucks.

First day of school and my wife and I signed up our kids 12 years girl and 5 years boy for after care with PROtential Sports they suppose to pick them up after school at 3:15pm and  take them to the Club house in Rivercrest community (Riverview FL) where we live and have sports and homework done. My daughter called me at 4:00pm to tell me they was not pick up by ANYBODY what irresponsible people and then I tried to call them to be inform what's going on because I was working not response at all I pick up my kids from school (leaving my work early), after that I tried to call again with any response. Finally after 1 one later the Lady with name Nyree who is one of the owners called me back saying she doesn't know what happen "are you kidding me" who runs business like that, my kids was unattended alone in their school and those clowns didn't not what happen.
Hopefully this help to the people know more about "PROtential Sport" and does not sign with them your kids will be in a very high risk place with the wrong people.
Now I understand one of the paragraph that was bothering me a lot but I was not believing in that moment and says this: " I understand that PROtential Sport is NOT my child care provider and that my child is only participating in the sport clinics offer. I understand PROtential sport has an open door policy for the children and/ or parents and agree that my child can be picked up or can be free to leave at any time during or after the session or chosen clinic." So who the hell is NOT responsible for a 5 years old child?. Any way I'm glad this happen in the first day so I can't imagine what will be happen in that place. 
And for those people (PROtential Sport) go the HELL for not take care properly about children.

Amazing!!! If you are looking for a delicious meal cooked to perfection in New Orleans than look no further. Literally every item we had was cooked to perfection and tasted incredible. I had the chicken and andouille gumbo to start, and my girlfriend had the Brussels sprouts. We shared the plaquemines chicken, and it was tasty and so tender. The asparagus and potatoes that came with it were also very well cooked with just the right amount of crisp. To top it all off, there is the loveliest outdoor patio to enjoy when the weather is right. I cannot recommend this place enough.

A review on the BonChon chicken (not necessarily on the cafe itself).

http://www.bonchon.com

Don't bother going there though.  There is absolutely no information that will help you recreate these transcendent wings at home on your own.

Also, if you live within an hour of this place and haven't tried the chicken wings, WHAT ARE YOU WAITING FOR???

I have a friend who abhors fried chicken.  I'm talking about fried chicken like the old standby (Kentucky Fried Chicken).  I mean she has a point, the skin is thick and heavy, the grease get everywhere and it definitely passes the clear test if you rub it on a napkin (or a wall).  I happen to be a glutton and love the traditional southern fried chicken so I disagree with her there but I introduced her to these delectable treats this past Saturday night and she is in love!

They are crisp, flavorful and reheat nicely in the toaster oven.  What is there not to like?  The hot is a bit above average on the spicy meter but is nicely balanced by the sweet and vinegary daikon radish pieces provided.  The soy and garlic is tasty and not spicy at all.  The size of the wings are generous and not at all like most places' buffalo wings (which are more like pigeon wings).

Order at least 30 minutes ahead of time to pick up.  I live in NE Montgomery County but continue to come down to the area for my haircuts.  I ALWAYS make time to call up and get at least 2 boxes on every trip.  It's fun for the entire family.

20 dollars for a box of 18 (a bit expensive but *well* worth it).
Call ahead at least 30 minutes.
Bring cash for best results.

Bon appetite.
Dined in and at the front desk you can see the guys in the kitchen making the burgers and gloves were not used not very sanitary

One of the rudest experiences I've had in town. This incident happened to me for the second time today with the same server. One of the servers here has some serious issues with people taking photos, and thinks it's acceptable to behave in such a manner with customers. I regularly go to cafes in much larger cities and take photos for my blog, which is my job, without any issues ever or nasty looks. Servers pretty much always are kind and understanding. 

I've asked at Milktooth multiple times if they have any issues with photography after noticing this particular guy make faces and act a fool for no reason. They've told me they do not have any photography restrictions however this particular person literally made a comment about me in a condescending tone to another coworker when I walked up to the bar to take a quick photo for my blog. First of all, okay gossip queen learn to do your job properly second, you do not EVER treat paying customers like that please save the attitude and eye rolls for someone else. Really disappointing, childish, and inappropriate behavior. What else are you expecting when working in an aesthetic cutesy cafe? We live in 2019. People will take photos. There's nothing wrong with taking photos. They should honestly be glad people are even willing to promote their decor and business after what I experienced today. 

Every time I've made the mistake of visiting this place, something is continuously off about the behavior of this staff, I swear I always end up leaving in a bad mood. Even when serving food they just plop it in front of you and off they go, no manners no conversational skills. Please train them better Milktooth. I will not be back here again for the mediocre food and beyond rude service. Luckily there's lots of other cafes in town that know how to act properly with customers.

True car experts.  Always upfront with what is "needed" for the car vs what some other shops may try to "sell" you.  Very very reasonable charges and fees.  I always feel I got what I paid for at a value.

This place has bad service. I have almost Walked out múltiple times. The wait people stand around talking to each other. The chips and water were dropped off with no Comment. We had time to eat all of the chips and drink the water. Just as we gave up and were leaving, someone noticed that we hadnt received our food. We hadnt even ordered. When we have stayed for the full meal, it takes forever to receive the Bill. Another reason to just Walk out.

We stopped in here based off the numerous great reviews but we were sorely disappointed. The waitresses were great and the half order of biscuits and gravy but that's about it. We waited for almost 45 minutes for food and it didn't even come out together. I even asked where it was which I hate doing because I know it's not the waitress' fault.  The eggs were terrible, I dunno what they were cooked in and the bacon was burnt to a crisp. I also got french toast that was nothing special except it was on baguette bread. Waste of $30. 
We will not be coming back.

Since its opening week I have been back several times (due to it being dog friendly). Each time the staff is extremely friendly and give great service. Managers do the classic(y) -- "Is everything going well?" routine and they seem to be genuinely asking.

The food is fantastic, apparently they are kind of a pilot location for full food service/bar service. So far, so well. If this is how the rest of these are going to be ran -- I definitely will be buying a membership.

This isn't the classic WOB with only beer, no food, no liquor. Its all inclusive and definitely my new favorite spot near USF because they have delicious food, huge patio with fire pits that they welcome dogs too, and smiling faces. Nothing better than that!

After reading some of these other critiques.... I must be super laid back... "Beef-o-Bradys" reference?.... that couldn't be further from the truth. If we are going to compare other food places, id say at least a little better than the old Rack in soho (similar in atmosphere but way more open concept). Mind you the bar layout is amazing -- Beef o Bradys? Like has that guy ever eaten there? EEEK, way better than Beefs -- and almost all restaurants around USF. We needed this location!

PS. GET THE MAC'N'CHEEEESE! To die for! Tacos are good on a budget!
Took a snowblower in for service on November 17th.  It is now January 16th and they still haven't repaired it nor have they returned my call.  I don't get it.

Fine service, the bar staff is fantastic. Drinks are always between correctly mixed to great. Wait staff is also above average.

The food is relatively average. It's never bad but nothing to write home about.

Do not miss mac&cheese.

This place was awesome!  Amazing food.  Great service.  It's busy so be prepared to wait.  I still don't understand why with all this southern food I still can't find a place that serves sweet iced tea!!

All I have to say is happy hour. This place is the best deal in town. The bar menu is expansive, the drinks are interesting and the prices are unheard of. The calamari with roasted peanuts, candied ginger and mango was a refreshing take on an overdone appetizer. The olives and crostini are fantastic and taste fresh and firm. The burger is probably the best burger i have ever had and is cheaper than carls jr. during happy hour. I dont understand why there isn't a line out the door each day.

Now I have never been here for dinner and i have also never eaten in the main restaurant area so take this review for what it is. But, if you are looking for amazing and creative food with great happy hour deals, then you should come by.

Here is my one word of caution. It's summertime in Tucson and the last few times i have gone, the restaurant has been extremely warm. I dont know if they need a new AC unit or what but it's been pretty bad. However, warm or not... I'll be back.

This is your typical local pub that is great for large parties, private events, or a chill date night.  They have plenty of seating at the bar or in the dining area and the service is good.  The menu has a lot of options and numerous specials.  I wanted to start my meal with sangria which they don't have but the bartender was happy to make  it for me and it was delicious!  For my entrée I went with the pulled pork sandwich with a side salad. You can substitute fries for a side salad for a $2 up charge. The salad had a lot of fresh toppings and was a decent size.  Save room for dessert as they had a lot of great choices including a bread pudding topped with vanilla ice cream.
Fair price, on time, no hidden anything, very nice movers.  No need to write a book.  This is all the review you need.

We loved everything we had! We ordered the Malibu, 2 street tacos, 2 Johnny Utahs,  a Shrimp Baja bowl & the fire roasted avocado... so good we had to get it again. My son ordered a quesadilla and was amazed that anything that good could come from the kids menu.   Highly recommend!

Such a beautiful place and the folks who work here are super nice. We didn't stay too long (maybe an hour), it's no joke that this is a small zoo, but our stay was shortened by the fact that we have small children. ^^'
Seriously though, they really take care of this place and the animals. The entire zoo is an obvious act of love for the animals they keep and the plant life they have there. I honestly felt that I was at a botanical garden half of the time and my girls (four and two), had a great time. We had an excellent visit and we're happy to say that we will be coming back. The lady who worked at the gate for the parking lot was an absolute doll, and so friendly I really hope that when we come back she's there again. We're not from around here and she had so much information about the zoo and the local area, she's a treasure really! If you want a nice zoo that's not overblown and will actually be an enjoyment for you and your family, come to this one. You won't regret it. I look forward to going back and bringing along family when they visit. :)

If you like your birds with a backdrop of highway traffic, this is the bird refuge for you! It's basically a big, dirty-looking lake ringed by a busy, traffic-jammed road. Yes, there's a reasonably maintained woodchip path,and a couple of small docks jutting into the lake so you can look for birds, but we didn't see much in the way of birds.  And the path is pretty dreary, as is the setting. Give this one a miss.

Listen, I have been here a handful of times and I have only had the burger...because it is one of the best burger's I have ever had.  They are always busy but the wait staff does a great job of keeping up with it.  Expect a wait for a table anytime after 7pm, but it's worth it.

A dependable mechanic!  I've taken my car here for over a year now and they reliably get work done on time and at or under the estimate!  They are nice, friendly people to work with.  I feel they are honest in stating what work needs to be done.  Their prices are a third of what the dealer I used to go to charged me.

Shop owner Jackie went above and beyond to help make my 13-year-old baby's birthday a special and delicious celebration. I needed a cake that could withstand a commute through airport security, on a plane and into Virginia. She was incredibly patient with me as I explained what I needed for the pup through many emails. The ordering process was simple. You can place your order online and I decided on a 6-inch peanut butter and banana cake. There are several flavor options as well as a 4-inch option. I wanted a simple design and Jackie executed it well. What I appreciate most is that she uses natural food coloring. The cake was about $30 after taxes. 

I needed the cake the day before my flight but my business schedule made it difficult for me to pick up the cake from the shop. While she doesn't generally offer delivery services, Jackie helped to arrange it for a nominal fee including working with the staff at my hotel to get the cake into my suite's refrigerator. She updated me through the process and I was beyond delighted about the amazing effort she made to make my life easier. 

Most importantly, the cake remained intact through my travels and the birthday boy loved it! It was also a hit with the miniature schnauzer next door. The cake is like a human cake in softness and freshness. I admit it... I had a taste of the frosting. Cream cheese (without sugar)!

Thank you for everything, Jackie! Wishing your business everlasting success!

I couldn't possibly have any more great things to say about this place. The venue is small but for good reason. Nothing like a small family owned Italian restaurant. The food is absolutely out of this world good. I suggest you try it, it's a little on the pricey side and the fact that they don't do reservations kinda bites, but the food is well worth the wait and the price. Please show support for this restaurant!!!

Excellent beef pho soup on Rt 309 near Hatfield pa. Decent prices. I get extra noodles. I will eventually try other items. There are a few other restaurants in this stop mall. They are next to a good Mexican place as well.

Just visiting from sunny Texas and Yelp didn't lead me astray. This place makes an excellent latté and the extra shot of espresso put me right where I wanted to be. Very cool scenery inside as well. I'd recommend this place!

People - 5/5. Everyone was super nice. The employee was very polite and helpful. They had a pitcher of water out so you didn't have to ask for a cup of water

Seating - 2/5. Was trying to study and none of the spots were good for prolonged sitting. Maybe thats on purpose. Small tables that were uneven. 

Music- 3/5. Was playing country music, just not my thing but the volume was okay

Food & Drink - 2/5. I always get a croissant and an espresso. The chocolate croissant was okay, would have been nice if they could heat it up it didn't look like they had equipment for that. The espresso was the most acidic thing I have ever tasted. I didn't enjoy it, but everyone else seems to like their other drinks so maybe thats the move.

Amazing amazing amazing!   I wanted something sweet during this Coronavirus lockdown so I googled bakeries nearby.  I had never been here so I stopped in.  The hazelnut/chocolate mousse was divine.  I bought a loaf of Italian bread also.  I have never had any better!  Delicious!
Disorganized. The terminal needs clear signs and directions for passengers. Most passengers are lost not knowing what to do.
Nicely's Jamaican food is soooo good! This is always my first choice for Jamaican food. The young lady behind the counter is always friendly, nice and welcoming. You will not be disappointed.

The best authentic Chinese food in the area!   Everything on the menu is spectacular.  
Henry, the manager, is always welcoming and helpful.  
Just go.

I'm a big fan of Mitchell & Ness. I love this store -- and more importantly, so does my fiance. I've shopped online many times but this was my first time visiting the brick & mortar location.

The selection is a bit limited, which is understandable considering how much they have online, but you can always ask for them to check in the back. I actually stopped in looking for a certain team's gear in a certain size, and they only had 2 or 3 random items -- two of which I've already bought! Oh well. Better luck on the internets, I guess. 

(For what it's worth, the employees were super helpful and pulled any items they could find in the size I wanted. Really nice and willing to help you find what you need.)

But the good thing is that there were a lot of sales! I managed to find a really cute striped button-up Yankees hoodie there in my size for around $40 -- much less than I've paid for anything with an interlocking NY on it before. It sounds like their stock rotates often, according to the sports seasons, so I must have caught them on the tail-end of baseball. There was still a lot of Phillies stuff, and it took a second for me to remember where I was hahah.

All in all, it's a nice store to browse, and hopefully you can catch a deal ..... but if you're looking for something specific, you're better off shopping online.
This is my new spot. My husband and I always wanted to try their square pie. Let me say it is so delicious!
Great place.  Always clean.  Nice free samples of donuts sometimes.  They also serve LeBus Multigrain bread which is my favorite!

How am I just now trying out this place after a year of being in Indiana? Hands down my favorite craft brewery that we have found so far in Indiana and I'm very comfortable saying the beer rivals the best in Colorado. The brewers here take inventiveness to a WHOLE new level! We did a tasting of all 8 that are currently available & were not disappointed by any of them. They all had solid flavors, were honest to good beer that was still true to its beer style, yet had that extra unique factor. Think the Willy Wonka of beer. I'm honestly not a big fan of crazy additions to beer that are just trying to hide the fact that the brewmaster can't just make an honest beer that honors the style- that is not the case here to be clear. Fun and crazy, yes. But still good IPA in its own right. The service was just as fun as the flavors. Live the vibe inside, spacious with plenty is seating. Outside seating was nice that it was offered but busy intersection, not the most conducive. I appreciate that they have plenty of options for BYOF (bring your own food) & free popcorn & water available too. The homage to stats? Total clincher for this nerd :)

I still visit the shop regularly and have a new favorite - chicken pot pies! I didn't know I missed eating them since having to go on a gluten and dairy free diet 10 years ago. My gluten-eating family actually really  likes them, too, so I pick up enough for all of us and make that dinner.   

A note to all the complainers about consistency and taste: you can't compare gluten free baked goods to regular wheat-based baked goods. It just isn't the same and never will be. It's all about redefining your expectations - it should be a good tasting gluten-free product. Period. If you are eating gluten-free as a "lifestyle choice" - get over yourself - those of us with allergies don't have a choice and appreciate having options that don't include baking it ourselves. 

Sorry, I'll get off my soapbox now.

We walked into this Home Depot late last Wednesday to get a washer and dryer. We easily found an employee to help us narrow down the choices, which at first seemed overwhelming. We had a pretty good idea of what we wanted, but he gave us a personal recommendation of a brand he had used for years. The only thing I did't like was that Home Depot outsources their delivery team, so make sure to check for dents or marks prior to signing for the delivery! We had no personal problems with this, but were warned so severely I felt that there had been a lot of issues with the delivery service.

I openly admit :  I cook often and thus can be a bit of a cranky food snob.

Yet I still find myself craving a BK Whopper a few times a year!

( Please forgive me for not joining the "All Fast Food is Evil" club yet. )

This is my nearest Burger King.  Sometimes the food is actually up to par.  You have a 50% chance.

I've quit going here several times.  Then a year or so passes and I try them again, out of convenience and perhaps HOPE?

NOPE!  Tried again tonight...  Just more disappointment...

The ownership and management of this BK location seem to be happily clueless for well over a decade now.  Other FFood joints in the area actually manage to run a professional and more consistent store.

While I still love a good BK Whopper I believe it's time for me to say goodbye to the Gateway BK store #87 forever......RIP

First time here. My tech was Minh. Very happy with my glitter pink and whie. While price was more than I have paid elsewhere, I really like how my nails look very natural.  Make an appointment because they were busy by 9:30 am. I had gone 2 weeks ago and they were slammed. I made my appointment and was taken right on time. Done in one hour. I will be back for sure.

I came to Invoke with a year of what I thought was good yoga practice under my belt. Invoke changed everything! 

Invoke was recommended to me by a friend and I was hooked after 3 classes. The unlimited two weeks for $30 is a steal, and a great opportunity to feel out all the teachers. Cheryl is by far my favorite teacher! She kicks your butt but also challenges you and supports you without hesitating. Laura is also fantastic. Her gentle voice helps you truly relax in yin classes and she has a great way of simplifying each pose for practitioners of all levels. Workshops give you the opportunity to deepen your practice as well as an alternative to the normal schedule. 

Invoke's atmosphere is inviting and the teachers always welcome new students. If you are new to Invoke I'd advise you to come early, as most classes fill up quickly. Oh, and be prepared to work up a sweat! You leave class feeling like you got beat up and rejuvenated at the same time! Best investment I've ever made for myself. You'll be cursing yourself out in the middle of class, but I promise it's worth it. Hooray for self-care. You deserve it!

This is one of the best quiet lunch spots in Bellevue.
The menu is huge, with something for everyone that likes Asian cuisine. They've upped their prices by 20-30% or so since my first review, but the food is even better.
I had never tried a Turmeric Curry before I saw it on the menu here, but it's now my favorite thing to get at Dao. The stir fry dishes and appetizers and really spot on as well, and the portion sizes are huge.
The service is friendly, and it's a nice quiet laid back atmosphere. Give it a try if you like Thai/Chinese/Sushi fusion restaurants. Dao is definitely a couple notches above your average strip mall Asian bistro.

Good food! No great food! Fast service! Great waitresses! Although you never want to get the granny to run the register...not very nice and would not honor the sign hanging up stating cheesecake on sale for 3.00! She told the waitress to take the sign down and that she had to charge me full price....that's okay...I'm good...integrity is everything to me!

Yum!! Grilled pork was smothered in maple syrup. Oxtail pho was devoured by the boy & hubs had chicken wings pan fried in fish sauce.

Spring rolls were superb and had one extra herb that I'm still trying to place.

REALLY nice atmosphere for a date! We made a dinner reservation early at 4:30, and it was nice because by 5:30, the place was packed and loud. So glad we went earlier! Our server was adorable and was so appreciative of our tip! She was very attentive! It's the cutest place and my favorite date spot now! Plus, it's a vegan restaurant so you don't feel guilty! Highly recommend!

We got potato croquettes (super cute with a yummy spicy mayo sauce), eggplant bao buns (really tender eggplant and crunchy daikon/carrots), Korean fried tofu (good spicy), mushroom risotto (SO YUMMY, creamy, and nice freeze-dried mushrooms), and a chocolate creme pot (a wonderful, velvety texture with bananas)!

My S.O. got an Old Fashioned cocktail and he loved it. I got their blackberry ginger soda (mocktail) and it was so delicious! Spicy and warm from the fresh ginger and incredibly refreshing!

I had an appointment to install a sink and a minor shower repair for last Friday between 5-9PM. By 9, the technician had not yet arrived. After I called Personal Plumbing, I was informed they were swamped and set up a time for Saturday morning. The technician arrived on time, but then said he did not have the basic parts required to finish the installation and stated he would call later that day after he picked up the parts from the plumbing supply company. I never received a call. Since then, I have left 3 messages and have not had a call back. Very unprofessional.

Love this place! Beer and Food is amazing! Employees are always happy and very helpful! You can tell that they really love their job! You can make reservations, which I would suggest, otherwise you do have over an hour wait. Its also a bit on the pricey side, but much worth it on date night :)

I placed a 7:30pm order via the Sonic App for a OREO Cheesecake shake add carmel topping. I arrived at 7:34pm, payment and check-in was very user friendly. The order was delivered within 2 minutes. I was surprised the carhop was not wearing gloves or a mask. She was friendly and professional. The drink did taste good, however there was no trace of carmel flavoring. Next time, I'll skip the flavor add.

This place is close to my office.  We frequent this eatery.  Burritos and tacos are great.  It's quick, good, filling and won't break the bank for lunch.  The only complaint I would have - tight corridors, and maybe it's not as clean during lunch.  They can't keep up with the clean up during rush times.   Even a cool place to swing by during the weekends for a quick lunch.  Can't get enough of the burritos!
This place has an amazing selection of tile as well as grout. The staff is very helpful and are approachable.

I wish there was a 0 star rating , guy in the drive thru was so rude , I couldn't hear him thru the speaker , then I said hi! He replied I TOLD  YOU TO WAIT!  I just left I should share this location in my social media so people never go there I'm sure 500k followers will want to know about it!

We decided to go to Irish 31 for a night out with friends and what amazing night we had. Our server Cassie not only made us feel welcome but our drinks were never empty. The food was delicious but the service was top notch and we couldn't have had better service!  Thank you Cassie for making our night out enjoyable!!!
Haven't been in since the pandemic and was extremely disappointed. Food tasted bad and they had no coffee at 930 in the morning. I miss the quality pre-pandemic :(

Three stars for service ! Sherrod was excellent it's a shame they don't have a kitchen to back up such s great server! Drinks were good! Food not so much! Mussels were the best thing we had! Followed by my steak frites! Halibut came out over cooked! Sent it back & asked for my steak to be kept warm while my husband's halibut was made over! They missed the mark again! Thank heaven I had a groupon because they're over priced for what you get which would be ok if it was good!
Pizza was so burnt and hard. Definitely won't order from here again. No comparison to NY style at all.

Rasoi is absolutely fabulous and delicious food in a great environment. I've been eating Indian food since I was small, and this is high quality and tastes like it should! So glad to have this gem nearby in Ybor.

Wow!!!! Is all I can say. I am so glad I found this company. Everyone I spoke to was super nice and helpful. The pricing is very reasonable especially over replacing fixtures. We just bought our home and it was 80's style all the way. Not anymore. Our showers and tubs look amazing. I will be calling them back soon to do our counter tops and sinks. Highly recommend.

Always a perfect experience for me. Today for brunch I had Chorizo Benedict. They either have a world class chef there or somebody got really lucky with this dish; eggs cooked perfectly, avocado and tomato at peak ripeness, chorizo spicy but not overly so as with so many places, every detail of the plate was on point. Even Gordon Ramsey couldn't find fault! Yeah, they can get busy and sometimes you might have to wait longer than you would like. Get over it. The wait, if any, is always worth it. Prices are quite reasonable for this quality of ingredients and waterfront setting.

There for a private birthday party. We used upstairs space, which was great (except for the actual long step stairs).  Food, drinks and staff were all superb. Will go back next time we go through Philadelphia

This place is a hidden gem in the pines of Medford!  The staff is super friendly and accommodating and the food comes out hot and fresh.  As a new resident to the Garden State (in a suburb of Philly) I have been on a quest to find the best cheesesteak.  By far, Donkey's  is my favorite!  I love the poppy seed roll that it comes on and the meat is nice and thin.  I love having this place in my backyard....no need to make a trip to Philly for a decent cheesesteak!

The so-called "food" they serve here is hospital food quality.  I don't understand how this place (as well as the other place across the street from it) stays in business, when there are so many genuine cheese steak places in Philly.

For a proper cheese steak, you are much better off going to Pastificio's or, even better, Steaks N Shakes, where the prices are very reasonable without cutting back on quality of ingredients....AND the parking situation is far better at Steaks N Shakes.

Basically, Pat's is an incredibly over rated tourist trap.

After a long morning listening to layers tell lawyers how to be better lawyers, I had about an hour to catch some lunch and well I couldn't leave the hotel because my friend had went shopping with my car. 

So I decided to break my golden rule and try the hotel restaurant.  Avanzare is very nice and the choice of booths or table really let you decide how intimate of a meal you want.  Dining alone, I chose to sit at a table and watched as the staff was overloaded with my colleagues.  

Rather than forcing people to wait or turning them away, they brought in hotel staff to help the servers and hosts meet the demand.  The menu was very limited and because the hotel staff was supporting the service staff, I knocked off a star because I missed out on a great off menu chicken pasta special.  

I had a English style club sandwich (****stars)  the fried egg was a delightful delight but a field green salad just didn't fit right.  Kudos to the kitchen staff for hunkering down and getting my order out within 20 minutes.  

I agree with Lothari O.  I would search out this restaurant, but it has given me hope in hotel restaurants again.

After calling 5 pizza joints for pizza delivery on the 4th of July, the 5th told us to call Magazine Pizza for delivery. We were staying near the quarter in the Marriott and had no luck getting delivery. We called, ordered and expected sub par pizza to fill our kids up instead of walking back out in the heat with two little kids. This pizza blew our minds. It was awesome! We have a new pizza place when we visit New Orleans. Even the delivery driver was patient as she waited a couple extra minutes for us to come down to grab it in the lobby. I would highly recommend this to anyone who is lucky enough to try it!

Well I started my last review with "maybe I should wait...." and I should have trusted that instinct.  My interactions with the dealership were stressful and gave me anxiety.  

Claiming the Oil Changes that were worked into the sale was such a hassle, eventually I just stopped.

Trying to get a callback for something as simple as replacing my remote starter fob was impossible.  Twice they said "we'll get that information for you and call you back".  I ended up just going in to talk to them about it.

I decided to take my car back to them for an oil change in November 2021 cause I wanted them to check some other things out.  I'm not even kidding, when I walked in, I stood in the doorway while the dude walked around, no more than 5 feet away from me.  He didn't acknowledge me until my boyfriend walked in behind me.  Then he finally asked what he could do for us.

My car is out of warranty now, so as far as I'm concerned, I never have to go back there.
Chef was so rude. He mortified me by screaming across the restaurant calling me a "fat bitch". Wouldn't come here ever or give them a single dollar. Avoid!!!!!!!
Rude. Got rotten egg rolls. They hung up on me when I called.

This is like a Greek version of Chipotle. You go down the line and pick out what you want in your bowl, salad, or gyro. I love their seasoned chicken and tzatziki sauce. It was light and super filling. Definitely a nice change in a weekly lunch spot.

Isabella's has a big menu with a variety of choices.  Their problem is consistency.  I've ordered take out from there multiple times, but it seemed increasingly that something would be great one day and inedible the next.  My last attempt was when I ordered the penne ala vodka...it tasted like soap or chemicals.  Finally, I realized that the taste was probably vodka...like really cheap vodka.  They must have soaked he pasta in it...or used half the bottle.  Anyway, it was late and it seemed kind of weird to call and complain that the food tasted like booze. So, it went in the trash, and I gave up on them...lots of other options in the neighborhood.

We did the half-day City Tour with Muriel A.  on 1/27/17.  With her charming French accent, Muriel taught us a lot about New Orleans.  Clearly she is very knowledgeable about the city and has a passion for it.  Tours by Isabelle provide very professional, well organized and enjoyable tours.

Sara & Bill C.

If I could give them -1000 stars, I would. They are nothing but liars. Their ads are lies, their whole practice is a lie. I had a serious malpractice medical case. They would not hear or talk to me!!
FOR THE MONEY NOT THE PEOPLE!

I had the pleasure of having the lunch buffet here on two separate occasions and all I can say is WOW!

This is one of the best Indian lunch buffets I have ever had!  I sampled almost everything on the buffet and loved everything!  Favorite thing?   The samosas!  They were fantastic!  

I don't get to Yardley often, but if I am there this place will see me again.  And I'd love to visit for dinner sometime and get their full menu!

I really enjoyed my first meal here, and it won't be my last. I had the avocado salad, and sashimi appetizer. Both of them were fantastic. Decent sized portions at good prices, great taste, fresh sashimi. The service was also very good!

Sat outside for a yummy lunch here today.  They had huge umbrellas to keep us safe from the brutal sun.  We had a very tasty non traditional grilled cheese and a great BLTA-avocado.  Our service was perfect and all topped off with a great French Rose'.  Will definitely go back.

Great value and delicious food.  Portions are generous.  Ask for the bruschetta. It's not on the menu anymore, but it's so good.  The bread for the bruschetta is a big fluffy disc with garlicky bruschetta on top.  Mmmm.

Caesar salad is pretty standard.  Calamari doesn't have the over breading of many eateries.  We've had the bolognaise, vegetarian, shrimp (must admit they were skimpy on the shrimp- there were 6, yes I counted), gnocchi in cheese sauce. 

We appreciate the ambience.  Tables aren't overly crowded and the room itself is bright and quiet.  You never feel rushed.

Holy hotness! I was in the mood for something spicy so my boyfriend and I stopped by here completely on a whim. The first thing I noticed was the sign in the window that urged customers to call ahead. I'm a BIG fan of signage! This was obviously a hint that it may take them a while to cook my order. (I'm ok with quality food taking longer to cook) Honestly, I would much rather read a posted sign and make a decision based on the sign, than stand around like an idiot wondering where my food is or why it's taking so long. I appreciate that they at least took the time to post that sign. 

Moving right along...

I love food that makes me cry, so I went straight for the HOT tenders. Normally, when spicy food reaches a certain hotness, you can no longer taste anything (except pain.) These tenders were perfect! The seasonings were SO delicious! There was a gentleman that came from the kitchen to ask us how everything tasted and he mentioned that they just recently adjusted their recipe. So, for anyone that may have not been impressed with this place before, it might be worth another trip. In my opinion, yes I had tears running down my face, but I could still taste those awesome seasonings. THAT is great hot chicken! 

My boyfriend and I will definitely be returning for more!  (I'm eating my leftovers as I type this and shockingly enough, they are still delicious even after re-heating in the toaster oven.) 

My ONLY complaint (and it's a non-issue), one cup of ranch dressing is never enough for 6 tenders. Perhaps offer two?

The only issue, and not sure if anything can be done, but the ladies blast their videos and
talk  programs in the shower area.  It's so loud you can't even hear the TV. They have head phones but don't use them.  There are signs that phone use is permitted in the lounge area only but the sraff doesn't enforce it..

Amazing patio area! Highly recommend making a reservation during peak hours. Really cool atmosphere inside and out that feels green and fresh. Good for groups of friends because the tables are large. Salsa is also delicious with a kick of spice to it. 

Lunch menu is flame. Really neat bar area too!

He said: Some of the kids didn't really know about Thai, but they were game and tried it.  They all liked the food.  Curry great.  We sat next to the little fountain water thing and that was pleasant. Service quick. 

She said: I ordered the Pad Thai, but ended up stealing my husband's curry!  The food was simply delicious! Ended the meal with the banana dessert. Have it!  You need to try it!!! Yum!!!

Disappointing. We had good memories of dining at Maximo's over the years and this last time, it looks like everything- owner, chef, decor- has changed. It's still lovely but the food was nothing special and the service, while friendly, was just poor. Ordered a salad that came after the meal. Ordered Brussels sprouts that never came (although we were charged for them). Another salad was soggy. Ordered the fish of the day seared (supposedly with habanero infused oil)- had no taste and didn't come with the pasta. When reminded, the waitress brought the pasta much later - it was to have been my sons dinner and needless to say he was cranky. Another diners pasta with white sauce had no sauce- again, no taste. We honestly couldn't wait to leave. For that price, we certainly expected more and won't be back. Sad, especially after all the good memories.

Our server was Karion, excellent waitress she was so kind and very sweet. She seemed to really care about her customers (us) and she was able, though crowded and busy, to keep a conversation going with us-- and by us I mean a table of 5, she maintained eye contact with each of us. Great service and she even wrote a thank you with a huge smile on the check!!

We came to watch the game and get a good bite to eat. My parents got the large waffle cheese fries and the devils on horseback. The other couple with us also got the devils on horseback.

Very inexpensive and extremely good food, the hush puppies had jalapeños in them so I did not try them but I was told they were very sweet. 

I got the Uncle B's chicken tenders, so good and they came with waffle fries and I got 2 honey mustard dipping sauces. There was so much food I should have asked for more. It is served with one but I needed two. Along with my tenders I got a side salad with sprite. For dessert I had the salted caramel cheesecake, and it was the best whipped cream I have ever had.

I really recommend this sports bar if you are interested in a great atmosphere to watch the football game, get a good meal, and a beverage whether it is alcoholic or non-alcoholic. We sat in the bar area at a table, and while it was loud, it was not overbearingly loud-- I could still hear our server and the people I sat with. There are many TVs everywhere you look, 5 that are sitting almost in front of me from every perspective, and those are just the ones I can see. There are many more.

I called and ordered a Large cheese pizza with jalapeños and half chicken. They told me 20 minutes. I walked in 20 min later and the pizza was ready. The lady at the front (Danielle/Dee Dee) was extremely kind and checked my pizza to make sure it was correct. Turns out the jalapeño wasn't on the full pizza. She instantly turned to the manager and he not only let me keep the wrong pizza but also made me a small jalapeño pizza. They charged me for a small one topping instead of the large and gave me free ranch and a free drink. Their service was phenomenal and they made me feel like a valued customer. The mistake was so minute but they made up for it multiple times and even apologized for their mistake. The pizza and garlic bread was amazing. I'll definitely be coming here again. Thank you ABC Pizza!

You must try the Prince Albert!  LOL - Actual dessert's name is Prince Albert, anyway, it was delicious.  Ok, serious now...Totally reasonable priced, very relaxed atmosphere, and service was good looking, I mean good!!  Just go and you will see what I mean...

I used to go to Pierre and Carlo a couple years ago before moving to Chicago. I loved my stylist, Kim, but she has since left. I am back in town for a long visit and need a nice cut for a wedding I'm in. Barbara did my highlight and cut. The color was good, but the cut was weak. I should've brought pictures with me, but I found pictures at the salon and explained further what I wanted. She didn't seem to be listening to me.  I wanted something a little funky with choppy layers and I got a boring, round cut. She seemed scared to give me choppy layers. I asked her to do more at the end a couple of times, but it didn't work. I was tired of being there, so I just said it was fine and left. 

The stylist barely spoke to me the entire two hours I was there. She just gossiped with her assistant the entire time. I tried to make conversation, but she gave me short replies and went back to talking to her assistant. I don't love making small talk, but I don't like to be ignored either. We just met, so there's a lot to talk about and she made no effort. I am moving back to Philly in a year and am looking for a new salon - both for the long term and for my upcoming Philadelphia wedding. This one won't be my salon again. It's disappointing b/c I used to love it there; I guess it was just Kim that I loved and not the salon.

I will say my hair looked better than when I came in. I hadn't had time to get my hair done for a while, so I was in major need of a cut and color - almost anything was going to be an improvement. However, I'm now on Yelp looking for another hair salon in Philadelphia in the hopes of getting a better cut before my friend's wedding. It's a large salon, so maybe another stylist would be better. Or, maybe it was a just an off day. Or, maybe the stylist and I just didn't click. Regardless, I'm not going back to PIerre and Carlo. I wish I knew where Kim went!
This spot is a great place to hit and absolutely loved the food and great customer service!
Bang Bang shrimp and Fish and Chips! Good service, very nice and helpful. A simple good seafood restaurant.
A joint with good beer and a decent selection of tasty vegan food is alright in my book.

I will not go again and will discourage others from going. Went today because I had a deal for a day coupon. I think they mis-represented on the coupon. This place is not a restaurant. It's a market/deli where you can get reheated food. Nothing tastes great after it's been in a microwave, especially naan. The samosas were soggy. The cheese in my paneer was either soggy or crunchy (microwave?). The portions were small. And as others have referenced, the service was terrible. I could barely get their attention to pay.
Very good food.   I was in Nashville and wanted to best sea food.   This place was refered to us several times. We went and was very happy we did

Dear Wishing Town thank you for being the icing to my rushed civil wedding ceremony cake.  I came to you guys two days before the event and had a lot of questions and was rather pushy.  I wanted things changed and modified and you had no idea why.  Despite this the person helping me was patient, professional and had a very rosy disposition, she even called the baker to make adjustments.  The result?  A perfect cake and a lot of people I know asked where I got the cake (and it was just about $40 with all fresh fruit add on).  Needless to say I can't wait to order more fresh whipped creamy chiffon strawberry shortcake.
What a gem! Great place to walk around or enjoy the lake views, beautiful nature...nice playground as well.

Ignore the negative comments! Found this place on a top ten Nashville breakfast places to visit list. It absolutely deserves to be on that list. Many great looking menu items - hard to decide. Settled on Prime rib hash. It was incredible! Perfectly cooked yukon gold potatoes in the hash and topped with 3  PERFECTLY cooked eggs. Normally don't eat this much at breakfast,  but I ate every bite because it was soooooo good. My husband said his French toast was pretty yummy.  Great service - Eric was our waiter (I think) - food came quickly. Good coffee and cup was always full. Relaxing place. Everybody, including the mgmt, was very attentive- especially for a busy New Year's day. The restaurant was clean and orderly. Waited tables in a fine dining establishment many years ago and I am quite impressed with what 417 has to offer.

What a Blessing!!  Had the pleasure of attending Sunday mass at the Monastery of Poor Clares in Santa Barbara.  Came here at a time when we really needed help in giving us direction.  We meant to give our petition and prayer request to the Colettine Nuns who are at the Monastery.  After listening to the Priest's homily, life became clearer and calmer.  AMEN!!!!
The cubans are the way to go. Came on a whim and wasn't disappointed.  Just order the 10 inch. You won't regret it.

Ordered the mussels and clam steamer entree it was great. Fresh and about  a dozen of each with 2 sides. Fun place good service.
We got there by the Jolly Trolley from our hotel Sheraton Sand Key door to door for $5 for 2 of us. Great night

Always fresh, fast, friendly and most importantly, delicious! Our go-to in Franklin. Prices are good, especially with their coupons.

Gotta try the old world pepperoni!

Horrible. I wouldn't recommend this place at all. The tall guy at the front (around 6 pm on 5/6/21) lacks customer service skills and who's attitude was very off-putting and made the visit extremely uncomfortable.  The ice cream sandwich was given to us (as pictured) and was a complete mess. Don't waste your time here, go somewhere else.

Took my car here twice before and David was very knowledgable, personable, friendly, and easy to work with. They work well with insurance companies and rental companies. They do a fantastic job and definitely prove their trust. Will return if, God forbid, something happens.

Such a great location! Right near the Villanova Campus. A fresh offering of farm to table menu with healthy options for anyone to indulge in!! 
I had multigrain pancakes w blueberries and maple syrup, others in our party had avocado toast ( delightful!) and we could not stop raving about their bread!!
Will definitely be back again!!

If you're looking for a way to change your day for the worse, just try shopping or calling in to Dick's fishing department when you really need help.  

Simply put, you can't find an associate on the floor when you're there and they definitely don't want to answer the phone when you call in.  

Worse yet, when approaching a manager for help after being ignored for too long, he/she is only as helpful as the response they get from their walkie-talkie.  

I can get all that and better prices from Wal-Mart.  

So long, dicks!

The Good:
