We visited Firetruck Eastside the week before Christmas during the coronapocalypse.  Out & about exploring Tucson, we had our pooch with us and decided that it was lunchtime.  So we pulled into the parking lot and saw that they were open for outdoor seating.

It wasn't crowded, and they said they could accommodate the pup (who was leashed & lay under the table the entire time).  The wait staff was friendly & helpful, patient with me & the bride trying to figure out how we might split up the various dishes.  It was a nice day, so the patio was well-lit and warm.

All around nice ambiance.


The Bad:

Nothing, really.  The beer was wet & somewhat flavorful.  I'd've appreciated the opportunity to spend more time and try a few different flavors, but that was not to be for an afternoon lunch-on-the-fly.  The food was mostly-standard brewpub fare.  Nothing was overdone, but nothing stood out, either.  "Not particularly memorable" is the best phrase.


It was a fine lunch.  The food went down, stayed down & satisfied.  The beer quenched.  The pup stayed calm & happy.  We tipped well, which translates to happiness with the service.

We certainly wouldn't hesitate to go back, but Firetruck's not the place we're going to go looking for.

Took a chandelier purchased in London to SB Lampshades for repair.
Zack & Nancy did an excellent job repairing it, paying attention to fine details to preserve is authenticity and patina.  Have done business at this store in the past and the quality of knowledge and service has been consistent.  This well travelled old chandelier will light up our house and life.  Thank you for a job well done.

Food and beer are good, but we had the worst service ever. The waitress had to retake our order because "someone literally ate the paper I had it on," brought us extra drinks and had to be asked repeatedly to take it off of our bill, and couldn't remember what beer she had served us when we asked for another round (we were drinking the draft beer she recommended). We tried talking to the manager, but he was more concerned with telling us what he did right. Then the waitress came back and admonished us for telling on her to her boss. I will never eat there again.

Service is great, beer is cold but the food is terrible. 
I travel for work so I visit lots of restaurants, bars etc. including multiple Twin Peaks locations. The food here is terrible. I've visited a few times now because there's not many sports bar type places here in Boise. I've tried multiple different dishes and appetizers... all very below par. 
Lots of tvs, cold beer and beautiful women but don't come for the food
The staff was excellent and extremely helpful.  I would stay at this embassy suites again.

I got a pastry this morning, and it was absolutely superb. Unfortunately, I can't remember the name of it. It was mostly a chocolate ganache encased in a chocolate shell with little crunchy bits embedded here and there in the shell. The base tasted like a thin graham, and the topping was a light bar of chocolate over a citrus mousse. It was very tasty, though I thought the citrus flavor could have been stronger (the strong, earthy chocolate flavor overpowered it). The shop is very cute. I'd love to come here for a sandwitch some time.

We accidentally discovered this place while walking around China town. The experience is really unique and interesting.   They have frozen stones on which they pour liquids and make ice cream in front of you. While I liked the experience but the person making the ice cream was nit very interested in his job.  Nor did they show any interest in making the ice cream. No one was talking to kids taking videos n clicking pics. So there was no 2 way interaction. Also, the ice cream was strictly ok. They have multiple flavors and we liked the match and caramel ice creams. Over all it is definitely a ine time visit and a nice place to entertain out of town guests. Would recommend to check them out.

Recommended by Hunt Magazine we were very pleasantly surprised by the lovely ambience and the superb food. As anticipated, located in mushroom country, a good bit of the menu has dishes incorporating mushrooms. As a starter delicious mushroom soup was perfect for a cold winters day. Since we were there for lunch and being of light appetites we ordered 2 appetizers to share which worked out perfectly. The mushroom crepe & the shrimp were well presented and flavorful with just the right seasoning. For dessert we shared the pomegranate cheesecake which was exactly the right way to conclude the meal. The owner/manager & wife to the chef was very gracious and came over to talk to us giving us a little history of the restaurant as well as the local scene in Kennett Square. We'll certainly be back soon. It's BYOB for now but they're moving to a bigger location just a little ways away in Jan 2019 which will have a small bar for cocktails and wine selections to complement the menu. 

Went yesterday for my daughter's 40th birthday party and had a great time as anticipated. Met all our expectations in the warm ambience, excellent service & the superlative food we all enjoyed. What a memorable dining experience!  Portabello's is definitely in our A-list! Thank you Sandra & a salute to your husband the Chef for making our celebration worth the special occasion.

I cannot recommend Kelsey and Sophie highly enough. They were the BEST money we spent on our entire event, and yet they were so reasonably priced compared to all the other planners I met with! If you read this, Sophie, you could definitely get away with charging more, you guys are worth your weight in gold!!  

I got married 9/23/17 and it was PERFECTION thanks to these ladies. I worked most directly with Kelsey in terms of planning and coordination and day-of execution, as Sophie was committed to the other weddings the same day, booked before me. Let me say though, I would have had NO idea this team had any other weddings on my day unless they told me, because I felt exceptionally well taken care of the whole way through. 

Kelsey is incredible. She is so thoughtful, efficient, and all-around cool. She 100% understood what I was going for with every little decision, and she either made my exact vision happen or recommended an even better idea. She put up with all my resistance over lighting and facilities charges I didn't expect, and calmly walked me through why each little piece was necessary and happily helped me cut all the extra fluff. I felt like she TOTALLY respected our budget and still made our event feel lavish, even extravagant. 

What I most appreciate about Kelsey and Amazing Days is the work she put in behind the scenes in the final 6 weeks leading up to the wedding day. I was a mess - I had graduated from graduate school, started a new job, and moved apartments all within 3 months of my wedding (I would not recommend this strategy to other brides). It was all I could do to answer a few emails from vendors each night and stay on top of invites, the wedding party, etc. I felt like I was at my wits end and I was BARELY scratching the surface of what needed to really be done to make the wedding day happen. In swooped Kelsey, so professional, so organized, and she made everything happen and took away all my stress. I can't even list everything she did because I don't know it all, she was kind enough to never make me feel bad or guilty about all the things I was missing. 

My wedding day was INSANE. I'm biased, I know, but it was the most gorgeous thing I've ever seen, and it was the most fun day in the world!!! I loved every second of it, and the little glitches along the way were handled by Kelsey with grace. I didn't know half the things that went "wrong" with other vendors until months later, because Kelsey never let me stress about it, she just handled it all quietly and made it appear perfect in my eyes!!! I love you Kelsey!!! I'm already thinking about a little vow renewal event in a couple years just so I can work with you again....

I am in town visiting for my birthday and I was craving birthday crepes, so I went on yelp to find places in Reno and this place popped up. So glad we decided to go here. The crepes were amazing. We spilt a savory and a sweet one. They were huge, delicious, and a good price! Also the ladies were super friendly and kind. 

There were lots of crepes on the menu to choose from. They also had a lot of lattes and mochas to choose from. 

We are only here for a few days, but are already planning to go back!
Food is good but the racist manager covered in tattoos is very rude. Would never eat there again

What can I say? This place is awesome. Every dish we ordered was tasty, fresh, and professionally presented.  Loved my arugula salad and the sweet dressing. I had chicken added to this salad. The chicken was grilled but a bit over salted. Otherwise, the dish would've been perfect. We also ordered the salmon (yum!), and the egg on a gluten free bread with gorgeous roasted potatoes. Service was the best!!

Love this crazy bowls. Get the spicy Thai wrapper or as a bowl it's tasty. Add a black bean egg roll perfect to go with your meal. And of course the salsa bar for great flavor. Food is great, order carefully they seem to nickel and dime for a lot of stuff, also get your chips on a bowl not a bag, way better deal. Great place for a good quick healthyish meal.

Our new favorite Chinease,  Vietnamese, Dim Sum Restaurant.  Very nice dining area with very friendly staff.  Nice soft music and aesthetic furnishings.   The grilled lemon grass chicken with broken jasmine rice was delicious.  Everyone's stand by General Tsao chicken was seasoned just right, not to hot with a great homemade tsao sauce.  Steamed dumplings and the fish sauce was awesome.

When my friends and I asked the hostess about the origins of Capdeville, she told us that there are two things that the owners absolutely love: "Food and Rock n' Roll." Now take that idea and roll with it as you peruse the menu of eclectic entrees and appetizers ranging from Fried Red Beans and Rice to Fish Tacos. It proclaims itself to be an interpretation of a British social house that has been Americanized, and I think they have that spot on. The atmosphere gives a since of freedom that allows the patrons to be both elegant and relaxed, whichever one is more their style.

As far as food goes (and I think the other reviewers will back me up on this one), it is a complete necessity for someone at the table to order the Truffled Mac n' Cheese. Take about melt in your mouth deliciousness right there. It's incredibly rich, but by the time you've finished it, you'll still want more. The burgers are also pretty spectacular. I personally tried the Capdeville Burger, which was everything I wanted and more. The fries were delicious and perfectly seasoned (not too salty like at some places), and the price was absolutely acceptable. 

The only down-side of our trip was a not-exactly-delightful Pimm's Cup. You'd think it would be difficult to mess up a Pimm's Cup (my personal cocktail of choice), but whoever made it did a pretty good job of doing so. It seemed hastily made (perhaps because there was a pretty large crowd at the bar when we ordered) and wasn't worth the $9 they were going to charge. This problem, however, was immediately solved by our waiter who was super cool and took it back. We opted for Abita Strawberry instead, a great combination with the burger and fries. 

Which brings me to my favorite part: the staff. I suppose I'm not very familiar with the staff as a whole, but the waiter and the hostess that helped us were absolutely stellar. The waiter was very helpful and completely cool with us sending back the Pimm's cup (sometimes you get those stuffy types that don't hesitate to let you know how much of an inconvenience it is - not the case here). The hostess (Lauren) was also fantastic. She got us settled as soon as she could, and even paid us a few visits and answered the questions we had about the restaurant and the inspiration for it. And it was thanks to her that we got to have the most delicious dessert ever: King Cake Bread Pudding. I kid you not, friends. This bread pudding was made from a Haydel's King Cake that was brought in earlier that morning, and it was divine. 

So all-in-all, I think it's safe to say that Capdeville will be a place that I will frequent often for their delicious food and even more delicious prices.

I'm constantly amazed when I take my child here at the childless adults who are voluntarily eating here. But I know why. It's good!  They have the drive thru running like a machine and are so stinkin friendly! And kids night is a blast.

Stayed here recently on a family trip to Santa Barbara...As many others have stated, the place is somewhat outdated, but overall the hotel was clean and the beds were comfortable...Those are the two things that are a must for the wife...Price for the stay was comparable to others in the area ,unless you are trying to stay at motel 6 or something...There are some high end places to stay in the general area if that is what you desire, but the price is significantly more expensive ( double the price ).. .The front desk person ( Anthony ) was awesome... He is probably the main  reason I will be back to stay at this location... He was accomidating in our request for a room near the pool, and went out of his way to make our stay a good one...Good guy with a professional attitude...Hotel is one block off of the freeway at the beginning part of State Street... Located somewhat close to several reataurants and businesses, but this is not the area of State St. where you would be close to the beach and near all the higher end shops. That would be at the other end of State St. and a few miles away. I plan on staying here again on my next trip out to this area based on my interaction with front desk staff, its location to freeway, and also my experience on this trip... Good stay for a night or two...


Note:  This hotel backs up to an old cemetery... Check google maps...

HORRRRRRIBLE SERVICE!!!!!! Save your time and money I bought a battery for 40 and he told me he gave it to me for a deal I look it up and it's 20 dollars cheaper everywhere so I got scammed! Save your time and money !

The receptionist did not tell me that my vision insurance plan was not covered, even when I told her the provider as I made the appointment, and I wasted my morning arriving at the appointment that I couldn't even go on. When I called to complain, she hung up on me. The doctors were great, from what I remember when I had different accepted insurance. It's a shame that insurance BS and undertrained, unprofessional receptionists resulted in a horrible experience for me. BEWARE

This is a large practice with four offices and a business model geared to more volume than a typical dentist's office.

In that mode, it is very well run, pleasant and professional and given their excellent pricing I can't imagine going anywhere else.  I had two different practicioners for a cleaning as they utilized the hygienist's time just for the cleaning portion and a dental assistant finished my cleaning with the polishing and flossing phase.  This actually makes great business sense, especially since the cost savings are passed along to the patient.

I did not experience any attempts to up-sell as others have noted, and highly recommend this practice, especially if you want to avoid the unusually high fees smaller, private dentists feel comfortable charging for their work in Santa Barbara.

I hate getting blood taken.  Who doesn't!  I made an appointment for 730AM.  It ended up snowing that morning and since I live in the mountains, I mean, Manayunk it took some extra time to get there.  
I arrives at 745, was promptly greeted and taken back. It didn't matter that I was late there was only people waiting for the bus in the waiting room. 
They had 2 techs on staff which is a nice change from my local Quest.  I don't think it is very safe for them to only have 1 person alone at these locations.  They were quick and friendly.  Hit my vein and painlessly drew 7 vials of blood.

Great location, cool atmosphere, and very nice inside. Got a burger and fries and wasn't a fan of the burger nor the onion on the burger. They did not have Budweiser on a Monday for lunch on Fourth of July?!

Spinach Artichoke Dip was pretty good. Large portion!
Great food, great service, and very reasonable prices!!  I would recommend the kebab tasting platter and the fattoush :)

3 stars!! Well first off I'm new to downtown and parking was very very easy to find which I love. I walk in and I'm very confused of where to purchase my order. The lady seemed like she didn't want to be there which I get who wants to work but hey your in a environment where you serve ppl so fake it. Moving on to food, I wouldn't say it's  authentic but I get the urban cafe kind of theme they have going on. It's a cool place overall to grab a quick bite or work or even hang out. Beef pho lets just say no way jose, I should of listened to the other reviews.  Although I did like the idea of making your own rolls nice concept. Will definitely return to try something different but it's on my 50/50 radar.

They took our order, but they never made it. I ordered a caffe mocha and paid for it. Five minutes later, I realized they forgot us. Finally, the barista asked me what I ordered, and when I told her, she said they're all out of dark chocolate. I already paid, so it wasn't like I could walk away. I took the white chocolate instead, and even though it was frothy and delicious, I wouldn't order from them again. My boyfriend also ordered, and his order was forgotten. When they finally gave him his red eye, it was filled to the brim and overflowing, so he couldn't add creamer. Not my first choice next time. 

If you order, just make sure to ask if they have what you want.

The only thing that makes a good massage a great massage is that you feel comfortable from when you walk in to when you leave.
This place was made so that as soon as you walk in you are relaxed. Now I'm not a massage all the time type guy but when you can hardly move your neck from being on the phone and traveling all the time it becomes apparant you need some help.
Not to overpriced from what I hear but very relaxing and the only place i would go.

P.S. Men get massages too, DON'T JUDGE!  lol

Great location on the water.  Bar food which is fine but our grouper nuggets were barely warm (when they finally came) and they didn't taste very good either - weird breading flavor and the fish didn't taste very fresh.   Waitresses were nice but service was VERY slow.   Hopefully this was an exception for them but I won't be going back.

Found this place after flying into st. Louis and wanting a good local lunch place. It was an absolutely fantastic lunch experience. We had the Chili and a great cold cut sandwich with a super tasty house recipe sauce on it.  Both as good as I've ever had.  I loved the Chili so much I ended up talking to the owners about how they made it.  That just made my whole experience even better as they were such great people to meet and talk too.  I can't wait til my next trip to St. Louis.  I will be going there again for sure!

I've now been to this place a couple of times, and I have to say it's my favorite spot in town so far for vegan grub. My first choice was the French Dip (to which I added Daiya mozzarella) and it was reaaaally good. I love the way they cook their seitan in thin slices, which gives the whole thing a great texture. I got to try the Philly Cheesesteak as well, but the sauce wasn't very reminiscent of the standard Philly cheese I've had before, so I think it would have been better with just Daiya instead.

The next time we came I got the Cali-style burger with Daiya added and it was fantastic(it had ranch dressing and avocado, mmmm). The dill cauliflower-carrot veggie of the day was OK, but needed a little salt. 

Both of the cakes we tried were amazing, and the last one was called the "Cookie Monster Cake". The thing was massive, and pure awesome. The cream-filled chocolate chip cookie pie we snagged was heavenly as well. Just the right flavor and not overwhelming.

The staff was very friendly and I'm thrilled to have found this place! Another thing I really liked is that all of the items are vegan by default, but you can add dairy cheese and such if you want. Can't wait to go back!
Tina took excellent care of us today and the place was packed. She truly goes above and beyond!!!!! Very hard worker. I'm impressed. She even walked around the dining room to see if everyone was ok

Heard about this spot through Chopped and it did not disappoint. It's a pop-up counter inside of a market, rather than a restaurant. So if you want to grab a dish from Fritai but head over to the next counter for a drink or coffee, you can.

The food: bliss. The Caribbean flavors shine bright in these dishes and the pickled cabbage (which I was skeptical about) ended up being my favorite part. For reference, I ordered the rice/pork dish with pickled cabbage and fried plantains. Moderately priced too, ~$14 for lunch.

Horray to Biaggi's for their delicious vegan pizza!!  The taste of crust and toppings were amazing.  The vegan pizza is not on their menu, but the Manager Jeff Andrews was happy to accomodate our request.  Chef Brian used pesto sauce and toppings of my choosing to create this fresh tasting pizza.  Jake's exceptional service made it easy to order.   I am glad I have found a place were I can enjoy a new vegan option.  I will definitely come back again.

Have always been a Kenner seafood fan when I come into town but have anew favorite... Shrimp and roast beef poboys , shrimp pasta, blackened cobia, home made onion rings, all excellent and service very friendly and attentive.. Prices were excellent. Atmosphere typical New Orleans local mom and pop place. Laid back and relaxing . Crawfish bisque is so great !

ABSOLUTELY HORRIBLE. Not only did they do a horrific job but come 8 they kick u out, I didn't even sit under the dry for 3mins and she asked me to leave. If you value your money, time, and nails do NOT bother with this place. All I wanted was the acrylic off and a simple mani and pedi. I STILL HAVE ACRYLIC ON MY NAILS . So what they did to fix it was slab on an extra coat of nail polish.

IF I COULD GIVE ZERO STARS I WOULD! Just came back from an absolutely horrendous experience!!! What a WASTE OF TIME!!!!!! Made an appointment to see a 98 Lincoln Navigator, drove over an hour and also called right before we left to tell them we were coming!!!!!!! We get there and this classes girl gave us attitude and tells us the Navigator WAS SOLD THIS MORNING and that we should have called first!!!!!!!! ARE YOU KIDDING ME!!!!!!! NO HONEY, YOU SHOULD HAVE CALLED US RIGHT AFTER IT SOLD!!!!!!!!!!! WHY DIDN'T YOU TELL US WHEN WE CALLED TO TELL YOU WE WERE ON OUR WAY!!!!!!!!!! WE WASTED THE TRIP, GAS  AND TOLLS!!!!!!!!!!!!!! Just a suggestion, instead of giving your potential customers a lot of attitude, like we are bothering you, take customer service courses and learn how to treat people with RESPECT!!!!!!!!!!
Really good Mexican food in New Hope.  Fajitas: excellent.  Chimichangas: I'm told they're excellent. Carne Asada tacos: same.

Smooth dark coffee absolutely bitter free. 
Homemade cookies excellent.
The decor sports the use of reclaimed farmhouse wood and a old metal farmhouse sliding door.
Happy people work here 
Stop in and enjoy a great cup of coffee!

Rich provided excellent service for us. We just bought a home with a gas Jen-Air cooktop/range that didn't seem to be igniting properly. He cleaned out the burners as best as could be done (the previous owner had clearly never cleaned or cared for the stove at all). The stove performance is greatly improved. Rich walked me through all the steps of what he did and how to clean them. He was also very honest about the fact that to get it working like new would cost as much (or more) than the cost of replacing the entire stove (due to the brand and cost of replacement parts). He was friendly, polite and definitely came prepared and competent to do the job.

so five guys for me would get 4 stars, and this gets 3. why? it's basically five guys with smaller portions.

prices are about the same. i got a large order of fries and a burger for like $11 something. at five guys it would be about that as well, but i would get more fries that i can even ask for, and probably not even finish them. here, a large order looked like just a couple average handfuls. good fries though :)

burger was also pretty good, but once again, smaller than five guys. one thing i think they do better though, is their bun. it's fluffy and has sesame seeds, it's really good.

also, they have milkshakes! didn't try one, but they sound good. (kinda pricey yet again though =/ )

good options, and it's cool that they list some specialty burgers so you don't have to think about which toppings go good together. it's also pretty cool that they have a "challenge" burger with like 16 patties or something. man vs food nation???? lol

good place if you're craving a burger and good fries, but not the place to go if you wanna get the most bang for your buck.

After my complaint, the manager graciously left me a voicemail apologizing and offering to correct the order.  I took Mac Grill up on their offer and the result was very positive. The food was very good and hot, the bread arrived, and we were not charged. I appreciate the manager's efforts and will definitely order again. An apology goes a long way. Thank you, James!!

Rude waiter. No smile. No welcoming. Banging plates. Excuse me every one has job n everyone has to perform their duties. It's ur duty to make guests feel welcoming. Probably u should be doing something else. Because even though we were nice n smiling with u, u had some issues with us.
Best Pho on the Westbank by far! Their Vietnamese crawfish are to die for too! Must try if you're in this side of the river!

Does the Vanderbilt Medical Center location not open at 5 am?Thats what is on the door.If this is fact than can someone please tell me why at 5:20 I walk in, heading toward the sandwich counter, an employee walks by me, another employee is in the corner, and I am standing there waiting to get service. After a few minutes of waiting I realized no one was going to take my order. No one greeted me, no one said " someone will be with u in a minute", and no one obviously wanted my business. I finally walked out, and that same employee who passed by me the first time didn't try to stop me. I am flaburgasted at the uncaring of this establishment. If I was a crook I could of walked out of there with a handful of bagels etc. instead of everyone unboxing the truck maybe someone should pay attention to the paying customers coming in and out of this establishment.
I love this place! Fast service. Friendly staff. New York style thin crust pizza and I can get a slice instead of a whole pizza. Beautiful salads. And yummy cookies for dessert!

Obviously the Mustang gurus, RDW is also a great place to bring any vehicle for service, repairs, or anything really! Honest, knowledgeable, and friendly; I completely trust them and recommend them to you!

Overall our stay wasn't horrible but it wasn't great. We stayed 3 nights. The first impression was great, we arrived very early and they let us check in which was an absolute blessing. Once we arrived in the room we went to take a nap because we had been traveling since 3 am and realized we had no remote... went downstairs and asked for one they said no problem we will send someone up! 3 hours later still no remote... asked again and left to go to dinner came back several hours later and again.. no remote. We went down again and said hey what's going on and someone brought us a remote within the next 10 min. Front desk staff was very nice and I'm not sure what the mix up was but oh well nothing to lose our minds over. Then after the first night we noticed that around 3 am the room would get UNBAREABLY HOT and then would begin to cool down around 6-7am (the thermostat said 87 degrees and would not allow me to change it) this caused for very poor sleep during our stay. Again not the worst hotel but not the best. For the location and the price i guess we got what we paid for.

Staff was so polite! They have samples of all the juices so you don't have to take a gamble. Parking is a little funky over there but worth it to get to this place. Pricing is higher but these are fresh off the tree juices. Definitely worth every penny.

Blush is definitely more of a bar than a nice restaurant. It is actually really pricey for food however. They left all the doors open, when it was 45 degrees out (which is quite cold for SB) and i couldn't even take my coat off. Really loud and rowdy partying going on and the music was blaring; not what we expected for a romantic dinner.

The worst part however, is that the online menu is completely off: we went there for our first time to try their rib eye and new york steaks, and were told that they no longer serve them, in addition to other items on the menu. We left, so I don't know if their current food is any good. We were really bummed :(

I've tried some of the hyped up donut places(VooDoo donuts and Duck donuts) and they never gave me the "omggguuhhhh" reaction. Tried Hole in One donuts for the first time in Tampa one morning and I don't care for any other donuts but Hole in One. The glazed crossiants!!!! 
Super excited that one opened around Tarpon Springs (lives in Spring Hill). Thank youuuu!!!

Please open one in Spring Hill!!!!

For many years Lelia's has dominated the medical scrubs market in the Reno area. Her only real competition has been the small boutique at Renown, until recently when a uniform store opened at the Legend's Outlets in Sparks. Lelia's has a wide variety of brands, styles, colors, and products including compression socks, shoes, and nursing supplies, which is why I continue to shop here. I always seem to find something I like, and sometimes I don't have time to wait for an online order to arrive. Also, if you want something special and they're able to find it, they'll order it for you.

However, I always dread shopping here. I've had an awkward experience nearly every time. The staff is simply unprofessional and unfriendly. There is a few core staff that I recognize from the past few years that I have overheard exchanging very explicit personal stories as well as talking down about customers as soon as they leave the store. I have also frequently observed staff on personal calls while customers are shopping. When asking for help finding a size or color I have received sighs making me feel as if I'm bothering them by asking for assistance. Most recently, when inquiring about a student discount I was grilled with hostile questions; it was as if she'd do anything to prevent me from receiving the meager 10% savings.   

I find their scrubs to be very spendy. When comparing their prices to those online, Lelia's has a significant markup. Her prices are also higher than those at Renown. The store hours also seem limited; closing at 6pm Monday through Friday and 4pm on weekends. Clearance items are final sale, which is understandable, but the regular return policy is for store credit only, no refunds, and must be done within seven days. I can't believe I continue to go back, but the simple truth is Lelia's offers what nurses want and we have nowhere else to go locally for this selection.

We have been to Lavi twice now, first for dinner and today for pajama brunch.  I am hoping the neighborhood supports this new addition to Ardmore. The food we have enjoyed so far is familiar but all with a unique twist. We had the brie appetizer-- yummy-- and I had the duck drumettes and partner had the ravioli. All was delicious. Today we had Eggs Benedict. The owner, Kay and chef, Z plus the staff are kind, friendly, and accommodating. They have done a great job making the space cozy and comfortable-- a surprise given the appearance of the building from the outside. We are looking forward to going back and getting, what seems to be the favs, the salmon and the shrimp and grits.  Not sure about the negative reviews here, sound like a different restaurant than we went to. Maybe a case of regular new restaurant growing pains? They seem to be planning lots of things to make Lavi appealing and interesting-- tastings, comedy night, maybe music down the road... At any rate, we welcome them and whatever fun they come up with to our neighborhood.
As far as alcohol, they serve House Frozen Margaritas, a few Beers on tap & Bottled Beer. Enjoy.

Today was the first time I tried this café. Everything was outstanding and I cannot wait to come back. Most sandwich shops anymore have fake turkey but not here. Their gobbler sandwich had fresh turkey, fresh bread, fresh cranberry sauce and was delicious. I had their strawberry chicken salad which was phenomenal. To top it off their homemade cookies are to die for. By 1130 the line was out the door which is definitely a sign that they are doing something right. Good job.

Tucson local favorite since the 70's but was sold & now sold again to big food corporation gone national. Sunday afternoon remodeled store became busy after our arrival. Ordered large spicy grinder , spicy means jalepeno s on long fresh roll with not much meat in it plenty of jalapeño s . Order sat as name never called to pick it up? Tried to use restroom couldn't get in for 15 minutes an employee was in there changing clothes & probably taking a break? Give four stars based on the egee  drinks and past reputation .
Restrooms are disgusting you need a hygiene service like Enviro Master. Our company uses them and they do a fantastic job they can make your restroom fixtures look like brand new

A very nice hotel diner serving breakfast, lunch and dinner. My wife and I are serious foodies and seldom eat at hotel restaurants. However in this case, we were pleasantly surprised at the lovely décor, the delicious food, and the attentive service at Café Milano.

The menu offers a very broad selection of items and it caters well to international guests. We enjoyed both breakfast and lunch here and found the food to be reasonably priced and a cut above what we expected. The service is prompt and friendly and our waitress was even patient with the neighboring table of gluten-free food neurotics.

We found Café Milano to be an inviting and moderately priced hotel diner suitable for singles, couples or families any time of the day. The atmosphere is welcoming and the food is well prepared and quite tasty. We would dine here again and recommend this nice hotel restaurant to others.

I find most of the people that work there are not good with customers.  Some check your id for packages some don't. Not very consistent.  They lose packages. Make sure you get receipts for packages and don't just drop off. 

Update!  Given the wrong package yet again.

The best Cheesesteaks!!!!  As a transplant from South Jersey to NC, I make sure to take the trip up there at least twice a year and always have to get a Gaetano's Cheesesteak.  I even had someone bring me down Cheesesteaks and Gaetano's separated everything for them so it wouldn't go bad.  Even told them the best way to heat everything up and put it together!!!

Traveled alone for my birthday. I wasn't very impressed. 
Reminded me of downtown Los Angeles skid row. Dirty and the streets smelled like urine. Homeless in every corner yelling for money. 

Stopped at 3 different spots to find something to eat and was ignored. People seemed over worked and moody. I ended up eating take out at the Hard Rock Cafe majority of my trip. 
For being the street of main attraction I feel businesses need to hire vibrant energetic friendly welcoming staff. 
The rest of the town especially Jackson square were friendly.

For a friendly double date I suggested we go to Pier 48 because MikeG suggested it on his IG page & he was even featured on WTHR news channel saying how good Pier 48 was. We'll my husband and I were very disappointed. If it wasn't for the appetizers the rating would have been 1star. The appetizers were amazing but with 1 flaw. My husband's boom boom shrimp were not fully removed of the brown vein that holds the waste from the shrimp. At least the one I bit into still had it. BUT my Crab Beignets were FIRE!!!! That I'd go back for! The fish & chips had absolutely NO flavor! It tasted as if it was just soaking in tap water all day. My husband said his steak had an extreme amount of garlic & it ruined the steak as well as his breath. If I were to go back it'd only be for the Crab Beignets. The jelly with them was EVERYTHING!

Five stars for this wonderful PIP print shop here in Santa Barbara! High-quality printing services, with fast delivery, excellent pricing, and attentive, personal service. Ani, Laurence, Becky, and team do a fantastic job taking care of clients large and small, and offer a wide range of printing options and services. My wife and I are so happy we finally found them for all our printing needs. Highly recommended!

I really like this store. I liked that the staff wasn't there with vacant judgmental looks on their faces, instead they are little ladies trying to help you find everything you are looking for.
I found about ten books and five cds without skimming the surface of this place.

I have been here twice, once for dinner, and today for lunch.

Pros: Nice ambiance, the waitress was very nice, and the fried goat cheese salad was really good (would've been nice if the salad was tossed, then the goat cheese placed on top). The fries are crispy.

Cons: The other dishes I ordered, the chicken souvlaki sandwich and the fish tacos, were not good. The sandwich had no flavor and the chicken's consistency was off. The fish tacos were confusing. The fish was a little crispy and charred but was also sweet which threw the whole taco off. The waitress said this was spicy, but it wasn't, at all. The pico de gallo was mushy and flavorless, and there was a giant piece of avocado and that was basically the whole taco.

My stomach hurt when I got home.

Advice: Make your fish tacos more savory - not sweet. Add salt. Rejuvenate the pico and add corn to the mix. Instead of avocado, make it guacamole, or dice up the avocados. Redo the souvlaki sandwich.

These guys have a serious problem here especially in the drive thru portion.   6pm on a weeknight and the cars are lined up in the drive thru.  The first sign this is a problem is when I pulled up and pushed the call button no one answered it for 5 minutes.  The other issue this place has is that the app will notify you that the prescription is ready but when you come in, it's not ready. Just as today,  they said they weren't ready. So I get to  sit here  waiting in the Drive-Thru line for about 35 minutes total. They need to hire more staff or find a better way of doing this because this is ridiculous.  I'll never use it again I'll just go in next time.

Ok, so I'm kind of on the border between 2.5/3 stars....here's why. 

The evening started out alright...we got seated promptly and our server was really nice.  It was a Monday night so it wasn't really busy and our food came out impressively fast.  The seafood is decent--the general appeal for me is the variety and the convenience when I want to eat seafood...I mean, the food isn't going to knock you off your feet, but if you want some pretty good seafood (and variety) then this place is a good option.  It was AFTER we were served our food that things went down hill.  We never saw our server again that night until we hunted her down for the check.  Actually, we never saw her after she took our order because food runners brought our meal out...seriously...DID. NOT. SEE. HER.   There was no "do you need a drink refill" or "is everything OK" or "do you need anything"....did I mention that it was a Monday night? and that it wasn't busy?  and they were FULLY staffed (I think I saw 3 hostesses).  When we finished our food, we literally sat there for 30 mins trying to find SOMEONE to bring us our check.  It was really ridiculous.  

I've been to other Joe's before and as with this one, the food is fine, but the service alone may deter me from returning to this location.

I have slight addiction to sewing and fabrics! Jo-Ann Fabrics is a chain and they to do have a lot of stuff for not just sewing but all types of crafting. This is also my go to store for Wilton candy supplies. Some of the fabrics are of lower quality but there is some nice material here and the staff is usually helpful. There are several different classes offered every month that are fun.

Love, love, LOVE this place! I have only been at lunch time, but the food has never let us down. The catfish, the greens, the cabbage, the sweet potato....all perfection! The Asian flavors with the addition of the grill is mouthwatering. I highly recommend this place to any foodie out there!

We tried out the Rooftop bar and surprised to see the beautiful view of the clearwater intercostal waterway.  My husband and I had a Red Blend wine that we really enjoyed.  There was a group of 7 of us and we were all able to sit st the bar. The valet was complimentary and they were quick and courteous.  There was blues band in the downstairs bar area.  In addition, we walked around and looked at all the history this gorgeous building has to offer including newspapers from the 1920s and the original speakeasy entryway was set up as a mini museum.

We ordered 2 double decker pontoon boats months ahead of time and the day before we were to get them, they lost our reservation and we got stuck with 1 pontoon and a fishing boat. After being on the lake the service at the restaurant was the worst I've ever dealt with. They said there was an hour wait for our food to come out, which would have been fine, but they continued to take orders and other people were getting seated after us and still receiving their food. The waitress proceeded to cancel our order after sitting there for an hour and a half saying the kitchen was too busy. I would never recommend this place to my worst enemy

We've used Chem Dry of Tampa twice about a year apart. Both times we've been very pleased. Their great service begins with a person setting up the appointment, confirming a day in advance the time, and again the cleaner phoned that he was 20 minutes away. Our carpets were cleaned with a protection on them and dried within a couple of hours. I questioned why its called Chem Dry, and learned that chem stands for chemistry and the cleaning solution is safe for the environment etc. Once our house is pet free, will get these guys back for a total deep cleaning.
Awesome food and great prices. We came in a Sunday at mid day and there was no wait and fast service. I haven't had better Mexican food in the Nashville area.

This will be my last time going to boba cup everything is going downhill. From no longer ordering from a person to ordering on the kiosks the kiosks malfunction and the staff had an attitude like it's my fault. First off these tweens don't know who they are speaking to as I have a masters degree in IT I told them it wasn't working and it changed my order they just looked dumbfounded. They are so rude here must be stafff changes or basically layoffs because who needs people when u have robots.
Service was perfect! We ordered half the menu everything was great! Will definitely be there again!

Yesterday evening, the manager demonstrated a poor attitude and was very confrontational to our large party who were all INDIVIDUAL guests due to a situation that arose resulting from the server being inattentive and not organized possibly due to her inexperience.  He aggressively forced guests to pay an unpaid bill they were not responsible for AT ALL which was TOTALLY inappropriate and without real proof whose bill it was.... Very poor treatment of guests and poor judgment!  When attempting to provide feedback on the experience and service, he would not listen.

Today, I took my 2017 Yukon Denali in to have the AC repaired. It was there last week same problem. They wanted $845 to fill the AC system with refridgerant. This is $20 of parts and maybe one hour of service.  I had to pay the $145 for the diagnostic. This vehicle has 37K miles, 1000 miles out of warranty. The MSRP of my 2 year old Yukon was $79,800.

I declined using GMC service beyond the diagnostic performed.

I then took my car to the collision shop that repaired my front bumper. They quoted me $180 to fill my AC. It turned out that my AC needed more than a charge. Which means the GMC RENO misdiagnosed my problem. So, back to GMC RENO. Prior to my going to GMC, I had a service writer on a call about my problem. He said that he would credit the charge for the misdiagnosed problem. Cool! 

I arrived at the dealer, and the man who wrote up the order said that i had to pay another diagnostic cost. I said, "I just spoke with a different service writer 1.5 hours ago, he agreed to not charge me another diagnostic". 

In short, the service writer at GMC called me a liar. He refused to confer with the other service writer. He became testy with me to make his point. He insisted that I must pay another diagnostic charge, and didn't want to hear my story that I spoke with his colleague just an 1.5 hours earlier. 

I left RENO GMC. They will never see my Yukon Denali again!  

Carson City has a GMC dealer, Michael Hohl. I purchased my 2008 Yukon Denali from them. 

Let's go back 2.5 years ago when I was shopping for a new $80K truck. I tried to buy my truck at RENO GMC. I made my selection. Gave the salesman my business card. NO CALL back. I just wanted their best price. I called GMC in Placerville, CA. Two days later, they delivered me a new GMC Yukon Denali to my home. 

So, who is running the show at Reno GMC?

Alright twin oak, you might just have the best happy hour deals in St. Louis. Half off beer and half off signature pizzas? That's quite unbelievable. This is my second time there, a 12 inch pizza and 2 beers for 14 bucks just cannot be beat. 

Definitely a 5 star if you go during happy hour, 4 star otherwise. Happy hour is 330 to 530 if I remember correctly.

Got to try 6 of their pizzas so far off the signature pizza menu. The ones that stick out are the meatorologist and the big easy. But all of em were good so just order whatever toppings and sauce you like the most.

Their pizzas are a 12 inch solid size, wood fired thinner crust. This place is the bomb, If you can't make the happy hour, it is still definitely good enough to just go chow down on some awesome tasting pizza. If you can make the happy hour your wallet won't be hurting after drinking multiple beers and getting pizza.

I'll definitely be back twin oak!!

CLASS-ACTION AGAINST MILGARD...
WE'RE A SMALL GROUP AND WILL REPRESENT YOU. YOU DON'T HAVE TO BE AT THE FRONLINE. THE MORE NAMES WE'VE TO WORK WITH, THE BETTER WE'RE OFF. PLEASE EMAIL US PICS AND AS MUCH INFO AS POSSIBLE. 

nancysb123@hotmail.com 
713.503.4472

If you purchased a custom S550 Convertible from Mercedes Benz and the interior color is not what you have picked out, the doors will not close properly, the power windows is making an odd sound, interior woodwork is not flush...not as depicted their catalog, website images and in showrooms, dings, dents and scratches everywhere, miss match stitching on leather seats, etc. Will you sign off and let Mercedes Benz send their technicians out to fix all their oops? How about okey Mercedes to have a local mechanic fix what you think and believe is wrong with your brand new custom car? 

DO NOT allow Milgard into your home to fix all their oops on brand new custom windows and doors!!! I've read so many reviews and so many people allow them to do so. After Milgard installed my trumped up custom ESSENCE SERIES patio door...two doors will not close. They sent me an email saying..."Hi Nancy, thank you for requesting Milgard's services. A representative will contact you within 24 hours to schedule an appointment with our technicians".  I NEVER contact them for services. I demanded they do the door over, do it right or refund my money so I can move on with anther company. They didn't like that at all and from that point on things went sour and very aggressive. 

Their middleman and Milgard have my downpayment and told me to expense my mistakes since I refused their services. They refused to acknowledge the woodwork on their Essence Series is a joke, the swing wasn't what I had agreed to with their middleman as it's clearly not functional and impossible to fix as it's one unit. The doorhandles and hinges color and style wasn't what I picked out, removing it will leave more markings and scratches on top of dings and dents. The doors will not close properly if/when new hinges are install. The stain job is horrific, clearly not done by a professional licensed stainer.

What a terrible disappointment, from the soggy shrimp cocktail to the undersized, chewy, terribly overcooked lobster tails to the dirty salad plates.  The creme brûlée cheesecake tasted like it just came from a box and they poured smuckers caramel sauce on top. No torch ever touched this cake!  That was $120 (2 ppl) we'll never get back.  What a waste.

I love those crispy and spicy chicken tenders. It is a perfect combined taste with a bun, their special sauce, and some tangy pickles which they called a slider. I also like their crunchy, thick, and real French Fries. They are also offered a student discount which is a great deal for me :D
A hint of note is trying their middle hotness level and go from there. The crazy level is really spicy, but it's worth the try (more flavorful too). Also, you should buy their special ice cream on the grocery side. The taste is different delicious since ice cream is made of condensed milk. It also helps cool down if you accidentally choose a hotter level than you can handle.
Nice place nice people and good healthy food choices!! The parking was easy too and I really enjoyed being able to eat outside

Wasn't really packed when I came. Cant review the food since I was waiting almost an hour just for an appetizer so we winded up just paying for drinks and walked out!! Gave an extra star for waitress being empathetic about it. There's no reason why one should be waiting so long on tacos and chips.

Eh, I extremely mediocre food. Smoked salmon belly appetizer woefully overcooked and leathery. Bland New England clam chowder. And terrible fish and chips. Small pieces of tilapia they try to pass off as cod beer battered and rubbery. The crust was thick and half  cooked. Won't dine here again.

Fantastic vet! Dr. Boyer and her team are top notch. They are professional and kind. I was able to get a full puppy check up, all of her shots, and lots of great information for a relatively decent price. Highly recommended!

It's been 15 days since I sent an email to this company about my wedding photos they ruined. Still not one word back from them. They clearly have zero conscience when it comes to their business reputation. Still a 1 star.

We took the New Orleans Walking Tour of the French Quarter with Suzy. She was a great tour guide. Very friendly and knowledgeable. She took her time with us in terms of pace especially since we were traveling with my friend's toddler. We went to about 6 places and had 3 mini dishes and 3 bites. They were all tasty. In my many years coming to and eating in NOLA, I never had a muffuletta. This was one was nice. We also had beef brisket with a red horseradish. This was my favorite. My next favorite was the jambalaya. It had a nice balance of heat to it. The boudin was also something surprisingly nice. It almost tasted like a hush puppy with meat. The toddler loved that one too. My only critique would be to have 1 more dish vs a bite (we had a cookie and praline). I wasn't as full as I would have thought given all the spots. However, Suzy was excellent. We learned about the city and the food as we went along the tour. It is a nice way to get to learn about the area and the food of the French Quarter.

We all agree an event's success is largely based on the quality, quantity and deliciousness of the food. Our recent bridal shower was amazing. We used Pasta Plus carry out service and it was perfect. The mostaccioli and pasta con broccoli (customized in a creamy white sauce v. their traditional red) was scrumptious. The ginormous sandwich, cut into minis, were the perfect delicious alternative to pasta. Thanks, Pasta Plus.

Would never use this place for anything other than strings and picks. Poor selection and only cheap brands. Unfriendly, uncaring staff. Driven way out of my way multiple times after calling to confirm that they had certain items only to show up and they say they've never had them. Overall bad experience every time I go.

More of a big box store aimed at selling to beginners.
Pepper Fries! Serious good local burger joint.
Great food!! huge portions. I get the chile rellenos. Theyre HUGE and delicious. The guacamole taste like paste though. Great food and large portions though. I'm definitely a fan.

I had a great time at Iron Hill over the Christmas holiday. My husband and I both had beer flights and sampled just about everything on tap that evening. The food and beer were both great.

I do wish they included more information about each beer. Their web site lists facts like bitterness factor, color, etc. But this interesting and helpful info isn't on the menu in the brewery itself! Weird.

The venue is rather loud. I enjoyed the ambiance (low lighting, etc), but don't come in expecting to enjoy an intimate conversation with your companions.
Whenever I'm in Santa Barbara I try to stop by here, the food is great and the view is amazing. Service is always great, parking is shared with the beach so plan accordingly.

OK ladies and gentlemen Suzy-Jo does not play around when it comes into making a donut. These guys are colossal, huge in size, I'm sorry to say , actually I'm not but they make Dunkin Donuts look like a joke. Krispy Kreme does not match up either with Suzy Jo, basically this little lady runs things in the donut realm. 

Fresh cake style donuts made every day for you to enjoy, family owned and ran since the '50s. They make creative donuts, also they are not small, I think I mentioned that earlier. Filled donuts , and I mean they are filled, where others give it one shot where you need to eat two thirds of the donut just to find the filling. Its doesn't matter where you bite into it your gonna taste the filling. I loved the peanut butter fluff, along with the Bavarian (aka. Boston Cream ). 

This is a great establishment, great staff , fresh coffee, and a warm inviting smell of fresh donuts ! You can't go wrong here, also I will bet you can't eat more than 2 of their filled donuts, one and a half  I am willing to say.

Donut rankings as of now, Federal you're next  up to bat.

1) Suzy-Jo

2) everyone else can battle for second in my opinion

WORST EVER.  Super rude.  I was told that I'm a "pain in the ass".  Right to my face.  Also was told I texted and called too much,  That because I had to call you back to my house 3 times in one day because the new hot water heater continued to leak.  I was charged $850 just for the installation of the heater.  If they did the job correctly the first time 4 years ago I wouldn't need to call them.  STAY FAR AWAY FROM THIS COMPANY!!!!!!

I made a poor choice with Power Home Remodeling. Do better than I did.  If you've recently signed a contract, hopefully you're still within the cancellation window...cancel!

Usually, a "Superior product" comes with service that's at least a little better than a trip to the DMV and a quality finished product. Not at Power Home Remodeling! You'll pay a premium price to be treated like a number & get to enjoy less-than acceptable work!

In my experience, this company cares about its profit more than its customer's satisfaction. 

1) allowing (encouraging?) sales to set inaccurate expectations - sales has no knowledge of current production times related to supply chain issues

2) no proactive communication along the way.

3) a poor finished product

My project was delayed by 15 weeks from the expectation set by sales and by 6 weeks from the revised "promise date" set at inspection - I learned of each of the delays when I called. You read that right...when I called.

I understand the supply chain issues. But to make sure sales doesn't know the real story about true expected install timeframes is downright corrupt, and it's not too much to ask that someone there would pick up the phone and make an outbound phone call to give a heads up on status. 

DAY BEFORE the "Promise install Date" set at inspection I thought to myself, "they promised they would be here in the morning to start, but I haven't heard any confirmation, so maybe I should call before I start taking down blinds/curtains & moving furniture". So I call & nope. "It'll be next week."  

It wasn't. It was six weeks. 

If, as you say in your reply to this review that, "the satisfaction of [your] customers throughout the entire remodeling process [is your] top priority" then, communication about delays (breaking promises) seems table-takes to make your actions match the words, no?

Here is a snippet of a recent conversation I had with Shane, one of PHR's managers, regarding the (apparently novel) suggestion/expectation of proactive communication:

Shane: "If we were going to call every single one of our customers about delays in their projects, we would have to hire a person to do JUST that job alone!"

Me: "Yes. Yes, you absolutely should if that's what it takes. It's not too much of an ask from your customers. In fact, I had sliding doors done by another company in the same industry. Supply chain issues affected them too, but the difference was that they at least called me to let me know, and they're a smaller company"

Shane: "That's exactly why they could call. They can call because they're smaller"

Wut?  "Smaller" = "more resources to make phone calls" ???  I'm not tracking...

Here's an idea, PHR: hire the person you evidently need in order to be able to communicate with your customers, and you won't need to pay the marketing department so much to manage your online reputation. Whatdya think? No? Hire more door-knockers instead? Ok. Gotcha.

I should have had the "smaller" company do my windows too. They were less expensive. I chose PHR because of the difference in expectations around install.  The "smaller" one said it'd be 30 weeks-ish. PHR at sales appt said 9 to 11 weeks, and because they boast this exclusive manufacturer relationship...so it made sense to me.

Nope. I was just duped. Turns out the "smaller" company is also honest up-front.  And, yes, I'm frustrated. Mostly tho with myself for not sniffing a snake.

Windows installed today. They're nice as Power didn't make them. 

The work Power did was not commensurate with what you'd call "a superior product"  The trim work is horrible, didn't bother to clean up afterwards. Sure did the work fast tho!

And!!  They DID make an outbound phone call!  While the crew was still caulking the trim, they called to collect the balance

In summary:

Corrupt, dishonest sales process. 

Horrible service. 

Shoddy work. 

 My review on Google won't show up unless you use Duck Duck Go

Great experience ! It was 1st spot on our BBQ and burger tour that I made up for Chester county. It was very satisfying ! Atmosphere was classic BBQ Decor no frills home style feeling. It is byob and tables are set with three different types of BBQ sauce. Hot BBQ , sweet and bold, and vinegar BBQ. There is also a hand washing stashing and plenty of napkins for you to clean up after eating their delicious food. The owner Jim , introduced himself and made us feel at home ! He was happy to chat BBQ ! 

We started off with the smoked bacon (a must have) then ordered the ribs, brisket and spicy pulled chicken. For sides we had the mac and cheese , smoked beans (phenomenal ), corn bread , dirty corn and whipped sweet potatoes. The brisket and ribs were our favorites ! All sauces and almost everything on the menu Is also gluten free !:) 

We will be back ! Stop in and give it a try, you will be hooked !

Save yourself some trouble and go to the one in Hyde Park. This Lululemon stinks. The customer service is absolutely horrible, and they're really not trying to help you. I went in with something I wanted to return, but I had ripped the tags off, within 24 hours of buying the item... I know this certain stores have protocols on returning items, but I feel like they were not even trying to work with me. It's disappointing that when you spend such a great deal of money on one item, that the people selling you the item really do not care if you love it or not. I have visited this store several times, and the customer service is always bad. You really are better off going to Hyde Park, even if it is additional gas, it's worth it for the better customer service.

Okay, so I've been to Pueblo Nuevo 3 times since my first review, and I'm sad to say that my opinion has changed. 

While I still love the beef tongue tacos, that's about the only thing worth visiting Pueblo Nuevo for, in my opinion. 

Went again this afternoon for a quick lunch. I nibbled from Andy's Pueblo Nuevo special, which included refried beans, rice, a pork taco, beef empanada, and pork burrito. 

While it was a great deal (all that food for 8 bucks? Wowza!) It was all very very BLAND. One look around at the clientele answered all of my questions about Pueblo Nuevo. Without going into too many details, it's pretty obvious that they cater to a certain *type* of diner. And the packed restaurant tells me that they do it well. 

But...I crave a more authentic experience than that. Give me some spice, Pueblo Nuevo! Give me some authenticity! I know they have it in them; the beef tongue tacos, delicious horchata, and yummy churros tell me that they KNOW how to prepare authentic Mexican dishes well. But the blandness of the remaining items on the menu tells another story. 

I'm going to throw this out there: as it is, Pueblo Nuevo reminds me of a sit-down Taco Bell. And while I love Taco Bell on occasion, when I go to a restaurant, I'm expecting just a little more than that. 

I'll probably visit again for the beef tongue tacos, but not for anything else. 

Try harder, Pueblo Nuevo.

Wow soooooo good! Let's start with the staff... they are amazing!! I absolutely loveee their sushi. I get their DTSP roll every time. Can't pass up! + you can grt them for just $5 at happy hour. Also their bruschetta app is another goodie. Highly rec! Great location too.

What a fantastically splendid experience. Everything from the ambiance to the service and especially the food was on point.

Online reservations were easy and we were delighted to get in New year's Eve at 7:30 p.m. We actually did arrive a bit early but they were willing to accommodate and seat our party of four at a comfortable table near a window. This place is gorgeous. Simple yet elegant and thoughtfully decorated. 

We tried an array of cocktails. All were good. My favorite cocktail of all time is a New York sour. This is not on the menu but they were able to recreate their version and it hit the spot. 

For our appetizers and entrees:

Chicken liver paté 5/5.  Really, really enjoyable. Portioned out on four pieces of wedged toast and caramelized onions. Geez it was delicious.

Pimento cheese 5/5. Again, they did this right. I'm a southern girl so I know my way around this dish. They used the right amount of pimento and the perfect consistency. The house made chips held up nicely and were a great accompaniment.

Octopus 4/5.  I've had more tender renditions of this dish. It did not pack a ton of flavor but overall enjoyable.

Bacon wrapped dates 4/5. This has never been a favorite of mine but the table did enjoy this.  I would probably skip this on my next visit but it's worth a try. 

House special black grouper with cauliflower puree 5/5. My friend and I were filling up pretty quickly from the appetizers so asked our server if it was possible to split this dish. No problem, not only did the chef split it and plate it perfectly, they also split the bill as well. 

Tamarind glazed pork shank 5/5. This was my husband's dish and he cleaned his plate.  There was a flavor explosion in this dish.  The pork was so tender and slow cooked. 

Speaking of service, Amy was phenomenal. Very personable, she took her time at the table with us discussing our best choices for cocktails and food dishes. She really is a pleasure. 

This experience was perfection. Very happy to have rung in the new year with this as my last dining experience of 2020. Barterhouse is going down easily as one of my top 5 restaurants in Tampa Bay.

Just had Shane tint the windows in my home, what a difference!!! Why there are not more reviews on this shop is beyond me. Shane was such a professional and came well prepared for the job, he brought his own drop cloth to protect my floors along with his own trash bags for all of his waste. The poor guy worked until 8:30 PM to make sure everything was perfect, I'm very happy with his service as well as the price, great value for the money.

EU + IMCPL = BF 4-eva. I was bummed to find that the library did not have a collection of 2009 short stories that was cited by various lit geeks and included on lists of notable collections published for the year by several major newspapers. But did you know that you can ask the library to add a book that is not in their stacks? I requested they consider purchasing Paul Yoon's "Once the Shore" and several weeks later it was waiting for me on a hold shelf. I love you, Indianapolis Public Library. xoxox

Wish I could give them negative 5. Called tech support twice and both times no one ever came in the line just silence. Shit service!! Everything has been shit since I started with them. Assholes I say. .

I usually buy organic meat at whole foods because I think it is healthy and safe. However, I think they should clean their freezer more frequently. I bought organic ground pork today and found some rotten meat stick on the fresh meat. It already went bad, dark and stinky. I can't imagine what will happen if I didn't check the meat and cook them whole. It would go to my 10-month old baby's stomach. It is a really bad experience and hope Wholefoods can deal with the healthy issue seriously.

From the time you walk into Bertha Miranda's until the time you walk out the door you are treated like family!  There are daily lunch specials each weekday.   Today's special was a chimichanga that comes with rice and refried beans and also a generous cup of albondigas soup to start (Mexican meatball).  I decided that the special was a great way to try a variety of things for only $8.95!   A large basket of chips and salsa are promptly brought to the table literally as soon as you sit down.   The salsa has a great flavor but not chunky.   The soup is served hot and made from scratch, full of veggies and meatballs.  Chimichanga is perfectly created with a light flaky outer crust and full of shredded beef and cheese.  Plenty of parking but a very narrow parking lot.  Great atmosphere, friendly staff.  Highly recommend !

I didn't know this place existed until my friend suggested we meet here for lunch.  It was easy to find and I went in before noon to find just two tables occupied.  There was ample seating to choose from.  I told the woman working at the counter that I was waiting for my friend but I went to check out the menu first.

Once my friend arrived, we went up to order.  I ordered the Pad See Ew and Pad Thai for her.  There were plates, cutlery, napkins, and sauces at the front.  The woman got us cups of water which we brought to our table.  Right after we ordered, there were a steady stream of others coming in for lunch and take out.  I'm glad we didn't have to wait too long for our food.  When it was ready, the woman brought it to our table.

The portion size isn't large but good to fill me up for lunch.  The noodles were a bit greasy but could have been more flavourful.  If I could eat spicy food, I would have added a sauce to it to give it some more flavour and heat.  My friend added Thai Sriracha to her noodles and she liked the sauce.  The woman came to check on us a few times during our meal.  She was prompt to bring us a takeout container and bag for leftovers.  She was really friendly and helpful.

Next time, it would be good to try their half or whole chicken.  I liked the atmosphere being casual and didn't get noisy inside.
Incredible service & amazing food. Make sure to save room for the Sweet Corn Cheesecake!

I wish I could give more than 5 stars! We have gone for breakfast and lunch. Food is always fresh and tasty. Friendly host and waiting staff. The whole family eats well whenever we go to Caleb's and their prices are so reasonable. Great place for kids also.

I had a gift certificate for this place for their spa. I had my first massage (just chill- kinda your standard Swedish) and it was great. I had a bit left on the card so I went to the Devon location and got the refresh (I think). Based on the description it was the just chill plus a body scrub which I was SUPER excited about. When I got in there I was informed it was a body butter which bummer but fine. The masseuse seemed like he was in a major rush the entire time and the process was rote to him. Doesn't exactly make for a relaxing spa experience. At the end of it I didn't even get the body butter that made the massage worth apparently $30 more than the other one. I feel like a jerk complaining about a massage but it was something I was really looking forward to and it was a bit of a let down. I probably will not be back to this location.

Prepare your self to be here for at least 20 mins 

The bank itself is very old, no drive-through, and the staff moves extremely slow.

Sheesh, the food was TERRIBLE! The chicken and vegetables for the fajitas were probably frozen, and definitely flavorless. The fajitas weren't anywhere near sizzling and the refried beans and rice were cool. One of the salsas definitely used canned tomatoes. There was really nothing redeeming about this place. Even the service was pretty bad. I had barely started my meal when someone tied to take my plate away. My guess is that that food is so bad that they're often taking away almost full plates. Don't go there.
eh its alright. expensive and i dont think its that worth it. but at least go once to try it.
Went for dinner, poor menu selections. If you want good Polish food find some place to eat at. I will never go back.  0 stars

After extensive research and interviewing four property management companies, we chose Todd and PMI and we are glad we did. Our property is 2,000 miles away from us and we don't worry about it because Todd is really good at what he does. He keeps us informed of everything in a timely manner. VERY timely.  No lag in his responses, even with the difference in time zones. We also appreciate his professionalism, patience, and flexibility. Todd deals with the builder's warranty department, which not all property managers do. This is our first investment property and he's been extremely helpful in our learning process. We like how Todd conducts business.

Pros: 
- Convenient for when you're looking to feed an overworked sleep-deprived film crew at 6AM on a Sunday on a tight budget

Cons:
- Food is so bad that even overworked sleep-deprived film crew would rather starve 
- Eggs tasted like paper, toast tasted like cardboard, potatoes felt like decomposing fruits (not that I would know)

I cannot understand how they can mess up the most basic sides.. but hey, I guess they're not exactly known for their eggs, toast, and potatoes

Paying a bit more for Dunkin' Donuts sandwiches/croissants is much more worth it.

For a place that only sells chicken tenders & fries, Cane's knows their business. Your options are chicken tenders, chicken tenders, or chicken tender sandwich. 

CANE'S SAUCE pays the bills here. This special blend of magic is a staple of the franchise. Hard to explain, but the sauce has a unique kick of spice and welcomes dipping across the board. Being a Cane's sauce fanatic, I always order inside and pump extra cups, avoiding the $0.30/xtra sauce fee. 

Crinkle-cut fries are a great friend of mine, and very respected in the potato community. Arguably better than the chicken, crispy edges with a soft interior give the fries a thorough, smooth bite. Coleslaw is another featured side, which compliments the chicken masterfully. Texas toast bread with every combo--original i guess, compared to a customary biscuit, but never know how to utilize it. Like, do I make a chicken sandwich with this? Just seems extra, given what you already have. 

***With a recent expansion of Raising Cane's across the region, this location is met with open-arms. Again, the sauce alone will bring you back. SIDE NOTE: Grab a drink, the ICE here -- top notch. Pebble-shaped marble style ice makes a nice flurry noise when almost empty; Also allows for a swift straw cut to the bottom***

For an economy motel this was actually decent. Friendly front desk. The room itself was clean and not at all bleak. The hallway does smell like cat pee though. The room is the important part and for what you're paying you're getting a good deal.

Stay Away. My first impression was when i pulled up to the valet. No help getting luggage out nor greeted at car door. The guy just stood there and watched and I was traveling solo so it's not like I was letting other guest out and leaving luggage with them to go self park the car. Cool. I'm a able bodied young man so It's not like i was incapable of retrieving luggage from my car but still when you're in the customer service business there needs to be some modicum of CUSTOMER SERVICE. Walk up to the front desk. I'm standing right there in from of the person checking in customers. have to wait for him to finish his conversation with another co work before he greeted me...strike two.

NOISE: As previously stated in another review YOU CAN HEAR EVERYTHING from within your room: doors slamming and people going in and out. Strike three. 

The final straw that broke the camels back to write this review is that as I was returning from Mandeville for a wedding, I went to my middle compartment (where I last left my cable to charge my phone) my charging cable was gone. I'm not implying valet stole it but last i checked cables don't come with legs. Asked the guy who valeted my car upon arrival and of course he hadn't a clue. Asked the guy at the front desk and he said that the manager of valet was not in today. It was a fruitless effort. It ends with arriving to my room at 3:15 after a full day of being gone and it still had not been turned over. 

Do yourself a favor and spend your money at another more reputable establishment. 

*rooms are small, satellite cable is shoddy, wifi is ok.

Seeing how my fiance has been burning up in Hell's Kitchen, I decided to treat him to a winter retreat in Santa Barbara.  I was a little skeptical at first when I found this B & B deal combined with dinner at a local French Restaurant.  (Last time I found a hotel online, I swore I would never put myself through that again...) But I digress....My man and I arrived at the Cheshire Cat Inn and were swept through the rabbit hole (so to speak).  Our room was amazing upgraded to a beautiful suite complete with an in-room Jacuzzi and a cozy fireplace.  From the apricot homemade scones, to the friendly staff this was an inn to remember.  Now I have to find new holidays to warrant another escape. (I don't celebrate Valentine's Day but Memorial Day is so far away!
The customer service guy and his manager was rude and condensing. I will never do business with us bank again
We tried unlimited Korean BBQ for $20. Unlimited sides are really good. Of course, the BBQ was the best part of our meal. We'll come back.

This place is excellent!

Their Classic cheeseburger and Fries blows "In and out" away!
A little cheaper too!

This is my favorite cheesestake in Philly , It's a must when you visit Philadelphia. One small issue is that they have to do something to organize the waiting lines . A number must be giving to customers and must be shown outside the restaurant so everyone can see it when they call your number for picking up instead of calling names and no one can hear it specially if you're waiting outside .

Always funny to tell your friends that I'm going to get Crabs at Cooters! Then we met one of the funniest bartender and server, Dick Whitehead Lmfao! Not sure if it was a joke but it made our day and enjoyed the all-u-can eat crab legs and happy hour!!! Happy hour specials are too good to pass up, but parking is a nightmare so you may have to walk here.

Well Florida.. we finally did it. We got decent Chinese food. It took, well forever, but it's finally here. 

Tried the General Tso's and Orange chicken, both pretty damn good.

Rice was on point. 

Egg rolls, while nuclear hot for a period of time that almost didn't add up scientifically, very good.

We'll be back, and you should too. If they don't stay open we have only each other to blame.

We had a nice lunch here... enjoyed the Cajun shrimp fondue appetizer first!! Would recommend it for a creamy, spiced, hearty dip to go on slices of bread. It's rich and filling in a delicious way. 

Then we had the Italian pizza- slice shown in the picture. It's satisfying with nice cheese and meats and mushrooms. Personally, I prefer a more doughy or crispy crust than the soft one we had. So I probably would not re-order the pizza. 

Our table-mate had a nice looking spinach salad with fried goat cheese that I got to try- that was yummy! I would be interested in their other salad offerings. 

The inside is chic for a strip center restaurant. Nice for a lunch out or a date! Reasonable staff. Solid experience!

LOVE THIS PLACE! Love it so much I'm still sitting here for dinner writing the review. Really yummy!!!! EXTREMELY FRIENDLY!!! Will be back! Thank you so much! Carlynn was our server and she was sweet. DAVE IS AWESOME! We were asking him what to get and he surprised us with "The Kevin" which was also delicious! We also ordered the godfather flat bread and volcano roll! Every was perfect! Glad we came. Can't wait to come back, and also check out the brunch on Saturday and Sunday!!

Prepare for tar stains and dead bees in the sand. I unfortunately stepped on both!  great sunsets with views of the rigs, not too busy, nice quiet area, lots of lady bugs, and a really cool sailboat wreck to take pictures of if the tide allows. There were some sweet dogs off leash and plenty of free parking too, btw. It was relaxing to sit on the the rocks and ledges above the sand and just look out.

I'm no fan of large museums, but I have a soft spot for quirky collections--- especially when they are full of masterpieces. 

The Barnes' foundation is just that and is about the right amount of art that I can tolerate without getting overstimulated. 

It's probably the only museum in the world in which the audioguide (or some sort of introduction to the museum itself) is unmissable.
