It'll tell you things like how Mr. Barnes sought to improve defects in painting by matching them with other smaller paintings that drew attention away from the flaws. 

I hear it's moving. I guess the positive side is that it'll be easier to get there. The downside is that it'll probably become incredibly crowded.

Isabelle Rossi my BershireHathaway agent in Manasquan NJ is professional and friendly. She met with us when it was convenient for us. She took note of the requirements we were looking for in our forever home. Isabelle showed us many homes but we finally decided to buy a house that was closer to home.

Who doesn't love cheese? Well this was a perfect spot to get your grilled cheese. 

The workers were wonderful and very pleasant. The food was prepared quickly and when they say 2 mins it's literally 2 mins. I ordered the  Brielle  which was brie, cranberry chutney , caramelize onions , pine nuts on brioche with a side of kettle chips.  The sandwich was cheesy and very tasty. I like the combination of cranberry and the brie , it blended well together and it was delicious. 

I would definitely come back here again.

Even during rough pandemic times wait staff was wonderful. Allison was our waitress. She was over the top fun and attentive. We have a minor issue with the meal. And I mean minor. The manager and Allison bent over backwards. Food was delicious. Definitely going back.

Me and my friends stumble into this place as it was opening for the New Year after everything else was closed. We ordered a thin crust, The Hill and a deep dish, South Side Classico. Both pizzas came out fairly quick and they were both delicious! 

I would definitely hit this place up again when I'm back in St. Louis

Well since this is the only SA that I've been to I can't really say that it is the worst. But I found this amazingly cute, brand new peach dress for only $6 so I can't complain. Though it IS true that they have a plethora of leotards and shirts from the 80's.

I had the veal picatta. It was a VERY small serving. Cost extra to have vegetables instead of pasta. My meal was outstanding but expensive. $20 for a lunch. Our server was attentive till we got our food, then we never saw him again. We had to ask another waitress to send him over so that we could pay our bill.

Didn't expect it but, Byrne's Grilled Pizza was really magnificent. Clean and cute atmosphere. Byrne's Grilled Pizza has a fantastic Byrne's Specialty Pizza, that was my first time trying it here, and I was surprised with how good it was. Team ensured that everything was fine. I definitely recommend and would come again!

Wish I could put negative stars. Y'all hire racist staff. You're slow as fuck and you're good doesn't even taste that good. Fuck this business no wonder y'all lost all the kids from that high school up the street. You guys suck at your job.

I just wrote an awful review of another place so I am balancing my karma with a few positive ones. 
Palermo is really very very good. I personally know they use top fresh ingredients and staples of the pizza biz. They have sent rolls back that were not fresh. Pizza is consistently very very good (cheesy and with more oil than some prefer). I think it is best most consistent in pottstown. Family owners are nice enough but a little impersonal at times. Staple in the area...always a good bet and excellent cheese steaks and sammy's to boot.
They dont deliver because they said it diminishes the quality of finished product. They have principles and quality food. a definite good place to chow

The atmosphere, the food, the portions and the service are all superb at Tufo House!!

My daughter and I really love Korean food and we really admire the culture! We were super happy to find this homey, cozy and an absolute gem! Finally, we don't have to drive all the way to Sparks for decent Korean food!

My Kat really enjoyed the tasty, and richly spiced Topokki. She craves it every week, mm ok more than once a week. My favorite was the firery, spicy, very refreshing and comforting Kimchi Soft Tofu Soup!! That great combination of flavors and textures out of this world! Seriously their food is addiction! 

Gosh! We can wait to be back!

Very disappointed with their service.  Went in for an oil change - it took an hour and 40 minutes.  They ran out of an oil filter and had to order one from elsewhere - rather than telling me, I was stuck waiting and waiting until I finally said something and was informed it would be "only a few more minutes."  So the customer service was less than stellar.

The actual servicing was questionable as well.  When I left, my check engine light was still on and my front right tire still looked underinflated.  I did not want to go back in for fear that it would be another half hour for them to do something they should have done anyway.  Avoid this place at all costs!

Update: Turns out I had a nail in my tire that was causing the underinflation and causing the check engine light to come on.  Turns out these guys either suck or do not bother doing a good job because they completely missed it.  Terrible.

I had to take my friend to the airport one morning and he was hell bent on getting to district donuts. He loves their coffee and cinnamon rolls. I've only tasted their coffee, so I decided to give their food a chance. I ordered the fried chicken biscuit. Hands down the best chicken biscuit I have ever had. The chicken was fried throughly, crispy and full of flavor. The biscuit is similar to church's chicken biscuits, honey covered, crunchy edges and a soft middle. My mouth is watering as I think about it. Will definitely be returning for another, SOOOON!

Wow. Baja Cafe screams "Tucson" in its choice of entrees and ingredients. A true comfort food. The menu consists of a mix of local southwestern flavors, traditional and modern breakfast.

Baja cafe offers the quality food of a higher end breakfast joint without the frills, wait or price.

The menu itself is an experience, and is often rotating with new specials. Though some diverse entrees might not agree with less ecclectic breakfast traditionalists, there is definitely something here for everyone!

Hands down, my favorite place to get a poboy, in the city that founded the poboy. Interestingly, it is named Parkway Bakery because it was indeed just that back in the day: a French bread bakery. My good friend used to walk there as a kid and pick up a loaf of bread and a sandwich almost every day in the summertime in New Orleans. Oh, the history!

Great atmosphere here. Great place to watch the game, hang out and have a drink with friends, bring guests from out of town, pick up a sandwich for a picnic - you name it, this is the place!

I tend to avoid meat, though I do lapse occasionally. Therefore, I know this is the best Reuben sandwich in the city. By a wide, wide margin. Sometimes when I'm on my no meat kick, I'll get a Reuben without the meat, which is basically a grilled cheese on rye with sauerkraut. Even this is amazing. They just have the best ingredients you can possibly assemble, I guess.

The oyster-shrimp combination poboy is fantastic. Again, best in the city. I also hear that the BBQ beef is amazing, as well as the roast beef. Brent, the hubby, always gets a hot turkey sandwich. He adores it.

Tip for the uninitiated: you can order your food at the bar. Even if there's a line, and you don't have a seat, you can order from a bartender. It's much faster than the line! 

The beer is always icy cold. The only thing I can say that's not terrific is the dessert. Save your calories, and have another beer!
Horrible service.  Insulting staff.  I've never felt compelled to write a review until this experience.  Avoid this place!
Great firm, great people. Darrell is the best broker in this market.

Rooster Soup, you have won me over! I had been hearing such great things about this place and very happy that I finally made the trip over for take out. 

I walked in at about 7pm on a weekday and was wondering how long it would take to get two orders of soup. The wonderful waitress told me she could have it ready for me in just five minutes! YAY! I decided on one order of the Smoked Matzo Ball and one Sweet Potato and Coconut Curry, and as promised, off I was in just five minutes!

Both of the soups were FANTASTIC. The Matzo Balls were seasoned and very flavorful. Compared to the Matzo balls I've had in the past, I liked these better (sorry Famous 4th). 

The Sweet Potato and Coconut Curry was a true winner. Very rich and creamy, with a decent amount of sweet potato chunks. I truly enjoyed it! This soup is usually topped with lentils, but since I'm allergic, they left them out of my order. Just something to keep in mind for anyone with food restrictions and/or dislikes!

My order of two soups (one quart sized, one medium) came out to roughly $20 and was the perfect amount of food for two people. 

Another thing to note is that 100% of their profits go to the Broad Street Ministry's Hospitality Collaborative to feed those in need. Enjoying a delicious meal and supporting a great mission? Count me in. 

I'm really looking forward to returning to Rooster Soup and trying more of their soups and even some sandwiches next time! Hopefully I'll be able to dine in then too! :)
Who doesn't like Starbucks? Aliens, maybe? Yeti? People who don't like good coffee?
If I could give 100 stars I would. Hands down best sandwiches in Philly. Everything is out of this world - food, service, decor, pickles. Heaven.

While in the area for an errand, I looked on Yelp for anything interesting before leaving.

A bread bakery?  I didn't know south jersey had one...
 to be honest, I always buy baguettes from Panera.

The man running the place was genuinely friendly. 
I picked up a French baguette and an apple monkey bread.  Both were fresh.
 I do wish the baguette was a little crustier, but otherwise good.  The monkey bread was doughy bready sugary comforting goodness.

I'm jealous of the Yelpers who scored pizza breads--I'll have to come back next time I'm around.

We have a ten month old son who cannot sit still... Debra was the "master of distraction" as she gave our boy his first haircut! The expertise was obvious and well worth the visit from the south. A fun environment for youngsters and a memorable one for us and our little guy. Thank you!

oh no tart l you are wrong. this place is nasty. husband and i ate here once. everything on the buffet had been sitting there for hours and trays that were empty were not bothered to be refilled. we swore never to go back. and it's in lions head... not sylvan park.

I was really excited when this restaurant opened in KOP because I had heard great things about the one in DC. In terms of atmosphere and menu options, this place would still get 4-5 stars. BUT the service has been so atrociously bad (both times that I've eaten here) that I will most likely never go back. 

My first time at Founding Farmers, my table ordered the skillet cornbread as an appetizer and were presented with a different bread basket that arrived at the same time as our entrees arrived. We told the waiter, but we said not to worry about it - and assumed it was a fluke. It wasn't that busy that night, but we had empty water glasses for the majority of the meal. Our table sat for about an hour and probably were checked on twice at most. Still, the food was good so a few weeks later I decided to go back. 

My second time was what really got me. My friend and I had to ask 3 times over the course of 20-25 minutes before we were given water. I ordered the southern chicken and someone brought out the wrong entree. I understand that sometimes it happens but it took about 10 minutes to get our waiter's attention to switch the entree. It stayed on my bill which I felt like wasn't really fair given the circumstance (when my meal finally arrived I ended up taking it home in a box because we were finishing up). My friend and I both ordered a cocktail and although we finished them while waiting for our food, we weren't asked if we wanted a second drink the entire time we were there. I really couldn't believe it was that bad on a Friday night. 

Not sure if it's a combination of being poorly managed, hiring bad or inexperienced waiters, or some sort of disconnect between the kitchen and other staff. Really disappointing experiences for a place with so much potential

I was here for brunch with Groupon. They said that we could only choose  to eat from a less than half the menu if we had Groupon. Not worth the money we paid.  disappointed!! But the service was good..

Best truly quality restaurant I've been to in SB. The care of each dish is amazing. The staff is super courteous and attentive. If you want truly authentic food served piping hot and flavourful, do yourself a favor and go enjoy this place. The Doro Wat Stew is a must. Heater Outdoor and indoor seating, this place has a great vibe. 10/10. Don't sleep on this place SB! Go support!

After doing business with TN Appliance Exchange for over 3 years, I can say their quality is on the decline, and I've had to stop doing business with them. They installed a refrigerator that was so dented I returned it, and they replaced it with one that stopped working the next day. I was without a refrigerator for a week while they tried to fix it. During that time I had guests in town and lost a full fridge of groceries. They ended up picking up the broken refrigerator and refunding me for the appliance. I bought a new one from HH Gregg for less.  

I was crazy to do business with them again, but I had a good experience with a washer and dryer in the past so I tried one more time. This time the dryer worked for 3 months before it broke. They replaced it and that one broke. They tried to fix it over the course of 3 weeks but couldn't. I finally gave up and went back to HH Gregg. 

I can't tell you how much time and money I lost because of this company. I never recouped what I spent on the washer/dryer. They give people a longer warranty to leave social media comments so you are probably not getting the full picture online. I hope my story saves other people from losing the time and money I did by doing business with them. I learned a long hard lesson about purchasing refurbished and won't go down that rode again if I can help it.

This place is great! In the winter and the summer. More skiing terrain than most of, if not all other ski resorts in Idaho. 
Two disc golf courses that are not for the faint of heart. Mountain biking, hiking. Rock climbing, if you can find the bolted route. If you're going to climb do you research. Know before you go! This place is an out door enthusiast Dream.
OK time to come clean. I worked there from 1998-2003 so I am a bit bias. I have massive love for this place. I have never worked for or been treated better at a place of employment. 
I returned to Bogus in the summer of 2015 only a few things have changed, and it's for the better. Like an outdoor amfatheate with live concerts on weekends. Nice to see Mike, Tim, and Bob. These men are awesome. They are the stewards of Bogus. If you see them thank them.
If you live in the Treasure Valley take advantage of this wonderful place. If you are passing through and wondering if you should drive up, yes go have fun, stay the night.

The food was really tasty. There are places to sit indoor and outdoor. It I is really nice. Food is good. The chicken  sandwich is very very laRge. I also had the hush puppies and they were so delicious. I would recommend this place to friends for sure.
This place rocked a few years ago so we thought we would go there again since we were back in town.  The food was just ok the service sucked.

Great experience here. Got an excellent, tremendously relaxing, and very professional full-body massage and some good advice for looking after my injured trapezius muscle. Lovely atmosphere and friendly folks working here -- I appreciated all the thoughtful touches, like getting to choose an essential oil to scent the room during my massage.

My wife and I had just arrived in Philly and were feeling a bit peckish so we left our Ritz Carlton room and walked around to find the Capital Grille, a chain steakhouse which I had never heard of before. We got in with no problem. We ordered a spinach salad with warm bacon dressing to share, which was pretty decent. My main was the dry aged porterhouse steak which was cooked to a nice mid rare but could've used more seasoning. My wife ordered the split roasted chicken, It was a whole frickin' chicken, who the hell can eat a whole frickin' chicken, I forgot we were in Philadelphia. She managed to eat a quarter of it, I think I helped her out with one other piece. So we wrapped up the rest of it with some grilled asparagus that we also had in a doggie and brought it back to our hotel room....Yep...... we had a picnic lunch in Independence park the next afternoon with the remaining chix and some cheese and bread bought at the Reading market. Booyah

By the way.....we didn't have dessert.

How oh how will I ever eat another Po' Boy from anyplace else?  This is the cats pajamas!.. Great Atmosphere.. and the worlds best Po' Boys!.. Gotta get the fries with a side o' gravy and grab a bag a Zapps chips too!!  I split a Shrimp and Oyster and a Beef with a friend .. impossible to leave here hungry or let down!  Just plain ole YUMMMmmmmmm!

We heard good things and read good reviews on yelp! so we checked them out for dinner Friday night. Yes, all you can eat but we ordered off the regular (Hibachi dinner) menu because my partner didn't want just sushi that night. We did each order a Philly roll to go with our dinner and let's begin with the negatives: 

1. We should have showed you our Buy 1/50% off 2nd dinner coupon from a money mailer. Because after you brought us the bill, you then told us that that coupon was only good for the all-you-can-eat sushi, not the hibachi dinner menu. Our bill was $66 - too expensive for this type of food. 
2. Your service is WAY TOO FAST!! You brought out our hot hibachi dinners too soon; we were still eating our soup, salads, and sushi appetizers! Your servers need to time the serving better or else you will lose customers who don't want to be rushed. 
3. Your servers need to pay attention to their tables! When we order hot green tea, we MAY wish to drink a second cup (never asked). When we drink water, we may need a refill (or iced tea). You were quick to bring out our meals, but slow to follow up to make sure everything else okay.

Now for the positives: the food was delicious, the sushi fresh, and place busy, and the price for all you can eat acceptable. 
Will we be back? Yes!! But only for sushi because $22.95 is a good price for the all-you-can-eat menu (which includes non-suchi items too).

I called Lowry to have them install a range hood over my stove.  They told me they do that work and everything I researched on the internet said to get an HVAC guy to do it.
The morning of the appointment, the technician called and said they did not do that type of work.
I guess when the receptionist, who had no background in HVAC makes the decision, you suffer the lack of expertise she has in making the decision for an appointment.

Mother's Day brunch was a disappointment. I don't know they were ready for everyone. Seemed like a hit and a miss. Not enough help.. inlet station was 2 small pans and the same guy was trying to cut the over done / dry prime rib. Utensils were missing.. foos wasn't replenished enough and chocolate fountain was a "goopy" mess (chocolate was seized up).. bottom-less mimosas were $16.99 and buffet was $29.99 / person. Not really with the price. Disappointed.

Blown away by the food here! We visited SB on vacation and ended up here twice!

Pros: 
AMAZING recommendation by the to-go cashier to order the local crab. It was sweet, delicious, and large for the price. The Clam Chowder bread bowls were so delicious we ordered them a second time to go. 
GREAT location on the wharf and fast to-go service. 

Cons:
The wait to sit at the restaurant inside or on one of the limited tables outside (4). Better choice is to order the To-Go items and sit on the benches.
Benches covered in bird poop so, watch out! 
Those seagulls are mean and will grab food right off your plate.

We will return!! Highly recommended.

After years of hearing about the hype and the awful experience I had with Party City last Oct (read Yelp review!) coupled with the fact I was privy to the knowledge that a fair amount of stuff would be on sale, I made my maiden voyage to Masquerade.

After we made our way past the mostly plus size $5 corset display and stripper attire masquerading as club attire on the sidewalk in we walked. The left is party decorations as well as children's costumes while the right side is a hybrid of a Hot Topic/Adult costume/make-up counter. What I liked about this place was the variety; it reminded me largely of a place I had visited in NYC called Ricky's (Also year round if I'm not mistaken!). Wigs,hats, stockings, and petticoats galore!

Apparently the place has switched owners and the new owners are clearing out the top adult toy/lingerie section since it doesn't match their tastes. I really enjoyed the corset/pin-up/steampunk/etc section because although it was pricey, it was definitely better quality than the cheesy one-time-use costumes most Halloween places sell for top dollar.
Average sushi...nice location.

Great experience.  Most importantly, obviously, the beer was quite good. The pale ale was the standout.  The IPA was made with toasted coconut, which was evident in the subtle beer that was not like a typical resiny, piney IPA.  The saison was quite tangy and probably a better brew for warmer weather.  The food menu is limited as they don't have a real restaurant - all the food is prepared behind the bar.  The kimichi was quite spicy and hot, with real zing.  The ham, cheese and fig spread sandwich was fairly good.  The fig spread though was a bit too sweet, so a sharper/saltier cheese would have worked better.  The pate was good (especially with the mustard that came with the sandwich), although I prefer a coarser grind.  The pate also came with arugula and pickled vegetables.  As noted by others, all the dishes came with their bread, which we found quite good.
The grill cheese sandwich on the lunch menu is not like anything you have ever tried.  Feels like eating a Philly cheesesteak without all the fat.  The female wait staff unusually cranky.

I can't say enough about how great this company is!  The owner, Brett,  receives an A+++ in customer service.  He is professional, friendly, genuine and "at the top of his game" in running a successful business with the utmost integrity.  The man who did my car, Drew, was very nice, and he did a wonderful job.  I had an Express Wash for $40!  I was amazed at the reasonable price and work quality.  I will use the service often.

I've been a Victory fan for many years, thought I'd visit the new Philly location. Hit on all cylinders - always excellent beer, food was very good - roast pork sandwich, not Dinic's but solid - and fantastic, friendly service. Kudos to second floor bartender (sorry I didn't catch her name) and server Nillie who was also super helpful. Management clearly makes the customer the priority and is hiring the best people. Will be back!

The food was good, but not the best. If you go for the pho, go simple. 15 was good, the rest were kind of greasy. The lunch special is good. If you've never tried crunchy noodles, this is the place to try them.
We love the Reef! They were busy but made a table happen for us. Molly was an awesome server, super attentive and personable. Best quesadilla ever! Great place to be on a Saturday night!

Really good food. Very filling. Service was slow and forgot most of our requests such as straw, Splenda, slay and pepper etc but overall food was good. Drinks could are good but I have had better ones.  Price doesn't really match with serving and flavors but looking forward to their happy hour next time as that has better offers. Trust

I've been fortunate enough to travel to Lockhart, Texas (aka "Hill country") to visit family and indulge myself with barbecue.  Percy Street is the most authentic I've had outside of the Lone Star State.  I love that they have both single and family-style sides and portions on the menu.  Start your meal off by choosing a drink from their plethora of beers and then get down to business with the brisket!  Don't scrimp on their house-made barbecue sauces, either.  I'm limited when it comes to their sides, but my friends really seem to enjoy them (especially the mac and cheese).  The cafeteria-style seating creates a nice atmosphere, but the bar is just as nice to sit at, as well.  Definitely a favorite restaurant of mine.

Oh and by the way, the Hillbilly Gatorade is not for the faint of heart (or stomach).  Miller Hi-Life, pickle juice, and barbecue sauce make for an interesting combo...

Having moved away, it has been far too long since I visited one of Philly's best restaurants, Will BYOB.  I even attended opening night back in 2012 (see my original review below), and we've had many, many memorable wine dinners here over the years.

I'm happy to say Will is still as great as ever!

One of the best dining deals in Philly is their Sunday night 4-course prix fixe dinner for $45.  We enjoyed:

WHITE GAZPACHO: With white grape, white cucumber, lemongrass sorbet

SMOKED WHITEFISH TARTAN: Grilled sourdough, horseradish, dill

SOFT SHELL CRAB: Green tomato, green asparagus, saffron tzatziki...this was amazing!

CHEESE: Brie du Pommier, a cow's milk cheese from France, with champagne truffle marmalade, molasses-rye brioche

BYOB of course (good stems), so we brought a 2012 Roses de Jeanne / Cédric Bouchard Champagne Blanc de Noirs Côte de Val Vilaine.  Champagne goes with everything!

Service by Benjamin was excellent, and Chef Chis even gave my chefy friend a tour of the tiny kitchen at the end of the meal.

We are regulars at ocean prime. This and Flemings are the only chains we like locally. Ocean prime consistently has excellent food, drinks, and service. I could eat just the side dishes. Yum!  We also love taking our own bottle of wine because they have no corking fee. Tip accordingly though!

Great small breakfast place. I had the philly sandwich and it was great. My husband had the lumberjack which he enjoyed thoroughly. They did not come with potatoes so we ordered a plate and shared them. Highly recommend

If you don't have reservations, don't bother. The food isn't worth more than about a 15 min. wait. With no reservation, we  were first told that it would be an hour but then we were told it would be about 45 mins. We waited over an hour and a half and the best thing that I can say about this place is the bartender Myles was terrific! The customer service at the front was terrible. It was extremely evident that they weren't used to handling large crowds. I was ready to go someplace else except I was in the minority and the restaurant came highly recommended. Once we were seated and our food came I was disappointed. It was very average. I couldn't believe that we had waited so long for average food (shame on us!). The next night we went to Oceana's where again there was a wait. Only you could tell they were used to dealing with large crowds and waiting customers. AND the food there was so much better!

I've been coming here for a few years, since it was on Broad Ripple Avenue. It's so soothing and fun, and there are so many materials. Yesterday I tried a new technique, and the employee (maybe the owner? her name is Christen) gave me tips on how to do it well. We'll see how it turns out once it's glazed and fired, but I was pretty pleased with it for my first time.

There are a lot of families and small parties here, but it's normal to come in alone or with just one friend. Everything is set up with lots of explanations and suggestions. I wish some of the pieces were a bit cheaper, but considering all the materials you can use to decorate them, it's understandable. Definitely check it out. You might get into a new hobby.

Value: cheapest in the area at the time that I looked, that was a big plus.  Had lots of perks like the complimentary breakfast, coffee, and wine and cheese.  

Staff: the cleaning staff really banged around a lot in the room next to ours.  Sounded like they were playing some game where the object is to hit the wall every 10 seconds, and then vacuum in between.  For 15 minutes.  On the other hand, they did a darn good job of tidying up the room.  The front desk staff were beyond kind and had a very calm, beach-goer attitude about them. 

Location: a block from the beach, how much better can ya get!  Also within walking distance to lots of local restaurants and the pier.

First time in Nashville! Our uber driver recommended it and we absolutely loved it! We ordered Mac n cheese with the hot chicken and we shared it. It was a good size portion and it hit the spot. Drinks are delicious too!

Had my first appointment with Cheyenne today. She was the best! I sort of knew what I wanted, but not really....and she gave me such a great post-COVID haircut!!! I had 5 inches of just blahhhhh and she shaped me up and gave me life again. Cheyenne was pleasant and easy to talk to. The salon was clean and quiet, but a steady stream of customers came in. If you're looking for a new stylist, I recommend Cheyenne at Fringe.
One of the more beautiful restaurants in New Orleans. Great wine selections and really good food, great for sharing.

Ordered scallops with risotto for $20.95.  Boy was I disappointed when my platter had a total of THREE medium size SCALLOPS.  Will never go back! This was our fourth time here.  The waiter was great!  He went back and talked to someone.  He felt bad but there was nothing he could do...not an extra scallop or even a dessert.  Terrible customer service.

The food was very good. Our waiter made our dining event a fail. We ordered a 4 course family style feast. The waiter "plopped" our salad down on the middle of the table and ran. We had all taken a bit, when 50% of our salad was 0removed because our pasta course had arrived. The veal was right on top, time wise. When we tried to flag Mike, the waiter down, we had no luck. He was in front of the restaurant eating bread! He got us silverware, never refilled our drinks and we spent forever while he sat at a friend's table chatting.
I have been at this restaurant several times and always had outstanding service. This waiter made our dining experience, more like an expensive trip to Applebees.

Food is pretty good. I went there last night and my kids and I ate outside. The tables and ground outside were disgusting. There were used masks left on tables, the ground was sticky and stained. I doubt the tables have been washed in a long time. A good power wash is greatly needed.
Great Italian sub on a fresh whole wheat roll. Service prompt and friendly.  Order filled quickly.

The Saenger Theater is so beautiful. I came to see D.L. Hughley and Eddie Griffith. I truly enjoyed the atmosphere.  They had a bar outside of the theater. The inside of the theater is indescribable.  The appearance of a starry sky when you look up is nice.  The fancy decor is something you have to witness up close and personal.

I'm a regular here and I was really disappointed with the service today. I ordered a Greek salad with chicken after waiting over an hour for my salad and being told by the server that my food was being plated I had enough and left. He also tried finding a manager but the manager wasn't around.

Every meal we had there was terrific! The service was excellent also! Would recommend it to anyone looking for either an appetizer or a sandwich or a dinner meal. You can't go wrong! And Jason and Brian are excellent bartenders!!!

Here's to another review for Walk On's. My mom got in on Wednesday and we are taking her to all of our favorite spots. 

Tonight we had the usual, literally the usual, tunatini, cheese fries (no bacon), slides, and red fish tacos.  The wife and I loved our food as usual. Mom really loved the cabbage with the tunatini, the fries and the sliders. She was slightly less impressed with the fish tacos as she's not accustomed to the blackened seasoning. 

For dessert, now this is not our usual, bread pudding and beignets. I'm not a sweets person so I say I can't judge. The wife and mom loved the desserts. Kudos walk ons another win!

Nallely was also great. Attentive and moved us to warmer seat!

Inexpensive, surprisingly fresh and delicious, low calories. We had the Lukes TrIO, lobster bisque, clam chowder.

Simple menu, friendly, hip staff, cape cod wharf decor

Very good, the rolls remind me of the bread of grilled cheese

Byob, A+

Not a negative, but for the special seasoning ddid't seem right on the shrimp roll, for me, it left an odd aftertaste. But on the lobster and crab roll it seemed to fit perfectly, again, that's just me.

Give it a try, we will definitely come back for more
Excellent fresh and authentic Korean food. A healthy alternative to the heavy local cuisine. Large portion sizes and friendly service.

I went here two years ago and it was terrible. Two years later, I decided to give it another chance. The foods definitely improved, though I still think they aren't great. However, I will recommend the Spicy Garlic Chicken Wings since that was my favorite out of all the dishes that I ordered (General Tsao, Fried Rice, Peking Pork, Tofu & Veggie).

If you dine in, be warned that the air is polluted with their oily smoke since their venting isn't good. The place isn't fancy and it is small, so seating is limited.

By far my favorite airport for any long delays. It is very well organized and is rare to find any crowding during the week. Out food selections are pretty good both in the lobby like area before security and at the food courts after security station. It has kinda spoiled me for when I go onsite for work and their airports are a disaster and nobody knows anything.

I don't think there is a better deal for late night after bar munchies than Empire.  Slices are the perfect way to end the nigh and you really can't beat the price!  We went there on a Friday after some bar hopping and it was way worth it!

It's good when you go to an ethnic food restaurant and people of that ethnicity are actually eating there. The food is authentic and delicious. I asked for something not on the menu and they accommodated happily. The decor is modern and very open. The owner Bobby is friendly and checked on us during our meal.  We had one mishap when the wrong naan came out but they promptly remade what we ordered and it was hot and fresh and yummy. 

We enjoyed the chicken rahra and chicken saag with an order of garlic chili naan. Portions were great, not too little or too much. We usually prefer made to order rather than buffet so I can't comment on that but it seemed popular on the weekend we visited. We have now been here twice and had a consistently good experience, with and without kids in tow. We will be back!

Taps are dirty / beer is flat and the food sucks 
They managed to screw up a baked pretzel app.  How do you do that?
Not a fan

I came here on a Friday morning, just before 7.  I came for two specific (maple bar and Cinnamon roll) donuts and two individual milks, this doesn't seem like too much of a request.  When I walked in there were three people in front of me, one person working the counter, and the person ordering was ordering about 2 dozen.  I knew this wasn't going to end well.  About 5 minutes into waiting, another person showed up to help work the counter and the line started to move.  By the time I got to the front I realized they were out of maple bars, and had a very  limited selection to substitute.  I picked out my two donuts, got to pay and asked for two milks.  Nope. no can do.  Out of milk.  I left the shop defeated and not optimistic about my donuts.  In the end, they tasted alright, nothing special.  For a place that does one thing - they didn't do it well.  There are too many other places to try, I won't be back.
The service was really great, all of the workers there are really friendly, and the food is delicious& inexpensive!

Don't miss the BYOB opportunity! There's a wine & spirits right across the road by ShopRite, but nevertheless, the food itself here is enough to leave you happy. Authentic Sichuan dishes comparable to Chili Szechuan and Han Dynasty, but slightly cheaper and just as flavorful. 

Our group of 5 came on a Saturday night and found the place quite occupied. Service was attentive from the start, and the two middle-aged waitresses were happy and accommodating of our needs. 

FOOD: 
Dan Dan Noodles - heavier on the sesame/peanut sauce, which is authentic. Comes in a small bowl so perfect for sampling as you won't get full on the carbs! 
Beef and tripe in chili oil - I thought this was perfect! Not drowned in chili oil and flavored just right. And plenty of meat, which is always a plus. 
Spicy boiling fish filet - delicious! Plenty of filet and comes in a huge pot loaded with dried chili peppers. It looks different from what you'd typically get from other places, but it's actually quite authentic in terms of what you'd receive in China. 
Cumin lamb - AMAZING flavors. Meat is tender and juicy. Favorite. 
Peking duck - the only letdown of the night but it's an out-of-place dish at a Sichuan restaurant so what do you expect. No points deducted. 

We were comfortably fed - no leftovers and no food coma. Happy to return!

I went for the instagram/boomerang opportunity and I can't say that I'll be rushing to go back. First, there's a $5 cover. I don't get places in Philly that charge cover; to me it just feels out of place. My friend and I went early-ish on a Friday night (10:30) after reading from reviews that the line gets ridiculous. After talking to the bouncer, though, there's really no way to tell what time the line's going to get long so you kind of just have to take your chances. Luckily, when we went, there was no line to get in. 

I also just want to take a moment to point out how it's confusing to find the entrance to this place. It's on 16th street between JFK and Market despite the address being listed as Market St. But I guess if you go when the line is long you won't have any trouble finding the entrance. 

Ok, then you get inside, and the place is actually quite large and, because it's so popular right now, it's filled with everyone and anyone. Obviously, the crowds mentioned in other reviews predominate, but honestly everyone is there. My friend and I tried the dance floor. It was overcrowded and sticky from everyone's spilled drinks. We lasted about 20 seconds out there. We spent most of our time in the upstairs bar. Space to dance, quieter, and ample position for people watching on the dance floor. 

I will say, I'm obsessed with the organized process to get in the ball pit though! You go and basically make a reservation for the pit. They take your number so that instead of waiting in line, you're free to enjoy what the rest of the place has to offer and the text you when it's your turn. When you show up, you have about 5 min in the pit (which sounds short but honestly you don't need a ton of time in there, the novelty wears off quick). They have a bag drop system that can also hold your drinks for you that is monitored by staff and then a place for your shoes that is moderately watched (but no "coat check" type system like that for your bags/drinks) so maybe don't wear your nicest pair of shoes here. I had fun and I think this would be a cool spot to go out with a bunch of people, but once you've tried the ball pit the novelty of the place has worn off and really you're just at another dance club in a basement that has a more lax dress code.

Food is very good and super fresh.  The variety of menu options is great from Malaysian, to Japanese to Indian.....  The Rainbow roll is amazing and their cheese cake is to die for!!  Our new favorite restaurant.  Can't forget to mention the is service great and staff is so friendly.

The WORST SERVICE EVER!!!!! We were treated horribly. No one seems to know what's going on with the reservations and I got charged for two nights even though we stayed one. When I tried to get the charge reversed over the phone, I was given little to no help to pin down the manager who could give it to me. Spend the extra ten bucks and stay somewhere else. It's not worth it.
Best hoagies and cheesesteaks in the area without question.

Excellent breakfast and the location is historic. Make sure you try the Poblano's Hot Sauce with your breakfast. It's locally made in Tucson and very flavorful. 

Their deserts are really good as well.

I went to English Ivy's last night for the first time. Considering we've been in the downtown area for 5 years, it was about time. We kept avoiding it due to smoking, but found they had a nonsmoking dining room which kept the majority of the smoke at bay in the bar area. The atmosphere was dark, like a good Midwestern supper club. Our waiter was very helpful and the staff was attentive.

Our meal was the $10.95 surf and turf that included soup or salad, bacon-wrapped steak, 6 shrimp, roll, vegetables and potato. Extremely good value. There were also cheap drinks. They have other food specials every night of the week.

Overall, I would recommend English Ivy's to anyone who misses their local, hometown place and enjoys a variety of good food and drink.

My son had his 5th birthday party here as its one of his favorite places to go. Paul and the rest of the Airheads team exceeded our expectations. They were very accommodating and made sure every child had a great time. They setup everything in the room and helped to make it very stress free for me as the mom which is not what I'm used to! 

Thank you for an amazing experience and I highly recommend Airheads for a birthday party or just for a few hours of fun jumping! Great for little kids and big kids!

Been going to Lester's for several years now.  Have been to the Ladue location (near Highway 40), but seem to go to the location at 14810 Clayton Road.  I believe that it used to be a Fitz's Root Beer location before it was converted to Lester's.

We tend to get there about 11:20 AM - the place is large, but seems to never be very full.  It never gets very full - unless there is March Madness going on.  I have never been here during dinner hours; only for lunch.

I think one of their good deals is the hamburger and fries for an additional $1.  Some of their items - especially their larger salads are over priced.  I'm a huge advocate of eating out often, but eating out cheap!

Service is usually pretty good - never great, never awful.

I am a huge fan of Papa Johns pizza. Unfortunately I will no longer be ordering because of the service I witnessed while trying place an order over the phone. The employee seemed novice and barely knew anything I was asking. Everything I asked, he put me on hold to ask someone else. Then when I tried to pay by credit card, I got down to the last 4 digits and he asked me to repeat. Very frustrating therefore I will be ordering elsewhere from now on. Great pizza, horrible service. Get with it.

The service here is not consistent. I've always been willing to just let it go and not say/review something, but today specifically I arrived 10 minutes early for an oil change appointment. I then waited an hour and fifty minutes for my vehicle to return. I was also charged for diesel on my Yaris which they changed when it was brought to their attention, but still a ridiculous oversight.

Oh ING, how I love your $0.75 iced coffee refills and assortment of baked goods.  How you infuriate me once every so often when you're closed and I have to settle for Bonte, Miel, or Dunkin Donuts...

Good(s):
Outstanding coffee 
Pumpkin Cheese Muffin - Mmmm
Blueberry Crumb Muffin - Mmm

Bad(s):
Closed more often than the average coffeeshop

This used to be our spot. Breakfast, quick, easy, and usually pretty good. The last two times we've noticed a serious decline in food quality. Today was our last visit. We won't be back. My French onion soup was filled with rye bread crumbs, not the usual toasted roll. It was salty and inedible. Bacon was obviously from earlier in the day which they tried to compensate by giving us a plate full. You noticed the plate of bacon go back uneaten and the soup barely touched... but no one cared. Not even a question of "how was everything?" At check out. Sorry. Like your server heard me say... we'll make the drive to Tiffany's.

Based on reviews and us starving as getting in late we did a to go order here.. 

The salad was nice, romaine lettuce all fresh..

The onion rings were huge and tasty.. Not overly battered which was nice..

I got the lobster roll and like people say it was fresh.. Not the best I have ever had, still goes to the cape for that.. But decent..

The roll was toasted perfectly!!!

Latin night is ridiculous the guy who teaches is a fake and how would any one know really unless your a latin american?
All salsa lessons are a scam people. No one dances this way in any latin american country. These are lessons for a real dance competition!
Argentina Spain and USSR are the best in Salsa. Don't get laughed at please stop. Your being used in a made up world a tourists idea of making quick cash
Stop being a follower and stop this business

My potatoes were overcooked no seasoning the bread that they brought to the table for the crab claws was Honda as a rock the crab claws smell like fish it was not a guest is variance for me I have been waiting to go to this place since I am Ylance In New Orleans Dee the car was wonderful but the food was not up to par

Chuck put in our tub a few months ago and we loved it right away. Really nice guy. Lots of experience and it shows. Arrived there on time and made the situation stress free for us. Don't usually write reviews for this kind of thing but we even had a question about our tub a few days ago, called Chuck, and he gave us our answer right away and even offered to stop by if we couldn't figure it out. Was easy to find what we needed to after the call. Thanks!

Do Not buy a Toyota from these people! I was in need of a new vehicle when my Lexus needed a new transmission. The staff at this dealership informed me that my best option would be a new vehicle since I owed more than my vehicle was worth and that I'd need a cosigner. I brought in a cosigner to get a new Camry, after purchasing the vehicle I realized that the vehicle they sold me did not have standard safety features, blind spot warning and rear cross alert which I wanted nor did the finance personnel put any of the paperwork for the vehicle in my name. When going back to the dealership for these issues I was told I need to keep the vehicle for a year before I would be able to upgrade out of it. 3 mos after I returned to the dealership and I am told I can get a totally new camry in my own name with zero money down but they cannot fix the paperwork for the vehicle I am currently in that was inaccurately titled in my cosigners name only!!!! Save yourself the headache the sales staff and finance department at this location are rude pushy do not listen to customers and will not stand behind their deals or fix their mistakes!!!!

LOVE this place! The people are friendly, the service is really good and they have an amazing selection and a great variety for people who are gluten intolerant. It is a bit more expensive - but worth every bit of it!

Listen, you don't go to a place like sushi kingdom for the service. You go because you can get fresh, delicious, all you can eat sushi for $25 a person. Seriously I won't eat sushi anywhere else... Those of us with big appetites can spend triple what you spend here for the same quality sushi. Service is for the most part quick and straight to the point. If it's busy, it's obviously gonna be slower. Ive always consistently had great food here. Only problem is that they don't always give you everything you order off the sheets.

Today was my first time at this store and I am hooked!  I took a slow walk around the entire store and saw a ton of items that are gluten free that I never even knew existed and their prices (at least on the things I have bought in the past) are definitely cheaper than Whole Foods. 

I even bought 2 sprouter jars for $5 something each. These are larger than the much more expensive (and plastic) ones on Amazon. They even have a great selection of seeds for sprouts, beans, and micro-greens at a much lower cost. I could get into SO much trouble there....lol... They have SO many cool items (that I know are cheaper than I have seen elsewhere or have never seen before).  They have great quality and a nice selection of produce. It's not as large as Whole Foods, but the prices were better on most of the items I know the price for at WF.  A huge percentage of their produce is organic, but doesn't seem to be as expensive. They have high standards for items to even be sold at their store.

I also scoped out several items that will make a yummy and quick gluten free lunch when I am out and need something.  That's hard to find around here.

You will want to bring your own bags or they can pack your goods in a box.  I had 2 boxes FULL!  ;)  They also have a GREAT selection of homeopathic "medicines" and essential oils for very reasonable prices.

Be sure to pick up their little coupon book when you go inside. I easily used four today buying things I normally would. Two of the items cost less than a dollar after the coupon.

Don't support this business! Would give 0 stars if I could. Maybe people are just like this in Reno but the lady behind the counter mumbled so much she was barely understandable and when we finally could understand she was being a huge bitch and not friendly to the customers (us) at all. One of us got a coffee the rest choose to just leave because she wasn't worth trying to deal with when we could go somewhere else just as easily and not be treated like crap and shown some actual service.

**Update, after writing this just have to say that the one of our group who did get a coffee said it tasted like crap and messed up his order completely because she wasn't listening at all when he ordered (even with no one else there), plus when asking for the wifi she gave it to him and asked him not to give it to any of the rest of our group because we didn't order.  ONCE AGAIN I HIGHLY SUGGEST AVOIDING THIS PLACE AT ALL COST UNTIL THEY GO OUT OF BUSINESS AND ARE REPLACED BY SOMEONE WHO KNOWS HOW TO MAKE COFFEE AND SHOW DECENT SERVICE!

First time at Growler. I have to admit, after reading previous reviews I was a little concerned that we might have slow service, but it was a terrific time all around for the four of us. We arrived 4:45ish. Snagged a table on the patio which had a gorgeous view of Pusch Ridge. Beer selection was fun, food was really good. Two of us got salads. Two got sandwiches. We all commented on how good the food was. Were definitely coming back. Plus, I logged onto their WiFi while I was there and was sent a $5 coupon to come back.  See you soon!!

COUGHING WARNING: Their Tobacco flavored products made both myself AND *surrounding people* (like my girlfriend) cough seriously. 

The TOBACCO FLAVORED products made her cough clinically worse for 6 months. And she was only a bystander, who does not vape herself. Finally after switching from the Zeus Tobacco flavored products, after 6 months of coughing, she stopped coughing entirely. 

No other E-juice made me do this. Zeus may have other flavors that do this, but I am absolutely positive about their Tobacco Ejuices.

Zeus, if you are reading this, please I hope you can change your Tobacco flavored Ejuices to some other formula. This is a health hazard. I am only reporting this because I want to help you guys and inform others that there is a serious health hazard involved with your Tobacco flavored Ejuices. Please isolate the formula, as I am sure there are other customers who have experienced the same. 

Good luck to you, if you are able to secure a better formula, please let me know and I will happily update this review. This is nothing personal, just a PSA health warning. Thanks.

Don't understand why it is not 5 stars!!!

Amazing restaurant, located in a quite residential area. Great choice of tapas and cocktails, which you can enjoy inside or on the outside terrace. The place was completely booked on a Saturday night and we were almost ready for take away. However when the manager came over and saw we were getting take away, he quickly set us up in a private dining room they have. He was extremely polite and super nice!

The food was delicious and came quickly. Must try: ceviche de la casa, fried calamari (melting in your mouth), and crab cake! Loved it so much that decided to have lunch in another outlet in St. Petersburg the next day!

It took 3 months to process two 1-hour tapes and all I received was 15 minutes of choppy, incomprehensible video. They didn't even check their work.

Additionally, I received an email from the ScanCafe CEO Anderson Schoenrock politicizing their problems, blaming delays on supplemental unemployment insurance benefits being provided by the CARES act. To me, this reads as the CEO blaming his poor performance on external factors rather than his own incompetence. I do not wish to reward this type of business leadership.

The noise is still bothersome and the wait can be long, but I just can't stay away  The ice cream is that good and the endless combinations of flavors and toppings keep me a regular customer. The boba is also  one of the best in town--great consistency and it's great that they have different types of boba. Try the crystal boba! So good.

Room is clean, and the breakfast was as expected in a Residence Inn. This is the layout of 20 years ago where it is a residential feeling to it. There is a reason Marriott has abandoned this format- it just doesn't work. Noisy air conditioner and outdoor main doors are not ideal.

For the condition of the hotel, it appears to be nice. The desk chair was broken (see photo) and the room had a bad odor. I told the front desk and they said they would send ozone machine down to room so it might be fixed. Bed was comfortable and the shower did not have mold, which is fantastic. One closet did not have hangers and the couple it did have in the other closet were really not good.

This could either be 3 or 4 stars depending on if you like the exterior door setup..
fabulous hoagies!!  best prosciutto ever snd the abruztzi spead is amazing!!!  the staff is always helpful when you can make a decision because everything rocks!!!

Went to NaBrasa for Valentine's Day. It was a really nice atmosphere for Valentine's Day. The food was amazing, as always with Brazilian, we left stuffed. We were a little early for our reservations so we got a drink at the bar. Bar tender was really nice and the three of us had a lovely conversation about tequila. Waiter was great and so were the gouchos. The salad bar, which shouldn't even really be called a salad bar since it has so much on it was amazing. Of the three brazilian places I've been so far, Na Brasa is quite posibly the best.

Possessing one of the more popular patios in the district, the Taproom has evolved quite a bit since I moved to town 12 years ago. At that time the food was inspired, you could stretch out your legs and order from an amazing array of beers. I recently had a burger that is on the short list for my worst all time. It was bland, luke-warm, and unadorned. It may or may not have been previously frozen and/or microwaved. Not so good for a place with "Grill" in the name. That being said, the beer is still great and I like the staff, they are simply overworked.

First of all, they use cheese whiz in a can. NOT REAL CHEESE!
Second, the bread was at least a day old. It was chewy and old.
Third, they barely sprinkled peppers and onions on the steak.
I was a tourist, led astray by a resident who obviously felt loyal to Geno's
Then, at the airport on my way home, I meet two women who told me about Max's Steaks only a few blocks away. So mad that I didn't get to eat a real Philly steak sandwich, complete with real cheese and flavor.
Don't eat at this place, no matter how many people are standing in line.
Max's steakhouse only takes a few minutes, they have indoor seating and everything is fresh.
Wish I read reviews on this dump, before eating at Geno's

Steven is a HAIR God!!! Never have i ever left his salon disappointed. Completely gifted and smart, i totally trust him and refuse to go anywhere else. If i cant fit into his busy schedule i will wait until there is an opening and I don't care it my hair gets a bit outgrown but then again my hair still looks good even after a couple months of it being done. Steven is the best and if you want quailty hair service then just listen and trust him, he is the professional and he know what works!

Delicious new restaurant by the Cunningham Group in the Bottling District connected to The Bottleworks Hotel.  Great cocktails and delicious Chilean Sea Bass!  Would definitely come back next time I'm in Indy!  Indy has a great up and coming food scene!

Friendly family owned restaurant great for breakfast lunch or dinner! Great Reuben sandwich and burgers and meatloaf sandwich!The owner and his wife are so friendly and greet us personally! love their sweet potato fries!

Great service. Multiple wait staff making sure we had all we needed. Francisco was amazing and, in honor of my mother's birthday, he gave us a complimentary sangria and dessert. 
We had the risotto special with proscuitto, melone and mozzerella. Really wonderful flavor combos. 
Also had the N.Y. Strip with Lyonnaise potatoes. 
Finished with chocolate mousse and vanilla mascarpone with brandied cherries. 
This is a fine establishment with a creative menu.

I am completely a fan of Bobbi. I would highly recommend her expertise. She knows what she is doing and makes you feel special every time you see her. I am a patient with facial distortion. I went to see if there was anything possible to help me smile again. She has done that. With my condition there is no perfect, but she has faithfully done wonders!! She helped in ways I couldn't imagine. I now have confidence and I smile. I have even taken a few family pictures.

Food was excellent, but service was hideous. The owner was so rude to us. The restaurant is BYOB and we brought Bloody Marys and Greyhounds. The waitress didn't say anything to us but after we were 3/4 through the meal, the owner came up to us and started yelling at us and telling us we has to buy the mixers at the restaurant. We apologized profusely and told her that we did not know but she glared at us disapprovingly and told us we could not bring mixers to a restaurant. We uncomfortably tried to finish the rest of our meal, but couldn't wait to get out of there. There is no reason for a restaurant owner to be so rude and mean. I will never go back and will try to spread the word.

Service: Slllloooooooooow. Waitress was nice but inattentive. No explanation for the food taking more than 40 minutes to arrive. The place wasn't all that busy. 
Food: Meh. I had a polenta dish that was nearly inedible. Undercooked polenta. It was a mish-mosh of beans, mushy polenta, and other stuff.  The wife had a halibut with red beans and rice although finding halibut was like finding a needle in a stack of rice.
Beer: The high point - I had a smoked cherry porter that was very good.
This was our first visit here and we wanted to like the place. I can't recommend it and can't think of a good reason to go back. 
P.S. My wife thought the waitress sounded like Reese Witherspoon. Go figure.
Our server Anthony was a rockstar!   The Alehouse nachos are delicious.  It's a great place to come and just hang.
Good place for unique SHOES, sweaters, and jackets. This place is cheeeeap and really, really big and clean! Definitely wish they had more goodwills like this!

Short version - Wonderful sales, service, and price!

Long version: What a great experience!  Just bought my first RV, a used Heartland Wilderness travel trailer in amazing condition.  The salesman, Bill Runyan was incredibly helpful and thorough in making sure we understood options and tradeoffs, and worked with us to get something that worked in our price range.  He also went "beyond the sale" in making sure we understood how everything in the camper worked and gave us solid advice on things we need to do, things to consider, and things to not worry about immediately.  Tina Ives in Service worked with our schedule to get the right hitch installed and to coordinate between parts, sales, and the service tech to make sure everything was set to go on time.  She also helped understand price/options on some add-ons that helped us make an informed decision on whether to buy there or elsewhere, resulting in some things being acquired through Amazon.  Steve was the service tech who not only did an amazing job installing the load balancing/anti-sway hitch, but who also took the time to train me in detail on the use of the hitch and manage practicing me through all aspects of use of it.  Being new to all of this that was a fantastic help.  Thanks to the Harberson RV team for an outstanding experience and great advice that will help us get started in the world of RV'ing!!!
I had the Tomahto burger and it was awesome! Parm fries were delicious as well and milk shake was great. We were all very satisfied with our burgers.
A very cool, eclectic room ! This was a great little room that was very clean ! Great pillows, great management on site. It was very close to the shopping district. Price was very reasonable.

I've always loved the food that Faubourg Bistro has done at 700 Club. It's always been inventive, delicious, and fresh. Matt and his team catered my wedding in Slidell for 160 people plus and I cannot say enough good things. From the very beginning Matt made suggestions to help the flow of the evening, presented an incredible menu to choose from, and I credit so much to him and his team as to why the wedding went so smoothly. All of my guests raved about the food. Matt's team also went above and beyond helping us set up and tear down the event. Matt checked out the venue multiple items and his care and attention to detail and planning meant the world. If you are in need of a caterer for an event, there is nowhere else you should seek out. And if you're not, just go by 700 Club and get some of their amazing food!! :D
Happy hour 3-6pm for $5 .the buck O chicken is worth it 4 big pieces for $5 and came with crunch fries is very worth of a meal !!!

I had the opportunity to checkout Taphouse 23 today for brunch with friends!  I have been there many times in the past, but I haven't been since COVID began..but glad we got the chance to give it a shot again!
I enjoy the openness of Taphouse 23...it was a beautiful day out and the windows were open ...it helped with the patrons comfortability level! 

The waitstaff were friendly and prompt and this helped with the extra star in my updated review!

The food continues to be consistently good!  Today, I had the Pork Belly as an appetizer and the Mussels for my main course!  Great decisions by me I must say..both were flavorful!

There are a good variety of cocktails and beer choices to compliment most people's tastes!

I am going to keep Taphouse 23 as a cool spot out in Bridgeport!
Delicious ribs good service way to good give the cook a raise now !!! He makes this place what it is the food I couldn't even save some for work I ate it all

Very good Indian food. I got a veggie thali which came with Saag Paneer, Dal makhani, Aloo mattar, tamarind & mint chutney, naan, saffron rice, onion bhajia and raita. My friend had lamb roganjosh, garhwali naan (naan filled with cheese and crack) and drums of heaven. Very good service, ambience and nice crowd. Highly recommended.
I really enjoy Humble Taco.  The food is decent and the margaritas are really good.  Just wish they would offer a frozen option.  May not be for the purist, but I really enjoy them frozen better.

Ahhh, I remember fondly the days that I could stop in regularly for a quick snack back when I lived right around the corner.  This place not only has great prepared foods, but also an excellent wine selection and unique international gourmet groceries -- somewhat surprising, given the university neighborhood.  The number of more expensive bottles on hand almost makes me question the business sense of the owner, but then I know that he's got the right idea.  Also check out B-Line and one of his now numerous other businesses serving the central Tucson area.

Beyond awful!  The dry-cleaning service is VERY BAD QUALITY.  They miss stains and they do not treat wrinkles.  The lady working at the front desk is rude and thinks it's funny to laugh when your clothes look worse than when you brought them to her to clean!  Don't bother asking for a refund or for her to re-clean your clothes -- she'll balk at the request!  DO NOT GO HERE!  There are many other dry cleaners in the city that are MUCH better than this place.

This is my favorite breakfast spot.  The omelette of the day never disappoints and the coffee is always wonderful.  Whether I'm there by myself at the counter, meeting friends for breakfast/lunch, or meeting any combination of my family there - we always walk away full of delicious food and an excellent atmosphere.  

This location is my favorite, but I'd pick any of the Patachou locations around town above any other breakfast place.  

It's a winner in my book.

I like this place. Clean. Good view. Breakfast buffett is good. Warm food. Pretty classy actually. You have to pay $50 valet mandatory as there is no other parking around. Unless you park far away and walk.
To me the beignet fries were so were so much better than cafe de monde across the street. The king cake beignet bites were also delicious.

John, I did not say anything while eating. There was another party in the restaurant at the time. 

However, in my opinion the issue is the fact that the cook (you) knowingly put out an inferior product. Do you deny buying lower quality bacon? Do you deny that your grill isn't properly cleaned? (foreign food bits were pronounced on the pancakes). Do you deny cutting corners? These denials are the issue, not me failing to rub your nose in it.
First time here and will definitely be back. We just moved to St. Pete and have been looking for an old school seafood place, we found it! Great service, apps and entries .
Slaw Pee Joe is my favorite! Great American food. Great feel to what you're eating. A frequent favorite of my friend and myself.

Went in, was told wait time would be 20 minutes. 35 minutes pass and still no update or acknowledgement as we're sitting in the lobby. We go up and ask if there's any change on the wait time and we're asked to wait an additional 15 minutes, since they're "overloaded" with To-Go orders. Even though the seating chart shows all of these tables open, and this group of 3 was sat before us even though they just entered. Absolutely ridiculous. Are we just supposed to sit here infinitely since it's too much of hassle to let us know we're wasting our time?

French Truck is my favorite place to get coffee in the entire city! I don't go there often, but I try to stop by whenever I'm in the area. I love love love their iced lattes with the almond & coconut milk blend. They don't make their own nut milks unfortunately but it's okay because their coffee more than makes up for it.

It's usually crowded but they move quickly and are always friendly.

Really cute place in the old New Orleans. Food is really good and tasty. Flavor combinations are great and all tasted fresh. Great experience when visiting NOLA. But for sure it is a touristy place, good thing was I by myself so I got a seat right away, otherwise waiting time for lunch on Saturday was more than 1h... so not sure if locals go there but it is really nice for the experience. Will definitely go back!!

Quickly becoming a favorite all around outdoor sports store. There corporate position is to give back, to the environment, there  employees, nice stance on things. The quality of the merchandise is through the zoom room. With all the cutting edge stuff anyone could ask for. Extremely knowledgeable staff that's totally into sharing there knowledge anyway they can to
Make a more positive impact on the client
Always great food and service. Outside tables are nice. Always had a different type pizza love them all yummy.

Best spot in the quarter for a strong drink and a meal that surpasses your typical bar food. Service is excellent, staff is friendly, music is good, and seating is spacious outside/inside. Y'all gotta come on by here!

Love the atmosphere and food at Pane Rustica! We went on a Sunday afternoon and the line wasn't too long. Food did take little longer than (my husband) expected but the place was packed. Everything had amazing flavor! We ordered a slice (HUGE!) of their bacon pizza, chicken Cesar salad, Smoked Teriyaki sandwich, and the kids had chicken fingers. All portions are very generous! This is like an upscale Panera bread.

First off, when places tend to mix two cultures' worth of food it rarely ever goes well. Second, who chooses Tex-Mex and Creole/Cajun to go together? Odd.

We ordered the steak/chicken fajitas and the chimichurri steak. Both were decent but really nothing special. I did get a side of jambalaya and it was easily the worst jambalaya I'd ever had. I guess it'd be fine if you'd never really had authentic creole food? Who knows.
I was rear-ended in my rental and the attendant went above and beyond to complete and make copies of my paperwork quickly so I would not be late for my flight.  Great service.

Stopped by for a quick breakfast egg, bacon, potato and bean burrito.  First off, the smell of the burrito was disgusting and when I finally decided to unwrap the burrito the quality of the tortilla was poor and consisted of half cooked eggs and a french fry or two, no beans and no bacon.  It was the worst burrito I had ever seen and didn't think twice about not eating it.  I called Viva and spoke with Flor who referred me to Eleanor (the manager).  Well, Eleanor, is Spanish speaking only, that's okay, I speak Spanish too.  When I voiced my concern, she blew it off. She didn't care nor wanted to resolve the problem.  Poor service, awful food and I will never go back there again.  DISGUSTING!

Have been back several times since my last review... 
The beer is still expensive and they removed my favorite appetizer (calamari) from the menu. 
That said, i'm adding a star because the service has improved and the live music is appreciated.

Great place for happy hour! Can't beat $1 oysters! Unfortunately I only tried the oysters and clams, and not the rest of the menu. Delicious happy hour cocktail which was a red sangria drink. Great atmosphere and great service. This place was recommended to me and I will recommend to others.
Good food and I would have given more stars if it wasn't for the service. The people are cold and unfriendly! Shockingly bad service. But fast and good typical Chinese food.
Nice atmosphere and good food! Always crowded but they keep the flow in s good way! Recommend and will go back again.

Nice burger joint with a fun atmosphere!! My friend and I stopped here for a bite to eat after coming in town to see the Van Gogh exhibit at Newfields. This was a quick drive away and we hit it at a good time when they weren't busy. Tasty burger and fries, plus we shared a "blackout ding dong cake" that we both loved. Nice little place to check out.

I ran into one of the actors from "The Wire" while at D.B.A for my first time.  And I was with 9 of my closest friends who all happen to be huge fans of the show.

I don't really remember much else after that, I think I blacked out.  It was just so exciting.

I'd have to say hands down the best pizza in South Tampa. Holy cow not only is it delicious but it's TASTY!!!! it has such amazing flavor to the crust and sauce, their ravioli is fantastic!!... Even the damn bread sticks are to die for!  I will never order pizza anywhere else again!!! They deliver too which is awesome and super convenient. Well done guys.
A must stop in New Orleans! Such a quaint and historical spot. Really enjoyed the muffuletta and pimms cup. Jambalaya was so so. Overall great service and food for a reasonable price.

Chalk-painted caucasian upcycled nonsense parading around as an antiques market. 

I'm a big fan of mid century architecture, clothing, cars, furniture, etc. and not in a weird rockabilly way. I just appreciate how much design and style that used to be put into everything. 

This is one of those places where bored housewives pick up gorgeous solid wood dressers at thrift stores and chalk paint the hell out of them and then try to hawk them for $300. Bonus points for painting "LIVE LAUGH LOVE" across the top of the dresser. There were a few original items peppered in but the majority was overpriced and already ruined. 

Every time I see Chip & Joanna Gaines take a sledgehammer to a gorgeous 1950s pink tile bathroom I die a little inside and this is how I felt walking through this place. 

But then something incredible happened...I found a cat inside the store. A friendly little black kitty cat with white feet and white on the tip of his (her?) tail. Suddenly all the shiplap and sliding barn doors faded away and I was in complete bliss just petting this cat. 

This market is only open once a month and they have another location in Ybor City that I want to check out. Its worth the drive just to pet this cat, seriously. 10/10 would pet again.

Their esquites are EVERYTHING! Ive tried quiet a few items on the menu and have absolutely loved all of them. Fantastic food with so much flavors and the menu makes you feel like youre really getting something unique. I just cannot get enough!

This was so delicious! My friend and I happened upon this place and it floored me with how delicious it was. First they give you a dope shot of tumeric, lime, and ginger. Then i had the butternut squash tamales which were some of the best tamales ive ever tasted and definitely the best black beans ive ever had. Also Wendy was super nice and came and said hi to us and you could easily tell her passion for making great food. And to top it all off the price was pretty grand too!

Just loved dinner at the bar. The salisbury steak special, the sea bass and the kale salad were all wonderful, but not as good as the service. The beers on tap were interesting and diverse, although no porters or stouts. We will be back.

They have very very good food made fresh. Big portions, great prices. Normally I have to pack it up to go. I love eating here. If I want some Mexican food I normally come here. They have tasty horchata and the decor is fun.

I wasn't quite due for an eye exam, but seeing how I recently obliterated my glasses I figured it was a good idea to update my prescription. Unfortunately my insurance only covers one exam every two years. So I turned to Groupon, and I am so glad I did - Baird Optical was quite the find! 

I have a total case of white coat syndrome, but Dr. Baird made me feel perfectly comfortable. I received a thorough eye exam; the update to my prescription I needed, and was thoroughly satisfied with the service. I was allowed to browse through the glasses and felt no pressure to buy a pair on the spot. I walked out with my prescription to return at my own convenience!

As a bonus he keeps evening hours which are awfully convenient, and provides easy scheduling through his website. It also doesn't hurt that he's easy on the eyes. #sorrynotsorry #punabsolutelyintended

Very worse place I've ever been. They got our order wrong. Never refilled our drinks even after asking. Choked down food. Half our party left to go get food somewhere else. The problem is they don't have enough people working. Two guys trying to do it all. Don't waste your time

My mother already had eaten here and really liked it. Gary and I were prompted to try it when a Groupon crossed my path. It was a perfect opportunity to give Pita Jungle a try! I think everyone and his mother must've shown up when we did, because of that Groupon. It was packed and the staff was running! But it was an upbeat, happy kind of busy. People were busy chomping down, in seating both inside and out, and they looked happy.
     The menu has something for everyone. Even if you are vegan or gluten-free, you'll find some options (although Pita comes with most items). It was a tough decision, as we had choices from what seemed like Middle Eastern, Greek, Indian and American fare. Gary and I settled on an appetizer of Baba Ganoosh, a very well prepared smoky, thick eggplant dip commonly eaten with pita bread. A+ in my book. Next, Gary got the chicken Shwarma (a seasoned meat) served with vegies in a huge pita. I got a beautifully plated Chicken Tikka Masala, rice in the middle surrounded by a "moat" filled with sauce and chicken. More A+ scores! It was a fantastic sauce, texture, and flavor, and I enjoyed every bite of my meal! We had a kiwi-strawberry green tea-tasty, but they need to watch out for melting ice watering down the iced tea's flavor.
     I saw a very impressive lavosh-like dish filled with meat and veggies going by to the table next to us. Looked like a signature dish I'll just have to sample next time.
     Our youthful server was delightful and caring. We chitchatted with an elderly woman eating a lamb pita concoction next to us. She enjoyed it as well. We were pleasantly surprised at the quality of the food, given it's a pretty speedy food establishment. It is sit-down, server style. Given how busy it was, the chefs/staff did a super job delivering our order within a very reasonable time period. 
      Also, I noticed a raised long table on one side, perfect if you come alone and want to sit with others. Nice way to meet others, too, if you're single. Very fun menu, great quality good, upbeat, contemporary ambiance. Can't wait to try something else on the menu next time. I think they will be around for a long while!

Best gelato ever. I can't get enough of the Coco and Rum. But there are a lot of good options and no one I know has ever been less than satisfied with their choices. You can also get takeout gelato to make sure your fridge is always stocked. 

I haven't dined here yet but I will update my review once I do.

Amazing!!! Authentic!!! Delicious!!!
Everything is made fresh to order and the Tajini sauce is the best I've ever had. 
The owners are friendly and happy to take a minute and talk to you :)
This place is a Must Visit next time I am in Tuscon
Thank you Just Kabob for making me a delicious meal and making me feel welcome.
Great place..... I had a pizza, open oven cooked   It was really cook to perfection large serving. It was more than enough had some for later. Enjoyed this place   Will be back

Garbage. 

I stopped in today for SNS- it's not quite everywhere yet, I needed a quick fix, and saw that they do it. When I arrived it was quiet, occupied but not busy. The man at the desk told me it would be $40- typical.
