So I sat and waited a few minutes, and before the tech started, she said something (I couldn't understand) to the man- and he told me it will be SEVENTY FIVE DOLLARS. I literally laughed at them- I thought they had to be kidding, especially since he'd already quoted me $40. He asked if I usually go "here-" I said no- and he said "Ohhhh, well rittenhouse very expensive." HE GAVE ME THE SNOTTY SALES LADY IN PRETTY WOMAN TREATMENT. 

OH. Well excuse me. I'll retreat back to "my side of the tracks." Yknow, I don't get out much, I had no idea that rittenhouse place is so expensive. 

Thankfully I was able to get in last second with someone who's done my nails a few times, and takes great pride in doing it meticulously. I didn't bother trying originally because it was so last minute, but she squeezed me in- for $37.

This restaurant would make Gordon Ramsay's head explode. The pages-long laminated menu with pictures of entrees lets diners know that Lincoln Square doesn't really have a specialty. If you're looking for great ambience and fresh food, go somewhere else. I had a veggie wrap that was choc full of pre-cut, formerly frozen vegetables. I hear, though, that breakfast is a markedly better experience, but I won't be returning to try it.

Odd name, but great service.  I first started using Dick and Rips about 13 years ago when my office moved to the area.  Since that time, dozens of my colleagues have entrusted Dick and Rips for their auto services, tires and more.  Chuck (the service manager) is honest, pleasant and courteous.  He has saved me a stack of cash on a number of occasions.  Case in point, one of the aluminum wheels on my wife's car was cracked.  Rather than sell me a new wheel at $600, he found an aluminum welding shop that made it good as new for $200.  When I needed a tire removed from a rim on my RV, he did it for free.  This place rocks.

Kind of a dive inside, some quirky details such as the puffer fish lights but clearly needs an update.  The food is pretty good though.

I had the ginger beef and it was great, the mushrooms soaked up the ginger flavor like little sponges.  

The hot and sour soup was disappointing though, more like hot and sweet.  It wasn't bad but just didn't fall into what hot and sour soup should be.

Overall it was good food and I will have to give their cocktails a try.

I was here visiting from Texas. My sister lives in New Jersey and we chose to try Legal. The staff was awesome - our waitress, an expecting redhead, was knowledgeable, efficient and attentive- even when she dropped my plate she shined. 

The manager Peter promptly offered a replacement and something for me to eat while i waited. 

Awesome staff.. Professional and engaging. Not to mention funny and good sports. 

Love this place and highly recommend it.
The creme Brule was too caramelized on top burned and inside had a too soft texture. I wanted to tell waitress but she was too busy. The establishment should not have even served it.

Still absolute trash, run by a seriously terrible person who has no respect for food, no respect for customers, and is willing to serve both rancid and raw ingredients to customers.  Places like this should be run out of town on a rail - the only reason garbage can thrive like this in Reno is because the locals allow it to.  Stop paying for bad food!!!
Es un lugar familiar y la comida es excelente, grandes porciones llenas de sabor! 100%

The wild mushroom and leek pasta was not good. I had to reorder 3 times because they were out. Barely any sauce and pasta did not feel fully cooked. Not much taste at all, felt like I could make this better at home. 3 stars because the staff was nice and spinach gnocchi pasta was the only good pasta. service was also 
slow.

Super busy parking lot, crazy busy inside but they are very organized.  When we walked in the door we were directed to the next available DMV associate who was extremely pleasant and oh so friendly.  She checked us in and in no time we were called back to be assisted.  Everything went smoothly and as quickly as it posaibly could have gone.  This building was surprisingly clean and the experience wasn't the nightmare I had imagined it would be or had experienced in the past!  Yay!!

Cool place in the Westin, 27th floor, for drinks and apps. You'll see a lot of "reserved" tables empty. They are for hotel guests which we were but didn't know to reserve. Outside space, great city views.

I cannot pass up the smell of caramelizing sugar!  Pralines & Candy Apples were delicious!  & Pecan Fudge was eye-rolling  good!

Any time I'm in a city with a Savannah's Candy Kitchen, I'm there!

Only photos can describe the variety of old-fashioned treats in this Southern confectionery!

I first came to this place on New Years Eve. As soon as I saw Czech Pilsner I knew I wanted to stop in. Unfortunately, because it was stale and warm I went with an IPA and I was good. I went with the Kobe burger and was please. Everything down to the fries were cooked perfectly for me. I would definitely check this place out again. Just keep in mind, service is slow and I found feeling somewhat invisible for a moment. But my inner San Dog came out and I got what I came for. So guess they need a push for better service.

I try to get all of my meet from this place.  It is always fresh and tastes great.  They have great deals on 3 pounds of ground beef or turkey.  They also have the best bacon you will ever eat in your life!! It even smells better that store bought bacon while it is cooking!!! They also have a nice selection of cheese and butter :-) Butter makes me smile... :-)

Decent pie's and other deserts, but sadly very poor customer service. Ordered pies here for Thanksgiving, also sent an email confirming availability, yet when I called in they said that the pies are unavailable and that no exception can be made because order date has passed. Will have to see if somewhere else has pre-order still open ... disappointing.

I'm giving it a two because of the fries.   

The good. The place isn't crowded.   That may be a sign though. 

The bad.  Why is it that a new place opens up and they have po-boys?   Tons of places out there like that, but somehow another pops up.   I didn't know this walking in.   Why not bbq meat?   Who cares that the Joint was just there...Bywater could handle another one.   Alas, this is the basic menu of Jugheads.

The ugly.   Heavy Metal, Acid Rock playing.   Not so appetizing. 

The food.   Chicken Gravy Po-Boy.   The chicken was cooked to perfection, but had too much breading.   Better if they had a grilled option for this sandwich.   The fries were probably the best I've tasted in awhile.

The service.  Cook and server were nice and the food came out in a reasonable amount of time.

Sadly, I doubt with this menu that this restaurant will be a success.   Noodle shop?  Grilled meat on a stick?  Something other than po-boys.
Great food and atmosphere. The lemon pancakes and avocado toast were amazingly delicious as was the sunny side salad. If I lived here I'd make this a regular habit

3-star food. 

But... 5-star service!

This place is very understated in its decor - definitely not pretentious and feels a bit like a cross between a cafeteria-style and sit-down restaurant. Definitely casual. When we arrived the Chef was chillin' outside. We'd never eaten here before and our waiter was super helpful (even though he was young and new - he really gave us great guidance and was very personable! It was clear he wanted to do a great job). 

We wanted to try a bunch of different items on the menu and ended up ordering the following (for 2 people - the first 3 items are appetizers):

1. ŠOPSKA SALATA-BOSNIAN STYLE SALAD (3-stars)
2. BOSNIAN STYLE TZATZIKI AND AJVAR (VEGETABLE SPREAD) (3-stars)
3. SARMA U JAPRAKU-STUFFED GRAPE LEAVES (4-stars)
4. .GRILLED CHICKEN BREASTS (3-stars)

During our visit, the Chef hand-delivered every person's order to their table. It was clear he genuinely cared how we felt about our food. At the end of our meal, he told us we should order the Chef Alisha's Surprise Plate next time...

We picked the items we did because we don't eat red meat. The truth is that the chicken was good but a bit bland and nothing really special (thus, the 3-stars). However, from what our waiter told us - the real winner at this restaurant is the Beef Goloush (the dish made famous by "Diner's, Drive-In's, and Dives"). Everything we ate that was more traditional to the restaurant was good - like the stuffed grape leaves and the tzatziki. 

So in summary: Excellent service, but only go here if you're willing to truly try the authentic Bosnian dishes (i.e. you eat red meat). Their other food is a bit bland... However, we liked it because it felt healthy (i.e. wasn't over sauced, greasy, etc.). But... we wouldn't necessarily go back any time soon because I can make healthy bland food on my own. This is sadly why I can't give more than 3-stars even though I want to support them.

Whatever you do, if you place an order for a special occasion cake from this place, be aware that they close early - 2pm. They will not call you on pickup day that your cake is ready. Then if you come anytime after 2pm to get the cake you dropped $60 for, you're out of luck. Plan on an alternate dessert plan for the special occasion. They will make sure to call you after the special occasion is over so they can get the cake off their hands. The food is also mediocre at best.

Some of the best pupusas and tamales in New Orleans!  I've been maybe seven times and have not had a bad meal or bad services.  Prices are good too!

They appear to get your beer from the bar next door but this is more amusing than a hassle.
I love Raley's... this is my favorite place to get my groceries, everyone is so helpful and Manager Sean is awesome. Very helpful

Excellent, friendly service with good food. If it was about 20% less expensive, I'd definitely be back and give them 5 stars.

Really cool vibe from this small Cafe and Coffee Shop on the Northside of Nashville. Everything we ordered was good, some bordered on excellent.

Live music on Sunday morning. Not too loud, just a guy and an acoustic guitar which added to a nice experience here.

Delicious! I got the chicken korma, samosas, and naan and was completely full. One curry can easily feed two or last you for two days. My only two complaints are that A) the cost, as is with most Indian restaurants, is very steep. And B) the parking isn't great. But the interior is really cozy and intimate. This would be a great place for a date!

Don't stay here....Terrible front desk! Stood at the front desk for at least 10 minutes waiting to check in. Lo and behold,  the front desk person was the woman I passed on the way in. She stayed outside sitting on a bench and smoking a cigarette while I waited for her to finish. She checked me in with a 1st floor room. Before I went to the room I asked if she had something on a higher floor.  She said no. The room was terrible (landscaping lights shone in window/noisy/ no privacy). I went back to the front desk and said I wasn't going to stay in the room. She argued with me saying the room was fine and I had to stay in it. As I started to leave the hotel she said she would find me another room on the 2nd floor.  This clerk named Dawn was horrible.  Never will stay at a Holiday Inn again.

I can't say enough about this school!  My son had an awful experience at Educare Preschool and we are blessed to have found this school!  My son's teacher, Miss. Christine is so kind and warm hearted!  She has a family photo album for the kids to look at whenever they want - she hugs my son and comforts him if he's ever sad.  My son just loves her so much!  The office staff is very informative and their attention to detail is spot on!  They have a cleaning lady constantly sanitizing the rooms and toys all day everyday!  The school smells AMAZING!!!  They have the best fingerprint system to check your child in and out and you need a special key to get through the doors.  I feel safe leaving him there all day.  It's one of those too good to be true kind of preschools but it really is true!  Bright Beginnings is a blessing

Look no further...your perfect breakfast awaits at The Frog Pond. Warm, friendly greetings "Good Morning Friends" and quick seating. Our server Kelsey was the right amount of chipper, knowledgeable and welcoming. The menu was incredible; something for everyone. The food came out within about 10 minutes, piping hot and FRESH! Let's not forget about the strawberry preserves made "with love." Delicious! We will visit again before our vacation ends.

Typical small airport! In and out of security line in about 5 to 8 minutes. Rental car check in and check out is a breeze. Everything appears to be well laid out and well organized. The personnel are mostly friendly and willing to assist.

This restaurant is a horrible establishment. Spend less time scolding employees and overwhelming patrons  (trying to shove margaritas, apps, specific entrees, desserts down their throats... the server sounds like a miserable robot relaying the spiel), and more time actually listening to what the patron wants. The server seemed great. She was extremely busy, but nonetheless rocking it and keeping a smile on. Table across from us came in after us, received food before us, complained that it took too long, and got it discounted. We waited for our food patiently. The manager never checked on us. They kissed ass to the people complaining. We were a little more decent than them, and received no kind of attention from the managers. We did witness our server, however, getting a "talking to" by a manager while at a computer. How unprofessional! 

Overall, the food was okay and the place was packed, so the amount of time we waited for our meals was understandable. Had we not had to witness the way the managers "run" the place, we would have left with a much more positive experience. Our server was absolutely wonderful for all that she put up with.

Fountain Street Auto is one of the most reliable, dependable shops you will find. I have been to plenty of places that will charge $300 to replace a rear view mirror, and this is not one of those places. Brian will give you an honest assessment of the problem and tell you what needs to be done. Most recently, my check engine light was on, my car had an extremely rough idle and would not exceed 2,000 RPMs. The AAA tow truck told me that I might need to replace the transmission. Yikes.   After the guys at Fountain St replaced my O2 sensor for far less than the cost of transmission work, my car sounds better than it has in 6 months.

At one point we really enjoyed this place but on our last visit we got burnt nachos with a chopped up hamburger thrown on top and congealed cheese that looked like it sat under a heat lamp for who knows how long. Server was slow, didn't check up often, and when we complained about the nachos and a manager came over, she simply threw down a coupon for a free appetizer on our next visit and walked away. Too far gone for me to want to give them a second chance.
I went a couple months ago and got Cajun chicken wings and fries. Had a lot of flavor but was definitely on the salty side

I would not recommend this nail salon to anyone! I stopped in for a mani and pedi since it was so close to home and was not impressed! When I first walked in no one greeted me or even asked what I was getting done, they didn't even ask to pick a color! They started getting busy and I felt like my pedicure was rushed! I specifically stated I wanted a gel manicure with the same color polish on my toes! That did not happen, the colors did not match! I don't think I will be returning!

Fast, cheap, yummy. Key is to not order the BOGO items. The First visit, we had the BOGO cubans...hence the previous unhappy review. This time we were HUNGRY and got a reg. gyro, Jr. Gyro, 10 mild wings, and cheese fries (Oink oink)!!

The large gyro was too much for hubby to eat, the Jr. Would've been perfect for him. But the taste was dead on perfect. They had a lil sauce inside to flavor the meat, and a small side of sauce to use while eating :) 

The wings were AMAZINGLY crunchy! We wished they had some buffalo or wing sauce on em. They were dry wings and had ranch for dipping.  

The cheese fries were kick A$$ :) 

Now off to digest and run the guilt away. Soooooo worth it!  Btw we couldnt even come close to finishing any of it.
Definitely recommend. I had the Cajun shrimp boil and it was very tasty. Shrimp were fresh as advertised. Look forward to my next visit.

You hit the nail on the head Jill, Eleven Eleven was heaven. On my date night last week I had the most wonderful time. Now some credit has to be given to Josh for being a fantastic date, but the food was pretty darn fantastic. We started out with the stuffed artichoke bottoms, perfect balance of spicy sausage and sweeter tomato sauce. Then I had the bacon wrapped pork tenderloin. How can you go wrong with pork wrapped in more pork? It was cooked to perfection with a delicate fig sauce. Only critique on that dish would be the three cheese risotto. I had much higher expectations. It came across more like a heavy sticky rice with a cheesy aftertaste. Regardless, my mouth is watering just remembering. I rounded out the evening (and my hips) with the bread pudding. It couldn't have been much better. That being said, my date's steak was beyond over cooked and was sent back, but I'll leave that to his own review. 

The wine list was limited, but not brief. There were far more options by the bottle than by the glass. And who am I to argue with a bottle? Brian, our server, was attentive for most of the meal. That was until he was given a large private party and basically disappeared. But I can't hold that against him. He was apologetic and understanding.

Whether I'm looking for somewhere nice to have a sit down meal, or looking for a quick to-go breakfast on my way to work, this place is always one of the first that comes to mind. The portions are huge! The service is quick and friendly. The food is amazing. There's not much more I could possibly wish for from The Cup Café!

The crunchy French toast with a side of their thick cut bacon is as close to perfection as it gets.

Breaking Grounds is my favorite coffee shop and cafe hands down! Where to begin? One of my favorite aspects of Breaking Grounds is the atmosphere.  The employees are so friendly, helpful, and want to make you feel right at home - well, they are successful! The cafe is decorated with beautiful art (also for sale) created by autistic artists.  As the weather warms, there is outdoor seating. 

But what's a coffee shop without delicious coffee? Breaking Grounds has that too! The Bali is my favorite and there is a delicious French press as well.  They also have a wonderful array of teas.  The food, from the sandwiches and paninis to the pastries and bagels, are all yummy!  I have yet to order something that hasn't hit the spot.  Bring your kids while you catch up with friends. There are games, books and hot chocolate that can keep the children happy and occupied.

Breaking Grounds employs developmentally disabled adults and the proceeds go to Zefer Foundation.  Next week, the Zefer Foundation is launching the Zefer Arts Alliance, across the street from Breaking Grounds.  I'm looking forward to checking out the Zefer Arts Alliance.  If it's anything like Breaking Grounds, it will be amazing!

One of the all time great Philadelphia eateries and a prime example of AMAZING Italian Market food. I've been coming here as long as I can remember and pretty much ordering the same thing every time.
Roast pork, provolone, raw onions.
Easy.
Basic.
Delicious.
Filling.
This is the third best roast pork sandwich I've ever had (first being Shank's near Penn's Landing, P&G Deli in Prospect Park). The bread is excellent and the sharp prov melts perfectly. You MUST eat this sandwich within 5 to 8 minutes (even 8 is pushing it) or it will be soggy and messy,.
Don't go inside, order from the window, get it wrapped up and eat at one of the tables in front of Lorenzo's right next door. Everytime I'm even remotely in the area, I have to get a sandwich from George's, it's the best. The roast beef is very good too. Same deal as the pork, messy and wet, very lovely and tender meat. I'm not a huge tripe guy, but the sauce they use is excellent.
That's all i've had from George's.
Why order anything else?

I went in to the store yesterday and took my sweet time browsing through sections as this was my only break from being home with my kids. 
I noticed that the larger woman with short hair who had greeted me at the front door was inconspicuously (I'm sure she was hoping she was) following me from department to department.. first through the make up aisle then the hair section. I actually stopped in the hair section and started asking her about straightening products they offered. She was very helpful with showing me a few items. 

I usually get my hair products from Aveda salons where I get my hair done so I'm actually rather skeptical of anything they had to offer. 

I then started browsing the skin aisles which are on the complete opposite side of the hair section and the larger lady with short hair seemed to find her way there too. I didn't say anything because I had nothing to hide.. but it's a horrible way to feel as a customer. 

I'm actually a licensed esthetician who has worked at Miraval, massage envy and European wax center in the past and am used to much more expensive products than what was offered ULTA.. I was here because I was completely out of face wash and moisturizer at home and instead of ordering my usual products online and waiting for shipping I would just rather go buy something. I had used Clinique as a teenager and my mother sold Lancôme as well and I knew ULTA had SOME of their items. 

I went to pay for my items and low and behold the larger woman followed me there and even had another worker come around from behind the counter while I was paying for my items to see if I had stolen anything there.. like I said I didn't say anything because I had nothing to hide but the entire experience was an embarrassment for everyone involved. 

Now I walked in wearing a $200 coach purse, my $300 lucky brand eye glasses, my Tommy Hilfiger sandals and was not dressed down.. the only  thing I could think of that COULD have made me look suspicious depending on the level of intellect the person had that was judging me, was that I was black. There were plenty of other people walking around the store but I was the only person being followed. 

I will never shop here again. I may as well have gone to a Walgreens in the hood.

I give 5 stars because my experience was beyond great. Yes I was there in the middle of the afternoon after the lunch rush. The staff were friendly and all working very hard. The employee suggested the chicken avocado BLT which was delicious. Bread was fresh, the lettuce tomato and avocado was extremely fresh. Chicken could have been more seasoned however that's not allowed in public restaurants because of the public's many health issues. The restaurant itself was very clean considering the construction going on next door to them. I had a wonderful healthy lunch for under $10. Also it was such a hot day and the mall was not very cool but this restaurant was very cool. The owner obviously cares about the comfort of his customers and employees. I will return.
We love this place! So unique and I love their cider selection!! I'm gluten free and completely feel comfortable here!!

What a great atmosphere and a terrific selection of wines
Right in the BoDo area
Great time and a few TVs for all the sports

Crappy food and service!!! Everything was bland and too expensive! $13 for a small BLT nasty buffalo wings with the damn feathers still on the wings! Don't waste your money here unless your drunk and starving! Also the fettuccini Alfredo was tasteless as well!

My dining experience at Mona Lisa was absolutely wonderful.  Our server and another woman (who I assumed was an owner or manager) were very attentive and seemed genuinely interested in making sure our experience was enjoyable.  I took advantage of the free glass of wine for mentioning yelp, which was a delicious glass of  L'Ardi Dolcetto, a lighter red, that I may not have thought to try otherwise.  When I was ready for another glass with my meal, our server did an excellent job of offering a pairing.  Also, when I mentioned that I had a dairy allergy, I felt confident that they knew how to accomodate this. 

For my meal I ordered one of the specials, which was talapia over angel hair pasta.  It was a delightful medley of seafoody goodness (talapia, lobster, and shrimp).  They had a very extensive dessert list as well, which included several liqueurs, including 4 different kinds of cellos (raspberry, peach, orange, and of course lemon).  I will definitely be returning to try another meal and will probably be stopping at the bar after a long day at work to sample another delicious wine...or cello.

We stumbled into this restaurant after walking for a while. It looked so amazing we couldn't pass it by. What a great decision. There is a wine store in the back that you can peruse your own bottle of wine for the table. Food was amazing: tomato soup to die for. Scallops were delicate. Cannoli sweet and tender. Wine amazing. HIGHLY recommended.

Came here on a Wednesday and had a free chicken sandwich and 2 pieces of chicken through t-mobile. The drive thru line appeared long so i mobile ordered and went inside to pick up.

Upon entering, the line for service was long (at least 8 people) and the interior was truthfully filthy. There were napkins, uncooked fries, and even a piece of chicken on the floor in the back. Employees also seemed to have no care about restaurant appearance either. I saw some in the back with no mask on eating and employees handle money, pick up food, and grab new food with the same glove.

I waited over 25 minutes inside the restaurant for a sandwich and 2 pieces of chicken. I understand some delay but 25 minutes! Many other customers seemed fed up and the drive thru line didn't seem any faster.

I asked for spicy on all my chicken, but i saw the employee grab mild pieces of chicken instead, right in front of me. The food itself was not good, the chicken was very dry and heavy (despite asking for light meat) and the sandwich seemed poorly assembled. Sandwich also had little flavor. Also the sauce portion is nothing close to what i believe you need for the food portion.

Even though it was free, i was still extremely disappointed by the atmosphere, cleanliness, service, and taste. I would not recommend this establishment to anyone and this is my first ever popeyes experience.

As other users said this is an UPSCALE corner store/deli.  

They have a great concept for buying beer in PA and their selection is unmatched in the city.  Don't go in here trying to find a bargain though, a 6 pack of hand chosen beer can cost anywhere from $15-$30.  They have a deli stocked with Boar's Head meats and a small selection of pre-packed goods, Asian candies , chips, and other small snacks.  

Overall this is a great place to find a good bottle of beer, but just be ready to spend a few $$$.

We called and made a reservation for 8pm- same day reservation. No one told us anything about any unusual aspects of this restaurant. We came and no tables were available but were seated about 15 or so minutes after we arrived. We ordered our food and then waited 2 hours for our dinner. 2 hours after being seated. They don't do drinks- we ordered one salad. But it took 2 hours- we ate at 10pm. I asked where our food was and she told us "oh didn't you know, meals here take 2 1/2 hours".  No we weren't. I have ate at fine dinning restaurants all over the world and never have I waited 2 hours for flavorless chewy steak. The restaurant holds about ten tables - three waiters. She also noted because I substituted a side that added 5 or so minutes to my wait time- but apparently green beans take more than 5 minutes - they take 2 hours. I'm sorry but we're talking about a restaurant in perkasie - not in freakin some posh area in center city- get over yourselves.
